title: For Clever Admins: Configuring languages in Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting your own language 3. Setting the language for your school organization 4. How teachers configure student languages 5. Frequently asked questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is available in multiple languages and locales including Spanish, Australian English, and British English! As an Clever admin account owner, you can set your own language preference, set a default language for your entire school organization, and, if needed, step in to help configure language settings for students via their teacher’s profile. This article covers all three scenarios. Once a language is set, it'll stay in place every time that user logs back into Clever.

 Please note: This feature is currently in early access. If you don’t see the options for updating languages described in this article, they’ll be available to you soon! 

 

 
 Setting your own language

 You can update your personal language preference from your Clever Dashboard.

 • 
 Click your name in the top right corner of the dashboard.

• 
 Select Profile .

• 
 On the Overview page, find the language setting and select your preferred language.

 
 Your Dashboard will display in your chosen language every time you log in.

 

 
 Setting the language for your school organization

 Admins can set a default language for their entire school organization. This setting applies to students, teachers, and staff. Please note that individual language settings will override the school organization’s preferred language.

 • 
 From your Clever Dashboard, navigate to Portal .

• 
 Select Portal Settings .

• 
 Click Customization .

• 
 Choose your preferred default language and save.

 
 

 

 Please note: If a teacher has already set a language for an individual student, that student-level setting won't be overwritten by an org-level change. 

 

 
 How teachers can configure student languages

 Teachers play an important role in managing language settings for their students. They can update language settings for individual students or an entire class through their Teacher Portal. Here's how each works and when you might need to step in.

 

 Teacher updates an individual student's language 

 Teachers can go to My Students > Student Roster , select a class, click the student's name, and choose Update Language under Student Tools .

 

 Teacher updates an entire class's language 

 Teachers can go to My Students > Student Roster , select a class, click More Class Tools , and select Update Language to apply a language to all students in that class at once.

 If a teacher isn't available to update a student's language, you can use Clever's Access Portal As feature to act on their behalf. This lets you log in as that teacher and make the update directly from their Teacher Portal view.

 Please note: Remember, any changes you make while in a teacher’s Portal are live changes. Exercise caution when impersonating and making changes in a Teacher Portal. 

 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 Can students set their own language? 

 • 
 Not at this time. Language settings for students are controlled by teachers or admins to keep choices aligned with how schools manage bilingual or multilingual instruction.

 
 Who can set the default language for a school organization? 

 • 
 Currently only Clever Admin Owners can set the default language for the school organization.

 
 If I set a default language for the whole org, will it override individual student settings? 

 • 
 No. Language settings applied at the student level by a teacher take priority and won't be overwritten by a school-level change.

 
 If a student's Clever Portal is in Spanish, will their apps also be in Spanish? 

 • 
 Not automatically. Clever's language setting applies to the Clever Portal only. Whether an individual app is available in Spanish depends on that app's own configuration. Check with each app provider for guidance. If the application is available in Spanish, then update your language preferences in both Clever and that application for a consistent experience.

 
 What about users with dual roles like a teacher who's also an STL? 

 • 
 Language preference is set at the user level, not the role level. So whatever language a user selects will apply regardless of which role they're currently in.

 
 Do you have resources I can share with other administrators or teachers? 

 • Yes. Please check out our one-pagers for administrators and teachers. 
 
 Is this feature available at no additional cost? 

 • 
 Yes! Language settings are available to all schools on Clever at no extra charge.

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: How do I update my profile settings? 

• 
 For Teachers: Configuring languages in Clever 

• 
 Clever Portal: Overview

title: For Teachers: Configuring languages in Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting your own language 3. Setting languages for students 4. Frequently asked questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is available in multiple languages and locales including Spanish, Australian English, and British English! You can customize the language your Clever Portal displays both for yourself and for your students. This article walks you through how to set your own language preference, update the language for an individual student, and change the language for an entire class. Once a language is set, it'll stay in place every time that user logs back into Clever. 

 Please note: This feature is currently in early access. If you don’t see the options for updating languages described in this article, they’ll be available to you soon! 

 

 
 Setting your own language

 You can set your own language preference directly from your Clever Portal at any time.

 • 
 Navigate to the profile icon in the top right corner of your Portal.

• 
 Select Account Settings .

• 
 Under Language Settings , choose your preferred language from the drop-down.

 
 Your Portal will now display in your chosen language every time you log in.

 

 
 Setting languages for students

 You have two options for updating student language settings: one student at a time or an entire class in bulk.

 Updating an individual student's language 

 • 
 From your Teacher Portal, go to My Students .

• 
 Select Student Roster , then click on the student's class.

• 
 Click the student's name.

• 
 Under Student Tools , select Update Language .

• 
 Choose the language from the pop-up and save.

 
 

 

 Updating the language for all students in a class 

 • 
 From your Teacher Portal, go to My Students .

• 
 Select Student Roster , then choose the class you'd like to update.

• 
 Click More Class Tools .

• 
 Select Update Language and choose the language for the class.

• 
 If you have a student with a different language preference from the reset of the class, you’ll still be able to customize individual student languages as needed.

 
 

 

 Please note: Remember, once you configure language settings they’ll stay in place every time that user logs into Clever. 

 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 Can students set their own language? 

 • 
 Not at this time. Language settings for students are managed by teachers or admins. This keeps language choices consistent with how your school structures bilingual or multilingual instruction.

 
 Will the language setting reset if an admin changes settings at the school level? 

 • 
 No! A language you set for an individual student won't be overwritten by school-level changes. Student-level settings always take priority.

 
 If a student's Clever Portal is in Spanish, will their apps also be in Spanish? 

 • 
 Not automatically. Clever's language setting only applies to the Clever Portal itself. Whether an app is available in Spanish depends on that app's own settings. Check with each app provider for guidance. If the application is available in Spanish, then update your language preferences in both Clever and that application for a consistent experience.

 
 When I set the language preference for a class, can I still change it for an individual student? 

 • 
 Yes. While setting the language at the class level is a quick way to update a large group of students, you can still customize individual students' languages outside the class preference.

 
 What if I have both a teacher and STL role? 

 • 
 Your language preference applies to your user account, not your role. So whichever language you set will follow you regardless of which role you're logged in as.

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Teachers: Overview of the Clever Portal

title: Clever + TrustEd Apps Management Suite (TAMS): Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Step 1: Reach out to Clever support 3. Step 2: Setup in TrustEd Apps Management Suite 4. Step 3: Launch app in Clever 

 

 Overview

 Clever offers SSO via LTI 1.3 to the TrustEd Apps Management Suite (TAMS) app provided by the 1EdTech consortium. To connect with the TAMS app, please follow these steps. 

 
 Step 1: Reach out to Clever support

 Reach out to Clever support to request an invitation to connect to the TAMS app. Once this request has been made, support will reach out within 1-3 business days with a unique Client ID for you to copy and paste into your TAMS dashboard. 

 
 Step 2: Setup in TrustEd Apps Management Suite

 • 
 Log in to your TAMS dashboard as an administrator and navigate to the Compatibility Check portal.

• 
 From Compatibility Check , select TrustEd Apps and choose Setup .

• 
 From Connection Details , copy and paste the following URLs into the corresponding input fields. Remove the angle brackets < > when copying URL values.

 • 
 Name: <your preferred name for the TAMS app>

• Client ID: <Clever support will send you a client ID to copy/paste>
• Issuer ID: https : //www . clever . com
• OIDC URL: https : //clever . com/oauth/lti/anon/receive/67a1459fc73e7b92121ab6ff
• JWKS URL: https : //clever . com/oauth/lti/jwks
 

 
 
 Step 3: Launch app in Clever

 Once the connection in TAMS is established, accept the app invite in Clever and configure your application sharing permissions. If you need guidance on setting up sharing permissions, please see this article .

title: SFTP Syncs: Understanding Clever's SFTP Specifications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Why each file matters 3. Understanding key fields and requirements 4. Frequently asked questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 If you’re utilizing an SFTP sync or an SIS-Managed Auto Sync, Clever uses SFTP (Secure File Transfer Protocol) to safely move data from your SIS (Student Information System) into Clever.

 Think of these SFTP specifications as a blueprint . When you provide data in the correct format, you're building a bridge that allows students and teachers to access their learning apps instantly. Even if you're using an Identity Provider like Google for sign-in, these files are the essential foundation that tells Clever who your users are so they can be matched to their accounts. 

 By understanding the "why" behind our data requirements, you can ensure a smoother sync and a better experience for your end users. 

 
 Why each file matters

 Clever’s SFTP upload consists of five core files (schools.csv, students.csv, teachers.csv, sections.csv, enrollments.csv). For your initial sync to run successfully, we require all 5 files, even if you're only using sample data for some of them. Each file plays a specific role in creating a complete picture of your school organization:

 • schools.csv : This is your foundation. It identifies every building in your school organization so we can group users and sections correctly. Even if you are a single school organization, you still must include a schools.csv with your single school listed so that Clever can associate all other data in your account with this school.
• students.csv : The source for student accounts. Without this, students can't log in to the Clever Portal.
• teachers.csv : This identifies the educators. It creates their accounts and allows them to manage their digital classrooms and use tools like Teacher Pages.
• sections.csv : These are the "classes." This file defines the groups where learning happens (e.g., 3rd Period Algebra). The sections.csv must exist so that classes can be tied to a school, and teachers can be assigned to those sections.
• enrollments.csv : This file connects students to the specific sections created via the sections.csv above. Without this, a student might be in Clever but won't see any of their assigned apps or class resources. 
 
 

 
 Understanding key fields and requirements

 Each field in the SFTP specification serves a specific purpose for authentication, rostering, or user experience. Clever has a limited set of required fields, but there are additional fields that it may be helpful to sync to ensure your end users have the best experience with Clever. In the table below, we’ve summarized required and recommended fields for syncing data to Clever. For a full list of accepted fields and values, please see our Clever SFTP specifications .

 

 
 Field 

 
 Required or recommended? 

 
 Rationale 

 
 Impact 

 
 Unique Identifiers (school_id, student_id, teacher_id, section_id) Required These are the most critical pieces of data. They act as the source of truth that ties a user to their data across different files. For example, the student_id in your students.csv must match the student_id in enrollments.csv, so Clever knows which student belongs in which class. If these IDs change in your SIS , Clever treats them as brand-new users or sections. This can result in "duplicate" accounts and, more importantly, a student losing their progress or saved work in their learning applications. 
 First_name and last_name Required We need names to identify accounts within the Clever Dashboard and the Portal. Name data is essential for identifying user accounts and customizing their experience. 
 User emails Recommended - may be required dependent on your setup For most school organizations, the email address is the match point between Clever and your IdP ( Identity Provider ), such as Google or Microsoft. If the email in Clever doesn't match the email in your IdP , SSO ( Single Sign-On ) will fail because Clever won't know which Google or Microsoft account belongs to that user. 
 User Number (teacher_number, school_number, student_number) Recommended This can be the same as your user ID number.
 Some applications specifically look for user numbers, while Clever uses the user ID for internal linking.

 If an application requires a student_number or teacher_number and it isn’t populated, this can cause data compatibility issues. 
 
 

 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 Why can’t I just upload a list of students? 

 • 
 A list of students tells us who is in the school organization, but not where they're learning. We need the other files (such as enrollments and sections) to ensure they're connected to the right teachers and schools.

 
 What happens if I leave a required field blank? 

 • 
 If a required field is missing, the record (or the entire file) may fail to sync. You'll see these errors in your Clever Dashboard under the Sync section, where you can troubleshoot exactly what's missing.

 
 Can I use my own headings for the CSV files? 

 • While it is recommended to use the headers listed in Clever's specifications, if you're manually uploading data to the Clever Dashboard, you'll have the option to map the headers used in your files to the relevant fields in Clever. Learn more here. 
 
 Why can’t I sync data directly from Google or Active Directory to Clever? 

 • 
 While Google handles authentication, it typically doesn’t store rostering data such as which student is in which teacher's 3rd-period math class. The CSV files provide this essential mapping.

• 
 If you want to avoid creating users in two places, Clever offers IDM (Identity Management). This tool takes your MIS data and automatically creates the users in Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID for you!

 
 Do I have to provide the student_number if I already provided a student_id ? 

 • While student_number isn't required, and both student_id and student_number may be the same, we recommend including both because some applications specifically look for a Student Number, while Clever uses the ID for internal linking. Providing both ensures maximum compatibility with all your apps.
 
 Why do I need a school_id in every file? 

 • 
 The school_id ensures that every user, section, and enrollment is associated with the correct physical location. 

 
 
 Related articles: 

 • SFTP Syncs: Overview 
• SFTP Syncs: SFTP Sync FAQ 
• SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever 
• SFTP & Manual Upload: How do I format my CSV files?

title: Clever + ParentSquare: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Required fields 4. Support contacts 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through setting up ParentSquare in Clever. 

 Before reading this guide, please make sure your school organization is set up with Clever! If your school org hasn't been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup . For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide . 

 
 Setup process

 If your school organization is already on Clever, you can connect ParentSquare’s application by accepting your invite or following these instructions:

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for ParentSquare
• You'll be brought to a page where you can authorize your ParentSquare request. As Clever SSO and rostering for ParentSquare is provided at no cost to your school organization, you can mark that it has already been purchased.
• You'll also be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the ParentSquare icon in their Clever portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request .
 
 Once you have shared data with ParentSquare, the ParentSquare team will set up the initial sync and will reach out to you to discuss communicating with teachers.

 

 
 Required fields

 Please make sure that you are sharing the following fields in your data sync from your SIS to Clever in order for ParentSquare to receive this data:

 Student Records 

 • 
 Required: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, ext, grade, student_number

• 
 Optional: dob, email, gender, home_language, state_id

 • 
 Language fields (Please see this article for more information on how to add these fields to Clever and how to share them with applications):

 • 
 home_language 

• 
 Home_language_code 

 

 

 
 Contact Records 

 • 
 Required: district, name, sis_id, students, email, phone, relationship, student_relationships, type

 
 Teacher Records 

 • 
 Required: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, email, ext, title

 
 Staff Records 

 • 
 Required: district, name, schools, staff_id, email, ext, roles, title

 
 District Admin Records 

 • 
 Required: district, name.first, name.last, email, title

 
 Attendance.csv File 

 Attendance data fields. Please see this article for how to add attendance data to Clever.

 

 Column header Required Format Notes 
 Attendance_id NO String 
 Unique and constant id for attendance record.

 Uniqueness is set at the SIS level and not guaranteed by Clever.

 
 Attendance_date YES Date The date on which attendance was taken. 
 Attendance_status YES Option (enum) 
 Possible values:

 *Absent 

 *Tardy 

 
 Excuse_code NO String Any district code relating to the attendance record. 
 Student_id YES String Unique and constant id for relevant student. Corresponds to ‘sis_id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Section_id NO String If attendance_type is “section”, the relevant ID of the section. Corresponds to the ‘sis_id’ field on the section object in Clever. 
 School_id NO String Unique and constant id for the school that the attendance record is assigned to. Corresponds to ‘sis_id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Attendance_type NO Option (enum) Supported values: “daily” or “section.” If not available, Clever will calculate based on whether or not there is a corresponding “section_id” on a given record. 
 
 

 
 Support contacts

 ParentSquare 

 Contact info: support@parentsquare.com 

 

 Clever 

 Support.clever.com (click the chat icon in the corner of the screen)

 Contact Clever with issues that relate to:

 • 
 Getting specific data fields into Clever

• 
 Setting up data sharing rules

 
 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 Why am I getting a “data warning”? 

 • A data warning occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. You will see a data warning for ParentSquare if a student is missing student contacts. Please see this article for help syncing student contacts to Clever.
 
 

 Why is a student or teacher missing from ParentSquare? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to ParentSquare:
 • 
 In your SIS data

• 
 In your Clever data

• 
 Is shared with ParentSquare in your data sharing permissions

 

 
 

 Are staff supported? 

 • Yes, ParentSquare supports staff with Clever.

title: For Clever Admins: Audit Logs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Using audit logs 3. What events are logged? 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 Audit Logs provide a chronological record of the most important changes happening across your school organization’s Clever account. They’re a clear trail of actions that impact your account’s security, user access, and data syncs. This tool helps you proactively monitor, ensuring your school organization’s data stays secure. It’s your go-to spot for keeping your sensitive information protected in an increasingly complex digital landscape. By using Audit Logs, you can:

 • 
 Investigate suspicious activity : Quickly review recent changes to understand what happened, when it occurred, and who was involved.

• 
 Troubleshoot faster : Quickly check for recent changes to authentication methods or sharing rules that may impact user logins.

• 
 Maintain compliance : Record actions taken in Clever to meet your organization’s cybersecurity compliance and incident response requirements.

• 
 Monitor high-risk changes : Regularly review updates to catch potential security issues. 

 
 
 Using audit logs

 For security reasons, Audit Logs will maintain data for actions taken within the last 60 days in your Clever Dashboard.

 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Support tools > Audit logs to review information captured in your Audit Logs.

 

 The main table displays your logs starting with the most recent event. You can narrow down the list using filters to find exactly what you're looking for. You can filter by:

 • 
 Date Range : View events from a specific window of time up to 14 days.

• 
 Action Taken : Filter by specific event types, like updated MFA policy or app sharing changes.

 
 Click on any row to see more detailed information about a specific logged event.

 You can also export a CSV of your audit logs at any time by clicking the Export CSV link. This will allow you to export any 14 day period of events within the past 60 days.

 

 

 
 What events are logged?

 Clever logs any events that significantly impact your school organization’s security or your students’, teachers’, and staff members' access to Clever. This includes: 

 • User login changes
 • Updates to identity providers (IdPs)/login methods (i.e., adding, removing, or editing a login method)
• Updates to multi-factor authentication (MFA) policies
• Updates to Clever Badge settings 
 

• App connection changes
 • Adding or removing an app
• Updates in sharing permissions
• Changes in field access settings 
 

• Portal organization changes (both by the admin team and School Tech Leads)
 • Adding, removing, or renaming categories
• Adding, removing, or renaming links
• Reordering resources and categories
 

 
 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 Who can see Audit Logs? 

 • 
 Clever Admin Owners and Clever Admins can access Audit Logs.

 
 How long does Clever store these logs? 

 • 
 For security reasons, Clever stores Audit Log information for 60 days. If you need records for a longer period, we recommend exporting your logs to a CSV regularly.

 
 Does the Audit Log show student personal identification information (PII)? 

 • 
 Audit Logs don’t store or display PII. Audit logs notify you of the change made, the users it impacts at a high level (i.e., org-wide or user-specific), and the admin who made the changes.

 
 Can I use Audit Logs to "undo" a change? 

 • 
 While Audit Logs show you what changed and might even show previous configurations in the detail view, they don't replace configuration management and versioning. You’ll still need to manually adjust your settings if you want to roll back a change.

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admin: Custom admin roles

title: For Clever Admins: Managing Library restrictions and application approvals
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Approving newly added library applications 3. Blocking resources 4. Turning off Library 5. Supporting teachers when restricting the library 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever Library is a safe way for teachers to explore new classroom tools. Instead of finding new applications anywhere on the internet that may have varied data privacy standards, teachers can try new apps from the Library with data-privacy guardrails and admin oversight. Resources in the Library can be added to a teacher’s Teacher Page for easy access by students and teachers.

 As a Clever Admin, you have primary control over what teachers can access in the Clever Library. You’re able to block Library applications that your school organization hasn’t approved or may not want teachers utilizing, and similarly you can leave approved apps accessible to teachers.

 When a teacher adopts a Library application with SSO, Clever shares only the absolute minimum information: student first name, last initial, grade level, and course enrollment.

 Clever does not share any sensitive information with Library applications, including student number, SIS ID, email, state ID, school information, or any demographic information about the student. All Library applications sign a universal data privacy agreement .

 This article will guide you through setting up Library restrictions. If you’re looking for information on encouraging your teachers to explore the Clever Library for safe application use, please see this article . 

 
 Approving newly added library applications

 Library application approval controls allow administrators to customize the Library to fit their school organization’s needs. 

 On your Clever Dashboard > Library controls > Settings tab, you’ll find the option to Require approval for new Library apps as well as Subscribe to new Library app announcements . These controls ensure that as new apps join the Library, you can stay on top of what is available to your teachers.

 

 If you elect to require admin approval for new applications added to the Clever Library, you’ll receive a notification in the Dashboard for applications that are in review and require your approval. We also recommend subscribing to announcements or selecting team members to receive them so that you can be sure to review new applications in a timely manner for teachers.

 
 Blocking resources

 If your school organization prohibits particular resources, you may also add them to a block list. For instance, if your school’s firewall blocks specific websites, you can also block them in the Library as well. Blocking a resource will prevent teachers from adding it to their Clever Portal. It’ll also remove the application from the Portal and Teacher Pages of any teachers who had already added it. When they search for and attempt to add the application, the Install button will appear gray and unclickable, with a note to contact their Clever Admin for more information.

 Clever recommends checking the teacher installs for an application before blocking to ensure that no teachers are currently using it. You can check which teachers are using a Library application via the Library controls > Applications tab and clicking the Export teacher install data button.

 If teachers are currently using an application you wish to block, you should notify them. See our section below on supporting teachers for more information.

 To edit the list of resources currently being blocked by your school organization, please navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Applications > Library Controls > Applications tab . To block a single application, click the Block button and confirm you want to block and remove the application for teachers. To block multiple applications, check the boxes next to each application you’d like to block, then click the Block X selected button to block all selected applications.

 If a teacher searches for or tries to install a blocked resource, Clever will gray out the install button and display the message below that the application has been blocked.

 

 To block all applications and select only approved apps:

 • 
 Click the top checkbox next to the Application Name column to select all apps.

• 
 To preserve the installs of any allowed apps, use the search bar to individually search for and uncheck these applications. If you do not remove these applications from the list, they’ll be deleted from Teacher and student Portals. 

 
 Once you have unchecked any applications you’d like to allow and preserve data for, click the Block X selected applications button to block all other applications. See the video below for a quick tutorial on how to allow only specific applications:

 

 

 Note: Blocking a resource doesn't prevent teachers or students from going around the safety of the Clever Portal and accessing the resource directly. 

 

 
 Turning off Library

 If your school organization would like to disable all Library data sharing, you can do so by navigating to Clever Dashboard > Applications > Library Controls > Settings . You’ll see the option to Disable Clever Library. 

 When disabling the Library, you’ll also have the option to remove all existing library installs from teachers and students.

 Before removing library installs for teachers and students, we recommend reviewing the Teacher Installs data downloadable on your Applications > Library controls > Applications tab and reaching out to any teachers currently using library applications to inform them of this change.

 Clever will request that communication be sent to teachers to alert them of the upcoming changes to their accounts. Once complete, our team will securely remove the Library resources from their Clever Portal.

 

 
 Email Template for informing teachers of library application removals 
 Subject: Important update regarding Clever Library applications 

 Hi [Teacher Name],

 We are writing to let you know about an upcoming change to our digital resource settings. To ensure we are meeting the highest standards for student data privacy and security, we are updating our permissions regarding the Clever Library.

 As part of this update, we will be turning off the Clever Library to allow for a closer review of our digital footprint. This means the Library apps you currently use will no longer appear in your or your students' Clever Portals after this date.

 Thank you for your partnership in keeping our student data safe.

 Best,

 [Admin Name]

 
 

 
 Supporting teachers when restricting the Library

 Some teachers may depend on applications available in the Clever Library. If you plan to block certain applications or turn the Library off entirely, we recommend informing teachers in advance.

 Clever doesn’t notify teachers directly in advance about Library changes made by your school organization. Instead, we suggest you share the update internally because:

 • 
 You can choose the timing and secure any necessary team approvals.

• 
 Clever can’t email every user due to subscription preferences.

• 
 Teachers are more likely to read an email from your school system than from Clever.

• 
 Hearing about changes directly from your team builds trust.

 
 You can find and download a list of Library applications your teachers are using on your Clever Dashboard's Applications > Teacher installs page. You can use this list to inform teachers of applications you plan to block or to tell only teachers currently using the Library that you plan to disable it . 

 Below are talking points you can use when preparing your message to teachers about changes to the Clever Library.

 

 Balancing teacher autonomy with security and data privacy 

 The Clever Library gives teachers the flexibility to discover and quickly start using new applications with their students. While every Library application signs a universal data-sharing agreement, some school systems have stricter requirement

title: For Clever Admins: Troubleshooting - Classroom MFA as an Entra ID EAM prompting Microsoft Authenticator
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Issue: Receiving Microsoft Authenticator prompts with Clever as your External MFA Method 3. Troubleshooting if your migration is complete 4. Troubleshooting if you haven’t completed migration 5. Additional considerations 

 

 Overview

 When using Classroom MFA as an External MFA Method for Microsoft Entra ID, some school organizations may find that new, unregistered users are prompted to install Microsoft Authenticator or use other Microsoft-native MFA methods instead of being redirected to Clever’s Classroom MFA.

 This guide helps Clever Admins configure their Microsoft Entra ID environment to ensure Classroom MFA is the primary requirement and to prevent conflicting Microsoft security defaults from interrupting the login flow.

 Please note: The settings described in this article take place entirely within the Microsoft Entra Admin center. These prompts are controlled by Microsoft and are outside of Clever’s technical control. Please contact Microsoft Support for help with their interface or configurations. Clever can only troubleshoot if redirection issues persist after you have followed all Microsoft-recommended steps. 

 

 
 Issue: Receiving Microsoft Authenticator prompts with Clever as your External MFA Method

 Due to changing policies on Microsoft’s side, some school organizations are receiving a prompt for new users to enroll in Microsoft Authenticator. This is occurring for some users because Microsoft is migrating toward a unified authentication methods policy, and legacy settings or registration campaigns are overriding the Clever External MFA Method configuration.

 After configuring Classroom MFA as an External MFA Method, users may encounter the following behavior:

 • 
 The login flow doesn’t immediately redirect the user to Clever to enroll in their designated Classroom MFA method (e.g., Badge, Picture, or PIN).

• 
 Users are prompted to enroll in the Microsoft Authenticator app before they can access their applications.

• 
 Users who have previously registered a Microsoft MFA method (like SMS or Authenticator) are able to use that method to bypass Clever’s Classroom MFA.

 
 

 
 Troubleshooting if your migration is complete

 To confirm if your migration is complete, please check your Authentication Methods > Manage Migration screen.

 

 

 

 If you’ve already completed the migration to the Microsoft Entra ID Authentication Methods policy, follow these steps to prioritize Clever:

 • 
 Disable Microsoft Registration Campaigns: In the Entra ID admin center, navigate to Protection > Authentication methods > Registration campaign.

 • 
 Select Edit and change the State to Disabled , then select Save .

• 
 Alternatively, you can keep the campaign enabled but specifically exclude the group of users assigned to Clever Classroom MFA.

 

• 
 Disable System-Preferred MFA: 

 • 
 Navigate to Protection > Authentication methods > Settings > System-preferred multifactor authentication.

• 
 Select Edit and change the State to Disabled , then select Save .

 

• 
 Disable or Exclude Microsoft Authenticator: 

 • 
 Navigate to Protection > Authentication methods > Policies.

• 
 Select Microsoft Authenticator .

• 
 Under the Exclude tab, exclude any Entra group that should be using only Classroom MFA.

• 
 Repeat steps above for any enabled Built-in Authentication Method to exclude Classroom MFA groups so that they are not targeted. 

 

 
 
 Troubleshooting if you haven’t completed migration

 If your Microsoft tenant is currently in the Pre-migration or Migration In Progress state, legacy policies may still be prompting users. To check your status, navigate to Protection > Authentication Methods > Policies > Manage Migration.

 

 

 

 The recommended resolution here is to complete your migration to the Entra ID authentication methods policy. Microsoft will be requiring this of all users. You can learn more in their article here. 

 If you aren’t ready to complete your migration, please reach out to the Microsoft team for best steps on allowing External MFA Method logins without the prompts from Microsoft Authenticator or other Microsoft-native MFA.

 

 
 Additional considerations

 Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR): If your users use Entra ID SSPR, Microsoft requires at least one native Microsoft method (like a phone number or email) to be enabled. In this specific scenario, you may not be able to limit users strictly to the Clever External MFA Method.

 • 
 Conditional Access: Ensure your Conditional Access policy for Clever External MFA Method is set to Require multifactor authentication and NOT Require authentication strength , as the latter can sometimes conflict with External MFA Methods.

• 
 External Guest Users: If your tenant includes guest users (e.g., vendors or consultants), ensure you use Exclude groups rather than disabling methods tenant-wide, or they may lose their ability to log in.

 
 For more detailed information on policy migration, please refer to Microsoft's official documentation .

title: For Clever Admins: Custom MDM configurations for the Clever iOS App
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Custom configurations and use cases • SharedDeviceModeOn • BrowserPasswordLoginOn • DistrictId 3. Setup instructions 4. Troubleshooting 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 This article explains how to configure the Clever iOS app (version 6.1 and later) using a Mobile Device Management (MDM) solution such as Jamf or Mosyle. These settings allow you to customize the app's behavior for your school organization’s specific needs.

 Common configuration options that can be deployed via your MDM's Managed App Configuration feature include:

 • 
 SharedDeviceModeOn: Automatically logs students out after they launch the first resource that takes them out of the Clever application.

• 
 BrowserPasswordLoginOn : Opens a web browser for password logins instead of the in-app login screen

• 
 DistrictId : Pre-configures the app with your school organization’s custom Portal URL, skipping the school picker.

 
 You can use your MDM to configure these settings for all your managed devices or for a specific subset.

 
 Custom configurations and use cases

 

 SharedDeviceModeOn

 This custom configuration can be enabled when students share iOS devices, and you want to ensure they log out of Clever between device swaps.

 When a student launches a resource that takes them out of the Clever Portal (like a rostering/SSO application or an external link), the Clever app will automatically log them out when they return. This helps younger learners who may not remember to log out manually.

 

 BrowserPasswordLoginOn

 When this configuration is applied, if a user (student, teacher, staff, etc.) opens the iOS application and selects the Password option to log in manually with a username and password, the application will automatically redirect the user to log in to Clever via the device's web browser. If you have a password manager set up in your web browser, user credentials will be automatically saved and entered for you to log in.

 This configuration is best for school organizations:

 • 
 That log in with external IdPs via the Password tab, and would like those credentials saved by their browser’s password manager: Credentials for IdPs like Google are often saved by the device browser’s password manager. Using this configuration allows your users to continue using that device browser (and its autofill capabilities) to log in to the IdP.

• 
 With teachers who frequently use iPads to access Clever: Teachers can log in to the Clever Teacher Portal via a web browser. The Clever app only supports the Student Portal.

• 
 That prefer students to manually enter login credentials: Web browsers can autofill login credentials for younger students who may struggle to type them each time.

 
 

 DistrictId

 If your students log into their iOS devices with a username and password, enabling this setting will streamline their login process by removing the need to select their school organization on the login page. When a student taps Password on the login screen, they'll go directly to your district's login page.

 You can find your school organization’s Clever ID via your Clever Dashboard. Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data browser , and you’ll find the ID at the top of the page.

 

 

 
 Setup instructions

 These instructions provide general guidance on managing applications in your MDM. The Clever Support team does not have insight into your specific MDM setup. If you need additional support around setting up application configurations, we recommend reaching out to your MDM directly.

 ​You can use your MDM to configure these settings for all your managed devices or for a specific subset.

 • 
 Create the configuration profile: In your MDM console, create a new Managed App Configuration (also called "App Configuration" or "Custom App Settings" depending on your MDM).

• 
 Add the configuration keys: Use the configuration in the property list (.plist) format in the relevant dropdown below for the settings you'd like to apply. Depending on your MDM, you may be able to use the entire .plist sample, or you may need to work with your MDM to properly set these values per their specifications. We do recommend always setting all three keys, even if you're setting some to false. This ensures consistent behavior and properly overwrites any previous configurations on the device.

• 
 Copy and paste the .plist provided for your preferred scenario below, then modify the values for your district: 

 
 
 SharedDeviceModeOn 
Use this configuration when you want students logging in with a username and password to skip the school picker and they share iPads on which you want students to be automatically logged out after launching a resource: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plist version="1.0">
 <dict>
 <key>SharedDeviceModeOn</key>
 <true/>
 <key>BrowserPasswordLoginOn</key>
 <false/>
 <key>DistrictId</key>
 <string>YOUR_DISTRICT_ID_HERE</string>
 </dict>
</plist> 
 Replace YOUR_DISTRICT_ID_HERE with your school organization's Clever ID found on your Clever Dashboard > Data browser page (example: 51e76ab1d93412f47b000c32).

 
 
 BrowserPasswordLogin 
 Use this configuration when your school org uses manual credentials for Clever logins, your teachers use iOS devices and the Clever application, or your schools use many Saved Passwords applications. <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plist version="1.0">
 <dict>
 <key>SharedDeviceModeOn</key>
 <false/>
 <key>BrowserPasswordLoginOn</key>
 <true/>
 <key>DistrictId</key>
 <string>YOUR_DISTRICT_ID_HERE</string>
 </dict>
</plist> 
 Replace YOUR_DISTRICT_ID_HERE with your school organization's Clever ID found on your Clever Dashboard > Data browser page (example: 51e76ab1d93412f47b000c32). 
 
 DistrictID 
 Use this configuration to simply pre-configure the district, without changing logout or browser behavior: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plist version="1.0">
 <dict>
 <key>SharedDeviceModeOn</key>
 <true/>
 <key>BrowserPasswordLoginOn</key>
 <false/>
 <key>DistrictId</key>
 <string>YOUR_DISTRICT_ID_HERE</string>
 </dict>
</plist> 
 Replace YOUR_DISTRICT_ID_HERE with your school organization's Clever ID found on your Clever Dashboard > Data browser page (example: 51e76ab1d93412f47b000c32).

 
 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Settings aren't being applied

 • Verify that the app version is 6.1 or later.
• Check that your MDM is targeting the correct app bundle ID: com.clever.InstantLogin
• Ensure the configuration profile is assigned to the correct device groups.
• Try force-closing and reopening the Clever app after the profile is deployed.
 
 Students still see the school picker

 • Confirm that DistrictId is set to your correct district ID (not your district's shortname or URL)
• Verify the profile has been successfully installed on the device.
 
 Password login still shows in the in-app screen

 • Confirm that BrowserPasswordLoginOn is set to true (not false or missing).
• Make sure all three keys are present in your configuration, even if some are set to false.
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What if I don’t know where to find these settings in my MDM? 

 • 
 Clever Support has limited insight into your school organization’s specific MDM settings. If your MDM setup doesn’t match the instructions above, please reach out to your MDM directly to determine where you should set up App Configurations.

 
 What if I have followed the steps and troubleshooting tips above, and the application still isn’t behaving as expected? 

 • 
 Feel free to reach out to Clever Support! Please be sure to include:

 • 
 Your MDM solution (Jamf, Mosyle, Intune, etc.)

• 
 The configuration plist you're using

• 
 A description of the expected vs. actual behavior

 

 
 Can I customize the app behavior using the plist examples above? 

 • Yes! Feel free to set any of the criteria as True or False to customize your user’s login experience. For the best login experience, we

title: Troubleshooting: Expired SAML certificates
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Why SAML certificates expire 3. How to update your certificate 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever can utilize Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) to offer a secure and seamless Single Sign-On (SSO) experience for users through identity providers (IdPs) such as ADFS and Microsoft Entra ID. For this connection to remain secure and functional, Clever and your IdP must use valid security certificates to confirm the user attempting to log in and verify user identities.

 If these certificates expire or Clever or one of your Clever admins rotates them without an update to your IdP, users may experience login failures. 

 
 Why SAML certificates expire

 You can think of SAML certificates as digital "padlocks" and "keys". Clever provides your IdP with a "padlock" (public certification), which your IdP uses to lock login data before sending it to Clever. Clever then uses its "key" (private key) to unlock it.

 For security reasons, SAML certificates are only valid for a limited period (typically several years). When a certificate reaches its expiration date, it must be replaced with a new one to maintain a secure connection.

 To avoid login errors, you should ensure you are always using the most up-to-date SAML certificate available via Clever. 

 
 How to update your certificate

 When Clever updates its security certificates in its public metadata.xml file, Clever Admins must ensure their Identity Provider (IdP) recognizes these changes. If the IdP is not updated, students and staff will be unable to log in via SAML.

 Depending on your IdP, you may need to manually update your settings or ensure your system is automatically pulling the latest metadata from Clever. Below are the update methods available for each IdP:

 

 
 IdP

 
 Update Method

 
 Automatic?

 
 IdP's support documentation on updating certificates and metadata

 
 ADFS Federation Metadata URL Yes (if configured; default is every 24 hours) 
 • Learn about monitoring your certificate and automatic updates here 
 
 
 Microsoft Entra ID Manual Field Edit No 
 • Entra ID documentation 
 
 
 Okta Manual Field Edit No 
 • Okta documentation 
 
 
 Duo SSO Manual XML upload No 
 • Duo documentation 
 
 
 Rapid Identity Manual XML paste No 
 • RapidID documentation 
 
 
 
 Please see the instructions and documentation linked below for each IdP for updating Clever’s SAML certificate in their system. You can find Clever’s most recent SAML Certificate here . The recommended instructions are intended to be a guide for rotating your SAML certificate in your IdP. If you find that the instructions don’t match your exact experience, or if you have any questions regarding the navigation of your IdP system, please contact your IdP provider directly as Clever has limited insight into their systems.

 
 ADFS 
 Set ADFS to automatically pull updates from Clever 

 You can get a deep dive on monitoring your Relying Party Trust and setting up for automatic updates here. 

 1. Open AD FS Management > Trust Relationships > Relying Party Trusts. 2. Right-click Clever > Properties > Monitoring tab. 3. Ensure the Metadata URL is: https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml 4. Check Monitor relying party and Automatically update. 
 
 Microsoft Entra ID 
 Follow Microsoft’s instructions here .

 1. Go to Entra Admin Center > Enterprise Applications > Clever . 2. Select Single sign-on > Basic SAML Configuration > Edit . 3. Manually update the Identifier or Reply URL if they have changed in the metadata. 

The URL should match the following: https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml 
 
 Okta 
 You can find Okta’s instructions here. 

 Okta does not support metadata file uploads or URL polling for custom SAML apps.

 Admins can manually extract information from Clever’s metadata.xml file and input that information into Okta’s admin UI. 

 • In Okta Admin, go to Applications > Clever > General tab.
• Under SAML Settings, click Edit > Next .
• Manually update the Destination, Audience URI, or Certificate fields using data parsed from Clever's XML.
 
 For the Clever-signing-certificate, admin must replace Clever’s certificate for Clever’s custom SAML app in Okta. You must navigate to the Advanced Settings of the Clever app in Okta.

 • 
 Log into the Okta Admin Console. 

• 
 Navigate to Applications > Applications > Select Clever .

• 
 Go to the General tab.

• 
 Scroll down to SAML Settings and click Edit .

• 
 Click Next to reach the Configure SAML page.

• 
 Click Show Advanced Settings .

• 
 Find the Signature Certificate field.

• 
 Click Browse files... , select your clever.crt, and click Upload Certificate .

 
Click Next and then Finish . 
 
 Duo 
 Duo requires a manual file upload whenever Clever's metadata changes. You can find Duo’s instructions here .

1. Download the latest XML from https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml . 2. In the Duo Admin Panel, go to Applications > Clever . 3. Under Service Provider , click Choose File and upload the new XML. 
 
 RapidID 
 RapidID supports copy and pasting Clever’s metadata.xml. Find their instructions here. 

 1. Copy the raw text from https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml . 2. In RapidIdentity, go to Configuration > Security > Identity Providers > Federation Partners. 3. Edit the Clever partner, go to the Metadata tab, and paste the new XML text into the field. 
 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 How do I find the latest certificate? 

 • 
 You can always find Clever's current public certificate and metadata at: https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml .

 
 Do I need to update immediately? 

 • 
 To ensure users don’t encounter login issues, Clever recommends keeping your SAML certificate up-to-date with the most recent version.

 
 What if I update my certification and I’m still seeing login errors? 

 • 
 If your teachers or students are still experiencing Clever SSO login issues related to a SAML-based IdP after updating your certification, please check your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Access logs and see this article for additional troubleshooting steps . If you’re unable to resolve the issue, please contact our support team at support.clever.com .

title: For Teachers: Discontinuing Clever Messaging
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Why is Clever discontinuing Clever Messaging? 3. Timeline and changes 4. Next steps for teacher <> student communication 

 

 Overview

 Clever will discontinue Clever Messaging on December 31, 2025. We’re proud to have built a teacher < > student messaging product in 2020 to support schools with urgent needs when learning was often pushed fully remote due to COVID-19. As digital tools for classroom communication have expanded, many districts have chosen to implement platforms dedicated to secure messaging, and fewer and fewer teachers and students regularly use Clever for their classroom messaging needs. 

 
 Why is Clever discontinuing Clever Messaging?

 As the education technology landscape has evolved, many districts have implemented specialized communication platforms. To ensure Clever is devoting all our resources to delivering the most secure and robust identity platform for education, we have made the difficult decision to discontinue the built-in direct messaging feature between teachers and students. This will help Clever be better positioned to support schools with new, free classroom tools like Substitute Access on Clever, Teacher and Student Portals that adhere to accessibility standards, and a faster student iOS app. 

 
 Timeline and changes

 Teachers and students can continue to message each other directly on Clever until December 31st, 2025. After that date, there will no longer be an option for direct messaging between teachers and students in the Clever Portal. However, your ability to communicate with your students through your Clever Portal isn’t going away entirely.

 The Class Announcements feature will remain available . You can continue to post one-way updates, reminders, or general announcements to all of your students or classes at the same time. 

 
 Next steps for teacher <> student communication

 Your district has been alerted to this change. If you have questions about how to communicate with students going forward, please reach out to your school's IT team. Many districts already have a dedicated tool in place for secure teacher-to-student messaging that they will continue to use moving forward.

title: For Teachers: Google Classroom add-ons
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to use a Google Classroom add-on 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever works directly with our application partners using Clever LMS Connect to offer Google Classroom add-ons. Google add-ons simplify the login process, enabling students and teachers to access learning applications via single sign-on (SSO) in Google Classroom!

 Please note that any add-on powered by Clever won’t allow you to link directly to content in your learning application, but it will simplify the login process to the application for your student right in Google Classroom!

 

 
 How to use a Google Classroom add-on

 To use a Google Classroom add-on, your admin will need to add it for your school organization’s Google Workspace. Once they’ve added it to the Google Workspace you’ll be able to: 

 • 
 Create a course item on your Google Classroom page, such as an assignment.

• 
 In the add-ons box, click the application you’d like to add. 

• 
 When prompted, select Sign in with Google .

• 
 If Clever powers the add-on, you’ll receive a prompt to provide Clever.com access to your Google Account. Please click Allow to provide access.

• 
 Click Add attachment on the application’s page, and you’ll successfully add a link to the application that students can use to log in without leaving Google Classroom!

 
 If Clever powers the add-on, your students must be actively logged into their Clever Portal so that Clever can automatically log them into the application. If a student attempts to access the add-on without an active Clever session, Clever will prompt them to log in.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I use the add-on if we don’t use Clever? 

 • 
 If Clever manages the add-on, your users must have a Clever account to utilize the Google add-on, as Clever’s single sign-on supports it. If you have questions about an add-on, please reach out to your IT team or administrator directly. Clever doesn’t have insight into the add-ons your school may be utilizing.

 
 What will happen if a student attempts to use the add-on without a Clever Portal session active? 

 • 
 Clever will prompt them to log in, then they can access the application.

 
 Can teachers link directly to specific assignments, lessons, etc., in the application? 

 • 
 No, the add-on will not link to specific content in an application. It will sign in students and teachers without additional login steps.

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Teachers: Clever + Google Classroom

title: For Application Partners: Building a Google Classroom add-on with Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements and building the add-on 3. The school experience 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever works directly with our application partners, utilizing Clever LMS Connect to offer Google Classroom add-ons. School administrators can install the add-on via their Google Workspace Marketplace to simplify the login process for districts and schools by enabling students and teachers to access their learning applications directly in Google Classroom via Clever’s single sign-on (SSO). The add-on can’t be used to link to specific content in your application, but will simplify logins for students and teachers directly in Google Classroom.

 Learn more about Clever LMS Connect here or sign up for a call with our team . 

 
 Requirements and building the add-on

 Clever is excited to partner with you in building a Google Classroom add-on! Please make sure your application meets the following requirements:

 • 
 You’re a Clever LMS Connect partner. Learn more here or sign up for a call with our team .

• 
 Your success manager will work with you to add an addendum to your Clever contract regarding the Google add-on, or to include this language if you’re a new partner and interested in an add-on.

• 
 Once the addendum is in place, the Clever team will require assets and copy to appear in the Google Workspace Marketplace. This includes your application name, a short description, a detailed description, and an icon. You will send these to your Clever Success Manager.

• 
 The Clever team will work to build the add-on for your team, reaching out with any additional questions or information needed. This process can take up to a week, but is usually completed more quickly!

 
 

 
 The school experience

 A school’s Google Workspace Administrator will search for and install the add-on via the Google Workspace Marketplace. Installation can take up to 24 hours to complete.

 Once the installation is complete, teachers can easily link to the add-on in Google Classroom course items such as assignments and quizzes. Students with an active Clever session will be able to single sign-on into the application directly via Google Classroom.

 For more details on the administrator experience, see this article . For more information on teacher experience, please see here.

 

 Please note: The add-on will provide SSO into your application, but teachers can’t use it to link directly to content in your application. 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 How much engineering work does creating an add-on require from our team? 

 • 
 None! Clever will complete the necessary work to build the add-on.

 
 Is there a fee for requesting or building an add-on? 

 • 
 No! Clever offers the Google add-on build to partners using Clever LMS Connect.

 
 Can I share the add-on with schools that don’t use Clever? 

 • 
 No. Schools that don’t use Clever will not be able to SSO into the application, since it is powered by Clever.

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Application Partners: Clever LMS Connect 

• 
 For Clever Admins: Google Classroom add-ons 

• 
 For Teachers: Google Classroom add-ons

title: For Clever Admins: Google Classroom add-ons
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Partners currently supported 3. Getting the add-on for an application 4. Informing your teachers 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever works directly with our application partners using Clever LMS Connect to offer Google Classroom add-ons. Google add-ons simplify the login process for Google Education Plus customers, enabling students and teachers to access learning applications via single sign-on (SSO) directly in Google Classroom!

 For the best experience, we suggest using Clever LMS Connect in your Clever account for the smoothest integration between your Google Classroom, applications, and Clever. 

 
 Partners currently supported

 • Progress Learning 
 
 
 Getting the add-on for an application

 • Navigate to your Google Workspace Marketplace and search for the application. Ensure you’ve located the application that says By: Clever. Inc. 
• Once you locate the application, select Admin Install. 
• You’ll receive a notification that you are about to install the application for your Google Workspace. Click Continue. 
• Review and agree to the application’s terms and conditions for accessing your Google Classroom data. During this step, you can select if you’d like to identify specific groups or organization units (OUs) that will have access to the add-on. Click Finish. 
• Configure the necessary settings if you’ve selected to add the application to specific groups or OUs and continue your setup.
• You’ll complete the add-on installation by selecting Done. It can take up for 24 hours for the app to be installed for your entire Google Workspace domain, organizational units, or groups. 
 
 
 Informing your teachers

 When you’ve completed the installation process for the Google add-on, you’ll want to inform your teachers that they can start using it in Google Classroom!

 

 
 Click here for a recommended email template to inform your teachers. 
 Hi [teachers],

 We’ve added [application] directly to Google Classroom! This means you can easily link directly to the application in your Google Classroom, and students won’t need to log in again. You won’t be able to link directly to specific content in the application, but it will provide easier access for your students to the app!

 To add a direct link to the application to your Google Classroom:

 • Create a course item on your Google Classroom page, such as an assignment.
• Under the add-ons, click [application] . 
• Select Sign in with Google .
• You'll receive a prompt to provide Clever.com access to your Google Account. Please click Allow to provide access.
• Click Add attachment on the application’s page, and you’ll successfully add a link to the application that students can use to log in without leaving Google Classroom!
 
 This add-on is powered by Clever, so your students must be actively logged into their Clever Portal to be automatically logged into the app.

 You can learn more in Clever’s Help Center here .

 We hope this makes your logins and classroom management smoother!

 Best,

 [Name] 

 
 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 Can I use the add-on if we don’t use Clever? 

 • 
 No, your users must have a Clever account to utilize the Google add-on, as Clever’s single sign-on supports it.

 
 What will happen if a student attempts to use the add-on without a Clever Portal session active? 

 • 
 Clever will prompt them to login, then they’ll be able to access the add-on.

 
 Can teachers link directly to specific assignments, lessons, etc., in the application? 

 • 
 No, the add-on will not link to specific content in an application. It will sign in students and teachers without additional login steps.

 
 How will we be notified about new application add-ons? 

 • 
 This article will be updated with new partners that have built add-ons, and we’ll notify schools when apps they roster via Clever have new add-ons available!

 
 How can I let an application partner know that I’m interested in them utilizing Clever LMS Connect? 

 • 
 Navigate to the LMS Connect tab in your Clever Dashboard and click the Submit app interest form button. This will let the application know you’re interested in them using Clever LMS Connect.

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Clever LMS Connect

title: For Teachers: Back to School recommendations
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Review your student roster for accuracy 3. Customize your Teacher Pages for the new school year 4. Prepare to troubleshoot 5. Check out analytics 6. Helpful videos and courses 

 

 Overview

 This guide is for teachers preparing for Back to School with Clever. If you haven’t used Clever, we recommend you take a deeper dive into learning about Clever and setting up your account by accessing our Clever Academy certification for teachers ! If you’ve used Clever before, we recommend reviewing this article to set yourself up for the first day of school. 

 
 Review your student roster for accuracy

 When your schools complete setting up your classes and student rosters in your school’s student information system (like PowerSchool, Infinite Campus, Skyward, etc.), your school’s IT team and Clever Admin will allow this data to update in Clever as well. Once the data is updated in Clever, you’ll see student rosters for the new year in your Teacher Portal! We recommend looking in your Clever Portal via your My Students > Student Roster tab .

 

 

 

 If you notice any issues with students or classes, please reach out directly to your school administrator or IT team to get it resolved. The Clever team can’t assist with missing students or classes in Clever as your school organization manages this data. 

 
 Customize your Teacher Pages for the new school year

 Teachers can create personalized Teacher Pages to house a variety of learning resources they want their students to access, such as PDFs, links, and applications. At the start of each school year, we recommend reviewing your existing Teacher Pages to ensure that:

 • 
 They’re shared with the correct classes

• 
 They contain all the resources you want your students to have access to

 
 If you create Teacher Pages per course, you may also want to create new Teacher Pages for any new classes you’re teaching. Learn more about Teacher Pages by watching our video below or checking out our Help Center article on Teacher Pages .

 

 

 
 Prepare to troubleshoot

 • 
 Learn how to generate a backup login code via your Portal > My Students > Student Roster . If a student’s login credentials to Clever aren’t working, you can still get them logged in so they can start learning!

• 
 Learn how to generate and void Badges for students to speed up logging in. Check out this video to learn how to manage Badges.

• 
 Know how to open a resource for all of your students at the same time!

• 
 If you or a student is missing an application in Clever, please reach out to your Clever Administrator to resolve the issue.

• 
 Running into application login issues? Review this article to learn about what could be happening. 

 
 
 Check out Analytics

 Did you know you can view student access to resources from your teacher Portal? Check out a daily or weekly overview of student activity in Clever to better understand the resources your students are accessing. Learn more here. 

 

 
 Helpful videos and courses

 • 
 Become a Certified Clever Teacher! 

• 
 Clever Teacher Tips video series 

• 
 Quick start - Cybersecurity basics for teachers 

 
 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever Portal: Teacher Pages 
• For Teachers: Overview of the Clever Portal 
• For Teachers: What's Clever?

title: Arbor API sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Customizations 4. School year rollover 5. Default field mappings 6. Setting up email write back 

 

 Overview

 This article provides an overview of the Arbor API sync. This sync:

 ✓ Runs every 4 hours ✓ Syncs staff X Doesn’t sync student contacts 

 
 Setup

 New to Clever? Please contact your Customer Success Manager for setup directions. You can sign up here if you haven’t yet connected with a district onboarding specialist.

 

 The Arbor sync allows Clever to connect to your Arbor Management Information System (MIS) to import data for Clever rostering, badges, and other services. 

 

 To set up an Arbor sync connection, please follow these directions:

 • 
 Share the Name and DFE number of each school you would like to connect to Clever with our support team.

• 
 Clever will then use this information to send out a connection request.

• 
 Navigate to System > Partner Apps (API Users) on a school MIS site, or System > Partner Apps (API Users) on a MAT MIS site to approve the request for data access.

 • 
 To view an app that is trying to connect with you, select Pending requests from the left-hand menu. 

 Please note: If you want more information on navigation within Arbor or this setup process, we recommend checking out Arbor’s Help Center here . 

 
 

• 
 Once accepted, our team will handle the rest! 

 
 
 Customizations

 • 
 The Arbor sync can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every four hours, but the time of day at which the sync occurs isn’t customizable.

• 
 Clever can filter exports from Arbor to limit the schools, teachers, and students included in the sync. Reach out to Clever Support to filter exports. 

 
 
 School year rollover

 Your syncs will be automatically paused in late July - resume in early September (specific date will be emailed to you directly) unless you manually modify it in your settings. 

 

 We automatically pause your data each year to reduce disruption during your busiest time. Pausing the sync will enable you to update data in your MIS or CSV files without interrupting student, teacher, or staff access over the summer. Learn more about sync pauses here .

 

 When you update your Pause dates, we recommend updating your rollover dates in your sync settings. Learn more about rollover dates here. 

 
 Default field mappings

 Below are the default field mappings for Arbor syncs managed by Clever. Please note:

 • 
 If a field is blank, the field is either not pulled by default or inaccessible to Clever.

• 
 If you want to change a field mapping, please contact Clever Support to learn the available options. 

• 
 If a field is not coming through with the correct value, Clever Support may need to complete a value mapping. Value mappings are common for grade, FRL_Status, ELL_Status, and IEP_Status.

 
 

 Schools 

 

 
 Clever

 
 Arbor

 
 Arbor table

 
 School_id uniqueObjectId UkDfe_EducationalInstitution 
 School_number establishmentNumber UkDfe_EducationalInstitution 
 School_name ukDfe_EducationalInstitution UkDfe_EducationalInstitution 
 
 

 Students 

 

 
 Clever

 
 Arbor

 
 Arbor table

 
 Student_id uniqueObjectId Student 
 Student_number studentNumber Student 
 First_name legalFirstName Student 
 Last_name legalLastName Student 
 DOB dateOfBirth Student 
 Grade academicLevel.curriculumGrade.displayName Student 
 School_id uniqueObjectId UkDfe_EducationalInstitution 
 
 

 Teachers 

 

 
 Clever

 
 Arbor

 
 Arbor table

 
 Teacher_id staff.uniqueObjectId AcademicUnitLead 
 First_name staff.legalFirstName AcademicUnitLead 
 Middle_name staff.legalLastName AcademicUnitLead 
 Last_name staff.legalLastName AcademicUnitLead 
 Teacher_email staff.primaryEmailAddress.emailAddress AcademicUnitLead 
 School_id uniqueObjectId UkDfe_EducationalInstitution 
 
 

 Sections 

 

 
 Clever

 
 Arbor

 
 Arbor table

 
 Section_id uniqueObjectId AcademicUnit 
 Course_number academicUnitCode AcademicUnit 
 Teacher_id academicLeads.0.uniqueObjectId AcademicUnit 
 School_id uniqueObjectId UkDfe_EducationalInstitution 
 Grade academicLevel.curriculumGrade.displayName AcademicUnit 
 Course_name displayName AcademicUnit 
 Term_start academicYear.startDate AcademicUnit 
 Term_end academicYear.endDate AcademicUnit 
 Term_name academicYear.academicYearName AcademicUnit 
 
 

 Enrollments 

 

 
 Clever

 
 Arbor

 
 Arbor table

 
 Section_id academicUnit.uniqueObjectId AcademicUnitEnrollment 
 Student_id student.uniqueObjectId AcademicUnitEnrollment 
 
 

 
 Setting up email write back

 The Arbor Auto Sync integration offers email write back. If you are interested in utilizing this feature along with your auto sync, please contact your Customer Success Manager. If you aren't sure who your Customer Success Manager is, please reach out to Clever Support.

title: For Families: Clever's Digital Learning Directory
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing and using the Digital Learning Directory 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 We know many families are curious to understand better the digital resources and tools used with their students at school.

 If your school organization has enabled it, Clever’s Digital Learning Directory allows you to see a list of all the learning applications your schools are using through Clever.

 The Digital Learning Directory provides a list of the applications each school utilizes, which grades use those applications, and an overview of each application with a link to learn more at the application’s website.

 

 Please note: Your school organization's team must turn on the Digital Learning Directory. Please contact your school organization directly if you’d like to be able to use this feature to preview digital resources your schools are using. 

 

 
 Viewing and using the Digital Learning Directory

 To view the Digital Learning Directory for your school organization, navigate to the URL your schools provided. It will look like this:

 http://clever.com/digital-learning/exampleschoolname 

 The Digital Learning Directory will list only learning applications that your school organization has added to their Clever account.

 

 You’ll be able to filter by school, view an overview of each application, determine which grades are using the application at which schools, and navigate to the application’s web page to learn more.

 We recommend filtering to the school your student is at and looking at the applications for their grade level to understand best which resources your student may be using in their current school year.

 If your school organization hasn’t enabled or has disabled the directory, you’ll receive the following error:

 

 Please contact your school organization's IT team directly with any questions about allowing access to the Digital Learning Directory. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Why did I receive a “Digital Learning Directory disabled” page? 

 • 
 Your school organization has disabled or not enabled the Digital Learning Directory. Please contact your school’s team directly with any questions about allowing access to the Digital Learning Directory.

 
 What if I don’t see a resource that my student says they use? 

 • 
 Only applications added in Clever will show in the Digital Learning Directory. If you have questions about the applications your school organization is utilizing, please reach out to your student’s teacher directly.

 
 I see an application that I’d like my student to be using. Who do I contact? 

 • 
 If you have questions about the applications your school is utilizing, please reach out to your student’s teacher directly. The Clever team can’t provide access to your school organization's resources. 

 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Digital Learning Directory 

• 
 For Teachers: Digital Learning Directory

title: For Teachers: Clever's Digital Learning Directory
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing and using the Digital Learning Directory 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 We know many teachers are curious to understand better the digital resources and tools used at their school or district.

 If your district or school has enabled it, Clever’s Digital Learning Directory allows you to see a list of all the learning applications your schools are using through Clever.

 The Digital Learning Directory provides a list of the applications each school utilizes, which grades use those applications, and an overview of each application with a link to learn more at the application’s website. 

 Clever Tip: Your school or district’s Clever Admin must turn on the Digital Learning Directory. Please contact your IT team if you’d like this feature enabled for your school. 

 
 Viewing and using the Digital Learning Directory

 To view the Digital Learning Directory for your school or district, navigate to the URL your school or district team provided. It will look like this:

 http://clever.com/digital-learning/exampleschoolname 

 The Digital Learning Directory will list only learning applications that your district or school has added to the Clever account.

 

 You’ll be able to filter by school, view an overview of each application, determine which grades are using the application at which schools, and navigate to the application’s web page to learn more.

 If your district or school hasn’t enabled or has disabled the directory, you’ll receive the following error:

 

 Please contact your district or school’s IT team directly with any questions about allowing access to the Digital Learning Directory. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Why did I receive a “Digital Learning Directory disabled” page? 

 • 
 Your district or school has disabled or not enabled the Digital Learning Directory. Please contact your district or school’s IT team directly with any questions about allowing access to the Digital Learning Directory.

 
 What if I don’t see a resource that I or my school uses listed? 

 • 
 Only applications added in Clever by your Clever Admin will show in the Digital Learning Directory. If you have questions about the applications your district or school is utilizing, please reach out to your internal team directly.

 
 I see an application that I’d like to start using! Who do I contact? 

 • 
 If you have questions about the applications your district or school is utilizing, please reach out to your internal team directly. The Clever team can’t provide access to your district’s or school’s resources.

 
 I’m using a Clever Library application but I don’t see it in the Digital Learning Directory, why is that? 

 • 
 The Digital Learning Directory will only include applications added by your district or school.

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Digital Learning Directory 

•
