 For Families: Digital Learning Directory

title: For Clever Admins: Clever's Digital Learning Directory
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up and viewing your Digital Learning Directory 3. Access for families and teachers 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 We know cybersecurity requirements are rapidly changing, and many states now require districts and schools to share which digital resources are used with students openly.

 Clever’s Digital Learning Directory generates a website where teachers and families can see all the learning applications your schools are using through Clever with just the click of a button.

 The Digital Learning Directory will provide teachers and families with a list of the applications each school is utilizing, which grades those applications are used in, and an overview of each application with a link to learn more at the application’s website.

 
 Setting up and viewing your Digital Learning Directory

 To activate your Digital Learning Directory, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Digital Learning Directory tab. 

 From here, you can toggle the Digital Learning Directory for your schools on and off. Clever automatically provides a link to your school’s Digital Learning Directory. Currently, this link is not customizable and is determined by the district shortname listed in your Portal URL. 

 To preview the Digital Learning Directory for your schools, turn the directory on, then navigate to the provided URL.

 

 Here, you’ll see the Digital Learning Directory that Clever has automatically generated based on the learning applications you have in your Clever account. The directory will display the following application types:

 • 
 SSO/Rostering

• 
 SSO-only

• 
 Rostering-only

• 
 SSO (Saved Passwords)

• 
 SSO (SAML)

 
 

 
 Access for families and teachers

 You can provide access to the Digital Learning Directory by sharing the URL on your Portal > Portal settings > Digital Learning Directory tab . Depending on the group you are sharing the directory with, we also recommend sharing the following articles as well:

 • 
 For Teachers: Digital Learning Directory 

• 
 For Families: Digital Learning Directory 

 
 If you disable the Digital Learning Directory, users will receive the following error when navigating to the directory in the future:

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 How should we share this link with families? 

 • 
 We recommend that you include your Digital Learning Directory link in any documentation that you have for families where you want to proactively share these resources with them.

 
 What types of applications will show in the Digital Learning Directory? 

 • 
 SSO/Rostering, SSO-only, rostering-only, SSO (Saved Passwords), and SSO (SAML) applications.

 
 Can we customize our Digital Learning Directory URL? 

 • 
 Not at this time.

 
 What will happen if we don’t have the directory setup but someone tries to navigate to our URL? 

 • 
 They will receive an error page notifying them that your school doesn’t have the directory setup.

 
 Can I print a list of Digital Learning Directory applications? 

 • 
 Not at this time

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Teachers: Digital Learning Directory 

• 
 For Families: Digital Learning Directory

title: For App Partners: Getting attendance data from districts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What attendance data is shared with app partners 3. Learn more about the district experience 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is piloting a new way for districts to send student attendance data to select educational applications. 

 These apps can use attendance information for many purposes. Here are some use cases with some example students and districts:

 Here are some examples of how you may use attendance data that’s shared with an app:

 
 Use case 

 
 Details 

 
 Emergency response & student safety During an unexpected fire drill, the school’s safety system syncs attendance data via Clever’s API. Teachers confirm which students are present, and the system alerts the administration that Alex, a 3rd grader, has not checked in. Staff quickly confirm that Alex was picked up early, preventing unnecessary panic and ensuring an accurate headcount. 
 Student transportation optimization Each morning, your district’s transportation system pulls attendance data via Clever’s API. When a student is marked absent, the system removes them from the bus route for that day, reducing unnecessary stops and optimizing fuel efficiency. This helps buses run on time and improves transportation logistics. 
 Parent communication for absences Liam’s school uses an app to send automatic alerts when a student is absent. When Liam misses school without a parent excuse, his mother receives a text notification in her preferred language, reminding her to submit a reason. This helps ensure proper documentation and reduces chronic absenteeism. 
 Absenteeism's impact on learning outcomes David, a high school junior, struggles in math and has been absent frequently. The district’s assessment tool is integrated with Clever’s attendance data. Likewise, his teacher notices a correlation between his absences and declining test scores. With this insight, David is given extra tutoring sessions during advisory periods, leading to improved test performance and engagement. 
 Personalized learning adjustments Sophia, a 5th grader, is using a math app to learn multiplication concepts. Due to frequent absences, she has fallen behind. With Clever’s attendance data integration, the math app’s adaptive learning system identifies gaps in her knowledge and automatically assigns personalized exercises, helping her catch up at her own pace. 
 
 

 If you’re interested in receiving attendance data, keep reading!

 This article provides a high-level overview of attendance data for application partners. If you’re a district or want to learn more about the district experience, read this article.

 

 
 What attendance data is shared with app partners

 Clever is ingesting, storing, and exposing negative attendance data to application partners. This means any value that is not “Present”, including:

 • 
 Absent

• 
 Tardy

• 
 Excuse_code, if shared by the district:

 • 
 For example, “ED” for Early Dismissal, or “UA” for unexcused absence.

• 
 We currently don't filter any data sent by districts, so data is being stored as-is.

 

 
 

 Attendance data is stored for up to 6 months from the date it was recorded. 

 
 How to gain access to attendance data

 If you’d like access to attendance data, follow these steps:

 • 
 Ensure you are on version 3.1 of the Clever API.

• 
 Talk to your Application Success Manager within Clever to express your interest.

• 
 Once the first two steps are done, and a district you’re connected to uploads attendance data, you can make a request to the Clever API with a district-app token to receive this data. 

 
 
 Learn more about the district experience 

 Check out the district-facing article for more information! 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 How do districts share the attendance data? 

 • 
 They share via their existing SFTP box, ensuring compatibility with any SIS.

 
 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admin: Attendance data in Clever

title: For Clever Admin: Attendance data in Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What attendance data is shared with app partners 3. Use cases for attendance data 4. How to add attendance data 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is offering a new way for districts to send student attendance data to select educational applications.

 This article provides a high-level overview of attendance data in Clever, how it’s added, and other logistical info.

 

 
 What attendance data is shared with app partners

 Clever is ingesting, storing, and exposing negative attendance data to application partners. 

 Any application using Clever for rostering can request access to attendance data through the Clever API at no additional cost to them or you. 

 Clever will grant access to approved application partners, and schools are always in control of whether or not to share their attendance data. If you’d like to use attendance data with a specific app, please contact the app provider directly to confirm support and request setup.

 Clever is ingesting, storing, and exposing negative attendance data to application partners. This means any value that is not “Present”, including:

 • 
 Absent

• 
 Tardy

• 
 Excuse_code, if shared by the district:

 • 
 For example, “ED” for Early Dismissal, or “UA” for unexcused absence.

• 
 We currently don't filter any data sent by districts, so data is being stored as-is.

 

 
 

 Attendance data is stored for up to 6 months from the date it was recorded. 

 
 Use cases for attendance data 

 Here are some examples of how you may use attendance data that’s shared with an app:

 
 Use case 

 
 Details 

 
 Emergency response & student safety During an unexpected fire drill, the school’s safety system syncs attendance data via Clever’s API. Teachers confirm which students are present, and the system alerts the administration that Alex, a 3rd grader, has not checked in. Staff quickly confirm that Alex was picked up early, preventing unnecessary panic and ensuring an accurate headcount. 
 Student transportation optimization Each morning, your district’s transportation system pulls attendance data via Clever’s API. When a student is marked absent, the system removes them from the bus route for that day, reducing unnecessary stops and optimizing fuel efficiency. This helps buses run on time and improves transportation logistics. 
 Parent communication for absences Liam’s school uses an app to send automatic alerts when a student is absent. When Liam misses school without a parent excuse, his mother receives a text notification in her preferred language, reminding her to submit a reason. This helps ensure proper documentation and reduces chronic absenteeism. 
 Absenteeism's impact on learning outcomes David, a high school junior, struggles in math and has been absent frequently. The district’s assessment tool is integrated with Clever’s attendance data. Likewise, his teacher notices a correlation between his absences and declining test scores. With this insight, David is given extra tutoring sessions during advisory periods, leading to improved test performance and engagement. 
 Personalized learning adjustments Sophia, a 5th grader, is using a math app to learn multiplication concepts. Due to frequent absences, she has fallen behind. With Clever’s attendance data integration, the math app’s adaptive learning system identifies gaps in her knowledge and automatically assigns personalized exercises, helping her catch up at her own pace. 
 
 

 
 How to add attendance data

 You’ll follow these steps to add attendance data once it’s available for one of your applications:

 • 
 Your district uploads attendance data to Clever via your SFTP folder (like how you might upload other CSVs) as an attendance.CSV file. 

• 
 Clever processes the file and makes attendance data available to approved applications you're connected to. 

 • 
 If you’d like to check if any apps accept attendance data (or you’d like to add them to the approved list for your district), please contact Clever Support by clicking the chat icon in the bottom right. Work through the prompts and submit a ticket.

 

• 
 Apps that have been granted attendance access by Clever can request this data through our secure API.

 
 Clever Tip: For now, there is no dashboard upload or visibility for attendance. Clever handles setup and troubleshooting internally. 

 

 
 CSV specifications for attendance data

 You should format your attendance CSV as follows before sending it to your SFTP box . 

 Column header Required Format Available via API? Notes 
 Attendance_id NO String YES 
 Unique and constant id for attendance record.

 Uniqueness is set at the SIS level and not guaranteed by Clever.

 
 Attendance_date YES Date YES The date on which attendance was taken. 
 Attendance_status YES Option (enum) YES 
 Possible values:

 *Absent 

 *Tardy 

 
 Excuse_code NO String YES Any district code relating to the attendance record. 
 Student_id YES String YES Unique and constant id for relevant student. Corresponds to ‘sis_id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Section_id NO String YES If attendance_type is “section”, the relevant ID of the section. Corresponds to the ‘sis_id’ field on the section object in Clever. 
 School_id NO String YES Unique and constant id for the school that the attendance record is assigned to. Corresponds to ‘sis_id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Attendance_type NO Option (enum) YES Supported values: “daily” or “section.” If not available, Clever will calculate based on whether or not there is a corresponding “section_id” on a given record. 
 
 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 How far back does attendance go? 

 • 
 Attendance data is stored for up to 6 months from the date it was recorded.

 
 Will attendance data be available no matter what SIS we’re using? 

 • 
 Yes! Since you’re sharing via your Clever SFTP box, attendance data is available no matter what SIS you’re using!

title: For Clever Admin: Custom admin roles
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Creating a custom role 3. Assigning the role to a user 4. Deleting a role 5. List of granular permissions available 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 We know every admin team has a unique structure and needs. Custom admin roles grant specific access in Clever based on your team's unique roles. Custom roles minimize risk by ensuring users have the minimum access required in Clever. 

 For example, you may have a curriculum coordinator who only needs access to analytics and the Clever Portal for a few specific schools. Or, you might have an SIS admin who only needs access to Clever’s sync settings. The levels of customization are nearly endless!

 This article will guide you through creating and assigning a custom role, along with the granular access it provides.

 

 

 
 Creating a custom role

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team. 
• Click Actions > Create custom role. 
 
 

 • 
 Name your role and add a description. If your new role requires access to specific schools, click Specific Schools , then select the schools from the dropdown menu. For this example, we're creating a role for a curriculum coordinator. Role names must be unique (for example, you can’t have two roles named “Math Curriculum Coordinator, but you can assign multiple people to the same role). 

 
 

 

 Please note: While custom roles allow school-specific scoping within the Clever Dashboard, integrated application partners may receive admin data at the district level. If a school-scoped admin is shared with an application, the application may grant the school-scoped admin district-wide visibility. To maintain strict data least-privilege, we recommend reviewing which administrative users are shared with apps. 

 • 
 Decide which permissions users with this role will need. We recommend reviewing the list of granular permissions to understand each set of permissions as you create your custom role. Please note that custom roles scoped to specific schools will only include the Support Tools permissions. 

 In this case, the curriculum coordinator needs access to view Clever analytics. However, we don't want them to manage these settings.

 
 

 • 
 They also need access to our Portal to add and remove resources.

 
 

 • 
 Once you're satisfied with the permissions, click Proceed to review .

• Review your new role and the permissions and then click Create role .
 
 

 

 
 Assigning the role to a user

 Assigning a user to the role immediately after creating a role 

 1. If there's a user you want to assign the role to immediately, you can do so right after you’ve created the role. This step is optional.

 2. Click Assign .

 

 

 Assigning a user to a role after it’s created 

 If you’d like to assign a user to a role after it’s created, head to your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team > find the role > Actions > Assign .

 

 You can also add administrators via CSV and assign existing roles in bulk. Please see this article for more information.

 
 Deleting a role

 If you no longer want a custom role, you can delete it by going to your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team > find the role > Actions > Delete .

 If there are any users assigned to the role, you must first remove them. After removing any members, you’ll be able to delete the custom role.

 

 
 List of granular permissions available

 Please note: These permissions only control what users can do in Clever. The permissions don’t determine a user’s role inside an application. For app-specific role behavior, please contact the application’s support team. 

 

 Below are all the options you can turn on or off for custom roles. If your custom role is assigned to specific schools, you may see fewer permission options available.

 Admin Team Management 

 • 
 View admin team members

 • 
 View all admin team members

 

 
 Analytics 

 • 
 Manage analytics settings

 • 
 Setup and manage Analytics settings for your Clever account

 

• 
 View analytics data

 • 
 View Edtech and Portal Analytics data

 

• 
 View analytics reports

 • 
 View participation and resource usage data

 

 
 

 Application Management 

 • 
 Application Sharing Permissions For Connected Apps

 • 
 View all applications

 • 
 View application sharing permissions for applications connected with the district (Includes access to user data)

 

• 
 Manage

 • 
 All applications

 • 
 Manage application sharing permissions for applications connected with the district (Includes access to user data)

 

• Specific applications
 • Manage application sharing permissions for specific applications connected with the district as defined by this role (Includes access to user data)
 

 

 

• 
 Manage Clever subscriptions

 • 
 View the Clever App Store, purchase and submit payment for Clever products

 

• 
 Clever LMS Connect

 • 
 View

 • 
 View Clever LMS Connect applications in Clever LMS Connect 

 

• 
 Manage

 • 
 Create, update, and delete LMS Connect applications

 

 

• 
 Manage application connections

 • 
 Add or delete an application connection, configure content mappings, field access settings, and saved password application connections (Includes access to user data)

 

• 
 View application data quality and issues

 • 
 View data quality issues with data shared with specific applications

 

• 
 View teacher-installed applications

 • 
 View login data associated with teacher-Installed Clever Library applications

 

 
 

 Portal Management 

 • 
 Manage Portal settings

 • 
 Manage Portal settings including the district's Portal URL, enabling Portal customization for School Tech Lead users, setting the default icon for the Portal, and substitute access

 

• 
 Manage Library settings

 • 
 Enable or disable the Clever Library and block or allow applications for the district

 

• 
 Messaging settings

 • 
 View

 • 
 View District Messaging settings

 

• 
 Manage

 • 
 Enable or disable Messaging settings

 

 

• 
 Manage Portal notifications

 • 
 Create, edit, and delete Portal notifications to students, teachers, and/or staff

 

• 
 Teacher email settings

 • 
 View

 • 
 View and update teacher email settings, such as if they can receive emails on Clever training resources.

 

• 
 Manage

 • 
 View and update teacher email settings 

 

 

• 
 Manage the Portal

 • 
 Customize and categorize the Portal, and add and edit Portal links

 

 
 

 SSO + Login Management 

 • 
 Manage Clever Badges

 • 
 Configure, enable, print, and void Clever Badges (Includes access to user data through bulk downloading Badges only)

 

• 
 View access logs

 • 
 View all data associated with users logging into Clever and applications

 

• 
 Manage SSO settings

 • 
 Manage all SSO settings on the SSO settings page, such as student and teacher access to Clever and login methods.

 

• 
 Manage Classroom MFA settings

 • 
 Configure, update, and delete Classroom MFA settings (Includes access to user data)

 

• 
 Manage IDM settings

 • 
 Configure, update, and delete Clever IDM settings (Includes access to user data)

 

• 
 Manage MFA bypass codes

 • 
 Reset MFA bypass codes for specific users (includes access to user data through bulk downloading Badges only)

 

• 
 Manage management of Clever Badges

 • 
 Download and/or void all badges (Includes access to user data through bulk downloading Badges only)

 

 
 

 Support Tools 

 • 
 Manage the Portal as a staff, teacher, or student user

 • 
 Troubleshoot issues by accessing the Clever Portal as the user (Includes access to user data)

 

• 
 Support Tools

 • Manage the Portal as a staff, teacher, or student user
 • Troubleshoot issues by accessing the Clever Portal as the user (Includes access to user data)
 

• 
 View

 • 
 View data browser, search, troubleshoot sharing, and troubleshoot login tools to support Clever users (Includes access to user data)

 

• 
 Manag

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Complete Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 3. What does this mean for you? 4. Learn more 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Clever Complete is the platform solution purpose-built for edtech scalability, connecting applications to any school through a single, secure integration for rostering, SSO, and LMS connections.

 If you currently use Clever, nothing will change in your Clever experience. Clever Complete is a platform solution for application partners to simplify district and school data management into one system: Clever.

 If you do not currently utilize Clever, application partners you work with may utilize Clever Complete to more easily synchronize data from your schools. As always, your student data privacy is a top priority for Clever. Learn more about our practices, FERPA, COPPA, GDPR, and state-level compliance in our Trust Center .

 Whether you currently use Clever or not, you’ll be able to continue managing your data via your existing systems while experiencing the benefits of simplified integrations with your application partners. 

 
 Features

 Clever Complete utilizes Clever’s existing 1EdTech OneRoster® integrations to help applications more smoothly integrate with schools without requiring schools to learn any additional systems. Applications using Clever Complete will be able to more easily integrate with your school because they’ll have access to:

 • 
 A single integration point for rostering, SSO, and your LMS

• 
 Additional data fields related to demographics and attendance

• 
 Best-in-class data visibility and management

 
 

 
 What does this mean for you?

 If you’re currently using Clever, nothing will change! You’re going to continue getting all of the great benefits of Clever that you always have.

 If you’re not currently using Clever, and one or more of your applications utilizes Clever Complete, Clever will serve as a subprocessor of your data. This means that Clever will serve as a middleman between any system you use to send data to your applications, and the application itself. This will improve your implementation experience with applications as data is normalized and simplified via Clever for application partners to ingest.

 Clever is committed to student data privacy and security, complying with COPPA, FERPA, GDPR guidelines. Learn more about our data privacy and security practices here .

 Whether you currently utilize Clever or not, when you use an edtech vendor that leverages Clever Complete you’ll benefit from:

 • 
 Easier onboarding and faster implementation timelines for new edtech tools

• 
 Improved cybersecurity

• 
 Higher quality application integrations and improved troubleshooting

 
 

 
 Learn more

 If you’re interested in learning more about how applications will utilize Clever Complete, please check out our article For Application Partners: Clever Complete Overview . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 If I already roster my application via another service, and the vendor wants me to begin rostering via Clever Complete, will I need to take action? 

 • 
 Yes, the Clever team will help you configure your data flow to route through Clever Complete instead of your current provider.

 
 Will Clever Complete impact how my users log in to an application? 

 • 
 No. Schools and applications can continue using their preferred authentication methods while ensuring a seamless login experience for students and educators.

 
 We don’t currently use Clever, how will Clever Complete impact our current environment? 

 • 
 You do not need to utilize Clever for an application partner to utilize Clever Complete to synchronize data from your schools. You can continue managing your data via your existing systems.

 
 Can I choose how I want to roster within Clever? 

 • 
 Yes, districts can decide how they want to roster, whether using Clever Secure Sync, OneRoster, or other supported methods. Clever Complete ensures that no matter how a school chooses to roster, applications receive the data they need.

 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • For Application Partners: Clever Complete Overview

title: For Clever Admin: Access logs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features of access logs 3. How to use Access logs with login error messages from end users 4. Frequently asked questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Access logs give you a holistic snapshot of any attempts to access Clever. Use Access logs to spot any suspicious activity early, to keep your schools’ users and data safe. You can also use Access logs to troubleshoot users who are having trouble accessing Clever.

 

 

 

 For example, you might notice that a user’s login keeps getting stuck on an MFA timeout, meaning the user has entered their password correctly, but that they are not completing their MFA security method. That could mean that their password has been compromised, and that they need a reset. 

 This article covers each feature of access logs and how you might use them.

 

 
 Features of access logs

 Filter functionality 

 You can quickly filter access logs by date, IP Address, status, user, and user type. For instance, if you believe a certain user’s account has been compromised, you can drill down to that user to see their access history. In addition, you can look via reference ID (such as an error code a user has shared with you).

 

 User access activity 

 All users (students, teachers, short-term substitute teachers , staff, and Clever Admin) access activity shows up as a summary: 

 

 

 Individual access log summary 

 If you click the down arrow, you can see useful information, including:

 • the authentication method (e.g. Substitute Badge, MFA, etc.)
• 
 the final user action

• 
 their user ID

• 
 email address

• 
 error code(s) if relevant

 
 

 

 Access logs will also include details on Clever Admin and School Tech Lead (STL) impersonations. The authentication method listed will indicate impersonation, and the log summary will contain details such as the impersonation’s start and end time, the user account impersonated into, device details, etc.

 

 An example of an impersonation event in Access logs 

 

 Emailing CSV exports 

 Exports are available only as downloads and will be emailed to the admin who exports them from the Clever Dashboard. You can also email a CSV export by day, which includes all access log data organized by column. If you want to send these exports to a security management solution, you’ll need to send them manually.

 

 

 To pull an export:

 • Click Export CSV 
• Select your dates. You can export data for up to seven days at a time, up to 60 days in the past. If you need more than 7 days of data, you can export the data for multiple 7-day timeframes.
• Click Export CSV: 
 
 

 

 CSV exports include: 

 • 
 Timestamp

 • 
 When the event took place

 

• 
 Authentication_context_id

 • 
 This ID relates to a single user’s single login attempt. For instance, if a user logged in with a primary method, requested an MFA code, had an MFA timeout, and then successfully entered an MFA prompt, these would all be under the same Authentication_context_id.

 

• 
 User_id

 • 
 The user’s Clever ID

 

• 
 User_type

 • 
 Teacher, student, staff, or Clever Admin

 

• 
 Client_ip

 • 
 The user’s IP address

 

• 
 Auth_method

 • 
 Their primary authentication method, such as SIS, Google, or Clever Passwords.

 

• 
 Second_factor

 • 
 Their MFA method (such as Mobile app MFA, SMS code, etc.)

 

• 
 Event

 • 
 Part of the login process, such as:

 • 
 Login_success

 • 
 Part of the login process was successful (such as a first method, second method, or backup login code). 

 

• 
 Login_failure

 • 
 Part of the login process was unsuccessful.

 

• 
 MFA_prompted

 • 
 The user was prompted to enter an MFA prompt

 

 

 

• 
 Error

 • 
 There was an issue with the login process, such as a duplicate ID, no match to a user in your schools, etc.

 

 
 

 
 How to use Access logs with login error messages from end users

 When students or teachers can’t log in to Clever, they’ll receive an error message that provides information about the error, a copyable Reference ID, possible troubleshooting steps, and instructions to contact their school’s IT support.

 

 While the login error may suggest steps towards resolution, if you need additional information, please navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Access logs and input the Reference ID for more details.

 If you’re still unsure how to resolve the issue, please contact Clever Support and provide the user’s Clever ID and the Reference ID so our team can help troubleshoot further. 

 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 

 • 
 What’s the length of logging shown in Clever? 

 • 
 Five days.

 

 
 

 • 
 What’s the length of logging shown from the CSV download? 

 • 
 You can download up to seven days of data from the past 60 days. 

 

 
 • 
 What does it mean if an authentication method is “Impersonation?” 

 • 
 This means that a Clever Admin impersonated a teacher or student account, or if this setting is enabled for your school, an STL impersonated into a teacher account. Click the Access log entry for more details on the impersonation.

 

 
 

 • 
 What fields can you search via? 

 • 
 As of now, you can only search via reference ID.

 

 
 

 • 
 What errors might we see in a log? 

 • 
 Wrong email, wrong pw, incorrect 2nd factor

• 
 Pending MFA: users have 10 minutes to complete their MFA challenge, if nothing happens, Clever will fail the login. 

• 
 Timeout error: Meaning it took longer than 15 seconds for Clever to download your data.

 

 
 

 • 
 What are the differences between our classic login logs versus new access logs? 

 • 
 Length of logs shown:

 • 
 2,000 logs or 14 days in login logs

• 
 5 days for access logs with no limit on # of logs

 

• 
 Download limits:

 • 
 5k logs for login logs

• 
 14 days with access logs (you can also request a longer history by contacting Clever Support).

 

• 
 Access logs also include:

 • 
 Combined logs into one view when there are multiple access touch points, so it’s easier to troubleshoot.

• 
 More detail around methods of authentication, such as MFA, Substitute Badge access, etc.

• 
 Better error messaging with more detail on access errors.

title: For Teachers & Students: Claiming your Clever account
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to Claim your account 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 If you’re new to Clever, your IT team may share directions for you to claim your Clever account. This article explains how to claim and set up your account. 

 IMPORTANT These directions only apply to you if your IT team has set up account claiming in Clever. If you have questions about how to access your Clever account, please contact your IT team. Clever’s Customer Support team can’t help with accounts for students, teachers, and staff since those accounts are managed by your IT team, not Clever. 

 

 
 How to Claim your account

 Step 1: Get to the account claim page 

 Option 1: If your IT team has sent you a link to claim your account, please click that link. 

 

 Option 2: Otherwise, go to your Clever Portal login page and click get help logging in. 

 

 

 Then, click Claim your account. 

 

 

 

 Step 2: 

 Complete the account claim steps. These will vary based on how your school’s IT team has set it up.

 You’ll be asked to submit information such as your account number, email address, and grade. This is to prove you’re really you, and not someone trying to access your account. 

 You have a limited number of attempts to claim your account, so please be careful to type your information in correctly. Fields other than email are case sensitive, so please keep this in mind when inputting answers.

 

 

 In this example, the student is first asked for their email address. 

 

 

 Then, they’re asked for their grade. 

 

 

 Step 3: Setting up your account 

 Next, you’ll set up your Clever password and get your username. This will look different based on how your IT team has set it up.

 

 Option 1: 

 If you see a screen like the one below, you’ll set your password yourself. Type your password twice, then click Save .

 

 Then, you’ll be shown your Username for Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID. Be sure to remember your username and password so you can log in to Clever!

 

 

 Option 2: 

 If you’re shown your username for Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID first, your IT team has already set a password for you.

 Be sure to remember your username so you can log in to Clever!

 

 Next, you’ll be shown your password. Click the Show button to see your password. Then type it twice to make sure you can remember it. When you’re done, click Go to login .

 

 

 Step 4: Log in to Clever 

 You’re all set! After seeing your username and password, you’re ready to log in to Clever.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 I’m having trouble setting up my account, who do I contact? 

 • Please contact your IT team for help. They manage your account. Clever Support can’t help with account creation or claiming. 
 
 

 Related articles:

 • For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever 
• For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins 
• For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Teacher logins

title: For Clever Admin: Setting up account claim for your users
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Rolling out account claiming to your users 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Getting new users set up on Clever (or any software) can require some legwork, especially if they don’t know their credentials or your Clever Portal login URL (e.g. Clever.com/in/chicago ).

 With Clever’s account claiming feature, students, teachers and non-instructional staff can get their accounts set up quicker, in a more secure way, and with less tech support. 

 This feature is exclusively available to Clever IDM customers and those on our updated Clever Passwords SSO method .

 Simply direct them to an account claim link unique for your schools, and users answer a few security questions to validate their identity. They’ll get their username and password for Clever as well as destinations like Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID.

 This article walks through the Clever Admin setup process to turn this feature on for your schools. You can see the end-user experience here. 

 
 Setup process

 The account claim process is available to end users when a new user is created via Clever IDM. 

 It can also be triggered manually by going to a user’s profile > Support Tools > Reset Account Claim. Here’s how to configure account claim for your schools:

 Step 1: 

 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Access control tab . Scroll down to Account Claiming. Here, you'll set up an account claim policy for each user type. 

 

 Step 2: 

 Set up account claiming for each user type. In this article, we’ll start with students; feel free to start with any user type.

 To enable users to claim their accounts, you’ll need at least two security fields for students and three for teachers/staff. You have several options for the primary security field. Options include fields like the user’s email address, State ID, and district username. Fields other than email address will be case sensitive, so please consider case when choosing fields.

 You can modify the question displayed to make it easy for users to understand what's being asked, including a hint if needed.

 

 

 Step 3: 

 Next, add a secondary security field. You have options like grade, state ID, email address, and more. Optionally, you can add additional security fields by clicking + Add field .

 

 

 Step 4: 

 Determine what happens after users claim their accounts.

 You have two options for how users claim their new passwords. You can have them manually set their password according to the complexity settings you set from your Password Settings page. Or you can regenerate a password using the format found in the IDM Setup page and show this new password to the user. When being shown the new password, users are prompted to practice entering it twice to encourage memorization.

 

 Step 5: 

 Determine how many days the user has before their account claim expires. 

 You can set distinct expirations for end users to access their new accounts via this claim flow from 1-21 days. If the end user does not claim their new account within that time, the claim will automatically expire. This helps remove lingering access points to your identity accounts.

 Then, decide which Clever IDM destinations this should apply to. This allows you to select the places where the password will be reset when the claim flow is complete.

 For example, you could be using Clever for both Google and Active Directory but only want the claim flow to apply to Google because end users are expected to manage AD credentials directly in Microsoft. Or you might want all of your IDM destinations (including Clever passwords) to be synchronized.

 

 Step 6: 

 Click on Allow Students/Teachers/Staff to Claim their Accounts and then click Save . Account Claim is now activated for the specific user type. If you’d like to turn it on for another user type (e.g., teachers) repeat these steps.

 

 

 

 
 Rolling out account claiming to your users

 When you’re ready to start using account claiming for your schools, you have two options for users to access the setup flow. We recommend using both to take full advantage of account claiming:

 • 
 You can copy the URL to the claim flow page and share it with users who need to set up an account.

• 
 You can also add a link to the account claim flow directly from your school’s Clever sign-on page.

 
 

 

 Suggested settings for using Account Claim during Chromebook sign-in 

 Account Claim can be used directly from the Clever sign-in screen on a Chromebook when the Allow access to account claim page from sign-on page setting is enabled. 

 Requirements:

 • 
 The Chromebook’s OU must be configured to redirect to the Clever sign-in page for access. See Single Sign-on (SSO): Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever as an SSO Profile in Google for instructions. Note: Account Claim is only appropriate for non-badge users. To have your non-badge users see your Portal sign-in page, either remove “?specify_auth=badges” from your sign-in page URL ( instruction 5c ) or have users bypass the automatic camera by clicking Log in from the Badge scanning page.

• 
 The Allow access to account claim page from sign-on page setting must be enabled. 

 
 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can account claim be turned on for only certain grades? 

 • 
 Not currently. However, users will only know about it if you share the URL to the claim flow or if they find it in the troubleshooting section of your Clever Portal login URL. Furthermore, claims are only generated in the three following situations:

 • 
 a new user is created by IDM

• 
 A Clever Admin manually resets the claim on an individual user from the Support Tools button on their overview page.

• 
 A bulk password reset is initiated (this must be requested through Clever Support)

 

 
 Are we required to apply the account claim settings to a Clever IDM destination? 

 • 
 No! You can have the students’ account claim credential be separate from any Clever IDM destinations. However, you can simplify credentials for users by applying the same credentials to any Clever IDM destination(s) your schools use.

 
 Is account claim appropriate for Clever Badge users? 

 • 
 No. The Clever Badge is already a form of login - if you have your Clever Badge, there’s nothing you need to claim. Account claim is intended for users that will log into Clever with a username and password and can be used with any of the following login methods:

 • 
 Clever passwords

• 
 Google

• 
 Entra ID (SAML)

• 
 Active Directory (SAML or LDAPS)

 

 
 Are security fields case sensitive? 

 • 
 Email addresses won’t be case sensitive, but for security reasons, other fields (such as IDs) will be.

 
 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever IDM: Overview 
• For Teachers & Students: Claiming your Clever account

title: For Clever Admins: Returning to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Step 1: Review and set up the sync with your Student Information System (SIS) 3. Step 2: Review and add applications 4. Step 3: Review and edit your identity provider (IdP) 5. Step 4: Recommended next steps 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Welcome back to Clever! We’re so excited to help you get set up. Clever offers many features, and getting started with the basics is easy. One of the biggest questions districts returning to Clever ask us is how long it’ll take to have their account up and running. Account set-up times vary based on each district’s unique configuration, but we’ve seen many districts successfully get started with Clever in just a week!

 If you are returning to Clever and still have an existing account, please navigate to schools.clever.com and input your username and password to get logged in. If you’ve forgotten your password, click the Forgot your password? button to receive a reset email.

 If you previously deleted your account and are starting over from scratch, please sign up for a new account here. You’ll also want to navigate to our article Setting up Clever: Overview as this will have the best information for setting up your account from the start. 

 
 Step 1: Review and set up the sync with your Student Information System (SIS)

 If you are getting your account up and running again, you’ll either need to:

 1. Choose your student information system (SIS) 

 2. Update your previous SIS selection

 3. Get the sync up and running with your same SIS again

 Syncing data with Clever can be done with any SIS. Your sync sends information from your SIS to Clever so we can securely share the data you authorize with the applications you specify.

 Navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page in the dashboard. You’ll either find that you have not configured your sync:

 

 

 

 or that you previously set up your sync and had data syncing with Clever:

 

 

 

 If you have not configured your sync yet 

 You will need to choose your SIS from the drop-down menu. If you can’t find your SIS listed here, select Other . 

 There are three ways to sync data to Clever: 

 • 
 Clever-managed Auto Sync 

• 
 SIS-managed Auto Sync 

• 
 SFTP Sync 

 
 For any SIS-managed or SFTP syncs, our teams have limited insight into the export processes within your SIS. If you have any questions on how to export data from their system please work with your SIS team directly.

 If you need guidance on what sync type you should use, you can learn more here: Which sync type should I choose? 

 

 

 If you have previously configured your sync 

 Please determine if you will continue to use the same sync. There are three ways to sync data to Clever: 

 • 
 Clever-managed Auto Sync 

• 
 SIS-managed Auto Sync 

• 
 SFTP Sync 

 
 Aeries, Blackbaud, Infinite Campus, PowerSchool, and Skyward all support Clever-Managed Auto Syncs. You will likely need to update settings on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page . Please locate the setup instructions for your sync in our Help Center.

 SIS-managed and SFTP syncs require the use of an SFTP box for uploading data to Clever. If you are using one of these syncs, you'll find the settings for setup on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. If you are setting up a SIS-managed auto sync, you’ll need to work with your SIS team directly. If you are setting up a manual SFTP , you can find instructions for setting up the sync here .

 

 Please note: If your SIS has changed, please contact Clever Support to update your sync type. 

 

 
 Step 2: Review and add applications

 Clever seamlessly syncs data with any of your schools' applications that you connect to Clever via secure API connection. Examples include Newsela, Reading A-Z, and Mathspace. This automates the transfer of rostering data, saving you time in application setup and maintenance. Clever is the only rostering partner that can transfer your data via API integration to applications, with a network of 1000+ applications for you to connect with, at no cost to your schools.

 Navigate to your Applications > My Applications page to check if any applications are still connected to your Clever account. If you have any rostering, rostering/SSO, or SSO applications still connected, you’ll need to contact the application team directly to let them know you’d like to start syncing data via Clever.

 To add new applications to Clever , navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add applications page , and then request your desired application(s).

 Once you’ve requested a learning application, each app partner has its own approval process. If you have questions about getting your request approved, impacts on existing application data, or changes to the login flow for your students and teachers, please reach out to the application partner directly.

 

 Sharing Data 

 After you have added a new application to Clever, you’ll need to set data sharing permissions for the application. Sharing permissions specify what data you want to make available to a specific application, and ensure security of your data as you are in control of what is shared.

 • 
 From the Applications page , find your newest application(s) from your list of connected learning applications. 

• 
 On the right side, choose Share data now . 

 
 Find more information about sharing data with new applications here: Sharing Permissions: Sharing data with new applications .

 

 
 Step 3: Review and edit your identity provider (IdP)

 

 If you decide to utilize the Clever Portal, you'll choose how students, teachers, and staff will log into Clever and access all your online learning applications. Utilizing the Clever Portal creates a one-stop-shop for your teachers to access applications, manage their online resources for students, and troubleshoot technology issues.

 To review your settings or set up single sign-on (SSO):

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings .

• 
 Check to see if you have previously set up an IDP. If you have, click Edit to review the settings previously configured. You can also add any additional IdPs if you’d like.

• 
 If you did not previously configure an IdP, choose the identity provider (e.g. Google , Active Directory , LDAP , etc.) that works best for users in your school district. Find more information here: Single Sign-On (SSO): identity provider (IdP) Options .

 
 Our Help Center offers step-by-step instructions for setting up each login method within your Clever Dashboard: Single Sign-On (SSO): Identity Provider (IDP)/Login Method Options .

 

 

 

 
 Step 4: Recommended next steps

 Continue your Clever Education in Clever Academy 

 Whether you want to become a Clever Certified Champion who knows all about Clever, or you are looking for some quick guides to get you started , check out our free online training for both administrators and teachers in Clever Academy .

 Explore our add-on paid offerings Clever IDM, Classroom MFA, and Professional Development 

 Get more out of your Clever account by utilizing Clever IDM to seamlessly manage the life cycles of your Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID accounts. Gain access to powerful tools like password and account resets. Learn more here .

 Secure your end user’s accounts with Clever’s Classroom Multi-factor Authentication (MFA). Use student and teacher-friendly authentication factors such as picture, Badge, or PIN logins. Learn more here .

 Want to learn more about your Clever account or offload the work of training your teachers? Check out Clever’s Consulting or Professional Development services.

 Inform your teachers and prepare them for Clever rollout 

 If you’ve chosen to use Clever Portal with your district, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page to provide instructions on how they will access Clever. You can

title: For Clever Admin: Protecting Entra with Clever Classroom MFA
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup in Entra 3. Setup in your Clever Dashboard 4. Using MFA with Windows Device login via Web Enabled Login 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 If your school organization uses Entra, you can use Clever Classroom MFA as an external MFA method to protect logins and meet Microsoft’s MFA requirement in a student-friendly manner. 

 

 External MFA Methods lets you choose an external provider (like Clever) to meet Microsoft’s multi-factor authentication (MFA) requirements when a school organization's user signs into Microsoft Entra ID. Classroom MFA helps increase students' digital citizenship skills as they navigate our digital environments in a safe and responsible way.

 Example of Classroom MFA protecting Entra with login pictures: 

 

 

 Example of Classroom MFA protecting Entra with a PIN: 

 

 

 This solution doesn’t require any Clever software deployed on-premises. After your end users complete primary Entra ID authentication (such as their username/password) they’ll be redirected to Clever's cloud service to complete MFA, and subsequently redirected back to Microsoft's service. This article walks through the setup process.

 

 

 

 There are two parts: setup in Entra, and setup in your Clever Dashboard. You must complete both setup portions for Clever Classroom MFA to function in Entra.

 

 

 
 Setup in Entra

 Part 1: Create the group(s) of users you want to turn on MFA for 

 In the Entra or Azure console, go to Groups > All Groups > New group . Create the needed group(s). See Microsoft’s article on group management for more information.

 

 

 Part 2: Adding External MFA Methods 

 • 
 If using the Entra console, go to applications > App registrations > New registration . If using the Azure console, go to App registration > New registration . You must have Privileged Role Administrator access or higher.

 
 

 • The fields here should be filled in as follows:
 • 
 Name: you can name this anything, but we recommend a clear name, like Clever Classroom MFA

• 
 Supported account types: For increased security, set the account types to Single tenant only, unless your Entra design crosses multiple tenants (e.g. staff in one tenant and students in a secondary tenant).

• 
 Redirect URL: Select web as the platform, then copy/paste the redirect URL from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > MFA > Integrations tab. 

• 
 Click Register. 

 

 
 

 • 
 After clicking Register, you’ll be taken to the Overview screen. Copy and paste the Application (client) ID. You’ll need it for the next step, as well as for the Setup in your Clever Dashboard section .

 
 

 • 
 If using the Entra console, go to Protection > Authentication methods > Add external method . If using the Azure console, Security > Manage > Authentication Methods > Add external method. 

 
 

 • Configure the fields as follows:
 
 • 
 • 
 Name: you can name this anything, but we recommend a clear name, like Clever Classroom MFA

• 
 Client ID (from your Clever Dashboard) : Grab this here: Clever Dashboard > Authentication > MFA > Integrations tab > Copy Client ID. If you don't see the integrations tab, you'll need to start by adding at least one MFA policy. Once you add a policy, you'll see the integrations tab. 

• 
 Discovery Endpoint : Grab this from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > MFA > Integrations tab. 

• 
 App ID : App ID from the previous screen (in Entra)

• 
 Request Admin Consent : this needs to be clicked 

• 
 Enable and Target : See step six for more information on this step 

 

 
 

 • 
 Enable and target controls which groups are targeted for the EAM. It is up to your school organization to determine which group(s) you want to enable Classroom MFA for. Just keep in mind that this must align with the number of licenses you’ve purchased for Clever Classroom MFA. For instance, if you have 10,000 licenses but target 20,000 users, you would have an overage. 

 • 
 Click the Enable button.

• 
 Click set Target > Select Targets .

 

 
 Please note: Remember, you can either select all users or groups. You cannot select individual users. 

 • 
 • Select either all users or the group(s) you’d like to enable external MFA method. 
 

 
 

 

 Part 3: Enforcing MFA for users in Entra 

 Next, you’ll enforce MFA for users. This makes MFA required for users. There are two options: enforcing for individual users, or setting up a conditional access policy which lets you enforce MFA for a group of users.

 

 Option 1: Enforcing for individual users 

 • Go to Entra.microsoft.com > Users > All Users > Ellipsis (…) > Per User MFA. 
 
 

 • Select the user and click Enable .
 
 

 

 Option 2: Setting up a conditional access policy 

 • 
 This allows settings to be applied to a group of users. However, we’re currently troubleshooting this feature, so you may run into issues getting this to work on enforcing MFA on device logins.

• 
 To set up a conditional access policy, go to Home > Protection > Conditional Access > Create new policy .

• 
 Set the Assignments to include the group of users you targeted in step six in part one .

 
 

 • 
 Set the Target resources to All Cloud apps . Add other restrictions as needed.

 
 

 • 
 Under Access controls > Grant , select Grant access, Require multifactor authentication, and click Select .

 
 

 

 Now that setup is complete in Entra, move to the next section to get things set up in Clever.

 

 
 Setup in your Clever Dashboard

 After you’ve completed setup in Entra, head to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > MFA > Integrations tab . If you don't see the integrations tab, you'll need to start by adding at least one MFA policy. Once you add a policy, you'll see the integrations tab.

 

 

 • 
 Click Add Application (client) ID(s) from Entra to establish integration .

 • 
 You may have more than one application (client) ID if you have multiple Entra tenant(s) within your school organization.

 

 
 

 • 
 Add the Application (client) ID from Entra. If you need it, you can get it from the app overview page of Entra ID:

 
 

 • 
 In the Integration page in your Clever Dashboard, click Add directory (tenant) ID(s) from Entra to establish integration .

 • 
 You may have more than one directory (tenant) ID if you have multiple Entra tenants within your school organization.

 

 
 

 • 
 Add the Directory (tenant) ID(s) from Entra. If you need it, you can get it from the app overview page of Entra ID:

 
 

 • 
 After completing both the Entra ID and Clever Dashboard setup, ensure you’ve set up at least one MFA policy that will cover Entra ID users. If you need help with setup, check out For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA setup .

 
 IMPORTANT For Clever Classroom MFA to protect Entra ID, you must include the same users in your MFA policy/policies that you targeted in the Enable and target step during the Entra setup process. If users aren’t included in the MFA Policy, they will not be able to log in to their device and will see an error message. Also: the “Skip enforcement date” button in the MFA setup doesn’t affect Entra ID - MFA will be required for all users in the MFA policy whether this button is clicked or not: 

 • Once all setup is complete, we recommend you test at least one user by logging in on their device and ensuring Classroom MFA comes up as expected. You should wait at least an hour after completing Entra and Clever setup steps to allow all settings to go live. 
 
 
 

 Using MFA with Windows Device login via Web Enabled Login 

 Please note: This requires a Windows 11 device version 22H2 with KB5030310 or higher. 

 You can use Clever Classroom MFA with Windows web enabled login. This is useful if you plan to use shared devices/kiosks where you don't have a primary user logging in locally but rather over the web.

 To enable MFA for web-enabled login, use the following settings:

 • 
 Ensure that MFA is enabled on the user directly.

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Badge Invalidation
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What's changing with Clever Badges 3. How to void Badges 4. How a teacher can void individual student Badges 5. Generating and printing Clever Badges 

 

 Overview

 Clever is updating its Clever Badges policy to strengthen security and keep Badges up to date. Admins should void and print Badges annually during natural transitions like summer to keep them secure before the first day of school. Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature expiration dates. 

 

 Voiding and printing Badges annually is critical for protecting student data. Leaving Badges active indefinitely can create security vulnerabilities that put sensitive information at risk. Implementing expiration dates is a vital step in safeguarding your district.

 
 What's changing with Clever Badges

 Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature an expiration date. If a printed Badge has an expiration date, it will continue to work past that date. Newly printed Badges will not have an expiration date. 

 Admins should still void and print Badges during your district’s natural transition period, such as a summer or winter rollover.

 

 Clever Tip: Looking for a quick way to refresh your Badges during rollover? Clever Badges printed to a PDF will match the square 2" Avery labels so you can easily print and stick them to an ID card, inside a folder, or in another secure spot!

 
 How to void Badges

 

 To void all Clever Badges in your district: 

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges page.

• 
 On the Overview page , check the box to select all schools

• 
 Once you’ve selected all schools, you’ll see the option to Void All Badges at the top of the page. Click the Void All Badges button 

• Select your reason for voiding all Badges, and click Confirm
 
 

 

 To void an individual student’s Clever Badge: 

 • 
 Use the search bar at the top of your Clever Dashboard to search for and navigate to the student's profile page

• 
 Choose the Support Tools option to the right of the page

• 
 Select Void Clever Badge and confirm that you’d like to void the Badge 

• After voiding the current Badge, you’ll then see the option to Create Clever Badge from the Support Tools menu. 
 
 

 
 How a teacher can void an individual student Badge

 Teachers also can void and regenerate individual student Badges via their Clever Portal. This can be useful if a student’s Badge becomes invalid in the middle of the school year, and a teacher needs to generate a new one quickly. Teachers can void Badges by:

 • 
 Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu 

• 
 Select the student's class

• 
 Select the student from your roster and click Void Badge.

• 
 The Badge will be voided. You can then click View Badge to download a brand new Badge for that student. 

 
 

 
 Generating and printing Clever Badges

 Please note: Badges printed to PDF will match the Avery label template size for 2” square Avery labels perfectly! Additionally, printing Badges on sticky labels is a great way to ensure students don't lose them, which is more likely to occur when printed on paper. Clever also highly recommends providing students with Badge holders such as those seen HERE . 

 Generating and printing Badges in bulk 

 By navigating to Authentication > Badges , you can select to download the Badges for individual schools, multiple schools, or all enabled schools. Clever Admins can choose to download active Badges or those that are associated with new users who have never had Badges generated previously. 

 

 Depending on the file size, Clever will either download the Badges directly to your computer or email you a secure .zip file that can be downloaded to your desktop if the file exceeds our standard download limitations. The file will contain the following:

 • Folders with the names of each school 
 • Subfolders with student Badges by grade level, including the Badge QR codes and names of each student.
 • PDF of the grade level Badges
• PNG images of Badges
 

• Subfolders with student Badges by sections (i.e., courses/classes), including the Badge QR codes and names of each student.
 • PDF of the grade level Badges
• PNG images of Badges
 

 

 
 

 Generating Individual Student Badges 

 Badges can also be generated or voided on a per-student basis. To do this, please begin by searching for the student using the Clever Dashboard search bar or by navigating to the Data browser > Students tab . Once you've located the student's record to access their profile page, select Support Tools in the top right to void their Badge or generate a new one. 

 Please note: If the option to void or generate a Badge isn't available for a student, the student isn't included in the scope specified when you or another admin set up Clever Badges. 

 

 

 
 

 Related Articles: 

 • 
 Clever Badges: Overview and Setup 

• 
 For Teachers: Clever Badges for Students

title: Adjusting Skyward Multi Factor Authentication to resolve issues with Skyward Autosyncs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adjusting Skyward settings 

 

 Overview

 Skyward has implemented a requirement in their SMS 2.0 product so secured users that are configured as a "System Wide User" and those that have access to the Financial Management or Human Resources are required to use Multi Factor Authentication (MFA) when logging in with a Skyward Password. The MFA requirement will be removed if system wide access, HR and Finance Access are removed from a secured user.

 If you've set up your Clever sync via a “System Wide User” you’ll need to make some updates to keep your Skyward sync running as expected.

 

 
 Adjusting Skyward settings 

 To remove sync issues related to MFA being on in Skyward, follow these steps.

 • 
 Go to the Product Setup (portal), then navigate to Skyward Contact Access > Security - SE > Setup > Configuration > Authentication Configuration. 

 • 
 On this screen, locate the Secured Users without System Wide Access " option, and uncheck the Require MFA box.

 

• 
 Change the “Clever” user such that it is no longer a "System Wide User" by going to Product Setup (portal) > Skyward Contact Access > Security - SE > Super User - SU > Secured User - SU , to view the Secured User list.

• 
 Locate the "Clever" user in the list, then click the Edit button on the right-hand side of the screen.

• 
 On the edit window, set the "System Wide Access" option to No , then click Save .

• 
 Add the “Clever” user to an appropriate security group by going to Product Setup (portal) > Skyward Contact Access > Security - SE > Security Groups - SG > Web Student Management - WS .

• 
 On the Web Student Management screen, you'll want to configure the user in a way that makes sense for your district. Be sure to the user is added to a security group with access to Entity 000 and that the user has Level 5 access under the AD - *Administration . If you’re not familiar with setting up this configuration, contact Skyward Customer Success for help.

title: For Teachers: Short-term Substitute Access to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to set it up 3. The substitute experience 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever's Substitute Access feature makes it easy for you to securely provide temporary access to your resources on Clever for your short-term substitute. Instead of sharing your personal login credentials, you can now generate a Sub Badge that allows substitutes to log in and view the resources and Teacher Page you have provided them access to. This allows you to maintain secure credentials while also giving substitutes the tools they need to manage your online resources effectively for the day.

 Please note: Not all school organization have enabled Substitute Access. If you don't see the option in your Clever Portal, please reach out to your IT or administrative team about enabling this feature. 

 

 This article is for teachers and substitutes using the short-term Substitute Access feature in Clever. If the substitute has their own account in Clever and is listed as a co-teacher on the classes you teach, please refer to this article .

 
 How to set it up

 Provide your substitute access to your resources and Teacher Page on Clever by following the steps below:

 • 
 Log in to your Clever Portal. 

• 
 Navigate to Pages > Teacher Pages on the left side of your Clever Portal.

• 
 Click on the Teacher Page you want to provide the substitute access to.

• 
 In the top right-hand corner, click Share with substitute. 

 
 

 • This will take you to a preview of what the substitute will see when they log in with their Badge. In the image below, Google is greyed out. For security purposes, the subset of “restricted impersonation” apps will not be accessible. Restricted impersonation apps include Google Suite, PowerSchool, and any other applications set by your school’s Clever Admin.
 
 

 • To generate a Sub Badge that will provide login access for the substitute, click Create Substitute Badge and select the date you want the Badge to expire. The expiration date must be within 14 days.
 
 

 • Once the date is selected, click Create Substitute Badge to confirm the date.
• Now you are ready to click Download Substitute Badge to download the Badge to your computer.
• Provide the For Substitutes: Substitute Access to Clever Help Center article to your substitute for extra information and instructions.
 
 

 

 
 The substitute experience

 Substitutes will scan their Sub Badge to access Clever and will be limited to viewing only the Teacher Page you provided them access to. From there, they can log into and use any applications available on that page as the main teacher. For security reasons, certain "restricted impersonation" apps will not be accessible to substitutes, but students will still be able to use these apps from their own Clever Portal. This includes Google Suite applications, PowerSchool, and any other applications set by your school's Clever Admin.

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I create more than one Sub Badge at a time? 

 • 
 For security purposes, only 1 Substitute Badge can be created at a time, even if you have more than one Teacher Page. If you have multiple Teacher Pages that need to be shared, we recommend creating a Teacher Page that houses all of the resources in one and sharing that page. 

 
 Can subs use a Badge to access/log into their Chromebook? 

 • 
 Not at this time, no. We recommend providing guest access to a device and then having a sub go to clever.com /badges.

 
 What if a sub’s device doesn't have a camera? 

 • 
 At this time, Sub Badges are only available as QR codes, so the sub’s device must have some sort of camera to scan the Badge.

 
 How should I provide the Sub Badge to the substitute? 

 • 
 We suggest including it in your sub plans!

 
 Will subs be able to access every application on my Teacher Page? 

 • 
 No. Clever doesn’t allow substitutes to access Google or PowerSchool applications by default for security reasons. Your Clever Admin may also add additional applications to the restricted app list. The substitute will see these apps greyed out with a note that they are inaccessible to the substitute.

 
 Will subs be able to access everything I can access in the third party apps they have access to? 

 • Yes, any applications on the Teacher Page will be accessible to the sub. When the sub accesses the application, they'll be logged in as you, the main teacher. Greyed-out applications are inaccessible to subs, as set by your Clever Admin.
 
 How long can I give subs access to my Teacher Page? 

 • You can grant subs access for up to 14 days, allowing you to set a custom date within that timeframe. 
 
 Can I void a Badge ahead of the expiration date? 

 • Yes, you can void a Badge at any time. To void the Sub Badge navigate to your shared Teacher Page, click Share with Substitute , then click Void Badge .

title: For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA implementation steps and timeline
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Implementation steps 3. Timeline 4. Frequently asked questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 This article briefly overviews the steps for setting up Classroom Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) and a timeline for onboarding and implementation. The timelines provided are a general guideline and will vary based on your district or school’s unique setup. Our onboarding engineers will be able to share a more specific timeline for your district during your onboarding process. If you want more specific guidance on the Classroom MFA setup process, please see our article For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA setup . 

 

 
 Implementation steps

 While this article provides a general overview of the implementation steps for setting up Classroom MFA, each setup process may look different depending on your needs and your system setups. An onboarding engineer will support your Classroom MFA implementation process. If you have questions regarding your district’s setup steps, please contact your onboarding engineer directly.

 • 
 Select users who will be required to use MFA 

 • 
 By group: Students, teachers, or staff. Student subsets can be grouped by schools, grades, or groups (IEP or ELL status).

• 
 By specific user: identify specific users that will be required to use MFA.

 

• 
 Select MFA methods 

 • 
 Choose the MFA method you’ll allow for each user type. Options include picture logins, Badges, PINs, Mobile apps, App-based tokens, biometrics or security keys, text messages, or voice calls.

 

• 
 Configure MFA settings 

 • 
 Set user enforcement dates, re-authentication frequency, and guardian support specifications.

 

• 
 Set Policy name 

 • 
 Set a distinct name for your policy with a description for easy editing in the future. You can create multiple policies to configure settings for specific subgroups.

 

• 
 Preview and complete MFA configuration 

 • 
 Review of summary of your selections. You can always come back and edit your settings at any time.

 

• 
 User self enrollment 

 • 
 Users will be prompted to set up MFA based on your enforcement guidelines.

 

• 
 Optional: Set up Classroom MFA to protect Google Workspace and Chromebooks 

You can configure additional settings in your Google Workspace to enable Clever to protect both your Google Workspace and Chromebooks in addition to the Clever Portal. See this article for more information .
 
 

 
 Timeline

 

 This is an example timeline for setting up Classroom MFA. Implementation timelines will vary based on your district goal and timeline. 

 

 Week 1 

 • 
 Work with a district partnerships specialist to learn more general information about Classroom MFA, receive a quote for your district, verify your Classroom MFA order, and activate your services.

• 
 Once your service is activated, you’ll begin working with a Classroom MFA onboarding engineer to discuss the specifics of your configuration based on your current environment, including what you are hoping to protect with Classroom MFA (Clever Portal, Google Workspace accounts, Chromebooks, Entra ID, etc.).

 
 Week 2 

 • 
 Begin setting up Classroom MFA policies with guidance from your onboarding engineer. You’ll configure settings for which users to manage, second-factor authentication options for each group, authentication frequency, etc.

• 
 Test logins for your users and adjust MFA policies as needed.

 
 Week 3 

 • 
 Discuss rollout plan and change management for end users.

• 
 Explore training resources for end users.

 
 Week 4 

 • 
 Ensure successful enrollment for end users.

• 
 Continued support from the Clever team.

 
 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQ)

 How long does it take to set up Classroom MFA? 

 • 
 Most districts take approximately one to two weeks to set up Classroom MFA within Clever. End user training and rollout typically takes an additional two weeks, depending on how you’d like to notify and train your users.

 
 Can this timeline be expedited? 

 • 
 This timeline varies greatly depending on each district or school’s current environment. The timeline also depends on your availability to test and make changes to your Classroom MFA setup, both within Clever and other systems (e.g., Google).

 
 What is the difference between your district partnerships team and your onboarding engineers? 

 • 
 Our District Partnerships team helps educate you on Classroom MFA, assists in verifying your order, and gets everything set up and activated. Our onboarding engineers ensure the technical implementation runs smoothly and meets your district’s unique requirements.

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Overview 

• 
 For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA setup .

• 
 For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Google Workspace SAML Setup Guide

title: Clever IDM: Implementation steps and timeline
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Implementation steps 3. Timeline 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 This article briefly overviews the steps for setting up Clever Identity Management (IDM) and a timeline for onboarding and implementation. The timelines provided are a general guideline and will vary based on your district or school’s unique setup. Our onboarding engineers will be able to share a more specific timeline for your district during your onboarding process. If you are looking for more specific guidance on setting up your destination, please see the relevant article below:

 • 
 Clever IDM: Google setup 

• 
 Clever IDM: Active Directory setup 

• 
 Clever IDM: Entra ID setup 

 
 

 
 Implementation steps

 While this article provides a general overview of the implementation steps for each Clever IDM destination, each setup process may look different depending on your needs and unique system setups. An onboarding engineer will support your Clever IDM implementation process. If you have questions regarding your district’s setup steps, please contact your onboarding engineer directly.

 Please note: In addition to the implementation steps described below, additional steps may need to be taken to adjust your student information system (SIS) sync to Clever to set up additional SFTP syncs. Our onboarding engineers will assist with any additional steps as needed! 

 

 
 Google 
 • 
 Connect to Google 

 • 
 Provide Clever with access to manage the provisioning of users in Google via a Google Super Admin account.

 

• 
 Select Users 

 • 
 Select which user types you’d like to provision for: students, teachers, and staff.

 

• 
 Set login credentials 

 • 
 Determine formulas for each user group’s username and passwords.

 

• 
 Set up organization units (OUs) 

 • 
 Determine formulas for sorting user types into your Google OUs.

 

• 
 Configure groups (optional) 

 • 
 Depending upon your setup and implementation plan, this step may come after initial account provisioning (step 7).

• 
 Determine within Google if your groups meet the required Clever specifications, and edit as needed. Clever can manage both security and distribution groups.

• 
 Within Clever, determine the groups you’d like to manage and create group membership rules for each.

 

• 
 Summary 

 • 
 Review a summary page of all the selections made for each user type for user account settings, OUs, and if included, groups.

 

• 
 Preview & Provision 

 • 
 Preview your Clever IDM setup, including updates, new users created, changes to OUs, etc.

• 
 With approval from your team and a Clever IDM onboarding specialist, begin provisioning. 

• You’ll see a note in your account that IDM Provisioning is disabled and requires review from a Clever specialist. Our team must review your setup before your first provision to protect your destination accounts and prevent issues with SSO for your end users. Once your team and a Clever IDM onboarding specialist have approved your preview, begin provisioning.
• 
 Review the provisioning process to ensure no errors or unexpected changes occur.

 

• 
 Password reset and account recovery (optional) 

 • 
 As part of Clever IDM, you can configure password reset and account recovery options for any destination and user type you choose to provision for (students, teachers, staff). This enables you to configure settings such as forcing users to reset passwords on first login, allowing teachers to reset student passwords, and setting up account recovery options should a password be forgotten.

 

 
 
 

 
 Active Directory 
 • 
 Connect to Active Directory 

 • 
 Provide Clever with access to connect to your Active Directory server via LDAPS. You will need to work within your server to locate the LDAPS URL, SHA-256 fingerprints of SSL certificates, and Base DN to provide them in Clever for this secure connection.

 

• 
 Select Users 

 • 
 Select which user types you’d like to provision for: students, teachers, and staff.

 

• 
 Set login credentials 

 • 
 Determine formulas for each user group’s usernames and passwords.

 

• 
 Organize OUs 

 • 
 Determine formulas for sorting user types into your Active Directory OUs.

 

• 
 Configure groups (optional) 

 • 
 Depending on your setup and implementation plan, this step may come after initial account provisioning (step 7).

• 
 Within Clever, determine the groups you’d like to manage and group membership rules.

 

• 
 Summary 

 • 
 Review a summary page of all the selections made for each user type for user account settings, OUs, and, if included, groups.

 

• 
 Preview & Provision 

 •
