 Preview your Clever IDM setup including updates, new users created, changes to OUs, etc.

• 
 With approval from your team and a Clever IDM onboarding specialist, begin provisioning. 

• You’ll see a note in your account that IDM Provisioning is disabled and requires review from a Clever specialist. Our team must review your setup before your first provision to protect your destination accounts and prevent issues with SSO for your end users. Once your team and a Clever IDM onboarding specialist have approved your preview, begin provisioning.
• 
 Review the provisioning process to ensure no errors or unexpected changes occur.

 

• 
 Password reset and account recovery (optional) 

 • 
 As part of Clever IDM, you can configure password reset and account recovery options for any destination and user type you choose to provision for (students, teachers, staff). This enables you to configure settings such as forcing users to reset passwords on first login, allowing teachers to reset student passwords, and setting up account recovery options should a password be forgotten.

 

 
 
 

 
 Microsoft Entra ID 
 • 
 Connect to Microsoft Entra ID 

 • 
 Provide Clever access to manage user provisioning on your Entra ID domain. The user authorizing the access request must have admin access to the relevant Entra ID instance.

 

• 
 Select Users 

 • 
 Select which user types you’d like to provision for: students, teachers, and staff.

 

• 
 Set login credentials 

 • 
 Determine formulas for each user group’s usernames and passwords.

 

• 
 Summary 

 • 
 Review a summary page of all the selections made for each user type for user account settings.

 

• 
 Preview & Provision 

 • 
 Preview your Clever IDM setup including updates, new users created, etc.

• 
 With approval from your team and a Clever IDM onboarding specialist, begin provisioning. 

• You’ll see a note in your account that IDM Provisioning is disabled and requires review from a Clever specialist. Our team must review your setup before your first provision to protect your destination accounts and prevent issues with SSO for your end users. Once your team and a Clever IDM onboarding specialist have approved your preview, begin provisioning.
• 
 Review the provisioning process to ensure no errors or unexpected changes occur.

 

• 
 Password reset and account recovery (optional) 

 • 
 As part of Clever IDM, you can configure password reset and account recovery options for any destination and user type you choose to provision for (students, teachers, staff). This enables you to configure settings such as forcing users to reset passwords on first login, allowing teachers to reset student passwords, and setting up account recovery options should a password be forgotten.

 

 
 
 
 Timeline

 The timelines shared below are general guidance for approximately how long most districts take to complete their Clever IDM setup process and a recommended timeline from our teams. This timeline may vary depending on your district or school’s unique needs and setup.

 

 This is an example timeline for setting up Clever IDM. Implementation timelines will vary based on your district goals and timeline. 

 Week 1 

 • 
 Work with a district partnerships specialist to learn more general information about Clever IDM, receive a quot

title: Clever Admin: Clever sync logic
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Primary school assignments 3. Auto-generated section names 4. Section grade assignments 

 

 Overview

 This article breaks down the logic of how Clever determines primary school assignments, creates auto-generated section names, and selects grade levels of sections.

 This can be helpful if you're running into confusion on how any of these values are determined or if you'd like to change any of them.

 

 
 Primary school assignments

 School association for students and teachers is based on section enrollments. If a student or teacher is enrolled in sections at multiple schools, Clever will associate the user with the related schools. A district should NOT attempt to connect a teacher or student with multiple schools by uploading the user multiple times in the students.csv or teachers.csv. Additionally, Clever will assign a primary school association. This will be the school that displays in their Clever Portal. When an application accesses the data for the user, the application will have access to both the user's primary school association, as well as their secondary associations. Primary school is assigned based on where the user has the most section enrollments . For example: If a student is enrolled in 2 sections at School A and 4 sections at School B, their primary school assignment in Clever will be School B (regardless of the school assigned in the SIS).

 You can see the primary school on the user's profile page, along with the other schools they're associated with listed in their sections.

 

 

 

 Additional details:

 • If a user has the same number of sections at two or more schools, Clever will choose the school where the total number of students enrolled in the user's sections is the greatest.
• If a student or teacher's section enrollments change during the year, their primary school in Clever may also change.
• If a student or teacher is not enrolled in any sections, Clever will use the school the user is assigned to in your SIS data.
• A user will be shared with an application if either their primary or secondary school is shared.
• 
 For information on how staff are associated with multiple schools, please see this article . 

 
 

 Please note: If you'd rather Clever use student and teachers' primary school exactly as it's listed in your student information system (SIS), please contact Clever Support. We can disable our default logic for either students, teachers, or both. Once this logic is disabled, Clever will look to your SIS data as the source of truth for primary school assignments. 

 

 

 
 Auto-generated section names

 Section names in Clever are auto-generated to ensure uniqueness, improving recognition and usability for teachers and students. The auto-generated name is: "course_name" - "teacher_last_name" - "period" If course_name is not provided, the section " name" provided in your SIS data will be used. If neither course name or section name are provided, Clever will use ”teacher_last_name - period” 

 

 

 

 Please note: Clever cannot customize the format of auto-generated section names. However, you can choose to sync your own section names to Clever by following the instructions based on your sync type here and letting Clever know to turn off the auto-generation logic. 

 

 
 Section grade assignments

 Section grades are automatically updated based on the most common student grade in the section. If you would like Clever to assign the section grade based on the grade provided by your student information system, reach out to Clever Support at support.clever.com and they can turn the default grade logic off for you.

title: Clever + PebbleGo: Setup guide & rollover process
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Recommended sharing rules 4. Required fields 5. School year rollover 

 

 Overview

 This article contains information to help your school district set up with Clever and PebbleGo. You can also review the PebbleGo rollover process to help you prepare for Back to School season. 

 
 Setup process

 • 
 Make sure your school district is set up with Clever! 

 • 
 If your school district hasn’t been set up with Clever, you can get started here .

 

• 
 If your school district is already using Clever, you can add PebbleGo by following these instructions:

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and navigate to Applications > Add Applications .
• Search for PebbleGo and click on it when it appears in the list.
• You’ll then be brought to a page where you can request the application. When requesting PebbleGo select whether or not you have made a purchase.
• You can then opt to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• On the right side of the page, you can view the fields required by PebbleGo.
• When you have made your selections, click Send integration request .
 

 
 

 
 Recommended sharing rules

 PebbleGo recommends that you share data by school. The simplest way is to set up your sharing using Share by Rules in Clever. You can create a sharing rule by following these steps:

 • 
 Set up your sharing rules:

 • 
 If you are setting up sharing rules for PebbleGo for the first time, please only share the school within your district that have active PebbleGo subscriptions. Reach out to customersuccess@capstonepub.com for assistance. 

 

• 
 Configure your sharing rules by limiting sharing to only the schools and students that should be synced.

 
 • 
 • 
 Once PebbleGo receives your data, they will have an additional filtering process. 

 

 
 Once you've shared data with PebbleGo in Clever, you'll need to work with PebbleGo to finish your sync. PebbleGo’s rostering team will guide you through this process.

 IMPORTANT Once you have spoken with PebbleGo’s rostering team and your data is received by the PebbleGo team, setup may take 5-10 business days, depending on the time of year. 

 

 
 Required fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. 

 PebbleGo requires the following data fields:

 Student Fields: 

 • 
 first and last name

• 
 district

• 
 school

• 
 sis_id and student_number

 
 Contact fields: 

 • 
 district

• 
 name

• 
 sis_id

• 
 students

 
 Teacher fields: 

 • 
 First and last name

• 
 district

• 
 school(s)

• 
 sis_id

• 
 Email

 
 District Admin fields: 

 • 
 district

• 
 first and last name

• 
 email

• 
 title

 
 

 
 School year rollover

 PebbleGo will continue to sync and be active through Clever as long as the school's subscription is active. Near your expiration date Capstone’s renewal team will reach out to discuss your upcoming renewal of PebbleGo.

title: For Teachers: Clever LMS Connect
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 3. Learn more 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 For districts that choose to use it, Clever LMS Connect connects learning apps with the district’s Learning Management System (LMS) like Google Classroom, Canvas, and Schoology.

 Clever LMS Connect works in the background to seamlessly sync assignments and grades from learning apps to the LMS gradebook, along with the one-click logins you’re used to with Clever.

 

 
 Features

 Clever LMS Connect connects learning applications to your LMS through Clever and creates a seamless learning experience for students and teachers by:

 • 
 Saving time: You and students can log in to applications with single-sign on directly from the LMS (if your district has enabled this)

• 
 Eliminating grade double entry: grades and assignments automatically sync between the LMS and learning applications, so students' submissions and grades automatically show up in the LMS.

• 
 Adding content and assignments: When you add an assignment in an app compatible with Clever LMS Connect, it’ll automatically show up in your LMS. This means that students can easily access applications and find assignments in their LMS.

 
 View the videos below to see how Clever LMS Connect works for you and your students.

 Canvas 

 

 

 Google Classroom 

 

 Schoology 

 

 

 

 
 Learn more

 Clever LMS Connect is available for free to districts, and will be available on a per-app basis as learning applications build their Clever LMS Connect integrations. 

 If you’re not sure if your district is using Clever LMS Connect, or which applications are available, please contact your school or district's tech support or IT team. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 How do I know if my district or school is using Clever LMS Connect? How do I know which apps are available? 

 • 
 If you’re not sure if your district is using Clever LMS Connect, or which applications are available, please contact your school or district's tech support or IT team.

 
 

 If I create an assignment in an app compatible with Clever LMS Connect, will it automatically show up in the LMS? Are there any additional steps I need to take? 

 • 
 There are no additional steps teachers need to take to enable assignment sync. 

 
 

 Will Clever LMS Connect pass grades back to our SIS? 

 • 
 Not at this time. Clever LMS Connect will only sync grades and assignments between applications and your LMS.

 
 

 Is Clever LMS Connect a separate charge for districts? 

 • 
 Clever LMS Connect is free for districts.

 
 

 Will Clever LMS Connect work if our districts use multiple LMSs? 

 • 
 At this time, Clever LMS Connect will only work with one LMS per school district. We hope to allow for multiple LMSs per school district in the future.

 
 

 Which LMS systems will work with Clever LMS Connect? 

 • 
 We currently integrate with the three LMSs that are most used by schools - Google Classroom, Schoology, and Canvas. We plan to integrate with more in the future.

 
 

 Who do I contact for technical support with Clever LMS Connect? 

 • 
 Please contact your school or district's IT/tech support team for help.

title: For Students: Password recovery and resets
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up account recovery for Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, or Clever passwords 3. Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page 4. Resetting your password in the Clever Portal 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 This article is an overview of the ways that students can recover and reset the password that they use to log into Clever.

 You will only be able to recover and reset your password if your school has set this up for you.

 
 Setting up account recovery for Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, or Clever passwords

 If your school has set it up, you can use a backup method like email, security questions, or your phone number to get back into your Active Directory, Entra ID, Google Workspace, and Clever passwords accounts right from the Clever login screen! This helps if you’re having trouble with your password. As you read this article, it'll be helpful to know that Active Directory, Entra ID, and Google Workspace may also be referred to as third-party accounts.

 If you are not sure what credentials you use to log in to Clever, your login screen will give you a hint! Navigate to your school’s login screen, and compare the icons below for each login type:

 

 If you follow the steps below, you’ll be able to reset your password if you get locked out of Clever or your school’s Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID accounts. It’s like having a backup plan for logging in!

 To set up your backup method (if your school has this option), go to your Student Portal.

 • Click on your name in the top right so you can choose Account recovery from the menu. If you do not see the Account recovery option, your school has not given permission for you to set up an account recovery option. 
• Click on Set up recovery methods. What options you see will depend on what your school has set up for you. 
• Choose a recovery method and input the information.
• Depending on which recovery method you pick, you'll see instructions on what to do next.
 
 It’s also possible that your school may have provided a recovery method for you using information in their records. For example, if you give your school your cellphone number, they may set up SMS as your account recovery option. If you’d like to update the information your school organization provides, you can do so using the recovery method setup described above.

 
 

 Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page

 Now that you’ve set up your recovery method, you can use it to get back into your Clever, Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID accounts if you ever get locked out.

 To reset Clever and third-party passwords: 

 • From the Clever login screen, click on get help logging in. 
• Choose Reset your account passwords. 
• Input your school organization email address (not your recovery email). This should not be a personal email like student@gmail.com , student@hotmail.com , student@icloud.com etc. 
• You’ll be prompted to confirm your identity using a method you set up in your Portal settings. 
• Now you can set up a new password for your account. Just type in a new password. If your school has any rules for passwords, you can check them by clicking See requirements . When you’re done, you can use your new password to log into your accounts. 
 
 To reset only your Clever password: 

 • Select Get help logging in! to reset your password using the email address linked to your school organization's Clever account. 
• Choose Reset only your Clever password 
• Enter the email that you signed up with to receive password reset instructions, and click Send . 
• You'll receive an email with a link to reset your password. If you do not receive the email, please check your spam folder.
 
 
 Resetting your password in the Clever Portal

 Alternatively, if you can get logged into your Student Portal and want to change your passwords (like if you accidentally shared your password with a friend), you can do it in your Portal. To reset your password in your Clever Portal:

 • Click on your name in the top right corner, then choose Set new password. You don't need to use the recovery method for this. 
• Input your updated password and click Set new password . That’s it, you’re done! 
 
 Please note: Updating your password in Clever may also update your Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID passwords as well. Check in with your teacher if you are not sure what your password is! 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 What if I can’t log in to Clever? 

 • 
 If you’ve forgotten your username or aren’t sure how to reset your password, please reach out to your teacher for help.

 
 What if I don’t see the option for account recovery in my Clever Portal? 

 • 
 Your school has not set up account recovery via Clever at this time. If you need to reset a password, please contact your teacher for help.

 
 What if I don’t have the option to change my password in my Clever Portal? 

 • 
 Your school has not set up password reset via Clever at this time. If you need to reset a password, please contact your teacher for help.

 
 What are the password reset requirements? 

 • Passwords cannot start/end with a space
• Characters cannot repeat more than 3 times
• Passwords cannot contain any part of the user’s name
 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever

title: Clever IDM: Account recovery and password reset
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Enabling account recovery and password reset for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID 3. End user experience 4. Resetting all user passwords in bulk 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 This article overviews the account recovery and password reset functionality available through Clever IDM for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID. If you are not currently utilizing Clever IDM, you cannot configure account recovery and password reset through Clever for these identity providers (IdPs). If you’d like to learn more about Clever IDM, please see our article, Clever IDM: Overview. 

 
 Enabling account recovery and password reset for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID

 

 Please note: A small number of school districts are using this feature with a slightly different user interface. The product works similarly, but screenshots and steps might differ slightly. 

 

 To enable this feature, navigate via Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Password Settings tab , click on Enable Reset , and then follow the prompts outlined below.

 

 

 

 

 • Select Identity Providers 
 • This step is where you’ll choose the destinations that users can reset passwords for – Active Directory, Microsoft Entra, Google. For Active Directory, Entra ID, and Google, only the destinations with an active connection via IDM will be available.
 

• Select user groups 
 • 
 Choose for each user type (students, teachers, and staff) who will have permissions to reset their own passwords.

• 
 For students and teachers, you can set up Delegated Password Reset to allow School Tech Leads and District Help Desk staff to change these user’s passwords.

• 
 For students, you can either allow all students to reset passwords or allow students in certain grades and schools to reset their own passwords. 

 

• Student configuration (if selected user group) 
 • Password 
 • Use Clever's recommendations: Students will be required to use long, easy-to-remember passwords that have a minimum length of 8 characters.
• Configure your own password complexity requirements : Designate minimum and maximum password lengths, and check off requirements for use of lower case letter, upper case letter, number (0-9), symbol (e.g. !@#$%^&*). 
 

• Settings 
 • Check settings to: 
 • Confirm students cannot reuse their previous passwords 
• Confirm students cannot use passwords commonly found in public data breaches. 
• Show helper text (Clever will display a hint provided by you for end users when they create a new password. Ex: Must be ten characters with upper and lower case letters). 
 

 

• Account recovery methods 
 • Select your recovery method(s): SMS, Email, or Security Questions. If your schools use Classroom MFA, and SMS or voice calls are used as MFA methods in one of your district's MFA policies, SMS can’t be selected as a recovery method. If you select SMS or email as an account recovery method, you’ll also have the option to choose a Clever field as the default for account recovery. For example, if you select email as the account recovery option, you can use an extension field with the student’s personal email addresses as the default for student account recovery. Users can update the account recovery SMS number or email later. However, selecting a bulk field ensures that users have at least one recovery method defined without relying on the end user to configure it. The automatic account recovery information will stay up-to-date if the data in the Clever field changes. If you select Email as a recovery method, you’ll also have the option to block users from using a specific domain for email recovery. Please include all domains you’d like to block on the initial setup. Adding additional domains after setup will remove users who previously set up a recovery email with that domain from receiving reset emails. An example of a domain you may wish to block is your Google Workspace domain if your users use Google credentials for SSO to access Clever. Suppose your users have forgotten their Google password when logging into Clever. They’ll also have forgotten their Google password for email, which means they can't access the recovery email sent if they use their school Google account as their recovery email. Blocking this domain can help prevent users from using an email they won't have access to for recovery. 
 

 

• Teacher configuration (if selected user group) 
 • Password 
 • Use Clever's recommendations: Teachers will be required to use long, easy-to-remember passwords that have a minimum length of 8 characters.
• Configure your own password complexity requirements : Designate minimum and maximum password lengths, and check off requirements for use of lower case letter, upper case letter, number (0-9), symbol (e.g. !@#$%^&*). 
 

• Settings 
 • Check settings to: 
 • Confirm teachers cannot reuse their previous passwords 
• Confirm teachers cannot use passwords commonly found in public data breaches. 
• Show helper text (Clever will display a hint provided by you for end users when they create a new password. Ex: Must be ten characters with upper and lower case letters). 
 

 

• Account recovery methods 
 • Select your recovery method(s): SMS, Email, or Security Questions. 
 • If your schools use Classroom MFA, and SMS or voice calls are used as MFA methods in one of your district's MFA policies, SMS can’t be selected as a recovery method. 
• If you select SMS or email as an account recovery method, you’ll also have the option to choose a Clever field as the default for account recovery. For example, if you select email as the account recovery option, you can use an extension field with the student’s personal email addresses as the default for student account recovery. Users can update the account recovery SMS number or email later. However, selecting a bulk field ensures that users have at least one recovery method defined without relying on the end user to configure it. The automatic account recovery information will stay up-to-date if the data in the Clever field changes. 
• If you select Email as a recovery method, you’ll also have the option to block users from using a specific domain for email recovery. Please include all domains you’d like to block on the initial setup. Adding additional domains after setup will remove users who previously set up a recovery email with that domain from receiving reset emails. An example of a domain you may wish to block is your Google Workspace domain if your users use Google credentials for SSO to access Clever. Suppose your users have forgotten their Google password when logging into Clever. They’ll also have forgotten their Google password for email, which means they can't access the recovery email sent if they use their school Google account as their recovery email. Blocking this domain can help prevent users from using an email they won't have access to for recovery. 
 

 

 

• Staff configuration (if selected as a user group) 
 • Password 
 • Use Clever's recommendations: Staff will be required to use long, easy-to-remember passwords that have a minimum length of 8 characters.
• Configure your own password complexity requirements : Designate minimum and maximum password lengths, and check off requirements for use of lower case letter, upper case letter, number (0-9), symbol (e.g. !@#$%^&*). 
 

• Settings 
 • Check settings to: 
 • Confirm staffs cannot reuse their previous passwords 
• Confirm staff cannot use passwords commonly found in public data breaches. 
• Show helper text (Clever will display a hint provided by you for end users when they create a new password. Ex: Must be ten characters with upper and lower case letters). 
 

 

• Account recovery methods 
 • Select your recovery method(s): SMS, Email, or Security Questions. If your schools use Classroom MFA, and SMS or voice calls are used as MFA methods in one of your district's MFA policies, SMS ca

title: Clever + Prodigy: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Required fields 4. Support Contacts 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through how to get set up with Prodigy in Clever.

 Before reading this guide, please make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: clever.com/signup . For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide.

 Clever and Prodigy have teamed up to build Prodigy teacher training in Clever Academy, our free training hub.

 

 

 
 Setup Process

 If your district is already on Clever, you can connect Prodigy’s application by accepting your invite from them OR following these instructions:

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications .
• Search for Prodigy
• You will then be brought to a page where you can authorize your Prodigy request. 
• You will be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the Prodigy icons in their Clever Portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request!
 
 Once you have shared data with Prodigy, the Prodigy team will set up the initial sync and will reach out to you to discuss communicating with teachers.

 

 Recommended data sharing 

 Before sharing your district's data with Prodigy:

 • 
 Ensure that students and teachers are in their correct grade levels and that classes are updated for the current school year in your Student Information System (SIS).

 
 Prodigy recommends that you “Share all records in my district” in Clever. You can find more info about sharing data here . 

 

 
 Required fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. Prodigy requires the following data fields to be sent:

 • 
 Student: email, first name, last name

• 
 Teacher: first name, last initial 

 
 
 Support Contacts

 Prodigy 

 Use this form to submit a Customer Support ticket under the category School / District Administrator Inquiries 

 You can reach Prodigy’s Customer Support team by phone toll-free at 1-866-585-4655. 

 Assistance is available from Monday-Friday 10 AM-5 PM ET.

 Alternatively, you can send an email to support@prodigygame.com . 

 Clever 

 Support.clever.com (click the chat icon in the corner of the screen)

 Contact Clever with issues related to customizing field access.

title: For Clever Admins: Clever & Google
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever Identity Management (IDM) 3. Google Classroom roster import application 4. Google for Education Plus + Clever IDM: Google Bundle 5. Google License Manager 6. Clever LMS Connect 7. Classroom MFA 8. Google as a login method for Clever 9. Clever Badges & Chromebooks 10. Google SSO Apps 11. Google Classroom application add-ons 

 

 Overview

 Clever and Google work better together! With specially designed tools that simplify Google management in school settings, such as Clever Identity Management (IDM), Google Classroom roster import, and Clever LMS Connect, you can save yourself and teachers hours of effort. You can also improve your school security and student login experiences with Clever Badges and Classroom MFA. This article highlights how you can use Google and Clever together to deliver a seamless and secure digital learning experience for your schools.

 Interested in deep-diving into how to best use Clever and Google together? Check out our course, Clever & Google: Secure Connected Classroom Certification available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Clever Identity Management (IDM)

 Clever IDM helps districts automate the account provisioning and lifecycle management for Google Workspace (as well as Active Directory and Entra ID). This affordable offering provides school districts with a simple workflow to automate account creation, archival and management for Google accounts directly with Clever. Clever IDM automatically syncs users from your SIS to Google daily to ensure your Google access is always up-to-date. Click here for a one-page overview of Clever IDM, or see our articles Clever IDM: Overview or Clever IDM: Google Setup to learn more. 

 
 Google Classroom roster import application

 The Google Classroom roster import application eases admin and teacher workloads by automatically provisioning Google Classroom courses based on your district’s SIS + Clever data. The application is available at no additional cost to U.S. and Canadian school districts with a Google Standard Edition subscription or a Google Workspace for Education Plus subscription for staff and students.

 To learn more, check out our article Clever + Google Classroom: Overview or our Google Classroom Resource Hub .

 

 
 Google for Education Plus + Clever IDM: Google Bundle

 Clever’s Google for Education Plus + Clever Identity Management (IDM) package, also known as the Google Bundle, combines Clever’s identity management solution with Google’s Workspace for Education Plus to save you time and money. This bundle ensures that students, teachers, and staff always have access to the systems they need. For $7.25 per user per year, you get:

 • 
 Google Workspace for Education Plus 

• 
 Clever IDM 

• 
 Google Classroom roster import 

• 
 Clever Badges 

 
 The Google Bundle establishes interoperability across the tools used daily in your district, focusing on data privacy and security. Click here for a one-page overview of the Google Bundle, or see our article Google for Education Plus + Clever IDM: Google Bundle Overview to learn more.

 

 
 Google License Manager

 For districts using Google products, we offer an optional Google License Manager. This manages license access for selected users via your Organizational Units in Google, so you don’t have to apply or remove them manually. Learn more here . 

 
 Clever LMS Connect

 Clever LMS Connect is a no-cost offering to districts and schools that allows you to connect Clever with your LMS so that your users can launch applications directly from their LMS and sync assignments and gradebooks between apps and Google Classroom. By utilizing Clever LMS Connect with Google Classroom, you’ll save teachers time by eliminating grade double entry and enabling them to add application content and assignments easily. Learn more about Clever LMS Connect in our article For Clever Admins: Clever LMS Connect. 

 
 Classroom MFA

 Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a second layer of security for Clever without the requirement of a second device. Classroom MFA protects applications that use Clever for single sign-on with age-appropriate second factors like Clever Badges or login pictures. Use Classroom MFA to protect Clever Portal, Google Workspace, and Google Chromebooks. This solution helps districts stay ahead of cybersecurity threats without disrupting learning.

 Click here for a one-page overview of Classroom MFA, or see our article For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Google Workspace SAML Setup Guide . 

 
 Google as a login method for Clever

 To make logging into Clever as streamlined as possible for your users, why not use the Google credentials they’re already familiar with for their Clever login credentials?

 You can easily set Google as the identity provider (IdP) for Clever within your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings . For step-by-step instructions, please see our article Single Sign-on: Log in with Google .

 
 Clever Badges & Chromebooks

 Clever works seamlessly with Chromebooks, providing students and teachers access to all their learning applications while only logging in once per device. Utilize Clever Badges to enable students to login without typing credentials, or configure settings in your Google Admin Console to instantly log students into their Chromebook and Clever Portal when they enter their login credentials into their Chromebook. Learn more about the various integrations for Clever and Chromebooks in our articles below:

 • 
 Single Sign-on (SSO): Setting up Badges into Chromebooks and Chromebook logins with Clever as your organization-wide SSO Provider 

• 
 Single Sign-on: Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever as an SSO Profile in Google 

• 
 Single Sign-on (SSO): Chromebooks - Troubleshooting 

 
 

 
 Google SSO Apps

 If you are utilizing Clever as the identity provider for Google, either as an organization-wide SSO Provider or an SSO Profile in Google, we recommend setting up the Google SSO applications available through Clever. These applications provide a smooth login experience via Clever Portal where end users will simply click the application and automatically be logged into their Google resources such as Drive, Docs, and Sheets. Learn more about the setup in our article Adding Google SSO applications .

 

 
 Google Classroom application add-ons

 Clever works directly with our application partners that use Clever LMS Connect to offer Google Classroom add-ons. This simplifies the login process, enabling students and teachers to access learning applications via single sign-on (SSO) directly in Google Classroom! Learn more here .

title: For Application Partners: Sending and receiving Clever LMS Connect requests from districts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Inviting districts to use your application with Clever LMS Connect 3. Receiving district requests for your Clever LMS Connect integration 

 

 Overview

 This article provides an overview of how you can both invite and receive requests from districts for your integration with Clever LMS Connect. If you are not using Clever LMS Connect yet or are just getting started, please see our article For Application Partners: LMS Connect .

 With Clever LMS Connect, you can easily integrate with district LMSs via the Clever API. Districts can launch your application directly from their LMS using SSO while saving teachers time with gradebook syncs, and students jump into learning with seamless assignment syncs. 

 
 Inviting districts to use your application with Clever LMS Connect

 Within your Clever Dashboard there are a couple of ways you can invite districts to connect with your application via Clever LMS Connect. Each option is explained below:

 To invite districts to connect via the LMS Connect tab: 

 • Navigate to your LMS Connect tab and scroll down to view your district list.
• Click the Invite button in the LMS Connect Invites column. 
• Your invite will be sent to the district’s Clever Admins. You can add additional contact emails and include a customized invitation message. 
• Click Send invitation to send the invitation to the district. 
• You will need to wait for the district to approve the invitation on their side before continuing the setup.
 
 To invite districts to connect via the District’s page in your Clever Dashboard: 

 • Use the search bar at the top of the screen to search for a district. 
• On the district page select the LMS Connect: Invite to Connect button. 
• Your invite will be sent to the district’s Clever Admins. You can add additional contact emails and choose to include a customized invitation message.
• Click Send invitation to send the invitation to the district. 
• You will need to wait for the district to approve the invitation on their side before continuing the setup.
 
 
 Receiving district requests for your Clever LMS Connect integration

 

 Districts can also request to connect with your application via LMS Connect. These requests will appear on your Home Page: 

 

 

 You can also navigate directly to the District’s Page in your Clever Dashboard to review the request:

 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Application Partners: LMS Connect

title: For Teachers: Helping your students set up and troubleshoot Classroom MFA
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Helping students set up Classroom MFA 3. Downloading and voiding your class MFA Badges 4. Troubleshooting student MFA 5. Using backup login codes to support students 

 

 Overview

 This article has information for teachers on how to help their students set up and troubleshoot Clever’s Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (Classroom MFA for short). As an optional add-on feature, Clever offers school organizations Classroom MFA to provide an extra layer of security for student, teacher and instructional staff logins. Classroom MFA helps increase students' digital citizenship skills as they navigate our digital environments in a safe and responsible way.

 You’ll only have access to Classroom MFA if your school organization has added it to your Clever account. 

 
 Helping students set up Classroom MFA

 If your school organization requires Classroom MFA for students, they will need to activate this in order to log in to Clever. You can view which students haven’t activated their MFA by navigating to your Clever Portal > My Students > Student Roster.

 You can find in-depth instructions for students to set up their Classroom MFA here: For Students: Setting up Classroom MFA . 

 We’ve gathered a few resources to help you set up MFA for your students: :

 • 
 Check out this slide deck you can use to walk students through the setup process ! (for grades 1-4)

• 
 If you prefer to play a live video, you can show: Getting students setup on Classroom MFA 

• 
 You can also use this Kahoot! to help students learn more about what MFA is and why it’s important.

• 
 You may also want to share our article, For Families: What’s Classroom MFA? with your student’s families to help them better understand Classroom MFA.

• 
 Introduce online security by watching CISA’s Secure Our World video playlist .

• 
 Start a conversation about digital citizenship with Common Sense Education’s K-12 Digital Citizenship Curriculum .

 
 Seeing activation status 

 If the student hasn’t activated their Classroom MFA, you’ll see that indicated by their name in the student roster (as indicated in the red boxes).

 

 
 Downloading and voiding your class MFA Badges

 If your students are using a Badge for Classroom MFA, you can download a class set of Badges by going to Clever Portal > My Students > Student Roster > Selecting the class you wish to print badges for.

 

 

 What if one of your students has lost their Classroom MFA Badge? No problem! You can void it and create a new one for them under Student Tools (see red arrow in screenshot).

 

 This will download a new MFA Badge to your device, which you can print out for the student. The next time the student logs in, they will be prompted to follow a login flow to validate the badge.

 If your student forgets their login MFA pictures, you can help students by reviewing their picture selections and helping them get logged in or by initiating a reset of their picture selections from your Teacher Portal.

 
 Troubleshooting student MFA

 Viewing student picture MFA selections 

 If a student forgets their login pictures, you have a couple options to support them in class. If you have time to personally support them and help them recall their login pictures, you can choose to reveal their pictures in your teacher portal. By revealing the pictures you can then coach them on a way to better remember them like creating a fun, memorable story about the two pictures. 

 • 
 Navigate to Student Roster from your Teacher Page > Click the student’s name > Click Show MFA pictures to review the pictures to help your student get logged in on their own device 

• Click Hide MFA pictures once you are all set. 
 
 Resetting Picture, Badge, and PIN MFA 

 Another option you have to support students who can’t login with their MFA for some reason, is to do a reset of MFA methods. If you reset a student’s MFA, they will need to go through the enrollment process all over again to log in. This will give them the option to select new login pictures, download and print a new Badge, or create a new PIN depending on the MFA policy they are in. 

 Reset may be a good option when a student has shared their login pictures with friends or if you don’t have time to individually support them. 

 • 
 Navigate to Student Roster from your Teacher Page, select the student, and Click Reset MFA. 

• 
 Click Reset. 

• Your student will be prompted to reset their MFA pictures on their device. 
 
 

 
 Using backup login codes to support students

 If your student cannot remember or lost their second authentication factor you will have the option to provide them with a short-term backup login code. On the student’s login screen, they’ll need to select Can not login? 

 

 The student will then see the screen on which to enter their backup login code on their device:

 

 To generate a backup code to provide your student: 

 • 
 From your Clever Portal, click the My Students drop-down on the left-hand side and select Student Roster 

• 
 Select the class the student is in from the Filter by class drop-down

• 
 Locate the student record from the list and click Get backup login code on the right-hand side 

• A seven-digit backup code will be provided. Write this code down and pass it along securely to your student. The code will expire in 15 minutes. 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Teachers & Staff: Setting up Classroom MFA 

• For Students: Setting up Classroom MFA

title: For App Partners: Viewing district training activity
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing general training 3. Steps to take based on training 

 

 Overview

 Our application partners have asked us about which districts have engaged with Clever training about how Clever works, so they know how to best support each district. 

 This article walks through how to view general training activity data from the Clever Apps Dashboard. Training activity is measured via districts’ engagement with Clever Academy, our free online training hub. 

 Please note: This feature will be rolled out over time, so if you don’t see it for your districts hold tight as we finish rollout. 

 

 
 View general training data

 From your Clever Apps Dashboard home page, click on a district. From the Overview tab, you’ll see a section named Clever Academy Usage.

 From here, you’ll see aggregated data for that district.

 

 

 Key 

 

 District Overall: Yes or No

 Lets you know if anyone in the district has registered for any Clever Academy course since the district account was created.

 

 Lifetime number of certified clever champions: (numeric value)

 Displays the total number of Clever Certified Champions in the district for all time. A Clever Champion is a Clever Admin, School Admin, or Teacher who has completed Levels 1-4 of our certification courses.

 

 Clever Champions in [year] 

 Display the total number of Clever Certified Champions in the district year to date for the current calendar year, Jan 1-Dec 31.

 

 
 

 Steps to take based on training data

 You know your districts best: you work with them regularly and have typically built relationships with them!

 The training data is meant to be one input in your overall strategy to support your districts. Below are some ways you could use the data to inform your support strategy. 

 • If the district has no engagement in training at all: 
 • Consider sharing out these articles about Clever Academy training:
 • 
 For Clever Admin 

• 
 For Teachers 

• 
 For School Tech Leads (AKA School Admin) 

 

 

• If the district has Clever Champion engagement for past years, but not this year: 
 • Consider sharing a reminder about Clever Academy, especially if the district is having any trouble (such as submitting a lot of support tickets to your team, data sharing issues, low utilization, etc). 
 

• If the district has Clever Champion engagement for this year: 
 • This lets you know that the district has some expert Clever users! This might make integrating and working with them a little easier since they’ve demonstrated their Clever knowledge. 
 

 
 

 For districts with no or limited Clever Academy engagement, you can customize this email template: 

 

 
 Hello, [district user]! 

 As you may know, our most successful districts are those that are well-trained. 

 In addition to our training materials [here], I wanted to highlight free training from our single sign-on and rostering partner, Clever. 

 Completing Clever’s training can minimize login, rostering, and account access issues and ensure that you're getting the most out of [app name]. 

 Here are a few resources you might find helpful! 

 • 
 For Clever district Admin: Become a Clever Certified Champion 

• 
 For School Admin: Become a Certified Clever Champion 

• 
 For Teachers: Become a Certified Clever Champion 

 
 You and your district users can find other courses: on everything from troubleshooting to cybersecurity resources from Clever.academy ! 

 

 Thanks for everything you you do, 

 

 - [Your name] 

 [Your role] 

 [Application name]

title: For Families: What's Classroom MFA?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is Classroom MFA? 3. How will my student(s) use Classroom MFA? 4. Troubleshooting 5. FAQ 

 

 Overview

 In this article, you can learn more about what Classroom Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) is, why your student’s school is requiring it, and how your student will use it when logging into Clever. If you are experiencing any other issues, please contact your student’s teacher for further assistance. 

 
 What's Classroom MFA?

 MFA enables users to log into programs by requiring them to verify their identity using two login methods (or factors). For example, when logging into your online bank account, you may be prompted to enter your username and password, and then to verify your login again by entering a text message code sent to your phone. This means you have used two factors to login! A username and password and a text code.

 MFA enhances login security and can help prevent unknown logins to student accounts and exposure of sensitive student information called personally identifiable information (PII). It is considered to be an industry best-practice to implement MFA on digital accounts to avoid account compromise by bad actors. Many districts and schools are being required to implement MFA for greater account and data security. Classroom MFA also helps increase students' digital citizenship skills as they navigate our digital environments in a way that's safe and responsible.

 Clever’s Classroom MFA allows students to securely log into their Clever accounts using two different login methods (or factors) to ensure they are logging into the correct account. Depending on the school district’s settings, Classroom MFA may not necessitate a second device, such as a cell phone, for verification. If your student’s school or district is using Clever’s Classroom MFA, your student may be required to use any of the following options that have been pre-selected by your district/school: 

 • 
 Login pictures

• 
 Clever Badges

• PIN (6 digits)
• 
 Authenticator app

• 
 Text message (SMS)

• 
 Voice call

• 
 Biometrics or security key 

 
 
 How will my student(s) use Classroom MFA?

 Based on the settings configured by your student’s district or school, they will either be prompted to set up their Classroom MFA for the first time or, if they have previously set up their second factor, they will enter it when logging in.

 Please check out this video for more information on Classroom MFA and the setup process for your student:

 

 

 

 If your student is setting up Classroom MFA for the first time, they should follow these steps: 

 • 
 Go to their school or district's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/district-URL ) and sign in the way they normally would.

• 
 They’ll be prompted to set up their additional login factor for Classroom MFA.

• 
 Once they have set up Classroom MFA they can use Clever as they normally would! Be sure your student doesn’t forget what they set up - Clever may ask them again every time they log in, every seven days, or every 30 days depending on the school’s requirements.

 
 For more specific instructions to help your student set up Classroom MFA login pictures or Classroom MFA Badges please see our article For Students: Setting up Classroom MFA . If you have questions about your student’s Classroom MFA options, please reach out to their teacher directly.

 If your student has previously set up their Classroom MFA, they should follow these steps to login:

 • 
 Go to their school or district's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/district-URL ) and sign in the way they normally would.

• 
 They’ll be prompted to enter their additional login factor for Classroom MFA.

• 
 Once they have entered both login factors, they’ll be logged into their account! See the troubleshooting section below for what to do if your student makes a mistake logging in, or forgets their second factor.

 
 
 Troubleshooting

 If your student is attempting to log into Clever with Classroom MFA and forgets their second factor or makes a mistake, they should select Try Again. 

 If your student can’t remember how to login, they will need assistance from their teacher, or a guardian if your district has set permissions for families to help with MFA resets.

 For help your student should select the Can not login? button pictured below. 

 

 The student will either be presented with a screen for a backup login code from their teacher, or if your district has enabled guardian support they’ll see the option to ask a teacher or a guardian for help.

 

 If guardian support has not been enabled for your student's district. 

 

 If guardian support is enabled for your student's district. 

 If your student selects Ask guardian you will receive an email with a backup code to the email address that your student’s district has on file. If you need to update this email address, please contact your school directly. The Clever team cannot update this email address for you.

 

 If your student selects Ask teacher, the teacher will need to provide a backup code to your student within 15 minutes for the student to login. If the teacher will not be able to provide a backup code within 15 minutes, please contact your student’s teacher directly for further assistance. If your student is able to receive a backup login code from their teacher they can enter it in the screen pictured below and click Log in .

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 What is MFA? 

 MFA stands for Multi-factor authentication and is a login process that requires the user to enter two different pieces of information when logging in, for example, a username and password, and then entering a code sent to you via text message.

 How often will my student be prompted to log into Clever with Classroom MFA? 

 Depending on the settings your district configures, Clever may ask students for a second factor of authentication again every time they log in, every seven days, or every 30 days.

 How do I know what my student’s additional authentication factor is? 

 When your student attempts to log into Clever, they’ll either be prompted to set up their additional factor for the first time, or they’ll be prompted to enter a factor they’ve already set. For questions on the factors your student has available, or if they’ve forgotten any of their login credentials, please reach out to your student’s teacher directly.

 What if my student cannot get logged in? 

 If your student's district has configured settings so that you can help your student by providing them a one-time backup code, your student may be able to request you receive this code at the email you provided to the school. Otherwise, please reach out to your student’s teacher directly. The Clever Support team does not have access to your child’s login information for security reasons.

title: Google for Education Plus + Clever IDM: Google Bundle Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Google Workspace for Education Plus 3. Clever IDM 4. Google Classroom roster import application 5. Clever Badges 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever’s Google for Education Plus + Clever Identity Management (IDM), also known as the Google Bundle, combines Clever’s identity management solution with Google’s Workspace for Education Plus to save you time and money. The bundle is currently available only to school organizations in the United States. It includes:

 • 
 Google Workspace for Education Plus 

• 
 Clever IDM 

• 
 Google Classroom roster import 

• 
 Clever Badges 

 
 The Google Bundle establishes interoperability across the tools used every day in your school organization, with a focus on data privacy and security. Click here for a one-page overview of the Google Bundle.

 
 Google Workspace for Education Plus

 Google Workspace for Education Plus allows you to transform your institution’s digital learning environment. As Google’s most comprehensive solution, Education Plus provides advanced security, insights, and user-friendly tools that enrich and customize learning. Take a look at these tools and the specific needs each tool addresses for your school organization:

 • 
 Collaboration and productivity: Docs, Sheets, Slides, Forms, Sites, Calendar, AppSheet, Core, Google Cloud Search

• 
 Privacy, security, device, and access management: Admin

• 
 Classroom management and plagiarism detection: Classroom, Assignments

• 
 Storage: Drive

• 
 Video conferencing: Meet

• 
 Email and messaging: Gmail, Chat, Groups

• 
 Visibility and analytics: BigQuery, Looker Studio

 
 For more information on the tools included in Google Workspace for Education Plus, please see Google’s Education Plus One-Pager .

 
 Clever IDM

 Clever IDM saves time and keeps accounts secure by automating account provisioning and access management for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID. Clever IDM automatically creates and updates usernames, passwords, and organizational units (OUs) based on the data you sync to Clever from your student information system (SIS) or Human Resources Information System. Maintain secure, up-to-date accounts with audit logs that allow you to control access and enforce strong passwords. With Clever IDM, you can prevent classroom disruptions by ensuring that everyone has the access they need at the right time.

 Clever’s teams will provide ample training and setup guidance to ensure a smooth configuration of Clever IDM with your existing systems.

 To learn more about Clever IDM, see our article Clever IDM: Overview .

 
 Google Classroom roster import

 Clever is the only premier rostering partner for Google Classroom. Google Classroom roster import saves your teachers time by automatically creating and managing your Google Classroom rosters at scale based on the data you are syncing from your SIS to Clever.

 Google Classroom course names will match Clever section names, and rosters will update automatically as students change sections in your SIS and Clever. You can set specific access dates for teachers and students to align with school start dates or professional development days.

 For a complete overview of the Google Classroom roster import application, please see our article Clever + Google Classroom: Overview .

 
 Clever Badges

 Clever Badges are a free login tool available to any school organization using Clever. Use Badges with your Google Bundle to seamlessly integrate your tools and provide instant secure access for any student.

 Students flash their Badge, a physical passcode similar to a QR code, at their device webcam to log into their device and learning applications with no typing required!

 Clever Badges are compatible with Chromebooks, iOS devices, Android, and Windows devices. They also work with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and Internet Explorer browsers.

 You can configure settings in your Google Admin Console for students to use their Badge to log directly into their Chromebook and Clever Portal.

 For more information on utilizing Clever Badges at your school, check out our article Clever Badges: Overview and Setup .

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Who should I contact for more information about purchasing Google Workspace for Education Plus and Clever IDM? 

 • 
 Reach out directly by completing this form and a member of our team will be in touch to share more details.

 
 What is the cost of Google Workspace for Education Plus and Clever IDM? 

 • 
 For more information on the cost of the Google Bundle for your school organization, please reach out to our team here .

 
 Can I still purchase the Google Bundle if I previously bought from another Google reseller? 

 • 
 Yes! Licenses can be transferred to Clever from another reseller. If you have Legacy Google licenses, you can transfer within 30 days of your renewal date.

 
 Is the Google Bundle offer available outside the United States? 

 • 
 At this time, Clever can only offer the Google Bundle to school organizations in the U.S.

 
 

 Interested in deep-diving into how to best use Clever and Google together? Check out our course, Clever & Google: Secure Connected Classroom Certification available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 Clever + Google Classroom: Overview 

• 
 Clever + Google Classroom: Setup guide 

• 
 Clever IDM: Overview 

• 
 Clever IDM: Google Setup 

• 
 Clever Badges: Overview and Setup

title: For Clever Admins: How to set up Clever LMS Connect
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up your Clever LMS Connection 3. Troubleshooting 

 Overview

 You can directly connect your Learning Management System (LMS) to Clever LMS Connect from your Clever Dashboard! This article will provide you with an overview of the setup process. 

 Please note: You can connect your LMS only if one or more of your Clever applications is available to connect (otherwise, you’ll see the opportunity to join our waitlist). If you're on the waitlist, you'll get notified once one of your connected apps builds a Clever LMS Connect integration. 

 

 
 Setting up your Clever LMS Connection

 Click the drop-down for the LMS you'd like to connect.

 If you use Schoology as your LMS, you will be required to set up LTI SSO. Failing to set up LTI SSO will prevent gradebook and assignment syncs from functioning. If you use Canvas as your LMS, it is highly recommended that you use LTI SSO for the best LMS Connect experience.

 

 
 Connect with Schoology 
 • 
 From the Clever Dashboard > Applications > LMS Connect page and click Connect your LMS . We currently support three LMSs: Canvas, Schoology, and Google Classroom.

 
 

 • Select your Learning Management System (LMS) & click Next: Connect your LMS. At this time, it’s only possible to connect one LMS. 
 
 

 • Before you can finish connecting your LMS, Clever must prepare your data. You'll see a loading bar in the bottom corner showing the progress, and you'll also get an email when the process is done. In the meantime, you can select any apps you would like Clever LMS Connect to be available for. 
• When ready, you can continue the setup process by clicking Continue setup on your LMS Connect page . 
• Click Next: Log into Schoology. 
 
 

 • 
 Configure your LMS source.

 • 
 Enter your LMS URL or check No Custom URL. 

• 
 Connect your LMS account.

 

 
 

 • 
 Sign into your LMS. Each LMS may have a different sign-in method. 

• 
 Once signed in, you’ll see confirmation that your account is connected:

 
 

 • Scroll down in the window and click Validate Configuration. 
 
 

 • You’ll now see the validation results and can click Continue Setup . 
 
 

 • After your LMS is successfully connected, Clever will begin account matching to connect accounts. This can take a few days. You’ll be notified via email when it’s complete. You can send connection requests to your available applications while matching is in progress, so there’s no need to wait! 
 
 Please note: We match IDs between your LMS and Clever. If those IDs don't match up, we'll let you know and help you create and upload a simple file to map the IDs and get everything working smoothly. 

 

 • On your LMS Connect page, you’ll see that matching is in progress. 
 
 

 • Please note that when using Clever LMS Connect with Schoology, Clever requires you to utilize LTI SSO for the best experience. Learn more here .
 
 

 

 
 

 
 Connect with Canvas 
 • Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Applications > LMS Connect page and click Connect your LMS . 
• Select your Learning Management System (LMS) & click Next: Connect your LMS. At this time, it’s only possible to connect one LMS. 
 
 

 • Before you can finish connecting your LMS, Clever must prepare your data. You'll see a loading bar in the bottom corner showing the progress, and you'll also get an email when the process is done. In the meantime, you can select any apps you would like Clever LMS Connect to be available for. 
• When ready, you can continue the setup process by clicking Continue setup on your LMS Connect page . 
• Click Next: Log into Canvas. 
 
 

 • 
 Configure your LMS source.

 • 
 Enter your Canvas URL. Be sure to include https:// at the beginning 

• 
 Create your Canvas Developer Keys. Click Open Canvas Developer Keys in the second step to get to the needed screen. You must be a Canvas Super Admin to access the Developer Keys screen. 

• Click the + Developer Key > + API Key button, then complete the fields as follows: 
• 
 Key Name

 • 
 Edlink

 

• 
 Owner Email

 • 
 accounts@ed.link 

 

• 
 Redirect URIs (One Per Line)

 • 
 https://ed.link/api/authentication/canvas 

• 
 https://ed.link/sso/administrator 

 

• 
 Redirect URI (Legacy):

 • 
 Please leave blank.

 

• 
 Vendor Code (LTI 2):

 • 
 Please leave blank.

 

• 
 Icon URL

 • 
 https://ed.link/icon.png 

 

• 
 Notes

 • 
 You can add any private notes about this integration.

 

• Click Save .
 

• Enable the key in Canvas by clicking the checkmark under the State column on the Developer Keys page.
• In Clever, mark this step as Complete .
• In Canvas, go to the new Developer Key you created in step 4.
 • Copy/paste the number above Show Key and paste into the Client ID field in Clever. 
• 
 Click Show Key and copy/paste the Key into the Secret Key field in Clever.

• 
 

• Sign into Canvas. 
• Once signed in, you’ll see confirmation that your account is connected.
• Scroll down in the window and click Validate Configuration. 
 
 

 • You’ll now see the validation results and can click Continue Setup . 
• After your LMS is successfully connected, Clever will begin account matching to connect accounts. This can take a few days. You’ll be notified via email when it’s complete. You can send connection requests to your available applications while matching is in progress, so there’s no need to wait!
• On your LMS Connect page, you’ll see that matching is in progress. 
 
 Please note: We match IDs between your LMS and Clever. If those IDs don't match up, we'll let you know and help you create and upload a simple file to map the IDs and get everything working smoothly. 

 • When using Clever LMS Connect with Canvas, Clever strongly recommends you to utilize LTI SSO for the best experience. Learn more here .
 
 
 

 
 Connect with Google Classroom 
 • To ensure a quality data matching experience with Google Classroom, Clever requires districts to connect with Clever's Google Classroom roster import application. As Google Classroom allows teachers to modify their own classrooms, this presents a challenge with maintaining data parity for Clever LMS Connect if a district isn't using Clever to roster Google Classroom courses. Please start your setup by adding the Google Classroom roster import application . For more information, click here , or reach out to Clever Support. 
• Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Applications > LMS Connect page and click Connect your LMS . 
• Select your Learning Management System (LMS) & click Next: Connect your LMS. At this time, it’s only possible to connect one LMS. 
 
 

 • Before you can finish connecting your LMS, Clever must prepare your data. You'll see a loading bar in the bottom corner showing the progress, and you'll also get an email when the process is done. In the meantime, you can select any apps you would like Clever LMS Connect to be available for. 
• When ready, you can continue the setup process by clicking Continue set up on your LMS Connect page . 
• Review the steps you'll need to complete in the modal. 
 
 

 • Configure your LMS source. • 
 To start, open the Domain-wide Delegation Settings Page via the link in Clever and click Add new . 

• In the pop-up modal, enter the following configuration details:
 • Client ID: 105544177237393168096
• Oauth scopes (comma-delimited): https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.courses.readonly,https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.rosters.readonly,https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.coursework.students,https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.courseworkmaterials,https://www.googleapis.com/auth/classroom.coursework.me 
 

• Click Authorize. 
• Go back to your Clever Dashboard to reconnect Google on the Applications > LMS Connect page.
 

 
 • 
 • 
 • Look for the yellow banner that says Google Account not connected , then click Connect .
• Follow the steps in the pop-up modal to re-authenticate using your Google Super Admin account 
 

 

 
 • After your LMS is s

title: For Clever Admins: LTI SSO for LMS Connect
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. LTI flow for teachers and students 3. Setting up LTI SSO for Canvas 4. Setting up LTI SSO for Schoology 

 

 Overview

 If you’re utilizing Clever LMS Connect, Learning Tools Interoperability Single sign-on (LTI SSO) enables your students and teachers to SSO into applications directly from your learning management system (LMS). 

 If you’re using Schoology as your LMS, Clever requires you use LTI 1.3 SSO to ensure the best LMS Connect experience.

 If you’re using Canvas as your LMS, Clever strongly suggests you use LTI 1.3 SSO to ensure the best LMS Connect experience. 

 If you’re using Google Classroom as your LMS, this feature is not available.Google Classroom does not use the LTI standard, but your users can still use Clever SSO with Clever LMS Connect for a seamless login experience.

 

 
 LTI flow for teachers and students

 When you set up LTI SSO for an LMS Connect app, teachers can choose to add the app for any of their courses/classes in the LMS. 

 Then, students and teachers can log into that app with one click from the LMS, without needing to go to Clever to log in. This feature is in addition to assignment and gradebook sync from LMS Connect apps.

 You must set up LTI SSO for each LMS Connect app, and the app must support SSO functionality. 

 For any apps that you don’t set up for LTI SSO, gradebook and assignment syncs will still work as usual. To access these apps, users will need to use another login method, such as Clever SSO from the portal.

 

 See a demo of LTI in this video:

 

 
 Setting up LTI SSO for Canvas

 Setting up LTI SSO for Canvas is strongly recommended to ensure the best experience with LMS Connect.

 • 
 Log in to Canvas. Select your school from the Admin menu.

• 
 Click Developer Keys. 

 
 

 • 
 Click + Developer Key > + LTI Key .

 
 

 • 
 Fill in the following fields:

 • 
 Key Name: You can name this app whatever you’d like: this is internal and won’t be seen by teachers or students.

• 
 Method: Choose enter URL .

• 
 JSON URL : Paste in JSON URL from Clever Dashboard > LMS Connect > Launch from LMS > Click Set up to open modal > Copy over the URL. 

• 
 Owner Email: You can leave this blank, or optionally put your email if you want other admin to know who added the app.

• 
 Redirect URLs: Leave this field blank

 
• Notes : Add any internal notes you’d like for internal purposes. 
• 
 Click Save . 

 
 

• 
 On the Developer Keys page, copy the Client ID for the LTI Key you just created. You’ll need this for the next step.

 
 

 • 
 On the Canvas left sidebar, go to Settings > Apps > View App Configurations .

• 
 Click + App .

 
 

 • 
 Paste in the Client ID you copied in step 5.

 
 

 • 
 Click save and install the app when prompted.

 
 

 

 Repeat these steps for any other app(s) you want to enable for LTI SSO.

 

 To ensure this works correctly, you can use Canvas’ Act as User functionality to test things out. 

 
 Setting up LTI SSO for Schoology

 Setting up LTI SSO for Schoology is required by Clever to ensure the best LMS Connect experience. Failing to set up LTI SSO will prevent gradebook and assignment syncs from functioning.

 There are two steps to add an LTI SSO app to Schoology:

 • Adding the app
• Installing the app
 
 

 Adding the app to Schoology 

 • 
 In Clever, head to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > LMS Connect page. For the app you want to set up LTI SSO for, go to the Launch from LMS column and click Set up . 

 • 
 Keep the set up modal open in a tab, you’ll need the fields listed here in following steps. 

 

 
 

 • Head to Schoology and log into your account.
• Click the waffle icon > App Center. 
• Click on My Developer Apps near the top of the screen. Don't see My Developer Apps? Check out this article to fix that. 
• Click Add App . 
 
 

 • Complete each section:
 • 
 App name : You can name the app anything (but we recommend a clear name since this is the name teachers and students will see). 

• 
 Description : Write in a description of the app.

• 
 Category : Select the subject.

• 
 Recommended for : Select whichever applies, Instructors, Students, Admins (or all three).

• 
 Available for : Select “Only people in my school” 

• 
 Leave the remaining boxes unchecked/unfilled. You can upload an App Logo and Feature Graphic - these images will display in Schoology.

• 
 Select LTI App 1.3, and more fields will appear, as shown below.

 

 
 

 • 
 In the additional fields that show up after selecting LTI App 1.3, follow these instructions:

 • 
 Next to LTI App 1.3, leave the Launch app in Schoology checkbox unchecked , unless the app you’re adding has told you to check it.

• 
 Can be Installed for : Select the categories that apply.

• 
 Configuration Type : Manual.

• 
 Privacy : Send Name and Email/Username of user who launches the tool

• 
 For Domain/URL, Custom Parameters, OIDC Login Init URL, and Redirect URLs: You’ll copy and paste these from the setup modal that you opened in Clever in step 1.

• If you are asked for or encounter a requirement in Schoology requesting either a Tool Public Key or a JWKS URL, copy the same URL used for the OIDC Login Init URL, and replace everything after /lti/ with jwks . If this doesn't resolve the issue, please contact Clever Support to troubleshoot further.
• All other boxes can remain unchecked. Check the Terms of Use box at the bottom of the page.
 

 
 

 • Click Submit . Your screen should now say, Your App has been created . 
• Next, click Install LTI App , then I Agree , and Add to Organization . 
 
 

 Installing the app 

 • 
 After you’ve added the app, click Install LTI 1.3 App , to install it. 

 
 

 • 
 Agree to the prompt and select Add to Organization .

• Click Install/Remove .
 
 

 • Select which group(s) applies: All Users , All Courses , or All Groups . 
• Click Submit . 
 
 

 Testing the app 

 Students, teachers, and staff (if shared with the LMS) can now access the app when they log in to Schoology.

 To test things out, go to your App Center > Organization Apps . Find the learning app you installed and click on it. Check that it’s working as expected.

title: For Clever Admins: Paid Services
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Consulting - Sync or SIS ID Conversions 3. Professional Development 4. Premium Support 5. FAQs 

 Overview

 Clever offers Consulting and Premium Support for districts seeking a higher level of support. As a partner in helping you simplify digital learning, we provide VIP dedicated support and expert coaching customized to your needs.

 Please note that we offer incredible free support to ensure that you get all of your questions answered and all the help you need. If you are interested in free support feel free to open a ticket with our dedicated support team. 

 
 Consulting

 Clever Consulting is a perfect solution for any school district looking to gain invaluable uninterrupted working time with a dedicated Clever Specialist. Customized to suit the unique needs of your district, our specialists collaborate with you to optimize your Clever instance, ensuring seamless operation. With our tailored support, you can confidently focus on what truly matters: student learning, while we handle the intricacies of your digital infrastructure.

 Clever Consulting includes:

 • 
 30-minute consultation planning call 

• 
 90- minute dedicated Zoom (90 minutes)

• 
 Customized to meet your district’s needs 

• 
 Immediate support for technical issues

 
 

 Not sure if Clever Consulting is the right solution for your school district? The possibilities are endless and can be customized to fit any Clever topic you would like to dig into. Here are some of the ways that other school districts have used Clever Consulting: 

 • 
 Train newly added Clever Admins

• 
 SIS ID/Sync Conversions

• 
 Sharing Rules support for applications

• 
 Best Practices 

• 
 Rollover Support

• 
 Clever Badge Implementation

 
 

 

 

 Sync or SIS ID Conversions

 Clever Consulting offers personalized assistance to school districts navigating SIS or Sync Conversions, providing dedicated support at every step. Your district will have access to a dedicated specialist, ensuring a smooth transition process from start to finish. 

 What’s Included:

 • 
 Fast-tracked timeline: conversions complete within 2 weeks 

• 
 Multiple virtual meetings: One 30-minute kickoff call followed by three 15-minute check-ins 

• 
 Ongoing email support 

• 
 Conversion checklist 

• 
 One dedicated agent who is familiar with your district and needs 

• 
 Clever notifies your applications Please note that if we can not get in touch with an application, we can still move forward with the conversion 

 
 

 Interested in learning more about Clever Consulting?

 Please use this link to learn how we can set your school district up for success through Clever Consulting. We can work together to customize your Consulting session to best meet your district’s needs!

 

 
 Professional Development

 Schedule a customized virtual teacher or school tech lead training that is tailored to your school's needs. Trainings are:

 

 Created by and for educators: Our trainings are developed by former teachers. Trainings are designed with best practices straight from the field to support every learner's development. Tap the technical experts: Learn from former Clever support specialists that have worked to solve technical issues for thousands of districts, teachers, and tech coaches. Certified professional development: Trainees receive certificate of completion, which your district can count towards professional development time.

 Trainings include:

 • Live, interactive virtual training (90 minutes)
• Customized to meet your district’s needs 
• Live Q&A and Troubleshooting support 
• Follow-up emails with additional tips 
• Access to Clever Resource Hub 
• Certificates of completion 
• Access to online recording (30 days) 
 
 How to learn more about Clever-led Training

 Please use this link to learn how we can set your school district up for success through Clever Professional Development. We can work together to customize your training sessions to best meet your district’s needs!

 

 
 Premium Support

 Elevate your district's support experience with Clever's Premium Support, tailored for school districts seeking a higher level of assistance. With Premium Support, districts will receive dedicated support, personalized attention, and streamlined response times that go beyond the exceptional services offered through free Clever Support to ensure your district's success.

 Premium Support includes:

 • 
 3 Business hour first reply response time for Clever Admins

• 
 24/7 Emergency support for outages and security issues

• 
 Email support for Clever Admins

• 
 Case Management Hub to view all open cases

• 
 Priority phone support for Clever Admins

• 
 Dedicated support team

 
 

 

 Interested in Premium Support?

 If you are ready to purchase, you can navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Training & Support > Clever Premium Support button and click Get started .
