 Feel free to contact integrations@clever.com for further information regarding Premium Services.

 

 Renewals

 Renewing your Premium Support subscription is easy! Before the end of your current subscription, you’ll receive notifications via email and on your Billing page to renew.

 

 You can click the Renew subscription button to begin renewing. The renewal process is identical to the purchasing process. You’ll see a quote to renew your Premium Support subscription. You can select a subscription length of one, two, or three years. Then, you’ll choose your desired payment method.

 

 Once you complete your renewal purchase, your subscription will be extended 1 to 3 years, depending on the subscription length purchased. 

 If you would like to adjust your Premium Support subscription, please contact integrations@clever.com to speak with our district partnerships team. 

 
 FAQs

 How much is Clever Consulting? 

 • 
 $1,200/per session

 
 

 How much is Premium Support? 

 • 
 $4,000 per year for districts up to 30 schools. 

• 
 For larger districts, we charge an additional $100/school/year for schools beyond the 30 included in the base price.

• 
 Multi-year discounts are available.

 
 How much is Clever Training? 

 • Pricing is as follows:
 • 1 facilitator, $500.00/session. 0-20 participants.
• 2 facilitators, $1,250.00/session. 21-60 participants.
• 3 facilitators, up to $2,000.00/session with volume discounts. 60-150 participants.

title: Single Sign-on (SSO): Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever as an SSO Profile in Google
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup • Configuring SSO • Configuring OU targeting • Testing on the Web • Google settings for users and devices • Testing on a Chromebook 3. Known issues 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever works seamlessly with Chromebooks, providing students and teachers with access to all their learning applications while only logging in once per device. This means students are logged into their Chromebook, into Clever, and into any applications that the student has access to.

 Google supports configuring other identity providers (IdPs) within the Google Admin Console. This article covers configuring Clever as an individual SSO Profile in Google.

 

 

 Advantages of the SSO Profile configuration:

 • 
 Students can use their Clever Badge for all Google sign-ins, not just school organization-managed Chromebooks.

• 
 The school organization can set up different SSO providers for various user groups (e.g., Clever for student Badge users, Entra ID for staff).

• 
 The SSO Profile configuration can target specific devices, so the Badge prompt only shows by default on the desired devices.

 
 

 
 Setup

 The setup process has several parts, so please set aside roughly two hours to work through it. Please follow the instructions listed carefully to ensure proper configuration, and test it after setup!

 Part 1: Configuring SSO

 • 
 In your Clever Dashboard, go to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges > Chromebook tab . If you haven't already, add the Chromebook Login for SSO Profiles application to your Clever account by clicking the Add application button. The button will only show if you haven't added the application already. 

 • 
 Adding the application automatically shares it with all users in your schools. Clever recommends sharing the app with all users, even if they aren’t going to use Badges into Chromebooks. This way, if you ever expand Badges into Chromebook settings, you won’t need to adjust sharing. The app won’t be visible in the teacher and student Clever Portals.

• 
 Users won’t be able to log into Chromebooks with this app until you’ve configured the settings in Google Admin Console. You’ll configure these settings later in this article.

 

• 
 Log into Google Admin Console and go to the SSO with third party IdP settings page.

• 
 Under the Third-party SSO profiles section, click Add SAML Profile . 

• Copy the following information from the Authentication > Badges > Chromebook tab in your Clever Dashboard to the Google Admin Console SAML profile form: • SSO profile name: “Clever - Badges” or any name of your choice. This won't be seen by end-users
• IdP entity ID : paste the IdP Entity ID 
• Sign-in page URL: paste the Sign-in page URL 
 • If you want all users to log in to Chromebooks exclusively using Badges, you can add ?specify_auth=badges to the end of this value. This will make the Clever Badge camera the first screen the user sees upon opening the Chromebook.
 

• Sign-out page URL : leave blank
• Change password URL : leave blank, because Badges don’t use a password
• Certificate: Download the verification certificate from your Clever Dashboard, then click Upload Certificate to upload it. The certificate file downloaded from Clever will be a .crt file. Google’s UI says that the file must be PEM or DER, but .crt will be accepted 
• Click Save 
 

• On the next page, note the Entity ID and ACS URL listed under SP details . You will need these in the next step. 
• In the Configure SSO in Clever section (on the Authentication > Badges > Chromebook tab ), paste the values from Google Admin Console:
 • Entity ID: paste the “Entity ID” noted in step six from Google’s SP details
• ACS URL: paste the “ACS URL” noted in step six from Google’s SP details Please note: Make sure the Entity ID and ACS URL are entered in the correct fields. They look very similar, and it’s easy to mix them up! The ACS URL should include “/acs”. 

 

• 
 Optional If you utilize Clever Passwords and your Clever Passwords username matches your Google username, you have the option to configure settings to pre-populate usernames for your users on the Clever login page. To do so, check the box next to the Send email address in the URL as the login hint parameter setting.

 

 
 

 
 

 
 Part 2: Configuring OU targeting

 Setting up OU targeting lets you determine who should be using Badges into Chromebooks. For example, first and second grade students or those who have IEPs. 

 First, ensure that you have one or more Organizational Units or Groups in Google containing the users and devices that should have access to the Badges into Chromebooks feature.

 • Return to the SSO with third-party IdP settings page in the Google Admin Console and scroll down to the Manage SSO profile assignments section. 
• Click Get started if you’ve never set up an SSO assignment or Manage if you have. 
 
 

 If you’ve never set up an SSO assignment, click on GET STARTED. 

 

 

 If you already have SSO assignments set up, click on MANAGE. 

 

 

 • In the sidebar, find the Organizational Unit, Group, or individual users for whom you want to use this feature. Then, in the main window, select Another SSO profile, and in the dropdown select the Clever profile created in Part 1. We recommended naming it “Clever - Badges.” This won't be seen by end users.
 
 

 In this screenshot, we’ve selected the Students OU. You might select an OU based on user, grade, etc. based on how you’ve structured your Organizational Units. 

 • 
 In the Select what happens if users go directly to a Google service without first signing in with this SSO profile's third-party IDP section , you may select either option depending on the user experience that works best for your school organization: 

 
 

 • 
 • Have Google prompt for their username, then redirect them to this profile's IDP sign-in page: Select this option if users should always use Clever to sign in to Google, including sign-ins on browsers and unmanaged devices (Recommended for most setups).
• Require users to enter their Google username and password to sign in: Select this if users should only use Clever to sign in to Google when initially logging in to a managed Chromebook device or if users should have the option to log into managed devices with either Badges or Google credentials. Users will have to use their Google username and password to sign in to Google on browsers, non-Chromebook devices, and unmanaged Chromebooks. Choose this option if you're having issues with logins on some devices. If you have questions, please contact our support team .
 

 
 • 
 Repeat for any other user or device OUs or groups that should use the Badges into Chromebooks experience. For the best user experience, you should target both users AND their devices. Targeting the device as well as the user will cause Clever Badges to be the default login method. Otherwise, users must first type in their Google email address for Google to recognize that they are in a targeted OU.

 
 

 
 Part 3: Testing on the Web (optional)

 If you selected the Have Google prompt for their username, then redirect them to this profile's IDP sign-in page setting in step four of the Configure OU targeting section , you can test on the Web without a Chromebook device. 

 If you selected Require users to enter their Google username and password to sign in , skip this part and proceed to Part 4: Google Settings for users and devices .

 To test on the web you'll need:

 • 
 A Google user in one of the targeted OUs

• 
 A Clever account whose email matches the Google user

• 
 A Badge for this Clever account

 
 Here's what to do:

 • In an Incognito or Private browser window, go to accounts.google.com and enter the email address for the Google user account. You should be redirected to Clever to sign-in.
• Follow the Clever prompts to scan your Badge. You should be authenticated with Clever and then signed in to t

title: Sync or SIS Conversion: Notifying applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Why is app notification important? 3. The district’s role 4. Clever's role 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 If you’re switching sync types or student information systems (SISs) and/or changing sis_ids, notifying applications is one of the most critical parts in ensuring that historical data is preserved for students and teachers in the application’s platform. In fact, this piece takes up the bulk of the timeframe during a sync or SIS conversion as we work with your applications to ensure a smooth transition! 

 
 Why is app notification important?

 Our system generates unique Clever IDs for each record based on the sis_id that we receive from the district. If student_ids, teacher_ids, staff_ids, or school_ids change in Clever, the Clever ID will also change. 

 Changing user records in this way can cause issues in Clever and connected applications because many of our application partners use the Clever ID as the key identifier in their platform to manage records and preserve historical data. As such, we must notify the application support teams and give them a heads-up! This notification allows the applications to make any necessary changes needed to ingest the new data. 

 
 The district's role

 Before starting a sync or SIS conversion in Clever, we recommend that districts proactively reach out to all SSO, Rostering applications that they’re connected with in Clever to make them aware of the upcoming changes to the data. This helps to expedite the conversion process and ensures that everyone is on the same page. Here are a few things to keep in mind when reaching out to the applications: 

 • The tentative date you plan to start the sync or SIS conversion in Clever.
• Which SIS data fields will change (eg. student_id, teacher_number, email_address, etc)?
• Confirm what key identifier the application uses to manage records and preserve historical data (eg. Clever ID, state_id, teacher_id, email_address, etc).
• Does the application have to match the data on their end prior to the sync conversion? Or will they need to ingest the new data first and then match it? 
• Will the application need anything from the district or Clever, such as a crosswalk file of the data changes?
• Advise the application that you'll pause the app sync in the Clever Dashboard until they confirm that everything is good to go within the application. Request that the application notify you directly once it’s okay to resume the app sync from Clever. 
 
 

 

 
 Clever’s role

 The Clever team will contact some applications but not all! Our main goal is to contact SSO & rostering applications that use Clever IDs or SIS IDs as key identifiers. 

 However, there are some applications that use other SIS fields (eg. student_number, email_address, etc.) to preserve data that would also need to be notified, and this is where the district comes in. The district would ultimately be responsible for informing all SSO & rostering applications of the pending changes in Clever. 

 
 FAQs

 Is there a list of applications that should be notified? 

 • Unfortunately, we don't have a comprehensive list! As such, we recommend notifying all SSO & rostering applications that you’re currently connected with in Clever.
 
 When should I start reaching out to the applications? 

 • The sooner, the better! We recommend reaching out at least a week in advance of starting the sync conversion process with Clever. 
 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Pause your sync for a specific app 
• Secure Sync: Sync or SIS ID conversions

title: For Clever Admins: Tasks
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What are tasks? 3. Using tasks 4. Dismissing tasks 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 Over the school year, there are a variety of actions for you to take as a Clever Admin to ensure Clever works smoothly for you and your district. 

 Tasks help you keep track of everything: from prepping for summer school, to addressing data errors and everything in between. 

 
 What are tasks?

 Tasks highlight opportunities, issues, and errors that need attention, connecting you to resources you need and surfacing new features. With tasks, you have one more layer of support to ensure you’re getting the most out of Clever.

 

 

 In addition to tasks created by Clever, District Curriculum Leads can create a task to remind Clever Admin to accept any pending application invitations. 

 
 Using tasks

 Think of tasks as your Clever to-do list. They're there to remind you of important actions and other information.

 For example, if there's an issue with your sync, you might see a task suggesting you take action to fix the issue. 

 Or, if a new feature has launched that might make your life easier, you might see a task encouraging you to check it out. 

 Consider logging in to Clever regularly to check for any new tasks to keep your Clever account in tip-top shape. 

 

 
 Dismissing tasks

 If there’s a task that’s not relevant to you, you can dismiss it by clicking the X to the right of the task. 

 
 FAQs

 Who else in my district sees tasks? 

 • All users with the role of Clever Admin see tasks. District Curriculum Lead and District Help Desk users don't see tasks.
 
 Does a task go away for everyone if I dismiss it? 

 • No, it only goes away for you!

title: Clever + i-Ready: Setup guide & rollover process
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Recommended sharing rules 4. Required fields 5. School year rollover 6. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 This article contains information to help your school district set up with Clever and i-Ready. You can also review the i-Ready rollover process to help you prepare for Back to School season. 

 
 Setup process

 • 
 Make sure your school district is set up with Clever! 

 • 
 If your school district hasn’t been set up with Clever, you can get started here .

 

• 
 If your school district is already using Clever, you can add i-Ready by following these instructions:

 
 • 
 • 
 Log into your Clever Dashboard and navigate to Applications > Add Applications .

• 
 Search for i-Ready and click on it when it appears in the list.

• 
 You’ll then be brought to a page where you can request the application. When requesting i-Ready, select whether or not you have made a purchase.

• 
 You can then opt to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.

• 
 On the right side of the page, you can view the fields required by i-Ready.

• 
 When you have made your selections, click Send integration request.

 

 
 Once requested, please reach out to i-Ready’s Roster Support team at rosters-support@cainc.com or your i-Ready Partner Success Manager to let them know you are ready to sync via Clever. Data will not automatically sync to i-Ready as backend setup work is needed to provide your district access. Their team will help you get started! 

 
 Recommended sharing rules

 i-Ready recommends that you share data by school. The simplest way is to set up your sharing using Share by Rules in Clever. You can create a sharing rule by following these steps:

 • 
 Set up your sharing rules:

 • 
 If you are setting up sharing rules for i-Ready for the first time, please follow these instructions: Sharing Permissions: Sharing data with new applications .

• 
 If you need to edit existing rules, please follow these instructions: Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules .

 

• 
 Configure your sharing rules by limiting sharing to only the schools and students that should be synced.

 
 • 
 • 
 Once i-Ready receives your data, they will have an additional filtering process. 

 

 
 Once you've shared data with i-Ready in Clever, you'll need to work with i-Ready to finish your sync. i-Ready’s rostering team will guide you through this process.

 IMPORTANT Once you have spoken with i-Ready’s rostering team and your data is received by the i-Ready team, setup may take 5-10 business days, depending on the time of year. 

 

 
 Required fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. 

 

 

 

 i-Ready requires the following data fields:

 Student Fields: 

 • 
 first and last name

• 
 district

• 
 school

• 
 sis_id and student_number

• 
 State ID

• 
 DOB

• 
 Grade (0-12)

• 
 Class enrollments

 • 
 In order to log in to i-Ready, students need to be enrolled in a class that is shared with i-Ready. 

 

 
 Staff fields: 

 • 
 First and last name

• 
 district

• 
 school(s)

• 
 sis_id

• 
 Email

• 
 Class enrollments (for teacher role)

• 
 State ID

 
 Section fields: 

 • 
 School ID

• 
 Section ID

• 
 Name

 
 

 i-Ready can sync site/school district-level administrators from Clever to i-Ready. If your district selects this option, the following Clever Staff roles translate to the following i-Ready roles: 

 

 
 Clever Role

 
 i-Ready Role

 
 Clever Admin i-Ready District Admin 
 Clever Help Desk i-Ready District Admin 
 Clever District Curriculum Lead i-Ready District Admin 
 Clever School Tech Lead i-Ready School Admin 
 Clever Portal Access (Staff role) i-Ready School Admin 
 
 

 Unsure of what role a user should be in Clever? Check out this article for more information: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions .

 

 
 School year rollover

 Summer usage, i-Ready account rollover processes & preparation

 In addition to Clever’s Rollover processes, which are linked here , i-Ready also has its own account rollover process. 

 • 
 At the end of the district’s school year, i-Ready pauses nightly syncs through the summer unless otherwise discussed with the school district. 

 • 
 If you would like to continue syncing for Summer School, you must contact the i-Ready team by emailing rosters-support@cainc.com .

 

• 
 After the last day of school, i-Ready will be accessible to users (as long as they are rostered in Clever) until the i-Ready Account Rollover, which typically occurs about two weeks before the start of the next school year. 

 • 
 Don’t you know when your i-Ready Account Rollover will occur? Ask your district administrator, or if you are a district-level i-Ready administrator, follow these steps . 

 

• 
 In preparation for the new school year, please follow Clever’s rollover processes to release your new academic year data.

 • 
 As a reminder, the sync hold needs to be released in Clever before i-Ready can receive your new year data. 

 

 
 After the i-Ready account rollover occurs, i-Ready is not accessible until the new school year data is shared with i-Ready and has been processed. Once data is shared with the i-Ready team, it can take up to five business days to get your account up and running for the next school year. 

 
 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data error”? 

 • 
 A data error occurs when the record is incompatible with i-Ready’s integration with Clever, resulting in the user or section not appearing in the application. Data errors must be resolved for users or sections in order to be successfully processed by the application. Reach out to Clever Support if you have questions about how to resolve your data error.

• 
 A data warning occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular data warning.

 • 
 Please note that section name data warnings can be ignored for i-Ready.

 

 
 

 Why are student usernames inaccurate in i-Ready (e.g., an ‘AUTO_’ username)? 

 • 
 My district’s students log in through a single sign-on (SSO) portal.

 • 
 For school districts utilizing SSO, the “username” column in i-Ready has no functionality and can be ignored.  Teachers and Students should log in through their district portal by clicking the appropriate i-Ready tile or icon. 

 

• 
 My school district’s students log in directly through https://login.i-ready.com/ .

 • 
 Suppose your district logs in directly through i-Ready, and a student needs their username corrected. In that case, a school or district administrator can update the username field by following these linked steps in i-Ready Central. 

 

 
 

 How can my district sync demographics from Clever to i-Ready ? 

 To see demographic reporting in the i-Ready Dashboard, you’ll need to sync demographic data via Clever by following these steps: 

 • 
 You’ll need to sync demographic data to Clever from your SIS or CSV file (depending on your sync type).

• 
 Once your data is in Clever, you’ll need to make sure it’s shared with i-Ready. In your Clever Dashboard, navigate to the Applications > My applications > i-Ready > Settings tab > scroll to the Field Access box.

• 
 Click on Advanced Settings. 

• 
 Choose Customize fields shared with i-Ready (see a how-to here for instructions and screenshots) and click Customize fields .

• 
 Here, you can edit which fields are shared with the app.

 • 
 Sensitive Student Information fields in Clever require an additional sharing step with i-Ready. Please see step-by-step instructions here .

 

 
 
 Type of information

 
 Clever field

 
 i-Ready demographic field

 
 Personal information gender gender 
 race ethnicityId 
 hispanic_ethnicity hispanicOrLatino

title: Clever + VVAAS Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Extension Fields 3. SAS/VVAAS Roles for Single Sign-On 4. Standard Fields 5. SAML Setup in your Clever Dashboard 

 

 Overview

 Virginia’s Visualization and Analytics Solution (VVAAS) is a web-based tool available to all Virginia K-12 divisions. The VVAAS portal displays measures of students’ academic growth over time based on SOL testing data, diagnostic reports for student groups, and student projections to help educators improve student achievement and inform classroom instruction. Reports provided by VVAAS, in tandem with other data sources, can help educators make decisions regarding curriculum changes, identify and support long-term student goals, and help reveal trends of student groups needing additional support.

 This article covers the extension fields you will need to add to Clever for the VVAAS application (including user roles), the required standard fields, and the SAML setup steps to take within your Clever Dashboard.

 Please note, all users that need VVAAS access need accounts created (synced or manual) prior to the file upload. 

 All Clever Admins who need access to VVAAS will need a second Clever account. The account can be a "custom staff" or a "custom teacher". The Clever Admin will need to login from Clever.com and select from the drop-down Login as Teacher/Staff to access VVAAS in the Clever portal.

 

 
 Extension Fields

 Please add the following extension fields to your Clever rostering sync for each teacher and staff user who should have access to the VVAAS application. To learn how to add extension fields to your Clever sync, see: For Clever Admins: Syncing custom (i.e. extension) fields to Clever .

 

 
 Clever Extension Field 

 
 SAML Attribute Name 

 
 Description 

 
 ext.primary_school_code PrimarySchoolCode Four-digit state-assigned School number that identifies the school, center, program, or placement that employs the account holder. 
 ext.additional_school_codes AdditionalSchoolCodes One or more additional school codes separated by the “|” character.
 For example, 160|3245|23

 
 ext.role Role Code used to identify the account holder's role in the school division and determine what level of access is needed. Select From: VVAAS_DIVISION_ALL VVAAS_DIVISION_NS VVAAS_SCHOOL_ALL VVAAS_SCHOOL_NS VVAAS_SCHOOL_SPLIT VVAAS_SCHOOL_WRS VVAAS_TEACHER_WRS See more about the roles below . 
 
 

 

 
 SAS/VVAAS Roles for Single Sign-On

 

 
 Role Name 

 
 Required Attributes 

 
 Role Privileges 

 
 Example of User Type 

 
 VVAAS_DIVISION_ALL 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName Users will have access to ALL reports and data associated with the division, schools, and students within the division. Position examples: • Superintendent • Central Office staff (instructional, student services, special education, and/or data) • Multi-school staff (instructional coaches, administrators 
 VVAAS_DIVISION_NS 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName Users will have access to all reports and data associated with the division and schools within the division but NO access to student data or reports.
 

 Position examples: • Central Office staff who should not have access to student-level data • Multi-school staff who should not have access to student-level data 
 VVAAS_SCHOOL_ALL 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName 4. PrimarySchoolCode (exactly 1) 5. AdditionalSchoolCodes (0 or more) Users have access to ALL associated school reports and student data at those schools. Note: Optional to provide additional schools for this user. Position examples: • Administration • Counselors • Instructional Coaches • Special education 
 VVAAS_SCHOOL_NS 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName 4. PrimarySchoolCode (exactly 1) 5. AdditionalSchoolCodes (0 or more) Users have access to school reports, but NO student reports or data. Note: Optional to provide additional schools for this user. Position examples: • Non-core classroom teacher 
 VVAAS_SCHOOL_SPLIT 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName 4. PrimarySchoolCode (exactly 1) (with student access) 5. AdditionalSchoolCodes (0 or more) (no student access) Users have access to School Reports and Student Reports for the Primary School. They have access to only School Reports for Additional Schools (No Student Report access for additional schools). Position examples: • Instructional coaches that support the division but are assigned to one school • School Administrators 
 VVAAS_SCHOOL_WRS 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName 4. PrimarySchoolCode (exactly 1) 5. AdditionalSchoolCodes (0 or more) 6. LocalProviderID Users have access to primary school reports and rostered student data through pre-populated Custom Student Reports (CSR). They have access to school reports only at additional schools. Note: DivisionCode, PrimarySchoolCode, and LocalProviderID must match the MSC file to receive access to a roster. Position examples: • Classroom teacher 
 VVAAS_TEACHER_WRS 1. Email 2. FirstName 3. LastName 4. PrimarySchoolCode (exactly 1) 5. LocalProviderID Users only have access to rostered student data through pre-populated Custom Student Reports (CSR). They have NO access to school reports. Note: DivisionCode, PrimarySchoolCode, and LocalProviderID must match the MSC file to receive access to a roster. Position examples: • Classroom teacher 
 
 

 
 Standard Fields

 These are the standard Clever fields you will need to make sure to include in your sync.

 

 
 Clever Field 

 
 SAML Assertion 

 
 Notes 

 
 First Name FirstName First name of the VVAAS account holder. 
 Last Name LastName Last name of the VVAAS account holder.
 

 
 Email Email This is the account holder's work email address. This address is used for sending emails generated by the VVAAS system and account management. 
 SIS ID for teachers Staff ID for staff LocalProviderID Locally defined identification number must be unique within the division (e.g., employee ID, teacher ID, tax ID). This Local Provider ID must match what was submitted in the Master Schedule Collection. Please note, the Teacher ID in Clever represents SIS ID on the Teacher file. 
 
 

 
 SAML Setup in your Clever Dashboard

 
 • 
 Click on the VVAAS application in your dashboard, then go to the Settings tab and scroll to the SAML Details box.

• 
 Complete this Clever VVAAS SSO Implementation Form .

• 
 Next, fill out the attributes for 

 • 
 Staff and Teachers. Please leave District Admins blank. 

 • 
 Please note, the roles “Staff” and “Teacher” refer to their role type in Clever, not necessarily their actual job in the division. If you are a PowerSchool district, all synced users will come in as teachers.

• 
 Overview of Attributes:

 • 
 In Clever (green circle) select from dropdown list.

• 
 Local Provider ID may be in Clever - verify the value matches MSC file Local Provider ID for teachers . This value links teachers and students in VVAAS.

• 
 Custom Attribute must be added to Clever (file or sync) and then selected in dropdown list (red circle) .

 

 

 

 
 

 • 
 • All attributes must be updated in SIS sync or file upload even if you don't intend to use the attribute. You need to have the attributes added to Clever first. If you need help with this reach out to Clever support. 
 • 
 Role field must be completed 

• 
 Primary school code must be completed for those at the school building level , leave blank for Division users . (School number provided matches school codes in SRC/MSC file for state/federal reporting)

• 
 Additional school code can be blank if you are not using this option in your division 

 

 

 
 

 Sample Staff file example: 

 
 staff_id 

 
 ext.role 

 
 ext.primary_school_code 

 
 ext.additional_school_codes 

 
 ##### VVAAS_Division_All 
 ##### VVAAS_Division_NS 
 
 

 

 

 Sample Teacher file example: 

 
 sis_id 

 
 ext.role 

 
 ext.primary_school_code 

 
 ext.additional_school_codes 

 
 #### VVAAS_School_All 111 112 
 ###### VVAAS_School_WRS 222 
 
 

 

 

 Note: 

 • 
 • 
 The first column needs

title: Clever + Verkada Guest Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Connect with Verkada via Clever 3. Initiating the Clever sync in Verkada Command 

 

 Overview

 Verkada supports rostering via Clever. District administrators can manage and update user and roster information in their SIS that syncs to Clever. Clever data syncs nightly with Verkada Guest to support the use of Student Guest types, including:

 • 
 Late Arrival (tracking students who arrive to school late)

• 
 Student Pick Up (tracking when a student is picked up by their parent/guardian)

• Self sign-out (tracking when a student leaves school early) 
 
 
 Connect with Verkada via Clever

 Districts that are new to Clever can get started by signing up here. Clever is a free service for schools and districts! Existing Clever districts can request Verkada Guest from the Clever Dashboard. Log in to your administrator account and select Applications > Add Applications . Search Verkada Guest and click Send integration request :

 

 The Verkada team should reply to connection requests within 24 hours. 

 Verkada Guest requires only student and student contact data to be shared via Clever’s sharing settings . For security and privacy, please only share data that is required for your district’s needs.

 

 While in Clever, locate your unique district Clever ID from the Data browser. You’ll need to paste that ID in Verkada Command in the next step:

 

 

 

 
 Initiating the Clever sync in Verkada Command

 Follow these steps to complete the setup process:

 • 
 In Verkada Command, go to All Products > Guest . 

• 
 In the left panel, click the Guest Settings icon

• 
 Under Organization, select Schools to access the school settings.

• 
 Select the Sync students with Clever option

 
 

 

 

 • 
 When prompted, enter your Clever district ID number.

• 
 Assign a school from Clever to the corresponding school site in Verkada Guest. This links the school and the Guest site.

 
 

 If you need assistance completing the setup process, please refer to the Verkada Guest Overview or contact Verkada Support .

title: For Application Partners: Clever LMS Connect
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 3. Viewing interested districts 4. Learn More 

 

 Overview

 Clever offers a solution to application partners that allows you to integrate with district LMSs easily via the Clever API. With Clever LMS Connect, districts can launch your application directly from their LMS using SSO while saving teachers time with gradebook syncs, and students jump into learning with seamless assignment syncs. 

 

 Please note: As seen in the graphic above, LMS Connect does not sync grades between the LMS and SIS, and vice versa. 

 

 
 Features

 Clever LMS Connect connects your learning applications to district LMSs through the Clever API and is the easiest path to interoperability:

 • Fast-track LMS integrations without overhauling your roadmap. Build on your existing Clever integration and be ready to support Canvas, Schoology, and Google Classroom in as little as 3-4 weeks.
• Sell confidently, knowing you can meet every district LMS requirement. Meet district RFP requirements for LTI 1.3, gradebook, and assignment syncs.
• Onboard schools quickly without data-matching headaches Clever LMS Connect will seamlessly match class and user data between your application and LMSs, so you don’t have to.
 
 Application partners using Clever LMS Connect can also benefit from working with the Clever team to create a Google add-on for their application. Clever’s SSO powers the add-on and simplifies access to the application for students and teachers directly from Google Classroom.

 View the video below to see the LMS Connect teacher and student experience. Please note that this demo is subject to change and is meant for illustrative purposes only.

 

 

 Please note: LMS Connect automatically syncs assignments and grades from applications to the district's LMS. However, if the district has cross-listed sections in their LMS, this functionality isn't currently available. 

 

 

 
 Viewing interested districts

 Districts can express their interest in using Clever LMS Connect with your learning application via their district’s Clever Dashboard. These requests can help you to track how many districts are interested in utilizing Clever LMS Connect with your learning application.

 

 Within your Clever Dashboard, these requests will appear on your Home Page under the District Overview .

 

 

 

 See below to learn more about integrating with Clever LMS Connect.

 
 Learn more

 You’ll see a LMS Connect tab in your Clever Application Dashboard where you can schedule a call to get started:

 

 If you're interested in utilizing Clever LMS Connect, you can also: 

 • Sign up for a call to learn more and get a live demo of the experience in Google Classroom, Canvas, and Schoology.
• Review developer documentation .
• Learn more in Clever Academy.

title: Clever + Great Minds® Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Onboarding Steps 3. Required Data 4. Accessing Great Minds via Clever 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Great Minds supports Single Sign-On (SSO) and Rostering integrations with Clever.

 • Single Sign-On (SSO) : Users can access their Great Minds products anytime using their Clever login credentials.
• Rostering : District administrators can manage and update user and roster information in their SIS that syncs to Clever. Clever data syncs nightly with Great Minds. 
 
 
 Onboarding Steps

 • Your Great Minds Digital Implementation Specialist will send you an invitation to our Great Minds Application via Clever. If you don’t see an invitation, you can send a request to our application.
• When you accept the invitation, please share only schools and sections that will be utilizing Great Minds products. This can be done through Clever sharing rules .
• Once you’ve launched your district and shared your data with Great Minds, their Digital Implementation Support team will complete your onboarding.
• Your designated Digital Administrator will receive an email when your onboarding is complete. Note: Users do not get an email once they have been rostered in Great Minds. Please distribute this information to the users in your organization. 
 
 
 Required Data

 Great Minds requires the following data from Clever to roster users in our digital platforms:

 
 Required Field 

 
 Required Metadata 

 
 District Name 
 School Name 
 
 Student

 
 • First Name
• Last Name
• Grade
• District
• School(s)
• Student ID: Can contain alphanumeric characters and underscores only. Cannot contain spaces.
 
 
 
 Teacher, School Admin, District Admin 

 
 • First Name
• Last Name
• Teacher/Staff ID (see sis_id, below)
• Email: Can contain alphanumeric characters and the following supported characters only: + = , . @ and -. 
 
 
 User sis_ids sis_id maps to Student ID, Teacher ID, and Staff ID in Clever. sis_id's for users are required and must :
 • Be unique in your district for all user roles
• Never be changed 
• Never be reused for different users 
• Only contain alphanumeric characters and underscores (no spaces)
 
 If you need to change a user's sis_id or reuse a sis_id (not recommended), contact your Great Minds Digital Implementation Specialist to work with them to implement this change.

 
 Section district, school, name, grade, primary teacher, students
 Each class is assigned a unique Section_id in Clever. The section_id must remain the same throughout the school year to avoid deleting existing sections and creating new sections in Clever and Great Minds. 

 
 
 

 Please note: Data in Great Minds reflects the data synced from your SIS and shared via Clever. Historical data in Great Minds is retained through the persistence of local IDs (sis_ids) that generate globally unique Clever IDs. Changing local IDs (sis_ids) in your SIS will result in the deletion of existing records and the creation of new records in Clever and Great Minds. To ensure that historical data is maintained in Great Minds throughout a given school year, districts are encouraged to use stable/consistent IDs for all records (student, section, teacher) that require data retention in Great Minds. If you anticipate sis_ids changing throughout the school year, please contact Clever Support to discuss the change. 

 

 
 Accessing Great Minds via Clever

 If your District uses Clever access to the Great Minds platform, you may log in using one of the following two options: Option 1: Log in through the Clever website 

 • Choose your role.
• Find your District.
• Sign in using your Clever integration method (email or badge).
• Locate and launch the Great Minds application.
 
 Option 2: Log in through the Great Minds Digital website 

 • Navigate to digital.greatminds.org .
• Select the Clever button.
• Enter your School Name and select the correct school from the drop-down menu.
• Enter your login credentials or select an alternate login method (e.g., Google) to access your account. 
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Does Great Minds support multi-role users? 

 • Yes. Great Minds supports multiple roles for staff users only (not students). Either using your SIS import or Custom Data through Clever, a user needs two roles and will need to be rostered as two separate users with the following information:
 • Each entry must have different sis_ids.
• Each entry needs to be the same email.
• Each entry has different roles.
• Each role should link to the same Clever User ID.
 

 
 Can we access sync reports to help us correct any rostering/data errors? 

 • Yes. Clever sync reports can be accessed by administrators in the Manage application in their Great Minds account. See Clever Sync Reports on the Great Minds Help Center for more information.
 
 Can I add users that are not in my rostering data source (SIS)? 

 • Teachers and students must be in your rostering data source to appear in Great Minds. However, administrators can be created manually in Great Minds if necessary. Contact the Great Minds Support Team for more information. 
 
 How can support staff (e.g., coaches) access Great Minds? 

 • See Custom data: Non-teaching staff for more information on adding non-teaching staff in Clever.
 
 By default, custom staff are assigned a Portal Access Only role in Clever, which means they will only have access to the applications that have been shared with them on a district level.

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Sharing Permissions: Overview 
• Custom data: Non-teaching staff 
• For Staff: School Tech Lead vs. Portal Only Permissions

title: For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA setup
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Getting started • Step 1: Select users who will be required to use MFA • Step 2: Select MFA methods • Step 3: Configure MFA settings • Microsoft Edge device trust • Step 4: Set Policy name • Step 5: Preview and complete MFA configuration 3. Exploring the MFA Dashboard • Managing Classroom MFA Badges 4. User self-enrollment 

 Overview

 This article walks through the setup process for Classroom MFA.

 For more information on how Classroom MFA works, pricing, and FAQs, please refer to this Help Center article. If you are interested protecting Google through the Workspace application, please refer to this Setup Guide . 

 For more robust information, including teacher training resources and email templates to roll out Classroom MFA, we recommend going through our Clever Academy Classroom MFA Quick Start Guide .

 

 

 
 Getting started

 You can begin setting up Classroom MFA right in your Clever Dashboard. Click here to jump right in and start your first MFA policy setup. 

 

 In this guide, we'll mention end-users. End-users are people who will use MFA while accessing Clever, like students, teachers, or instructional staff.

 

 Step 1: Select users who will be required to use MFA: 

 • By Group 
 • Students
 • All students
• Students by schools, grades, and/or groups (IEP or ELL status)
 

• Teachers
 • All Teachers
• Teachers by schools
 

• All Staff
 

• Or by specific users 
 
 

 Classroom MFA can be set up with unique policies that you can combine subsets of users in. For your first policy, you may want to start with a small subset of test users rather than all teachers or staff. However, the decision is up to you. Learn about the differences between Staff and Teacher users here . District administrators also have some MFA options natively built into Clever, which you can read about here. 

 

 Step 2: Select MFA methods 

 You can choose multiple MFA methods for your end users to choose from. When an end user (e.g. teacher) sets up their MFA, they'll get a list of possible MFA methods to set up. In this step, you'll define what MFA methods will be displayed as possible methods to an end user. It’s a good practice to have at least one other authentication method available to users so they can have a backup method. This way, if they are not able to use one method for any reason (e.g. their cell phone is not charged) they can use a backup method to successfully log in. This saves time by providing an alternative to users.

 • Picture Logins 
 • End-user uses two pictures that they select to verify their identity.
• Pictures can vary for age group. Younger students can utilize cartoon images, and older students can use realistic pictures. During policy setup, you'll choose which images the policy will use.
• This method is only permitted for students.
• Recommended for K-6 students, as it is easy to use.
• Clever admins and STLs can view student MFA picture selections from the Actions menu on the student's profile. Find out more here . 
• This option does not require a second device. Clever tip: You can set up separate MFA policies for students in younger grades and older grades (ex: K-2 and 3-6) to customize which login pictures students can utilize. 

 

• Badges 
 • End-user scans a printed or downloaded QR code via a device camera as a second factor after they provide their password.
• Recommended for students in grades 7-12. It pairs well with a password as the first authentication method. Recommended for teachers as a non-phone alternative.
• This option doesn't require a second device.
• Clever admins can bulk print student and teacher MFA Badges via the MFA Dashboard. 
• Teachers can manage MFA Badges for their students right from their Teacher Portal. Find out more here . 
 

 
 Please note: If your students already use a Clever Badge to SSO into Clever , we advise against using the MFA Badge as well because it will result in needing two badges to log into Clever. This is less secure than a Badge + other authentication method. 

 • PIN 
 • End user sets their own PIN by entering a 6-digit PIN code that they select to verify their identity. For security reasons, students cannot use use their ID number, birthday, sequential numbers (e.g., 123456, 678901), or repetitive digits (e.g. 000000, 111111). Attempting to use an ID number, birthday, sequential or repetitive digits will surface an error during setup that states, “PIN is too weak.”
• This method is only permitted for students.
• Recommended for students in grades 4-8.
• This option doesn’t require a second device.
• Teachers can help manage MFA PINs for their students right from their Teacher Portal. Learn more here .
 

• Mobile app 
 • End-user uses an app that generates a verification code - a Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP). They'll use this code to verify their identity during MFA.
• Choose an app that you recommend your end user should use to generate verification codes:
 • Google Authenticator or
• Microsoft Microsoft Authenticator
 

• Authenticator apps are not dependent on wifi or internet networks to generate codes so these are great options for those with network concerns.
• We recommend using this method as it has a good combination of security strength and convenience. 
 

• App-based token 
 • End-user uses an app that generates a verification code - a Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP). They'll use this code to verify their identity during MFA.
• Choose an app that you recommend your end-user should use to generate verification codes:
 • Google Authenticator or
• Microsoft Authenticator
 

• We recommend using this method as it has a good combination of security strength and convenience. 
 

• Biometrics or security key (e.g., Apple Touch ID, Windows Hello, Yubikey) 
 • End user uses a physical hardware key that plugs into the user device or is included in the device for authentication.
• Please note that Clever does not provide or re-sell hardware tokens. These must be district-provided. 
 

• Text message 
 • End user provides a phone number and receives a verification code by text message (SMS). Text messages do not support International numbers.
 

• Voice Call 
 • End user provides a phone number and receives a verification code through an automatic voice message (call). Voice phone numbers support extensions. Voice phone numbers do not support international numbers.
 

 
 

 Step 3: Configure MFA settings 

 • Enforcement date (required)
 • End users will be prompted while logging into Clever to set up MFA 30 days before the enforcement date. They can skip the MFA setup until the enforcement date. After that, an end user will be required to set up MFA before being able to access Clever.
• When you select Skip enforcement date , it will make MFA optional for end users. They can set up MFA within their own profile settings but won’t be required to add it. 
• If you’re testing Classroom MFA, you’ll want to set the enforcement date within 30 days of when you want your test policy users to get set up, so it automatically triggers their enrollment.
 

• Re-authentication frequency (required) Please note: Chromebooks clear cookies every time a user logs out. Because of this, Classroom MFA will be required at every Chromebook login—even if your policy is set to Every 7 days or Every 30 days . This is a known limitation of Chromebooks. 

 • Select how often a user will be prompted to MFA while logging into Clever:
 • Every time - If the user is on a shared device and is signed out from Clever, they will be required to use Classroom MFA every time they log in to Clever. However, if they are using a 1:1 device, they will only need to authenticate once every 24 hours when this option is selected.
• Every 7 days - end user will use MFA every 7 days to log in to Clever.
• Every 30 days - end user will use MFA every 30 days to log in to Clever.
• Never - you can define secure networks (such as your school network) whe
