title: For Clever Admins: Neutral Third-Party Security Rubrics
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Recommended Third-Party Security Rubrics 3. Learn More 

 

 Overview

 Edtech vendors play a key role in supporting comprehensive school security. However, for K-12 administrators—especially those managing tech responsibilities alone—vetting vendors for data security can pose a challenge.

 To support administrators in this process, we rely on trusted experts. This article provides you with neutral third-party security rubrics to help you select vendors with a cybersecurity-focused mindset. 

 
 Recommended third-party security rubrics

 It is important for every school district to develop policies and priorities that best suit your district. We recommend using rubrics created by neutral third parties, such as the Consortium for School Networking (CoSN) and the Cybersecurity Infrastructure and Security Agency (CISA):

 • CoSN: K-12CVAT: K-12 Community Vendor Assessment Tool 
• CoSN: Cybersecurity toolkit on authentication management 
• CoSN: NIST Cybersecurity Framework Resources Alignment for K-12 
• CoSN + CISA: Online Cybersecurity Toolkit 
• CISA: Online Toolkit: Partnering to Safeguard K-12 Organizations from Cybersecurity Threats 
 
 Clever has a close partnership with CoSN, and we’ll continue to support the creation of neutral third-party rubrics to provide districts with unbiased evaluation sources. Discover key steps to select edtech vendors with a cybersecurity focus to help protect student data on Clever’s Blog: K-12 Administrator guide to evaluating edtech vendor security . 

 

 
 Learn more

 Clever is proud to join forces with the White House and the Cybersecurity Infrastructure and Security Agency (CIS) by signing the Secure by Design Pledge. We’re committed to supporting a secure, interoperable digital learning ecosystem and providing free and low-cost resources to school districts that strengthen cybersecurity. Learn more about our commitments here .

 You can also learn more about Clever’s comprehensive security program in our Trust Center .

title: For Clever Admins: Adding Clever Academy courses to your professional development LMS
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Technical requirements 3. Process to add Clever Academy courses to your LMS 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever Academy , our free training hub, has dozens of courses for teachers, School Tech Leads , and Clever Admin . Content covers everything from onboarding to advanced features.

 We’ve received feedback from schools that they’d like to host Clever Academy courses inside the learning management system (LMS) they use for teacher professional development. 

 Good news! You can host Clever Academy courses in your LMS, if it’s compatible. This article runs through the technical requirements and process to add Clever Academy content to your LMS. 

 
 Technical requirements

 Currently, Clever Academy courses can be shared with you as xAPI and CMI5 files, which you can add to your LMS. 

 Supported LMSes for the xAPI launch type are:

 • Cornerstone onDemand
• Litmos
• Absorb
• Sumtotal
• Saba
• Relias
• ThinkZoom
• Seismic Learning - Formerly Lessonly
• Docebo (In Beta)
• 360 Learning
• Thought Industries (In Beta)
• UKG
 
 

 Supported LMSes CMI5 launch type are:

 • Bridge
• TalentLMS (In Beta)
 
 

 Integration features When you add a Clever Academy course to your LMS, you have access to assignment engagement, such as which learners have completed the course. Specifics will vary based on the LMS you’re using.

 

 
 Process to add Clever Academy courses to your LMS

 Follow these steps to get Clever Academy course(s) added to your LMS:

 • Review the technical requirements section of this article and ensure you are using a supported LMS.
• Log in to Clever Academy and check out the course catalog .
• Find one or more courses you want your users to access in your LMS
 • NOTE: Ensure the content is relevant to the learner - for instance, if you’re looking to train your teachers, ensure the course is designated for teachers. 
 

• Email the Clever Academy team at academy@clever.com and share:
 • Which LMS you're using
• Which user type(s) you want to share courses with (such as teachers, School Tech Leads)
• The links to the course(s) you'd like to add to your LMS
• The rough timeline to get the content added (we need a minimum of two weeks lead time)
 

• We will prepare the relevant xAPI or CMI5 file(s) and share them with you. 
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What if my LMS isn’t listed? 

 • 
 We currently only support the listed LMSes - feel free to check back periodically to see if more LMSes are added.

 
 Is there a cost for this feature? 

 • 
 This feature is currently free!

 
 Where can I find more technical information? 

 • 
 Technical documentation is owned and maintained by the software that powers Clever Academy, Skilljar.

title: For Teachers & Staff: Setting up Classroom MFA
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What's Classroom MFA? 3. Setting up Classroom MFA 4. Adding authentication methods 5. Using Classroom MFA after setup 

 Please note: If you’re an admin trying to set up Classroom MFA for your teachers and staff, please reference this article . 

 Overview

 This article has information for teachers and staff on setting up Clever’s Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (Classroom MFA). As an optional add-on feature, Clever offers school organizations Classroom MFA to provide an extra layer of security for teacher and student logins.

 You’ll only have access to Classroom MFA if your school organization has added it to your Clever account. 

 For information on how to help your student’s set up their Classroom MFA, please see our article For Teachers: Helping your students set up and troubleshoot Classroom MFA .

 

 
 What's Classroom MFA?

 MFA is a way to log in to programs that requires you to use two methods (or factors) to prove your identity, like your username and password on your computer and a text message code sent to your phone. You likely already use MFA when signing in to other programs, such as your online bank account.

 

 Clever’s Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) creates a second layer of security for student and teacher accounts without the need for a second device. This will help you to protect sensitive information through secured access without disrupting learning in your classroom.

 

 Classroom MFA works by requiring a second factor of authentication for students and teachers using one of the following options that has been pre-selected by your school organization: 

 • Picture logins (students only)
• Clever Badges
• PIN (students only)
• Authenticator app
• Text message (SMS)
• Voice call
• Biometrics or security key
 
 

 
 Setting up Classroom MFA

 • 
 Go to your school organization's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/schoolorg-URL ) and sign in the way you normally do. You'll be prompted to set up Clever Classroom MFA.

 
 

 • You’ll be presented with one or more authentication methods. Read more about authentication methods below. Choose a method and click Next step . 
 
 

 • 
 Follow the instructions to set up the authentication method. You can learn more about authentication options below. 

• 
 Once you’ve finished the instructions, click Exit settings . 

• 
 Optional: If your school offers more than one authentication method, you can click Set up another method to add another authentication option. 

 
 
 Adding authentication methods

 Reminder: Authentication methods are used to prove your identity. You must add at least one authentication method to use Classroom MFA.

 

 It is best practice to add multiple methods if your school has made other options available to you. This way, if you’re not able to use one method for any reason (e.g. your cell phone is not charged so you can’t get a text message ) you can use another method to successfully log in. This saves time by providing an alternative method for yourself.

 

 Text message (also called SMS) 

 A numeric code is texted to a cell phone number you chose; you can type this code into your Clever login screen. Setup tips: 

 • Make sure the number you add when setting up Classroom MFA will go to a phone you can access during the school day, as you’ll need the code texted to this number to log in to Clever. 
• Also, keep in mind that this will not work for a landline. If you can only access a landline, consider an automated voice phone call.
 
 

 Automated voice phone call 

 A numeric code is shared via an automated voice phone call to a number you choose (can be a landline or cell phone); you then type this code into your Clever login screen. Setup tips: 

 • This will trigger a phone call to the number you added, so ensure you’ll be able to answer the phone and hear the code shared.
 
 

 Mobile app 

 You install an app on a cell phone that provides you with a numeric code you type into your Clever login screen. Setup tips: 

 • To access this option, you’ll need to install an authenticator app (such as Google Authenticator or Authy) to a mobile device or tablet and follow the Classroom MFA setup steps in Clever. Be sure you can access this device during the school day. You’ll need to scan a QR code, so ensure the camera works on the device.
• Authenticator applications work without requiring cell service or network so this is still a great option if you have cell service concerns.
 
 

 Setup steps, Google Authenticator App: If your school organization uses the Microsoft Authenticator App, please see the directions here . 

 • 
 On your cell phone , install the app by searching “Google Authenticator App” in the Google Play Store (Android) or the App Store (Apple). The app icon looks like this:

 
 

 You can also click the links below to access the download page for the apps, but be sure to download the app onto your phone or mobile device , not on your computer:

 

 • 
 On your computer , open the Classroom MFA setup steps (this will show up after you attempt to log in to Clever), or, if you’re already logged in, go to the top right corner of your Clever screen and click the person icon (👤), then MFA settings .

• 
 In the Classroom MFA setup directions, choose the App authentication method and click Next step until you see the QR code (it looks like a square bar code).

• 
 From your phone , open the authenticator app and select Scan a QR code . You may need to grant permission for the app to use your device’s camera. 

• 
 Scan the QR code on the computer screen using your device’s camera. 

• 
 After scanning the code, click Next step on your computer.

• 
 On your device , the app will provide you with a code. On your computer , type this code into the provided space on the Classroom MFA setup screen. 

• 
 Click Done .

• 
 Click Exit settings . You’re all set.

 
 After this initial setup, when prompted to use Classroom MFA, you won’t need to scan a QR code: you’ll just open the Google Authenticator App and tap on the account you added . You’ll be provided with a numeric code to type into Clever. The code will change every 30 seconds. 

 

 Setup steps, Microsoft Authenticator App: If your school organization uses the Google Authenticator App, please see the directions here . 

 • 
 On your cell phone or mobile device , install the app by searching “Microsoft Authenticator App” in the Google Play Store (Android) or the App Store (Apple). The app icon looks like this:

 
 

 You can also click the links below to access the download page for the apps, but be sure to download the app onto your mobile device, not on your computer.

 

 • 
 On your computer , open the Classroom MFA setup steps (this will show up after you attempt to log in to Clever), or, if you’re already logged in, go to the top right corner of your Clever screen and click the person icon (👤), then MFA settings .

• 
 In the Classroom MFA setup directions, choose the App authentication method and click Next step until you see the QR code.

• 
 From your mobile device , open the authenticator app, and review any user/privacy terms. If prompted to log in, you can press Skip in the top right corner (unless you have an account your school organization has given you directions to log into).

• 
 Press the Add account button, then Work or school account , then select Scan a QR code . You may need to grant permission for the app to use your device’s camera. 

• 
 Scan the QR code on the computer screen using your device’s camera. 

• 
 After scanning the code, click Next step on your computer.

• 
 On your mobile device , select the account you’ve just added, and the app will provide you with a code. On your computer , type this code into the provided space on the Classroom MFA setup screen.

• 
 Click Done .

• 
 Click Exit settings . You’re all set.

 
 After this initial setup, when prompted to use Classroom MFA

title: For Students: Setting up Classroom MFA
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is Classroom MFA? 3. How do I use Classroom MFA? • Setting up login picture logins • Setting up Clever MFA Badge logins • Setting up PIN logins 4. Troubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 In this article, you can learn more about what Classroom MFA is and how to use it when logging in. If you are experiencing any other type of issue, please reach out to your teacher.

 

 

 
 What is Classroom MFA?

 Classroom MFA is a way to safely log into your Clever account using two different login methods (or factors) to prove that you are logging into the correct account. Classroom MFA may require you to use any of the following options:

 • Picture logins
• Clever Badges
• PIN (6 digits)
• Authenticator app
• Text message (SMS)
• Voice call
• Biometrics or security key 
 
 
 How do I use Classroom MFA?

 There are a few different login methods (factors) that you may be asked to use. Here are the general steps that you should follow. Click here to learn about Classroom MFA login pictures, or click here to learn about Classroom MFA Badges.

 • Go to your school or district's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/district-URL ) and sign in the way you normally do.
• You'll be prompted to set up Classroom MFA.
• Once you have set up Classroom MFA you can use Clever as you normally would! Don’t forget what you set up - Clever may ask you again on every time you log in, every seven days, or every 30 days depending on your school’s requirements. 
 
 
 Setting up login picture logins

 • 
 Go to your school or district's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/district-URL ) and sign in the way you normally do.

 
 

 • 
 Choose your login animal > Click Next. Depending on your grade, your login animals may look different!

 
 

 • 
 Choose your login food > Click Next. Depending on your grade, your login food options may look different!

 
 

 • 
 Take a look at what you chose - don’t forget them! Click Done 

 
 

 • 
 You should now be logged into your Portal! 

 
 

 

 
 Setting up Clever MFA Badge Logins

 • 
 Go to your school or district's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/district-URL ) and sign in the way you normally do.

 
 

 • 
 Select Badge (if given the option)

 
 

 • 
 Click Download and print Badge 

 
 

 • 
 Once you have secured your Badge, click Next Step 

 
 

 • 
 Hold up your Clever MFA Badge to the camera. If you don’t have this, please ask your teacher for help.

 
 

 • 
 You should now be logged into your Portal! 

 
 

 

 If you've lost your Clever MFA Badge, or it won't work, don't worry! Your teacher can void your old one and print you a new one.

 

 
 Setting up PIN logins

 • Go to your school or district's login screen (For example: Clever.com/in/district-URL ) and sign in the way you normally do. 
• Select PIN if given the option. 
• Enter your 6-digit PIN code twice. You cannot use sequential numbers (e.g., 123456, 678901) or repetitive digits (e.g. 000000, 111111). If your PIN is not secure enough you’ll see an error that says, “PIN is too weak.” They must match in order to save them! 
• You should now be logged into your Portal! 
 
 
 Troubleshooting

 If you forget your second login method (MFA factor) or make a mistake, don’t worry! Click Try Again. 

 

 

 If you can’t remember how to login, select Can not login? and your teacher will be able to provide you with a single-use backup login code.

 

 

 Once your teacher has given you a backup code you can enter it > Click Log in.

title: Clever Roles: Staff - How to add to Clever (classic user interface)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Uploading staff for SFTP districts 3. Uploading staff for auto sync districts 4. Important Upload Tips 5. Associating staff to multiple locations or the district office 6. Editing & removing staff or updating their roles 7. Districts using Infinite Campus API or Skyward API syncs 8. Automated Staff SFTP for auto syncs 

 IMPORTANT This article shows directions for the classic user interface for adding staff (which some legacy districts use). If you notice the Staff page of your account looks different from the documentation below, please view this article instead . 

 Overview

 Clever Admins are able to provide various non-instructional staff (i.e. non-teaching staff) access to Clever. For an overview of Clever Roles and permissions, please see this article . 

 Clever Tip: If your schools use the Infinite Campus API or Skyward API sync type, skip below to this section for information on how staff are automatically synced to Clever. If your schools use Active Directory, click here to automatically sync staff data to Clever. 

 For most schools, there are two ways to add staff to Clever: 

 • Staff as part of your normal sync or a one-time sync upload.
• Custom Staff , added individually on a per-user basis.
 
 Please see the table below to determine the best method for your account. thod for your account. Please note that many districts manage staff in bulk using a staff.csv and manually add individual accounts using Custom Staff as needed . Please see this section if you use a Clever-Managed Auto Sync that can't sync staff. 

 
 

 
 Custom Staff

 
 Staff

 
 Manually create staff 
 Manually associate staff with multiple schools 
 Manually edit staff 
 Manually delete staff 
 Bulk create staff via CSV 
 Automate bulk create staff (CSV file) (SFTP districts only) 
 Bulk associate staff with multiple schools (CSV file) 
 Bulk edit staff (CSV file) 
 Bulk delete staff (CSV file) 
 View staff Support Tools > Custom Data > Staff Data browser 
 Recommended for: 
 Creating individual staff

 Small schools / districts

 
 Medium and large districts

 
 Documentation Link Link 
 
 

 

 
 Uploading staff for SFTP districts

 You can manually upload staff from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page by uploading a staff.csv file. This method is best for adding staff members in bulk if they are not synced to Clever by your student information system (SIS). Please note: You must explicitly share staff users with your learning applications once you create them in Clever. To upload a staff.csv: 

 • From your Clever Dashboard, select Data sources > SIS sync > Upload .
• If you need an example file, click Download example files or download your existing staff.csv. Add a row to the file for each staff member you would like to add to Clever. Do not remove any staff users you still want to access Clever. If you remove a user from the staff.csv before re-uploading it their record will be deleted. To maintain existing account, add new user information to the file before re-uploading.
 
 • Upload your staff.csv file. When the cloud displays as green, select Upload. The staff updates will be reflected in Clever in about 15 minutes.
 
 

 • 
 
 Schools that sync data to Clever via SFTP can also upload the staff.csv directly to their district's Clever SFTP folder. For instructions, visit: SFTP: How do I upload my data to Clever? 

 For additional information about Clever's SFTP and CSV formatting specifications, click here . 

 

 
 Uploading staff for auto sync districts

 If your school's SIS isn't automatically syncing staff members to Clever, you can upload them in bulk directly from your Clever Dashboard. You'll navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data tab and upload a staff.csv file under "Manual upload." Please note: You must explicitly share staff users with your learning applications once created in Clever. To upload a staff.csv:

 1. From your Clever Dashboard , select Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data tab . 2. If you need an example file, click Download example files or download your existing staff.csv. Add a row to the file for each staff member you would like to add to Clever. Do not remove any staff users you still want to access Clever. If you remove a user from the staff.csv before re-uploading it their record will be deleted. To maintain existing account, add new user information to the file before re-uploading. 

 

 3. Upload your staff.csv file. When the cloud displays as green, select Upload . The staff updates will be reflected in Clever in about 15 minutes. 

 

 

 For additional information about Clever's SFTP and CSV formatting specifications, click here . 

 

 
 Important Upload Tips

 When uploading staff to Clever, there are a few important details to consider: 

 • Each unique staff member requires a unique staff_id. 
• Always include all staff in the staff.csv file if you still want them to have access to Clever. If you remove a user from the staff.csv before re-uploading the file their record will be deleted. 
• The school_id must match the school_id (i.e. sis_id) for a school in Clever. To locate this value, search for the school from the top search bar and select the school to bring up the school's profile page. Under School Information, find the School ID. Use this school_id value in the staff.csv.
 
 

 

 

 • For staff that are not associated with a specific school or are associated with the district office, use the school_id value: DEFAULT_DISTRICT_OFFICE
 
 • Uploaded staff are auto-assigned the role Portal Access . Portal Access users don't have any administrative rights and can only single sign-on (SSO) to applications from their Clever Portal. Applications must first be shared with them by a Clever Admin.
• For instructions on manually adding individual staff members to Clever via the Custom Data feature, visit: Adding custom staff to Clever .
 
 

 For information on admin and staff roles in Clever, please visit: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions .

 

 
 Associating staff to multiple locations or the district office 

 Multiple schools: 

 To associate a staff member with multiple schools, create a separate row at each location. The only field that will be different for each row is the school_id. For example, if a staff member should be associated with three schools, it should look like this:

 

 All schools: 

 To associate a staff member with all schools in the district, enter school_id: district 

 

 Default District Office : 

 There is a default auto-generated district office in Clever. To associate a staff member with the district office, enter the school_id: DEFAULT_DISTRICT_OFFICE 

 

 

 
 Editing & removing staff or updating their roles

 • From your Clever Dashboard, select Data sources > SIS sync > Upload tab. 
• Under Download existing files, select staff.csv. The downloaded staff.csv file will contain all pre-existing staff in Clever for your district. 
• Edit the records for staff as desired and save the file. 
• Next, upload the file. When the cloud displays as green, select Upload. The staff updates will be reflected in Clever in about 15 minutes. 
 
 To update a staff member's role from Portal Access to School Tech Lead , please follow the instructions here: Clever Roles: School Tech Lead. 

 Please note: You should list all staff members in the staff.csv every time you upload a new file to Clever! The staff member will be deleted from Clever and connected applications if their record is not populated in the staff.csv. 

 

 
 Districts using the Infinite Campus API or Skyward API sync

 Infinite Campus API 

 Staff are synced to Clever if: 

 • Teacher is not checked for their district assignment in Infinite Campus.
 
 All staff members that do not have 'Teacher' checked will appear as 'Administrator' in the OneRoster API and will be synced to Clever as Staff. If a user has 'Teacher'

title: For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is Classroom MFA? 3. Pricing 4. Getting Started 5. Frequently Asked Questions 

 Overview

 Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a second layer of security for Clever without a second device. Classroom MFA protects applications that use Clever for single sign-on with age-appropriate second factors like Clever Badges or login pictures. This includes the Clever Portal, Google Workspace, and Google Chromebooks. This solution helps districts stay ahead of cybersecurity threats without disrupting learning.

 

 

 By providing a dual layer of authentication, Classroom MFA can help you meet cybersecurity insurance requirements and worry less about compromised credentials for your students, teachers, and instructional staff. As a bonus, Classroom MFA helps increase students' digital citizenship skills as they navigate our digital environments in a way that's safe and responsible.

 

 Classroom MFA works by requiring a second factor of authentication for students and teachers via Clever Badges, pictures, authenticator apps, SMS, voice calls, or biometrics/hardware tokens, for any Clever-based SSO login. With the ability to require re-authentication for login attempts outside your designated country or network, rest assured potential bad actors will hit your MFA wall and get turned away.

 

 
 What is Classroom MFA?

 This is a way for your students and teachers to securely MFA into Clever to protect all of your Clever applications without the requirement of a second device. Classroom MFA protects the Clever portal for your users. Any application that a student or teacher accesses from the Clever portal will have a layer of protection.

 

 Classroom MFA currently supports the following factors:

 • Picture logins (students only)
• Clever Badges
• PIN (students only)
• Authenticator app
• Text message (SMS)
• Voice call
• Biometrics or security key
 
 
 Pricing

 For more information on pricing, please contact our team here .

 Districts can preview Classroom MFA before purchasing here in your Clever Dashboard ! After completing the configuration and viewing a free preview, IT administrators can purchase Classroom MFA directly in the Clever App Store. Clever’s digital learning platform (including single sign-on, rostering, messaging, and analytics) remains completely free for districts. 

 
 Getting Started

 You can learn how to get started with Classroom MFA by checking out our article For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Setup or connecting with one of our cybersecurity specialists here: https://www.getclever.com/mfa-contact-a-specialist 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 How is Classroom MFA different from other MFA options on the market available? 

 • Most MFA solutions available on the market are not designed for the classroom and require a second device to be present with their user. Classroom MFA eliminates the need for a second device making it accessible to teachers and students. 
 
 

 Will Classroom MFA work for my staff users? 

 • Yes, your staff will be protected for any applications that are accessed only via single sign-on through Clever. If you would like to learn more or have other requirements for staff users, please connect with a cybersecurity specialist here: https://www.getclever.com/mfa-contact-a-specialist 
 
 

 Can students use the same Clever Badge they already use to log into Clever as their MFA Badge method login? 

 • No, a Clever Badge and MFA Badge are not the same thing. For maximum security, we strongly recommend that districts assign an alternative MFA factor for students who already Badge into Clever. 
 
 

 Which applications are covered by Classroom MFA? 

 • Classroom MFA will cover all Single Sign-on (SSO) and Rostering & Single Sign-on (SSO) applications. This includes the Clever Portal, Google Workspace, and Google Chromebooks.
 
 

 How can Classroom MFA protect Google? 

 • Classroom MFA can protect Google through the Workspace application. Learn how to set up the Workspace app to protect Google for your district here !
 
 How often will students, teachers, or staff be asked to use their second method of authentication? 

 • During the MFA configuration process, you'll be able to choose to have users re-authenticate every time they login, every 7 days, or every 30 days. You can also add IP addresses for secure networks (such as your school network) on which you can choose to never require a second factor.
 
 Can I turn off teacher troubleshooting tools for Classroom MFA? 

 • If you configure Classroom MFA for students, teachers will have some tools available in their Portal to troubleshoot login issues for students. For example, teachers can reveal student MFA pictures or reset them. It is not possible to turn off teacher troubleshooting tools for Classroom MFA at this time.
 
 Can students use Classroom MFA when logging into a Chromebook with a Clever Badge? 

 • Yes! Check out the video below to see what the login flow looks like:

title: For Clever Admins: Account recovery and password reset
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Resetting your Clever Admin passwords 3. Setting up your Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID account recovery and password reset 4. Clever Passwords password reset 5. Resetting a student, teacher, or staff password 

 

 Overview

 This article briefly overviews the various account recovery and password reset options available through Clever. Please review the relevant section below and use the links for more information on your account recovery and password reset options.

 
 Resetting your Clever Admin passwords

 For help resetting your own passwords or MFA, please see our articles:

 • 
 For Clever Admins: How do I reset my password? 

• 
 Getting help resetting your Multi-factor Authentication access 

 
 
 Setting up Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID account recovery and password reset 

 If you are utilizing Clever IDM for either Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID, you can enable account recovery and password resets for any users you are currently provisioning accounts for (students, teachers, and staff). For more information on the setup and troubleshooting of Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID account recovery and password resets, please see our article Clever IDM: Account recovery and password reset . 

 
 Clever Passwords password resets

 If you are utilizing Clever Passwords as the identity provider for Clever, you can customize whether students, teachers, and staff can reset their passwords via Clever. For more information, please see our articles Single sign-on (SSO): Clever Passwords - Log in with Clever (classic) or Single sign-on (SSO): Clever Passwords - Log in with Clever (updated) .

 
 Resetting a student, teacher, or staff password. 

 As the Clever Admin, you may be able to assist students, teachers, and staff with password resets.

 If you are utilizing Clever IDM and would like to reset an end user’s Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID password, please see this article for more information: Clever IDM: Account recovery and password reset .

 If you use Clever Passwords as your identity provider, please see these articles for more information:

 • 
 Single sign-on (SSO): Clever Passwords - Log in with Clever (classic) 

• 
 Single sign-on (SSO): Clever Passwords - Log in with Clever (updated) .

 
 If you’d like to provide your end users with troubleshooting resources we recommend:

 • 
 For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever 

• 
 For Students: Password recovery and resets 

• 
 For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students 

• 
 For School Tech Leads: Account recovery and password reset for Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Clever Passwords

title: For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Resetting teacher passwords • Setting up account recovery • Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page • Resetting your password in Clever Portal 3. Resetting student passwords 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Not being able to log in is no fun. However, if your school district is using Clever passwords, Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google Workspace as a login method, you may be able to reset your password and the passwords of your students (students may also have the ability to reset their passwords). This depends on whether your school district has enabled this feature, and which login methods they have enabled for it. 

 
 Resetting teacher passwords

 Not all school districts allow teachers to reset their passwords. If you don’t see the option to reset your password, or if these steps don't resolve the issue, please get in touch with your school district's tech support contact directly for help.

 Setting up account recovery 

 If your school has enabled it, you can use a backup method (such as email, security questions, or phone number) to regain access to your Clever, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Google Workspace accounts right from your Clever login screen. This is handy if you cannot log in with your password successfully.

 To set up your backup method (again, if your school had enabled this functionality), you need to be logged into your Portal.

 • 
 Click on your blue icon in the upper right corner to bring up your settings. 

• 
 Click on Set up recovery methods. The recovery methods available to you depend on what your school district has enabled.

• 
 Choose a recovery method and input the information.

• 
 Depending on what you choose for a recovery method, you will be prompted on how to continue.

 
 

 

 It’s also possible that your school organization may have provided a recovery method for you using information in their records. For example, if your schools collect personal email addresses, they may set up your account recovery email as your personal email address. If you’d like to update the information your school organization provides, you can do so using the recovery method setup described above.

 Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login screen 

 To reset Clever and third-party passwords: 

 • From the Clever login screen, click on get help logging in. 
• Choose Reset your account passwords. 
• Input your school district email address (not to be confused with a recovery email you have set up). 
• You’ll be prompted to confirm your identity using a method you set up in your Portal settings. Select how you’ll confirm your identity.
• You can now set up your new password for both your Clever and third-party accounts by inputting a new password. Your school may have password requirements, which can be viewed by clicking on See requirements . Once completed, you can use your new password for your third-party account(s). 
 
 

 To reset Clever and third-party passwords: 

 • Select Get help logging in! to reset your password using the email address linked to your district's Clever account. 
• Choose Reset only your Clever password 
• Enter the email that you signed up with to receive password reset instructions, and click Send. 
 
 

 Resetting your password in Clever Portal 

 Alternatively, if you do have access to your Teacher Portal and want to update your password for any reason (e.g., you feel your passwords may have been compromised), you can do so by following these steps from your Teacher Portal:

 • Click on your blue icon in the upper right corner to bring up your settings. 
• From underneath Log in password click Change passwords to update . 
 
 This is independent of the account recovery process and doesn’t require you to go through the recovery method.

 Please note: Your district’s settings will determine which passwords you can reset through Clever. In the screenshot above you’ll see this user will reset both their Google and Clever password. Please pay close attention to the passwords you are resetting. 

 

 
 Resetting student passwords

 If your school has enabled it, you can reset student passwords for Log in with Clever and third-Party accounts. Best of all, it can be done right from your Teacher Portal! 

 To update a student’s password:

 • Click on My Students in the menu on the left side of your Teacher Portal.
• Choose Student Roster .
• You can find the student by filtering by class and then clicking on the student from the roster.
• You’ll see Student Tools on the right side of the Portal under the student’s name. From there, click on Set new password . Depending on your district’s settings, this will reset the student password for any login method managed by Clever. In the video above, you'll see the student passwords will be reset for both Google and Clever passwords. 
 Once updated, your student should be able to log in successfully with the new password you've created.

If your school has enabled it, students may also be able to reset their own passwords. To see how students can reset their own accounts, check out this article: For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever . 
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 How long do I need to wait after resetting my password before logging in? 

 • 
 You can login immediately after resetting your password!

 
 What if I don’t have the option to reset my password? 

 • 
 Reach out to your school district's tech support contact for further assistance.

 
 Can Clever reset my password for me or tell me what my password is? 

 • 
 No, the Clever Support team cannot reset your password for you or provide you with your password. Please reach out to your district directly if you’re having issues with your password.

 
 What are the password reset requirements? 

 • Passwords cannot start/end with a space
• Characters cannot repeat more than 3 times
• Passwords cannot contain any part of the user’s name

title: What is SAML?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is SAML? 3. How SAML works 4. How you can use SAML with Clever 

 

 Overview

 This article will give you an overview of what SAML is and how to use it. By the way, SAML stands for Security Assertion Markup Language, in case you were wondering! 

 
 What is SAML?

 So technically, SAML is an open standard for exchanging authentication and authorization data between two parties. It’s commonly used for single sign-on (SSO), allowing users to sign in once and authenticate securely with multiple third-party applications. SAML is one of the methods Clever uses to help you access a variety of connected applications just by signing in to Clever! 

 
 How SAML works

 Two primary roles in a SAML exchange are Service Providers (SP) and Identity Providers (IdP).

 • Service Providers : SPs are services or applications (like BrainPOP or Remind) that users want to access. Service Providers coordinate with Identity Providers to authorize and grant access to those services or apps.
• Identity Providers : IdPs (like Active Directory or Google) authenticate the user (making sure you’re you!) and share authorization information with SPs. Identity Providers package the users' information into a well-known format known as a SAML Assertion.
 
 The thing to know about SAML is that both SPs or IdPs can kick off the sign-in process into an app; it just depends on where you, as the user, visit first:

 • If you visit the application first, the application (or SP in SAML terms) will ask the Identity Provider for information about the user and then use that information to process the login, essentially asking the Identity Provider if you’re legit.
• If you first visit an SSO application (or IdP like the Clever Portal) and then navigate to an application from a list of approved applications, the IdP will forward information to the Service Provider, giving them a heads up that you’re who you say you are.
 
 To illustrate this, let’s look at the following scenario: You’re attending the Educators of the Year conference (yay, you!). When you arrive, you need to pick up your attendance badge and a color-coded lanyard to allow you into specific sessions at the conference. 

 

 

 

 You can get your badge and lanyard in one of two ways. Option 1 : Check in, then go to the venue for your session (like going through an IdP and then a Service Provider/app) Checking in to the event involves contacting the event coordinators, who then verify registration and put together a packet with your badge and lanyard. Option 2: Head to the venue and get checked in (like going to the Service Provider/app, and having it verify you via an IdP) Alternatively, you can check in at the venue, which then contacts the event coordinators who verify your registration. Regardless of the check-in method, the result remains the same- your registration is verified, and you have the necessary badge and lanyard to participate in the sessions. In this scenario, the event coordinator acts as the Identity Provider while the venue acts as the Service Provider. The packet put together by the event coordinator is similar to the SAML assertion. You have two options for checking in. The first method, early check-in, is similar to the IdP-initiated flow. The second method, checking in at the venue, is similar to the SP-initiated flow. 

 
 How you can use SAML with Clever

 You can add any SAML connection as an IdP into Clever! We offer support for Active Directory Federation Services (AD and Entra ID), Okta, and Rapid ID. However, any SAML connection can be created by following the same steps outlined in this article . Clever won’t have insight into the IdP other than those we offer support for. 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Single sign-on (SSO): Configure a SAML-based IDP for access to Clever

title: For Clever Admins: Clever LMS Connect
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 3. Learn More 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is excited to announce that we're offering a solution to districts that allows you to directly connect your LMS with Clever. Clever LMS Connect allows districts to launch applications directly from their LMS, save teachers time by eliminating grade double entry, and easily add application content and assignments to their LMS.

 

 

 Please note: As seen in the graphic above, LMS Connect does not connect your LMS to your SIS and vice versa. 

 

 
 Features

 Clever LMS Connect connects learning applications to your LMS through the Clever API and creates a seamless learning experience for students and teachers by:

 • 
 Saving time: Teachers and students can launch applications directly from the LMS (compliant with LTI launch protocol requirements).

• 
 Eliminating grade double entry: Teachers can sync grades and assignments between your LMS and learning applications.

• 
 Adding content and assignments: Teachers can automatically insert content and assignments directly from applications into the LMS. This means that students can easily launch applications and find assignments in their LMS.

 
 View the videos below to see the LMS Connect teacher and student experience.

 Canvas 

 

 

 Google Classroom 

 

 
 
 Learn More

 Clever LMS Connect is available for free to districts, and will be available on a per-app basis as learning applications build their LMS Connect integrations. We encourage you to reach out to applications that you are already rostering through Clever, and let them know that you're interested in using Clever LMS Connect with their application.

 If you're interested in partnering with Clever to be one of our first districts to implement LMS Connect, please fill out our interest form . Plus, be sure to check out our product releases for new updates on Clever LMS Connect. 

 If none of your connected Clever apps have built an LMS Connect integration, you'll see a waitlist on the LMS Connect page in your Clever Dashboard: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > LMS Connect page.
• Click Join waitlist & fill out your information.
 
 

 Clever will notify you when one of your apps becomes available to connect. 

 

 Whether you're on the waitlist or you've already set up LMS Connect, you can also request that more of your Clever apps build integrations by clicking the app interest form:

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What’s the difference between Clever LMS Connect and Google Classroom roster import? 

 • 
 Google Classroom roster import allows districts to provision Courses in Google Classroom based on data in the SIS. Clever LMS Connect allows districts to sync assignments and gradebooks between applications and Google Classroom.

• 
 To ensure a quality data-matching experience with Google Classroom for Clever LMS Connect, Clever requires districts using Google Classroom to connect with the Google Classroom roster import app. As Google Classroom allows teachers to modify their own classrooms, this presents a challenge with maintaining data parity for Clever LMS Connect if a district is not using Clever to roster Google Classroom courses. 

 
 

 Do you know if Clever LMS Connect for Google Classroom will work with the free version of Google Fundamentals or would it require a paid tier with Google? 

 • Districts using Google Classroom with Clever LMS Connect are required to connect with Clever’s Google Classroom roster import app to ensure a quality data-matching experience. As Google Classroom allows teachers to modify their own classrooms, this presents a challenge with maintaining data parity for Clever LMS Connect if a district is not using Clever to roster Google Classroom courses. 
• 
 Using the Google Classroom roster import application requires a Google Workspace for Education plus subscription.

 
 

 Will Clever LMS Connect pass grades back to our SIS? 

 • Not at this time. LMS Connect will only sync grades and assignments between applications and your LMS.
 
 

 Is Clever LMS Connect a separate charge for districts? 

 • LMS Connect is free for districts.
 
 

 Will Clever LMS Connect work if our districts use multiple LMSs? 

 • At this time, LMS Connect will only work with one LMS per school district. We hope to allow for multiple LMSs per school district in the future.
 
 

 Which LMS systems will work with Clever LMS Connect? 

 • 
 We plan to integrate with all major LMSs, especially starting with those that are most used by schools - Google Classroom, Schoology, and Canvas. We plan to integrate with more in the future.

 
 

 Can I use Clever LMS Connect if I already have LTI integrations set up with certain apps in my LMS? 

 • Yes, with Clever LMS Connect you can manage all your rostering and LMS connections in one place. The advantage of Clever LMS Connect is you’ll only need to connect your LMS to Clever once, and then you can quickly connect with applications. You’ll no longer need to manage LMS data matching with applications individually.
 
 

 Does Clever support cross-listing with LMS Connect? 

 • LMS Connect automatically syncs assignments and grades from applications to your LMS. However, if your district has cross-listed sections in your LMS, this functionality isn't currently available.

title: SFTP Syncs: SFTP web upload feature with field mapping
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Initial file upload 3. Updating your data after your initial sync 4. Review existing field mappings 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 The web upload tool now makes it easier to upload your schools' data to Clever by helping you match the columns in your files to the columns Clever requires, such as school_id, using field mapping. A field mapping is when you assign the column headers you sync to Clever via CSV upload to a Clever field. You can associate a column header from your uploaded file to multiple required Clever fields.

 For example, you could map your school number field to both School_id and School_number in Clever. Keep in mind that the reverse is not available, as you can’t associate a Clever field with multiple column headers. 

 This is a useful tool for districts that manage their Clever files and don’t have a way to edit the files they are uploading. For example, if you are using a SIS-Managed Auto Sync and your SIS is not able to edit a file header for you. Keep reading to learn how to upload your files with field mappings! 

 

 

 

 
 Initial file upload

 Step 1: Prepare the following 5 required files to upload to Clever:

 • 
 schools.csv

• 
 teachers.csv

• 
 students.csv

• 
 sections.csv

• 
 enrollments.csv

• 
 staff.csv (optional)

 
 Make sure your files include all data that is marked as required for each file in the Clever SFTP Specifications. 

 

 Clever Tip: View the correct column headers for each file type in the How to format your files section of this Clever Academy Quick Start Guide. 

 

 Step 2: Upload your files on your Data sources > SIS sync > Upload tab as shown: 

 

 

 

 Step 3 : After you click Upload , you’ll check that each required file (schools, students, teachers, sections, and enrollments) has the correct column headers.

 

 

 

 The required column headers are listed first. Once you fill out the required column headers, the optional fields will appear. Click Next step after going through each data type. During these steps, you’ll make sure that the column headers match the corresponding fields in Clever. We do not recommend changing mappings for any ID fields (school_id, student_id, teacher_id, section_id, etc.) after a first sync as this will cause data to be deleted and recreated, potentially causing a loss of historical data. 

 Step 4 : Once you’ve completed each CSV file, you’ll get to Review & submit . Here you can review your column headers and the Clever fields. If everything looks correct, you can click Submit . After approximately 30 minutes, your uploaded data will be reflected in Clever.

 

 

 

 
 Updating your data after your initial sync

 Any time you need to update your data in Clever, whether adding a new student or adjusting a field mapping, you'll need to repeat the above steps. Just be sure to include ALL records for each file type you upload. Any data missing from the files will be deleted from Clever.

 Once you’ve uploaded your files, you will be prompted to review your field mappings to make adjustments as needed. If your field mappings don’t need to be changed, you can click Submit .

 Please note: Please do not change the field mappings for any sis_id values (school_id, student_id, teacher_id, section_id, staff_id) after your initial data sync. Changing sis_ids may cause a loss of historical data. Please contact Clever Support if you need to change sis_ids. 

 

 
 Review existing field mappings

 You can view your field mappings on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings tab at any time.

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 What does it mean to complete a field mapping? 

 • A field mapping is when one field is linked to another. With the field mapping tool, you assign a header (field) in your CSV files to a field in Clever.
 
 How do I know which field in my CSV files should I should map to each Clever field? 

 • You should choose the fields that are most closely related. For example, a value of 123456 in your student CSV titled student_code may fit for both student_id and student_number. You can map the student_code field in your students.csv to both the student_id and student_number fields in Clever.
 
 Do I need to change my field mappings after my first sync? 

 • Only if you have added new fields! Before each new data sync, you should review your existing field mappings to confirm that no changes are needed. 
 
 Can I change my sis_id mappings? 

 • Please consult Clever Support if you need to change any sis_id (school_id, student_id, teacher_id, section_id) values. Changing ID field mappings may result in a loss of historical data.
 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • SFTP Syncs: Viewing Clever CSV files 
• 
 SFTP Syncs: Troubleshooting CSV formatting errors 

• 
 SFTP & Manual Upload

title: For App Partners: Clever Concierge
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How it works 3. Input CSV Guidelines 4. Output CSV format 

 Please note: Clever Concierge is currently only enabled for applications that support Secure Sync. 

 Overview

 Clever Concierge is our premium district onboarding service for partners implementing at scale. We’ll help you match your existing customers and quickly invite them to connect via Clever. 

 
 How it works

 1. From your Clever Dashboard > click Clever Concierge (left-side menu)

 

 

 2. Upload a customers.csv file containing your existing customer list

 3. Clever will perform a customer match based on the data you have uploaded

 4. You’ll receive a district match confirmation. You can work with your CSM to confirm the right folks are included in the bulk invite campaign. Please contact your Customer Success Manager with any questions about Clever Concierge. If you’re unsure of who to contact, feel free to reach out to contact app-success@clever.com . 

 
 Input CSV Guidelines

 When creating an input CSV to upload to Clever's Concierge, we recommend using our CSV template as a guide. The filename for the CSV file should be the following (all lowercase letters): customers.csv

 

 

 Be sure to include as many of the fields listed below as possible. We strongly encourage you to include at minimum the District Name and NCES ID or MDR for best results.

 

 • District Name
• NCES ID or MDR (providing accurate MDR numbers or NCES IDs will provide best results)
• District State
• District Contact Name
• District Contact Email
• Date the district should go live with SSO
• Names of school sites using your application
• NCES IDs of school sites using your application 
 
 
 Output CSV format

 After you have uploaded your CSV, please allow up to a week for review. Our team will provide you with a CSV containing the following fields:

 

 • District Name
• District Clever ID
• State (if applicable)
• Launch Status (if applicable) 
 
 
 Related Articles 

 • For App Partners: Navigating the Clever Dashboard 
• For App Partners: Transferring existing districts to Clever

title: Clever IDM: Account links
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Linking accounts between Clever and a destination 3. Common troubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM account links are the glue that ties a Clever IDM account and a destination, such as Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID, together. When an IDM sync runs, we create a link between what's in Clever and what's in the destination and provision a destination account and email based on the Clever account. 

 
 Linking accounts between Clever and a destination

 When an IDM sync runs, Clever creates a link between a Clever account and an account in either Google, AD, or Entra ID. If Clever IDM can't find an existing account with the relevant username or email address for a user, it will create a new account in the destination and link it to the corresponding account in Clever. Links are helpful in preventing issues. An example would be when a student graduates that happens to have the same username as an incoming freshman. For example:

 • User 1, jsmith graduates
• User 2, jsmith is an incoming freshman
• We don’t want user 2 taking over user 1’s Google account
• Clever throws a conflict to flag this issue for the Clever admin
 
 Without the links, we would erroneously automatically match Clever user 2 with the old jsmith account.

 Occasionally, because of inaccurate data in Clever or a destination, Clever IDM will incorrectly link a Clever account to an account in a destination. 

 For instance, if a user's email syncs to Clever with a typo or it’s the incorrect email altogether. In such cases, the link connects that Clever user to the wrong email address.

 When this happens, you can remove the link, fix the typo, and run a new Clever IDM sync. There are two ways to unlink an active user. For archived users, you'll need to use the second method as their Profile Page will no longer be available in Clever. 

 • You can remove the link by going to the Clever Dashboard > User’s profile page > Details tab > Clever IDM Information box > click Unlink user. As long as you correct the data in Clever and/or the destination, the new link will be correct. 
• Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > User Management > IDM > Events tab and click the drop-down menu for the user you need to unlink. You'll see an Unlink user button. Again, as long as you correct the data in Clever and/or the destination, the new link will be correct. 
 
 

 The link creation lifecycle follows these steps:

 • Clever IDM creates a link when we find a matching account in a destination or when it provisions a new one. • Example: Joyce is a new student at the school district and her email address in Clever is provisioned in the destination and linked to her Clever account.
 

• If your school district syncs the user's email and username from your SIS, the link is updated automatically if the username changes. If you rely on a template-generated username or email, you must unlink to create a new template-generated username or email.
 • Example: A student is adopted and now has a new last name. Their last name change alters their username and email. Their link is automatically updated since you’re syncing their username and email from the SIS!
 

• If the Clever user is no longer in your SIS, we archive them via IDM. If a user returns to Clever within one year of departure and is in an Archive OU, their Clever account will be restored and moved from the Archive OU to the appropriate active OU based on your Parent/Sub-OU settings. If a user returns after one year, a new Clever and destination account will be generated.
 • Example: Joyce has the opportunity to be an exchange student for 6 months, so she leaves her school district temporarily. When she gets back, her account is restored.
 

• Once they’re archived in IDM, the destination account as disabled.
• If desired, you’ll need to delete the accounts manually
 • Example: A couple of years later, Joyce graduates. Many Clever admins keep graduated seniors in archive OUs for a few months after graduation before deleting them
 

• Once the Clever user and the destination user are entirely deleted, we delete the link upon the next IDM sync.
 
 

 
 Common troubleshooting

 Here are a few examples of some common issues: 

 
 Issue

 
 Resolution

 
 A user gets married, changes their name, and has a new email address. The link for that user is connected to the old email. If your school district syncs the user's email and username from your SIS, the link is updated automatically if the username or email changes.
 If you rely on a template-generated username or email, you must unlink to create a new template-generated username or email. 

 Once updated, the account will be correctly linked after the next Clever IDM sync.

 
 There’s a typo in a user’s email that has been synced to Clever. Now that user is linked to the wrong email address. If your school district syncs the user's email and username from your SIS, the link is updated automatically if the username or email changes.
 If you rely on a template-generated username or email, you must unlink to create a new template-generated username or email. 

 Once updated, the account will be correctly linked after the next Clever IDM sync.

 
 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Clever IDM: Sync reports & issue troubleshooting 
• Clever IDM: Google Setup 
• Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup 
• Clever IDM: Entra ID Setup

title: Clever IDM: Organizing OUs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Organize OUs • Parent OU • Sub-OUs • Archive OUs • Ignored OUs 3. Building sub-OUs 

 

 Overview

 This article will help you understand how Clever IDM organizes users within your existing Active Directory or Google OU structures during the IDM setup process. The organization of these is very similar for both destinations, Active Directory and Google.

 You can find the general setup for each destination type here:

 • Clever IDM: Google Setup 
• Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup 
 
 

 
 Organize OUs

 OU stands for organizational unit, a group that an administrator can create in Active Directory or the Google Admin console to apply settings to a specific group of users, such as students or 7th graders.

 Clever IDM will organize users into your existing OU structure and create optional sub-OUs based on Clever user data. A preview will display the resulting OU structure based on your selections during setup.

 

 Parent OU 

 Select the primary OU where all users for the user type should reside. In the next step, the school organization can identify which sub-OUs Clever should create to organize students. Clever requires the following OUs for Google and Active Directory:

 • Parent OU for student users
• Parent OU for teacher users
• Parent OU for staff users
• Parent OU for archived users
 
 Sub-OUs 

 Clever can create sub-OUs to organize users based on data in Clever, such as school, grade level, and graduation year. Please note that sub-OUs aren't required.

 Here are a few popular options: 

 Student sub-OUs:

 • {School}
• {School} > {Grade}
• {School} > {Graduation year}
• {Grade}
• {Graduation year}
 
 Graduation Year : If synced to Clever, IDM will use the Graduation_year field from the school organization's SIS. Otherwise, IDM will automatically calculate the Graduation Year for students K-12 based on the student's Grade field. In calculating graduation year, Preschool, PreKindergarten, and TransitionalKindergarten will be treated as grade -1. Kindergarten is treated as 0.

 

 Students in the following grade levels will NOT have a Graduation Year unless provided by the school organization: InfantToddler, 13, PostGraduate, Ungraded, Other.

 Teacher sub-OUs:

 • {School} 
 
 Staff sub-OUs:

 • {Department}
• {Job code}
• {custom formula}
 
 Clever recommends implementing sub-OUs to assist with organization and permissions. 

 Archive OUs 

 Clever allows the school organization to determine the parent OU to which a user will be assigned when an account is removed from Clever. If a user is removed from Clever, they will be placed in the Archive OU during the next Clever IDM sync.

 If a user returns to Clever within one year of departure and is in an Archive OU:

 • If a user returns to Clever within one year of departure and is in an Archive OU, Clever will restore their account and move it from the Archive OU to the appropriate active OU based on your Parent/Sub-OU settings.
• Clever will generate a new Clever account and Active Directory or Google account if a user returns after one year. 
 
 Please note: For Google, Clever IDM will manage the archival of users with any free Fundamentals, Fundamentals-archive, and Gmail-only licenses, as specified in your Clever IDM archive OU setup . To manage users with paid licenses, please use the Google License Manager in your Clever Dashboard . 

 

 How Clever IDM protects against the accidental archiving of multiple users by detecting unusual behavior:

 • We calculate the percentage of users marked for archiving for each user type (e.g., students, teachers, and staff).
• If this percentage exceeds 20%, the archive will be automatically paused, and Clever admins will receive a notification.
• This notification will appear on the IDM dashboard, under the Tasks section.
• Clever Admins review users to determine whether or not they should be archived.
 
 Ignored OUs 

 Ignored OUs are OUs in your destination (Active Directory and Google) that you want Clever to ignore. Clever won't move users out of these OUs, and you'll manage them manually. You can use ignored OUs to implement more rigorous controls, such as applying settings for specific individuals or groups of users; for example you might have an ignored OU for users that blocks YouTube.. Multiple OUs can be selected.

 By default, Clever IDM deactivates accounts when users are removed from Clever – unless they are in an ignored OU. The default behavior for users in ignored OUs is to ignore those accounts entirely. However, you can update this behavior as outlined below.

 Clever IDM offers three options for student, teacher, and staff accounts in ignored OUs, giving you more flexibility. These options are available in the Organize OUs step of the IDM Provisioning Setup:

 • When users are removed from Clever, automatically suspend user accounts in ignored OUs.
 • Clever IDM will update user accounts with this setting.
 

• When users are removed from Clever, don’t automatically suspend user accounts in ignored OUs.
 • You’ll suspend these user accounts.
 

• Don’t match, update, or suspend users in ignored OUs. I will manage these user accounts myself.
 • You’ll handle all updates for users in ignored OUs. 
 

 
 

 
 Building sub-OUs

 Clever IDM can support OU modifications, which allow you to organize users based on data in Clever. The IDM format builder walks you through creating formats for sub-OUs. Please review our Clever IDM: Formulas article to learn more about using the format builder.

 

 To access the formula builder for Clever IDM:

 • Click on the Organize OUs step in the provisioning setup for Google or Active Directory.
• Click Edit in the OU for which you want to create a format. 
• Click Next step in the blue box on the bottom right to get to the Build your format step. 
• Click Build your format to create sub-OUs. To learn more about your options with building a format, review our Clever IDM: Formulas article . 
• You’ll have several options to build a format:
 • Block builder
 • Use a premade sub-OU format (can be edited)
• Build a sub-OU format using blocks
 

• Enter a format string (Advanced) 
 

• On the right, you can preview the data. If the format looks correct, click Save format . 
 
 Please note: The block builder approach for building formats doesn’t currently support extension fields or advanced function use. If you need to use your extension fields or write more complex functions that you know Clever supports but don’t see as an option, you’ll need to use the advanced option of entering a format string. See the Clever IDM: Formulas article for assistance with format strings. 

 

 If an error occurs with your format, you can view it in the red error box: 

 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever IDM: Google Setup 
• Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup

title: Clever SSO Policy
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. SSO Policy settings 3. Disallowed users 

 Overview

 Clever Single sign-on (SSO) allows your schools’ students, teachers, and staff to log in to Clever and access their connected applications. Once users log into Clever, they simply click on an application icon in their Portal and are logged in to the learning app automatically via SSO.

 For school districts that use Clever Passwords to log in as well as another Identity Provider (IDP), you can use our SSO Policy to assign which users can use Clever Passwords as a login method and which users must use another IDP. This can be helpful for making sure your users are logging in with the method that you want them to use. 

 
 SSO Policy settings

 If your district uses Clever Passwords and another IDP (such as Google or Active Directory) and you only want specific users to be able to use Clever Passwords, you can adjust this under Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > SSO Policy . 

 

 

 
 Disallowed users

 If a user who isn’t authorized to use Clever Passwords attempts to log in with Clever Passwords, they’ll receive an error message that indicates they need to use another configured login method. The message isn’t customizable and will recommend they use your other configured login method(s).
