title: Sharing Permissions: Unshare All Data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Share by rules 3. Share by schools, share by district, school site sharing 4. Restore previous sharing settings 

 

 Overview

 This article explains how a district can unshare all data with an application without disconnecting from the application. The instructions are specific to your sharing type, which can be viewed on the Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page. 

 
 Share by rules

 Please note these directions are for standard sharing. If using guided sharing, please switch sharing setup view to standard sharing.

 

 If sharing by rules:

 • Navigate to the application in Clever
• Click Edit Sharing 
• Click Edit Sharing Rules 
• Delete all existing sharing rules (Don't worry, you can restore your previous rules using these directions .)
• Click Add Another Rule 
• Click Custom Share by Student 
• Add the rule Student SIS ID > equals any of > X Please note: you will need to click Create "x" to add this value 
• Click Done Editing 
• Click Save Sharing Rules 
 
 
 Share by district, share by school, school site sharing

 If sharing by district, school, or school-site sharing, please follow these directions. (Don't worry, you can restore your previous rules using these directions .)

 • Navigate to the application in Clever
• Click Edit Sharing 
• In the top-left, click Change 
• Select Share by rules 
 Please note these directions are for standard sharing. If using guided sharing, please switch sharing setup view to standard sharing.

 

• Click Custom Share by Student 
• Add the rule Student SIS ID > equals any of > X Please note: you will need to click Create "x" to add this value 
• Click Done Editing 
• Click Save Sharing Rules 
 
 
 Restore previous sharing settings

 To restore previous sharing permissions;

 • Navigate to the application
• Click Past Rules 
• Locate the rule you would like to restore and click the dropdown
• Click Revert to these rules

title: Single sign-on (SSO): Adding Google SSO applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. Adding a Google SSO app 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 For schools and districts that have set up Clever as an IDP into Google, using our Google SSO apps can provide a better Google login experience than using the Saved Passwords version of those applications. Google SSO apps work on all devices. In this article, we’ll review the requirements and best practices for configuring Google SSO apps. 

 Which Google SSO apps are available

 • 
 Gmail

• 
 Google Calendar

• 
 Google Classroom

• 
 Google Drive

• 
 Google Docs

• 
 Google Keep

• 
 Google Meet

• 
 Google Sheets

• 
 Google Slides

 
 Interested in deep-diving into how to best use Clever and Google together? Check out our course, Clever & Google: Secure Connected Classroom Certification available in Clever Academy! 

 
 Requirements

 The Google SSO applications require configuring a SAML connection between your Clever account and your Google workspace. You can configure this SAML connection in 3 ways.

 • 
 Setting up Clever as the organization-wide SSO Provider in your Google Admin Console. Learn about this setup in our article Single Sign-On (SSO): Setting up Badges into Chromebooks and Chromebook logins with Clever as your organization-wide SSO Provider .

• Setting up Clever an SSO Profile in Google. Learn about this setup in our article Single Sign-On: Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever as an SSO Profile in Google .
• The Google Workspace app for districts with a Clever MFA subscription - see configuration instructions here: For Clever Admins: Clever MFA Google Workspace SAML Setup Guide .
 
 Setting up Clever as your organization-wide SSO provider is the simplest configuration. If you have not already set up one of the above SAML connections, you will need to do so before proceeding.

 If you configure more than one SAML connection for different users, ensure that the sharing permissions for the Chromebook Login for SSO Profiles or Google Workspace apps match the OU enrollment in your Google workspace. The individual Google SSO apps will use the same SAML connection that is configured for that user. Only share Google SSO apps with users who have a SAML connection. You can share the Google SSO apps with all users that have a working SAML connection regardless of which SAML connection that user utilizes.

 IMPORTANT If using on shared iOS devices with native Google apps, ensure that users explicitly log out of all Google accounts within the apps. If users do not explicitly log out, the Google session will remain accessible on the shared device within those apps. 

 

 
 Adding Google SSO applications

 Adding a Google SSO applications is easily done from your Clever Dashboard. 

 • Go to the Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page and search for the Google SSO application you’d like to add. Ensure that you are not selecting the Saved Passwords version of the application.
 
 

 • Send the integration request.
• Confirm that you have completed the SAML Connection.
• The application should now have the status of Launched in the Applications table. Please see this article to understand the application's status. Remember that your sharing rules for the application must include users in OUs managed by Clever in your Google Workspace in order for the logins to be successful. 
 
 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Why use Google SAML rather than a Saved Passwords version of the app? 

 • The SAML app can be a better choice because: 
 • 
 With the SSO version, you don’t have to set up the user's credentials. Users are automatically logged in when they click on the icon in their Clever Portal. 

• 
 With the SSO version, there is less room for error when users log in the first time.

• 
 SAML connections are a secure industry standard for performing SSO

 

 
 Is there ever a situation where we should choose the Saved Passwords version? 

 • No. Once configured, the Google SSO apps provide a superior experience. 
 
 Does this work with Clever's Google Workspace app, which is included with Clever MFA? 

 • Yes. These Google SSO apps will work for any users who use the Clever Google Workspace app to access Google. 
 
 
 Related articles: 

 • Single sign-on (SSO): Setting up Badges into Chromebooks and Chromebook logins with Clever as your organization-wide SSO Provider 
• Single sign-on (SSO): Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever as an SSO Profile in Google 
• Single Sign-On (SSO): Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever as an SSO Profile in Google 
• For Clever Admins: Clever MFA Google Workspace SAML Setup Guide 
• Single Sign-On (SSO): Chromebooks - Troubleshooting

title: Clever IDM: Exporting data to your SIS (also known as writeback)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Default fields 3. Setting up writeback 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM allows sync data export via download and SFTP from the User management > IDM > Exports tab .

 Using this feature, Clever IDM customers can write Clever IDM-generated data back to their SIS if their SIS can import CSV data. This provides another option for schools and can save Clever admin time.

 

 
 

 Default fields

 The default fields exported from Clever to write back are Google email or AD/Entra ID userPrincipalName and passwords for any destination. Clever IDM export files include rows for all IDM-managed accounts by user type. Other fields may be available depending on your need. Please reach out to Clever Support and we may be able to help.

 

 
 

 Setting up writeback

 Follow these steps for setting up writeback to your SIS:

 Steps 1-2 should be completed in Clever 

 • If exporting passwords, you must be using Clever IDM and have Allow password export enabled for each user type. This is configured in the Set login credentials step in the IDM wizard. 
 
 

 • Enable SFTP access for IDM exports under the Exports tab in IDM.
 
 

 

 Remaining steps should be completed in your SIS 

 The specific steps to follow will depend on your SIS. We’ve included general information for SISes we know support imports or writeback, but this is not an exhaustive list. If unsure, your best bet is to contact your SIS directly. 

 

 PowerSchool 

 The best way to set up writeback with PowerSchool is using their Data Import Manager (DIM), which supports automated scheduled imports. You can find DIM in PowerSchool by going to Data & Reporting > Imports. Learn more about PowerSchool DIM here .

 If you do not need to writeback the Global ID or email fields, you can also use AutoComm instead of DIM. Learn more about PowerSchool AutoComm here . Clever will deliver AutoComm compliant files to the SFTP box in addition to the standard export files.

 

 Infinite Campus 

 Reach out to the Infinite Campus Technical Services team to explore options for scheduled CSV imports.

 

 Alma 

 Alma doesn't support regular automated imports from files sent to an SFTP folder, but they do support manual import via their import tool. As long as it’s a field in Alma’s import library for that entity, a Clever-formatted CSV can be used. For more information, be sure to reach out to their team directly.

 

 If you know of any other SISes that support data imports, please let us know by contacting Clever Support and referencing this article! To contact Clever Support, click the chat icon in the bottom right corner and work through the prompts.

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Clever IDM: Overview

title: PowerSchool Plugin Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup • Troubleshooting Installation Errors 3. Updates 4. Customizations 5. School Year Rollover 6. Troubleshooting 7. Sync Errors 8. Default Field Mappings 

 

 Overview

 ✓ Runs every four hours ✓ Syncs all students with an active enrollment status ("enroll_status" = 0) ✓ Syncs teachers that are assigned to an active school and have an active staff status ("Status" = 1) ✓ Syncs co-teachers ✓ Syncs student contacts from PowerSchool v12.x (unlimited contacts). Please note, we highly recommend consolidating contacts prior to using the upgraded PowerSchool sync. More information on contacts can be found here . ✓ Works alongside Google and O365 SSO into PowerSchool. This allows SSO access to PowerSchool using Google or O365. It does not provide SSO to PowerSchool via Clever. More info here .) ✓ Sync users to the staff user role in Clever. Staff sync is available for districts that have set up the PowerSchool plugin sync option for the first time after February 2025. If you’ve been using the PowerSchool plugin sync prior to February 2025 and would like staff to be synced, please contact Clever Support to discuss potential options. ✓ Can sync the Central Office (school_id = 0) or any teachers assigned to this site upon request. Please reach out to the Clever Support team to get started. ✓ Can sync users to the staff user role in Clever upon request. This will require a new version of the plugin to be installed. Please reach out to the Clever Support team to get started. Alternatively, staff can be manually added to Clever using these directions . 

 

 

 
 Setup

 New to Clever? Please contact your district onboarding specialist for setup directions. If you have not yet connected with a district onboarding specialist, you can sign up here . If you have an existing Clever account and would like to transition to the PowerSchool plugin sync, please contact our support team. Please note, the process of changing sync types can take 2-3 weeks. We highly recommend reviewing the conversion process as outlined here . 

 ⚠️ IMPORTANT: If upgrading from the legacy PowerSchool Sync , please confirm the following before proceeding: • Your current PowerSchool sync is active (no sync status errors). If your sync is broken, the plugin will not successfully generate for your account. • There are active enrollments for the current academic year. • SSO into PowerSchool SIS using an identity provider (Google or O365) as outlined here has NOT been enabled. • After you've received confirmation from Clever Support that your conversion is complete, you can enable SSO if you'd like, as well as remove the Clever User utilized for your previous PowerSchool Auto Sync. • Student contacts have been consolidated and upgraded to Unlimited Contacts if you plan to sync them to Clever. More information on contacts can be found here .

 To securely install the upgraded PowerSchool sync, please follow these directions:

 Download the Plugin 

 The PowerSchool plugin allows Clever to connect to your PowerSchool Student Information System (SIS) to import data for Clever rostering and Single Sign-On (SSO) services.
 
 The plugin contains a secure token that is specific to your district. Do not share the plugin with individuals outside your district or transmit the plugin through insecure methods such as email.

 

 • Log in to the Clever Dashboard
• Navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page.
• Under PowerSchool Plugin , click Download PowerSchool Plugin to download to your local device
 
 

 Install the Plugin 

 • Log in to PowerSchool as an administrator.
• On the start page, choose System Management in the main menu on the left side.
• Under Server, click Plugin Configuration .
• Click Install .
• Under Plugin Installation File , click Choose File 
• Select the file named clever_plugin.zip from your device and click Open. (Do not unzip the file and install the .XML file) 
• In PowerSchool, click Install If the plugin installs correctly, it will take you back to the Plugin Management Dashboard. 
• A popup will appear requesting confirmation of registration. Click Register . The popup may take a moment to appear. Skipping this step will prevent a successful installation. 
• On the Plugin Management Dashboard, click the Enable/Disable checkbox next to the Clever Plugin, which will be named “District {{your District ID}}” (example: “District 5a2b7742d220a50001d0880f”) .
• In the sidebar that opens, click Enable . 
 
 🛑 STOP ! Be sure you Enabled the plugin in Steps 10 & 11! 
 
 If your account is using the legacy PowerSchool sync, do not disable the Clever Admin access user in PowerSchool until you have received email confirmation of the upgrade from the Clever team. Disabling access will block the transition process.

 If you are an existing Clever district, you will receive an email from our team once the transition is complete. The "Sync Type" on the SIS sync page will also be updated. If any additional actions are required our team will contact you via email. 🚨 Do NOT "Regenerate Client ID and Secret" for the plugin; it will break your sync . If you are informed that you need to regenerate the Client ID and Secret, please contact Clever Support.

 

 

 
 Troubleshooting Installation Errors

 
 
 Error(s)

 
 How to Fix

 
 "No active enrollments found for the new school year. Sync will resume once enrollments are published" The PowerSchool plugin sync is unable to find active enrollments for the current academic year. Clever automatically rolls over to the new school year on August 1st. To resolve the error, publish enrollments for the new academic year. 
 "Plug-in installation error" In the Clever Dashboard, click Download to generate the plugin. Once downloaded, install the plugin in PowerSchool. You can view and download your district's latest plug-in here: https://schools.clever.com/sync/settings . When installing the plug-in, please confirm:
 • You have clicked the enable button for the plug-in
• Any previous plug-in versions have been deleted
• You have installed the entire .zip file that downloaded from the Clever Dashboard (installing only the plugin.xml file will break the sync)
 
 
 "User passwords no longer exportable from PowerSchool" 
 Your district is using one or more legacy password fields that are no longer available to Clever. 

 In PowerSchool v23.5.0.0 and later , the following fields are no longer exportable: 

 • Students.Student_Web_Password
• Teachers.Password
• Teachers.TeacherLoginPW
 
 In order to sync passwords to Clever, create a custom field in PowerSchool to store password data. Once created and populated with data, contact Clever Support and let us know the exact name of the table. We will update your sync configuration to pull the data into Clever.

 
 "Update SSL certificate in order to continue syncing with Clever" Please update the SSL certificate for your PowerSchool account. For assistance with this process, please contact PowerSchool support. 
 "Registration was rejected by the publisher. Contact the publisher of this plugin for resolution. 
 Bad Request" 

 This error typically indicates that the registration token in the plugin has expired. Please reach out to Clever Support to have a new plugin generated. 
 
 

 

 Updates

 Clever’s team may need to provide an updated version of the plugin to ensure we meet your district’s needs. You do not need to disable PowerSchool SSO to update your plugin. If Clever has indicated you need to install an updated version of the plugin, you can update the plugin using these directions:

 • Download the updated plugin as described here .
• Log in to PowerSchool as an administrator.
• On the start page, choose System Management in the left sidebar.
• Under Server, click Plugin Configuration .
• Click Install .
• Under Plugin Installation File, click Choose File .
• Locate the existing upgraded sync - your plugin will have the name format: Distri

title: Sync Reports: Error messages
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Schools 3. Students 4. Teachers 5. Sections 6. Contacts / Student Contacts 7. Staff 

 

 Overview

 This article provides more details on the sync errors found in the error files downloadable from the Last Attempted Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. These errors indicate specific records that have failed to sync to Clever as a result of not meeting all requirements to successfully sync to Clever.

 

 

 

 Please note: It is common for the Last Attempted Sync page to display errors, and not all errors need to be resolved. In general, if an error is not impacting user access or causing downstream errors with applications, the error does not need to be resolved. Error counts may change and resolve over time as data changes in your student information system (SIS) throughout the school year. 

 

 Schools

 
 Error

 
 Description

 
 Duplicate school_id used School_id must be unique to each site. If this error occurs, confirm the school_id is a field in your SIS that is unique to each school site. See our article this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions and a video tutorial. 
 Missing required field Schools must have the following fields populated to process: 
 • School_id
• School_name
• School_number
 
 
 
 

 
 Students

 
 Error

 
 Description

 
 Missing required field Students must have the following fields populated to process: 
 • School_id
• Student_id
• Last_name
• First_name
 
 
 School "X" not listed in data syncing This error is rare for students. This indicates the School_id on the student's profile doesn't match a School_id in the Schools.csv file. To resolve the error: 
 • Confirm the student is enrolled at the correct school site
• Update export settings for the schools.csv to include the missing school 
 
 See our article this article for step-by-step directions and a video tutorial.

 
 
 

 
 Teachers

 
 Error

 
 Description

 
 Duplicate teacher_id used 
 Teacher_id must be unique to each teacher. Begin by confirming the teacher_id is not assigned to another teacher. If the teacher_id is NOT unique. Correct the user's teacher_id in your SIS and upload another data set to Clever.

 If the teacher_id is unique, this error is usually the result of the teacher being enrolled at two schools. Most SISs will export a user once per school site. Though this will prompt an error in the sync report, the teacher will successfully be processed into Clever and be connected to all their sections across both schools. As such, the error can be ignored.

See this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions. 
 Missing required field Teachers must have the following fields populated to process: 
 • School_id
• Teacher_id
• Last_name
• First_name
 
 
 School "X" not listed in data syncing. This indicates the School_id on the teacher's profile doesn't match a School_id in the Schools.csv file. To resolve the error: 
 • Confirm the teacher is enrolled at the correct school site
• Update export settings for the schools.csv to include the missing school 
 
 See this article for step-by-step directions for troubleshooting.

 This error is common for PowerSchool districts. Please see "Missing Staff" in this article for more information on resolving this error. 

 
 
 

 
 Sections

 
 Error

 
 Description

 
 Duplicate section_id used 
 Section_id must be unique to each section. This error is usually an indicator that an alternate field from the SIS should be used for section_id to ensure the value is unique. If a globally unique identifier (GUID) is not available, consider combining two fields to create a unique value. Example: "section_id" + "course_number" + "period"

See this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions. 
 Missing required field Sections must have the following fields populated to process: 
 • School_id
• Section_id
• Teacher_id
 
If Teacher_id is missing, it is usually the result of a missing section assignment in the SIS. This can often occur before the beginning of a school year when teacher hiring and assignments are being finalized. The error will resolve once enrollments are assigned. 
 School "X" not listed in the data syncing This error is rare for sections. This indicates the School_id on the section's profile does not match a School_id in the Schools.csv file. To resolve the error: 
 • Confirm the section is enrolled at the correct school site
• Update export settings for the schools.csv to include the missing school 
 
 See this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions.

 
 
 No students

 
 Sections must have at least one (1) active student enrolled in order to process. This error is common prior to a term when enrollments are still being finalized. This error can also occur if school_id, section_id, or student_id is not consistent across CSV files. 

 See this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions.

 
 
 Student XXX is not listed in the data syncing.

 An enrollment was included in the enrollment.csv for student_id: XXXX but the student_id: XXXX does not exist in Clever 
 
 

 
 Contacts / Student Contacts

 
 Error

 
 Description

 
 Duplicate identifier 
 Contact_sis_id must be unique to each contact. This error is usually an indicator that an alternate field from the SIS should be used for contact_sis_id to ensure the value is unique. Contact_sis_id is optional. If a unique id is not available, we recommend removing contact_sis_id from the students.csv

See this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions. 
 Missing required field Student contacts must have the following fields populated to process: 
 • Contact_name
• Contact_type
 
 
 
 

 
 Staff

 
 Error

 
 Description

 
 Missing required field Staff must have the following fields populated to process: 
 • School_id
• Staff_id
• Staff_email
• First_name
• Last_name
 
 
 School "X" not listed in data syncing This indicates the School_id on the staff member's profile does not match a School_id in the Schools.csv file. To resolve the error: 
 • Confirm the staff member is enrolled at the correct school site
• Update export settings for the schools.csv to include the missing school 
 
 See this article for step-by-step troubleshooting directions.

title: For Clever Admins: Troubleshoot Sharing tool
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Using the tool 3. Match types 

 

 Getting started with the Troubleshoot Sharing tool? Check out our Product Spotlight: Troubleshoot Sharing Tool available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 The "Troubleshoot sharing" tool is the perfect utility for quickly identifying why or why not a user is shared with an application. The tool is available to Clever Admins and District Help Desk users. You can find the Troubleshoot Sharing tool in two places:

 1. From the Clever Dashboard, navigate to Support tools > Troubleshoot sharing 

 2. You can also access the tool from a student or teacher profile.

 • From a student profile, click Student Tools > Troubleshoot user sharing 
• From a teacher profile, click Teacher Tools > Troubleshoot user sharing 
• From a teacher profile, click Teacher Tools > Troubleshoot user sharing 
 
 

 
 The Troubleshoot sharing tool can be used to troubleshoot sharing for the following record types: 

 Students

 Teachers

 Staff

 The tool does not support troubleshooting sharing for the following record types: 

 District Admins

 Sections

 

 
 Using the tool

 1. Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Support tools > Troubleshoot sharing tool 2. Select a user using Clever ID, SIS ID, Name, or Email and select the Application Name 

 The tool will immediately check if the user is shared

 3. Review the Sharing Status 

 The user is not shared with the application. As a result, they will not have an account created in the application nor will see the application in their Clever Portal. 

 The user is shared with the application. If the application supports rostering, the student will have an account created in the application. If the application supports Single Sign-On (SSO), the user will see the application in their Clever Portal. 

 4. Review the Next Steps 

 Below the sharing status, Clever will provide recommendations on how to share or unshare an application with a user.

 

 The two most common adjustments are: Option 1 (RECOMMENDED): Update the user in your student information system (SIS). This usually involves enrolling the student in a specific section or school in your SIS. The most common issue is the user is not enrolled in a section with a specific subject or grade level. Option 2: Update the app's sharing rules If a user is not shared, you can always update or add a sharing rule that matches the properties for that user. Directions for updating sharing rules can be found here . 

 

 
 Match types

 The Troubleshoot Sharing tool will run an analysis across all sharing rules for the selected application. For each rule, it will return one of the following match types. A user will be shared with an application if at least one (1) rule is a "Matching" rule. 

 
 Match type

 
 Description

 
 Matching The user matches all the rule's requirements to be shared with the application. Matching rules are outlined in green. Example: The user is associated with a school that is shared. Since this satisfies all criteria for the rule, the user is shared. 
 Partial Matching 
 The user matches some, but not all, the rule's requirements to be shared with the application. Example: This user is associated with a school that is shared, is enrolled in a section grade is 3-6 , but is not enrolled in a section that section name contains 'math' . Since the user does not meet all criteria, the user is not shared via the rule. 

 
 Non-Matching The user does not match any of the rule's requirements to be shared with the application Example: This user is not associated with a section at a school listed. Since the user does not meet any criteria, the user is not shared via the rule.

title: Clever + Imagine Learning Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup Process • Recommended Sharing 3. Required Fields 4. FAQs 5. Support Contacts 

 Overview

 Use this guide to help connect to Imagine Learning applications.

 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup or by accepting your invitation from Imagine Learning. For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide .
 
 Important: Imagine Learning has several apps on Clever. Please ensure you connect to the correct application(s) your district has purchased. 

 
 Application Name

 
 Icon

 
 Description

 
 Imagine Math Imagine Math is a research-based, adaptive, online supplemental math solution suite for students in Pre-K through Geometry. Through personalized learning paths driven by the Quantile® Framework, Imagine Math engages students with motivational contests and charitable donations, intentionally scaffolds learning for grade-level success, and provides on-demand access to live, certified math teachers when students need it most. 
 Imagine Math Facts Imagine Math Facts teaches fluency in addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to elementary and middle school students through game-like activities with built-in rewards in an immersive 3-D world—helping each student gain math automaticity. 
 Imagine Language & Literacy Imagine Language & Literacy teaches foundational reading, reading comprehension, and language development skills to Pre-K to Grade 6 students through engaging digital activities, personalized learning paths, and offline resources. 
 Imagine Español Imagine Español teaches Spanish foundational reading, reading comprehension, and language development skills to Spanish-speaking students in Pre-K to Grade 2 through engaging digital activities and offline resources. 
 Imagine Reading Imagine Reading teaches reading comprehension, academic discourse, and information synthesis skills to Grade 3 to 8 students through media-rich text sets (for online close reading), teacher-mediated discussion materials, and offline group project guidelines. 
 Imagine Lectura Imagine Lectura builds strong readers, critical thinkers, and confident biliterate communicators through the study of high-quality, authentic Spanish language text sets with students in grades 3-5. 
 Galileo K-12 Galileo is a Reading/ELA benchmark assessment that assesses students 3 times per year to appropriately place them on their individual instructional path. This comprehensive suite offers real-time reporting tools to reliably track student growth and provide the data to support effective teaching and learning, resulting in high student achievement against college and career readiness standards. 
 
 

 

 • If your district is already on Clever, you can connect Imagine Learning’s applications by accepting your invite OR following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select: Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for Imagine Learning
• You will then be brought to a page where you can authorize your Imagine Learning request. As Clever SSO and rostering for Imagine Learning is provided at no cost to the district, you can mark that it has already been purchased for the district.
• You will also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the Imagine Learning icons in their Clever portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request!
 

 
 Once you have shared data with Imagine Learning, the Imagine Learning team will set up the initial sync and will reach out to you to discuss communicating with teachers. 

 

 Recommended Sharing 

 Before sharing: 

 • Make sure to match product applications with purchased license counts. 
• Ensure that students and teachers are in their correct grade levels and that classes are updated for the current school year in your Student Information System (SIS).
 
 Imagine Learning recommends that you “Share by Rules” in Clever (you can find more info about “Share by Rules” here ). Create a sharing rule by following these steps:

 

 • Access the Sharing Permissions page for Imagine Learning. To do this, log in to your Clever account and click the icon for Imagine Learning in the sidebar. From the 'Data Sharing' tab, click Share by Rules.
• Configure your sharing rules by limiting sharing to only the schools, and students that should be synced, and by only sharing sections with the appropriate subject.
 
 • For Literacy products we recommend “Homeroom/Advisory” or “English/Language Arts”.
• For Math products we recommend “Homeroom/Advisory” or “Math”.
 
 Important: To avoid delays in product access, Imagine Learning must receive your updated and completed sharing rules at least 7 business days prior to your target launch date. 

 
 

 Required Fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. Imagine Learning requires the following data fields be sent: Student:

 • name
• district
• school
• sis_id and/or student_number
• credentials
• grade
 
 Teacher/School Admin/District admin:

 • name
• district
• school(s)
• email
 
 *A few things to keep in mind for Math Products: 

 • In order for teachers to see students, and for student accounts to function properly, students must be assigned to a classroom.
• Each educator should only be shared as a teacher or staff or a district admin. Accounts shared in more than one role may have the wrong role assigned in-product. 
• Teachers can only be assigned to one school.
• Students should only be shared with one math class, where possible. 
• Co-teachers are not supported in sections. 
 
 
 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data error”? 

 • A data error occurs when the record is incompatible with Imagine Learning’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Errors must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. Reach out to Clever Support if you have questions about how to resolve your data error.
• A data warning occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning.
 
 Why is a student or teacher missing from Imagine Learning? Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Imagine Learning:

 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
• Is shared with Imagine Learning in your data sharing permissions
 
 Please note, for the Math suite, a user may be missing because they are not enrolled or they are assigned to more than one school. What if a teacher (and/or student) already has an Imagine Learning account outside of Clever? When existing Imagine Learning users (both teachers and students) login through Clever, they will be prompted to type in their previous Imagine Learning username and password. Once a user's account has been linked to Clever, any progress previously made on the account will show up when they log in through Clever. Can teachers and students be assigned to multiple school locations? The Math suite only supports educators and students in one school. The Literacy suite supports educators in multiple schools, but not students. Can multiple teachers/staff be assigned to the same course/section? Imagine Learning can support multiple teachers or staff assigned to the same course/section. Are staff supported? Yes, Imagine Learning supports staff with Clever. Who should I ask for help with an issue? If it’s related to data within Clever, or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever. If you are r

title: Clever IDM: Sync reports & issue troubleshooting
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Tasks 3. Events 4. Troubleshooting tips for destination-specific issues 5. Reports 6. Email Notifications 7. Email not updating in Clever 

 Overview 

 This article runs through how the Clever IDM sync works and how to identify and resolve issues with your sync. By default, IDM syncs occur once per day, in the evening hours. At that time, Clever will compare the data in Clever to the accounts in Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID and create, update, or archive users appropriately. 

 • Users added to Clever will have accounts created in Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID
• Users deleted from Clever will have their accounts moved to the Archive OU in Google/Active Directory. In Entra ID, the account will be disabled.
• Users updated in Clever will be updated in Google, Active Directory and/or Entra ID if their user or organizational (e.g., school or grade level) attributes are changed.
 
 If you would like to customize your district’s IDM sync time or frequency, contact Clever support.

 
 Tasks 

 The Tasks tab contains a list of issues that need to be resolved for users. Below are the possible tasks and the actions required to resolve them: 

 
 Task

 
 Detail

 
 Action

 
 # user/s has/have unsupported characters and their account/s could not be created. These users have unsupported characters in fields used to create their email addresses and were not created in Google/AD/Entra. Contact support to resolve users that have unsupported characters. 
 # user(s) has missing fields and their account(s) could not be created These users have missing fields in your SIS used to create their email addresses and were not created in Google/AD/Entra. Please contact support for a list of users with empty fields. 
 [User email] already exists in Google/AD/Entra and their accounts could not be created. The email already exists in Google/AD/Entra, but has not been matched to an existing Clever user. 
 If this Google/AD/Entra user is the same as a Clever user, select “Yes match users” If this user is different from an existing Google/AD/Entra user, select “no, create a new account” which will allow you to add a custom email address for this user.

 

 
 # of users already exist in Google/AD/Entra and their accounts could not be created. There are more than 10 users that already exist in Google/AD/Entra, but have not been matched to an existing Clever user. Contact support to match more than 25 users. 
 
 

 
 Events

 After your initial Clever IDM sync, you can continue to track changes to your users via the User management > Clever IDM > Events tab . On this tab, you can select a date range for which you’d like to review Clever IDM creates, updates, archives, and matched users. You can also apply filters to examine changes by destination (if you have multiple configured), event type (create, update, etc.), or specific users by name or SIS ID.

 Once you find the event for the user and destination you’d like to review, click the dropdown arrow to examine more details.

 

 If you find that Clever IDM is managing a user in an unexpected manner, and you need help troubleshooting, please contact Clever Support .

 Please see the section below on Sync history reports if you’re interested in downloading CSV files to review your Clever IDM sync changes.

 
 Troubleshooting tips for destination-specific issues

 Below are descriptions of possible issues you may see in your Events or Sync History reports. Please click the dropdown menu for the destination you'd like to review issues for.

 

 
 Google Issues 
 

 
 Issue

 
 Description

 
 Resolution

 
 Conflict with existing email address (conflictWithExistingUser) The user's email in Clever does not match the expected Google email, but an existing Google email match is found, however, we don't have an existing link between the Clever user and Google user. There are 3 options to resolve this:
 1. On the Tasks tab, click Yes, match user. This will create a link between the user’s Clever account and their Google account. 

 2. On the Tasks tab, click No. You will be prompted to enter a custom email for the user.

 3. Contact support. This option is required if there are more than 25 Conflicts.

 
 conflictWithExistingGoogleUser The email already exists in Google, but has not been matched to an existing Clever user. Options for resolving:
 • 
 Manually add the email to the sis_email field for the user.

• 
 Click “Yes, match” in the task section of the IDM Dashboard.

• 
 Contact support. This option is required if there are more than 25 conflicts.

 
 
 Clever user already has this email address (conflictWithAssignedEmail) Two Clever users have the same email, or an alias for the user in Conflict exists on a different user. Ensure each Clever user has a unique email, or remove the conflicting alias from the target Google account 
 email: unrecognized identifier: email The user doesn’t have a value for the email field set in Clever. To resolve this issue, you can update the email value in the SIS. 
 Formula (template) Unable to compute template for a user's Google attribute. The user is missing an attribute required in a template. e.g. If the template error is on "OU" and the error is on student.graduation_year, please ensure you are syncing graduation_year for that student from the SIS. 
 Incorrect formula format IDM is not able to complete the action described by the template. This often occurs because there is a missing field, group, etc. Check the Description column of the Issues Report for more specific information about what is missing. 
 password: could not compute password for user Users could not be created due to password template not being available in Clever data Check the password template. What fields are in the template? If those fields do not exist on the affected user(s), the district must add those fields in the SIS.

 
 unsafe archive This shows when you have more than 20% of users in a user type are flagged for archival. This is shown to prevent accidental archival. Click Archive in the IDM Dashboard under the Tasks tab if you do intend to archive more than 20% of users. 
 googleapi: Error 500: Internal error encountered., backendError Undetermined Push a manual IDM sync to resolve, or contact Clever Support. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 Active Directory Issues 
 

 
 Issue

 
 Description

 
 Resolution

 
 Conflict with existing email address (conflictWithExistingUser) The user's email in Clever does not match the expected Active Directory email, but an existing Active Directory match is found, however, we don't have an existing link between the Clever user and Active Directory user. There are 3 options to resolve this:
 1. On the Tasks tab, click Yes, match user. This will create a user association in clever

 2. On the Tasks tab, click No. You will be prompted to enter a custom email for the user.

 3. Contact support. This option is required if there are more than 25 conflicts.

 
 Conflict with existing Active Directory email address (conflictWithExistingADMail) User conflicts with a target Active Directory account having a different UserPrincipalName, but the target mail attribute matches the user. If the user does match the target Active Directory entry, click 'Yes, match user.'" If not, remove the Active Directory mail from the target entry so the user can be assigned.

 
 Clever user already has this Active Directory email address (conflictWithAssignedADMail) Two Clever users have the same email, or an alias for the user in conflict exists on a different user. Ensure each Clever user has a unique email, or remove the conflicting alias from the target Active Directory account 
 conflictWithAssignedADSamAccountName Two Clever users would have the same ADSamAccountName, preventing them from syncing to AD. To resolve this issue, ensure users will have unique ADSamAccountNames based on Clever data. 
 conflictWithExistingADSamAccountName A user

title: Clever products: Purchasing, billing, and renewals
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Purchasing 3. Billing & Invoices 4. Renewals 

 Overview 

 This article describes the purchasing flow for paid Clever products, including Clever IDM and Classroom MFA. If you’d like a general overview of each product see below:

 • Clever IDM: Overview 
• For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Overview 
 
 

 Please note: If you’re purchasing with a Purchase Order, we’ll email you the quote associated with your purchase at the end of the process for your district to utilize to create your Purchase Order. 

 

 
 Purchasing

 This section describes the purchasing flow for Clever products.

 From the Clever Dashboard > Billing page , you can view subscription costs, and related product information. You can also complete a request via the relevant area of the Dashboard (i.e., if you'd like to purchase Clever IDM, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > User Management > IDM page). For questions related to purchasing or terms, please contact Clever Support.

 

 1. Select licenses 

 Before purchasing a Clever product, identify how many users you’ll need licenses for. Please note that both Clever Classroom IDM and Classroom MFA require a minimum purchase of 1,000 user licenses.

 

 
 

 2. Pricing summary 

 View the pricing and determine the subscription length. By default, the subscription length is one year. You can also choose a two or three-year subscription in this step, which may come with a discount.

 

 
 3. Payment method 

 Select your payment method:

 Credit card Enter the credit card details and mailing address. Select your district’s tax status. Click Review your order. Purchase order To complete your order with a Purchase order select Purchase order and enter your district’s billing zip code and tax status.

 

 
 4. Review your order 

 Confirm the order details. If everything is correct: 

 Credit card Select Pay . Your card will be charged the amount listed on the invoice. Purchase order Confirm the quote information is correct and click Review your quote .

 

 
 5. Purchase complete (for credit card orders) or Quote emailed (for Purchase orders) 

 Credit card Your purchase is complete and you can proceed with setting up your product! If you indicated your district is tax exempt, you’ll receive an email reminder to upload your tax exemption form. Purchase order 

 The email with your quote will contain a Submit purchase order button. Additionally, you can navigate to view your in progress order here . Clicking that will bring you to a page where you can upload a copy of your PO and add a billing contact.

 When you click Submit purchase order on that page, we’ll activate or renew your product subscription (giving you access to the tool) and send an email to the billing contact you provided with instructions on how to pay. The billing contact will receive a link to the payment page, where you complete the payment process via credit card, bank transfer, or check.

 If you indicated that your district is tax exempt, you’ll receive an email reminder to upload your tax exemption form.

 

 
 Billing & Invoices 

 
 

 
 

 
 Unit Price 
 Clever IDM 

 For more information on pricing, please contact our team here .

 Discounts may be available for multi-year subscriptions

 

 Clever Classroom MFA 

 For more information on pricing, please contact our team here .

 Discounts may be available for multi-year subscriptions

 
 Payment Payment is required for total users before implementation. 
 Billing frequency Annual 
 Overages Overages up to 10% are not billed. If the total number of users managed by Clever IDM or Classroom MFA is over 10% of the paid amount, the district will receive an invoice with the difference at the end of the year. 
 Payment methods supported Credit card, ACH Payment, Check 
 Related terms Section 6 of the Additional terms for Schools 
 
 

 You can view your orders and subscription information by navigating from the Clever Dashboard > Billing page. Here, you’ll find the following information:

 • Invoice due date
• Subscription information
• Invoice total and cost breakdown
• Total licenses
• Invoice recipient
 
 If you need to update the invoice recipient for your account, please contact Clever Support for assistance.

 

 
 

 Renewals

 Renewing your Clever product subscription is easy! Before the end of your current subscription, you’ll receive notifications via email and in your Clever Dashboard .

 

 An example of product renewals appearing in the Dashboard Tasks section. Click the renew button in the email or Clever Dashboard to begin renewing. The renewal process is identical to the purchasing process described above. You’ll see a quote to renew your subscription based on the number of users currently managed by Clever IDM and/or Clever Classroom MFA and the subscription length. You can select a subscription length of one, two, or three years. Then, you’ll choose your desired payment method. Once you complete your renewal purchase, your subscription will be extended.

 You can also renew multiple products simultaneously, even if their subscription periods vary. For example, if you utilize Clever IDM and Premium Support, and both products are due for renewal, you can select to renew both subscriptions. Click the renew button for one of the products, and you’ll see the following screen:

 

 When you select Edit renewal subscriptions, you can check a box to indicate whether you’d like to renew both products together or only one product, then click Confirm selection. 

 

 Once you confirm which products you want to renew, you can edit the license count or add additional users. For example, you can edit the license count by adding teacher licenses to your subscription even if you only use Clever IDM for staff and students currently.

 

 Once you confirm your licenses for the upcoming subscription period, you’ll continue through the purchasing steps described above (pricing summary, payment method, order review, and completion of purchase).

 You can renew multiple subscriptions at any time, even if they have different expiration dates. Your Pricing summary will show that Clever prorates the cost based on your existing prescription periods. Renewing multiple products can help ensure they’re on the same renewal cycle, making them easier to manage.

 

 If you have questions or need help adjusting your subscription, please contact our Clever Support team by clicking the chat icon in the corner of this screen.

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever IDM: Overview 
• For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Overview

title: Clever + TalkingPoints: Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Required Data 2. Setup Process 

 We’re excited that you’ve chosen to partner with TalkingPoints via Clever! Follow the steps here to get started.

 Required Data

 
 Record Type

 
 Required Fields

 
 Students 
 • First name
• Last name
• Home language
• ELL status (optional)
 
 
 Parent / Guardian 
 • First name
• Last name
• Phone number 
 
 
 Teachers / Staff 
 • First name
• Last name
• Work email address 
 
 
 Classes 
 • Student rosters
• Teacher rosters 
 
 
 
 Setup Process

 1. Connect with TalkingPoints on Clever 

 The TalkingPoints team will send an invitation to connect with your district to your Clever account. Admins can accept the connection request by following Clever’s instructions here: For District Admins: Apps - App invites. 

 If your district has not yet been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup. 

 You can also connect by requesting TalkingPoints via our Clever Dashboard. Log in and select Applications > Add Applications . Search TalkingPoints and click Request application. 

 

 

 2. Add the required data to Clever 

 Important: TalkingPoints requires home_lanugage and student contact fields in order to successfully integrate. Please read on for information on how to adjust your sync if you’re not already sending this data to Clever. 

 Depending on your Clever data sync, you may need to adjust the sync to include home language data and student contact data. 

 

 • District-managed SFTP sync : Per Clever’s SFTP instructions , you can adjust your SFTP sync to include home language and contact data at any time. 
 
 • SIS-managed SFTP sync : Reach out to your SIS partner directly to add home language data and student contact data to your Clever sync.
 
 • Clever-managed Auto Sync : Contact Clever support to discuss modifying your sync.
 
 • Unsure how you sync data to Clever? Contact Clever Support . If you are not able to send home_language to Clever via your sync, you may also use Custom Data to upload these fields directly in your dashboard. To learn more, see instructions for Custom Data: Students . 
 
 3. Share data with TalkingPoints. 

 TalkingPoints recommends sharing all records within the district. More information regarding sharing data can be found here .

 4. TalkingPoints reviews your data for all required information If there is any missing information, TalkingPoints will contact your school district and Clever to make the necessary changes. 5. TalkingPoints begins setting up your district’s TalkingPoints account. 

 Please note: • TalkingPoints syncs with Clever every night and will update new data to your TalkingPoints account as part of this process. • You can manually add staff who are not in your SIS to TalkingPoints directly (ex: after school coordinators). • Home languages can be edited directly in TalkingPoints. The Clever sync will not override manual edits. 

 

 Support Contacts

 If the issue is related to data within Clever or coming from Clever, contact Clever Support . If the issue is related to functionality within TalkingPoints, please contact your TalkingPoints Partner Success representative.

title: For Teachers: Clever + Google Classroom
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is Google Classroom roster import? 3. How does Classroom roster import work? 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 Overview

 With Google Classroom roster import, you can get straight to learning without worrying about the extra work that goes into getting your students set up in their Google Classroom courses! Once your school sets up the application via Clever, you will no longer need to create classes manually in Google Classroom. Your Classrooms will automatically be created based on your SIS and Clever data. 

 
 What is Google Classroom roster import?

 Google Classroom roster import is an application allowing your district to connect data from Clever to your Google Classroom accounts. This means your Google Classroom course names will match Clever section names, and rosters will update automatically as you or your students change classrooms. We save you time by taking these time-consuming tasks off your plate! 

 
 How does Google Classroom roster import work?

 Google Classroom roster import will be connected to Clever by your district administrator. Once a sync has completed your Google Classroom accounts will be updated with course data that exists within Clever. You will then have the option to accept or decline Clever-created courses in Classroom. Please note that courses will appear in Google Classroom once your district administrator(s) sync the classes for the term. This will typically be a few days before the start of instruction for students. Keep an eye out for these suggested synced classes and be sure to click Accept ! 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Can I still manually create courses in Classroom? Will existing courses that I created be overwritten by Clever-created courses? 

 • Teachers can still create courses and will have the option to decline any Clever-created courses and continue managing class rosters manually. Any courses that are created outside the Clever sync will not be edited or deleted by the Clever sync.
 
 Can I add and remove students from Clever-created courses? 

 • Yes! The sync will not overwrite your changes.
 
 Can I edit the Classroom names that Clever assigned? 

 • Yes! The sync will not overwrite your changes.
 
 Will co-teachers be able to access Clever-created courses? Can I add co-teachers to Clever-created courses? 

 • Yes, co-teachers are synced from the Clever data. Primary teachers can also manually add and remove co-teachers on their Clever-created courses in Google Classroom. Co-teachers will not be able to view courses until the primary teacher has accepted the course and it's in an active state.
 
 What happens if I accidentally decline a Clever-created course? 

 • The district admins who manage your district's Clever account can restore courses that you accidentally declined.
 
 I'm seeing a lot of courses from other terms: can those be removed? 

 • You can either archive or decline the courses that you do not want to manage in Google Classroom.
 
 Is it possible to merge existing classes? 

 • This is currently not supported, but we'll update this page if that changes!
 
 

 
 Additional Resources

 • Check out this training slide deck for more information about what teachers can expect!

title: Clever + Google Classroom: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Getting Started 

 
 Overview

 This article is a step-by-step walkthrough for setting up Google Classroom roster import via Clever. 

 • For an overview of the capabilities of Google Classroom roster import, please see this article . 
• For detailed requirements and troubleshooting, please see this article . 
 
 Google Classroom roster import helps you securely sync rosters from any SIS and automatically create and update courses in Google Classroom. It is available as an app in the Clever app gallery for any US or Canadian district using Google Standard Education or Google Workspace for Education Plus for staff and students. Your district’s Google Workspace Super Administrator will need to help set up Google Classroom roster import.

 

 If you'd like step-by-step directions, teacher training resources, and more, check out our Quick Start Guide on Google Classroom roster import in Clever Academy !

 

 
 

 Getting Started

 Once you've had the Google Classroom roster import app added to your Clever Dashboard, here’s how to proceed. Please note that approval of your request to connect with the app is automated, and can take around 24 hours to be approved , so please factor this into your timeline for getting set up! To begin, access the setup wizard from the Google Classroom roster import app overview page : Step 1: Review details and FAQs about Clever’s Google Classroom roster import integration. Step 2: Preview your data. You need student and teacher email addresses in Clever in order to utilize this integration. If email addresses are not stored in your SIS, you will need to manually upload them via csv file . 

 Google Classroom roster import uses the following fields to create and roster courses in Google Classroom:

 • students : email, Clever section id
• teachers : email, Clever section id
• sections: name, period, sis id, Clever section id
• optional: additional fields used in naming the course, see Step 4 for more information
 
 Step 3: Sign in to connect your Google account. Please note that a Google Workspace Super Administrator must complete this setup. The Super Admin whose token is being used needs to have an EduPlus license assigned. Step 4: Configure course names. By default, course names and periods in Google Classroom are set by your section name and period in Clever. If you think the default name would be unclear to teachers, you can modify the naming convention using our course naming tool. This allows you to configure how courses should be named in Google Classroom by using the fields available in Clever. 

 Any fields selected must be populated with data or it will result in an error. In the example below, a school district chose Course Number , which isn't a required field in Clever. Since this isn't required, the warning box will appear, and if there is an error with any field, it will show in the preview on the right. Additionally, once courses are created and accepted by teachers, the course names will not be updated dynamically again unless a teacher manually changes the course name in Google Classroom.

 

 

 Step 5: Enable ‘Clever Roster Import’ in the Google Admin console and confirm this step is complete within your Clever dashboard. Step 6: Set your sharing permissions to indicate which users should be connected to Google Classroom roster import. You can roster your entire district, select schools, or even specific users. Please see more about Clever sharing permissions here . Many districts prefer to share only the current term's sections to avoid confusion for their teachers. See the course rollover article for more information. Step 7: Schedule your access dates for teachers and students. Teachers will see courses when the teacher access date arrives, allowing them to prepare for the upcoming semester. Students will be added to courses on the student access date, allowing them to see the courses teachers have accepted. Data will sync for each user type at 12:30 AM PST/3:30 am EST on the access date input in the application’s settings. If you ever need to go back and modify your configurations, including your first sync date, in the setup wizard, you can go back to it by clicking on this link on the overview page: Step 8: Finished! Google Classroom courses will be created based on your Clever data. When teachers log into Google Classroom, they’ll have the option to approve or reject the Clever-created courses. Teachers will need to 'accept' the courses in order to have them syncing automatically!

 

 • For detailed requirements and troubleshooting, please see this article . 
• If you need further assistance with the setup, please reach out to Clever Support here 
• If you’re interested in learning more about the teacher experience or sharing information with your district’s teachers, please see this Help Center article .
• For an overview of the capabilities of the Google Classroom roster import application, please see this article .
• For an overview of handling summer or semester rollover, including how to communicate with teachers, see this article .

title: Clever's Business Model
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Clever’s digital learning platform 2. Clever IDM 3. Classroom MFA 

 

 

 Clever’s digital learning platform

 Clever’s digital learning platform, which 77% of US K-12 districts use to connect to their digital learning resources, is and always will be free for schools globally. The free digital learning platform includes rostering, single sign-on, the Clever Portal, and Analytics all in one place. We believe that technology has the ability to empower teachers, create new ways to learn, and improve education outcomes for all students. And we believe everyone - all students - should be able to access EdTech quickly and securely. Our innovative business model is why we can offer Clever’s digital learning platform to districts for free. We’re grateful to application partners who use and pay Clever for secure, automated rostering so that educators and students can seamlessly log in to their digital learning resources for free. We also work with our application partners to ensure their pricing is not higher for districts that use Clever. We continue to provide world-class training and support at no cost. This includes self-paced, on-demand training for district administrators, school tech leads, and teachers on how to get started with Clever. Plus, find documentation customized for your role in our help center, or open a ticket with our dedicated support team. Clever gives educators transparency, visibility, and control over data sharing . Student data is used only for educational purposes at the direction of the district - Clever never sells student data. 

 
 Clever IDM

 Districts can add Clever IDM to their free Clever digital learning platform to automate identity management to help keep Google and Active Directory in sync with their SIS. We heard districts telling us they were looking for account provisioning solutions and wondered if Clever could help solve this problem. That’s why we developed Clever IDM to provide a simple way to manage accounts. For more information on pricing, please contact our team here . We’re proud to offer Clever IDM at a fraction of the total cost of alternative identity management solutions because we want to be able to support all Districts in having access to improve their user security. 

 
 Classroom MFA

 Districts can strengthen the security of their free Clever accounts by adding Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)—no second device required. Classroom MFA protects applications that use Clever for single sign-on, using age-appropriate second factors like Clever Badges or login pictures. This applies to the Clever Portal, Google Workspace, and Google Chromebooks.

 We designed Classroom MFA to help districts stay ahead of cybersecurity threats without disrupting learning. For more information on pricing, please contact our team here .

 We’re proud to offer an MFA solution built specifically for the classroom—no extra devices needed, making it easy for both teachers and students to use.

title: Clever IDM: Google Setup
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup • Connect to Google • Select your IDM Management Level • Select Clever Users • Data written to Google • Group Membership • Summary • Preview and provision • Events 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM helps districts automate account provisioning and lifecycle management for Microsoft Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID, and Google Workspace. This affordable new offering provides school districts with a simple workflow that can be completed in minutes to automate organizational unit (OU) and account management directly with Clever. Clever IDM automatically syncs users from your SIS to Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google daily for an entire year. Your Clever IDM dashboard can be found here . A full overview of Clever IDM and the setup process can be viewed below:

 
 

 

 
 Setup 

 You can work through setting up your IDM configuration at any time; however, you won’t be able to complete the setup to start provisioning accounts without review from an onboarding specialist or our support team. Our team must review your setup before your first provision to protect your destination accounts and prevent issues with SSO for your end users. Contact your onboarding specialist for configuration review. If you're not sure who to contact, reach out to Clever Support .

 Click on the following links to find information about: • Setting IDM login credentials • Organizing OUs • Building sub-OUs with the IDM format builder 

 

 Connect to Google 

 In order to provision accounts, Clever must be granted access to manage the provisioning of users on your domain. The user authorizing the access request must have the ability to grant the following permissions: 

 • View and manage the provisioning of users on your domain
• View and manage organization units on your domain
• View domains related to your customers
 
 

 Connect to Google troubleshooting

 If you encounter either of the following errors (screenshots), please ensure that the Google Account you are connecting to Clever has the right level of access. For more information, see: For Clever Admin: Minimum Google Admin access required to set up Clever IDM .

 

 
 

 Select your IDM Management Level 

 

 Next, choose whether you’d like to set up Full Provisioning and Password Management or Password Management Only . Both options support Identity Management in Clever—it just depends on what works best for your district’s timing and goals.

 

 Full Provisioning and Password Management 

 This option automates account security and password management through Clever IDM. Since it requires more setup time, many districts choose this path at the beginning of the school year or during a long break. It’s best for:

 • 
 Automatically provisioning, updating, and archiving user accounts

• 
 Configuring password reset, account recovery, and account claiming settings

• 
 Building and maintaining OUs and groups from your SIS data

 
 

 Password Management Only 

 This lighter setup gives you access to Clever’s secure password management tools for matched users, with the flexibility to configure it at your own pace.

 It includes the same powerful IDM matching and collision detection as the full setup to ensure the right Clever user is matched to the right destination user—but requires less time to implement. It’s best for:

 • 
 Piloting Clever IDM or starting setup mid-year

• 
 Accessing Clever’s password management tools

• 
 Managing matched users between systems

 
 

 
 

 Select Clever Users 

 Clever IDM can provision accounts for students, teachers, and staff. You may select for Clever to provision accounts for one or more of the following: Students Students are users that are enrolled as students in educational courses or sections. Teachers "Teachers" is the term Clever uses to refer to a district's instructional staff. These are the individuals that are directly managing a classroom and students. Staff "Staff" is the term Clever uses to refer to both non-instructional staff (e.g. secretaries, bus drivers, counselors) and instructional staff that are not directly in charge of a classroom (e.g. school tech leads, instructional coaches, and principals). These users are often a different employment designation than teachers and may even be managed outside the Student Information System (SIS) in a HR Information System (HRIS) 

 Users with multiple user roles If a user is assigned to multiple user roles (i.e. Teacher and Staff) in Clever, they will be provisioned a Google account if either role is selected for Clever IDM provisioning. If the district chooses to provision accounts for both teachers and staff and a user is assigned to both teacher and staff roles in Clever, during Clever IDM setup the district will be able to select the primary role to be used for OU and group structure. Users with existing Google accounts Users with the same email address in Clever and Google will be matched/organized in the OU structure as outlined in step: “Organize Google OUs”. Clever will NOT update the email address for existing Google accounts. To ensure accurate matching, please confirm accurate email addresses are synced to Clever from your student information system (SIS) prior to enabling Clever IDM. 

 

 Additionally, Clever IDM allows you to customize which subsets of users that you would like to provision. If you would like to filter by user type, grade, school, or extension field please contact Clever Support so that we can make those adjustments for you.

 

 Graduation rollover date 

 If Clever IDM is set to manage your students, you will be prompted during this step to configure a graduation rollover date. 

 

 

 

 If you do not sync student graduation year directly from your SIS, Clever IDM will use this rollover date to calculate a graduation year for use in formulas. To ensure that this calculation associates your data with the correct school year, set a graduation rollover date. Clever IDM calculates the graduation year based on the student’s synced grade level. Data synced before the graduation rollover date will be associated with the previous school year for graduation year calculations. Data synced after the graduation rollover date will be associated with the upcoming school year for graduation year calculations. Providing a graduation rollover date enables Clever IDM to automatically update any information based on the graduation year (OUs, groups, etc.) as grade levels change during rollover. If your expected graduation rollover date changes, you can edit the graduation rollover date anytime by navigating to your Select Users step in Clever IDM.

 

 
 Data written to Google 

 There are two pathways for Clever to set user data in IDM: creation and user modification. 

 Creation 

 In the case of user creation, the following fields are synced to Google:

 • User first name
• User last name
• User type (student, teacher, staff)
• Primary email address as configured by the district
• Organizational unit as configured by the district
• Initial password as configured by the district
 
 All newly created users are assumed to be unsuspended. Modification If a user exists in both Clever and Google data, IDM will optionally update the user. Updates occur when a user's first name, last name, user type, or organization unit changes. When updating a user, the following fields are synced:

 • User first name (if changed)
• User last name (if changed)
• Organizational unit as configured by the district (if changed)
• Suspension status
 • Suspended if a user exists in the Google Directory under a managed OU but not in the most recent Clever dataset.
• Unsuspended if a user was previously suspended (i.e., not in the Clever dataset) but has been restored in the Clever dataset within 300 days.
 

• Archive management
 • Users with free Fundamentals, Fundamentals-archive, and Gmail-only licenses will have their archive behavi

title: Clever IDM: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Pricing 3. Get Started 

 Getting started with IDM? Check out our courses available in Clever Academy ! 

 Overview

 Clever IDM helps districts automate account provisioning and lifecycle management for Microsoft Active Directory, Entra ID, and Google Workspace.  Clever IDM automatically syncs users from your SIS to Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google daily for an entire year. Your Clever IDM Dashboard can be found here .

title: Clever + Kahoot!
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does this affect current Clever customers? 3. Will there be any changes to Clever’s existing data privacy policies? 4. When will Clever rostering and SSO be available for my international customers (school districts)? 5. How will Kahoot! and Clever integrate their platforms? 

 Overview

 Kahoot!, a global learning platform company, recently acquired Clever, Inc. The partnership between the two companies, with distinct and highly complementary offerings, provides improved learning solutions and offerings for U.S. schools and districts while accelerating the global expansion of Clever's solutions. Clever's big vision includes reaching even more students and working on new ways to make learning more enjoyable. We are thrilled that joining the Kahoot! Group will make that vision a reality even sooner. 

 

 
 How does this affect current Clever customers?

 While we're excited about the changes, rest assured that we haven't changed. Clever is continuing to do what we do best. Our team's dedication to incredible customer support and reliability is unchanging. Our industry-leading commitment to student data privacy, security, and compliance is only getting stronger and will continue to be a key focus when scaling internationally. Finally, our innovative business model (i.e., working with our app customers to offer Clever's Digital Learning Platform to districts for free) isn't changing. We're remaining an open platform, accessible to all schools and companies. We do not expect that the acquisition will result in any significant changes at this time (or in the future) for existing Kahoot! or Clever customers. Both Kahoot! and Clever customers can expect business as usual and continue using both platforms as they did before. Over time, the ambition is to deliver an even more robust experience for all customers on the platform. The Clever platform will continue to be an open platform. 

 
 Will there be any changes to Clever’s existing data privacy policies? 

 Clever remains fully committed to honoring all of the privacy practices documented in our current privacy policy as well as the commitments we've made to protect student data while complying with COPPA, FERPA, state student data privacy laws, and all contractual agreements. However, as Clever's international presence grows, we will need to expand our privacy and security compliance and data management programs to comply with local laws in other countries. As a result, you will likely see changes to our privacy policy as these additional protections are implemented. The acquisition will not impact Clever's privacy practices for our U.S. clients and partners. Clever's school, district, and student data will be protected as it is today and will not be impacted by the acquisition by Kahoot!. All U.S. student data will continue to be stored in the U.S. 

 

 
 When will Clever rostering and SSO be available for my international customers (school districts)?

 Learn more about using Clever internationally here . 

 
 How will Kahoot! and Clever integrate their platforms?

 We are actively building a plan for how Kahoot! and Clever will operate synergistically, including with regard to partner integrations. We are two companies with the potential to grow stronger, empowering users (e.g., districts, schools, teachers, students, parents, and lifelong learners) globally with industry-leading EdTech tools and resources. Our two teams will have a talent force of over 500 employees, leveraging deep application and product development expertise, technology, marketing, and engineering capabilities to deliver innovative solutions to customers worldwide. Kahoot! 's global learning community will also have the opportunity to discover the benefits of Clever, while Clever's app partners will be able to reach an international audience through Kahoot! 's global presence.

title: For Clever Admins: Syncing students’ preferred names
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Syncing preferred name data 3. Viewing preferred names in Clever 4. The student and teacher experience 5. Syncing preferred names as extension data 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 School districts using SFTP , SIS-Managed Auto Sync , or Infinite Campus API Sync OneRoster 1.2 sync type can sync preferred first, middle, and last names in their student data to Clever. 

 For example, if a student’s legal name is Jacob, but they go by Jake, you can sync Jake as their preferred first name. 

 When you sync preferred name data, this shows up in the students’ Clever Portal and in applications that accept this data. Some applications may not accept preferred name data.

 Please note: Clever previously had a feature that allowed students to change their name in their Clever Portal. Preferred name syncing replaces this feature, and students can no longer change their names themselves. 

 

 
 Syncing preferred name data

 To sync preferred name data, include one or more of the following fields in your Clever sync for students:

 • 
 preferred_last_name

• 
 preferred_middle_name

• 
 preferred_first_name 

 
 For more on exporting these fields, you can find specific information based on your sync type:

 • 
 SFTP Syncs: Overview 

• 
 SIS-Managed Auto Syncs 

• 
 Infinite Campus API Sync 

 
 Once you’ve synced preferred name field(s), you must go into each application and share the field(s) , as preferred name is considered a sensitive field. See here for information on sharing sensitive fields . If you don't see the field as an option in your field access settings for a specific application, this means that the application doesn't accept the preferred name field at this time. Please contact Clever Support to determine if you can utilize an extension field instead .

 

 
 Viewing preferred names in Clever
