 As a Clever Admin, you can view a student's preferred name (if populated) by comparing their Profile Page in your Clever Dashboard to the name in their Clever Portal. Start by going to: Clever Dashboard > search for student > student’s profile page . Note the name syncing at the top of their Profile Page. This will be values provided in the first_name and last_name fields of your data sync. Next, head to the top right corner of the Profile Page and click Support Tools > Access Portal as user and review the name in the top right corner of the student's Portal. If the preferred name is syncing, it will display in the Portal and be different than the synced name on their Profile Page.

 

 
 The student and teacher experience

 Students 

 Students can see their name in the upper right corner of their Clever Portal: 

 
 Students can also see their preferred name in applications if that app accepts and shares preferred names.

 Teachers 

 The students’ preferred name (if populated) will show up on the teacher’s Clever Portal on the My Students > Student Roster page. 

 

 
 Syncing preferred names as extension data

 If an application doesn't currently accept preferred names as a field, but you'd like to send preferred names, you can provide the preferred names to Clever via an extension field. 

 Depending on your sync method with Clever, there are a few options you can consider for uploading extension data to Clever. Learn more about extension data here .

 Once the extension data is in Clever, our support team can assist with providing this data to your learning applications as the name fields for your students. Please contact Clever Support with questions or to further explore your options for adding preferred names as extension data to sync to your learning applications.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can students change their names themselves? 

 • 
 No, this feature has been sunsetted. 

 
 

 Do preferred names show up in Class Analytics? 

 • 
 Presently, preferred name does not show up in Class Analytics. 

 
 

 Is it possible to sync teachers’ preferred names? 

 • 
 Presently, this feature is only available for students.

title: Clever + Google Classroom: Requirements & Troubleshooting
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. Teacher experience 4. Troubleshooting Errors on the Sync History page 5. Using the Google Classroom Course Information Lookup Tool 6. As-Needed: Reprovision any courses that teachers declined by accident 7. FAQs 

 Overview

 ➕ The Google Classroom roster import application is available to request in the Clever application gallery ! 

 U.S. and Canadian school districts with a Google Standard Edition or Google Workspace for Education Plus subscription for staff and students have the option to roster Google Classroom courses via Clever. If you’re interested in utilizing this rostering method, please visit your Clever Dashboard to accept your invite or request the application . Once the application is connected and the scheduled sync start date has arrived, Clever will provision Google Classroom courses based on your district’s SIS + Clever data. Google Classroom course names will match to Clever section names and rosters will update automatically as students change sections in your SIS + Clever. The Google Classroom roster import application will:

 Create Classroom courses and enroll students, teachers, and co-teachers based on Clever section data. Automatically update rosters based on changes in Clever. Allow teachers to manually add and remove students and co-teachers to Clever-created courses. Allow teachers to rename Clever-created courses. Allow teachers to accept or reject Clever-created courses.

 The application will NOT: 

 Create, modify, or delete user accounts in Google Workspace. Modify courses that teachers manually create in Classroom. Write Classroom grades back to your district’s SIS (but should be compatible with other grade passback solutions). Overwrite changes that teachers make to their courses, such as the course name or manual changes to enrollments Sync any Guardian info to Classroom or modify/overwrite any Guardian data

 

 If you are already the district administrator for a Clever account, you can r equest Google Classroom Roster Import from your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page. Districts interested in Classroom rostering who don't already use Clever can create a Clever account here to request the application.

 Requirements

 

 • Districts must have a Google Standard Edition or Google Workspace for Education Plus subscription for staff and students in order to utilize the Google Classroom roster import application via Clever. If your district has questions about upgrading to Education Plus, please contact na-k12-gwfe@google.com . 
 • If your district has a paid 'legacy' subscription (also called “G Suite Enterprise for Education”) for staff and students then your district has access to this tool. To maintain access your district will need to renew the Education Plus tier when you are up for renewal.
 

• One of your district's Clever Admins will need to coordinate with your district’s Google Workspace Super Administrator for the initial setup of Google Classroom roster import.
 • The domain for the Super Admin (_ ex @school.org_) must match the domain for your teacher's Google Accounts (also @ school.org ). For more info, see the setup guide here . 
 

• If you’re interested in finding out about your Google Workspace subscription or for help identifying your Google Workspace Super Admin, please log into your Google admin console .
• Teachers, students and the Super Administrator can be in different subdomains, but they must all be part of the same primary domain .
• The application requires all Classroom users to have their Email field in Clever populated with the Google email. The emails need to already be provisioned. This Classroom rostering application does not generate any user accounts.
 • For help with generating user accounts, read here for more info on Clever IDM! 
 

 
 

 Teacher Experience

 Once rostering is turned on for Google Classroom then teachers will have the option to accept Clever-created courses. They can also reject them and continue managing their rosters manually. Courses created outside Clever will not be modified or deleted by the Clever connection. 

 Troubleshooting Errors on the Sync History page

 If you notice that some rostering data is not successfully populating within Google Classroom automatically, please review the Google Classroom roster import Sync History page of your Clever Dashboard. From that page, you will be able to download CSV files of the sync errors or successfully synced reports:

 

 

 Please note: When sections match, we may or may not have updated the class. It'll also show as an update, but this doesn't mean that anything changed. It's just indicating that it is the new current state of the Google Classroom class. 

 

 Below are some sync errors you might see, what they mean, and how you can resolve them! 

 Error: 403: @CannotDirectAddUser - Unable to directly add the user to the course. Please check that the user account exists and is within the course admin's domain. 

 • Reason: The user can’t be added because they are in a different domain (e.g. gmail.com or @ student.school.org ) or their Google account could not be found.
• Resolution: Look up the user’s email in Clever and Google and make sure the user’s email address matches an active, already-created Google account that belongs to the primary Workspace domain.
 
 Error: 400: @UserAccountSuspended - The user account specified in the request is suspended 

 • Reason: The user's Google account has been suspended. The user is still active in the SIS/Clever data and being shared with Google Classroom roster import.
• Resolution: Check Google and the SIS to ensure that the user account is active in both systems or is removed in Clever.
 
 

 Error: 403: The caller does not have permission – unable to create Course because of issue with primary teacher 

 • Reason: The teacher account has not been created in Google Workspace or is in a separate domain than the Super Admin.
• Resolution: Contact Google Workspace Support if you need help managing your synced users and subdomains. Teacher accounts should be created before syncing and the Super Admin should be able to manage all of the users who are synced with Classroom roster import.
 
 

 Error: 400: @JoiningUserNotEnabledForRosterImport Joining user is not enabled for SIS Roster Import via the current integrator (Clever). 

 • Reason: Unable to import user due to insufficient licenses or roster import settings.
• Resolution: Please ensure the target user has sufficient workspace licenses and is managed by the connected Google service account. In addition, please ensure Clever is enabled for roster import: in Google Admin Console, go to Apps > Google Workspace > Settings for Classroom > Roster Import, and select ON-CLEVER. Note: this setting must be set for all OUs containing students or teachers that have courses managed by Google Classroom roster import. 
 
 Error: 403: Request had insufficient authentication scopes. Reconnect Clever with a Google Super Admin account. 

 • Reason: Your latest Google Classroom sync failed because the sync did not have permission from a Super Admin account to modify your data in Classroom.
• Resolution: Please visit the settings page and use the Disconnect button under Google Authentication. Then press the Reconnect button and accept all of the requested permissions with a Google Super Admin account . 
 
 Error: 400: Bad Request - invalid_grant - token has been expired or revoked 

 • Reason: Your latest Google Classroom sync failed because the connection between Clever and Google Classroom has broken.
• Resolution: Please visit the settings page and reconnect Clever with a Google Super Admin account , making sure to include all of the requested permissions. 
 
 Error: 400: admin_policy_enforced 

 • Reason: Your latest Google Classroom connection failed because your Google Workspace domain has blocked the connection to Clever.
• Resolu

title: For Teachers: How to leave an app review
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Reviewing Library (teacher-added) apps 

 Overview 

 Clever provides teachers with the ability to review applications through Clever Library. Teacher reviews provide useful insight for other teachers and district administrators as they explore which resources would be most beneficial in their classrooms.

 

 
 

 Reviewing Library (teacher-added) apps 

 Please note: Teachers must have logged into the application once before the option to review will appear. 

 

 • Log into Clever Portal
• Search for the app name in the search bar in the upper right corner
• Click Details next to the app when it appears in the dropdown menu
• Click Leave a review in the upper right corner
• Complete the app review

title: For App Partners: Reviews for your Clever Library listing
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How teachers can leave a review for your application 3. Best practices for requesting reviews from teachers 4. How to read teacher reviews for your application 5. How to reply to teacher reviews 

 Overview 

 Teacher reviews and comments are extremely valuable for curriculum leaders when evaluating existing or new edtech apps to purchase. In addition to leaving a rating, teachers often provide helpful comments about the accessibility and usability for decision makers to consider when assessing your application. You can help increase the number of reviews on your app detail page by asking teachers who are using your application to leave a review. Once teachers leave a review, you will be able to read and reply to the review in your dashboard. Reviews and any replies will also be published on your application’s public-facing profile page in the Clever Library.

 How teachers can leave a review for your application 

 Whether you have a Secure Sync or Library integration, once you are in the Clever Library, your application is eligible to receive verified teacher reviews. A verified teacher review means that the teacher has logged into your application at least once before leaving a review. 

 

 Teachers can leave reviews from their Clever Portal by navigating to your application via the search bar, and clicking 'Details'. 

 

 If you have a profile page in Clever Library, teachers can navigate to your Library listing's URL where they'll be able to leave a review (if they have already installed and used your application). This is useful if you want to direct teachers to a specific page where they can leave a review.
 Please note: You can identify teachers who installed your application in the Clever Library via your API. 

 Best practices for requesting reviews from teachers 

If your application doesn't have reviews, we encourage you to reach out to teachers directly to as for them to leave a review and comments in Clever. Use this sample messaging to ask teachers who have used your app to leave a review. Clever is also proactively prompting teachers to leave reviews for the applications they use when they log into their Clever Portal.
 • Use value-based messaging when asking for reviews. When sending emails asking for reviews, include some tips, ideas, or guidance on how to get the most out of using your application. View sample messaging here .
• Optimize the timing of when you ask teachers for reviews. Consider timing your ask around when the teacher has used your product independently or with students, within a certain number of days after logging into your application, or during a time when they're likely to engage with your product.
• Send one review request per teacher. If a teacher doesn't respond to your request for a review, don't send them a second request.
 
 Guidelines

 • Be open to all types of feedback. Teachers trust the feedback of other teachers, so having a range of reviews on your application gives you an opportunity to establish trust and authenticity with your users, in addition to receiving valuable product feedback.
• Per FTC guidelines , don't offer money or prizes in exchange for reviews. If you offer an incentive for teachers to leave reviews, you must instruct the reviewer to indicate that this was an incentivized endorsement.
 
 Clever is excited to be a trustworthy source of teacher reviews. With this in mind, we reserve the right to flag any unusual review behavior.

 How to read teacher reviews for your application 

You can view all teacher reviews in your Clever Dashboard. Navigate to the Library tab, where you will see all your reviews listed. Depending on your integration(s), either Secure Sync or Library, you'll be able to see which version the reviewer is accessing next to their name.
 

 Important things to note about reviews

 • Reviews will be sorted based on the date they were submitted.
• You do not have the ability to delete reviews.
• However, you do have the ability to reply to reviews. Reviews will be shared publicly on your profile page. Continue reading below for guidance on how to reply to teacher reviews.
 
 How to reply to teacher reviews 

Applications can reply to each individual review directly in their dashboard by clicking on the 'Respond' button next to the review. 
 
 Teachers will see a notification in their Clever Portal that their review has a response.

 

 Your response will be visible to all visitors on your Application Detail Page.

 

 After leaving your response, you can return to your dashboard any time to edit or delete your response.

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Library privacy
text:
For the most comprehensive information about how data is handled for Library applications, please review Clever’s Universal Data Sharing Agreement (DSA) .

 Overview

 The Clever Library lets teachers install applications for themselves and their students, write reviews, and share apps with other teachers.

 This article outlines information on the data provided to Library applications via Clever. For more information on Library settings and access, please see this article . 

 

 
 

 What data do Clever Library Apps receive?

 Clever Library applications added by teachers receive a limited amount of data. These applications do not receive all fields like Secure Sync applications. Find the data Library apps receive for each record type below:

 

 Teachers

 • Clever ID
• Name
• Email
 
 

 Students

 • Clever ID
• Name (first name, last initial)
• Grade
 
 

 Sections

 • Clever ID
• Name
• Subject
• Grade
• Section number
• Teachers
• Students

title: For Teachers: Making a purchase request in Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I make a purchase request? 3. What happens after a purchase request is made? 

 

 Overview

 The Clever Library is a place for teachers to discover, learn about, and adopt new resources for their classrooms. Teachers can browse applications across multiple subjects and grades. Clever also surfaces popular apps in your district and across Clever, as well as apps new to the Library. Resources in the Library are added to a teacher’s Teacher Page for easy access by students and teachers.

 If you don’t see a Library tab in your Clever Portal, your Clever Admin has disabled the Library. Learn more here .

 

 
 

 How do I make a purchase request?

 Please note: In order to make a purchase request, the desired application must first be added to your Teacher Page from the Clever Library. Not all Library apps are available for purchase (such as free apps).

 In order to add an application from the Clever Library, follow these steps:

 • 
 Navigate to the Library page in your Clever Portal. Find this option in the upper right corner. If you know the name of the application, you can use the search bar. 

• 
 Click on the desired application and you’ll be taken to the application page. If you don’t see an application, it may not be available in the Library.

• 
 From the application page, click Install . If the Install button is gray and not clickable, your Clever Admin has blocked this application. Learn more here or contact your admin or IT team for more information.

• 
 Complete the form on the following page. You can choose which Teacher Pages to display the application on.

• 
 Click Allow and continue .

 
 Once an application has been added to your Teacher Page via the Clever Library, you have the ability to request that your district purchase the resource. There are two ways to make a purchase request: 

 Option 1

 • Select the application you want to request a purchase for from the appropriate Teacher Page in Clever Portal.
• Choose the Upgrade option below the application icon.
• Fill out the form presented and click Send Request .
 
 Option 2

 • In the Clever Portal, search for the application by name using the search bar in the upper right corner.
• Once the application appears in the dropdown menu, click the link labeled Details . This will take you to the application page in the Clever Library.
• Click Request an upgrade .
• Fill out the form presented and click Send Request .
 
 

 
 

 What happens after a purchase request is made?

 Once you’ve submitted your purchase request, Clever Admins and School Tech Leads at your district or school will be notified. Those individuals will determine if they will take action on your purchase request. If you have questions, please reach out to your IT or tech support team.

title: For App Partners: Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does the Clever Library work? 3. Understanding Teacher Sign-Ups with the Clever Library 4. How "Sign Up with Clever" works for teachers 5. How "Sign Up with Clever" works for students 

 

 Overview

 

 The Clever Library is a place for teachers to discover, learn about, and adopt new resources for their classroom. Resources in the Library can be added to a teacher’s Teacher Page for easy access by students and teachers. 

 

 

 

 
 How does the Clever Library work?

 

 When a teacher adopts a Library application with SSO, Clever shares only the absolute minimum information: student first name, last initial, grade level, and course enrollment. Clever does not share any sensitive information with Library applications, including student email, student number, SIS ID, state ID, school/district information, or any demographic information about the student. 

 

 In the Clever Library, teachers can:

 • 
 Add applications for themselves and their students

• 
 Review applications for other teachers to see

• 
 Recommend and share applications to other teachers

 
 Please note: The Clever Admin for each school organization can turn off the Clever Library and block individual resources that they don’t want teachers to access. Please see this article for more information on district and school controls over Library applications. 

 

 
 Understanding Teacher Sign-Ups with the Clever Library

 

 Sign Up with Clever enables teachers to create accounts for themselves and their assigned students in your application using data from their existing Clever accounts. This streamlines onboarding by automatically setting up accounts without manual data entry. Applications added by teachers through Clever also appear as SSO resources in the Clever Portal for both teachers and their students. 

 

 Currently, only existing teachers in a Clever district can use Sign Up with Clever when utilizing their Clever Library; new teachers who attempt to Sign Up with Clever are directed to have their district IT administrators set up an account with Clever.

 

 Teacher sign-ups with the Clever Library differ from district connections primarily in management and data access. With teacher sign-ups, individual teachers independently decide to connect your application via the Clever Library and can add or remove access at any time. In contrast, district connections are authorized and managed by district-level Clever Admins, who control Sharing Permissions to grant access en masse. Teacher sign-ups provide only essential data—teacher and student names, emails, sections taught, and Clever IDs—while district connections share the full Clever Data Model and support overnight data syncing. Please note that data from teacher sign-ups can only be accessed on login; unlike district connections, teacher sign-ups do not support overnight data syncing.

 

 Click Analytics on the left sidebar of your app dashboard to see the total number of students and teachers who are using your application. You can use the “Source” filter to identify the usage coming from teacher sign-ups, district connections, or both. 

 
 How "Sign Up with Clever" works for teachers

 

 If your application supports Sign Up with Clever, teachers can create an account in your product either via a Login with Clever button on your website, or via your product’s listing on the Clever Library . Learn more about Clever Library integrations here .

 

 

 

 Teachers will then be presented with a screen to confirm that they would like to share their profile information to create accounts in your product for themselves and their students. Teachers may also take this opportunity to review your application’s terms of service and privacy policy.

 

 

 

 After confirming their approval, the teacher will be taken to your product to complete their onboarding and start using your product. 

 Immediately upon signup, a Single Sign-On (SSO) link to your product will also appear on the Clever Portal for the teacher and all of his or her students. Specifically, the link will appear on the teacher’s Class Page, to distinguish it from resources that are managed at the district level. 

 After signing up, a teacher may log in to their account in your product either through the SSO link on their Clever Portal. Alternatively, if you have a Log In with Clever button on your site, they can go to your site directly to log in. 

 
 How "Sign Up with Clever" works for students

 Once a teacher has used Sign Up with Clever to create an account for themselves and their students, the teacher’s students may log in either via the SSO link on their teacher’s Class Page, or by going to you site directly and clicking “Log in with Clever”. 

 In the typical case, students should not need to create their own accounts, as your product should provision student accounts automatically when teachers sign up. 

 Any new student who joins a teacher’s class will automatically have any SSO links added to their portal, and your application should provision an account for the student the next time they or their teacher logs in.

title: For Clever Admins: Clever API v3.0
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Announcing API V3.0! Clever’s newest API version simplifies data management for partners and districts. The next-generation API offers a new flexible data model to help partners meet the fast-evolving and unique needs of their district customers in a scalable way. 

 
 Features

 API v3.0 will include the following features: 

 Support for multi-role users

 In v1.0 and v2.0, users with multiple roles had a Clever ID for each role. This could often be confusing in downstream applications where users might have duplicate accounts or struggle to access the correct account. API v3.0 provides flexible user-role data to streamline the experience of provisioning teachers, staff, and contacts so applications can quickly scale access for all users on their platform. If a user has multiple leadership roles (at the district, school, or teacher level), they will now have a single Clever ID. 

 What does this mean for school districts?

 This updated data model will not be very noticeable in the Clever Dashboard or Portal. Users with multiple roles will still have separate Portals in Clever. However, downstream applications will now be able to identify users with various roles and link those accounts accordingly. Some applications will also allow users to toggle between accounts. 

 Contacts have per-student relationships

 We’re adding even more granularity to our contact relationships to make it easier for partners to support and connect with family members. If a contact is a parent to two children, has a nephew in the district, and is an emergency contact for a family friend, they’ll have one Clever ID, and you’ll be able to see those unique relationships with every student. 

 What does this mean for school districts?

 Contact data will be more accurate and relationship types will be much less rigid. This update recognizes the complexity of the different types of relationships individuals may have as students' contacts. This change will address the security risks of assigning only one relationship type to a contact who may otherwise have other relationship types with other students. 

 Changes to staff

 API v3.0 will support a consistent experience for how districts and applications refer to Staff (formerly ‘School Admins’). Furthermore, role types for Staff will now be available through the API, making it easy to distinguish between the different levels of permissions Staff have in Clever (e.g., School Tech Lead vs. Portal-Only). 

 What does this mean for school districts?

 This update will improve the alignment between how our school district and application partners communicate about staff users. By updating the way we refer to this user type in the API, we hope to close the loop on some miscommunication that occurs when rostering staff users. In addition, exposing the staff role field in the Clever API will give application partners more flexibility in provisioning account permissions. 

 Content assignment

 Some applications allow you to map resources to specific courses, classes, or users. This often requires district administrators to work with each application directly to assign these resources manually. To address this pain point, we’ve created a feature that helps you manage content mapping directly from your Clever Dashboard! To learn more about content assignment, check out this article: For Clever Admins: Content Mapping. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 When can we expect to see these changes? 

 • It is up to each application to decide when to adopt the new API version. Even for our early adopters, it will take some time to implement these changes and migrate their data over. If you would like to know more about how an application will utilize our new API, we recommend reaching out to them directly.
 
 How will I know what version of the API the application is on? 

 • You can always reach out to Clever Support for more information about the API version an application uses. However, how each application uses v3.0 will be up to them. Clever doesn't guarantee any specific user experience changes as a result of this new API version release. If you have more specific questions, we recommend reaching out to the application directly.

title: PowerSchool Version 20.11.0
text:
This article is for districts that have updated to PowerSchool version 20.11.0+ and need to update their PowerSchool permissions to grant Clever sync access. 

 Please Note: • This sync type is no longer available for new setups. If new to Clever or updating your configuration to pull data from PowerSchool, please reference our article PowerSchool Plugin Sync. • If your schools use the North Carolina state-based PowerSchool setup, your sync method is SFTP and the sync is managed by your district. If you are interested, Clever is integrated with NCEdCloud for teacher and student logins! Click here to learn more. • If your schools use ESchoolPLUS as your SIS, please see our article on SFTP Syncs . This article is intended for districts using PowerSchool as their SIS. • If your district's PowerSchool account has any custom setup, these instructions may not be exactly correct.

 

 
 

 Updating Permissions

 PowerSchool 20.11.0+ requires permissions updates for Clever to sync successfully via DDE. To update your permissions, follow these directions: 

 • Log in to PowerSchool as an admin 
• Navigate to: System > Security > User Access Roles > {click on Role assigned to the Clever access account} > Export/Edit 
• Confirm the correct permissions have been granted to the Clever Sync access account to export the following records as outlined in S ection 3 of this article: Setting up your PowerSchool Auto Sync - Schools - Students - Sections - Teachers - CC - Terms
• Depending on your PowerSchool setup, you may also need to adjust your Data Export Manager (DEM) settings. To grant access to Data Export Manager, follow these directions: A. Navigate to System > Security > Users by group and search for the Clever user account. B. Click on the Clever user account and select the tab Admin access and roles C. Under Roles and Schools , select the Action: Edit D. Check the box for Data Export Manager Export Access and click OK E. Click Submit to save your changes
• Once you've made these changes, navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page and select Sync Now. 
• Clever will initiate a sync with PowerSchool. To confirm the results, please allow 10-15 minutes for the PowerSchool sync to complete. The sync status will display in your Clever Dashboard .

title: Clever IDM: Entra ID Setup
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup • Connect to Entra ID • Select your IDM Management Level • Select Clever users • Data written to Entra ID • Data written to a student information system (SIS) • Summary • Preview and provision • Events 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 IMPORTANT Moving to the new service principal connection? Edit your existing Entra ID IDM connection on the IDM page of your Dashboard . Click Disconnect, then follow the instructions below to connect to Entra ID. If you don't see the new configuration, please reach out to Clever Support . Failure to do so before June 1, 2026, will result in a failed IDM sync. 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM helps districts automate account provisioning and lifecycle management for Microsoft Active Directory, Google Workspace, and Entra ID. This affordable new offering provides school districts with a simple workflow that can be completed in minutes to automate account management directly with Clever. Clever IDM automatically syncs users from your SIS to Entra ID, Active Directory, and/or Google daily for an entire year. Your Clever IDM dashboard can be found here .

 A full overview of the Clever IDM setup process is below. If you haven’t already purchased Clever IDM, you'll be asked to purchase after completing the setup process. More information on pricing and purchasing can be found here .

 

 

 

 
 Setup

 You can work through setting up your IDM configuration at any time; however, you won’t be able to complete the setup to start provisioning accounts without review from an onboarding specialist or our support team. Our team must review your setup before your first provision to protect your destination accounts and prevent issues with SSO for your end users. Contact your onboarding specialist for configuration review. If you're not sure who to contact, reach out to Clever Support .

 Please note: Click on the following link to find information about Setting IDM login credentials. 

 Connect to Entra ID 

 To provision accounts, Clever must be granted access to manage the provisioning of users on your Entra ID domain. The user authorizing the access request must have admin access to the relevant Entra ID instance.

 • 
 Click Sign in with Microsoft Entra ID to authorize the installation of the Clever IDM application in your Entra ID tenant. 

• 
 Now locate the Clever IDM application within your Entra ID tenant and assign the User Administer role. See Microsoft support for more information. Note: The Clever Identity Management app is owned by Clever and will not appear under App Registrations. Assign the User Administrator role from the Roles area of Entra: a. In a new tab, search for roles and administrators at entra.microsoft.com b. Select Microsoft Entra roles and administrators c. Under All roles search for User Administrator d. Click on User Administrator e. Click on + Add assignments f. Click on No member selected g. Search for Clever Identity Management and check the box next to the app h. Click Select i. Click Next j. Enter a justification like: Enables Clever IDM to manage users k. Click Assign l. Return to Clever

• 
 Click Check role assignment. If this doesn't work at first, try again in 30 seconds.

• 
 Once the Clever IDM application has successfully been assigned the User Administrator role, click Next: Select Management Level to proceed to the next step.

 
 If you run into issues:

 • 
 Check that your Entra admin user can grant the following permissions:

 • 
 Read domains

• 
 Read all groups

• 
 Read all users’ full profiles

• 
 Sign in and read user profile

 

• 
 Ensure that you have granted the Clever IDM application the User Administrator role. See Microsoft support for more information.

 
 If you run into issues authorizing the installation or assigning the User Administrator role, you may see an Enable Single-tenant App registration toggle. Toggle the value on and proceed to the optional advanced connection instructions.

 Provide the additional information requested to enable installation of a custom app. To locate the additional information, log in at https://entra.microsoft.com . Follow the onscreen instructions in your Clever Dashboard to complete the entry. After clicking Save , use the step by step instructions above in step 2 to verify that your custom app has the User Administrator role; use the app name you provided when registering the app to assign the role.

 If you are still having trouble connecting, reach out to your onboarding specialist or Clever Support .

 Select your IDM Management Level 

 

 Next, choose whether you’d like to set up Full Provisioning and Password Management or Password Management Only . Both options support Identity Management in Clever—it just depends on what works best for your district’s timing and goals.

 

 Full Provisioning and Password Management 

 This option automates account security and password management through Clever IDM. Since it requires more setup time, many districts choose this path at the beginning of the school year or during a long break. It’s best for:

 • 
 Automatically provisioning, updating, and archiving user accounts

• 
 Configuring password reset, account recovery, and account claiming settings

• 
 Working in tandem with Entra's dynamic membership groups to write to fields in Entra. 

 
 

 Password Management Only 

 This lighter setup gives you access to Clever’s secure password management tools for matched users, with the flexibility to configure it at your own pace.

 It includes the same powerful IDM matching and collision detection as the full setup to ensure the right Clever user is matched to the right destination user—but requires less time to implement. It’s best for:

 • 
 Piloting Clever IDM or starting setup mid-year

• 
 Accessing Clever’s password management tools

• 
 Managing matched users between systems

 
 

 Select Clever users 

 Clever IDM can provision accounts for students, teachers, and staff. You can select for Clever to provision accounts for one or more of the following:

 • Students 
 • Students are users that are enrolled as students in educational courses or sections. 
 

• Teachers 
 • Teachers is the term Clever uses to refer to a district's instructional staff. These are the individuals that are directly managing a classroom and students.
 

• Staff 
 • Staff is the term Clever uses to refer to both non-instructional staff (e.g., secretaries, bus drivers, counselors) and instructional staff that are not directly in charge of a classroom (e.g., school tech leads, instructional coaches, and principals).
 

 
 These users are often a different employment designation than teachers and may even be managed outside the Student Information System (SIS) in an HR Information System (HRIS)

 

 Users with multiple user roles If a user is assigned to multiple user roles (i.e., Teacher and Staff) in Clever, they will be provisioned an Entra ID account if either role is selected for Clever IDM provisioning. If the district chooses to provision accounts for both teachers and staff and a user is assigned to both teacher and staff roles in Clever, during Clever IDM setup the district will be able to select the primary role to be referenced.

 

 Additionally, Clever IDM allows you to customize which subsets of users that you would like to provision. If you would like to filter by user type, grade, school, or extension field, please contact Clever Support so that we can make those adjustments for you.

 

 Graduation rollover date If Clever IDM is set to manage your students, you will be prompted during this step to configure a graduation rollover date. 

 

 If you do not sync student graduation year directly from your SIS, Clever IDM will use this rollover date to calculate a graduation year for use in formulas. To ensure that this calculation associates your data with the correct school year, set a graduation rollover date. Clever IDM calculat

title: Clever & Edtech Acronyms / Glossary
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Glossary 

 

 Overview

 This article is a glossary of terms and acronyms frequently used in edtech. Like much of the tech landscape, edtech has its own set of specific acronyms and terminology. To help you navigate your experience with Clever, please reference the glossary below whenever you encounter an unfamiliar term. Found an unknown term or acronym that is not covered in this glossary? Please reach out to Clever Support to make a suggestion.

 
 Glossary

 • Account Claim — Account Claim is a tool for Clever IDM customers to make it easier for end users to set up their Clever, Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID accounts. New accounts created by Clever IDM can be accessed by end users by providing two or more points of private information for students and three or more points for adults, all without IT intervention – making onboarding and account distribution easier than ever for time-constrained IT teams.
 
 

 • AD, ADFS — Active Directory or Active Directory Federation Services (Microsoft Windows identity service)
 • Active Directory is a Clever-supported Identity Provider ( IdP )
• Support in Clever for Active Directory:
 • As an Identity Provider: Single Sign-On (SSO): Log in with ADFS , Clever Badges: How do I enable Badges into Active Directory On-prem managed Windows devices? 
• With our Identity Management service, Clever IDM: Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup 
• As a source for Staff user accounts: Staff Setup: Syncing staff with Active Directory 
 

 

 
 

 • API — Application Programming Interface - a way for two programs or apps to talk to one another. Programs have APIs so that they can share data easily. Clever syncs with the APIs of many popular Student Information Systems ( SISes ). Clever’s Application partners sync to Clever’s API.
 
 

 • BTS — Back to School - the time period around the first day of the school year when school organizations are preparing for the return of students for the new school year.
 
 

 • Clever Academy - Clever's free online training and certification program for educators and admins. Y ou can go directly to Clever Academy here: https://clever.academy . 
 
 

 • Clever Admin — Also known as a district admin, or DA , this is an individual from your school or district who administers your Clever account.
 
 

 • Clever LMS Connect – A solution that enables application partners to connect directly to district and school Learning Management Systems (LMS) . Districts and schools can also connect their LMS directly with Clever to simplify application use.
 
 

 • COPPA – Children’s Online Privacy Protection Rule – Federal law protecting children’s online privacy.
 
 

 • Course - a course is an academic subject. Courses are often assigned a specific course code and title to track academic credit. Examples: Algebra 101, 3rd Grade Science, Concert Band.
 
 

 • CS — Customer Support or Clever Support - our world-class support team. Learn more about contacting the Customer Support team here. 
 
 

 • CSV — Comma Separated Value - a file type using text to represent data fields separated by commas with the file extension .csv. CSV files are used in SFTP syncs to export data to Clever. CSV files can be opened in programs like Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets. 
 
 

 • DA — District administrator - an individual from your school or district who administers your Clever account. Sometimes referred to as a Clever Admin . 
 
 

 • ELL — English Language Learner 
 
 

 • Entra ID — (Microsoft Windows identity service)
 • 
 Entra ID is a Clever-supported Identity Provider ( IdP )

• 
 Support in Clever for Entra ID:

 • 
 As an Identity Provider: Single Sign-On (SSO): "Log in with Office 365/Entra ID" , 

• 
 With our Identity Management service, Clever IDM: Clever IDM: Entra ID (Azure) Setup 

• 
 SSO to Entra ID: Setup guide for Clever SSO into Entra ID 

 

 

 
 

 • FERPA – Federal Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA). Federal law that governs the access to educational information.
 
 

 • FRL — Free or Reduced Lunch. May also be referred to as FRP (free or reduced lunch program).
 
 

 • FTP — File transfer protocol - The File Transfer Protocol is a standard communication protocol used for the transfer of computer files from a server to a client on a computer network. This is how your CSV files get to Clever for an SFTP sync.
 
 

 • FTP Client — FTP Clients allow you to transfer files via Secure File Transfer Protocol safely. Examples of FTP Clients would be FileZilla or WinSCP.
 
 

 • GAC — Google Admin Console - The Google Admin console is a central place to manage your Google Workspace services. • Google is a Clever-supported Identity Provider ( IdP )
• 
 Support in Clever for Google:

 • 
 • 
 As an Identity Provider: Single Sign-On (SSO): Log in with Google , Clever Badges: Chromebook login flow 

• 
 With our Identity Management service, Clever IDM: Clever IDM: Google Setup 

• 
 See For Clever Admins: Clever & Google for additional information 

 

 

 

• Google Classroom roster import — a rostering application that enables admins with Google Workspace for Education Plus to automatically create classes, populate, and sync rosters directly to Classroom from their Student Information System (SIS) through Clever.
 
 

 • IAM — Identity and access management
 
 

 • IC — Infinite Campus , a student information system (SIS) used to sync data to Clever.
 
 

 • IDM — Identity Management - Clever IDM helps districts automate account provisioning and lifecycle management for Microsoft Active Directory, Google Workspace , and Entra ID . Clever IDM automatically syncs users from a Student Information System ( SIS ) to Active Directory, Google, or Entra ID daily for an entire year.
 
 

 • IdP — Identity Provider - what districts use to manage identity at their district to allow access to Clever. Many districts already have a username and password that students use to access applications on their school site. Common login methods are Google and Active Directory Federation Services ( ADFS ).
 
 

 • IEP — Individualized Education Program 
 
 

 • Instant Login (IL) — Instant Login is a way for users to log in once and then access all their connected applications without having to log in again.
 
 

 • Instant Login Link (ILL) — A custom link that allows districts to embed application login URLs directly into their district's Single Sign-On portal, website, or Learning Management System (LMS). This allows district users to access Clever applications via your district portal using their Clever credentials.
 
 

 • LEA — Local education agency , a public school district, or a public charter school in the United States.
 
 

 • LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol is a protocol for two systems to talk to each other but it is not an identity provider. Clever uses LDAP to match user information from a server (typically, Active Directory ).
 
 

 • LMS — Learning Management System - a software application for the administration, documentation, tracking, reporting, and delivery of electronic educational technology (also called e-learning) education courses or training programs.
 
 

 • LTI — Learning Tools Interoperability (IMS standard) is an education technology specification developed by the IMS Global Learning Consortium (now known as 1EdTech Consortium). It specifies a method for a learning system to invoke and to communicate with external systems.
 
 

 • Mapping — The process of linking data fields from one database to another. In Clever, mapping typically means bringing a field from the SIS into a Clever field.
 
 

 • MDM — Mobile Device Management - typically districts or schools use a Mobile Device Management (MDM) software (i.e. JAMF, Intune) to add the Clever app to the users' school devices, which allows the district to automate, control, and secure administrative policies on their network. 
 
 

 • MDR — MDR is a company that pro

title: For App Partners: How do I enable two-factor authentication?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up two-factor authentication 3. Disabling Two-Factor Authentication 4. Account recovery 

 Overview 

 Two-factor authentication adds another level of security to your account. If you enable two-factor authentication, you'll be asked to enter a verification code in addition to your username and password every time you log in to Clever. This extra verification prevents unauthorized parties from accessing your account, even if your password has been compromised.

 You can set up two-factor authentication for your Clever account in two ways:

 • Via a text message sent to your phone or device (SMS)
• Via the authenticator application Authy (available for iOS and Android )
 
 Please note: Due to the requirement for SSO or 2FA to log in to the Clever apps dashboard, non-human user accounts will no longer be supported. 

 

 
 Setting up two-factor authentication 

 1. Log into your Clever Account 2. Click on your name in the upper right-hand corner, and select Profile 3. Scroll down to select Enable TFA 4. Select how you’d like to receive verification codes, and then enter your phone number to proceed. 

 Please note: If you choose to use Authy and have not already downloaded the app, you will be prompted to do so.

 5. When prompted, either enter your code from Authy or request a code via SMS. 6. The next time you log in to Clever, you will be prompted to enter your verification code. Enter the verification code received via Authy or SMS. You can ask Clever to remember you for 30 days on your device so that you do not need to authenticate every time you log in. This option is not recommended if you are on a public or shared computer, or don’t have password protection on your personal computer. 

 
 Disabling Two-Factor Authentication 

 Once you have two-factor authentication enabled, you can disable this setting at any time from your Clever Profile page . You’ll be prompted to enter your current Clever password to disable two-factor authentication. Please note that you cannot disable two-factor authentication if you have lost access to your two-factor authentication method. If you have lost access to your two-factor authentication due to having a new device, new phone number, or inability to receive an SMS message from Authy, please see the Account Recovery instructions below. 

 
 Account Recovery 

 If you are unable to access your two-factor authentication code, it will be necessary to complete account recovery as outlined here: Restoring Authy Access on a New, Lost, or Inaccessible Phone Clever Support is unable to disable two-factor authentication for your account. The account recovery must be completed by our two-factor service provider, Authy. tags: 2FA, MFA

title: For Clever Admins: Cybersecurity best practices
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever products and features that support your cybersecurity practices 3. Cybersecurity recommendations for your district or school organization 

 

 Overview

 Cybersecurity and protecting user accounts with access to sensitive information continues to be a key priority for districts as more malicious actors target school districts and exploit vulnerabilities in their IT infrastructure. This article discusses some key cybersecurity best practices when managing your Clever account for schools and districts located in the US and Canada. As always, we recommend evaluating your current cybersecurity practices, environment, and risk tolerance when making decisions for your school regarding authentication and digital identity management. Please also consult the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency’s website and their report on cybersecurity . Use this article for general cybersecurity reminders regarding your management of Clever, Clever users, and Clever integrations. 

 If you'd like more in-depth support on district cybersecurity, such as creating a cybersecurity blueprint for your school, check out Quick start - District cybersecurity in Clever Academy . We also have teacher training, Quick start - Cybersecurity basics for teachers .

 

 
 Clever products and features that support your cybersecurity practices

 Review a summary below of Clever’s various no-cost and paid solutions for supporting your school’s cybersecurity efforts:

 No-cost security functionality 

 • Clever Admin multi-factor authentication (MFA) : All Clever Admins must set up MFA for admin accounts.
• Sharing Permissions : Configure sharing permissions for your learning applications to ensure that the application only receives data for users who need access to the application. If a user is no longer coming in via your sync, their access to any edtech tool they’ve been shared with is removed.
• Field Access settings : Adjust field access settings in addition to sharing permissions to minimize the data you are sharing and ensure that only the required data fields are shared with individual applications.
• Clever Portal Single sign-on (SSO) : Secure application accounts by providing teachers, students, and staff with one secure set of credentials that they can use to access Clever and any learning application instead of utilizing multiple insecure usernames and passwords.
• Access Logs : Utilize Clever’s robust access logs to track user logins, including IP addresses from which Clever is accessed.
• Substitute access : Help teachers securely provide temporary access to select applications on Clever for their short-term substitutes.
• Role-based access control (RBAC) : Admins can granularly manage administrators and staff at scale by giving them the minimum permissions necessary to accomplish their responsibilities on Clever. 
 
 Optional paid security solutions 

 • Classroom MFA : Ensure your staff, teacher, and student accounts are protected with layers of security with Clever’s Classroom MFA. Use school-friendly second factors like Badges, PIN codes, login pictures, and phone calls to secure accounts.
• Clever Identity Management (IDM): 
 • Life cycle management: Use Clever IDM to ensure your end users gain and lose access to their Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID accounts at the right times. Automatically manage user group membership in Google and Active Directory as well.
• Account claiming: Help users securely gain access to their new Google, Active Directory, or Entra ID accounts. There is no need to email usernames and passwords or provide a paper list to teachers; use automated account claiming to help end users gain access to their accounts easily via URLs and email notifications.
• Password generation and management: Use Clever IDM to generate secure, random passwords and phrases for end users based on the criteria you set. Provide delegated and self-serve reset and account recovery capabilities to subgroups of users with built-in complexity settings while Clever prevents usage of passwords found in public data breaches. 
 

 
 
 Cybersecurity recommendations for your district

 We recommend you review these recommendations at least once a semester to make your schools as secure as possible. 

 Keeping your Clever Admin accounts secure 

 IMPORTANT Given the unique permissions Clever Admin accounts have, we recommend carefully reviewing these recommendations whenever a new Clever Admin needs to be added or removed from your district. 

 • All Clever Admin accounts should have strong, unique passwords or passphrases that are not reused on any other service. A Clever Admin can reset their password here by following the instructions here .
 
 

 • The Clever Admin Owner of your district should be assigned to the appropriate staff member. This admin user role has special privileges, including deleting all your data from Clever and creating custom roles for other admins! For a complete list of permissions for the Clever Admin Owner, please see this article .
 
 

 • All Clever Admins must have Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) turned on.
 • If Clever Admins use an Identity Provider (IdP) such as Google to access their accounts, ensure they have MFA turned on via the IdP for access to Clever.
 

 
 

 • Audit and remove any administrative accounts that are no longer in use here .
 
 Protecting student, teacher, and staff accounts 

 • Certain district logins, like Active Directory and Google, also act as IdPs, granting access to additional resources (like Clever). Ensure students, teachers, and staff use strong, unique passwords and passphrases to access their IdP accounts. See Clever IDM for ways to ensure that your IdP accounts are always up-to-date and secure.
 
 

 • Review Clever access to Clever for anyone no longer in the district. Also, review access permissions for those who may have changed roles (i.e., a principal had a staff account but has switched to a teacher role).
 • We recommend that you implement automated account provisioning for your district identity accounts so accounts are managed automatically (so, for instance, staff members who have left your school or district no longer have access to their email accounts). See Clever IDM for more information about automated account provisioning for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID.
• You can also automatically add or remove admin accounts in Clever by syncing Clever Admins via SFTP. 
 

 
 • Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for teachers and staff to protect access to sensitive services across your organization. Consider implementing MFA for your students, as well.
 
 

 • Verify authentication attempts via Clever Access logs regularly to look for any unusual activity (such as suspicious IP addresses or multiple login attempts from an unknown user). 
• Void and reprint badges each year during your natural rollover period (i.e., summer or winter break).
 
 Keeping your sync safe 

 • Ensure that all administrative logins to your SIS, including automated service logins (such as for Clever-managed Auto-Sync ), use strong, unique passwords or passphrases.
 
 

 • If your district uses SFTP to sync data to Clever, and you believe your SFTP password was compromised, please reach out to Clever Support to reset it immediately by clicking Contact Us and answering the prompts.
 
 Protecting other applications 

 • Adding your district’s apps to Clever to access with SSO is a more secure way of offering access to users versus creating and maintaining a username and password for each app. Likewise, we recommend you regularly review any apps that are not on Clever and add them if an SSO and/or rostering integration is available.
 
 

 • Periodically audit apps you’ve added to Clever to ensure you’re sharing data responsibly:
 • Review the apps you’ve connected to in Clever, along with the data you’re sharing with them. You can review the data be

title: Sharing Permissions: Tips & Troubleshooting
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sharing rule recommendations 3. Troubleshooting 4. Check to see if a user is shared with the app 

 

 Overview

 This article covers common questions and recommendations for Share by Rules . 

 Clever Tip: To troubleshoot why a student, teacher, or staff member isn't shared with an application, use the Troubleshoot Sharing tool in the Clever Dashboard! 

 

 
 Sharing rule recommendations

 Sharing rules can be tricky! Below are suggestions on how to share / exclude the desired data from being shared with an application To view an example rule in a larger format, right-click on the image and select Open Image in New Tab . 

 
 I would like to share...

 
 Example Rule

 
 
 ...teachers without sharing any associated sections or students. ✓ Teachers X Sections X Students

 

 
 Rule Type: Share by teacher ALSO SHARE no teacher sections 

 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by teacher or Standard sharing: Share by teacher. 

 
 
 ...only specific teachers and do not want to share any associated sections or students. 

 ✓ Teachers X Exclude specific Teachers X Sections X Students 

 Rule Type: Share by teacher ALSO SHARE no teacher sections Add Filter: 
 • Teacher SIS ID > does not equal any of > XXX
 
OR
 • Email > does not equal any of > XXX
• Please ensure the email addresses match exactly as they appear in Clever (including uppercase and lowercase letters), as they are case-sensitive.
 
 

 
 
 ...teachers and their sections without sharing students.

 

 ✓ Teachers ✓ Sections X Students 

 N/A. It is not possible to share a section without the associated teachers and students. This request is usually made by accounts that don't want students and parents previewing their class assignments. To remove the ability to view classroom assignments, please see this article . 
 
 ...teachers and their sections and their students

 ✓ Teachers ✓ Sections ✓ Students 

 
 Rule Type: Custom Share by Section 

 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by section or Standard sharing: Share by section. 

 
 
 ...teachers and their sections and their students, but exclude some teachers

 ✓ Teachers X Exclude Specific Teachers ✓ Sections ✓ Students 

 Rule Type: Custom Share by Section Add Filter: 
 • Teacher SIS ID > does not equal any of > XXX
 
 

 See tutorial videos for Guided sharing: Excluding data and Standard sharing: Excluding data .

 
 
 ...teachers and their sections and their students, but exclude specific students

 ✓ Teachers ✓ Sections ✓ Students X Exclude specific Students 

 Please note: this rule is only recommended if excluding specific students when an entire school is shared with the application. For all other scenarios, please see the next row. Rule Type: Custom Share by Student ALSO SHARE student sections by course name Add Filter: 
 • Student SIS ID > does not equal any of > XXX
 
 
 
 ...teachers and their sections and their students, but exclude specific students

 ✓ Teachers ✓ Sections ✓. Students X Exclude specific Students 

 If sharing by section, it is not possible to exclude a single student from being shared. As a workaround: 
 • Create a custom section in Clever that does not include the targeted student(s) with the same school, grade level, subject, etc. as the section from the SIS 
• Exclude the SIS section in the sharing rule 
 
 Example custom section: Rule Type: Custom Share by Section Add Filter: 

 • Section SIS ID > does not equal any of > XXX
 
 
 
 ...staff and School Tech Leads

 ✓ Staff / School Tech Lead 

 
 Rule Type: Custom Share Staff ALSO SHARE no associated schools 

 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by staff or Standard sharing: Share by staff. 

 
 
 ...specific staff or School Tech Leads or exclude specific users ✓ Staff / School Tech Leads

 X Exclude specific Staff / School Tech Leads 

 Rule Type: Custom Share Staff ALSO SHARE no associated schools Add Filter: 
 • Staff SIS ID > does not equal any of > XXX
 
OR
 • Email > does not equal any of > XXX
• Please ensure the email addresses match exactly as they appear in Clever (including uppercase and lowercase letters), as they are case-sensitive.
 
 
 
 ...a bulk list of a subset of individual users by a specific field such as sis_id (i.e. students, teachers, staff)

 
 Rule Type: A custom share rule based on the user type (i.e. share by students, share by teachers, etc.)

 Steps to take: 

 Individual users can be entered in bulk if separated by semi-colons.

 To create a bulk list to copy into your sharing rule, create either a Google Sheet or Excel file with the users you need shared.

 Use the =textjoin(";",true,[range]) formula.

 For example, if you have a list of sis_ids in cells A2:A501 that you need to delimit by semi-colon and bulk paste into a sharing rule, click into a new cell and enter: =textjoin(";",true,A2:A501). The formula output will be a list of all IDs from row A2 to row A502 that you can copy and paste into the sharing rule. 

 

 
 ...Clever Admins, District Curriculum Leads, District Help Desk, or a Custom Role ✓ Admin Team Rule Type: Custom Share District Admin rule. Learn more here . 
 
 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Have a troubleshooting scenario that is not covered in this article? Please let reach out to our support team to make a suggestion!

 

 
 Record Type

 
 Problem

 
 Description

 
 Student Student not shared The most common reason that a student is not shared with an app is:
 • The student is not enrolled in a section that meets sharing requirements
 
To resolve the issue, enroll the student in a qualifying section in the SIS and sync with Clever. 
 Student Student shared that should not be shared The most common reasons that a student is shared with an app in error are: 
 • The student is enrolled in sections at multiple schools and their additional school is being shared with the app
• The student is enrolled in a section where students of a different grade level are being shared. If a section is shared, all students associated with the section are shared with the application, regardless of the student’s grade level. 
 
As a workaround for these scenarios, custom sections may be utilized as outlined here . 
 Teacher Teacher not shared The most common reasons that a teacher is not shared with an app are:
 • The teacher is not enrolled in sections in the SIS / Clever. 
• The sections they teach do not meet sharing requirements. 
 
To resolve the issue, either enroll the teacher in a section in the SIS or add a Custom Share by Teacher rule for the app. 
 Teacher Teacher shared that should not be shared The most common reasons that a teacher is shared with an app in error are: 
 • The teacher is enrolled in sections at multiple schools and their additional school is being shared with the app
• The teacher is a co-teacher on a section that is shared
• The teacher is enrolled in a section where students of a different grade level are being shared. If a section is shared, all teachers and students associated with the section are shared with the application, regardless of the teacher or student’s grade level. 
 
As a workaround for these scenarios, custom sections may be utilized as outlined here . 
 Staff Staff are not shared Staff are shared if sharing by school. If using sharing rules, an additional Custom Share by Staff rule is required. 
 Teacher and Staff (user has both account types in Clever) Teacher or staff user is not shared One user can have both a teacher and staff account in Clever, but you may want to customize which account is shared with a particular application. If you want to share only one account type with a specific application, we recommend structuring sharing rules around ID and referring to the specific teacher_id or staff_id for the user you want to share. 
 Student Contacts Student contacts are not shared Student contacts are shared with an application using Field Access permissions. When enabled, all contacts are shared –

title: Setup guide for Clever SSO into Entra ID
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Prerequisites 3. Configure Clever as an IdP for Entra ID 4. Configure Entra ID as a Service Provider (SP) for Clever 5. Verify federated authentication between Clever and Entra ID 6. Removing Clever as an IdP for Entra ID 7. Troubleshooting 

 Please note: Microsoft’s current version requirements can be found here . 

 

 Overview

 In this article, you'll learn the steps to configure Clever as a SAML identity provider (IdP) for Entra ID. Once configured, users will be able to use their Clever credentials or Badges to sign in to Entra ID, including on Windows deployments.

 

 Please note that if your school organization would like to utilize Clever Badges for sign-in to Entra-joined Windows devices, you will need to complete the steps listed below to configure Clever as an IdP for Entra ID. Once Clever is configured, students will be able to use Badges to sign in to their Entra-joined Windows devices. 

 
 Prerequisites

 To configure Clever as an IdP for Entra ID, the following prerequisites must be met:

 • An Entra ID tenant, with one or multiple custom Domain Name System (DNS) domains (i.e., domains that aren't in the format * . onmicrosoft.com )
 • If the federated domain hasn't yet been added to Entra ID, you will need to have access to the DNS domain to create a DNS record. This is required to verify the ownership of the DNS namespace
• Learn how to add your custom domain name using the Azure Active Directory portal 
 

• Access to Entra ID with an account with the Global Administrator role
• Access to Clever with an account with the Clever admin role
 
 To test federation, the following prerequisites must be met:

 • A Clever environment, with users already created
 
 Please note: Users require a matching field in Clever that is set to the same value as the ImmutableId in Entra ID. 

 • 
 • If your Entra users don’t have an ImmutableId set, use a stable, unchanging field as the identifier. Clever recommends using email, and this article will refer to email throughout. If you choose a different field, be sure to substitute it wherever email is referenced.
• If the ImmutableId does not match a field in Clever, please update your data in Clever to contain a field that matches the ImmutableId. Learn more about custom fields here . 
 

 
 • Individual Entra ID accounts are already created: each Clever user will require a matching account defined in Entra ID. These accounts are commonly created through automated solutions, for example:
 • School Data Sync (SDS)
• Entra ID Connect sync for an environment with on-premises AD DS
• PowerShell scripts that call the Microsoft Graph API
• Provisioning tools offered by the IdP - this capability is offered by Clever as a dedicated offering
 

 
 Please see here for Microsoft’s current version requirements. 

 
 Configure Clever as an IdP for Entra ID

 • To add Entra ID, please navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page, and search for the Entra ID app in the search bar. Click to request the application. 
 
 

 

 • Once the app is ready, the Clever admin will be notified via email to accept the invitation to the app
• Sign in to Clever as a Clever admin, then go to your Entra ID page via Application > My application > Entra ID, and click Begin setup to set up which of your users can access the app. Don’t worry, you can always come back and edit this setting at any time. 
 
 

 • Once you have set up sharing, go to the Settings tab and configure the Entra ID SAML app with the following details: 
 
 

 

 
 
 Variable 

 
 Value 

 
 Assertion Consumer Service Url https://login.microsoftonline.com/login.srf 
 Entity ID urn:federation:MicrosoftOnline 
 Name ID Format urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:email 
 

 

 • Ensure that the Attribute for each user type is set to the field in Clever that contains the matching value to the ImmutableId in Entra ID. If you are using the recommended value of Email, select Email from the dropdown. If you are using a custom field, select Custom from the dropdown and enclose the custom field name in double curly brackets using all lowercase, for example, {{ext.field_name}}.
 
 The other parameters should already be pre-configured. Please take note of the Identity Provider Metadata URL value, as it will be required in the next step. For example: ">https://samlidp.clever.com/saml-azure-ad/metadata/<GUID> ;

 Please note: Federation won't work if the app has a launch date set in the future. Once the application is ready to be used, ask Clever Support to change the launch date. 

 

 
 Configure Entra ID as a Service Provider (SP) for Clever

 The configuration of Entra ID consists of changing the authentication method for the custom DNS domains. This configuration can be done using PowerShell. 

 Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser -Force
 
 

 Note, depending on your environment, the command above may give the following error: Set-ExecutionPolicy: Operation is not supported on this platform. In these cases, simply proceed with the remaining commands.

 Modify the $idpMetadataUrl and $DomainName variables of the following script to match your environment. When prompted to authenticate to Entra ID, use the credentials of an account with the Global Administrator role.

 Please note: In the code below, https://clever.com/logout should be in "double quotes" - there is a glitch with our Help Center preventing these from showing up, but please add them when you use the script. 

 

 Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser
Import-Module Microsoft.Graph

$domainId = "your domain here"
$idpMetadataUrl = "your SAML metadata URL here"

$xml = [Xml](Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $idpMetadataUrl -ContentType "application/xml").content

$cert = -join $xml.EntityDescriptor.IDPSSODescriptor.KeyDescriptor.KeyInfo.X509Data.X509Certificate.Split()
$issuerUri = $xml.EntityDescriptor.entityID
$signinUri = $xml.EntityDescriptor.IDPSSODescriptor.SingleSignOnService | ? { $_.Binding.Contains('Redirect') } | % { $_.Location }
$signoutUri = https://clever.com/logout 
$displayName = "Clever Identity"
Connect-MGGraph -Scopes "Domain.ReadWrite.All", "Directory.AccessAsUser.All"

$domainAuthParams = @{
 DomainId = $domainId
 IssuerUri = $issuerUri
 DisplayName = $displayName
 ActiveSignInUri = $signinUri
 PassiveSignInUri = $signinUri
 SignOutUri = $signoutUri
 SigningCertificate = $cert
 PreferredAuthenticationProtocol = "saml"
 federatedIdpMfaBehavior = "acceptIfMfaDoneByFederatedIdp"
}

New-MgDomainFederationConfiguration @domainAuthParams
 
 

 Note, the configuration may take up to 60 minutes to update before end users will be redirected to your Clever Portal login when navigating to https://portal.azure.com/ .

 
 Verify federated authentication between Clever and Entra ID

 

 From a private browser session, navigate to https://portal.azure.com and sign in with a Clever account:

 • As the username, use the email as defined in Clever
• The user will be redirected to Clever to sign in
• After Clever authentication, the user will be redirected back to Entra ID and be signed in
 
 

 

 

 

 
 Removing Clever as an IdP for Entra ID

 

 To remove Clever as an IdP for your Entra ID domain, run the following command using PowerShell: Remove-MgDomainFederationConfiguration -DomainId < EntraDomain.org > For more information, see Microsoft Support or reach out to your Microsoft support contact.

 

 
 Troubleshooting

 

 Entra is not recognizing my user 

 This usually happens when the ImmutableId field in Entra is not being matched for this user in Clever. This may look like Microsoft error AADSTS51004 or AADSTS90072.

 

 To correct:

 • Double check what the ImmutableId is set to for this user in Entra using the following PowerShell - replace [ user@example.org ] with the UserId for your user. Check the value displayed after OnPremisesImmutableId.
 
 get-mguser -UserId [ user@d

title: Blackbaud API Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. School Year Rollover 4. Default Field Mappings 5. Troubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 ✓ Syncs every six hours. 
 ✓ Manual syncs can be triggered in Clever Dashboard by navigating to Data sources> SIS sync > Settings > Sync Control and clicking Sync Now 
 ✓ 
 Syncs students, teachers, sections, schools

 • To sync to Clever, students and teachers must have usernames populated in Blackbaud
 
 
 ✓ Syncs multiple student contacts per student • This is an optional setting in Blackbaud under Rostering options 
 
 
 ✓ Supports co-teachers 
 ✓ Supports custom field mappings 
 X Does not sync users to the “staff” role in Clever. Staff can be added to Clever using Custom Staff 
 
 

 If interested in using the Blackbaud API Sync with an existing Clever account, please contact support. Clever highly recommends that any transitions between SISs or sync types be completed during the summer. For more information on changing sync types, please click here .

 

 
 Setup

 1. Create API Credentials 

 To generate credentials, follow these directions . Clever recommends the following settings: 

 Provider 

 • Name: Clever
• Provider: Standard
• Select Use Legacy OAuth 1.0 authentication 
 
 Rostering Options 

 • School Years: Current year
• Multi-term classes: One record per term
• Offering Types
 • Academic Groups: Required
• Activity Groups: Optional
• Advisory Groups: Optional
 

• Courses: Return all
• Parents: Include or exclude all parents
 
 Gradebook Options Clever does not support grade passback. No options need to be selected. 

 Please note: The following fields require the district to set Integration Mappings in Blackbaud in order for the data to pass to Clever: • Gender • Ethnicity For more information on Integration mappings, click here .

 2. Enter API Credentials in Clever 

 In the Clever Dashboard, navigate to Sync > Settings and enter the following credentials from Blackbaud 

 • Web Access URL: https://{ schoolDomain}. myschoolapp.com/ims/oneroster/v1p1/ 
• Consumer Key: “Key: from Blackbaud
• New Consumer Secret: “Secret” from Blackbaud
• Confirm New Consumer Secret: “Secret” from Blackbaud
 
 3. Sync 

 On the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab , find Sync Controls and click Sync Now. 

 

 Please note: The Blackbaud API sync can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every six hours, but the time of day that the syncs occur isn't customizable. Syncs can always be initiated at any time from your Clever Dashboard on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab > Sync Controls box > Sync Now button. 

 

 
 School Year Rollover

 Data for new school years will be passed to Clever once the district has rolled over to the new school year in Blackbaud. If a district would like to rollover sooner, follow these directions in Blackbaud: 

 • Navigate to Core , hover over Settings in the navigation, and select Integration settings . On the Integration settings page, choose OneRoster Settings .
• Open the Clever configuration and choose Roster Options 
• For School Years , check Next Year 
• Click Save 
 
 In Clever:

 • Navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab , find Sync Controls and click Sync Now . Please allow 15-20 minutes for the sync to complete. 
 
 If rolling over to the new school year, the sync will likely prompt a sync hold. Review the sync hold – if everything looks correct, click Approve Changes to allow the new changes to process into Clever. For more information on sync holds, please see this article: Sync Holds. 

 
 Default Field Mappings

 Below are the default field mappings for the Blackbaud OneRoster API sync that are managed by Clever. If you'd like to customize field mappings, please contact Clever Support. 

 • If a field is blank, the field is either not pulled by default, or not accessible to Clever
• The Blackbaud OneRoster API sync does not support custom field mappings, but it does support value mappings
• First_name fields depend on settings within Blackbaud and are based on the user first name or the user preferred name. Clever will sync whichever name is in the user.givenName field of the Blackbaud API. If you wish to alter the first_name field, please work with Blackbaud to alter the field surfaced via the API.
• If a field is not coming through with the correct value, Clever Support may need to complete a value mapping. This is common for: Grade, FRL_Status, ELL_Status, and IEP_Status.
 
 

 Please note: If you’d like to sync additional data to Clever, you can do so via our automated custom data sync for auto sync districts and schools. Please reach out to Clever Support for access to your Clever SFTP Directory. You'll need to set up any automatic exports to the remote site, as Clever doesn't provide support for automation scripting. 

 

 Schools 

 
 Clever

 
 Blackbaud Education Management Field

 
 OneRoster Field

 
 School_ID "org-sch-" + school ID + "-" + school level ID org.sourcedId 
 School_Name school level name org.name 
 School_number school level abbreviation org.identifier 
 State_ID
