 Low_Grade High_Grade school level grades (uses integration mappings for values) org.metadata.gradeRange 
 Principal 
 Principal_Email 
 School_address School_city School_state School_zip Core > Settings > School & grade levels school level address (else if blank, uses main address) org.metadata.address1 org.metadata.address2 org.metadata.address3 org.metadata.city org.metadata.state org.metadata.postCode 
 School_Phone 
 
 

 Students

 Student_Number user id user.identifier 
 State_ID user State ID (Contact card - General information) user.metadata.stateId 
 First_Name either (depends on given name setting): - user first name - user preferred name, else if blank then first name user.givenName 
 Middle_Name user middle name user.middleName

 Gender* demographics.sex

 Hispanic_Latino demographics.hispanicOrLatinoEthnicity

 ELL_Status Located in Accommodations demographics.metadata.ellStatus

 Student_street Student_city Student_state Student_zip user address, primary (Contact card - Address tile) user.metadata.address1 user.metadata.address2 user.metadata.address3 user.metadata.city user.metadata.state user.metadata.postCode 
 Student_Email user email (Contact card) user.email

 * Requires integration mappings in Blackbaud 

 

 Contacts

 Teachers

title: Secure Sync: Changing SIS IDs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. The Process 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 When you sync your data to Clever, you are required to provide an SIS ID for each user type, section, and school as follows:

 Data Type SIS ID Field 
 School School_id 
 Student Student_id 
 Teacher Teacher_id 
 Section Section_id 
 Staff (optional) Staff_id (if your school syncs staff) 
 Contacts (optional) Contact_sis_id (cannot be matched if ID changes occur) 
 
 

 These identifiers are what Clever uses to generate the Clever ID, so if the SIS ID changes, the Clever ID will change as well. The Clever ID is what creates the user and school accounts in Clever and in most connected applications. For this reason, Clever does not recommend changing SIS IDs, especially during a school year. We recommend that all SIS ID changes be made in the summer between academic years. 

 If an SIS ID change is being requested by an application partner: Clever Support can update the data available to the application without affecting the core data in Clever or the data available to other applications. Please submit a support case with the following information: • The name of the application (Example: NWEA) • The record type (Example: Students) • The name of the field to be used as SIS ID (Example: State_id)

 

 Here’s an outline, with an accompanying flowchart, of how changing an SIS ID can result in the loss of historic data.

 • Student Jamie Greene has an account in Clever with student_id 7301.
• Clever created the Clever ID of 641cbb82f81de671203cfc66 for Jamie’s account based on the SIS ID.
• The student is shared with several applications via Clever and has accounts in each of those apps based on that Clever ID.
• At some point, the school changes Jamie’s student_id to C732001 and syncs the new ID to Clever, so student_id 7301 is no longer syncing.
• Clever now sees this as a new student and deletes Jamie’s account associated with student_id 7301 since this ID is no longer syncing to Clever.
• Clever creates a new account based on C732001 with a new Clever ID of 529ava3902efa349206cfs01.
• Jamie’s connected applications are no longer receiving Jamie’s original Clever ID because it no longer exists.
• The applications create a new account based on Jamie’s new Clever ID and delete or archive the previous account based on Clever ID 641cbb82f81de671203cfc66. This results in a loss of historical data and any progress since it is a completely new account.
 
 Please note: When a teacher_id changes and data is not preserved, the teacher will lose their historical data in Clever and connected applications, as well as their Teacher Pages and resources they have added in their Clever Portal. 

 

 How does Clever help schools preserve data? 

 How do we preserve the Clever IDs if you need to change your sync and/or SIS IDs? By running our matching tool! Our matching tool matches up the existing SIS IDs in Clever with the new SIS IDs your schools are syncing. Please note that we can only match school_id, student_id, teacher_id, and staff_id values to preserve data. We are unable to match contact_sis_id, and section_ids are unlikely to be matched as well, since section_ids are expected to change during a conversion. If we run into duplicate entries or other issues, this can slow the process, potentially adding several days to a week to the conversion. 

 
 

 The Process

 IMPORTANT: Reusing SIS IDs Clever treats SIS IDs as unique identifiers and uses them to generate Clever IDs. To avoid data issues in Clever and connected applications, do not reuse SIS IDs within 300 days of when they were last used. Reusing IDs can add more than a week to the sync conversion process. 

 If a change to the SIS ID is required, the following process will be followed. 

 • Contact Clever Support Submit a new support case at support.clever.com and let us know the record type that needs to be updated and the name of the field that should be used as the SIS ID in Clever. 
• Migrate data We will enable protection settings for your account to ensure your teachers and students are minimally affected by the data changes. During this period, all data syncing between the district, Clever, and connected applications will be paused. However, users will still be able to access Clever and their applications.

 Once the safety settings are applied, we ensure the necessary data from your SIS is syncing to Clever. If the data looks good, we will complete a matching process to preserve historical data where possible.

⚠️ Please allow up to 2-3 weeks for the matching process to be completed. During this time, your account may have a sync status of "On Hold" or "System Maintenance In Progress". This is normal and will resolve once Clever Support has finished the migration process. 
• Confirm data changes, sync with apps The final step of the process is for our team to review the changes with you. Once the review is complete, you will release the hold. Once the updated data is live in Clever, please allow up to 48 hrs for the updated data to update in your connected applications. Application partners manage pulling data from Clever and processing it into their system. If you have any questions about the effect of data changes in an application, please contact the application's support team. They will be happy to assist!
 
 Ready to get started or have a question? Contact our Support Team . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Which SIS IDs can Clever preserve? 

 • School_id, student_id, teacher_id, staff_id. The Clever team cannot preserve contact_sis_id data. Additionally, our migration tool is not configured to preserve section IDs, as it is expected that section IDs will change during a sync conversion. However, if section IDs need to be preserved for an app connection, you can speak with the Clever team to discuss potential options.
 
 Is there an option if I need more guidance and would like additional services from Clever for my sync conversion? 

 • Yes! We offer Sync Conversion Consulting Services. The sync conversion process is a collaborative effort involving the district, Clever, and connected applications to achieve a smooth transition. While our sync conversion team is committed to helping you meet your goals, opting for our paid services can provide additional consultation and support to streamline the process further. Contact our team here to learn more . 
 
 Why does the sync or SIS ID conversion take 2-3 weeks? 

 • 
 Anytime you execute record linkage between data sets, the integrity of the data is always at risk. Sync conversions involve numerous steps that must be performed accurately, and depending on the complexity and size of the data, this can take time.

• 
 During a school break is the best time to complete a sync conversion because the account is often already in a sync pause status. Many districts are reaching out to our teams to complete a sync conversion during the winter or summer break, which can impact timelines for completion. 

 
 What if I don’t need any historical data preserved? I don’t need to maintain their historical data in Clever or the connected applications. 

 • If this is the case, you can forego the sync conversion process. Teachers will lose any Teacher Pages created in Clever, and all users will lose historical data in their connected applications. Please provide in writing that you do not want our teams to preserve the historical data and that you understand that your users will no longer have any historical data in Clever or their connected apps.

title: Clever Portal: Launch a Kahoot
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Launching a Kahoot 3. The Student experience 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 PLEASE NOTE If you’re not seeing the Launch a Kahoot! button, make sure you have added the Kahoot! app from the Clever Library . 

 Overview

 Teachers can save classroom time by launching Kahoot! directly from their Clever Portal by going to their Kahoot! icon > three vertical dots ( ⋮ ) > Launch a Kahoot! 

 

 
 Launching a Kahoot

 Before you begin, ensure that you have the Kahoot! application in your Clever Portal. If your district hasn't added it for you, you can add it as a Clever Library app. Click here to learn how to add Kahoot! as a Clever Library app. When you’re ready to launch a Kahoot!, make sure your students have logged in to Clever and have the Clever Portal open on their devices . Then, follow these steps to start your game:

 • Navigate to Create.kahoot.it and either create a Kahoot or select an existing one you want to use.
• Start the Kahoot and copy the game PIN .
• In the Clever Portal, go to Kahoot! app > three vertical dots ( ⋮ ) > Launch a Kahoot! 
• Paste the game PIN into the e nter Game PIN field. 
 • You can optionally check the Populate student initials box, which will automatically add students' initials instead of letting them add their own nicknames.
 

• Click Next, choose your students .
• Click Next , then select the class(es) or individual student(s) you want to launch the Kahoot for. Click Continue when ready.
• Ensure all students have their Clever Portal open on their device. Once all students are logged in, click Launch Kahoot. 
• The Kahoot! will open in the students’ Clever browser tab. A confirmation will appear on your screen.
• Once your students are in the game, don't forget to share your screen to show the questions in Kahoot! 
 
 

 
 The student experience

 Instead of navigating to kahoot.it to enter the game PIN, when teachers launch Kahoot! to students through Clever Portal, students will automatically enter the game and be prompted to create their nickname. After teachers select Launch Kahoot!, the game will launch for students in the same tab that Clever was in, where they’ll be prompted to enter their nickname. After they enter their nickname and select the Ok, go button, they can start playing. If you've chosen for their initials to populate, they'll be dropped right into the game without needing to click anything.

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Why don’t I see the Launch a Kahoot feature in my Clever Portal? 

 • This feature is currently only available for teachers that have access to the Kahoot! application on Clever.
 
 How can I gain access to the Kahoot! application if my district hasn't added it for me? 

 • For an enhanced experience, contact your school or district administrator to see if access to the Kahoot! EDU application can be enabled for you.
• Alternatively, you can install the Kahoot! application from the Clever Library. Learn how to install applications from the Clever Library here. 
 
 Note: Some districts choose to disable the Clever Library. If you don’t see it in your Clever Portal, it’s likely that your district has turned off this feature.

 

 How does Kahoot treat adding initials for students who have the same initials? 

 • If multiple students share the same initials, the first student to launch the app will claim those initials. Any students who try to join afterward with the same initials will see a message that the nickname is taken and will be prompted to choose a different one.

title: Single Sign-on (SSO): Setting up Clever Badges and Chromebook logins with Clever via the Legacy setup in Google
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever and Chromebook Logins 3. Setting up Clever logins via the Legacy setup in Google • Prerequisites for setting up Badge or credentials-based logins • Part 1: Configuring SSO • Part 2: Configuring User and Device settings • Part 3: Testing on a Chromebook 4. Adding additional identity providers as SSO Profiles in Google 5. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 Clever works seamlessly with Chromebooks, providing students and teachers access to all their learning applications while only logging in once per device. Google supports several ways to configure utilizing other identity providers (IdPs) within the Google Admin Console. This article covers how to configure Clever as the organization-wide single sign-on (SSO) provider for Google, which is a legacy setup and no longer our recommended workflow for most districts.

 IMPORTANT Our general recommendation is to set up Clever as an individual SSO Profile in Google , as this offers more customization. For instance, if you want some students to log into Chromebooks with Clever Badges but other students to use a Google email and password. If you’d like to set up Clever as an individual SSO Profile in Google, please see this article . 

 

 
 Clever and Chromebook logins

 There are two login flows for using Clever with Chromebooks:

 • Students use Clever Badges to log into Chromebooks then access Clever. 
 • Students open their Chromebook and scan their Clever Badge
• This automatically logs the students into their Chromebooks and Clever Portals (they don't need to log in again).
• Students can click on any learning application to begin their learning session.
• Use case: This is best for younger students and any students who might struggle with typing as they can access learning apps with zero typing.
 

• Users log into Chromebooks manually, using credentials, then access Clever. 
 • Users open Chromebooks and log in with their school-provided Google login credentials.
• After logging in, the Clever Portal is automatically opened for them (i.e., the user does not need to log in again).
• Users can click on any application icon to begin their learning session.
• Use case: This is best for older students who don't use Badges.
 

 
 Depending on your preferences, each method can be configured separately for different Organizational Units (OUs) in your Google Admin Console.

 
 Setting up Clever logins via the legacy setup in Google

 
 The simplest method to configure either Badges into Chromebooks logins or set up your users’ Chromebooks to launch and log them into Clever automatically is to set up Clever as the organization-wide SSO Profile for your Google account.

 

 If Clever is set as the organization-wide SSO Profile for your Google accounts, you can add additional SSO profiles for other IdPs. You can learn more about this in Google’s support documentation here .

 

 Prerequisites for setting up Badge or credentials-based logins

 Prerequisites for Badge Logins 

 Please note: Clever Badges into Chromebooks are not compatible with Kiosk Mode , and you must have the "set up SSO with third-party identity provider" checkbox selected under the Third-party SSO profile for your organization section of Google Admin. 

 

 • Enable Clever Badges. 
 • Head to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges. Select Begin Setup. 
 

• Confirm Google email addresses are populated for students in Clever. 
 • 
 To confirm if email addresses are populated, search for a few test students and confirm on their profile page if the email field is populated.

If you utilize a student information system (SIS) Auto Sync and email addresses are missing, please navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings page to specify which field in your SIS contains student emails. After your next sync, email addresses should be added to Clever. Please note: If you do not see the SIS field name containing student email addresses, contact Clever Support for assistance. 
 

 
 Prerequisites for credential-based logins 

 • Confirm Google SSO 
 • 
 Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings . Confirm Google is configured as a login method. For more information on setting up Google SSO, see this Help Center Article . 

 

• Confirm Google email addresses are populated for students in Clever. 
 • 
 To confirm if email addresses are populated, search for a few test students and confirm on their profile page if the email field is populated.

If you utilize a student information system (SIS) Auto Sync and email addresses are missing, please navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings page to specify which field in your SIS contains student emails. After your next sync, email addresses should be added to Clever. Please note: If you do not see the SIS field name containing student email addresses, contact Clever Support for assistance. 
 

 
 Part 1: Configuring SSO

 Follow these step-by-step instructions for the best experience. These settings ensure that you set up Clever as an SSO mechanism into Google for your intended students without impacting how other students and teachers log into Google today in their web browsers.

 Please note: Need more info about these settings? Check out this article on Google Workspace Admin Help. 

 

 • 
 In your Google Admin Console , navigate to Security > Authentication > SSO with third-party IDP and click on Legacy SSO Profile.

• 
 Using the information from the Authentication > Badges > Chromebook tab page in your Clever Dashboard, complete the following in the Google Admin Console Legacy SSO Profile form.

 • 
 Tick the box for Enable legacy SSO profile.

• 
 Copy your unique Sign-in page URL and paste it into the form.

• 
 Copy your unique Sign-out page URL and paste it into the form.

• 
 Download your verification certificate and then upload it into the Google Admin Console.

• 
 Tick the box for Use a domain specific issuer.

• 
 Copy the network mask into the form.

 • 
 Do not enter network masks other than the one provided from Clever. Entering a different network mask will result in undesired behavior when accessing Google and Chrome devices.

 

• 
 Leave Change Password URL as-is. Leaving this URL blank will allow students to change their password.

 

• 
 Click Save and continue to the next step.

 
 Part 2: Configuring user and device settings

 You can apply these settings to individual Organizational Units (OUs), if you want to enable Badges into Chromebooks for only certain schools, grade levels, or types of users. The student account and the device they’ll be using for Badges into Chromebooks need to be in an OU with these settings! You may consider setting up a test OU with a test student and a test device.

 These settings allow students to log in with their Clever Badges from the moment the Chromebook screen turns on. They also enable the transfer of cookies, which lets them log into Clever and Google whenever they use a Clever Badge. 

 Please note: You can quickly find each setting in Google by using Ctrl + F or CMD + F and typing in the name of the setting. 

 See below for written instructions, a video guide, or click here for a step-by-step clickable tutorial of the setup! 

 

 

 Configuring user settings 

 • Navigate to Devices > Chrome > Settings > Users & browsers and scroll down to the Security section.
• Set Lock Screen settings to Do not allow locking screen .
• Set the following Idle settings.
 • Set Action on idle to Logout 
• Set Action on lid close to "Logout" 
• Set Lock screen on sleep to Don't lock screen 
 

• Scroll a little bit until you reach the Single sign-on and SAML single sign-on login frequency settings in the Security section.
 • Set Single sign-on to Enable SAML-based single sign on for Chrome devices .
• Set SAML single sign-on login frequency to an appropriate duration.

title: Clever + Brainpop: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. BrainPOP applications 3. Setup process 4. Required fields 5. Troubleshooting 6. Frequently Asked Questions 7. Support contacts 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through how to get set up with BrainPOP apps in Clever. Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: clever.com/signup or by accepting your invitation from BrianPOP. For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide . 

 Please note: BrainPOP has several apps on Clever. The BrainPOP Suite app powers rostering services. You’ll receive separate invites to BrainPOP, BrainPOP Jr., ELL, Español + Français, and/or Science SSO apps, depending on your school subscriptions. Please approve the SSO apps and leave the BrainPOP Suite app connected. 

 

 
 BrainPOP applications

 

 
 Application Name

 
 Icon

 
 Description

 
 BrainPOP Suite BrainPOP offers cross-curricular movies and supporting features like coding projects, movie making, concept mapping, games, and primary source activities. Recommended for elementary and middle schoolers, as well as English language learners. BrainPOP Suite powers rostering for all BrainPOP products. 
 BrainPOP BrainPOP offers cross-curricular movies and supporting features like coding projects, movie making, concept mapping, games, and primary source activities. Recommended for upper elementary and middle schoolers. 
 BrainPOP Jr. BrainPOP’s K-3 resource, offering cross-curricular movies and supporting features like coding projects, movie making, concept mapping, games, and primary source activities. 
 BrainPOP ELL BrainPOP’s comprehensive English language learning program, featuring animated movies that model conversational English while seamlessly introducing grammar and vocabulary. 
 BrainPOP Español BrainPOP Español offers cross-curricular movies and supporting features in Spanish. Recommended for upper elementary and middle schoolers. 
 BrainPOP Français BrainPOP Français offers cross-curricular movies and supporting features in French. Recommended for upper elementary and middle schoolers. 
 BrainPOP Science BrainPOP Science is a phenomenon-driven middle school science product that provides robust investigations, units, embedded assessments, and actionable diagnostics for 6-8th grade across Physical, Life, and Earth & Space Science. 
 
 

 
 Setup process

 If your district is already on Clever, you can connect BrainPOP’s applications in one of two ways: If BrainPOP invites you to connect, you can follow the directions in the emails you receive. You’ll receive separate invites to the BrainPOP Suite, along with BrainPOP Jr., ELL, Español + Français, and/or Science SSO apps, depending on your school subscriptions. You can also request the apps needed to get going by following the steps below. 

 Part 1: Set up rostering 

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and click Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for BrainPOP Suite – this app manages rostering for all BrainPOP applications. 
• You'll then be brought to a page where you can authorize your BrainPOP request. As Clever SSO and rostering for BrainPOP are provided at no cost to the district, you can mark that it has already been purchased for the district.
• You'll also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the BrainPOP icons in their Clever portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request !
 
 Part 2: Set up Single Sign-on (SSO) 

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and click Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for the BrainPOP product your district will be using, such as BrainPOP Jr. or Español – this app will manage Single Sign-on (SSO) into the app for your district users.
• You'll then be brought to a page to authorize your BrainPOP request. As Clever SSO and rostering for BrainPOP are provided at no cost to the district, you can mark that it has already been purchased for the district.
• You'll also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the BrainPOP icons in their Clever portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request !
 
 Once you have completed both portions above, the BrainPOP team will set up the initial sync and will reach out to you to discuss communicating with teachers. 

 Recommended sharing 

 Before sharing, ensure that students and teachers are in their correct grade levels and that classes are updated for the current school year in your Student Information System (SIS). BrainPOP recommends that you share all data for your district. The simplest way to do so is to select “Share All Data” in Clever. If you plan to share specific schools, you can find more info about “Share by Rules” here . You can create a sharing rule by going to the Sharing Permissions page for BrainPOP Suite. To do this, log in to your Clever account and click the icon for BrainPOP Suite in the sidebar. From the Data Sharing tab, click Share All Data . 

 
 Required fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. BrainPOP requires the following data fields be sent:

 • Student: name, school, sis_id, district, schools, graduation_year, credentials
• Teacher: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, credentials, email, state_id, teacher_number, title
• School Admin: district, name, schools, staff_id, email, credentials, title, roles
• District admin: district, name.first, name.last, email, title
 
 

 IMPORTANT Be sure to keep the BrainPOP Suite app connected, as this powers rostering for all BrainPOP applications. 

 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Apostrophes Please keep in mind that if you're syncing any users whose names have apostrophes, they must be the vertical type (') instead of a curled version. If it's curled, it will trigger a data error and filter out the user!

 Commas BrainPOP doesn't currently support syncing users with commas in their name. Please remove the comma from the user's name in your data sync with Clever to prevent errors or issues. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data error”? 

 • A data error occurs when the record is incompatible with BrainPOP’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Errors must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. Contact Clever Support if you have questions about resolving your data error.
• A data warning occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning. 
 
 Why is a student or teacher missing from BrainPOP? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to BrainPOP:
 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
• Is shared with BrainPOP in your data-sharing permissions
 

 
 Can multiple teachers/staff be assigned to the same course/section? 

 • BrainPOP can support multiple teachers or staff assigned to the same course/section.
 
 Can multiple teachers/staff be assigned to the same course/section? 

 • BrainPOP can support multiple teachers or staff assigned to the same course/section.
 
 Why do I have to approve invites to multiple applications? 

 • BrainPOP has multiple student- and teacher-facing SSO applications in Clever (BrainPOP, BrainPOP Jr, BrainPOP ELL), all of which roster via the BrainPOP Rostering application. 
• To use any BrainPOP product via Clever, please ensure that you’ve connected ‘BrainPOP Suite’ and the SSO appli

title: Clever + NWEA MAP Growth: Sharing Program status
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Required Fields 3. Sharing Program Status: Setup Process 4. Frequently Asked Questions 5. Support Contacts 

 

 Overview

 Before reading this guide, please ensure your district is set up with Clever and that you've added the NWEA MAP Growth app to your district's Clever account! If your district hasn't been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: clever.com/signup . For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide . A District Admin must add the application in Clever. To add the NWEA MAP Growth app, search NWEA MAP Growth in the search bar of your Clever account . When it comes to rostering for NWEA MAP Growth with Clever, one of the most common requests from districts is for the capability to summarize test results by program (e.g., 504, IEP, special ed, etc.). To address this need, the MAP system supports importing program designations from Clever, making program data available in several MAP Growth reports. The “ Sharing Program Status: Setup Process ” section of this article explains how to share programs from Clever to NWEA MAP Growth.

 
 Required Fields

 Check the Data Quality tab of the NWEA MAP Growth app in your Clever Dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. NWEA MAP Growth requires the following data fields to be sent: Student: 

 • sis_id
• name
• district
• school(s)
• grade
• race
• gender
• dob
• hispanic_ethnicity
• state_id (if you’re using NWEA as part of a state program)
• frl_status, iep_status, ell_status and extension fields are optional
 
 Teacher: 

 • district
• name
• school(s)
• sis_id
• email
 
 If you’re using NWEA as part of a state program, you may have additional fields required by your state, including but not limited to the state_id field at the school level. Please see your state program documentation for the fields required by your state. 

 
 

 Sharing Program Status: Setup Process

 If you’d like to import program designations from Clever into MAP, please read the following setup process.

 Step 1: Find the MAP Custom Names for the programs you'll sync 

 When Clever sends program data to MAP (e.g., if a student is an ELL), the MAP system will attempt to import the data using the MAP Custom Names for the programs you’ve defined. Therefore, a good first step is to identify the MAP Custom Names that are already entered the MAP system (if any). To find the Custom Names for the programs in your district: 

 • Log in to MAP at teach.mapnwea.org .
• In the left navigation menu, click Modify Preferences > Modify District . 
• Scroll to the Programs section. A list of programs showing the NWEA Standard Name and Custom Name appears. 
• Note the Custom Names for programs you want to sync using Clever.
 
 Please keep in mind:

 • The NWEA Standard Name isn't used to match program data from Clever. 
• The Custom Name is the name that shows on reports. 
• It's possible for multiple Custom Names to have the same NWEA Standard Name, and it's also possible for the Custom Name to be identical to the NWEA Standard Name. 
• If you need a program added (such as Individualized Education Plan (IEP)) that isn't a default value in the MAP System, you'll need to add the program in NWEA if you want this program data to sync from Clever. Follow these steps:
 
 • In the left navigation menu of NWEA, select Modify Preferences > Modify District .
• Click Add Program , then select Other as the Standard Name and paste the program name, like "Individualized Education Plan (IEP)" into the Custom Name text field.
• Click Submit to save.
 
 

 Step 2: Learn about Clever standard fields 

 Clever has three standard fields that are pre-populated in the Clever system.

 • ell_status
• frl_status
• iep_status 
 
 These three Clever standard fields, if shared with the MAP System, will automatically link up with the following MAP Custom Names if the MAP Custom Names are present in your MAP system (see chart below). This table provides an overview of values in Clever and what you would see in the MAP System under Modify Preferences > Modify District . 

 Step 3: Identify if you need to modify or create new extension fields in Clever 

 Now that you understand that Clever has three default fields that align with a small handful of common school programs, you can determine if you need to add or modify extension fields in Clever to identify additional programs in your school (e.g., talented & gifted, after-school classes, etc.). The easiest way to do this is to compare the extension fields in Clever to the list of MAP Custom Names:

 • If the extension fields in Clever perfectly match (including spelling and punctuation) the list of MAP Custom Names, then no action is needed, and your data should transfer accurately.
• If you have extension fields in Clever (e.g., Talented & Gifted) that aren't on the list of MAP Custom Names, you will need to create new MAP Custom Names to match these extension fields.
• If you have MAP Custom Names (e.g., After School Program) that don't have a matching Clever extension field, you will need to create a new (or modify a current) extension field to match the MAP Custom.
 
 Please note: The extension fields in Clever must perfectly match the MAP Custom Name (including spelling and punctuation ). 

 Step 4: Creating new extension fields in Clever 

 You can create new extension fields in Clever by following the directions in this Help Center article . The exact process will depend on the sync type you have set up to connect your Student Information System (SIS) to Clever. If you need help syncing the data from your SIS into Clever in the correct format, contact Clever Support by clicking the blue chat box in the bottom right corner of the Help Center. The names of the Clever extension fields that the MAP system accepts are listed in the right-hand column in the table below. The data you'll enter in the CSV file to assign a particular student to a program will be the exact Custom Name of the program you have listed in the MAP system. Let's look at an example of students who are assigned different programs in Clever (via extension fields) that will then be sent over to MAP as a Standard Name or Custom Name: In the above example, you see that:

 • Student 123 will be assigned to two programs in MAP: the Remote Learning program and the Gifted and Talented program.
• Student 456 will also be assigned to the Remote Learning program, as well as the Section 504 program.
• Student 789 is assigned to two programs in MAP: the Remote Learning and Economically Disadvantaged programs.
 
 

 Step 5: Share data with NWEA 

 Now that the data is synced to Clever, you can share the data with NWEA. The instructions for sharing custom fields in Clever can be found here . Since these fields are considered sensitive, you must provide special permission for Clever to share these fields with MAP. Refer to For District Admins: Syncing Sensitive Fields (i.e., ELL status, IEP status, FRL status) for instructions. 

 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I add multiple “other” programs as Clever Extension Fields? 

 • For example, a district might have a program with a Custom Name in MAP called “Volleyball” and another program with a Custom Name called “Economically Disadvantaged”.
 
 You can add multiple "other" extension fields! If you're using a CSV file to upload your extension fields, you'll simply list all of the extension fields for the student within the ext.other column, separating each field with a comma, as shown in this example, a student with sis_id 789 is assigned Remote Learning and Economically Disadvantaged: 

 What if I have multiple programs that might match a different custom field name (such as ELL)? 

 • If no students will ever be assigned to both programs (for example, a district might have “ELL Spanish” and “ELL Arabic” programs and students can only ever be assigned to 1 of these at

title: For Teachers: Using Clever as a long-term substitute teacher
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What’s Clever 3. How to get access to Clever 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 If you’re a long-term substitute for a school, this article is for you! This article will run through what Clever is at a high level, how to get access, and FAQs. If you've been assigned a Sub Badge via Clever's Substitute Access feature, please find more information here . 

 
 What’s Clever

 Clever connects school organizations with the applications and programs they use every day.

 Students and teachers sign in to Clever and can then access hundreds of applications and programs. With just one username and password, students and teachers can get into many edtech programs, making digital learning quicker and easier. In addition, Clever shares up-to-date class rosters with apps and programs, so the class list is always accurate across all programs. When a new student is added or removed from a class, Clever shares this update with all programs connected to Clever. Finally, with Clever, teachers have access to troubleshooting tools. They can easily help students who are having trouble logging in without needing to contact the IT department.

 

 

 

 Why might you need to use Clever as a sub? 

 Substitute teachers may cover a class for a short-term leave, like when someone is sick, or you may be covering a long-term leave, like a parental or medical leave. If you're a long-term sub who needs to work with students in their online curriculum, using Clever is the best way to keep track of students and their technology-based learning.

 

 
 How to get access to Clever

 The school organization you are working with set up Clever, so you’ll need to reach out to the schools you’re working at to request access. Typically, a school's IT or technology team manages Clever, so you should ask your point of contact (such as the principal or a school admin) to reach out to the IT team. Once you’ve been added to Clever by the school organization, you’ll log in using their unique Clever web address, such as Clever.com/in/school-organization-name . If you don’t know this URL, you can also click here to search for the school and district . Keep in mind that the school organization's account must be add you before you can access Clever. 

 To learn more about Clever, you can check out our free, on-demand training: Clever Academy ! In as little as 10 minutes, you can learn more about Clever’s powerful features and use them to support digital learning. Please note that you must already have a Clever account before you can access Clever Academy. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Who can I reach out to if I don’t have Clever access? 

 • Please reach out to your main point of contact at the school where you’re subbing (e.g., a principal).
 
 What if I am a short-term sub covering for a few days? 

 • Clever's Substitute Access feature will be the best fit for you! Instead of being assigned your own Clever account and login, the teacher you're subbing for will provide a Clever Badge that enables you to log into their Teacher Pages for the days you are covering. Access is limited to 14 or fewer days.

title: For Clever Admins: Adding a long-term substitute teacher
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to add a substitute 3. How subs log in to Clever 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 A common piece of feedback we’ve heard from substitute teachers is that they often aren’t given any access to edtech programs, making it hard for them to support students.

 Good news! You can use Clever to give both long-term and short-term substitute teachers access to Clever and your school organization’s edtech programs.

 

 IMPORTANT This article covers the process for adding long-term subs to your schools. For short-term subs (roughly 14 days or less), please have teachers use our short-term substitute teacher feature . 

 Short-term access enables them to support students by:

 • Troubleshooting logins for students using Clever
• Creating or viewing Teacher Pages
• Launching apps and logging students out of Clever
• Accessing edtech programs to check on student progress and offer support
 
 
 How to add a substitute

 Long-term substitutes 

 For a substitute covering a class for a longer period (such as parental leave), we recommend the following steps.

 Please note: For the teacher to log in, they must have an email that matches the Identity Provider (IdP) your school organization has set up. For example, if you use Google as your IdP, the teacher must have a Gmail address (either a personal one or one provided by your school organization). Alternatively, you can add Clever Passwords as an IdP, which subs access via a username and password (and doesn’t require a specific email address/provider). 

 Option 1: Add them as a teacher via your Student Information System (SIS): This option is ideal, as it automatically creates their account in Clever and shares it with the section(s) they’ll be teaching in any connected apps.

 • If possible, have the teacher added as the teacher of record in your Student Information System (SIS) and added as the teacher for the sections they’ll be covering. We also recommend providing them with an email address for your school organization as this is often required by apps and will help the sub to access Clever. 
 • Note: If adding them as the teacher of record isn’t possible, you can also add them as a co-teacher . Some edtech programs don’t support co-teachers, so please check with any critical programs directly.
 

• If you’re able to follow step one, your data sync will add the teacher to Clever and provide access to the sections you’ve added them to, along with any edtech programs those sections are shared with. As such, they’ll have access to Clever and edtech programs in the same way as any other teacher.
• Inform the substitute that they have a Clever account and an email account. You can share this article with them , which runs through how Clever works for teachers and how they can access it. 
 
 Option 2: Add them as a custom teacher in Clever If you add the sub as a custom teacher, you’ll have to take more manual steps than with option one.

 • If option one isn’t viable, you can add the long-term sub as a custom teacher in Clever. Note: Please wait 15 minutes after adding the custom teacher before moving to step two; this allows the custom teacher to sync across Clever.
• Add them as a co-teacher for all the sections they’ll be teaching.
 
 

 • Inform the substitute that they have a Clever account. You can share this article with them , which runs through how Clever works and how they can access it.
 
 Once you've added the substitute teacher in Clever, they'll be able to log into Clever around 15 minutes after their account syncs. However, it may take up to 24 hours for applications to sync and create their accounts, as most app syncs occur nightly. If it’s been more than 24 hours and the sub is not showing up, we suggest contacting the app(s) in question or Clever Support. 

 Please note: Please remember that, like any user added via Clever, subs who access Clever may count towards your license caps for your edtech programs. License management varies considerably from app to app, as some apps might offer an unlimited school organization or schools license while others charge per user. If you’re unsure if adding subs will affect any app subscriptions, please check in with the app directly.

 

 Short-term substitutes 

 The best method for managing short-term substitutes covering a class for 14 days or less is our Substitute Access feature . This feature makes it easy for teachers to provide their substitutes with temporary access to Clever securely. Instead of sharing their personal login credentials, teachers can generate a Sub Badge that allows short-term substitutes to log in and view the resources and Teacher Page to which they have been given access. This allows teachers to maintain secure credentials while also giving substitutes the tools they need to manage their online resources effectively for the day. You can learn more here .

 If the Substitute Access feature doesn't seem like the right tool for your scenario, you can also follow the steps above for adding the substitute to your account as a custom teacher and assigning them as a co-teacher to the sections they'll be teaching. This method can take up to 24 hours for subs to access learning applications, which is why the Substitute Access feature is preferred. 

 
 
 How long-term subs log in to Clever

 Subs with school organization email addresses 

 If the sub has an email address assigned by your school organization, they can sign in using that email at your school's Clever URL (such as Clever.com/in/school-nickname ). If your school organization uses Clever Passwords as your Identity Provider (IdP), the teacher will use the username and password that you’ve configured for signing in (you can check that here if you’re not sure).

 Subs without school organization email addresses 

 If the sub doesn't have an email address from your school organization, they can sign in using a personal Google email account if your organization uses Google as an identity provider . Just be sure to use their Google email address when creating their account. If the sub does not have a school email address, and your school doesn’t use Google as an identity provider, the sub will need to sign in via Clever Passwords. You can add this as an IdP here .

 For those using the Substitute Access feature, please read more about their login process here .

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Can you provide substitutes with access for a specific date range? 

 • Yes! When adding the sub as a Custom Teacher, you can determine a date for them to lose access. Learn more about custom teachers here .
 
 Will subs have access to Teacher Pages? 

 • Subs will have access to build their own Teacher Pages, whether they’re a regular teacher created via your SIS or a custom teacher. If the teacher the sub is covering for has Teacher Page(s), that teacher can also share their Teacher Page with the sub. You can find out more about sharing Teacher Pages here .

title: Clever IDM: Pushing and Pausing syncs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Pushing a sync 3. Examples of when to pause 4. Pausing a Clever IDM sync 5. Resuming a Clever IDM sync 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM’s default behavior is to sync once daily, at night. At that time, Clever will compare the data in Clever to the accounts in Google/AD/Entra and create, update, or archive users appropriately. 

 There may be some reason that you want to temporarily pause your nightly Clever IDM sync, though. This article will walk you through the process of pushing, pausing, and resuming your Clever IDM syncs. 

 

 

 

 

 
 Pushing a sync

 To initiate a manual sync of IDM:

 • Navigate to Clever Dashboard > User Management > IDM. 
• Click on the desired destination.
• Click Edit <destination> provisioning. 
• In the bottom right, click Provision <destination> 
 
 
 Examples of when to pause

 Some examples of when pausing your Clever IDM sync may be helpful: 

 • Encountering issues with IDM? Pause your sync while you understand what the issue is. Resume your sync once you have identified the issue.
• Making big changes to your Clever sync (summer rollover, semester rollover, changing an SIS)? Pause your sync. When the majority of your changes are complete, resume the sync. This will ensure that you don’t have too many rapid, unnecessary changes in your provisioned Google/Active Directory/Entra accounts while the SIS changes are happening. 
 
 
 Pausing Clever IDM syncs

 On the Clever IDM Dashboard , select the destination that you want to pause the syncs for. Click the Pause <Destination> Syncs button and confirm the pause in the pop-up. Once you have confirmed the pause, your destination’s Clever IDM syncs should be paused. On the Clever IDM Dashboard, the destination will now have a Paused indicator, and a banner will appear, informing you that the syncs are paused. While Clever IDM syncs are paused, you'll still be able to change your configuration settings and preview these changes. You won't be able to provision the changes until you resume the IDM syncs. If you have Clever IDM for more than one destination (e.g., for Google AND Active Directory), you can pause them each individually. This means that your Google IDM sync can still continue while your Active Directory IDM sync is paused. However, this also means you must manually pause both of them if you don't want any IDM syncs to run. 

 
 Resuming Clever IDM syncs

 To resume Clever IDM syncs, click the Resume <destination> Syncs button on the IDM Dashboard . 

 A popup will appear, asking you to confirm the change. When you press confirm, Clever IDM will automatically kick off a preview of your accounts for you. Please review these changes and verify they are correct before your next nightly IDM sync. If you notice any unexpected changes, you may pause your syncs again at any time as you resolve the issues. Another way to resume Clever IDM syncs is to click the Resume Syncs button in the banner that informs you that your IDM syncs are paused. It works exactly the same as the button on the IDM Dashboard.

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever IDM: Overview 
• Clever IDM: Summer Rollover 
• Clever IDM: Sync Reports

title: Clever + Virginia Wizard: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Required fields 4. Getting support 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through how to get set up with Virginia Wizard in Clever. Before reading this guide, please make sure your division is set up with Clever! If your division has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: clever.com/signup or by accepting your invitation from Virginia Wizard. For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide .

 
 Setup process

 • If your division is already on Clever, you can connect Virginia Wizard’s application by accepting your invite (please email wizard@vccs.edu to ask for an invitation to connect) OR following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for Virginia Wizard .
• You'll then be brought to a page where you can authorize your Virginia Wizard request. As Clever SSO and rostering for Virginia Wizard is provided at no cost to the division, you can mark that it has already been purchased for the division.
• You'll also be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the Virginia Wizard icons in their Clever Portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request .
 

 
 Once you have shared data with Virginia Wizard, the Virginia Wizard team will set up the initial sync.

 Recommended Sharing

 Before sharing:

 • Ensure that students and teachers are in their correct grade levels and that classes are updated for the current school year in your Student Information System (SIS).
 
 Virginia Wizard recommends that you “Share all records in my district” in Clever. You can find more info about sharing data here. 

 
 Required fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. Virginia Wizard requires the following data fields be sent: Student: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, credentials, dob, email, ext, gender, grade, graduation_year, hispanic_ethnicity, location, race, state_id, student_number Teacher: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, credentials, email, ext, state_id, teacher_number, title School Admin: district, name, schools, staff_id, credentials, department, email, ext, roles, title District admin: district, name.first, name.last, email, title 

 
 Support Contacts

 Virginia Wizard The Wizard team offers training for counselors and teachers on how to use Clever with student logins and rostering. Just request this by emailing wizard@vccs.edu . Clever Support.clever.com (click the chat icon in the corner of the screen) Contact Clever with issues that relate to the steps outlined in this article, or see Clever Academy for more information on how to get the most out of Clever.

title: Requesting a New Clever Account: Shared Student Information Systems
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Exporting your data 3. Communicate changes with applications 4. Historic data 5. Logins may be impacted 6. Alternatives to having your own account 7. Frequently Asked Questions 

 ​

 Overview

 Often charter schools (and sometimes small districts) share a Student Information System (SIS) with other schools or a larger school district. Since Clever syncs data from your SIS, many districts and schools in this scenario share a Clever account, however, there are times when a district or school may prefer to maintain its own Clever account. Below are a few items to take into consideration when choosing if your school should manage its own Clever account. NOTE If your SIS is managed by a separate team (e.g. a school district or charter school network), you will want to reach out to that team to consider the following information together. 

 
 Exporting your data

 If you share an SIS account with a district or group of schools, you'll need to know how to export and upload just your school’s data. This can be done via an SFTP Sync , but you may need to work with the district or charter network team to ensure just your school’s data is exported. Clever isn't able to provide guidance on the export process from your SIS when using an SFTP sync. 

 ​

 
 Communicate changes with applications

 Are you currently syncing data to any applications through the existing Clever account? If so, please contact your application representative if you are anticipating a change to your existing Clever account. Creating a new Clever account will provision users with new Clever IDs, potentially impacting historical data within connected applications. 

 ​

 
 Historic data

 ​If you have any students or teachers currently accessing an existing district account, Clever won't be able to preserve historical data across these accounts. Individual application partners may be able to assist, but you will need to reach out to them directly.

 Additionally, if you have students who are highly transient (move between schools in the district or charter network frequently), please consider that these students will not have data preserved as they potentially move between Clever accounts. 

 
 Logins may be impacted

 If multiple schools share a Google Admin Console, Badges into Chromebooks can only be supported for one Clever account. The setup for Badges into Chromebooks requires the unique sign-in/sign-out URL for Clever, and this can only be input in Google once.

 Clever Admin cannot have the same email address on multiple Clever accounts. If you wish to have the same admins on multiple accounts, a unique email address will be necessary for each account.

 ​

 
 Alternatives to having your own account

 Many large districts prefer to use the School Tech Lead role to help manage individual school accounts within a larger district account. School Tech Leads have additional permissions such as the ability to customize their school’s Clever Portal, utilize School Site Sharing to control their school’s sharing rules for applications, and help with troubleshooting logins for users in their school. For more information on School Tech Leads please see our articles Clever Roles: School Tech Lead and Sharing Permissions: Enabling School Site Sharing for School Tech Leads . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Can I use a Clever-Managed Auto Sync or API Sync for an individual account if I share a Student Information System (SIS)? 

 • This depends on your SIS. Reach out to Clever Support for additional information.
 
 If I choose to share a Clever account with my district/network, can I update the emails for just my school? 

 • It's common for districts to have one email domain stored in their SIS, while an individual school may also have its own unique domain. If you need to update emails in the existing account to reflect your preferred domain for student logins, you can do so by manually side-loading emails. See our article Manually Uploading (i.e. side-loading) User Emails to Clever for more information, or reach out to Clever Support for additional assistance.
 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Clever Roles: School Tech Lead 
• Sharing Permissions: Enabling School Site Sharing for School Tech Leads 
• Manually Uploading (i.e. side-loading) User Emails to Clever

title: TN Universal Reading Screener - Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Step 1: Sending Clever the required fields 3. Step 2: Sharing extension fields with Applications used for the TN Universal Reading Screener 

 

 Overview

 Tennessee school districts can use Clever to integrate applications used for the Tennessee Universal Reading Screener and meet the state’s data requirements. While solutions may vary, districts can leverage their Clever Sync and use extension fields to manage their data for the TN Universal Reading Screener. Please reference the steps below to include these required data fields in Clever. The following data fields are required by the Tennessee Department of Education: 

 • District ID (District Number) - 5-digit ID* 
• School ID (School Number) - 4-digit code*
• Student Unique Student Identifier - USID
 • Clever standard field - State ID
• Clever extension field - studentstateid.ext
 
 
• Teacher License Number (TLN) 
 • Clever standard field - State ID
• Clever extension field - teacherstateid.ext (Highly recommended)
 
 
 
 

 
 Data Element 

 
 ColumnName 

 
 Definition 

 
 Length 

 
 Sample Value / Additional Info 

 
 
 District ID (District Number)

 
 DistrictID

 
 The number in the school directory assigned by the state to each district.

 
 Max Length: 3 Data Type: Numeric 

 
 Source: School Directory / Department List Provided (column A in district tab). Unique in state, non-duplicated. 

 
 
 School ID (School Number)

 
 SchoolID

 
 The number in the school directory assigned by the state to each school within a district.

 
 Max Length: 4 Data Type: Numeric 

 
 School Directory / Department List Provided (column D in schools tab) Unique in district, may be duplicated across state 

 
 
 State Assigned Student ID (USID)

 
 SSID

 
 Unique student ID generated by the state and assigned to a student.

 
 MaxLength: 9 DataType: Numeric 

 
 Usually 7 digits; trim leading zeroes as needed

 
 
 Teacher License Number (TLN)

 
 TLN

 
 Unique number assigned to an educator by the state when they are first entered into the teacher Certification and Licensure system

 
 Data Type: Numeric Usually 6 digits; trim leading zeros as needed.

 
 If teacher has no TLN, schools/districts should use the last 4 of the instructors SSN for the TLN field. Growth scores will not be calculated for those teachers if no TLN provided before final spring cumulative file. 

 
 

 

 
 Step 1: Sending Clever the required fields

 You can send Clever each field using the standard fields below: School ID (School Number) - 4-digit code*

 • schools.csv: Clever Standard Field - School_ID
 

 Student Unique Student Identifier - USID

 • students.csv: Clever standard field - State_ID
 

 Teacher License Number (TLN) 

 • teachers.csv: Clever standard field - State_ID
 

 If you are unable to use the standard fields in Clever to send your data, you can leverage extension fields using the instructions below: For SFTP and SIS-Managed Auto Syncs: There are a couple of options for uploading custom field data to Clever via SFTP/CSV files:

 • Districts can add a custom/extension field column to one of the existing rostering files being synced to Clever (schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv, and enrollments.csv). The column name must begin with 'ext'.
 • For example: Fields such as 'Teacher License Number' or could be added to the teachers.csv with columns named 'ext.teacher_license_number' or 'ext.teacherstateid' to your CSV files.
 
 
• If your district currently syncs data to Clever via SFTP, you can also upload custom fields via CSV files directly to your Clever SFTP folder by uploading to a subdirectory named /extensionfields. The files should be named:
 • schools_ext.csv
• sections_ext.csv
• students_ext.csv
• teachers_ext.csv
• staff_ext.csv
 
 
 
 For Clever Managed Autosyncs: For the following auto-syncs, custom fields can be added by reaching out to Clever support to help set up custom field mapping using extension fields.
 • PowerSchool
• Infinite Campus Auto Sync
• Skyward Auto Sync
• Illuminate Auto Sync
• Aeries Auto Sync
• SchoolInsight Auto Sync
• iNow Auto Sync
 
Please reach out to Clever Support and we can get this started for you.

 
 Step 2: Sharing extension fields with Applications used for the TN Universal Reading Screener

 You can share your custom fields with any app by follow these steps:

 • Navigate to the overview page for the app and click on the Settings tab
• Under Field Access , select Advanced Settings 
• Select Customize fields shared with {app} and click Customize fields 
• On the Edit Student Fields page, select Next 
• On the Edit Contact Fields page, select Next 
• On the Edit District Admin Fields page, select Next 
• You are now on the Edit Extension Fields page. Any extension fields that you have uploaded to Clever will be found in the left column under Fields . Click on the fields you wish to share with the app to bring it to the right column under Share with {app} . Once you have selected the desired fields to share, select Save .

title: Clever IDM: Preview Sync Visualizer and Events
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to use Preview Sync Visualizer 3. How to use Events 

 

 Overview

 Clever enables you to check your configuration settings by user with the Sync Visualizer Preview tool. The Sync Visualizer Preview tool helps Clever admins understand the changes to be made to different users that are currently being managed by Clever IDM before the changes occur. This tool only shows the differences for users whose information changed. Users with no changes won’t show up in this tool.

 To review all creations, matches, updates, and archivals after your Clever IDM sync has run, we recommend utilizing the Events tab on your Clever IDM overview page. The events tab will allow you to filter and locate specific users to view more details on how Clever IDM is managing their destination account. 

 
 How to use

 The Sync Visualizer Preview tool is available during setup at the Preview and Provision step . Please refresh your preview before attempting to use this feature To use this feature:

 • Click Check a user 
• Search for user by name or email
• Once you select a user, you will see information based on the action being done for the user. 
 
 For users that will be created, you will see: 

 • The locations where the user is or will be placed
• First and last name
• Clever IDM username
• If the user needs to change their password on next login
 
 

 For users that will be updated, you'll see: 

 • Current Value of field(s) to be updated
• Updated Value for field(s)
 
 

 For users that will be archived, you'll see: 

 • Original location of the user
• New archive location
 
 

 For user accounts with current issues, you'll see: 

 • Issue type
• Description
• More details
 
 With this tool you can view and confirm expected changes to your users quickly and easily. If you discover unexpected changes, you can go back through the configuration to adjust what’s needed.

 
 

 How to use Events

 After your initial Clever IDM sync, you can continue to track changes to your users via the User Management > IDM > Events tab . On this tab, you can select a date range for which you’d like to review Clever IDM creates, updates, archives, and matched users. You can also apply filters to examine changes by destination (if you have multiple configured), event type (create, update, etc.), or specific users by name or SIS ID.

 Once you find the event for the user and destination you’d like to review, click the dropdown arrow to examine more details.

 

 

 It is also possible to unlink a user via the Events tab. It is necessary to unlink a user if Clever IDM incorrectly links a Clever account to an account in a destination due to an error in the user's data.

 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > User Management > IDM > Events tab and click the drop-down menu for the user you need to unlink. You'll see an Unlink user button. As long as you correct the data in Clever and/or the destination, the new link will be correct.

 

 For archived users, this is the only way to unlink a user since they no longer have a Profile Page in Clever.

 If you find that Clever IDM is managing a user in an unexpected manner, and you need help troubleshooting, please contact Clever Support .

title: Troubleshooting: Issues with Shared Devices
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Best practices for logging out of Clever and connected apps 3. Logging out of Clever on any device 4. Logging out of Clever and applications on iOS 5. Shared devices notifications 6. Time-out and inactivity 

 

 Overview

 When logging out, it's important to remember that single sign-on (SSO) can actually be broken down to up to 3 (three) separate login processes behind the scenes: 

 • Identity provider login (e.g. Google)
• Clever login
• Application login (e.g. Khan Academy)
 
 If a student is accessing a shared device, we recommend that they log out of each application and device after their session. Therefore users should log out of the application, the Clever portal, and the identity provider (Google, ADFS, etc.) per the steps below: 

 Best practices for logging out of Clever and connected apps

 To ensure that a user's user session is ended before logging another user in, please follow these steps: Step 1: Log the user out of any learning applications 

 Step 2: Log the user out of Clever 

 • From the Clever Portal , click on the user's name in top right corner, select Log out 
 
 Step 3: Log out of Google (if applicable) or the district's Identity Provider 

 Step 4: Log in to Clever as new user 

 • Navigate to https://clever.com/login and search for the new user's school. Once the school is located, they will log in with their Badge or credentials. They should now be successfully logged in to their account!
 
 Logging out of Clever on any device

 Users can log out of the Clever portal by navigating to the top right corner of their portal (where they can find their name), and then selecting 'Log Out': 

 

 Please note: If your school organization is using Google as your identity provider (IdP), you can apply a setting from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page that will automatically log users out of Google when they log out of Clever. This is helpful in situations where multiple users are logging into Clever on a shared device. For more information, see this article . 

 

 

 Logging out of Clever and applications on iOS

 On an iOS device, double-click the home button (on newer iPad Pros, swipe up from the bottom of the display) to expose the application thumbnails. Swipe up on the Clever app and any learning apps that were launched. This will end a user's session when the device is shared. 

 

 Shared devices notifications

 On certain devices, upon initial sign in to Clever, users will be asked whether or not they should be remembered on the device. If they select No, only I use it , then their Clever session will remain intact for 2 hours. 

 

 If the user selects Yes, Clever will treat the device as a shared device. In this case, users will be prompted with an option to sign in as a different user after 10 minutes of inactivity on the Clever portal. 

 Clever Tip: This feature gives users who share devices the option to log in to another account, in case logout best practices weren't followed. 

 

 Time-out and inactivity

 Logging out

 
 

 Users are logged out of Clever automatically if: 

 
 • If a user is inactive in Clever for 24 hours, they will automatically be logged out. Currently, there is no way to customize this behavior. 
• The browser is quit (e.g. "Quit Google Chrome " ). Closing the browser window will not log the user out of Clever. 
 
 

 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Troubleshooting: Canvas Login Issues
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Common errors 

 Overview

 This guide is meant for district admins to troubleshoot issues their users are experiencing while attempting to access Clever with their Canvas credentials. If you're just getting started with Canvas and Clever, please take a look at the article on Setting up single sign-on (SSO) with Canvas . To identify issues in your single sign-on (SSO) setup, view your Clever Dashboard > Support tools > Troubleshoot login tool.

 When a user selects Log in with Canvas , Clever will send the user to Canvas where they will enter their username and password. Clever never sees these credentials, but Canvas will redirect the user back to Clever with a token confirming the user logged in successfully. If it's the user's first time logging into Clever with Canvas, they may have to also approve Clever as an app they want to use. Clever will then match the Canvas user with a user in Clever, and log them into their app.

 
 Common errors

 Here are the common errors that we identify, and how to resolve them:

 • We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email or username. 
 
 

 • The unique identifier passed to us from Canvas doesn’t match any user in Clever. Most commonly, this is a user who isn’t yet synced to Clever. Alternatively, a field we match on (e.g. student number or email address) is missing in the SIS upload for the user in question. Please review your Access logs to see which user matches we tried to make and then verify the correct information in your SIS and within Clever.
 
 

 • We couldn’t log you in. Additional permissions are required. 
 
 

 • When a user logs in to Clever for the first time using Canvas, they are asked to give permission. If they deny permission, we don't get a valid code by which to authorize the user into Clever. Ask the user to try again, this time accepting the permissions to use Clever.
• If the user is receiving an error message when logging in, try logging into your Canvas administrator dashboard and enabling “Clever Instant Login” under Developer Keys > Inherited.
 
 

 • We couldn't log you in. 
 
 

 • This may indicate Canvas had an issue while we were attempting to log the user in. This can indicate that Canvas had a temporary network glitch or that there is a bug. If you are seeing this error occur frequently, please reach out as soon as possible to Clever Support so we can help you troubleshoot.
 
 • We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the email or username 
 
 

 • Please check your data in Clever to ensure that each user has a unique username for Canvas logins.
 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We're happy to help!

title: Troubleshooting: Expired LDAP SSL Certificates
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Expired SSL certificates 3. Downloading new certificate fingerprints 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 Clever will alert you ahead of when your LDAP SSL certificate is approaching expiration. A current SSL certificate is necessary to support LDAPS login methods and account recovery that relies on LDAPS . The Clever Admin owner of the account will receive an email notification 2 weeks ahead of expiration and there will be an alert visible on the SSO settings page in the Clever Dashboard. You must update your certificate to ensure continued access to Clever using your LDAPS login method. 

 
 Expired SSL certificates

 According to standard requirements, SSL certificates may have a lifespan of at most 2-3 years. As such, you may see examples of the following LDAP login error when your certificate expires.

 

 

 

 
 Downloading new certificate fingerprints

 To download the new SHA-1 fingerprint for your SSL certificate, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Login method tab and click Edit Settings in the LDAP Authentication box. 

 

 If your LDAP server is connected to the Clever account via the LDAP URL, you should be able to click Download From Server to get an SHA-1 fingerprint for the latest SSL certificate on the server. 

 

 Click Test Connection . If you see the message below, then it worked and you can now test a login. If you do not see this message or if your users are still having trouble logging in, please contact Clever support to troubleshoot. 

 

 Be sure to exit and save your new settings!

 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Will I be alerted if my LDAP Certificate has expired? 

 • The Clever Admin account owner, which can be seen on the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page, will receive an email from no-reply@clever.com with the subject: There are issues with your LDAPS Certificate , informing them that we have detected issues with your current LDAPS certificate. If your LDAPS certificate is managed by another member of your team, we recommend setting up an automatic forward based on the subject to the right member of your team.

title: Troubleshooting: ADFS, Office365, and SAML Login Issues
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 This guide is meant for people familiar with SAML and Active Directory who are trying to troubleshoot issues their users are seeing while using single sign-on (SSO). If you're just getting started with ADFS and Clever, we suggest accessing the article on Setting up single sign-on (SSO) with ADFS . To identify issues in your single sign-on (SSO) setup, view your Clever Dashboard > Support tools > Troubleshoot login tool.

 Troubleshooting

 Normally, when a user selects 'Login with ADFS', Clever will send the user out to your AD or another SAML server where they will enter their username and password. Clever never sees this username and password, but your server will redirect the user back to Clever with a token confirming the user logged in successfully. Clever will then "match" the user in your AD or another SAML system with a user in Clever, and log them into their app. 

 Occasionally errors do occur. Here are the common errors that we identify, and how to resolve them: 

 • The ID used during login doesn't match any user in Clever or LDAP data provided during login doesn't match any user in Clever 
 • The unique identifier passed to Clever by the SAML server did not match any user in Clever. In essence, a user (let's call her "Susan") was successfully able to prove who she was to the district's identity provider (IdP), but when the IdP spoke to Clever and said, "Hey, can you log Susan in for me?", Clever responded, "Sorry, we don't have a Susan here." The most common cause of this is a user trying to log in that is not yet synced to Clever, but it could also be that the field we match on, say student number or email address, is missing in the SIS upload for the user in question. We show the matches we tried to make in your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Access logs , so please check there first and then look up the corresponding information in your student information system (SIS) or Clever Data browser to see if you can determine the issue. 
 

 
 

 • No matching account found. The SAML server did not provide any login attributes 
• We did not receive any LDAP data that we could use to match a Clever user 
• We were unable to find an LDAP user for the user who logged in • This is similar to when a user can’t be matched (explained above), however these errors mean that we didn't get any value that we could match on. For instance, say you are matching on email. If your server sent us " sue.smith@espid.edu " and we couldn't find that user, Clever would return an ID error. If your server didn't send us any attributes at all, or if Sue didn't have an email on the SAML server , then we didn't receive any data to match on. Check in the SAML server to see that the impacted user has all the right attributes, and also take a look at your claims rules to make sure all your claims rules are set up and mapped to valid attributes on your server. 
 

 
 

 • Configuration issue. The 'Name ID' attribute is not set in the claim rules. 
 • A somewhat misleading error message, but easy to solve. While technically correct, what this error really means is that you should check your claims rules and ensure that the "Name ID" attribute is set, as indicated in the Help Center docs . 
 

 
 

 • The SAML response didn't include an encrypted assertion 
 • This error indicates there is something wrong with how Clever and your SAML server talk securely. This may be an issue with the certificate on your server. We recommend verifying your SSL certification installation using this tool . 
• If using Office365 or another SAML connection, under SSO Settings > Login Method > Active Directory Authentication > Edit Settings, please try checking the box to Allow unencrypted SAML assertions (recommended with Entra ID) and test another login. 
 

 
 

 • SAML certificate doesn't match. Update the SAML certificate in Clever to match the one used by your SAML server 
• Your LDAP server used an SSL certificate we did not recognize. 
 • This error indicates the certificate stored in Clever does not match. We recommend uploading a new metadata.xml file in your SSO settings .
• To resolve these errors automatically in the future, you can use a metadata URL rather than a manually uploaded file.
 

 
 

 • No success code received from your SAML server 
 • A fairly rare error, this indicates that the status code we received back from your AD or SAML server wasn't a success status code. Often this is a rate-limiting issue, but it can also indicate your server is misconfigured, the network is down, or another server bug. We recommend checking that your AD or SAML server is operational and responding to authentication requests. 
 

 
 

 • Unknown error. Please contact Clever Support with the error reference ID for assistance. 
 • This is an error with unknown origin. Please reach out to Clever Support. 
 

 
 • LDAP data provided during login matches to multiple users in Clever
