• Multiple accounts are linked to the LDAP data we received 
• SAML attributes received matches to multiple users in Clever 
 • Please check your data in Clever to ensure that each user has a unique username for LDAP or SAML logins.

title: Troubleshooting: Google login issues
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshooting checklist 3. Google Login Flow 4. Troubleshoot login tool 5. Common errors 

 Overview 

 This guide if for district admins familiar with Google SSO through Clever. Use this information to troubleshoot login issues. If you're just getting started with Google and Clever, take a look at the article on Single Sign on (SSO) with Google . 

 
 Troubleshooting checklist 

 Does the user have an active Clever account? 
 Is the email field populated in Clever for the user? 
 Is the email address in Clever correct? (up-to-date and no typos) 
 Are there any other users in Clever with the same email address? Their email address must be unique . 
 Is the user entering the correct email address? i.e. their school-provided email address, not their personal email address 
 
 
 Google login flow 

 When a user clicks Log in with Google from your district's Clever login Portal URL , Clever sends the user to Google where they'll enter their district-provided Google email address and password.

 Clever doesn't have access to Google passwords for users; however, once the user has entered their credentials, Google will redirect the user back to Clever providing a "token" that tells Clever this user has successfully logged in. If it's the users first time logging into Clever via Google, the user may be prompted to approve Clever as a Google SSO application. 

 Once the user is logged in, Clever shares the user's token with any associated applications they attempt to log into. This allows the user to access their Clever apps via single sign-on (SSO) through Clever without needing to enter their credentials again. 

 Please note: When a Clever admin accesses the Portal as a user, they are not actually logged into Clever via Google. 

 

 
 Troubleshoot login tool 

 There are occasional login issues that come up with district users. The first step in investigating a user login issue is to use the Troubleshoot login tool .

 

 
 Common errors 

 Here are the most common errors, and how to resolve them:

 • The email used during login doesn't match any user in Clever 
 
 • The unique identifier passed to us from Google didn’t match any user in Clever. In essence, a user (let's call her "Susan") was successfully able to prove who she was to Google, but when Google came to tell Clever "Hey, can you log Susan in for me?", we responded "Sorry, we don't have a Susan here". The most common cause of this is a user trying to log in that isn’t yet synced to Clever, but it could also be that the field we match on, say student number or email address, is missing in the SIS upload for the user in question. We show the matches we tried to make in your Access logs , so check there and then look up the corresponding information in your SIS and/or Clever Data browser to see what might be going on.
 
 • A user didn't grant permission to use Clever 
 
 • When a user logs in to Clever for the first time using Google, they're asked to give permission. If they deny permission, we don't get a valid code to log them in with, and show this error. Ask the user to try again, and accept the permissions to use Clever.
 
 • Google encountered an issue during login. If this happens frequently, contact Clever Support for help 
 
 • A fairly rare error, this indicates Google had an issue while we were attempting to log the user in. This can indicate that Google had a temporary network glitch, or that there is a bug. If you are seeing this error occur frequently, please reach out as soon as possible to Clever Support so we can work with Google and help you troubleshoot.
 
 • The email used during login matches to multiple users in Clever. 
 
 • Multiple users in Clever have the same email address. For example, siblings Sarah Smith and Samantha Smith both have the email address sarah.smith@example.com . Samantha’s email address will need to be updated to the correct email in the data syncing to Clever.
 
 • Google is not enabled as a login method for this user 
 
 • A user attempted to login using an identity provider (IdP) that is not configured for their user type. For example, a student tried to login using Google credentials when they should login with Clever Passwords.
 
 

 • User signs into their Chromebook and clicks to log into Clever with Google, but the screen spins or bounces them back to the login page. 
 
 • This error typically occurs if Clever is blocked in the security settings on the user's device. We recommend confirming that Clever is not blocked in the user's content settings at chrome://settings/content . 
 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Troubleshooting: Logging in to applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Application is missing from Clever Portal 3. User is not logged in after clicking on the application icon in the Clever Portal 4. Using the Access Portal As feature to troubleshoot 

 

 Clever Tip: To troubleshoot problems logging in to applications, we highly recommend using the Troubleshoot Login tool . More information on this resource can be found here . 

 Overview

 
 
 This article explains how to troubleshoot common application issues in the Clever Portal. It outlines steps to resolve missing applications, login failures, and how to use the "Access Portal As" feature. You'll also learn how to gather error messages and escalate issues to Clever or the application's support team.

 

 
 Application is missing from the Clever Portal 

 If a user does not see an application in the Clever Portal, there are a few things you can check: 

 • Does the application support SSO?
 
 

 • Does the application support SSO for this user type?
 
 

 • Is the user successfully shared with the application? More information on constructing sharing rules can be found here .
 
 If sharing by district: confirm the user is active in Clever. If sharing by school: confirm the user is associated with the school or is associated with at least one section at the shared school. If sharing by rules: confirm the user meets all criteria for at least one of the sharing rules. The most common criteria that result in users not being shared are not meeting the requirements for enrolled sections. Ensure that they are enrolled in a section that meets the grade level, subject, course, etc. that is being shared with the application. You can review the Clever profile for a user, including associated sections, by using the search feature, located at the top of your Clever Dashboard. 

 • Is the user successfully shared with the application? Use the Troubleshoot Sharing tool to confirm. More information on this resource can be found here .
• Check to make sure that the application hasn't been hidden from users by a Clever Admin. You can find more information here .
• Check to make sure that the application has not placed any filters on your application. You can learn more about how to request a change to an app filter here .
 
 

 

 
 User is not logged in after clicking on the application icon in the Clever Portal 

 If a user clicks on an app icon in their Clever Portal and receives an error from the application's website page , please contact the application's support team for assistance. 

 

 If the user clicks on the app icon and receives an error that is a Clever website , please contact Clever's support team with the error message.

 Please note: If the user has recently changed schools or classes, or was recently synced to Clever, please allow 24 hours for their connected applications to be updated so they can log in.

 Clever Tip: If you are seeing an error at a web URL that is not a Clever address, it will be necessary to contact the application to fix the issue. Please reach out to the application's support team to investigate further! 

 
 Using the Access Portal As feature to troubleshoot

 Using the Access Portal As feature in your Clever Dashboard is a great way to troubleshoot and support teachers, students, and Portal-only staff with their technical issues. 

 Clever makes it easy for admins to access the Clever Portal as an individual user, allowing them to see precisely what they see. When troubleshooting, this can help diagnose an issue, retrieve an error code or message, or gain visibility into what might be confusing. If you encounter an error code within a user's application account, you can capture an image of it and share it directly with the application's team to learn more. You should only use the Access Portal As feature for viewing purposes, and you should never edit anything as another user. Please note that using the Access Portal As feature won’t inform you whether a user can log into their Clever account successfully. To troubleshoot Clever logins, please see this article . You can utilize the Access Portal As feature when on a user’s profile page by clicking on the Support Tools button in the upper right corner and selecting Access Portal as the user. You will then be taken to a screen to Start Portal session , which will take you to the user's Portal. For security purposes, if you’re accessing an admin or staff Portal, they’ll be sent a notification email letting them know their Portal has been accessed.

 Be sure to log out of the user's Portal once you are done by clicking End Portal session in the upper right corner.

title: Troubleshooting: Troubleshoot Login tool - error messages
text:
This article contains: 1. Overview 2. Result Types 3. District Issues 4. Clever Issues 5. App Issues 

 

 Overview 

 This article describes the possible results and actions that may be identified by the Troubleshoot Login tool . 

 
 Result Types

 
 Result type

 
 Description

 
 Success The key criteria passed the system check. 
 Information The system check was unable to automatically detect the error. Please see the info message for additional details on how to proceed. 
 Warning The system check has indicated this issue may have contributed to the login failure. These issues may need to be resolved to restore logins. 
 Failure The system check has indicated this issue is causing the login failure. These issues must be resolved to restore logins. 
 
 

 District Issues

 The following issues are resolved at the school district level. If a School Tech Lead (STL) is using the Troubleshoot Login tool and the resolution message indicates the point of resolution is the district, they will see an Email district admin button that will provide a quick link to email the district tech support contact. If no tech support contact is listed, the contact button will not appear, pictured below: To update your district support contact, please follow the directions found here . 

 

 
 Result

 
 Result type

 
 Description

 
 The user is not shared with the app Message: "This user is not shared with [app]. You may need to adjust sharing rules, or update the data syncing to Clever to make sure that the user is covered by an existing sharing rule."
 Additional info: Options to fix users that aren't shared:

 • Update the user's information in your SIS to ensure they meet the sharing permissions set for the application or
• Update the sharing permissions to share the user. See these articles on sharing permissions. 
 
 
 The user is not shared with the library app Message:
 If the user is a teacher: "This user isn't shared with [app]. If they'd like to use [app], they can install it in the Clever Library." Otherwise: "This user isn't shared with [app]. Teachers can install [app] in the Clever Library and share with this user."

 Additional info: Library applications can only be added by teachers. Contact the teacher and request they add the application for their classroom using these directions .

 
 The user is filtered out by app-side filters Message:
 "This user is being filtered out by the application. Please refer to [app page link] to see what types of filters are in place. If you believe the filter has been placed incorrectly, please reach out to the application. If you would like help understanding the filter, please reach out to Clever support."

 Additional info: To update or remove the filter, contact the applications' support team. Information on app-side filters can be found here . 

 
 The user has data quality errors Message:
 "This user was flagged by an app-specific data error. Data errors must be resolved for users to be successfully processed by the application. To resolve this error, you will need to update the data syncing to Clever. For more information about this error, please navigate to Data Quality."

 Additional info: Application data errors must be resolved in order for login access to be restored. Update the data in your SIS and resync with Clever. 

 
 The user has not logged in to Clever in the last 7 days 
 Message: "This user has not logged in to Clever in the last 7 days. We recommend confirming that the user is logging in at the correct URL: {portal URL}. If the user sees an error when they try to log in, you can copy the reference ID and look it up in the Login Logs."

 Additional info: Often, users report that they're unable to log in to an application because they are actually attempting to log in to the resource directly instead of through Clever. We recommend testing the login to the application using the Access Portal as user feature to confirm the login is successful. Once confirmed, provide directions on how to log in to Clever to access the application. 

 
 The user has data quality warnings Message:
 "This user was flagged by an app-specific data warning. This means that the user has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular data warning."

 Additional info: Data quality warnings usually don't prevent logins to applications. However, it is best practice to resolve warnings when possible. If you're unable to resolve the error, contact the app to see if they require the warning to be resolved in order to successfully log in. 

 
 The district has not added the app Message:
 "Your district has not added this app. You can request the app from the Request Applications page of your dashboard."

 Additional info: Applications can be added by navigating to Applications > Add Applications 

 
 The district hasn’t launched this app yet 
 Message: "Hang tight! The application is preparing your district for launch. Users will not be able to access the application until the launch date: {launch date}. If you need to expedite the setup process, please reach out to the application directly."

 Additional info: SSO to applications is only available to launched applications. Applications can adjust the launch date for you using these directions . 

 
 The app hasn’t approved the district’s request 
 Message: "We are waiting on the application's team to accept your request and proceed with the implementation. If you need to expedite the request process, please reach out to the application directly."

 Additional info: SSO to applications is only available once an application has accepted your school district's request and has launched. If you need to expedite the request process, please reach out to the application provider directly, as Clever does not have insight into their acceptance process.

 
 The district has deleted the app from their dashboard 
 Message: "Your district has removed this app. If you'd like to add this app again, you can request it from the Request Applications page of your dashboard. If you do, please note that your user accounts will likely not be restored automatically. Additional setup will likely be required to match users to their historical data."

 Additional info: SSO is not available if an app has been removed. Please follow the message's directions to restore access. 

 
 The district has disabled SSO into the app for [user type] until [end date] 
 Message: "Your district has disabled SSO into this app for [user type] until [end date]. You can change this setting here [link to app settings in the dashboard]."

 Additional info: SSO can be disabled for specific apps. This feature is commonly used prior to the beginning of a school year. See here to re-enable SSO.

 
 The district has hidden the app from the [user type] portal until [end date] Message:
 "Your district has hidden this app from the [user type] portal until [end date]. You can unhide this app by changing the icon visibility settings in the portal. [link to help center article]"

 Additional info: Apps can be hidden from specific users in the Clever Portal. See here to unhide an icon.

 Message: "This application is for rostering only and does not support SSO for users through Clever. Try adding or requesting an SSO (Saved Passwords) application for this resource!"

 Additional info: Clever can only support SSO for applications that have built an SSO integration. Clever can build an SSO (Saved Passwords) integration if the app doesn't have one. To request an SSO (Saved Passwords) application, follow the directions found here . Learn more about application types here .

title: Troubleshooting: Troubleshoot Login tool
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Using the Troubleshoot Login tool 3. Result types & error messages 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 The Troubleshoot user login tool is the perfect utility for quickly identifying root cause issues of login errors and missing icons in the portal. You can find the tool in two places: 1. Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Support tools > Troubleshoot login tool for Clever Admin and on the Clever Dashboard home page for School Tech Leads. 2. You can also access the tool from a student or teacher profile. From a user profile, c lick Support Tools > Access Portal as user. 

 
 Using the "Troubleshoot Login" tool

 1. Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Support tools > Troubleshoot login tool. 

 2. Select a user using Clever ID, SIS ID, Name, or Email and selecting the name of the application they having problems accessing. The tool will complete a root cause analysis of the issue 

 

 3. Review of the Test summary 

 

 4. Review the Recommended Action This will indicate who will need to take action to resolve the issue. There are 3 possible options: District, App, or Clever 

 5. Review the Issue Details shown underneath the Recommended Action. This will provide information on how to address any issues that have been flagged by the tool. To simplify communication, we have provided a Copy issue description function to allow you to easily include this information in your communication with the district, app, or Clever. 

 Additionally, the tool will highlight related Help Center articles that can be leveraged to assist with resolving the issues detected. 

 Please note, there may be multiple issues that will need to be addressed to restore the login for a user. 

 
 Result types & error messages

 For more information on result types, error messages, and how to resolve issue, please see this article. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Which apps display in the app dropdown? 

 • The application dropdown will only display applications that have been added to the district's Clever account. If an application does not display, this means the application has not been added to the district's Clever account. 
 
 Will Library apps appear in the Troubleshoot Login tool? 

 • Yes. Applications installed by teachers through Clever Library will display in the Troubleshoot Login tool

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Instant Login links
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do Instant Login links work? ​​​​​​​ 3. Finding an app's Instant Login Link ​​​​​​​

 Overview

 Instant Login Links allow districts to embed application login URLs directly into their district's Single Sign-On portal, website, or Learning Management System (LMS). This allows district users to access Clever applications via your district portal using their Clever credentials. When district users click on an app's Instant Login Link, they are logged directly into the app via single sign-on (SSO) rather than into their Clever Portal. 

 
 

 How do Instant Login links work?

 

 • When a user clicks on an app's Instant Login link from your district portal, they are redirected to your SSO Identity Provider (e.g. Google, Active Directory, etc.) to sign in. For additional information about Identity Providers (IDPs), visit: Single Sign-On (SSO): Identity Provider (IdP) Options .
• Once the user is successfully authenticated, they will be logged in and taken to the application. They will not see their Clever Portal.
 
 

 • Please note: If a user has already authenticated with your Identity Provider (IdP) and is currently logged in, they will skip the IdP login screen and be taken directly into the app. 
• If there are multiple login methods available for accessing Clever, users may need to re-authenticate when using an instant login link. 
 
 

 
 Finding an app's Instant Login Link

 District admins can locate an app's Instant Login Link from the Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page by clicking on the name of the application to open the app overview page. Then navigate to the app Settings tab to locate the link under Application Information . Copy the Instant Login Link to embed into your district portal, website, or LMS.

 If you'd like to use the app icon in your district Portal, you can download the application icon image from this page as well.

 

 Please note that Instant Login links are not available for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications and that each individual learning application determines which of the following user types they support: 

 • Students 
• Teachers 
• Staff 
• Clever District Admins 
 
 If a user type is not listed under SSO Supported User Types in the app Settings tab, users of that type won't be able to access the application from your district portal via the Instant Login Link.

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Requesting SSO for applications that aren't yet on Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Requesting an application 2. What happens when single sign-on (SSO) becomes available for a Saved Password app? 

 Clever Tip: For a Saved Passwords app that already exists in Clever see: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Overview & Setup 

 Requesting an application 

 Don't see an application you would like to use? No problem! Clever can securely build a connection for almost any application. To add a new application, click Request app name below the search results:

 

 To help Clever get the application ready for your district, submit the Login Page URL, as well as a working username and password for testing purposes: 

 

 We’ll email you once the application is ready for your district so you can continue with the next steps. 

 What happens when single sign-on (SSO) becomes available for a Saved Password app? 

 Once single sign-on (SSO) becomes available for a Saved Password application, the app will be updated on the Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page. If your district is already connected to a Saved Password app you will be able to continue to use it. The Clever team will work with you to switch over to the higher-quality single sign-on (SSO) application. 

 Get started with these helpful resources:

 • How do I test and pilot Saved Password applications? 
• How do I convert added links to Saved Password applications?

title: For Clever Admins: Navigating the new login experience
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Timeline 3. What to expect 4. Why Clever updated the login experience 5. Email templates for communication with your schools about the new login page 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 We know how important smooth logins are for your users. Clever’s new login experience simplifies logging in to minimize challenges and get to learning quickly. This article explains what’s changing, when changes will roll out, and how to prepare and communicate with users who may be impacted.

 

 Please note: All updates listed in this article do not apply to all schools. Please review the notes listed in each phase to determine which users or schools will be impacted. 

 

 
 Timeline

 Clever’s login experience will be rolled out in six phases between February 3rd and June 2026. This timeline is a rough estimate of when updates will occur, and some dates may shift as we continue to refine the experience. Districts can also choose to opt in to more frequent communications about ongoing login changes here . Learn more about what these changes will look like below.

 

 

 
 What to expect

 Two panel view (Released)

 

 This is an example school configuration with Google and Badges, but each school’s screen will vary based on your login methods. 

 

 This is an example school configuration with Microsoft and Badges, but each school’s screen will vary based on your login methods. 

 

 Impacted users: All Clever users 

 Clever’s login page is getting a refreshed look with two panels and new photography, while keeping the same familiar login flow. These updates are designed to make logging in easier, more secure, and more intuitive for all users.

 

 New District login flow (Released)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Impacted users: Users who do not log in with a custom portal URL and instead go to clever.com/login 

 Users who log into Clever without a district portal URL will see a new, student-friendly login page.

 • 
 Badge users will no longer need to search for their school. They can simply click the Badge icon to log in faster. 

• 
 All other users will click the Password icon, then select their school, and follow the preferred login process from there. 

 
 This update helps students and staff get where they need to go more quickly, with fewer steps.

 

 Login and Help pages (Released)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Impacted users: All Clever users

 All users will see a new district/school login screen with the district’s pre-configured login options. Users will also be able to see their district’s default custom icon if one has been uploaded. If an icon has not been uploaded, users can expect to see a pre-populated icon. 

 If users need help with logging in, they will no longer see the Having trouble? prompt on their login screen. Now, users will click Get help to be taken to their new Help page where users can find everything they need on one screen. 

 Login hints for usernames and passwords will no longer be supported. If you need another way to support users during login, you can customize your login button labels. Learn how to customize them here . If you use SAML authentication and want your login button to show your Identity Provider instead of Account , you can learn how to update it here. 

 New Badge flow 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Impacted users: Clever Badge users

 Badge users will begin to see and hear a new success animation when they log in with a Clever Badge. If a login attempt is unsuccessful, users will see a clear error message explaining what went wrong.

 

 Clever Passwords flow 

 

 

 

 Impacted users: Clever Passwords users

 Clever Passwords users will first enter their username. Once verified, they will be asked to enter their password. If there's a typo or the account doesn't exist, the user is notified right away, so they don't have to enter a password to find out. This change makes the login experience clearer and easier for users and aligns with industry standards. 

 

 Admin login flow 

 

 Impacted users: Clever Admins

 The Clever Admin login page is getting a refreshed look to help simplify logins. This new login screen will include Google and SSO options so that admins have a designated login screen. 

 
 Why Clever updated the login experience

 Our goal with the new modernized login experience is all about getting learners, especially your youngest ones, to learning faster while reducing login-related help desk tickets that jam up your cues. 

 

 The login is the secure gateway to learning where trust is verified. We want every login experience, regardless of which user type or how old the user is, to feel as seamless as using the rest of Clever is. But over the years, we’ve received a lot of feedback around how our diverse community accesses Clever and the challenges they face. That’s why we’re doing this overhaul of the login. It started in 2025 with improvements we made to error messages so students and staff can better understand what the issue is and address it quickly. We also added multi-role logins making it easy to toggle between your teacher and staff accounts without having to log out and back in. 

 

 The changes you’ll see in 2026 are a continuation of this effort. We’ve examined every step of the login flow, worked through every common login challenge, and of course, rigorously tested our changes with all types of end users so we know it works. The changes you will see over the first half of 2026 will result in a modern, seamless, and secure login experience that prioritizes your students and helps them focus on learning above all else. 

 
 Email templates for communication with your schools about the new login page

 
 Click here for an email template to inform teachers of the two-panel view update 
 Subject: Important Update about change to Clever login look 

 Hi everyone,

 I'm writing to let you know that starting on February 3rd , the Clever login page is getting a fresh now look. The background picture is being updated to a new image that will only take up the left side panel (see example image below). There will be a few more small visual changes to the Clever login over the following months too. 

 Here is the important part: This is a visual update only . The login process itself is not changing.

 • 
 Clever Badges still work exactly the same way.

• 
 Passwords/Google/SSO logins still work exactly the same way.

• 
 You do not need to do anything to prepare.

 
 Why the change? Clever is redesigning the login screen to be more focused and faster, making it easier for our younger students to find their login path and get to learning quickly.

 What to expect: Because the screen looks different, students might ask if they are in the right place. Please assure them that this is the official, secure Clever login page. 

 Here is a preview of what this initial new login screen looks like so you can recognize it:

 

 

 If you run into any issues, please reach out to our help desk as usual.

 Best,

 [District Administrator Name]

 
 
 Click here for an email template to inform teachers of the new district login page and the removal of login hints 
 Subject: Important Update about change to Clever login look 

 Hi everyone,

 I’m writing to let you know that starting on April XXth [expected in late April but Clever will share the exact date] , the Clever login page is getting another small visual update. The sign-in buttons you normally see will be much bigger and bolder, but the sign-in options you use to log in on a regular basis will not change. 

 Here is the important part: This is a visual update only . The login process itself is not changing.

 • 
 Clever Badges still work exactly the same way.

• 
 Passwords/Google/Microsoft SSO logins still work exactly the same way.

• 
 You do not need to do anything to prepare.

 
 Why the change? Clever is redesigning the login screen to be more focused and faster, making it easier for our you

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Testing User Logins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to test single sign-on (SSO) logins 3. Tips and Troubleshooting 

 Overview

 You’re almost ready to roll out Clever single sign-on (SSO) at your district saving students and teachers hours of class time each week! Before you do, you will want to run one last test to make sure your setup is working properly. This step should only take a few minutes to complete. 

 
 

 Testing SSO logins

 

 • First, make sure you have the Clever login credentials for a few students and teachers.
• Then, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page to copy your district's unique Clever login Portal URL. For instructions on customizing your Portal URL, visit this article . 
• Open an incognito browser window (e.g. Chrome , Firefox & Edge ). 
• Paste your district's Portal URL in the address bar and hit enter. This will lead you to your district's unique Clever login page. 
• Attempt to log in as various users to see if you are successful. 
 
 

 
 

 Tips and Troubleshooting

 

 • If you can't use an incognito window, a regular browser window is fine. You will just need to make sure you’re logged out of Clever, and any other Identity Providers (e.g. Google, Active Directory), before attempting to log in as another user. Otherwise, you will continue to be logged in to your account rather than the one you are testing. 
• If you don’t have access to student or teacher login credentials, have a small group of teachers test their own logins to see if they are successful. You can share the login instructions with them that are downloadable from your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page. 
• Utilize your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Access logs to make sure logins are successful, and find information about any errors you’re seeing. You can search for specific users and see more details on what caused the issue. For more about Login Logs, visit this article .
 
 

 Once you’ve tested Clever SSO, and have confirmed successful logins, make sure to let your district users know they can log in! Track the success of your user logins and Portal usage from your Clever Dashboard > Analytics > Portal analytics page. 

 If you continue to have issues, please visit these helpful troubleshooting articles ! 

 

 If your tests have been successful, see this article for details on launching Clever at your district!

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Chromebooks - Troubleshooting
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshooting Clever Badge Setup 3. Context on required and recommended settings 4. Common Chromebook errors 5. FAQ 

 Overview

 This article has troubleshooting tips for common Chromebook setup issues and provides Clever Admins with a brief overview of how Clever can be used to simplify Google Chromebook logins for your end users. There are two methods for using Clever with Google Chromebooks: 

 1) Users use Clever Badges to log into Chromebooks, then access Clever. 

 • Students open their Chromebooks and scan their Clever Badge.
• This automatically logs the students into their Chromebooks and Clever Portals (they do not need to log in again).
• Students can click on any learning application to begin their learning session.
 
 

 

 2) Users log into Chromebooks manually, using credentials, and then access Clever. 

 • Students open Chromebooks and log in with their school-provided Google login credentials. 
• After logging in, the school organization’s Clever Portal is automatically opened for them (i.e., students do not need to log in again). 
• Students can click on any application icon to begin their learning session. 
 
 Depending on your preferences, each method can be configured separately for different Organizational Units (OUs) in your Google Admin Console.

 

 For either of the login flows above, Clever can be configured as either an organization-wide identity provider (IdP) for Google or an SSO Profile. Clever does recommend using the SSO Profile setup. For instructions on setting up Clever as an SSO Profile in Google, please see this article . For instructions on setting up Clever as an organization-wide IdP, please see this article .

 
 Troubleshooting Clever Badge setup

 Below are some troubleshooting tips we put together in case you see unexpected issues logging into Chromebooks with Clever Badges. 

 Test Chromebook logins with Clever Badges 

 • Create a test Organizational Unit (OU) and apply the Clever Badges settings to the OU.
• Choose a student to test and generate a Clever Badge for them. Note, this must be a real student unless you have a test user with a Google account syncing with Clever.
• Move that student to the test OU.
• Move the Chromebook you’re using to the test OU.
• Log in to the Chromebook using the student’s Clever Badge.
 
 

 Check your setup 

 • Require a change of password on the next sign-in Users are unable to log in using SSO when the reset password on first login functionality in the Admin Console is active. Make sure you're not using this functionality for any users accessing Google using SSO, including a Clever Badge login to a Chromebook. This is a limitation of Google SSO Using 3rd-Party IdPs. For more details on this topic, see Google article. 
• Generate Clever Badges. Make sure you’ve generated Clever Badges for your users. You can find instructions for generating Clever Badges here .
 
 

 Troubleshooting tips 

 If Chromebooks do not prompt users to log in with Clever Badges: 

 • Check the OU settings for your devices. The Chromebooks you want to set up to use Clever Badge logins should be in an OU with the Clever Badges settings applied.
• Check your device settings. Kiosk mode and public sessions should be turned off for the Chromebooks you want to use with Clever Badges.
• Check your version of Chrome. You should be using Chrome OS version M52 or later.
 
 If an error message appears after the Clever Badge is scanned: 

 • Check the Chromebook OU settings for your students. Students who log in with Clever Badges should be in an OU with the Clever Badges settings applied. If students are not in the correct OU, their badges cannot authenticate into Chromebooks.
• Validate the Clever Badge is working. Test the Clever Badge by using it to log in at https://clever.com/in/badges . If that login isn’t working, the badge is likely invalidated. You may have to generate a new Clever Badge for the student.
• Check your Clever sync for Google email addresses. Students using Clever Badges to log into Chromebooks should have Google email addresses synced to your Clever account. Clever uses this information to match users with their Google accounts. You can always check your synced data via the Clever Dashboard > Data browser .
• Confirm if Google is allowed in your network filters, including http://accounts.google.com/signup/oauth . 
 
 

 
 Context on required and recommended settings

 Some school organizations have asked us why certain settings are required when setting up Clever Badges, and if there’s any flexibility in what’s required or recommended. This section provides context on why settings are required, and any flexibility.

 Setup single sign-on 

 • 
 All of these settings are required in order to set up SSO.

 
 

 Chrome User Settings 

 • 
 Lock screen and idle settings are required to avoid a local device password prompt, which users don’t have access to. The password is encrypted and part of the process that powers Clever SSO into the Chromebook.

• 
 Single sign-on setting is required to enable SSO for Chromebooks.

• 
 SAML single sign-on login frequency settings are recommended to be every two weeks, but you can change this without causing issues.

• 
 Homepage URL and Startup pages are recommended to send the user directly to Clever on device startup. If you prefer another URL, that won’t affect SSO functionality.

 
 

 In Chrome Device Settings: 

 • 
 Disable guest mode to avoid confusion on student devices, as guest mode will not work with Badges.

• 
 Set Sign-in Screen to Never show user names and photos . It is recommended to avoid confusion. Enabling this will show previously logged-in users but will prompt for the local password instead of the Badge or Clever credential.

• 
 Set Single Sign-On IdP Redirection to Allow users to go directly to SAML SSO IdP page is recommended to eliminate the need for users to type their Google username prior to logging in. It may be required in some deployments depending on OU assignments

• 
 Set Single Sign-On Cookie Behavior to Enable transfer of SAML SSO Cookies into user session is recommended to allow user to be signed in to Clever already once signed in to the device

• 
 Single sign-on camera permissions is required to enable Clever to access the device camera if you plan to use Clever Badges.

• 
 Set Managed guest session to Do not allow managed guest sessions is recommended to avoid confusion on student devices as guest mode will not work with Badges.

 
 

 
 Common Chromebook errors

 Below are a list of common errors you may run into either with Badges or credentials-based logins. Steps for troubleshooting are also provided.

 

 
 Error/Issue

 
 Behavior

 
 Resolution

 
 OU-based login errors Logins not working for Badges or credentials. Errors may say "Couldn't sign you in, contact domain admin" or "Error 400" It is extremely common that school organizations/schools apply settings to a student OU, but not a device OU. Please make sure that whatever settings you apply to your device OUs are also applied to your student OUs, and if you change settings for one OU you change them for the other as well. 
 Login loop When a user tries to log into their Chromebook it brings them back to the sign in page without displaying any errors. This most commonly occurs for credentials-based logins, but can also occur for Clever Badges. Toggle options in the SSO Profile assignment section of the Google Admin Console. Under Manage SSO Profile assignments, in the SSO Profile, change settings from Have Google prompt for their username, then redirect them to this profile's IDP sign-in page to the option Require users to enter their Google username and password to sign in and then back again. Restart a Chromebook and test the login again. It may take up to 10 minutes for the policy updates to apply on the device. 
 Couldn't sign in. Contact domain admin. User receives an error that

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Lenovo Stoneware / webNetwork
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Single sign-on (SSO) with Stoneware 3. Can I use the Clever Portal instead of the Stoneware Portal? 4. Setup Preparation 5. Configuring the Public webApp for Clever 6. Finishing setup with Clever support 7. Adding applications to the Stoneware Portal 

 Overview 

 Clever’s single sign-on (SSO) allows students and teachers to safely access education apps with a single username and password. You can rely on your existing user management system - single sign-on (SSO) supports common user management systems like Lenovo Stoneware. 

 To use single sign-on (SSO) with Stoneware, you’ll need the following: 

 • Active SIS sync with Clever 
• webNetwork version 6.3.0.167 and above 
 
 Before you start setting up single sign-on (SSO) with Stoneware, it’s important to look at the attributes in Stoneware and the data that’s synced to Clever - in order for users to be able to authenticate, there needs to be an attribute in Stoneware that matches the user’s sis_id. Additionally, we recommend having your users’ mail attribute match the email address in Clever, though this is not mandatory. 

 Single sign-on (SSO) with Stoneware

 

 • When you set up Stoneware, you define usernames and passwords for your users.
• When you set up single sign-on (SSO) with Stoneware, you create a hidden SAML application on your Stoneware portal, so when students authenticate to Stoneware, they are logged in to the hidden SAML application.
• When users click on an single sign-on (SSO) link, Clever queries the SAML application to see which user is logged in. The SAML application uses claims rules to identify the user, and we grant them access to the application if they are correctly set up in Clever and in Stoneware.
 
 Please note: Because credentials are only entered on the Stoneware Portal log in screen, Clever will never know the usernames and passwords of your users. 

 Can I use the Clever Portal instead of the Stoneware Portal?

 It’s possible to log in to the Clever Portal using your Stoneware credentials once single sign-on (SSO) with Stoneware is set up. However, as the Stoneware set up process requires adding single sign-on (SSO) links to the Stoneware Portal in order for users to be able to log in, districts generally prefer to use the existing Stoneware Portal. 

 Setup Preparation 

 If you haven't finished signing up for a Clever account yet, you can choose "Custom Username & Password" and "Synced Passwords" to start. Once you have access to the Clever Admin Dashboard, take the following steps: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page. 
• Choose the shortname for your ingle sign-on (SSO) portal URL. The URL will be www.clever.com / shortname . Remember to use something that your students and teachers will remember easily. 
• Keep Identity System set to Google Apps or Clever Passwords for now 
• Add the contact information that students and teachers should reach out to if they have trouble with logging in to Clever - this should be someone who can help them reset their Stoneware credentials and/or make sure they are shared with the application they're trying to access through Clever. 
• Click Save . 
 
 Configuring the Public webApp for Clever 

 You’ll need to configure Stoneware to connect with Clever single sign-on (SSO). In order to do so, you’ll need to set up a public webApp for Clever. 

 Creating a Public webApp 

 • Open webAdmin, right-click Applications Admin , Create Public Application , and enter Clever SSO as the webApp name. 
• Open the Clever SSO Properties tab and set the following properties: 
 • Description: CleverSSO 
• Application Type: Public 
• Context Path (Alias) /CleverSSO 
 • You may need to refresh the page for this option to appear 
 

• Assigned Relay(s): This should assign the app to each relay in your environment by default. //More complex setup? Reach out to StonewareUse the green ‘+’ icon to assign the app to each relay in your environment 
• Please note that Assigned Link should be blank 
 

 
 Setting up Single-Sign-On to the Clever webApp Open the Clever SSO Authentication Tab and set the following properties: 

 

 • Type: ADFS 
• Identity: Use the Wizard to add an attribute that matches data in Clever 
• SAML Initiation: Service Provider 
• Assertion Consumer: https://clever.com/oauth/saml/ 
• PFX Certificate: Click on the Wizard button 
 • Set Algorithm to RSA and Strength to 2048 
• Click the Generate button, then the Overwrite button 
 

• Certificate: Use the wizard to configure the certificate as you would any other self-signed certificate. 
• SAML Logout Behavior: Full Logout 
 
 Setting up Attributes for single sign-on (SSO) 

 Open the CleverSSO Attributes or Provisioning tab and set the following properties: 

 • Attribute Assertion: Enabled 
• Type: SAML 
• Attribute Assertion Parameters: Use the green ‘+’ button to add the following attributes: 
 • clever.any.sis_id = <the Stoneware Attribute that matches Clever sis_id> 
• clever.any.email = @@attr:mail@@ 
 • This attribute is optional but recommended! 
 

 

 
 Once you’re done here, hit save and reload the page! 

 Testing the Public webApp Navigate to https:// <dns of your webNetwork system>/swPublicSSO/SAMLMetadata/CleverSSO - this should display a page like this: 

 

 You’ll want to double-check that the two attributes at the bottom match the attributes you set up in the CleverSSO webApp. 

 If this page does not load or does not properly display the attributes, double-check your webApp configuration and assignments, and try reloading this page. 

 Finishing setup with Clever support 

 Once you’ve finished the above, please contact Clever Support and provide the following information: 

 • The URL that you used to test your CleverSSO webApp 
• An application that you’ve added to your Portal 
 
 We’ll help you finalize setup and test your first logins! 

 Adding applications to the Stoneware Portal 

 You can add Clever apps to the Stoneware Portal using single sign-on (SSO) links - this allows users to access applications seamlessly, thanks to the public webApp you’ve set up. Once you’ve acquired the direct link from your Clever Dashboard, you can add it to the Portal by adding a new Link Object to your portal for each application and assigning it to the appropriate users. 

 The Link Object should look like this: 

 
 
 Please note: single sign-on (SSO) will only work for users who both have access to this Link Object and are shared with the application in question through Clever. 

 If you have any questions about setting up Clever SSO, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): "Log in with Office 365/Entra ID"
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Preparing for Setup with Clever 3. Setup in Entra ID 4. Setting up Entra ID SSO to Clever 5. Setting up Clever 

 

 Overview

 To use single sign-on (SSO) with Entra ID/Office 365, you'll need to make sure you have: 

 • Active SIS sync with Clever
• Entra ID Premium OR Entra ID and PowerShell Proficiency
 
 Not sure if you have Entra ID? If you have a paid subscription to Office 365 for your organization, you have a free subscription to Entra ID.

 

 
 Preparing for Setup with Clever

 If you haven't finished signing up for a Clever account yet, you can choose Google to start. Once you have access to the Clever Dashboard , take the following steps: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings. 
• Choose Add Login Method 
• Choose SAML Authentication 
• Enter your metadata URL (you can find out how to obtain your metadata URL below)
• Check the box for Allow unencrypted SAML assertions 
• Click Save 
• Add the contact information for whom students and teachers should reach out to if they have trouble with logging in to Clever - this should be someone who can help them reset their Entra ID credentials and/or make sure they are shared with the application they're trying to access through Clever.
 
 

 • Next, navigate to Portal > Portal settings and choose your district's Portal URL. The URL will be ">www.clever.com/in/<shortname> ; . Remember to use something that your students and teachers will remember easily.
 
 

 
 Setup In Entra ID

 You'll need to configure Entra ID to connect with Clever single sign-on (SSO). In order to do that, you'll need to: 

 • Add the Clever app to Entra ID
• Set up SSO to the Clever App
• Set up Claims Rules to allow Clever to match Entra ID users to Clever records
• Assign users to the Clever App in Entra ID
 
 Adding the Clever App to Entra ID 

 • 
 To add Clever from the gallery, perform the following steps: 

 • 
 In the Entra ID portal , on the left navigation panel, click Entra ID icon.

 

• 
 Navigate to Enterprise applications . Then go to All applications .

 

• 
 Navigate to Enterprise applications - All applications > Categories > Add an application and search for Clever . 

 

 

 
 
 

 Setting up Entra ID SSO into Clever

 For single sign-on to work, Entra ID needs to know what the counterpart user in Clever is to a user in Entra ID. In other words, a link relationship between an Entra ID user and the related user in Clever needs to be established. 

 In Clever, assign the value of the user name in Entra ID as the value of the Username to establish the link relationship. 

 Configure Entra ID single sign-on 

 In this section, you enable Entra ID single sign-on in the Entra ID portal and configure single sign-on in your Clever application. 

 To configure Entra ID single sign-on with Clever, perform the following steps: 

 • 
 In the Entra ID portal , on the Clever application integration page, find the Manage section and select single sign-on .

• 
 On the Select a single sign-on method page, select SAML .

• 
 On the Set up single sign-on with SAML page, click the edit/pen icon for Basic SAML Configuration to edit the settings.

 

 

• 
 On the Basic SAML Configuration section, enter the values for the following fields: 

 a. In the Identifier (Entity ID) text box, type a URL using the following: https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml 

 b. In the Reply URL (Assertion Consumer Service URL) text box, type a URL using either of the following: https://clever.com/* or https://clever.com/oauth/saml/assert 

• c. In the Sign on URL text box, type a URL using the following pattern: ">https://clever.com/in/<shortname> ; 
• 
• 
• 5. Next, open the User Attributes & Claims section. 
 
 

 There will be required claims that you will not need to adjust at all. 

 Additional Claims rules are used to allow Clever to determine which student or teacher is logging in. To do this, we match Entra ID attributes to data in Clever. Claim name will be the Clever field and the value is the field in Entra ID . In order for users to be able to log in, there needs to be an attribute for each user type that exactly matches data in a field in Clever. We recommend adding three claims - one each for: 

 • Students 
• Teachers 
• Staff 
 
 To view the possible Clever Claim name attribute values for each user type, see this article .

 

 • 
 To generate the Metadata URL, navigate to SAML Signing Certificate and copy the App Federation Metadata URL. 

 

 
 Grant users access to Clever 

 In this section, you will enable students and teachers to use Entra ID single sign-on by granting access to Clever. 

 To assign users to Clever, perform the following steps: 

 • 
 In the Entra ID portal, open the applications view, and then navigate to the directory view and go to Enterprise applications then click All applications .

 

• 
 In the applications list, select Clever .

 

• 
 In the menu on the left, click Users and groups .

 

• 
 Click the Add button. Then select Users and groups on Add Assignment dialog.

 

• 
 On the Users and groups dialog, select the appropriate users from the Users list.

• 
 Click Select button on the Users and groups dialog.

• 
 Click Assign button on the Add Assignment dialog.

 
 

 If you don't properly configure the settings above, or if a user isn't in a group assigned to Clever, they may receive a Microsoft error that states, "Your administrator has configured the application Clever to block users unless they are specifically granted ('assigned') access to the application. The signed in user is blocked because they are not a direct member of a group with access, nor had access directly assigned by an administrator."

 To resolve this error, please review the steps above and ensure the user is in a group assigned to Clever.

 

 
 
 Setting up Clever

 
 • 
 In a different web browser window, log in and navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings. 

• 
 On the SSO Settings page, perform the following steps:

 a. Select Add Login Method . b. Select SAML Authentication. 

 

 c. Enter the Metadata URL you pulled from the Entra ID console in the Metadata URL text box.

 

 d. Check the box for Allow unencrypted SAML assertions 

 d. Click Save. 

 
 If you have any questions about setting up Clever SSO, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 

 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: Single sign-on (SSO): Configure a SAML-based IDP for access to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. Next Steps 

 Overview

 You can add any SAML connection as an identity provider (IdP) into Clever! We offer support for Active Directory Federation Services ( AD and Entra ID ), Okta, and Rapid ID . However, any SAML connection can be created by following the same steps outlined in this article. Clever only supports one SAML IdP setup per account.

 

 
 

 Requirements

 Here's what your Identity Provider (IDP) must have: 

 
 • Support for SP-initiated sign on using a Redirect Binding
• Response must be sent to a specified POST Binding ( https://clever.com/oauth/ saml /assert ) 
• Response must contain an assertion encrypted using Clever's public key (found in the metadata file)
• The assertion must be signed with a private key owned by the IDP
• The assertion must contain an AuthnStatement with a SessionIndex
• The assertion must contain at least one attribute which can uniquely identify a Clever user
• Multiple attributes are allowed and only one needs to match a user, but only one user must be matched.
• Attributes should be in the form clever.[user type].[field] and contain the field they wish to match on.
 • For example, an attribute with the name clever.student.sis_id with a value of 12345 will attempt to find a student with the SIS ID of 12345. A user type of 'any' may be used to match both students and teachers with the same field. 
 

• A LogoutRequest must be accepted at a Redirect Binding. The LogoutResponse should be sent to the specified HTTP-Redirect Binding ( https://clever.com/oauth/ saml /assert ) 
• There cannot be a trailing whitespace or a newline at the start and end of the certificate.
 
 

 Please note: Clever doesn't have insight into the configurations or set-up steps that allow your IDP to meet the above requirements for custom SAML connections. You can find more information here: Single Sign-On (SSO): Identity Provider (IDP)/Login Method Options 

 Next Steps

 
 Once you have verified the above requirements and have either the URL or .xml file, you can navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings . 

 • Click Add Login Method in the top right corner, then select SAML Authentication . This will allow for a SAML connection to Clever.
 

 

 
 

 • Input your metadata URL in the text box. 
 
 

 • Select your Identity Provider from the dropdown. If your Identity Provider is not available, select SAML IdP .
 
 

 • Select Save .
• To finish the setup process:
 
 • 
 • Go to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Customize tab and customize your login screen by clicking on the gray pencil icons.
• Add the Tech Support Contact details for the person that students and teachers should reach out to if they have trouble logging in to Clever; this should be someone who can help them reset their credentials and/or make sure they are shared with the application they’re trying to access through Clever.
• Add any login hints for your login methods (e.g., if you’re using one login method for students and one for teachers). 
 

 
 

 

 

 You should now be set up! Try a few logins and see if you run into any issues. If you have questions about this process, please contact our support team.

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): "Log in with Canvas"
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Set-up 

 Overview 

 This article describes how districts can set up Canvas as a login method for Clever, meaning users will sign in to Clever with their Canvas credentials. If you are interested in configuring logins to Canvas from Clever, please see this article . 

 Set-up 

 To set up Canvas as a login method for Clever, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. In the upper-righthand corner, click the Add Login Method button. From there, select Canvas Authentication and enter your Canvas instance URL. If you run into any issues with this, put in a request to Clever Support. A connection will be set up to match Canvas fields against Clever fields and authenticate users. Here are the default matches: 

 Canvas field - [Clever fields] 

 email -> [email] login_id -> [email, username] sis_user_id -> [sis_id] unique_id -> [email, credentials.district_username, sis_id, student_number] 

 If these default matches do not work for your district, please let us know in your support request.

title: Single sign-on (SSO): "Log in with NCEdCloud"
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Configuring NCEdCloud IAM 3. Customizing your Clever login option 4. Clever Portal URL 

 

 Overview

 Clever single sign-on (SSO) allows North Carolina students and teachers to log in using their NCEdCloud IAM credentials. This article details the configuration process for setting up Clever single sign-on (SSO) with NCEdCloud IAM. 

 North Carolina Districts that are not currently on Clever should sign up for Clever here! 

 

 Configuring NCEdCloud IAM

 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page.
• Select Add Login Method to the right-hand side. 
• Select Active Directory Authentication as your Identity Provider (IdP). 
 
 

 • Please note: This option establishes a SAML connection with NCEdCloud IAM. This will not establish Active Directory as your Clever SSO login method. 
 
 • Enter the NCEdCloud IAM Metadata.xml file URL: 
 
 • https://s3.amazonaws.com/assets.clever.com/SAML/NCEdTest.xml 
 
 

 

 

 • Select Save . 
 
 

 Customizing your Clever login option

 To customize the login option from your district's Clever login page :

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. 
• Select the Customize tab. 
• Click on the pencil icon to change the Login with Active Directory label to Login with NCEd Cloud . 
 
 

 • Select Save . 
 
 

 Clever Portal URL

 Every Clever district has the ability to customize a unique Clever login URL to share with district users in order to simplify the Clever login process. This URL is a direct way for users to log in to your district's Clever Portal rather than navigating to Clever.com and selecting their school from a drop-down menu. To do this: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page.
• Click on the URL shortname in the Clever Portal URL box to edit it. The shortname is the part after https://clever.com/in/ .
• Update the editable text to the new shortname you'd like to use.
 • For example : Updating the shortname to NorthernSchoolDistrict would change your Portal URL to: https://clever.com/in/northernschooldistrict 
 

• Select Update to save your changes.
• Share the updated Portal URL with your district users so they can navigate directly to your district's Clever login page!
 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever Support ! We are happy to help!

title: For Families: How to download a Clever Badge for your student
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Downloading Student Badges from the Clever Portal 2. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Downloading Student Badges from the Clever Portal

 Students can download their own Badges from within the Clever Portal. Once a student logs in, they can find a Badge by clicking their profile icon in the top right corner, then selecting Download Badge . 

 

 Once they click Download Badge, their Badge will be saved as a PDF to the downloads folder on their device. Once downloaded, this PDF can be printed for use by your student. For information about Badges and how to use them, check out this article: For Students: How do I log into Clever using a Badge? 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Why don’t I see a “Download Badge” option? 

 The Download Badge option is only available for students that have Badges enabled for them by their school organization. If you don’t see the Download Badge option, please contact your school organization's tech support contact. If you do not see a support contact listed, please contact your student's teacher. 

 

 How am I supposed to log in without a Badge? 

 In order to download a Badge, you will first need to log into the Portal using a non-Badge login method. To do so, follow the instructions here: Logging in to the Portal . If you’re having trouble logging in, feel free to ask your teacher or school organization tech support contact for help. 

 What if I don't have a printer? 

 If you don't have a printer, a great alternative is to use your phone to display the Badge! Some options include: 

 • Take a picture of the Badge using your cellphone camera and scan the Badge from your photos app. 
• Email the Badge to yourself and scan the Badge through the email app on your phone 
 
 Additional information on using your phone to display a Clever Badge can be found here . 

 What if I lost the Badge? 

 If you lose the Badge, you can always download a new one from the Portal. It’ll be the same as the one you lost so, if you find your old one, both will continue to work. 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Families: Troubleshooting - Logins to Clever

title: For Families: Clever Badges
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Logging in with a Badge 3. Using a 6-digit Badge PIN 4. What if my student forgot their Badge PIN? 5. What if I cannot print my student's Badge? 6. Devices without cameras 

 Overview 

 Clever Badges are an easy and secure way for your student to log in to Clever by scanning a Badge into a device's webcam rather than needing to enter a username and password! Badges are tied to the student. As such, changes in school, teacher, or section will not affect the Badge. 

 
 Logging in with a Badge 

 1. Your student will first need to obtain a Clever Badge from their teacher or school. 

 2. Then, they will navigate to the Clever login page for your student's school organization, or go directly to clever.com/badges. Please do not attempt to use a QR reader to log your student in. Clever Badges are designed to be used directly with Clever and a QR reader won't log your student into Clever.

 • Please note: There may be multiple schools by the same name, so please be sure to select the school associated with your student's school organization! 
 
 3. Select the Clever Badge Log in option. 

 

 4. Your internet browser may ask you to allow clever.com to use your device's camera. Select Allow . 

 

 5. Hold your student's Badge up to the device's camera and wait for the green checkmark to log your student into Clever! 

 

 

 6. Once your student is logged into their Clever Portal, they can click on the application icon they'd like to use: 

 

 

 
 

 Using a 6-digit Badge PIN 

 If your student's school organization has enabled Badge PINs for your student's account, your student will be asked to create or enter a 6-digit PIN. Be sure to have your student create a PIN that they will remember! 

 

 

 
 

 What if my student forgot their Badge PIN? 

 1. If your student forgot their 6-digit Badge PIN, please contact your student's teacher or school to help them log in to Clever. 

 

 Please note: If you are unsure who to reach out to at your student's school, you can typically locate the appropriate contact information by selecting the 'Get help signing in' or 'Get help logging in!' option from your school's Clever login page. 

 

 2. Once your student is logged into their Clever Portal, they can reset their 6-digit PIN by clicking on their name in the top-right corner, and selecting Reset your Badge PIN . 

 

 

 Clever Tip: Teachers can help students log into Clever using a backup code, or by resetting the student's password! Check out this Help Center article for more information: For Teachers: How do I help my student log into Clever? 

 

 
 

 What if I cannot print my student's Badge? 

 If you do not have access to a printer, try the following: 

 • Save the Clever Badge to the photo app on your phone
• Open the website clever.com/badges on your student's computer or device.
• Open the photo of the Badge on your phone, turn your phone's screen to face the computer, and hold it up to the camera.
• The badge should successfully scan! Tip: If it does not scan at first, try adjusting the distance and angle from the camera! 
 
 

 
 

 Devices without cameras 

 If a student does not have access to a device with a camera, they can log in using the school organization's normal login method (e.g. Log in with Google, Log in with Active Directory, Log in with Clever).

 

 If you require assistance with identifying their username and password for one of these login methods, please reach out to your school organization's tech support contact .

title: For Families: What is Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What is Clever? 2. How can my child log in to Clever? 3. What should I do if I have a problem with Clever or a learning program? 4. Is Clever safe for my child to use? 

 What is Clever?

 Clever is an easy way for your child to log in and learn with all of the online programs they use at school. With their own personal portals, students can learn with resources selected by their teacher and district. 

 Schools use Clever so students can have all of their digital resources in one place. Clever also eliminates the need to remember multiple usernames and passwords. 

 

 
 How can my child log in to Clever?

 Your student can visit clever.com/login to find their school and log in to Clever with their school-provided username and password. If your child has a Clever Badge, they can use their Badge to sign in at clever.com/badges . 

 Your school district may create a parent/guardian account that you can use to help your child log in. Learn more about parent/guardian accounts. 

 
 

 

 
 What should I do if I have a problem with Clever or a learning program?

 If your child is unable to log in to Clever or if you encounter a problem with one of the programs on Clever, please contact your school. Your school will be able to help your child access their account, and can help troubleshoot any issues you may have run into. 

 Similarly, if you'd like more information on the applications and tools your child has access to, or believe they should have access to additional learning materials, please contact your school directly. The Clever team can't assist with student access to learning materials as your school manages this.

 

 
 Is Clever safe for my child to use?

 Clever has worked with school districts and learning applications to create a safe, secure, private, experience for every student. Clever is set up and managed by schools - so every resource your child sees on Clever has been selected by their school. The data in Clever is only used to support your child’s education - it is never sold or used for advertising. 

 Detailed information about Clever’s privacy, security and compliance policies and practices can be found in the Trust Center .

title: For Families: Troubleshooting - Shared Devices
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Directions - General 3. Directions - Log in with Google 4. Directions - Chromebooks 5. Need additional help? 

 

 Overview

 If your household has multiple students with Clever accounts using this same device, this article will help make sure that your children are properly logged out before the other logs in!

 

 Directions - General

 To ensure that your child's user session has ended before logging the other in, please follow these steps: Step 1: Log out of Clever 

 • From the Clever Portal , click on the student's name in the top right corner, select Log out. 
 
 Step 2: Log in to Clever as new user 

 • Navigate to https://clever.com/login and search for your school. Once your school is located, click on the Google button ​and enter your school email address and password. You should now be successfully logged in to the new user's Clever account!
 
 Directions - Log in with Google 

 

 Step 1: Log out of Clever 

 • From the Clever Portal , click on the student's name in the top right corner and select Log out. 
 
 Step 2: Log out of Google 

 • Pro tip: you can click on this link to sign out! 
 
 Step 3: Log in to Clever as a new user 

 • Navigate to https://clever.com/login and search for your school. Once your school is located, click on the Google button ​and enter your school email address and password. You should now be successfully logged in to the new user's Clever account!
 
 Directions - Chromebooks

 If your students share a Chromebook, we recommend setting up multiple profiles on the device. Once configured, each student should use their personal profile to prevent conflict between accounts. 

 Step 1: Set up multiple profiles. Directions can be found here . 

 Step 2: Sign in to the additional profile(s) 

 Before you can sign in another person, be sure you've added the second person to your Chromebook. Then follow these steps: 

 • Sign in to your Google Account.
• At the bottom right, select the time.
• Select your Google Account email.
• Select Sign in another user .
• You'll see a reminder to use the feature only with people you trust. Select OK .
• Pick a person, then enter their password. You can add up to 5 users.
 
 Switch between accounts you're signed in to 

 After you've added multiple people, you can switch between them: 

 • Sign in to your Google Account.
• At the bottom right, select the time.
• Select the person you'd like to switch to.
 
 All active people share some settings, such as networks. Other settings, like profile pictures and bookmarks, are specific to each person.

 Need additional help?

 We recommend clearing the browser history (cookies, cache, etc.). For Chrome, you can clear the cache by following these steps: 

 • On your computer, open Chrome.
• At the top right, click More .
• Click More tools > Clear browsing data .
• At the top, choose a time range. To delete everything, select All time .
• Next to "Cookies and other site data" and "Cached images and files," check the boxes.
• Click Clear data .
 
 If you are using an alternate browser, please see the following documentation: 

 • Apple Devices 
• Windows Devices 
 
 If the above steps have been completed but the login does not work, please have your students log in using a private window. This will ignore all existing user sessions, allowing other users to log in: 

 • On your computer, open Google Chrome .
• At the top right, click More New Incognito Window .
• A new window appears. In the top corner, check for the Incognito icon .
• Navigate to https://clever.com/login and log in using the instructions outlined in Step 3.
 
 If you are still running into issues, feel free to contact Clever Support !

title: For Families: Troubleshooting - Clever iOS app
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Basic Troubleshooting 3. Advanced Troubleshooting 

 

 Please note: The Clever iOS app requires iOS 16.6 or later and is compatible with iPads and iPhones. If you're using older devices that don’t support iOS 16.6+, your users can still access Clever via a web browser on their iOS device by opening the browser and navigating to your school organization’s Portal URL 

 Overview

 If you or your student are having issues with their iOS device and Clever, here are some recommended troubleshooting steps you can try to help. 

 
 Basic Troubleshooting

 • Before you begin, please make sure your device is using iOS version 16.6 or above.
• Turn the device off and on again, then try again.
• Uninstall, then re-install the Clever app, then try again.
 
 If the above steps don't resolve your issue, you can also try accessing Clever directly from a web browser, such as Safari. If using Clever Badges, you can login at https://clever.com/badges in the Safari app.

 If you encounter issues accessing Clever in a web browser, we recommend these steps:

 • Ensure that the iOS device has internet connectivity by navigating to any webpage in the Safari app.
• Ensure that “Private Mode” is not enabled for Safari on the iOS device.
• Ensure cookies are enabled for Safari. This includes the device-wide setting, as well as for the Clever domain ( clever.com ). Settings > Safari Block All Cookies . Make sure Block All Cookies is toggled off -- you will know it is off when the slider turns grey.
• Ensure Javascript is enabled for Safari. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Safari > Javascript .
• Ensure camera access is enabled for Safari. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Safari > Camera .
 
 

 
 Advanced Troubleshooting

 The steps below are for troubleshooting issues logging into Clever via a web browser on the iPad, such as Safari. The steps below will not work if you are attempting to troubleshoot in the Clever app. 

 • Ensure that on the device, the iOS network deny list is not enabled. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > General > Restrictions > Websites .
• Ensure that requests to Clever servers are not blocked or filtered by a firewall or network filtering software. See our Help Center article: Which Clever's domains should my network grant access ?
• In iOS 13, Safari has a new setting option that is enabled by default after updating an iPad from iOS 12. Please ensure that Safari's setting for “Request Desktop Website” is disabled. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Safari > Request Desktop Website .
• In iOS 14, users have the ability to choose a default browser other than Safari. Please ensure that Safari is the default browser for any device that will be used with the Clever app. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Browser > Safari .
• Ensure that no other software affects the way that Safari operates.
 
 If the issue still persists, feel free to open a ticket with Clever Support!

title: For Families: Troubleshooting - Apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. App is missing 3. Clicked on app, received an error 4. Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password 5. Problems within apps 

 Overview 

 Occasionally, students will have issues with their applications (i.e. apps) in Clever. Here are some steps you can take to help your student if they are having trouble with a specific application. 

 
 App is missing 

 If your student is missing an application in Clever, please ask your student's teacher for help. Your student's school organization will likely need to add the app to their Clever Portal for them. 

 

 

 
 Clicked on app, received an error 

 If your student clicks on an app icon in their Clever Portal and receives an error from the app's website page, please ask your student's teacher to contact the Clever administrator or the application's support team for help . 

 Please note: If your student has recently changed schools or classes, please allow 24 hrs for their connected applications to be updated so they can log in. 

 Clever Tip: If you are seeing an error at a web URL that is not a Clever address, it will be necessary to contact the application to fix the issue. Please have your student's teacher reach out to the application's support team to investigate further! 

 Here are examples of common errors you might see for specific applications:

 

 

 

 
 Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password 

 Did you know? Applications that show a Clever prompt to enter usernames and passwords require the Clever browser extension. Please see this article for details.

 If a Clever page asks your student to enter a username and password for an application, enter the correct login information for that application, and click 'Save Login'. Clever will save this information and automatically log your student in next time they click the icon from the Portal!
