 If your student clicks on an application and sees the page below, this means the login credentials they entered are incorrect. Please enter your student's correct username and password for the application, and then select Update & Log in .

 

 If you are still having trouble, ask your student's teacher to make sure you have the right username and password. They can contact Clever Support if further help is needed!

 

 
 

 Problems within apps 

 After your student is logged into an app successfully through Clever, any issues within the app would need to be addressed by the app provider. 

 Clever is not able to resolve issues within the various applications . One way to think of it is, Clever helps a student "get in the door" of an application but, once inside, we have little insight into any issues that come up. Some issues might include: 

 • The app freezing
• Not being able to access grades or textbooks
• Your student runs out of time during a test
• The app suddenly logs your student out
• Missing lessons or assignments
• Accessing the microphone or camera
 
 I f your student is running into an issue within the application, please ask your student's teacher to contact the application's support team for help.

title: For Families: Installing the Clever Browser Extension
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Download Links 

 

 Overview

 If your student uses Clever to learn from home, you may need to download the Clever browser extension to ensure that your student's learning applications work correctly. Downloading the Clever browser extension is essential to ensuring that your student can continue their learning uninterrupted from school, home, or both. The internet browsers that Clever supports are Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Microsoft Edge. 

 Please note: The Clever browser extension is not required for iOS devices (e.g., iPads, iPhones) as these devices use the Clever app, which acts as the browser extension. Please ensure you have downloaded the Clever App from your device's App Store. For devices where the browser extension cannot be downloaded (e.g., Android, Kindles, Fire tablet), SSO (Saved Passwords) applications will not be accessible. For details, visit: Clever Browser & Device Compatibility 

 

 
 Download Links

 Please see the links below for information and instructions for installing the Clever browser extension:

 • Google Chrome Instructions and Install 
• Microsoft Edge Instructions and Install 
• Mozilla Firefox Instructions and Install 
 
 

 Related Articles: 

 • How do I use Clever on iOS devices (iPads, iPhones)? 
• For Families: Troubleshooting - Clever iOS app

title: For Families: Troubleshooting - Logins to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I get help? 3. Login Directions • Log in with Clever • Log in with Google • Log in with Active Directory • Log in with Badges 3. Logging out 

 

 Overview 

 The first step to log your student into Clever is ensuring you are using a Clever-compatible device or browser. Please visit this article for details. Once you have confirmed your device is compatible, you can navigate to your district's Clever login page, or to clever.com/login to search for your student's school in order to log in. 

 • Please note: There may be multiple schools by the same name, so please be sure to select the school associated with your student's school district! If you cannot find their school, please ask their teacher how to log in to Clever. 
 
 

 
 How do I get help? 

 L ogin credentials for Clever are managed by the school district. If you are unsure of your student's login credentials, receive an error when logging in, or your student's login suddenly stops working, please reach out to your teacher or the district's help desk for assistance. 

 You can find your district's technology support contact information on the Clever login screen. In most cases, districts provide an email address, phone number, or instructions guiding users to the appropriate contact person or department for assistance. 

 If you do not see a support contact listed, please contact your student's teacher. 

 

 

 
 Login Directions 

 Please navigate to the appropriate section below for instructions specific to your district's login method (e.g. Google, Active Directory, etc.). 

 Log in with Clever 

 

 If your student is using the Log in with Clever option, or only has the option to enter a username and password, enter their district credentials. If your student receives an error stating Invalid username or password , this means the credentials your student has entered are incorrect for their Clever account. If allowed by your school, your student can select the Get help logging in! option to reset their password.

 Please note: Not all schools allow students to reset their own passwords. If you don't see the password reset option, or if this doesn't resolve the issue, please reach out to your student's teacher or school administrator for help with their Clever credentials. 

 Log in with Google 

 

 If your student is using the Log in with Google option, they will be redirected to sign in using their school-provided email address and password. They will not log in using a personal Google (@ gmail.com ) email address. If your student receives one of the below errors, here is what to do: 

 

 
 Error

 
 Details and next steps

 
 Wrong password. Try again or click Forgot password to reset it. This error means they entered an incorrect password for their school-provided email address. They’ll need to enter the correct password for their account or reset it (if applicable). 
 We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email xxxx@gmail.com . Try again with your school issued email address. This error means that the email address they entered isn’t the same one linked to their Clever account. Please make sure you are using their school-provided email address. If you believe your student is using the correct email address, please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. The Clever team cannot provide login credentials. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the email xxxx@example.com This error means that the email address your student is attempting to log in with is duplicated in Clever. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. The Clever team cannot edit email addresses or provide login credentials. 
 We couldn’t log you in. You may receive this error with additional details or by itself. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for further assistance in troubleshooting. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 

 Please note: Your student's login credentials are configured by the school district. If you are unsure what they are or are unable to reset your student's password, please visit this section . 

 Log in with Active Directory 

 

 If your student is using the Log in with Active Directory method, they'll be redirected to sign in to their school account through Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS). Student usernames and passwords for ADFS are managed by your student's school administration. Clever doesn't have access to these credentials. Their credentials are usually the email and password they use to log in to their computer at school.

 If your student receives one of the below errors, here is what to do:

 
 Error

 
 Details and next steps

 
 We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email address or username. This error means the email address or username they entered isn’t in the district or school’s Clever account. Please make sure you are using their school-provided email or username. If your student is using the correct email or username, please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. The Clever team cannot provide login credentials. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the student’s email or username. This error means that the email address or username your student is attempting to log in with is duplicated in Clever. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. The Clever team cannot edit email addresses or provide login credentials. 
 We couldn’t log you in. You may receive this error with additional details or by itself. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for further assistance in troubleshooting. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 Log in with Badges 

 • Select the Clever Badge log in option. You can also go directly to clever.com/badges. 
 
 

 • Your internet browser may ask you to allow clever.com to use your device's camera. Select Allow .
 
 

 • Hold your student's Badge up to the device's camera and wait for the green checkmark to log your student into Clever!
 
 

 Please visit this article for additional instructions for helping your student log in with a Badge.

 

 
 Logging out 

 To log out of Clever, navigate to the Clever Portal, hover over your student's in the top-right corner, and click Log out . 

 
 

 Users are also logged out of Clever automatically after: 

 
 • 24 hours of inactivity 
• The browser is quit (e.g. Quit Google Chrome ). Note: Closing the browser window will not log the user out of Clever.

title: For Families: Clever security policies for student data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Full Control from your school organization 3. Student Data Privacy at Clever 4. Infrastructure 5. Additional Resources 

 

 Overview

 Many of the nation’s largest school organizations rely on Clever to sync student information with learning applications, in part because security is a top priority. Clever keeps student information safe by ensuring: 

 • Full school organization control over data access rights at all times;
• A robust privacy policy that governs Clever's use of student data; and
• Protection through a tested and secure infrastructure.
 
 

 
 Full control from your school organization

 School organizations control data access at all points in the process, from signup all the way through account provisioning. School orgs determine: 

 • What data Clever can access (always read-only);
• How often they sync data with Clever;
• Which applications can access data; and
• How much data each application can access (via data sharing permissions ).
 
 Each school organization can fine-tune these settings at any time through the Clever Dashboard. If a school organization chooses to discontinue their Clever sync, they can do so at any time. Clever will delete their account and remove any residual data immediately upon request. 

 
 

 Student Data Privacy at Clever

 Clever syncs basic information to manage user accounts in online learning applications, such as student and teacher identifiers, section names, and enrollment information. 

 All rostering data that is synced with Clever is protected by industry-leading infrastructure (see below). Clever will never use or sell student information for marketing or other purposes. 

 Additionally, Clever adheres to FERPA compliance under the FTC's School Officials Exemption, meaning that Clever provides a service on behalf of schools that schools themselves would usually provide. Furthermore, all Clever-supported applications agree to adhere to FERPA requirements in the terms of our partnership. 

 To learn more about data privacy, you can read Clever's full Privacy Policy. 

 

 
 Infrastructure

 Clever has spent years developing one of the best security infrastructures in K-12 education. Key features of Clever's industry-leading infrastructure are: 

 • AES256 data encryption for data at rest;
• TLS with strong ciphers, with a preference for those with perfect-forward secrecy, for data in transit;
• Multiple factor authentication;
• Third-party security audits and code reviews;
• Responsible disclosure (“bug bounty”) program;
• Full-time security administrator; and
• Employee background checks.
 
 The syncing process is encrypted at all times using 256-bit encryption, which is double the standard used by the consumer banking industry. In addition, Clever's production environment is completely isolated from the internet, and all access is logged and secured using multiple factor authentication . 

 Clever has a full-time security administrator and invests in regular third-party audits in order to ensure continual improvement of security protocols. 

 
 Additional Resources

 To learn more about our security practices, you can read our comprehensive security program.

title: For School Tech Leads: Getting Help
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Contact the school or district 2. Contact the application 3. Contact Clever 

 Contact the school or district 

 Here are common examples of when your school's support contact will be best equipped to provide support: 

 • You or your student is unable to log in to Clever
• An application is missing from the Clever Portal
• Students or teachers are missing from Clever or an application
• Data is missing or incorrect in Clever or an application
• Adding a co-teacher to a class
• Changing your user type in Clever
 

 You can find your district's tech support contact information by clicking the Get help signing in! link on the Clever login screen. From here, you should see an email or phone number where you can reach the support contact for your district.

 ​ 

 Contact the application

 Here are common examples of when an application’s team will be best equipped to provide support: 

 • You or your students click on an application in the Clever Portal and receives an error on the application's page or URL 
• You are receiving an error message on the application’s website
• You are seeing incorrect data within the application, but correct data within the Clever Portal
• You are having trouble accessing grades, homework, textbooks, resources, or running reports within the application
• Your sections have access to the incorrect content within the application
• You have questions about features within the application
• The application does not save a student’s progress
• The application is frozen or logs you or your students out
 

 We recommend reaching out to the application's support team directly. Their contact information can usually be found by searching the app's name and "support contact" in Google.

 Contact Clever

 For all other questions, Clever Support can be reached by selecting Contact us from the top of the Help Center ( support.clever.com ). Our team is available via email, Monday - Friday 7am - 7pm ET.

title: For Students: How do I log in to Clever using a Badge?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Log into Clever using a Badge 2. Log into Clever with a Badge on a Chromebook 3. Log into Clever with a Badge on the Clever App 4. Using a 6-digit Badge PIN 5. What if I forgot my Badge PIN? 

 Clever Badges are an easy way that you can log into Clever using your computer's webcam! 

 

 Log into Clever using a Badge

 To log in using Badges, ask your teacher for your Clever Badge. From the Clever login page, you can click the ' Clever Badge Log in ' button. You can also go directly to clever.com/badges. You cannot use a QR reader to log into Clever. 

 

 

 Your browser may ask you to allow clever.com to use your computer's camera. Click Allow while visiting this site or Allow . If you accidentally clicked Block or Never allow , you can click here to learn how to turn your camera back on. 

 

 Hold your Badge up to your computer camera and wait for the green checkmark to log you in to Clever! 

 

 

 Log into Clever with a Badge on a Chromebook

 To log in using Badges with your Chromebook, ask your teacher for your Clever Badge then follow these steps:

 • 
 Open your Google Chromebook 

• 
 Click the Clever Badge Log in button. 

• 
 Hold your Badge up to the camera.

 
 Watch this video to learn how to log into your Chromebook with a Badge. 

 

 

 

 Log into Clever with a Badge on the Clever iOS app

 Make sure that you have already downloaded the Clever application on your device. 

 To log in using Badges with the Clever application, ask your teacher for your Clever Badge then follow these steps:

 • 
 Open the Clever application

• 
 Allow the camera access to your device if you haven’t already

• 
 Hold your Badge up to the camera

 
 Watch this video to learn how to log into the Clever app with a Badge. 

 

 

 

 Using a 6-digit Badge PIN

 If your district has enabled Badge PINs for your account, you will be asked to create or enter a 6-digit PIN. Be sure to create a PIN that you will remember! 

 

 

 

 What if I forgot my Badge PIN?

 If you forgot your 6-digit Badge PIN, please contact your teacher to help you log into Clever. Your teacher can generate a backup code to help you log in to your Clever account using the instructions found here: How do I help my student log in to Clever? Please note that backup codes must be used within 15 minutes of when they are generated, and each backup code can only be used once!

 

 

 

 Once you are in your Clever Portal, you can reset your 6-digit PIN by clicking on your name in the top-right corner, and clicking Reset your Badge PIN . 

 

 

 

 Clever Tip: Teachers can help students log into Clever using a backup code or by resetting the student's password! Check out this Help Center article for more information: As a teacher, how do I help my student log in to Clever?

title: For Students: What is Clever and how to use it
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is Clever? 3. What is single sign-on (SSO)? 4. What is the Clever Portal? 5. Favoriting resources 6. Searching for resources 

 Overview

 In this article, you can learn more about what Clever is and how to use it in your class. If you are struggling to log into Clever, try reviewing this article . If you are experiencing any other type of issue, please reach out to your teacher. 

 
 What is Clever?

 Clever is an easy way for you to access all your learning applications. With Clever, you only have to remember one username and password, or use a Clever Badge , to log into all your applications. 

 
 

 What is single sign-on (SSO)?

 Single sign-on (SSO) is what Clever uses to log you into all your learning applications. You only have to sign in once (a single sign-on) to the Clever Portal, then you can click on your learning applications without signing in again!

 
 

 What is the Clever Portal?

 The Clever Portal is where you can access all your learning applications. Here is an example of a student's Clever Portal:

 

 Your Clever Portal might look different, depending on the applications your school has added to Clever. If you are missing an application you need in your Clever Portal, contact your teacher! 

 If your Portal has a lot of icons and resources, consider “favoriting” and searching for the icons and applications you use the most! 

 
 

 Favoriting applications

 Here's how to favorite an application: 

 • Log into your Portal at your district specific URL (ask your teacher if you need help with this step!) 
• Scroll to the appropriate application icon and click the heart in the upper right-hand corner of the icon 
• This will add the application to the 'Favorites' category at the top of your Portal 
 
 To remove an application from your ‘Favorites’, simply click the heart again in the top right corner! 

 Please note: Favoriting resources is only reflected in individual portals. Resources that teachers favorite will not be favorited for their students. 

 

 
 

 Searching for resources

 You can also search for district added resources and links and your teachers' pages. Simply navigate to the search bar at the top of your Clever Portal. When a result is clicked, you will be able to immediately be logged into the resource or redirected to the link or teacher page.

title: For Students: Troubleshooting - Apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. App is missing 3. Clicked on app, received an error 4. Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password 5. Problems within apps 

 

 Overview

 Here are some steps you can take if you are having trouble logging in to applications from your Clever Portal. If you still are experiencing issues, please ask your teacher for help. 

 App is missing

 If you are missing an application from your Clever Portal, please ask your teacher for help . Your district will need to add the application to your Clever Portal.

 

 
 Clicked on app, received an error

 If you click on an application in your Clever Portal and get an error on the application's page, please ask your teacher to contact your district administrator or the application's support team for help . Clever Tip: If you are seeing an error at a web URL that is not a Clever address, it's likely that the application can help you fix the issue. Please have your teacher reach out to the application's support team to investigate further! 

 Here are examples of common errors you might see for specific applications:

 

 

 
 Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password

 If a Clever page asks you to enter your username and password for an application, enter the correct login information for that application, and click Save Login . Clever will save this information and automatically log you in next time you click the icon from the Portal!

 

 If you click on an application and see the page below, this means we were not able to log you in with the username and password you provided. Please enter your updated username and password for the application, and click Update & Log in .

 

 If you are still having trouble, ask your teacher to make sure you have the right username and password. They can contact Clever Support if they still need help! 

 

 
 Problems within apps

 After you are logged into an app successfully through Clever, any issues within the application would need to be addressed by the application's support team. 

 Clever is not able to resolve issues within applications . Some example issues the Clever cannot help with are: 

 • The app freezing
• Not being able to access grades or textbooks
• Your student runs out of time during a test
• The app suddenly logs your student out
• Missing lessons or assignments
• Accessing the microphone or camera
 

 I f you are running into an issue within the application, please ask your teacher to contact the application's support team for help.

title: For School Tech Leads: Account recovery and password reset for Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Clever passwords
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Set up Account Recovery 3. Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page 4. Resetting your password in Clever Portal 5. Resetting a student or teacher's password 

 

 Overview 

 If your school district has enabled Active Directory (AD), Entra ID, Google, or Clever passwords as a login method for Clever, you may be able to reset your passwords from Clever. Please note that this setup will only be available to you if your district-level admin has enabled this. In your Clever Portal, if you click on your name and do not see the Account recovery or Set new password in your Clever Portal, you must contact your district to reset your Google, Active Directory, Entra ID (i.e., third-party), or Clever password. 

 

 

 If you do see the option for password reset, you can reset your Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Clever passwords from within the Clever Portal. To ensure you can reset your password outside of the Clever Portal, you will need to set up an account recovery method.

 
 

 Set Up Account recovery

 You will need to set up account recovery to have the ability to reset your password for a third-party when you are not signed in to your Clever account. To configure account recovery, from your Portal click your name, Account recovery. 

 

 Once clicked, a modal will pop up where you can select your preferred account recovery method. Depending on what your school district has enabled, you may have the option to enter an email address, a phone number, or answer security questions when you need to reset your password. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 It’s also possible that your school organization may have provided a recovery method for you using information in their records. For example, if your schools collect personal email addresses, they may set up your account recovery email as your personal email address. If you’d like to update the information your school organization provides, you can do so using the recovery method setup described above. 

 

 
 Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page 

 To reset Clever and third-party passwords 

 • 
 From the Clever login screen, click on get help logging in. 

• 
 Choose Reset your account passwords. 

• Input your school district email address (not to be confused with a recovery email you have set up). 
• You’ll be prompted to confirm your identity using a method you set up in your Portal settings. Select how you’ll confirm your identity. 
• You can now set up your new password for both your Clever and third-party accounts by inputting a new password. Your school may have password requirements, which can be viewed by clicking on See requirements . Once completed, you can use your new password for your third-party account(s). 
 
 

 To reset only your Clever password 

 • Navigate to your district's login page and select Get help logging in. 
• Click Reset only your Clever password. 
• Please enter your school email address. If you are unsure of the email address to utilize, please contact your district support contact. Clever Support cannot provide this information to you.\ 
• After you enter your email, Clever will send you a verification code through the method selected when setting up account recovery. Once you enter the verification code, you can set a new password: 
 
 The password must match the requirements set by your district and Clever. Here are some of the requirements from Clever:

 • Passwords cannot start/end with a space
• Characters cannot repeat more than 3 times
• Passwords cannot contain any part of the user’s name
 
 

 Once you've reset your password, you will be prompted to log in to the Clever Portal using your new password.

 

 
 

 Resetting your password in the Clever Portal

 To reset your password while in the Clever Portal:

 • Click on your name then Set new password . 
• Input your new password, confirm the new password, and click Set new password to save. 
 
 The password must match the requirements set by your district (example: minimum of 10 characters, including 1 number).

 
 Resetting a student or teacher's password (for school admin)

 If you're a Clever School Tech Lead trying to reset a student or teacher's password, please check out this article !

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Account recovery and password reset 

• 
 For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever 

• 
 For Students: Password recovery and resets 

• 
 For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students

title: For School Tech Leads: View usage data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 1. How to use filters 2. Description of available data 3. How to export data 

 Overview 

 At Clever, we understand how important it is to track logins and application usage among your students and teachers. Using Portal Analytics , you can see graphs and statistics that show how many users are accessing their resources, how often they log in, and which schools or apps are being used the most! This data is provided as a general overview and is not viewable "by student" or "by teacher". This article will give you a breakdown of the available data and how you can use filters to narrow down the information you see. 

 Please note: As a S chool Tech Lead, you will only see the specific data for your assigned school sites. As such, you will not see the "Logins by school" list unless you are connected to multiple schools in Clever. If you do not see one of your school sites, please contact your district help desk. 

 

 

 

 
 To get to the Portal Analytics page from your School Tech Lead Portal, you can navigate to the Clever Dashboard, by clicking Dashboard from the top menu 

 Once in the dashboard, select the blue View details button to the right of, Clever in your school 

 

 

 
 How to use filters

 First, let's take a look at the available filters. You can use the filters to narrow down the usage data and focus on specific users, applications, and school sites. Different filter sets will cause charts to change- if the filters chosen do not apply to a chart, the chart will temporarily be hidden.

 

 Time Range Here you can select the time ranges that you want the charts to show. Select School year to date if you'd like to see the usage data for the entire school year so far. 

 Units Here you can select whether you'd like to view your data as a percentage (75% of all students have logged in) or in absolute terms (7,500 students have logged in). 

 Schools All Schools - an overview of the data for the entire district If you want to focus on a specific site instead of All Schools , you can select it from the drop-down. Multiple school sites can be selected from the drop-down. Please note: the "Logins by School" graph is disabled when you select a single school. 

 App Select All Apps if you want to view login data for all the apps connected to your Clever account. To look more closely at a specific app's usage, select it from the drop-down. When you select an app, the "Usage by App" graph is disabled. 

 App Type Here you can select whether you want to see usage data for teacher-added, district-added, or both. 

 Grades To look at the analytics for specific grade levels, select the grade(s) from the drop-down. Grades are based on student grades, not the grade level for a section or a teacher. 

 Users Here you can select whether you want to see usage data for students, teachers, or both. 

 Please note: the "Teachers" filter cannot be used in conjunction with the grade filter 

 
 Description of available data 

 Now that we know how to use filters to focus on specific areas, let's take a look at the different measures! The statistics shown are based on the users who are being shared with apps. The metrics are not based on the total number of users in your Clever account. 

 

 Usage by scoped users: This number reflects the number of logins from the users that are shared with your applications. 

 Unique users: This number reflects the unique number of users who have logged into an application. 

 Total Logins: This number reflects the overall number of user logins.

 Daily Unique Users 

 

 Daily unique users: This chart reflects the unique number of users who have logged into an application per day. Hover your mouse over a date to see the number of daily unique users for that day. Please note that this chart is disabled if you are using the School year to date time frame filter. 

 

 Logins by app 

 This number reflects the number of unique user logins to an individual application. Click on Ascending or Descending to order apps by the number of unique logins.

 Logins by school 

 This number reflects the number of unique user logins from users at each school site you administer. Click on Ascending or Descending to order schools by the number of unique logins. 

 Please note: you will only see the number of logins by school for the sites you administer. 

 

 Login Method / Device / Browser 

 

 Login Method : This number reflects the number of unique user logins by login type. Users will be counted twice if they log in using different login methods- but only once if logging in multiple times using the same identity provider. 

 Device : This number reflects the number of unique user logins for each device. Users will be counted twice if logging into different devices but only once if logging in multiple times to the same device type. 

 Browser : This number reflects the number of unique user logins for each device. Users will be counted twice if logging in through different browsers- but only once if logging in multiple times through the same browser.

 

 
 How to export data

 Located at the top right of the Portal Analytics page, the Export all data button will download a CSV of the data you've currently filtered in your analytics view.

title: For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. New login page 3. Login Directions • Log in with Clever • Log in with Google • Log in with Active Directory 4. Backup Codes 5. Setting up a backup login method for Active Directory, Entra ID, Google, or Clever passwords 6. Logging out 7. How do I get help? 

 Overview

 This article is an overview of how students can get logged into Clever, and some basic steps for troubleshooting. If you are having trouble getting logged in, please reach out to your teacher directly. Clever’s support team can’t help if you don’t know your login credentials.

 • 
 To get into Clever, go to your school’s Clever login page. If you don’t know the page, visit clever.com/login and search for your school or district. If you can’t find it, ask your teacher for help! 

• 
 To log in, use the username and password your school gave you. It might be your student number, ID, or email. If you’re not sure what they are, ask your teacher for help. 

• 
 From your Clever Portal, click on the application icon you’d like to use!

 
 
 

 

 
 New login page

 Starting February 3, Clever is getting a new login page! If your login page looks a little different, don’t worry. Everything still works the same, so you can log in just like you always do. Check out the picture below to see what it could look like. Your screen might look a little different and that’s okay. You can still log in like usual. 

 

 

 
 Log in with Clever passwords

 

 If you are using the Log in with Clever button, you’ll need to use the username and password from your school. It might be your student number, ID, or email address.

 If you see the error Invalid username or password , this means that the username and/or password you have entered aren’t correct for your Clever account. Please ask your teacher for help. 

 

 
 Log in with Google

 

 If you are using the Log in with Google button, you’ll need to use your school Google email and password to log into your Clever account.

 If you see the error message " Wrong password. Try again' , this means that either your email or password is incorrect. Please ask your teacher for help. 

 If you see a message saying “We couldn't log you in, we can't find a Clever account for the email xxxx@example.com ,” it means the email you used isn’t the one linked to your Clever account. Make sure you’re using the email your school gave you. If you’re sure you’re using the email your school gave you, please reach out to your teacher directly for more help. Clever cannot provide your login credentials.

 If you see any other message saying We couldn’t log you in, please contact your teacher for help. Provide them with as much information as you can about the error such as any other description of the error, the copyable Reference ID, and Admin troubleshooting steps. Clever cannot provide your login credentials. 

 
 Logging in with Active Directory 

 

 If you click the Log in with Active Directory button, you’ll be taken to sign in with your school account through Active Directory Federation Services or Microsoft360. Use the username and password your school gave you. This is usually the same email and password you use to log on to school computers.

 

 If you see a message saying We couldn’t log you in, We can’t find a Clever account for the email or username it means the email or username you used isn’t linked to your Clever account. Make sure you’re using the email or username your school gave you. If you’re sure you’re using the email or username your school gave you, please reach out to your teacher directly for more help. Clever cannot provide your login credentials.

 If you see any other message saying We couldn’t log you in, please contact your teacher for help. Provide them with as much information as you can about the error such as any other description of the error, the copyable Reference ID, and Admin troubleshooting steps. Clever cannot provide your login credentials.

 If you click on Log in with Active Directory or Log in with Office365 and the login page fails to load, try clearing your cache . If accessing Clever from a Chromebook, we recommend signing out of the Chromebook and restarting the device .

 

 
 Using a Backup Code

 If you don’t know your Clever login info, your teacher can give you a Backup Code to help you log in. Just click the Get help link on the Clever login page, then choose Use backup code . Enter the code from your teacher and click Log in .

 

 

 

 
 Setting up a backup login method for Active Directory, Entra ID, Google, or Clever passwords

 If your school has set it up, you can use a backup method like email, security questions, or your phone number to get back into your Active Directory, Entra ID, Google Workspace, and Clever passwords accounts right from the Clever login screen! This helps if you’re having trouble with your password.

 

 Find our step-by-step student guide for account recovery and password resets here .

 

 
 Updating your password for Active Directory, Entra ID, Google, or Clever passwords

 If your school has set it up, you can easily change all of your account passwords at one time through Clever! Read more about resetting your passwords here .

 

 
 Logging out

 To log out of Clever, navigate to the Clever Portal, hover over your name in the top-right corner, and click Log out .

 
 

 Users are also logged out of Clever automatically after: 

 
 • 24 hours of inactivity 
• The browser is quit (e.g. "Quit Google Chrome " ). Closing the browser window will not log the user out of Clever. 
 
 

 
 How do I get help?

 Need help? 

 Your school manages your login info. If you’re not sure of your login details, see an error message, or something isn’t working, ask your teacher or the district’s help desk for help. You can find your district's tech support contact information by clicking the Get help link on the Clever login screen. From here, click District contact . You should see a box appear with email or phone number to reach your district's support contact. If you don’t see it, reach out to your teacher. 

 ​ 

 

 

 

 

 Want to learn more about Clever? Check out Clever Academy !

 

 

 

 Still can’t log in? Ask your teacher for help! They can help you using the directions found here .

title: For School Tech Leads: Sharing data with apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What is data sharing? 2. Which applications am I able to share data with? 3. Sharing data 

 

 What is data sharing?

 The final step in adding an application to your district's Clever Dashboard is setting your sharing permissions. Sharing rules let Clever know which of your data you'd like to make available to an application, and which users should be able to access an application via Clever. Clever will not share data with an application until your district sets the application’s sharing permissions. The application will only appear on the Portal for teachers, students or staff that have been shared with the application. 

 
 Which applications am I able to share data with?

 As a School Tech Lead you are only able to update sharing rules for applications that say, School site sharing , under the Sharing column on the Applications tab. This setting is configured by your district's Clever Admins. For applications with School site sharing enabled, you can adjust the sharing permissions for these applications to grant or remove access for specific users and classes. 

 
 Sharing data

 When school site sharing is enabled, School Tech Leads will be able to share data for their school by creating sharing rules. Examples of sharing rules include: 

 • All the math classes in a particular school;
• The math classes for grades 3-6 in the district, except for in two schools; or
• One specific teacher’s 11th-grade homeroom class.
 
 For information on creating sharing rules, please see this article .

 
 

 Related articles: 

 

 Sharing Permissions: Enabling School Site Sharing for School Tech Leads

title: PowerSchool: How do I unlock my PowerSchool account if I made too many sign in attempts with incorrect password?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to fix this error 

 

 Overview

 You may see a Sync Status error if the PowerSchool account you’ve provided for the Clever sync has an incorrect password - some districts’ PowerSchool settings will lock an account after a series of sign-in attempts with a wrong password. If this has happened, you'll see this error on your District Dashboard: 

 Account locked due to sign in attempts with incorrect password. 
Please contact your SIS administrator to remove this restriction. 
 

 
 How to fix this error

 • Log into PowerSchool from an administrator account (not the account you've shared with Clever).
• Navigate to the System Administrator view (click on the left menu link for ‘System’).
• Click on the link for ‘Security’.
• Click on the link for ‘Locked Accounts’.
• Verify that the account username shared with Clever is not listed here. If this account is listed, remove it.
 
 Some PowerSchool systems also place IP restrictions on locked accounts for extra security. After removing the lock on the account, we recommend checking and removing additional IP restrictions with the following steps:

 • From the System Administrator view, click back to ‘Security’.
• Click on the link for ‘Sign In Attempts Restrictions’.
• At the bottom of this page you’ll see a link for ‘View Disabled IP Addresses’. Click the link and verify that Clever's IP addresses are not listed:
 
 • 54.241.134.131 
• 50.18.217.135 
• 54.241.154.41 
 
 If you are unable to resolve this error following the recommendations above, please contact Clever Support for additional assistance.

title: Sync Error: How do I resolve "The password on the SIS account needs to be reset"?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Fixing the error 

 

 Overview

 If you notice an error message on your Clever sync that says, "The password on the SIS account needs to be reset," this article is for you. 

 This may occur if you've updated the password for the Student Information System account shared with Clever. Some SIS accounts require users to set a new password after an administrator resets their password, and some require passwords to be changed periodically.

 If your sync is experiencing this error, you'll receive an email and see this sync status on your Clever Dashboard:

 

 

 
 

 Fixing the error

 Step 1: Log in to your SIS as an administrator and reset the password for the Clever access account. You can see the username you’ve shared with Clever on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab.

 Step 2: In a new window, log in to your SIS with the Clever access account username and the new password.

 Step 3: Enter the temporary password and create a new password. 

 Step 4: Update the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab in your Clever Dashboard with the new password.

 Clever will try to sync at the next scheduled time, so you should see your sync status update within a few hours. You can also press Sync Now on your Sync Settings page to initiate a sync. You should see the next sync process within 30 minutes.

title: Sync Error: How do I resolve "Security preferences need to be set for this account"?
text:
Some SISs periodically request security preferences to be updated for active accounts. These preferences safeguard your account and allow for recovery if a password is forgotten.

 When Clever encounters this error, your sync will not complete successfully. You’ll notice the following Sync Status message on your Clever Dashboard: 

 

 How to fix this error: 

 • Log into your SIS using the username and password you've shared with Clever.
• Follow the instructions to update your security preferences.
• Save your changes.
• Log out of your SIS and log back in with the same credentials to confirm that the error is resolved.
 

 Once the security preferences are set and you can log into your SIS, you should see your sync resume shortly.

title: Sync Error: How do I resolve "SIS is not currently available at the URL provided"?
text:
Many of Clever’s automatic syncs rely on the URL for your SIS to sync data. When Clever is unable to access this URL your sync cannot complete successfully.

 

 Some common causes for URL errors include:

 • URL includes a typo
• URL does not direct to a valid SIS login page
• URL is not accessible from outside of your district’s network/VPN
• URL access is blocked by firewall restrictions
• District needs to grant access to Clever's IP addresses
 
 How to fix this error:

 Determine the correct URL to provide Clever:

 • Find the URL you’ve shared with Clever on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab.
• Make sure that the URL you entered matches the one you use to log in to your SIS.
 • Note for PowerSchool users: Clever drops the "/admin" extension on PowerSchool URLs, so it's normal to not see this extension.
 

• Confirm that you can use this URL to log in to your SIS from outside of your district’s network/VPN. 
• If you’re not able to access the URL you provided, determine the correct URL and update it in Clever (check for typos, adjust firewall, etc.).
 
 Update your URL in Clever:

 • Log in to your Clever Dashboard and navigate to the Sync Settings page to update your SIS URL in Clever.
• When you click “Update” Clever will attempt another sync with your SIS, so you should see your Sync Status update within a few hours.
 
 Grant access to Clever IP addresses:

 In the event that your district blocks IP addresses, you may need to grant access to these IP addresses or clear them from being blocked. Clever's machines run on three IP addresses – these are the ones that you will need to grant access:

 • 54.241.134.131
• 50.18.217.135
• 54.241.154.41 
 
 Your district may also need to make sure ports 443 and 80 are open.

title: Sync Error: How do I resolve "Invalid username/password specified?"
text:
Many of Clever’s Auto-Syncs rely on a read-only SIS account to sync data. When the username and password for this account are incorrect, your sync cannot complete successfully. 

 

 How to fix this error:

 • Check your credentials by logging into your SIS with the username and password you've provided to Clever. (You can find these credentials on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab. If you are unable to log in with the credentials it means they have expired, changed, or are no longer valid.
• Update the credentials in your SIS. We strongly recommend creating a separate read-only account in your SIS, just for the Clever sync. You can confirm the required permissions for this account in the Auto-Sync section of our Help Center.
• Once you've updated the credentials, confirm that they are working by using them to log into your SIS.
• With new credentials in hand, navigate back to your Sync Settings page and enter the new credentials required to connect to your SIS, including username and/or password.
• Once entered, click ‘Update.' Clever will attempt another sync with your SIS as scheduled, so you should see your Sync Status update within a few hours.

title: Sync Error: What are "Insufficient Permissions"?
text:
Many of Clever’s Auto Syncs rely on a read-only SIS account to sync data. When the permissions for this account aren’t set correctly, your sync cannot complete successfully. You’ll see an error similar to these: 

 

 

 

 How to fix this error:

 • Find the article about your SIS in the Auto Sync section of the Help Center , and download the attached setup guide (found in each article).
• Confirm that the SIS account you’ve created for the Clever sync has access to all of the resources listed in your Permissions Guide. You can find the credentials for this account on your Sync Settings page. 
 
 Once you’ve updated the permissions for this SIS account, you should see your sync status change with your next sync!

title: Is Clever available to all North Carolina LEAs and Charter Schools?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Getting started 

 

 Overview

 Yes! LEA and Charter School Administrators in North Carolina have the ability to easily integrate over 200 additional leading educational applications into their NCEdCloud IAM Service p ortal for students and teachers. 

 The Clever integration leverages industry leading security standards like SAML and OAuth in conjunction with Clever's powerful data sharing tools to give administrators in North Carolina the ability to easily deploy software to the classroom. 

 

 
 Getting started

 To get started: 

 • Tech Directors should visit the NCEdCloud IAM Service website under Target Applications ( https://ncedcloud.mcnc.org/target-applications )
• Click on “Target Application Request Form”
• Select Clever from the list of Target Applications.
• Sign up at https://www.clever.com/district-signup and our district and school onboarding team will be in touch shortly to assist with your setup!
 
 

 If you have any issues, please contact Clever Support .

title: Skyward Auto Sync
text:
This article covers the following: 1. Overview 2. Customization 3. School Year Rollover 4. Troubleshooting 5. Permissions 5. Default Field Mappings 

 Overview

 ✓ Syncs every six hours, by exporting via Skybuild ✓ Utilizes your Skybuild's default file export settings. For customization, see below. ✓ Supports co-teachers for sections, using the Teacher Alpha Key to determine the primary teacher ✓ For information on our Skyward API sync, please visit this article. 

 
 Customization

 • The Clever team can set your Skyward sync to run nightly instead of every 6 hours, but the time of day syncs occur cannot be customized. The sync can be initiated at any time from your Clever Dashboard on the Data sources > SIS Sync > Settings tab > Sync Controls box > Sync Now button .
• Syncs can also be customized to pull additional fields available through Skybuild. To request additional fields, please contact Clever Support. 
• Clever can filter exports from Skyward to limit the schools, teachers, or students included in the sync.
• Clever can help increase field length character limits so that longer field values aren't truncated in your sync.
• To request customization and filtering for your Skyward sync, contact Clever Support .
 
 

 Please note: If you’d like to sync additional data to Clever, you can do so via our automated custom data sync for auto sync districts and schools. Please reach out to Clever Support for access to your Clever SFTP Directory. You'll need to set up any automated exports to the remote site, as Clever doesn't provide support for scripting. 

 

 
 School Year Rollover

 Clever’s Skyward sync includes data for the current school year in Skyward. Once your Skyward system has transitioned to the new school year and there are active enrollments, Clever will sync the new school year data. 

 
 Troubleshooting

 

 Common sync errors and resolution steps 

 Below are some sync errors you may see in your Clever Dashboard. We recommend the following steps to resolve.

 

 
 Error

 
 Context and resolution

 
 Invalid username/password specified. Please check SIS credentials on settings page. The credentials you have provided Clever on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page don't match the credentials in Skyward. Please follow the setup instructions on your Sync Settings page to confirm your credentials. 
 The password on the SIS account needs to be reset. Please log in to Skyward to set a new password, then update SIS credentials on the settings page. Skyward requires admin passwords to be reset periodically. The password for the Clever user in Skyward needs to be reset and then updated on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page . 
 Skyward is not currently available at the URL provided. Please update the URL in your system settings page if necessary. 
 Either the URL provided on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page is inaccurate, or your Skyward instance is down. 

 • Confirm the URL on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources> SIS sync > Settings page matches your Skyward URL.
 
 
 
 Sync: Internal Errorpage.waitForEvent: Timeout 

 120000ms exceeded while waiting for event "popup"

 
 This error occurs when districts have a pop-up enabled on their Skyward login page. Please remove any pop-ups and the error should resolve.

 
 
 Could not execute download of file builder query. Delete the saved query to rebuild it from your sync settings.

 
 This error may occur if there are pop-ups or multi-factor authentication requirements for the Clever user. Please confirm that there are no pop-ups or multi-factor authentication requests during the login process to resolve this error.

 
 
 

 

 Missing Records 

 If an entire record is missing, we recommend checking the following: 

 
 
 Record Type

 
 Troubleshooting

 
 Student 
 • Must have a current Entry record in the Entry/Withdrawal section of Skyward. The entry and exit date cannot be the same day!
• If missing students are in pre-Kindergarten grade level, please reach out to Clever Support to adjust your sync configuration.
 
 
 Teacher 
 • Must be listed as Active in Skyward for the current school year
• Make sure the teacher is in the correct security group
 
 
 Staff 
 • Clever doesn't automatically sync records from Skyward to the Staff role in Clever. Note that you can still upload staff manually or via SFTP. 
 
 
 Sections 
 • Each section must have a teacher assigned to it
• Check the filters that are in place for the ClassC export
 
 
 
 If the record (student, teacher, section) is in Clever but data is missing or incorrect for the record (i.e. incorrect email, missing username), navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings page. 

 Here you can view which fields Clever is pulling for each record and field. In the below example: 

 • If a teacher's email address was incorrect, confirm the email is correct in the "Work Email Addr" field in Skyward 
• If a teacher is missing State_teacher_id, you will see that Clever is not currently pulling this data. Please contact Clever Support to see what options may be available to pull this data. 
 
 

 Email or Username Length 

 • The default field length for many fields is 15 or 20 characters. If you notice any characters being cut off, you can increase the field length by navigating to Export File Builder > StaffC (or whichever file) > Edit Selected Fields > Length (change it) > Save.
 
 
 

 
 Permissions

 In order to facilitate an automatic sync with Skyward, we recommend the following:

 • Create an individual SIS account only for Clever -- no one else should log in with the same credentials
• Limit Clever's SIS account to read-only access
 
 The following document outlines the specific account permissions required for Skyward: Setup Guide 

 
 Default Field Mappings

 Below are the default field mappings for Skyward auto syncs that are managed by Clever. Please note:

 • If a field is blank, the field is either not pulled by default, or not accessible to Clever.
• If you want to change a field mapping, please contact Clever Support to learn what options are available. 
• If a field is not coming through with the correct value, Clever Support may need to complete a value mapping.
 
 Schools 

 
 Clever 

 
 Skyward Field 

 
 Skyward Area 

 
 School_id Entity ID Entity 
 School_number Entity ID Entity 
 School_name Entity Name Entity 
 School_phone Phone Number Entity 
 School_city City Entity 
 School_state State Entity 
 School_address Street Address Entity 
 School_zip Zip Entity 
 
 

 Students 

 
 Clever 

 
 Skyward Field 

 
 Skyward Area 

 
 Student_id Student ID/Internal ID General 
 Student_number Student_number General 
 DOB Birthdate General 
 Student_email School Email Address General 
 Race Ethnicity Desc General 
 Gender Gender (M/F) General 
 Hispanic_Latino Hispanic/Latino Ethnicity General 
 First_name Student First Name General 
 Last_name Student Last Name General 
 Grade Student Grade General 
 State_id State Student Number NCLB 
 School_id Entity ID Entity 
 Graduation_year Student Graduation Year General 
 Home_language Language Description General 
 Weighted_gpa Cumulative GPA GPA 
 
 

 Student Contacts 

 
 Clever 

 
 Skyward Field 

 
 Skyward Area 

 
 Contact_name E1 Full Name Contact 1 
 Contact_email 
 Contact_phone Primary Phone Contact 1 
 Contact_relationship Relationship Short Description Contact 1 
 Contact_type always "other" Contact 1 
 Contact_sis_id Name ID Contact 1 
 
 

 Teachers 

 
 Clever 

 
 Skyward Field

 Teacher_id Staff Internal ID Staff 
 Teacher_number Employee ID Staff 
 School_id Entity ID Entity 
 Teacher_email Work Email Address Staff 
 First_name First Name Staff 
 Last_name Last Name Staff 
 Title Title Staff

 Teacher_id Staff Internal ID Staff 
 Section_number Se

title: SchoolInsight Auto Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. SchoolInsight Auto Sync Functionality 2. Customization 3. School Year Rollover 4. Troubleshooting 5. Permissions 6. Default field mappings 7. Setting up an automated SFTP sync with SchoolInsight's OneRoster export 

 

 IMPORTANT This article is for districts that are already syncing using the Clever-managed sync. If your district is new to Clever, please see this section for directions on how to configure an automated sync data using SchoolInsight's OneRoster export utility. 

 

 SchoolInsight Auto Sync Functionality 

 ✓ Syncs data from SchoolInsight every six hours, by exporting via the Query Builder in Reports ✓ Syncs all teacher records (including those marked as inactive), unless customization is requested X Cannot sync co-teachers at this time 

 
 Customization

 • SchoolInsight syncs can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every six hours.
• Syncs can also be customized to pull additional fields available through the Query Builder in Reports.
• Clever can filter exports from SchoolInsight to limit the schools, teachers, or students included in the sync.
• To request customizations for your SchoolInsight sync, request assistance from Clever Support . 
 
 

 
 School Year Rollover

 By default, Clever exports data for the current site year in SchoolInsight. To sync data for a different site year, request assistance from Clever Support .

 If you receive this error message when changing to a new school year, it means that your enrollments have not been set for the new term and our sync cannot access them:

 

 Once the new enrollments are finalized, your sync will start running again. 

 
 Troubleshooting

 If a record (student, teacher, section) is in Clever but data is missing or incorrect for the record (i.e. incorrect email, missing username), navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings. 

 Here you can view which fields Clever is pulling for each record and field. In the below example: 

 • If a teacher's email address was incorrect, confirm the email is correct in the "email_address" field in SchoolInsight 
• If a teacher is missing State_teacher_id, you will see that Clever is not currently pulling this data. Please contact Clever Support to see what options may be available to pull this data. 
 
 

 
 Permissions

 Clever requires specific permission levels in order to facilitate an automatic sync with SchoolInsight. In addition to these permissions, we also recommend the following: 

 • Create an individual SIS account only for Clever -- no one else should log in with the same credentials
• Limit Clever's SIS account to the access described in the attached document.
 
 
 Default field mappings

 Below are the default field mappings for SchoolInsight syncs that are managed by Clever. Please note:

 • If a field is blank, the field is either not pulled by default, or not accessible to Clever.
• If you want to change a field mapping, please contact Clever Support to learn what options are available. 
• If a field is not coming through with the correct value, Clever Support may need to complete a value mapping. This is common for grade, FRL_Status, ELL_Status, and IEP_Status.
 
 Schools

 
 Clever

 
 SchoolInsight

 
 School_id internal_school_id 
 School_name school_name 
 
 

 Students 

 
 Clever

 
 SchoolInsight

 
 Student_id internal_student_id 
 Student_number student_number 
 State_id state_id_number or mosis_student_id or state_province_id 
 First_name student_first_name 
 Last_name student_last_name 
 Middle_name student_middle_name 
 Student_street mailing_address_1 
 Student_city mailing_city 
 Student_state mailing_state 
 Student_zip mailing_zip 
 Gender gender 
 DOB birth_date 
 Grade grade_level 
 Hispanic_Latino ethnicity 
 Race race 
 School_id school name 
 Student_email student_email_address 
 
 

 Student Contacts

 
 Clever

 
 SchoolInsight

 
 Contact_name contact_first_name + " " + contact_last_name 
 Contact_email contact_email 
 Contact_phone contact_cell_phone or contact_work_phone or contact_home_phone or contact_other_phone 
 Contact_phone_type cell or work or home 
 Contact_relationship contact_relationship 
 Contact_type contact_custodial_status (default: guardian) 
 Contact_sis_id contact_id 
 
 

 Teachers 

 
 Clever

 
 SchoolInsight

 
 Teacher_id Created by Clever sync 
 Teacher_number Created by Clever sync 
 First_name first_name 
 Last_name last_name 
 Teacher_email email_address 
 School_id school name 
 
 

 Sections

 
 Clever

 
 SchoolInsight

 
 Name course_name - teacher_last_name - period 
 Course_number course_number 
 Section_id class_id 
 First_name instructor_first_name 
 Last_name instruction_last_name 
 School_id school name 
 Grade min_grade_level_code 
 Course_name course_title 
 Period class_section 
 
 

 Enrollments 

 
 Clever

 
 SchoolInsight

 
 School_id 
 Section_id class_id 
 Student_id student_internal_id 
 
 

 Setting up an automated SFTP sync with SchoolInsight's OneRoster export

 If you're setting up your SchoolInsight sync for the first time, you can use SchoolInsight/TeacherEase's OneRoster Export tool to sync data following the instructions below: 

 • Go to your Clever SFTP box using the credentials on your Sync Settings page and create a folder titled "oneroster11" in the folder with your username (ex: home/happy-bookcase-1111-oneroster11). If you have difficulty with this step, please reach out to our support team for assistance.
• From your SchoolInsight Home page, click on Import/Export . 
• Under Enterprise Data Access , click on Scheduled Exports. 
• Click on Create Scheduled Export .
• Select OneRoster Export in the drop down menu. 
• Fill in the required fields of School, Description, Time to Run, Academic Year, and Alert Email, then select the Custom SFTP Account button. Enter the credentials from your Sync Settings page into the fields (SFTP Server Address, SFTP Port, Username, Password). 
• In the directory field, enter oneroster11 with no other characters.
• You must create a name for the ZIP file. Ex: OneRosterYourDistrictName (i.e. OneRosterMeadowlarkSchools)
• Select all checkboxes in the screenshot below for the export
• Save your settings.

title: PowerSchool Auto Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Customizations 4. Syncing Student Emails 5. Syncing PowerSchool Student Contacts 6. School Year Rollover 7. Troubleshooting - Incorrect data for a record - Updating credentials 8. Sync Errors 9. Default Field Mappings 

 

 Please Note: • The legacy PowerSchool Auto Sync will be deprecated at the end of July 2026. If you currently use this sync, you must upgrade to the PowerSchool Plugin sync to avoid disruptions. Please reference our article PowerSchool Plugin Sync . • If your schools use ESchoolPLUS as your SIS, please refer to our article on SFTP Syncs . This article is intended for districts using PowerSchool as their SIS.

 
 Overview

 ✓ Runs every four hours, by exporting via Direct Database Export (DDE) ✓ Syncs all students with an active enrollment status ("enroll_status" = 0) ✓ Syncs teachers that are assigned to an active school and have an active staff status ("Status" = 1) X Student email addresses are not synced by default. Information on syncing student emails from PowerSchool can be found here . X Cannot sync the Central Office (school_id = 0) or any teachers assigned to this site X Cannot sync co-teachers. As an alternative, districts can manually add co-teachers to sections directly within Clever. More information can be found here on co-teachers. X Cannot sync staff to Clever. Staff can be manually added to Clever using these directions .

 
 

 

 
 Setup

 PowerSchool + Gsuite Integration The Clever-managed Auto Sync is not compatible with PowerSchool instances that use the Gsuite integration or any other secondary single sign-on (SSO) integrations. If your district utilizes Gsuite with PowerSchool or another SSO integration, please see our directions for setting up the PowerSchool Plugin Sync or an SFTP sync . 

 New to Clever? Please contact your district onboarding specialist for setup directions. If you have not yet connected with a district onboarding specialist, you can sign up here . If you have an existing Clever account and would like to transition to the Clever-managed auto sync for PowerSchool, please contact our support team using support.clever.com for details on changing your sync type. Please note, the process of changing sync types can take 2-3 weeks. We highly recommend reviewing the conversion process as outlined here .

 

 
 Customizations

 • Your sync can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every four hours, but the time of day that the syncs occur is not customizable. Syncs can always be initiated/forced at any time from your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab > Sync controls box > Sync Now button .
• Clever can filter exports from PowerSchool to limit the schools, teachers, or students included in the sync. Reach out to Clever Support if you would like to filter exports.
• To request customizations or filtering for your PowerSchool sync, please submit a request to Clever Support . 
 
 
 Syncing Student Emails

 For information on syncing student emails, please see this article . 

 
 Syncing PowerSchool Student Contacts

 Clever pulls student contact data from the Students table in the DDE in PowerSchool by default. For reference, here are the default contact fields:

 • Emerg Contact 1
• Emerg Contact 2
• Emerg Contact 3
• Emerg Phone 1
• Emerg Phone 2
• Emerg Phone 3
• Emerg 1 Ptype
• Emerg 2 Ptype
• Emerg 3 Ptype
• Emerg 1 Rel
• Emerg 2 Rel
• Emerg 3 Rel
 
 However, if you're using PowerSchool version 12+, the student contact table resides in the Data Export Manager (DEM) and allows for unlimited student contacts. In order for Clever to sync these contacts from the DEM, you will need to first grant Clever access to the DEM. You can find more information here: PowerSchool v12+: Student Contacts . Alternatively, you can upload student contacts directly to Clever. With this option, your district can maintain maximum control and flexibility over which contacts you'd like to populate in Clever. You can upload contacts directly on your district dashboard. They will populate in Clever within about 15 minutes and should be available within your connected applications the following day. You can view instructions on how to upload contacts here: Manually Uploading Student Contacts to Clever . 

 
 School Year Rollover

 On July 2nd, Clever will begin syncing data for the next school year. If you would like to begin syncing enrollments for the new School Year before August, please request assistance from Clever Support. If you notice the previous academic year's data syncing alongside the current academic year data, please contact Clever Support requesting that the filters for TermID and DateLeft be updated for your configuration. 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Missing Records 

 If an entire record is missing, we recommend checking the following in PowerSchool:

 
 Record Type

 
 Troubleshooting Steps

 
 Students Clever will only sync students that meet the following conditions: 
 • Record is active
• enroll_status = 0
 
 
 Teachers Clever will only sync teachers that meet the following conditions: 
 • Record is active ("Active" box is checked)
• Assigned to a school, not the district office (school_id = 0)
• status = 1
 
 
 Sections Clever will only sync sections that meet the following conditions:
 • Minimum of 1 active student enrolled
• No_of_students > 0
• TermID >= 3200 (please refer to TermID for current academic year)
 
 
 Staff Clever does not automatically sync records from PowerSchool to the Staff role in Clever. There may be users such as administrators, custodians, and para-professionals who might not use PowerSchool directly but need access to applications through Clever. If those users are missing from your Clever data, there are two options to sync them to Clever. Option A) Sync as teacher To sync the user as a teacher, confirm they meet the record requirements as outlined above. Option B) Manually add to Clever using Custom Data If the user requires access to teacher features or will need to be connected to a section, add the user as a Custom Teacher . If the user does not require teacher features and only will be using Clever for Single Sign-On (SSO), add the user as Custom Staff . 
 
 

 Incorrect data for a record 

 First, check if the data is correct in PowerSchool. You can view the default field mappings for PowerSchool syncs here . 

 If you have customized your sync, you can view your custom mappings by navigating to the Sync > Settings page in your Clever Dashboard. In the Sync Mappings box, click on View field mappings. In this example: 

 • If a teacher's email address was incorrect, confirm the email is correct in the "Email_addr" field in PowerSchool. 
• If a teacher is missing State_teacher_id, you will see that Clever is not currently pulling this data. Please contact Clever Support to see what options may be available to pull this data. 
 
 

 Updating credentials 

 If you need to update your PowerSchool credentials for any reason, you can do so on your Sync page and simply input the updated credentials and click Update . 

 

 
 Sync Errors

 Below you can find solutions to common sync errors that may display in your Clever Dashboard. 

 
 Error Message

 
 Solution

 
 
 “PowerSchool is not currently available at the URL provided. Please update the URL in your system settings page if necessary.”

 
 Some common causes for URL errors include: 

 • URL includes a typo 
• URL does not direct to a valid SIS login page 
• URL is not accessible from outside of your district’s network/VPN 
• URL access is blocked by firewall restrictions 
• The district needs to allowlist Clever's IP addresses 
 
 You can update the PowerSchool URL on the Sync > Settings page. 
 
 “Invalid username/password specified. Please check SIS credentials on the settings page.” 

 
 Your district's PowerSchool login URL has changed or the credentials have expired, changed, or are no longer valid. 

 You can update your distric

title: Infinite Campus API Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview ​ 2. Requirement: Portal Accounts 3. Setup 4. Troubleshooting ​ 5. School Year/Term Rollover 6. Default Field Mappings 

 Overview

 This article provides information on the setup, maintenance, and troubleshooting of the Infinite Campus API sync. 

 Please note: In July 2025, Infinite Campus recently updated portions of its URL format. If you are encountering a sync error, please start by confirming the URL on your sync settings page matches the Base URL in your Infinite Campus instance. For more information, see below. 

 If you utilize or plan to utilize this sync, please note:

 • Your system version
 • Clever pulls data from Infinite Campus via the OneRoster Provisioning API, which is free for districts. In order to sync your data through the OneRoster API you'll need to make sure your Infinite Campus is on version 1733 or higher. • Districts using OneRoster provisioning prior to Campus 1733 are free to continue using it • District who were not using OneRoster provisioning prior to Campus 1733 may contact Campus Support to have the functionality enabled 
 

• Which version of OneRoster you plan to utilize. 1edtech (formerly IMS Global) has released the final version of the OneRoster v1.2 format. See below for information on how to set up your connection with OneRoster 1.2 or how to upgrade your existing connection. 
 
 The Infinite Campus API Sync:

 • Runs every six hours, by querying the OneRoster API
• Syncs all students who are designated as "active today" (activeToday = 1)
• Syncs all active staff members (employment.active = 1)
• Supports co-teachers for sections (sectionInfo_teacherPersonID, sectionInfo_teacher2PersonID, etc)
• Students and teachers need Infinite Campus Portal accounts in order to sync to Clever (see below)
• Support custom field mappings (please contact Clever Support to set this up)
• The available fields can be found here 
 
 Please Note: Infinite Campus OneRoster API sync cannot sync ell_status, frl_status, and iep_status. They can be uploaded via Custom data. Visit this article for instructions . SFTP upload of custom data is available to all schools. Please reach out to Clever Support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. You'll need to set up any automatic exports to the remote site, as Clever doesn't provide support for automation scripting. 

 

 
 Requirement: User (Portal) Accounts

 In order for a person to be accessible in the Infinite Campus OneRoster API (and thus synced to Clever) the person needs a User (Portal) account in Infinite Campus.

 For documentation on creating user accounts, please review this documentation . To create user accounts in bulk, please review this documentation .

 To verify if a person has a user account, search for the user in Infinite Campus. If the person has a username, they have a user account. In the example below, "Amelkin, Federique" has a user account but "Freda, Irene" does not. As a result, "Freda, Irene" will not be accessible to Clever via the Infinite Campus OneRoster API. To add a user account in Infinite Campus, click Add User. 

 

 
 Setup

 1. Obtain API credentials 

 In order to configure the API sync you'll first generate API credentials in Infinite Campus: 

 • Navigate to Instruction > Settings > Digital Learning Application Configuration (or System Administration > Learning Interoperability in the Classic Navigation)
• Click Add Application , scroll to the bottom, and click Connect to Other Applications 
• Enter Clever as the application name 
• Click Generate New OneRoster Connection 
• For OneRoster version, select your OneRoster version > Generate 
• Share ClientID, Client Secret, Base URL and Token URL 
 
 

 Please Note: If you already have connected Infinite Campus to Clever and would like to move from OAuth 1.0 OneRoster v1.2 with OAuth 2.0, please go to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page and click the Upgrade button. Then, start at step 4 in the directions above. After moving to OneRoster 1.2 with OAuth 2.0, we recommend deleting or disabling your old connection that used OAuth 1.0. 

 IMPORTANT Regarding Staff: Before upgrading, be sure to check that any non-instructional staff you wish to include are associated with one of the following roles in Infinite Campus: counselor , districtadministrator , principal , siteadministrator , systemadministrator , or proctor . 

 

 

 

 2. Change sync type of Clever account 

 If you already have a Clever account and are switching to the API sync, please contact Clever Support . In your message, please specify which version of OneRoster (v1.1 or v1.2) you plan to use. Our team will need to switch your account sync type before you can move forward. If you are just getting started with Clever, the IC API sync will be the default when you choose your sync type for your account. You're free to move on to the next step!

 3. Enter API credentials in Clever 

 Please note: As of July 2025, Infinite Campus has recently updated portions of its URL format for the API sync. In order to continue to successfully sync to Clever using the Infinite Campus API sync, you may need to ensure your BaseURL in Infinite Campus matches the URL on your sync settings page. Please confirm the <portaldomain> and <appName> portions of the URL match in Infinite Campus and on your sync settings page . You can see examples of the URL format under Base URL below. 

 Once your account's sync type is the IC API sync, please take the credentials for the API and enter them into the Sync Settings page in your Clever Dashboard:

 • Base URL : Enter the base URL copied from Infinite Campus 
 • For OneRoster v1.2, the URL should look like this: /campus/api/oneroster/v1p2/ /ims/oneroster">https://<portaldomain>/campus/api/oneroster/v1p2/<appName>/ims/oneroster 
• For your OneRoster v1.1, the URL should look like this: /api/oneroster/v1p1/ /ims/oneroster/v1p1">https://<portaldomain>/api/oneroster/v1p1/<appName>/ims/oneroster/v1p1 
 

• Client ID: Enter the Client ID copied from Infinite Campus
• Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret copied from Infinite Campus
• Confirm Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret copied from Infinite Campus 
 
 4. Sync 

 Once you input and save your sync credential, the sync will automatically begin running and complete within approximately 30 minutes. You can also click the Sync Now on the Sync Settings page to start a sync at any time. 

 Please note: Infinite Campus syncs can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every six hours, but the time of day that the syncs occur is not customizable. Syncs can always be initiated at any time from your Clever Dashboard on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab > Sync Controls box > Sync Now button. 

 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Common sync errors and troubleshooting steps 

 Below are some sync errors you may see in your Clever Dashboard. We recommend the following steps to resolve:

 
 Error

 
 Context and resolution

 
 There are currently no active academic sessions in your Infinite Campus instance. Your sync will resume once your academic sessions become active again. 
 Your current school year has ended, but the new school year has not yet begun.

 Clever begins pulling enrollments 21 days before the enrollments become active.
