If the start of school is more than 21 days away but you'd like to see their new school year enrollments syncing into Clever, please reach out to Clever Support and let us know the first day of school, as well as when you'd like your enrollments to begin syncing.

 
 Invalid Key/Secret specified. Please check SIS credentials on settings page. The credentials you have provided Clever on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page don't match the credentials in Infinite Campus. Please follow these setup instructions and ensure the URL, client ID, and client secret match in Infinite Campus and Clever. 
 InfiniteCampus API is not currently

title: Aeries Auto Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting Up your Aeries Auto Sync 3. Aeries Auto Sync Requirements ​ 4. Customization ​ 5. School Year Rollover ​ 6. Troubleshooting 7. Default field mappings 

 

 Overview

 This article contains information on the setup, maintenance, and troubleshooting of the Aeries API sync. The Aeries API sync:

 

 ✓ Runs every four hours, by querying the Aeries API

 ✓ Pulls all active students, teachers, and schools (“InactiveStatusCode” = blank)

 ✓ Syncs co-teachers by pulling the TeacherNumber1, TeacherNumber2, and TeacherNumber3 fields from the Sections table

 ✓ Supports Flex Scheduling (For schools using Flex Scheduling in Aeries, only a Staff record will be required)

 
 

 Setting up your Aeries Auto Sync 

 Introduction

 Clever allows the student data in Aeries to automatically sync with authorized applications. This eliminates the need for manual CSV transfers or complicated SFTP uploads.

 To sync data, Clever needs access to the Aeries API. The best practice when using Clever is to create a new Aeries API certificate for the Clever sync.

 Create an API Certificate for Clever

 1. Log into your Aeries administrator account for the current school year. 2. Select Security > API Security from the menu on the left. 3. Add a new certificate named Clever . 4. Grant read permissions to the following items in the Student Data and Scheduling tables:

 • Student Data
• Special Programs
• Contacts, Class Schedules/History
• Enrollment History
• Test Scores
• Teacher Data
• Staff Data
• Master Schedule
• Course Data
• Classes
• Schools
• Terms.
 
 5. Save the certificate. 6. Click Display Certificate Details? then copy/paste your API certificate into the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab of your Dashboard. 

 

 
 Aeries Auto Sync Requirements 

 For the Aeries Auto Sync to work properly, your setup and user data must meet these requirements: 

 • Your SIS must be connected to the internet.
• Teachers at your district must have a Staff (STF) record in Aeries and be tied to a section to show up as a teacher in Clever.
• For teachers, InactiveStatusCode must be blank ( TCH.TG / STF.TG = blank). Any value in this field will prevent the record from syncing. 
 
 
 Customization 

 • Aeries syncs can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every four hours, but the time of day that the syncs occur isn't customizable. Syncs can always be initiated at any time from your Clever Dashboard on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab > Sync Controls box > Sync Now button. 
• Syncs can also be customized to pull additional fields available through the Schools, Students, Courses, Sections, Staff, or Teachers tables. 
• Clever can filter exports from Aeries to limit the schools included in the sync. 
• Some districts create schools without master schedules - or schedule students without placing them in sections (typically elementary schools). Because Clever requires sections to process data, our system can automatically create sections for these students based on their teacher. Please submit a request to Clever Support with a list of your schools (name and school number) and we will program our system to create sections in these schools. 
• To request customizations or filtering for your Aeries sync, request assistance from Clever Support . 
 
 Please note: If you’d like to sync additional data to Clever, you can do so via our automated custom data sync for auto sync districts and schools. Please reach out to Clever Support for access to your Clever SFTP Directory. You'll need to set up any automatic exports to the remote site, as Clever doesn't provide support for automation scripting. 

 

 
 School Year Rollover 

 Clever will begin syncing new school year data when summer school ends and the Sections (SEC) and MasterSchedule (MST) tables are active and available. To activate these tables, schedules must be copied over (Scheduling Process > Functions > Copy Scheduling Results to SEC & MST).

 Additionally, Clever filters out inactive enrollments. We use the enrollment's “DateEnded” field from the Aeries API to determine if an enrollment is expired, or inactive. An enrollment is considered inactive if the current date is one day past the "DateEnded" date. Of note, a section with no active enrollments on the Aeries API will appear on the Data sources > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page as an error, and won't successfully sync to your Clever instance.

 
 Troubleshooting 

 

 Common sync errors and resolution steps 

 Below are some sync errors you may see in your Clever Dashboard. We recommend the following steps to resolve.

 

 
 Error

 
 Resolution

 
 There are no active enrollments in Aeries. This may be because your active calendar year has ended, in which case your sync will resume when the Sections (SEC) and MasterSchedule (MST) tables are active and available for the new school year. 
 • 
 Please confirm that rollover has been completed and published for the new school year. Ensure the MST tables (for each school) and CAR records (for all affected students) are available.

• 
 Once confirmed, trigger a re-sync with Clever on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab using the Sync Now button.

• 
 If you have confirmed enrollments are active for the new year and the issue persists, please contact Aeries support.

• Must have InactiveStatusCode set to null ( STU.TG = blank). Any value in this field will prevent the record from syncing. 
 
 
 
 

 

 Missing Records 

 If an entire record is missing, we recommend checking the following: 

 
 
 Record Type

 
 Troubleshooting

 
 Student 
 • Must have InactiveStatusCode set to null ( STU.TG = blank). Any value in this field will prevent the record from syncing. 
 
 
 Teacher Teachers at your district must have a Staff (STF) record in Aeries and be tied to a section to show up as a teacher in Clever. 
 • 
 InactiveStatusCode must be blank (any value in this field will prevent record from syncing)

• 
 Must have all required fields on the teachers/staff table

• 
 Make sure the records don't have a "Leave Date"

 
 Please note, any non-teaching staff that are assigned to sections in Aeries will automatically be associated with the Default District Office entity in Clever. They will still be connected to all associated sections, regardless of their primary school designation in Clever.

 
 Staff 
 • Clever doesn't automatically sync records from Aeries to the Staff role in Clever. 
 
 There may be users such as administrators, secretaries, and para-professionals who might not use Aeries directly but need access to applications through Clever. If those users are missing from your Clever data, there are two options to sync them to Clever.

 Option A) Sync as teacher 

 To sync the user as a teacher, assign them to a section.

 Option B) Manually add to Clever using Custom Data 

 If the user requires access to teacher features or will need to be connected to a section, add the user as a Custom Teacher . 

 If the user doesn't require teacher features and only will be using Clever for Single Sign-On (SSO), add the user as Custom Staff . 

 
 Sections 
 • In order for a section to sync the school must have a master schedule. If a school doesn't use master schedules, Clever can auto-generate sections based on teacher/student assignments. Contact Clever Support if you would like this feature enabled. 
• A section with no active enrollments won't sync to Clever. We use the enrollment's “DateEnded” field from the Aeries API to determine if an enrollment is expired, or inactive. An enrollment is considered inactive if the current date is one day past the "DateEnded" date.
 
 
 
 

 Incorrect Records 

 If the record (student, teacher, section) is in Clever but data is missing or incorrect for the record (i.e. incorrect email, missing username), navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab in your Clever Dashboard. Under Sync Controls , click on View

title: Clever-managed Sync: How do I push a manual sync with my SIS?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I push a manual sync with Clever? 

 

 Overview

 

 If you have missing or incorrect data in Clever, you may want to have a data sync from your student information system (SIS) to Clever occur sooner than the scheduled time. Pushing a sync can help resolve data errors faster than waiting for the next sync. Learn more about manually starting a sync based on your sync type below. 

 

 
 How do I push a manual sync with Clever?

 If your district utilizes a Clever-Managed Sync , you can manually initiate a sync with your SIS from your Clever Dashboard ! If your district has a SIS-Managed Sync , you'll need to contact your SIS rep for information on initiating a manual sync to Clever. If your district manages an SFTP sync , you can trigger a sync whenever you like by uploading a new set of files to your SFTP directory. Uploading too many times in a short period may trigger a sync upload error. For a Clever-Managed Sync, navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab , click the Sync Now option under Sync Controls, and your SIS will start syncing with Clever. Syncs typically take around 10-15 minutes to process but could take up to 60 minutes depending on Clever's district sync traffic, the number of updates to be processed, and the time of year (e.g. Back to School season is often busier).

 

 After about 15 minutes, check your Data sources > SIS sync page to see if the sync has been completed. The sync notification will say "Successful" once the sync has been completed. If it has completed, you'll see up-to-date information about your most recent sync as well as any data warnings that might need to be addressed! 

 For sync errors, please visit the following articles for guidance: Clever Sync Errors .

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Active Directory - Claims Rules
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I use claims rules? 3. Defining the Field in Clever 4. Applying the claims rules to single sign-on (SSO) 

 

 Overview

 When users sign in to their Identity Provider to use single sign-on (SSO), the identity provider sends us a piece of data and tells us which field in Clever contains matching data. Using this, we can identify the user and give them access to their applications. 

 Claims rules define which attributes are sent to Clever from the identity provider and which fields Clever should use to perform the match. You will need to find a claims rule for each user type (e.g. students, teachers, staff, etc.) that you want to use single sign-on (SSO). 

 Please note: Claims rules are only necessary for Active Directory, Lenovo Stoneware / webNetwork, LDAPS, and Office 365 / Entra ID. 

 How do I use claims rules?

 Picking the right identity provider attribute 

 First, look at the data in your Clever account - you can click on a record in the Clever Dashboard > Data browser to view all the information that’s currently synced in Clever. 

 Next, compare the data in Clever to the data attributed to users in your identity provider (IdP). Are there matches? In order for users to be able to log in, the data in the IdP attribute and the data in the field in Clever must match exactly. 

 Additionally, make sure you’re picking something that’s unique across all users. If a claims rule matches multiple users, Clever won't know which user to match it against and the login attempt will fail. 

 If you can find a match, great! That’s the attribute you should use for that user type. If not, you’ll want to sync additional data from your SIS in order to be able to create a match. 

 Defining the Field in Clever 

 

 Once you’ve found a match, you’ll want to take note of the field in Clever it matches to. What’s the name of the field in the left column of the user's Detail view? Make a note of it! 

 

 Please note: Extension fields can't be used as an option at this time. 

 

 The field in Clever is expressed in the claims rule “clever. <user type or any> . <field name> ” Here are a few commonly used fields: 

 
 Students 

 
 • clever. student . email 
• clever. student . s is_i d 
• clever. student . student_number 
• clever. student . credentials . district_username 
 
 
 
 Teachers 

 
 • clever. teacher . email 
• clever. teacher . sis_id 
• clever. teacher . teacher_number 
• clever. teacher . c r edentials . district_username 
 
 
 
 Staff (Previously: School Admins) 

 
 • clever. staff . email 
• clever. staff . staff_id 
• clever. staff . credentials . district_username 
• clever. school_admin . email (outdated) 
• clever. school_admin . staff_id (outdated) 
• clever. school_admin . credentials . district_username (outdated) 
 
 
 
 District Clever Admins 

 
 • clever. district_admin . email 
 
 
 
 

 You’ll notice that users share multiple fields - to match on the same field for multiple user types, you can use ‘any’ in place of the user type to try matching against all user types. 

 • clever. any . email 
• clever. any . sis_id 
• clever. any . credentials . district_username 
 
 Applying the claims rules to single sign-on (SSO) 

 Click the link below to jump to Instant Login setup for your identity provider! 

 • Active Directory 
• Lenovo Stoneware / webNetwork 
• LDAPS 
• Office 365 / Entra ID

title: Managed Auto Sync Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever-Managed Auto Syncs 3. SIS-Managed Auto Syncs 

 

 Overview

 Clever offers two types of "Managed Auto Syncs" that school organizations can utilize to securely sync (i.e., transfer) rostering data from their student information system (SIS), management information system (MIS), or student management system (SMS) to Clever. Clever Admins can then selectively share specific data with their school organization's connected learning applications. This article will detail the types of Managed Auto Syncs that Clever offers.

 Please note: If your SIS, MIS, or SMS is not listed below, or you would prefer to manually upload data, please visit this article for information on how to get started: Which sync type should I choose? 

 

 
 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs

 How does it work? 

 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs are automated daily syncs primarily managed by Clever. These syncs securely transfer rostering data from your SIS, MIS, or SMS to Clever. With these types of syncs:

 • Clever integrates with your data system to regularly export data and securely transfer it to Clever. 
• Once data is synced to Clever, Clever Admins can selectively share that data with connected learning applications.
• The Clever Support team manages these types of syncs, so minimal help, if any, is needed from your SIS, MIS, or SMS provider.
• Clever does not charge for Clever-Managed Auto Syncs!
 
 

 List of Clever-managed Auto Sync SISes: 

 • Aeries 
• Arbor 
• Blackbaud 
• Bromcom 
• ICON
• Infinite Campus API 
• PowerSchool 
• PowerSchool Plugin 
• Skyward 
• Skyward API 
 
 

 

 Can I customize what rostering data is synced to Clever from our SIS, MIS, or SMS? 

 In many cases, when setting up your Clever-Managed Auto Sync, the Clever Support team can assist with adding various filters or mappings to ensure the data in Clever reflects your school organization's needs. We can often remove unwanted fields or include additional fields in the sync. However, there are some limitations regarding how much data we can filter or how many fields we can map. There are also ways for Clever Admins to determine which fields (e.g., student number, teacher email, staff ID) Clever should map to from your SIS, MIS, or SMS so that the data populates correctly in Clever. This can be done from the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings page. Please note that there are cases where Clever Support will need to map specific fields on behalf of your schools. These fields will not show the option to Edit under the Actions column. Please contact Clever Support for more information or if a field is unavailable for mapping. 

 What are the next steps? 

 If your school organization is interested in setting up a Clever-Managed Auto Sync, the first step would be to identify if your data management system (SIS, MIS, or SMS) allows for this. Please see the Systems that support Managed Auto Syncs section of this article for more information. If your SIS, MIS, or SMS is not listed, an alternative option would be to set up a secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) sync type to securely transmit data to Clever or to manually upload data to Clever via the Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page. Please visit this article for more information: Which sync type should I choose? 

 
 SIS-, MIS-, SMS-Managed Auto Syncs

 How does it work? 

 For SIS-, MIS, and SMS-Managed Auto Syncs, Clever partners directly with the school organization's data management provider to offer an automated sync option that the school organization's SIS, MIS, or SMS provider would primarily manage. 

 • Clever does not require credentials or access to your system directly. 
• To take advantage of these SIS-, MIS-, and SMS-managed syncs, please select your system from the Clever sign-up page when requesting a new Clever account.
• Clever will then provide instructions directing you on how to configure your sync to Clever.
• Clever doesn't charge school organizations to use this sync type or any other aspects of our core platform. We don't have insight into costs (if any) charged by a SIS, MIS, or SMS provider to configure an automated SFTP export to a vendor.
 
 List of SIS-managed Auto Sync SISes: 

 • Arbor
• Ascender
• Illuminate
• SchoolInsight
• ADAM
• Aspen
• Beehively
• Chalkable iNOW (formerly STI)
• Classe365
• Educate
• Edupoint Synergy
• eSchoolData
• FACTS SIS
• Focus SIS
• Genius SIS
• go.edustar
• Harmony
• iPASS
• JMC
• JCampus
• Jupiter iO
• Lumen AXSIS
• Mersys Campus
• MiDataHub
• MiStar
• MMS
• OASIS
• OnCourse Systems for Education
• ProgressBook (formally DASL for Ohio)
• Q
• QuickSchools
• RDS
• Realtime Inc.
• Rediker
• FACTS SIS - RenWeb
• SalamanderSoft
• SAM Spectra
• SchoolBrains
• SchoolSpeak
• School Pathways
• School Time
• Sycamore
• TEAMS
• TxEIS
• Tyler Schoolmaster
• Tyler v9
• Tyler v10
 
 Can I customize what roster data is synced? 

 With SIS-, MIS, and SMS-managed syncs, Clever has little insight into how data is filtered and exported from your data management system, so while we can provide guidance, we cannot implement changes or, in most cases, troubleshoot beyond reviewing the files we receive. To resolve any issues, please contact your SIS, MIS, or SMS representative (or whoever configured the Clever SFTP export) directly for support. 

 What are the next steps? 

 After creating your Clever account, please visit your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page for instructions on how to activate your SIS-, MIS-, SMS-Managed Auto Sync.

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • What is Clever? 
• Sign up for Clever here! 
• Which sync type should I choose? 
• Single Sign-On (SSO): What is SSO?

title: For Clever Admins: Staff Setup - Syncing staff from Active Directory (AD)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. Active Directory staff sync preparations 4. Setting up the sync to Clever 5. FAQs 

 Overview

 This article is an overview of how Clever admins can set up an automated sync with the district’s Active Directory server to sync non-instructional staff and school admins to Clever as staff users. 

 This sync between Active Directory and Clever will supplement any existing syncs between the district’s SIS and Clever and will only be used to sync non-instructional staff users (i.e. non-teaching staff). While the Active Directory sync will supplement data from the existing sync, once enabled, it will be the only method for syncing staff to Clever, aside from Custom Data. 

 
 Requirements

 • Your district utilizes an Active Directory server to manage non-instructional staff and school admins. 
• The Active Directory Sync toggle has been enabled from your Data sources > SIS sync > Staff tab. 
 
 Once the Active Directory Sync toggle is enabled, non-instructional staff and school admins cannot be created through a manual CSV upload. Clever will thereafter ignore any staff.csv files that may continue to be uploaded by the school organization. However, the custom staff feature will continue to work for individual account creation. Staff already uploaded to Clever via CSV will be preserved, and migrated to custom staff. 

 

 
 Active Directory staff sync preparations

 Staff sharing rules in Clever 

 If you already have non-instructional staff in Clever, make sure your staff and school admin sharing permissions are in a good place for all of your district’s connected applications, so we’re not unintentionally sharing new users en masse. 

 For additional information, please visit: Staff Setup: Sharing staff with applications 

 Existing staff in Clever 

 If your district has already synced staff to Clever via a staff.csv or admins.csv file, please make sure their 'Staff_id' stays the same if you'd like to preserve their historical data in Clever. This field is the key identifier for staff members in Clever so, if an existing user is synced from Active Directory with a different 'Staff_id', a new account will be created for them in Clever. 

 Create a new bind account 

 Create a new bind account with read-only permissions that Clever can use to query your LDAP server. Once created, take note of the account's userDN (i.e. users distinguishedName) and password found in the Attribute Editor - you’ll need that for setup! 

 Review your OU structure in Active Directory 

 Identify whether classroom teachers are grouped in Organizational Units (OUs) together with non-instructional staff members. 

 • If not, that’s great! Take note of the OU names that include only non-instructional staff. 
• If so, there’s a risk of creating additional user accounts in Clever for those who are already in Clever as teachers! This means that users would have both a teacher and a staff account in Clever. To counter this: 
 • We suggest bulk-adding an extension attribute to staff member’s profiles that would distinguish these two staff types from one another in your server (e.g. extensionAttribute = staff if non-instructional and teacher if instructional). 
• Once the extension attribute is added, the attribute can be used in an LDAP filter during step 2 of the setup below - The staff query. 
 

 
 Create a one-time school mapping CSV file 

 We need a one-time upload to map existing schools in Clever to school names in Active Directory. The file should have two columns: 

 • School_sis_id (as it appears in Clever) 
• Active_directory_name ( should correspond to the field your query will be pulling for school) 
 
 

 You can locate the school_sis_id or school_id in Clever on the school's profile page:

 

 
 Setting up the sync to Clever

 • Navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data tab
• Toggle on Active Directory Sync to reveal a new setup tab 
• Select set up active directory sync to configure the Active Directory sync in 4 steps 
 
 

 • Step 1: Configure the connection between Clever and your district’s Active Directory server by entering your district’s LDAP URL and port, certificate fingerprints, and the users DN and password for the read-only bind account set up in preparation. All of these steps are required to establish the connection. 
• Step 2: Add staff queries to designate the OUs from which Clever should sync staff user accounts. Districts can set up multiple queries if needed, as well as set up filters to scope down the information pulled. For help writing your LDAP filter, see this article . An example is shown below: During this step, you’ll also indicate which Active Directory user attributes should map to the available Clever staff fields. 
• Step 3: Optionally configure a sync for staff extension/custom fields such as security group, building code, etc. The Clever extension field will always begin with ‘ext.’ 
• Step 4: Upload your prepared school mapping CSV so we will be able to link the existing school sis_ids in your district’s Clever account with the appropriate school names in Active Directory. 
 
 Please note: If you'd like to add staff with multiple school associations in Clever, you can select a field in Active Directory that contains multiple values separated by commas. As long as these values are contained in the school mapping CSV from step 4, our system will parse these out and assign the staff user accordingly to the multiple schools within Clever. 

 After the sync is configured and a first sync runs, you can view the results on the Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data > Active Directory sync page . Syncs will run automatically every 24 hours but can be triggered from the Active Directory tab as needed. Like your normal SIS Sync report, you can track staff account creations, updates, deletions, and errors from this tab. 

 By default, if a user account from Active Directory does not have a school association, Clever will assign the staff member to the Clever-generated 'Default District Office' school site. To prevent this, please populate a school in the user attribute chosen for the staff school field mapping in step 2 above. 

 

 
 

 FAQs

 If syncing staff from Active Directory is enabled, what will happen to existing staff accounts? 

 • If enabled, existing staff accounts that were uploaded via a staff.csv will be preserved and will be transitioned to Custom Staff . These records will display under Data sources > Custom data > Staff tab . All custom staff that were created prior to Active Directory being enabled will also be preserved. 
 
 If syncing staff from Active Directory is disabled, what will happen to existing staff accounts? 

 • If disabled, any staff added to Clever via Active Directory will be removed from Clever upon the next sync with your district's student information system (SIS). The only staff that will remain in Clever are those that were created as Custom Staff: Data sources > Custom data > Staff tab. 
 
 If syncing staff from Active Directory, can I manually add custom staff? 

 • Yes. If Active Directory sync is enabled, staff can still be manually created by navigating to Support tools > Custom data > Staff 
 
 
 How often does the Active Directory staff sync run? 

 • Once daily, but on your Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data tab , you'll have the option to manually sync data at any time if you need a user to be updated immediately. 
 
 What happens during a sync pause if I am syncing staff from Active Directory? 

 • Your staff will continue to sync from Active Directory even during a pause.
 
 What should I do if a staff user is not syncing via the Active Directory sync and is missing in Clever? 

 • Please be sure to review your OUs to ensure that the staff user is in an OU that is set to sync to Clever. If you've confirmed the user is in a selected OU, please contact support. 
 
 Can I sync staff via Entra ID instead of Active D

title: PowerSchool v12+: Student Contacts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Enabling Access to Data Export Manager (DEM) 3. Enabling Page-Level Permissions to Data Export Manager (DEM) 4. Default Student Contact Fields for v12+ 

 

 Overview

 PowerSchool version 12+ now supports unlimited student contacts. These contacts no longer live on the Students table in Direct Data Export. As a result, Clever requires additional permissions to sync these contacts. 

 Please note: Enabling the sync with PowerSchool v12 contacts will result in previously added student contacts being deleted from your Clever account. Additionally, if the district's data sync is on pause, new student contacts will not be reflected in your District Dashboard until a successful sync occurs after the pause ends. 

 

 
 Process

 • Enable access to Data Export Manager (DEM) for the Clever PowerSchool account.
• Enable page-level permissions to Data Export Manager (DEM).
• Contact Clever Support to enable the new contacts export for your account. 
 
 

 
 Enabling Access to Data Export Manager (DEM)

 • From the main menu, click System on the left sidebar 
• Under Security, click Users by Group 
• Select the Clever access account from the list of users 
• Go to the Admin Access and Roles tab 
• Click the edit button (pencil icon) for the role for District Office (Home School) 
• Enable Data Export Manager Access for this role by choosing Data Export Manager/Export/Edit Access (No Group Defined) 
• Click Submit 
 
 

 
 Enabling Page-Level Permissions to Data Export Manager (DEM)

 1. Navigate to System > Security > Access to Page Permissions (/admin/security/changepageaccesspriv.html). Set Turn modify permissions = On and click Submit 

 

 

 2. Navigate to the Data Export Manager (DEM) page under Functions > Importing and Exporting > Data Export Manager (admin/datamgmt/exportmanager.action) . Click Modify access privileges 3. Find the security group assigned to Clever and set Level of Access to Full . Click Submit 

 4. Navigate to System > Security > Access to Page Permissions (/admin/security/changepageaccesspriv.html). Set Turn modify permissions = Off and click Submit Final Step: Once you have enabled the above permissions, please contact Clever Support to enable the new contacts export for your account. We will enable this feature and reply via email when this process is complete. 

 
 Default Student Contact Fields for v12+ 

Once the process is complete, the student contacts will be pulled from the default fields listed below:

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Active Directory - Password Recovery
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. District Setup 

 

 IMPORTANT: This Password Recovery feature is no longer available for new configurations. We have released an improved password recovery feature, and you can find details here: Clever Admin: Account recovery and password reset . 

 Overview

 Districts can allow users to easily reset their Active Directory passwords through Clever. Districts that are using SAML as their Single sign-on (SSO) method provide Clever with secure access to their Active Directory server via an LDAP Bind Account, allowing district users to configure their own password reset method (either through email or text message) in Clever. 

 District Setup

 To use the Clever Active Directory password reset feature, your district will first need to set up Active Directory as your login method from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page.

 For instructions on how to set up Active Directory as your district’s login method, visit: Single sign-on (SSO): Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS) .

 Once this is established, select the Manage Account Recovery button. This will launch the District AD Account Recovery set-up wizard. 

 

 You will first need to enter the URL for your district’s LDAP server and the SHA-1 fingerprint.

 

 Please note: If you don't know what your SHA-1 fingerprint is, you can select Download from server , and Clever will find it for you. 

 Next, configure the LDAP server:

 • User attribute to match to the specified Clever field
 • For the Search Attribute, provide the Active directory field you think would best serve as a field to search on in Active Directory and the Clever field it will match with. This field should be unique across all user types (e.g. students, teachers, school admins, etc.). 
 

• The base DN
• The Bind Account username and password
 
 Please note: The Bind Account will need to have write access, allowing Clever to find student and teacher accounts to reset their passwords. 

 

 Lastly, you will be able to set additional configurations, which include:

 • Surfacing the search field (user identifier) to users
• Selecting which user types are allowed to use this feature
• The AD password requirements
• Email domains you would like to exclude from being used as recovery methods
• Whether or not teachers (and School Tech Leads) can reset their students' AD passwords
 
 

 Select Finish . Students, teachers, and School Tech Leads can now reset their AD passwords from their Clever Portals! 

 For instructions on how users reset their Active Directory password through Clever, visit: For staff: How do I use Account Recovery?

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): "Log in with ADFS"
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. The user experience 4. Configuring ADFS 5. Configuring Clever SSO with ADFS 

 Overview

 Clever single sign-on (SSO) enables users to be securely authenticated into connected applications via their Clever Portal. Users log in to Clever just once with one set of credentials, or using a Clever Badge , and then click on the various app icons to be logged in automatically via SSO. Clever application partners rely on Clever's verification system of determining that a user is, indeed, who they say they are. Without Clever SSO, each application would need to maintain its own database of users and their credentials. 

 This article details the setup process for using ADFS as your district's Clever SSO method. 

 Requirements

 In addition to the basic single sign-on (SSO) requirements , you’ll need the following: 

 • Active Directory Federation Services 2.x, 3.x or 4.x 
• Metadata file accessible over HTTPS with a certificate signed by a valid certificate authority 
• Make sure the following IP addresses are allow listed in your firewall: 
 • 54.241.134.131 
• 50.18.217.135 
• 54.241.154.41 
 

• At least one real account to use to test the connection: In order to test the connection after set-up, you’ll need the credentials of a student or teacher. You won’t be able to test with other admin credentials; it needs to be someone whose information is also in Clever. If this is problematic, we recommend creating a test teacher or student in your SIS, syncing with Clever, and using that account instead of a real one to test the connection.
 
 

 The user experience

 • When you set up Active Directory, you define usernames and passwords for your users.
• When users access the Clever Portal or a single sign-on (SSO) link , they’ll be prompted to log in to Clever using these credentials by clicking Log in with Active Directory 
• Once they click this button, they will be redirected to your Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS) login page.
• After users successfully log in to your Active Directory server, your ADFS instance will use claims rules to tell us which user is logging in.
 
 

 Please note: Because the credentials are entered only on the Active Directory login page, Clever will never know the usernames and passwords for your users. 

 Configuring ADFS

 You'll need to configure Active Directory to connect with Clever single sign-on (SSO). You'll need to update two areas: 

 • Relying Party Trusts 
• Claim Rules 
 
 Relying Party Trust 

 • In ADFS Management, open Trust Relationships > Relying Party Trusts .
• Click Add Relying Party Trusts and input https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml where it asks for Federation Metadata address
 
 

 • On the next page, you can leave the default display name ( Clever.com ) or change it to any display name you choose.
 
 

 • On the next page, select Permit all users to access this relying party 
 
 

 • Review your choices and click Finish .
 
 Clever should now be listed as a Relying Party Trust. 

 Claim Rules Clever.com will now appear in the list of Relying Party Trusts. Right-click the display name and select Edit Claims Rules . These rules will ensure that Clever matches students and teachers appropriately when they log in. 

 • Select Add Rule - the default on the first page should be Send LDAP Attributes as Claims 
• On the next page, you can set the Claim Rule Name to anything you’d like. 
• Select Active Directory as the attribute store - there will be two adjacent drop-down menus. 
• For the first attribute, select SAM-Account-Name on the left and Name ID on the right. 
• Two new drop-downs should appear. Use the new row of drop-downs to define a claims rule - a field in your Clever SIS sync that contains the same unique identifier as a field in Active Directory. 
 
 • The left drop-down will contain the attribute you’d like to send 
• The right drop-down will contain the Clever field that we will match that data with. A list of supported entries for Outgoing Claim Type , as well as more information on Claims Rules can be found in our article: Understanding Claims Rules . 
 
 Here’s an example of fully set up claims rules: 

 

 In the above sample: 

 • SAM-Account-Name matches to sis_id for students in Clever 
• E-Mail-Addresses matches to emails for teachers in Clever. 
 
 This is what our systems will use to authenticate the user logging in and give them access to the correct applications through Clever. 

 Important Note: You'll need to personalize the LDAP attribute and Outgoing Claim Type based on the data available in Active Directory and Clever for your district. Regardless of the personalized claims rules for your district, you must have the first claims rule pictured above (SAM-Account-Name -> Name ID). 

 

 6. Select Finish to finalize setup in ADFS! 

 Configuring Clever SSO with ADFS

 

 • Find your Metadata URL - in your ADFS Management Console, browse to Service > Endpoints > Metadata > Type: Federation Metadata. It should take the format: https://<ADFS server name>/federationmetadata/2007-06/FederationMetadata.xml 
• Make sure your certificates are set up correctly - to do so, put your Metadata URL into SSL Checker . This tool will let you know if there are any issues that might prevent us from accepting your Metadata URL.
 
 Inputting your Metadata URL 

 Take the following steps in the Clever Admin Dashboard: 

 • Under your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page, c hoose the shortname for your SSO portal URL. The URL will be www.clever.com/in/ <shortname> . Remember to use something that your students and teachers will remember easily. 
• Under Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page , click Add Login Method and select Configure SAML Authentication . Input your metadata URL in the text box and select Save. 
• To finish the setup process, add the contact details for the person that students and teachers should reach out to if they have trouble with logging in to Clever; this should be someone who can help them reset their Active Directory credentials and/or make sure they are shared with the application they’re trying to access through Clever. Add this at your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Customize tab. 
 
 You should now be set up - try a few logins and see if you run into any issues! If you have questions about this process, please submit a request. 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): "Log in with LDAPS"
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Notes 

 Overview

 Clever can connect to your district's existing LDAPS provider to log students and teachers into their Clever Portal account. LDAPS is a more secure variant of LDAP that uses Transport Layer Security (TLS) to ensure that usernames and passwords are transmitted securely. For security reasons, Clever does not support using non-secure LDAP with single sign-on (SSO). We cannot provide support for upgrading your existing LDAP system to LDAPS at this time, but this guide may be helpful if you are using Active Directory.

 
 Setup

 To provision accounts, Clever needs permission to connect to your LDAP server. To establish a successful connection with Clever, you will need to provide the following information for your LDAP server: 

 • LDAPS URL 
• SHA-256 fingerprints of server’s SSL certificate
• Base Distinguished Name (Base DN)
• One of: 
 • Username prefix/suffix and the field in the LDAP server we use to find the user once we have access, or 
• Username and password for a bind account, a field in the LDAP server we use to find the user once we have access, and the field in the LDAP server used as the end user’s username. 
 

• The property in your LDAPS accounts that we can match with data in Clever - if you're not sure what this could be, please see this article about claims rules. 
 
 

 In addition to providing the information above, if you use a firewall please give access to the Clever external IP addresses ( 54.241.134.131 , 50.18.217.135 , 54.241.154.41 ) on port 636 to ensure a successful connection with your server.

 Once you're ready, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings , and click Add Login Method on the top right. Select LDAP Authentication to open the setup wizard.

 Please note: Changing the certificate on the LDAPS server without updating the SHA1 fingerprint here will cause logins to fail until resolved. 

 

 
 Notes

 

 • If using a BIND account, the account will need read and search permissions on the base DN. We recommend you limit BIND account permissions to minimize the exposure of sensitive directory information.
• If using a BIND account, the BIND Account Search Attribute and the BIND Account Username Attribute should NOT be the same.
• If a district is using sAMAccountName and userPrincipalName, there is a high likelihood that their server is Active directory.
• Use the "Match Automatically" tool to see what user objects look like. 
 
 

 As always, feel free to contact Clever Support if you run into any issues or have any questions about setting up LDAPS as your identity provider! 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: For SFTP districts: How do I add district offices or departments in the schools file?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding offices or departments to Clever 

 

 Overview

 If you're syncing data to Clever via SFTP by uploading CSV files, you can edit the schools.csv file to include district offices or departments that will appear as a "school" in Clever. 

 This article provides additional information on how to edit your files to add additional offices or departments in Clever. 

 
 Adding offices or departments to Clever

 If you are syncing via the SFTP method, you can edit the schools.csv file to include district offices or departments that will appear as a "school" in Clever. 

 Clever Admins syncing staff members to Clever must associate the staff with a particular school or schools. Most staff (i.e., Principals, APs, Dean of Students, etc.) are admins related to specific schools. 

 However, some staff members don't serve specific schools. In this case, we’ll want to create "schools" in Clever that are actually district-level departments or offices. For example, a school could be named Office of the Superintendent, Department of Secondary English Language Arts , or Division of Instructional Technology. As long as the School_ids are unique and consistent across syncs, they don't have to be real. 

 For example, below is a schools.csv file that contains "schools" that are truly offices or departments: 

 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • How do I add non-teaching staff and administrators to Clever?

title: Sync Report error: Records not processed due to missing school
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to troubleshoot this error 

 

 Overview

 

 There are a number of errors that may surface on your Last attempted sync page related to individual data types. For example, you may see that students or teachers fail to sync to Clever due to being assigned to a school site that doesn't exist in your files and/or Clever. 

 All students and teachers must be assigned to a school in order to be synced with Clever. There are a few reasons you may see the error “records not processed due to missing school”: 

 • The ‘School_id’ field is blank for some or all students/teachers; or
• The ‘School_id’ field in the ‘students.csv’ or ‘teachers.csv’ file does not match a listed ‘School_id’ field in the ‘schools.csv’ file.
 
 
 How to troubleshoot this error

 • Visit the Sync Report page of your Dashboard for detailed information about data that Clever was unable to process.
• Click the blue icon 'Download Changes' to download a list of records affected by this error. 
• Make sure that ‘School_id’ is populated for all records in your ‘students.csv’ and ‘teachers.csv’ files.
• Compare the ‘School_id’ values in ‘teachers.csv’ and ‘students.csv’ to make sure they’re all present in ‘schools.csv’. A common cause of mismatched IDs between files is a lost a leading zero - click here for more information on identifying and resolving this error.
 
 
 Related articles: 

 • Sync Reports: Error messages 
• Sync Report error: Sections not processed due to missing students 
• Sync Report error: Records not processed due to duplicate IDs

title: Sync Report error: Records not processed due to duplicate IDs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to troubleshoot this error 3. Teachers at multiple school sites 

 

 Overview

 There are a number of errors that may surface on your Last attempted sync page related to individual data types. For example, you may see users fail to sync to Clever due to the use of duplicate IDs. 

 Clever requires a unique ID for every record we process, and each data type contains at least one unique record ID field value: 'School_id', 'Teacher_id', 'Section_id', or 'Student_id'. If two or more records share an ID value, Clever will only process one record with that ID. The following error may be surfaced: duplicate 'section_id (school_id, teacher_id, student_id)' used' 

 These unique IDs are used to make connections between data types and therefore need to be unique and consistent across all 5 files. For example, a school record in your ‘schools.csv’ file with 'School_id' “12345” should be identified as “12345” for each student attending that school in the ‘students.csv’ file. 

 How to troubleshoot this error

 • Visit the Sync Report in your Dashboard for detailed information about data that Clever was unable to process. 
• Click "Download Changes" to download a list of records affected by this error. 
• Correct these errors in your uploads by determining a truly unique identifier for all records, or filter the data before you upload to Clever.
 
 

 Teachers at multiple school sites

 To avoid receiving this duplicate ID error, teachers at multiple school sites should still only be listed once in the teachers.csv file with one school_id as their primary school. However, they can then be associated with sections from multiple schools in the sections.csv file.

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Sync Reports: Error messages 
• Sync Report error: Sections not processed due to missing students 
• Sync Report error: Records not processed due to missing school

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Log in with Google
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Optional Settings • Side-loading emails • Forcing Google logout when users log out of Clever • Append Domain • Override Google domain 

 IMPORTANT Admins must mark Clever as a trusted app in Google, or students, teachers, and administrators may lose access to Clever. Learn more here: For District Admin: Google SSO 3rd party confirmation 

 Overview

 Clever single sign-on (SSO) allows students and teachers to log in using their district email addresses provisioned by Google. This means users will need to use their district-assigned Google email and password to log in to Clever (not an @ gmail.com email address). 

 Clever Tip: Google authentication supports multiple domains! No special configuration is required. Simply ensure that users' school email addresses are synced to Clever and they will be successfully authenticated into Clever. 

 

 
 Setup

 Step 1: Ensure that all district users have a district-provided Google email address in Clever. To verify this: 

 • Navigate to the Data Browser to make sure the Email field is populated for district students, teachers, and staff. By using the Add Filter option, you can check to see if emails are populated by choosing the Email value as the Property, Contains as the Operator, and input @ as the value. This will bring up a list of emails for the user type. 
 
 

 

 • Please note: District-provided Google email addresses can be synced to Clever from your student information system (SIS) or side-loaded to Clever , thus bypassing the sync. For more information on side-loading emails, read here . 
 
 Step 2: Admins can configure the single sign-on (SSO) settings from the Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. 

 • Select Add Login Method to the far right.
• Select Google Authentication as the Identity Provider.
• Confirm the Google email source.
• Select Save .
 
 

 Step 3: Test various users to ensure their login credentials work. To do this: 

 • Ensure you have the credentials for a few students, teachers or staff.
• Navigate to your district’s Clever login page .
• Enter the credentials of the user you are testing.
• You should be successfully logged into Clever as the user!
 
 Once the Google login method setup steps are completed, the Google login option will automatically appear on your district's Clever login page.

 Here's an interactive tutorial

 ** Best experienced in Full Screen (click the icon in the top right corner before you begin) ** 

 https://www.iorad.com/player/1996456/Adding-Google-as-an-Identity-Provider--IDP- 

 
 Optional Settings

 
 Google Email Source 

 
 If Google email addresses are not stored in your SIS, you can manually upload the emails to Clever in a separate file. To use this option, select: Google emails are NOT stored in my district's SIS. 

 Please note: manual email upload is not available to districts using Override domain. 

 To do this, please follow the instructions in this article . 

 Force Google logout when users log out of Clever 

 If your district is using Google as your identity provider (IdP), you can apply a setting from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page that will automatically log users out of Google when they log out of Clever. This is helpful in situations where multiple users are logging in to Clever on shared devices. When using a shared device, it is important to log the first user out of Google (in addition to Clever) before the next user logs in. Otherwise, some devices will not allow the second user to log in. Districts can help to eliminate this issue by forcing the user's Google logout when they log out of Clever. When enabled: 

 Chrome/Edge/Firefox 

 When a user logs out of Clever, the coinciding Google session will also be terminated and the user will be automatically redirected to Clever's login page.

 Safari 

 When a user logs out of Clever, they will be redirected to Google's logout page. To begin a new Clever session, users must manually navigate to Clever's login page. 

 To enable Force Google Logout: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page and select the Edit settings option for Google. 
• Select Force Google logout when users log out of Clever 
• Select Save . 
 
 

 For more info rmation about supporting users on shared devices: Troubleshooting: Issues with Shared Devices 

 

 
 Append Domain: 

 You can pre-populate your Google domain for users to facilitate even easier logins! Simply use our "Append Domain" tool. 

 • Navigate to Authentication > SSO settings > Login method tab. 
• Select Edit settings for Google Authentication
• Check the box for Append my district's Google domain when users sign in 
• Enter your district's Google domain without the @ symbol
• Select Save 
 

 

 Once complete, users will see your Google domain pre-populated when they select Log in with Google . If your teachers or staff use a different domain than students, they can override the pre-populated domain by simply typing their entire email address. Once they enter '@,' the prep-populated domain will disappear. 

 

 

 
 Override Google domain: 

 
 Occasionally, there are situations where districts change their Google district domain but do not want to update all user email addresses in their SIS to the new domain. In cases like this, district admins can provide Clever with the district's new Google domain, and Clever will update user emails behind the scenes ! Email domain override only supports one domain. Districts that use multiple domains (example: @ cleveracademy.com and @ students.cleveracademy.com ) are not supported.

 Please note: The email addresses will still show the older domain when viewing a user's profile page in Clever; however, the user will be able to log in using the email address with the new domain. This will only work if users have an email address in Clever, and the username remains the same. For example, JaneSmith@district.org could be appended to JaneSmith@schooldistrict.org by Clever as long as 'JaneSmith' remains the same.

 
 Directions: 

 • Navigate to the Authentication > SSO settings > Login method tab. 
• Select Edit settings for Google. 
• Select Google email addresses are NOT stored in my district's SIS , click Next 
• Enter your district's Google domain. 
• Select to which user types to override (e.g. Teachers, Students, or both) 
• Once saved, users will use their email with the district domain you have set here to log in to Clever
 

 Please note: Clever does not have access to any Google passwords for district users, so password-related issues will need to be resolved by the district. For troubleshooting tips, please visit: Troubleshooting: Google User Login Issues. 

 
 

 Interested in deep-diving into how to best use Clever and Google together? Check out our course, Clever & Google: Secure Connected Classroom Certification available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: SFTP Syncs: OneRoster
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Preparing my Clever sync to accept OneRoster files 3. Syncing student contacts via OneRoster 4. Troubleshooting 5. Default field mappings 

 Overview 

 As a member of the IMS Global Learning Consortium, Clever supports the IMS Global OneRoster standard. School organizations can send OneRoster 1.0 , 1.1 , or 1.2 formatted CSV files via Clever’s secure SFTP data pipeline. It’s the easiest, most secure way to support OneRoster using our proven platform without compromising functionality or data control.

 

 
 Preparing my Clever sync to accept OneRoster files 

 To prepare your sync for OneRoster files follow the steps below:

 1. Contact Clever Support : Our team will help you create a transition plan and timeline. Just let us know if you’re using OneRoster 1.0, 1.1, or 1.2.

 2. Review the field mapping documentation: Whether you are new to Clever or transitioning your sync type, you'll be provided with a field mapping document. This shows how OneRoster fields translate to the Clever fields your apps require.

 • Please note: Clever strictly interprets OneRoster 1.0, 1.1, 1.2 to ensure compatibility with evolving standards. You can find official 1.1 formatting here .
• Required files: 
 
 • 
 academicSessions.csv

• 
 classes.csv

• 
 courses.csv

• 
 demographics.csv

• 
 enrollments.csv

• 
 orgs.csv

• 
 users.csv

• 
 manifest.csv (Must list all 7 files above)

 
 3. Automate transmission: Send your CSVs to your Clever SFTP box. Use the correct subdirectory for your version:

 • 
 • 1.0: /oneroster 
• 
 1.1: /oneroster11 

• 
 1.2: /oneroster12 

 

 
 4. Share data: Once complete, you can leverage Clever’s data-sharing interface to scope student rosters with applications and personalize your Clever Portal.

 
 Syncing student contacts via OneRoster

 You can sync student contacts via the users.csv . To connect a contact to a student, use the agentSourceID as shown below: 

 

 The users.csv spec can be found here: https://www.imsglobal.org/oneroster-v11-final-csv-tables#_Toc480293266 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Below you can find solutions to common sync errors that may display in your Clever Dashboard if you are using a OneRoster SFTP sync.

 
 
 Error Message 

 
 Solution 

 
 
 "A manifest.csv file is required for a OneRoster v1.1 SIS data sync"

 
 Ensure the manifest.csv is in the OneRoster zipped file. Zip the files together as a group; zipping the folder itself will cause this error. The file should include all the required files: 

 • academicSessions.csv 

 • classes.csv 

 • courses.csv

 • demographics.csv (optional - mark as absent if you're not using this file.) 

 • enrollments.csv

 • orgs.csv

 • users.csv

 
 
 Could not find orgs.csv file

 
 List the orgs.csv as bulk in the manifest.csv and ensure the file contains all required headers. 

 • 
 OneRoster 1.0 CSV formatting and specifications 

• 
 OneRoster 1.1 CSV formatting and specifications 

• OneRoster 1.2 CSV formatting and specifications 
 
 If the error persists, please reach out to Clever Support to confirm your OneRoster version (1.0. 1.1, 1.2). 

 
 
 Encountered zero enrollments data

 
 • 
 Confirm the enrollments.csv isn't empty (common during rollover periods).

• 
 Confirm the enrollment date format. It must be YYYY-MM-DD format per OneRoster specifications.

• 
 Check your users.csv and confirm users are enabledUser field is set to “True.”

 
 
 
 Invalid manifest.csv

 
 Confirm your manifest.version in the manifest.csv is set to 1.0.

 
 
 A manifest.csv is required for a OneRoster 1.1 sync

 
 • 
 Ensure your manifest.csv is present in the zipped file.

• 
 Ensure the manifest.version in the manifest.csv is 1.0.

• 
 Confirm all files in your manifest.csv are listed as either “bulk” or “absent”.

• 
 Ensure you have compressed (zipped) the files as a group. Compressing as a folder will cause this error.

 
 
 
 Failed to start decoding manifest.csv

 
 • 
 Confirm manifest.csv is formatted per OneRoster specifications ( 1.0 , 1.1 or 1.2 ). Check file headers and files listed as “bulk” or “absent”. Manifest.version should be 1.0.

• 
 Confirm you zipped the files as a group rather than zipping a folder.

 
 
 
 

 Below you can find solutions to common solutions to general data issues that may occur if you are using a OneRoster SFTP sync.

 
 
 Error Message 

 
 Solution 

 
 
 User (student, teacher, etc.) missing from Clever

 
 • 
 Please confirm all required fields are populated per the relevant OneRoster specification:

 • 
 OneRoster 1.0 CSV formatting and specifications 

• 
 OneRoster 1.1 CSV formatting and specifications 

• 
 OneRoster 1.2 CSV formatting and specifications 

 

• 
 Please confirm the username field is populated. This is required.

• 
 If using OneRoster 1.1 or 1.2, ensure that the role field is populated in the users.csv.

 
 
 
 Missing terms or enrollments

 
 Dates must use YYYY-MM-DD format to process.

 
 
 Missing section or enrollment

 
 Clever requires sections have a primary teacher and at least one student assigned.

 • 
 Check the enrollments.csv and make sure that the teacher and students have the following fields populated:

 
 • 
 status = active. If tobedeleted or inactive are marked, the enrollment will not sync to Clever.

• 
 schoolSourcedId = SourcedId (orgs.csv). This maps to the student's school_id in Clever.

• 
 userSourcedId = SourcedId (users.csv). This maps to the student_id and teacher_id.

• 
 role = student or teacher

 
 Check the users.csv and make sure that the teacher and students are active (status = active & enabledUser = true).

 
 
 Student enrollments are persisting after the end date has passed

 
 Clever ignores start/end dates. To remove enrollments, please remove them from your enrollments.csv entirely.

 
 
 Missing demographic data - date of birth

 
 Confirm date is formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.

 
 
 Missing demographic data - race

 
 Ensure you are using a value accepted by OneRoster and that each value is marked as “true” or “false” in your demographics.csv.

 • 
 americanIndianOrAlaskaNative

• 
 blackOrAfricanAmerican

• 
 nativeHawaiianOrOtherPacificIslander

• 
 white

• 
 demographicraceTwoOrMoreRaces

• 
 hispanicOrLatinoEthnicity

 
 If Hispanic_Latino is missing in Clever, ensure that hispanicOrLatinoEthnicity is the only field marked as true for the student in the demographics.csv, all other values should be marked as false.

 
 
 Missing demographic data - gender

 
 Confirm sex is populated in the demographics.csv.

 • 
 For OneRoster 1.1 only male or female are accepted values.

• 
 For OneRoster 1.2 male, female, other, or unspecified are accepted values.

 
 
 
 Issues with lineitems.csv

 
 Please mark as “absent” in the manifest.csv. This file cannot sync to Clever.

 
 
 Issues with the results.csv

 
 Please mark as “absent” in the manifest.csv. This file cannot sync to Clever.

 
 

 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever Support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 
 Default field mappings 

 Schools

 
 Click here for OneRoster 1.1 default mappings 
 

 
 Clever

 
 OneRoster 1.1 file

 
 OneRoster 1.1 Field Name

 
 School_id orgs.csv SourcedID 
 School_name orgs.csv Name 
 School_number orgs.csv SourcedID 
 State_id orgs.csv metadata.stateId 
 Nces_id orgs.csv identifier 
 Low_grade orgs.csv metadata.gradeRange 
 High_grade orgs.csv metadata.gradeRange 
 Principal orgs.csv metadata.principal 
 Principal_email orgs.csv metadata.principalEmail 
 School_address orgs.csv metadata.address1 metadata.address2 
 School_city orgs.csv metadata.city 
 School_state orgs.csv metadata.state 
 School_zip orgs.csv metadata.postCode 
 School_phone orgs.csv metadata.phone 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 Click here for OneRoster 1.2 default mappings 
 

 
 Clever

 
 OneRoster 1.2 file

 
 OneRoster 1.2 Field Name

 
 School_id orgs.csv SourcedID 
 School_name orgs.csv Name 
 School_number orgs.csv SourcedID 
 State_id orgs.csv metadata.st

title: Sync Report error: Sections not processed due to missing students
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to troubleshoot this error 

 Overview

 There are a number of errors that may surface on your Last attempted sync page related to individual data types. For example, you may see sections not process due to missing enrollments. Sections must have active students enrolled to be processed into Clever. We use the ‘enrollments.csv’ file to associate students with sections using their unique IDs; each ‘Student_id’ listed next to a ‘Section_id’ means that student is enrolled in that section. You may see a “records not processed due to no students enrolled” because: 

 • A section’s ‘Section_id’ is not present in the ‘enrollments.csv’ file; or
• A ‘Student_id’ listed in the ‘enrollments.csv’ file is not present in the ‘students.csv’ file.
 
 The error is often surfaced as: invalid 'Course Roster' due to error 'no students' 

 
 How to troubleshoot this error:

 • Visit the Sync Report page of your Dashboard for detailed information about data that Clever was unable to process.
• Click 'Download Changes' to download a list of records affected by this error.
• Search for the ‘Section_id’ of the affected section(s) in your ‘enrollments.csv’ file.
• Make sure all sections with students enrolled are listed in your ‘enrollments.csv’ file.
• If you can find the ‘Section_id’ in the ‘enrollments.csv’ file, copy the ‘Student_id’ of students enrolled in that section and search for them in the ‘students.csv’ file.
• Make sure all students enrolled in sections are listed in your ‘students.csv’ file.
 
 It’s also possible that certain records are not processed due to formatting errors in the CSV files. Please see this article for troubleshooting CSV formatting errors. 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Sync Reports: Error messages 
• Sync Report error: Records not processed due to missing school 
• Sync Report error: Records not processed due to duplicate IDs

title: SFTP Syncs: Sync status errors
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview ​ 2. Received export with no headers ​ 3. Received export with zero data ​ 4. Unknown Column Names 5. Bad headers ​ 6. Missing Required SFTP File 

 

 Overview

 This article provides insight into the various sync status errors you might see on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page. Sync errors will typically prevent future syncs from processing successfully until the error is addressed.

 
 

 Received export with no headers

 All five CSV files require column headers as listed in Clever’s SFTP Instructions. Uploading a CSV file without column headers will result in an error message like this: 

 

 You can take the following steps to fix this error:

 • When you export data directly from your Student Information System (SIS) or through an SQL query, double-check that the export includes a header row.
• Headers must match Clever’s header names exactly in the SFTP Instructions. 
• If you’re having trouble exporting data from your SIS, please contact your SIS directly; Clever is unable to troubleshoot exporting data from your SIS and SQL for SFTP Syncs.
 
 If you need help formatting your CSV files, see the following Help Center article: How do I format my CSV files? 

 Once your files include header rows according to Clever’s specified file format, upload all five files again. Your sync status should change once your data is processed. 

 
 Received export with zero data

 Clever requires all five CSV files to process your data upload. If one of your files is empty, you’ll see an error like this: 

 

 You can take the following steps to fix this error: 

 • When exporting data directly from your SIS or through an SQL query, double-check that all active schools, students, sections, teachers, and enrollments are exporting.
• If you’re having trouble exporting data from your SIS, please contact your SIS directly; Clever is unable to troubleshoot exporting data from your SIS and SQL for SFTP Syncs.
• If your semester or school year has recently changed, check that the correct term is selected to export.
• For more helpful tips on semester and school year rollover, click here 
• Once all five files include active data, upload all files to Clever again. Your sync status should change once your data processing completes.
 
 

 
 Unknown Column Names

 Clever only recognizes column headers that exactly match those listed in our SFTP Instructions . If your file contains a header that Clever doesn’t recognize, you’ll see a similar error in your Dashboard:

 

 To resolve this error: 

 • Click on the link in the error message if you'd like to map the fields you synced to fields in Clever.
• Check that all column header names in the identified file match those listed in the SFTP Instructions .
 
 
 Bad Header Errors

 Clever sometimes encounters unreadable headers and you’ll receive an error. This error usually occurs when the number of fields in the CSV header row is different from the number of fields in data rows.

 Common causes of this issue are: 

 • Trailing commas
• Extra commas in field values
 
 For more information on troubleshooting CSV formatting errors see this article: How do I troubleshoot CSV formatting errors? 

 

 
 Missing Required SFTP File

 Clever requires all 5 CSV files in each SFTP upload (schools.csv, students.csv, teachers.csv, sections.csv, and enrollments.csv). If you are missing one of these 5 files, you will receive this error in your Dashboard: 

 

 To resolve this error: 

 • Upload all 5 files to Clever in your next upload
• Because every sync overwrites the data currently in your Clever account, each file needs to contain the previous data that should remain in Clever, as well as any desired changes.
 
 If you have any questions about sync errors, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: SFTP Syncs: Recommended tools (e.g. FTP Clients, CSV editing tools)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. CSV & Text Editing Tools 2. FTP Clients 

 CSV & Text Editing Tools

 Clever recommends using CSV and text editors to avoid file formatting errors. See this help article about formatting errors, and this article about sync status errors due to formatting.

 
 CSVEdit 

 
 We love using CSVEdit to view CSV files without worrying about dropping leading zeros!

 
 
 Sublime Text 

 
 If you need to modify your data before uploading your CSV files to Clever, Sublime Text is an easy-to-use text editor. Text editors are also great for identifying trailing comma issues. 

 
 
 TextEdit 

 
 If you’re a Mac user, TextEdit is your Mac’s default text editor. We recommend using a text editor to safely view and save CSV files without skewing data. 

 
 
 Notepad 

 
 Notepad is a simple text editor that you’ll find on your PC if you’re a Windows user; it’s a helpful tool to view CSV files. 

 
 
 Notepad++ 

 
 A free Windows-based text editor, Notepad++ is great for viewing and editing CSV/text files. You can use tabs to open multiple files, and we love the search function! 

 
 
 

 
 FTP Clients

 FTP Clients allow you to safely transfer files via Secure File Transfer Protocol. For more information about uploading data to Clever with an FTP client, see this help article .

 
 OpenSSH 

 
 OpenSSH is a suite of security-related network-level utilities based on the Secure Shell (SSH) protocol. 

 
 
 WinSCP 

 
 WinSCP is a free FTP client designed for Windows.

title: SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever
text:
This article covers the following: 1. Overview 2. Option 1: Clever's Web Upload Tool 3. Option 2: FTP client 4. Locating your SFTP credentials 5. Clever’s SFTP Host Keys 

 Overview

 This article will cover how to upload data to Clever for SFTP and SFTP SIS-Managed Auto Syncs. You have a couple of options for getting your school or district data into Clever (and, therefore, any other app you connect to Clever):

 • Option 1: Manually uploading the data via the Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page.
• Option 2: Using an automated SFTP transfer ( see here for more information ).
 
 
 Option 1: Clever's Web Upload Tool

 

 Onboarding to Clever and want to learn about uploading CSV files? Check out our Onboarding - Getting your schools’ data into Clever course, available in Clever Academy! 

 

 You can find the Web Upload tool from your Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page. From there you can drag and drop each file, or click the cloud to browse and attach each CSV file. Select Upload and wait until the top of the page displays Your files have been uploaded before leaving the page. 

 Please note: While not always required, it is best practice to always upload the entire set of files ( schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv and enrollments.csv ) every time you do an upload. 

 

 To learn more about this process and how you can map your fields to Clever fields, please review this article .

 Why is the Web Upload Tool Missing? 

 The Web Upload Tool is only available to districts that are SFTP. The Web Upload Tool page will redirect districts that use SIS-managed SFTP syncs or Clever-managed syncs to the Custom Data page:

 

 How can I add staff? 

 If your school district uses an SIS-managed SFTP sync or a Clever-managed sync, you can upload staff on the Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data page via the following options:

 • Sync with your SIS auto sync
• Upload a staff.csv to your Clever SFTP directory
• Active Directory sync (if staff members have Active Directory accounts)
• Add additional staff as Custom Data on the Custom Data page 
 
 

 

 
 

 Option 2: FTP client

 Another option for sending files to Clever is using the Secure File Transfer Protocol , also known as SSH File Transfer Protocol. The following outlines the process for manually uploading files through an FTP client . Many districts write a script to upload files via SFTP automatically. At this time Clever does not provide support for setting up these automations.

 There are many FTP clients available (e.g. Transmit , FileZilla or WinSCP ). When you choose an FTP client, first confirm that it will support SFTP, as not all clients will. SFTP is not the same as FTP over SSH, FTP over SSL, FTPS or Simple File Transfer Protocol . 

 Each client's interface will look a little different, but each will involve similar steps. Check out this interactive tutorial , or follow the instructions below which show Filezilla's interface: 

 • Download and install an FTP client program that supports SFTP.
• Log into your Clever dashboard and locate your SFTP credentials in the SFTP Settings box on the Dashboard > Sync > Settings page (see below for more information).
• Open the FTP client program, enter your SFTP credentials ("host" is the SFTP Upload URL), and click 'Quickconnect'; Note: some FTP clients will require a modified URL configuration such as sftp.clever.com . Please reference their help documentation.
 

• Once the link between your 'Local Site' and 'Remote Site' is established, you should see the files already stored in the host site appear on the right side of the window.
• Browse the 'Local Site' (your computer's hard drive, on the left side of the window) until you find all five of the CSV files you would like to upload. Drag them from the 'Local Site' to the 'Remote Site' into the folder with your district's username. In the image below, the username is wise-information-1089. (Please note: our system will only process files that adhere to our naming protocol– schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, enrollments.csv, sections.csv). Please be consistent with capitalization to avoid duplicate files.
 

• This may prompt a pop-up to appear, pictured below. If so, allow the program to overwrite preexisting data in the host site with the incoming data. (You should only have 5 files in your 'Remote Site' at one time).
 

• Clever will begin processing any updated files or new uploads approximately five minutes after they have been uploaded to your FTP box. Please note that it may take 30 minutes for the sync to fully complete.
 
 Clever does not move or delete files after processing, so please be sure your SFTP folder settings are configured to always write over existing files with newly uploaded files! 

 

 
 Locating your SFTP credentials

 You can find these credentials on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. To ensure the safety of your information, we will never send or request your SFTP password over email or on the phone. Please follow the instructions below to retrieve your SFTP username and password: 

 • Log into your Clever account at http://schools.clever.com . You'll need to use the email and password that you created when you signed up for your Clever account.
• From the left sidebar, click Sync . 
• Click on the Settings tab.
• You'll see the SFTP username and password. We suggest copy/pasting the password into your FTP client to prevent any typos.
 • Please note: 
 • Districts cannot change their SFTP usernames or passwords. 
• Please use the information in the SFTP Settings box (not the box titled SFTP Settings - Saved Passwords Applications). 
• For the Veracross SIS, don't include the leading "s ftp://" " at the beginning of the SFTP UPLOAD URL, otherwise you'll get a sync error. 
 

 

 
 

 
 Clever’s SFTP Host Keys

 Every time your district's SFTP client connects to Clever, it will check our SFTP server's Host Key to ensure your data is being safely transmitted to the correct server.

 Each SFTP client handles Host Keys slightly differently. The following is documentation on some of the leading SFTP clients: 

 • WinSCP 
• Filezilla 
 
 The following are Clever's Public SFTP Host Keys: 

 |1|2JTJAY17moTn4gIUyBLtYzhGjVQ=|g6AJPvQiCXDxHzLkqB/gJQzfGkQ= ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 AAAAE2VjZHNhLXNoYTItbmlzdHAyNTYAAAAIbmlzdHAyNTYAAABBBPr8wQCP3VKLB0K/8wKI2oK+ICS+VRLRk8k0CC0nwXXAHHMVCOGZyVVJT6mM/zmpygV3z6Dfn45vuCB7ohjVnU8= 
 |1|Cmwc8pyP2jol9g2ycotT+9HqX9U=|idyXWAmWX4Hf7i0F0FclE067Hu8= ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 AAAAE2VjZHNhLXNoYTItbmlzdHAyNTYAAAAIbmlzdHAyNTYAAABBBPr8wQCP3VKLB0K/8wKI2oK+ICS+VRLRk8k0CC0nwXXAHHMVCOGZyVVJT6mM/zmpygV3z6Dfn45vuCB7ohjVnU8= 
 The following are Clever's Public Key Fingerprints: 

 sftp://sftp.clever.com 

 256 76:0c:bb:e5:f7:df:97:c3:f2:77:0d:9a:2e:d7:92:18 (ECDSA) 
 2048 f3:62:21:31:be:64:d0:a6:7d:60:73:d6:de:f9:bd:39 (RSA) 
 sftp://credentials-sftp.clever.com 

 2048 26:d5:98:79:6f:ed:50:95:48:f2:22:e5:12:98:93:92 (RSA) 
 

 Should you have any issues formatting your CSV files, please visit this article .

title: SFTP Syncs: Troubleshooting CSV formatting errors
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Error codes 3. Trailing commas 4. Extra commas in field values 5. Leading zeros 

 

 Overview

 When you upload a CSV that contains one or more errors, you’ll see error messages in Clever. This article runs through errors and how to fix them for both automated SFTP and manual CSV uploads. When uploading data on the Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page, you’ll see the errors listed shortly after uploading the CSV file(s). You can find Clever's CSV file templates here .

 Please note: If you want to download the most recent CSV files uploaded to Clever, you can do so at the Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page. Learn more here ! 

 When errors occur from an SFTP sync, you’ll see the errors listed in the Status section on the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page. 

 One way to avoid some common errors is to understand how your files work together. This graphic is helpful to explain this: 

 Please note: To avoid CSV formatting errors, we recommend exporting data from your SIS directly in CSV format, and uploading those files directly to Clever without manipulating them. Excel is commonly used to view CSV files, but it often imposes formatting that skews data and results in SFTP upload errors. For error-free viewing and saving of CSV files, we recommend using an application built for CSV compatibility, such as CSVEdit, Sublime Text, Apache OpenOffice, TextEdit, or Notepad. 

 

 
 Error codes and how to handle them

 The table below lists the errors you may see when you run into an SFTP error and how to resolve the errors.

 Of note, it is common for schools to see specific data errors on the Last Attempted Sync page, and these errors do not need to be fully resolved for the Clever sync to continue operating. These errors indicate a specific record that failed to sync to Clever, and as long as these errors are not directly impacting user access or applications it is typically safe to ignore these errors. Error counts may change and resolve over time as data changes in your SIS throughout the school year. 

 

 

 
 Error: 

 
 Step(s) to take: 

 
 The following required file is missing: [file] Upload the required missing file(s).
