 The following required file is empty: [file] Ensure the file(s) in question contain the required data. The file should have a row of headers followed by at least one row of data. See the SFTP spec for the list of required data. 
 The following required file contains no data: [file] Ensure the file(s) in question contain the required data. The file should have a row of headers followed by at least one row of data. See the SFTP spec for the list of required data. 
 The following file is missing required headers: [file] [missing header] Double-check the file(s) have the missing header(s). See the SFTP spec for the list of required headers. 
 The following file includes unsupported headers: [file] [incorrect header] Double-check the header(s) in the file(s) are spelled correctly and do not contain any characters other than letters. See the SFTP spec for the list of supported headers. 
 The headers in the following file could not be parsed: [file] Check that the file is in CSV format. 
 The following file includes missing commas: [file] [lines] Ensure there are no missing commas in the file. 
 The following file includes extra commas: [file] [line] Ensure there are no extra commas. Jump to this section for more information. 
 The following file is missing required values: [file] [column header] [line] Add required value(s) for files. 
 The following students are in the enrollments.csv but missing from the students.csv: [student_id] Add respective students to the students.csv file. 
 The following sections are in the sections.csv but missing from the enrollments.csv: [section_id] Add respective sections to the enrollments.csv file. 
 The following teachers are in the sections.csv but missing from the teachers.csv: [teacher_id] Add respective teachers to the teachers.csv file. 
 The following schools are in the students.csv but missing from the schools.csv: [school_id] Add respective schools to the schools.csv file. 
 The following schools are in the teachers.csv but missing from the schools.csv: [school_id] Add respective schools to the schools.csv file. 
 The following schools are in the sections.csv but missing from the schools.csv: [school_id] Add respective schools to the schools.csv file. 
 The following schools are in the enrollments.csv but missing from the schools.csv: [school_id] Add respective schools to the schools.csv file. 
 The following schools are in the admins.csv but missing from the schools.csv: [school_id] Add respective schools to the schools.csv file. 
 The following students are in the guardians.csv but missing from the students.csv: [student_id] Add respective students to the students.csv file. 
 
 

 
 Trailing commas

 Saving an Excel file as a CSV file can create extra commas at the end of each row. Trailing commas can result when columns are deleted or column headers removed. When the file is uploaded to Clever, an extra trailing comma will skew subsequent rows of data, preventing them from being processed. 

 To identify this issue, we recommend opening the file in a text editor (like Notepad for PC, or TextEdit for Mac). You’ll see trailing commas at the end of each row, like this: 

 

 Take these steps to resolve the issue: 

 • Open a new, blank spreadsheet in Excel.
• Type in only the column headers for the data you will include in your upload.
• Save the file as MS-DOS Comma Separated with a new name (e.g., “students2.csv”).
• Open the old file (with the extra commas) and copy the rows below the column headers.
• Paste the rows into the new file under your typed column headers.
• Save your new file as MS-DOS Comma Separated again.
• Once you’ve saved the data into the new file, you can close it and rename it to “students.csv” (the required file name for uploading to Clever).
 
 

 
 Extra commas in field values

 Your data may sometimes include a comma within a field value (e.g., if a student’s last name is “Potter, Jr.”), and the comma-separated values file will treat the comma as a field separator unless the entire field value is enclosed in double quotation marks.

 The following record would NOT be processed to Clever. The three commas in the data row separate that record into four fields, but there are only three fields in the header row.

 

 This record would get skewed to look like this:

 

 The following record WOULD be processed to Clever. Enclosing the "Potter, Jr." field value in double (straight) quotation marks tells Clever that the entire last name is "Potter, Jr."

 This record would be processed as: 

 
 Leading zeros

 Numerical values (in fields like ‘school_id’, ‘student_id’, and ‘teacher_id’) often begin with “0”, but Excel removes these leading zeros, which changes the value listed for that field. For example, student_id “009844” becomes “9844”. When uploaded, Clever no longer recognizes that student: student “009844” is deleted and a new student, “9844”, is created. Once this incorrect data, caused by leading zeros, is uploaded to Clever, it may cause a hold due to changes in data. If the hold is approved, those unique SIS IDs can cause many issues, such as users or sections being re-created and losing their associated historical data in connected apps. If you need to open your CSV file in Excel, import your data as text to avoid dropping leading zeros: 

 • In Excel, click Data > Get External Data > Import Text File… 
• Browse to select your CSV file and click Get Data. 
• Choose Delimited as the file type and click Next. 
• Check the “Comma” box as the delimiter and click Next. 
• Select all columns in the data preview and change their column data format to “Text”.
• Click Finish. 
 
 Please note: Leading zeros are not permitted in the "grade" column. Our specifications will error out the

title: SFTP & Manual Upload: How do I format my CSV files?
text:
This article covers the following: 1. Overview 2. What's a CSV file? 3. Creating CSV files 4. Required fields 5. What will my files look like? 6. Uploading multiple student contacts 7. Multi-site users (users at multiple schools) 8. Quote Surrounded Fields 9. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 Overview 

 If your schools are brand-new to Clever, we recommend going through our onboarding courses in Clever Academy to access step-by-step support on getting set up with Clever.

 

 This article describes how to set up the data files you'll send to Clever if you choose to manually upload your school or district's data or if you choose to set up an SFTP sync. As a refresher, you have a few options for getting your school or district data into Clever (and therefore any other app you connect to Clever):

 • Option 1: Manually uploading the data via the Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page.
• Option 2: Using an automated SFTP transfer ( see here for more information ).
• Option 3: Use a managed automatic sync ( see here for more information ).
 
 If you choose options one or two, you must properly format and upload five comma-separated value (CSV) spreadsheet files to get your data into Clever. All CSV files should follow Clever’s file formatting requirements, regardless of which applications you use. For more technical information, please refer to our SFTP Instructions , which provide highly detailed directions for setting up your CSV files. You must be logged in to view the SFTP Instructions. In addition, you can check out this article for CSV formatting errors. 

 
 What’s a CSV file? 

 CSV files store a line of text for each data record, where the field values for that record are separated by commas. Here’s what comma-separated values look like: 

 

 This data can also be represented in a table format, which is what you see when opening the file in a spreadsheet application like Excel. Here’s the same data represented as a table: 

 

 
 Creating CSV files

 The best way to generate the five files you’ll upload to Clever is to export data directly from your Student Information System (SIS) in CSV format. Typing data by hand or manipulating data once it’s exported can lead to unexpected file formatting errors, which can prevent your data from being processed in Clever.

 Excel is commonly used to view CSV files, but it often imposes formatting that skews data and results in SFTP Upload Errors. For error-free viewing and saving CSV files, we recommend using an application built for CSV compatibility, such as CSVEdit, Sublime Text, Apache OpenOffice, TextEdit, or Notepad. For more information about troubleshooting file format issues, see this help article .

 The file names for the CSV files should be the following (all lowercase letters): 

 • schools.csv
• students.csv
• teachers.csv
• sections.csv
• enrollments.csv
 
 The templates for these CSV files can be found here . This graphic is helpful to explain how your files work together: It is important that the file names are consistent in case (upper/lower case) in each upload. Uploading "Schools.csv" vs. "schools.csv" will result in two different files being stored in the district's SFTP box and can result in sync errors. If you will be manually creating your CSV files, we highly encourage you to view this playlist of videos .

 

 

 
 Required fields

 Our SFTP Instructions include all possible fields you can upload and indicate whether each is required by Clever (bolded in the SFTP specifications and marked “YES” or “NO” under “Required”). The screenshot below shows some of the possible fields for schools.csv, where ‘School_id’ and ‘School_name’ are required, but ‘State_id’ is not required.

 

 Optional fields may be left blank, or you can leave them out of the CSV altogether. However, Clever recommends including as many fields as possible to take full advantage of reporting features in current applications, and to ensure compatibility with future app connections. Depending on which applications you connect your Clever account to, additional fields may be required beyond Clever’s minimum required fields. Check your district’s personalized SFTP instructions on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page to learn which fields are required by your connected applications.

 Please note: NCES_id and MDR_number are not accepted fields. If you need to populate NCES ID or MDR values for your schools or district please contact Clever Support using the blue chat box in the bottom right corner of the Help Center. 

 

 
 What will my files look like?

 See the screenshots below for examples of how each file should look. Yellow highlighted areas represented the minimum required fields for each file. Please note, the screenshots below do not show all possible fields for each file. Please refer to Clever’s SFTP Instructions to confirm all accepted fields. You can also download example files from the Web Upload page.

 Please see our note under “How should I create my 5 CSV files?” - if you view files in Excel, we recommend closing without saving to avoid formatting errors.

 

 schools.csv

 

 students.csv

 

 teachers.csv

 

 staff.csv

 

 sections.csv

 

 enrollments.csv

 

 

 
 Uploading multiple student contacts

 If you would like to sync more than one guardian/emergency contact for each student, you can list the student record multiple times in the students.csv file. Each row must have the same student information (student_id, name, email address, etc.) but the contact columns will have different information for each contact. Even though the student_id is listed multiple times, the student records will not receive a "duplicate id error" in the Sync Report for this particular instance. Please note - if providing student contacts, 'Contact_type' and 'Contact_name' columns are required.

 Below is what student contacts look like in the students.csv file:

 

 

 
 Multi-site users (users at multiple schools)

 Users can be associated with multiple schools in Clever. Here's how that is done!

 Students 

 Each student should only be associated with one school_id in the student.csv, otherwise you will receive a "duplicate sis_id" error. Students will be associated with multiple schools via their enrollments. If a student has enrollments in sections at multiple schools, they will be associated with the respective schools. 

 Teachers 

 Each teacher should have only have one record in the teachers.csv file, otherwise you will receive a "duplicate sis_id" error. Additional school associations will be processed from the sections.csv file. Clever will process all of the teacher's section assignments, giving them access to sections at multiple schools. Additionally, they will be associated with the respective schools. 

 Non-teaching staff 

 To associate a staff member with multiple schools, create a separate row at each location. The only field that will be different for each row is the school_id. For example, if a staff member should be associated with three schools, it should look like this:

 

 To associate a staff member with all schools in the organization, enter school_id as district 

 

 

 
 

 Quote Surrounded Fields

 If a value for a field contains a comma or quotes, it will skew the CSV and result in one or more records not processing. To resolve the issue, export the CSV with quotes surrounding the fields. 

 For example: 

 
 Before 

 
 After 

 
 Ford, Tom "Ford, Tom" 
 Thomas "Tom" Ford "Thomas "Tom" Ford" 
 Math, 2nd Period "Math, 2nd Period" 
 123 Baker Street, Apt. 102 "123 Baker Street, Apt. 102" 
 
 Many SISs will have the option to surround field values in quotes upon export. For example, in PowerSchool's Data Export Manager, it is located under Export Options: 

 For information on how to surround field values in quotes upon export, please contact your SIS directly. 

 Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets If manually constructing your file in Excel or Google Sheets, they do not hav

title: SFTP Syncs: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Manual sync overview 3. Review SFTP instructions 3. Export data 4. Format files 5. Upload data 6. Update your data 7. How often can you sync your data? 

 Overview

 In this article, we’ll review data upload options for when automated syncs aren’t available. The secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) sync allows school organizations to “push” data to Clever. School organizations generate five required CSV files ( schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv, and enrollments.csv ) from their data management system (such as a student information system (SIS) or management information system (MIS)) or manually create the files in accordance with Clever's specified file format . These files include data fields for each of the required five data types.

 You may include the optional staff.csv file for your non-instructional staff , but it isn't required.

 
 Manual sync overview

 As mentioned above, Clever requires five different data files ( schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv, and enrollments.csv ). Each file has a job/function:

 
 File

 
 Description

 
 schools.csv Creates school profiles. Even if you are a single school organization, your one school must be listed in this file. 
 students.csv Creates student profiles. 
 teachers.csv Creates teacher profiles. 
 sections.csv Creates sections, i.e., classes and associates each one with a teacher 
 enrollments.csv Creates student enrollment associations to sections from the sections.csv 
 staff.csv (optional) Creates non-teaching staff profiles. Examples include principals, counselors, headmaster, etc. 
 
 These files cascade and depend on one another. If there are no schools, then Clever cannot process students or teachers. If there are no students, Clever cannot process enrollments. If there are no enrollments, then Clever cannot process sections. The graphic below shows how the files work together: 

 
 Review SFTP instructions

 Review Clever’s SFTP Instructions to learn how to format your CSV files and to identify which fields are required by Clever and any learning applications you'll use in Clever. Please note that fields in bold are required by Clever, and fields highlighted in yellow must remain consistent across all CSV files. The templates for these CSV files are available here . 

 If you’ve already signed up for a Clever account, select your sync type on the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. If you'll be using an SFTP sync, you'll find the Clever SFTP Specifications specific to your school organization, which include fields required by your connected applications.

 Not yet signed up ? Take a look at our general SFTP instructions here . If you have questions about the instructions, please request help before proceeding by clicking the chat icon in the bottom right corner of this page.

 

 
 Export data

 • Export your data from your data management system such as a SIS or MIS. Be sure to export all required fields.
• Data can be exported directly from your data management system or through an SQL query.
• If you’re unable to produce the required fields, please contact your SIS or MIS team directly. Clever is unable to troubleshoot data exporting from your system and SQL for SFTP Syncs.
• We also recommend automating the process of exporting data from your SIS and uploading it to Clever.
 
 Please note: If you do not utilize a data management system such as a SIS or MIS, you can manually create the required CSV files for syncing data to Clever in a program such as Excel or Google Sheets. 

 

 

 
 Format files

 • Format the five required CSV files ( schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv, and enrollments.csv ) according to Clever’s specified file format . If you're including the optional staff.csv file, make sure to format that as well.
• If you run into any issues, see Troubleshooting CSV formatting errors for help!
 
 

 Clever Tip: Making your files yourself? Check out our tutorial videos for each file type: Schools.csv , students.csv , teachers.csv , sections.csv , enrollments.csv. 

 

 
 Clever Tip: Using a MIS to export data files? Click this drop-down to preview commonly used MIS fields and how they map to Clever fields. 
 
 Schools 

 
 Clever

 
 MIS Field

 
 School_ID (must be a unique value) DfE number 
 School_Name Name 
 School_Number DfE number 
 
 

 Students 

 
 Clever

 
 MIS field

 
 School_ID DfE number 
 Student_ID (value must be unique) 
 UPN Number

 MIS Generated ID #

 
 First_Name Forename 
 Last_Name Surname 
 Middle_Name Middle Name 
 Grade Year group 
 
 

 Teachers 

 
 Clever

 
 MIS Field

 
 School_ID DfE Number 
 Teacher_ID 
 MIS generated #

 TeacherNumber

 
 First_Name Forename 
 Last_Name Surname 
 Middle_Name Middle Name 
 Title Title/role 
 
 

 Sections 

 
 Clever

 
 MIS Field

 
 School_ID DfE Number 
 Section_ID Registration Group Number 
 Teacher_ID MIS generated #
 TeacherNumber

 
 Teacher_2_ID, Teacher_3_ID, etc. 
 MIS generated #

 TeacherNumber

 
 Name Clever-generated: course_name - teacher_last_name - period 
 Grade Year Group 
 
 
 
 Upload data

 • Securely transfer the data to Clever using one of these two options:
 • Admins can upload files manually via the Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page. Need help using the Web Upload tool? See this article .
• Or, admins can upload data directly to the Clever SFTP folder using an FTP client (e.g., Transmit, FileZilla, or WinSCP). Click here for instructions on uploading data using an FTP client, or check out our clickable tutorial . You’ll need your SFTP credentials from your Clever Dashboard > Sync > Settings page. This SFTP option also allows districts to write a script that automates the upload process.
 

 
 Onboarding to Clever and want to learn about uploading CSV files? Check out our Onboarding - Getting your schools’ data into Clever course, available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Update your data

 • Repeat the steps above to update the data in Clever when student, teacher, or roster information changes.
• Be sure to include all five files in your upload, and include ALL records for each file type. Your files should always contain all data. Clever will handle necessary additions, deletions, and updates.
• You can always check your sync status on your Clever Dashboard homepage - here are some common error messages .
 
 

 
 

 How often can you sync your data?

 • You can sync as often as you want! However, if your sync has been automated it may be set to sync once a day.
• When a new set of files is sent to your SFTP directory we will automatically trigger a sync. Uploading too many times in a short period of time may trigger a sync upload error. 
 
 
 Related articles: 

 • SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever 
• SFTP & Manual Upload: How do I format my CSV files? 
• SFTP Syncs: SFTP Sync FAQ

title: For Clever Admins: iOS devices & the Clever app
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I add the Clever iOS app? 3. Expected Login Behavior 4. What happens when a user selects a learning application? 5. Best practices for using multiple apps in one session 6. Best practices for logging out of Clever 7. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 Students can log in to Clever on iPads using the Clever Single sign-on (SSO) application. The application allows students to access the Clever Portal and their Teacher Pages directly in the application, rather than launching Clever in a web browser. This article outlines the process of adding the app and its functionality for students.

 

 Please note: The Clever iOS application doesn't support teacher, staff, or administrator logins. These users should access Clever via a web browser on their iOS device. 

 

 
 How do I add the Clever iOS app?

 The Clever application is available for free via the Apple App Store . You can add the application to your devices manually, or use the Apple School Manager (ASM) and your MDM solution to find the Clever application and install it wirelessly to your iOS devices. Please see more information below on updating versions.

 The Clever iOS app requires iOS 16.6 or later and is compatible with iPhones and iPads.

 If you are using older devices that don’t support iOS 16.6+, your users can still access Clever via a web browser on their iOS device by opening the browser and navigating to your school organization’s Portal URL (found on your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page ). From the browser, students can continue to log in to their Portal with their Badge or login credentials.

 

 
 Expected Login Behavior

 Students logging in with Badges 

 • 
 Please be sure your devices have the Clever iOS app from the Apple App Store.

• 
 Users will click on the Clever app to open it.

• 
 The first screen students will see is the Clever Badge camera.

• 
 Students have the ability to toggle between the front-facing and back-facing cameras.

• 
 Students will need to hold their badge up to the camera on the mobile device.

• 
 If the student is using a valid badge, they will see a green checkmark with sparkles signifying a successful login!

 • 
 If the student sees a badging error message, they may need to reach out to their teacher or a Clever Admin for assistance. 

 

 
 • 
 The student will then be logged into the Clever application and their Clever Portal. They can access all features of their Clever Portal, including their Teacher Pages, directly in the Clever app.

• 
 Please see our section below on what happens when a user selects a learning application to learn more about the user experience beyond logging in.

 
 You can preview the student experience in our videos below:

 

 

 An example of what the login could look like for students using a Badge and Classroom MFA pictures.

 

 

 
 

 Logging in with credentials 

 • 
 Please be sure your devices have the Clever iOS app from the Apple App Store .

• 
 When opening the Clever app, users will see the Badge Camera.

• 
 They’ll select the “Password” tab at the bottom of the screen.

• 
 The user will locate their school from the Clever login page.

• 
 They’ll then select the method they want to use to login (ex: Login with Google or Login with Clever).

• 
 The user will type their credentials and then be logged into the Clever application and their Clever Portal. They can access all features of their Clever Portal, including their Teacher Pages, directly in the Clever app.

• 
 Please see our section below on what happens when a user selects a learning application to learn more about the user experience beyond logging in.

 
 

 

 Please note: The Clever app will remember the user's school organization after their first login on the device, so this user won't need to go through the process of selecting their school again if the Safari browser and/or Clever app were never fully closed, and device history has not been deleted. 

 

 
 What happens when a user selects a learning application?

 After users sign into the Clever app and they select an application, they'll be automatically authenticated into that learning application either in your device’s default web browser or the app’s standalone iOS application.

 The login functionality is determined by application partners on a per-app basis.

 

 
 Click here for a list of apps that currently redirect to their standalone iOS app

 Please note: Some applications don't provide mobile support, which means that you may not be able to access them via your phone or tablet. If you're unsure, please reach out to the application to confirm. 

 

 
 Best practices for using multiple apps in one session

 We aim to connect students, teachers, and staff to as many of their resources as possible. After a user is logged in to Clever: 

 • 
 They can click on one of their connected applications.

• 
 This will open the application or external link either in the device’s default web browser or the iOS application.

• 
 If a user wants to go back to their Clever Portal, they should go back to the Clever application. From here they can launch an additional application, or access the same app again.

 
 
 

 
 Best practices for logging out of Clever

 Whether using a shared device or not, the best practice is to always log out of your school organization's Identity Provider (IDP) in addition to Clever when you are finished. To do this, follow these steps: 

 Step 1: Log out of the Clever application.

 • From the Clever Portal, click on your name in the top right corner, and select Log out.
 
 Step 2: Log out of any learning applications and your school organization's Identity Provider (IdP) on your web browser.

 • For example: Google, Active Directory, LDAP, Canvas, Clever Passwords
 
 

 For information regarding best practices on shared devices, please see this Help Center article: For Families: Logging your students out of Clever on a shared device. 

 

 If you have any additional questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Will the Clever application log students out after any period of time? 

 • Clev

title: For Clever Admins: Updating your Support Contact
text:
Updating your Tech Support Contact 

 To abide by Clever's stringent security and data policies , Clever Support will never provide user login credentials through any of our communications channels if a district user reaches out to us directly. 

 For this reason, each district should have a Tech Support contact for users to contact in the event of Clever login issues. This information can be updated by going to the Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings. 

 

 The Tech Support contact information will be displayed when users select Get help > Support Contact from your district login page.

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Setup & Configuration
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Portal Configuration 3. Identity Providers (IDPs) 4. Customize login buttons 5. Updating your Tech Support Contact 6. Rolling out Single sign-on (SSO) to your district 

 

 Getting started with SSO? Check out our guide, Onboarding - Setting up your schools' Clever login method , available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 ​Clever Single Sign-On (SSO) enables users to be securely authenticated into connected applications via their Clever Portals. Users log in to Clever just once with one set of credentials, or using a Clever Badge , and then click on the various app icons to be logged in automatically via SSO. Clever's application partners rely on Clever's verification system of determining that a user is indeed who they say they are. Without Clever SSO, each application would need to maintain its own database of users and their credentials. 

 Clever Admins can set up Clever SSO from their Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. This article details the four main steps required to enable Clever SSO.

 

 
 Portal Configuration

 

 The first step is to set a custom Portal URL for your district, which your students and teachers will use to access Clever and their connected applications. To set a custom Portal URL for your district, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page. The Portal URL will be ">www.clever.com/in/<shortname> ; . Remember to use something that your students and teachers will remember easily. To change your district's Portal URL, click the URL text box to edit the short name. Make sure to click Save . For more resources on managing the Portal, click here. 

 

 
 Identity Providers (IdPs)

 Clever SSO allows students and teachers to access education apps with the username and password they already use in your district. To add a login method for Clever, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings . In the top right corner, click Add Login Method and select the login method you wish to add for Clever.

 Please visit the articles listed below for specific details about configuring Clever SSO for your district's Identity Provider (e.g. Google, Active Directory, Canvas, LDAP, etc.) 

 • Clever Passwords 
• Google 
• Active Directory (LDAPS) 
• Active Directory (SAML) 
• Entra ID / Office 365 
• Canvas 
• NCEdCloud 
• Lenovo Stoneware / webNetwork 
 
 ​ 

 

 
 

 Customize login buttons

 The Clever login page can be further customized by:

 • Editing the login method text (e.g. 'Log in with Google' can be updated to 'Log in as Teacher')
• Editing the login method order
 
 

 If you’ve set up SAML authentication for your school organization, you can update the login button label from your Clever Dashboard using the steps below:

 • Go to Authentication > SSO Settings > Login Method 
• Under your SAML Authentication, click Edit Settings 
• Under Step 3: Select your Identity Provider , choose your IdP from the dropdown
• Click Save 
 
 

 

 
 

 Updating your Tech Support Contact

 To abide by Clever's stringent security and data policies , Clever Support will never provide user login credentials through any of our communications channels if a district user reaches out to us directly. 

 For this reason, each district should have a Tech Support contact for users to contact in the event of Clever login issues. This information can be updated from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. 

 

 The Tech Support contact information will be displayed on your district's Clever login page. 

 

 

 

 
 

 Rolling out single sign-on (SSO)

 Clever has provided some helpful resources for when your district is ready to roll out Clever single sign-on (SSO) at your district: 

 Distributing login information to teachers 

 Once you've configured your single sign-on (SSO) settings, we recommend sending an email letting your teachers and staff know how to log in to Clever to access their connected applications. 

 Clever provides a downloadable PDF with login instructions (1) and an email template (2) for each connected application so that district admins can easily share login information with teachers. 

 

 To locate these resources, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page and click on the name of the specific application to open the app overview page. 

 The Login Instructions PDFs include:

 • Your district's Portal URL
• Basic instructions for how to log in to Clever
• Instructions on how to access the application from their Clever Portals.
 
 

 The email contains:

 • Your district's Portal URL
• Your district's Tech Support contact
• Basic information about the app
• A link to a video providing an overview of Clever SSO
 
 If you select V iew Email , you'll see a preview of the email that will be sent to your teachers letting them know they now have access to that particular application. Select Send to send the email to teachers.

 Clever Tip: The Troubleshoot login tool can help you identify and diagnose common login issues for your users. 

 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Identity Provider (IdP)/Login Method Options
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Identity Provider Options 3. Multiple Identity Providers 4. Resetting Passwords 

 

 Overview 

 Clever Single sign-on (SSO) allows your district's students, teachers, and staff to log in to Clever and access their connected applications. Once district users are logged into Clever, they simply click on an application icon in their Portal and are logged in to the learning app automatically via SSO. 

 The first step in setting up SSO in Clever is to select your district's Identity Provider (IdP). If your district already uses one of the Identity Providers listed below, we encourage you to use that one for Clever SSO. If your district prefers to use an alternative SSO method, check out Clever Passwords ! 

 Once you have determined which IdP to use as your Clever SSO method, you will navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings to complete the setup. Click here for a list of helpful articles for setting up and managing Clever SSO for each IdP. 

 Clever Tip: If you need help determining which IDP to use, or setting up Clever SSO, please contact the Clever Support team for help! 

 

 

 
 Identity Provider Options 

 Below is a list of Clever-supported Identity Providers (IDPs). Click on the hyperlinked name of an IDP to learn more about the process and setup: 

 
 IdP 

 
 Requirements 

 
 Things to Know 

 
 
 Active Directory / ADFS 

 
 • Active Directory Federation Services 2.x or 3.x 
• Metadata file accessible over HTTPS with a certificate signed by a valid Certificate Authority 
• Can create a custom SAML 
 
 
 • Requires manual Claims Rules setup 
 
 
 
 Canvas 

 
 • Must already be using Canvas LMS and able to provide the URL for your school or district’s Canvas instance 
 
 
 • Requires some manual testing to ensure correct setup. 
• Does NOT require the Canvas app integration with Clever 
 
 
 
 Clever Badges 

 
 • Compatible with iOS devices, Chromebooks or Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge or Internet Explorer > 2013 or later
• Computers/Devices with webcams
• Printer needed
 
 
 • Available for students only
• Students log in with a physical Badge
• Teachers, District Administrators and School Tech Leads * can print student Badges.
 
 * For their school(s) only 

 
 
 Clever Passwords 

 
 • Users can reset their own passwords (optional) 
• Teachers can reset student passwords (optional) 
• District Administrators can reset student & teacher passwords 
 
 
 
 Google 

 
 • Users must have their district-provided (Google) email addresses in Clever 
 
 
 
 LDAPS 

 
 • LDAP Server 
• Allowlist Clever’s external IPs 
• Credentials for a BIND account 
 
 
 • Requires some manual testing to ensure correct setup 
• Works best with the Clever Portal , as LDAP does not allow for persistent sessions 
• Do NOT send BIND account credentials (or any other credentials) over email 
 
 
 
 Office 365 / Entra ID 

 
 • Options : 
 • Entra ID Premium 
• Entra ID and PowerShell Proficiency 
 

 
 
 • Requires manual Claims Rules setup 
 
 
 
 Lenovo Stoneware / webNetwork 

 
 • webNetwork 6.3.0167+ 
 
 
 • Works best with Single sign-on (SSO) links embedded in Stoneware portal 
 
 
 
 Rapid Identity 

 
 • Users must have their district-provided email addresses in Clever 
 
 
 • Requires setup in Rapid Identity per these instructions 
 
 
 
 

 
 Multiple Identity Providers 

 Districts can set up Clever Single sign-on (SSO) to allow for multiple identity providers. However, o nly one SAML Connection (ADFS, Entra ID, Stoneware, etc) can be active at a time. 

 

 
 

 Resetting Passwords

 You can allow students, teachers, and staff to reset passwords to recover their Clever accounts for destinations (such as Microsoft Active Directory, Microsoft Entra, or Google Workspace) that you have set up in Clever IDM . To learn more about how to reset passwords, please see Clever Admin: Account recovery and password reset. 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): What is SSO?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does SSO authentication work? 3. Single sign-on (SSO) Requirements 4. Additional SSO options 

 Overview

 Clever single sign-on (SSO) enables users to securely authenticate into connected applications via Clever Portal. Users log into Clever just once with one set of credentials, or using a Clever Badge , and then click on the various app icons to be logged in automatically via SSO. Clever application partners rely on Clever's verification system of determining that a user is, indeed, who they say they are. Without Clever SSO, each application would need to maintain its own database of users and their credentials. 

 
 How does SSO authentication work?

 • A user logs in to Clever via the district's Identity Provider (IDP) (e.g. Active Directory, Google, etc.) from the Clever login page.
• Clever authenticates the user through the district's IDP.
• If the user is verified, Clever creates a "token" (i.e. a piece of data) that identifies the user and then logs them in successfully.
• The user then clicks on the appropriate application icon to open an application, and Clever provides the user's token to the application which authenticates the user. This means the user does not need to enter login credentials a second time. 
• The user is logged into the application successfully!
 
 Clever Tip: The Clever Portal also supports universal single sign-on for non-integrated Clever applications via SSO (Saved Passwords) authentication. Read more about this here ! 

 
 Who can use single sign-on (SSO)?

 The following users can access applications through Clever single sign-on (SSO): 

 • Students 
• Teachers 
• Staff 
• District admins 
 
 Please note that each individual learning application determines which of the user types listed above they support. Some apps only support two or three user types. 

 To find out which user types are supported by an application, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page, and click on the application name to open the app overview page. If a user type is not listed under SSO Supported User Types , users of that type won't be able to access the application through Clever. 

 

 
 Single sign-on (SSO) Requirements 

 There are certain requirements that should be met in order to ensure a seamless Clever single sign-on (SSO) experience for users:

 1. Users must have a record in Clever (via Secure Sync) 

 • In order for users to be able to log in, they must have an existing record in Clever. For more information on syncing rostering data to Clever, please see What is Secure Sync? 
 
 Please note: For best results, make sure that there is only one record for each user in Clever. If there are multiple records in Clever for the same user, users may not be able to log in. The only caveats to this are teachers who are also considered staff and have both a teacher and staff account in Clever , or district admins who are also teachers or staff. 

 2. District Portal URL 

 • Each district has the ability to customize a unique Clever login page URL to share with district users. This URL is a direct way for users to log in rather than navigating to Clever.com and selecting their school. Custom URLs are in the format of https://clever.com/in/<YOUR CUSTOM SHORTNAME> (e.g. https://clever.com/in/northernschooldistrict) 
• Your district's Portal URL can be customized from the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page in Clever. Visit this article for additional instructions.
 
 

 3. Configure your Clever SSO login method (Identity Provider) 

 • For help determining which Identity Provider (IdP) your district should use and how to set it up, please see these articles: 
 • Single Sign-On (SSO): Identity Provider (IdP) Options 
• Single Sign-On (SSO): Setup & Configuration 
 

 
 4. Tech Support Contact 

 • To abide by Clever's stringent security and data policies , Clever support will never provide user login credentials through any of our communications channels if a district user reaches out to us direct. 
• For this reason, each district should have a Tech Support contact for users to contact in case of Clever login issues. This information can be updated from your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. 
 
 

 • The Tech Support contact information will be displayed in your district's 'Get help logging in' page. Please visit these articles for help troubleshooting user login issues.
 
 

 
 Additional SSO options 

 Allowing users to access Clever applications via your existing district portal, website or Learning Management System (LMS) 

 • Single sign-on (SSO) is designed to work with your existing district portal or website. You can allow users to authenticate into applications without using the Clever Portal by embedding single sign-on URLs (i.e. Instant Login Links) into your portal, website, or Learning Management System (LMS). 
• For information on Instant Login Links, please visit: Single Sign-On (SSO): Instant Login links 
 
 Using the 'Log in with Clever' option 

 • Some applications provide a ‘ Log in with Clever’ option from their website login pages and mobile apps. This option allows your users to log in to the application from the app website using their Clever credentials! 
 
 

 If you have any questions about Clever SSO, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever SSO Policy

title: Temporarily disable Clever logins using Access Control
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up access control 2. Updating access control 3. User experience 4. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 Access control allows district admins to determine when students, teachers, and staff can access the Clever Portal and apps via Clever. Access control is intended as a temporary measure to limit potential student, teacher, or staff access to course-related information during sensitive time periods. When logging in to the Clever Portal or some connected applications, students, teachers, and staff are able to infer some information about which students and teachers are assigned to which classes (such as if they log into a reading app via Clever and see that they’re listed under 5th Period ELA with Ms. Thompson). By using access control, district admins can prevent students, teachers, and staff from viewing course-related information in Clever Portal or their connected applications by disabling login access. Note that disabling Access Control for students also restricts Badges into Chromebooks even if a student is logging into the Chromebook for a purpose unrelated to the Clever Portal.

 Please note: Users may still be able to log into third-party applications if the applications support login methods other than Clever. 

 

 
 Setting up access control

 You can set up access control by following these steps:

 • Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page.
• Go to the Access control tab and click Edit . 
• Select the user type(s) you wish to disable access for.
• Select the Start and End Dates for disabling access. 
 
 

 
 

 Updating access control

 You can return to the access control options to edit Start and End Dates at any time. You can also immediately restore access by clicking Enabled next to the relevant user type. 

 
 

 User experience 

 Any user who tries to log into Clever or an application via Clever while their access is disabled will see a Clever login error that tells them their login method is not enabled and that they'll need to reach out to a Clever admin if they need access. 

 
 

 FAQs

 What type of information can users infer? 

 • Students
 • Teachers by viewing Teacher Pages and apps they will use in their upcoming courses via Clever
• Teachers and classmates in some connected apps
 

• Teachers
 • Class and student associations from viewing sections and apps via Clever
• Class and student associations from viewing sections in connected apps
 

• Staff
 • Duties based on available apps
 

 
 Are there other options for hiding schedules from students, without blocking access to Clever? 

 You can also hide Teacher Pages for up to 90 days. This will prevent students seeing Teacher Pages for their new teachers, but they may still be able to determine classmates or teachers via learning applications.

 Should we enable Access Control? 

 • You can see what students, teachers, and staff see in their Portals using the Access Portal as user feature. Decide based on what a typical Portal looks like whether you want to disable login access using access control.
 
 

 

 Will this prevent students from accessing applications via login methods other than Clever? 

 • Access control will only block your students from logging into the Clever Portal and using Clever SSO to log into other applications (e.g. through Log in with Clever buttons). Access Control cannot prevent users from logging into applications via other methods.
• If you want to block non-Clever login pathways to an application, we recommend reaching out to that application to discuss options with them directly. 
 
 How is this different from the hiding applications feature? 

 • Clever admin have the ability to hide applications and links in Clever. There are differences between hiding apps and access control:
 • The hide feature:
 • Only applies to individual applications and links – it can’t be applied to multiple resources at the same time. 
• Doesn't control users’ access to the Clever Portal. Users can still log in to the Clever Portal and see various features like Teacher Pages and Messaging.
 

• Access control:
 • Allows administrators to disable access to all Clever applications at once.
• Prevents selected user types from logging into the Clever Portal. 
 

 

 
 When exactly will users be able to start logging in on disable end date? 

 • Users will be able to start logging in at midnight local time on the selected disable end date. 
 
 What should I do if I want to enable access before my scheduled end date? 

 • From your Clever Dashboard, navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Access control tab , select Edit , and change the disable end date to whatever new date you want to use. If you select the current day, users will gain access immediately.

title: Clever Portal: Hiding applications and disabling logins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Hiding apps vs disabling logins 3. Hiding SSO or SSO (Saved Password) applications 4. How Clever Admins and School Tech Leads hide resources in the Clever Portal 5. How do hidden applications appear in Clever Portal 

 Overview

 This article is an overview of the various methods for preventing logins and hiding applications in Clever. A district may wish to hide applications in a Clever Portal for many reasons, from scheduling an application launch to hiding resources during testing periods. Clever's visibility settings allow districts to manage application access with ease. 

 
 Hiding apps vs disabling logins

 There are several options for Clever Admins and teachers to hide or limit access to applications through Clever. 

 If you want to:

 • Allow logins to Clever and the application, but hide the application in the Portal, you'll want to use settings for Hiding in Portal. 
• Allow logins to Clever, but disable logins to the application and hide it in the Portal, use App Visibility .
• Prevent logins to Clever and to applications via Clever using Access Control. 
 
 To better understand this, we have created a table that provides insight into these options.

 Please note that n one of the features listed in the table below will block direct access to apps rostered through Clever (e.g. users can still log into the app at the app's website outside of Clever if the application supports this). 

 

 
 Feature

 
 Access Control

 

 
 App Visibility Settings

 
 Hiding in Portal

 
 Teacher Page Category Hiding

 
 
 About the feature 

 
 Clever Admins can temporarily block Clever Portal access so that users can’t access Clever and their applications through Clever. 

 
 Clever Admins can hide specific applications in Clever and prevent users from using Log in with Clever (LIWC) buttons. The app will show up as disabled on Teacher Pages. 

 
 Clever Admins and School Tech Leads (if enabled) can hide specific apps and links on the District Page, but applications that are on Teacher Pages are still visible and can be used. 

 
 Teachers can enable this to hide categories and resources within the category on a Teacher Page. This doesn’t affect visibility outside of the specific category on that Teacher Page. 

 
 
 Who can configure 

 
 Clever Admins 

 
 Clever Admins 

 
 Clever Admins/School Tech Leads (STLs) 

 
 Teachers

 
 
 Staff/Teacher/Student Portal Experience 

 
 Specified user types can't log into Clever or use Login with Clever (LIWC) buttons 

 
 Specified user types can't log into Specific App or use LIWC buttons to access Specific App 

 
 Specified user types can't see the app in the Portal resource list or District Page (but can still see, use, and add on Teacher Pages) 

 
 Students will not be able to see the hidden category on a Teacher Page 

 
 
 Is the app visible in the Clever Portal? 

 
 No - there is no access to the Portal 

 
 No - the app isn't visible in Portal and shows up as disabled in Teacher Pages 

 
 Not in the resources list or District Page but is visible on Teacher Pages and the Clever Admin Portal 

 
 This feature doesn't affect visibility outside of the specific category on that Teacher Page 

 
 
 Does it block all Clever-Powered SSO (LIWC)? 

 
 Yes 

 
 Yes 

 
 No 

 
 No 

 
 
 Staff/Teacher/Student SSO Experience 

 
 See error above if users try to log into Clever or use the LIWC button 

 
 See error above if users try to log in using the LIWC button 

 
 Not visible in resource list or district page - LIWC works to log in 

 
 Category and category's resources not visible for students 

 
 
 Can it be set for specific dates? 

 
 Yes 

 
 Yes 

 
 Yes 

 
 No 

 
 
 Which users can this be applied to? 

 
 Staff, Teachers, Students - set by user type 

 
 Staff, Teachers, Students - set by user type 

 
 Staff, Teachers, Students - set by user type 

 
 Students 

 
 
 Does it hide the app everywhere? 

 
 Yes - no access to the Portal 

 
 Yes 

 
 No - hides the Portal link - apps on Teacher Pages will still be visible 

 
 No 

 
 
 Does it work for links? 

 
 Yes - no access to the Portal 

 
 No - only apps 

 
 Yes - can be used for apps or links 

 
 Yes - it hides all resources in that category while on that specific Teacher Page 

 
 
 Help Center Articles 

 
 Temporarily disable Clever logins using Access Control 

 
 Clever Portal: Hiding Applications Clever Portal: Disable logins to applications 

 
 Clever Portal: Hiding Applications 

 
 Clever Portal: Teacher Pages 

 
 
 Recommended Uses 

 
 Block user access during pre-school-year rollover to prevent early visibility of schedules/assignments 

 
 Block Clever-powered access to apps 

 
 • For links 
• Usable by STLs 
• When you want teachers to be able to add apps to Teacher Pages but don't want an app visible on general Portal 
 
 
 For teachers to hide resources they aren't currently using on their Teacher Pages 

 
 
 

 Hiding SSO or SSO (Saved Passwords) applications

 Step 1: Log in to the Clever Dashboard and navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > My applications and click the app you'd like to hide. This will disable the application for the selected users. Step 2: Go to the Settings tab and click Edit in the Application’s visibility box.

 

 Step 3: Select the user type you wish to hide the application from. Step 5 outlines hiding from multiple user types.

 

 Step 4: Select the Start and End Dates for hiding the application. If you aren’t sure when you’d like to unhide the application, you can leave that field blank to keep the application hidden until you set a date or unhide it.

 

 Step 5: To hide the application from an additional user type, just click Hide and set the dates for that user type. 

 

 

 How do I change when an application will be disabled? 

 You can return to the application visibility options to edit Start and End Dates at any time. You can also immediately unhide an application by clicking Visible next to the relevant user type.

 

 
 How Clever Admins and School Tech Leads hide resources in the Clever Portal

 Please note: Applications must be put into a category before they can be hidden. For more detailed instructions please read: How do I manage the Clever Portal? 

 

 Clever Admins, and if enabled, School Tech Leads, can hide applications directly via Portal settings. Hiding an application via Portal settings will hide the application in the District Resources section of the Portal, but if the teacher has favorited the app or added it to a Teacher Page, it won’t be hidden. Similarly, if a student or staff member has favorited the app, it won’t be hidden.

 When hiding an application in the Portal, you’ll also have the option to set different hidden dates per user type. For example, you can hide an application from teachers from June 20th to August 1st, but hide it from students June 20th to August 15th. This can be helpful when rolling out a new application.

 To hide an application:

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page. 
• 
 Locate the application you wish to hide and click the three vertical dots.

• 
 Select Hide/show in Portal. 

• 
 Select Hide temporarily .

• 
 Select all users (student, teachers, staff) you want to hide the application from for the same timeframe. If you want to hide the application for a separate user type for a different timeframe, you can use the Add another user type button after you configure the timeframe for your first user type.

• 
 Fill in the start and end dates for hiding.

• 
 Click Save. 

• 
 Be sure to select Publish changes in the top right corner of the Organize district portal page to apply the visibility settings.

 
 How do hidden applications appear in Clever Portal?

 When hiding is enabled, the application icon will display on the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page was grayed out to indicate it's hidden from us

title: For Clever Admins: Installing the Clever browser extension
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Browser Compatibility and Access 3. Clever browser extension installation & directions 4. Pushing out the Clever browser extension to all district users 5. Clicking the Clever browser extension 

 

 Overview

 The Clever browser extension is the key to ensuring that SSO (Saved Passwords) applications function properly because it facilitates the secure storing of user login credentials for these types of applications. In addition, the extension provides users with a seamless single sign-on (SSO) experience to these applications through Clever. If the Clever extension is not present on a device, the user will need to manually enter their login credentials when accessing SSO (Saved Passwords) applications through Clever. Single sign-on (SSO) is available for integrated applications in Clever without the browser extension. To find out which type of application an application is, Clever Admins can navigate to their list of applications from the Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page to locate the application and look under the App Type column. 

 For more about the different types of applications in Clever, please visit: For Clever Admins: Apps - Types of apps 

 

 
 

 Browser Compatibility and Access

 Clever supports the following internet browsers:

 • Chrome
• Firefox
• Microsoft Edge
• Apple Safari (we support this browser for general use with Clever, but not for SSO (Save Passwords) apps, as we do not currently offer a Safari Extension) 
 
 The Clever browser extension is not required for iOS devices (e.g., iPads, iPhones) as these devices use the Clever app, which acts as the browser extension. For details, visit: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: iOS Devices For devices where the browser extension cannot be downloaded (e.g., Android, Amazon Fire tablets, Safari), users will need to manually enter their login credentials for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications when accessing them through Clever. For additional details, visit: Clever Technical Requirements & Supported Devices. 

 It is expected that when a user clicks on the Chrome Extension, it will take them to clever.com /login. To streamline your login flow, Clever suggests directing your students and teachers to use your district’s portal URL which can be found in the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings. 

 

 Please note: If users are accessing SSO (Saved Passwords) applications from home due to remote learning, we recommend standardizing credentials for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications (e.g., student number as the required username and date of birth as the password). Doing this will make it easier for students and families to remember their login credentials when logging in to these types of applications. 

 

 
 Clever browser extension installation & directions

 Please see the links below for information and instructions for installing the Clever browser extension:

 • Google Chrome: Install | Directions 
• Microsoft Edge (New): Install | Directions 
• Firefox: Install | Directions 
 
 

 
 Pushing out the Clever browser extension to all district users 

 Please see the following articles for details on pushing out the Clever browser extension to all district users:

 • Chrome extension instructions 
• Microsoft Edge 
• Mozilla Firefox extension instructions 
 
 Clever Tip: If you are experiencing difficulty deploying or pushing out the Clever browser extension to your users, or need further instruction, please contact the browser provider's support team directly. 

 

 
 Clicking the Clever browser extension 

 The primary function of the Clever browser extension is to facilitate logins for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications.

 When the extension is clicked, the following actions will occur based on the user's browser state:

 • When already logged into the Portal in another tab, clicking the extension will take the user to their Portal
• When logged out, but using a browser where the user has logged into the Clever Portal before, clicking the extension will take the user to the district Clever Portal login URL
• When in an incognito window, new browser, or after clearing browser history, a click will take the user to the Clever school selection screen
 
 To streamline your login flow and provide a consistent experience, we suggest directing your students and teachers to use your district’s Portal URL, which can be found in the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page.

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever Technical Requirements & Supported Devices 
• SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: iOS Devices 
• For District Admins: Apps - Types of apps

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Portal - Adding External Links (Hyperlinks)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding external links 3. Deleting external links 

 Overview

 In addition to Clever applications, Clever Portal supports adding links to other digital resources that are used in the classroom. External links work just like bookmarks, providing one-click navigation to digital resources. You can customize external link visibility based on user type, grade level, and school sites.

 External links do not authenticate users into the linked website. If you would like to authenticate users into the website, you can request Clever build a SSO (Saved Passwords) application by visiting https://schools.clever.com/applications/request. 

 If you’d like to add links to any Google resources, please see this article . 

 
 Adding external links

 To add a link: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal .
• Click Add to the Portal. 
• From the dropdown menu select Add a link. 
• A modal will pop-up for adding the link. Select an icon or upload a custom icon (optimal icon size should be 200x200).
• Fill out the required fields for the link Title and URL.
• Select which user types, grades, and schools you would like to add the custom link for. Please note that if adding for selected schools, the link will only display for the user if the selected school matches the user's primary school association.
• Choose one or multiple categories to organize the link into.
• Click Add. 
• Be sure to also click Publish Changes i n the top right of the Organize district portal page to save your link. 
 
 

 

 
 Deleting external links

 To delete a link:

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal .
• Click the three vertical dots on the link you want to delete.
• Select Edit. 
• Select Remove link. 
 
 

 

 
 Related articles: Clever Portal: Customizing the Clever Portal

title: For Teachers: Overview of the Clever Portal
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 

 Overview

 Schools and districts use Clever as a repository for digital learning resources and applications, providing easy access for staff, teachers, and students. Teachers can add assignments, links, applications, and other resources to their Clever Portals and share them with students. This article highlights some of the main features of the Clever Portal for teachers while also providing links to associated articles that provide more detailed information about each. 

 

 

 Getting started with the Clever Portal? Check out our course Clever Academy for Teachers: Level 1 available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Features

 

 Teacher Homeroom 

 When first logging in to Clever, teachers are directed to their Clever Portal, which allows them to access their Teacher Pages, Announcements, Clever Library, student support tools, and more!

 

 T eacher Pages 

 Teachers can create multiple personalized Teacher Pages to house a variety of learning resources for themselves and their students. Once a teacher has designed their Teacher Page(s), and they've added applications and resources (e.g., learning applications, links to various websites, PDF files, etc.), teachers can share their page(s) with students. Teachers can design multiple Teacher Pages and can even create a different Teacher Page for every class or subject if desired. Teachers in the same school can also share their Teacher Pages with each other to view. Examples of the resources that can be added to Teacher Pages include:

 • District-wide applications 
• Various Clever Library applications 
• Links to online tools (e.g., Wikipedia.org , Dictionary.com )
• Homework or assignments
• Community websites (e.g., local public library)
• And more!
 
 Teachers can also create private Teacher Pages for accessing resources and applications that they do not wish to share with students. 

 

 For detailed information about Teacher Pages, please visit: 

 • Clever Portal: Teacher Pages 
 
 

 Clever Library 

 If your district has this feature enabled, the Clever Library provides a place for teachers to discover, research, and adopt new classroom resources. The resources in the Clever Library can be added to individual Teacher Pages for easy access by teachers and students. Teachers can browse a catalog of over 500 popular resources that they can then add to their Teacher Pages quickly and easily. The Clever Library is organized by grade level and content area and contains descriptions and screenshots of available resources. The search can also be filtered by grade, category, and more! 

 

 For detailed information about adding applications from the Clever Library, please visit: 

 • For Teachers: Library - adding applications 
 
 Teacher Announcements 

 Announcements are perfect for when teachers have a message to send to an entire class of students. Teachers can send announcements to all students enrolled in a single class or to all students in all classes. Students can then securely view their teacher's announcement directly from their Clever Portals.

 

 For detailed information about Announcements, please visit: 

 • For Teachers: Announcements 
 
 Teacher Analytics 

 With many schools and districts implementing distance learning for students, Clever's Teacher Analytics allows teachers to more closely monitor how their students are using Clever (e.g., logging in to clever, accessing associated applications, etc.). 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 For detailed information about Teacher Analytics, please visit: 

 • For Teachers: Analytics 
 
 Student Roster 

 From the Student Roster tab, teachers can verify a student's Clever login status, download class Badges for individual students or an entire class, create backup login codes for students who are unable to log in, log student out, troubleshoot logins, see student and guardian information, and in some cases, can reset student passwords. Please note that districts need to have Clever Badges and the reset password feature enabled for teachers to access these options. 

 

 

 For detailed information about features available from the Classes tab, please visit: 
 • For Teachers: Clever Badges 
• For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins 
 
 

 Related articles: 

 • Clever Portal: Teacher Pages 
• For Teachers: Library - adding applications 
• For Teachers: Analytics 
• For Teachers: Clever Badges 
• For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins

title: For Clever Admins: Managing Clever Portal
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Managing your school organization's Clever Portal • Adding & editing categories • Adding custom external links • Moving application icons • Adding a custom Portal icon 3. Previewing the Clever Portal 4. Why Clever updated the Portal management and SSO experience 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Getting started with Clever Portal? Check out our course Quick Start - Setting up your Portal available in Clever Academy! 

 Overview

 From an admin perspective, there are two primary components in Clever; the Clever Dashboard and the Clever Portal . 

 The Clever Dashboard is where you’ll manage your school organization’s Clever account, from setting up applications, to troubleshooting logins, to configuring tools like Classroom MFA.

 The Clever Portal is where you and your end users (teachers, students, and staff) will securely access applications. 

 In Winter 2025, Clever made updates to the Portal management experience to provide Clever Admins a more efficient way to organize the Portal for end users that also adheres to a least-privileged access model. Previously, admins had one spot to access their applications and organize the district Portal. Now, admins have their own Portal where they can find all of the digital resources scoped for Clever Admins, and in their Clever Dashboard they can organize the end user’s Portal experience to ensure that each individual can easily find and access just the tools they need. This approach ensures that managing a student’s resources doesn't inadvertently grant an admin unnecessary permissions or data exposure within those third-party apps.

 If you want to learn more about your Clever Dashboard, check out our article here . 

 If you’re interested in learning more about organizing your school’s Clever Portal, keep reading below! 

 
 Want to try out organizing your Clever Portal without making changes? Check out our interactive tutorial here! 

 

 
 Managing your school organization's Clever Portal

 Please note: Previously, Clever Admins both managed the district Portal and accessed applications through SSO directly from the Portal. With the new experience, district Portal management now occurs in the Clever Dashboard, while the Portal itself is reserved for SSO access only. Learn more about this change here. If you are missing application icons in your Portal due to missing SSO user scopes, and you’d like to re-add these links, please see here. 

 Adding & Editing Categories

 From your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page , Clever Admins can create custom categories to group applications, making it easier for users to locate the applications they need (i.e., Google apps, math applications, reading applications, and newly added applications). To create a new category:

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page.

• 
 Select the Add to the Portal button.

• 
 From the dropdown menu choose Add a category .

• 
 Click the pencil icon next to the New Category to name the category. 

• 
 After naming your category, either:

 • 
 Use the three vertical dots next to an existing resource to move it into the category

• 
 Drag and drop an existing resource into the new category or

• 
 Select the Add links button to add a new external link to the category. Each category must contain at least one resource for changes to save.

 

• 
 When you’re done adding your category and adding resources to it, click Publish changes. 

 
 To edit existing categories:

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page.

• 
 From here, you can edit category names by clicking the pencil, delete a category by clicking the trashcan icon, or rearrange categories using the drag & drop modal on the right side of your screen. You can also rearrange categories one at a time using the up and down arrows next to the trashcan icon.

• 
 Click the blue checkmark next to your category name to confirm it, then select Publish changes to complete any changes you’ve made.

 
 Please note: When setting up sharing rules for any application not assigned to a category (in the default More Apps section), you’ll receive a prompt asking if you’d like to add the application to a category. For a category to appear in this dropdown, you must first create it via your Organize district portal page . 

 Adding custom external links

 Custom external links are URLs that you can add to your Clever Portal for end users to easily locate. This may include resources like a school website or calendar, as well as links to other resources or applications. If a login is required, external links don’t automatically log users in to the resource. To add a custom external link:

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal .

• 
 Click Add to the Portal. 

• 
 From the dropdown menu select Add a link. 

• 
 A modal will pop-up for adding the link. Select an icon or upload a custom icon (Note: Optimal icon size should be 200x200)

• 
 Fill out the required fields for the link Title and URL.

• 
 Select which user types (audience), grades, and schools you would like to add the custom link for. Please note that if adding for selected schools, the link will only display for the user if the selected school matches the user's primary school association.

• 
 Choose one or multiple categories to organize the link into.

• 
 Click Add. 

• 
 Be sure to click Publish Changes on the top right of the page to finalize your updates.

 
 To delete a link: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal .
• Click the three vertical dots on the link you want to delete.
• Select Edit. 
• Select Remove link. 
 
 Moving and duplicating application icons

 To help organize your district's resources to make it easy for students and teachers to find what they need, you can move or duplicate application and resource icons to different categories in your Clever Portal. To do so, follow the steps below:

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page.

• 
 Click the three vertical dots in the top right corner of the icon.

• 
 Select Move to new category from the dropdown, and select the category that you’d like to move the application to. If you’d like to include the resource in multiple categories, select Copy resource and then move the duplicated resource to the new category. You can also drag and drop resources to organize them if you prefer.

• 
 Select Publish Changes to the top right of the page once you are finished making updates.

 
 Clever Tip: If you are experiencing issues dragging and dropping the application icons, please try adjusting your browser zoom settings to a smaller size and try again! 

 Adding a custom Portal icon

 Clever admins can upload a custom icon for your school organization's Clever Portal. Having a custom icon helps users quickly identify that they are logging into the correct Portal. Click here to see where the icon appears on the Clever login page. You can set your icon by going to Clever Dashboard > Portal settings > Customization tab . These are the required image specs:

 • Upload your icon in .png format only
• Maximum image size is 200px by 200px
• Can be color or black & white
• Should be high-quality, non-blurry, or distorted
• For best results, upload a square image
 
 Once you have uploaded the icon, click Update . Now users will be able to view the icon on your school organization's login page.

 

 Please note: Since School Tech Leads are staff users with a school association, they have the unique ability to organize the Portal of a specific school. Clever Admins can only edit the Portal for all of their schools, i.e., the district Portal. If you are a Clever Admin that would like to edit individual school Portals, you can add yourself to Clever as a STL. You can have both Admin and STL accounts in Clever. Learn more

title: For Clever Admins: Updating the Clever login URL for your district
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Instructions 

 Overview

 Every Clever district has the ability to customize a unique Clever login URL to share with district users in order to simplify the Clever login process. This URL is a direct way for users to log in to your district's Clever portal rather than navigating to Clever.com to locate and select their school from a drop-down menu. 

 Custom Clever Portal URLs are in the format of: https://clever.com/in/ <YOUR CUSTOM SHORTNAME> 

 • For example: Northern School District might use https://clever.com/in/northernschooldistrict 
 
 Instructions

 Please note: If you are changing your Portal URL, Clever will not redirect users from the old URL to the new one. Please be sure to communicate accordingly with students, teachers, & staff if changing the URL shortname so they can log in successfully with the new one. 

 To update your district's Clever Portal URL, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page:

 • Click on the URL shortname in the 'Clever Portal URL' box to edit it. The shortname is the part after https://clever.com/in/ .
• Update the editable text to the new shortname you'd like to use.
 • For example : Updating the shortname to NorthernSchoolDistrict would change your Portal URL to: https://clever.com/in/northernschooldistrict 
 

• Select 'Update' to save your changes.
• Share the updated Portal URL with your district users so they can navigate directly to your district's Clever login page!
 
 

 If you have any questions about customizing your district's Clever login Portal URL, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Clever Portal: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What's the Clever Portal? 3. How do students, teachers, and staff access Clever Portal? 

 

 Overview

 This article provides a brief overview of Clever Portal and how users access it. For detailed information on how to customize and use Clever Portal, please reference the Related articles section below. 

 
 What's the Clever Portal?

 Clever Portal gives students, teachers, and staff a single, secure login for all digital resources. Each student, teacher, and staff member can access a personalized Portal using a single set of login credentials, or for students, a Clever Badge , to single sign-on (SSO) into every learning application they use— even those that don't yet roster with Clever. 

 Clever Portal: 

 

 • Is intentionally designed for teachers: Clever Portal is intuitive for teachers to navigate and quickly access the edtech the district has already purchased. 
• Works everywhere: One username and password unlock any connection application or device. Clever offers SSO for thousands of resources, while personalized portals show only what’s relevant for each user. 
• Provides personalized, secure access: Clever uses data directly from your student information system (SIS) to deliver a customized experience for each user, eliminating the need for extra setup steps. Students easily find resources and immerse themselves in the learning process. 
• Is available in multiple languages and locales: Admins and teachers can configure language settings for their Clever Portal to set a preferred language. Languages currently available include Spanish, British English, and Australian English. 
 
 

 

 
 How do students, teachers, and staff access Clever Portal?

 Students, teachers, and staff can access their Clever Portal by:

 

 • Navigating to your district’s unique Portal URL.
• Typing in their district username and password, or using their Badge . 
• Users will be logged into their Clever Portal and can access all their resources with a single click - no additional logins are required.
 
 You configure user's credentials for the Portal during SSO setup. Click here to learn how to set up SSO. 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Managing the Clever Portal 
• For Clever Admins: Configuring languages in Clever 
• For Clever Admins: Updating the Clever login URL for your school organization 
• For Clever Admins: Apps - Add/request or remove apps 
• SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Overview & Setup

title: Custom Data: Teachers
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Manually creating custom teacher 3. Bulk uploading custom teachers 4. Viewing custom teachers 5. Deleting custom teachers 6. Logging in as a custom teacher 

 Overview

 If you would like to add teachers to Clever that don't currently sync from your district's SIS, you can create custom teacher accounts for them in Clever on an individual basis or by bulk upload. Custom teachers can be added to existing sections using the Add co-teacher feature in Clever or can be added to custom sections . Custom teachers can only be added as the primary teacher to custom sections and as co-teachers to SIS-synced sections .
