 Please note: Please allow up to 24 hours for your custom teachers to sync with connected applications. If you are not seeing your district's custom teacher accounts appear in applications they are shared with, you can reach out to the application's support team directly to confirm whether they support co-teachers on their platform. 

 

 
 Manually creating a custom teacher

 If you'd like to add a teacher to Clever that is not being synced from your district's student information system (SIS), you can create them as a custom teacher directly from your Clever Dashboard using one of the following methods:

 1) From a section's profile page: 

 • Navigate to a section's profile page by searching for the section using the search bar at the top of the Clever Dashboard .
• Under the Details tab of your section, select Add co-teacher from the right side of the page.
• Select Create a teacher. 
• Then, enter basic demographic information about the user in the subsequent pop-up box to create their custom teacher account.
 
 2) From the Custom Data page.

 • Navigate to Data sources > Custom data > Add custom data > Teachers. 
• Enter basic demographic information about the user in the subsequent pop-up box to create their custom teacher account.
 
 

 

 
 Bulk uploading custom teachers via a CSV upload

 Formatting the file

 The file must be named teachers.csv and saved in the CSV (Comma Separated Value) file format. The minimum header requirements are the following:

 • school_id
• teacher_id
• last_name
• first_name
• teacher_email
 
 Please see our spec section 3.3 for more detailed information on formatting the file, the field values that are accepted, and information about additional headers. Sample files can be downloaded here .

 Uploading the file

 To use the web upload option:

 • From the left-hand navigation, click Data sources > Custom data. 
• Select Add custom data and then Upload Records . 
• Select teachers.csv and then choose the file from your device.
• Once the upload icon displays a green checkmark , select Upload .
 
 Clever will automatically trigger a sync within the next five minutes to begin processing the new data, taking about 15-20 minutes to complete. The newly uploaded data will not be fully available in Clever until then. 

 SFTP Upload

 SFTP Upload is available to all schools. If your primary sync method is not SFTP, please reach out to Clever support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. 

 To manually upload files to your district's SFTP folder, please:

 • 
 Navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page to locate your district's SFTP Settings and corresponding credentials. 

 
 
 • log in to your clever sftp box, and create a new subfolder named customteachers .
 • for instructions on accessing your district's sftp folder, please visit: 

 • 
 Log in to your Clever SFTP box, and create a new subfolder named customteachers .

 • 
 For instructions on accessing your district's SFTP folder, please visit: SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever 

 

• 
 Upload the teachers.csv to the customteachers subfolder 

• 
 Once the files finish processing (within 15-20 minutes), you will be able to see the new custom sections from your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 

 
 

 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever will not send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 
 Viewing custom teachers

 Clever admins can view up to 200 successfully created custom teachers through the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. If your district has more than 200 custom teachers, all records can be viewed from the Data Browser or by using the search bar at the top of the Clever Dashboard to locate individual custom teacher records.

 Please note: If the custom teacher(s) will be joining the district's account in the Fall through the normal SIS sync, the T eacher_id used for the custom account should match their Teacher _ id in the SIS.

 
 Deleting custom teachers

 To remove all custom teachers at once, please upload a blank teachers.csv file to the Data sources > Custom data page > Upload Records option. However, please note that the file must contain column headers . Please feel free to use the teachers.csv file contained in this sample file set to remove teachers in bulk: Download. To remove only some teacher records via the bulk upload, simply upload a file using one of the formats listed above. Please be sure to include all records in the file that you do not want to be deleted. As long as the record is present in the file, it will not be deleted from Clever. If a record is missing from the file, it will be deleted. To remove an individual custom data record, please navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page.

 • Select the corresponding tab for the record type you would like to remove.
• Locate the record, and under the Actions column, click the icon to delete the record.
 
 Please note: Manually deleting teachers individually using the trash icon will delete the user entirely from Clever. However, if the teacher was bulk uploaded to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data pag e, they would still be present in the custom teachers.csv file even after the trash icon is selected. If the user is still present in the following uploaded custom teachers.csv file with the same teacher_id (i.e., teacher SIS ID), the user will be re-added to Clever. Conversely, uploading blank files over custom data only deletes bulk uploaded custom data and not manually created custom data. Manually created custom data will need to be deleted manually by clicking on the trash can under the Actions column in the Custom Records . 

 

 
 

 Logging in as a custom teacher

 Custom teachers will log in to Clever the same way teachers syncing from your SIS log in. Just make sure to add any details (e.g., email, teacher_id, etc.) required by your selected login method. To check to see what your login method is, navigate in your Clever Dashboard to SSO & Portal > SSO Settings > Login method. 

 

 Please note: If you are utilizing the teacher_number field for login credentials, Clever does not currently support syncing a unique teacher_number for custom teachers. The teacher_id (sis_id) will be used to populate the teacher_number field. 

 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Custom Data: Overview (i.e., sections, students, contacts, teachers, staff & fields) 
• Custom Data: Students 
• Custom Data: Teachers 
• Custom Data: Staff 
• Custom Data: Sections

title: Custom Data: Students
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Individually create custom students 3. Bulk create custom students 4. Removing custom students 5. Viewing custom students 6. School year rollover 

 

 Overview

 If you would like to add a student that doesn't sync from your district's SIS, you can create a custom student account in Clever on an individual basis or bulk upload through a CSV file. 

 IMPORTANT Custom students cannot be added to sections synced from your Student Information System (SIS). They can only be added to custom sections. 

 

 
 Individually create custom students

 To create an individual custom student, navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. Click Add custom data > Students and a “Create student” form will appear . Once created, custom student accounts can be treated like an SIS-synced student account. 

 

 

 
 Bulk create custom students

 How do I format the file?

 The file will need to be titled "students" and saved as a CSV file type. The minimum header requirements are the following:

 • school_id
• student_id
• last_name
• first_name
 
 Please see our spec section 3.2 for more detailed information on the file format, what values are accepted, and information about additional headers. A sample file can be downloaded here: Download. 

 Please note: C ontacts for custom students cannot be uploaded in the custom students.csv file. They need to be uploaded in a separate custom contacts.csv file. Instructions on how to do so can be found here. 

 Web Upload

 If you would like to use the web upload option: 

 • From the left-hand navigation, click Support tools > Custom data 
• From the top-right, click Add custom data and select Upload records 
 
 Once uploaded, you will see the following status message: 

 

 

 

 SFTP Upload

 SFTP Upload is available to all schools. If your primary sync method is not SFTP, please reach out to Clever support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. 

 To manually upload files to your district's SFTP folder, please:

 • 
 Navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page to locate your district's SFTP Settings and corresponding credentials. 

 
 
 • log in to your clever sftp box, and create a new subfolder named customstudents .
 • for instructions on accessing your district's sftp folder, please visit: Settings page" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000T1IT&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl00000Fxp4J">

 • 
 Log in to your Clever SFTP box, and create a new subfolder named customstudents .

 • 
 For instructions on accessing your district's SFTP folder, please visit: SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever 

 

• 
 Upload the students.csv to the customstudents subfolder 

 
 Once the files finish processing (within 15-20 minutes), you will be able to see the new custom sections from your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page.

 

 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever will not send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 
 Removing custom students

 Removing individual custom students 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page.
• Select the Students tab at the bottom of the screen.
• Locate the record, and under Actions , click to delete
 
 

 Removing custom students in bulk 

 Custom students can be removed in bulk by uploading a blank students.csv file under Support tools > Custom data > Add custom data > Upload records . The file must contain column headers. Please feel free to use the students.csv file contained in this sample file set to remove students in bulk: Download 

 This is to prevent a "duplicate identifier" error when the student eventually comes through the normal SIS sync with the same sis/student_id. 

 Please note: Uploading blank files over custom data only deletes bulk uploaded custom data and not manually created custom data. Manually created custom data will need to be deleted manually by clicking on the trash can under the Actions column in the Custom Records . 

 

 
 Viewing custom students

 Five minutes after the file is uploaded, Clever will automatically trigger a sync in order to process the custom students into our system. Once the sync has successfully completed - around 10-15 minutes after initial file upload - districts can view up to 200 successfully created custom students through the Custom Data page in the dashboard. If the district has 200+ custom students, all can be viewed in the Data browser or via the search bar in the dashboard. 

 Please note: If the custom student(s) will be joining the district's account in the Fall through the normal SIS sync, the student_id used for the custom account should match their student_id in the SIS. 

 

 
 School year rollover

 Once the normal SIS sync is running for the next school year, Clever recommends deleting all custom students from the previous academic year. To remove custom students, please follow the directions outlined here .

title: Custom Data: Non-teaching staff
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Creating custom staff in Clever a. Adding custom staff individually via Clever b. Bulk uploading custom staff to Clever 3. Staff permissions in Clever 4. Updating staff roles 5. Removing staff 6. Connecting staff to sections 

 

 Overview

 A district's non-teaching staff (e.g., principals, counselors, front desk, etc.), including any employees who do not have a designated roster in a district's student information system (SIS), are referred to as staff in Clever. 

 Individual staff users can be added directly to Clever, bypassing a district's SIS sync. Once added to Clever, they can log in using the district's existing login methods and leverage Clever single sign-on (SSO). 

 Please note: Only Clever Admins have permissions to create custom staff in Clever. For details, please visit: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions. 

 

 IMPORTANT Custom staff cannot be assigned to sections. However, if a staff member needs co-teacher access to sections from a teacher's perspective, Clever Admins can create a custom teacher record for them to associate them with sections. Please visit this article for more: For Clever Admins: Co-teachers. 

 

 
 Creating custom staff in Clever

 Custom staff can be created individually directly within Clever, or uploaded as a csv in bulk. Custom staff accounts are independent of a district's student information system (SIS) sync. So, for example, if a district's SIS is syncing staff to Clever, or they're being uploaded manually per this article , this staff data will not overwrite custom staff added directly via the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 

 Adding custom staff individually via Clever 

 To add a custom staff member individually in Clever, please:

 • Navigate to the Clever Dashboard . 
• Select Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data. 
• Select Add custom data. 
• Select Staff. 
 
 

 Please fill out the form, which begins by associating the staff member with their school(s).

 • For all schools, click the switch icon under the Schools section. 
• For one school, begin typing the name in the search box to select it.
• For multiple schools, type and select each school from the search bar. 
 
 

 Clever Role 

 • For an overview of the Portal Access Only vs. School Tech Lead roles, please see our Clever article visit: For Staff: School Tech Lead vs. Portal Only Permission. 
 
 Username & Password 

 • These fields are only required if:
 • Your district uses Clever Passwords , and the district username or password is needed for users to log in to Clever. 
• An application partner requires a username or password to be populated within their integrated application(s). 
 

 
 Expiration Date ​​​​​​ 

 • Enter an optional Expiration date if you want the record to be automatically deleted on the selected date.
 
 Notifying custom staff of their new account via email 

 • If you would like the user to be notified via email that their account has been created, check the optional Also notify custom staff of their new account via email box. The notification email can also be sent after their custom staff record has been saved using the envelope icon under Actions .
 
 

 

 Bulk uploading custom staff to Clever 

 To bulk add multiple custom staff, please:

 • Prepare your Staff.csv file. You can download a staff.csv template here .
• Navigate to the Clever Dashboard . 
• Select Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data. 
• Select Upload records. 
• Upload your Staff.csv file.
 
 
 Associating staff to multiple locations via bulk upload 

 To associate a staff member with multiple school sync one row to provision the account and then assign additional schools directly from the Clever Dashboard by following these steps. 

 • Navigate to Data sources > Custom data > Staff tab then click the pencil icon under Actions . 
• Select additional schools that should be associated with custom staff > Save Changes. 
 

 

 
 Please note: Uploading a custom staff.csv file will not override/delete any staff members you've added manually via the steps in the previous section! 

 

 Next steps after creating staff 

 It will take between 10-15 minutes for the new record(s) to be created in Clever. This process is complete when: 

 • The user's name is hyperlinked in blue on the Custom data page . 
• Or, they can be located by using the Clever search bar at the top of the Clever Dashboard .
 
 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever will not send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 Finally, please be sure to share any needed applications with them! For instructions, visit: Sharing Permissions: Sharing staff with applications. 

 
 Staff permissions in Clever

 By default, custom staff are assigned a Portal Access Only role in Clever which means they will only have access to the applications that have been shared with them on a district level. 

 The alternative Clever staff role that provides greater permissions within Clever is the School Tech Lead role. 

 

 
 Updating staff roles

 Most staff updates happen by uploading an updated staff.csv to Clever (see this article for details). This includes changes to name, email address, title, and so on. However, if you wish to update the role of a staff user from Portal Access to School Tech Lead :

 • Use the Clever search bar at the top of your Clever Dashboard to locate the user and open their profile page.
• Click the drop-down arrow to the right and select Update Clever Role. 
• A pop-up will appear, allowing you to select the role of School Tech Lead. 
• Select Save. 
 
 Once staff have been added to Clever, visit this article to see how to share applications with them: Sharing Permissions: Sharing staff with applications 

 

 
 Removing staff

 To remove individual custom staff from Clever:

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 
• Select the Staff tab at the bottom of the screen. 
• Locate the user, and under Actions , click to delete. 
 
 

 To remove individual custom staff from Clever via bulk upload:

 • Prepare your Staff.csv file as mentioned above . Please include every record you want to keep in Clever. If a record is in the new file, our system will keep it. If a record is missing from the file, our system will delete it from Clever.
• Select Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data. 
• Select Upload records. 
• Upload your Staff.csv file. 
 
 To remove all custom staff from Clever: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 
• In the top-right corner, click Delete all staff. 
• On the next screen, click Confirm. 
 
 

 

 
 Connecting staff to sections

 Staff cannot be connected to sections in Clever. To associate a user with an existing section:

 • Add the user as a custom teacher. 
• Connect the custom teacher to a section using the Add co-teacher feature .
 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Staff: School Tech Lead vs. Portal Only Permissions 
• Sharing Permissions: Sharing staff with applications 
• Custom Data: Overview (i.e., sections, students, contacts, teachers, staff & fields) 
• Clever Roles: Staff - How to add to Clever

title: Clever Badges: How do I enable Badges into Chromebooks with Zscaler?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up Clever Badge logins with Zscaler 

 

 Overview

 This article is an overview of setup instructions for using Clever Badges with Zscaler. 

 
 Setting up Clever Badge logins with Zscaler

 Follow these outlined steps to ensure successful authentication of Clever Badges while using Zscaler services at your district. 

 Required Zscaler Settings

 The following update needs to be made in Zscaler in order to successfully use Clever Badges on Chromebooks: 

 • Change from Form-Based to configuring SAML Authentication type required in Zscaler Authentication settings 
 
 

 • Enter the following “Exempted URLs”: 
 • accounts.google.com 
• . amazonaws.com 
 

 
 Required Google Admin Settings

 Please follow the instructions listed in the Zscaler documentation to make the necessary updates in your district’s Google Admin Console to successfully use Clever Badges on Chromebooks: 

 • https://help.zscaler.com/zia/saml-configuration-example-google-apps

title: Clever Badges: How do I enable Badges into Active Directory On-prem managed Windows devices?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview ​ 2. Setup steps a. NetBIOS domain b. Locating user credentials c. Downloading software 

 Please note: These instructions only apply to Windows devices that accept Active Directory user credentials. If your users use Entra ID credentials to access Windows devices, please follow the steps listed in our Setup guide for Clever SSO into Entra ID . When using the Entra ID instructions, ensure you do not have the MSI file for this AD On-prem configuration installed on your Windows devices. 

 Overview

 Clever Badges make accessing apps simple for young students by logging them in to Clever without requiring a username and password. Students hold their physical Clever Badge up to their device camera to log in. However, students often still need to enter a username and password in order to access their device to get to Clever. To simplify that experience, we also offer Badges into Windows for devices that utilize Active Directory logins. When you set up Badges into Windows, students can log in to their device and Clever with just their Badge.

 Student experience

 For students, the login experience is simple. The device's login screen prompts them to scan their Clever Badge. They hold the Badge up to the camera, which instantly logs them into the device, Clever, and all of their Clever applications.

 

 If your district or school is using shared Windows devices for students, the device will remember the login option the previous user selected. Thus, if a user selects Other user , as shown in the image below, the Windows device will provide that same option for the following user who logs into the device. Ensure the user selects the correct login method from the device login page. 

 

 

 
 Setup Steps

 There are three basic steps to setting up this configuration:

 • Provide your NetBIOS domain 
• Locate user credentials 
• Download software 
 
 The setup process should take no more than 10 minutes, and you won’t need to configure settings within Active Directory.

 Clever’s setup wizard will guide you through the Badges setup. Navigate to the wizard by going to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges > Windows tab .

 

 
 Finding your NetBIOS domain

 Each Active Directory instance has a NetBIOS domain, which is different from your DNS domain name. To retrieve your NetBIOS domain, open Windows PowerShell as a user logged into the domain and paste in the command below:

 Command: $ENV:USERDOMAIN 

 The result will look like this:

 

 Paste that domain into Clever , and you’re ready to move on to the next step.

 

 
 Locating User Credentials

 To log students into Windows devices equipped with Active Directory logins, Clever needs the student's Active Directory (AD) credentials. Student AD credentials in Clever must stay in sync with actual student AD credentials. If they get out of sync, students can still log in to the devices with Active Directory, but won’t be able to utilize the Badge login option.

 

 Clever Tip: We recommend disabling password changes in Active Directory for students who will be using this setup. 

 There are three different ways that you can locate user credentials:

 • Select them from the dropdown (recommended)
• Use a custom configuration (recommended)
• Upload a csv (not recommended)
 
 Select from dropdown (recommended)

 The easiest and most reliable way to provide those credentials is to indicate where they are within your existing SIS sync. For example, if student AD usernames are their email addresses, and passwords are their student numbers, and those fields are already syncing to Clever, you would select those fields in the dropdowns within the Badges into Windows configuration wizard.

 

 Make sure that the credentials for the sample student match the Active Directory credentials you have on file.

 Custom configurations (recommended)

 If student AD usernames and/or passwords are syncing to Clever already but aren’t neatly contained in a single field, you can use a custom configuration to provide those credentials. Instead of selecting a single field, you’ll choose “Custom” in the appropriate dropdown list and paste a formula as pictured below:

 

 

 We support the following fields for custom formulas:

 • sis_id
• email
• student_number
• state_id
• credentials.district_username
• credentials.district_password
 
 

 Examples of using formulas for credentials 

 Example student: Scott Kelly Email: skelly@cleverusd.edu Username: skelly19 

 Student AD usernames or passwords are... Formula [fill in portions in brackets] Example formula Sample result for the student above 
 Username from the sync plus my district's email domain {{credentials.district_username}}[add domain name here] {{credentials.district_username}}@ cleverusd.edu skelly19@cleverusd.edu 
 A specific word/phrase that's the same for every student [add that word/phrase here] apple apple 
 First initial, last name {{substr name.first 0 1}}{{name.last}} {{substr name.first 0 1}}{{name.last}} skelly 
 Their email without the domain {{textBefore email "@"}} {{textBefore email "@"}} sykelly 
 Something else... Contact us via the form in our Help Center and we'll create a formula for you! 
 
 

 Once you’ve entered the appropriate formula make sure that the credentials for the sample student match the Active Directory credentials you have on file.

 Uploading Credentials (not recommended)

 If your student's Active Directory credentials aren’t included in or can't be derived from your synced data using custom formulas, you have the option of uploading those credentials directly to Clever via CSV. Clever doesn't recommend CSV upload as it can require frequent upkeep to ensure that credentials are present for all Badge users. For Badge logins to work successfully, credentials must stay in sync. We recommend re-uploading credentials at least once a week to ensure that new students can utilize the integration. To upload credentials, choose “Username uploaded from .csv” and/or “Password uploaded from .csv” in the dropdown.

 

 File Name (required): students.csv Formatting (required): student_id, username, password

 Sample file: Web Upload Tool: You can also automate that upload via SFTP to the “ credentials-sftp.clever.com/clever-windows ” folder. The SFTP upload URL, username, and password of your SFTP Settings - Saved Passwords Applications can be found at Clever Dashboard > Sync > Settings .

 

 For more information on the tools that you can use to access and upload files to your credentials SFTP server, please see this Help Center article: What are some recommended tools for SFTP syncs? 

 

 
 Downloading software

 Finally, you’ll want to install Clever’s software on any machines you want to have this login option. The installer is an MSI file, so you can remotely install this software in the same way that you would any other piece of software on your Windows machines.

title: Clever Badges: How do I enable both ADFS and Badge logins for Chromebooks?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. Setup 4. Customizing the Login Flow (optional) 5. Chromebook Safety Feature 

 Overview

 Clever works seamlessly with Chromebooks, which allows students and teachers to access all of their learning applications while only logging in once per device. There are two methods to use Clever with Chromebooks: 

 • Users use Clever Badges to log into Chromebooks 
• Users log into Chromebooks, then access Clever 
 
 Each method can be configured separately for different Organizational Units (OUs). Since Clever integrates with many identity providers, including Active Directory, Clever can serve as the authentication pathway for both Badge and Active Directory users! See below for details on this integration. 

 
 Requirements

 • Clever enabled as the third party identity provider in the Google Admin Console ( Security Settings > Setup SSO with third party identity provider )
• Active Directory enabled as an identity provider in Clever ( Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Login method tab) 
• Staff and students that'll use Chromebooks must be active users in Clever
• Staff and students who'll use Chromebooks must have their Google email address populated in Clever
 
 

 
 

 Setup

 Step 1 - Setup Active Directory authentication into Clever To pass through authentication for non-Badge users, we'll need to add Active Directory as a login method for Clever. Directions for adding Active Directory can be found here: Single sign-on (SSO) with Active Directory Federation Services. 

 Step 2 - Upload student/teacher Google emails to Clever In order to authenticate both Clever Badge and Active Directory users into Chromebooks, the district will need to ensure that all students and teachers have Google emails synced to Clever. If these emails are't populated in the SIS, we can enable manual email uploads as described here: How do I upload emails that are not in my SIS? 

 Step 3 - Customize login flow (optional) Districts can adjust settings in the admin console to tweak the login flow to best meet the needs of your district. See below for more details!

 Step 4 - Apply settings and test logins Apply the settings for Clever single sign-on (SSO) into Chromebooks as described here: 

 • https://schools.clever.com/badges/chromebook/wizard 
 
 For districts that are actively using Active Directory to sign users into Chromebooks, we recommend completing login tests at a time when most users are't utilizing their Chromebooks to reduce interruptions to learning. 

 
 Customizing the Login Flow (optional)

 Option 1: Bypass badge login By default, once users open the Chromebook and select "Next" they'll be presented with the badge login screen. If the district would prefer, this behavior can be adjusted so the district's general login page is presented first, facilitating faster logins for users logging in via Active Directory. 

 To implement this option, begin by navigating to the Security Settings page in the Google Admin Console and scroll down to the SSO section. Under Setup SSO with third party identity provider, let's adjust the "Sign-in page URL"

 

 Currently your sign-in URL should look something like this: 

 • https://samlidp.clever.com/chromebook-login/assert/582df1eb56619d0100000482?specify_auth=badges 
 
 This URL contains a parameter that forwards users directly to the badge authentication page: 

 Let's adjust the URL by removing: ?specify_auth=badges The result should look like this: 

 • https://samlidp.clever.com/chromebook-login/assert/582df1eb56619d0100000482 
 
 This will send all users to the general login page for your district! From there, users can select their preferred login method. Here is what the login page will now look like:

 

 Option 2: Pass-through Google authentication into Clever Portal 

 Pass through authentication ensures that all users are automatically logged into the Clever Portal when logged into a Chromebook. To configure this setting, navigate to the "User Settings" page in the Google Admin Console. Depending on how you have applied the settings, you may need to adjust the Homepage, Pages to Load on Startup, or both. Currently the settings will look similar to below:​ 

 To enable pass-through authentication, add: ?skip=1&specify_auth=google to your district URL. Your district-specific URL can be found here: https://schools.clever.com/portal. 

 The result should look like this: https://clever.com/in/cleverdemo?skip=1&specify_auth=google 

 Login experience 

 • Chrome OS 98 and below: Open Chromebook, press “Next” 
• If a user logs in using: 
 • Badges: user selects 'Log in with Clever Badges'. 
 • The user scans their Badge and is authenticated into Clever and Google/Chromebook! 
 

• ADFS Credentials: 
 • User selects 'Log in with Active Directory.' The Active Directory login screen is presented. User logs in to ADFS. ADFS authenticates the user into Clever, Clever authenticates the user into Google/Chromebook. 
 

 

 
 

 3. Clever portal launches and learning begins! 

 
 Chromebook Safety Feature 

 We'll soon offer an optional query param on the Badges login screen called `chromebook_safety.' If it is present, the login screen won't show the "Alternate Login" button to allow non-Badge logins. This serves as a way to definitively prevent users from averting web filters and accessing websites outside of Clever. To enable this feature, add the following as a query param on the Chromebook Sign-On URL: https://samlidp.clever.com/chromebook-login/assert/YourDistrictCleverID?specify_auth=badges&chromebook_safety=true 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Single Sign-on (SSO): multiple IDPs for Google Workspace

title: Custom Data: Sections
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Creating individual custom sections 3. Creating multiple custom sections via upload 4. Editing custom sections 5. Deleting custom sections 6. Sharing custom sections with apps 

 Overview

 Custom sections are a great way to create rostering data within Clever that doesn't exist in your district's student information system (SIS). This can be helpful in cases such as summer school, intervention groups, after-school programs, and more! 

 Custom sections will not overwrite any sections being synced to Clever by a district's SIS, nor can they be overwritten by sections synced by the SIS. Custom sections are also indistinguishable from synced SIS sections in most Clever-integrated applications. 

 Only Clever Admins or School Tech Leads can create custom sections in Clever. For more details about Clever roles, please visit: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 

 

 
 Creating individual custom sections

 There are two ways to create custom sections in Clever. The first method is creating individual sections from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 

 Step 1: Add section info 

 • Navigate to Data sources > Custom data. 
• Click Add custom data > Sections .
 
 

 • Begin by entering a section name and the associated school location.
 • We recommend being as descriptive as possible with section names (e.g., course_name - teacher_last_name - period). 
 

• Enter the class information (i.e., grade, subject, and period).
 
 

 • If desired, enter the optional term and course information. These fields may be helpful with identifying the section in Clever and connected applications.
 
 

 • Enter an optional section expiration date if you want the section to be automatically deleted on the selected date.
• Once all required fields are populated, click Next to proceed. 
 
 

 Step 2: Add Teachers 

 • Click the primary teacher who should be associated with the section. Clever allows up to nine co-teachers for each custom section. 
 • Please note: If a teacher is not yet in Clever or in your SIS, please see this article for instructions on creating a custom teacher in Clever: Custom Data: Teachers. 
 

 
 

 Clever Tip: To select teachers from a school location other than the one where the section is located, click the white search box to the right to bring up a list of schools. Select additional school location(s) to bring up a list of teachers from selected school(s). 

 

 • Click Next to proceed.
 
 

 IMPORTANT Note that not all applications support co-teachers. We recommend checking with the application’s support team to confirm if co-teachers are a supported feature. 

 

 Step 3: Select Primary Teacher 

 • Next, indicate which of the teachers will be the primary teacher for the section. If an application does not support co-teachers, the primary teacher will be the one associated with the custom section in applications.
• Click Next to proceed.
 
 Step 4: Add Students 

 • Select which students should be enrolled in the section. 
 • Please note: Clever can support up to 10,000 students enrolled in a single section. However, Clever can only support up to 100 students enrolled in a single section for Teacher Pages to function correctly. 
 

 
 Clever Tip: To select students from a school location other than where the section is located, click the white search box to the right to select students from other schools. You can further filter the list of students by selecting only certain grade levels. Please visit Custom Data: Students if the student is not yet in Clever or your SIS. 

 

 • Click Next to proceed.
 
 Step 5: Preview 

 • Review the custom section to ensure the details look correct before clicking Create Section .
 
 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever will not send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 Step 6: Share with applications 

 • Once the custom section is available in Clever, you can share it with connected applications. Please see this article for details.
 
 

 
 Creating multiple custom sections via upload

 Clever Admins can also create custom sections in bulk by uploading two CSV files ( sections.csv and enrollments.csv ) to Clever. The custom sections file will create the section, while the enrollments file will associate students to that section. 

 Please note: Bulk uploading custom sections will not impact custom sections that were created manually from the Custom data page. 

 

 Step 1: Create your sections.csv and enrollments.csv 

 • Download the CSV file templates for uploading custom data by clicking Download , and then use the instructions below to modify the sections.csv and enrollments.csv.
• Please ensure the files are named sections.csv and enrollments.csv before uploading them, or Clever will not process them successfully. 
 
 

 Sections.csv 

 Column Header Notes 
 
 School_id 

 Must match the school_id (i.e., SIS ID) for a school that exists in Clever 
 
 Section_id 

 Must be a unique ID that is not associated with other sections 
 Name Unique name for the section (e.g., Algebra 101 - Jones - Period 1A) 
 
 Teacher_id 

 Must match a teacher_ID (i.e., teacher SIS ID) for a teacher that exists in Clever 
 Course_name (e.g., Algebra, Geometry, English, Creative Writing, etc.) 
 Subject Subjects should adhere to Clever's specifications 
 Term_name If creating custom sections for summer, include "Summer" in this field for easier data sharing 
 
 

 The fields listed above are required , but you can also include additional optional fields listed in Clever's SFTP specification for the sections.csv . Please note that extension fields are not supported in the custom sections.csv. For information on how to add extension data to custom sections, please see our article For Clever Admins: Syncing custom (i.e. extension) fields to Clever . 

 

 Enrollments.csv 

 
 Column Header 

 
 Note 

 
 
 School_id 

 
 Must match the school_id (i.e., SIS ID) for a school that exists in Clever 

 
 
 Section_id 

 
 Must match the section_id in the sections.csv file 

 
 
 Student_id 

 
 Must match an existing student_id (i.e., SIS ID) for a student in Clever 

 
 
 

 

 Uploading the sections.csv and enrollments.csv 

 There are two ways for districts to upload sections.csv and enrollments.csv files to Clever. 

 Option 1 (Recommended): Manual Upload 

 • Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page.
• Click Add custom data at the top right, and then choose Upload records .
 
 

 

 Option 2: Uploading the files to your district's SFTP folder 

 The second option for uploading the sections.csv and enrollments.csv is to access your district's Clever SFTP folder and manually upload the files. 

 Please note: This option is available to all schools. If your primary sync type is not SFTP sync type , please reach out to Clever support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. Visit this article for additional details. 

 To upload files to your district's SFTP folder, please:

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab to locate your district's SFTP Settings and corresponding credentials. 
 
 Settings page" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000V7lB&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl00000FxRmk">

 • Log in to your Clever SFTP box, and create a new subfolder named customsections . 
 • For instructions on accessing your district's SFTP folder, please visit: SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever 
 

• Upload the sections.csv and enrollments.csv to the customsections subfolder 
 
 Once the files finish processing (within 15-20 minutes), you'll be able to see the new custom sections from your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 

 

 
 

 Editing custom sections

 You may need to edit a custom section for several reasons,

title: Custom Data: Overview (i.e., sections, students, contacts, teachers, staff & fields)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding custom data 3. Browsing custom data 4. Removing custom data 5. Custom data reports 

 Overview

 Custom data allows Clever Admins to create rostering data, including fields and records, directly from the Clever Dashboard that doesn't exist in the district's student information system (SIS) or aren't being synced to Clever. Common uses of custom data include: 

 • Adding summer school information
• Organizing unique groups of students into sections (e.g., afterschool programs) 
• Adding non-teaching staff members who are not in the district's SIS 
• Maintaining historical data that was deleted from the SIS 
 • For example, if a section is deleted when a teacher leaves midyear, but you want to preserve historical app data for that section. You can build a custom section in Clever using the original SIS section data so app data is preserved. 
 

 
 

 IMPORTANT Please note that custom data can't be used until you start running a sync with your Student Information System (SIS) . In addition, if Clever has not synced with your SIS in more than 6 months, you won't be able to add new custom data to Clever. Also, Custom data will run during a sync pause (such as during summer school) but not a sync hold (when your sync has stopped due to unexpected changes to data) . 

 

 Clever Admins can upload custom fields for any data type to track data not currently being synced by the district's SIS (e.g., IEP status, Dietary Requirements, Extracurricular Activities, etc.). The types of records that can be created using custom data can either be associated with or include:

 • Students 
• Teachers 
• Staff 
• Sections & Enrollments 
• Student Contacts 
• Custom fields 
 
 Custom data won't overwrite any rostering data being synced to Clever by your district's SIS, nor can it be overwritten by your SIS-synced data. Custom data is also indistinguishable from your synced SIS data to most Clever-integrated applications. 

 Clever Tip: When creating custom records, we recommend using the unique SIS identifier known as the SIS ID (i.e., teacher_id, student_id, contact_id, section_id, or staff_id, respectively) if one exists for each record in your SIS. This way, if, at some point, your district's SIS begins sending those same records in the standard SIS data sync, the SIS record will take precedence over the custom data record. Failure to match SIS IDs will result in duplicate records in Clever. 

 

 
 

 Adding custom data

 Clever supports creating custom records in Clever for the following record types: 

 • Students 
• Teachers 
• Staff 
• Sections & Enrollments 
 
 

 Custom schools cannot be created in Clever. To add schools, districts must sync them from their SIS. Summer school locations should be included in the standard SIS sync before pausing the sync over summer. Please visit For District Admins: Pausing the sync with your SIS for more about sync pauses. 

 

 Admins can create custom data records either individually or in bulk using CSV uploads via the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. To upload data: 

 • Download CSV templates .
• Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data .
• Click Add custom data in the top right-hand corner. 
• Choose to add records individually by clicking on the corresponding selection or in bulk by clicking Upload records . When creating the file(s), make sure to use the School_ID field and not the School_number field. 
 
 

 

 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever won't send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 
 
 
 
 Custom data won't overwrite SIS-synced records and will appear in Clever with a "Custom" label on their Clever profile page.

 

 Please note: SFTP Upload of custom data is available to all schools. If your primary sync method is not SFTP, please reach out to Clever Support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. 

 

 
 Browsing custom data

 From the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page , districts can select the appropriate custom data tab (i.e., Students, Teachers, Sections, or Staff) to view any custom data for that respective data type. In addition, records can be edited or deleted by using the icons to the far right of the record name under the Actions column. 

 If you notice that some of the custom data names aren’t clickable, this is most likely an indication that the data is also synced from your SIS with the same SIS ID. 

 For example, staff member Jen Cutler has a custom staff account with the SIS ID of 32401969, but they also have a staff account being synced from the SIS with the same SIS ID. This will cause the custom data to be unclickable.

 

 

 In this screenshot, the custom staff account for Jen Cutler is unclickable. 

 Of note, in the case of staff, even if the SIS ID is different but the email is the same, it will cause the custom staff account to be unclickable.

 

 
 Removing custom data

 Please note: Clever does not maintain historical records of users deleted through custom data. 

 

 Removing individual records 

 • To remove an individual custom data record, please navigate to the Data sources > Custom data page.
• Select the corresponding tab for the record type you would like to remove.
• Locate the record, and under Actions column, click to delete the record.
 
 

 Removing records in bulk 

 Please note: • To remove only certain records, upload a new file via the bulk upload using one of the accepted formats mentioned above. Please include every record you want to keep in Clever. If a record is in the new file, our system will keep it. If a record is missing from the file, our system will delete it from Clever. • Uploading blank files over custom data only deletes bulk uploaded custom data and not manually created custom data. Manually created custom data will need to be deleted manually by clicking on the trash can under the Actions column in the Custom Records . 

 

 Students 

 • To remove all bulk custom students at once, please upload a blank students.csv file to the Data sources > Custom data > Upload records option. However, please note that the file must contain column headers . Please feel free to use the sample students.csv file contained in this sample file set to remove students in bulk: Download . 
 
 Teachers 

 • To remove all bulk custom teachers at once, please upload a blank teachers.csv file to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page > Upload records option. However, please note that the file must contain column headers . Please feel free to use the teachers.csv file contained in this sample file set to remove teachers in bulk: Download. 
 
 Staff 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page.
• In the top-right corner, select Delete all staff. 
• Select Confirm. 
 
 Sections 

 • To remove all bulk custom sections at once, please upload a blank sections.csv file to the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data > Upload records . However, please note that the file must contain column headers . Please feel free to use the sections.csv file contained in this sample file set to remove sections in bulk: Download. 
 
 

 
 Custom data reports

 The Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page > Reports tab will show how custom data has changed since the latest custom data sync. This tab also displays the most recent sync status. The Previous column represents the number of records of each data type that existed before the latest successful sync. Select any of the Download files options to review recent errors or changes to your district's custom data. 

 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Custom Data: Students 
• Custom Data: Teachers 
• Custom Data: Staff 
• Custom Data: Sections 
• For District Admins: Syncing custom (i.e. extension) fiel

title: Summer School: Sync settings
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. If you choose to pause your SIS data sync (Recommended) 3. If you choose not to pause your SIS data sync 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 Overview

 Clever can support multiple sync configurations for summer school, each with its own benefits and possible limitations. We recommend thinking through the following points to help you make informed decisions about the best summer school options for your district:

 • Who will specifically need access to Clever and connected learning applications over the summer? (e.g. all students, teachers, and staff, or only a subset of users, etc.)?
• Which Clever resources do district users need to access or utilize over the summer?
• Will your district need to add new users over the summer or before the start of the new school year?
• Will data need to be dynamically updated in Clever via your student information system (SIS) sync, or will a Clever admin be managing the manual summer school updates in Clever?
• If you're using Clever IDM, please also review Clever IDM: Summer rollover .
 
 Please review the rest of this article to learn of the options available to you based on your district's needs during the summer months! For specific details about school year rollover, please visit this article . 

 IMPORTANT In 2026, Clever automatically pauses and removes pauses from syncs based on your school organization’s country: USA: Syncs pause on May 29, and we remove pauses between July 27–31. Canada: Syncs pause on June 30, and we remove pauses on August 14. UK: Syncs pause on July 15, and we remove pauses on September 1. Australia and Brazil: Syncs pause on December 20, and we remove pauses on January 20. For all other countries, we use the USA dates by default. You can modify these dates (or remove them altogether) under your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. 

 

 
 If you choose to pause your SIS data sync (Recommended)

 For 2026, Clever automatically pauses syncs based on the dates listed above unless you specify otherwise. You can modify these dates (or remove them altogether) under your Sync > Sync Settings page. During the pause, Clever will stop processing data updates. This allows districts to make changes to their student information system (SIS) data or CSV data files without allowing those changes to flow into Clever and interrupt student, teacher, or staff access to Clever or their connected applications over the summer. Current school year data will be "frozen" in Clever for the duration of the district's sync pause. 

 We recommend using this option if: 

 • Your district won't need to update summer rosters often and/or there are minimal changes that need to be made in Clever over the summer.
• District users need to access their connected applications and associated data from the previous school year during the summer (e.g. students who need to complete summer homework in apps).
 
 This option allows: 

 • Clever admins to supplement existing roster data during the pause via Clever's custom data feature . Custom data can be used for creating summer school sections, intervention groups, after-school programs, adding out-of-district students, and more! Custom data does not overwrite data synced from the SIS, nor can data from the SIS overwrite custom data. This is a perfect option for summer school! Visit this article for details.
• Clever admins to make updates for the upcoming school year in the SIS without the updates affecting Clever or applications during the sync pause.
• Clever admins to continue adjusting application sharing permissions for district-wide apps to meet application vendor needs.
• District users to continue logging in to Clever, and connected applications, to access their data from the previous school year.
 
 

 If your district is moving forward with initiating a sync pause, please see this article for instructions. The next step of the process is Stage 3: Uploading custom data 

 

 Please note: If you'd like to pause syncing your data to a particular application, please see this article: For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app . This is a perfect tool for semester rollover and summer school, but shouldn't be confused with an SIS sync pause. 

 

 

 
 If you choose not to pause your SIS data sync

 If your district prefers to continue syncing SIS data to Clever over the summer, the first step is to Clear the sync Pause Start and End Dates on the Sync > Settings page. Once removed, data will continue to sync automatically for Clever-managed auto syncs or whenever your district uploads CSV data files to your SFTP directory for SFTP syncs.

 Clever admins will still be able to utilize the custom data feature in Clever to add any supplemental rostering data that is not available in the SIS.

 

 We recommend using this option if: 

 • Your users need access to their resources and learning applications over the summer, but not historical data from the previous year or semester.
• You anticipate a large number of students entering your district over the summer will need access to Clever before the new school year. You would prefer their accounts to be created directly from your SIS sync instead of manually uploading the students using custom data .
• Your summer school data changes often in your SIS, and you would like a more automated process for updating Clever.
 
 This option allows: 

 • Clever admins to create and manage custom rostering data in Clever.
• Clever admins to continue adjusting application sharing permissions for district-wide apps. 
• Your district's SIS to continue dynamically updating Clever with ongoing rostering updates daily.
• New students, teachers, sections, and staff to be added to Clever via the SIS sync. This data will continue to be synced to connected applications.
 • Please note: Some application(s) stop syncing to Clever over the summer. Please reach out to the app partner(s) directly to understand their summer rollover process. 
 

 
 

 Important things to consider: 

 • Access for students and teachers between school years may be impacted as the data in Clever is updated over the summer via the SIS sync. Therefore, the content that is available to them in their applications, or their ability to access applications, might change.
 • For example: 
 • If a student's grade is updated from 4 to 5, depending on the application, they may no longer have access to their 4th-grade data. 
• A teacher may not be able to access their rosters from the previous school year. 
 

 

• For more detailed information about how this might affect your connected applications, please reach out to the app support teams directly.
 
 

 The next step of the process is Stage 3: Uploading custom data 

 IMPORTANT If you do not pause your SIS data sync over the summer, any new rostering data in your SIS will replace the previous school year's data in Clever. This means that students and teachers who are not attending summer school may lose access to Clever and their connected applications over the summer! 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 When can I enter new school year dates for the upcoming school year?

 • The school year dates will be updated to the following school year in mid-summer. The start and end dates need to be entered by a Clever admin in the School Year Dates section on the Sync page under the settings tab .
 
 

 However, there's no need for schools to worry about setting these dates until closer to the start of the next school year. 

 

 Can we pause data syncs to specific applications?

 • You can pause syncing data to any app using App Sync Pause .
• The SIS sync pause in Clever freezes all data in Clever. This ensures that data synced to all connected applications will remain the same over the summer if your SIS sync is paused.
 
 

 How does Clever determine the primary location for a user? 

 • By default, Clever assigns users' primary schools where the majority of their s

title: Term Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. SFTP sync rollover procedure 3. Auto Sync rollover procedure 4. Recommended school organization actions 5. Changing SIS IDs (student_id, teacher_id, etc.) 5. Badge Invalidation 

 

 Overview

 This article covers how Clever handles class roster data as the term/semester changes during a school year. These processes may vary depending upon your sync type and student information system (SIS). Recommended school organization actions in the Clever Dashboard for rollover periods are listed below.

 For information about rolling over to the next school year, please see: How does Clever handle school year rollover? 

 SFTP sync rollover procedure

 Clever will import the second term rosters as soon as you include them in the CSV files you regularly upload. As the data in your SIS changes, you can expect to see the following behavior with your sync:

 • When your CSV files no longer include first term classes or enrollments, Clever will place your sync on hold because the number of student-class enrollments will change significantly.
• To review the hold, navigate to your Clever Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page.
• If the data looks correct, click Approve . Once the hold is cleared, your new term rosters will begin populating in Clever as they become active in your SIS. If the the data looks incorrect, please contact Clever Support for assistance.
 
 For more information on clearing holds, please see: How do I release a hold? 

 
 Auto Sync rollover procedure

 Clever pulls the students, teachers, and rosters that are active in your SIS. As the data in your SIS changes, you can expect to see the following behavior with your sync:

 • When first-term classes are no longer active in your SIS, or as enrollments change, your Clever sync may go on hold: "pending significant changes in the data."
• To review the hold, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page.
• If the data looks correct, click Approve . Once the hold is cleared, your new term rosters will begin populating in Clever as they become active in your SIS. If the data looks incorrect, please contact Clever Support for assistance.
 
 For more information on clearing holds, please see: How do I release a hold? 

 
 Recommended school organization actions:

 1. Review your school organization's sync information 

 • For SFTP syncs: Check your inbox for emails from Clever. 
 • If you receive any error message in your email such as, "Error Processing Clever SFTP Upload" please review the sync status on your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page. 
• See this article to resolve any sync status errors you may have: "How do I resolve SFTP sync status errors?" 
• If your sync is still "On Hold", navigate to your Last Attempted Sync page to approve the changes. 
 

 
 • For Auto Syncs: Review your Clever Last Attempted Sync page as soon as your term data rolls over in your SIS.
 • If your sync is still "On Hold", navigate to your Last Attempted Sync page to approve the changes. 
 

 
 2. Review your sharing permissions 

 If you are currently utilizing sharing by rules for any applications, we recommend reviewing any sections where rules that filter on Term Name . If a rule contains a filter for Term Name, you may need to adjust your sharing permissions to ensure the desired sections for the new term are available to the application. In the example below, the account will want to update the Term Name filter when transitioning from the Fall → Spring term. 

 

 You can review and edit your sharing permissions by selecting the link near your listed application under the "Sharing" section. In the screenshot below, the links to review are those with "Sharing by rules" : 

 

 

 Please note: If you want to pause syncing your data to a particular application, please see this article: For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app. This is a perfect tool for semester rollover and summer school, but shouldn't be confused with an SIS sync pause. 

 

 3. Review section_ids 

 For term rollover, it is important to make sure that the ID #s for sections (the section_id field in Clever) are different after a term or school year rollover. This is because Clever's system 'remembers' sections that have been deleted, based on their section_id. If you have any questions about where to find your section_id based on your sync type, please do not hesitate to contact Clever Support! 

 4. Changing SIS IDs (student_id, teacher_id, etc.) 

 During term rollovers, there are many school organizations that approach Clever about changing the primary identifiers (commonly called SIS IDs) for one or more record types. The following values are SIS IDs in Clever: 

 • Student_id
• Teacher_id
• Staff_id
• School_id
• Section_id
 
 Clever highly advises against changing these values in the middle of a school year. Doing so presents a significant risk of loss of historical data for users mid-year. If your school organization has a critical need to adjust SIS IDs mid-year, please reach out to Clever support for consultation. Commonly the need to change the SIS ID for users is due to a school organization needing to meet the data requirements for a specific connection. Clever Support may be able to help you meet this data requirement without disrupting your existing data in Clever. If you require changing SIS IDs mid-year and cannot wait until the summer academic rollover, please see this article: Changing SIS IDs . Please note, it may take up to 2 weeks to complete the migration process. If you intend on changing SIS IDs, please plan accordingly to ensure minimal disruption to students and teachers. 

 

 Badge invalidation 

 If your school organization uses Clever Badges, please review your Badge policy and ensure you void and regenerate all Badges during your school organization’s natural transition period, like summer or winter rollover. This will keep your school organization’s Badge policy secure and up to date.

 Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature an expiration date. If a printed Badge has an expiration date, it will continue to work past that date. Newly printed Badges will not have an expiration date.

 We suggest voiding and reprinting Badges during your school organization’s natural transition period, such as summer or winter rollover (e.g., void and reprint Badges in July 2026 and annually in July after that).

 If you would like to learn how to void all Clever Badges and generate new Clever Badges, please see this article: Clever Badge Invalidation.

title: Clever Badges: Troubleshooting issues with Badges
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Common Issues and Resolutions 

 Overview

 This article details some troubleshooting tips districts can use when students have issues with Badges, including invalid Badges and logging into Google Chromebooks using Clever Badges.

 
 Common Issues and Resolutions

 

 Badge is invalid

 

 This error message means that the student is no longer included in the scope of users who are assigned Badges.

 Here's an example: Let's say your district's Badge scope is for students in third grade and below. At the end of the year, the third-grade students move on to fourth grade. If any of those new fourth-graders tried to use their old Badge, they would get this invalid message, since they are no longer included in the scoped users for Badges.

 A teacher can generate a new Badge for the student by: 

 • Logging into their Clever Portal > My Students > Student Roster . 
• 
 Choose the class of the student you want to print a Badge for.

• 
 Click the student.

• 
 Select the Create new badge button and print your student’s new Badge. 

 
 

 

 Your connection is not private error

 If your web filter is blocking access, you will see a "Your connection is not private" error when using a Clever Badge on the Chromebook login page. You may need to adjust your web filter in order to use Clever Badges with Chromebooks.

 This occurs because Chromebooks do not load custom root certificates (for instance, those used by web filters) before sign-in occurs. Therefore, you will need to allowlist certain URLs so that they are not intercepted and the correct certificate is presented during the login process.

 

 Allow-listing Necessary Domains

 The following domains must be allow-listed for Clever Badges to function properly:

 • *. clever.com 
• *. s3.amazonaws.com 
• accounts.google.com (if your district uses Clever Badges for Chromebooks)
 
 With these changes, we believe that districts using any common web filter (such as iBoss, Lightspeed, Cipafilter, Securly, ZScaler , and Fortinet) can use Clever Badges with Chromebooks.

 

 Popup window interrupting the login flow

 Occasionally Google will incorporate popup windows that can not be avoided and disrupt the login flow, requiring users to click through to close the popup in order to successfully log in. 

 

 Zip file is invalid

 Occasionally Badge zip files on Windows devices produce an error that says Windows can’t open the zipped file. This happens when a school’s section names exceed the character limit Windows allows for file paths. You can fix the issue in one of two ways:

 • 
 Use a zip tool like 7-Zip or WinRAR to open your Badges for printing.

• 
 Update your Windows Registry to support longer file paths:

 • Open RegEdit .
• Go to: Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem 
• Find the key: LongPathsEnabled .
• Set its value to 1 .

title: Clever Badges: Chromebook login flow
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Badge login flow 3. Recommend login flow for logging in with Google 

 

 For more information on how Clever works with Chromebooks, please see Single Sign-On (SSO): Chromebooks or navigate to the Badges into Chromebook Set up Guide in your district dashboard.

 Overview

 Clever Badges can be generated for any students to utilize as an easy login method that requires no typing. This article demonstrates the different login flow options available to students who use a Clever Badge to log into their Chromebook. 

 
 Badge login flow

 Please note: The login flow for users is dependent upon the Chrome OS. The login flow for most users (Chrome OS 99 and above) is outlined below. Since the login flow for third-party IdPs is managed by Google, it is not possible for Clever to alter the login flow or revert to the previous behavior of Chrome OS 98 and lower. Occasionally Google will incorporate popup windows that can not be avoided and disrupt the login flow, requiring the users to click through to close the popup. 

 

 

 Students who are using Badges will go through the following login flow: 

 1. Upon device power on, users will be directly taken to the Clever Badge login screen.

 

 

 2. The Clever Badge login screen will appear. If prompted, grant access to the device camera and scan your Clever Badge. 

 If the Clever Badge is valid, a green checkmark will appear and the Clever Portal will launch on the student's Chromebook. If a red "x" appears, please follow these directions to void and generate a new Clever Badge. 

 
 Recommended login flow for logging in with Google 

 

 Please note: The button to log in with Google cannot be removed. 

 Though the primary login method for Chromebooks may be Clever Badges, users can still log in using their Google email and password. Users should experience the following login flow when they want to log in with their Google credentials: 1. Chrome OS 98 and below: Select Sign in with a different account 

 Chrome OS 99 and above: In the bottom right, select Enter Google Account info 

 

 2. Enter your Google email and password. Click Next. The user is now logged in to the device.

title: For Clever Admins: Pausing the sync with your SIS
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. When a district's sync is paused 3. When a district sync pause ends or is cancelled 4. Scheduling or Initiating a pause 5. Modifying a scheduled pause 6. Removing a pause 

 Overview

 Clever allows districts to pause the sync from their student information system (SIS). When a sync is paused, the c urrent rostering data is essentially "frozen" in Clever for the duration of the district's sync pause. 

 IMPORTANT In 2026, Clever automatically pauses and removes pauses from syncs based on your school’s country: USA: Syncs pause on May 29, and we remove pauses between July 27–31. Canada: Syncs pause on June 30, and we remove pauses on August 14. UK: Syncs pause on July 15, and we remove pauses on September 1. Australia and Brazil: Syncs pause on December 20, and we remove pauses on January 20. For all other countries, we use the USA dates by default. You can modify these dates (or remove them altogether) under your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. 

 The two primary use cases for pausing data are school year rollover, and planning for summer school. Please see the following articles for details: 

 • School Year Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations 
• Summer School: Sync settings 
 
 

 Please note: If you want to pause syncing your data to a particular application, please see this article: For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app. This is a perfect tool for semester rollover and summer school, but shouldn't be confused with an SIS sync pause. 

 

 
 When a district's sync is paused

 When your district's SIS sync to Clever is paused:

 • Rosters will not sync from your SIS to Clever: SFTP uploads will not be processed, and automatic syncs will not run (this means that changes made in your SIS will not interrupt student and teacher access to applications)
• Applications may still sync rosters from Clever, allowing them to sync records updated via custom data. If you'd like your application(s) to stop pulling data from Clever, please reach out to the application partner directly to learn more about the options available.
• You can continue to update sharing permissions with applications
• Your sync status will appear as “Paused” in your Clever Dashboard
• All team members on the account will receive an email notification the day before the pause begins
• All team members in your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page will receive an email notification the day before the pause ends.
 
 

 
 When a district sync pause ends or is canceled

 When a district's sync pause ends or is canceled: 

 • Clever will resume syncing with your SIS (auto-syncs), or process your next upload (SFTP syncs)
• If your data has changed significantly, your account will go on hold 
• You can confirm that your account is syncing correctly by checking the sync status on your Dashboard
• All team members on the account will receive an email reminder on the last day of the pause
 
 

 
 Scheduling or Initiating a pause

 A sync pause can be initiated or canceled by an admin from the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page by entering pause start and end dates. 

 

 Pause Dates 

 • Pause Start Date : This will be the first day your sync goes on pause.
• Pause End Date : This will be the last day your sync is paused.
 
 To schedule an indefinite pause: 

 • Enter a Pause Start Date 
• Leave Pause End Date blank 
• Select Update 
• To begin syncing again, select Clear at any time. 
 
 Sync pauses run from 12:00 AM on the pause start date to 11:59 PM on the pause end date, local time. 

 

 ☀️ Summer Pause: Clever recommends the following dates when pausing your sync: Pause Start Date: the last day of the spring term. This will ensure data from the normal school year is not deleted. Pause End Date: the day before you want to resume syncing data. For example, if you wish to resume syncing with your sis on August 1st, set the Pause End Date to July 31. 

 

 

 
 Modifying a scheduled pause

 To modify a sync pause that has already been scheduled, navigate to your Sync Settings page:

 • Click within the box next to Pause Start Date or Pause End Date .
• Select the desired date(s).
• Select Update to confirm the change.
 
 

 
 Removing a pause

 To remove a sync pause and resume syncing data to Clever at any time, navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. Under Schedule Sync Pause, click Clear.

title: For Clever Admins: Assigning "subject" to sections
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does Clever determine ‘subject’? 3. What if my sync doesn’t include a ‘subject’ field? 4. How can I ensure Clever chooses the correct subject? 5. Miscategorized Subjects 

 

 Overview

 Syncing subjects accurately is essential to getting the most out of Clever. Many learning applications require sections to have subjects, and assigning subjects correctly will also make it easier to share data by sections. This article outlines how Clever determines ‘subject’ for each section and how to sync ‘subject’ with Clever to ensure accuracy.

 
 How does Clever determine ‘subject’?

 If your data sync includes a ‘subject’ field, Clever categorizes sections into subject categories based on the values you provide. For example, ‘subject’ field values “English”, “Reading”, and “Writing” are all sorted into “english/language arts”. Everything that can’t be sorted (including blanks) will be assigned to the subject “other”.

 All sections will be assigned one of the following subject values:

 • arts and music
• english/language arts
• homeroom/advisory
• interventions/online learning
• language
• math
• PE and health
• science
• social studies
• technology and engineering
• other 
 
 If Clever can't determine the subject based on the values given in the 'subject' field, then our system looks to values in the section 'name' field, and lastly, to values in the course_name field.

 
 What if my sync doesn’t include a subject field?

 If you aren’t syncing a ‘subject’ field, Clever categorizes sections into the subject categories above based on what terms are included in the ‘section_name’ field. For example, sections with section_name values of “Algebra II Honors 3A”, “5th Grade Mathematics”, and “P4 - Calc - Johnson” are all assigned the subject “math”.

 If Clever can’t determine the subject based on section 'name’, Clever will look at the ‘course_name’ field for terms that indicate subject.

 Any section whose subject cannot be determined will be assigned the subject “other”.

 
 How can I ensure Clever chooses the correct subject?

 There are two ways to make sure Clever chooses the right subject for each section:

 1. Add ‘subject’ field to your sync 

 Syncing a ‘subject’ field to Clever with values that exactly match the subject categories listed above is the best way to make sure subjects are accurate in Clever. As long as you provide one of Clever’s accepted subject values, Clever will use the exact value you provide.

 If you’re syncing data with Clever via Auto Sync, you’ll want to determine which field is used to sync subjects to Clever, then populate that field with the above subject values.

 • If you’re using a Clever-Managed Auto Sync , contact Clever Support to determine which SIS field is used for subject. You can also tell us if you’d prefer to pull a different subject field from your SIS.
• For SIS-Managed Auto Syncs , please reach out directly to your SIS to determine which fields can be used to send subject values to Clever.
 
 For SFTP Syncs , add a column with header “Subject” and include a value for each section that exactly matches one of the subject categories listed above.

 2. Change the value of section ‘name’ or ‘course_name’ 

 If you’re not able to sync a ‘subject’ field to Clever, we will make our best guess based on the terms included in the section ‘name’ and ‘course_name’ fields. To improve accuracy, try some of these tips:

 • Include one of Clever’s subject values in your section or course names.
• Use full terms whenever possible. For example, “comp” is interpreted as “composition” (subject: “english/language arts”), not “computers” (subject: “technology and engineering”).
• Creative names can sometimes be miscategorized, so opt for simple and commonly used terms. For example, “astronomy” gets matched to “science”, whereas “rocket ships” would be matched to “other”.
 
 
 Miscategorized Subjects

 Clever makes the best-educated guess to sort each section into the above subjects. If you see a large number of sections assigned the wrong subject value, please request help from our Support Team .

title: Clever Badges: How do I manage Badge PINs?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview ​ 2. Setting Badge PINs 3. Troubleshooting forgotten PINs 

 Overview

 As a security precaution, some districts require students to enter a secure PIN when logging in to Clever with a Badge. In addition, Clever requires a Badge PIN for all students in seventh grade or above who log in using a Badge, minus students with an IEP or ELL status. Clever Admins can disable Badge PINS for students in these grades levels on an individual basis, but not for a group of students. 

 A district can choose to adjust the PIN requirement settings for Badges to begin at an earlier grade. This can be managed from the Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges > Settings tab. 

 

 Please note: Classroom MFA users who are in seventh grade or above won’t be required to enter a PIN when using a Badge to log into Clever if they are enrolled in an active policy. 

 

 
 

 Setting Badge PINs

 To set a PIN, students will navigate to clever.com/badges , hold their Badge up to the camera, and then be authenticated to the page displayed below. The first time a student uses a PIN-enabled Badge, they will see the screen below prompting them to set a PIN. On this screen, the student will enter their new PIN twice and then be signed in to Clever! After this initial setup, your students will be able to log into Clever using their Badge and their PIN. 

 Badge PINs can be manually enabled or disabled from a student's details card in the Clever Dashboard > Data browser or from the student profile page using the Support tools drop-down arrow. These Badge management options are available to Clever Admins, District Help Desk, and School Tech Leads.
