 Please note: Pin requirements are set when a Badge is first generated. As such, if a student's ELL or IEP status changes later, Clever will not automatically update the requirement for a Badge Pin. Also, any student who has a Badge outside the scoped Grades with Badges setting will need a PIN (including IEP or ELL students who are set to Create individually rather than Auto include in Step 1 of the Badges settings ). To reset the student's pin requirements, their current Badge should be voided and a new Badge generated for them. 

 
 

 Troubleshooting forgotten PINs

 • Students : Here is a resource to share with students who need help with their PIN: For Students: How do I log in to Clever using a Badge? 
• Teachers : Here is how teachers can help their students log in using a backup PIN: For Teachers: How do I help my student log into Clever?

title: For Clever Admins: Syncing demographic data/sensitive fields
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Syncing demographic data to Clever 3. Clever-Managed Auto Syncs 4. SIS-Managed Auto and SFTP Syncs 5. Sharing sensitive data with applications 

 Overview

 Clever can sync a variety of demographic data, such as ELL status, IEP status, and Free/reduced-price lunch (FRL) status, among others. Because the data in these fields is considered sensitive, Clever doesn't automatically share this data with any applications connected to your school organization. Still, these fields can be included in the application sharing if needed. 

 Clever Tip: Not all apps support all demographic data/sensitive fields. If you have specific demographic data you need to share with an app, please contact the app directly to check if they support syncing that field. 

 

 
 Syncing demographic data/sensitive fields to Clever

 The first step is to ensure that sensitive fields are synced to Clever. Please navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data browser > Students tab to verify if these data points are available. Use the filter option to filter students by the demographic field in question. Then, select the value drop-down menu. If this search is not available, it likely means the data is not in Clever, and therefore, you can't search using these fields. 

 

 
 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs

 School organizations that utilize the Clever-managed auto-sync can customize their sync from the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings > Students tab . If the SIS field you wish to export is not available in the dropdown, please contact Clever Support for assistance.

 Please note: OneRoster API syncs cannot sync ell_status, frl_status, and iep_status. They can be manually uploaded via Custom data. Visit this article for instructions . 

 

 
 SIS-Managed Auto and SFTP Syncs

 School organizations that utilize an SIS-managed SFTP Auto Sync or an SFTP sync will need to contact their SIS, or whoever configured the SFTP export, directly to begin syncing these data points to Clever. This data will need to be added to the students.csv export for the school organization with the column headers:

 • ell_status
• iep_status
• frl_status
• preferred_last_name
• preferred_middle_name
• preferred_first_name 
• gifted status
• home_language ( see this section for a list of available languages)
• section_504_status
• disability_status 
• disability_type
 
 Please also review Clever's general SFTP guidelines here . These instructions contain information about Clever-supported column headers and formatting requirements.

 

 Supported home languages 

 Check out this article for all of the possible options for home languages. Given the number of languages supported, this list is not included in our general SFTP guidelines .

 When designating the home language within the students.csv file, you can use either the home language or the home language code.

 

 
 Sharing sensitive data with applications

 Sharing demographic data/sensitive fields with applications 

 You can locate the Field Access information by navigating to a specific application's overview page from your Clever Dashboard and choosing the application Settings tab. Currently, Field Access gives you an overview of the data your applications have indicated they're using. Each learning application partner configures its required/optional fields for school organization data. Please get in touch with the application support teams directly if you have questions. Select the Advanced settings option, and then Customize fields shared with. You can customize the student data available to the application from the Edit Student Fields tab. 

 

 • The list on the left shows the fields available to share with the application. 
• The list on the right shows the list of fields currently being shared with the application. 
 
 Move field(s) from the left to the right to share the sensitive data with the application. Once your changes have been made, select Next and Save . The application should sync the newly shared data within the next 24 hours. Setting up sharing rules to share data based on demographic data/sensitive field information 

 By configuring application sharing permissions , school organizations can determine what data they would like to make available to applications. Sharing permissions also help schools to determine which users (e.g., teachers, students, staff) will have access to different applications in their Clever Portals.

 Custom sharing rules allow school organizations to share only data that meets certain criteria. Once the field data has been synced to Clever and is available in the Clever Dashboard , sharing rules can be configured to share by the data associated with fields. For example:

 • Share only students whose ELL_Status is 'Y'
• Share only students whose gifted_status is 'N'
 
 Configuring sharing rules to share using field data is completed by creating a sharing rule to 'Custom share by student.' From there, school organizations can select the field they would like to use for sharing data. Please visit Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules for more information about setting up sharing rules. 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules

title: Clever Badges: Piloting at your district
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Tips for a successful pilot 

 Overview

 Clever Badges make it even easier for your students to access their learning applications through Clever so that your teachers can spend more time instructing and less time troubleshooting username and password errors.

 Clever Badges are offered at no charge to districts and only require that your student’s laptop or desktop device have a webcam enabled. Students will go to clever.com/badges or your district's login page and then hold their Badge up to the camera to log in.

 If your students are using Chromebooks, or Windows devices with Active Directory logins, Clever Badges can be used to log students into their computer, Clever, and connected apps all at once! To set up these integrations, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges page. Then select the Chromebook or Windows tab depending, and follow the instructions in the wizard (this should take about 10 minutes). 

 

 Tips for a successful pilot 

 • Start small . Pick one or two elementary school sites at which you can initiate your pilot. Our distribution process allows you to select which schools should participate. Include a media specialist, school site leader, or teacher that embraces the adoption of new technology to help you champion this new tool!
• Educate your teachers. Using this helpful article , you can show teachers how to generate badges so that they can help maintain Badges for new students.
• Make them fun and easy. Whether you place your badges on student folders or create lanyards for students to wear around their necks, ensure your Badges will be be easy to use and won’t be easily lost or damaged.
 
 Start now! Get a head start so that you can roll out more broadly for next back to school.

title: Clever Badges: Overview and setup
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup Requirements & Instructions 3. Generating & Printing Clever Badges 4. Logging in with Clever Badges 5. Voiding Badges 6. Badge Invalidation 7. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 With Clever Badges, districts can allow students to use a physical Badge to log in to Clever without needing to enter login credentials (i.e., a username and password). Instead, students can simply hold their Clever Badge up to their device webcam to be logged in automatically. By default, Clever Badges are intended for younger students or those with special needs. However, if your district would prefer to use Badges for older students as well, please visit this article for information on setting up Badges that include pin requirements: Clever Badges: How do I manage Badge PINs? Please check out the following short video to see how Clever Badges can save both teachers and students time in the classroom! 

 

 

 This article is to help you set up your Clever Badges. If you need help troubleshooting an issue, please see the following:

 • Clever Badges: How do I troubleshoot issues with Badges into Chromebooks? 
• Single Sign-On (SSO): Chromebooks - Setup & Troubleshooting 
 
 

 
 Setup Requirements & Instructions 

 Setup Requirements 

 You will need the following items for the implementation and use of Badges: 

 • Compatible devices and/or internet browsers:
 • Chromebooks 
• iOS devices (e.g., iPads, iPhones)
• Windows devices 
• Browsers : Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and Internet Explorer (2013 or later)
• Android devices with a webcam and a compatible mobile browser: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, or Internet Explorer
 

• A printer to print physical Badges on paper or labels
• Computers and devices with webcams
 • Please note that any variation of webcams will work as long as they are compatible with student devices. 
 

 
 This video is incredibly helpful for helping you set up Clever Badges for your schools.

 

 

 Setup Instructions 

 To begin, on your Clever Dashboard, please navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges > Settings tab. Under the Access box, click Edit 

 

 Step 1: Specify students 

 Please begin by choosing which grade levels and students you would like to enable for Badges. The drop-down menu will allow you to select specific grades. Clever will also automatically provide you with the option to include students with IEPs or students who are ELL . 

 Please note that Clever will automatically invalidate badges as students age out of their district’s policy. For example, if badges are enabled for Grade 2 and below, once a student is promoted from 2nd grade to 3rd grade, their badge will be invalidated. 

 

 Step 2: Add Schools 

 You can now choose all schools or the specific schools you would like to enable for Badges, which will work in conjunction with the student grade settings you selected in the first step. Once done, select Save to generate your student Badges! 

 

 

 

 Step 3: Edit Visibility (only if using Badge with Chromebook) 

 If students will use Badges to log into Chromebooks, make sure Visibility is on. Navigate to Authentication > Badges > Settings , then click Edit under Visibility. Turn the toggle on so you see a green check mark.

 

 

 

 

 
 Generating & printing Clever Badges

 

 Clever Tip: If Badges aren't available to print for a school or grade level, navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges > Settings tab > Access box and confirm if Badges are enabled for the correct school and grade levels. 

 

 Please note: Badges printed to PDF will match the Avery label template size for 2” square Avery labels perfectly! Additionally, printing Badges on sticky labels is a great way to ensure students don't lose them, which is more likely to occur when printed on paper. Clever also highly recommends providing students with Badge holders such as those seen here . 

 Generating and printing Badges in bulk 

 By navigating to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Badges , you can select to download the Badges for individual schools, multiple schools, or all enabled schools. Clever Admins can choose to download active Badges or those that are associated with new users who have never had Badges generated previously. 

 

 Depending on the file size, Clever will either download the Badges directly to your computer or email you a secure .zip file that can be downloaded to your desktop if the file exceeds our standard download limitations. The file will contain the following:

 • Folders with the names of each school 
 • Subfolders with student Badges by grade level, including the Badge QR codes and names of each student.
 • PDF of the grade level Badges
• PNG images of Badges
 

• Subfolders with student Badges by sections (i.e., courses/classes), including the Badge QR codes and names of each student.
 • PDF of the grade level Badges
• PNG images of Badges
 

 

 
 

 Generating Individual Student Badges 

 Badges can also be generated or voided on a per-student basis. To do this, please begin by searching for the student using the Clever Dashboard search bar or by navigating to the Clever Dashboard > Data browser > Students tab . Once you've located the student's record to access their profile page, select Support Tools in the top right to void their Badge or generate a new one. 

 Please note: If the option to void or generate a Badge isn't available for a student, the student isn't included in the scope specified when you or another admin set up Clever Badges. 

 Badge options highlighted" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000bokD&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl00000irLWP"> Badge pins can also be updated from a student's profile page in Clever. For more information, please visit: Clever Badges: How do I manage Badge PINs? 

 
 Logging in with Clever Badges

 Below is the expected login flow for a student using their Badge to authenticate with Clever:

 • The student will navigate to either clever.com/badges or the district's custom Clever login page . Clever Badges are designed to be used directly with Clever, and a QR reader won't log you in to Clever.
• They will select the Clever Badge login option.
• If they haven't previously provided access, they will be prompted to allow Clever access to their device camera so they can scan their Badge. 
• At this point, their webcam will activate so they can hold their Badge up to the camera. Once Clever's sensors recognize the Badge, the user will see a green check mark and be logged in successfully.
 • Please note that if the student sees a red X, their Badge login was unsuccessful. 
 

 
 
 Please note: Clever Badges are an independent login method, separate from other login methods such as Google, Active Directory, and Clever Passwords, which means Clever does not have access to your students' login credentials for alternative Identity Providers (IdPs). Please get in touch with your district's tech support for issues with student usernames and passwords. 

 

 
 Voiding Badges

 Clever Badges can be voided and regenerated if needed. If a student loses their Badge, the best practice would be to direct them to immediately reach out to their teacher, IT support, or a Clever Admin. The missing Badge should be voided to protect the student's information. After you void a badge, you can issue a new one. Voiding Clever Badges is a safeguard for protecting students. Therefore, this isn't a feature that can be disabled. Clever Badges are comparable to a student's login credentials. If their login credentials or Clever Badge are compromised, they should be reset. To void a student's Clever Badge, Clever Admins can navigate to the student's profile page using the Clever Search bar at the top of the Clever Dashboard . Choose the Support Tools option to the right of the page to void the student's Badge. A new Badge can also be generated from the Support Tools menu. 

 Clever Badges can also be void

title: School year rollover: Overview, preparation & recommendations
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Summer sync pauses 3. What to expect when your sync is paused 4. Pause Dates 5. Rollover and section_ids 6. Troubleshooting data for new school year 7. Preparing for the first day of school 8. Changing your SIS, sync method, or SIS IDs 

 Overview

 This article outlines Clever’s school year rollover process and how school organizations can prepare. For summer school recommendations, check out this article .

 In Clever, a school year rollover is typically the period during which a school organization transfers data from the previous school year to the upcoming one. For schools that operate on a year-round schedule, this may be when students move from one grade level to another, or when all class rosters change (e.g., students change teachers, teachers change classes, etc.)

 

 
 Summer sync pauses 

 IMPORTANT In 2026, Clever automatically pauses and removes pauses from syncs based on your school organization’s country: USA: Syncs pause on May 29, and we remove pauses between July 27–31. Canada: Syncs pause on June 30, and we remove pauses on August 14. UK: Syncs pause on July 15, and we remove pauses on September 1. Australia and Brazil: Syncs pause on December 20, and we remove pauses on January 20. For all other countries, we use the USA dates by default. You can modify these dates (or remove them altogether) under your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. 

 

 During the pause, Clever stops processing data updates, letting your school organization update SIS (Student Information System) or CSV files without interrupting students, teachers, or staff access over the summer.

 Current school year data will essentially be "frozen" in Clever for the duration of the sync pause.

 Please note: If you'd like to pause syncing your data to a specific application, please see this article: For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app . This tool is perfect for semester rollover and summer school, but it shouldn't be confused with a SIS sync pause. 

 

 If you wish to freeze your data within Clever over the summer, we advise setting your sync pause to a date before the last day of school . Please note that your SIS may attempt to delete current data on the last day of school. We recommend reaching out to your SIS to determine if this is the case so that you can plan accordingly. 

 Once the sync pause has ended, Clever will automatically resume syncing with Clever-managed syncs and will resume processing SFTP data file uploads. 

 Unless otherwise specified in your settings, Clever automatically removes pauses between July 28 and August 1 to allow Clever admins enough time for review and testing (e.g. ensuring new users are able to log in, app sharing rules are correct, etc.) before the start of school. The Pause Start Date and Pause End Date can be viewed and modified by admins from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. 

 

 

 

 Did you know? Application partners manage the app syncing process on their end, so each app sync could be unique. For questions about how apps handle school year rollover, please reach out to the app partner directly. 

 

 
 What to expect when your sync is paused

 While your SIS sync to Clever is paused: 

 • 
 All Clever admins with Sync pause email notifications turned on will receive an email notification the day before the pause begins. You can see all fellow Clever admins associated with your account at Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page.

• Your sync status will show as Paused in your Clever Dashboard. 
 
 

 • Please note: Updates to data will not sync from your SIS to Clever during a pause, so changes made in your SIS will not interrupt student, teacher or staff access to Clever or their connected applications. • SFTP uploads will not be processed, even if data is sent to Clever
• Clever-managed syncs will run but not process any data changes
 

• Applications may still be retrieving data from Clever over the summer even if your SIS sync is paused.
 • If you'd like your application(s) to stop retrieving data from Clever during the sync pause, you can pause app syncs in Clever using the App Sync Pause settings. For more on this, please see Pause your sync for a specific app . 
 

• Clever admins can still update sharing permissions for applications.
 
 

 When your sync pause ends or is canceled: 

 • All Clever admin with Sync pause email notifications turned on will receive an email notification the day before the pause ends. You can see all fellow Clever admin at Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team .
• Clever will once again resume:
 • Syncing with your SIS (auto-syncs)
• Or processing your next SFTP upload (SFTP syncs)
 

• 
 If the rostering data in Clever changes significantly once the sync resumes, your sync will be placed on an automatic hold as a safeguard to provide you time to review the rostering changes before they are processed. Of note, if Clever detects an unusual amount of potentially disruptive data changes, the Clever Support team will be notified when you attempt to approve these changes to assist you with review. Learn more about sync holds .

 • Admins can review sync changes before they are processed from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page by using the Download Changes option to the right of each data type.
 
 

 
 

 

 
 Pause Dates

 Admins can make adjustments to the sync Pause Start Date and Pause End Date from the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page as follows:

 • Pause Start Date : This will be the first day your sync goes on pause, which should coincide with the last day of the current school year.
 • Please note: Your current school year data should still be active in your SIS on this day. 
 

• Pause End Date : This will be the last day your sync is paused, which should be the day before your SIS data is active for the upcoming school year. 
 • For example: If next school year's data will be active in your SIS on 8/15/25, your Pause End Date should be set for 8/14/25 . This might also be your last day of summer. 
• Sync pauses run from 12:00 AM on the pause start date to 11:59 PM on the pause end date, local time. For example, if you wish to resume syncing with Clever on August 10th, please set the Pause End Date to August 9th.
 

 
 Please note: If possible, we recommend unpausing your sync a week or two in advance of the new school year to give you time to review the changes, see if there are any errors, and address any issues that may arise. 

 

 
 Rollover and section_ids

 In Clever, a school year rollover is typically the period during which a school organization transfers data from the previous school year to the upcoming one. Rollover is a critical time to consider your section_ids in Clever. Clever maps all historical data to unique Clever IDs, which are directly tied to your SIS IDs (school_id, student_id, teacher_id, section_id). 

 If these IDs are reused for new classes, Clever and your integrated apps may treat them as ongoing sections from last year, leading to merged rosters and inaccurate historical data. To prevent this, Clever can reset the Clever IDs for your sections, establishing a clean slate for the new school year.

 Starting July 2026 , we’re introducing a unique rollover hold that triggers if it’s been 300+ days since your last rollover, and there are significant data changes.

 Unlike a standard hold, you’ll be prompted to confirm if these changes represent a new school year before approving or discarding them. Depending on your choice, Clever will either discard your hold, accept your approval and refresh your data, or send it to Clever Support for a quick review to ensure everything stays accurate downstream. We’re here to help you navigate this process and keep your data clean for the upcoming year! More details will be available in this article in July.

 

 Reusing Section IDs: Clever discourages reusing se

title: Secure Sync: Changing Student Information Systems
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Process 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 Overview

 Is your district moving to a new student information system (SIS)? No worries—Clever has you covered! Clever can assist in keeping your downstream application data clean and ready to go.

 This process is a partnership; we are here to ensure your success! Since multiple steps are involved, we recommend planning and starting the process as early as possible to ensure minimal disruption to your students and teachers. 

 

 

 Please note: To prevent user disruption, Clever does not recommend completing this transition mid-year. Summer or winter breaks are the best time to work through this process due to the required sync hold that must be enabled during the conversion process. 

 

 
 

 Process

 • Identify how you will sync data to Clever Clever provides districts with multiple methods to sync their data. If your district is transitioning to one of the SISs listed here , you can use an Auto Sync to send data to Clever. If your SIS is not listed, you can use an SFTP sync. Learn more about syncing data via SFTP here . 
• Confirm the primary identifiers for records Clever recommends keeping the primary identifier (student_id, teacher_id, staff_id, and school_id) the same when transitioning SISs. Maintaining the same sis_id will protect you from duplicate or lost records. If your sis_ids are changing, Clever can complete a matching process to preserve as much historical data as possible. You can learn more about the process for changing SIS IDs here . 
• Populate data in your new SIS To complete the transition in Clever, you will need active data in your new SIS. We recommend completing this 3-4 weeks before you would like the conversion process to be completed. 
• (Optional) Make a note of your current field mappings if your sync type supports field mappings Some Clever-Managed Auto Syncs allow districts to view or complete their field mappings on the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings page. If you see your field mappings available, we recommend taking a screenshot of each data type’s mappings for future reference. 
• Contact Clever Support Once data is ready in your SIS, contact Clever Support and let us know the name of your SIS and your preferred method of syncing (Auto Sync, SFTP, OneRoster, etc.). We will consult with you to ensure your account is set up for success! 
• Configure your Auto Sync or SFTP files Before Clever can complete the migration, we will need a successful data connection. If you have chosen an Auto Sync, work with our support team or your SIS provider to set up the data sync. Setup directions for Clever-managed Auto Syncs are linked here . Configure files using the directions found here if you will be syncing via SFTP. Please allow up to one week to troubleshoot getting your sync or SFTP files successfully configured. 

• Migrate data Contact Clever Support and let us know when you are ready to upload your first set of data from your new SIS into Clever. Before you upload new data, please consult our team on which applications to pause your data sync with. We will also enable protection settings for your account to ensure your teacher and students are minimally affected by the changes in data. All data syncing between the district and Clever will pause during this time. However, users will still be able to access Clever and their applications. Once the safety settings are applied, we will have you sync your uploaded data into Clever! If the data looks good, we will complete a matching process to preserve historical data where possible. Please allow up to 2-3 weeks for the matching process to be completed. During this time, your account will appear with a sync status of "On Hold" or "System Maintenance In Progress." This is normal and will be resolved once Clever Support has finished the migration process. 
• Confirm data changes, sync with apps The final step of the process is for you to approve the data changes. Since you have switched to a new SIS, there are probably significant changes to your data, and the sync will be placed on hold pending your approval. Review the data on your SIS sync page and confirm or discard the changes as appropriate. For more details on Sync Holds, see here . 

 Once the data is live in Clever, please do not remove any application pauses until you have received confirmation from the application that it is ready for new data. Applications that are not paused will receive the data within 24 hours. 

 Application partners manage pulling data from Clever and processing it into their system. If you have any questions about the effect of data changes in an application, please contact the application's support team. They will be happy to assist! 
 
 Ready to get started or have a question? Contact our Support Team . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Is there an option if I need more guidance and would like additional services from Clever for my sync conversion? 

 • Yes! We offer Sync Conversion Consulting Services. The sync conversion process is a collaborative effort involving the district, Clever, and connected applications to achieve a smooth transition. While our sync conversion team is committed to helping you meet your goals, opting for our paid services can provide additional consultation and support to streamline the process further. Please let us know if you're interested in exploring this option or have any questions! 
 
 Can we upload a partial data set and send the rest of the data at a later time? 

 • No, any accounts/data currently in Clever that you want to continue syncing to Clever must be included in your initial sync from your new SIS. For example, suppose you have ten teachers currently in Clever and only upload seven in your initial sync. Only including seven teachers will delete the three you didn't include from Clever and their connected applications. Even if you add the existing accounts later, Clever and their connected apps will see them as new accounts, and they will lose all historical data.
 
 What if I don't need any historical data preserved? I have old data in Clever and we haven't been actively using it, so I don't need historical data maintained. 

 • If this is the case, you can forego the sync conversion process. Teachers will lose any Teacher Pages in Clever that they have created, and all users will lose their historical data in their connected applications. Please provide in writing that you do not want our teams to preserve the historical data and that you understand that your users will no longer have any historical data in Clever or their connected apps.
 
 Why does the sync or SIS ID conversion take 2-3 weeks? 

 • Anytime you execute record linkage between data sets, the integrity of the data is always at risk. Sync conversions involve numerous steps that must be performed accurately, and depending on the complexity and size of the data, this can take time.
• During a school break is the best time to complete a sync conversion because the account is already often in a sync pause status. Many districts are reaching out to our teams to complete a sync conversion during the winter or summer break, which can impact timelines for completion.
 
 tags: sync conversions

title: For Clever Admins: Troubleshooting - Changing email addresses or email domains in Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to update user emails for a SFTP or SFTP (SIS-Managed) sync 3. How to update user emails for a Clever-Managed auto sync 4. How to update manually added (side-loaded) user emails 5. Important considerations when updating user emails 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 As you use Clever, you may need to update your student, teacher, or staff email addresses or domains (@ example.com ) if you change the format for user emails. Updating emails is a quick change that Clever sees as an update to user accounts. You can change any data point for your users, such as their email address, grade, last name, or graduation year without any need to notify the Clever team.

 Please note: If you will be changing your user’s SIS IDs (such as teacher_id, student_id, or school_id), please contact Clever Support. 

 

 
 How to update user emails for an SFTP or SFTP (SIS-Managed) sync

 If you utilize an SFTP or SFTP (SIS-Managed) sync, you will need to work on updating the email addresses included in the CSV files you are sending to Clever. If you manage your students.csv, teachers.csv, and staff.csv, please update the student_email, teacher_email, and staff_email columns to include the new email addresses for your users. If you only update emails for one user type, you only need to update that file. Once you have your updated files, you can upload them to Clever to update your user’s email addresses.

 If your SIS manages the sending of your CSV files to Clever on your behalf, please contact your SIS team directly for more information on updating the email addresses included in these files. Once you’ve worked with your SIS team to update the emails included in these files and they send the updated email addresses via the CSV files, the emails will update in Clever.

 
 How to update user emails for a Clever-Managed auto sync

 Please review the default fields for your sync type in the article that corresponds with your sync type below:

 • 
 Skyward API Sync 

• 
 Skyward Auto Sync 

• 
 Infinite Campus API Sync 

• 
 Infinite Campus Auto Sync 

• 
 Powerschool Plugin Sync 

• 
 Powerschool Auto Sync 

• 
 Aeries Auto Sync 

• 
 Blackbaud API Sync 

 
 To update your email addresses in Clever, you will want to work to update the email addresses in the field Clever is pulling from your SIS. If you have a custom mapping in place for emails, or if your sync type supports custom mappings and you’d like to explore syncing emails from an alternate field in your SIS to Clever, please contact Clever Support for further assistance.

 

 
 How to update manually added (side-loaded) user emails

 If you manually manage student and teacher emails by uploading student_emails.csv and teacher_emails.csv, you’ll want to update these files with the new user emails. Once you’ve updated the email addresses for your users in each file, please upload the new files via your Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page.

 

 For more information, please see our article Manually Uploading (i.e. side-loading) user emails to Clever . 

 
 Important considerations when updating user emails

 • 
 Updating all users' accounts will likely cause a sync hold. Once the data sync completes, you can approve the hold on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page. For more information on sync holds please see our article For Clever Admins: Sync Holds .

• 
 If you use Clever Passwords as your identity provider (IdP), please check if you are using the “Email” field for your usernames. If so, you will need to reset Clever Passwords once the new emails sync. This is because Clever Passwords' usernames and passwords are not updated dynamically. Click here for more information on resetting passwords. 

• 
 Do you utilize SSO (Saved Passwords) applications, and if so is the "Email" field utilized for the username field for any Saved Passwords applications? If so, after syncing new email addresses, you may need to clear the cache if users are having issues logging into those SSO (Saved Password) applications.

• 
 Do you utilize any SAML applications? If so, are you using emails to match accounts? The emails will need to match between Clever and the application to ensure SAML login success.

• 
 Please notify any application partners syncing email addresses via Clever of your upcoming email changes to ensure they are prepared for the changes.

• 
 If Clever Administrator, District Help Desk, or District Curriculum Lead emails are also changing , these will need to be updated on the individual’s Clever Dashboard > Profile page . Each admin will need to update their own email by selecting the "Edit" button in the "Account information" box on their Profile page.

• 
 Please review and update your Tech Support Contact email if required.

• 
 If you utilize Clever Identity Management (IDM): 

 • 
 If you provision Google accounts via Clever IDM, by default IDM references the SIS email field as the source of truth when matching and updating accounts. Updating email addresses in Clever will cause Clever IDM to update the emails for these users in Google.

• 
 Clever IDM for Active Directory and Entra ID by default looks first to the District Username field as a source of truth. Updating user emails won't directly impact emails in Active Directory or Entra ID unless you also update the username field.

 

 
 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Do I need to notify Clever that my user’s email addresses will be changing? 

 • 
 No. You only need to notify Clever if your user’s sis_ids (teacher_id, student_id, staff_id, etc.) will also be changing.

 
 Why did my sync go on hold when I updated email addresses? 

 • 
 Sync holds are to prevent Clever Admins from making unintended changes to their data when large data changes occur. Review the changes on your Last Attempted Sync page and if the changes are as expected you can approve the hold.

 
 What steps should I take to ensure minimal disruption to students and teachers? 

 • 
 Review the Important considerations when updating user emails section above for guidance on steps you can take to prevent disruptions.

title: Troubleshooting: Data incorrect in Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshooting • SFTP syncs • SFTP (sis-managed) syncs • Clever-managed auto syncs 3. FAQs 

 Overview 

 There may be discrepancies between the data in your student information system (SIS) and Clever. Some common issues are missing teacher, student, staff, or section data, or perhaps a user's primary school in Clever doesn't match what exists in your SIS. To change the data syncing to Clever, in most cases you'll need to update data in your SIS or the files your SIS is sending to Clever. It's not possible to manually update data in Clever that is synced via your SIS. Below is a quick guide to help you troubleshoot missing or incorrect data in Clever. 

 Data incorrect in app: Most applications sync with Clever daily. Please allow up to 24 hours for newly updated or shared data in Clever to be reflected in your connected applications. For more information, visit: For Clever Admins: Apps - Sync times . If the data is correct in Clever but incorrect in an app, please contact the app's support team directly. Applications are responsible for syncing data from Clever. 

 
 

 

 
 Troubleshooting 

 Depending on the type of student information system (SIS) sync you have configured with Clever, the process might be different. Your sync type (how your data is getting to Clever) can be found on the Data sources > SIS sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 SFTP sync: 

 If your sync type is SFTP (Secure File Transfer Protocol), this means that Clever is receiving CSV files directly from your Student Information System (SIS). The configuration and transmission of these files are managed by a contact at your district rather than Clever. 

 
 
 Steps to fix data issues 

 
 • 
 Confirm the data is correct in your student information system (SIS).

• 
 Upload an updated set of data files to Clever. Please allow up to 30 minutes for the sync to complete. 

• 
 On the Sync page, confirm your sync is not on hold or hitting a sync error .

• 
 Review the data files you are sending to Clever for accuracy. You can download them on the Data sources > SIS sync page.

• 
 If the data is incorrect in the data files, contact your SIS manager -- Clever Support cannot assist with investigating data export errors.

• 
 If the data is correct in the data files but incorrect in Clever, please contact Clever Support.

 
 
 Tip to fix rollover issues 
 The data in Clever will rollover once we receive data for the new school year and the resulting data hold has been released. Please contact the sync manager at your district if you have any questions. 

 
 
 

 SFTP (sis-managed) sync: 

 If your sync type is SFTP (SIS-managed), this means that Clever is receiving CSV files directly from your SIS. The configuration and transmission of these files are managed by your SIS rather than Clever. 

 
 Steps to fix data issues 

 
 • 
 Confirm the data is correct in your student information system (SIS).

• 
 Contact your SIS support team to upload an updated set of data files to Clever. Please allow up to 30 minutes for the sync to complete. 

• 
 On the Sync page, confirm your sync is not on hold or hitting a sync error .

• 
 Review the data files you are sending to Clever for accuracy. You can download them on the Sync > Last Attempted Sync page. Learn more here !

• 
 If the data is incorrect in the data files, contact your SIS support team -- Clever Support cannot assist with investigating data export errors.

• 
 If the data is correct in the data files but incorrect in Clever, please contact Clever Support.

 
 
 Tip to fix rollover issues The data in Clever will rollover once we receive data for the new school year and the resulting data hold has been released. Please contact your SIS if you have any questions. 
 
 

 Clever-Managed Auto Sync: 

 If your sync type is Clever-Managed Auto Sync, this means that your syncs are automated daily and are primarily managed by the Clever Support team. These syncs securely transfer rostering data from your district's student information system (SIS) to Clever.

 
 
 Steps to fix data issues 

 
 • Confirm the data is correct in your student information system (SIS).
• 
 Navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page and push Sync now. Please allow up to 30 minutes for the sync to complete.

• 
 On the Sync page, confirm your sync is not on hold or hitting a sync error .

• 
 If you've confirmed the data is correct in your SIS, use the documentation for your sync type to troubleshoot the data. 

 
 • 
 • 
 Aeries 

• 
 PowerSchool 

• 
 Infinite Campus API 

• 
 SchoolInsight 

• 
 Skyward Auto 

• 
 Skyward API 

• 
 Blackbaud API Sync 

 

 
 
 Tips to fix rollover issues 
 • All syncs will encounter a hold when you rollover to the new school year. Please confirm the hold has been released in your Clever Dashboard. 
• 
 See the rollover directions for your sync type to troubleshoot issues related to rollover. 

 • 
 Aeries 

• 
 PowerSchool 

• 
 Infinite Campus API 

• 
 SchoolInsight 

• 
 Skyward Auto 

• 
 Blackbaud API Sync 

 

 
 
 
 If the above steps do not resolve your issue, please contact Clever Support. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Below are some common questions about incorrect data.

 Why is a student or teacher's primary school wrong in Clever, but correct in our SIS?

 • For context, Clever dynamically updates the primary school assignment for students based on the total number of sections they have at a particular school site. For example, if a student has seven sections at School A and three sections at School B, their primary school would be School A in Clever. 
• If you'd rather Clever use students' primary school exactly as it's listed in your SIS, we can disable our default logic for either students or teachers or both user types. Once this logic is disabled, Clever will look to your SIS data as the source of truth for primary school assignments instead of the student or teacher's sections. Please note that this is a global change that will affect all users of the same type in Clever and not just individual students. 
• For more information, please see this article from our Help Center: For District Admins: Primary School Assignment .
 
 How can a teacher or student be associated with more than one school site?

 • Teachers and students can only have one primary school site in Clever. However, their additional school sites are associated with their Clever account through their section data. 
 
 Why are the section names in Clever different than what I expected?

 • Section names in Clever are auto-generated to ensure uniqueness, improving recognition and usability for teachers and students. However, districts or schools can choose to sync their own section names to Clever. For more information on section names, please see this article that has information on how to sync section names: For Clever Admins: Section Names 
 
 Why is a student and/or teacher not syncing/showing up in Clever?

 • If you have gone over the troubleshooting steps above and the user is still not syncing to Clever, you can reach out to Clever Support. Some auto syncs have specific criteria as to what is required for certain user types to sync to Clever. 
 
 Why are some of our sections showing the wrong subject?

 • If your data sync includes a subject field, Clever categorizes sections into subject categories based on the values you provide. For example, subject field values English , Reading , and Writing are all sorted into english/language arts . Everything that can’t be sorted (including blanks) will be assigned to the subject other . You can find more in-depth information here: For Clever Admins: Assigning "subject" to sections .
 
 Why are my sections not syncing to Clever? 

 • In order for section data to sync to Clever, the section must have students and a primary teacher assigned to it. 
 
 Why are classes with multiple grade levels showing up as "other"?

 • C

title: For Clever Admins: How often will my data sync to Clever?
text:
To see when your data last synced to Clever, view the Sync Status on your Clever Dashboard: The frequency of your uploads to Clever depends on your sync type, as outlined below. 

 Clever-Managed Auto Sync

 For most Auto Syncs, data will sync from your SIS to Clever every 4-6 hours. You can initiate a new sync from the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page of your Dashboard:

 

 Syncs can be customized to occur nightly instead. A nightly sync will occur sometime between 10 pm and 4 am ET, but we can't specify an exact sync time. Please contact Clever Support to request a nightly sync.

 SIS-Managed Auto Sync

 Your SIS will upload data to Clever once per day. If you have questions about the exact time of your data upload, please reach out to your SIS provider directly.

 SFTP Sync

 Data will update in Clever each time you upload new data. We recommend that you upload data at least daily.

 If you schedule an automated SFTP upload, we recommend scheduling it to run before 7 pm EST because many applications update their data starting at 8 pm ET. If you have questions about the update schedule of a specific app, please reach out to the application directly.

title: Manually Uploading (i.e. side-loading) User Emails to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Enabling email uploads 3. Formatting your CSV files 4. How to upload your email files 5. Updating emails 6. Viewing email upload errors 

 Overview 

 When setting up Clever single-sign on (SSO) with Google, you'll need to have Google emails populated in the email field within Clever. If your district doesn't store Google emails in your student information system (SIS), you can upload an additional CSV of emails for your users and the data will be merged within Clever.

 Student_emails.csv and teacher_emails.csv files can only be uploaded via the web browser. SFTP transfer isn't supported. 

 

 
 Enabling email uploads 

 If the district uses Google as a login method for Clever, the district can enable email uploads by following these steps:

 • Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings .
• In the configuration for Google Authentication, click on Edit Settings .
• On the pop-up screen, select Google email addresses are NOT stored in my district's SIS . 
• Select Next .
• On the next screen, check the box next to I'll manually upload emails . 
• Select Save .
• Upload your files from your Data sources > SIS sync > Upload. 
 
 

 If your district doesn't use Google as a login method for Clever but would like to be able to upload emails through a CSV file, please contact our team, and we'll enable this feature for your district. 

 

 
 Formatting your CSV files 

 In order to use this tool, you'll need to create a CSV file that contains two columns: student_id/teacher_id & email. Be sure you're using the student_id/teacher_id values as highlighted below, and not the Clever ID. 

 File Name: student_emails.csv 

 
 Field

 
 Required

 
 Format

 
 Description

 
 student_id Y String Student’s sis_id, so Clever can tie the email to the associated student. Must be valid Student ID of an existing student and match the value Student ID on the student profile: 
 email Y Email Student's email address. Must match format x@y.z 
 
 

 File Name: teacher_emails.csv 

 
 Field

 
 Required

 
 Format

 
 Description

 
 teacher_id Y String Teacher's sis_id, so Clever can tie the email to the associated teacher. Must be valid Teacher ID of an existing teacher and match the value Teacher ID on the teacher profile: 
 email Y Email Teacher's email address. Must match format x@y.z 
 
 

 This file will need to contain a list of the email and the corresponding sis_ids for users. For Google districts, this file will be created using a combination of information from your SIS and your Google admin console. 

 Teachers and students emails require separate uploads. As such, if you wish to upload both student and teacher emails, you will upload two files: 

 • student_emails.csv 
• teacher_emails.csv 
 
 Include all users' emails that should be overwritten in your upload. Removing a user's email from the CSV file before re-uploading the file will cause the email address record to be deleted. As an example, if you manually upload user A and user B on day 1, and then on day 2 you need to add user C's email, you would need to upload a file including users A, B, and C, not just C. 

 
 How to upload your email files 

 To upload user emails, navigate to Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page in Clever. You can upload the files under the Emails section. 

 Include all users' emails that should be overwritten in your upload . Removing an existing user email from the file before re-uploading the file will cause the email address record to be deleted as outlined in the section above. 

 Once you have selected the CSV file(s) from your local directory and the cloud status turns green, select 'Upload' to upload user emails to Clever.

 

 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever won't send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 Updating emails 

 In order to update information after the initial upload you’ll be able re-upload the email files(s) with additional information, or you’ll be able to include emails in the SIS data feed for new users who are not listed in the additional file. Always include all user's emails that should be active in Clever in your upload . Removing a user email from the file before re-uploading the file will cause the email address record to be deleted. 

 Viewing email upload errors 

 To view errors for your email uploads, navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Upload page and select 'student_emails_errors.csv' or 'teacher_emails_errors.csv' on the right hand side.

title: Manually Uploading Student Contacts to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Formatting your CSV file 3. How to upload your CSV file 4. Updating contact data 5. FAQ 

 Overview

 Some applications require student contact data that may not be stored in your district's student information system (SIS). If your district does not store student contact data in your SIS, or would like to add additional student contacts outside of your Clever sync, you can now upload student contacts to Clever using a contacts.csv file. Student contacts can be uploaded via the web browser or via SFTP transfer! 

 

 IMPORTANT Please make sure that contact IDs are never re-used. Clever treats contact IDs as a unique identifier and use it to generate Clever IDs. Likewise, failure to do so may result in security issues. 

 

 
 

 Formatting your CSV file

 To manually upload student contact data to Clever, you will first need to populate a contacts.csv file containing information pertinent to your student contacts. Please click here to download a sample contacts.csv template that can be populated with the district's student contact data using the guidelines shown below. 

 File Name: contacts.csv 

 
 Field

 
 Required

 
 Format

 
 Description

 
 contact_id Y String Unique identifier for the contact 
 student_id Y String Student’s sis_id, so Clever can tie the contact to associated students. Must be valid Student ID of an existing student and match the value Student ID on the student profile: 
 first_name Y String Contact’s first name 
 last_name Y String Contact’s last name 
 type Y String Type of contact - e.g. “family”, “emergency”, or “guardian” 
 relationship N String Contact’s relationship to student - e.g. “Father”, “Aunt”, or “Neighbor” 
 phone N Integer A 10 or 11 digit phone number 
 phone_type N Option Type of phone contact. Supported values: Cell, Home, Work. 
 email N Email Contact’s email address. Must match format x@y.z 
 
 

 
 How to upload your CSV file

 Web Upload

 You can upload your contacts.csv file from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data tab.

 Once uploaded, you will see the following status message: 

 Please note: Please allow 10-15 minutes for custom data to process. Though Clever will not send an email notification that the data has been processed, the results will be viewable on the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 SFTP Upload

 If you prefer to upload your contact data via SFTP:

 • Connect to your Clever SFTP directory using the credentials found on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page under SFTP Settings . If you use a Clever-managed Auto Sync and don't have SFTP credentials available on this page, please contact Clever Support and our team can assist with providing access to a remote site for custom data contact uploads.
• Create a directory called: customcontacts: 
• Add the contacts.csv file to the customcontacts directory . 
 
 The file will be processed by Clever in 10-15 minutes. 

 Reviewing the results

 Once your contacts are uploaded to Clever, you can search for a contact record using the search bar, or by locating a specific student's record and navigating to the Guardians tab. 

 

 

 
 Updating contact data

 In order to update information after the initial upload, re-upload the contact.csv file with the edited or altered data. Manually adding or editing contacts is not supported. When re-uploading your contact file, be sure to re-upload the entire file with any new data added to your original file. Uploading a file with only new contacts will delete previously synced contacts. 

 

 
 FAQ

 Can these contacts be shared with applications? 

 • Yes! Contacts uploaded via contacts.csv can be shared with applications that support/require student contact information. Contact data is shared implicitly with student records, so there is no need to update your sharing permissions to include student contacts.

title: For Clever Admins: Primary school assignments
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Students & Teachers 2. Students & Teachers: SIS primary school assignment 3. Staff 

 

 Students & Teachers

 School association for students and teachers is based on section enrollments. If a student or teacher is enrolled in sections at multiple schools, Clever will associate the user with the related schools. A district should NOT attempt to connect a teacher or student with multiple schools by uploading the user multiple times in the students.csv or teachers.csv. Additionally, Clever will assign a primary school association. This will be the school that displays in their Clever Portal. When an application accesses the data for the user, the application will have access to both the user's primary school association, as well as their secondary associations. Primary school is assigned based on where the user has the most section enrollments . For example: If a student is enrolled in 2 sections at School A and 4 sections at School B, their primary school assignment in Clever will be School B (regardless of the school assigned in the SIS).

 You can see the primary school on the user's profile page, along with the other schools they're associated with listed in their sections.

 

 

 

 Additional details:

 • If a user has the same number of sections at two or more schools, Clever will choose the school where the total number of students enrolled in the user's sections is the greatest.
• If a student or teacher's section enrollments change during the year, their primary school in Clever may also change.
• If a student or teacher is not enrolled in any sections, Clever will use the school the user is assigned to in your SIS data.
• A user will be shared with an application if either their primary or secondary school is shared.
 
 

 
 

 Students & Teachers: SIS primary school assignment

 If you'd rather Clever use student and teachers' primary school exactly as it's listed in your student information system (SIS), please contact Clever Support. We can disable our default logic for either students, teachers, or both. Once this logic is disabled, Clever will look to your SIS data as the source of truth for primary school assignments.

 

 
 Staff

 For information on how staff are associated with multiple schools, please see this article .

title: For Clever Admins: Syncing custom (i.e. extension) fields to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Formatting your custom fields file(s) 3. Uploading custom fields to Clever 4. Uploading custom fields via your district's SIS sync 5. Viewing and managing custom field data in Clever 6. Using custom fields in app sharing rules 7. Sharing custom fields with apps 

 

 IMPORTANT: Please check with your application partners to see if they have the ability to sync custom data (extension) fields from Clever. Not all applications support extension fields. 

 Overview

 If your district would like to send data fields outside of the standard fields listed in our Data Specifications , you can do so by uploading custom/extension fields either directly through your Clever Dashboard , or through your district's existing sync.

 District admins have the ability to add custom fields for the following data types:

 • Schools
• Sections
• Students
• Teachers
• Staff
 
 Our system doesn't accept custom fields for enrollments or student contacts at this time.

 

 Custom fields can often be added to your existing sync, using the steps below for SFTP syncs, or with assistance from Clever Support for Clever-managed auto syncs. If you'd like to upload extension data independently of your existing sync (or have been instructed by Clever Support to do so), here’s what you can expect the process to like:

 • 
 Create your custom fields file. 

 • 
 Make sure you use the correct SIS ID for the user type.

 

• 
 Upload your file to Clever. You can either:

 • 
 Upload directly to the Clever Dashboard , or

• 
 Add it to your SFTP directory .

 

 
 OR

 If you have a Clever-managed auto sync and want to sync custom fields directly from your SIS, please contact Clever Support to review your options.

 

 
 

 Formatting your custom fields file(s)

 The CSV format is the same for students, teachers, schools, and sections: sis_id, {custom fields}

 sis_id, {custom fields} 
 The CSV format for staff is:

 staff_id, {custom fields} 
 Custom fields columns must be prefixed with 'ext.'

 • For example, a custom field named 'nickname' should be labeled 'ext.nickname'. Please refer to the example below for students:
 
 

 • Extension headers may only contain one period. For example, a file with 'ext.favorite.food' will not process (ext.favorite_food is valid).
• Extension headers may not contain spaces.
 
 Please note: Extension field files that are uploaded directly to a district’s SFTP folder must be named with all lowercase letters (e.g., schools_ext.csv, students_ext.csv, etc.). 

 

 

 
 Uploading custom fields to Clever

 Custom fields can be uploaded through the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. Select Add custom data > Fields . A window will pop up allowing you to upload a CSV file for each data type that you would like to create custom fields for. When you upload custom extension data, it will take about 15 minutes for the new data to sync to Clever. The sync may also go on hold due to large changes in data. Click here to read more about holds and how to resolve them.

 Please note: If your district already syncs the same custom field through your SIS sync or another custom data sync, those values will take precedence over this custom field upload. 

 

 All Syncs:

 • 
 All schools can upload custom fields via CSV files directly to your Clever SFTP folder by uploading to a subdirectory named /extensionfields. 

• 
 If your primary sync type is not SFTP sync type , please reach out to Clever support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. 

• 
 Once the /extensionfields subdirectory is created, you can upload the following files inside it:

 • schools_ext.csv 
• sections_ext.csv 
• students_ext.csv 
• teachers_ext.csv 
• staff_ext.csv 
 

 
 

 
 Uploading custom fields via your district's SIS sync

 

 Extension fields cannot be added directly from the Infinite Campus API or OneRoster syncs. To add extension fields to these sync types, please upload the extension field data directly to the Clever Dashboard as outlined here or learn about using an SFTP folder here . 

 

 SFTP and SIS-Managed Auto Syncs: 

 If your district only syncs data to Clever via SFTP/CSV files: 

 • Districts can add a custom/extension field column to one of the existing rostering files being synced to Clever ( schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv, enrollments.csv, and staff.csv ). The column name must begin with ' ext' .
 • For example: Fields such as 'Program Code' or 'Gifted Status' could be added to the students.csv with columns named 'ext.program_code' or 'ext.gifted_status' to your CSV files.
• Don't create an extension field with a space in the name. For example, a name like ext.iep status can fail, so you'd want to use ext.iep_status .
 

 
 • You can also upload custom fields via CSV files directly to your Clever SFTP folder by uploading to a subdirectory named /extensionfields. The files should be named:
 • schools_ext.csv 
• sections_ext.csv 
• students_ext.csv 
• teachers_ext.csv 
• staff_ext.csv 
 

 
 

 For information about uploading data via SFTP, please visit: SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever. 

 

 For the following auto-syncs, custom fields can also be added and customized via your Clever Dashboard:

 • Illuminate Auto Sync
• Aeries Auto Sync 
• SchoolInsight Auto Sync 
• iNow Auto Sync
• PowerSchool (legacy) 
• PowerSchool Plugin Sync 
• Infinite Campus Auto Sync 
 
 For the following syncs, custom fields can also be added and customized by reaching out to Clever support:

 • Blackbaud API sync 
• Infinite Campus API sync 
• Skyward Auto Sync 
• Skyward LMS API 
 
 

 
 Viewing and managing custom field data in Clever

 You can take a look at your most recently uploaded custom fields by navigating to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data > Reports . Here, you can view details about the record type, the fields that were added, and the time of the last update. This page also includes an error report detailing any errors in the files. You can also download your district's current custom field files. 

 

 

 You can view custom field data for each record type on the Profile Page for the school, student, teacher, staff, or section it is associated with, or by exporting all data for a record type from your Clever Dashboard > Data browser . 

 
 Using custom fields with app sharing rules

 You can use custom extension fields to create unique sharing rules for an easier, more seamless sharing process with your connected applications. Whether an application accepts extension fields or not, you can still use extension fields in sharing rules! You can use multiple extension fields in a single sharing rule to create more specificity, and extension fields can be used in any share by rule (share by student, share by teacher, share by section, and share by staff). 

 

 

 

 
 Sharing custom fields with apps

 Many applications allow or require custom field data. To share custom fields with apps, follow these directions:

 • Navigate to the overview page for the app and click on the Settings tab
• Under Field Access , select Advanced Settings 
• Select Customize fields shared with {app} and click Customize fields 
• On the Edit Student Fields page, select Next 
• On the Edit Contact Fields page, select Next 
• On the Edit District Admin Fields page, select Next 
• You are now on the Edit Extension Fields page. Any extension fields that you have uploaded to Clever will be found in the left column under Fields. Click on the fields you wish to share with the app to bring it to the right column under Share with {app}. Once you have selected the desired fields to share, select Save. 
 
 For more information on editing field access for applications, please see this article .

title: For Clever Admins: Student Contacts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Syncing student contacts to Clever 3. SFTP and SIS-managed SFTP Syncs 4. Clever-Managed Auto Syncs 5. Duplicate Contacts 6. Contact types 

 Overview

 
 Student contacts are typically parents, family, guardians or close friends of students who can be contacted by the school or district in the event of an emergency or other important communication need. Clever supports multiple contacts for each student. Once in Clever, this data can be synced to your district's connected applications. 

 To review student contact data in Clever, admins can navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Data Browser > Contacts tab. 

 

 
 Syncing student contacts to Clever

 Depending on if your district is syncing rostering data to Clever via a Clever-managed auto sync, SIS-Managed auto sync, or SFTP sync, student contact data will vary. This section details the requirements and processes for the different sync types. 

 

 SFTP and SIS-managed SFTP Syncs 

 For SFTP syncs and SIS-managed SFTP syncs , the following seven fields (i.e. columns) can be added to the students.csv upload to Clever: 

 Required fields:

 • Contact_type (required): Primary, Secondary, Parent/Guardian, Emergency, Family, Other . 
• Contact_name (required): The name of the contact. 
 
 Optional:

 • Contact_sis_id: The unique identifier for the contact
• Contact_relationship : Parent, Grandparent, Self, Aunt/Uncle, Sibling, Other 
• Contact_phone : 10- or 11-digit phone number without any characters (e.g. parentheses, dashes) 
• Contact_phone_type: Cell, Home, Work, Other 
• Contact_email : Contact email address in the standard email format of username@demo.com 
 
 Multiple student contacts: 

 • For students with multiple contacts, create multiple rows for the student; one for each contact. 
• Each row will contain the identical student details (e.g. student_id, student_number, etc.), but will have different contact information on each row. 
 
 

 Clever Tip: There is no limit to the number of student contacts that can be synced to Clever via an SFTP sync! 

 

 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs 

 These are the required and optional fields for syncing student contacts to Clever:

 Required: 

 • Contact_type (required): Primary, Secondary, Parent/Guardian, Emergency, Family, Other . 
• Contact_name (required): The name of the contact. 
 
 Optional: 

 • Contact_relationship : Parent, Grandparent, Self, Aunt/Uncle, Sibling, Other 
• Contact_phone : 10- or 11-digit phone number without any characters (e.g. parentheses, dashes) 
• Contact_phone_type: Cell, Home, Work, Other 
• Contact_email : Contact email address in the standard email format of username@demo.com 
 
 Find your Clever-Managed Auto Sync below for instructions specific to your SIS!

 Aeries 

 We pull all contacts associated with a student from the following fields on the Student endpoints of the Aeries API: 

 • CON.FN + CON.LN 
• CON.EM 
• CON.CP 
• CON.RL 
• CON.OR 
 
 Infinite Campus API Sync 

 By default, the Infinite Campus API sync pulls student contacts from the following fields: 

 
 Clever Field 

 
 Infinite Campus Field 

 
 
 Contact_name 

 
 identity.firstName + identity.lastName 

 
 
 Contact_type 

 
 This is flagged within IC: Guardian/Relative = Census > Relationships 

 
 
 Contact_phone 

 
 contact.homePhone 

 
 
 Contact_email 

 
 contact.email 

 
 
 Customization of the IC API sync is not available at this time. 

 Illuminate 

 If you have questions about student contact data for your Illuminate Auto Sync, please reach out to our support team for more information. 

 PowerSchool 

 By default, Clever pulls student contact info from the following fields in PowerSchool: 

 • Emerg Contact 1 
• Emerg Contact 2 
• Emerg Contact 3 
• Emerg Phone 1 
• Emerg Phone 2 
• Emerg Phone 3 
• Emerg 1 Ptype 
• Emerg 2 Ptype 
• Emerg 3 Ptype 
• Emerg 1 Rel 
• Emerg 2 Rel 
• Emerg 3 Rel 
 
 The PowerSchool Auto Sync can be customized so that Clever pulls student contacts from different fields than those listed above. Visit our PowerSchool Help Center article for instructions on requesting a custom mapping! 

 SchoolInsight 

 If you have questions about student contact data for your SchoolInsight Auto Sync, please reach out to our support team for more information. 

 Skyward Auto Sync 

 Clever pulls eight fields for Skyward emergency contact, forming cohesive student contacts in Clever: 

 • E1_Full_Name 
• E1_Relation_Dsc 
• E1_Cell_Phone 
• E1_Pri_Phone 
• E1_Street_Addr 
• E1_City 
• E1_State 
• E1_Zip_Code 
 
 It is not possible to have more than one contact record per student. However, we can customize the sync to pull from other fields instead. 

 • For example: If you want to use Parent_Name and Parent_Phone instead of E1 Full Name and E1 Cell Phone, we can do that! 
 
 

 Skyward API Sync 

 Clever syncs student contacts from the guardian endpoint and pulls multiple contacts per student. We can customize the Auto Sync to pull student contact information from different fields, as long as those fields are accessible via Skyward’s API. Contact Clever support for more information! 

 
 

 Duplicate Contacts

 Our system will attempt to merge contacts to prevent duplicate contacts in your Clever data. The best way to prevent duplicate contacts is to sync contact_sis_id for each contact. If a contact_sis_id is not available, Clever will attempt to merge contacts in the following logic:

 • If contact_email is available, we will merge contacts by matching on: contact_name + contact_email 
• If email is not available and contact_phone is available, we will merge contacts by matching on: contact_name + contact_phone 
• If neither contact_email or contact_phone are available, we will try to merge contacts by matching on student_id + contact_name + contact_type + contact_relationship + contact_phone_type 
 
 The above logic relies on exact alphanumeric matches, including case sensitivity. For example, Clever will not match "John Doe" and "john doe". 

 
 Contact types

 When Clever normalizes your contact data, we look to the following keywords to match to Parent/Guardian, Emergency, Primary, Secondary, Family, and Other in Clever:

 Contact Type Accepted keywords 
 Parent/Guardian 
 • guardian
• mother
• father
• parent
• mom
• dad
• stepmother
• stepfather
• stepparent
 
 
 Emergency 
 • emergency
 
 
 Primary 
 • primary
 
 
 Secondary 
 • secondary
 
 
 Family 
 • family
• aunt
• uncle
• relative
• grandmother
• grandfather
• grandparent
• sibling
 
 
 Other 
 • other
 
 
 
 If Clever is unable to match a keyword to a contact type, that contact type will be blank in Clever.

title: Clever Roles: Staff - How to add to Clever (updated user interface)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Uploading staff for SFTP districts 3. Uploading staff for auto sync districts 4. Important Upload Tips 5. Associating staff to multiple locations or the district office 6. Editing staff & removing staff or updating their roles 7. Districts using Infinite Campus API or Skyward API syncs 8. Automated Staff SFTP for auto syncs 

 IMPORTANT This article shows directions for the updated user interface for adding staff (which most districts use). If you notice the Staff page of your account looks different from the documentation below, please view this article instead . 

 Overview

 Clever Admins are able to provide various non-instructional staff (i.e. non-teaching staff) access to Clever. For an overview of Clever Roles and permissions, please see this article . 

 Clever Tip: If your district uses the Infinite Campus API or Skyward API sync type, skip below to this section for information on how staff are automatically synced to Clever. If your district uses Active Directory, click here to sync data with Clever automatically.

 

 Clever intuitively knows what options are available to sync your staff based on your sync type . You can view these on your Clever Dashboard by going to the Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data tab . From there, choose which option works best for your school district.

 If you’re unsure of your sync type, you can navigate to your SIS sync page and find out by looking under Sync Type at the top of the page.

 

 

 This is an example of an SFTP sync type. 

 For most districts, there are two ways to add staff to Clever: 

 • Staff as part of your normal Sync or a one-time Sync upload.
• Custom Staff , added individually on a per-user basis.
 
 Please see the below table to determine which method is best for your district. Please note that many districts will choose to manage staff in bulk using Staff and will manually add individual accounts using Custom Staff as needed. If you use a Clever-Managed Auto Sync that is not able to sync staff, please see this section . 

 
 

 
 Custom Staff

 
 Staff

 
 Manually create staff 
 Manually associate staff with multiple schools 
 Manually edit staff 
 Manually delete staff 
 Bulk create staff via CSV 
 Automate bulk create staff (CSV file) (SFTP districts only) 
 Bulk associate staff with multiple schools (CSV file) 
 Bulk edit staff (CSV file) 
 Bulk delete staff (CSV file) 
 View staff Support Tools > Custom Data > Staff Data browser 
 Recommended for: 
 Creating individual staff

 Small schools / districts

 
 Medium and large districts

 
 Documentation Link Link 
 
 

 

 
 Uploading staff for SFTP districts

 For SFTP syncs, there are three options to sync your staff data to Clever (which you can find on your Staff Data tab) :

 • Via your SFTP sync • An SFTP client (credentials provided on your Staff Data tab )
• Manually upload it on the Upload tab 
 

• Active Directory sync
 • If all staff members have accounts in Active Directory (AD), you can quickly sync them using our AD sync tool
 

• Use custom data
 • Individually add custom staff or make edits to existing staff data on a per-user basis - no staff.csv required
 

 
 

 

 For additional information about Clever's SFTP and CSV formatting specifications, click here . 

 

 
 Uploading staff for auto sync districts

 If you’re unsure of your sync type, you can navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page and find out by looking under Sync Type at the top of the page.

 If you're using the PowerSchool Plugin Sync and want to upload staff, please follow the instructions in this section.

 

 

 This is an example of a Clever-managed auto sync. 

 Suppose your district's student information system (SIS) isn't automatically syncing staff members to Clever (you can sync staff with Infinite Campus API and Skyward API). In that case, you can navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data page to view the options to sync your staff to Clever. 

 

 Please note: Staff users must be explicitly shared with your learning applications once they have been created in Clever. 

 • From your Clever Dashboard, select the Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data tab.
• You will be able to view your staff sync options as follows:
 
 • Sync staff data as part of your regular SIS auto sync, if syncing with Infinite Campus API and Skyward API
• Active Directory
• Custom Data
• Upload a staff.csv file via an SFTP box (provided by Clever). Learn more about SFTP boxes here . 
 • If you use this option after your initial upload, to add more staff to Clever, open the staff.csv and add an additional row for each staff member you would like to add. If you need an example file, click Download example files . 
 

 
 Please note: DO NOT REMOVE any staff users you want to be able to access Clever. Removing a user from the staff.csv before re-uploading it will cause their record to be deleted. Instead, add the additional user information to the file before re-uploading. 

 

 For additional information about Clever's SFTP and CSV formatting specifications, click here . 

 

 
 Important Upload Tips

 When uploading staff to Clever, there are a few important details to consider: 

 • Each unique staff member requires a unique staff_id. 
• Always include all staff in the staff.csv file if you still want them to have access to Clever. Removing a user from the staff.csv before re-uploading the file will cause their record to be deleted. 
• The school_id must match the school_id (i.e. sis_id) for a school in Clever. To locate this value, search for the school from the top search bar and select the school to bring up the school's profile page. Under School Information, find the School ID. This is the school_id value that should be used in the staff.csv.
 
 

 

 

 • For staff that are not associated with a specific school, or are associated with the district office, use the school_id value: DEFAULT_DISTRICT_OFFICE
 
 • Uploaded staff are auto-assigned the role: Portal Access . Portal Access users do not have any administrative rights and will only be able to Single Sign-On (SSO) to applications from their Clever Portal. Applications must first be shared with them by a Clever Admin.
• For instructions on manually adding individual staff members to Clever via the Custom Data feature, visit: Adding custom staff to Clever. 
 
 

 For information on admin and staff roles in Clever, please visit: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions. 

 

 
 Associating staff to multiple locations or the district office 

 Multiple schools: 

 To associate a staff member with multiple schools, create a separate row at each location. The only field that will be different for each row is the school_id. For example, if a staff member should be associated with three schools, it should look like this:

 

 All schools:
