 To associate a staff member with all schools in the district, enter school_id: district 

 

 

 To see which school(s) a staff member is associated with, head to your Clever Dashboard and search their name via the search bar at the top.

 

 

 Default District Office : 

 There is a default auto-generated district office in Clever. To associate a staff member with the district office, enter the school_id: DEFAULT_DISTRICT_OFFICE 

 

 

 
 Editing staff & removing staff or updating their roles

 • From your Clever Dashboard, navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Upload tab. 
• Under Download existing files, select staff.csv . The downloaded staff.csv file will contain all pre-existing staff in Clever for your district. 
 
 

 • Edit the records for staff as desired and save the file. 
• Next, upload the file. When the cloud displays as green, select Upload. The staff updates will be reflected in Clever in 10-15 minutes. 
 
 To update a staff member's role from Portal Access to School Tech Lead , please follow the instructions here: Clever Roles: School Tech L

title: Clever Roles: Staff - How to log in to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Logging in 3. Staff who are also teachers 

 See this article for information on how to add your staff members into Clever!

 

 Overview

 Your district's non-instructional staff (i.e. non-teaching staff), or employees who do not have a designated roster in your district's Student Information System (SIS), are referred to as staff in Clever. Occasionally, they will also be referred to as school admins. The two terms are synonymous. 

 Here are some examples of the types of staff members Clever supports: 

 • Curriculum Leads
• District Help Desk users
• School-specific Tech Leads
• Principals
• Nurses
• District members
• After-school Coordinators
 
 

 For information on the different types of staff members and their permissions in Clever, please see this article: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 

 

 Logging in

 Once you have set up staff in Clever, there are two ways for them to log in: 

 • They can navigate to your district's Clever portal login URL which is located in your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page. Staff will use the same single sign-on (SSO) method you've designated for your teachers and students (e.g., if your district uses Google, staff can log in with their Google credentials). 
• Or from Clever.com . by selecting to log in as 'Teacher or Staff'. 
 
 

 Please note: If you're using ADFS, LDAPS, or any other Identity Provider (IDP) that requires manual setup, you'll need to edit your claims rules to allow staff members and school admins to access Clever. If you have any questions about claims rules please see this Help Center article. 

 

 Staff who are also teachers

 Clever supports users being both teachers and staff! If an individual is both a teacher and staff user in Clever, they'll log into Clever, and then select their name in the top right to toggle between accounts as needed.

 

 

 If you have any questions about how district staff should access Clever, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: For Clever Admins: Co-teachers
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding co-teachers in SFTP syncs 3. Adding co-teachers in SIS managed syncs 4. Syncing co-teachers in Clever managed auto syncs 5. Manually adding existing teachers as co-teachers 6. Creating new custom teachers 7. Removing co-teachers 8. Co-teachers in applications 

 Overview

 Co-teachers are additional teachers assigned to sections in your SIS but are not the primary teacher. Clever's ability to sync co-teachers depends on your sync type. Read on to learn more!

 

 Please note: Some connected applications may not support co-teachers. Please contact the application partner directly to confirm whether they support co-teachers. 

 

 • If co-teachers are missing from your connected application, first check your data in Clever . If co-teachers are missing in Clever, please read the information below on how to add them to Clever.
• If co-teachers are syncing to your Clever account but missing from your connected application, please reach out to that application team to learn how their system handles multiple teachers.
• Clever supports a total of 10 teachers per section, including one primary teacher and up to 9 co-teachers.
 
 

 Clever Tip: Use co-teachers to give special education, intervention, and other unique teachers direct access to their students. 

 

 
 Adding co-teachers in SFTP syncs

 The "Teacher_id" column in the sections.csv file indicates the primary teacher for that section. Multiple teachers are added to sections through the columns "Teacher_2_id", "Teacher_3_id", and "Teacher_4_id", etc. Clever supports up to 9 co-teachers per section. A sections.csv file formatted for co-teachers looks like this: 

 Please note: Each teacher_id must correspond to a record in the teachers.csv file. Check out Clever's SFTP spec for more information on CSV formatting; page 13 outlines the multiple teacher_id fields. Once the multiple teacher_id fields are added to the sections.csv file, as well as the teachers.csv file, upload the updated files to Clever. 

 

 
 

 Adding co-teachers in SIS-Managed Auto Syncs

 Please contact your SIS if you'd like co-teachers added to your sync. Similar to the process for SFTP syncs, your SIS will need to add multiple teachers through the "Teacher_2_id", "Teacher_3_id", and "Teacher_4_id" fields in the sections.csv sent to Clever. Click here to learn more about SIS-Managed Auto Syncs.

 

 
 

 Syncing co-teachers in Clever-Managed Auto Syncs

 To learn if your Auto Sync supports co-teachers, please go to the Clever-Managed Auto Sync page here and select your SIS page. The Auto Sync's "Functionality" section at the top of that page will indicate if the Auto Sync can support co-teachers. Auto Syncs that support co-teachers will bring them into Clever automatically. For example: 

 
 

 Manually adding existing teachers in Clever as co-teachers

 

 If your sync doesn't support the export of co-teachers, your district can manually add co-teachers to sections from a section's detail page. To do this, open the section's detail page and click Add a co-teacher . Sections can be easily accessed using the search bar at the top of your Clever dashboard.

 Please allow up to 24 hours for manually added co-teachers to be synced by connected applications. If you don't see co-teachers appear in the application, you can contact the application's support team to confirm they support co-teachers on their platform. 

 

 
 

 Creating new custom teachers in Clever

 If you'd like to add a teacher who doesn't sync from the SIS, you can do this by clicking create a teacher in the modal. 

 You'll enter basic demographic information about the user in the subsequent screen in order to create this user.

 Please allow up to 24 hours for your custom-created teachers to sync by connected applications. If you do not see custom-created teachers appear in the application, you can contact the application's support team to confirm they support co-teachers on their platform.

 

 
 

 Removing co-teachers

 The process of removing a co-teacher from a section will depend on how the co-teacher was added to the section. If a co-teacher was added manually, admins will be able to remove the association directly from the section profile in Clever. 

 

 If this option is not available, the co-teacher association was synced directly from your student information system (SIS). To remove the co-teacher, the association will need to be removed in the SIS. Once removed, the co-teacher association will be removed on the next sync with Clever. 

 

 
 

 Co-teachers in applications

 While all rostering applications on Clever have access to shared co-teacher data, it depends on the application partner whether they support co-teachers or not. The application partners also take care of managing permissions and access control for co-teachers within their app.

 If you have any questions about co-teacher behavior in an application, please don't hesitate to reach out to the application's support team directly. They'll be more than happy to assist you!

title: For Clever Admins: Sync Holds
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Default hold thresholds 3. Custom hold thresholds 4. Reviewing sync reports • If the sync changes look good • If the sync changes look incorrect 5. School year and term rollovers 

 Overview

 When your most recent Student Information System (SIS) sync to Clever is about to change a significant amount of rostering data, Clever automatically initiates a sync hold to prevent the changes until a Clever admin reviews and approves them. Sync holds are a safeguard against potentially incorrect or unintended changes being processed and synced to your connected applications. This ensures you're always aware of significant changes before they go through. 

 Please note: Significant changes to custom data can also trigger a hold. Holds are not limited to only your synced data. 

 When a sync is on hold, you can select Review Hold under the sync hold alert or can review the information from their Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page before accepting or declining the changes. An email will also be sent to the Clever admins on your account that have their Sync emails enabled . 

 

 If deletions for schools, students, teachers, or staff exceed 95% and you choose to accept the changes, a Clever support ticket is automatically created for review by a Clever support agent. Support cases are not automatically created for sections, contacts, or terms. 

 
 

 Default hold thresholds 

 Your school organization's data automatically goes on hold when at least 75% of any data type (e.g., schools, students, teachers, sections) is being updated, or over 10% is being deleted. These thresholds can be customized . 

 

 
 

 Custom hold thresholds 

 Clever admins can set custom hold thresholds for all record types (e.g. schools, students, teachers, sections) on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. Custom hold thresholds are helpful for:

 • Small school organizations where small data changes may represent large percentage changes
• Clever admins planning large updates who want to avoid a hold for known changes
 
 You can set the percentage threshold for both updates and deletions per record type. Here are the allowed ranges:

 • Updates 
 • 0 - 100% - Schools, Students, Sections, Teachers, Contacts, Terms
 

• Deletions 
 • 0 - 90% - Schools, Students, Sections, Teachers, Contacts
• 0 - 100% - Terms
 

 
 Holds for updates can be disabled if needed. Holds for deletions can't be disabled and have a maximum threshold of 90%. Example view of custom hold thresholds:

 

 

 
 Sync reports 

 You can review your sync reports from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page. The report includes a table summarizing the data changes that triggered the sync hold. These changes won't be processed in Clever until an admin approves them.

 Select Download Changes to view updates, deletions, warnings, and errors for each record type. 

 

 If the sync changes look good

 Select Approve Changes. 

 • If the changes are less than 95% deletions, Clever will immediately begin processing the new data.
• If the changes are more than 95% deletions or updates for any record type, a support case will be generated for final review. This is an extra safeguard for districts to ensure there is no negative effect on students, teachers, and applications. Please include a note explaining why significant changes are being made. Clever Support will use this information to review the hold. If everything looks correct, the team will release the hold. If any concerns arise, they'll follow up via email. 
 
 

 If the sync changes look incorrect

 If the changes were not intentional, please follow these steps: For SFTP syncs : 

 • Select Discard Changes. 
• Depending on your sync type, you'll: 
 • SFTP syncs: Review and correct the CSV file(s) that triggered the hold, then re-upload your CSV files to Clever via your SIS, or via Data sources > SIS sync > Upload .
• SIS-managed SFTP syncs: Clever has little insight into how data is filtered and exported from your SIS, so we're not able to help troubleshoot beyond looking at the files we receive. If you can't determine why the data changes were incorrect, reach out to your SIS rep to investigate on their end. 
 

• Submit another upload: If the revised data matches your existing data in Clever closely enough, the new upload will override the hold, and the changes will process automatically.
 
 For Auto Syncs : 

 • Select Discard Changes. 
• Review the data in your SIS to see if you can determine why the synced data is changing so significantly. It may be necessary to loop in your SIS rep for assistance. 
• Once the issues have been addressed in your SIS, initiate a sync to Clever using the Sync Now option on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page.
• If the revised data matches your existing data in Clever closely enough, the new upload will override the hold, and the changes will process automatically.
 
 

 
 School year and term rollovers 

 When a school year or term rolls over, sync updates from your SIS will typically trigger a sync hold. In most cases, the rollover changes will contain significant updates to the rostering data in Clever. Here are some changes that would be expected:

 • Sections : 100% deleted, 100% created
• Students : 100% updated, 10-25% deleted, 10-25% created
• Teachers : 10-20% deleted, 10-20% created
 
 If you're not sure about the hold, navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync page in the Clever Dashboard and select Download Changes for each record type. This downloads a .zip file which includes the creations, updates, deletions, warnings and errors for the related record type. 

 

 For detailed information regarding school year and term rollovers, please visit:

 • School Year Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations 
• Term Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations 
 
 Related articles: 

 
 • For Clever Admins: Pausing the sync with your SIS 
• School Year Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations 
• Term Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations

title: For Clever Admins: Sync Reports
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Reviewing your Sync Report 3. Viewing data errors and change logs 

 Overview 

 By navigating to your Sync Report , you can see the details of the latest upload to Clever, including the number of records created, updated, and deleted as compared to the data previously in Clever. 

 

 Reviewing your Sync Report

 

 Last Sync The date and time of the last successful sync for your data in Clever. If your account is paused or on hold, it will reflect the last sync to fully process into Clever. As such, the Last Sync date time may be different than the last time Clever received data from your SIS.

 Record Type Record type describes the type of record being referenced. Please note, enrollments are rolled into Sections and will be reflected in the Sections change logs. Existing The number of active records in Clever prior to the most recent sync. Created The number of records created in Clever during the most recent sync. Updated The number of records updated in Clever during the most recent sync. Deleted The number of records deleted in Clever during the most recent sync. Errors The number of errors related to the record type for the most recent sync. Download Changes Click this button to download CSV files that outline the changes for each record type for the most recent sync. Also included are any errors for the record type. 

 Viewing data errors and change logs

 The Sync Report page offers the ability to review and resolve errors for the data sync to Clever. To view data errors: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync page.
• Download a CSV file of the affected records by selecting the Download changes option for the desired record type.
 
 The following articles may help with troubleshooting some of your district's sync errors:

 • Sync Reports: Error Messages 
• Sync Report error: Records not processed due to duplicate ID's 
• Sync Report error: Records not processed due to missing school 
• Sync Report error: Sections not processed due to missing students 
 
 If you have any questions about sync reports, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Clever Roles: Staff vs. Teachers
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Teachers 3. Staff 4. Can a user be both staff and teacher? 5. What features are available to teachers and staff? 6. Clever Portal: Differences for staff vs. teachers 

 Overview

 This article discusses the differences between teachers and non-instructional staff in Clever and provides details about users who might be considered both staff and a teacher.

 
 Teachers

 Teacher is the term Clever uses to refer to your school organization's teaching staff. These are the individuals who directly manage a classroom and students. Teachers in Clever have unique tools and features that allow them to facilitate digital learning in the classroom. Some resources available to teachers in Clever include:

 • Clever Library 
• Login Tools 
• Teacher Pages 
• Teacher Analytics 
 
 

 
 Staff

 Staff is the term Clever uses to refer to staff who are not directly in charge of a classroom (e.g., principal, front desk, counselor, etc.). Clever has two user roles associated with the staff user type:

 • Portal Access : This is the default role provided to staff members in Clever. This role provides permissions to access the Clever Portal and any shared applications.
• School Tech Lead : This role in Clever has additional permissions that allow them to manage the Clever Portal on a school-by-school basis. 
 
 

 
 Can a user be both staff and teacher?

 Yes! An individual can be both a teacher and staff in Clever. In most instances, these users can access both user accounts using the same set of credentials. 

 Please note: Teacher accounts cannot be converted to staff accounts, and vice versa, within Clever. If you need to change a user from teacher to staff, or from staff to teacher, you'll need to create a new account for them of that type. 

 School organizations can link teacher and staff accounts as long as the email for each record matches (this includes case sensitivity). If the user has one account syncing from your student information system, and you need to make a custom account for another role for that user, that's totally fine! You'll need to be sure to use the same email. To check if the user's accounts are linked, you can search for the user in the search bar in the Clever Dashboard. If the accounts are linked you will see the teacher/staff toggle button (shown below). 

 

 The user will log in to Clever using one set of credentials. As long as the teacher and staff accounts are linked, the user can easily toggle between accounts by clicking their name and selecting the account they want to be logged in to. 

 

 

 An example of the dropdown a user with multiple accounts will see when clicking their name. 

 

 You can learn more about switching between accounts here .

 
 Clever Tip: If you're accessing Clever via a login method other than the Clever login page (ex: links in another Portal) you may have a different role choosing experience. In this scenario, Clever will prompt the user to select the role they want to login with: As a security measure, users with both a teacher and a staff account will be asked to re-enter their username and password after selecting the account they want to access. This is to protect your account in case there is a delay between your initial login attempt and selecting your account type. 

 

 For details about configuring sharing permissions for staff, please visit: How do I share staff with applications? 

 Please note: If your school organization uses 'Log in with Clever' for single sign-on (SSO) (in addition to a third-party identity provider like Google), these users might have different credentials for each account. Their admin should be able to provide information around login credentials. 

 

 
 What features are available to teachers and staff? 

 
 User type in Clever:

 
 Teacher 

 
 Staff 

 
 
 User role in Clever:

 
 Teacher 

 
 School Tech Lead 

 
 Portal Access 

 
 Single Sign-on 
 SSO to applications via Clever ✅ ✅ ✅ 
 Customize Clever Portal for school ✅ 
 Teacher Pages 
 Create and share Teacher Pages ✅ 
 Teacher Tools 
 Manage and print Clever Badges ✅ ✅ 2 
 Generate backup login codes ✅ 
 Reset student passwords 1 ✅ 
 Log a class of students into an application ✅ 
 Log a class of students out of Clever ✅ 
 View Clever login status for students ✅ 
 Clever Library 
 Add Clever Library applications ✅ 
 Review Clever Library applications ✅ 
 Clever Dashboard permissions 
 View login metrics for applications at school ✅ 
 Create custom sections ✅ 
 Manage application sharing permissions 3 ✅ 
 
 Notations: 1 Clever Passwords or external (third-party) identity provider passwords if enabled by Clever Admin; 2 Only have access to Badges for their schools; 3 Must be enabled on a per-application basis by a Clever Admin

 
 
 

 

 
 Clever Portal: Differences for staff vs. teachers

 Teacher Portal 

 Teachers can create personalized Teacher Pages to house a variety of learning resources they want their students to access. Some examples include:

 • Applications managed by your school organization
• Various Clever Library applications 
• Links to online tools (e.g. Wikipedia.org , Dictionary.com )
• Homework or assignments in Google Drive
• Community websites (e.g. local public library)
 
 

 Non-Teaching Staff Portal 

 The non-teaching staff Clever Portal has more limited functionality than the Teacher Portal. However, staff do have the ability to:

 • Single sign-on (SSO) into applications that are shared by the school organization
• Find all business applications, resources, and links in one place
• See school updates on the Portal Announcement board
• Easily find any resources using the search option

title: For Clever Admins: Sync Status
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sync Successful 3. Sync in Progress 4. Sync Pending 5. Sync On Hold 6. Sync Error 7. Sync Paused 8. Sync Out of Date 9. Resources 

 

 

 Overview

 Please note: This Help Center article explains the status of your sync to Clever. If you're trying to troubleshoot sync errors or issues, sync holds, or syncing data to applications, you will want to check out some of our other resources listed at the bottom of this article.

 If you want to learn more about your school or district's sync status, you've come to the right place! Your sync status is available in real-time on your Clever Dashboard and offers a snapshot of your Clever data sync, including your current sync status and when your data last synced successfully.

 When your sync processes, your sync status will show one of the following:

 • Sync Successful 
• Sync in Progress 
• Sync Pending 
• Sync On Hold 
• Sync Error 
• Sync Paused 
• Sync Out of Date 
 
 Review the sync details below your sync status to learn more about steps you may need to take to resolve an issue.

 Looking for additional support on quickly getting up to speed on Clever? Check out our Clever Academy Quick Start videos 

 

 
 Sync Successful

 Your sync is fully functional. No action needed!

 

 
 Sync in Progress

 When the sync is pushed or started and being processed by Clever, you will see the “In Progress” label. Once the sync is completed, the label turns into “Successful”. Most syncs process within 10-15 minutes. This status also displays when a hold is released on an account. It means that Clever is processing the most current data and the account will be fully operational shortly!

 

 
 Sync Pending

 Sometimes we may hit an unexpected error while getting your Auto Sync configured. This sync status means that our team is working on resolving this error as soon as possible and will reach out if we need assistance!

 

 
 Sync On Hold

 When Clever encounters a significant change in the data syncing from an SIS, we place the sync on hold to prevent potentially incorrect or undesired changes from being sent to your applications.

 Click "Review Hold" to view the sync report and to approve or discard the changes. For more information on Holds, see this article . 

 

 
 Sync Error

 There is an issue with your sync that Clever is unable to resolve on your behalf. The above errors are examples, but you may see other detailed messages for sync errors. Please review SFTP Sync Troubleshooting and Auto-Sync resources to learn how to resolve your error.

 

 

 
 Sync Paused

 This means your sync is currently paused, and Clever will not process updates to your data. For details on scheduling, adjusting, or canceling a pause, see this helpful article .

 

 

 
 Sync Out of Date

 If a new set of data is not successfully processed in over 6 months, your sync may display "Sync Out of Date". To resolve this issue, upload a fresh set of data to Clever. If there are significant changes to your data, it may result in a Sync Hold . Review and approve the sync changes to finish processing the updated data. 

 

 
 Resources

 Please see the following Help Center articles for issues with sync errors, sync holds, issues with syncing data to applications, or how to create a sync pause with a specific app:

 • SFTP Syncs: Sync status errors 
• For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app 
• For Clever Admins: Apps - Data Warnings and Errors 
• For Clever Admins: Sync Holds 
• See the troubleshooting sections for the following Clever-managed auto syncs:
 • Aeries Auto Sync 
• Blackbaud API Sync 
• Infinite Campus API Sync 
• PowerSchool Auto Sync 
• Skyward API Sync 
• Skyward Auto Sync

title: What is Secure Sync?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Auto Syncs 3. SFTP Syncs 3. Changing Syncs 

 Overview

 Think of Clever Secure Sync as a bridge between your Student Information System (SIS) and online learning applications. Secure Sync maintains up-to-date roster information in your learning applications by securely transferring your roster uploads or connecting directly with your SIS. Clever is engineered to be the most secure and convenient way for districts to send vendors data and grant access to learning applications.

 The Secure Sync process can be summarized in four steps:

 • Your school district’s data syncs to your Clever account through an Auto Sync or an SFTP sync.
• Sharing permissions with each connected application are set up to determine which data to share with that application.
• Applications pull all available data from your Clever account, typically on a nightly basis.
• Student, teacher, and staff accounts are automatically created in the applications. From that point on, applications regularly update any changes to your enrollment information as indicated in your Clever account. 
 
 Continue reading to learn about Clever’s different sync types. With all Clever sync types, the school district has complete control over data access and management of the Clever account. 

 Please note: Clever can only connect to one student information system (SIS) per district account. If your district uses more than one SIS, you will need to set up more than one Clever account. 

 Auto Syncs

 An Auto Sync with Clever involves basic initial setup and requires minimal IT support from your district because Clever syncs directly with your SIS to securely transfer student roster data. There are two types of Auto Syncs: Clever-Managed and SIS-Managed Auto Syncs. Curious if Clever offers an automated sync with your SIS? Check out this article. 

 SFTP Syncs

 This option allows school districts to “push” data to Clever. Districts generate multiple files in a Clever-specific CSV format and submit them to Clever using Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP). The SFTP sync allows districts to upload data using an FTP client or Clever’s Web Upload tool. Districts control exactly what data to include in their upload files, therefore this option provides districts a greater level of customization but requires more robust setup and management. Clever also recommends automating the process of exporting data from your SIS and uploading it to Clever. For more information on SFTP syncs, read this article. 

 Changing Syncs

 Moving to a new student information system (SIS)? No worries—Clever has you covered! Clever can assist in keeping your downstream application data clean and ready-to-go. For more information on what this process of changing syncs will entail, read this article . 

 If you have any questions about Secure Sync, please reach out to Clever Support ! We are happy to help!

title: Clever Roles: School Tech Lead
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Upgrading an existing staff user 3. Manually adding a School Tech Lead 4. Bulk uploading School Tech Leads via CSV 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Getting started with the School Tech Leads? Check out our course Clever Academy for STLs: Level 1 available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Overview

 District admins can assign certain users the School Tech Lead role which provides them with limited admin permissions in Clever for their associated school(s).

 School Tech Leads have been given additional permissions in Clever to allow them to support in troubleshooting for your district! 

 

 School Tech Leads have all of these additional unlocked features!

 • Can search for students, teachers, sections, and schools.
• Troubleshoot student and teacher logins for applications.
• Impersonate teachers (Access Portal as) if enabled by a district admin . To enable this feature, head to the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team > Access control tab. 
• Reset individual user passwords. This feature is available for districts using Clever Passwords and those that have purchased Clever IDM .
• View users' Google, Entra ID, and/or Active Directory passwords (for districts that have purchased Clever IDM ).
• Print Badges for their school site, specific sections, and individual students. They can also create Badges for individual students.
• Create individual custom sections for their associated school(s).
 
 

 Please note: To enable School Tech Leads to make updates to application sharing permissions for their school(s) only , please visit: Enabling School Site Sharing for School Tech Leads 

 

 

 
 Upgrading an existing staff user

 • School Tech Leads must first be added to Clever as staff before being assigned the School Tech Lead role. If the individual is a teacher in Clever, or is not yet in Clever as staff, please visit: Adding custom staff to Clever 
• Once the user is in Clever, navigate to their profile page using the search feature at the top of the Dashboard
• From their profile page, click the drop-down arrow on the top right of the page
• Click Update Clever Role 
• Select School Tech Lead 
 
 

 
 Manually adding a School Tech Lead

 • In the Clever Dashboard, click Data sources > Custom data 
• In the top right corner, click Add custom data. 
• In the drop-down menu, select Staff . ​​​​​​ 
 
 

 • Fill out the fields for the School Tech Lead you want to create. You’ll need to select which schools they are assigned to, or click Assign to all schools , as well as enter some basic identifying information. 
 
 IMPORTANT: To make them into a School Tech Lead, you must select School Tech Lead in the drop-down for Clever Role. 

 

 

 • Optional: Check the box to email the staff member informing them that their account has been created. 
• Click Create Staff 
 
 The Username and Password field only needs to be populated if: A) The district login method is Clever Passwords and 'District Username' or 'District Password' is selected for staff logins B) The district login method is Active Directory or LDAPS and the username field in Clever is utilized for staff matching (claims rules) C) You would like to utilize the District Username and District Password fields for staff logging into SSO (Saved Passwords) applications 

 

 
 Bulk uploading School Tech Leads via CSV

 To add one or multiple School Tech Leads via an uploaded file to Clever, please:

 • Prepare your Staff.csv file. You can download a staff.csv template here .
• Navigate to the Clever Dashboard .
• Select Data sources > Custom data. 
• Select Upload records .
• Upload your Staff.csv file.
 
 To view existing custom staff data, from the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 

 

 Please note: Any changes made for a School Tech Lead from the Clever Team page will overwrite the data previously uploaded via a staff.csv file. For example, if Jonathon Jones is uploaded as a School Tech Lead via the staff.csv and then his role is changed to Portal access only from the Team page, this will overwrite the School Tech Lead role assigned to him in the original upload. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I manually remove STL access through a CSV upload? 

 • STL access must be revoked in the same way that STL access was granted. If a staff member was manually upgraded through the dashboard, STL access should be revoked through the dashboard as well. If a staff member was upgraded in a CSV, update the CSV to represent their new status and re-upload the CSV file.

title: Clever Roles: District Help Desk
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding District Help Desk users 3. Removing District Help Desk users 4. Switching between user accounts if you have multiple accounts in Clever 

 Overview 

 The District Help Desk role is typically assigned to staff members who support district users. This role provides limited district admin permissions in Clever. Here is a graph detailing the permissions available to users in this role compared to a Clever Admin: 

 

 Adding District Help Desk users 

 

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update District Help Desk users via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 Clever admins can add District Help Desk users to Clever by following these steps:

 • Go to Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team 
• Click Add team member on the top right
• Add the user's information in the pop-up box, selecting the District Help Desk role
• Click Save 
 
 

 Once the user has been added to Clever, they will receive an email with a link to create an account. If they do not receive this message, please have them check their spam folder for a message from ' no-reply@clever.com '. 

 District Help Desk users should use the 'District admin log in' from the Clever login page to access their Clever Admin account.

 

 
 Removing District Help Desk users 

 

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update District Help Desk users via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 Clever Admins can remove District Help Desk users from the Team page by clicking the trash icon to the far right of the user's name. 

 

 

 For more information about the various admin and staff roles in Clever, please visit: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 

 If you have additional questions about roles in Clever, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 
 Switching between user accounts if you have multiple accounts in Clever 

 Many administrators add themselves as teachers or staff users in Clever to better understand these end users’ experiences. If you have multiple accounts in Clever, and the accounts are connected, you can easily toggle between any accounts by clicking your name and selecting the account you want to access. You can easily switch between accounts if they are connected by the same email address (case sensitive) or sis_id values. 

 

 An example of the dropdown a user with multiple accounts will see when clicking their name.

title: Clever Roles: District Curriculum Lead
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding District Curriculum Leads 3. Removing District Curriculum Leads 4. Switching between user accounts if you have multiple accounts in Clever 

 

 Overview

 The District Curriculum Lead role is typically assigned to staff members who work closely with the district's courses and curricula, and can provide recommendations and insight into how applications are being used at schools. District Curriculum Leads are administrators with higher-level permissions than regular staff or school tech leads (STLs) in Clever. Here is a graph detailing the permissions available to users in this role compared to other roles: 

 
 

 
 Clever Admin

 
 District Curriculum Lead

 
 District Help Desk

 
 School Tech Lead

 
 Staff (Portal Access Only)

 
 Application sharing permissions 
 Manage application sharing permissions Can see the general permissions set up for an app but not any specific information about which school(s) an app is shared with. Must be enabled on a per-app basis by district admin 
 Add or delete applications 
 Clever Portal permissions 
 SSO to applications via Clever SSO 
 

 Will be able to see all district applications

 
 Customize Clever Portal For connected schools 
 Manage SSO settings 
 Clever Badge permissions 
 Enable Clever Badges for district 
 Manage and print Clever Badges Can print for individuals Can print for individuals, sections, and school site 
 Clever Dashboard permissions 
 Access Clever Dashboard 
 View login metrics and connected applications 
 Change SIS sync settings 
 Access Data Browser 
 Access Clever Analytics Enable & View Edtech, Portal, and Reports (plus SFTP transfer of Reports) Edtech, Portal, and Reports Edtech, Portal, and Reports Edtech and Portal for associated schools 
 Access to record profiles Can access roster information via an application's overview page 
 Access to support tools Troubleshoot Login tool 
 Reset Clever Password for individual users 
 Create custom data Only individual custom sections 
 Access Portal As Can only impersonate teachers. Must be enabled by District Admin in Role Settings 
 
 

 

 

 Adding District Curriculum Leads

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update District Curriculum Leads via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 

 Clever admins can add District Curriculum Leads to Clever by following these steps:

 • Log in to the Clever Dashboard 
• Select User management > Admin team from the left navigation menu
• Select 'Add team member' on the top right
 
 

 • Add the user's information in the pop-up box, selecting the District Curriculum Lead role
• Click 'Save'
 
 

 Once the user has been added to Clever, they will receive an email with a link to create an account. If they do not receive this message, please have them check their spam folder for a message from ' no-reply@clever.com '. District Curriculum Leads should use the 'District admin log in' from the Clever login page to access their Clever Admin account. 

 Removing District Curriculum Leads 

 

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update District Curriculum Leads via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 Clever Admins can remove District Curriculum Leads from the Team page by clicking the trash icon to the far right of the user's name. 

 

 For more information about the various admin and staff roles in Clever, please visit: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 

 If you have additional questions about roles in Clever, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 
 Switching between user accounts if you have multiple accounts in Clever 

 Many administrators add themselves as teachers or staff users in Clever to better understand these end users’ experiences. If you have multiple accounts in Clever, and the accounts are connected, you can easily toggle between any accounts by clicking your name and selecting the account you want to access. You can easily switch between accounts if they are connected by the same email address (case sensitive) or sis_id values. 

 

 An example of the dropdown a user with multiple accounts will see when clicking their name.

title: For School Tech Leads: Student & teacher login errors, password resets
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Reset student and teacher passwords 3. Troubleshoot Login tool 4. View a teacher’s Clever Portal 

 Overview 

 As a School Tech Lead, you have numerous troubleshooting tools at your disposal! These tools will help you locate student, teacher, section, and school records in Clever to allow you to support users with login issues. 

 Clever Tip: The search bar on your Clever Dashboard is a great tool that allows you to easily search for teachers, students, sections, and your school site for troubleshooting missing or incorrect data. Incorrect data can prevent a user from logging in to Clever and may result in incorrect data within their scoped applications. 

 

 

 

 
 

 Reset student and teacher passwords 

 If your district uses Log in with Clever or a Third-Party account such as Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google as your login method, you may be able to reset student and teacher passwords. 

 Please note: This feature will only work if your district uses Log in with Clever or a Third-Party account (Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google) as a login method to Clever and your district admin has enabled the password reset feature. 

 

 Resetting student and teacher Clever passwords: 

 • Log in to your Clever Portal, and from your Dashboard, search for the user. 
• Click Support Tools , then Change password in the top right-hand side of their profile. 
• Input new password and select Save. 
 
 

 
 Troubleshoot Login tool 

 

 Troubleshoot Login is the perfect tool for quickly identifying root cause issues of login errors and missing icons in the portal. Often, your teachers and students may come to you with questions about logging in to Clever or applications. 

 You can find the tool in two places: 

 • Click Troubleshoot Login on your Dashboard 
• Search for the affected teacher or student's profile in your Dashboard and select Troubleshoot user login on the upper right-hand side of their Profile Page. 
 
 Using the Troubleshoot Login tool 

 • Navigate to the Troubleshoot Login tool. 
• Select a user using Clever ID, SIS ID, Name, or Email, and select the name of the application they having problems accessing. The tool will complete a root cause analysis of the issue. 
• Review of the summary of checks 
• Review the Recommended Action This will indicate who will need to take action to resolve the issue. There are 3 possible options: District, App, or Clever. 
• Review the Issue Details shown underneath the Recommended action. This will provide information on how to address any issues that have been flagged by the tool. To make communication simple, we have provided a Copy issue description function to allow you to easily include this information in your communication with the district, app, or Clever. Additionally, the tool will highlight related Help Center articles that can be leveraged to assist with resolving the issues detected. Please note, there may be multiple issues that will need to be addressed to restore the login for a user. 
 
 

 Pro Tip: Check out this article that describes result messages and additional information on how to resolve errors. 

 

 
 View a teacher’s Clever Portal 

 

 Please note: You will only have access to this feature if it has been enabled by your district admin 

 Clever now gives you the ability to access the portal as a teacher to identify the source of an issue. For security reasons, the teacher you are accessing the portal as will receive a notification each time you access their Portal. 

 When to use the "Access Portal as user" feature for a teacher 

 Teacher Access Portal sessions should be reserved for the following scenarios: 

 • A teacher is reporting they are unable to log in to one of their connected applications 
• A teacher is reporting that sections are not loading inside a connected application 
• A teacher is reporting that students are missing from their roster within a connected application 
• Print Badges for an entire class 
 
 

 To print Badges for a teacher’s class, head to: For Teachers: Printing Clever Badges 

 How to use the "Access Portal as user" feature for a teacher 

 Directions 

 • Log in to your Clever Dashboard 
• Search for the impacted teacher in the Search bar 
• Select Access Portal as user in the top right-hand corner from the Teacher Tools 
• A box will pop up asking for details on your session. Please enter the details and check the box agreeing that you will not alter their Portal or share sensitive student or teacher information. 
• Select Begin Portal session. You will be authenticated into the teacher’s portal to troubleshoot accordingly! 
• When done troubleshooting, select End Portal session to securely return to your Clever account. 
 
 

 tags: password reset, reset password

title: For School Tech Leads: Edtech Analytics
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to navigate the dashboard 

 

 Please note: If you select the Edtech Analytics tab in Clever and see the Get started message, this means that Edtech Analytics has not been set up by your Clever Admins. Please contact your Clever Admins if you would like this feature set up 

 

 Overview

 Edtech Analytics shows you all of your assigned schools' app usage in one place down to the school and grade level, whether or not the application is integrated with Clever. Best of all, it’s free! 

 This will allow you to Identify edtech usage trends and opportunities for intervention. Ensure everyone is getting the best out of the edtech your schools have invested in. Please note, Edtech Analytics is only available for Chromebook and Windows devices running Chrome and Edge browsers. Edtech Analytics is not supported for mobile devices. For Microsoft Intune as the mobile device manager on Windows as the operating system, please see:

 • For Chrome 
• For Edge 
 
 
 

 

 
 How to navigate the dashboard

 When you first log in to Clever you will see an overview of data for the last 7 days from your home page. To access the full report, click View details. 

 

 

 Please note: Edtech Analytics only reports data from devices with the Clever Plus extension enabled and does not include usage on personal mobile devices, including iOS devices. 

 

 

 Below is helpful information on navigating the Edtech Analytics dashboard! 

 Filters: 

 • You can filter data by time range, including last school year, this school year, the last 28 days, or the last 7 days. You can also filter by school and grade level. You'll only be able to see data for schools assigned to you as an STL, and only schools and grades with usage data will be shown in drop-downs. 
 
 Reported Users 

 Students reported and teachers reported is usage from devices where the Clever Plus extension is installed. To be counted, users need to: 

 • Be synced to Clever (learn more here about syncing to Clever) 
• Have an email address (Chromebook) or a username (Windows device) 
• Have an Active Directory or Google session running on their device 
• Be actively using the application (i.e. spends at least 10 seconds on the app page) 
 
 These numbers are updated every 4-6 hours. 

 You can view reported users by day by hovering over the historical trendline. 

 

 Please note: Edtech Analytics only reports data from devices with the Clever Plus extension enabled and does not include usage on personal mobile devices, including iOS devices. 

 

 Applications 

 • Total apps: This shows the total number of allow listed edtech apps identified by Clever. We’re constantly adding to this list so you may see the number of apps increase as these are added. Don’t see an app on our list ? Let us know so we can add it. 
• SSO apps: Single Sign-On (SSO) applications that have been added at the district-level.
• Library apps : the t otal number of Clever Library applications added to Clever Portal by your teachers. 
• Other apps: Show the total number of allow listed Edtech applications not added through Clever, or any weblink—regardless if it’s accessed through Clever or not. 
 Each section is clickable so you can view only those specific apps categories in the full list underneath. 

 

 
 

 Applications - Usage data 

 • The Students % and Teacher % metrics show the percent of students and teachers using the app, out of total reported by the Clever Plus extension. 
• Active time per user is the average time a student and teacher is actively using the app during the time range selected on the page (since initial setup, in the last 28 days, in the last 7 days). 
 
 In the example above: 

 • 984 students and 97 teachers are reported describes users that are: 
 • synced to Clever 
• have the Clever Plus extension installed on Chromebooks and/or Windows devices 
• have been actively using a allow listed Edtech application. 
 

• 4% of the 984 students and 4% of the 97 teachers used Word Munchers for 10 minutes on average during the current school year.

title: For School Tech Leads: Data missing or incorrect
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Locating your school site's data 

 Overview 

 Using the search bar allows you to easily search for teachers, students, sections and your school site for troubleshooting missing or incorrect data. Incorrect data can prevent a user from logging in to Clever and may result in incorrect data within their scoped applications. The search bar on your STL Clever Dashboard. 

 Please note: Data syncs to Clever from your student information system (SIS). If data in Clever is incorrect, please contact your registrar or data clerk so they can make the necessary updates in your SIS. 

 Locating your school site's data 

 Searching for schools: On a school’s Overview page you will be able to view all the data associated with that site in Clever. 

 

 

 Searching for students: 

 Student Profile pages allow you to view the following: 

 • The sections the student is enrolled in 
• All of their associated data in Clever 
• Their guardian contacts 
• Their scoped applications 
• Their most recent logins to Clever 
 

 This allows you to determine whether data in Clever is accurate, they have access to the necessary applications and whether they’re having any difficulty logging in! 

 

 

 

 Searching for teachers: 

 Teacher Profile pages allow you to view the following: 

 • The teacher’s rostered sections 
• All of their associated data in Clever 
• Their scoped applications 
• Their most recent logins to Clever 
 

 This allows you to determine whether data in Clever is accurate, they have access to the necessary applications and whether they’re having any difficulty logging in! 

 

 

 Searching for sections Viewing sections Profile pages allows you to: 

 • Confirm section data 
• Confirm that the necessary teachers are associated with the section 
• Confirm that all of the correct students are rostered to the section 
• View the applications shared with the section

title: For School Tech Leads: Custom Sections
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I manually create custom sections? 3. How do I edit custom sections? 4. How do I delete custom sections? 5. How do I share custom sections 

 

 Overview 

 The Custom Sections feature allows district admins and School Tech Leads to create and manage sections manually within the Clever dashboard. This feature can be used to create sections for summer school, intervention groups, after-school programs, and more!

 Custom Sections are created and managed from the Clever dashboard and behave just like sections from your SIS. Once the custom sections are created, districts can share them with applications through the same sharing permissions interface.

 

 Please note: School Tech Leads are not able to view or edit Custom Sections created by other School Tech Leads or district admins. 

 

 
 How do I manually create custom sections? 

 You can create individual sections from your Clever dashboard. Step 1: Add Section Info To create custom sections, log in to the Clever and select Create Custom Sections 

 

 Next, select Add Custom Data > Sections 

 

 Begin by entering the name and school to be associated with the section. The following fields are required: 

 • Section Name
• School
• Subject
• Grade
 

 Once all required fields are populated, you will be able to press Next to proceed. We recommend adding any information that may be helpful in identifying the section in Clever and connected applications. Step 2: Add Teachers Next, select the primary teacher to associate with the section. You can also add up to 9 co-teachers for each custom section. Clever has provided filters to assist with locating staff members by school. 

 Please note: Not all applications support co-teachers. We recommend checking with the application’s support team to confirm if co-teachers are a supported feature. 

 Step 3: Select Primary Teacher Next, indicate the primary teacher for the section. If an application does not support co-teachers, this primary teacher will be the teacher that is connected to the custom section. 

 Step 4: Add Students Next, select students to be enrolled in the section. Clever has provided filters to help locate students using school, grade level, etc.

 Step 5: Preview Review the custom section to confirm the section information, including teachers and students, are correct. When ready, select “Create.”

 Step 6: Share with applications The custom section will be available to share with connected applications following your district's next successful sync with Clever. 

 
 How do I edit custom sections? 

 To edit a section, click on the pencil icon under ‘Actions’. This will take you back to the custom sections wizard where you can make adjustments as desired. 

 

 
 How do I delete custom sections? 

 Sections can be deleted from Clever by clicking on the under Actions . 

 
 How do I share custom sections? 

 Custom sections are shared with applications through the same interface as sections from the SIS. 

 Once a custom section is created, we recommend reviewing the district's sharing permissions to ensure the section is shared as desired. You may need to adjust your sharing permissions to ensure it is included. For more information on sharing rules, see this article: How do I set my sharing permissions for applications? .

title: For School Tech Leads: Clever Portal - Managing and organizing
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is the Clever Portal? 3. Accessing your Clever Portal 4. Adding & Editing Categories 5. Adding Custom External Links 6. Adjust a resource's visibility 7. What does "Category Locked" mean? 

 

 Overview

 School Tech Leads can organize and manage the Clever Portal for the schools that they are associated with. With this feature enabled, School Tech Leads can arrange tiles, add new categories, and add custom links for their schools.

 

 

 Please note: School Tech Leads can only edit the Portal for schools they are associated with. Only Clever Admins can edit the district Portal. For more information please see our article For Clever Admins: Customizing Clever Portal . 

 

 Want to learn more about customizing the Clever Portal as a School Tech Lead? Check out our course Clever Academy for STLs: Level 1 available in Clever Academy! 

 

 

 
 What is the Clever Portal? 

 The Clever Portal puts learning at teachers’ and students’ fingertips. Each student and teacher can access a personalized portal with single sign-on (SSO) to every learning application they use. As a School Tech Lead, you can organize and manage the Clever Portal for the schools that you are associated with. Customizations include:

 • Adding new categories 
• Adding custom links 
• Arranging links & categories 
 
 

 Please note: A Clever Admin will need to enable Portal organization for School Tech Leads in your district. If you don't have the ability to edit the Portal, be sure to reach out to your admin to have them enable this feature. 

 

 
 Accessing your Clever Portal 

 To access the Portal organization area for your school’s Clever Portal, start by logging into Clever, then navigate to the Dashboard tab at the top of the page.

 

 

 Next, select the Organize School Portal button from your Dashboard to begin organizing and managing the Portal for your school.

 

 

 
 Adding & Editing Categories 

 As a School Tech Lead, you can easily create custom categories to group resources that are frequently used by your school users to make it easier for them to locate these resources. To do this:

 • Select the school Portal you would like to organize from the dropdown at the top of the page labeled Organize as X school. 
• Once you've selected the school you'd like to edit, you can select the Add to the Portal button to add a category, a link, or add an application to a category.
• Any category you add or edit can be moved to the desired spot on the Clever Portal by dragging the category in the right-hand panel. 
• To delete a category, select the trashcan next to the corresponding to the category you wish to remove. 
• Select Publish Changes after you are finished!
 
 

 
 

 Adding Custom external links 

 Custom external links are perfect for adding resources that haven’t already been added to your district’s Clever Portal that are frequently utilized by your school. Some commonly added resources are:

 • The school website
• Monthly lunch menus
• Bus routes
• Forms (tech liability forms, field trip forms, teacher nomination forms, etc.)
• Morning announcements
• Field day information
• Learning resources
 
 To add custom external links: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard and select the Organize School Portal button.
• Select the school Portal you would like to organize from the dropdown at the top of the page labeled Organize as X school. 
• Once you've selected the school you'd like to edit, you can select the Add to the Portal button and select Add a link. 
• A modal will pop-up for adding the link. Select an icon or upload a custom icon (Note: Optimal icon size should be 200x200)
• Fill out the required fields for the link Title and URL.
• Select which user types (audience), grades, and schools you would like to add the custom link for. Please note that if adding for selected schools, the link will only display for the user if the selected school matches the user's primary school association.
• Choose one or multiple categories to organize the link into.
• Click Add. 
• Be sure to click Publish Changes on the top right of the page to finalize your updates. 
 
 Clever Tip: Before adding custom external links to your school’s Clever Portal, make sure to scroll through the current Portal and look at the list of the integrated applications your district has. To look at the existing integrated applications your district has, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > App sharing rules . 

 

 
 

 Adjust a resource’s visibility 

 Visibility allows you to hide an icon from a subset of users for a specific period of time. Visibility settings can be set per user type. To hide an icon, follow these steps: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Organize School Portal page. 
• 
 Locate the application you wish to hide and click the three vertical dots.

• 
 Select Hide/show in Portal. 

• 
 Select Hide temporarily .

• 
 Select all users (student, teachers, staff) you want to hide the application from for the same timeframe. If you want to hide the application for a separate user type for a different timeframe, you can use the Add another user type button after you configure the timeframe for your first user type.

• 
 Fill in the start and end dates for hiding.

• 
 Click Save. 

• 
 Be sure to select Publish changes in the top right corner of the Organize district portal page to apply the visibility settings.

 
 

 
 What does "Category Locked" mean? 

 District-level categories can’t be modified by School Tech Leads. These categories will appear with the label Category Locked . If you wish to edit a district-level category, please contact your Clever admin.

title: For School Tech Leads: Badges
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What are Clever Badges? 3. How do Clever Badges work? 4. Technical requirements for Badges 5. How do I generate Badges from my Portal? 6. Devices without cameras 

 

 Overview 

 Clever Badges are a great tool to ease logins in the classroom at your school site. This article will discuss how Clever Badges work, the technical requirements, and how you can generate Clever Badges for students at your school site. 

 
 What are Clever Badges? 

 Clever Badges allow students to use a physical Badge to log in to Clever in lieu of typing in credentials to authenticate users. Badges are an easier way for the youngest students to access their apps. School Tech Leads can print Badges for individual students, entire school sites, or sections, as long as Badges have been enabled by a Clever Admin.

 Badges are tied to the student. As such, changes in school, teacher, or section will not affect the Badge. Grade-level access for Badges is managed by your district administrator. If Badges are not available for the desired grade level, please contact your district support contact . For more information about enabling Badges for your district, please see this Help Center article .

 Please note: Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature an expiration date. Existing printed Badges will continue to work past the expiration date. Admins are expected to void and print Badges annually during transition periods, like summer, to keep them secure ahead of the first day of school. See this article for more information. 

 

 

 

 
 How do Clever Badges work? 

 • Students navigate to clever.com/badges and click Allow in the browser prompt to give Clever access to their camera. They can also go to their district's Clever URL and click the Badges icon. Clever Badges are designed to be used directly with Clever, and a QR reader won't log the student in to Clever.
• The webcam will activate and students should hold up their Badge to the camera. Once the code is scanned the screen border will flash green.
• Students will then be redirected to their Clever Portal.
 
 

 Pro Tip: Security Badges provide direct access to a student's Clever account and should be protected just like a password. Anyone who has access to a Badge can sign in as a student and access their accounts in both Clever and connected applications. Please protect the security of your students and do not share images of the QR code publicly on social media. 

 

 
 Technical Requirements for Badges 

 You will need to following items to implement and use Badges: 

 • Supported web browser (Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari) 
• Printer 
• Computers/devices with webcam 
 
 

 
 How do I generate Badges from my Portal? 

 Badges can be downloaded using the directions below. They can also be downloaded by teachers and parents/guardians using these directions: 

 • How to download a Clever Badge as a teacher 
• How to download a Clever Badge as a parent/guardian 
 
 For School Tech Leads: Printing Individual Badges 

 • From your Clever Portal, navigate to your Dashboard
• Once in the Dashboard, search for the student in the search bar 
• If the student does not have a Badge generated, select Create Clever Badge on the right-hand side of their Profile
• If the student's Clever Badge is already generated, select a download option
 
 Please note: If you select Void Clever Badge the student's Clever Badge will no longer function and you will need to generate a new Badge for them. 

 For School Tech Leads: Printing school site and section Badges 

 1. From your Clever Portal, navigate to your Dashboard.

 

 2. Once in the Dashboard, choose Badges. 

 

 3. In the "Overview" tab, you will have two options to generate Badges. Whenever a new student joins your school (within the configured grade range for Badges), you can click New to generate Badges for only those new students at the school. 

 Or, you can select All to generate Badges for all the students at your school. Clicking All will not void any existing Badges. It will simply regenerate any existing badges as well as create new badges for any students who did not yet have one. 

 

 4. Once you select an option, the Badges will either be emailed to your or immediately start downloading, to a zip file with PDFs of the Badges. You'll have the option to either print by grade level or by section. 

 

 

 
 Devices without cameras 

 If a student doesn't have access to a device with a camera, they can log in using the district's normal login method. (e.g. Log in with Google, Log in with Active Directory, Log in with Clever). If you require assistance with identifying their username and password for one of these login methods, please reach out to your district's tech support contact . 

 

 

 Please let us know if you have any questions! You can always submit a ticket to Clever Support.

title: For School Tech Leads: Announcements
text:
School Tech Leads have the ability to send announcements to the schools they administer through the Clever Dashboard. School Tech Leads assigned to multiple schools will send notifications to all schools they are associated with.

 

 This article contains the following: 1. How do I create an announcement for my users in the Clever Portal? 2. What will the announcement look like in the Clever Portal? 

 

 How do I create an announcement for my school's users in the Clever Portal?

 To create an announcement, log in to Clever and select Create portal notifications . 

 

 • Select Create notification . 
• Select the Start Date, End Date. 
• Enter a title 
• Enter the body content 
• Select which users you would like to see the Portal Notification 
• If you would like to provide users with a link, enter the URL and the text you would like displayed. Example: 
 

 

 Please note: URLs must be prefixed with https:// to be valid. 

 

 What will the announcement look like in the Clever Portal?

 Users that are scoped to receive the message will see a notification in the toolbar of their Portal. 

 

 Please note: Announcements made by School Tech Leads cannot be "prioritized". The prioritization feature is limited to Clever Admins. 

 For instructions on how district administrators can create announcements, please see: For District Admins: How do I create an announcement in the Clever Portal?

title: For Clever Admins: How do I delete my district's Clever account?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Instructions 3. Retention limits and inactivity 

 Overview

 Only the Clever Administrator "owner" can delete a district's Clever account. Please note that this action is permanent, and the account cannot be restored once deleted. All data in connected applications will also be deleted for district users.

 We strive to improve our products and services consistently and would love to understand why you plan to discontinue using Clever. Any feedback you are willing to provide would be greatly appreciated. Please let us know by contacting Clever Support ! 

 

 
 

 Instructions

 If you'd like to proceed with deleting your Clever account, please: 

 • Confirm you're the Clever account owner on the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page.
• Click on your name in the upper right-hand corner and select Profile .
• Select Delete district account .
 
 

 • Confirm your intention in the pop-up by providing a reason for the deletion.
• Type "CONFIRM DELETE", then click Delete district account .
 
 

 

 
 Retention limits and inactivity

 In addition to requested deletions, Clever has set certain retention limits so that some Student Data expires after extended inactivity criteria are met. Please see Clever Additional Terms of Use for Schools for more information: https://www.clever.com/trust/terms/schools

title: For Clever Admins: How do I subscribe to status updates?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Subscribing to status updates 3. Managing status update subscriptions 4. Unsubscribing from status updates 

 

 Overview

 There are a variety of Clever status updates available online, or that can be communicated to Clever admins in various ways. This article details the different status update options, the alert methods available, and how to subscribe to receive them. Clever system status dynamic updates are always available at https://status.clever.com . From this page, users can: View the status of a variety of Clever services: 

 View previous messages and incidents: 

 

 Review system metrics: 

 

 

 
 

 Subscribing to status updates

 To subscribe to receive updates regarding Clever services, select Subscribe to updates at the top right of the Clever Status page. 

 

 Choose your method of delivery: 

 • Email: Get email notifications whenever Clever creates, updates, or resolves an incident.
• Phone: Get SMS notifications whenever Clever creates or resolves an incident.
• Webhook: Get a webhook notification whenever Clever creates, updates, or resolves an incident, or changes a component status.
• Tweet: Follow our status channel - @ssostatus
• RSS Feed: Add Clever's status page to your RSS feed. 
 
 Most Clever admins will select to receive updates via email. To do this, enter your email address and select Subscribe via email .

 

 The next page will allow you to choose which components you want to monitor. Clever recommends the following settings: 

 • Single Sign-On
• SIS Sync (choose your district's sync type)
• Support
 
 Select Save to save your notification settings.

 

 

 
 

 

 Managing status update subscriptions

 • Open https://status.clever.com. 
• Click Subscribe to Updates 
• Enter your email address, and select Subscribe via email. 
• You should receive a message that states: " Your email is already subscribed. Use the form below to manage your subscription." 
• Update the components you want to follow or unfollow. 
• Select Update Preferences 
 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 Unsubscribing from status updates

 • Open https://status.clever.com 
• Click Subscribe to Updates 
• Enter your email address, and select Subscribe via email 
• You should receive a message that states: " Your email is already subscribed. Use the form below to manage your subscription." 
• Select Cancel Subscription 
• Select Unsubscribe from Updates

title: For Clever Admins: How do I reset my password?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Reset Password 3. Update Password 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 Clever district admins can easily reset their passwords in case of a lost or forgotten one. This article details the process for resetting a district admin password.

 IMPORTANT When resetting your password, you'll also need access to the authentication method you've set up for Multi Factor Authentication (e.g. your cell phone to receive a text message code, Google/Microsoft authenticator app, etc.) If you don't have access to the authentication method you set up, another Clever Admin at your district can reset MFA for you . Alternatively, you can contact Clever support by clicking the chat icon in the bottom right corner. 

 

 
 Reset Password

 To reset your District Admin password for Clever,

 • Visit schools.clever.com and click Forgot your password? 
• Enter the email address associated with your admin account and click Send 
• An email will be sent with a link to reset your password. If you don't receive the email, please check your spam filter and make sure no-reply@clever.com is added as a contact to your email account.
 
 Quick link: https://schools.clever.com/reset 

 

 

 
 Update Password

 If you would like to update your Clever password, click on your name in the top-right of the district dashboard and click Profile . Click Edit to update your password. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 When attempting to log in to Clever, why do I see a note about my password being part of a public data breach? 

 • Clever determines that a password is unsafe if it's been made public in an unrelated data breach. We perform privacy-preserving checks for passwords which have been leaked online. If a Clever Admin password matches any of these, we require the DA to reset their password.
 
 

 Are there recommendations for choosing a secure password? 

 • Use a password management solution like 1Password to use strong/unique passwords for all logins.
• Combine three or four random words. For example: “student-task-fish-fly”. These combinations are usually easy to remember and very secure.

title: For Clever Admins: How do I update my email preferences?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Instructions 

 Overview

 Clever admins have the ability to edit email preferences from their Clever Dashboard in order to manage the types of emails they will receive from Clever. 

 Instructions

 Step 1: From the Clever Dashboard , click on your name in the top right corner and select 'Profile'. 

 Step 2: Navigate to the 'Emails' tab. 

 Step 3: Update your email preferences as desired. When disabling a category of emails, the 'Disabled' box for an email category should appear as a blue box with white text. 

 ​Step 4: Select 'Save' to update your email preferences. 

 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 ​

title: For Clever Admins: How do I update my profile settings?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Personal details 3. Change password 4. Multi-factor authentication 

 Overview

 On the Profile page of your Clever Dashboard, you can edit your user information, change your password, and enable multi-factor authentication. To access your Profile page:

 • Log in to your Clever Dashboard. 
• Click on your name in the upper right-hand corner and click Profile .
• Under Account Information, click Edit .
 
 

 
 

 Personal details

 From here, you can update your personal details.

 

 
 

 Change password

 To change your Clever account password, click Change Password . You'll be prompted to enter your existing password and then your new password twice. Click Update to save your changes. 

 
 

 Multi-factor authentication

 Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds an additional level of security to your account, and is required for Clever Admin who use a username/password to log in. With MFA, you'll add another factor (such as a text message code or security key) to verify your identity alongside your username/password.

 

 

 This extra identity confirmation prevents unauthorized individuals from gaining access to your Clever District Administrator account even if your password is compromised. To learn more about MFA authentication, read this article.

title: For Clever Admins: How do I update sync settings?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Auto Sync Settings 3. SFTP Sync Settings 4. School year & pause dates 5. Sync hold thresholds 

 Overview

 The Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page is where you can view and edit details pertaining to your Clever data sync. The features and editable fields on your Sync Settings page will vary depending on your sync type. 

 
 

 Auto Sync Settings 

 For Auto Sync districts, the Sync Settings page is where you can:

 • View or edit your SIS URL (make sure that the URL you enter matches the one you use to log into your SIS)
• View or edit your SIS username
• Change your SIS Password
• View your sync schedule and initiate a new sync
• View default field mappings or request sync customizations
• Schedule, adjust, or cancel a sync pause 
 
 

 
 

 SFTP Sync Settings 

 For SFTP Sync districts, the Sync Settings page is where you can:

 • View your SFTP credentials to upload data to Clever via FTP Client 
• Upload files directly from your Clever Dashboard via the Web Upload Tool 
• Download your district-specific SFTP Instructions (these instructions will automatically update as you continue to add learning applications to your Clever Dashboard - use them to determine which roster data to include in your uploads and how to format your data files )
• Schedule, adjust, or cancel a sync pause 
 
 

 
 

 

 School year & pause dates 

 Below your SIS Sync Settings, you can also adjust settings based on your school year, including:

 • Share your school year dates with us (so we can best support you throughout the year)
• Schedule, adjust, or cancel a sync pause 
 
 

 
 

 Sync hold thresholds 

 Finally, at the bottom of the Sync Settings page, you'll see the Sync Hold Thresholds section. When your district's most recent attempted sync will change a significant amount of data in Clever, our system initiates an automatic sync hold. This hold prevents the changes from processing until reviewed and approved by a district admin or by Clever. You can modify the thresholds that trigger a sync hold to prevent unnecessary holds. To prevent accidental data deletions, we do not allow for disabling delete thresholds completely. Learn more about sync hold thresholds here .

title: For Clever Admins: Setting up multi-factor authentication (MFA) for Clever Admin
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What's MFA? 3. Setting up MFA 4. Adding authentication methods 5. Using MFA after setup 6. Viewing MFA activation status 7. Admin access control 8. Resetting MFA for admin users from the Team Page 9. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview 

 Clever offers all Clever Admins complimentary multi-factor authentication (MFA) to provide an extra layer of security for admin accounts (Clever Admin, Curriculum Lead, or District Help Desk). This article explains MFA and how to set it up for your Clever account. 

 IMPORTANT Secure logins are required for all Clever Admins, either using multi-factor authentication (MFA) or single sign-on (SSO) Why is this required?
