Login credentials can be subject to malicious attacks, resulting in unauthorized access to personally identifiable information (PII). As a trusted guardian of sensitive data, we want to support you in curbing ever-evolving threats and keeping your logins safe. 

 

 
 What’s MFA? 

 MFA is a way to log in to programs that requires you to use two methods (or factors) to prove your identity, like your username and password on your computer and a text message code sent to your phone. You likely already use MFA when signing in to other programs, such as your online bank account. 

 Please note: If you previously set up two-factor authentication in Clever for your admin account using the Authy authentication app or SMS, you can upgrade your authentication method to a more secure choice such as, Google and Microsoft authenticator apps, hardware tokens, and voice messages. 

 Having two methods makes it much harder for someone to hack into your account because they’d need to access to both your username/password and the second method you used to prove your identity. The more protected your account credentials are, the safer your account! To use MFA, you’ll need to add an authentication method , such as a text message, phone call, or push notification to your phone. This second method is how MFA confirms your identity in addition to your username/password. 

 
 Setting up MFA 

 Please note: If you’re an admin trying to set up Clever MFA for your district users (such as teachers and staff), please reference this article . 

 

 • Go to Clever Dashboard > Profile . 
• Under the Multi-factor authentication section, click Activate MFA . If you previously had set up two-factor authentication (TFA/2FA), click Upgrade MFA .
• Clever's supported authentication methods will be presented. You can learn more about authentication options below . Choose a method and click Next step . 
• Follow the instructions to set up the authentication method.
• Recommended: If you’d like to add more than one authentication method, click Set up another method to add another option. This will enable you to still log in should one of your second methods be unavailable (e.g., you don't have cell service for a text message).
• Click Exit settings . 
 
 
 Adding authentication methods

 You must add at least one authentication method to use MFA. Authentication method options are listed below. Adding at least one other authentication method as a backup method is good practice. This way, if you are not able to use one method for any reason (e.g., your cell phone is not charged, so you can’t receive a text message), you can use a backup method to log in successfully. Having multiple methods saves time by providing multiple options.

 Please note: Clever only supports US numbers for SMS and voice calls at this time.

 

 Text message (also called SMS) 

 Clever texts a numeric code to a cell phone number you choose. You then type this code into your Clever login screen. Setup tips:

 • Text messages require cellular service. We do not recommend this authentication method if you do not have reliable cellular service in your work environment. Please consider a Mobile app. 
• Be sure you can access your cell phone during the school day. You’ll need the code texted to this number to log in to Clever. 
• A text message will not work for a landline. If you can only access a landline, consider an automated voice phone call.
• International phone number support: if your country code is not currently supported, please use an alternate method.
 
 

 Why doesn't Clever support email as an authentication method? You can reset your account password using your email. Using MFA with email would provide a single point of failure: a malicious actor could both reset your password and access your account with your email account. 

 

 Automated voice phone call 

 Clever sends a numeric code via an automated voice phone call to a number you choose (it can be a landline or cell phone). Then, you type this code into your Clever login screen. Setup tips:

 • An automated voice call will be sent to the number added, so ensure you can answer the phone and hear the code shared.
• International phone number support : if your country code is not currently supported, please use an alternate method, like a Mobile app. The Mobile app method will work with most time-based one-time password (TOTP) apps.
• If you need to update the phone number, remove this factor and re-add the factor with the new number.
 • To remove an authentication method from MFA: 1. Click on Profile in the top-right of the Clever Dashboard 2. Under Multi-factor authentication, click Settings 3. Click on the authentication you would like to remove, click Remove , then click Confirm 4. When complete, click Exit settings 
 

 
 Mobile app 

 You install an app on a cell phone that provides you with a numeric code you type into your Clever login screen. Setup tips:

 • Clever highly recommends using Mobile app as a second factor. It is highly secure and is available even if you do not have cell service. This is a great option if you do not have cell service at school.
• To access this option, you’ll need to install an authenticator app to a cell phone and follow the MFA setup steps in Clever, so be sure you’ll have access to the phone during the school day.
• To set up the mobile app method, you will need to scan a QR code or use the authenticator key with your time-based one-time password (TOTP) mobile app. See the instructions for your mobile app for scanning QR codes or using authenticator keys.
• Clever supports one Mobile app configuration per account. Check the instructions for your mobile app to see if you can mirror your configuration across multiple devices. To use a different mobile app or reconfigure on a different device, you must remove the mobile app authentication configuration associated with your current device and re-add mobile app authentication using your new app or device.
 
 Setup steps, Google Authenticator App: 

 • On your cell phone , install the app by searching “Google Authenticator App” in the Google Play Store (Android) or the App Store (Apple). The app icon looks like this:
 
 

 

 On your phone , you can also click the links below to access the download page for the apps: 

 • On your computer , in the Clever Dashboard, navigate to Dashboard > Profile > MFA > Settings.
• Click Mobile app and click Next step. A QR code will appear. 
• On your cell phone , open the authenticator app and select Scan a QR code . You may need to grant permission for the app to use your phone’s camera.
• Using your phone's camera, scan the QR code on the computer screen.
• After scanning the code, click Next step on your computer.
• On your cell phone , the app will provide you with a code. On your computer , enter this code into the provided space on the MFA setup screen. 
• Click Done .
• Click Exit Settings .
 
 After this initial setup, when prompted to use MFA, you won’t need to scan a QR code: you’ll just open the Google Authenticator App and tap on the account you added . You’ll be provided with a numeric code to type into Clever. Setup steps, Microsoft Authenticator App: 

 • On your cell phone , install the app by searching “M

title: Impersonating or creating users for demos, training, and troubleshooting
text:
The Access Portal As tool is not for troubleshooting logins to Clever . For information on troubleshooting logins to Clever, please see this article .

 

 This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Which user types can impersonate another user? 3. Instructions to impersonate a real user 4. Instructions to create a demo user 5. Extra Credit: Clever Academy 

 Overview

 School and district teams often need to see the Clever Portal from a student, teacher, staff, or other administrative perspective for troubleshooting, demos, or training. Clever’s impersonation tools let administrators and STLs access the Portal of a user to investigate issues or confirm what a user should see. Impersonation permissions vary based on your Clever account type.

 For training or demo needs where accessing an actual account isn’t necessary, districts can instead create demo users via custom data. These accounts offer a safe way to explore features, test configurations, or model workflows without touching real data.

 Together, impersonation and demo accounts give organizations flexible, secure options for support and training.

 

 Please note: Administrators who are Clever Admins or District Help Desk roles, and School Tech Leads can use this tool. School Tech Leads only have access to Teacher Portals and can't sign in as students. 

 

 
 Which user types can impersonate another user?

 Please reference the table below for more information on which user types and impersonate other users. The only role that can impersonate all other roles by default is the Clever Admin Owner. Additional impersonation permissions can be assigned via Custom Roles. 

 

 
 User type

 
 
 Can impersonate into

 

 Clever Admin Owner Clever Admin Non-Owner District Help Desk District Curriculum Lead School Tech Lead 
 Clever Admins By default, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only 
 District Help Desk By default, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only 
 District Curriculum Lead By default, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only 
 School Tech Lead By default, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only 
 Portal-only staff By default With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only With custom role, view-only 
 Teachers By default By default By default With custom role, view-only If turned on for their school organization 
 Students By default By default By default With custom role, view-only 
 
 
 Instructions to impersonate a real user

 Impersonating students, teachers, and Portal-only staff 

 If you’d like to impersonate a student, teacher, or Portal-only staff, there are two ways to do so: 

 • Search for the user using the search bar at the top of your Clever Dashboard. From the drop-down, hover over the user and select the Portal as user button. 
• Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data browser , locate the user, and click their name to navigate to their profile page. Once on their profile page, click the Support Tools button and select Access Portal as user to access the user's portal. 
 
 With either option, you'll start the Portal session on the next screen. Once in the user’s Portal, you can access any of their connected applications and use any tools to which the user has access.

 When you’re done, remember to log out of any applications you’ve accessed and select End Portal session . 

 

 

 Impersonating Clever Admins, District Curriculum Leads, District Help Desk, STLs, and custom admin roles 

 You can also impersonate a Clever Admin, District Curriculum Lead, District Help Desk, STL, or administrator with a custom role. When impersonating these user types, you’ll have view-only access to their account (both the Clever Dashboard and Portal) and won’t be able to make changes. To impersonate one of these user types:

 1. Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page.

 2. Select the Actions button, and choose Access Portal as user. The user will receive an email notification that an admin has accessed their account. 

 Access Portal as user drop down" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000UDCj&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl00000XhaoR"> 

 If you want STLs in your school organization to have the ability to view teacher accounts for training and troubleshooting, view this help center article . 

 

 Please note: The browsers that support this feature are Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. 

 

 
 Instructions to create a demo user

 If you'd like to create a brand new demo user, so you can use the account without accessing or modifying an actual user's account (like building a Teacher Page), you can create a fake teacher, student, or staff user using Clever's custom data feature. This can also be used if you’d like to preview Classroom MFA to see what the login experience would look like for a particular user type.

 You can learn how to create a custom teacher here , a custom student here , and a staff user here. Once you've created a user account, you can log in to the account via the credentials you set when creating the custom user. Just be sure to use the student login screen ( Clever.com/in/your-district-url ), and not the District Admin login screen .

 
 Extra Credit: Clever Academy

 If you want to learn more about the Student and Teacher Portal features you can view teacher training content in Clever Academy.

title: For School Tech Leads: Clever Academy - Learn more about Clever!
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How can teachers access the Clever Academy? 3. How can I view my courses or certifications? 

 

 Overview 

 Clever Academy is our free online training and certification program for educators. You can access it through your Clever Portal or at https://clever.academy . Go through all the courses and after successful completion, you’ll become a certified Clever Champion. NOTE: You must begin at Level 1, though you can test out of courses if you have the prerequisite knowledge. 

 

 

 Course outline:

 Level 1: Getting started with Clever In this first course, you’ll get an overview of what Clever is and how it can your and teachers’ lives easier! Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 1. This course is a prerequisite to Level 2 and beyond.

 Level 2: Learning the basics Clever’s your school's one-stop shop for digital learning. Get the lowdown on portal features, and start to customize your school's portal. Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 2. This course is a prerequisite to Level 3 and beyond.

 Level 3: Setting your district up for success STL tools are your secret weapon for support teachers and students! Dig into analytic data, training teachers, and troubleshooting tech issues! Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 3. This course is a prerequisite to Level 4 and beyond.

 Level 4: Becoming a Clever Champion Become a Clever Champion. Learn how to use your STL powers to support every aspect of digital learning at your school. Complete this course and you’ll be a certified Clever Champion Admin!

 
 How can I access Clever Academy? 

 Staff and School Tech Leads can access the Clever Academy by logging into the Clever Portal with their district-specific URL or directly going to https://clever.academy . 

 

 
 How can I view my courses or certifications?

 You can see the courses you've registered for or completed at any time by navigating to Clever Academy , clicking person icon in the top right, and selecting My Profile. From here, you'll see a list of all your learning paths and courses. Any course with a certification that you've completed will have a View certificate link next to it. Click the link to view the certificate, and then print it if you'd like! Certificates are valid for two years. If it has been more than two years since you completed a course, you would need to re-take the course to become re-certified.

title: For Clever Admins: How do I browse and search data in Clever with the Data Browser or Search Bar?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Using the Search Bar 3. Using the Data Browser 4. Searching with filters in the Data Browser 5. Exporting data via the Data Browser 6. What data is shared with connected applications? 

 Overview

 Clever Admins have two ways to review their district or school’s SIS rostering data in Clever. The first method Clever Admins can use is the search bar at the top of the page from the Clever Dashboard to quickly find a specific school, student, teacher, section, staff member, or application in Clever. The second method is the Data Browser. Clever Admins can use the Data Browser to view and filter the rostering data (schools.csv, teachers.csv, students.csv, sections.csv, and staff.csv) currently in Clever. This includes both custom data and data synced by your district or school’s student information system (SIS). Clever Admins can access the Data Browser from the left menu sidebar in the Clever Dashboard .

 

 
 

 Using the Search Bar

 The first method for locating data or applications in your Clever account is utilizing the Search Bar at the top of your Clever Dashboard.

 Begin typing in the Search Bar, and all possible related content will appear:

 Use the icons below the search bar to filter your results further. For example, if we only want students with the name “Sarah” to appear in our results, we can click the Student filter icon, and our search results will narrow to just students:

 

 You can also take actions such as viewing their profile for more information, accessing the user’s Portal, or resetting their MFA method directly from the Search Bar by clicking the user to view a dropdown menu.

 

 Actions you can take directly from the Search Bar will vary based on the user type or application you are viewing, as well as settings configured for that particular user type, but may include:

 • 
 Viewing a user’s profile in the Clever Dashboard

• 
 Accessing the user’s Portal

• 
 Resetting a password

• 
 Updating a staff role from Portal Access Only to School Tech Lead

• 
 Editing application sharing

• 
 Requesting an application

 
 In addition to viewing search results and taking actions directly from the Search Bar, you can also navigate to a full page of search results by selecting the See all X results text at the bottom of your search.

 

 This option will bring you to a full page of search results, on which you can filter by data type and take actions similar to those within the Search Bar. The full-page results will allow you to review your search results more completely.

 

 An example of a full page for search results. 

 
 

 Using the Data Browser

 The second method for viewing your district or school's information in Clever is to navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data browser . Once in the Data Browser, select the appropriate data tab at the top of the page to view district schools, students, teachers, staff, sections, terms, courses, or contacts.

 After you've selected a tab, you can see context about each record, including:

 • Data source: This is where the data came from, such as your SIS, a custom data upload, Active Directory, etc. 
• Last Modified: The last time the record was changed or updated.
 
 

 

 
 

 Searching with filters in the Data Browser

 You can drill down further by clicking Add Filter to search for specific records using one or more filters. Once the list populates, click the name of a record to view the record's associated details.

 Filters and hyperlinks allow you to easily review the data in Clever. From the appropriate tab, use the filter feature to search for specific records. Then, narrow your search results by adding additional filters. Finally, click the record name to open the user or record's details window. There are additional features available to Clever Admins from the user or record's details card. For example:

 • Voiding or generating Clever Badges for students (if enabled at your district or school).
• Navigating to a user or record's profile page in Clever.
• Impersonating users to access their Clever Portal(s).
• Troubleshooting login issues
 
 

 
 

 Exporting data via the Data Browser

 You can easily export data from the data browser by clicking the Export button in the top right. The records will be emailed to the email address associated with your Clever account. 

 

 

 
 

 What data is shared with connected applications?

 Clever Admins can determine which data is shared with the district or school's connected applications by setting their sharing rules . The Data Browser and Search Bar surface the district/school's entire set of rostering data in Clever, but not everything that is visible in Clever is necessarily shared with every application. 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Sharing Permissions: Overview 
• For Clever Admins: Apps - Data access 
• For Clever Admins: How do I view a user's Clever Portal?

title: For Clever Admins: How do I use the Clever Dashboard?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever Dashboard sidebar 3. Clever Dashboard homepage • How to utilize the Applications area of your homepage 4. Finding more information on Clever’s features, products, and tools 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview 

 Clever Dashboard is where Clever admins can review the status of their sync, manage their applications, view district data, configure Clever Identity Management (IDM), Classroom Multi-factor Authentication (MFA), make necessary adjustments to their account, and more.

 In Fall 2025, the Clever team reorganized the sidebar to simplify the location of tools you use every day. While we understand that change can be difficult, the new navigation groups tools into similar categories, making them easier to locate. Below, we’ve included a handy guide to help you navigate the updates.

 Additionally, the Clever home page also has a new, more modernized look! Consider the new Dashboard Homepage your Clever command center. You’ll still be able to access the same information previously found on your home page, in addition to new status features that enable you to assess at-a-glance how your Clever account is performing. Learn more below.

 
 Clever Dashboard sidebar 

 Below, you can compare the previous sidebar to the updated sidebar, as well as find a table that maps the previous location of features, tools, and products to their updated locations. 

 

 

 Feature, tool, or product 
 Pre-Fall 2025 location

 
 Updated location

 
 Home page 
 Home 

 Dashboard home 
 My applications Applications > My applications Applications > My applications 
 Add applications Applications > Add applications Applications > My applications 
 Google license manager Applications > License manager User management > License manager 
 Teacher installs Applications > Teacher installs Applications > Teacher installs 
 Library settings Applications > Library settings Applications > Library settings 
 Customize Portal SSO & Portal > Customize Portal Portal > Organize district Portal 
 Portal settings SSO & Portal > Portal settings Portal > Portal settings 
 SSO Settings SSO & Portal > SSO settings Authentication > SSO settings 
 Badges SSO & Portal > Badges Authentication > Badges 
 Communication (Portal announcements) SSO & Portal > Communication Portal > Communication 
 Edtech Analytics Analytics > Edtech Analytics Analytics > Edtech Analytics 
 Portal Analytics Analytics > Portal Analytics Analytics > Portal Analytics 
 Reports Analytics > Reports Analytics > Reports 
 Custom data Support tools > Custom data Data sources > Custom data 
 Data quality Support tools > Data quality Support tools > Data quality 
 Access logs Support tools > Access logs Authentication > Access logs 
 Troubleshoot login Support tools > Troubleshoot login Support tools > Troubleshoot login 
 Troubleshoot sharing Support tools > Troubleshoot sharing Support tools > Troubleshoot sharing 
 Clever admin checklist Support tools > Clever admin checklist Support tools > Clever admin checklist 
 Data browser Data browser Data browser 
 Sync Sync Data sources > SIS sync 
 LMS Connect LMS Connect User management > Admin team 
 IDM IDM User management > IDM 
 App Store (Purchasing/renewals for Clever IDM, Classroom MFA, CI Solutions + Clever Badges, premium support, consulting, and trainings) App store 
 Billing: Billing information for any paid services

 Renewals : Can be under Billing if you have a subscription or on each product page (ex: IDM renewals can be completed under User management > IDM > Renewals)

 CI Solutions + Clever Badges: Authentication > Clever Badges > Badges+ tab

 To book consultings and trainings : Training & Support > Clever Premium Support, Clever Consulting, or Clever Training

 
 Support Center Training & Support Training & Support 
 Clever Academy Training & Support Training & Support 
 
 

 

 

 Clever Dashboard homepage 

 Previously, Clever’s homepage included an overview of your sync, task notifications, and a list of your applications:

 

 

 With the updated homepage , you’ll still see this information, along with additional status cards that provide more information on the tools you’re using in Clever. This includes:

 • 
 An updated sync report with more insights into sync changes.

• 
 An overview of user logins, allowing you to quickly verify if logins are operating successfully.

• 
 A quick summary of applications you’re sharing data with, apps that you still need to set up sharing for, and pending app invites, as well as the ability to pin important applications to your home page. Learn more below.

• 
 Task notifications to guide you on the next steps to take with your Clever account.

 
 The new homepage is customized based on the features you’re using in Clever. Below is an example homepage, although yours may look different:

 

 

 How to utilize the applications area of your homepage

 In the image above, you’ll notice that the Applications area of the home page contains a lot of valuable information about your applications. There’s a concise overview of the number of applications you’re currently sharing data with, pending application invites, and apps with no data shared. Your top six applications with the most users shared will appear by default, but you can also pin applications you’re interested in keeping an eye on. Additional actions you can take from the Applications box include:

 • 
 Clicking the icon above your pending app invites or apps without data shared will navigate to that application and complete the setup.

• 
 Click View all to navigate to your My Applications page .

• 
 Click the pin icon in the top right corner to select applications to pin in this area. Applications you pin are unique to you. Pinning applications can help you keep track of the application usage you’re most interested in for your schools. If you’d like to change the order of your pinned applications, please delete the app from your pins and re-add it.

 
 
 

 Finding more information on Clever’s features, products, and tools 

 Browse the articles linked below to learn more about the various features, tools, and products you can find in your Clever Dashboard.

 Dashboard Home

 • 
 Select the 'Dashboard Home' option to access your account overview page including district-wide app details, the latest sync information, and Clever updates.

 
 Applications

 • 
 Select My applications to view all your connected apps; select a specific application to view its data warnings and set sharing permissions . 

• 
 Select Add applications to add a new application .

• 
 Select LMS Connect to configure your Clever LMS Connect setup or review settings.

• 
 Select Teacher installs to view the apps teachers have added via Clever Library .

• 
 Select Library Settings to determine how your district will manage sharing data from Clever Library .

 
 Data sources

 • 
 Select SIS sync to view settings for the sync with your student information system and data created in Clever via the sync with your SIS.

• 
 Select Custom data to manually create custom data that is not synced to Clever by your SIS.

 
 Data browser

 • 
 Select Data browser to view all your district's data being synced to Clever.

 
 User management

 • 
 Select IDM to set up or review your configuration for Clever IDM , including reviewing recent syncs.

• 
 Select License manager to manage any paid licenses for Google.

• 
 Select Admin team to manage your team settings and add Clever admins !

 
 Authentication

 • 
 Select Access logs to view users' single sign-on (SSO) attempts into your Portal. 

• 
 Select SSO settings to set up how your users log into Clever .

• 
 Select Badges to configure student Badges for your district students. With Clever Badges , students can log in to Clever by holding up a physical Badge to the computer's webcam instead of typing in credentials!

• 
 Select Classroom MFA to configure your Classroom MFA setu

title: For Clever Admins: How do I access Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. District Admin Owner 3. Accessing the Clever Dashboard 4. Logging in to Clever 

 Overview

 District admins can access the Clever Dashboard by going to the Clever login page ( https://schools.clever.com ) and entering your email address and the password you set when you signed up for Clever. Once here, District admins do the following in Clever: 

 • Manage the flow of data from their student information system (SIS) to Clever to their district’s connected applications
• Modify account settings for the whole district, such as the sync settings between the SIS and Clever, sharing permissions with connected applications, and single sign-on (SSO) credentials
• Use Single sign-on (SSO) to access learning applications as a district admin
 
 District Admin Owner

 If you were the one to sign up for Clever for your district, congratulations, you are the "owner" of the account! This means you are the first Clever district admin. The owner of the account can add new district admins, and any administrator can add new school admins. For more information on how to add new admins, please see this article on how to manage your team .

 Accessing the Clever Dashboard 

 The Clever Dashboard is where you can manage your district's data and account settings. You can access the Clever Dashboard by going to the Clever login page ( https://schools.clever.com ) and entering your email address and the password you set when you signed up for Clever, or when you were added to your district’s account. Read this article to learn how to use the Clever Dashboard .

 

 Logging in to Clever 

 The Clever Portal is where users in your district access connected applications. To access your Clever applications as a district admin, you’ll use a customized portal URL for your district that your students and teachers will use to access the Clever Portal. Usually, this URL follow the format: < clever.com/in/<district shortname >. Once there, you have two options for accessing the Portal:

 • Log in with your Identity Provider (IDP) credentials 
 • If your single sign-on (SSO) setup is configured to allow for district admin access (e.g. your Google Apps email address is the same as your district admin email address), you can use the ‘Log In’ button on this screen to sign in to the Clever portal. Please note that for some IDPs this may take some custom configuration.
 

• Log in with your Clever credentials 
 • Admins can also log in using the ‘Log in as a district admin’ link at the bottom of the page. This will allow you to use the same username and password you use to access the Clever Dashboard to access your applications. If you ever need to update your email or password, you can do so in the top right-hand corner of your account and click Profile . From here you will select Edit under Account Information to update your credentials.
 

 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Custom Formulas
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Field names 3. Operations 

 Overview

 Districts can use our tool for manipulating SIS synced data to match the usernames/passwords that have been created outside of Clever for SSO (Saved Passwords) apps. This article will help if you have set up the SSO (Saved Passwords) Formula Type to be Custom Formula. By combining the field names and operations below, you should be able to create a credential format that will work for your district. 

 

 
 Field names

 Clever Tip: Copy these field names from this article and directly paste them into the formula box in the Saved Passwords settings on the district dashboard. 

 {{credentials.district_username}} = username from SIS feed 

 {{credentials.district_password}} = password from SIS feed 

 {{name.first}} = first name from SIS feed 

 {{name.last}} = last name from SIS feed 

 {{email}} = email from SIS feed 

 {{student_number}} = student number from SIS feed 

 {{_id}} = Clever ID 

 {{sis_id}} = SIS ID 

 {{graduation_year}} = graduation year in whatever format the district syncs it 

 {{dob}} = birthday from SIS feed Extension Fields Extension fields can also be used for custom formulas. The name of the extension should match the name of the field uploaded to Clever. {{ext.extension_field_name}} For example, to use the field listed in the image below, the formula field name would be: {{ext.other_id}} 

 

 

 
 Operations

The following operations are supported: 
 
 Operation

 
 Description

 
 Example

 
 Plain Text If a district has the same username or password for all of their users, just type that in the box. 
 Example: Password for all users is apple. Type in: apple

 

 

 
 Fields Insert the whole contents of a field in Clever 
 Example: Password for all users is their student_number. Type in: {{student_number}}

 

 
 substring If you want just a portion of a field, but not the whole thing 
 Example: Username contains the last two digits of a students’ birth year. Type in: {{substr dob 2 2}}

 The “2 2” means start in after the second character, and grab the next 2 digits (i.e. in 2018 start after "0" and grab "18"). 

 Another example: Username contains first or last initial:

 {{substr name.first 0 1}} or {{substr name.last 0 1}} 

 

 
 if Assign a different template based on whether a condition is met (Similar to the Excel / Google Sheets "if" formula) 
 Example: One school with Clever ID “596e5bd20a932cd57c000032” should have password “Cats”, all others should use password “Dogs”. Type in: {{if equals school "596e5bd20a932cd57c000032" "Cats" "Dogs"}} 

 
 formatDate Reformat something that’s coming into Clever as a date Example: Student Birthday is coming in in format: “2003-02-11T00:00:00Z”, we want it to be “MMDDYYYY”. Type in: {{formatDate dob "MMDDYYYY"}} More formats available here . 
 toUpper Makes things upper-case Example: Student password is their first name in all caps. Type in: {{toUpper name.first}} 
 toLower Makes things lower-case Example: Student password is their last name, all lower-case. Type in: {{toLower name.last}} 
 textBefore: Grab everything before a specific value Example: Student username is their email without the domain. Type in: {{textBefore email "@"}} 
 replace 
 Replace all instances of one string with another string.

 
 Example: Remove hyphens from last name Type in: Type in: {{replace name.last "-" ""}} "" = no character

 " " = space

 
 

 Operations may be used together. For example: 

 If you wanted the first letter of first name lower case, type in: {{toLower substr name.first 0 1}}

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: School-Specific Login URLs for ThinkCentral
text:
The ThinkCentral Saved Passwords application requires different login URLs for each school you'd like to use it with. Here's a quick guide (using Chrome) to finding the URLs for your schools:

 1. Go to the ThinkCentral login page at https://www-k6.thinkcentral.com/ePC/start.do 

 2. Enter your State and District from the dropdown menus:

 

 3. Once your State and District are selected, right click on the 'School' dropdown menu and click 'Inspect' (or 'Inspect Element' on Firefox): 

 

 4. A box will open at the bottom of the page. Click the arrow on the left of highlighted bar. A list of your district's schools will appear: 

 

 5. Take note of the 8-digit values for each school. The login URL for each school should be https://www-k6.thinkcentral.com/ePC/start.do?orgID= plus the 8 digit school code. 

 • For example, if a school's code is 01234567, then the login URL for that school is https://www-k6.thinkcentral.com/ePC/start.do?orgID=01234567 .
• You can use the school URLs from this process in your credential uploads for ThinkCentral.

title: SSO (Saved Passwords): Converting external link apps to Saved Passwords apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Instructions 

 Overview

 So you're ready to start using Saved Passwords applications in your Clever Portal! You've installed the Clever browser extension for all of your students, added the apps you want in your Clever Dashboard , and tested them out to make sure everything's working like a charm. 

 But, if you're like many districts, you've already created custom links for some of the applications in your portal. How do you make a seamless transition from those links to the new, shiny Saved Passwords applications?

 Instructions

 • See who the link is shared with by going to your portal and clicking edit on the relevant link: 
• Add the app you'd like to change from a link to an app at Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications . Click Request App. Set up the sharing rules, and set the credentials for the app if an SSO (Saved Passwords) app . 
• Delete the link you replaced as an app in your Clever Portal by going to Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district Portal, clicking the three vertical dots next to the link, select Edit link, then select Remove link from the modal. 
 
 Just like that, you've replaced your custom link with a Saved Passwords application! If you'd like to make your users' experience even more seamless, you can bulk upload their credentials so they don't even have to type them in the first time they use the application.

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Testing and Piloting
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Testing functionality for different users 3. Testing successful logins 4. Testing unsuccessful logins 5. If anything goes wrong... 6. If everything goes right... 

 Overview

 Before you roll out a new Saved Passwords application to all users in your district, we recommend testing a few logins. In most cases, users will need to enter their login credentials the very first time they access the application but will be automatically logged in via single sign-on (SSO) each time thereafter. 

 For devices other than iOS, please make sure that you are using a Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, or Internet Explorer (2013 or later) browser , and have the Clever browser extension installed . For iOS devices, you will need to use the Clever app as it functions as the Clever browser extension for Saved Passwords apps. 

 Visit these articles for details about the Clever browser extension , Saved Passwords apps on iOS devices , or managing user credentials ! 

 Testing functionality for different users

 To ensure students and teachers are being logged into the app successfully, you'll need credentials from both user types for Clever Portal and the Saved Passwords application you are testing. 

 Before testing, it is important to make sure the users are shared with the application and have the app icon in their Clever Portals. To do this, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page , and click on the application name to open the app overview page. Confirm the users you are testing are shared with the application via the Select Users tab. 

 Testing successful logins

 From your Clever Dashboard , use the Access Portal as feature to log in as a student or teacher. Click on the app icon and enter the correct credentials for the application. Once you click Save Login you should be brought to the application website and successfully logged in. 

 Clever Tip: If the application has different login URLs for each school, make sure to test a few different student and teacher accounts to make sure they all work as expected. If you are unsure how to set up school-specific login URLs, please contact Clever Support! 

 Testing unsuccessful logins

 You'll also want to make sure you know what to do when things go wrong! To test what happens when a user is logged in unsuccessfully, use the Access Portal as feature to log in as a student or teacher. Click on the app icon and enter the wrong credentials. You should see a page that looks like this:

 

 Enter the correct credentials, and select Update & Log in to reset the user's credentials. 

 If you have any issues updating the credentials, or if you need additional instructions on troubleshooting logins, please visit this article! 

 If anything goes wrong...

 If any of the tests above don't go as expected, let us know ! We're always looking for ways to improve our Saved Passwords application integrations, so your feedback is very much appreciated. As always, any screenshots you can provide will make the troubleshooting process that much easier.

 If everything goes right...

 If the tests above work as expected, we recommend piloting the Saved Passwords application with a subset of students and teachers before rolling it out to the entire district. Here are a few quick steps for creating a successful Saved Passwords pilot: 

 • First, you'll want to complete the steps above to ensure users are being logged into the Saved Passwords app successfully. If you run into any issues you can submit a ticket to our support team.
• Choose a few classrooms to pilot the Saved Passwords application. We recommend choosing classes with students who are old enough to log into the application easily outside of Clever in case of any hiccups.
• Provide them with login instructions, and allow them to test from their Clever Portals to ensure they are logged into the app successfully.
 
 You can find more information about SSO (Saved Passwords) applications here .

title: Clever Roles: Clever Admin
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever Account Owner 3. Transferring Ownership to a new Account Owner 4. Adding Clever Admins 5. Logging in as a Clever Admin 6. Removing or Updating Clever Admins 7. Switching between user accounts if you have multiple accounts in Clever 

 Overview

 Districts or schools can provide multiple team members with Clever Admin access to assist with account management. Users who are assigned the Clever Admin role, will have account-wide access with varying permissions. This article will explain how to manage team members from Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page.

 Please note: By default, only Clever Admin owners can impersonate other Clever Admins and STLs via the Access Portal as feature. Impersonation permissions for other Clever Admins can be can be granted via custom roles. Learn more about impersonation permissions here . 

 

 
 Clever Account Owner

 Each Clever Portal has a single account owner. The owner is indicated by the word (Owner) following their name on the Admin team page . The account owner’s name and email address are shared with applications connected to the district’s or school's Clever account and, therefore, they are the primary point of contact for all Clever-related issues.

 The owner has unique privileges that are unavailable to Clever Admins which include the ability to:

 • Transfer account ownership to another Clever Admin 
• Delete the Clever account
 
 With the exception of the above privileges, all Clever Admins have equal permissions to view or modify the district Portal. This includes adding or removing applications, updating sharing permissions, managing Badges, and much more! 

 
 Transferring ownership to a new Account Owner

 Each Clever account may have only one owner at a time, and only the current owner can transfer the role to another team member. If you do not see the option to Change Account Owner , then you are not the account owner. The current Account Owner can transfer ownership to another Clever Admin by clicking the Add team member button and selecting Change Account Owner in the dropdown from the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page. This option will only be available if there are other Clever Admins on the Clever account. Once ownership is transferred, the options to add/remove Clever Admins or transfer ownership will only be available to the new Account owner. If you need to transfer the ownership to an individual who isn't currently a Clever Admin, you'll need to first create an Admin account for them. If the Clever Account Owner listed on the account is no longer with the district, please contact​ Clever Support for help. Here are the details about what we will need to make the update on your behalf:

 • We will need written approval from a member of the district’s leadership team (e.g. Superintendent, Director of Technology, Curriculum Director, etc.) who is verifiable on the district's website.
• Please feel free to CC this administrator on the Clever support ticket so they can respond directly to the communication thread. 
• Once Clever has written confirmation, we can transfer ownership of the district's Clever account. 
 
 
 Adding Clever Admins

 Any users who are assigned the Clever Admin role are also able to add other Clever Admins to Clever by following these steps:

 • Log in to the Clever Dashboard 
• Select User management > Admin team from the left navigation menu
• Select Actions in the top right of the page
• Select Add team member from the options available
• Add the new Clever Admin 's information in the pop-up box
• Select Add 
 
 

 

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update Clever Admins via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 

 Once a team member has been added to the account, they will receive an email with a link to create an account. If they do not receive this message, please have them check their spam folder for a message from no-reply@clever.com . 

 

 
 Logging in as a Clever Admin

 All Clever Admins should use the District admin log in option from the Clever login page to access their Clever Admin account.

 Alternatively, Clever Admins can navigate to Clever.com and choose the District admin option using the blue arrow dropdown at the top right of the page. 

 

 Please note: Clever District Admin can also use a Single Sign-on (SSO) Identity Provider (IDP) to sign in (such as Google or Active Directory) that's powered by user email address. You'll just need to add the IDP to your district first. If you prefer that all District Admin be required to use SSO to sign in to Clever instead of a username and password, please contact Clever Support by clicking the message icon in the bottom right corner. You must be a Clever Admin (owner) to be able to make this request. 

 

 

 
 Removing or updating Clever Admins

 Removing Clever Admins

 

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update Clever Admins via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 Clever Admins also have permission to remove other admins from the Team page. To remove another Clever Admin , click the trash icon to the far right of the user's name. 

 

 

 

 Updating Clever Admins

 

 Please note: Accounts utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync can sync, remove, and update Clever Admins via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports. 

 Once a Clever Admin account has been created, other Clever Admins listed on the district account have the ability or update another Admin's Title or Clever Role. To make these updates for another Clever Admin , click the pencil icon to the far right of the user's name. 

 

 

 To update the email associated with the Clever Admin account, the admin user must log into their account > click Profile > Overview > Edit > update the email address > Update. 

 Edit > Update" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000UB4T&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl000004os6A"> 

 

 
 

 

 
 Switching between user accounts if you have multiple accounts in Clever 

 Many administrators add themselves as teachers or staff users in Clever to better understand these end users’ experiences. If you have multiple accounts in Clever, and the accounts are connected, you can easily toggle between any accounts by clicking your name and selecting the account you want to access. You can easily switch between accounts if they are connected by the same email address (case sensitive) or sis_id values. 

 

 An example of the dropdown a user with multiple accounts will see when clicking their name.

title: Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Determining the best role for a user 3. Permissions by role 

 

 Overview

 District admins can provide various staff and admin users with access to Clever at varying permission levels. For example, some users may be able to view and modify district-wide settings in Clever, while others may only be able to make modifications specific to their school.

 In Clever, a "role" can only be assigned to a district admin or staff user. Teachers always have the same permissions, primarily access to their associated sections (i.e. courses/classes), students, and applications. 

 Clever Tip: This article runs through our default roles. You can also create custom roles with granular permissions, as outlined in this article . 

 

 
 Determining the best role for a user 

 Clever roles should be based on an individual's position in the district and the level of permissions the district wants them to have in Clever. Users assigned district admin (i.e., Clever Admin, District Curriculum Lead, or District Help Desk) roles will have district-wide access with varying permissions, while users in staff roles will have more limited permissions. You can also offer highly granular permissions by creating a custom admin role . For detailed information about each role, please click on the links below. 

 District admin roles: 

 • Clever Admin 
• District Curriculum Lead 
• District Help Desk 
 
 Non-instructional staff roles : 

 • School Tech Lead 
• Portal Access 
 
 Teacher vs. Non-instructional Staff A common question is whether a user should be added to Clever as a Teacher, School Tech Lead, or Staff (Portal Access Only).

 If a user is responsible for teaching or assisting a student with educational content (i.e. substitute, teacher aide, instructional coach, etc.), we recommend adding the user as a Teacher . Directions for adding custom teachers and associating them with sections can be found here . If a user is responsible for helping users access and use technology but is not directly responsible for the instruction of educational content, we recommend adding the user as a School Tech Lead . This is usually an individual with a title such as "technology coach." If a user does not support students in the classroom directly, we recommend adding the user as Staff (Portal Access Only) . This role is usually best for secretaries, custodial staff, counselors, etc. 

 

 
 Permissions by role

 Please see the graph below for a breakdown of permissions by role. 

 
 

 
 Clever Admin

 
 District Curriculum Lead

 
 District Help Desk

 
 School Tech Lead

 
 Staff (Portal Access Only)

 
 Application sharing permissions 
 Manage application sharing permissions Can see the general permissions set up for an app but not any specific information about which school(s) an app is shared with. Can see the permissions set up for an app but can't modify permissions. Must be enabled on a per-app basis by district admin 
 Add or delete applications 
 Clever Portal permissions 
 SSO to applications via Clever SSO 
 Customize Clever Portal For connected schools. STLs cannot add resources to or edit categories created by Clever Admins. 
 Manage SSO settings 
 Clever Badge permissions 
 Enable Clever Badges for district 
 Manage and print Clever Badges Can print for individuals Can print for individuals, sections, and school site 
 Clever Dashboard permissions 
 Access Clever Dashboard 
 View login metrics and connected applications 
 Change SIS sync settings 
 Access Data Browser 
 Access Clever Analytics Enable & View Edtech, Portal, and Reports (plus SFTP transfer of Reports) Edtech, Portal, and Reports Edtech, Portal, and Reports Edtech and Portal for associated schools 
 Access to record profiles Can access roster information via an application's overview page 
 Access to support tools Troubleshoot Login tool 
 Reset Clever Password for individual users 
 Create custom data Can create individual custom sections 
 Access Portal As Students, teachers, and Portal-only staff by default. Can impersonate other admins and STLs with custom role permissions . Students, teachers, and Portal-only staff Can only impersonate teachers. Must be enabled by District Admin in Role Settings 
 App Store access to get quotes and purchase Clever+ products 
 Clever IDM permissions 
 View student IDM passwords 
 View teacher IDM passwords 
 View staff IDM passwords 
 Reset student IDM passwords 
 

 When delegated resets are enabled for that destination and user type

 
 

 When delegated resets are enabled for that destination and user type

 
 Reset teacher IDM passwords 
 

 When delegated resets are enabled for that destination and user type

 
 

 When delegated resets are enabled for that destination and user type

 
 Reset staff IDM passwords 
 

 When Staff self service resets are enabled for that destination

 
 Classroom MFA permissions 
 Create and adjust MFA policies 
 Add users to MFA policies 
 Reset MFA configuration for other users 
 

 Can reset students, teachers, and non-instructional staff

 
 

 Can reset students, teachers, and non-instructional staff

 
 

 Can reset students and teachers

 
 Generate MFA bypass code for other users 
 

 Can generate for teachers

 
 

 

 Can generate for teachers

 

 
 

 

 Can generate for teachers

 

 
 
 

 Clever Tip: Non-instructional staff can also be uploaded to Clever without a specific role assigned. For more information, visit: Adding multiple staff to Clever. 

 Please contact our support team if you have additional questions about roles in Clever. We're happy to help!

title: Sharing Permission: View sharing permission history
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Where can I see past sharing permissions? 3. How do I revert sharing to a previous configuration? 

 

 Overview

 Clever Admins are now able to view historical changes made to an application’s data sharing permissions. This is available for every application a district is connected to on Clever. 

 
 Where can I see past sharing permissions?

 Historical sharing configurations can be seen in the Past Rules tab when viewing an application’s profile page in the dashboard: 

 

 The sharing history is broken up by date and exact timestamp, and the page is limited to showing the 20 most recent historical rules. 

 
 How do I revert my present data sharing permissions to a previous configuration?

 • Navigate to the specific application’s profile page 
• Click on the Past Rules tab at the top 
• Identify the exact date and timestamp of the previous sharing configuration you’d like to bring back for the application 
• Click the blue Revert to these rules button to replace the current sharing configuration with this previous one 
• For final confirmation, hit the Revert sharing button to push this change into production 
 
 

 As with normal sharing changes, this reversion should go into effect instantly and thereafter alter what data an app can sync for your district. It typically takes up to 24 hours for data changes to be reflected in an application once sharing permissions are altered. 

 Please note: For more information on setting sharing permissions, please see: How do I set my sharing permissions for applications?

title: Sharing Permissions: App-recommended sharing permissions
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Accept or decline a sharing recommendation 

 Overview:

 Recommended sharing settings allow an application to communicate sharing best practices directly to your district or school in the dashboard. Even if you have already configured your sharing permissions with an application, they can recommend an update to these settings. You then have the option to accept or decline the application’s recommended sharing settings directly in your Clever Dashboard. 

 
 Accept or decline a sharing recommendation:

 If one of your connected applications has recommended an update to your sharing rules, you’ll receive an email notification.

 

 Additionally, you will see the status of Sharing requested under Applications in your dashboard, as well as on the Overview page of the application. 

 

 

 To review the application’s recommended update to your sharing rules, select Click here on the profile page of the application or View sharing request on the Applications page. Selecting either of these will open up a popup window displaying the recommended update. 

 

 To accept the update to your sharing rules, select Share. 

 IMPORTANT If you select Share, the application’s recommended sharing settings will replace any current sharing rules that your district has configured for this application. 

 If you would not like to update your sharing rules at this time, select Decline and your sharing settings for the application will remain the same.

 If you are not sure about the application’s reasoning behind the recommended sharing rules, please reach out to the application’s team directly as they’ll be best able to work through with you what should be shared and make any necessary adjustments to their recommendation.

title: Sharing Permissions: Sharing staff with applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Share by district 3. Share by school 4. Share by rules 5. Sharing a user that has both a teacher and a staff account 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Staff (non-teaching faculty) can be shared with applications in the same way as teachers and students! Before sharing staff with an application, we recommend reviewing the Single Sign-On (SSO)-supported user types on the application’s Overview page.

 

 

 
 Share by district

 If your sharing type is set to share by district, staff can be shared or unshared by checking the box that says Also share all staff with [application name] . 

 
 Share by school

 If you've selected to share by school, s taff members for selected schools are automatically shared. It is not possible to unshare staff associated with shared schools. 

 
 Share by rules

 Staff must be shared explicitly by creating an additional sharing rule. 

 

 
 Follow the steps below if you’re using the Guided sharing view to share your staff members and see a tutorial video here : 
 • Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications and select the application 
• Under Shared Data , select Edit Sharing 
• Select Add Another Rule 
• Select Staff members need access in the drop-down menu 
• Select the desired staff utilizing the filters provided. You can select staff based on their email, schools administered, staff ID, or title. If you choose to share by email, make sure the email addresses match exactly as they appear in Clever (including uppercase and lowercase letters), as they are case-sensitive. 
• Select which school-association information you would like to share. Please note that this does not share additional students, teachers, or sections. This selection only determines which school-associations are available to the application for the shared staff members. If you would like to sync the staff to the application without a school association, select no associated schools. If you would like to sync the staff to the application with specific school associations, select these associated schools and choose the desired schools. If you would like to sync the staff to the application with all their synced school associations included, select all associated schools. 
• Select Done Editing 
• Select Save Sharing Rules 
 
 
 

 
 Follow the steps below if you’re using the Standard sharing view to share your staff members and see a tutorial video here : 
 • Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications and select the application
• Under Shared Data , select Edit Sharing 
• Select Edit Sharing Rules 
• Select Add another rule 
• Select Custom Share Staff 
• Select the desired staff utilizing the filters provided. You can select staff based on their email, schools administered, staff ID, or title. If you choose to share by email, make sure the email addresses match exactly as they appear in Clever (including uppercase and lowercase letters), as they are case-sensitive. 
• Select which school-association information you would like to share. Please note that this does not share additional students, teachers, or sections. This selection only determines which school-associations are available to the application for the shared staff members. If you would like to sync the staff to the application without a school association, select no associated schools. If you would like to sync the staff to the application with specific school associations, select these associated schools and choose the desired schools. If you would like to sync the staff to the application with all their synced school associations included, select all associated schools. 
• Select Done editing 
• Select Save Sharing Rules 
 
 
 

 
 Sharing a user that has both a teacher and a staff account

 Users can have both a teacher and a staff account in Clever. This can get tricky if you want to specify which, or if both, accounts are shared with a particular application. If you need to ensure a specific staff user account is shared, we recommend adding a staff sharing rule that specifies the user’s Staff ID or email. 

 

 

 

 To confirm if a specific account type (teacher or staff) is shared with an application, use the Troubleshoot sharing tool and pay specific attention to which user account you select. 

 

 

 Be sure to select the account type (teacher or staff) that you want to ensure is shared with a particular application. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 How can I confirm if a staff user is shared with an application? 

 • 
 Use the Troubleshoot sharing tool and input the user’s name. If there is a chance the user has both a teacher and a staff account, be sure that you select the staff account.

 
 What types of sharing do I need to add staff users to? 

 • 
 Whether you share by district, share by school, or set sharing rules, you’ll need to check a box, or create a specific rule to share staff users.

 
 Can I share both a teacher and staff account for a single user with an application? 

 • 
 Yes! Clever allows you to share both teacher and staff accounts with applications, however, how the application handles that data may vary. For example, some applications can only sync one account type.

 
 What permissions will my staff user have in their applications? 

 • 
 This varies based on application. The Clever team can not advise what specific permissions or roles a staff user will have within a learning application. Please reach out to the application team for more information.

title: For Staff: Troubleshooting - Data (e.g. rosters, classes, and school association)
text:
Clever Tip: If rosters, sections or any data tied to your school's Clever account needs to be updated, please contact your registrar, clerk or district administrator for support as you are unable to make these changes within Clever. 

 Your district's data is synced to Clever from your school's Student Information System (SIS). This data sync is managed by your district's Clever account owner or district administrator. There may be times when you notice that your rosters are not up to date, that you have classes missing from your Clever Portal or that you're associated with the wrong school site.

 If you are noticing incorrect information in Clever, please confirm that the information is correct within your district's student information system. You can also contact any person at your school who manages the SIS or enrollments (a clerk, registrar, etc.) to verify the information is correct within the SIS.

 If you have already confirmed information is correct in the SIS and are still seeing incorrect data in Clever, please reach out to your district's tech support contact to troubleshoot the issue further. If they need additional help, your district administrator can open a request with Clever Support! 

 Here are examples of incorrect data you might see in Clever that your district administrator can help with: 

 • Missing students on your roster
• Incorrect students on your roster
• Incorrect, duplicate or missing classes in Clever
• Adding a co-teacher to your class
• Incorrect information for yourself or a student (a misspelled name, email, grade level, etc.)
• Updating a Clever username for yourself or a student 
• Duplicate accounts
 

 Data is incorrect in an application

 Application partners manage syncing data from Clever and implementing it in their system. If you notice that information is incorrect in application but correct in Clever. Please contact the application's support team directly. 

 We recommend providing the following information: 

 

 • District name + state
• School name
• Teacher email address
• Description of problem
 

 Example: 

 

 • Clark County School District, NV 
• Boulder City High School 
• sample@nv.ccsd.net 
• Missing class roster - Algebra I, 6th period 
 

 Please note, applications sync with data on a daily basis. If the missing or incorrect data was recently changed in your student information system, please allow at least 24 hrs for the data to be updated in applications.

 

 If you are a School Tech Lead , for instructions on how to locate data in Clever, see For School Tech Leads: Data missing or incorrect

title: For Staff: Troubleshooting - Apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. App is missing 3. Clicked on app, received an error 4. Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password 5. Data incorrect in apps 6. Problems within apps 

 

 Overview 

 Occasionally, you or your students will have issues with your applications (i.e. apps) in Clever. Here are some steps you can take if you or your students are having trouble logging in to applications from the Clever Portal. 

 
 App is missing 

 If you or your student is missing a district-provided application from your Clever Portal, please reach out to your district's support contact . Your district manages which teachers and students can access each learning application through Clever, so they will need to add the application to your student's Portal. 

 ​​ 

 
 

 Clicked on app, received an error 

 If you or your students click on an application in the Clever Portal and receive an error on the application's page, please reach out to the application's support team for help .

 Clever Tip: If you are seeing an error at a web URL that is not a Clever address, it's likely that the application's team is best equipped to help fix the issue. Please reach out to the application's support team to investigate further! 

 Here are examples of common errors you might see for specific applications:

 

 

 
 

 

 Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password 

 If a Clever page asks you or your student to enter their username and password for an application, enter the correct login information for that application, and click ' Save Login '. If the device has the Clever browser extension downloaded, Clever will save this information and automatically log the student in next time they click the icon from the Portal!

 

 If your student clicks on an application and sees the page below, this means we were not able to log your student in with the username and password they provided. Please have them enter their updated username and password for the application, and click ' Update & Log in '.

 

 If you are still having trouble, please confirm that the username and password entered is correct to log into their account directly on the application's page (i.e. at schoology.com ). If you can successfully access the app directly through their website, but not through Clever, please reach out to Clever Support for help! 

 Did you know? Applications that prompt users to manually enter credentials as shown above are called SSO (Saved Passwords) applications. Learn more about these applications in this Help Center article: For Teachers: What are Saved Passwords applications? 

 

 
 

 Data incorrect in apps 

 If the data in an application is incorrect (e.g. rosters, students, and school associations), please see this article . 

 
 

 Problems within applications 

 After you click on an application from the Clever Portal, your experience within their product is controlled from the application’s end. If you are running into an issue within the application, please contact the application’s support team for help. 

 Here are common examples of when an application’s team will be best equipped to provide support:

 • You are receiving an error message on the application’s website
• You are seeing incorrect data within the application, but correct data within the Clever Portal
• You are having trouble accessing grades, homework, textbooks, resources, or running reports within the application
• Your sections have access to the incorrect content within the application
• You have questions about features within the application
• The application does not save a student’s progress
• The application is frozen or logs you or your students out
• You need to merge or match old account

title: For Staff: Troubleshooting - Logging in
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Problem logging into Clever • Log in with Clever • Log in with Google • Log in with Active Directory • Log in with Canvas • Log in with LDAP 3. Resetting staff passwords • Setting up account recovery • Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page • Resetting your password in Clever Portal 4. Problem logging into an app 6. Logging out 6. How do I get help? 

 

 Overview 

 This article outlines the various login issues or error messages that you may encounter when attempting to log in to Clever. Please note, not all error messages are included in this article. Please double-check that you are typing in the correct login credentials and that you have an account in Clever. If your login issues persist after reviewing this article, p lease contact your district's tech support contact directly for assistance. You can find their contact information by selecting the Get help logging in! option from your district's login page.

 

 For instructions on how to help your students log in to Clever, please visit For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins 

 

 

 
 

 Problems logging in to Clever 

 Log in with Clever 

 If you see the error Invalid username or password, this means that the username and/or password you entered are not correct for your Clever account. 

 

 Select Get help logging in! to reset your password using the email address linked to your school's Clever account.

 Please note: Not all districts allow staff to reset their own passwords. If you don't see the option to reset your password, or if this doesn't resolve the issue, please contact your school's tech support contact directly for help .

 

 Log in with Google 

 Enter is your school-provided email address (not an @ gmail.com address). The password you enter will be the password associated with your school email address.

 Below are possible errors you may encounter when logging in and steps to resolve:

 

 
 Error

 
 Details and next steps

 
 
 Couldn't find your Google account

 

 This is a Google error. Please confirm you're using your school-provided email, not your personal email. If you still receive the error, please reach out to your school support contact to determine your correct email. 
 
 Wrong password. Try again or click Forgot password to reset it. 

 

 This is a Google error. You’ve entered an incorrect password for their school-provided Google email address. Please enter the correct password for their account or reset it if you’re able. If you can’t reset your password, please reach out to your school support contact for help. 
 We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email xxxx@gmail.com . Try again with your school issued email address. This is a Clever-related error. This error means that the email address you entered isn’t the same one linked to your Clever account. Please make sure you are using your school-provided email address. If you believe you’re using the correct email address, please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. The Clever team cannot provide login credentials. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the email xxxx@example.com This is a Clever-related error. This error means that the email address you’re attempting to log in with is duplicated in Clever. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. The Clever team cannot edit email addresses or provide login credentials. 
 We couldn’t log you in. This is a Clever-related error. You may receive this error with additional details or by itself. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for further assistance in troubleshooting. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 

 For any other login error that states We couldn’t log you in please contact your school’s IT team directly for assistance. Provide them all of the descriptive information in the error, the Reference ID, and any suggested Admin troubleshooting steps. The Clever team cannot provide your login credentials or edit your email address in Clever.

 For any other login error that states We couldn’t log you in please contact your school’s IT team directly for assistance. Provide them all of the descriptive information in the error, the Reference ID, and any suggested Admin troubleshooting steps. The Clever team cannot provide your login credentials or edit your email address in Clever.

 Log in with Active Directory 

 When selecting this option, you should be redirected to your district's login page.

 Below are possible login errors you may encounter and steps for troubleshooting:

 

 
 Error

 
 Details and next steps

 
 
 We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email address or username.

 

 This error means the email address or username you entered isn’t in the district or school’s Clever account. Please make sure you are using your school-provided email or username. If you’re using the correct email or username, please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. The Clever team cannot provide login credentials. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the student’s email or username.

 

 This error means that the email address or username you’re attempting to log in with is duplicated in Clever. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. The Clever team cannot edit email addresses or provide login credentials. 
 We couldn’t log you in. You may receive this error with additional details or by itself. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for further assistance in troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 If you have issues logging in to Active Directory, please contact your school’s tech support directly as Clever doesn’t have access to login credentials for users.

 Log in with Canvas 

 If you see the error message We couldn't log you in, we can't find a Clever account for the email or username , this means that Clever is unable to locate an account based on the credentials you provided. 

 For any other login error that states We couldn’t log you in please contact your school’s IT team directly for assistance. Provide them all of the descriptive information in the error, the Reference ID, and any suggested Admin troubleshooting steps. The Clever team cannot provide your login credentials or edit your email address in Clever.

 

 Log in with LDAP 

 If you encounter an LDAP login error after entering your login credentials, please contact your school support contact as Clever does not have access to login credentials. 

 
 

 
 Resetting staff passwords

 Not all school districts allow staff to reset their passwords. If you don’t see the option to reset your password, or if these steps don't resolve the issue, please get in touch with your school district's tech support contact directly for help.

 Setting up account recovery 

 If your school has enabled it, you can use a backup method (such as email, security questions, or phone number) to regain access to your Clever, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Google Workspace accounts right from your Clever login screen. This is handy if you cannot log in with your password successfully.

 To set up your backup method (again, if your school had enabled this functionality), you need to be logged into your Portal.

 • 
 Click on your blue icon in the upper right corner to bring up your settings. 

• 
 Click on Set up recovery methods. The recovery methods available to you depend on what your school district has enabled.

• 
 Choose a recovery method and input the

title: For Staff: How do I use Zoom through the Clever Portal?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I create a Zoom account? 3. How do I log in to Zoom? 4. How do I set up a virtual meeting? 5. How can I get help with using Zoom? 

 

 Overview 

 Clever has partnered with Zoom to create a seamless way to provide account creation, as well as Single Sign-On (SSO) access for teachers, staff, and district admins.

 

 How do I create a Zoom account? 

 Adding Zoom and creating accounts are managed at the district level. District administrators can find directions for adding the Zoom integration here . If you do not see the Zoom app in your Portal, please contact your district help desk. 

 

 How do I log in to Zoom? 

 Step 1: Log in to the Clever Portal 

 Step 2: Click on the Zoom icon.

 You are now logged in and will be directed to your Zoom profile! 

 

 How do I create a virtual meeting? 

 Step 1: Log in to the Clever Portal 

 Step 2: Click on the Zoom application in the Clever Portal

 Step 3: Click on "Schedule a Meeting"
