 Step 4. Enter the details for your meeting. If you would like to use the same meeting link for a recurring event (example: weekly team meeting), select "Recurring meeting"

 

 Step 5. Select "Save." On the next screen, click "Copy the invitation". 

 Step 6. Send the invitation info to the event participants. This may be in an email, a calendar event's details, or a link in the Clever Portal! 

 

 Pro Tip: Does your school use Google? You may be able to add the Zoom Scheduler extension for Google Chrome. This extension makes it easy to add a Zoom link to any calendar event in Google Calendar! 

 

 How can I get help with using Zoom? 

 Zoom has a large number of resources to support you and your students. These resources can be found at https://support.zoom.us 

 Here are some resources from their Help Center that Clever recommends to get started!

 Zoom Video Tutorials (1 Minute each!) 

 

 • How to Join a Meeting 
• Joining & Configuring Audio & Video 
• Sharing Your Screen 
• Meeting Controls 
• Meeting and Webinar Best Practices 
• Frequently Asked Questions 
 

 Zoom Technical Support 

 If you or your students encounter technical problems with Zoom, you can submit a support request . " Support Hours" are 24 x 7 , including public holidays, where agents or technical engineers are available for email or live support.

title: For Staff: Searching & favoriting apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Favoriting resources 3. Searching for resources 

 Overview 

 This article provides an overview of how teachers and staff can easily locate applications and categorize their favorite apps in Clever! 

 Locating and favoriting applications are features that are also available to your students !

 

 Favoriting applications

 Here's how to favorite an application: 

 

 • Log into your Clever Portal from your district's Clever login page, or from Clever.com . 
• Locate your favorite app icon, and click the heart in the upper right-hand corner of the icon. 
• This will add the app to the 'Favorites' section at the top of your Portal. 
• To remove an app from your ‘Favorites’, simply click the heart again in the top right corner! 
 

 Please note: Favoriting resources is only reflected in individual portals. Resources that teachers favorite will not be favorited for their students. 

 

 Finding apps

 Users can search for district-wide applications or district-wide app links from their Clever Portals. Simply navigate to the search bar at the top of your Clever Portal to search for the app.

 • Depending on the app, when the result is selected you will be either:
 • Logged in to the app if it is a district-wide application
• Be redirected to the app login page to log in (if a non-integrated app)
 
 
 

 If you are unable to locate the app via the Clever search bar, this means it is either not a Clever-integrated app nor a district-wide linked app.

title: Sharing Permissions: Enabling School Site Sharing for School Tech Leads
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I enable school site sharing for School Tech Leads? 

 Overview 

 The School Tech Lead user type in Clever is able to navigate from the Portal to the Clever Dashboard to help manage schools they are associated with. School site sharing means that a School Tech Lead can manage data sharing permissions on a per-school basis. For more information on adding School Teach Leads, please see this article . 

 

 
 How do I enable school site sharing for School Tech Leads? 

 To allow sharing rules to be managed at the school level: 

 1. Visit the application overview page and choose the Edit sharing button (for Saved-passwords apps click the Modify users button) and select Change next to the current sharing rule type.

 

 

 2. Change sharing rules to School Site Sharing. 

 

 3. Click Enable to allow School site sharing. 

 

 

 Please note: Enabling school site sharing will remove any existing sharing rules that you have. 

 Now your School Tech Leads can manage data sharing on a per-school basis! School Tech Leads will see the same interface that you do, except they will only see the schools that they are associated with in Clever. As a Clever Admin, you can also view and edit the same sharing rules. 

 

 

 Clever Tip: For directions on how School Tech Leads can use School site sharing, please see: For School Tech Leads: What is School site sharing? 

 

 Related articles: 

 

 For School Tech Leads: Sharing data with apps

title: Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is a sharing rule? 3. Choosing Share by Rules as a sharing type 3. How do I add a sharing rule? 4. Constructing sharing rules 5. Reverting to previous rules/Viewing who made changes 6. Multiple sharing rules and filters 7. Adding Notes 8. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 Overview

 By configuring sharing permissions for applications, Clever admins can determine what data will be shared with each district app, and which district users will be able to access the application from their individual Clever Portals.

 When sharing by rules, Clever will only share data that meets the specific criteria you've set. This is the most recommended sharing type as it provides districts/schools with the flexibility in determining specifically what data should be included, or excluded, in the sharing.

 Additionally, applications are dynamically updated as new data is synced to Clever from your district's student information system (SIS). The data shared with the application via sharing rules evolves based on the new data in Clever.

 

 For an overview of application sharing permissions, please visit: Sharing Permissions: Sharing data with applications 

 

 
 What is a sharing rule?

 A sharing rule allows districts to be specific about what data is being shared by data type (e.g. teacher, student, section, etc.). Once a sharing type is selected, district admins can continue to add filters to ensure only the data they want shared with applications is being shared.

 For example, sharing rules might include: 

 • All the math classes in a particular school
• The english classes for grades 3-6 in the district, except for in two schools
• One specific teacher’s 11th-grade homeroom class
 
 Think of a sharing rule as a sentence: 

 • "Share all records where <attribute> <is one of> <value> ”
 
 

 
 Choosing Sharing by Rules as a sharing type

 You can view and edit your sharing rules from your Applications > My Applications page by clicking on the application's name to open the Overview tab.

 • Click Edit Sharing 
 
 

 • If you're not already Sharing by Rules , you can change the sharing type to Sharing by Rules. 
 
 
 If you are using Guided Sharing, click here for instructions on how to switch to Sharing by Rules. 
 • Navigate into your sharing rules for the application.
• Click back to Step 1: Choose sharing method on the sidebar.
• Click Get started on the pop-up for Guided Sharing: How it works
• Select to share Specific sections, teachers, students, and/of staff 
 
 
 

 
 
 If you are using Standard Sharing, click here for instructions on how to switch to Sharing by Rules. 
 • Navigate into your sharing rules for the application
• Click on Change as shown below (in this example the school district was Sharing by Schools) 
• Select Share by Rules to change your sharing type 
 
 

 

 • After changing to Sharing By Rules in either Guided Sharing or Standard Sharing, you will then be prompted to choose which method you want to use (e.g. Custom Share by Student):
 
 

 • Create your rules and filters. Click here to learn more about the different types of rules and filters. Before saving your rules and filters, you can adjust them as many times as you’d like and preview data at the bottom of your screen before clicking save. This allows you to experiment and ensure the correct data is included in your sharing rules. For sharing rule recommendations, be sure to check out our tips & troubleshooting article .
• After creating each rule, select Done editing. 
• Once you have finished adding all of your rules and filters, select Save Sharing Rules. 
 
 

 
 How do I add or edit a sharing rule?

 If you already use Share by Rules, go to your Applications > My Applications page and click on the application name to open the app overview page . From the app overview page, click Edit Sharing . Use the Edit or Delete actions to update your sharing rules. 

 
 

 Constructing sharing rules

 Types of sharing rules 

 There are a few types of sharing rules available: 

 

 
 Sharing rule

 
 Purpose

 
 Custom Share by Teacher Share specific teachers by one of their unique identifiers (e.g. email address, teacher ID).
 • When sharing by teacher, you'll have the option to select whether to share their associated student and section data. Custom Share by Teacher sharing rules will not share student contacts, however.
 
 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by teacher or Standard sharing: Share by teacher. 

 
 Custom Share by Staff 
 Share your non-instructional staff. If Sharing by staff , you will have the option to select if you want to share their school associations. Clever recommends sharing "all associated schools." This will not share additional students, teachers, or sections.

 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by staff or Standard sharing: Share by staff. 

 
 Custom Share by Student Use this sharing type to filter by student attributes such as grade, gender, ELL_status, IEP, status, etc.
 • 
 When sharing by student, you'll have the option to select if you want to share their associated section data.

 
 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by student or Standard sharing: Share by student. 

 
 Custom Share by Section 
 Use this sharing type to filter by section attributes such as subject, school, course, and term.

 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by section or Standard sharing: Share by section. 

 
 Custom Share by Admin 
 Share specific admins by their assigned role in Clever. 

 See a tutorial video of Guided sharing: Share by admin or Standard sharing: Share by admins. 

 
 
 Filters 

 Rules are made up of Filters. A Filter is an individual requirement that must be met in order for a record to be shared. A rule may have multiple filters. Filters are created by creating a statement using the following:

 • Properties are attributes that districts can use to either include or exclude data with the application sharing rules. The fields available are dependent on the type of sharing rule you selected (e.g. share by teacher, share by section, share by student)
• Operators define how the property should be used. Available operators are:
 • equals any of
• does not equal any of
• contains
• does not contain
• contains any of
• starts with
• starts with any of
 

• Values are the actual values that the rule must meet in order to be shared. Values can be selected manually from the dropdown or by pasting multiple values separated by a semi-colon. Search for values is case-sensitive so it's important to ensure that you are using an exact match to the value that is synced to Clever. 
 
 Note: When using the School or Schools properties, you may enter or paste lists with any combination of school names, school sis_ids, or school Clever ids.

 For a tutorial video of using filters with Guided sharing, click here .

 For a tutorial video of using filters with Standard sharing, click here . In the below example, the filter has a requirement that a section has the subject of math in order to be shared: In this example, the filter is excluding any sections with the subject of PE and Health by using the does not equal operator. This means that every section with a subject other than PE and Health will be shared with the application. 

 
 Reverting to previous rules/Viewing who made changes

 By clicking on the Past rules tab on the application's page, you can view the sharing rules history, including the date and time the rules were changed and who changed them. Under the Past rules tab, you can click on View current rules (1) to see the current sharing rules and view the sharing rules history by date and time. By clicking on one of the small arrows next to the time (2), it will open up to show you what the sharing rules were previously. You can revert a previous rule set by clicking on Revert to these rules (3). 

 
 Multiple sharing rules and filters

 An application can h

title: Sharing Permissions: Sharing data with new applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What are application sharing permissions? 3. How do I start sharing data with an application? 4. How do I edit the sharing permissions for an application once the setup guide has been completed? 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 Overview 

 This article will walk you through sharing data with an application once the application has accepted your request to connect via Clever.

 

 

 

 
 What are application sharing permissions? 

 Once an application has been added to a district's Clever Portal, Clever Admins configure sharing permissions in order to determine what data should be shared with the app, and which users should be able to access the application from their Clever Portals. 

 Applications will not have access to any district data in Clever that has not been specifically shared with them via the application sharing permissions. 

 
 How do I start sharing data with an application? 

 Clever acts as a repository that stores the data from a district's student information system (SIS) which is then shared with each application. A district might share different data with each unique application depending on the application type, licensing contingencies, and so on. In Clever, this is done by setting up sharing permissions. Clever offers two experiences for sharing data: Guided Sharing and Standard Sharing . 

 • Guided Sharing is best for new users or those who don't set up sharing settings often.
• Standard sharing is best for experienced Clever users or those who need to use School Site Sharing. 
 
 The choice in experience is custom to each user -- a new admin at your district can choose to use Guided Sharing while an admin with more Clever experience may choose Standard Sharing. Users can choose to switch between these two experiences at any time by selecting Edit Sharing for an application and selecting Switch Sharing setup view in the top right. Your choice in sharing experience will be remembered and used anytime you edit sharing permissions for an app. 

 Here’s how to set your data sharing permissions for an application for the first time: 

 • From your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page , click on an application name to be directed to the application’s overview page. 
• Select Begin setup and follow the steps in the guide to finalize your connection with the application. 
 
 For your convenience, we are providing sharing instructions for both Standard and Guided sharing. 

 

 Whether you are using Standard Sharing or Guided Sharing, you have the option to test sharing rules and filters to configure and reconfigure multiple times and preview the data that will be shared. Your sharing rules will not change until you click Save . 

 Standard Sharing 

 
 Standard Sharing is recommended for users familiar with configuring sharing permissions or those who need to set up School Site Sharing. Click here to learn more about the steps of Standard Sharing. 
 Step 1: Set sharing

 You’ll specify which sharing type you would like to use in order to share data with the application. These settings will also determine which of your district users will have the application available to them in their individual Portals. 

 You have five options for how to share data: 

 1. Use the application’s sharing recommendation 

 2. Copy sharing rules from another app 

 • 
 • If you select this option, all records that are shared with the chosen application will also be shared with the one you're currently setting up. 
• Please note: copying the sharing only happens once - if you make changes to sharing with the original application, they will not be reflected in the app you're currently setting up (or vice versa)
 

 
 3. Share all records in the district 

 • 
 • If you select this option, all schools, students, teachers, and sections in the district will be shared. You will also have the option to include staff in the data sharing with the application. 
 

 
 4. Share all records for specific schools 

 • 
 • If you select this option all students, students, teachers, sections, and staff associated with the selected schools will be shared with the application. 
 

 
 5. Customize your sharing 

 • 
 • Select Click here to customize your sharing to navigate to the data sharing page to configure customized sharing rules. Read this article to learn more about how to share by rules. 
• Once you've selected your sharing type, select 'Save' to move on to step 2. 
 

 
 

 Step 2: Data validation

 Once you’ve shared data, we’ll check for issues that might prevent a record from being synced. Please note that you may need to wait a few minutes while we process your sharing settings. Any data error and warnings will appear on this page when processing is completed. 

 
 • If you see an error, this means that the flagged records will not be accessible in the application - users will not be able to log in and data will be missing. 
• If you see a warning, this means that the flagged records will be accessible but users may have a degraded experience within the application. 
• If you have data that is flagged, please review the impacted records carefully. We recommend resolving as many of these data quality issues as possible to ensure that all of the data is successfully synced with the application and all desired users are able to access the application. 
 • For more information on resolving these data warnings and errors, check out our guide to data warnings and errors. 
 

 
 Once you've reviewed any data quality issues, select 'Next' to move on to step 3.

 Step 3: Finalize Setup

 • Now that you have enabled data sharing with the application and resolved any data quality issues, the application connection is complete! 
• For some applications, you will need to take additional steps to finalize the app connection. Those additional steps will be outlined on this page. 
• For any application with single sign-on (SSO), users will be able to access the application on the launch date. 
 
 

 • Once you have read the instructions to finalize your app connection, select Finish .
• You’ll receive a prompt to add the application to a category. Choose a category from the dropdown if you’d like.
• If you need to go back to edit the sharing rules for an application, feel free to reference this guide for more information about the different types of sharing permissions. 
 
 
 

 Guided Sharing 

 
 Guided Sharing is recommended for users less familiar with sharing rules or for those who want a more guided experience. Click here to learn more about the steps of Guided Sharing. 
 Step 1: Choose sharing method 

 • Choose your sharing method. There are multiple data sharing options. 
 • Create new sharing settings specific to the application 
• Use the application’s suggested data sharing template (if provided) 
• Copy another app’s data sharing rules 
 

 
 Step 2: Choose who needs access 

 • Set up access for specific sections, teachers, students, and/or staff 
 
 Step 3: Preview & share user data

 • You can preview your sharing rules and, if everything looks correct, share your schools’ data with the application
 
 

 • You’ll receive a prompt to add the application to a category. Choose an existing category from the dropdown if you’d like. 
 
 
 

 
 How do I edit the sharing permissions for an application once the setup guide has been completed? 

 To edit sharing permissions, from your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page , click on the application's name to open its overview page. Under Shared Data , select Edit sharing . You can reference this guide for more information about the different types of sharing permissions. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Why is Clever unable to display the count of shared records? 

 

 • Clever may be unable to provide a preview of the count of shared records for districts with large data sets or highly complex rules. When this occurs

title: Sharing Permissions: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What data is shared with apps? 3. Guided vs Standard Sharing view 4. Sharing data with an app 5. Viewing the data being shared with an app 6. Editing app sharing permissions 7. FAQs 

 Overview 

 With sharing permissions, you can control exactly which users get access to each application in Clever. Whether your whole school organization uses an app or just a handful of users (e.g., four 3rd-grade math sections), sharing permissions let you decide who gets access and what data the application receives from Clever to roster and maintain user accounts.

 We know that not all applications are utilized by the same subset of users, so you can set up sharing permissions on an app-by-app basis. Sharing permissions are typically configured for each application based on its type, licensing requirements, and who needs access.

 

 

 
 What data is shared with apps?

 Clever provides each application with only the data necessary for its operation. To view or modify what's being shared , go to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page and then click on the application's name to open the app overview page. Here, you can click the download icons next to each data type to see the exact data the application receives.

 

 
 Guided vs Standard Sharing view

 When setting up sharing permissions, you'll first choose your setup view. There are two options: Guided sharing and Standard sharing. Pick whichever works best for you.

 • 
 Guided sharing is best for new users or those who don't configure sharing settings often. It walks you through the process with prompts and examples. Watch a preview of Guided sharing .

• 
 Standard sharing is best for experienced Clever users or those who need to use school site sharing. Watch a preview of standard sharing here. 

 
 Users can switch between these two experiences at any time by selecting Edit Sharing for an application and selecting Switch Sharing setup view in the top right. Your preference is saved and applied whenever you edit sharing permissions for an app.

 

 Please note: Whether you use Guided Sharing or Standard Sharing, you’ll always be able to fully review what data is included in your sharing settings before finalizing and saving your rules. This means that you can make adjustments to your rules freely to test including the data you need before hitting save! 

 
 Sharing data with an app

 When sharing data with your applications, it’s essential to understand the sharing permission options to ensure you are only sharing the users who need access. Review the six different options below:

 • App’s recommended sharing : Applications can recommend sharing settings to communicate sharing best practices directly to your school organization in the Clever Dashboard . If the application has recommended sharing permissions, they'll appear when you set up sharing. Use these as a starting point for your own sharing permissions.
• Share by District : All of your school organization's data in Clever will sync to the application. This includes admins, schools, teachers, students, staff, and sections.
• Share by Schools : Clever will only share data associated with the school(s) you select (e.g., students, teachers, sections, staff).
• Share by Rules : Clever will only share data that meets the specific criteria you've set. Share by Rules is the most recommended sharing type as it allows school organizations to determine precisely what data should be included or excluded. Learn more about Share by Rules here. 
• School Site Sharing : If your school org has designated School Tech Leads in Clever, you can give them the ability to configure sharing permissions for their associated schools. provide them with the ability to configure sharing permissions for their associated schools. Learn more here. 
• Copy Sharing: Copy the sharing permissions from another application that has already been configured. 
 
 For step-by-step directions on setting up sharing permissions, check out this article. We understand that sharing permissions can be tricky to think through, so check out our sharing permission tips and tricks ! And remember, you can always preview the data your rules will share before saving.

 Once you share data with an application, it'll dynamically update based on your sharing permissions. Allow up to 24 hours for sharing changes to be reflected in the learning application. If you need changes reflected sooner, contact the application directly.

 When setting up sharing rules for any application not assigned to a category in your Portal (for example, a new app you've just added or one in the default More Apps category), you'll see a prompt at the end of your sharing setup asking if you'd like to add the application to a category. If you do, choose a category from the drop-down.

 

 
 Viewing the data being shared with an app

 Once you've configured sharing permissions, you can download CSV files of the schools, students, teachers, staff, admins, and sections being shared with an application.

 Go to Applications > My Applications , then click the application's name to open the overview page. In the Application Receives box, click the blue icon to download a file of the data the application is receiving.

 Keep in mind that the Application Receives files include only a few basic details about each shared record. The application may have access to additional fields based on your field access settings. For example, the students.csv doesn't include student email addresses, but the application partner might be receiving them. For details on which data each application is syncing per record, visit: What data is available to my connected applications? 

 

 
 Editing app sharing permissions

 To edit sharing permissions, go to Applications > My Applications and click the application's name to open the overview page. Then, select Edit sharing under the Shared Data section. 

 If you have any questions about setting up your sharing permissions or what data is being shared with your applications, please reach out to Clever support. We are happy to help!

 

 
 

 FAQs

 Why is Clever unable to display the count of shared records? 

 

 • Clever may be unable to provide a preview of the count of shared records for school organizations with large data sets or highly complex rules. When this occurs, " Unable to display count of shared records " or " Sorry, your rules are too complex to display shared records" may display. 
• To view the count of shared records, please use the Application Receives section on the app overview page. 
 
 How do I add NCES IDs and/or MDR Numbers for schools in Clever? 

 • Reach out to our support team and provide a list of the numbers/IDs that need to be added and their corresponding schools. 
 
 How do I check to see which school is being shared as the users’ primary location? 

 • For one-off users, you can search for the user in the search bar of your Clever Dashboard to locate their profile and look at their primary school site. 
 
 

 For multiple users, you can click on the application in your Clever Dashboard and click the download button under Application Receives for the user type in question.

 

 Why does the data my application is receiving include a school that is not explicitly shared in my sharing rule? 

 • This is common in Share by Schools rules since they share all data associated with teachers, sections, and students. If a teacher or student is associated with multiple schools, this additional school will be implicitly shared as data associated with the student or teacher.
• This can also happen in Share by Rules if a student or teacher's primary school site is a school not explicitly shared, it will still be implicitly shared as their primary site.
 
 How do I share co-teachers with applications? 

 • First, be sure to ask the application if they support co-teachers, as some applications don’t. If they confirm tha

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Troubleshooting issues
text:
• Overview 
• Updating student credentials 
• Updating staff & teacher credentials 
• But my issue doesn't look like that! 
 
 Overview

 Every now and then you may have users who have trouble logging into Saved Passwords applications , or they need to update their credentials for the app. Here are some tips for troubleshooting these issues which will make the process as painless as possible.

 If your current district settings for the Saved Passwords application allow your users to manually enter their credentials the very first time they log into the app through Clever, Clever will save those credentials for the future providing a single sign-on (SSO) experience for users. 

 The challenge is that sometimes users will enter the incorrect credentials the first time around, and those are what gets saved to Clever. The great news is that those credentials can be updated in Clever! 

 In the vast majority of cases, if users are unable to log into a Saved Passwords application, they'll see a message like the one below:

 

 Updating student credentials

 To update a student's credentials, please follow these steps:

 • Go directly to the application's website login page outside of Clever, and confirm if the user's credentials are successful.
 • If the credentials are not successful, please contact the application provider directly to correct the error.
• If credentials are successful, attempt to enter those same credentials again in the screen shown above and then select 'Update & Log in'. Then, confirm the credentials work by accessing the application again through the Clever Portal.
 

 
 If for some reason, the 'Update & Log in' option in the screenshot above did not work with resetting the user's credentials, the student's teacher or a district admin can assist by following these steps: 

 • The teacher will log in to Clever. District admins can utilize the 'Access Portal As' feature from their Clever Dashboard to access the teacher's account in order to do the following:
 • Click on the 'Help​ a Student' option.
• Locate the student who needs their credentials updated. 
• The prompt will ask if you want to update the student's Clever login or generate a backup code. Click 'No' where it asks if this solved the problem. 
 

 
 

 • Locate the Saved Passwords application.
• When asked if the student has the app icon in their portal, say 'Yes.'
• Then update the Saved Passwords credentials. 
 
 

 

 • Finally, have the student test their login to the app to see if it works correctly (or district admins can 'Access Portal As' the student to test it for them).
 
 District admins can also set credentials for Saved Passwords apps through a bulk upload in the Clever Dashboard.

 Updating staff & teacher credentials

 If a staff member or teacher inputs incorrect credentials for an SSO (Saved Passwords) application, they can update them by following these steps:

 • Log in to the Clever Portal. 
• Select the application that has incorrect credentials. 
• Enter the correct username and password for the application, and click ' Update & Log in'.
 
 If for some reason the above steps do not work, please reach out to Clever Support for assistance. 

 Clever Tip : If you are still having trouble, please confirm that the username and password you've entered work to log into your account directly on the application's page. If you can successfully access the app directly through their website, but not through Clever, please reach out to Clever Support for help! 

 But my issue doesn't look like that!

 If the above doesn't fix the issue or you're experiencing a completely different problem, please try the following steps:

 • Check the version of the Clever browser extension you're using at chrome://extensions. If there is a more recent version of the extension, download it. For more information, visit either For Clever Admins: Installing the Clever Browser Extension or For Families: Installing the Clever Browser Extension .
• Update Chrome at chrome://help.
 
 If all else fails, our support team is more than happy to help troubleshoot with you. To help expedite the process, in your request please include :

 • The browser details provided by this website
• The version of the Clever browser extension you're using
• The version of Chrome you're using
• The student/teacher number of the user affected
• A screenshot and/or short description of the issue
 
 If you have any additional questions, please reach out to Clever Support ! We are happy to help!

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: iOS Devices
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How it works 3. Unsupported device errors 4. Troubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 SSO (Saved Passwords) applications are available to use with the Clever iOS application. Provided that the user has the Clever application installed on their device and is connected to SSO (Saved Passwords) applications in their account, once logged into Clever, they will enjoy a seamless single sign-on experience for more of their favorite education applications! Please note that the Clever iOS application only works on devices on iOS version 16.6 and up. 

 Please note: SSO (Saved Passwords) applications on iOS are not yet available for Clever Admins. 

 

 How it works 

 Clever iOS app for students 

 When using the Clever iOS application, users can log into their SSO (Saved Passwords) applications.

 Once the student selects the Clever application and logs in, they’ll see their student Portal. From here, they can click on any Saved Passwords application, and the app will load within the Clever application.

 Please note: For SSO (Saved Passwords) applications on iOS, they'll always open in the Clever app and never redirect to the native application downloaded on the device. 

 iOS device in a web browser 

 If a teacher, staff, or admin user is utilizing an iOS device to access Clever, they’ll need to access it via a web browser. Once logged into Clever in the web browser, the user can choose an SSO (Saved Passwords) application just like they would choose any other SSO application. The user will then be taken to the application's web page in the Clever app and their credentials will be entered for them, providing a single sign-on experience for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications on their mobile device.

 

 Unsupported device errors

 

 If you see an 'Unsupported device' error when attempting to open an SSO (Saved Passwords) application, it means this particular application is not currently supported on iOS devices. If you would like access to these applications on your iOS device, adding the application as an external link to your district's Clever Portal is recommended. For more information, please visit: How do I add custom external links to the Clever Portal? 

 For more about SSO (Saved Passwords) applications, please see For Teachers: What are SSO (Saved Passwords) applications? 

 

 Troubleshooting 

 We are always happy to help! For the fastest resolution, please make sure to check the following:

 
 • Is your mobile device running on iOS 16.6 or above?
• Is your Clever app updated to version 6.x or above?
 
 If you've confirmed both of these details, please move on to check:

 • Can the user log into the SSO (Saved Passwords) application successfully on a computer (non-mobile device)?
• Have users confirmed they can access the application outside of Clever?
 
 If the user can't log into the application successfully on a computer, please reach out to the application directly to troubleshoot login credentials.

 If the user can log in on a computer or access the application at Clever, please feel free to contact Clever support. Please provide all of the details of your troubleshooting steps above, as well as any videos or screenshots of the errors you're encountering. Please avoid sending personally identifiable information (PII) in any pictures or videos you provide.

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Managing Credentials
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Managing Credentials 3. Manual input from user at first login 4. Matching Credentials 5. Custom Credentials 6. Uploading Credentials 7. Reset or Remove credentials for users 

 

 Read SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Overview & Setup first to learn how to add SSO (Saved Passwords) applications to your Clever Portal!

 Overview

 SSO (Saved Passwords) applications have the ability to provide your users with a seamless SSO experience despite Clever not having a formal integration with the application you have added. In order for SSO to work properly with your SSO (Saved Passwords) applications, you will need to ensure that you have set up credentials correctly. This article will describe the options that you have as well as provide you with directions on how you can manage credentials. 

 
 Managing Credentials

 Once an application has been added to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page, the next step is to input username and password information for your users to log into the SSO (Saved Passwords) application. You can manage credentials by navigating to the 'Settings' tab of the specific application's Manage Credentials page. This article will go over multiple options for setting up your users' credentials for the specified application. 

 

 

 
 Manual input from user at first login

 By default, we will request that users enter their credentials manually the first time. After the first time, they will be automatically logged into their application with one click.

 

 
 Matching Credentials

 If usernames and passwords can be found in your SIS information, you can select which field this information is contained in by selecting the drop-down menu. The field options are as follows:

 • District username, district password, date of birth, email, SIS ID, student number & state ID
 
 

 >

 

 

 
 Custom Credentials

 To match the usernames/passwords created outside of Clever for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications, you can use the "Custom" option in the dropdown and use a formula from below. More information about custom formulas can be found here .

 Available fields: 

 • {{credentials.district_username}} = username from SIS sync 
• {{credentials.district_password}} = password from SIS sync 
• {{name.first}} = first name from SIS sync 
• {{name.last}} = last name from SIS sync 
• {{email}} = email from SIS sync 
• {{student_number}} = student number from SIS sync 
• {{_id}} = Clever ID 
• {{sis_id}} = SIS ID from SIS sync (student_id, teacher_id, staff_id) 
• {{graduation_year}} = graduation year from SIS sync 
• {{dob}} = birthday from SIS sync 
 
 

 Desired username or password Formula [fill in portions in brackets] Example formula Sample result for an example user 
 Username from the sync plus my district's email domain {{credentials.district_username}}[add domain name here] {{credentials.district_username}}@ cleverusd.edu skelly19@cleverusd.edu 
 A specific word/phrase that's the same for every student [add that word/phrase here] apple apple 
 Insert the whole contents of a field from either the SIS sync or the .csv upload {{FIELD NAME}} {{graduation_year}} 2030 
 First initial, last name {{substr name.first 0 1}}{{name.last}} {{substr name.first 0 1}}{{name.last}} skelly 
 Their email without the domain {{textBefore email "@"}} {{textBefore email "@"}} sykelly 
 Last 4 digits of a field {{substr FIELDNAME -4 4}} {{substr student_number -4 4}} 5678 
 Something else... Contact us via the form in our Help Center and we'll create a formula for you! Custom formulas are complicated and are most easily communicated via email. 
 
 

 
 

 Uploading Credentials

 When a user's credentials are uploaded via CSV, they will not be asked to log in the first time they access the application through Clever. If a user is not included in the credentials upload, the expected behavior is they will be prompted to manually enter their credentials at their first login, and then will be automatically logged in each time thereafter. 

 Method 1: Upload via Clever 

 You can securely upload credentials directly from the 'Settings' tab of the specific application's Manage Credentials page. To do so, first select Manual upload from .csv as the username and password which will enable a web upload tool to upload your CSV file. This does state "not recommended", this is due to the complexity of creating the files and managing the credentials on the district's end. 

 

 Upload the file and select Save . We have also provided a downloadable template for reference.

 

 Method 2: SFTP Upload 

 You can upload user credentials via the Credentials SFTP server. Files should be in the form of students.csv , teachers.csv , or staff.csv and uploaded to the appropriate folder for the application (e.g. /career-cruising). See tips to format your files to successfully upload credentials or download template files .

 The username and password of your Credentials SFTP connection are the same as for your SIS sync SFTP connection, and can be found under the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page in the SFTP Settings - Saved Passwords Applications box:

 

 For more information on the tools that you can use to access and upload files to your credentials SFTP server, please see this Help Center article: What are some recommended tools for SFTP syncs? 

 

 
 

 Formatting Credentials Uploads

 SSO (Saved Passwords) applications in the Clever Portal accept three files for setup: students.csv, teachers.csv, and staff.csv. Please find the specification on formatting below.

 Separate setup files are required for every SSO (Saved Passwords) application, even if the credentials used are the same for all applications.

 Generic specifications for students.csv

 

 Generic specifications for teachers.csv

 

 Generic specifications for staff.csv

 

 Uploading credentials: special cases 

 Some applications require more than just a username and password. For example, the application may require a school code or a pin number. For these applications, you will need to include an additional column in your credential upload. The required fields will be reflected on the 'Upload Credentials' page and will look something like this:

 

 You will also use an extra column when your users sign into an application from multiple URLs (i.e. URLs that are unique for each school in a district).

 Troubleshooting upload issues 

 Once you upload credentials, you'll receive a confirmation email with a summary of your upload. If there are any issues, the email will also list the number of rows that could not be processed. Here are some of the most common issues that prevent rows from being processed and how to resolve them:

 • Missing data: All of the fields listed on the 'Upload Credentials' tab of an application are required. Double-check that you're not missing any additional rows (see "Uploading Credentials: Special Cases" above) and that none of your rows have empty cells in them.
• Incorrect user IDs: Each User_ID must exactly match the SIS_ID of the user you are uploading credentials for. You can check each user's SIS_ID in your Clever Dashboard > Data browser .
 • Please note : Leading zeros (i.e. the "000" in "000123") do matter. If your users' SIS_IDs have leading zeros, make sure the zeros aren't getting stripped in your upload file.
 

• Duplicate rows: Clever will only process the first set of credentials for each User_ID. If a User_ID is included in multiple rows, all rows past the first will be listed as errors.
• Formatting errors: Sometimes simple formatting errors such as incorrect capitalization can cause errors. Make sure your headers match exactly what is listed on the 'Uploading Credentials' tab. Also, make sure you are saving the file as a CSV.
 
 

 
 

 Reset or Remove credentials for users

 Resetting credentials in bulk 

 To remove previously uploaded credentials for a SSO (Saved Passwords) application, please fol

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - App filters
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What are app filters? 2. How do app filters work? 3. How can we tell that data is filtered? 4. How do I view the data currently shared with the application? 5. How do I request a change to an app filter? 

 Please note: If a change to an app filter is needed, please contact the application's support team directly . In your request, please include your district's name, the change needed to the current filter, and the reason for requesting this adjustment. 

 What are app filters?

 App filters are a way for application partners to manage the data that districts share. Many applications only need access to limited data for their integrations. For example, some applications only require section data for specific subjects, while others only need data for schools that have licenses. To resolve this issue, applications can create filters to ensure they receive only the data they need, increasing data privacy and enhancing integration quality.

 

 How do app filters work?

 Step 1: Districts share data with the application .

 Step 2: Applications apply filters on the district data to manage exactly which data they are receiving, whereby ensuring that only relevant data is synced.

 Please note that apps can still filter data even if you have app sync pause enabled 

 

 How can we tell that data is filtered?

 If an app-side filter is applied to a district, the district will see an "App Filters" tab in the application area of the Clever dashboard. District Admins can view: 

 Shared Data : the data shared with the application by the district.

 Filters : the filters that were applied to the district data by the application.

 Final Data : the resulting filtered data that will be synced by the application. 

 

 

 If a user is shared with the application but is targeted by an app filter, the application will show as unavailable on their profile: "Currently unavailable - Use Troubleshooting tools to resolve"

 

 

 

 How do I view the data currently shared with the application?

 District Admins can download a CSV file of schools, students, teachers, or sections that are included in the data shared with that application. 

 To download these CSV files, click on the application icon from your Clever dashboard. On the Overview page, you'll see a box titled "Applications Receives". 

 You can click the blue download icon next to schools, students, teachers, or sections to download the list of data shared for that data type. These files will display the final data that is shared with the application. 

 

 

 How do I request a change to an app filter?

 If a change to an app filter is needed, please contact the application's support team directly . In your request, please indicate: 

 District: district name Request: list requested changes to app filter Notes: reason for requesting change to app filter 

 If the application has provided a Support Contact for you to contact directly, you'll find it at the top of the application overview page:

title: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Overview & Setup
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Getting started 3. Setup 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 Looking for additional support setting up Clever Portal? Check out our Clever Academy Quick Start - Setting up your Portal course 

 Overview

 SSO (Saved Passwords) applications are apps that don't currently have a rostering or single sign-on (SSO) integration with Clever, but can still be accessed via SSO through Clever Portal. This means di stricts can provide users with a seamless SSO experience for almost any learning application even if it is not an existing Clever partner. With the SSO (Saved Passwords) option, rostering data and user accounts are not synced to the app by Clever. This means they must be created within the application in advance, and user login credentials need to be manually set by the district admin, or by the users themselves. Clever will securely store these credentials so district users are logged into the applications automatically via single sign-on through Clever. This article and the video below provides a detailed overview of SSO (Saved Passwords) applications and the setup process for launching these apps at your district. 

 

 

 

 
 Getting started

 • District user accounts and credentials for the app: 
 • The first step is to confirm your district users have accounts and login credentials for the SSO (Saved Passwords) application. This would be done by working with the app partner directly before setting the app up in Clever.
 

 
 • Non-iOS devices: 
 • The second step is confirming the Clever browser extension is installed on all non-iOS devices that will be used to access the application through Clever. This includes home devices for users who will be accessing this app from home. The Clever browser extension facilitates single sign-on for SSO (Saved Passwords) apps. Please visit this article for more. 
 • The Clever browser extension is not required for iOS devices (e.g. iPads, iPhones) as these devices use the Clever app which acts as the browser extension. Please visit this article for more.
 

 

 
 Please note: For devices where the browser extension cannot be downloaded (e.g. Android, Kindle Fire), Saved Passwords apps will not be accessible. For details, visit: Clever Browser & Device Compatibility 

 

 
 Setup

 After configuring the above, there are three steps to set up any application for universal single sign-on (SSO) from your Clever Portal.

 1. Find and add an application 

 Visit the Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add applications page in your Clever Dashboard to find the application you would like to use. Once identified, click Request App . 

 

 

 Next, select if you would like to request that the application provider begin supporting rostering via Clever Secure Sync. The more requests Clever receives, the more likely it is to happen in the future.

 Finally, select Request Application .

 

 2. Configure user access 

 The next screen will provide you with an option to share the added application with particular schools. You can select the schools as listed and select Request with sharing or choose Request and share later . 

 

 Sharing rules can always be changed from the individual application's page under Modify user selection .

 

 Clever Tip: For more information on setting up your sharing permissions, check out How do I set my sharing permissions for applications? 

 3 . Provide your login information 

 • Selecting an example user 
• Provide your login information 
 
 Once you have added the application and set up sharing rules, you'll need to provide information on how Clever should log in your users. 

 First, select Begin matching on the added application's page: 

 

 Some applications require district specific information for logins to work. If this is the case, the first step will prompt you for the information Clever needs. You will need to obtain the information in these fields from the application. Please see the example below: 

 

 You can then choose whether credentials for this application will be set at a district level or if individual users will set their own credentials. 

 

 If you choose to have individuals set their own credentials, take note of the flow diagram that details the process, and click Confirm .

 

 Next, you'll need to provide login information: 

 1. Selecting an example user

 To begin matching credentials, select an existing user for the specified user type (e.g. student, teacher, staff). This step is meant to provide you with an example as you configure the credentials for this resource. We suggest entering a user for whom you already have the username and password. Once you have selected a user, you will see the data that’s synced from your SIS populated in the box below.

 

 2. Choosing a formula type

 Next, you'll choose a formula type for both Username and Password.

 

 You can choose from the following:

 • Exact user attribute 
 • This setting will pull data fields from a user’s profile as it is synced from your SIS. If we are not currently pulling in the fields you need, then reach out to Clever support to investigate possible alternate mappings.
 

• Manual input from user at first login 
 • This setting allows users to input their own credentials the first time they access the Saved Passwords resource via the Clever Portal. These credentials will be saved for all future logins. 
 

• Custom Formula 
 • You can use existing fields in the user’s profile to form custom strings for the Username/Password. Please see this Help Center article: How do I manage Saved Passwords application credentials? 
 

• CSV via SFTP 
 • When a user's credentials are uploaded via CSV, they will not be asked to log in the first time they access the application through Clever. 
• Upload a file containing each user’s credentials using SFTP. You can find the SFTP credentials on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page in the SFTP Settings - Saved Passwords Applications box . 
 

• One time CSV upload 
 • Upload a file containing each user’s credentials directly to the site using drag and drop. 
 

 
 You may choose any combination of these options for your username and password configurations. Once you have completed both fields, you will see a preview of the credentials in the card on the left. Click Confirm to save those changes. You can return and edit your formulas by clicking on the corresponding card on the left.

 For additional instructions, please visit: How do I manage Saved Passwords application credentials? 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Why do these applications require a lengthy setup? 

 • SSO (Saved Passwords) applications are not formally integrated with Clever. Our engineering team works to create a connection that will serve your users in the most seamless way possible! In order for our connection to work smoothly, we require you to set up the application beforehand and ask that you provide us with credentials. While these applications do take a bit more setup time, your users will enjoy a seamless experience once everything is done.
 
 What device(s) can I use with Saved Passwords applications? 

 • Applications with Saved Passwords can be used on any devices that support browser extensions with Chrome, Firefox, or Edge. Desktop computers, laptops, Chromebooks, and Windows devices are all supported. Saved Passwords is also supported on iOS devices running on iOS 9.0 and above with the most up to date Clever app installed.
 
 Saved Passwords functionality on iOS For more information on how to set this up, please see Clever Saved Passwords for iOS. 

 Can we use Google / Active Directory / Entra ID / Office365 to log into an application via Clever? 

 • SSO (Saved Passwords) apps will only function properly with applications that allow for a standard username and password. They will not work with any applications where the district uses a third-party identity provider (e.g. Google) to log in

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Safety thresholds
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Safety thresholds 3. How to know if your district-app connection is on hold 4. Next steps to continue syncing your district’s data with an app on hold 

 Overview 

 If your district makes large changes to the data that is synced to Clever, we put a hold on the sync to make sure the changes are intentional. In a very similar fashion, if changes to your sync or the sharing permissions for an application would cause a large percentage of data shared with the app to change or be deleted, app-side safety thresholds will not allow that data to be synced to the application until a member of the app team has had a chance to review the data. 

 App-side safety thresholds are an important part of syncing rostering information as it ensures that both you and the app team are aware of any potentially significant changes to your district’s data. Holds are a safeguard to prevent potentially incorrect or undesired changes from being synced to an application. 

 Safety Thresholds 

 By default, if your district’s most recent sync or an update in your sharing permissions results in over 75% of any data type (e.g. schools, students, teachers, sections) being updated or over 10% being deleted, an application safety hold will be triggered. Some applications may set different safety thresholds in order to better support you. 

 Once a safety hold is approved, the district’s data will be fully available in Clever’s API for the application to pull. 

 How to know if your district-app connection is on hold

 You will know if your connection to an application is on hold based on the status of the application as seen on the Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page . If the application has a status of Requires App Review , then a hold has been placed on your district’s connection to the application: 

 

 By navigating to the Overview page of the application, you can also view the application’s status: 

 

 For a description of each of the possible application statuses, please see What's my application's status? 

 Next steps to continue syncing your district’s data with an app on hold

 Please reach out to the application’s team directly to work with their team on reviewing your district’s recent changes and releasing the app-side hold.

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Field access
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Customizing student field access 3. Customizing student contact field access 4. Customizing District Admin field access 

 Overview

 The Field Access feature helps districts view and audit the data fields available to each connected application in the Clever API for students and student contacts. 

 You can find the Field Access information by navigating to a specific application in your dashboard and choosing the Settings tab: 

 

 Currently, Field Access gives you an overview of the data that your applications have indicated they are using. Each field is set up by each learning application, if you have any questions about this you will need to contact their support team. 

 If you need to add additional custom fields (i.e. extension data) to Clever, please see this article for more information. 

 
 Customizing student field access 

 You can edit which student fields are currently being shared with the application from this same page. Select Advanced settings > Customize fields shared with . You can then customize the student data that is available to the application from the Edit Student Fields page: 

 

 On this screen, you will find two lists:

 • The list on the left-hand side shows all the fields in Clever that you can choose to share with the application. 
• The list on the right-hand side shows the list of fields that are currently being shared with the application. 
 
 Once you have selected which student fields you would like to share, select Next and you will be presented with the ability to customize student contact field access. Click Save to finalize your selections.

 Please note: Some fields cannot be removed from the application's sync. For the fields that can be removed, the result may be a degraded experience depending on the application. 

 

 
 Customizing student contacts field access 

 Student contacts are usually students' parents and guardians. You can share student contacts with apps if needed.

 To customize student contact field access, select Advanced settings > Customize fields shared with. You'll be prompted to customize your student fields first and can click Next to access the settings for student contacts. You can choose to disable student contacts for the application or customize which fields are shared. Once you have selected which student contact fields you would like to share, select Next and you will be presented with the ability to customize district administrator field access. Click Save to finalize your selections. 

 

 
 Customizing District Admin field access 

 To customize district administrator field access, select Advanced settings > Customize fields shared with . Once you have selected which student and student contact fields you would like to share, select Next and you will be presented with the ability to customize district administrator field access. You can choose to disable district administrator access for the application or customize which fields to share. Click Save to finalize your selections.

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Data Warnings and Errors
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Types of data quality issues 3. Viewing data quality issues 4. Fixing data quality issues 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Even after your data syncs with Clever, there may be data quality issues that can affect application performance. The impact of these data quality issues varies by application. Reviewing data quality issues is an important troubleshooting step when user records are missing or users are experiencing problems within an application. If you have questions on how a data quality warning will impact usage of an application, please reach out to the application team directly. 

 

 Please note: For more information on how a data quality error or warning will impact the application's use, please contact the application team directly. The application team configures data quality errors and warnings. 

 

 
 

 Types of data quality issues

 Data Errors Impact: High If a record is flagged by a Data Error, the user or section won't appear in the application. Data errors must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. Data Warnings Impact: Low If a record is flagged by a Data Warning, the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning . Data Warnings can often be ignored.

 To contact the application team, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications and click on the application. If the application team has provided one, you may find a support contact on the app's overview page. If a support contact isn't provided, please search online for the app's contact information.

 

 

 
 

 Viewing data quality issues

 Data Warnings are specific to each application. You can view your Data Warnings via two methods: 

 • To view all data warnings: Support Tools > Data Quality 
• To view app-specific data warnings: Select an application, select Data Quality 
 
 

 Support Tools > Data Quality (recommended) 

 On this screen you will see five pieces of information: 

 Issue Type Data Type Field Number of issues Impacted Applications 
 The severity of the data quality issue; data error or data warning. More info can be found here . The record type affected (school, student, teacher, section, etc.). The field that is being flagged for data quality issues (ex: student_email). The number of records flagged for this data quality issue. The applications that may be affected. 
 
 

 To view the details of an issue, click on the number of issues. Here you'll see a description of the issue(s), the number of issues relating to that error, and the affected application(s). To view which specific records are related to the issue, click the download button to download a file containing a list of affected records. 

 

 Clever groups issues by issue type, record type, and field. As such, when you select an issue from the main Data Quality screen, you may see multiple related issues on the detail screen. For example: 

 

 

 
 

 Fixing data quality issues

 First, let's quickly review which errors must be fixed and which are optional: 

 Data Errors Impact: High - Must be fixed

 Data Warnings Impact: Low - Optional to fix. Contact the application directly to determine the impact on user experience. 

 To resolve most data quality issues, simply adjust the data in your student information system (SIS). Once the updated data is received in your next sync, the data quality issue will be removed. If the data was added via Custom Data in Clever, navigate to Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data and locate the record you'd like to edit. If you receive a data quality error about missing a specific field for all records: 

 • SFTP syncs: Add the field to your CSV exports and upload the updated CSV files to Clever. Directions for formatting your data files can be found here . 
• SIS-managed Auto Sync: R each out to your SIS and request that they add the field to your CSV exports. Once the updated data files are received, the data quality error will be resolved. Directions for formatting your data files can be found here. 
• Clever-managed Auto Sync: Contact our support team with the following information:
 • Record Type: i.e. Student, Teacher, Sections, etc. 
• Name of the field in Clever: i.e. Username, State ID, FRL_status 
• The exact name of the field in your SIS 
 

 
 

 
 

 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 

 What will happen if I don't fix a data quality error or warning? 

 • If you don't resolve a data quality error, users may be unable to access the learning application. If you don't fix a warning, the application experience may be degraded. For specific details on how errors or warnings will impact the application's use, please contact the application directly. Clever's teams can't provide insight into how an app experience may change based on data issues.
 
 Do I have to resolve a data quality error or warning? 

 • To ensure application access and for the best application experience, we recommend resolving data quality errors and warnings. For specific details on how errors or warnings will impact the application's use, please contact the application directly. Clever's teams can't provide insight into how an app experience may change based on data issues.
 
 How do I resolve any data quality errors or warnings? 

 • Ensure the data is properly input in your SIS, and that the field it's in is exporting to Clever. If you use a manual SFTP or SIS-Managed Auto Sync, please work with your SIS directly to ensure data is included in the CSV files you are sending to Clever. If you use a Clever-Managed Auto Sync, and you need additional fields added to your data sync, you can contact the Clever Support team for further assistance.

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Integration status
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What's my application's status? 

 

 Overview

 This article will walk you through sharing data with an application once the application has accepted your request to connect via Clever. 

 What's my application's status? 

 In your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page, you can view each application's integration status: 

 • 
 Hiding enabled: An administrator at the district has hidden the application from users in Clever Portal. Follow the directions outlined here to disable hiding. 

• 
 Incomplete SAML Setup : This indicates you will need to finish configuring the settings for an SSO (SAML) application. To finish the setup, click the application in your dashboard and click on the Settings tab. Under SAML details , click Edit and enter any missing information. The application will change to Launched once the SAML details are complete and users are shared. 

• Invited by app: Applications you are already using at your school organization may invite you to connect via Clever to simplify rostering. Learn more here . 
• Launch Date: XX/XX/XXXX : This is the date the application will go live. If you need the launch date adjusted, reach out to the app directly as any changes to this are controlled by the app.
• 
 Launched : Your integration is live! If the application supports SSO, the application is available to users in the Clever Portal. 

• 
 No Credentials Set: The next step is yours! Set the credentials that the SSO (Saved Passwords) app should use to log in users. More info here . 

• 
 No Data Shared : The next step is yours: set your sharing permissions ! 

 
 • 
 No Users Selected : The next step is yours! Select which users you want to share with the application.

• 
 Requested : This means that we are waiting on the application's team to accept your request and proceed with the implementation. If you need to expedite the request process, please reach out to the application directly as we do not have insight as to the application's review and acceptance process. 

• 
 Requires App Review: Some applications have set safety thresholds to prevent unintended changes in data from affecting users in their platform. If you see this message, please reach out to the application partner requesting they review and approve the changes. Information for our application partners on managing safety thresholds can be found here .

• 
 Setting Up : Hang tight! The application is preparing your district for launch. You can view the Launch Date by clicking on the application. Users will not be able to access the application until the Launch Date. If you need to expedite the setup process, please reach out to the application directly. 

• 
 Waiting On Approval: This means that we are still waiting on the application's team to accept your request and proceed with the implementation. You can click 'Remind app' to remind the application partner's team of your request. 

 
 

 • You will also see the ability to remind the app from the application's profile page:

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Sync times
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sharing permissions and data updates or changes 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 

 This article discusses the technical process for how applications sync data from Clever. Clever does not push the data to your learning programs. Applications manage the process of syncing data from Clever's API. 

 
 Sharing permissions and data updates or changes

 

 When you set sharing permissions for an application, you are choosing what data from your account to make available to the application through Clever’s API. Applications then pull the data from Clever to use in their system. 

 

 If you make an update to your Clever data (including modifying your sharing permissions), you can expect this change to be reflected in the application within 24 hours . If after 24 hours there is no change in the application, please review the troubleshooting steps in this article: Troubleshooting: App issues 

 If the data is correct in Clever but incorrect in an application, please reach out to the application directly! Their team may need to complete another sync with Clever to get your most recent data into their system. While applications each have their own sync schedules with Clever, most applications sync with Clever on a nightly basis. If you need a manual sync with the application initiated, please contact the application's support team. They may be able to push a manual sync to pull the most current data from Clever. 

 If you need to contact the application partner for more information, navigate to the app overview page. If the application has provided a Support Contact, you'll find it at the top of this page. 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 

 How often do applications pull data from Clever? 

 • While each application manages its sync times with Clever, most applications pull data nightly.
 
 When can I expect changes in my data in Clever to be reflected in connected learning applications? 

 • Typically within 24 hours. If you do not see data changes reflected in the learning application within 24 hours, please contact the application team directly. If you need changes implemented sooner than 24 hours, we recommend contacting the application directly to see if they may be able to sync data from Clever sooner.
 
 What steps should I take if data is correct in Clever, but incorrect in a learning application? 

 • Please reach out to the learning application directly to troubleshoot further.
 
 What does the last sync time on the application overview page indicate? 

 • The last sync time on the application overview page indicates the last time an application queried the API. The last attempted sync time doesn't indicate completion of a complete data sync, so if you see data missing in the application, please contact their team directly.

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Set launch date
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I set up an SSO and/or Rostering application for future launch? 

 Overview 

 When setting up a new application, the district is able to establish sharing permissions and resolve data quality issues weeks — or even months!— before the application launches. The launch date is the day that an application becomes available to teachers and students in the Clever Portal. This article will show you how to prepare apps for launch. 

 Please note: If you'd like to adjust the current launch date of an application, please reach out directly to the application and they will be happy to help you! After the district initially sets the date (as explained below), adjustments are managed by the application's team. 

 How do I set up an SSO and/or Rostering application for future launch? 

 1. Log in to your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the sidebar. 

 2. Add the application you want to set up. 

 3. You'll be brought to a page where you can authorize the request to add the app. Please share whether or not your district has already purchased licenses for the application. At the bottom, select Request Launch Date. 

 : 

 4. Once the request has been approved by the application's team, go to your Clever Dashboard and click on the app you're setting up. 

 For detailed information on your Application's Status , please see " What is my application's status? " Questions? Please reach out directly to the application's team for insight on the status. 

 5. You can view the launch date that you set for your app. Click "Begin setup" to manage your district's sharing permissions with the app.

 

 

 Once the sharing permissions are set up and any data quality issues have been resolved, you're all done! No further action is required by the district. On the day of the launch date, the new application will appear in the Clever Portal for all shared users.

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Transitioning applications to Clever
text:
What if my teachers and students already have existing accounts with an application?

 Requesting to add an application to your Clever dashboard will not disrupt students and teachers already using the existing accounts. 

 Most applications are able to preserve your existing accounts and match them to accounts created using Clever. Contact your specific Application Support Teams for details.

 Can I add applications mid-year without losing progress or historical data?

 You might be hesitant to add new application connections during the middle of the school year or semester. Fear not! With Clever you can connect to applications during the middle of the semester without losing progress or historical data within the app. 

 Clever provides a matchmaking tool to our partner applications, allowing them to migrate the data that you share in Clever with the existing historical progress in their application. You can seamlessly add your applications , share data, and we'll take care of the rest!

 

 If you have any questions at all about connecting with applications, please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team . 

 For a full list of available apps, be sure to browse through Clever's Application Gallery!

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - App invites
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing app invites 3. Accepting app invites 4. Declining app invites 5. Disabling app invite notifications 

 Overview

 Did you know you can save time setting up applications for your schools by accepting app invitations? If you already have an established relationship with an app partner, and it's also available on Clever, you may receive an invitation from the app partner to roster via Clever in your dashboard. By accepting the invitation, you can set up automated rostering and students can have a seamless learning experience through Clever. 

 
 Viewing app invites

 When sent an invitation, the district account owner will receive an email with the invitation request. The email will have the subject "Set Up {App Name}". It will provide useful information such as the name of the app, a custom message from the app, and a link to accept the invitation.

 

 Additionally, you will also receive the invite in your Clever Dashboard . You can find the invite by navigating to your dashboard and clicking View invite in the Applications tab. 

 

 
 Accepting app invites

 If you choose to accept an app invite, you can do so by clicking Accept Your Invitation in the email (which will redirect you to the invite in Clever) or the Accept on the application invite in the Clever Dashboard. 

 

 Once you accept the invite, you can begin the setup process and start sharing data with the app. You can learn more about our setup process in our article about Sharing Permissions .

 

 
 Declining app invites

 If you would like to decline an app invite, you can do so by clicking the Decline button on the application invite in Clever. 

 

 When you decline the invite, you'll see a brief survey from Clever. We request a short note as to why you are declining the app invite, which is only visible to Clever. You can also choose to send a message to the application explaining why you declined the invite. Once you've declined the invite, the app will disappear from your Dashboard. If the application has questions about your decision to decline the invite, they'll be provided with the email for your district contact. If an app invite is declined in error, contact Clever Support with the name of the district and application. Our team can restore the invite so the district may accept. 

 

 
 Disabling app invite notifications

 To turn off app invite notifications, click your name in the top-right corner, then select Profile .

 

 

 
 
 
 
 Navigate to the Emails tab and turn off the toggle next to Requests and invite changes .

title: For Clever Admins: Apps - Add/request or remove apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding an application 3. Application sharing permissions 4. What if my desired application isn’t listed? 5. What's my application's status? 6. Removing an application 

 

 Getting started with adding applications? Check out our guide, Onboarding - Adding Applications to Clever , available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview 

 Clever single sign-on (SSO) enables users to be securely authenticated into connected applications via their individual Clever Portals.
