This article explains how district admins can add or remove an online learning application to their district's Clever Portal in order to share the app with district users. To learn about the different types of Clever applications, please visit this article . 

 For specific details about each of the Clever-integrated SSO, Rostering or Library applications, visit our application gallery here . 

 

 
 Adding an application 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page. 
 
 

 The application search bar and filters 

 • Using the filters at the top of the page, you can search for applications by: 
 • Type (e.g. Rostering, SSO, Free) 
 • Please note: Some learning applications do require certain fees to use their products through Clever (e.g. licensing or subscription fees). Sorting by 'Free' provides a list of learning applications that are completely free to districts. 
 

 

 
 • Subject (e.g. Assessment, Communication, Math, Reading) 
• Name (e.g. i-Ready, SeeSaw, Big Ideas Math, Achieve3000) 
 
 
 If the app you are looking for is not available to request, please review this section . 

 • Once you locate the desired application, select 'Request App' to the right of the selection. 
 
 

 • Once you click Request App, you'll be taken to a page where you can view more information about the application, including its Edtech quality indicators if available. 
• You will then be prompted to authorize the request of the application. You will need to first: 
 
 • Select whether or not your district has already purchased the application, or would like to try a free trial of the app (if applicable). 
• Select a preferred Launch Date for the application. Please note: 
 • The Custom Launch Date you enter is a suggested or preferred date of launch that may later be updated by the application partner if it is not feasible. Please contact app partners directly to adjust app Launch Dates. 
• The Launch Date is the date the app will become available to all shared district users in their Clever portals. 
• For rostering apps, view the fields required by the application under the Required fields section. You can determine if your sync can meet these requirements. 
 

 
 Example of a rostering application Example of an SSO application 

 • Once you've made the appropriate selections, select Send integration request to submit the request to the application provider. 
• The application has now been requested! Please see this article to understand the application's status as it progresses to the Launch Date.
 
 Please note: If you previously rostered the learning application via another method (ex: manual data entry) and you have questions about whether accounts will be matched or managed moving forward, please contact the application partner directly. The Clever team doesn't have insight into how applications manage switching rostering services. 

 

 

 
 

 Application sharing permissions 

 Once the application has approved your connection, you will then specify which sharing type you would like to use in order to share data with the application. These settings will also determine which of your district users will have the application available to them in their individual Portals. Please follow the steps outlined in this guide to set up your sharing permissions for the first time. 

 After the application has been connected, district admins can add or modify the app sharing permissions to determine what district data should be shared with the app, and which district users should have access to the application from their Clever Portals after the Launch Date . The options available are: 

 • Share by District : All district data in Clever will be synced to the application. This includes district admins, schools, teachers, students, staff and sections. 
• Share by Schools : Clever will only share any data associated with the schools you've selected (e.g. students, teachers, sections, staff). 
• Share by Rules : Clever will only share data that meets the specific criteria you've set. This is the most recommended sharing type as it provides districts with the flexibility in determining specifically what data should be included, or excluded, in the sharing. Read this article to learn more about how to Share by Rules! 
• School Site Sharing : If your district has designated School Tech Leads in Clever, you can provide them with the ability to configure application sharing permissions for their associated schools. More info here! 
• Copy Sharing : This option will copy the same sharing permissions from another application that has already been set up. 
 
 Applications will not have access to any district data in Clever that has not been specifically shared with them via the application sharing permissions. Please visit this article for detailed instructions. 

 

 
 What if my desired application isn’t listed? 

 If your desired application is not available to request per the process outlined above: 

 • SSO or Rostering integrated apps: 
 • This means the app provider has a specific policy that requires them to send districts an invite rather than districts requesting their app (e.g. BrainPOP). Please contact the app partner directly to request their app. 
 

• Clever Library apps: 
 • Most Library apps can only be added individually by teachers to their Clever Portals. In most cases, these types of apps are not available as district-wide applications. For details, please visit this article . 
 

• Non-Clever integrated apps: 
 • Clever can build a secure connection for almost any application through our SSO (Saved Passwords) product. SSO (Saved Passwords) applications are apps that don't currently integrate directly with Clever, but can still be accessed through the Clever Portal providing users with a seamless single sign-on experience for the app. 
• To add an SSO (Saved Passwords) application, select the 'Request [ApplicationName]' below the search results. 
 

 
 

 • Then, enter some important details to help us configure this SSO (Saved Passwords) application before selecting the 'Request' option. You will receive an email once the app is ready for your district. For more, please visit: SSO (Saved Passwords) Apps: Overview & Setup .
 
 We also encourage you to contact non-Clever integrated application providers directly to let them know you’re interested in syncing your data through Clever. Your voice is extremely important as they consider working with us! If you do have a contact at the vendor, feel free to email us at application-interest@clever.com , and we can follow up about building an integration. For any app that doesn't require a per-user login, you can also consider adding a link to your Clever Portal for district users. Learn more about adding links here ! 

 
 What's my application's status? 

 You can view the status of all district applications from your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page or by clicking on the application name to open the app overview page. In the Dashboard you can see the App Status column which lists the status for each of your applications. Next to that is the Next step column which lists the next step that needs to be taken. Here's a list including every application status:

 • Requested : We're waiting for the application's team to accept your request and proceed with the implementation. If you need to expedite the request process, please reach out to the application prov

title: For Clever Admins: Applications - What types of apps are on Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Rostering apps 3. Single sign-on (SSO) apps 4. SSO (SAML) apps 5. Rostering and single sign-on (SSO) apps 6. Library apps 7. SSO (Saved Passwords) apps 8. External Links 

 Overview 

 Clever supports several types of learning applications. This article outlines the differences between each type of application. 

 

 

 Most applications are added by Clever Admins via the Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page. From this page, applications can be sorted by integration type to make it easier to locate the right one for your district.

 

 Clever is available at no cost to schools organization - they do not pay Clever to utilize our platform. However, some learning applications do require certain fees to use their products through Clever (e.g. licensing or subscription fees). The 'Request Applications' list can also be sorted by 'Free' which provides a list of learning applications that are completely free to school organizations. 

 For more information, please visit: How do I add or remove an application? 

 

 The following application types can only be added by a Clever Admin: 

 • Rostering
• Single Sign-on (SSO)
• Rostering & Single Sign-on (SSO)
• SSO (SAML)
• SSO (Saved Passwords) 
 
 The following application types can only be added to Clever by a teacher:

 • Library Clever Admins can recommend library apps. Learn more . 
 
 

 Once an application is launched or requested, you can find out what the application type is on the Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page under "App Type." 

 

 
 Rostering apps 

 Rostering applications support Secure Sync Rostering . This means the rostering data from your school organization's student information system (SIS) is synced to Clever, which is then synced to your connected rostering applications. Up-to-date rostering data is maintained in your connected learning applications by the secure transfer of your SIS data from Clever to the applications.

 

 
 Single sign-on (SSO) apps 

 These applications are integrated with Clever and support single sign-on (SSO). These applications do not receive rostering data from Clever.

 

 In addition to traditional SSO apps, we also offer SSO ( SAML ) apps. These apps are integrated with Clever for Single Sign-On (SSO) but require additional setup in order to authenticate users. Most SSO (SAML) applications have a setup guide available in Clever's Help Center, or you can reach out to the application partner directly for help with the setup process. If you're unsure about application URLs, usernames, or passwords, please contact the app team. SSO (SAML) applications do not provide applications access to rostering data. If interested in rostering for the application, please search for the app partner's "Rostering" application if available, as some SSO (SAML) apps offer both SSO and Rostering. If offered they are two separate apps you must add to Clever.

 
 Rostering and single sign-on (SSO) apps 

 These applications support both rostering and SSO! This means all rostering and single sign-on data is synced to the application by Clever.

 

 
 Library apps 

 The Clever Library is a place for teachers to discover, add, and review new resources for their classrooms. Resources in the Library can be added by teachers to their Teacher Pages for easy access by students. Library apps provide lightweight rostering and are able to create accounts for students/teachers! They are the easiest and most secure way for teachers to add applications for their classrooms and provide a seamless SSO experience. 

 District administrators have the ability to recommend and block Library resources, or disable the Library feature completely for their district. Information on managing the Library at the district-level can be found here . 

 
 SSO (Saved Passwords) apps 

 SSO (Saved Passwords) applications are apps that don't currently have a rostering or single sign-on (SSO) integration with Clever, but can still be accessed via SSO through the Clever Portal. This means di stricts can provide users with a seamless SSO experience for almost any learning application even if it is not an existing Clever partner. 

 With the SSO (Saved Passwords) option, rostering data and user accounts are not synced to the app by Clever. This means they must be created within the application in advance, and user login credentials need to be manually set by the district admin, or by the users themselves. Clever will securely store these credentials so district users are logged into the applications automatically via single sign-on through Clever. 

 Click here to read more about SSO (Saved Passwords) applications.

 

 
 

 External Links 

 Custom external links work much like bookmarks by bringing district users directly to the application's login page where they can enter their credentials for that application. These types of applications are not integrated with Clever. Click here to read more about how to add custom external links.

 
 If you have any questions about the types of learning applications in Clever, please reach out to Clever Support ! We are happy to help!

title: Clever + Lightspeed Setup Guide
text:
In order for your Lightspeed and Clever integration to be optimized, you’ll need to send Lightspeed your users’ directory services usernames. This may be the same as your AD usernames, your emails, or something completely different. You’ll be able to choose or create the format needed by your district with this tool. If you don’t know your DSU you should contact your IT department within your district for the format. You can find the link to create and send your DSU field on the “Settings” page of Lightspeed’s profile. 

 

 There are two different ways that you can locate user credentials: 

 • Select them from the dropdown 
• Use a custom configuration 
 

 Select from dropdown 

 The easiest and most reliable way to provide your directory services username is by indicating where they are within your existing SIS sync. For example, if student directory services usernames are their email addresses and those fields are already syncing to Clever regularly, you would select those fields in the dropdowns within the directory services username tool linked on the “Settings” page for Lightspeed. 

 

 Custom configurations 

 If student directory services usernames are syncing to Clever already, but aren’t neatly contained in a single field, we’re still able to get you set up! You can use a custom configuration to provide those credentials. Instead of selecting a single field, you’ll select “Custom” in the appropriate dropdown list and paste in one of the formulas below: 

 

 Example student: Scott Kelly 

 Email: sykelly@cleverusd.edu 

 Username: skelly19 

 

 Once you’ve entered the appropriate formula, make sure that the credentials for the sample student match the directory services username you have on file. 

 Have questions or need help creating a custom formula? Contact us .

title: Clever + Rosetta Stone Setup Guide
text:
From the Rosetta Stone team: 

 Before requesting Rosetta Stone apps in Clever, we suggest taking a look at our Comprehensive Documentation of Rosetta Stone Clever Integration which includes features and limitations of using Rosetta Stone through Clever as well as any limitations that may determine how you set your sharing rules.

title: Troubleshooting: Data missing or incorrect in an app
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshooting steps 

 Overview 

 There may sometimes be discrepancies between the data in Clever and in connected rostering applications. In most cases, you'll need to contact the application's support team to resolve the error . Before contacting the application, please use the troubleshooting steps below to confirm the point of contact for resolving the error. 

 
 

 

 
 Troubleshooting steps 

 Step 1: Confirm the data is correct in Student Information System (SIS) and Clever. Use the Data Browser or the search bar for reviewing data. Step 2: Confirm the data is shared with the app by reviewing the user or section's profile. For students and teachers, use the Troubleshoot Sharing tool to confirm the data is being shared with the app. 

 Please note: If the user has recently changed schools or classes, or was recently synced to Clever, please allow 24 hours for their connected applications to be updated so they can log in.

 If an application has filtered out the record, it will display a special message - to remove the filter, contact the app's support team. 

 Step 3: Confirm that the fields your district needs to be shared with an application are being shared with that app. You can do this by going to My applications on your Clever Dashboard > click on the application name > click on the settings tab > scroll to the Field Access box and click on Advanced settings to edit field access. See: For Clever Admins: Apps - Field access Step 4: Confirm there are no data errors. These can be viewed on the application's page under the Overview tab. See: Application Syncs: Data Warnings and Errors Step 5: Check the application's last sync time on the app's overview page. If the change in data occurred after the application's last sync, the change will not be reflected in the application until the next time the app syncs with Clever. Most apps sync every 24 hrs. Step 6: Check the application's integration status . If the status says Requires app review you'll need the application partner to review and approve your data changes. Some applications have set safety thresholds to prevent unintended changes in data from affecting users in their platform. 

 Step 7: If all items above have been confirmed, please contact the application's support team to investigate the issue . Application partners fully manage pulling data from Clever's API and processing it into their system. As such, Clever Support is unable to resolve the error. Each application team will be most familiar with the data fields they sync from Clever. Some application partners may only receive certain data fields from Clever depending on their current integration setup. If the application has provided a Support Contact for you to contact directly, you’ll find it at the top of the application overview page:

title: Troubleshooting: App issues
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. App is missing 2. User clicked on app, and received an error 3 . User c licked on app, and Clever asked for a username and password 4. Data incorrect in apps 5. Problems within apps 

 Please note: It's important to distinguish between an issue logging into Clever (a user can't access Clever) and an issue accessing an application from within the Clever portal (e.g after the user has successfully logged into Clever). If a user can't access Clever, that is a separate troubleshooting issue from what we cover here. Please see this article to help troubleshoot users' access to Clever: Troubleshooting: Troubleshoot Login tool . For more information about what team you should contact for support, we have a great video resource that can provide more insight: Getting support for district admin .

 Overview

 Occasionally users run into issues with their applications. This can be due to a user not being shared with an app to errors occurring within the application (which would need to be addressed by the application's support team). This article will go over some of the common issues and guide you on how to troubleshoot. 

 
 App is missing

 If a user is missing an app in the Clever Portal, please confirm the user is shared with the application. The most common reasons a user is not shared with an app are:

 • The user does not have any sections in Clever.
• The user does not meet the filter criteria for sharing rules (usually section grade level or subject)
 
 In both cases, you will want to confirm they are enrolled in sections in your student information system (SIS) and that the data is synced to Clever. Alternatively, you may need to adjust the sharing permissions for the application to ensure the user is shared. You can learn about sharing permissions here: Sharing Permissions: Overview .

 ​​ 

 

 
 User clicked on app, and received an error

 Clever has an excellent tool for debugging this scenario. Please see this article for information on using the "Troubleshoot Login" tool. In general, if a user clicks on an application in the Clever Portal and receives an error on the application's page, please reach out to the application's support team for help . Most login issues must be resolved by the application's support team. 

 Clever Tip: If they are seeing an error at a web URL that is not a Clever address, it's likely that the application's team is best equipped to help fix the issue. Please reach out to the application's support team to investigate further! 

 Here are examples of common errors you might see for specific applications: ​ 

 

 
 Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password

 If a Clever page asks a user to enter their username and password for an application, enter the correct login information for that application, and click Save Login. If the device has the Clever browser extension downloaded, Clever will save this information and automatically log the student in next time they click the icon from the Portal!

 

 If a user clicks on an application and sees the page below, this means we were not able to log in the user with the username and password they provided. Please have them enter their correct username and password for the application, and click ' Update & Log in '. 

 

 If they are still having trouble, please confirm that the username and password they entered will allow them to successfully log into their account directly on the application's page (i.e. at schoology.com ). If you can access the app directly through the app's website but not through Clever, please contact Clever Support for help! 

 Did you know? Applications that prompt users to manually enter credentials as shown above are called SSO (Saved Passwords) applications. Learn more about these applications in this Help Center article: For Teachers: What are Saved Passwords applications? 

 

 
 

 Data incorrect in apps

 If the data in an application is incorrect (e.g. rosters, students, and school associations), please see this article . 

 Please note: Most applications sync with Clever daily. Please allow up to 24 hrs for newly updated or shared data in Clever to be reflected in your connected applications. For more information, visit: How often will my applications sync with Clever? 

 

 
 

 Problems within applications

 After a user clicks on an application from the Clever Portal, their experience within the app is controlled by that app. Clever does not have any insight into the inner workings of an application. A good analogy would be to think of Clever as the key to opening the door to the application, but once that door is open, we do not go inside. If a user is running into an issue within the application, please contact the application’s support team for help. 

 
 Here are common examples of when an application’s team will be best equipped to provide support:

 • You are receiving an error message on the application’s website
• You are seeing incorrect data within the application, but correct data within the Clever
• You are having trouble accessing grades, homework, textbooks, videos, resources, or running reports within the application
• Your sections have access to the incorrect content within the application
• You have questions about features within the application
• The application does not save a student’s progress
• The application is frozen
• The application logs or kicks you or your students out
• You need to merge or match an old or preexisting account with the one created through Clever

title: Sharing Permission: Sharing Clever Admins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to share admins with applications 3. How to adjust admin sharing via Field Access 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Please note: All existing application connections established before April 7, 2026 will maintain the behavior of sharing all admins with applications. Moving forward, school organizations should plan to share admins with newly connected applications appropriately via sharing permissions. Learn more about how admins will be shared with existing applications here . 

 

 

 Overview

 Clever helps your team control which administrator team members can see specific data within connected applications. Just like you set sharing permissions for students, teachers, and staff, you can choose which admins have access to each application.

 

 Field Access being toggled off will take precedence over district admin rules being created. If you would like for the admins to be shared with applications, Clever recommends that you ensure the Field Access toggle is turned on. 

 

 Follow the steps below to update admin sharing settings for each application.

 

 
 How to share admins with applications

 To adjust whether administrators are shared with a given application:

 Follow the steps below if you’re using the Guided sharing view to share your admins and see a tutorial video here :

 • 
 Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications and select the application 

• 
 Under Shared Data , select Edit Sharing 

• 
 Select Add Another Rule 

• 
 Select District admins need access in the drop-down menu 

• 
 Select the desired admins utilizing the Role filter. You can either select the role from the dropdown menu or type the role. If you choose to type the role make sure the role entered matches exactly as it appears in Clever (including uppercase and lowercase letters), as this is case-sensitive. 

• 
 Select Done Editing 

• 
 Select Save Sharing Rules 

 
 

 Follow the steps below if you’re using the Standard sharing view to share your admin members and see a tutorial video here :

 • 
 Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications and select the application

• 
 Under Shared Data , select Edit Sharing 

• 
 Select Edit Sharing Rules 

• 
 Select Add another rule 

• 
 Select Custom Share District Admins 

• 
 Select the desired admins utilizing the Role filter. You can either select the role from the dropdown menu or type the role. If you choose to type the role make sure the role entered matches exactly as it appears in Clever (including uppercase and lowercase letters), as this is case-sensitive. 

• 
 Select Done editing 

• 
 Select Save Sharing Rules 

 
 

 
 How to adjust admin sharing via Field Access

 If you do not want to share admins with an app that uses share by school or school-site sharing, you can turn off admin access using Field Access. When the slider is grey, admins are not shared with the application.

 To adjust whether administrators are shared with a given application via Field Access:

 

 • Navigate to the application's overview page and select Settings 
• Under the Field Access section, select Advanced Settings 
• Select Customize fields shared with {app} and click Customize fields 
• On the Edit Student Fields page, select Next 
• On the Edit Contact Fields page, select Next 
• On the Edit District Admin Fields page, click on the slider selection for Share District Admins with {app}. 
• Confirm the slider selection is grey to remove admins from being shared, or green to share admins. Select Save 
 
 Once complete, admins will no longer be shared with the application. If you require administrator accounts in the application, please contact the application directly for assistance. 

 Please note: If you have uploaded extension fields to Clever. You may need to navigate through one additional "fields" page before selecting Save . 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Clever currently shares all admins with apps by default. Will this change when admin sharing permissions launch in April 2026? 

 • For existing app connections: Clever will keep the current behavior—all admins will continue to be shared. You don’t need to take any action.
 • If you use share by rules, Clever will automatically add a rule to continue sharing all district admins that looks like: Share all all district admins where [role] [equals any of] [all existing roles] created by Clever.
• If you use share by district, Clever will add a toggle and turn it on to continue sharing all admins.
• If you use share by school or school-site sharing, all admins will continue to be shared with apps. If you want more control over which admins are shared, you can switch to share by rules or share by district.
 

• For new app connections: Sharing depends on the setup you choose.
 • If you use share by rules or share by district, you will need to follow the steps here to add a rule to share district admins.
• If you use share by school or school-site, all admins will be shared by default.
 

 
 

 We use share by school and school site sharing. Why don’t we see the option to share admins? 

 • 
 With share by school or school-site sharing, all DAs will continue to be shared. You should switch share by rule if you want to share DAs granularly.

title: Clever + Reading Plus Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and Reading Plus: 1. Setup Process 2. Recommended Sharing Rules 3. FAQs 4. Support Contacts 

 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup. 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add Reading Plus by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for Reading Plus .
• You will then be brought to a page where you can authorize requesting the applications. When requesting an application, you can select whether or not your district has already purchased the application. When you click "Haven't Purchased" for applications that have free trials, you'll receive a prompt asking if you would like to try a free trial.
• You will also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request .
• Email integrations@readingplus.com to connect Reading Plus to your Clever account.
 

 
 Recommended Sharing Rules

 Reading Plus recommends sharing only the types of classes that will be using the program. We generally recommend including ELA, English, and Reading classes.

 If you’re using Reading Plus in your elementary schools and don’t have ELA, English, or Reading classes as separate sections in your SIS, please share the elementary school classes you’d like to have access to Reading Plus.

 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error? 

 • A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with Reading Plus' integration with Clever. The user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. 
• A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning. 
 
 Why is my data not showing up in Reading Plus? 

 • Depending on your sync type, Clever either pulls data from your SIS, or data is submitted to Clever from your SIS. That data is then made available to Reading Plus to sync to their system. There is a lag time between these processes, as the sync with Clever and the sync with Reading Plus may not happen at the same time. If you do not see updated data within 48 hours after making a change, please contact Clever Support . 
 
 Why is my student or teacher missing from Reading Plus? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Reading Plus:
 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
 

• If the user is missing from your Clever data, but in your SIS, please contact Clever Support .
• If it’s related to an aspect of Reading Plus, please contact https://www.readingplus.com/customer-support/ 
 
 Support Contacts

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Reading Plus 

 School Year Hours 

 Monday through Friday

 7:30 AM to 7 PM ET

 800-READ-PLUS: 800-732-3758 (option 1)

 https://www.readingplus.com/customer-support/ 

 F: 802-419-4786

title: Clever + Newsela Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Recommended sharing rules 4. Setting up Newsela for school and district leaders 5. Setting up co-teachers in Newsela 6. Users with old Newsela accounts 7. FAQs 

 Overview 

 This article runs through key steps when connecting with Newsela, including any unique aspects to ensure your users are set up for success!

 

 
 

 Setup Process 

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add Newsela by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for Newsela.
• You'll also be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the application through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request .
 

• Once you have made your request, it will be sent to the Newsela team for approval. This process usually takes one business day but may take as long as a week during peak periods.
• Once the request is accepted, Newsela recommends logging in a few users to confirm that they can sign in and import classes.
 
 
 Recommended sharing rules 

 Newsela recommends sharing data by school . You can share data for all your schools - including schools that may not have Newsela PRO licenses yet. Any users not covered by a Newsela PRO license who log in with Clever will be taken to Newsela's free product.

 For more information on sharing data with applications, please see: How do I set my sharing permissions for applications? 

 
 Setting up Newsela for school and district leaders 

 Newsela's Binder feature provides school and district leaders with the ability to aggregate student performance data. School and district leaders will need to be set up in Clever and log into Newsela with Clever to ensure Binder access.

 • Any administrators who need district-wide binder access should be added to Clever in the District Curriculum Lead or District Administrator role. 
• Any school administrators who need school level binder access should be added to Clever in the Staff role. 
 
 
 Setting up co-teachers in Newsela 

 Newsela supports co-teachers and will import co-teachers from Clever. If you are using the Clever integration, Newsela co-teachers have to be set up in Clever by a system administrator and cannot be set up by individual teachers in Newsela. 

 
 Users with old Newsela accounts 

 Many teachers create free Newsela accounts before their district purchases Newsela and are concerned about retaining data associated with their free accounts. 

 Newsela automatically merges user accounts with matching email addresses. If a teacher sets up a free Newsela account with their district email address, the system will recognize and merge their account the first time they log in to Newsela with Clever using that email address. As long as the email addresses match in all systems, users can easily change authentication methods. Users who log in using accounts associated with different email addresses will create duplicate accounts. 

 Newsela's support team can merge accounts if users provide both email addresses. Teachers who need accounts merged should contact Newsela's support team at https://support.newsela.com/hc/en-us 

 
 FAQs 

 Why am I getting a "data warning" or a "data requirement" error? 

 • A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with Newsela's integration with Clever. In this case, the user or section will not appear in the application. Data requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application.
• A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a data compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded experience within the application. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular data warning. 
 
 For more information on data compatibility, please see: What are data warnings and data errors? 

 Why is my student or teacher missing from Newsela? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Newsela:
 • Student or teacher is present in your SIS data
• Student or teacher is present in our Clever data and shared with Newsela
 

• If the user is missing from your Clever data, but in your SIS, please contact Clever Support . 
 
 Why are classes missing from Clever? 

 • Newsela only imports current classes from Clever. If a teacher attempts to sync a Clever class before the term start date, the class will not import.
• Teachers might be missing classes because the classes have either not been imported or because they've been archived. Teachers can resolve this by following these steps:
 • In Newsela, click on the Archived Classes tab.
• Select "Restore" to reactivate the missing classes. If there are no classes in the page, skip to the next step.
• Navigate back to the Classes tab.
• Click "Sync with Clever"
 

• If the class is missing from your Clever data, but in your SIS, please contact Clever Support . 
 
 Who should I ask for help with this issue? 

 • If the issue is related to data within Clever or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support . 
• If the issue is related to functionality within Newsela, please contact Newsela Support .

title: For Clever Admins: Content Mapping
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Getting started 3. Uploading your resource file 4. Assigning your resources 5. Finishing up 6. Editing your assignments 7. FAQ 

 Overview

 Some applications allow you to map resources to specific courses, classes, or users. While administrators often have to work with each application directly to create these mappings, this feature lets you manage content mapping right from your Clever Dashboard. 

 Once you’ve successfully mapped your resources, applications can automatically assign the correct content to your students and teachers. Please ensure the application knows you’re setting this up before you begin! 

 

 
 

 Getting started

 To use content mapping, navigate to the Content assignment tab on the application page in the Clever Dashboard and click Get started . 

 Please note: If you don't see this tab on the application page, the application doesn't support content mapping through Clever. Always check with an application before mapping content to avoid issues. 

 

 
 

 Uploading your resource file

 In order to use this tool, you'll need to create a resources.csv containing information pertinent to the resources you intend to map. 

 File Name: resources.csv 

 
 Field

 
 Required

 
 Format

 
 Description

 
 resource_id Y String The unique identifier of the resource as assigned by the application. 
 title Y String The name of the resource 
 
 

 Please note: The resource_id and title must match with values that the application recognizes. If you aren't sure what to use as the resource_id and title, you will need to reach out to the application provider. 

 

 When you're ready, upload your file in Step 1 of the wizard. 

 

 Download Example resources.csv file 

 

 
 Assigning your resources

 Once you have uploaded your resources.csv file, you can start assigning those resources to your courses in Step 2. 

 To add a course to a resource, click the button on the right side of the page. You can then enter a course name or course number to search for the courses you would like to map. Sections only map if they're associated with a course name or number, so please ensure you associate all sections with a course.

 You can map resources to a course across all schools or to a course at a specific school. Additionally, courses at different schools can map to the same resource. Click the blue link at the end of a mapping rule to see which school it's for. 

 

 

 
 

 Finishing up

 In Step 3 you can review your changes. You can always go back and edit your mappings by selecting Edit assignment rules .

 Otherwise, click Finish and you're all set! The application can now access your content mapping information and assign its resources to the intended users. 

 

 If your mapping is successful, you will see the following message: 

 

 Please note: After these settings are applied, the application will still need to sync with your account in order for you to see those changes. If you don't see any changes, contact the application to have them sync with your account. ✨ Additionally, please ensure you have set sharing rules for the application . If an application requires content mapping, both sharing rules and content assignments must be configured. 

 

 
 Editing your assignments

 To review and edit your content assignments navigate to the Content assignment tab. You'll see a summary of your current resources assigned. You'll also be able to edit your existing rules. Select Edit assignment rules and you will be taken back to Step 2 of the wizard where you can make changes as needed. 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Where can I find resource IDs? 

 • The application creates resource IDs. If you're unsure what to use, reach out to the application provider. 
 
 What if I don't see the Content assignment tab? 

 • If you don't see the Content assignment tab, that means the application doesn't have this feature available through Clever. You'll need to reach out to the application to discuss your options for assigning resources. 
 
 Why can't I see the course I would like to assign resources to? 

 • If you're unable to find the course you're trying to assign a resource to, please be sure that the course is included in the application's sharing permissions. For more on sharing permissions, please refer to this article. 
 
 What do I do if I upload an incorrect resources.csv file? 

 • You can always re-upload a new file! New files overwrite the previously uploaded files. 
 
 Do I have to assign my content this way? 

 • This is up to each application and how they choose to map content in their integration. If you have questions about other content mapping methods, you'll need to reach out to the application.
 
 

 If you have any additional questions please feel free to open a support request and our team will be glad to help further!

title: Clever + LiveSchool Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and LiveSchool: 1. Overview 2. Setup requirements 3. Single sign-on (SSO) 3. Required fields 4. FAQs 5. Support contacts 

 Overview

 This article is a guide for setting up LiveSchool and Clever.

 
 Setup Requirements

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add LiveSchool by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for LiveSchool.
• You will then be brought to a page where you can authorize requesting the applications. When requesting an application, you can select whether or not your district has already purchased the application. When you click "Haven't Purchased" for applications that have free trials, you'll receive a prompt asking if you would like to try a free trial.
• You will also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. Select Launch Now or Today's Date and LiveSchool will filter out your data until it is needed.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request!
 

• Once you’ve set up your Clever sync please reach out to us at support@liveschoolinc.com and let us know the following: 
 • the email address of your Clever manager 
• any yearly requirements to renew your data share 
• the requirements for additional sharing in your district, which may include: 
 • a PO 
• a request via your internal help center (please include links) 
• a vendor request form or DSA 
 

 

 
 
 Single Sign-On (SSO)

 LiveSchool automatically enables SSO for students and teachers on all devices. Those listed as Admin in Clever will not sync to LiveSchool and will not have the SSO option available to them. They will log in with a username and password at liveschoolapp.com 

 
 Required Fields

 
 Student Record

 
 Teacher Record

 
 
 • sis_id
• name
• school
• schools
• district
• state_id
• grade
• location
• student_number
• credentials
• email
 
 
 • district
• name
• school
• schools
• sis_id
• credentials
• email
• state_id
• teacher_number
• title
 
 
 
 

 
 

 FAQs 

 Why am I getting a "data warning" or a "data requirement" error? 

 • A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with LiveSchool’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in
• A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular data warning. 
 
 Why is my student or teacher missing from LiveSchool? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to LiveSchool:
 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
 

 
 School Administrators in Clever as Staff or Admin will not sync to LiveSchool. Reach out to support@liveschool.com for additional support. 

 

 Help! My student can't login to LiveSchool through Clever. 

 • Please reach out to your LiveSchool Support at support@liveschoolinc.com if your user is running into issues accessing LiveSchool once they are in their Clever Portal.
 
 

 Help! No one can login to LiveSchool through Clever! 

 • Please reach out to LiveSchool Support at support@liveschoolinc.com if your user is running into issues accessing LiveSchool once they are in their Clever Portal.
 
 

 Who should I ask for help with this issue? 

 • If the issue is related to content in LiveSchool or the LiveSchool application specifically, please reach out to LiveSchool Support .
 
 If not, please reach out to your SIS or to Clever Support respectively.

 Support contacts

 Clever Support 

 LiveSchool Support

title: Clever + Lexia: Contacting Support
text:
Lexia Support is happy to help districts using Clever who have any trouble or questions about Lexia.

 Contacting Support

 District staff can contact Lexia or Clever Support when they need help. 

 • If the issue is related to data within Clever, or data being sent to Lexia Learning from Clever, please reach out to Clever Support. 
• If the issue is related to a feature within a Lexia Learning program, please reach out to Lexia Support. 
• If Clever Support is contacted first the Clever team will send the inquiry to Lexia's support email address. In order to provide the quickest resolution possible, Clever's support ticket and Lexia's support ticket are then tied together and all replies are tracked by both support teams.
• Clever will then assign the ticket to a Clever Support agent that works closely with the Lexia integration, this facilitates a quick response time.
 

 Advanced Troubleshooting

 All Lexia Support team members have access to a Clever data dashboard in order to assist districts in resolving Clever related issues. If an issue is complex, the Lexia support ticket is immediately escalated to a senior Lexia Support team member for assistance.

 Collaboration

 In addition to as-needed support, Lexia and Clever have regular meetings to discuss the best ways to support districts. We have a mutual goal of assisting districts as quickly and efficiently as possible to minimize disruption to teachers and students.

title: Clever + Imagine Learning Classroom Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and Imagine Learning Classroom: 1. Setup Process 2. Recommended Sharing Rules 3. Data Rules 4. FAQs 5. Support Contacts 

 Note: LearnZillion has become Imagine Learning Classroom 

 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup 
• If your district is already on Clever, please reach out to your Imagine Learning Classroom account manager to let them know you’d like to connect with them on Clever:
 • If your district is not sure who your Imagine Learning Classroom representative is, then you can email support@learnzillion.com for assistance.
• Once in contact, Imagine Learning Classroom will send an invitation to connect with your district via Clever.
• To approve the invitation, a district administrator with the ‘Clever Admin’ role will need to log into their Clever Dashboard to review and accept the invite.
 
 
• Imagine Learning Classroom supports rostering and single sign-on (SSO) through Clever for student, teacher, and staff user types.
• To help curb user login issues, Imagine Learning Classroom recommends customizing the Clever Portal login page to include password hints and a district tech support contact to make it easier for SSO to be possible. More information on how to do this can be found here: How do I set up Single Sign-On? 
• Users will be able to utilize Clever SSO for logins, as well as continue to log in manually on the Imagine Learning Classroom site. Please refer to the instructions sent directly by your Imagine Learning Classroom representative for information on how to designate your Imagine Learning Classroom account password for manual logins.
 

 Recommended Sharing Rules

 Imagine Learning Classroom recommends creating a custom share by students rule for only the specific grade levels and school sites that have purchased licenses for Imagine Learning Classroom at your district.

 Data Rules

 • All teachers must have email addresses associated with their accounts. Users with no email address will not get synced over to Imagine Learning Classroom.
• The Clever "U sername " and/or the " student_number " field should be shared with the Imagine Learning Classroom application.
 • If the "U sername " field is shared with Imagine Learning Classroom, then this field will be used to generate the student usernames on Imagine Learning Classroom.
• If the " student_number " field is shared with Imagine Learning Classroom and the " Username " is NOT shared, then the " student_number ” field will be used to generate student usernames on Imagine Learning Classroom.
• If both " Username " and the " student_number " fields are shared with Imagine Learning Classroom, then Imagine Learning Classroom will default to using the " Username " for generating student usernames on Imagine Learning Classroom.
 
 
 

 More information on the exact data fields requested by Imagine Learning Classroom, as well as how Clever user roles are mapped to their program, can be found here: Clever Roster Sync and Instant Login. 

 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error?

 A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with Imagine Learning Classroom’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application.

 A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting Imagine Learning Classroom directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning.

 Why is my data not showing up in Imagine Learning Classroom?

 Depending on your sync type, Clever either pulls data from your SIS, or data is submitted to Clever from your SIS. That data is then made available to Imagine Learning Classroom to sync to their system. There is a lag time between these processes, as the sync with Clever and the sync with Imagine Learning Classroom may not happen at the same time. If you do not see updated data in Imagine Learning Classroom within 48 hours after making a change, please contact Clever Support .

 Why is my student or teacher missing from Imagine Learning Classroom?

 Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Imagine Learning Classroom:

 • User is in your SIS data
• User is in the Clever data browser 
• Teachers have email addresses associated with their Clever accounts
• The user’s email address is not associated with multiple Clever accounts (e.g. multiple teacher accounts or a teacher that also has a staff account)
• If the user is in your SIS, but missing from the Clever data browser, please contact Clever Support .
 

 Who should I ask for help with an issue?

 If it’s related to data within Clever, application sharing rules, or data coming from the SIS, please contact Clever Support . 

 If it’s related to an aspect of the Imagine Learning Classroom program such as questions regarding licenses or contracts, launch dates, or program content (e.g. lessons, assignments, reports), then please contact Imagine Learning Classroom Support .

 Support Contacts

 Submit a ticket through Imagine Learning Classroom Help Portal . Email: support@learnzillion.com Answers to FAQs can be found in Imagine Learning Classroom’s Help Portal.

title: Clever + Schoology Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever SSO via SAML 3. Clever SSO via Saved Passwords 

 Overview 

 Districts can easily set up single sign-on (SSO) for Schoology, providing a seamless login experience for every user. 

 There are two options for enabling SSO to Schoology from Clever: 

 • Clever SSO via SAML (recommended) 
• Clever SSO via Saved Passwords 
 
 Important notes about Clever SSO via SAML: 

 Login pathways If you choose Clever as your SAML Identity Provider (IDP) for Schoology, users will not be able to use another third-party IDP (e.g. Google, ADFS) to log into Schoology. Users can continue to log in to Schoology via app.schoology.com with their Schoology-managed credentials. If your district uses another third-party IDP to log into Schoology and would like to retain that login path, please see our Clever SSO via Saved Passwords setup guide . 

 iOS - Login flow All authentication methods, including Clever Badges, are compatible with both iOS login flows.

 • Log in directly via the Schoology app. If you click the app and search for your district, you’ll be redirected to the Clever login flow. Once you log in, you’ll be redirected to the Schoology app. 
• Log in via the Clever app (or at https://clever.com in a web browser). The user will log in to Clever and click on the Schoology icon. The user will be logged in to Schoology in the web browser . 
 
 Clever SSO via SAML

 Pre-work:

 The first step is to ensure your district users have accounts in Schoology. Please work with Schoology to ensure Schoology accounts are created prior to setting up SSO via SAML in Clever. Clever does not have a rostering integration with Schoology 

 Setup:

 In Clever: 

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and add the Schoology application via this link: https://schools.clever.com/applications/add/saml-schoology 
• Share the users that need SSO access to Schoology. 
• In your Clever Dashboard, click the Schoology app, click on Settings, and scroll to SAML Details. Click Edit. 
• Enter your Schoology domain (e.g. clever.schoology.com ). Do not include "https:// " or anything after ".com" 
• (Optional) Matching attributes will default to email for all users. If you’d like to use a different attribute to match users between Clever and Schoology, you can edit them on the Settings page as well. 
• Click Confirm. 
• Under SAML Details copy your metadata URL: 
 In Schoology: 

• Log into your administrator account and navigate to Tools > School Management > Integration: https://clever.schoology.com/system_settings/integration 
• Go to the Authentication tab and click Cloud /On-Premise Directory. 
• Paste the metadata URL from Clever in the Metadata URL field. 
• Click Fill fields below from metadata. 
• (Optional) "Match ID to Schoology Account Using field" should default to email. If you changed the matching attribute within Clever in Step 5, please choose the correct corresponding attribute here. 
• Click Save. 
 
 Once you’ve completed the above steps, you should be good to go! Make sure to test the Schoology app for each user type (student, teacher, etc.) and on different devices before rolling out to your users. If you encounter any problems, please contact Clever Support . 

 

 Clever SSO via Saved Passwords

 1. District user accounts and credentials for the app:

 • 
 • The first step is to ensure your district users have accounts and login credentials for the SSO (Saved Passwords) application. Please work with Schoology to ensure Schoology accounts are created and login credentials are configured. This must be done prior to setting up SSO in Clever. 
 

 
 2. Device setup

 • 
 • Install the Clever Chrome extension on all non-iOS devices that will be used to access the application through Clever. This includes home devices for users who will be accessing this app from home. The Clever browser extension facilitates single sign-on for SSO (Saved Passwords) apps. Please visit this article for more information. 
• The Clever browser extension is not required for iOS devices (e.g. iPads, iPhones) as these devices use the Clever app which acts as the browser extension. Please visit this article for more information. 
 

 
 3. Click here to add the Schoology app. 

 4. Select Request application. 

 5. On the next screen, select which schools you would like to have access to Schoology. Select Share. 

 6. Select Finish. 

 The base setup is now complete! Please see below for more information on the user experience and pre-populating user credentials. 

 User Experience (SSO via Saved Passwords)

 When users first click on Schoology in the Clever Portal, they will be asked for their Schoology username and password. Clever will save this username and password for future use and enable one-click access. 

 If you would like to pre-populate credentials for users, you can navigate to this page: https://schools.clever.com/applications/ilpro-schoology/settings and follow the directions listed here: SSO (Saved Passwords) Overview and Setup

title: Clever + BFW Achieve Setup Guide
text: A detailed step-by-step guide for setting up the BFW Achieve app in Clever is provided on the BFW Publishers support site . Please visit that article and support site if you need any assistance with connecting BFW Publishers apps to Clever.

title: Clever + Keyboarding Without Tears Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and Keyboarding Without Tears: 1. Setup Process 2. Recommended Sharing Rules 3. FAQs 4. Support Contacts 

 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add Keyboarding Without Tears by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for Keyboarding Without Tears. 
• Authorize the request. When requesting an application, you can select whether or not your district has already purchased the application. When you click "Haven't Purchased" for applications that have free trials, you'll receive a prompt asking if you would like a free trial.
• You will also have the option to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request .
 

• If your district is adding Keyboarding Without Tears for the first time:
 • Confirm you have sent a purchase order to KWT. If you haven’t received a response to the purchase order within 48 hours, please contact technology@lwtears.com 
 

• If your district has used Keyboarding Without Tears in the past:
 • Send an email to technology@lwtears.com once you’ve shared data in Clever to confirm your order
• Confirm your roster data does not exceed your grade level license data
 

 
 Recommended Sharing Rules

 It’s important to only share the one section that is using Keyboarding Without Tears in the classroom. The simplest way to do so, is to “Share by Rules” in Clever. You can create a sharing rule by following these steps: 

 • Access the Sharing Permissions page for Keyboarding Without Tears. To do this, log in to your Clever account and click the icon for the name of the app in the sidebar. From the 'Data Sharing' tab, click Share by Rules
• Select the type of rule you want to create. You can build off one of our pre-existing templates or create your own. We recommend choosing “Custom share by section”, as it will allow you to create your own rule that can apply specifically to classes using Keyboarding Without Tears
• Create your rule. You can share classes with certain course names or numbers, grades, subjects or schools
 
 We also recommend using this Help Center article when setting up custom sharing rules. 

 If you need any further assistance setting up sharing rules please contact Clever Support .

 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error? 

 • A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with Keyboarding Without Tears’ integration with Clever. The user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. 
• A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting Keyboarding Without Tears directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning. 
 
 How do I manually add a user or a co-teacher? 

 • You can manually add users as staff members, through a CSV upload to Clever through your District Dashboard, or by creating a custom staff account. For more information on adding staff users to Clever, check out the following Help Center article: How do I add staff and school administrators in Clever? 
• You can also add co-teachers to Clever either through your sync, or manually within the district dashboard. Find more information on how to do this here: How do I sync co-teachers? 
• If your district is using a Clever-managed sync , such as PowerSchool or Illuminate, you can see in the chart below if your SIS supports syncing co-teachers to Clever: 
 
 
 Supports Co-Teachers in Clever 

 
 Does Not Support Co-Teachers in Clever 

 
 
 Aeries Auto Sync 

 
 Powerschool Auto Sync 

 
 
 Illuminate Auto Sync 

 
 SchoolInsight Auto-Sync 

 
 
 Infinite Campus Auto Sync 

 
 
 Infinite Campus API Sync 

 
 
 Skyward Auto Sync 

 
 
 Skyward API Sync 

 
 
 I have teachers and students at more than one school site. Can they still connect to KWT through Clever? 

 • Keyboarding Without Tears doesn’t accept students or teachers that are associated with more than one school. You can change this in your SIS or you can modify the sharing rules to exclude the student or teachers’ secondary associations. Please contact Clever Support for help setting this up. 
 
 Why is my student or teacher missing from Keyboarding Without Tears? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Keyboarding Without Tears:
 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
 

• Also, make sure that the student or teacher doesn’t have any dual enrollments.
 
 Support Contacts

 Keyboarding Without Tears Support

title: Clever + Hapara Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and Hapara: 1. Setup Process 2. Required Fields 3. FAQs 

 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup. 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add Hapara by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for Hapara.
• You'll then be brought to a page where you can authorize requesting the applications. When requesting an application, you can select whether or not your district has already purchased the application. When you click "Haven't Purchased", we'll notify Hapara of your interest . You'll also be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request! You will receive a response within 1 business day (usually sooner). Initial integration requires a short session with Hapara Implementation to make sure you are aware of your options.
• If you already have Hapara up and running for the school year when you request Hapara through Clever, your current classes will need to be archived or deleted before the new Clever sync . This can be done over a weekend or holiday.
• After your initial integration, your class and student data will automatically sync to Hapara nightly.
 

 
 Required Fields

 In order for Hapara to integrate your data, your Teacher Google IDs and Student Google IDs must be synced with Clever. These Google IDs take the form of email addresses and should be synced to the teacher or student email fields in Clever. There are a couple of ways you can sync this data to Clever: 

 • If this data is already in your SIS, you can set these fields to sync automatically.
• If this data is not in your SIS, you can create a CSV file and manually sync the emails to Clever. You can find out more information about this option in this Help Center article:
 
 How do I upload emails not in my SIS? 

 If you would like to use this option, please reach out to Clever Support to enable the upload for your district!

 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error?

 A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with Hapara’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. 

 A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning. 

 Why is my student or teacher missing from Hapara?

 Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Hapara:

 • Student or teacher is in your SIS data
• Student or teacher is in your Clever data
• Student or teacher ID in your SIS matches their Google ID
 
 Who should I ask for help with this issue?

 
 
 
 • If it’s related to data within Clever, or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support .
• If the data is in Clever but not integrating into Hapara, please contact Hapara Implementation .

title: Clever + GradeCam Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and GradeCam: 1. Setup Process 2. Required Fields 3. FAQs 4. Support Contacts 

 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has't been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup. 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add GradeCam by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for GradeCam
• You'll then be brought to a page where you can request the application. When requesting an application, select whether or not your district has already purchased the application. When you click "Haven't Purchased" for applications that have free trials, you'll receive a prompt asking if you'd like to try a free trial.
• You'll also be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• Once you've made your selections, click Request!
 

• GradeCam supports rostering and single sign-on (SSO) for teacher users. Upon first SSO login through Clever, GradeCam will prompt teachers to either enter existing GradeCam login credentials or create new ones, allowing SSO ongoing. (SSO through Clever isn't required by GradeCam as teachers can log in directly at app.gradecam.com.)
• Once districts have shared data with GradeCam, the GradeCam team will set up the initial sync - they’ll reach out to let you know when that’s finished!
 
 

 
 Required Fields

 GradeCam requires Term Name for sections, and they would prefer to have Term Start and Term End dates as well. Depending on your SIS, setting this up can be a little tricky, so here are some general guidelines:

 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs: 

 For our Aeries, Infinite Campus API, PowerSchool, Illuminate, Skyward API and Skyward Auto Syncs , Clever pulls these fields by default. If the default field Clever is using isn’t the best for your district, you can set up a custom mapping by following the instructions in this Help Center article: How do I customize my auto-sync? 

 If you’re having trouble setting up the custom mapping, please reach out to Clever Support ! 

 For our Infinite Campus Auto Sync , mapping the term dates from the SIS can pull dates for the full year, which doesn't allow the classes to show correctly in GradeCam. For Infinite Campus Auto Sync districts, please make sure that each term shows the correct dates. Ex: a Q1 term may have a start date of 8/1/2021 and an end date of 10/15/2021 but it should NOT have a start date of 8/1/2021 and an end date of 7/1/2022 (full year). Please check your data browser to see if dates have synced correctly and reach out to Clever Support with questions.

 SIS-Managed Auto Syncs: 

 Please reach out to your SIS vendor and let them know that you'd like to have Term Name, Term Start, and Term End dates added to your sync.

 A full list of Clever-managed and SIS-managed auto syncs can be found here: Which SISs support an auto sync? 

 SFTP Syncs: 

 Your district will need to add the Term_name, Term_start, and Term_end fields to your Sections CSV file. You can use this Help Center article to help you format your file: SFTP: How do I format my CSV files? 

 
 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error? 

 • A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with GradeCam's integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application.
• A data warning error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning.
• For an in-depth look into any existing GradeCam data warnings or errors, please refer to the Data Quality page of the Clever dashboard. 
 
 Why is a teacher missing from GradeCam? 

 • Make sure that the teacher has sections in Clever - GradeCam won't import teachers without section assignments. You can check this by searching for the teacher in the Data browser .
• GradeCam only supports primary teachers. If a section has co-teachers, they won't be synced to GradeCam.
 
 

 
 Support Contacts

 • Clever Support 
• GradeCam Support

title: Clever + EVERFI Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and EVERFI: 1. Setup process 2. Required fields 3. FAQ s 4. Support contacts 

 Setup process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add EVERFI by following these instructions:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for EVERFI and click Request app .
• You'll then be brought to a page where you can authorize your EVERFI request. As EVERFI is provided at no cost to the district, you can mark that it has already been purchased for the district.
• You'll also be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request application. 
 

 
 Once you have shared data with EVERFI, the EVERFI team will set up the initial sync and will reach out to you to discuss communicating with teachers. 

 Required fields

 EVERFI recommends that you share all data for your district. The simplest way to do so is to select “Share All Data” in Clever. If you plan to share specific schools, (you can find more info about “Share by Rules” here ). You can create a sharing rule by following these steps:

 • Access the Sharing Permissions page for EVERFI. To do this, log in to your Clever account and click the icon for EVERFI in the sidebar. From the 'Data Sharing' tab, click Share by Rules .
 
 Description of Data to be provided to EVERFI through Clever:

 • Teachers: First Name, Last Name, Email, Subject, School, Section, Grade, Clever ID, Sections 
• Students: First Name, Last Name or Last Initial, Email (optional), School, Birthdate, Clever ID 
 
 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error? 

 • A data requirement ​error occurs when the record is incompatible with EVERFI’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. 
• A data warning ​error occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning. 
 
 Why is a student or teacher missing from EVERFI? Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to EVERFI: 

 • In your SIS data 
• In your shared Clever data with EVERFI
 
 What if a teacher (and/or student) already has an EVERFI account outside of Clever? When existing EVERFI users (both teachers and students) login through Clever, they will be prompted to type in their previous EVERFI username and password. Once users merge their old account with the new account, all past progress will show up on their new account. Can teachers and students be assigned to multiple school locations? Yes, EVERFI does allow teachers and students to be assigned to multiple school locations. However, all schools must be shared in order for the sync to work accurately. Can multiple teachers/staff be assigned to the same course/section? EVERFI does not currently support multiple teachers or staff to be assigned to the same course/section. How are building administrators provided access to the system and to building level location? Admin can log in via Clever to access the EVERFI courses and resources. Admin will have the same access any teacher has and be able to create courses. EVERFI does not currently have an Admin role to oversee the entire school/district. Who should I ask for help with an issue? 

 If it’s related to data within Clever, or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support .

 Support contacts

 
 
 
 EVERFI Team

 Alice Wagner, Vice President and Dakota Rubin, Operations

 clever@everfi.com

title: Clever + DreamBox Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and DreamBox: 1. Setup Process 2. Recommended Sharing Rules 3. FAQs 4. Support Contacts 

 Setup Process 

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://www.clever.com/district-signup 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add DreamBox by following these instructions:
 • Log in to your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for your DreamBox product – DreamBox Math, DreamBox Reading Park, and/or DreamBox Reading Plus.
• You will then be brought to a page where you can authorize requesting the applications. 
• You will also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the app through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request!
• Once your request has been approved by DreamBox, please contact DreamBox Support at https://www.dreambox.com/support to initiate the sync between your data in Clever and DreamBox and discuss communicating with teachers.
 

 
 Recommended Sharing Rules 

 DreamBox recommends that you only share schools, classrooms, educators, and students who should have access to each product. DreamBox recommends sharing the following subjects for each product: 

 • DreamBox Math: Homeroom/Advisory, Math, Technology, Engineering, and Other 
• DreamBox Reading Plus and Reading Park: Homeroom/Advisory, English/Language Arts 
 
 FAQs 

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data requirement” error? 

 A data requirement error occurs when the record is incompatible with DreamBox’s integration with Clever, and the user or section will not appear in the application. Data Requirements must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. 

 A data warning occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular Data Warning. 

 Why is my data not showing up in DreamBox? 

 Depending on your sync type, Clever either pulls data from your SIS, or data is submitted to Clever from your SIS. That data is then made available to DreamBox to sync to their system. There is a lag time between these processes, as the sync with Clever and the sync with DreamBox may not happen at the same time. If you do not see updated data within 48 hours after making a change, please contact Clever Support . 

 Why is my student or teacher missing from DreamBox Learning? 

 Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to DreamBox Learning:

 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
 
 Support Contacts 

 Who should I ask for help with this issue?
