 If it’s related to data within Clever, or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support. 

 

 If it’s related to an aspect of the DreamBox, please contact DreamBox Support .

title: Clever + Canvas Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever SSO via SAML 3. Clever SSO + Rostering 

 Overview 

 Clever has partnered with Canvas to create a seamless way to provide account creation, as well as Single Sign-On (SSO) access for users. There are two options for enabling SSO to Canvas from Clever: 

 • Clever SSO via SAML 
• Canvas SSO + rostering 
 
 If you are already using Kimono/grade passback and only want to use Clever for SSO, you can do so by using the first option, Clever SSO via SAML . Choosing this method will add a new SSO pathway to access Canvas from Clever while preserving your current rostering and grade passback setup. If your district intends to use Clever for rostering and single sign-on purposes using the Canvas SSO + Rostering application , please note the following cautions: 

 • Your district will be prevented from using Kimono, Canvas’ grade passback tool. 
• Your district's users will only be able to log in to Canvas through Clever. Users will no longer be able to log in to Canvas directly without being redirected to Clever to facilitate the login. (i.e. no hybrid logins). 
 
 For directions on how to log in to Clever from Canvas , please see the Help Center article Single Sign-On (SSO): "Log in with Canvas" 

 Setup Directions 

 Clever SSO via SAML 

 1. Add the Canvas SSO (SAML) application to your Clever Dashboard. 

 a. From the left menu bar, navigate to Applications > Add Applications and request the Canvas app . Select the option that says SSO (SAML). 

 

 d. Request the app and set your data sharing permissions. 

 2. Configure Clever SSO via SAML for Canvas in your Clever Dashboard. 

 a. In your Clever Dashboard, click the Canvas app, click on Settings , and scroll to SAML Details . Click Edit . b. Set your district's Canvas domain (e.g. clever.instructure.com ). Do not include https:// or any other value after .com . c. (Optional) If you would like to match users on a field other than email address, you can configure the matching attributes on a per-user basis from the settings page. d. Copy the Metadata URL which you’ll enter in your Canvas admin account. 

 3. Complete configuration in your Canvas administrator account. 

 a. Log in to your Canvas administrator account and navigate to Admin > Authentication. Click on the Choose an Authentication drop-down, then select SAML. Do not choose Clever. b. Paste the metadata URL you copied from Clever in the IdP Metadata URI field. c. Optional: Add your portal URL in the “Log Out URL” field. d. Click Save . e. The page will reload and automatically fill the SAML values. f. Test the setup by opening a new incognito window. Go to .instructure.com/login/saml">https://<YOURDOMAIN>.instructure.com/login/saml . You should be prompted to enter your Clever SSO credentials. You will then be logged in and redirected to Canvas. 

 
 
 
 
 Please note: Canvas will not automatically roster user accounts from successful SSO logins. Your district needs to work directly with Canvas to roster users through SIS or manual import options. 

 Clever SSO + Rostering

 To set up SSO for Canvas, please follow the directions below: 

 • Email your Canvas contact and let them know you intend to use SSO via Clever so Canvas can enable this on their side. 
• Navigate to Applications > Add Applications and request the Canvas app . Select the option that says SSO, Rostering . 
• Share Canvas with all intended users. 
• In Canvas, navigate to Admin > Authentication , then click Add an identity provider to this account and add Clever. There, you can enter your Clever District ID, choose a Login Attribute, and click Save. Your district ID can be found in the top-left of the Data Browser . 
• Confirm that users can log in to your district's instance URL . Please navigate to https://<your domain>. instructure.com/login/clever and confirm that a student and teacher can log in to Canvas using Clever. 
• Once these two steps are completed, reach out to Clever Support , and we can enable the Canvas icon on your Clever portal. 
 
 If you have any questions about setting up Clever SSO, please contact Clever Support. We are happy to help!

title: Clever + Amazon Business Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and Amazon Business: 1. Connect Amazon Business to your Clever account 2. Set up Clever SSO via SAML 3. Support Contacts 

 Connect Amazon Business to your Clever account

 1. If your district is new to Clever , create a Clever account at clever.com/signup . If you have a Clever account , log into your Clever Dashboard at schools.clever.com. 

 2. Add the ‘Amazon Business’ application to your Clever Dashboard: 

 a. On the left menu bar, click Applications > Add Applications b. Search for Amazon Business . c. Click Request App d. Enter a Launch Date (the first day users can access Amazon Business via Clever) and set your data sharing permissions. 

 3. Amazon Business will review your request and reach out to help configure your setup. 

 
 Set up Clever SSO via SAML

 Amazon Business utilizes a secure technology called SAML for SSO. It requires a couple of additional setup steps. If you have not yet heard from the Amazon Business team and would like assistance with the steps listed below, please contact Clever-SSO@amazon.com . 

 1. In your Clever Dashboard, click the Amazon Business app, click on ' Settings ', and scroll to ‘SAML Details’. Copy the ‘Metadata URL,’ which you’ll enter in your Amazon Business admin account. 

 a. Copy the Metadata URL field and open that address in a new tab. b. Right-click the page and save the Metadata file. 

 2. Log into your Amazon Business administrator account and navigate to the ‘Business Settings’ page 

 a. Select Clever as your identity provider (IDP). b. Select the default group and default buying role for new SSO users. c. Enter the Clever Metadata URL, which you found under ‘SAML Details’ in Clever. d. Select the attribute mappings listed in the table below: 

 

 e. Click Publish in the Status section f. Copy the SP Initiated Login URL, which you’ll need to enter in Clever 

 3. In your Clever Dashboard, click the Amazon Business app and scroll to ‘SAML Details’ 

 a. Enter the ‘SP Initiated Login URL’ (from your AB account) in the ‘Login URL’ field b. If necessary, update the default ‘attribute’ field for each user type. *Please note that the default attribute fields will likely be correct, so we recommend leaving them in place unless instructed otherwise.* 

 
 

 Questions? 

 Amazon Business : Email Clever-SSO@amazon.com 

 • Creating an Amazon Business account
• Setting up Clever SSO via SAML
• Administering your Amazon Business account
 
 Clever : Submit a ticket with Clever Support 

 • Creating a Clever account 
• Adding the Amazon Business application 
• Setting sharing permissions

title: Clever + ANet Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. New Clever districts 3. Existing Clever districts 4. Initial setup process 5. Recommended sharing rules 6. Teacher emails 7. How to set up single sign-on 8. Additional considerations • Creating Custom Sections • Term Changes • Maintaing Student Rostering Information 9. Who to contact for support 

 

 Overview

 This article is a guide to help connect the ANet learning application. 

 
 New Clever Districts

 • If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup or accept your invite to set up with Clever by clicking the Set up with Clever link in your email invitation from ANet.
• Set your password and log in to the Clever Dashboard (your username is your email address).
• Connect your student information system (SIS) data to Clever by going to Data sources > SIS sync and choosing your SIS from the dropdown menu. The instructions to connect your SIS data will appear once you’ve selected your SIS and will vary depending on your sync type. Check out this article for more information: Which sync option should I choose? 
 
 
 Existing Clever Districts

 If your district already uses Clever, connecting to ANet can take only a few clicks! These are the steps you can expect:

 • Accept ANet’s invite to connect or request to connect with ANet.
• Accept ANet's sharing suggestions or share the applicable roster data.
 
 
 Initial Setup Process

 Here are your steps to get started: 1. Connect with the ANet application by either accepting our invite or requesting our application.

 2. Share your data with ANet.

 Now it’s time to set your sharing permissions for the ANet application. If you accepted our “recommended sharing” you’re probably already done or nearly there! If you choose to decline ANet’s “recommended sharing” read this article to learn more about setting sharing permissions. More information about sharing permissions can be found below. Please note, you can always update data sharing at any time! 

 3.Review your data Review your ANet overview page to see what data you’ve approved to share. For an even deeper look at your data in Clever, review the data in the Data browser or simply search for a record in the “Search” box, located on the top of your Dashboard screen. If you need to make any updates to the data you are sharing with ANet, use the Edit sharing link on the ANet Overview page.

 4. Check your launch date 

 Once you’ve confirmed the right data is being shared, make sure that students can start logging into ANet at the right time by checking your launch date. 

 Your launch date is the day that the ANet icon (pictured below) will appear in the Clever Portal for students to login. If you need the launch date updated, please request that action from ANet Support. For more on launch dates, see this article .

 

 
 Recommended sharing rules

 ANet recommends you share your data by rules. This ensures Clever is only sharing the data that ANet needs, and additional data isn’t being shared unnecessarily. If you are accepting ANet’s invitation to connect, we will already have suggested sharing rules set up through the invitation. Accepting ANet’s invitation is the recommended process . If you are requesting to sync with ANet, you'll need to set up your own sharing rules. Read this article to learn more about how to Share by Rules or follow the guidance below:

 There are a number of 'Fields' districts can use to either include or exclude data with the application sharing rules. The fields available are dependent on the type of sharing rule you selected (e.g. share by teacher, share by section, share by student) 

 • The modifiers available are:
 • equals any of
• does not equal any of
• contains text
• does not contain text
• starts with text
 

 
 You should only share the classes that will be administering ANet assessments (subjects and grade levels). This is typically limited to only classes that are labeled ELA and/or Math in your SIS, and grades 2 through 12.

 

 If your special education or gifted classes are listed as something other than ELA and/or Math, and are administering ANet assessments, you will want to share these classes as well. 

 
 Teacher Emails

 Please note, ANet cannot process rostering information for classes whose teacher’s do not have school email addresses in Clever. Please confirm all teachers have unique school emails when sharing your rostering information with ANet.

 If you have any teachers without email addresses, these will appear in your data warnings, illustrated below. 

 

 

 
 How to set up single sign-on

 Students can use Clever SSO to log into their ANet application. To set up student SSO, please view this resource . We recommend testing student SSO prior to their ANet administration to limit unforeseen challenges. At this time the ANet application does not support Clever SSO for teachers. 

 
 Additional considerations

 Creating Custom Sections

 Clever Custom Sections allow district administrators to create and manage sections manually within the Clever Dashboard. Once custom sections are created in Clever, administrators can share them with ANet through the Clever sharing permissions interface. You can read more about Clever custom sections here . 

 A few examples for when you’d want to create Custom Sections are: 

 • <High School Only> One class period contains multiple courses. ANet can only assign one course per period, so if students take different courses in the same class, you will need to create Clever Custom Sections for each course within the period
• If you want to view your data differently than is currently coming through Clever (e.g. view data by homeroom as opposed to ELA and/or Math class)
• If there are students who do not need access to ANet products (assessments or quiz tool).
 
 Term Changes

 In your SIS, some sections may be assigned to a specific term. Students in a given section will have an active enrollment in Clever during the dates of that term. When a term ends, students in those sections no longer have an active enrollment. When this occurs, a student may not have access to ANet through Clever for 1-2 business days while the ANet team shifts the student from their old section to their new section. Please reach out to ANet Support in advance to coordinate logistics. 

 This resource provides additional information about terms. If you have any questions or concerns about the ability for a student to view their ANet interim during a term rollover, please contact ANet Support so we can strategize with your district. 

 Maintaining Student Rostering Information

 Throughout the year, ANet will automatically receive any student or enrollment changes that are made in your SIS through Clever. Please ensure that your Student Information System is up-to-date so students are properly enrolled at the time of their administration. You should expect these changes to be reflected in ANet within 48 hours of the update in your Student Information System. If you have questions about maintaining your student roster, reach out to ANet Support .

 

 
 Who to contact for support

 • If the issue is related to data within Clever or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support .
• If the issue is related to functionality within ANet, such as assessment availability, please contact ANet Support .

title: Clever + McGraw Hill Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup Process 3. Recommended sharing rules 4. Data requirements 5. Managing licenses & assigning content 6. Where can I go for support? 

 

 Overview

 McGraw Hill and Clever’s expanded partnership makes it easier than ever before for students to access digital learning tools. The integration supports account provisioning and access to any programs hosted on the ConnectED or Open Learning Platform. This guide contains important information about the setup process including integration requirements. 

 IMPORTANT: Please read before proceeding with the setup process Communication: Setting up a Clever integration requires a conversation with McGraw Hill Digital Integration Services. Make sure to schedule a project call with McGraw Hill. Switching rostering methods: If you’re moving to Clever from self-registered accounts, please be ready to work with McGraw Hill on a user clean-up process to delete old data (students & possibly teachers). If you’re switching from a different method, McGraw Hill may have to re-match your historic customer data with your shared Clever data to retain historical data. Data readiness: Your district should be sending Clever term and course data. McGraw Hill requires term data in order to create courses, classes, users, etc. Not having this will prevent your district from proceeding. Stable course data will allow your district to more easily assign digital content (e.g. so classes receive the correct textbooks) prior to launch. Interoperability: If you plan on having users access McGraw Hill from platforms outside of Clever (e.g. Canvas), please be prepared to send Clever the same unique user ID you use to identify those users on other platforms (e.g., SIS ID) in your SIS sync.

 

 
 Setup Process

 1. Make sure your district is set up with Clever. If your district does not yet have a Clever account, you can get started by navigating to https://clever.com/signup . 

 

 2. If your district is already on Clever, you can add McGraw Hill by following these steps: 

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation. 
• Search for McGraw Hill 
• When you request a connection with the application, you will also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the application through Clever. 
• Once you have made your selections, click Request.
 
 3. These requests will create a ticket with the McGraw Hill Digital Integration Services team, who will schedule an implementation call with you. The timing of these requests will be based on McGraw Hill’s current volume and having a conversation with McGraw Hill to develop your project plan.

 • McGraw Hill integration services must be configured at the Clever district/account level and include all users of your McGraw Hill K-12 content currently hosted on our ConnectED and Open Learning platforms. McGraw Hill Digital Integration Services may discuss the details of your full Clever integration on a scheduled implementation call. ​​​​​​
 
 4. FYI: If you are already discussing this integration with your McGraw Hill representative, you may receive an invitation to connect directly in your Clever Dashboard. If so, please check your Clever Dashboard to accept your app invite . 

 

 
 Recommended sharing rules 

 McGraw Hill recommends sharing only the classes and users at the school sites that will be using the program. This can include grades K-12 and subjects ranging from english/language arts, math, science, social studies, language, interventions/online learning, science, and arts and music. Please note: Data sharing permissions will vary according to your district’s purchasing agreement.

 You can find more information about setting up sharing rules in this guide . 

 
 Data requirements

 The following fields must be successfully synced to Clever in order for your data to successfully sync with McGraw Hill: 

 • Term names, start dates, and end dates 
• Course names 
• Teacher, student, and staff usernames 
• Teacher email addresses 
• Student email (recommended but not required) 
 
 Please note that user IDs (sis_ids) must be globally unique across your district. In other words, a teacher and a student cannot have the same sis_id in Clever.

 You can check if your data meets the requirements by navigating to the McGraw Hill > Data Quality tab in your Clever Dashboard. 

 In Clever, data quality issues will be represented as “data warnings” or “data errors”. For more information on resolving data quality issues, please review this article . 

 IMPORTANT: The data requirements outlined above are hard requirements for setting up rostering and SSO successfully with Clever and McGraw Hill. Failure to fulfill these requirements means McGraw Hill will not be able to create courses, classes, and users for your district. If you are not sending some of these required fields to Clever or are experiencing issues with data quality issues, please work with Clever Support to troubleshoot. 

 

 
 Managing licenses & assigning content

 Once you have resolved all of the data quality issues, please work with the McGraw Hill Digital Integration Services team to ensure your district’s licenses and McGraw Hill content have been properly assigned in their rostering platform, Access Manager. For more information on how to assign your content in McGraw Hill, please see their documentation . 

 
 Where can I go for support?

 Clever Support: 

 • For help troubleshooting rosters, SIS Clever syncs, or login settings, check out our Help Center where you can open a ticket with Clever Support .
 
 McGraw Hill Digital Technical Support: 

 • For help with McGraw Hill platforms, please contact integrationsupport@mheducation.com .

title: Clever + PLTW Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup Process 3. Recommended Sharing Rules 4. FAQ 5. Here to Help 

 

 Overview

 Clever has partnered with myPLTW to create a seamless way to provide account creation, as well as Single Sign-On (SSO) access for users. This article will review the setup process so that you feel ready to add myPLTW for your district! 

 
 Setup Process

 • Please note that integration between Clever and myPLTW could take up to a week and a half to complete. 
• Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://www.clever.com/district-signup .
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add the myPLTW application by following these instructions: 
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications. 
• Search for myPLTW . 
• Click the PLTW icon to authorize requesting the application. During this process, you can select whether your district has already purchased the application. If your district or school has an active PLTW program, then select Yes . Otherwise, select No and we will notify PLTW of your interest. You can also learn more about getting started with PLTW by visiting https://www.pltw.org/get-started .
• You will also be asked if you would like to request a custom launch date. The launch date is the first day users can access the app through Clever. 
• Once you have made your selections, click Request . 
• Note: Now that you’ve clicked the Request to Join button, please note that integration between Clever and myPLTW could take up to a week and a half to complete.
 

• If your district receives an invitation from myPLTW , you can refer to this link for instructions on how to accept application invites: For District Admins: How do I accept app invites? 
 
 

 
 Recommended Sharing Rules

 We recommend sharing only your related PLTW courses and including the subjects math, science, and technology and engineering. Please also share your district/school admins. 

 

 Required Fields 

 For myPLTW to integrate your data, you must include the following: 

 • Adult users (teachers, admins): email address 
• Students: first name, last name, district, state id, and date of birth 
• Districts: NCES ID (primary) 
 • If NCES ID is not available, send the district name 
 

 
 • Schools: NCES ID (primary) 
 • If NCES ID is not available, send the schools’ name and location 
 

 
 Data Considerations 

 Please Note: myPLTW cannot match rostering information for classes whose teachers do not have an email address in Clever. Additionally, if you do have an email address on file, please ensure that the email address in myPLTW matches the email address in Clever. If no match is found, an educator account cannot be created in myPLTW . 

 Maintaining Student Rostering Information 

 Throughout the year, myPLTW will automatically receive any student or enrollment changes that are made in your Student Information System (SIS) through Clever. Please ensure that your SIS is up-to-date so students are properly enrolled. You should expect these changes to be available in myPLTW within 48 hours of the update. 

 Clever Import Classrooms Guide 

 Once your Clever integration is complete, please refer to this link for instructions to import classroom(s) into myPLTW after a district/site launches in Clever.

 Please note once your Clever integration is complete, you will only be able to roster via Clever moving forward. 

 
 

 FAQs

 Why is my data not showing up in myPLTW ? 

 • Depending on your sync type, Clever either pulls data from your SIS or data is submitted to Clever from your SIS. That data is then made available to myPLTW to sync to their system. There is a lag time between these processes, as the sync with Clever and the sync with myPLTW may not occur at the same time. 
 
 Why is my student or teacher missing from a myPLTW roster? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to PLTW : 
 • Student or teacher is present in your SIS data 
• Student or teacher is present in our Clever data and shared with myPLTW 
 

• If the user is missing from your Clever data but is in your SIS, please contact Clever Support . 
 
 Why are classes missing from Clever? 

 • If the class is missing from your Clever data but is in your SIS, please contact Clever Support . 
 
 Why is a teacher missing from a myPLTW roster? 

 • Make sure that the teacher has sections in Clever; myPLTW will import teachers but teachers will not be able to view their associated sections in myPLTW . You can check this by searching for the teacher in the Data browser . 
• myPLTW only rosters primary teachers . If a section has co-teachers, they will not sync to your myPLTW roster. 
 
 Who should I ask for help with this issue? 

 • If it’s related to data within Clever or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support . 
• If the data is in Clever but not integrating into myPLTW , please contact us at 877.335.7589. 
 
 

 
 Here to Help

 The friendly PLTW Solution Center Team Members are here to answer all of your questions as well! Call PLTW at 877.335.7589 for live, one-on-one support 7 a.m.-9 p.m. Monday through Friday, or email them and they’ll follow up with you. 

 Project Lead The Way 5939 Castle Creek Parkway North Drive Indianapolis, IN 46250 Toll-Free: 877.335.PLTW (7589) Local: 317.669.0200 solutioncenter@pltw.org

title: Which Clever domains should my network grant access?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Domains 

 Overview

 Some districts have firewalls or web filters in place that only allow outbound connections to certain domains. If your network uses systems like these, you will need to grant access to Clever's domains so that your district will be able to successfully use Clever. 

 
 Domains

 The list of domains your district should grant access for Clever users are:

 • *. clever.com 
• *. s3.amazonaws.com 
• accounts.google.com (if your district uses Clever Badges for Chromebooks) 
• *. twilio.com 
 

 The exact syntax to use will depend on your network. If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Which sync type should I choose?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever-Managed Auto Sync 3. SIS-Managed Auto Sync 4. SFTP Syncs 

 xx Overview

 Clever is compatible with all Student Information Systems (SIS), and depending on the SIS we offer a Clever-Managed Auto Sync, an SIS-Managed Auto Sync, or an SFTP Sync. The following information helps to explain the three supported sync types: 

 
 Clever-Managed Auto Sync

 How does it work? 

 • For Clever-Managed Auto Syncs, Clever has written an integration that exports data directly from the SIS and securely transfers it to Clever.
• For these syncs, Clever requires configured credentials to access your SIS. As with all Clever syncs, you will still have complete control over data sharing and management of your Clever account.
 
 Can I customize what roster data is synced? 

 • The ability to customize data for Clever-Managed Auto Syncs is limited, but we may be able to remove unwanted fields or include additional fields in your sync to meet your district’s needs. Contact our Support Team to request help .
 
 What are the next steps? 

 • Before signing up for a Clever account, create a Clever read-only account in your SIS. 
 
 

 Check out this article for a complete list of Clever-Managed Auto Syncs!

 

 
 SIS-Managed Auto Sync

 How does it work? 

 • For SIS-Managed Auto Syncs, Clever has partnered directly with the SIS provider to offer an automated sync option. Clever does not require credentials or access to the SIS directly.
• To take advantage of these SIS-managed syncs, select the 'Automatic' option during signup, and Clever will provide instructions to initiate the sync from your SIS to Clever.
• In most cases, you will need to share Clever sync credentials with your SIS provider, and the SIS will generate and regularly upload data to Clever on your behalf. As with all Clever syncs, you will still have complete control over data sharing and management of your Clever account.
 
 Can I customize what roster data is synced? 

 • If you have questions about customizing, please contact your SIS directly.
 
 What are the next steps? 

 • After creating your Clever account, navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab for instructions on how to activate your SIS-Managed Auto Sync. 
 
 

 
 SFTP Syncs

 How does it work? 

 • The SFTP (Secure File Transfer Protocol) Sync option allows school districts to “push” data to Clever. Clever provides credentials for Secure File Transfer Protocol as well as a web-based upload option, and districts generate files to upload to their accounts.
• Clever recommends that you investigate automating the process for exporting and uploading data via SFTP; regular uploads to Clever mean current data in your apps.
 
 Which student information systems (SIS) are available? 

 • SFTP Sync is available for all SISs.
 
 Can I customize what roster data is synced? 

 • Because districts control exactly what data to include in their upload files, this option provides districts a greater level of customization but requires a more robust setup and management.
 
 What are the next steps? 

 • To learn more about the data upload process for districts using SFTP Sync, see SFTP Syncs: What is the sync process? 
• If you are interested in automating your SFTP uploads, Clever supports data ingestion via nightly SFTP transmission to a secure SFTP server. While this method does not allow for real-time data uploads and requires additional configuration, it may be a useful option for schools whose IT Departments have already prepared SFTP scripts. The SFTP data transfer to Clever requires the submission of multiple files in a specific CSV format .
 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: Does Clever work with schools outside of the USA?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. GDPR, UK, Canada, and Australia 3. Other Regions 4. Getting Started 

 

 Overview 

 Thank you for your interest! Clever is fully supported in the United States, UK, Ireland, Australia, and Canada . Although we can support usage in other countries under the circumstances outlined herein, we are not able to fully ensure local compliance in places outside those named areas. If you have questions about compliance in your country, email legal@clever.com . 

 

 
 GDPR, UK, Canada, and Australia 

 Clever’s services are provided to schools in the European Economic Area (the “EEA”), the United Kingdom (the “UK”), Switzerland, Canada, and Australia in compliance with local requirements, including the European Union’s (“EU”) General Data Protection Regulation ( “GDPR”) and its UK GDPR counterpart, Canadian PIPEDA, and the Australia Privacy Principles. For more information, please see our Additional Terms for Schools: 

 • European Data Processing Addendum 
• Canadian Privacy Notice 
• Australian Privacy Notice 
 
 

 
 Other Regions

 Clever has not specifically localized our services or product offerings for other regions apart from Canada, the UK, EU/EEA, Australia, and the USA. Although some of the regions that we are compliant with are considered to be world-class in terms of consumer and privacy regulations, Clever may not be aware of unique compliance requirements from some international jurisdictions.

 If your school is located in a region not named in the list above, Clever is open to providing services to you on an “as-is” basis and without any representation or warranty of compliance with local laws.

 Before you use Clever’s services, you should familiarize yourself with our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy and confirm (a) that you are comfortable with the terms of our services; and (b) that they meet the requirements of your local jurisdiction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, please note that Clever is subject to relevant sanctions laws and Clever is not available in regions that are prohibited by various sanctions regulations.

 
 Getting Started 

 Canada 

 Canadian schools can learn more about Clever in Canada and sign up for Clever here . 

 Once your account is created , your next step will be to sync your student data. Browse this list to determine whether your student information system supports Clever. 

 • 
 If it does , you can find instructions on how to sync here or on your dashboard

• 
 If it does not , you can sync your data by following our SFTP file instructions 

 
 Once your student data is synced, you can add applications , print Badges , and design your Clever Portal .

 

 UK, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, and other regions 

 Learn more about Clever in your country here. 

 Once your account is created , your next step will be to sync your student data. Browse this list to determine whether your student information system supports Clever. 

 • 
 If it does , you can find instructions on how to sync here or on your dashboard

• 
 If it does not , you can sync your data by following our SFTP file instructions 

 
 Once your student data is synced, you can add applications , print Badges , and design your Clever Portal .

 If you’d like Clever to be fully supported in your country, please contact us at international@clever.com . We are working to expand our services into more countries and would love to hear from you.

title: Does Clever require third-party "write access" to my SIS?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Auto Syncs 3. SFTP Syncs 

 Overview

 Security and privacy are top priorities at Clever. All roster data synced with Clever is protected by industry leading infrastructure and strict adherence to FERPA. Please read Clever's security whitepaper for more information on our privacy and security policies. 

 Clever does not require write access to your SIS. See below for specifics SIS access for your sync type, and click here to learn more about Clever’s sync options.

 Auto Syncs 

 Clever supports the guidance to not grant write access to third parties – which is why Clever is a read-only service . As a result, the permissions you should grant Clever for your student information system (SIS) are read-only .

 SFTP Syncs 

 If you are syncing via SFTP (Secure File Transfer Protocol) , Clever does not have any access to your student information system (SIS). SFTP Syncs require you to compile all roster data you upload to Clever, and you control which fields are included in your data uploads. Clever provides credentials for Secure File Transfer Protocol as well as a web-based upload option, and districts generate files to upload to their accounts. You will not be asked to provide Clever with your SIS’s URL or credentials, and Clever has no way of directly exporting data from your SIS. 

 If you have any questions about how to properly set up Clever, please request assistance from Clever Support.

title: How do I sign up for Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever offerings 3. Sign-up for Clever! 

 Overview

 Thanks for your interest in Clever! If you are a district or school interested in using Clever, use the sign-up link below to get started. Read on to learn more about Clever!

 

 Clever is an identity platform that helps schools with many daily tasks, such as:

 • 
 Automating the secure transfer of student rosters to authorized parties

• 
 Providing single sign-on (SSO) for students, teachers, and staff to access learning applications easily

• 
 Managing account life cycles for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID

• 
 Providing end users with school-friendly multi-factor authentication (MFA) solutions

 
 Before signing up, we recommend reviewing the following articles from our Help Center to learn more about us: 

 • What is Clever? 
• How much does Clever cost for districts or schools? 
• What is single sign-on (SSO)? 
• How does Clever help districts protect student information? 
 
 

 Clever offerings

 Clever is the identity platform for education that offers: 

 • 
 Secure Sync : Secure sync maintains up-to-date roster information in your learning applications by connecting with your Student Information System (SIS) to securely roster your data with your connected applications, all in one place . With the majority of K-12 schools nationwide on Clever, Clever is the safest and fastest way for districts to securely send vendors data and grant access to learning applications. 

• 
 Single Sign-On (SSO): SSO allows students and teachers to access all of their applications with a single set of credentials so that they can focus on learning, not logging in. 

• Clever Portal : A personalized portal through which users can use SSO to log in to every learning application they use. Customize your district’s or school’s Portal to add personalized resources like your school website or calendar. 
• Additional complimentary tools such as analytics, troubleshooting tools, and Access Logs.
• In addition to our no-cost complimentary tools, we offer districts other optional paid tools such as Clever IDM and Classroom MFA .
 
 

 Please note: To get a full overview of all the tools in Clever, check out our What's Clever? article! 

 Sign-up for Clever!

 If you're ready to sign up, please click here to complete the sign-up form. Our District Onboarding team will be in touch with you to discuss next steps. 

 If you already use Clever and are interested in learning more about Clever IDM or Classroom MFA, please contact our team here .

title: Setting up Clever: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Connect your student information system (SIS) 3. Add Applications 4. Choose your identity provider (IdP) 5. After onboarding, roll out Clever to your schools! 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Getting started with the Clever? Check out our onboarding courses , available in Clever Academy!

 Overview 

 Welcome to Clever! We’re so excited to get you set up. Clever offers many features, and getting started with the basics is easy.

 For the best onboarding experience, we encourage you to access the onboarding courses listed above in Clever Academy once you have set up your Clever account. It’s a comprehensive resource to guide you in setting up Clever!

 Below is a summary of the four essential steps to get your Clever account running. The first three steps are part of your onboarding journey, and the fourth is to get your schools using Clever so they can benefit from it!

 

 
 Step 1: Connect your student information system (SIS) 

 To start, you need to connect your SIS to Clever so we can help you share data you designate with relevant apps. Syncing data with Clever can be done with any SIS. Your sync is what sends information from your SIS to Clever so that we can share the data you authorize with the applications you specify. 

 

 Please note: Clever supports one source of truth per district . You must select a single Student Information System (SIS) or SFTP as your data source. Our system can't sync data from multiple SISs simultaneously. If your school organization has multiple schools using different systems, determine which single source you'll use for your Clever sync before getting started. 

 Navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync page in the dashboard and find your SIS from the drop-down menu. If you can’t find your SIS listed here, choose Other . 

 There are three ways to sync data to Clever: 

 • Clever-managed Auto Sync 
• SIS-managed Auto Sync 
• SFTP Sync 
 
 For any SIS-Managed or SFTP syncs, our teams have limited insight into the export processes within your SIS. Please work with your SIS team directly for any questions on how to export data from their system.

 If you need guidance on what sync type you should use, you can learn more here: Which sync type should I choose? 

 
 

 

 

 
 
 Step 2: Add Applications 

 Clever seamlessly syncs data with any of your schools' applications, or apps, that you connect to Clever. Examples include Newsela, Reading A-Z, and Mathspace.

 To add an application , navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add applications page , and then request your desired application(s). 

 Once you’ve requested a learning application, each app partner has its own approval process. If you have questions about getting your request approved, impacts on existing application data, or changes to the login flow for your students and teachers, please reach out to the application partner directly.

 

 Sharing Data 

 After you have added a new application to Clever, you’ll need to set data sharing permissions for the application. Sharing permissions specify what data you want to make available to a specific application.

 • 
 From the Applications page , find your newest application(s) from your list of connected learning applications. 

• 
 On the right side, choose Share data now . 

 
 Find more information about sharing data with new applications here: Sharing Permissions: Sharing data with new applications .

 

 
 Step 3: Choose your identity provider (IdP) 

 If you decide to utilize the Clever Portal, you'll choose how students, teachers, and staff will log into Clever and access all your online learning applications in one place with a single click!

 To set up single sign-on (SSO):

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page.

• 
 Choose the identity provider (e.g. Google , Active Directory , LDAP , etc.) that works best for users in your school district. Find more information here: Single Sign-On (SSO): identity provider (IdP) Options . 

 
 Our Help Center offers step-by-step instructions for how to set up each login method within your Clever Dashboard: Single Sign-On (SSO): Identity Provider (IDP)/Login Method Options . 

 

 

 
 Step 4: Roll Out 

 Woohoo! Now it’s time to share the exciting news with your teachers and make sure they’re all set.

 If you’ve chosen to use Clever Portal with your district, navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page to provide instructions on how they will access Clever. You can find instructions to download on your Clever Dashboard as shown in this screenshot: 

 

 If you’re using Clever just for rostering, make sure all apps your schools are using are added to Clever, and ensure teachers know how to log in to them.

 

 
 

 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Is there a quick list to walk me through the steps of setting up my Clever account? 

 • 
 Yes! We’ve created customized resources for districts and schools just getting started with Clever. Check out our Clever Admin Onboarding checklist and Onboarding Quick Start Guides for a handheld approach to setting up your account.

 
 Who do I contact if I have questions as I work on setting up my account? 

 • 
 You should have received a welcome email with detailed setup instructions when your Clever account was created. Please respond directly to this email with any questions and your dedicated Onboarding Specialist will respond to assist you. If you cannot find the email or any other emails from your onboarding specialist, please feel free to reach out directly to our support team at support.clever.com and they will put you in touch with your Onboarding Specialist.

 
 Can I add other administrators to help with account setup? 

 • 
 Yes! Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page of your Clever account and follow the steps in our article: Clever Roles: Clever Admin .

 
 What if I don’t know how to export data from my SIS? 

 • 
 Check out our onboarding resources in Clever Academy, or reach out to your district/school’s onboarding specialist and they can share where you can find additional instructions.

• 
 With SFTP syncs, we have limited insight into how to export data from your SIS. We recommend reaching out directly to your SIS for the best assistance with any SIS-specific export instructions for your SFTP sync.

 
 What if I don’t have a SIS? 

 • 
 You can manually create and upload CSV files to sync your data to Clever.

 
 How long does it take to set up my Clever account? 

 • 
 This varies for each district and school depending on your SIS, sync type, and the applications you hope to set up. Most schools can have their data sync and first application launched for rostering within a week!

 
 How do I know when I have completed onboarding with Clever? 

 • 
 You can expect a congratulatory email from your Onboarding Specialist when you have completed Clever onboarding. This email will provide next-step resources and instructions on how to reach our support team for any further assistance needed beyond onboarding.

 
 What are my next steps after syncing data, setting up an application, and adding a login method? 

 • Check out Clever Academy for more information on Clever Core and Clever+ tools! Once you have completed the onboarding process, please reach out to our support team with any questions about troubleshooting or resources to help you further utilize Clever.

title: Can small districts, charters, parochial, and private schools use Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Things to Consider 3. Getting Started 

 

 Overview

 Clever is a no-cost data integration and single sign-on (SS) service that gives back hours of teaching time to school organizations all over the country. Clever can be used by various types of school organizations including charter and parochial schools. 

 
 Things to consider

 Charter and private schools: 

 • Student Information System (SIS): Clever is designed to sync student data from your SIS, but we understand that many private and charter schools don't have an SIS. You can find a list of our SIS partners here . If you don't have an SIS or don't see your SIS on the list, you will have to create CSV files and upload them via SFTP .
 
 Local school district: 

 • If your private school or charter school isn't managed by a local school organization, you'll likely not find your school name on the drop-down menu when signing up. If this is the case, please Add your school name and don't sign up as a larger school organization. 
 
 School organizations with six schools or fewer: 

 • Syncing : When completing your first sync, you can find more information in these articles: 
 • SIS Partners 
• Which sync option should you choose? 
• Sync Error: What are "Insufficient Permissions"? 
• Sync Error: How do I resolve "Invalid username/password specified"? 
• All other Secure Sync topics 
 

 
 

 
 Getting Started

 Clever setup works best when there's a dedicated technology team managing the account. Before signing up, think about who'll be setting up and running things. You can get an overview of the different roles and permissions in Clever here . 

 

 Think you’ll need a little extra support? We’ve got you covered. Clever offers Consulting and Premium Support for districts looking for a higher level of guidance. As your partner in simplifying digital learning, we provide VIP dedicated support and expert coaching tailored to your needs. Check it out here .

 

 If you are still interested in using Clever and ready to sign up, please visit our Self-Service Guides on Secure Sync and Single Sign-on (SSO).

title: For Clever Admins: How do I add an STL to Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Bulk uploading School Tech Leads via CSV 3. Upgrading an existing staff user 4. Manually adding a School Tech Lead 

 

 Overview

 District Admins can assign certain users the School Tech Lead role which provides them with limited admin permissions in Clever for their associated school(s). To learn more about the School Tech lead role, read: Clever Roles: School Tech Lead . 

 As educators are increasingly relying on technology to support online learning, having a dedicated role in Clever to help teachers manage their digital classroom in Clever is now more important than ever.

 Clever Admins can add STLs to their Clever Portal today. Follow the instructions in this quick video or follow the instructions below.

 Bulk uploading School Tech Leads via CSV

 To add a School Tech Lead, or multiple School Tech Leads, via an uploaded file to Clever, please:

 • Navigate to Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data .
• Scroll down to the 'Manual Upload' section and select 'Download existing staff.csv'
 
 

 • The staff.csv file will contain all pre-existing staff in Clever for your district. 
 • Note : DO NOT REMOVE any staff users you still want to have access to Clever. Removing a user from the staff.csv before re-uploading it will cause their record to be deleted. Rather, just add the additional user information to the file before re-uploading. 
 

• Add the desired staff members to the file, entering 'STL' or 'School Tech Lead' in the Role column. 
• Navigate to Data sources > SIS sync > Staff Data > Manual Upload, and upload the updated staff.csv.
 
 

 • The upload changes should be reflected in Clever in approximately 10-15 minutes
 
 

 Upgrading an existing staff user to School Tech Lead

 • School Tech Leads must first be added to Clever as staff before they can be assigned the School Tech Lead role. If the individual is a teacher in Clever, or are not yet in Clever as staff, please visit: Adding custom staff to Clever. 
• Once the user is in Clever, navigate to their profile page using the 'Search' feature at the top of the dashboard.
• From their profile page, click the drop-down arrow on the top right of the page.
• Select 'Update Clever Role'.
• Select 'School Tech Lead'.
 
 

 

 Manually adding a School Tech Lead

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 
• In top right corner, click Add custom data . 
• In the drop-down menu, select Staff. 
 • Fill out the fields for the School Tech Lead you want to create. You’ll need to select which schools they are assigned to, or select 'Assign to all schools', as well as enter some basic identifying information. IMPORTANT: To make them into a School Tech Lead, you need to select 'School Tech Lead' in the drop-down for 'Clever Role'. 
• Optional: Check the box to send an email notifying the staff member that their account has been created. 
• Select 'Create Staff' 
 

 
 The Username and Password field only needs to be populated if: A) The district login method is Clever Passwords and 'District Username' or 'District Password' is selected for staff logins B) The district login method is Active Directory or LDAPS and the 'username' field in Clever is utilized for staff matching (claims rules) C) You would like to utilize the 'District Username' and 'District Password' fields for staff logging into SSO (Saved Passwords) applications

title: For Teachers: Clever + Zoom guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I create a Zoom account? 3. How do I log in to Zoom? 4. How do I set up my virtual meetings? 5. How do I update my virtual meetings to support password protection? 6. How do my students log in to Zoom? 7. Recommended Zoom settings 8. How can I get help with using Zoom? 

 

 Overview

 Clever has partnered with Zoom to create a seamless way to enable account creation and Single Sign-On (SSO) access for teachers, staff, and admins who need to support remote learning.

 

 

 How do I create a Zoom account?

 Adding Zoom and creating accounts are managed at the school organization level. Administrators can find directions for adding the Zoom integration here . 

 If you don't see the Zoom app in your Portal, please contact your administrator or IT team.

 
 How do I log in to Zoom?

 • Log in to the Clever Portal
• Click on the Zoom icon . 
 
 You're now logged in and will be directed to your Zoom profile! On this page, you can find your Personal Meeting ID URL that you'll share with your students. 

 Please note: The Zoom - Teachers & Staff application is only for use by teachers and staff. Do not add this application to your Teacher Pages. See " How do I set up my virtual meetings " for information on providing students access to Zoom. 

 
 How do I set up my virtual meetings?

 • Log in to your Clever Portal .
• Create a Teacher Page for each of your classes. Instructions can be found here .
• Click on the Zoom application in the Clever Portal.
• Click on Schedule a Meeting. 
• Enter the details for your class. For example: Topic: 1st Hr - Algebra When: 8:00am - 8:52am 
• Select Recurring meeting . 
• Select Save . On the next screen, click Copy the invitation. 
• Go to the Clever Portal and click on your Teacher Page for the corresponding class (Example: 1st Hr - Algebra). 
• Click Add in the top-right corner and select Link . Paste the Meeting UR L where it says Link URL → name the link (ex. Mr. Conn's Zoom) → upload the Zoom logo as the icon → Click Add link. 
• The Zoom icon will now appear on your teacher page for both you and your students to access. Any time you and your students want to join your virtual classroom, simply click on the Zoom icon!
• Repeat steps 3-10 for each of your classes. 
 
 Pro Tip: Consider adding your picture as your app icon for Zoom to create a more personalized experience! 

 

 
 How do I update my virtual meetings to support password protection?

 Zoom has updated the default settings for education customers to enable password protection for meetings automatically. However, if you created a meeting link prior to April 6th, please follow the directions below to update the link on your Clever Teacher Page. This will ensure students won't need to enter a password when joining your meeting:

 • Log in to the Clever Portal. 
• Click on the Zoom application in the Clever Portal.
• Click on Meetings from the left-hand navigation.
• Click on the name of the scheduled meeting you wish to adjust. 
• Copy the updated Join URL 
• Go to the Clever Portal → Click on your Teacher Page for the corresponding class (Example: Algebra 1) 
• Click the three vertical dots on the icon for your existing Zoom link and click Edit 
• Paste the updated Join URL where it says Link URL. 
• Click Update 
• The updated URL will now be available to your students. Repeat these steps for each of your Zoom links.
 
 For meetings scheduled moving forward, the meeting password can be found in the invitation and will automatically be included in the Join URL.

 
 How do my students log in to Zoom?

 Once you have created a Zoom icon for your students, they will simply log in to the Clever Portal > Click on your Teacher Page > Click on the Zoom icon that you added. They'll immediately be taken to your virtual meeting! 

 

 
 Recommended Zoom Settings

 

 Tips from Zoom: How to Keep the Party Crashers from Crashing Your Zoom Event 

 Waiting Room 

 Description: The Waiting Room feature allows the host to control when a participant joins the meeting. As the meeting host, you can admit attendees one by one or hold all attendees in the waiting room and admit them all at once Directions : Setup Tutorial: How to use 

 Mute upon entry 

 Description: Automatically mute all participants when they join the meeting. The host controls whether participants can unmute themselves Directions: Setup - Log in to Clever > Click on Zoom > Settings > Mute participants upon entry > toggle to green Tutorial: How to use 

 Disable screen sharing for students 

 Description: Allow only the teacher to share their screen or content during meetings Directions: Setup - Log in to Clever > Click on Zoom > Settings > Screen Sharing > Who can share? > Select "Host only" > Click "Save" Tutorial: How to use 

 Disabling In-Meeting Chat 

 Description: This will prevent the host, co-hosts, and participants from chatting for any meetings you host. The Chat option will no longer appear in the Meeting Controls.

 Directions: 

 • 
 • Sign in to Clever
• Click on the Zoom icon to sign in to the Zoom web portal.
• Click Settings .
• Click the Chat and Private Chat toggles to disable in-meeting chat.
• Click Save Changes .
 

 
 
 How can I get help with using Zoom?

 Zoom has a large number of resources to support you and your students. These resources can be found at https://support.zoom.us 

 Here are some resources from their Help Center that Clever recommends to get started!

 • Zoom Video Tutorials (1 Minute each!) 
• Meeting and Webinar Best Practices 
• Managing meetings with updated controls 
• Frequently Asked Questions 
 
 Zoom Technical Support 

 If you or your students encounter technical problems with Zoom, you can submit a support request . " Support Hours" are 24 x 7 , including public holidays, where agents or technical engineers are available for email or live support.

title: For Teachers: SSO (Saved Passwords) applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does my district provide user credentials for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications? 3. How do I reset my student's SSO (Saved Passwords) credentials? 4. How do I reset my SSO (Saved Passwords) credentials? 

 Overview 

 Single Sign-on SSO (Saved Passwords) applications are icons in the Clever Portal that automatically log students and teachers into websites that do not currently have a formal integration with Clever. From a user's experience, these icons appear to be the same as other resources in the Portal. In order for an SSO (Saved Passwords) application to automatically log users into a website, you and your students' must already have existing accounts with the website . Your district will provide Clever with credentials for these accounts, then Clever's system securely saves this login information to help users automatically sign into the website from the Portal! 

 Clever Tip: Make sure the Clever browser extension is downloaded on your school's devices to ensure users are able to log into these Saved Passwords applications! 

 

 
 

 How does my district provide user credentials for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications? 

 Your district administrator manages the process of adding SSO (Saved Passwords) applications to your Clever account. They also configure settings that determine how user credentials for the website are provided to Clever.

 If they have chosen to have users manually provide their credentials, you will see a prompt to enter login information the first time you click on the SSO (Saved Passwords) application in the Clever Portal.

 

 Once you enter the correct login information for your account with this application, click 'Save Login' . Clever will save this information and automatically log you into the application next time you click the icon from the Portal!

 If you click on an application and see the page below, this means we were not able to log you into the application with the provided credentials. Please enter your updated username and password for the application, and click ' Update & Log in '.

 

 If you'd like to learn about how Clever securely stores user credentials for Saved Passwords applications, feel free to read our blog post here !

 

 
 

 How do I reset my student's SSO (Saved Passwords) credentials? 

 If your student has trouble with an SSO (Saved Passwords) application, you can help them troubleshoot! Log into your Clever account, then follow these steps:

 • Go to Clever Portal > My Students > Student Roster > Select the class of the student having trouble.
• Click Troubleshoot app login .
 
 

 • Search for the app that requires new student credentials.
 
 

 • Determine if the student can see the application from their Clever Portal. If they can't, you'll need to contact an Admin for help.
 
 

 • Click Update credentials .
 
 

 • Enter the correct username and password. Click Set credentials .
 
 

 If you still need help, you can contact our Support Team by clicking the chat icon in the bottom right corner of Support.Clever.com .

 
 

 How do I reset my SSO (Saved Passwords) credentials? 

 If incorrect credentials are inputted for one of your SSO (Saved Passwords) applications, you are able to update them by:

 • Log in to your Clever Portal 
• Select the application that has incorrect credentials 
• Enter your updated username and password for the application, and click Update & Log in . 
 
 

 If for some reason the above steps do not work for you, please follow these steps to update your credentials in an incognito window:

 • On your computer, open Google Chrome .
• At the top right, click More New Incognito Window .
• A new window appears. In the top corner, check for the Incognito icon .
• Log in to Clever: clever.com/login 
• Click on the <<App Name>> ap p.
• You should be prompted to update your username and password.
• Click Save .
 
 

 Clever Tip: If you're still having trouble, please confirm that the username and password you've entered work to log into your account directly on the application's page (i.e. at schoology.com ). If you can successfully access the app directly through their website, but not through Clever, please reach out to Clever Support for help!

title: For Teachers: Rename a Class
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Renaming steps 

 Overview 

 Teachers can rename classes within their Clever Portal. By default, your class names will be what is listed in your district's student information system (SIS).

 Renaming a class is especially useful if your class names are long and difficult to remember (e.g. "Grade 5 Reading, Class 501 - 501 (14_10)"). 

 Please note: Renaming of classes will only apply to classes within the Clever Portal . If you would like to rename your classes within an application, please reach out to your Clever administrator or check in with the application's support team to see if they can help! 

 Renaming steps 

 1. From your Clever Portal, head to My Students > Student Roster.

 2 Choose the class you want to rename.

 3. Click Edit class nickname , update the name, and click Save .

title: For Teachers: Announcements
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sending an announcement 3. Deleting an announcement 4. The student experience 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 Clever enables teachers to send one-way announcements to keep students informed quickly and efficiently. These announcements are perfect for reaching an entire class or multiple classes at once. Teachers can easily create and send announcements directly from their Clever Portal. Students can’t reply to teacher announcements.

 

 
 Sending an announcement

 Clever Tip: Press the "shift + return" keys to move to the next line without sending the message! 

 

 To send an announcement to a specific class, follow the steps below: 

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Portal.

• 
 Click My students > Announcements in the left menu.

• 
 Click the +New Announcement button.

• Select the class you'd like to create a channel for.
• Enter a name.
• Select or upload an icon that will be associated with your channel.
• Click Create. 
• Type your announcement in the text box of the chat.
• Click Send to send your announcement.
 
 

 To send an announcement to all students, follow the steps below: 

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Portal .

• 
 Click My Students > Announcements in the left menu.

• 
 Select the All classes option or click on the class titled [Your name’s] Students. Make sure that the highlighted text under your page name shows All students.

• 
 Type your announcement in the text box of the chat.

• 
 Click Send to send your announcement.

 
 

 

 Clever Tip: You can also add attachments to your announcements! Click the paperclip icon in the announcement box and select a file that you’d like to attach. 

 

 
 Deleting an announcement

 If you need to delete an announcement, click the three vertical dots on the announcement you want to delete, then select Delete. Deletion is permanent and can’t be undone. Deleting it will prevent the announcement from being seen by you and your students.

 

 
 The student experience

 The announcements icon in the student’s Portal will display a dot with a number to show they have received an announcement.

 

 

 
 

 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I send an announcement to an individual student? 

 • 
 No. You can send announcements to an entire class or all of your students at once.

 
 Who has access to my announcement history? 

 • 
 Clever Admins for your school can access your announcement history.

 
 
 Can announcements be deleted or edited? 

 • Yes! You can delete Announcements by clicking the three vertical dots on the announcement and selecting Delete. Announcements cannot be edited once sent.
 
 What happens to announcements when the school year rolls over? 

 • 
 When the new school year starts, if you no longer have classes with the previous year's students, you won’t be able to send announcements to those students and will no longer have access to the announcement history.

 
 Is there a character limit for announcements? 

 • 
 Announcements are limited to 8,000 characters. 

 
 Can students reply to announcements? 

 • 
 No, announcements offer one-way communication from teachers to students.

title: For Teachers: Searching and Favoriting apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Favoriting resources 3. Searching for resources 

 

 This article provides an overview of how teachers can easily locate applications and categorize their favorite apps in Clever! 

 Locating and favoriting applications are features that are also available to your students !

 

 Favoriting applications

 Here's how to favorite an application: 

 • Log into your Clever Portal from your district's Clever login page, or from Clever.com . 
• Locate your favorite app icon, and click the heart in the upper right-hand corner of the icon. 
• This will add the app to the 'Favorites' section at the top of your Portal. 
• To remove an app from your ‘Favorites’, simply click the heart again in the top right corner! 
 

 

 Please note: Favoriting resources is only reflected in individual Portals. Resources that teachers favorite will not be favorited for their students. 

 Searching for applications

 Teachers can search for district-wide applications, Library resources, or district-wide app links from their Clever Portals.

 • Simply navigate to the search bar at the top of your Clever Portal to search for the app.
• Depending on the app, when the result is selected you will be either:
 • Logged in to the app if it is a district-wide application
• Provided the option to install the app if it is a Library app 
• Be redirected to the app login page to log in (if a non-integrated app)
 
 
 

 If you are unable to locate the app via the Clever search bar, this means it is either not a Clever-integrated app nor a district-wide linked app. Please see this article for instructions on how to add links to your Teacher Page for these types of apps.

title: For Teachers: Adding apps & resources
text:
This article contains directions for adding: 1. Overview 2. Library apps 3. Apps provisioned by your school organization 4. Links 5. PDFs 6. Missing an app? 

 Overview

 You can add various resources to your Teacher Page. This article will guide you through the process of adding these different types of resources available in Clever. 

 
 Library apps:

 The Clever Library is a resource hub for teachers to discover, learn about, and adopt new educational materials for their classrooms. You can add the resources in the Library to your individual Teacher Pages for easy access by students and teachers. If your school organization has enabled the Clever Library, you can follow the instructions in this article to locate and install resources. 

 

 

 
 Apps:

 Adding applications provided by your school organization to your Teacher Page is a quick and easy process. Please follow these steps:

 • Navigate to Pages > District page on the left-hand side of your Clever Portal.
• Click on the three vertical dots on any resource you want to add to your Teacher Portal and click Add to Teacher Page. 
 
 Please note: Resources that have a link icon in the top right-hand corner of the resource indicate it is a hyperlink and not an application that supports single sign-on. The resource in the image below doesn't have the link icon, suggesting it's an application that will log you and your students in instantly from the Clever Portal. 

 

 

 

 • A pop-up will display for you to select which Teacher Page you'd like to add the app to. Please note: If you are unable to locate an application provisioned by your school org, please reach out to your Clever admin to request that they share the application with you. 
• Click Add. 
 
 

 
 Links:

 Teachers typically add links as redirects to applications, resources that aren't provisioned by your school org, or aren't integrated with Clever. For these types of resources, the link might direct you and your students to the login page of a specific application, or a resource website. Single sign-on (SSO) isn't available for linked resources, and rostering data won't be synced by Clever; instead it must be updated manually within the resource as needed. Links are a great way to provide easy access to websites, videos, Google docs, and more! 

 Adding links to your Teacher Page is a quick and easy process! Please follow these steps:

 • Navigate to Pages > My Teacher Pages. 
• Select the Add option at the top of your Teacher Page and select Link .
• A pop-up will display allowing you to paste the URL for the page this link should direct to which should always begin with https:// 
• Enter a title to help your students locate the link.
• Choose the appropriate category for your link.
• Select one of the provided icons to represent your link, or upload your own icon! To upload your own icon, select Upload in order to search for the appropriate image from your desktop. Images must be in the .png file format, and less than 2MB. 
• Click Add link .
 
 

 

 
 PDFs

 Uploading resources as PDFs is quick and easy on Clever! Simply follow these directions: 

 • Navigate to Pages > My Teacher Pages 
• Select the Add option at the top of your Teacher Page and select PDF .
• Select the File to be uploaded from your device. The max file size is 10MB.
• Enter the File Name to display to students
• Select the Category where you want the file to be displayed on your Teacher Page
• Select the desired Icon or upload your own!
• Once the file has been successfully uploaded to Clever (Cloud is Green), select Add PDF 
 
 

 

 The file is now accessible to students on your Teacher Page! When the student clicks on the file, the PDF will open in their web browser (e.g. Google Chrome). They can download or print the PDF using the tools in the top-right corner: 

 

 

 

 Need help downloading your resource as a PDF to upload to Clever? Directions for popular programs can be found below: 

 • Google Docs File > Download > PDF Document (.pdf)
• Google Slides File > Download > PDF Document (.pdf)
• Microsoft Word File > Save as > File Format: PDF > Export
• Microsoft PowerPoint File > Save as > File Format: PDF > Save
 
 

 
 

 Missing an app?

 If you or your student is missing an application from your Clever Portal, please reach out to your school organizations support contact. Your school org manages which teachers and students can access each learning application through Clever, so they'll need to add the application to your student's Portal.

title: For Teachers: Analytics
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I access Analytics in my Clever Portal? 3. Student activity 4. Weekly overview 5. FAQ 

 

 Getting started with the Clever Portal? Check out our Product Spotlight: Using Class Analytics to support students , available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 With so many digital learning tools in use in school, it can be hard for teachers to keep track of student engagement and activities. Class Analytics in the Clever Teacher Portal gives you key information about which students are logging in to Clever and when they last logged in. It also shows which resources (applications, links, and PDFs teachers have added) the students have accessed in the past seven days. The Analytics page has two tabs: Student activity and Weekly overview . Continue reading to learn more about the features on each tab.

 

 

 
 How do I access Analytics in my Clever Portal? 

 • Log in to your Clever Portal at your district-specific URL. 
• Click on My Students > Analytics in the menu on the left side of the Portal. 
 
 

 

 Please note: The data on this page will update every 60 seconds. If you believe there is a discrepancy, please feel free to reach out! 

 
 Student activity

 The Student activity tab includes data and tools that allow teachers to easily view and manage student participation in one central location. It includes the most recent login for each student and which resources they utilized for the previous seven days. 

 This data can be filtered by class, resource, and level of activity. 

 

 

 

 Please note: This feature only shows the students' most recent logins. If a student has logged in multiple times, only their most recent login to Clever will appear. 

 

 
 Weekly overview

 The Weekly overview tab shows student login history for the previous seven days and the resources that they have been using.

 

 

 

 If a student has accessed more than two resources, you can click the and (number) more hyperlink and a pop-up window of the student's last accessed resources will appear. Teachers can toggle the ' Show resources' button if they would like to hide the resources and only see login data. 

 
 

 FAQ 

 Can I see the number of times each student logged in over the past week in addition to the most recent login? 

 The Teacher Analytics feature currently only shows the last time a student logged in to the Clever portal. We are looking to expand the kinds of student activity that appear on this page. Please use the feedback dialog on the page to let us know what actions you would like to take and additional information you would like to see. 

 Can I download or save the login analytics for a given week? 

 Not at this time. 

 Are there plans to add login data for more than the past seven days? 

 We plan to surface the last time a student logged into the portal for up to 30 days. 

 My student/student’s guardian said that they logged in to their EdTech resources this week. Why don’t I see them in the analytics? 

 Teacher Analytics currently only surface the last time a student logged in to the Clever Portal. If students access EdTech resources outside of the Clever Portal, we are unable to track or report that information. You can add links and applications to your Teacher Page , which will allow Clever to surface information about student activity.

title: For Teachers: Login Tools
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Opening resources for your students 3. Logging students out of Clever 4. View student login status 5. Log in with Clever - Download student usernames 6. Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (Classroom MFA) for students 

 Overview

 In your Clever Portal, you'll have access to a variety of Login Tools. This article details the different Login Tools available to teachers, including:

 • Opening resources for your students 
• Logging students out of Clever 
• Reviewing student login status 
 
 Clever Tip: Teacher tools are not currently supported on the Clever iOS application. 

 
 Opening resources for your students

 If students are logged into Clever using an internet browser window (e.g., Internet Explorer, Chrome, Edge, etc.), their teachers can open resources, such as applications, for them. This option is not currently available for students using the Clever iOS application. 

 Teachers can open a resource, such as an application, for their students, from either their Portal or any of their Teacher Pages (the process is the same). Opening a resource for students won't close out browser tabs or lock students into using only the opened resource.

 To open a resource for your students:

 • Navigate to your Portal on the Resources page or to one of your Teacher Pages (in this screenshot, we’re showing the Resources page). 
 
 

 

 • 
 Choose a resource to open, click on the three blue buttons in the upper right of the icon, and click Open for Students .

• 
 You can o pen the resource for an entire class, multiple classes, or a subset of students. 

 

• Click Open after your selection and a green box will appear, letting you know the resource was successfully opened on student devices!
 
 
 See below for a video showing the student view when a teacher has opened an resource for them:

 

 Please note: If a student doesn't have access to the resource you're opening, they'll see an error message. See this article for troubleshooting steps. 

 
 Logging students out of Clever

 Teachers also can log students out of Clever! Log your class out of Clever by following the steps below: 

 • Navigate to My Students > Student Roster in the menu on the left side of your Portal
• Filter by class.
• Select More class tools > Log out class . 
 
 

 

 

 • Clicking this button will log your students out of Clever. This will also be reflected under the Name column as students will appear as offline.
 
 

 

 

 Please note: If students are using Google Chrome with the Clever Extension installed, students will also be logged out of their applications. 

 Viewing student login status

 The My Students > Student Roster page shows which students have successfully logged into Clever. 

 • Select your class by clicking the Filter by class drop-down. 
• Review the student login status under the Name column. It will either say Offline or Online above the student's name.
 
 

 

 

 Clever Tip: Want to learn more about resetting passwords? Check out this article: For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students 

 

 
 Log in with Clever - Download student usernames

 If your district uses Log in with Clever you'll have the ability to download a list of your students' usernames within your Clever Portal! You can download the list of usernames for each of your classes.

 Step 1:

 From your Portal click on My Students > Student Roster on the left-hand side of your Portal. 

 

 Step 2: 

 Click on More class tools on the right side of the page. 

 

 

 

 Step 3: 

 Click Download student usernames in the dropdown menu. This will download a CSV file containing the students' usernames along with each student's first and last name. 

 Note: Usernames can also be downloaded via the Classes tab at the top of your Portal. 

 

 
 

 Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (Classroom MFA) for students

 If your school district requires Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (Classroom MFA) for students, they will need to activate it to log in to Clever. You can view which students haven’t activated their MFA by navigating to your Clever Portal > My Students > Student Roster.
