If the student hasn’t activated their Classroom MFA, it'll be indicated by their name in the student roster.

 

 

 

 You can find instructions for students to set up their Classroom MFA here: For Students: Setting up Classroom MFA . 

 If one of your students has lost their Classroom MFA Badge, you can void it and create a new one for them in your Student Roster by selecting a student and reviewing your options in the MFA column of the right sidebar.

 

 

 

 Voiding and creating a new Badge will allow you to download the new Badge to your device, which you can print out for the student. The next time the student logs in, Clever will prompt them to follow a login flow to validate the Badge.

 

 Be sure to visit this article to learn about Teacher Analytics which provides specific details about students logging in to Clever, and accessing their connected applications!

title: Clever Portal: Teacher Pages
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Creating a new Teacher Page 3. Adding & managing resources o n my Teacher Pages 4. Organizing my Teacher Page 5. Sharing with students and the student experience 6. Sharing resources with other teachers and viewing shared resources 7. Deleting a Teacher Page 8. Transitioning to a new school year 9. Troubleshooting 10. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Getting started with Teacher Pages? Check out our Product Spotlight: Building culture in your classroom with Teacher Pages , available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 Teachers can create personalized Teacher Pages to house a variety of learning resources they want their students to access. Some examples include:

 • District-wide applications 
• Various Clever Library applications 
• Links to online tools (e.g., Schooltube, Wikipedia.org , Dictionary.com )
• PDFs for vocab lists, booklets, permission slips, etc.
• Homework or assignments in Google Drive, Office Online, or Google Classroom
• Community websites (e.g., local public library)
 
 Before we talk about building Teacher Pages, let's talk about the Pages tab. From your Clever Portal > Pages, you'll see three options: 

 • My Teacher Pages , which house: 
 • Your Teacher Pages! All teachers start with two Teacher Pages: 
 • One that is private and not visible to students, which can house various resources that are not student-facing. 
• One that is scoped to students. Page visibility can be modified to include students in just one class, multiple classes, all students, and so on! You can make more pages (more on that below). 
 

• Any resources you’ve favorited by hovering on the icon and clicking the heart.
• All resources you’ve added to Clever via your Teacher Pages (such as Clever Library apps, links, and PDFs).
 

• District Page , which houses any apps, programs, and links your district has shared with you.
• Shared with Me Pages , which houses any Teacher Pages that other teachers at your school have shared with you.
 
 

 

 
 Creating a new Teacher Page

 • In the Clever Portal, click the Pages tab from the left-hand side menu and select My Teacher Pages from the drop-down
• Click Add in the top right-hand corner
 
 

 • Edit the page title, add a description, add class resources, and personalize your icon.
 
 

 • Design your Teacher Page by adding your desired resources , categories , and content.
• Once you're ready to publish your page to make it visible to students, click More page actions > Share at the top of your page, then select the classe(s) you’d like to share the page with.
 

• Click Update .
 
 

 Teacher Login Tools will become available to teachers once they have shared their Teacher Page with students. Visit this article for more!

 

 
 Adding & managing resources to my Teacher Pages 

 There are a variety of different resources that can be added to your Teacher Page!

 Adding resources 

 You can add four types of resources to your Teacher Page(s):

 • Clever Library applications
• District-added applications
• Links to websites
• Uploaded PDFs
 
 Library resources: 

 Clever Library is where teachers can find and adopt new classroom resources. If your district has enabled Clever Library, follow these instructions to add Library apps to your Teacher Page. 

 

 

 District-provisioned applications: 

 Apps your district has added to your Clever Portal say “District” below the app. Adding these to your Teacher Page can make them easier for you and your students to find and access. To add a district app to your Teacher Page:

 • Click Add at the top-right of your Teacher Page.
• Click App 
• A menu will display where you can search for the application and select it. Please note: If you can’t find a district-provisioned application, please contact your Clever district admin to request that they share it with you.
• Click Add <app name> to add it to your Teacher Page.
 
 

 If you notice an app on your Teacher Page has turned gray, this means that your district has removed your access to the app. To remove the app icon from your Teacher Page, click ⋮ (The three vertical dots) > Remove. 

 

 

 Please note: If you don't see the ability to add links or PDFs to your Teacher Page, your Clever administrator may have disabled this setting. Please reach out to your IT team or administrators directly for assistance. 

 Links: 

 You can add links to any website you want students to access. Links are a great way to provide easy access to websites, videos, Google docs, and more! To add a link:

 • Click Add in the top right-hand corner of your Teacher Page and click Link .
• A menu will display allowing you to paste the URL for the page this link should direct to, which should always begin with HTTPS://
• Enter a Title to help your students locate the link.
• Choose a category for your link.
• Select one of the provided icons to represent your link, or upload your own icon!
• To upload your own icon, click Upload to search for the appropriate image from your desktop. Images must be in the .png file format and less than 2 megabytes. 
• Click Add link 
 
 

 When you add a link, the icon will have a little link icon in the top right corner, so you'll know it's a link. This little identifier on the icon isn't visible to students.

 

 

 PDFs 

 If you have a PDF file, you can add that to your Teacher Page. Simply:

 • Click Add in the top right-hand coner of your Teacher Page
• Click PDF .
• Select the file to be uploaded from your device. The max file size is 10 megabytes.
• Enter the File name to display to students.
• Select the Category where you want the file displayed on your Teacher Page.
• Select the desired Icon or upload your own.
• Once the file has been successfully uploaded to Clever (Cloud is Green), click Add PDF 
• The file is now accessible to students on your Teacher Page! 
 
 The icon will have a red PDF icon in the top right corner so you’ll know it's a PDF. This little identifier on the icon isn’t visible to students. 

 Need help downloading your resource as a PDF to upload to Clever? Directions for popular programs can be found below: 

 • Google Docs File > Download > PDF Document (.pdf)
• Google Slides File > Download > PDF Document (.pdf)
• Microsoft Word File > Save as > File Format: PDF > Export
• Microsoft PowerPoint File > Save as > File Format: PDF > Save
 
 When the student clicks on the file from your Teacher Page, the PDF will open in their web browser (e.g., Google Chrome). They can download or print the PDF using the tools in the top-right corner: 

 

 

 
 Organizing Teacher Pages

 To keep your Teacher Page neat and easy for students to navigate, you can organize resources into different categories. Categories provide a way to separate your resources by unit, topic, etc. Please note:

 • Create new categories by clicking the Add button in the top right of your Teacher Page and then selecting Category .
• Categories can be reordered or deleted by using the arrow and trash icons next to the category name. Resources can be reorganized by dragging and dropping them into the appropriate categories.
• If you have a category that you don’t want students to see just yet (such as your next unit of study that’s starting in a few weeks), you can hide the category by clicking the eye icon in the top right of the category!
• When you’re ready for the category to be visible, click on the eye icon again. If a student is already on your page when you hide/unhide a category, they’ll need to refresh the page to see the change.
 
 

 Modifying links and resources 

 Deleting content Links and resources can be deleted from Teacher Pages by following these instructions:

 • From your Teacher Page, hover over the resource or link you'd like to remove and click the three vertical dots (⋮) . 
• In the menu that opens, click Remove 
 
 Copying content to another page You can also copy any link or resource to another Teacher Page that you've created:

 • From

title: For Teachers: Getting help and contacting support
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Contact the school or district 3. Contact the application 4. Contact Clever 

 

 Overview

 This article provides guidance on who to contact for support with Clever-related issues as a teacher. Teachers will learn when to reach out to the school's tech support, contact the application's support team directly, or reach out to Clever Support.

 

 
 Contact the school or district 

 Here are common examples of when your school's support contact will be best equipped to provide support:

 • You or your student is unable to log in to Clever
• An application is missing from the Clever Portal or the icon needs to be updated
• Students or sections are missing from Clever or an application
• Data is missing or incorrect in Clever or an application
• Adding a co-teacher to a class
• Changing your user type in Clever
 
 You can find your district's tech support contact information by clicking Get help at the bottom of your district's Clever login screen. From here, click the District contact. You should see a box appear with an email address or phone number for the support contact. 

 ​ 

 

 

 
 Contact the application

 Here are common examples of when an application’s team will be best equipped to provide support:

 • You or your students click on an application in the Clever Portal and receives an error on the application's page or URL 
• You are receiving an error message on the application’s website
• You are seeing incorrect data within the application, but correct data within the Clever Portal
• You are having trouble accessing grades, homework, textbooks, resources, or running reports within the application
• Your sections have access to the incorrect content within the application
• You have questions about features within the application
• The application does not save a student’s progress
• The application is frozen or logs you or your students out
 
 We recommend reaching out to the application's support team directly. Their contact information can usually be found by searching the app's name and "support contact" in Google.

 

 
 Contact Clever

 For all other questions, Clever Support can be reached by clicking the chat icon on the bottom right of this page. Describe your issue, and tell us if the resource provided fixes your issue. If you click no, you'll have the opportunity to submit a support ticket. Our team is available Monday - Friday 7am - 8pm ET.

title: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Shared devices
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Ho w should students log out of Clever and connected apps? 3. How do students log out of the Clever Portal on any device? 4. How do I log out of Clever and applications while using an iPad? 5. What is the shared devices notification? 6. What about time-out and inactivity? 

 

 Overview

 This article explains how students should properly log out of Clever and connected applications, as well as best practices for shared devices. You'll also find details on shared device settings and automatic logout rules, including inactivity timeouts and browser behavior.

 

 
 How should students log out of Clever and connected apps?

 When logging out, it's important to remember that Clever single sign-on (SSO) can be broken down into three separate login processes behind the scenes:

 • Identity provider login (e.g., Google or Active Directory)
• Clever Portal login
• Application login (e.g., Khan Academy)
 
 If a student is accessing a shared device, Clever recommends that they log out of each application and device after their session. Students should log out of the application, the Clever Portal, and the identity provider (Google, ADFS, etc.).

 

 
 How do students log out of the Clever Portal on any device?

 Users can log out of the Clever Portal by navigating to the top right corner of their Portal (where they can find their name), and clicking Log out. 

 

 

 
 How do I log out of Clever and applications while using an iPad?

 On the iPad, double-click the home button (on newer iPad Pros, swipe up from the bottom of the display) to expose the application thumbnails. Swipe up on the Clever app and any learning apps that were launched. This will end a student's session when the device is shared.

 Alternatively, students can log out of their Clever Portal and each application individually. To log out of the Clever Portal, navigate to the top right corner of their Portal (where they can find their name), and click Log out. Each learning application will have its own logout process.

 

 

 Clever Tip: Your school organization may have custom settings applied to the Clever application that cause users to be logged out of the Clever app any time they launch a resource that brings them away from the Clever Portal. If you have questions about this logout behavior, please contact your admin or IT team directly. 

 
 What is the "shared devices notification"?

 On certain devices, upon initial sign-in to Clever, students will be asked whether or not they should be remembered on the device. If they select No, only I use it; their Clever session will remain intact for 2 hours. 

 

 If the user selects Yes , Clever will treat the device as a shared device. In this case, users will be prompted to sign in as a different user after 10 minutes of inactivity on the Clever Portal.

 

 Clever Tip: this feature gives users who share devices the option to log into another account, in case the best logout practices were not followed 

 

 
 What about time-out and inactivity?

 
 Users are logged out of Clever automatically if: 

 
 • If a user is inactive in Clever for 24 hours, they will automatically be logged out. Currently, there is no way to customize this behavior. 
• The browser is quit (e.g., "Quit Google Chrome " ). Closing the browser window will not log the user out of Clever.

title: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - iOS (e.g. iPads and iPhones)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Basic Troubleshooting 3. Advanced Troubleshooting 

 Please note: The Clever iOS app requires iOS 16.6+. If you are using a device with an earlier iOS, you can still access Clever via the web browser. 

 Overview

 If your student is having issues with their iOS device and Clever, here are some recommended troubleshooting steps you can try to help. 

 
 Basic Troubleshooting

 • Before you begin, please make sure your device is using iOS version 9.0 or above.
• Turn the device off and on again, then try again.
• Uninstall, then re-install the Clever app, then try again.
• Attempt logging in via https://clever.com/badges in the Safari app.
• Attempt using the Clever app outside of your school network (such as on a home network or mobile hotspot).
• Ensure that the iOS device has internet connectivity by navigating to any webpage in the Safari app.
• Ensure that “Private Mode” is not enabled for Safari on the iOS device.
• Ensure cookies are enabled for Safari. This includes the device-wide setting, as well as for the Clever domain ( clever.com ). Settings > Safari Block All Cookies . Make sure Block All Cookies is toggled off -- you will know it is off when the slider turns grey.
• Ensure Javascript is enabled for Safari. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Safari > Javascript .
• Ensure camera access is enabled for Safari. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Safari > Camera .
 
 

 
 Advanced Troubleshooting

 • Ensure that on the device, the iOS network deny list is not enabled. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > General > Restrictions > Websites .
• Ensure that requests to Clever servers are not blocked or filtered by a firewall or network filtering software. See our Help Center article: Which Clever's domains should my network grant access ?
• If using Safari, please ensure that the setting for Request Desktop Website is disabled. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Safari > Request Desktop Website .
• In iOS 14 and later, users have the ability to choose a default browser other than Safari. Please ensure that Safari is the default browser for any device that will be used with the Clever app. You can adjust this by navigating to Settings > Browser > Safari .
• Ensure that no other software affects the way that Safari operates.
 
 If the issue still persists, feel free to open a ticket with Clever Support!

title: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Data (e.g. rosters, classes, and school association)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Data is incorrect in Clever 3. Data is incorrect in an application 

 

 Overview

 This article covers how to address data that's incorrect in Clever or a learning application you use.

 
 

 Data is incorrect in Clever

 Your district's data is synced to Clever from your school's Student Information System (SIS). If you are noticing incorrect information in Clever, please contact your school's tech support contact. 

 Here are examples of incorrect data you might see in Clever that your District Administrator can help with:

 • Missing / incorrect students on your roster
• Incorrect, duplicate, or missing classes in Clever
• Wrong school association
• Adding a co-teacher to your class
• Incorrect information for yourself or a student (a misspelled name, email, grade level, etc.)
• Updating a Clever username for yourself or a student 
• Duplicate accounts
 
 

 
 Editing Data in Clever 

 Records cannot be edited or manually created in Clever by teachers. We cannot assist with updating rosters, editing names, or adding classes. Please contact your district's tech support contact for assistance with these requests. 

 
 Data is incorrect in an application

 Application partners manage syncing data from Clever and implementing it in their system. If you notice that information is incorrect in an application but is correct in Clever. Please contact the application's support team directly. 

 We recommend providing the following information: 

 • District name + state
• School name
• Teacher email address
• Description of problem
 
 Example: 

 • Clark County School District, NV 
• Boulder City High School 
• sample@nv.ccsd.net 
• Missing class roster - Algebra I, 6th period 
 
 Please note, most applications sync data from Clever on a daily basis. If the missing or incorrect data was recently changed in your SIS, please allow at least 48 hrs for the data to be updated in applications.

title: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Apps
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshoot app login tool 3. App is missing 4. Clicked on app, received an error 5. Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password 6. Data incorrect in apps 7. Problems within apps 

 

 Overview

 Occasionally, you or your students will have issues with your applications (i.e. apps) in Clever. Here are some steps you can take if you or your students are having trouble logging in to applications from the Clever Portal.

 ​​

 
 

 Troubleshoot app login tool

 The Troubleshoot App Login tool helps you quickly solve student app issues. Use it to:

 • 
 Find out why a student can’t see or log into an app

• 
 Report a login issue to your Clever Admin

• 
 Set student credentials for SSO Saved Passwords apps

• 
 Connect with Clever Support if the issue needs more investigation

 
 

 

 

 
 App is missing

 If you or your student is missing a district-provided application, please reach out to your district's admin support using the Troubleshoot app login tool. Your district's Clever Admin manages which applications teachers and students have access to in the Clever Portal. 

 To reach out to your administrator: 

 • 
 Navigate to My Students > Student Roster > select the student who is missing an app.

• 
 Click Troubleshoot app login 

 
 

 • 
 Click the dropdown to select the app that is missing.

 
 

 • 
 Provide additional details for your admin.

 
 

 • 
 Click Send email to district admin . 

 
 

 ​​

 
 

 Clicked on app, received an error

 If you or your students click on an application in the Clever Portal and receive an error, please reach out to the application's support team for help . 

 Clever Tip: If you are seeing an error at a web URL that is not a Clever address, it's likely that the application's team is best equipped to help fix the issue. Please reach out to the application's support team to investigate further! 

 Here are examples of common errors you might see for specific applications:

 

 

 

 ​​​

 
 

 Clicked on app, Clever asked for a username and password

 If a Clever page asks you or your student to enter their username and password for an application, enter the correct login information for that application, and click Save . If the device has the Clever browser extension downloaded, Clever will save this information and automatically log the student in the next time they click the icon from the Portal!

 

 

 

 If your student clicks on an application and sees the page below, this means we were not able to log your student in with the username and password they provided. Please have them enter their updated username and password for the application, and click Update & Log in .

 

 

 

 You can also support your student by entering their credentials for them. To manually enter credentials for a student:

 • Navigate to My Students > Student Roster > select the student missing an app.
• Click Troubleshoot app login 
 
 

 • Click the dropdown to select the app that is missing.
 
 

 • Confirm if the student can see the application from their Clever Portal. You'll need to contact an Admin for help if they can't.
 
 

 • Click Update credentials .
 
 

 • Enter the correct username and password. Click Set credentials .
 
 

 

 If you are still having trouble, please confirm that the username and password entered is correct to log into their account directly on the application's page (i.e. at duolingo.com ). If you can successfully access the app directly through their website, but not through Clever, please reach out to Clever Support for help!

 Did you know? Applications that prompt users to manually enter credentials as shown above are called SSO (Saved Passwords) applications. Learn more about these applications in this Help Center article: For Teachers: What are Saved Passwords applications? 

 ​​

 
 Data incorrect in apps

 If the data in an application is incorrect (e.g. rosters, students, and school associations), please see this article . 

 ​​

 
 Problems within applications

 After you click on an application from the Clever Portal, your experience within their product is controlled from the application’s end. If you are running into an issue within the application, please contact the application’s support team for help. 

 Here are common examples of when an application’s team will be best equipped to provide support:

 • You are receiving an error message on the application’s website
• You are seeing incorrect data within the application, but correct data within the Clever Portal
• You are having trouble accessing grades, homework, textbooks, resources, or running reports within the application
• Your sections have access to the incorrect content within the application
• You have questions about features within the application
• The application does not save a student’s progress
• The application is frozen or logs you or your students out
• You need to merge or match old account

title: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Backup Login Codes 3. Problem logging into Clever 4. Problem logging into an app 5. Clever Badges 6. Reset a student's password 7. Logging out 8. How do I get help? 

 

 Overview

 First, please ensure the student is using a Clever-compatible device. Clever's system requirements can be found here .

 If your student is using a compatible device or browser and is receiving an error when attempting to log in to Clever, please ensure they are listed in your roster by navigating to My Students > Student Roster in your Clever Portal. This is to confirm the student has an active record in Clever. If the student is missing, please see this article . 

 

 

 Looking for additional support on quickly getting up to speed on Clever? Check out our Clever Academy Quick Start videos 

 

 
 Backup Login Codes

 Backup codes are temporary codes that allow students to log in to Clever when their login credentials are not working. These codes can be generated multiple times per day by various teachers, ensuring the student can log in continually until the primary login issue is resolved. Backup codes expire after 15 minutes , therefore the student does need to log in before the expiration time, or another backup code will need to be generated.

 

 To generate a backup code: 

 • From your Clever Portal, click the My Students drop-down on the left-hand side and select Student Roster 
• Select the class the student is in from the Filter by class drop-down 
• Locate the student record from the list and click Get backup login code on the right-hand side 
 
 

 • A seven-digit backup code will be provided. Write this code down and pass it along securely to your student. The code will expire in 15 minutes . 
 
 

 Clever Tip: Want a downloadable PDF for backup login code instructions? Find it in the work aid section here . 

 

 How does a student log in using a Backup Code? 

 • The student will go to your district's Clever Portal login page and select Get help . 
• They will then select Use Backup Code 
• They will enter the backup code, and select Log in. 
 
 

 For more information on login errors , please see For Teachers: How do I troubleshoot logging into the Clever Portal? 

 

 
 Problem logging into Clever

 Login errors can occur for several reasons. The most common issues are: 

 • The student inputs the incorrect login credentials 
• The student doesn't have an account in Clever 
• There is an issue with the district's login setup 
 
 

 

 If a student is having difficulty logging in with their credentials, i n the short term, you can log your student in using a Clever backup code . However, for third-party accounts (Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google, your school may have enabled it so that you can reset the student's password. Please learn more here: For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students . 

 

 If your student receives a login error and your district's login method is Clever Passwords (i.e. 'Log in with Clever'), please ensure the student is using the correct credentials. If you have confirmed they are using the correct credentials but the login is still unsuccessful, you may be able to reset their password if your district has enabled teachers to do so. 

 If you do not have the ability to reset the student's password, please have them log in to Clever using a backup code , and contact your district's technology support contact directly for assistance with the login issue. 

 
 Problem logging into an app

 If a student is encountering an error when trying to access an application, please see this article .

 

 
 Clever Badges

 Badges allow students to hold up a physical badge to their device's webcam to get logged in, instead of typing in login credentials. Teachers get more time to teach and students get more time to learn! Please note, that Badges must be enabled by your district before they can be used. 

 For information and instructions, please visit: 

 • For Teachers: What are Clever Badges? 
• For Teachers: How do I print Clever Badges? 
 
 

 
 Reset a student's password

 If your district's students see an option that says Password (Clever Passwords) or a third-party account such as Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google on their Clever login screen, like the screenshot below, you may be able to reset your student’s password.

 

 

 You can only reset students' passwords if your district has enabled it for teachers. To learn more about resetting passwords, check out this article: For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students . 

 
 Logging out

 To log out of Clever, navigate to the Clever Portal, hover over your name in the top-right corner, and click Log out . 

 
 

 Users are also logged out of Clever automatically after: 

 
 • 24 hours of inactivity 
• The browser is quit (e.g. "Quit Google Chrome " ). Closing the browser window won't log the user out of Clever. 
 

 
 How do I get help?

 L ogin credentials for Clever are managed by the school district. If you are unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please reach out to your district support contact or the district's help desk for assistance. 

 You can find your district's technology support contact information on the Clever login screen. In most cases, districts provide an email address, phone number, or instructions guiding users to the appropriate contact person or department for assistance.

 

 

 

 tags: password reset for students

title: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Teacher logins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Problem logging into Clever • Log in with Clever • Log in with Google • Log in with Active Directory • Log in with Canvas • Log in with LDAP 3. Problem logging into an app 4. Logging out 5. How do I get help? 

 Overview

 This article outlines the various login issues or error messages that you may encounter when attempting to log in to Clever. Please note that not all error messages are included in this article. Please double-check that you're typing in the correct login credentials and that you have an account in Clever. 

 Please note: If you're encountering an error from an application when attempting to access it via Clever, please contact the application's support team. Clever support cannot assist with troubleshooting application-side login errors. Learn more here . 

 

 If your login issues persist after reviewing this article, p lease contact your tech support contact directly for assistance. You can find their contact information by selecting the Get help logging in! option from your school organization's login page. 

 For instructions on how to help your students log in to Clever, please visit For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins 

 Problems logging in to Clever

 Log in with Clever

 

 If you see I nvalid username or password the credentials you entered don’t match your Clever account.

 

 Select Get help logging in! to reset your password using the email linked to your Clever account.

 Some school organizations don’t allow self-service password resets. If you don’t see the reset option or it doesn’t resolve the issue, contact your school’s tech support.

 

 Log in with Google 

 Enter your school-provided email address , not a personal @ gmail.com account. Use the password associated with your school email.

 Below are possible errors you may encounter when logging in and steps to resolve:

 

 
 Error

 
 Details and next steps

 
 
 Couldn't find your Google account

 

 This is a Google error. Please confirm you're using your school-provided email, not your personal email. If you still receive the error, please reach out to your school support contact to determine your correct email. 
 
 Wrong password. Try again or click Forgot password to reset it. 

 

 This is a Google error. You’ve entered an incorrect password for their school-provided Google email address. Please enter the correct password for their account or reset it if you’re able. If you can’t reset your password, please reach out to your school support contact for help. 
 We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email xxxx@gmail.com . Try again with your school issued email address. This is a Clever-related error. This error means that the email address you entered isn’t the same one linked to your Clever account. Please make sure you are using your school-provided email address. If you believe you’re using the correct email address, please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. The Clever team cannot provide login credentials. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the email xxxx@example.com This is a Clever-related error. This error means that the email address you’re attempting to log in with is duplicated in Clever. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. The Clever team cannot edit email addresses or provide login credentials. 
 We couldn’t log you in. This is a Clever-related error. You may receive this error with additional details or by itself. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for further assistance in troubleshooting. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 

 For any other login error that states We couldn’t log you in please contact your school’s IT team directly for assistance. Provide them all of the descriptive information in the error, the Reference ID, and any suggested Admin troubleshooting steps. The Clever team cannot provide your login credentials or edit your email address in Clever.

 Log in with Active Directory 

 

 When selecting this option, you should be redirected to your school's login page.

 Below are possible login errors you may encounter and steps for troubleshooting:

 

 
 Error

 
 Details and next steps

 
 
 We couldn’t log you in. We can’t find a Clever account for the email address or username.

 

 This error means the email address or username you entered isn’t in the school organization's Clever account. Please make sure you are using your school-provided email or username. If you’re using the correct email or username, please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. The Clever team cannot provide login credentials. Provide the error and the copyable Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 We couldn’t log you in. Multiple accounts are linked to the student’s email or username.

 

 This error means that the email address or username you’re attempting to log in with is duplicated in Clever. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for help troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. The Clever team cannot edit email addresses or provide login credentials. 
 We couldn’t log you in. You may receive this error with additional details or by itself. Please contact your school’s IT team directly for further assistance in troubleshooting. Provide the error and the Reference ID to your school’s team. 
 
 If you have issues logging in to Active Directory, please contact your school’s tech support directly as Clever doesn’t have access to login credentials.

 

 Log in with Canvas

 

 If you see the error message We couldn't log you in, we can't find a Clever account for the email or username , this means that Clever is unable to locate an account based on the credentials you provided. 

 For any other login error that states We couldn’t log you in please contact your school’s IT team directly for assistance. Provide them all of the descriptive information in the error, the Reference ID, and any suggested Admin troubleshooting steps. The Clever team cannot provide your login credentials or edit your email address in Clever.

 

 Log in with LDAP

 

 If you encounter an LDAP login error after entering your login credentials, please contact your school support contact as Clever doesn't have access to login credentials. 

 

 Problem logging into an app

 If you are having an error when trying to access an application, please take a look at this article . 

 Logging out

 To log out of Clever, navigate to the Clever Portal, click the person icon in the top-right corner, and click Log out . 

 

 
 

 Users are also logged out of Clever automatically after: 

 
 • 24 hours of inactivity 
• The browser is quit (e.g. "Quit Google Chrome " ). Closing the browser window will not log the user out of Clever. 
 

 How do I get help?

 L ogin credentials for Clever are managed by your school organization. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please reach out to your tech support contact or your school's IT help desk for assistance. 

 You can find your tech support contact information on the Clever login screen. In most cases, school organizations provide an email address, phone number, or instructions guiding users to the appropriate contact person or department for assistance.

 

 If you have any questions or need further assistance, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help!

title: For Teachers: Getting Started with Khan Academy in the Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Teachers • Getting Started • Creating a New Account • Logging into an Existing Account • Student Registration After Class Creation • Tips and Troubleshooting 3. Students ​​​​​​​• Getting Started ​​​​​​​• Creating a New Account ​​​​​​​• Logging into an Existing Account (13 years and older) 

 Overview

 Khan Academy can now be found in the Clever Library for free! The Clever Library is a place for teachers to discover, learn about, and adopt new resources for their classrooms. Teachers that have access to Clever and the Library can install Khan Academy and gain the benefit of automatic rostering of their classes for them, and their students gain Single Sign-on (SSO), so no new username or password to remember! If your district uses Clever, you’ll have access to these benefits whether you’re new to Khan Academy or already using us. 

 This article is for teachers new to using the free Clever Library app with Khan Academy. It is not for teachers in districts with a Khan Academy Districts partnership and roster at the district level using Clever Secure Sync. Below are the steps for new or existing Teachers and Students to get started via Clever Library. 

 
 Teachers: Getting started

 Log in to Clever, choose the Library tab, and find Khan Academy by browsing or using the search bar at the top of the Library. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Next, click on the blue Install button for Khan Academy. Then, choose which of your Teacher Page(s) you'd like to add Khan Academy. 

 

 Then you’ll be taken to Khan Academy. You'll be asked whether you want to create a new Khan Academy account or if you already have an existing school account you want to use: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Here's an example of what Khan Academy will look like once added to a Teacher Page: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 Teachers: Creating a New Account

 If you don’t already have a Khan Academy account through your school, click Create a new account , confirm or enter your school email, and then click Sign up . 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 This will create your new account, and your classes will automatically be rostered for you (this can take a few minutes the first time). After the rostering process is complete, you’ll be taken to your Teacher Dashboard: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 You can also go to the Khan Academy website and sign up directly. To do this, click on the Sign up link in the upper right corner of the home page, and then choose the sign up as a Teacher button. Click on the Continue with Clever : 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 If you choose that option and are already logged into Clever, you’ll be asked to confirm that you want to share your class data with Khan Academy: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 If you’re not logged into Clever, you’ll be asked to do that first. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 There’s one final step you’ll want to complete if you’re creating a new Khan Academy account via Clever Library, which is to verify the email you provided when you signed up. You’ll receive an email that looks like this: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Click on the Link this email address button in the email. You’ll be brought to the Khan Academy login screen; click on the Continue with Clever option: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 This will enable you to verify your email with us, which will add it to your Settings under Email. 

 

 
 Teachers: Logging into an Existing Account

 If you already have a Khan Academy account through your school, click Log into your existing account . You can log in to that existing account using Google (click on Continue with Google ) or by entering your school email address and your password. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Once you’ve logged into your Khan Academy account, you’ll be taken to your Teacher Dashboard, where you’ll see your Clever-rostered classes, as well as any other classes you had previously created. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 Student Registration After Class Creation

 Note that there is no class code, and you should not give one out ; your students need to complete their registration by logging into Clever and choosing the Khan Academy tile in their portal, then go through the steps to create or sign into their account. 

 Once in your Teacher Dashboard, you’ll see that, your classes have been created and your students have been rostered, but the class student count will show as zero. Once your students complete their registration and access the Khan Academy through the Clever portal, you’ll see them in your Student Roster as normal. 

 Until then, if you select one of your newly rostered classes, you’ll see that the students are listed as pending: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Please also note there is no class code, and you should not give one out ; your students need to complete their registration by logging into Clever and choosing the Khan Academy tile in their portal, then go through the steps to create or sign into their account. 

 Once your students have completed this process, the number of students in your Khan Academy class(es) will match what you see in Clever for your roster and you will be able to give them assignments or mastery goals. This is easy to do; to start assigning you just open “Assignments” and select “Assign” in the left-hand navigation and you’ll be asked to add a course, like this: 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 If your students haven’t completed signup by logging into Clever and choosing the Khan Academy tile, they won’t be reflected in your student count, and they’ll still show as pending in your Student Roster. 

 

 
 Tips and Troubleshooting

 There are a few other helpful things to know about how Khan Academy works with the Clever Library: 

 Syncing with Clever

 Your student and class data in Khan Academy will sync with your Clever roster each time you log into your Khan Academy account, so if you’re missing updates, try logging out and back in again. 

 Information only syncs one way, from your school district’s SIS to Clever to Khan Academy. In other words, the Clever roster “rules”; we consider your Clever roster the source of truth. This means that any student additions made to your district or schools’ SIS will flow through to Clever, and then on to Khan Academy, and you should not take any additional action in Khan Academy to add students. Additions to your rosters will automatically refresh the next time you log in. 

 Khan Academy receives very limited data on your students (first name and last initial only, and no email via the roster data). This means that you will see limited data on your students in your Teacher Dashboard, and email address updates made in Clever won’t flow through to Khan Academy. 

 

 
 

 Students: How do my students get started with Khan Academy through the Clever Library?

 Since the Clever Library is for teachers to discover applications they may want to use with their classes, students don’t have access to it directly. 

 Students see the Khan Academy tile in their student portal (through your teacher’s page) after you’ve found Khan Academy in the Clever Library, installed it, and completed your activation process. It’ll look something like this: 

 

 

 Students can favorite Khan Academy so it’s easy to find within their portal, and also search for it within their portal. Once your students find Khan Academy in their student portal, they select the tile to begin their activation process. 

 They’ll be able to create a new account for Khan Academy or (if they’re 13 or older) log into an existing school account (a Khan Academy account that has their school email associated with it). 

 

 
 Students: Creating a New Account

 The first time a student selects the Khan Academy tile, they’ll see a welcome message urging t

title: For Teachers: Clever Library - sharing applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I share the apps I installed from the Library with others? 3. What will teachers see when I share an app with them in Clever? 

 Overview 

 There are hundreds of apps in the Clever Library that you can securely sign up for at no cost. These apps will create accounts for your students automatically. Read more about the Clever Library here . 

 If you think that an app would be useful for other teachers, you can easily share it through Clever, email, social media, or your preferred channel! The people you share with can view the details about the app, read reviews, and browse screenshots. If they have a Clever account and have access to the Clever Library, they’ll be able to sign up for the app for free through Clever, too! 

 Please note: If you don’t see the Library icon in your Clever Portal, or if the Install button for a Library application is gray and unclickable, your Clever Admin has disabled the Library or blocked the application. Learn more here or contact your admin or IT team for more information. 

 

 

 
 How do I share the apps I installed from the Library with others? 

 Here are the different ways you can share an app you’re using via the Clever Library: 

 • Go directly to the app detail page in the Library: Search for the app you’re interested in, click the button to Share , and select to share with teacher(s) in your grade, or specific teachers. 
 
 

 • Respond to a prompt from Clever: To make it easier for you to let your peers know about apps you and your students are enjoying in the classroom, Clever may prompt you occasionally in your Clever Portal. In this prompt, we’ll ask you if you’d like to share one of the Library apps you’re using with other teachers in your school. 
 
 We hear from SO many teachers that they treasure these recommendations from their peers, so don’t hesitate to share! 

 

 
 What will teachers see when I share an app with them in Clever? 

 The people you choose will get a notification in their Clever Portal that you shared an app with them, accessible via the bell icon in their Clever Portal: 

 

 

 When they check out your recommendation, you’ll also receive a notification accessible via the bell icon in your Clever Portal: 

 

 Happy sharing!

title: For Teachers: Clever Library - adding applications
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Locating resources in the Library 3. Installing resources from the Library 4. What will my students see? 5. How do I remove a resource installed from the Library? 6. Why might my school system restrict the Library? 

 

 Overview

 Clever Library is a safe way for teachers to explore new classroom tools. Instead of finding new applications that may have varied data privacy standards, teachers can try new apps from the Library with data-privacy guardrails and admin oversight. When a teacher adopts a Library application with SSO, Clever shares only the absolute minimum information: student first name, last initial, grade level, and course enrollment. The resources in the Library can be added to individual Teacher Pages for easy access by students and teachers.

 

 
 Locating resources in the Library

 To find resources in Clever Library, please follow these steps: 

 • Log in to your Clever Portal. 
• From there you can either: 
 • Select the Library tab on the left sidebar to browse for resources and install apps for your students and classes. 
• Or, use the search bar at the top of the page to locate specific resources to install.
 

 
 

 If you follow the steps above and you are unable to locate Clever Library or locate a specific resource, either:

 • 
 Your Clever Admin has disabled the Library. You won’t see a Library tab in your Clever Portal. If you attempt to add an application directly via their website using a Sign Up with Clever button, you’ll see a notification that “Your district has disabled sign up with Clever”.

• 
 The app isn’t available in Clever Library and may be a district-level application that your Clever Admin must add.

• 
 The application isn’t a Library application, and you must add it to your Teacher Page as a link. 

 
 
 Installing resources from the Library 

 Adding resources from the Library is quick and easy! However, before installing a new resource, please be sure to follow your district and school policies for vetting new applications. This may include taking steps with your IT or administrative team to confirm the application abides by your district’s security and data privacy policies.

 Once confirmed, locate the resource in Clever Library, and select the blue Install button at the top right corner. 

 

 

 Please note: If you navigate to a Library application and see the Install button is grayed out, your school has blocked this application. You can read more about Library restrictions below or reach out to your IT team or Clever Admin for more information. If a Clever Admin blocks an application you’re already using, the application will be removed for your Teacher Pages and student Portals. 

 

 Installing an application will add it to your Teacher Page and also automatically set up single sign-on (SSO) accounts for you and your students. There is no need to manually input student information, or print out password cards! 

 Library applications require certain data points from Clever in order to set up single sign-on for you and your students. This includes data such as first name, last initial, grade. Library applications only receive a limited set of data from Clever, however, when installing a Library resource, Clever may ask you to verify that you want to share this data with the application.

 An example of the prompt you may receive to share data with a Library app 

 
 What will my students see?

 Students don't have access to browse resources in Clever Library. However, they will see any Library resources you made visible to them in your Teacher Page!

 

 When your students click on your Teacher Page icon from their Clever Portal, they'll be presented with your designed page and associated applications.

 

 For more information, please visit: For Teachers: What are Teacher Pages? 

 

 
 How do I remove a resource installed from the Library?

 

 If you no longer want a Clever Library app to show up on one of your Teacher Pages, head to the Teacher Page where you added the Library app.

 Then, click the three vertical dots and then select Remove from your Teacher Page.

 

 A pop-up will appear confirming you'd like to remove the app. Click Remove .

 

 

 The app will be removed from your Teacher Page and will no longer be visible to students on that Page.

 

 The library app will continue to appear on your Resources page unless you Uninstall it by going to Clever Library, finding the app, removing it from any Teacher Pages, and clicking Uninstall .

 

 

 For more information about Teacher Pages, please visit: For Teachers: What are Teacher Pages? 

 

 
 Why might my school system restrict the Library?

 Your school system’s Clever Admin can block specific applications in the Library or disable the Library entirely. Clever gives admins this control because school systems often have different policies about which applications can be used with students, and they must comply with student data privacy and cybersecurity laws that vary by state.

 With cyber threats on the rise—and attacks becoming more sophisticated through AI—some school systems are taking a stricter approach. This allows them to oversee where and how student data is shared. Since school systems, not teachers, are ultimately responsible for any student data breaches, many are tightening control over which applications students can access to better protect sensitive information.

 If you have questions about why a Library application has been blocked or why the Library is disabled, we recommend contacting your IT team. They can explain any review or approval processes in place for the applications you’d like to use with students.

title: For Teachers: Chromebooks - the student experience
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Log in with Clever Badge 3. Log in with Google 

 Overview 

 School organizations can configure Chromebooks to allow students to log in with both Clever Badges and Google credentials. Please reach out to your Clever administrator if you have questions about your school organization's Chromebook configuration. Find the expected login flows below for Clever Badges and Google credential logins to Chromebooks.

 

 
 

 Log in with Clever Badge 

 1. After opening the Chromebooks, students should select the Next button. 

 

 2. Once students select the Next button, the Badge screen will appear.

 

 3. The students will hold their Badges up to the camera. 4. A green checkmark will appear, indicating a successful login.

 

 

 
 

 Log in with Google 

 1. After opening the Chromebooks, students should select the Sign in with a different account button. 

 

 2. They'll then be prompted by Google to enter their Google credentials. 3. Students enter their Google credentials, and click Next, they'll be signed into the Chromebook.

title: For Teachers: Clever Badges - PINs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What are Badge PINS? 2. Setting student PIN 3. Troubleshooting forgotten PINs 

 

 What are Badge PINs? 

 For best security practices, your district requires a secure PIN for older students to log into Clever when using a Badge. Clever requires a PIN for all students grades 7 and above that login using a Badge. A district can choose to adjust this setting to require PINs for Badges beginning at an earlier grade. 

 
 Setting student PIN 

 To set a PIN, students will navigate to clever.com/badges , hold their Badge up to the camera, and then be authenticated to the page displayed below. 

 The first time a student uses a PIN-enabled Badge, they will see the screen below that will prompt them to set a PIN. 

 On this screen, the student will enter their new PIN twice and then be signed in to Clever! After this initial setup, your students will be able to log into Clever using their Badge and their PIN! 

 

 

 

 
 Troubleshooting forgotten PINs 

 In order for your student to reset their Badge PIN they will need to be logged in to their Clever Portal. If your student has forgotten their PIN, they can log in to Clever by using a Backup Login Code . You are able to generate a Backup Login Code from your teacher Portal. From the teacher’s Clever Portal: 

 • Log into your Clever Portal and navigate to My Students > Student Roster 
• Choose the student you wish to help 
• Click Get backup login code on the right-hand side for that student. You will see a six-digit backup code. Write this code down and pass it along securely to your student. The code will expire in 20 minutes. 
 
 

 

 How does a student log in using a Backup Code? 

 • The student will go to your district's Clever Portal login page and click “Get help signing in” under the password field 
• The student will then click “Log in with a Backup Code” 
 
 

 

 • The student will then enter the Backup Code on the next screen, and click Log in. This will log them into their Clever account 
 
 Once the student is logged into their Clever Portal, they can reset their 6-digit PIN by clicking on their name in the top-right corner, and selecting ' Reset your Badge PIN '.

title: For Teachers: Clever Badges - iOS
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Students who log in using Clever Badges 3. Logging in using credentials 4. Accessing Clever apps 5. Logging out of the Clever app 

 Overview

 This article will cover the expected student experience for the Clever iOS app on iPads or mobile devices running on iOS version 16.6 or later. If your students' experience doesn’t match what is described below, please reach out to your Clever Admin or IT team about updating your district to the latest version of the Clever App from the Apple App Store found here. 

 

 Please note: The Clever iOS application doesn't support teacher logins. If you'd like to log in to Clever on an iOS device as a teacher, please use a web browser, such as Safari, Google Chrome, etc. 

 

 
 Students who log in using Clever Badges

 These are the instructions for students who will be logging in to Clever using Badges on iOS devices:

 • Download the Clever app on your students’ iOS device.
• Select the Clever application.
• Display the student's Badge to the camera (your students have the ability to toggle between the front-facing and back-facing camera).
• If the student is using a valid badge, a green checkmark should appear with sparkles to signify a successful login!
• The student will be logged into the Clever Portal and all of its features, including Teacher Pages, directly in the Clever app. When they select a learning application, the app will either open in a web browser or via the iOS application. The app itself determines this behavior and it can’t be changed by Clever.
 
 

 
 Logging in using credentials 

 The Clever iOS app works best for students using Badges. Students and teachers who are not using Badges can log in to Clever directly from a Safari web browser. 

 • When opening the Clever app, users will see the Badge Camera.
• They’ll select the Password option at the bottom of the screen.
• The user will locate their school from the Clever login page. 
• They’ll then select the method they want to use to login (ex: Login with Google or Login with Clever). 
• The user will type their credentials and then be logged into the Clever application and their Clever Portal.
• Please see our section below on what happens when a user selects a learning application to learn more about the user experience beyond logging in. 
 
 Clever Tip: The Clever app will remember the user's school organization after their first login on the device, so this user won’t need to go through the process of selecting their school again if the Clever app was never fully closed, and device history has not been deleted. 

 

 
 

 Accessing Clever apps 

 

 

 After a student logs into the Clever iOS application, they’ll be able to click on one of their connected applications. The application or external link will open either in a web browser or the iOS application for the app. The app itself determines this behavior.

 If a student wants to go back to their Portal, they should navigate back to the Clever app. From here they can click a new app icon to launch a new app, or access the same app again by clicking its icon.

 

 For details about multiple users on a shared device, visit this article !

 

 
 

 Logging out of the Clever app 

 Students that share iOS devices will need to log out of the Clever application and any learning applications in order to ensure the next student can successfully log in to their own accounts. 

 Users can log out of Clever by clicking their name in the top right corner, then Log out .

 For security reasons, it's best that users on 1:1 devices also log out of the Clever application and any learning applications when they are done using the device.

 If a student doesn’t log out, they will remain logged in as long as their session is valid (~24 hours).

 For more troubleshooting tips, please see this Help Center article: Troubleshooting issues with the Clever iOS app.

title: For Teachers: Clever Badges for students
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I print Badges for a class? 3. How do I print a Badge for a student? 4. How do I void a Badge for a student? 5. Logging in with a Clever Badge 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Badges allow students to hold up a physical badge to their device's webcam to get logged in, instead of typing in usernames and passwords. Badges are perfect for students who have trouble typing or remembering complex passwords - especially younger learners. Teachers get more time to teach, and students get more time to learn!

 Badges are tied to the student. As such, changes in school, teacher, or section will not affect the Badge.

 If the administrators on your school organization's Clever account have enabled Badges there are several ways you can manage Clever Badges as a teacher. You can print, regenerate, and void Badges for individual students, print Badges for whole classes, and even void Badges for all students en masse at the end of a school year.

 

 

 Please note: If you don't see the option to download student Badges, from your Student Roster tab, this means that Badges are not enabled for your school or grade level. Please reach out to your school organization's tech support for more information. 

 

 Pro Tip: Security Badges provide direct access to a student's Clever account and should be protected just like a password. Anyone who has access to a Badge can sign in as the student and access their accounts in both Clever and connected applications. Please protect the security of your students and do not share images of the Badge publicly on social media. 

 

 
 How do I print Badges for a class?

 • Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu 
 Student Roster of the teacher Clever Portal" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000cGrl&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl000003y2Jl"> 

• Select the desired Class. 
• Click Download class badges . This will download all Badges for eligible students in your class.
 
 The Badges PDF format matches the Avery label template for 2” square Avery labels . 

 Please note: Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature an expiration date. Existing printed Badges will continue to work past the expiration date. Admins are expected to void and print Badges annually during transition periods, like summer, to keep them secure ahead of the first day of school. 

 

 
 How do I print a Badge for a student?

 Classes 

 • Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu.
• Select the student's class.
• Select the student from your roster and click Download Badge. 
• The Badge will download to your computer, which you can print and give to the student.
 
 

 
 How do I void a Badge for a student? 

 Classes 

 • Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu 
• Select the student's class
• Select the student from your roster and click Void Badge. 
• The Badge will be voided. You can then click Download Badge to download a brand new Badge for that student.
 
 

 To help your student log in with a backup code , please visit: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins 

 ​​

 
 Logging in with a Clever Badge

 • Students navigate to www.clever.com/badges . If prompted, click Allow to provide Clever access to the device camera.
• Hold the Badge up to the camera. Once the Badge is scanned, a green checkmark will appear.
• Students will then be redirected to their Clever Portal.
 
 Please note: Clever Badges are designed only to be used at clever.com/badges . Scanning the Badge with a QR code reader won't log you in to Clever. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Do all students have Badges? 

 • Not all students have access to Badges. Your school organization's administrators can choose which users have access to Badges. If your student doesn't have access to a Clever Badge and will need one, please reach out to your school organization's tech support contact .
 
 Can teachers and staff login using Clever Badges? 

 • No. Clever Badges are only available for students as a primary login method. Teachers can use a Clever Badge as a second factor if your schools use Clever’s Classroom Multi-factor authentication (MFA).
 
 What do I do if I see in my student has a Badge that expires soon? 

 • 
 Existing printed Badges will continue to work past the expiration date. You can reach out to an admin to learn more about when you should expect Badges to be annually voided and reprinted.

title: For Teachers: Logging in to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. New login page 3. Directions for logging in • Log in with Clever • Log in with Google • Log in with Active Directory • Log in with Canvas • Log in with LDAP • Log in with Clever - Change Password 4. Logging out 5. How do I get help? 

 Overview

 This article provides general login instructions for teachers. Please navigate to the appropriate section of this article for additional instructions for your district's single sign-on (SSO) method (e.g. Google, Active Directory, LDAP, etc.).

 • Go to your district’s Clever Portal login page. The web address usually looks like this: https://clever.com/in/ <district name> (for example, clever.com/in/cleverusd ).
 • If you’re not sure what your district’s Clever login page is, visit clever.com/login and search for your school. 
 

• Log in to Clever with your username and password. Your username and password credentials are set up by your school district. They may be your teacher number, ID, or email address.
• After you log in to your Clever Portal, you can open your applications, set up your Teacher Pages, explore the Clever Library, or assist a student.
 
 

 

 

 
 New login page

 Beginning February 3, Clever will have a refreshed login page. If your login page looks a little different, there’s no need to worry. You can still log in the same way you always have. Refer to the image below to see an example of the updated design. This example shows Google and Clever Badges as the login methods. Your login page may look slightly different depending on the login options your school uses.

 

 

 

 If you don’t use your school’s custom portal URL, go to the main login page . You’ll see a login screen like the image below. Select the Password option, then search for your school.

 

 

 
 Directions for Logging in

 

 Logging in with Clever Passwords

 

 If using the Log in with Clever option, enter your district username and password. Your username and password are set up by your school district. They may be your teacher number, ID, or email address. 

 Clever Tip: Your district may have login hints to help determine the correct username and password. Login hints will appear once you click on the username and password fields. 

 

 

 Need help? Your login credentials are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district support contact or help desk for assistance. You can typically locate their contact information by selecting Get help logging in! from your district's Clever login page.

 

 
 Logging in with Google

 

 If you use the Log in with Google option, you will be redirected to Google to sign in with your school-provided email address. Please note: using your personal Google email address won't work. 

 Need help? Your login credentials are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district support contact or help desk for assistance. You can typically locate their contact information by selecting Get help logging in! from your district's Clever login page.

 

 
 Logging in with Active Directory 

 

 

 If you use the Log in with Active Directory option, you will be redirected to sign in to your school account through Active Directory Federation Services. The username and password for ADFS are managed by your district administration. 

 Need help? Your login credentials are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district support contact or help desk for assistance. You can typically locate their contact information by selecting Get help logging in! from your district's Clever login page.

 

 
 Logging in with Canvas

 

 If you use the Log in with Canvas option, you will be redirected to sign in to your school account through Canvas. The username and password for Canvas are managed by your district administration. 

 Need help? Your login credentials are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district support contact or help desk for assistance. You can typically locate their contact information by selecting Get help logging in! from your district's Clever login page. 

 

 
 

 Logging in with LDAP

 

 If you use the Log in with LDAP option, enter your district-provided username and password. This is generally the same username and password you use to sign in to district devices and email.

 Need help? Your login credentials are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district support contact or help desk for assistance. You can typically locate their contact information by clicking Get help from your district's Clever login page. 

 Need help? Your login credentials are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district support contact or help desk for assistance. You can typically locate their contact information by selecting Get help logging in! from your district's Clever login page.

 

 
 

 Log in with Clever - Change Password 

 If you're using the Log in with Clever and your district has allowed teachers to change their passwords, you can do this through the following steps:

 • Log in to your Clever account.
• Click on the person icon in the top right corner of the screen and click Change Password .
 
 

 • Type your current password and desired new password (twice).
• Click Change Password .
 
 

 Please note: If you're logging into Clever through Google , Active Directory, LDAP or Canvas , Clever can't change your password. This is because our system does not have access to your actual password in these instances. If you’ve double-checked that you used the correct credentials but are still having trouble logging in, please contact your district's tech support contact for assistance. 

 

 
 Logging out

 To log out of Clever, navigate to Clever Portal, hover over your name in the top-right corner, and click Log out . 

 
 

 Users are also logged out of Clever automatically after: 

 
 • 24 hours of inactivity 
• The browser is quit (e.g. "Quit Google Chrome " ). Closing the browser window will not log the user out of Clever. 
 
 

 
 How do I get help?

 Login credentials for Clever are managed by the school district. If you're unsure of your login credentials or receive an error, please contact your district's support contact or help desk for assistance. You can find your district's tech support contact information by clicking the Get help link on the Clever login screen. From here, click District contact . You should see a box appear with email or phone number to reach your district's support contact. 

 ​ 

 

 

 

 

 Want to learn more about Clever? Check out Clever Academy !
