title: For Teachers: How do I use Single Sign-On (SSO)?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Single sign-on (SSO) from your district's Clever Portal ​3. SSO from a district website ​4. SSO from an application’s website 

 Overview 

 Single sign-on (SSO) allows you and your students to access hundreds of applications with one click using your existing Clever account. This way, you only need to remember one set of login credentials.

 There are a few options to use Clever's SSO based on your school or district's setup, listed below.

 

 
 Single sign-on from your district’s Clever Portal 

 With your Clever account, you can log in to your district’s Clever Portal, a single page with all of the SSO applications and links set up for you by your district. 

 To access an application, simply click on the icon, and you’ll be taken to the application and logged in automatically. 

 Please note: You and your students will only see the applications your district has set up for you - if your portal or a student’s portal is missing an app, contact your district support contact . 

 

 For more information on how to access your Clever Portal, see How do I log in to the Clever Portal as a teacher ? 

 

 
 

 SSO from a district website 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 In addition to the Clever Portal, your district might also embed Clever SSO links into other websites, such as a district website or an alternative portal like Stoneware or Encore. In these cases, after clicking on the Clever SSO link, you may be asked to log in to Clever. Once you have logged in to your Clever account, you will be taken directly to the application and logged in.

 

 
 

 Single sign-on (SSO) from an application’s website 

 

 Many applications will have a way that you can use Clever SSO directly from their login page - you and your students may simply click Sign In with Clever ” or Log In with Clever to log in to the application using your Clever account.

 If this is your first time signing in to the application via Clever, and if your district has not already created an account for you in the application, the application may ask to create a new SSO account for you based on your existing Clever account. Similar to what you may have experienced before when using “Log in with Google” or “Log in with Facebook,” Clever will ask you if you are okay sharing information with the application about yourself and the classes you teach for the purposes of creating SSO accounts:

 

 

 

 Once you click Allow and continue, you will be logged in to the application and will have SSO accounts created for you and your students! There is no need to manually create accounts or share out class codes. All accounts will be created automatically based on your classes in Clever.

 Once you’re ready to share the resource with your students, be sure to add the resource to your Teacher Page for easy access.

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Academy Course Catalog - Learn more about Clever!
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Learning topics for Clever Admins 3. How can I view my courses or certifications? 

 

 Overview

 Clever Academy is our free online training and certification program for educators. District Administrators can access it through the district dashboard, and School Tech Leads and teachers can access it through their Clever Portal. Alternatively, users can go directly to Clever Academy: https://clever.academy 

 By completing courses, you can learn how to get the most out of Clever for your district (in addition to certifications and badges)! After all, you're already the go-to person for all things edtech. We just make supporting educators a little easier. This content is designed to help you create a smooth, friendly experience with edtech for everyone in your district. 

 You can also access materials to lead trainings for your district, helping to spread the word about Clever’s useful features. Feel free to share these with relevant team members so they too can live the Clever life!

 

 

 
 

 Learning topics for Clever Admins 

 Clever Certified Champion courses

 Level 1: Intro to Clever & getting started (for Clever Admin) In this course, you’ll learn the basics of Clever, from our rostering and single sign-on features, to our suite of additional products. You’ll get the full scoop on how Clever makes it easier and safer for your district to connect to technology. You’ll learn about: 

 • Clever's time-saving tools
• How we protect your district's data 
• How your district’s users will interact with Clever 
 
 Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 1: Clever Cadet. This course is a prerequisite to Level 2 and beyond. Level 2: Getting your district going (for Clever Admin) Get acquainted with the Clever platform and complete the activities to start setting up your Clever account. You’ll learn how to set up your sync, your portal, and Clever Badges. Once your portal is set up, you’ll create user accounts and add applications to your portal. You’ll learn about: 

 • Setting up your district's account with SSO
• Setting up Clever Badges 
• Organizing your district's Clever Portal 
• Adding applications and team members 
 
 Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 2: Clever Coach. This course is a prerequisite to Level 3 and beyond. Level 3: Troubleshooting and advanced sharing rules (for Clever Admin) Learn to master the Clever features that will make your teachers’ and students’ lives easier! In this course, you’ll dig into troubleshooting tools and advantage features, such as adding custom data and connecting Clever to any app, whether it’s integrated with Clever or not. You’ll learn about: 

 • Helping teachers and staff log in to Clever
• Tackling common troubleshooting 
• Working with Advanced District Admin tools 
• Adding custom data 
 
 Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 3: Clever Captain. This course is a prerequisite to Level 4 and beyond. 

 Level 4: Advanced tools & teacher features (for Clever Admin) In this final level, you'll dig into Clever's most advanced features to help you manage your edtech resources. From advanced sharing rules, to analyzing user engagement data, and various teacher tools! You’ll learn about: 

 • Advanced sharing rules & Content mapping to share data precisely with apps
• Analyzing data with Edtech and Portal Analytics to inform your edtech strategy 
• Internalizing Teacher features to support teachers and lead training
 
 Complete this course, and you’ll be certified Level 4: Clever Champion, earning a badge, certificate, and ability to post your new certification on LinkedIn. 

 Clever & Google: Secure Connected Classroom Certification in this course you'll learn how you can use Clever and Google together to deliver a seamless and secure digital learning experience for your district or school. 

 You'll learn about: 

 • Using Google as a login method for Clever 
• Simplifying logins with Clever Badges and Chromebooks 
• Google SSO Applications 
• The Google Classroom roster import application 
• Clever LMS Connect 
• Classroom MFA 
• Clever Identity Management (IDM) 
• Google for Education Plus + Clever IDM: The Google Bundle 
 
 Complete this course and you’ll be certified, earning a badge, certificate, and ability to post your new certification on LinkedIn. 

 Trainer

 Clever Trainers Everything you need to lead an in-person or virtual training at your school, swag included!

 Quick Start Guides

 Quick start videos for DAs Only have a few minutes to learn about Clever? Here are some short videos to get you on your way! When you're ready to earn your official Clever certification, start the certification track by enrolling in Level 1: Intro to Clever. 

 Quick Start - Getting set up with Clever This Quick Start guide will take you through the steps to onboard your school district to Clever. You’ll learn about: 

 • Syncing your schools' data with Clever
• How to connect applications to Clever 
• How to add a login method 
 
 Quick Start - Setting up your Portal Use this Quick Start guide to learn the essentials of the Clever Portal, and how to make it work for the teachers and students in your school district. You’ll learn about:

 • How to customize and organize your district's portal 
• How to add commonly used external links 
• How Clever works for teachers
 
 Quick start - Troubleshooting tips We try to make Clever as easy to use as possible -- but we know there will be moments when you need additional support. We've compiled some of our most frequently asked questions in this course, and given you step-by-step instructions on how to solve them. Come here when you need a refresher or need to train others on these common troubleshooting tips. You’ll learn about:

 • Sync statuses and what they mean 
• What to do when there's "missing information"
• Refresher on logging in
• What to do when Badges aren't working
• What to do when students can't access an application
 
 Quick start - Maximizing Clever in your district So you've got Clever Portal up and running – and now you just need to make sure you roll it out to your users. In this course, we provide all of the steps you need to take, as well as the resources you need to maximize Clever in your district! You’ll learn about:

 • Determining strategies to push Portal usage in the district
• Choosing between a few options to train the district on the portal
 
 Quick start - District cybersecurity Use this guide to support cybersecurity for your schools. This is perfect to bolster your district's security practices!

 

 
 You’ll learn about:

 • The different types of cybersecurity threats that schools most frequently face
• Implementing the most vital cybersecurity best practices to help make your schools more secure
• Leading training for your teachers and staff 
• Next steps to stay in the know on cybersecurity
 

 For directions and information about the Clever Academy specifically for teachers and School Tech Leads (STLs), see these articles: For Teachers: How can I learn more about Clever? (Clever Academy) For School Tech Leads: Clever Academy - Learn more about Clever! 

 

 
 How can I view my courses or certifications?

 You can see the courses you've registered for or completed at any time by navigating to Clever Academy , clicking person icon in the top right, and selecting My Profile. From here, you'll see a list of all your learning paths and courses. Any course with a certification that you've completed will have a View certificate link next to it. Click the link to view the certificate, and then print it if you'd like! Certificates are valid for two years. If it has been more than two years since you completed a course, you would need to re-take the course to become re-certified.

title: Clever Technical Requirements & Supported Devices
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Devices & Internet Browsers 2. Clever Browser Extension 3. iOS Devices 4. Android Devices & Amazon Fire Tablets 

 

 Devices & Internet Browsers

 Clever is compatible with all computer devices (e.g., Microsoft Windows, macOS, Chrome OS/Chromebooks), but we have certain recommendations to provide users with the most optimized experience. Clever supports the following internet browsers.

 • Google Chrome
• Microsoft Edge
• Mozilla Firefox
• Apple Safari (we support this browser for general use with Clever, but not for SSO (Save Passwords) apps, as we do not currently offer a Safari Extension)
 
 While these browsers are compatible with Clever, we recommend using the Chrome browser for the best user experience. Please visit the following articles for specifics around using Clever on Chromebooks:

 • Single Sign-On (SSO): Chromebooks - Setup & Troubleshooting 
• For Teachers: Chromebooks - the student experience 
 
 

 
 

 Clever Browser Extension

 The Clever browser extension is required for specific applications to work correctly on computers and Chromebooks. Specifically, single sign-on (SSO) applications that are integrated with Clever do not require the Clever browser extension, but the browser extension is needed for SSO (Saved Passwords) applications to function correctly.

 • The Clever browser extension is not required for iOS devices (e.g., iPads, iPhones) as these devices use the Clever app , which is downloadable from the device App Store and functions in the same way as the Clever browser extension. 
• For devices where the Clever browser extension cannot be downloaded (e.g., Android), some applications may not be accessible.
 
 Please see the links below for information and instructions for installing the Clever browser extension for your browser:

 • Google Chrome: Install | Directions 
• Microsoft Edge (New): Install | Directions 
• Firefox: Install | Directions 
 
 

 
 

 iOS Devices

 The Clever app is compatible with iOS devices (e.g., iPads, iPhones) versions 9.0 or later and is available for download from the device App Store. 

 See the following article for more information regarding the Clever iOS app: iOS devices (e.g. iPads, iPhones) 

 

 
 

 Android Devices & Amazon Fire Tablets

 Clever does not currently have an app for Android devices. However, users can still use their Android devices to access Clever! Users log in to Clever from Clever.com or their district's custom Clever login page using their internet browser just as they would when using a computer or laptop. Clever recommends using the Google Chrome browser for the best user experience. Users must manually log in to SSO (Saved Passwords) applications when using Android devices. Amazon Fire tablets provide the same user experience as Android. For students to access Clever on the Amazon Kids web browser, you must add Clever.com as an allowable domain . 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For District Admins: Apps - Types of apps 
• Clever Portal: Installing the Clever browser extension 
• For Clever Admins: iOS devices & the Clever app

title: How much does Clever cost districts and schools?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does Clever generate revenue? 

 Please note: If you are outside of the United States, check out our international article for more information. 

 Overview

 Clever is a free service for schools and districts in the United States and Canada! This means Clever doesn’t charge schools or districts to utilize our platform. 

 We offer the following services at no charge to districts!

 • Secure Sync 
• Clever Portal 
• Single-sign on (SSO) 
• Clever Badges 
• Clever Analytics 
• Clever Library 
 
 Optional paid add-ons: 

 • Clever IDM is an optional, paid add-on for school districts. School districts can add Clever IDM to their free Clever digital learning platform to automate identity management to help keep Google and Active Directory in sync with their SIS. 
• Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) allows your students and teachers to securely MFA into Clever to protect all of your Clever applications without the requirement of a second device. This solution helps you stay ahead of cybersecurity threats without disrupting learning. You can add this as an optional, paid add-on.
 
 How does Clever generate revenue?

 Clever's partnerships with online learning providers (i.e. application partners) are what generate revenue for us. Our application partners pay Clever based on their usage of our Application Programming Interface (API) . Essentially, they purchase Clever’s technology, including complex integrations with various Student Information Systems (SIS). This allows our partners to focus on their core business needs instead of dedicating time to complicated district setups. With a Clever integration, application partners avoid building and maintaining custom integrations for every district—freeing up resources and streamlining operations.

 For more information regarding application partner pricing, please visit our pricing page here. 

 

 

 
 

 Related articles: 

 • Clever's Business Model

title: What's Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Secure Sync 3. Single sign-on (SSO) 4. Clever Portal 5. Clever Library 6. Clever Analytics 7. Clever IDM and Classroom MFA 8. Who should sign up for Clever? 

 

 Getting started with Clever? Check out our course Clever Academy for Clever Admin, Level 1 , available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 Clever automates the secure transfer of student rosters to authorized parties, ensuring learning programs always have updated information. We also offer single sign-on (SSO) access for students and teachers through the Clever Portal. Clever's core features are free for school organizations. This means Clever doesn’t charge schools or districts to utilize our platform. For more information about Clever's pricing, please visit: How much does Clever cost districts and schools? 

 

 Clever replaces outdated, insecure systems like emailed CSV files or manual vendor-specific bulk transfers. It's engineered to be the most secure way for school organizations to send data to vendors while granting users access to all their edtech tools with a single set of credentials via the Clever Portal.

 

 Accessibility 

 We understand how important it is for all users to be able to use the Clever platform easily. To ensure we meet the accessibility standards for all students and families, we conduct a voluntary audit each year and update the platform in line with the latest guidelines.

 Please reach out to our support team if you would like more information. You can review our full accessibility statement here .

 

 

 
 Secure Sync

 Secure Sync maintains up-to-date roster information in your learning applications. It securely transfers your roster uploads or connects directly with your Student Information System (SIS) to provision student, teacher, and staff accounts. 

 

 Data is shared according to the sharing permissions you set in your Clever Dashboard, so you're always in control. Learning applications regularly update your enrollment information with any changes. You can learn more about Secure Sync here .

 

 
 Single sign-on (SSO)

 Single sign-on (SSO) allows students and teachers to use a single set of credentials to sign in to all their learning applications. Clever integrates with your existing identity provider (IdP), such as Google or ADFS, or it accepts your own synced credentials. Learn more about SSO here .

 

 
 Clever Portal

 As part of Single sign-on (SSO), each student and teacher has access to a free personalized portal with their learning applications.

 With the Clever Portal, students spend more time learning instead of searching for applications or remembering passwords. Students and teachers can access their learning programs through the Clever Portal in three easy steps: 

 • Navigate to your school organization's custom Portal URL (the Clever login page).
• Enter their familiar username and password, or log in using a Clever Badge. 
• They'll be signed in to their Clever Portal, where they can access connected apps via SSO! 
 
 For more information on using the Clever Portal, visit: For Clever Admins: Managing Clever Portal .

 
 Clever Library

 Clever Library is a place for teachers to discover and adopt new resources for their classroom. Resources in the Library can be added to a teacher's Teacher Page for easy access. You can read more about Clever Library here . 

 

 
 Clever Analytics

 As a Clever admin, you can keep your finger on the pulse of student and teacher engagement with edtech programs. You can view data as charts in Clever, export them as CSVs, or send them to your data warehouse via SFTP. You can learn more about your analytics options here . Teachers also have access to Class Analytics to view student engagement data.

 

 
 Clever IDM and Classroom MFA

 While Clever's rostering and SSO services are free, we also offer powerful paid tools to further streamline your school organization's workflow:

 • Identity Management (IDM) : Clever offers IDM for Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID.
• Classroom Multi-factor authentication (MFA) : We offer an MFA solution designed for K-12 schools that does not require a second device for logins. 
 
 
 

 Who should sign up for Clever?

 Clever's optimized for management at the school organization level. To create an account, you'll need administrative access to your SIS and the authority to sign Clever's Terms of Service. 

 Typical roles with sufficient access include Chief Technology Officers, SIS managers, and other IT Administrators. Teachers and principals usually don't have enough SIS access to set up an account. 

 If you're ready to sign up, please go here: clever.com/district-signup . We're looking forward to working with you!

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • How much does Clever cost school organizations? 
• What's Single sign-on? 
• What's Secure sync?

title: How does Clever protect student data and privacy?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Full District Control 3. Student Data Privacy at Clever 4. Infrastructure 

 Overview

 School districts across the nation rely on Clever to sync roster data with their applications, in part because security is a top priority. Clever keeps student information safe by ensuring: 

 • Full district control over data access rights at all times;
• A robust privacy policy that governs our use of student data; and
• Protection through a tested and secure infrastructure.
 
 

 

 Full District Control

 Districts control data access at all points in the process, from signup all the way through account provisioning. Districts determine: 

 • What data Clever can access (always read-only);
• How often they sync data with Clever;
• Which applications can access data; and
• How much data each application can access (via data sharing permissions ).
 
 Districts can fine-tune these settings at any time through the Clever Dashboard. If a district chooses to discontinue their Clever sync, they can do so at any time. Clever will delete their account and remove any residual data immediately upon request.

 Student Data Privacy at Clever

 Clever syncs basic information to manage user accounts in online learning applications, such as student and teacher identifiers, section names, and enrollment information. 

 All rostering data that is synced with Clever is protected by industry-leading infrastructure (see below). Clever will never use or sell student information for marketing or other purposes. 

 Additionally, Clever maintains FERPA compliance under the FTC's School Officials Exemption, meaning Clever provides a service on behalf of schools that the schools themselves would usually provide. Furthermore, all Clever-supported applications agree to adhere to FERPA requirements in the terms of our partnership. 

 To learn more about data privacy, you can read Clever's full Privacy Policy 

 Infrastructure

 Clever has spent years developing one of the best security infrastructures in K-12 education. Key features of Clever's industry-leading infrastructure are: 

 • AES256 data encryption for data at rest;
• TLS with strong ciphers, with a preference for those with perfect-forward secrecy, for data in transit;
• Multiple factor authentication;
• Third-party security audits and code reviews;
• Responsible disclosure (“bug bounty”) program;
• Full-time security administrator; and
• Employee background checks.
 
 The syncing process is encrypted at all times using 256-bit encryption, which is double the standard used by the consumer banking industry. In addition, Clever's production environment is completely isolated from the internet, and all access is logged and secured using multiple factor authentication . 

 Clever has a full-time security administrator and invests in regular third-party audits in order to ensure continual improvement of security protocols. 

 If you’re interested in learning more, please read more about our comprehensive security program .

title: For Clever Admins: Zoom - transitioning from Upgraded to Basic accounts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. For teachers: adding a password login for Zoom 

 Overview 

 If your district participated in the free Zoom upgrade during school closures, the district Zoom account will revert to Zoom Basic on Wednesday, July 1, 2020 if your district does not upgrade to a Business or Education account. If your account reverts to Zoom Basic on Wednesday, your teachers will encounter two primary changes: SAML SSO through Clever will no longer work, as Zoom provides SAML SSO only to premium customers, and meeting length will be capped at 40 minutes. 

 If you plan to use Zoom through its Basic service after July 1st, we recommend that you: 

 • Communicate the above changes to teachers for awareness. 
• Let teachers know they can set a password to log in manually to their account with their email address by using the directions below. 
• Remove the SSO app from teacher dashboards by clicking “disconnect app” on the Zoom page in your Clever dashboard. This will prevent teachers from becoming confused if they attempt to SSO into Zoom after July 1st and receive an error message 
 

 For teachers: adding a password login for Zoom

 If you have previously logged in SSO via Clever, on Profile , you will see icons indicating SSO, but no icon for email.

 

 Unless restricted by your Account owner, you can easily add an email and password login for the same account. 

 • Sign out of your Zoom existing account.
• Go to zoom.us/signup 
• Enter your school-provided email address that matches your email in Clever.
• Click Sign Up .
• You will receive an email with Sign Up instructions. Click to Activate Account. 
• Enter your desired password twice. Note : Make sure your password meets the requirements .
• Click Continue .
• You can enter in email addresses of contacts to invite or choose Skip this Step. 
• Click Go to My Account. 
• This will take you to My Profile of the existing Zoom account. It should now show the SSO icon that it showed previously, as well as an email icon to indicate that you can log in with email address and password.

title: For Clever Admins: Zoom - Secure Experience
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Directions 3. Testing the Zoom Secure Experience 4. Student Experience 

 Overview

 We’ve heard from school organizations that want to improve Zoom security to ensure the safety of students and teachers. In response, we have worked with Zoom to develop a new solution for schools called the Zoom Secure Experience. This new experience “locks down” meetings in your domain – only teachers, staff, and admins can create meetings, and only verified users (including students) in your domain can join them. 

 Additionally, usernames for individuals joining will be locked. Students will have the default name: "First Name + Last Name" (Example: John Smith) as displayed in Clever. Notably, this Secure Experience does not require students to have Zoom accounts! 

 We think this is a great experience and now recommend it to our school organizations as a best practice. If you’d like to enable Zoom Secure Experience for your school organization, please follow the steps below. 

 Things to Note: 

 • The Zoom Secure Experience requires all users to authenticate via Clever before joining a Zoom meeting. 
• This experience does not give Zoom accounts to students. 
• These instructions assume you’ve already set up Zoom + Clever SSO for your teachers & staff. If you have not already done so, please start by following the instructions here. 
• If there are any users in your school organization who want to join Zoom meetings and aren't in Clever, you'll want to add them to your Clever upload (e.g., staff.csv). 
• 
 If certain users in your school organization need to continue hosting unauthenticated meetings, you can allow this by creating multiple Zoom User Groups and applying the settings below on a group-by-group basis. 

 
 

 
 Directions

 First, please confirm you have completed Zoom + Clever SSO for your teachers & staff as described here: https://support.clever.com/hc/s/articles/360040481852. 

 Clever Setup 

 • Log in as a Clever admin and click this link: https://schools.clever.com/applications/add/saml-zoom-external-auth 
• Add the Zoom External Auth application by selecting Request application. 
• Select the top-left checkbox to enable this application for all users in the school organization and select Request with sharing. 
 
 Please Note: T his is a special application that will not appear in any user-facing portals. This application must be added in addition to the Zoom - Teachers & Staff app you’ve already configured. 

 Zoom Setup 

 • Log in to Zoom 
• Navigate to Account Management > Account Settings 
• Turn OFF "Allow participants to rename themselves" 
• Turn ON “Only authenticated users can join meetings” 
• Turn OFF “Only authenticated users can join meetings from Web client” 
 Image: Steps 3-5 

• 
 Next to “Only authenticated users can join meetings,” click the lock icon to enforce this for all your users: 

• 
 Click Edit next to Sign in to Zoom (Default) 

• In a new tab, navigate to: https://schools.clever.com/applications/saml-zoom-external-auth/settings • Under 'SAML Details' copy the Metadata URL 
• Enter the copied Metadata URL in a new browser tab and press Enter. • Copy the value found between the tags: <ds:X509Certificate> 
• Configure settings in Authentication Configuration as follows and click Save Name : Sign in with Clever Authentication Method : Sign in to External SSO (If this doesn't show up as an option, please reach out to Zoom Support and let them know that External Auth needs to be turned on for your school organization's Zoom account.) Sign-in page URL : https://samlidp.clever.com/saml-zoom-external-auth/assert Identity provider certificate: Enter the X509 certificate from the Zoom External Auth app as identified in steps 8-9. Issuer : http://samlidp.clever.com/services/trust Email Address: <blank> Binding: HTTP-Redirect First Name: FirstName Last Name: LastName 
• In the Zoom dashboard, once you have clicked Save an option to download your “SP metadata XML” will appear. Download and open this file. Locate the entityID and Location values and enter those in Clever as the “Entity ID” and “Assertion Consumer Service URL”, respectively. Zoom: SP metadata XML: Clever: https://schools.clever.com/applications/saml-zoom-external-auth/saml (Note: your values will be different than what’s pictured) 
• Click Confirm to save the variables. 
 
 
 Testing the Zoom Secure Experience

 To test the Zoom Secure Experience: 

 • Create a new meeting using a school organization Zoom account and start the meeting 
• In the Zoom meeting, click the icon at the top left to locate the meeting URL 
• Copy the URL and attempt to join the meeting from a new private/incognito browsing window (make sure to NOT be logged in to Zoom or Clever) 
• You should be prompted to log in to Clever before proceeding. Please note, if using a private/incognito window you will need to search for your school before logging in. During a normal browsing session, if they are clicking on the Zoom link from Clever, they will be automatically authenticated! 
 
 
 Student Experience

 • Student clicks on a meeting link in the Clever Portal
• Student is prompted to authenticate with Clever. If they are already logged into Clever, the authentication will happen automatically! 
• Student enters the Zoom meeting. Their Zoom name will be auto-populated with"First Name + Last Name" (Example: John Smith) Join from your browser: if a student is joining from their browser, they will be prompted to enter a name, however, this name will be overwritten by the Clever authentication. 
 
 Pictured below: self-selected name 

 

 Pictured below: the correct Clever name

title: For App Partners: Tagging your app with key search terms in the Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to add tags to your application 3. Tips and best practices for tagging 4. Examples of common edtech search terms 

 

 Overview

 Teachers can search for applications in the Clever Library by name and using keywords. To improve their applications' discoverability, partners can add up to 10 tags directly in your Clever App Dashboard > Library > Marketing Collateral. 

 Adding tags will give partners the opportunity to provide additional definitions and characteristics about their application, making it easier for teachers to find the right application for their needs. 

 
 How to add tags to your applications

 • Sign into your Clever Dashboard 
• Go to the Library tab in the left-hand menu 
• Go to Marketing Collateral in the left-hand menu 
• Scroll down to App details and click Edit 
• Create tags for your app, then save your changes 
 
 The changes will go live on your app detail page as soon as you save. To see the results on and test the new search results, you can impersonate a teacher via your demo account. 

 

 

 
 Tips and best practices for tagging

 • To optimize search, make sure your tags are less than 24 characters each. 
• Add tags that reference specific skills (i.e. ‘addition’, ‘subtraction’) , features and capabilities (‘student-paced’, ‘gamified’) , or curriculum requirements (‘experimental method’, ‘earth and space science’). These are typical search terms that teachers query with. 
• Add non-Library categories as tags since the Library has 10 official categories. Do not repeat your existing categories as tags because the Clever search algorithm already surfaces your app based on its category. The 10 official categories include: Assessment, Authoring, Classroom Management, English, Math, Presentation, Science, Social Studies, Technology, Other. 
 
 

 
 Examples of common edtech search terms

 • SEL 
• RtI 
• MTSS 
• ELL 
• Adaptive Learning 
• Reading comprehension 
• Classroom management 
• Offline access 
• Group activities 
• Game-based learning 
• Computer science 
• Puzzles 
• Design 
• SPED 
• SAT 
• PSAT 
• Bilingual 
• Standards 
• Video 
• Inclusion 
• Common core 
• Fluency 
 
 For more examples, click here .

title: For Clever Admins: Configuring SSO for Zoom
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup Directions 3. Sending additional attributes 4. Security & Privacy 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 Clever has partnered with Zoom to create a seamless way to provide account creation, as well as Single Sign-On (SSO) access for teachers, staff, and Clever admins. To set up SAML SSO for Zoom, please follow the directions below.

 To configure Single Sign-On (SSO) for Zoom, you must have a paid account (Edu/Business) or a Free "Licensed" Trial account provided by Zoom. You can check your account type in Zoom by selecting Account Management > Account Profile . 

 

 Setup Directions

 • Confirm you have a Zoom Edu or Business account by logging in to zoom.us/signin and navigating to: Account Management > Account Profile . 
• Under your Account Profile in Zoom, apply for a “Vanity URL.” To ensure your Vanity URL is approved, please follow Zoom's guidelines for Vanity URL requests . 
 You won't be able to proceed to Step 3 until you have received an email from Zoom confirming your Vanity URL.

• Once your Vanity URL has been approved by Zoom, add the Zoom application to your Clever account by clicking on this link and selecting Request application . 
• Select which schools to share with Zoom and click Request with sharing . 
 Tip: You can easily select all schools in the district by using the checkbox in the top left. 

 
• Navigate to: https://schools.clever.com/applications/saml-zoom/settings • Under 'SAML Details' copy the Metadata URL
• Enter the copied Metadata URL in a new browser tab and press Enter. • Copy the value found between the tags: <ds:X509Certificate> 
• Navigate to: https://zoom.us/account/sso and select Enable Single Sign-On. 
• Choose to “Configure SSO Manually” and set the following values. Sign-in page URL : https://samlidp.clever.com/saml-zoom/assert Sign-out page URL : Leave blank Identity provider certificate : Enter the X509 Certificate value copied in Step 6 Service Provider (SP) Entity ID : Leave as default Issuer (IDP Entity ID) : http://samlidp.clever.com/services/trust Binding : HTTP-Redirect Signature Hash Algorithm : SHA-256 Security: Check “Save SAML response logs on user sign-in” (optional, but useful for debugging issues) Provision User : Leave as “At Sign-In (Default)" Remember to save your work at the bottom of this tab before moving on to the next step! 
• 
 Switch to the 'SAML Response Mapping' tab and set the following values (note that they are case sensitive). Do not set any additional attributes.

 Default user type: Basic Email address: NameID First name: FirstName Last name: LastName

 
• Navigate to https://schools.clever.com/applications/saml-zoom/settings. • Under 'SAML Details' select Edit. • Provide the subdomain of your approved Vanity URL and select Confirm. IMPORTANT: Provide only the subdomain. For example, if your Zoom Vanity URL is ‘ https://district.zoom.us ’, enter ‘district.' 
• You are now ready to test a teacher & staff login! Issues can be debugged by looking at the “SAML Response Logs” in Zoom settings. If you encounter any problems, please feel free to contact Clever Support ! 
 
 Download: School Administrator’s Guide: Rolling Out Zoom 

 Sending additional attributes

 Additional attributes can be sent to Zoom as part of the SAML assertion. This allows districts to identify and group users more easily. If sending additional attributes, please be sure to adjust the settings for all three user tabs: District Admin Attributes, Staff Attributes, Teacher Attributes and select Confirm. 

 

 

 SchoolID This field allows you to send the School for a user. The only accepted value from the drop-down is School . If selected, the value passed to Zoom will be the Clever ID. This value can be viewed in the Data Browser or on the school's profile page: 

 

 UserType This field allows you to send the user type for a user. The only accepted value from the drop-down is Custom. In the box labeled USER TYPE CUSTOM VALUE enter {{type}}. The values passed to Zoom will be one of the following: teacher, school admin, or district admin.

 

 

 Security & Privacy

 Data privacy is always a top priority. Only teachers & staff will have accounts created in Zoom, and only the following information will be provided to facilitate account creation for teachers & staff: 

 • First Name 
• Last Name 
• Email 
 
 Students attending a Zoom meeting will be prompted to enter their name. Directing students to enter only their first name will further minimize data sharing. See our FAQ section for more information on the student experience. 

 Zoom is COPPA, FERPA, and CCPA compliant. Learn more about Zoom’s best practices for schools and K-12 School and District Privacy Policy . 

 The usage of Zoom is governed by their terms of service . 

 

 FAQs

 What type of integration does Clever have with Zoom? 

 • Zoom can be configured as a Single Sign-On (SSO) application within Clever. Teacher accounts will be provisioned on-demand. Student roster information will not be shared with Zoom.
 
 Who will have a Zoom account? 

 • District admins, staff, and teachers will have accounts in Zoom.
 
 Where can teachers find more information on utilizing Zoom? 

 • A full guide for teachers to set up their virtual classroom can be found here . 
 
 How will students use Zoom? Can they call each other? 

 • Teachers can create meetings for their students to join, but students will not have their own accounts, and students will not be able to initiate meetings with each other. 
• Teachers may find it useful to post their custom Zoom URL on their Teacher Page within Clever. 
 
 Can we use Google to log into Zoom instead of going through the Clever Portal? 

 • Our partnership has been designed and tested using the Clever > Zoom integration. Clever is not able to support district users who access Zoom by any other methods.
 
 Will cloud storage for recordings be provided? 

 • Cloud storage will be dependent upon your license type. The features associated with each meeting plan can be reviewed here: https://zoom.us/pricing. 
 
 Can Staff users in Clever use Zoom? 

 • 
 
 Yes! Staff can log in via Clever and host virtual meetings through Zoom. A full guide for staff to sign in to Zoom and create virtual meeting links can be found here . 

 
 My district has multiple email domains. Do I need multiple accounts? 

 • Each district will be provided one Zoom account, even if your district has multiple email domains. Please enter the Vanity URL of your choice for Step 3. All teachers within your district will be associated with the district account when they log in to Zoom from Clever, regardless of email domain. 
 
 Do students and teachers have to download the Zoom app? 

 • No. Students and teachers can join by selecting "Join from your browser" once they have clicked on a meeting link. We recommend turning on Show a "Join from your browser" link for the entire organization using the directions from Zoom found here .
 
 

 Students receive the error message: "Zoom does not support Clever SSO for students." 

 • 
 In April of 2020 Zoom began enabling the setting "Only authenticated users can join meetings from Web Client" by default. With this new setting enabled, students will see a login error when attempting to join a meeting through the web browser.

 

 To resolve this issue there are two options: 

 
 • Download the Zoom app for the device ( download here ).
• Log in to Zoom as the Zoom account owner, navigate to Account Management > Account Settings and disable "Only authenticated users can join meetings from Web Client"
 

 

 

 Students joining meetings through the Zoom app are not affected. They can join a meeting without logging in

 
 How do I assign users to groups and licenses? 

 • Though Clever assists with provisioning accounts, assigning teachers and staff to users groups and assigning licenses must be completed manually by the district. Information on both of these topics can

title: For App Partners: Creating filters for district data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does app-side filtering work? 3. How do I filter district data? 4. What do districts see when data is filtered? 5. FAQs 

 Overview

 Districts oversharing data is a common problem for applications on Clever. To help resolve this, applications can create filters to receive only the data you need, giving you control over data sharing and enhancing integration quality. 

 
 How does app-side filtering work?

 Step 1: Districts share data with the application .

 Step 2: Applications apply filters on the district data to manage exactly which data they are receiving, whereby ensuring that only relevant data is synced. 

 Please note: if new schools are added to a district's sync or additional data needs to be shared, it will be necessary to update the app-side filters to allow this data to be accessible to you (the application). 

 

 
 How do I filter district data?

 Filtering is located on the District Profile page.

 • Click Create a filter 
• Determine if you would like to filter by school or more granular data using sharing rules (such as grade level, subject, etc.)
• Select the data that should be included in the sync. If using sharing rules to select more granular data, see the Tips and best practices section below. When done, click Next 
• Preview data. If correct, click Save 
• Clever will apply the filter to the district's data. The results are displayed under Final Data once it's done processing. Filters can take up to 24 hours to apply, depending on the size of the district data set.
 
 

 Tips and best practices for sharing rules 

 If you select Our data agreement requires more granular data sharing in step two of the data filter setup, you'll select the data your app needs using sharing rules. Sharing rules allow you to select specific data using rules, such as all students in a given grade, subject, course name, etc. An example of an App Filter set up using sharing rules. Here are some tips when using sharing rules:

 • You can include and/or exclude subsets of data. Data can be excluded by selecting the is not one of modifier.
• For values, you can either type in any value you’d like, or use it as a search bar to find and click on the value you want. Some values will not be available in a dropdown as the district has not authorized an application to see them.
• If you’d like to filter down a rule further, you can select the + AND option to add new criteria. This will act as an "and" statement, rather than an "or" statement.
 • Example Rule with Filters: Share all sections located at Pineapple Elementary School AND their section grade level is 1 AND the section name must contain the text "homeroom".
 

• Once you have finished creating your first rule, you can click Add Rule to add more rules.
• When you have multiple rules, the two rules don’t interact with each other. This means if you want to build off the existing rule, you'll want to add a filter to the rule itself, rather than create a new rule entirely. See the example below that shows two separate rules:
 • "Share all sections where grade is one of 6, 7, 8.”
• Also, "Match all staff where school is Pineapple Elementary.”
 

 
 Please note: Only one filter can be placed per school . If you need to further filter data for a school, please contact Clever Support, and our team will work with the district to adjust the initial data being shared to meet your specifications. 

 

 
 What do districts see when data is filtered?

 If an app-side filter is applied to a district, they will see an App Filters tab in the application area of the Clever Dashboard. Here they can view:

 • Shared Data : the data shared with the application by the district.
• Filters : the filters that were applied to the district data by the application.
• Application Receives: the resulting filtered data that will be synced by the application. 
 
 

 

 
 FAQs

 Why is Clever unable to display a preview of allowed records? 

 • Clever may be unable to provide a preview of allowed records for districts with large data sets or highly complex rules. When this occurs, Unable to display count of allowed records or Sorry, there is too much data… may display. However, you may still safely save your app filters, and they will be applied as intended.
 
 Can I filter data for a district if they have app sync pause enabled? 

 • Yes! You can still filter data even if they have paused their sync to your app.

title: Remote Learning Resources
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 

 

 Overview

 We’ve created a support page with resources to help you navigate remote learning with your schools. You can find these at clever.com/learningathome . 

 The page includes support for administrators, teachers, and families on how to help ensure a resilient digital learning environment in your school communities. 

 If there is anything else we can do to help, please let us know.

title: For App Partners: Setting Up License Usage Limits
text:
Please note: Licensing only applies to Secure Sync applications. This feature is not available for SSO only applications. 

 The licensing feature makes it possible for you to set license limits and to communicate more effectively with school districts about the number of licenses that they are using. This can help to prevent data oversharing and confusion about license limits! 

 Setting Up License Limits and Expiration Dates

 Licenses can be set on a district's overview page or when approving a district connection request. The approval email that is sent to the requestor will include information about the number of available licenses. 

 

 Once the expiration date for a set of licenses passes, you and the relevant district will see a small alert on your Clever Dashboards. No other changes will result from the expiration. 

 Filtering and Viewing Districts Based on Licenses Exceeded or Expired

 You can also view and filter districts in your app developer dashboard, based on whether the available licenses have been exceeded or expired. 

 

 Please note: it is only possible to set license limits based on the number of students or schools. Be sure to set an expiration date if you want the licenses to expire after a set period of time! 

 

 • The licensing feature does not restrict a district from exceeding their licenses when they set data sharing rules. This feature makes it easier to manage and communicate with districts about licenses, but it does not limit how many students or schools can be shared.

title: For App Partners: Manage inbound district requests
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Approve a request 3. Decline a req uest 4. Monitor pending requests 5. Launch dates 

 Overview

 In the Clever Dashboard, the Districts Overview page displays an alert if your application has new requests from districts that would like to connect through Clever. 

 

 

 By clicking on Invited Districts , you can view more details about these recent requests. District requests are divided into three types:

 

 • Sales Lead : These districts may not already use your app, but are interested in connecting through Clever.
 
 

 • Existing Customer : These districts already use your app and have requested to connect through Clever.
 
 

 • Free Trial Request : This district is interested in a free trial of your app.
 
 By clicking on Invited Districts , you can view more details about the districts that you have invited to connect with Clever. See our article about Finding and connecting with districts for more information. You can also click on the district name to view information about that district, as well as a district contact:

 

 Approve a request

 Once you are ready to start receiving data from a district, you can approve their request. The district will need to set sharing permissions for your application if they haven't already.

 Decline a request 

 If you decide not to integrate a district via Clever, click the Decline Request button to remove the request from your dashboard. This will send an email to the district with a message explaining the reason you are rejecting the request. This message is required in the message box when you reject a request. 

 If you are unsure whether or not you will be using Clever to integrate with the district, we recommend that you neither approve nor decline the request until you've settled on next steps. 

 Monitor pending requests 

 You can view and filter through your Pending Requests on the District Overview page: 

 

 

 Launch Dates 

 Districts are able to request applications for a future date. That way they can set sharing permissions in preparation for going live. You can set the launch date in your application dashboard by clicking on the hyperlinked district name under Pending Requests on the District Overview page. You are able to insert a date to launch for the district or choose Launch now if you want the district to immediately go live: 

 

 On the District Overview page you can filter and order districts based on their launch dates:

title: For Clever Admins: Back to School Checklist
text:
Get ready for Back to School

 Please access this checklist in Clever Academy for a comprehensive overview to prepare you for Back to School and the full year. You can easily check off each item as you complete it, and even print it out if that's your thing!

title: For App Partners: Adding internal notes to your districts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding an internal note 3. Viewing notes from your homepage 4. Adding notes to districts that aren't yet connected 

 

 Overview

 Application partners can leave internal notes on districts in the Clever dashboard. This is helpful for noting any special aspects for that district. These notes are viewable by your colleagues but not the district.

 

 
 

 Adding an internal note

 To add an internal note, navigate to a district's profile page , click Add District Note , in the District Info section, then write your note! 

 

 

 To edit a note, just click the small pencil icon next to the district note header, insert the updated text, and click save. You'll notice that whenever you update a note, the fields beneath the note, "Last Updated By" and "Last Updated," are updated to reflect the most recent editor and the time that the editing was done. 

 

 

 
 

 Viewing notes from your homepage

 Once you create a note on a district, a note icon will appear next to the district's name on your home page. The same icon will also appear for all the other people who have access to your team's Clever account. 

 

 

 
 

 Adding notes to districts that aren't yet connected

 District notes work with districts that you aren't connected with too! Navigate to any district in your "Pending Requests" or "Invited Districts" tabs to try it out:

title: For App Partners: Updating my email preferences
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Instructions 

 Overview

 Application partners have the ability to edit their email preferences in Clever in order to manage which communications they will receive related to district connections. Application email preferences do not include marketing emails from Clever! 

 Instructions

 To update your email preferences, follow these steps:

 Step 1: 

 From the application dashboard, click on your name in the top right corner and select 'Email Settings'. 

 

 Step 2: 

 Update your email preferences as desired. A category of emails will be disabled if 'Disabled' appears as white text with a blue background. There are five types of email options that can be enabled or disabled: 

 • Sharing: Notifications are sent when districts change their data sharing permissions 
• Connected districts: Notifications are sent when a connection to a district is updated (ex. if a district disconnects your application, you will receive a notification about it) 
• Requests and invite changes: Notifications about status updates on your requests and invitations to districts 
• Requests and invite initiations: Notifications about new requests from districts 
• Disconnects: Notifications about districts disconnecting from your app 
 

 

 Step 3: 

 Select 'Save' to save your updated email preferences.

title: For App Partners: How can I manage my account?
text:
This article contains information regarding the following two sections of the Settings tab in your application's Clever dashboard: 1. General 2. Instant Login 

 Account team members with Administrator privileges are able to edit their account settings from within the Clever Dashboard. This can mean anything from editing your account name to accessing your API credentials.

 

 General

 Profile Settings

 Here’s a quick overview of what you can do from your Clever Dashboard in the Settings tab under the General view: 

 

 Use this view to manage and edit your: 

 • Name: This is the display name your application will have in our Application Gallery and what is used when districts search to add your application from the district dashboard.
• Description: This description will display along with your application name in the district dashboard when a district searches for and adds your application.
• Categories: Select which categories your application falls under. This information can be used by districts to filter by when searching for applications in the district dashboard.
• Shortname: This name will be appended to the district signup URL.
• Website URL: This URL should take users straight to the main page for your application’s homepage/main login screen.
• District Signup URL: This URL can be distributed to districts who are interested in adding your application through Clever - it will take them directly to the screen within their district dashboard which will allow them to add it.
• Alternate Names: Add any alternate names which can be used by districts to search for your application from the district dashboard.
• Support Contact: Provide contact information for the go-to person to reach out to regarding support issues. Note: This contact information will be viewable to Clever Support and to District Admins and School Tech Leads if they use Clever's Troubleshoot Login Tool and the login issue is determined to likely be an app issue.
 
 API Credentials

 These API credentials are used to access our API. View our Postman collection here . You can also generate a new client secret for these credentials from this screen.

 Image Settings

 

 View your current application icon and logo which is visible to districts when they search for and add your application. If you would like to change either of these, please open a ticket with Clever Support .

 Additional Info

 

 View information on the current data resource types your application is scoped to view when accessing district data via our API explorer. Please contact integrations@clever.com if you would like to adjust these scopes or add additional ones.

 

 Instant Login

 View and edit your Instant Login settings to customize the SSO experience for your districts.

 Website Settings

 

 Redirect URIs: When districts click on your application’s icon, they will be taken to the URL specified here. You can add multiple redirect URIs based on what your application’s integration finds necessary, but users using the Clever Portal flow to log into your application will be taken to the first URL listed.

 Additional Settings

 

 Supported User Types: Adjust which user types can log into your application using the Clever Portal. There are 4 user types total: district admins, school admins, teachers, and students. 

 If you have any additional questions on how to manage your application using the Clever dashboard, please don't hesitate to open up a ticket with Clever Support !

title: For Teachers: Why did I receive an email from Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Why did I receive a "Your accounts are ready to use" email from Clever? 3. How do I access my new apps? 4. I don't know my username/password! 

 

 Overview

 This article discusses some common scenarios in which a teacher may receive an email from Clever. Please reach out to our support team if you have any questions about emails received from Clever not discussed below. 

 
 Why did I receive a "Your accounts are ready to use" email from Clever?

 When your district gives you access to a new application through Clever, we email you a summary of the apps newly available to you and your students. You can find these new apps in your Clever Portal and log in right away! Consider our email a virtual invitation to start using your new apps. To learn more about how teachers use Clever, click here . 

 

 
 How do I access my new apps?

 You can access your new apps by logging in to your Clever Portal. The email should have a "Get Started" link which brings you to your district's Portal. Enter your username and password to log in to Clever, and click on any app to access your account. Be sure to bookmark the link to your Clever Portal so you can easily find it again! 

 

 
 I don't know my username/password!

 First, we recommend reading this article , which covers how to log into the Clever portal as a teacher. You can also reach out to your district administrator -- they'll usually be listed on the "Login Help" page of your portal, and their email address is in the email you received.

title: For Teachers: How do I create a Clever account?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I create a Clever account for myself or my students? 3. I already have a teacher account in Clever. How can I have admin privileges? 

 

 Overview

 We're excited to hear that you're interested in using Clever! Student, teacher, and staff accounts are created when your district syncs information into Clever from your Student Information System (SIS). Your district administrator manages this process. Clever Support is not authorized to create accounts for users in your district manually. 

 
 How do I create a Clever account for myself or my students?

 If you are a teacher and want access to your district's Clever account, you will need to contact your District Administrator . They can ensure that your teacher account is created through the sync from the SIS or manually within Clever.

 If you'd like your students to have access to Clever accounts, you should also contact your District Administrator.

 If your district doesn't have a Clever account, your District Administrator or technology staff can sign up for one at no cost.

 

 
 I already have a teacher account in Clever. How can I add admin privileges?

 If you already have a teacher account in Clever, but you'd like to have admin privileges, please contact your District Administrator. Clever Support cannot change these permissions for your account.

title: For Teachers: Clever Academy - Learn more about Clever!
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Learning topics for teachers 3. How can teachers access Clever Academy? 4. How can I review my courses or print my certification? 

 Overview

 Clever Academy is our free online training and certification program for educators. You can access it through your Clever Portal or at clever.academy . 

 We offer a variety of courses based on your background and need. Check out the video, below, for more info. 

 

 

 
 

 Learning topics for Teachers

 Clever Certified Champion courses

 If you'd like to become a Clever Certified Champion Teacher, complete Levels 1-4. Learn more about these below. 

 Clever Tip: For the certification path (levels 1-4), you must begin at Level 1, though you can test out of courses if you have the prerequisite knowledge. All other courses can be completed in any order! 

 Course outline: 

 Level 1: Getting started with Clever In this course, learn what you need to know to get started with Clever, including an overview of what Clever can do to make your life easier! Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 1. This course is a prerequisite to Level 2 and beyond. 

 Level 2: Learning the basics In this course, you'll learn how to help students access Clever and their learning applications, along with handy troubleshooting tips. Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 2. This course is a prerequisite to Level 3 and beyond. 

 Level 3: Setting up your portal Clever can help you set up and manage your digital classroom! Discover the hidden gems of the Clever platform with these time-saving tools. Complete this course and you’ll earn a badge for Level 3. This course is a prerequisite to Level 4 and beyond.

 Level 4: Master classroom management In this final level, you'll dig into Clever's fanciest features to help you master classroom management. From communicating with students and families to monitoring edtech usage! Complete this course and you’ll be a certified Clever Champion Teacher!

 

 Quick Start Guides

 Quick start - Get going with Clever (for teachers) This course is for teachers who are new to Clever and don’t have much immediate time to deep dive into all there is to know.

 Quick start videos for teachers Only have a few minutes to learn about Clever? Here are some short videos to get you on your way! When you're ready to earn your official Clever certification, start the certification track by enrolling in Level 1: Getting started with Clever

 

 Product Spotlights

 Product Spotlight: Build your digital classroom with Teacher Pages 

 This lesson gives you the low-down on teacher pages. 

 Product Spotlight: Using Class Analytics to support students This lesson will show you how to use Class Analytics with your students!

 

 Trainer

 Clever Trainers for Teachers Everything you need to lead an in-person or virtual training at your school!

 

 
 

 How can I access the Clever Academy?

 Teachers can access the Clever Academy by logging into the Clever Portal with their district-specific URL or directly going to clever.academy . 

 

 
 

 How can I review my courses or print my certification?

 You can see the courses you've registered for or completed at any time by navigating to Clever Academy , clicking person icon in the top right, and selecting My Profile. From here, you'll see a list of all your learning paths and courses. Any course with a certification that you've completed will have a View certificate link next to it. Click the link to view the certificate, and then print it if you'd like! Certificates are valid for two years. If it has been more than two years since you completed a course, you would need to re-take the course to become re-certified.

title: For Teachers: What's Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How you can use the Clever Portal 3. Why Clever? 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Getting started with the Clever? Check out our course Clever Academy for Teachers: Level 1 available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 Clever is your school’s digital learning hub, providing one friendly place for all your resources and instruction. It streamlines your digital classroom by giving you and your students one-click access to all your learning apps in one spot. Instead of spending the first 15 minutes of class troubleshooting logins, Clever handles the technical hurdles so you can focus on teaching. Clever is so much more than a list of favorite apps or a bookmark bar; it does the work behind the scenes to ensure that your classes are always accurately rostered and student accounts are automatically created in those applications.

 This article will provide you with details on what Clever is and how it simplifies your digital teaching experience.

 

 
 How you can use the Clever Portal

 The Clever Portal is more than just a landing page; it’s a powerful toolkit designed to help you organize your instruction and support your students in real-time all while keep everyone’s data safe. By customizing your Teacher Page and utilizing built-in tools, you can turn the Portal into your classroom’s digital headquarters. Learn more about the various features of the Clever Portal below. 

 Clever Tip: Some features are optional and may be turned on or off by your Clever Admin. These are noted below by the tag (Managed by your Clever Admin). If you have questions, please reach out to your IT team directly. 

 

 

 Teacher Pages 

 
 You can use Teacher Pages to create a personalized environment for your students. Since Clever automatically keeps your class lists up to date behind the scenes, you can spend your time customizing the experience rather than managing rosters. You can:

 • 
 Organize apps your school org uses into custom categories like "Math Small Group" or "Morning Choice."

• 
 Add external links to helpful websites, YouTube videos, or Google Drive files.

• 
 Link your LMS assignments directly on your Teacher Page so students have one starting point for their entire day.

• 
 Share specific pages with different groups of students to differentiate instruction effortlessly.

 

 Classroom Analytics 

 Ever wonder if your students are actually logging into their assigned programs? Teacher Analytics gives you a window into student engagement. You can see exactly which students have logged in and which apps they’re using. This is especially helpful for monitoring independent work or checking in on student progress during remote learning days.

 Backup login codes 

 If a student forgets their password or loses their physical Badge, you don't have to wait for the IT department to save the day. You can generate a 20-minute "backup login code" directly from your Teacher Portal. This temporary code gives the student immediate access so they don't miss a minute of instruction while you work on a permanent fix. 

 Support for Substitutes (managed by your Clever Admin) 

 Planning for a sub shouldn't involve writing down 30 different passwords. With Substitute Access, you can generate a special Badge or login for a guest teacher. This allows them to access the resources they need to keep your classroom running smoothly without compromising your personal account security.

 

 Clever Badges (managed by your Clever Admin) 

 For younger learners or students who struggle with typing, we offer Clever Badges. These are physical codes that students simply hold up to their device's camera to log in instantly. It’s a game-changer for early elementary and special education classrooms where typing a complex username can be a major barrier to learning. Badge settings are managed by your Clever Administrator. If you have questions, please reach out to your IT team.

 

 Clever Library (managed by your Clever Admin) 

 Clever Library gives you a safe, streamlined way to discover and try new digital learning tools for your classroom. With built-in data privacy protections, you can confidently explore apps knowing only the minimum necessary student information is shared. When you install a Library app, Clever automatically creates and rosters accounts with SSO, so your students can start learning right away with no extra setup required. It’s an easy way to expand your instructional toolkit while staying aligned with your school’s privacy and security policies.

 

 Password and Security Management (managed by your Clever Admin) 

 In many school organizations, teachers have the power to reset student passwords or void and regenerate a lost Badge directly from their roster. This self-serve approach keeps the control in your hands and keeps your students learning.

 

 
 Why Clever?

 We know how much time it takes to manage a dozen different digital tools, and how critical it is to keep your student data private and secure while doing it. Clever solves the issue of managing multiple usernames and passwords for students by using Single Sign-On ( SSO ), which is an authentication method that lets you and your students sign into all your connected apps with just one set of credentials, like your Google email and password. Once you're in the Clever Portal, you can launch apps like Newsela, Khan Academy, or IXL instantly. You’ve also got troubleshooting tools at your fingertips that help you resolve login issues right away, instead of having to file a support ticket with your IT team.

 In addition to SSO, Clever offers the following benefits:

 • 
 Secure Sync Rostering : Ensures you and your students have accounts in all of your applications and that classes are kept up-to-date in those apps.

• 
 Clever LMS Connect: For supported applications, Clever can help automatically sync assignment grades back to your Learning Management System (LMS) such as Schoology, Google Classroom or Canvas.

• 
 Additional teacher tools: Classroom analytics, backup login codes, and password management options.

 
 Clever doesn't replace the tools you already love; it makes them easier to reach. If you use a Learning Management System ( LMS ), such as Schoology, Google Classroom, or Canvas, Clever is the perfect partner. Think of Clever as the secure front door to your digital school and your LMS as the virtual classroom where the work happens. Clever works alongside your LMS to ensure every student has the right access at the right time.

 Don’t have an LMS? No worries! Clever is the perfect spot for building out a virtual classroom with Teacher Pages .

 Clever also helps your IT and administrative team with account management, ensuring your students have access when they need it. Clever connects your school’s SIS ( Student Information System ) with your school organization’s learning applications to simplify the process of creating accounts in learning applications.

 This process, called rostering, automatically creates student accounts and adds them to the correct digital programs based on your current class lists. If a new student joins your class, as long as your school’s SIS is updated, the student will automatically get their accounts created in learning applications.

 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 How do I sign up for Clever? 

 • 
 Your school organization manages Clever accounts at the administrative level through its Student Information System ( SIS ). Since accounts are automatically created from school org data, you usually don't need to sign up. If you don't have access yet, reach out to your IT department.

 
 What should I do if a student can’t log in? 

 • 
 First, try generating a backup login code from your Teacher Portal. If they use Badges, ensure the camera is clean, and the Badge isn't folded. If the issue persists, your school organization’s tech support is the best point of contact.

 
 Can I use Clever

title: For App Partners: Create your Teacher Quickstart Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What is a Teacher Quickstart Guide 2. How do I create a Teacher Quickstart Guide 3. Tips for creating an effective guide 

 What is a Teacher Quickstart Guide

 Library applications can create a Quickstart guide on Clever to help teachers feel confident about setting up your application. This proven format generates a 10% increase in user engagement with your application. 

 The structure of this guide was developed in response to teacher feedback: the biggest barrier to using an app is finding the time and energy to get it set up. 

 

 Once your Quickstart Guide is published in Clever, your app detail page will show a link that teachers can access anytime, and any teacher that installs your app from the Library will be notified and nudged to use your guide if they have not yet logged in and used your application. Sometimes, teachers just need a reminder and easy steps - and Clever is here to help with that! 

 Teachers can immediately launch your application from the Quickstart Guide. 

 How do I create a Teacher Quickstart Guide

 • Log in to Clever 
• Navigate to Library > Teacher Quickstart Guide in your left hand navigation menu 
• To begin, first input Account Syncing information. The answer is yes only if your application requires teachers to take an additional action after Clever sends your app the rostering data. Example actions: 
 • Teacher is required to click a button to ‘sync’ their class from Clever. 
• Teacher is required to upload or input their classes into the application 
 

• Fill out the following How to use steps. Your application must fill out a minimum of 1 step, and can fill out a max of 3 steps. Note that there is a character limit per step to improve readability for teachers. 
 
 

 Tips for creating an effective guide

 A successful Quickstart Guide is one that motivates teachers to launch the application and take the simple steps involved in setup. A less successful guide might contain accurate Getting Started content, but may ultimately feel too complex for teachers to launch at that moment. 

 Tips: 

 • Avoid linking to your website’s help center articles instead of writing the essential content within Clever 
• If you’re uploading screenshots for each step, indicate which button(s)/sections teachers need to engage with, using arrows, callout boxes, etc.

title: For App Partners: Clever’s API change policy
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Switching to a new API version 3. Releasing a new API version 4. Deprecating an existing API version 

 Overview 

 Clever is committed to providing the best experience for developers building on our platform. In order to make it easier and faster to build on Clever, we constantly improve by adding new features and deprecating ones that are redundant. Here’s what to expect from Clever when we make changes to our API. 

 Switching to a new API version 

 As of Back to School 2021, you will need to set the major version (e.g. v1, v2) that you will be using in production. If you are moving between major versions in production, please reach out to our Partner Engineering team to ensure a smooth transition. 

 Within a major version, you can freely make calls against any associated minor version (for example, you could use both 2.0 and 2.1, but not 3.0) by updating your URLS (e.g. https://api.clever.com/v2.1/district ). 

 For detailed information on changing API versions, check out our developer documentation on Upgrading to the latest version of Clever’s API. 

 Major vs minor versions 

 We define a minor release as one that will only add new features (e.g. response fields, input parameters, endpoints, etc) and fix small bugs. Minor versions will be backwards compatible. 

 Major version releases may contain features that are not backwards compatible as well as feature deprecations. 

 Releasing a new API version

 Clever is committed to releasing planned API versions no more than once a year, based on the following release timeline: 

 • New API versions will be announced by November. 
• Rostering (data and events) API versions should be released by December. 
• Identity API changes should be released by March. 
 

 Any delay in this timeline will be communicated to customers in advance. In case of an emergency, Clever reserves the right to release an API version outside of this schedule to meet customer needs with proactive communications to partners. 

 When a new version is fully released, new applications will build to the newest API version, and will not be able to use any previous version of the API. 

 Updating versions after release 

 While we consider new versions code complete upon official release, we recognize that bugs can slip by our testing processes! For three months after a version is released, we will make updates to the version to fix any discovered bugs. Once three months have passed, we will freeze the version as-is and make no further updates. 

 Global changes 

 We sometimes need to make changes to all API versions - if we do, we will communicate this with partners as soon as possible. 

 Deprecating an existing API version 

 Clever will support no more than two major API versions at a time. This policy aligns with industry best practice to ensure a great front-end experience for you and your customers. This means that when a new major version is released, we will begin the deprecation and end of life process for the oldest major version. 

 We are committed to ensuring that deprecation goes smoothly for all customers. Therefore, the deprecation and end of life process is as follows: 

 • At least one year in advance of deprecation, Clever will send notice of deprecation to all partners. We will also reach out to partners who are using the API version to help determine their upgrade path and timeline. 
• Once an API version is deprecated, Clever will provide only limited support for bug fixes. 
• An API version will no longer be supported six months after it is deprecated. At this point, applications will be unable to access any of the version’s endpoints. 
 

 Upon announcement of API v3, we are officially beginning the deprecation and end of life process for API v1, which includes both v1.1 and v1.2. The version will be deprecated by Back to School June 2022 and no longer accessible by December 2022.

title: For App Partners: Billing FAQs
text:
