This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does Clever bill app partners? 3. What counts as a billable school? 4. What is the billing frequency for app partners? 5. When do I start getting billed for a school or user? 6. How can I view the number of billed schools or users? 7. What do I do if a school organization overshares data? 8. What tools can I use to manage data sharing? • Using licensing to monitor sharing • Applying app-side filters • Recommending sharing permissions • Modifying launch dates 9. I see schools shared with apparently no teachers, students, or sections. Why might this be happening? 10. What if there are discrepancies between school data in Clever and MDR data? 

 

 Overview

 This article contains frequently asked billing questions. The answers to these questions will vary depending on the terms of your current contract. Most contracts follow the same general structure, but reach out to accounts-receivable@clever.com with any questions. 

 
 How does Clever bill app partners?

 Clever bills by billable schools or users shared depending on your contract. For questions, contact support . 

 
 What counts as a billable school?

 We only charge for schools that are explicitly shared and launched. Explicitly shared: This excludes any schools that are shared via dually enrolled users. For example, student X is enrolled in classes at both Clever Middle School and Clever High School. Clever Middle School is shared with your application, but Clever High School isn't. We'll provide access to both school objects via the API, but will only bill for Clever Middle School. Launched: We only bill for launched connections, giving you time to test and get set up! Read more about setting launch dates here . 

 
 What is the billing frequency for app partners?

 Partners are billed annually upfront for the contracted annual platform fee. For partners who exceed the amount included in the agreement (overage), they'll be billed based on the number of connections at the time of overage multiplied by the months left in your contract. You'll receive an invoice showing the amount of your overage. 

 
 When do I start getting billed for a school or user?

 You're billed for any schools or users shared through launched school organization connections on the last day of the month that haven't been filtered out from within your Clever Dashboard. If a school is connected midway through the month, you will still be charged full price for that school for the rest of the month. 

 
 How can I view the number of billed schools or users?

 View the number of schools or users you’ll be billed for any given month on the Billing tab of your Clever dashboard. In the billing tab, you can select a billing period by selecting Billing period as of . 

 • This is updated nightly. 
• This data represents the total number of shared schools and users in launched school organization connections. 
• The number excludes any schools or users you’ve filtered out from within your Clever dashboard. 
• Schools or users who have been shared multiple times are counted only once for billing purposes. 
 
 From the billing tab, you can:

 • Change the billing period to see what you were billed for in previous months. 
• Request to have a CSV emailed to you for all of the schools or users you're currently being billed for, to keep for your records. 
 
 

 
 What do I do if a school organization overshares data?

 Use the billing tab to check the number of schools each connected school organization shares with your application via Clever. We recommend checking the tab monthly to avoid any over-sharing issues before your monthly invoice. 

 If a school organization shares too much data, there are two ways to remedy this. 

 • Reach out to the school organization to request that they adjust their sharing settings so that the appropriate amount of data is shared with your application. If the school org needs assistance, they can submit a ticket to our Support Team via the Help Center form . 
• You can also use the application-side filtering tool to remove excess data from the API. Any schools filtered out using this tool will also be removed from all future invoices. For more information on how to filter excess data, please see this article: Creating filters for school organization data. 
 
 Finally, you can use our License feature to flag school organizations that are oversharing, so that you can check this programmatically in your Clever Dashboard. For more information on how to set up licenses for a school organization in your dashboard please see this article: Setting Up License Usage Limits . 

 

 
 What tools can I use to manage data sharing?

 We have a few tools that help surface when a school organization shares too much data with your application. 

 Using licensing to monitor sharing

 The first is the licensing feature. This feature allows you to set the number of school or student licenses a school organization has purchased for your application. If a school org exceeds the number of licenses, you and the school org will be notified in the Clever dashboard. For more information on the licensing feature, please see this article .

 Applying app-side filters

 Applications can create filters to receive only the data they need, giving you control over data sharing and enhancing integration quality. For instructions on setting up filters, please see this article .

 Recommending sharing permissions 

 Smarter data sharing lets you recommend sharing settings to school organizations connected to your app. You can send updated recommendations even after school orgs have enabled data sharing with you. This article covers how to send sharing recommendations to a school organization.

 Modifying launch dates

 As mentioned above, you are only charged for launched connections. If a school org isn't ready to launch, you can modify the launch date to be further into the future until they are set up to begin using the application. This will allow them to set sharing permissions, resolve data quality errors, and complete all their steps on the school org end before you begin getting billed for the shared data. More information about setting launch dates can be found here: For App Partners: Managing school organization launch dates .

 
 I see schools shared with apparently no teachers, students, or sections. Why might this be happening?

 We provide access to schools that aren't explicitly shared with your application for users who are associated with multiple schools in Clever. As a result, you might see a school with very limited teacher, section, and student data. You aren't billed for schools not explicitly shared with your application via Clever. 

 
 What if there are discrepancies between school data in Clever and MDR data?

 You're invoiced based on the number of unique schools shared with your application via Clever. ‘Shared schools’ is defined by the total number of unique Clever IDs available to your application via the API, not by the total number of unique MDR numbers. This data doesn't always align perfectly with MDR data, depending on how the school organization has structured its schools in their SIS. 

 
 Need help? For more information, refer to your contract or reach out to accounts-receivable@clever.com

title: For App Partners: How do I add and manage account team members?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Adding team members 2. Managing team member permissions 3. Managing Individual Profile Settings 4. Recommended Permissions 

 

 Adding team members

 The Team tab in your Clever Dashboard lists all users currently set up as administrators. Only team members with Admin privileges can add new team members. To add new team members, navigate to the Team page and select Add Team Member. Enter the required details and select the desired user permissions. Click Save. The user will receive an email to activate their account and set a password. 

 Activation emails are valid for 30 days. If a user does not activate their account within the first 30 days, the pending user must be deleted and their account recreated. 

 

 
 Managing team member permissions

 To adjust, add, or remove permissions for a team member’s account, click the pencil icon underneath Actions to the right of the team member’s name. You can toggle permissions from this screen to best suit the team member’s needs. 

 

 • Administrator: Administrators can edit team settings and receive email notifications when connected school organizations change their sharing settings.
• Application Settings: This allows this user to update your app's settings, such as the name or redirect URIs.
• Application Secrets: Can Edit allows this user to reset the client secret for your app.
• Can disconnect school organizations: This allows this user to disconnect school organizations that your app is connected to with Clever.
• School organization Requests + Invitations: Can Edit allows this user to accept or reject inbound requests from school organizations and to invite schools to connect using Search.
• Data Browser + User Search: This allows the user to access the Data Browser and User Search pages in their dashboard. It also allows this user to log into your app using the Instant Login Testing tool.
 
 

 
 Managing Individual Profile Settings

 To make adjustments to your individual profile, such as editing your name or email address, click your name in the top-right corner of the page. 

 Profile Settings 

 

 Profile Settings allow you to edit your name, email, and phone number, in addition to changing your password and enabling two-factor authentication for your account. 

 Email Settings

 

 Email Settings allow you to adjust which email notifications you receive from Clever when there are updates to your school orgs or your accounts. 

 
 Recommended Permissions

 This table provides some recommended permissions for your users, based on their role in your organization:

title: For App Partners: What is single sign-on (SSO)?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How does single sign-on (SSO) work? 3. How do users log into the IdP? 4. Logging in to Clever 5. Logging in to your App 6. T roubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 Single sign-on (SSO) allows students and teachers to log in to your application through Clever. This means that as an application partner, you support every Identity Provider (IdP) that Clever supports. 

 To implement school organization-managed Clever SSO, your application needs a Clever Complete agreement and should use SSO as part of a Rostering + SSO integration. 

 
 How does single sign-on (SSO) work?

 While users may see logging into your application to Clever as one quick step, behind the scenes, single sign-on (SSO) actually consists of three separate logins: 

 • A user logs in to Clever via the district's Identity Provider (IDP) (e.g. Active Directory, Google, etc.) from the Clever login page.
• Clever authenticates the user through the district's IDP.
• If the user is verified, Clever creates a "token" (i.e. a piece of data) that identifies the user and then logs them in successfully.
• The user then clicks on the appropriate application icon to open an application, and Clever provides the user's token to the application which authenticates the user. This means the user does not need to enter login credentials a second time. 
• The user is logged into the application successfully!
 
 
 How do users log into the IdP?

 When users access the Clever Portal or an Instant Login Link, they’ll be taken to their district’s login page. When they click on one of the “Log in with <IdP>” buttons, they’ll be redirected to their identity provider to log in. 

 

 If users are already logged into their IdP, they will not need to log in again - they’ll skip right to the “Logging in with Clever” step. 

 Please note: If a district's IdP for Clever is LDAPS, a user is authenticated to Active Directory locally, and their district’s chosen identity provider is LDAPS, they will need to enter in their Active Directory credentials to log in to Clever. This is a limitation of the LDAP protocol. 

 

 Can users skip the 'Log in with IdP' page? 

 Users can skip this page by appending 

 ‘?skip’ 
 to the end of the Clever Portal or Instant Login Link URL (ex. ?skip">https://clever.com/in/<shortname>?skip ). 

 Please note: The above is for districts with a single IdP. If a district has multiple IdPs, they should contact Clever Support. 

 

 
 Logging in to Clever

 Once the user is logged into their IdP, it will securely send a piece of identifying information to Clever and tells us what that data should match in our records. This is generally non-PII information such as student number, sis id, or email. More information on this can be found in our article on Claims Rules . 

 Please note: The identifying information that we receive is never the users' IdP credentials. 

 

 
 Logging in to your App

 The user can then log in to your application by clicking on the icon in their Clever Portal (or if they’re using Instant Login links, they’ll automatically start the login process). 

 Instant Login uses the OAuth2, an open standard for securely accessing information. Here’s how it works: 

 • Clever redirects the user to a special URL for your application, along with a code. 
• Your application automatically uses the code to ask Clever for a token that grants additional access to our API. 
• Clever checks the code and the application credentials to make sure they are valid before returning an API token. 
• Your application uses the API token to get basic information about the user - at the very least, it will get the district id, user Clever id, and user type. 
• Depending on your implementation, your app may then search for the user in your existing database and/or provision an account for the user. 
 
 
 Troubleshooting

 While it looks simple to the end user, there’s definitely a lot of moving parts! For help with login issues, check out our Troubleshooting Resources.

title: For App Partners: Navigating the Clever Dashboard
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Search 3. Profile and Email settings 

 Overview 

 From the Clever application dashboard, you can review requests from districts, manage your existing accounts, and troubleshoot issues. 

 

 • Home: This will display an alphabetical list of the districts currently using your application. Next to the district you'll also see the day they connected, the last time they synced data to Clever, and the sync status of their account. You can also search for users or view a district's data warnings.
• Clever Academy: Here you can access our free online training and certification program, Clever Academy. Alternatively, users can go directly to Clever Academy here: https://clever.academy . 
• Data Browser : View and filter the data being shared with your application.
• Analytics : See how districts are using your application in the Analytics Overview page. This page aggregates login data from all of your active districts—find out what all the charts mean in this article .
• Dev Tools : Here you can access the Instant Login Testing tool, the Certifications (Clever integrations) of your application, and the Matchmaker tool, where you can match up accounts from outside Clever to Clever's data. Instructions for using this can be found here .
• Library: Create and manage your app’s Marketing Collateral, Teacher Quick start Guide, and access your Integration Guide; view and respond to teacher reviews; view app install and usage analytics. 
• Settings :
 • General: Settings related to profile, image, and other configurations Populate a District Outreach Contact that will be available to districts so they can efficiently contact your team for troubleshooting. Clever will also use this contact for school communications, such as app invites, sync requests, etc. If you’d only like the contact to appear on direct communications, and not in the Clever Dashboard for Clever Admins, you can check the box for Don’t show District Outreach Contact as a support option for districts. 
• Instant Login: Website, iOS, and additional settings. 
• Data Access: Settings related to which fields are available to your application via Clever’s API.
• District Setup: Set up global recommended sharing settings, add a message to districts who are setting up your app for the first time, enable safety thresholds, and request API V3 Migration CSVs.
 

• Team : The team page allows the account owner to add users to the application dashboard and gives them access to the available tools. You'll just need the name and the email for the user you're looking to add.
• Billing : The Billing tab is where you can check the number of schools each connected district is sharing with your application. We recommend looking at the tab monthly to get in front of any oversharing issues before your monthly invoice. NOTE: Your app must have a Secure Sync account to see this feature. 
• Clever Concierge: Clever Concierge is our premium district onboarding service for partners implementing at scale. We’ll help you match your existing customers and quickly invite them to connect via Clever. NOTE: Your app must have a Secure Sync account to see this feature. 
 
 Search 

 

 • Search by district name, Clever ID, or MDR number to quickly find the district you're seeking.
• From a district page, use the Omnisearch bar to find a student, teacher, administrator, or section. You can use the ID number or name to find the specific record.
 
 Profile and Email settings 

 Note: These settings can be found in the upper right corner of the dashboard. 

 

 • Profile Settings: User information, change your password, two-factor authentication. 
• Email Settings: Sharing rule updates, connected district changes, district requests.

title: For App Partners: Clever products overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Clever Secure Sync 2. Clever Single Sign-on 3. Clever Library 4. Clever LMS Connect 5. Clever Complete 

 Clever offers several products to both district and application partners, keep reading for more information!

 

 

 

 
 Clever Secure Sync

 

 Clever's rostering solution allows your team to partner with any district, regardless of their SIS. By building one Secure Sync integration to Clever, you can be assured of clean and normalized district data with every district connection you make. 

 Secure Sync cuts down on data errors with automatic data fixes and district requirements that are specific to your application. Districts use a powerful sharing interface to ensure only the data necessary for your program is coming through the API. 

 Learn more about Secure Sync here! 

 

 
 Clever Single Sign-on

 

 Clever’s no-cost single sign-on (SSO) solution increases instructional time by getting students and teachers into all of their digital software and resources with one username and password. With Clever's simple OAuth 2 integration, you can leave the security concerns to Clever and bypass username and password provisioning. Instead, users can access your platform directly through Clever. 

 Clever Badges are an added benefit for our SSO users that gives all students a unique and fun way to log in to your learning software—no typing necessary. Teachers don’t have to spend classroom time getting everybody set up, and with Clever's unique direct-device login for Google Chromebooks and Windows devices, even the youngest of learners can immediately start their lessons in your app without any help. For older students, Badges can also be paired with a 6-digit PIN to ensure additional security. 

 To learn more about the district experience with Clever Badges, check out the Clever Badges section of the Help Center for districts!

 

 
 Clever Library

 

 The library is a resource available for partners with free, freemium, and free-trial versions of your product. The Library supports and empowers teacher-driven adoption of your product, and surfaces this adoption data to school and district administrators to better facilitate purchasing conversations. 

 Users get to find and try your product organically, and you expand your network. If you already have a Single Sign-On integration, the developer time for the Library integration is quoted at about a week. 

 For more information about Clever Library, reach out to application-success@clever.com . 

 

 
 Clever LMS Connect

 

 

 Clever LMS Connect allows you to integrate with district LMSs through the Clever API, enabling assignments and gradebooks to flow seamlessly between your application and the district’s LMS. This saves teachers hours of time.

 

 You can now fast track interoperability, making it simple and secure for schools to use your application directly from their LMS. With Clever LMS Connect, you can enable districts to easily sync and launch your application within their LMS.

 

 For more information about Clever LMS Connect, connect with our team here . 

 

 
 Clever Complete

 

 

 
 Simplify district and school data management into one system with Clever Complete. Clever Complete builds on Clever’s existing 1EdTech OneRoster® integrations, allowing you to automate rostering via OneRoster for any school—even those not using Clever.

 

 We designed Clever Complete to replace the multiple integrations you manage with one secure, efficient solution. With Clever Complete, you get rostering, SSO, and LMS connections for any school through a single integration.

 For more information about Clever Complete, connect with our team .

title: For App Partners: Upgrading API versions (V2 > V3.1)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Benefits of upgrading 3. Timeline and transition overview 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 Overview

 This article briefly overviews the benefits of utilizing Clever’s API V3.1 and the transition process.

 Please note: You can upgrade to API V3.1 at any time for no additional cost ! Fill out our commit form here to let us know you're ready or contact your Application Success Manager at app-success@clever.com for more information. 

 

 
 Benefits of upgrading

 With the release of Clever’s API v3 and now v3.1, there have been several updates to the API that benefit application partners. By upgrading from v2 to V3.1, you gain access to: 

 • Support for multi-role users to streamline provisioning with a single Clever ID.
• Richer staff data to enable access to all users who support student learning.
• Improved student contact relationships
 • Utilize a student relationships array, which details each contact’s relationships with any students in the district’s Student Information System (SIS).
• For example, if a contact is listed as a parent for two students and an aunt for another, they will have one Clever ID and three entries in the student_relationships array. Please note: Clever has deprecated the API v2 contacts endpoint. If you require assistance, please contact Clever Support. 

 

• New content/resource mapping API
 • This feature allows districts to indicate via the API which resources they want shared with courses, classes, and users.
 

• Access to five new demographic data fields: Disability Status, Primary Disability Type, Section 504 Status, Gifted/Talented Status, and Home Language Code. For a complete summary of available new fields and the accepted field values, please see our Dev Docs: What’s New in API v3.1 .
• Access to attendance data .
• Access to Clever LMS Connect . This integration extends our existing rostering capabilities and supports assignment and grade passback from your application through Clever into a district LMS. 
 
 
 Timeline and transition overview

 The timeline for the transition from V2 to V3.1 varies from partner to partner based on your current setup. Some items that may impact the transition timeline include: 

 • If you utilize V2 student contacts versus V3 
• User types and data points match or are similar to Clever’s data model 
• How you plan to handle multi-role users (i.e. a user who is both a staff and a teacher in Clever) moving forward. In V3 these users will have the same Clever ID, and roles will be listed on their user record on the API. 
• Your development team’s bandwidth 
 
 For more details on your transition process and timeline, please reach out to our team at integrations@clever.com directly. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 What is the timeline to transition? 

 • 
 This varies based on a number of factors regarding your current setup.

 
 What are the benefits of upgrading? 

 • 
 You will be on the most current API version and gain access to a number of new data types. See the Benefits of upgrading section above for full details.

 
 
 Related Articles 

 • For App Partners: Clever LMS Connect

title: For Staff: School Tech Lead vs. Portal Only Permissions
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Account Types 2. Which account type do I have? 3. Staff permissions 4. Creating or updating staff 

 Account Types 

 There are four possible account types for different users in Clever: 

 • Clever Admin
• Non-teaching staff
• Teacher
• Student
 
 There are two types of permissions for non-teaching staff, which are detailed below:

 • Portal Access Only: Staff can log in to their Clever Portal to access applications that are shared with them, but they do not have any admin permissions within Clever.
• School Tech Lead (STL) : Clever Admins can assign certain users the School Tech Lead role, which provides them with limited admin permissions in Clever for their associated school(s). This allows them to support Clever for their associated schools.
 
 

 

 

 

 What account type do I have?

 If you are unsure which account type, please reference the Clever URL after logging in to your account. Staff accounts will lead to a Clever URL that ends with /staff/portal .

 If you are unsure whether you have a School Tech Lead or Portal Access account, please reference the top right corner of your screen after logging in. 

 • Portal Access will only see Portal after logging in to Clever.
• School Tech Leads will see both the Clever Dashboard & Portal. 
 
 

 

 Staff Permissions

 There are two primary roles for staff in Clever:

 Portal Access: 

 • Non-teaching staff with the Portal Access role can log in to applications via single sign-on (SSO) from their Clever Portal but have no other permissions within Clever. Please note that applications must first be shared with staff by a Clever Admin.
 
 School Tech Lead: 

 • View usage metrics for their district & school (using the Portal Analytics tab in their dashboard).
• Modify sharing rules for an application for their associated school(s).
• Manage and print Clever Badges for their school or individual students and sections . 
• Manage and customize the Clever Portal on a per-school basis.
• Create and manage custom section data .
 
 Please note: Sharing rules modification must first be enabled on a per-application basis by a Clever Admin by selecting School Site Sharing in the sharing permissions for any needed applications. See How do I enable School site sharing for School Tech Leads? for more detailed instructions. 

 School Tech Lead users cannot: 

 • Request or add new applications
• Change Student Information System settings
• View troubleshooting logs
• View or edit any Clever settings
• Browse SIS data synced with Clever 
• Add co-teachers to sections
 
 If you need assistance with these topics, please contact your district tech support contact for more information. Related articles: 

 • Clever Roles: School Tech Lead 
• Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 
• School Tech Lead Help Center Articles 
 
 

 Creating or updating staff 

 Clever accounts are managed by Clever Admins. If you are unsure if you have a Clever account or need to have an account created or updated, please reach out to your district's Tech Support Contact or help desk for assistance. 

 You can locate your district's Tech Support Contact information by selecting Get help logging in! from your district's Clever login page. In most cases, districts provide an email address, phone number, or instructions guiding users to the appropriate contact person or department for assistance.

 

 If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact Clever Support ! We are happy to help!

 Related articles: 

 • Clever Roles: School Tech Lead 
• Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 
• School Tech Lead Help Center Articles

title: For Staff: What is Clever?
text:
What is Clever?

 Clever is a secure pipeline between your district's data and the learning applications that you and your students use. We help your district's IT office provide applications with all the information they need to be seamlessly set up for your school.

 Clever Single sign-on (SSO) allows users to log into applications with a single set of credentials, configured at the district level. With the ease of only one username and password, Clever can help teachers reclaim valuable class time. Students and teachers can open their devices, sign into the Clever Portal, and click on their connected applications to automatically access the online learning program! 

 Clever Tip: If you notice that data is missing or incorrect in Clever or one of your connected learning applications, you’ll want to reach out to your district tech support contact to verify that the data is syncing from your Student Information System (SIS) and included in the sharing permissions for the application. Clever is unable to make changes on the district's behalf.

 Who can sign up for Clever?

 We recommend creating one Clever account for your school district that’s managed at the district level. In order to create and administer a Clever account, you need administrative access to your SIS and authority to sign Clever’s Terms of Service. 

 Examples of individuals with sufficient access to manage a Clever account include Chief Technology Officers, SIS/Database Managers, and other district IT Administrators. Non-teaching staff, such as Principals, typically do not have the access to the SIS that is needed to set up a Clever account, therefore we recommend reaching out to a colleague at the district level to create your district’s Clever account. 

 How can I access my district's Clever account? 

 If you’d like access to Clever as a non-teaching staff member, we encourage you to reach out to your district’s tech department. The Clever account owner must upload your non-teaching staff record to the Clever dashboard. If they need guidance in creating your non-teaching staff account, feel free to pass on this article .

title: For App Partners: District data Access
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up Data Access 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 Data access allows you to select which fields are available to your application via Clever’s API. The feature helps districts audit the fields each of their connected applications ingests and ensures you and our other application partners only receive and sync data relevant to your integration. Only application partners with the Rostering (Secure Sync) integration should expect to see the "Data Access" settings in their App Dashboard. Learn more about app integration options with Clever here . 

 If your app requires fields outside of Clever's API, you can have them upload them as extension fields. Learn more here . 

 

 Data access is broken into two categories for sensitive records types: 

 • Required: Fields that are marked as required must be sent by the district to your application
• Optional: Districts can select which optional fields they want to send to your application
 
 For non-sensitive record types please select all fields that your application can ingest. Districts cannot opt out of sending these fields if they are included from their SIS to Clever. 

 

 
 

 Setting up Data Access

 Start with your developer application! By setting and testing changes on your developer application, you can avoid any disruptions for your end users. 

 1. Navigate to the Data Access tab under Application Settings . Here, you’ll see separate modules for each data type, with the option to edit both required and optional fields for sensitive data types and non-sensitive data types. 

 Data Access page" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl0000006c7R&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EM1P000003Bg9y">

 2. Click Edit Required or Edit Optional (only available for student-related collections) to adjust the data access settings for your application. On the left hand side, you’ll see all fields available for the specific data type. Click on a field name to move it over to the Required field list. Fields under Required fields are made available to your application via the API. Fields saved under Available fields will no longer be available for your application once saved. You will be able to come back to this page and add those fields at a later date if you need them. 

 

 

 Please Note: - A field can be marked as either required or optional, but not both. - Fields that are greyed out under ‘Required fields’ are required by Clever to be made available to every application. - If you don’t utilize an entire record type (ex. Student contacts), simply click the Disable [data type] option for the specific data type. 

 3. Click Save and repeat for other data types.

 4. Test the settings with your developer application and sandbox district before adjusting them in your production application.

 

 
 

 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 

 How is data access different from data validation? 

 • Data access changes the fields that are available to your application via Clever’s API.
 • Example: Student DOB is not marked as a required or optional field in your dashboard, so it won’t be available to your application via the API even if the district includes it in their feed to Clever from their SIS.
 

• Data validation is a way to check that the data available to your application meets the requirements of your integration.
 • Example: Student number must be unique within a district. Please read "What are Data Warnings?" for more information on data validation.
 

 
 Can I adjust field requirements on a district-by-district basis? 

 • No. The field requirements set in your dashboard apply to all connected districts. Districts can select which optional fields they make available to your application via Clever, so the fields available to you via the API may differ slightly from district to district.
 
 How are districts notified of changes to field requirements? 

 • Districts will receive a notification within their Clever dashboard if you make any updates to the required or optional fields once the connection is launched.
 
 What is the difference between sensitive collections and non-sensitive collections? 

 • Sensitive collections are data that pertains directly to students (student + student contacts). Districts have more autonomy to exclude or include fields for these collections on a per app basis. 
 
 Some of my fields are missing, how can I get them? 

 • If your application requires ell_status, iep_status, or frl_status let us know and we'll be happy to add those fields for you!
 
 What if I want some of this data in the future? 

 • These settings will be available for you in your dashboard. If your application requires a piece of data in the future you'll simply be able to navigate here and add it. For the majority of districts, that data field will be available upon next sync.

title: For App Partners: Viewing district usage metrics
text:
​ This article contains the following: ​ 1. Overview 2. Filters ​ 3. Key Metrics ​ 4. District Analytics ​ 5. Sharing 

 

 Overview

 At Clever, we understand how important it is to have insight into how your application is used. With Clever Analytics , you can see graphs and statistics that show how your users are accessing your application and drill down into district usage! This article will give you a breakdown of the available data and how you can use filters to narrow down the information you see. 

 

 
 

 Filters

 First, let's take a look at the available filters: 

 

 

 You can use the filters to narrow down the usage data and focus on specific users, applications, and school sites. Different filter sets will cause charts to change- if the filters chosen do not apply to a chart, the chart will temporarily be hidden. 

 ​ Time Range

 

 Units

 

 Here you can select whether you'd like to view your data as a percentage (75% of all students have logged in) or in absolute terms (7,500 students have logged in). 

 Users and Grades

 

 Here you can select whether you want to see usage data for students, teachers, or both. 

 To look at the analytics for specific grade levels, select the grade(s) from the dropdown. Grades are based on student grades, not the grade level for a section or a teacher. Please note: the teacher filter cannot be used with the grade filter. 

 

 
 

 Key Metrics

 Now that we know how to use filters to focus on specific areas let's take a look at the different measures! The statistics shown are based on the users who are being shared with apps. The metrics are not based on the total number of users in your Clever account. 

 

 Usage by scoped users: This number reflects the number of logins from the users that are shared with your application. 

 Unique users: This number reflects the unique number of users who have logged into an application. 

 Total Logins: This number reflects the overall number of user logins. 

 Daily unique users

 

 Daily unique users: This chart reflects the unique number of users who have logged into an application per day. Hover your mouse over a date to see the number of daily unique users for that day. Please note that this chart is disabled if you are using the School year to date time frame filter. 

 Logins by district 

 

 

 Logins by district: This number reflects the number of unique user logins to an individual district. Click on Ascending or Descending to order districts by the number of unique logins. Click on the bar for a district to view analytics for that district. 

 Logins by school 

 

 Logins by school: This number reflects the number of unique user logins from users at each school site. Click on Ascending or Descending to order schools by the number of unique logins. 

 Login Method/SIS Type

 

 Login Method: This number reflects the number of unique user logins by login type. Users will be counted twice if they log in using different login methods- but only once if logging in multiple times using the same identity provider. 

 District SIS Type : This number reflects the number of your districts syncing to Clever with each SIS type. 

 

 
 

 District Analytics

 

 

 Analytics are also available at the district level, in "Analytics" tab on the district profile. 

 

 
 

 

 Sharing

 

 Export data: This button will download a csv of the data you've currently filtered in your analytics view. 

 Share: Share insights with members of your team by inviting them to Clever. These users will be given read-only permissions. Your app admin can adjust these permissions on the Team page.

title: For App Partners: Troubleshooting Application Logins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. User Clever login issues 3. Users who do not have access to your app 4. Users who cannot log in to your app through Clever 5. Student accounts are not correct in your app 

 

 Overview

 This article will walk you through helpful tips to troubleshoot user login issues with your application – for both district and Library-enabled connections. Library-enabled teacher sign-ups are much simpler than district connections - there are no sharing rules to configure or overnight syncs, and teachers can make changes directly regarding which of their classes are shared, rather than having to go through a district admin. However, as with any integration, there are potential things that could go wrong. 

 User Clever login issues

 The first step in troubleshooting a login issue is to verify that the user can log in to Clever. To do this, follow the steps outlined in our Common Single-Sign On (SSO) issues article to triage the issue and identify where the issue is occurring. Login methods are configured by the district, so if the user has issues logging in to Clever, we recommend directing the user to their district support contact for further investigation. 

 

 Clever Tips: • Occasionally, school districts may hide applications in their Clever Portal, which causes users to lose access to the app. You can find out more here: Clever Portal: Hiding Applications • School districts can pause the app sync, which could cause potential login issues (e.g., a new student’s account won’t be synced if on pause). You can view this status on your homepage and see more info here: For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app 

 

 Users who do not have access to your app

 District-enabled connections 

 For district-enabled connections, you can identify whether a teacher or student is configured to have access to your product by searching for their name in your Clever Dashboard. 

 First search for the name of the district, and then search for the name of the user: 

 

 
 Library-enabled connections 

 Please note: S ince Clever does not share sensitive information with Library applications, the user search feature is only available to district-enabled connections. 

 If a user has trouble logging into your Library application, they should reach out to their Clever Admin for further assistance (helpful articles to reference: For Clever Admins: Clever Library , Troubleshooting: Logging in to applications ). Your app’s Library integration can also always be tested on the Integration guide page found in your dashboard, under Library > Integration Guide .

 

 Users who cannot log in to your app through Clever

 If teachers or students can click on your product’s icon, but are not logged in successfully, there is likely an issue with how your product processes the login. For district-enabled connections, to allow your team to reproduce and identify the issue, you can initiate a Clever log-in to your application as a specific student or teacher by clicking the Log in as… button on the user’s profile page (accessed by searching for the user by name): 

 

 This kicks off the same OAuth process that occurs when that user logs in via Clever, and should surface any issues that the user is seeing. For Library-enabled connections, verify your Library integration is working as expected by navigating to Library > Integration Guide in your dashboard. 

 Student accounts are not correct in your app

 This issue most commonly occurs when your product has not synced the latest information from Clever. We recommend either syncing data on each teacher log-in, or adding a Sync with Clever or Import from Clever button to the page in your product where teachers manage their classes, for example: 

 

 In your Clever Dashboard, you can navigate to the profile page for a student or teacher to see the current information that has been synced from the district’s Student Information System (SIS): 

 If the data in Clever for a teacher is incorrect, for example if they are missing a student, it is likely that the district’s SIS is not accurate. In this case, direct the teacher to check with the person at their school who manages the SIS, often referred to as the school secretary or the school registrar.

title: For App Partners: Diagnosing district SSO issues via impersonation
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Impersonating District Users 

 

 Overview

 Clever makes it easy for application partners to view exactly what a user sees when they attempt to log in to your application from the Clever Portal. When troubleshooting, this can help you diagnose an issue, retrieve an error code or message, or gain visibility into what might be confusing for the user. 

 Please note: Only Secure Sync certified applications are able to use this feature. 

 For more information regarding Common SSO issues, please visit this Help Center article . 

 Impersonating District Users

 • Navigate to the Data Browser and select the tab for the user type you will need to debug.
• Input the name of the district or choose from the list of names in the drop-down.
 

 

 • Search for the user that you will need to test. To refine your search you can add an additional filter.
 

 

 

 

 • Select the user and a Detail View will pop up. Here you will be able to log in as the user by selecting, Log in as 
 

 

 • When you’re finished, remember to log out of the application as this user!

title: For App Partners: Staff vs. School Admin
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do school organizations sync staff to Clever? 3. What school organizations see 4. What applications see 5. How are staff shared with applications? 

 Overview

 A common point of confusion in Clever is the difference between School Admins and Staff users in Clever. These are the same user type in Clever but are represented with different titles depending on if you are a school organization or application partner. 

 

 
 Admin Dashboard for schools 

 
 Application Dashboard

 
 Clever API

 
 Staff School Admin School Admin 
 
 

 How do school organizations sync staff to Clever?

 The staff role is designed for users that are not connected to rosters but need access to applications, including school admins! By default, all staff users are created as Portal Access only users, meaning they'll have access to the Clever Portal for Single Sign-On (SSO) into applications just as students and teachers do. However, staff can be assigned the role of School Tech Lead which offers additional administrative capabilities. 

 Depending on a school organization's sync type, there are four ways that they can sync staff users to Clever: 

 • Uploading staff via CSV 
• Sync staff with Active Directory 
• Using Infinite Campus API or Skyward API syncs 
• Custom data: staff 
 
 What school organizations see

 The Data Browser in the school's Clever Dashboard shows staff under a single tab titled Staff . Selecting the Staff tab will show a list of staff/school admins that currently exist in Clever for the school organization. 

 

 

 What applications see

 The application-facing Data Browser shows staff/school admins under the School Admins tab. 

 

 Selecting the School Admins tab will allow applications to apply a filter and view staff/school admins associated with your connected school organizations. 

 How are staff shared with applications?

 Staff and school administrators can use SSO to access learning applications as administrators. Staff users abide by explicit sharing logic so that only staff that are intentionally shared with an application through a sharing rule will gain access to the program. This aligns with existing behavior for sharing all other data types. 

 Please note: The Staff tab on the Schools Dashboard and the School Admins tab on the Application Dashboard reflect the same data.

title: For App Partners: How to enable safety thresholds
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What are safety thresholds? 3. How to enable safety thresholds 4. How to customize safety thresholds 5. How to approve school organizations that require a review 

 

 Overview 

 When a school organization's data management system such as a student information system (SIS) or school management system (MIS) syncs to Clever or the school organization makes a change to their sharing permissions with your application, this can result in significant changes to the rostering data available in Clever’s API for this school organization.

 If you enable safety thresholds, Clever will check to see if there are major data changes every time a school organization syncs or changes their sharing settings, If there are large changes, we’ll prevent data from being updated in Clever’s API until the changes are reviewed and approved by your team.

 Enabling safety thresholds is an important part of syncing rostering information as it ensures that your app team is aware of any potentially significant changes to a school organization's data. Safety thresholds are a safeguard to prevent potentially incorrect or undesired changes from being synced to your application. 

 
 What are safety thresholds? 

 If a school organization's most recent sync or an update in their sharing permissions results in significant changes, a safety hold will be triggered. The school organization will go into the Requires Review state. Data for that school organization won't be updated in Clever until the changes are either reverted by the school or approved by a member of your team.

 Please note: Only application team members with the Admin role in Clever will receive and will be able to approve Safety Threshold notifications 

 You'll see a notification on the Home page of your Clever Dashboard regarding any school organizations in the Requires Review status.

 Syncs requiring a review will need to be manually approved on a per school org basis for these changes to be synced, which can be done in their profile page via the Sync Status tab. Once a safety hold is approved, the school organization’s data will be fully available in Clever’s API. 

 

 
 How to enable safety thresholds 

 To enable safety thresholds, navigate to Settings > District Setup tab in the dashboard. Once safety thresholds are enabled, Clever will begin checking for major changes any time a school organization syncs or changes their sharing settings. You can enable or disable this setting at any time, but if there are school orgs that are awaiting review when you disable the feature, their data will not be updated until their next sync or sharing change. 

 
 How to customize safety thresholds 

 By default, a school organization will enter the Requires Review state if:

 • Over 75% of any data type (e.g. schools, students, teachers, sections) being updated
• Over 10% of any data type (e.g. schools, students, teachers, sections) being deleted
 
 If these thresholds don't meet your needs, you can change them in Settings > District Setup . Here, you can change the percentages of updates and deletes that will trigger a safety threshold. Additionally, you can choose whether you would like creation events to also send a school organization to the Requires Review state. If you leave any box unchecked, we'll ignore that type of data change (such as data creations or deletions). So, for instance, if you leave the creations box unchecked for Teachers, we'll ignore any data creations for teachers. 

 

 
 How to approve school organizations that require a review 

 When you log into your dashboard, we’ll let you know if there are any school organizations that are awaiting review. You can click on this alert or filter for school organizations where Sync Status is Requires Review to see all accounts that have major data changes. 

 Once you click into a profile, you will see an alert that the school organization has major data changes that require your team’s review. You can click on this alert or on the Sync Status tab to view the changes. 

 

 If the changes look good - they’re expected, or won’t cause major issues - you can click the Approve changes button. Once the changes are approved, Clever will process the latest data changes and update the API accordingly. 

 If you’re not sure if the changes are intentional or if the changes could cause major issues, you can use the Email district link to reach out to the school organization's team. If the approval button is greyed out, please reach out the school org and ask them to push another sync with Clever. The following message will show when you attempt to click the greyed-out button if the data from the school organization is stale: "The district's data is out of date - please reach out to the district and have them re-sync with Clever." 

 Please note: While a school organization has the status of Requires Review , no changes will be processed unless the school org reverts changes or you approve them.

title: For App Partners: On-site training guides
text:
Going onsite for training? Need resources to give to your points of contact about Clever? Here are some handy one-pagers to help answer those questions. 

 Fill in your app name, the district's Clever Portal URL, and credentials in the required fields to create customized Clever professional development resources for your district customers. 

 Depending on your application's configuration and target grade levels, here are some variations of the guides to meet your needs: 

 • PD Guide for Admins (with Clever Badges and School Admins) 
• PD Guide for Admins (with Clever Badges) 
• PD Guide for Admins (with School Admins) 
• PD Guide for Teachers (with Clever Badges) 
• PD Guide for Teachers 
 
 To help users log in via Clever, here are some helpful single sign-on (SSO) directions and PDFs: You can see the login methods your districts are using to log into Clever by searching for the district using the district search tool at the top of the dashboard and clicking on the linked district name. This will bring you to the District Overview page. To see SSO methods for the district, scroll down to "Clever SSO Info". Here you can find a wealth of information including the login method(s) that the district set up through Clever, the support contact for the district, and the Instant Login Link the district can use to access your application. If you want to send instructions to your districts for how they log in, click "Download Login Instructions". This PDF walks users through logging in to the Clever portal URL with their credentials and selecting the correct application icon once they're logged in to Clever.

title: For App Partners: School year rollovers
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What if a district wants to add summer school rosters to Clever over the summer? 3. FAQs 

 Overview

 Clever auto-schedules SIS "sync pause dates" for all districts at the end of each school year, or districts can choose to customize their own sync pause dates based on their respective school year schedule. Clever provides districts with end-of-year and rollover guides to help them to plan for summer and the new school year rollover. No data from the district's student information system (SIS) will be synced to Clever during a sync pause. Essentially, the data in Clever will be "frozen in time" during the sync pause period. Depending on the district's school year dates, data will typically begin syncing again between August 1st and August 12th. 

 • First [Pause] Day : This will be the first day the district’s sync is paused, which should coincide with the district’s last day of the school year. 
• Last [Pause] Day : This will be the last day the district’s sync is paused, which should be the day before the district’s SIS data is active for the new school year. 
 
 While a district's sync is paused, Clever will not process any data, allowing the district to make rostering changes to their SIS without interrupting student or teacher access to your application(s). The prior school year's data will be "frozen" in Clever for the duration of the district's pause, allowing the previous school year's users to continue logging into your application without disruption. 

 What if a district wants to add summer school rosters over the summer?

 While pausing a sync prevents SIS-synced data from processing in a district's Clever account and, therefore, updating in their applications, Clever does provide the ability for Clever Admins to create custom data directly from their Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page during a sync pause. This includes custom students, teachers, staff (i.e., school admins), and sections. Custom Data is the method we recommend to districts who would like to continue adding data to Clever during a sync pause. 

 For more information, please visit: Custom Data: Overview 

 FAQs

 How can we see a district’s pause dates? 

 Check out a district’s modal on the District Overview page of your Clever Dashboard to view their school year and pause dates! Can districts still adjust their sharing permissions while their sync is paused? 

 Yes! What does it mean when a district’s sync pause ends? 

 Clever will resume the district's scheduled syncs with their student information system (SIS). Each SIS handles rollover differently, so we encourage districts to check out their SIS-specific information. Districts can learn more about school year rollover here . Once a district's pause has ended and the data for the new school year has synced to Clever, their sync will be placed on an automatic hold, allowing Clever Admins the opportunity to review the data updates before the changes process in Clever. Once reviewed, the district can submit a request to release the sync hold from their Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page. Please note that if the changes are above a certain threshold, a ticket will then be created with Clever Support to review the changes before the sync hold is officially released. This is an added security precaution to ensure massive amounts of data changes do not impact connected applications until they have been thoroughly reviewed by a district's Clever Admin and Clever Support. 

 What should we do if we need to offer summer school access? 

 Have the district contact Clever Support to determine the best way for them to sync summer school data with your application. What does it mean for a district to be syncing over the summer? 

 Clever will continue to sync their data throughout the summer for districts that opt out of using Clever's pause feature. However, during this time, applications will likely see a decrease in the number of students, teachers, staff (i.e., school admins), and sections syncing from Clever. How can we ensure our districts are ready for the new school year? 

 We encourage districts to check 3-4 things once their pause has ended to confirm they’re ready for the new school year:

 • Browse their data from their Clever Dashboard > Data browser in Clever to check that the new school year's rostering data looks correct. 
• Review the sharing permissions for each connected application to ensure they are correct. 
• Review their section_ids to confirm that the ID #s for sections (the section_id field in Clever) are different after a term or school year rollover. We ask districts do this because Clever 'remembers' the sections that have been deleted based on their section_ids changing. 
• If applicable, we ask Clever Admins to review their Clever Badges to ensure they are ready for the new school year. 
 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Sync Holds 
• Summer School: Sync settings 
• For Clever Admins: Troubleshooting Tools - How do I browse district data in Clever? 
• School Year Rollover: Overview, Preparation & Recommendations

title: For App Partners: District sync statuses
text:
You can see the status of a specific district's sync within the District Overview page on your Dashboard. 

 Hovering over the status icon or clicking to the district's Detail View will give you the sync status details a district sees on their Clever Dashboard. The details explain the next action step if any is required by the district or by Clever. 

 Here's an overview of what each Sync Status means for a district:

 Icon Details Next Steps Owner 
 Data is up-to-date No action required. This district's data is good to go! N/A 
 
 We noticed a significant change in the data, and it is being verified for accuracy. 

 
 Clever has notified the district that their sync has not been processed due to significant changes in the last upload, and the district needs to review and approve the changes. 

 District 
 
 Clever is setting up the data sync 

 
 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs only: The district has not yet synced data, but should within 24 hours. 

 Clever 
 Clever is setting up the data sync SFTP syncs: The district has not yet uploaded data to Clever. District 
 There are errors in this district's most recent upload. (Specific errors will be listed in the message.) 
 SFTP syncs: The district needs to make changes to their files in order to resolve the errors. Their most recent upload has not been processed. 

 Clever-Managed Auto Syncs: The district needs to resolve a user-facing error on their side with their sync. They will be notified of this error. 

 District 
 This district has currently paused their Clever data sync until <date>. The district has manually paused their sync. Syncing will resume on the date specified. District

title: For App Partners: Predicted next sync times
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Predicted next sync times 3. Sync types 

 

 Overview

 This article is for application partners and provides an overview of viewing district sync times and district sync types.

 

 
 

 Predicted next sync times

 On the district’s overview page of your Clever Dashboard, you can view the last time a district's SIS synced with Clever, as well as the next time our system predicts it will sync: 

 You can also view a detailed Sync Report on the Warnings tab on the district’s overview page. Many districts sync data to Clever on a regular schedule, so by looking at their previous syncs, we can infer when they will next sync. This is not guaranteed, as the district could change its sync pattern or miss a sync. Other districts sync data in an irregular pattern, and in those cases, we cannot predict the next sync.

 Understanding a bit about the different sync types provides clarity on a district's sync frequency as well.

 

 
 

 Sync types

 Auto Sync

 An Auto Sync with Clever involves a basic initial setup. It requires minimal IT support from a district, as Clever syncs directly with the district's SIS to securely transfer student roster data. These auto syncs typically run every two to three hours, although some districts are set to sync daily (per their request).

 SFTP Sync

 A Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) Sync allows school districts to “push” data to Clever. Districts generate .csv files in a Clever-specific CSV format and submit them to Clever using SFTP. Many districts automate the export of their files sent to an FTP client daily, but SFTP Syncs are more challenging to predict, as the district pushes the data to Clever. 

 For additional details on sync types, please see this article , which helps district admins choose which sync type is right for them.

title: For App Partners: Verifying district data quality
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Data Errors vs Warnings 3. Where to Find Data Errors/Warnings 4. Types of Data Warnings 5. Resolving Data Warnings 

 
 
 

 Overview

 Clever has minimum requirements in order to sync a district's data to our system. If your application has additional fields you require (or even prefer), we can flag a district's data with either Data Errors or Data Warnings. These let a district know their data needs to be further optimized for your application.

 To add additional fields to your data errors, or warnings, reach out to Clever Support ! 

 

 
 Data Errors vs Warnings

 • Data Errors are fields that don't meet the requirements you specified. Records with data errors won't be sent to your application at all, but they will show up under the Data Errors table in app and district dashboards. Please note: records with Data Errors won't be present in your API responses. 
• Data Warnings are fields that, if missing, will prevent your users from having an optimal experience with your application.
 • For example, if you prefer student contact information but creating users isn't dependent on these fields being populated, student contact fields will be listed as Data Warnings.
 

 
 
 

 Where to Find Data Warnings/Errors

 From the home page of your Dashboard, click on the name of a school district. Click on the Warnings tab: 

 

 
 

 Types of Data Warnings

 Missing Information 

 • Your application requires specific fields to be populated in Clever. Issues related to missing information show the number of records without data in those fields. 
• To resolve these issues, the district must update its data to include the missing values. They'll need to input the missing data into their student information system (SIS). If the school uses a manual SFTP or SIS-Managed sync, they'll need to work with their SIS to ensure that the data is exported as part of the CSV files they're sending to Clever. If they utilize a Clever-Managed Auto Sync, they can contact Clever Support for assistance with adding fields missing from their sync. 
 
 Non-unique Information 

 • Issues related to non-unique information will be listed in Data Warnings for the fields below. The issue will include every record that shares each non-unique field value. For example, users with duplicate usernames. 
• To resolve these issues, the school will need to update data in its SIS to provide a unique value for each record. Once the data is updated in their SIS and syncs to Clever, the error or warning will resolve.
 
 

 
 Resolving Data Warnings

 To download details about a district's data warnings click on the Download hyperlink next to the number of affected records: 

 

 For schools to resolve data warnings they will usually need to make changes directly in their SIS.

 Please note: For a school to resolve a data warning or error, they'll need to make updates in their student information system (SIS). The error or warning will resolve once they edit data in their SIS and it syncs to Clever. If a school is missing a data field entirely and uses a manual SFTP or SIS-Managed sync, they'll need to work with their SIS directly to add fields to the CSV files they are sending to Clever. If they use a Clever-Managed Auto Sync, they can contact Clever Support to explore options for adding additional fields to their sync. 

 

 If you have any questions about helping resolve data warnings for a specific school, please contact Clever Support!

title: For App Partners: Common Single sign-on (SSO) issues
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 1. Triaging Login Issues 2. Logging into an IdP 3. Issues Logging in to Clever 4. Issues Logging in to Your Application 

 

 Overview

 As mentioned in our article on For App Partners: What is single sign-on (SSO)? , SSO has many moving parts, which means many things can cause logins to fail. This article will help you navigate these issues and get your users logged in quickly and efficiently!

 

 
 Triaging Login Issues

 First, we’ll want to narrow down where the issue is.

 What are the exact steps the user is taking to log in? 

 Are they logging in with SSO or app-specific credentials (or are they entering credentials for one method into the other’s login page)? This step allows us to check the user is attempting to use Clever correctly. The correct login flows are: 

 Clever Portal 

 • Go to the Clever Portal URL for your district ( ">https://clever.com/in/<shortname> ).
• Click the “Log in with <Identity Provider (IdP)>” button.
• Enter in their IdP credentials.
• They’ll see a loading screen with a book, followed by the Clever Portal.
 
 Instant Login Links 

 • Click on the link provided by the district.
• Click the “Log in with <IdP>” button
• Enter in their IdP credentials.
• They’ll see a loading screen with a book, followed by your application’s page.
 
 Log in with Clever Button 

 • Click on the "Log in with Clever" Button.
• Enter the name of your school in the search.
• Click the “Log in with <IdP> button.”
• Enter in their IdP credentials.
• They’ll see a loading screen with a book, followed by your application’s page.
 
 Are they seeing any error messages? 

 Error messages can help determine at which login stage the issue is occurring. Clever error messages look like this:

 

 We’ll go into specific error messages below. As part of the troubleshooting process, we recommend providing screenshots of any errors.

 

 Logging into an IdP

 If a user is having issues logging in to their Identity provider (IdP), they should contact their district support contact. The user can find the contact information on their portal login page. You can also see the contact in their SSO Settings. To see their settings, click on the district in your Dashboard . The SSO info for each district will be available on the Overview page.

 

 Here are some common issues in this category:

 

 Incorrect Portal 

 Sometimes, your Clever users, especially young students using Log in with Clever buttons, will end up at a different district's Portal URL and won't be able to log in. To ensure they’re using the correct Portal URL, have them check the district's name at the top of the page. Does it match their district's name? If not, you can provide them with their Portal URL or have them click the Not your district button to try their search again. 

 Invalid SSO link 

 SSO links are great for embedding in district portals or distributing as bookmarks! However, sometimes, these URLs are incorrectly added. The correct format is https://clever.com/oauth/instant-login?client_id=<your client id>&district_id=<district id>

 “Not successfully authenticated by your identity provider” 

 This error occurs when the IdP sends us a code representing a login failure. Although it is relatively rare, we have noticed that most cases are caused by saved/cached passwords in Google Chrome . Clearing the browser history usually resolves this issue.

 Incorrect Username / Password 

 If the user can't log in with their credentials, direct them to their district support contact, who should be able to help them get their correct credentials. 

 Please note: Clever cannot provide users with their credentials or reset passwords for users.

 

 Issues Logging in to Clever

 Once the user successfully logs into their IdP, the IdP attempts to send information to Clever and find a matching user. If we find a match, we’ll log the user in. The two most common login issues are listed below. If you see something else, direct the user to their district support contact, who will work with Clever to resolve the issue.

 Not Authorized 

 Some IdPs, like Google and Canvas, require the user to allow Clever to receive identifying information such as email addresses. If the user does not grant Clever access, we can't log them in. The user will need to log in again and authorize the data access.

 Unable to find Matching User 

 The majority of login issues stem from missing data in Clever; either the user needs to be synced with Clever, or their record needs to include the identifying information needed to make a match with their IdP data.

 If the IdP setup is misconfigured, this error can also occur. We recommend directing the user to their district support contact for further investigation.

 

 Issues Logging in to your Application

 If the user is able to log in to their IdP and Clever but isn’t able to log in to your application, this usually suggests that something has interrupted the OAuth2 flow. Clever has provided the ability to replicate the login flow from Clever to your application in the Clever Dashboard. To recreate the login error: 

 • Select the district for the user.
• Search for the user reporting the error.
• Verify the user is shared by the district.
• Click on the name of the user to load their Detail View . Select Log in as [student name] to test the connection with your app. 
 
 If the login to your application fails, this suggests that something has interrupted the OAuth2 flow. Here are the most common issues:

 App Icon Missing / “Ask your administrator to add this app to your Portal” 

 Users can only log in to applications if their data is shared with the application. If their data isn't shared, the app icon won't appear in the Portal, and they will see the above error if they attempt to use an SSO link or Log in with Clever button.

 To resolve the issue, have the user contact their district support contact. Their district administrators will need to share their data with your application.

 Logging in to Incorrect Account 

 If districts have shared devices, users may find themselves logged in to the account of the user who was previously logged in.

 Unable to Log into Application 

 If your users are seeing an error message after clicking on your application icon or SSO link, we recommend the following steps:

 • Confirm the user’s Clever ID is in your app’s database.
• Make sure the record tied to the user’s Clever ID isn't deactivated or otherwise prevented from logging in on the application side.
• Please reach out to Clever Support with the Clever ID of the user and a screenshot of the error message. We’re happy to help investigate the login issue!

title: For App Partners: Tracking district SSO usage
text:
Overview

 Clever surfaces information about how many users are logging in to your application through Clever. You can find this data on the Analytics page of your Dashboard:

 

 Filters

 The top of the Analytics page allows you to filter data based on time range, units (percentage or absolute), user type, and grades.

 

 Key Metrics

 The Key Metrics section gives you a broad overview of the last seven days. It displays the usage percentage, number of unique users, and number of total logins:

 

 

 Daily Unique Users chart

 This displays the number of unique users that logged into your application via Clever on a specific day. 

 

 

 Finally, there is one more breakdown of the unique users by district, school, login method, and district SIS type:

title: For App Partners: Transferring existing districts to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Matchmaker 3. How it works 4. Input CSV guidelines 5. Output CSV format 6. Privacy 

 

 Overview

 This article will go over how to convert your existing district customer accounts to Clever user accounts and includes information on our Matchmaker tool, as well as CSV file formatting and guidelines.

 

 
 

 Matchmaker

 When converting your existing district customers to Clever user accounts, you'll need to match your existing records to records in Clever. Doing this preserves historical data and ensures that records are not deleted and then re-created.

 Clever's Matchmaker is a tool that takes records from your application and matches them with records in Clever. You can access Matchmaker by logging into your Clever dashboard and clicking Matchmaker on the left menu bar. 

 

 Please note: Matchmaker is currently only enabled for applications that support Secure Sync since it requires access to full district records. 

 

 
 

 How it works

 • A district authorizes your application through Clever and shares records.
• You upload a CSV with your existing student, teacher, admin, school, or section/enrollment records.
• Clever's Matchmaker matches the records you uploaded with the records shared with your application in Clever.
• Clever emails you a CSV with a list of both matched records and a list of unmatched records.
 
 

 
 

 Input CSV guidelines

 When creating an input CSV to upload to Clever's Matchmaker, follow the guidelines below to help produce accurate matches. Use this example CSV as a guide.

 • It must be a valid CSV file (in accordance with RFC 4180 )—that means:
 • Every row should have the same number of columns, separated by commas
• Any values with commas in them should be surrounded by a matching set of quotes
 

• It must contain a column header for each field
• It should contain as many fields as possible—the more information you provide about each record, the better chance it will have of being matched
• It must contain a unique internal identifier for each record—you should use this identifier to associate matched records with the original accounts in your application
• It should contain exactly one record for each user—if a user has multiple accounts in your application, please choose one to match with Clever since duplicate records will not be matched
 
 

 
 

 Output CSV format

 Clever's Matchmaker outputs a CSV containing all of the fields from your input CSV, including additional identifying fields from Clever: 

 • Column headers for your input fields will begin with app (e.g. app.email )
• Column headers for Clever fields will begin with clever (e.g. clever.id )
• The matchmaker_results column will contain a string telling you whether or not the record was matched. There are five possible values for this column:
 • match: The record was matched
• from app, no match: The record from your application could not be matched to any record in Clever
• from app, duplicate: The record from your application was a duplicate of another record in your application. To match these records, we recommend you pick one record to serve as the representative for all groups of duplicate records, remove the rest, and re-run Matchmaker.
• from Clever, no match: The record in Clever could not be matched to any record from your application
• from Clever, duplicate: The record from Clever was a duplicate of another record from Clever. To match these records, you may need to work with the district and Clever Support to clean up their data.
 

 
 Once you receive your matching output, you can associate the matched records in your application with the corresponding Clever ID (found in the clever.id column).

 

 
 

 Privacy

 Matchmaker will only match application records against district records that have been shared with your application in Clever. Matchmaker input and output CSVs are purged from Clever's system 30 days after upload. 

 If you have further questions about Clever's Matchmaker, please submit a ticket to our support team.

title: For App Partners: District Data Sharing Permissions & Recommended Settings
text:
This article contains the following: 1. District Data Sharing 2. Other Recommended District Settings 3. The District Experience 

 

 Overview

 Learn about several of the available settings available to application partners to help your districts succeed! This includes setting up recommended data sharing permissions for your districts. As a best practice, we recommend configuring these settings before inviting districts to your application; however, these settings can also be updated at any time in the future. Please visit this article for instructions on how to invite districts to your application in Clever here: For App Partners: Inviting & connecting districts to your app 

 
 District Data Sharing

 To access your District Setup page to modify the settings detailed in this article, please: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Settings page.
• Select the District Setup tab.
 

 The District Setup tab includes the following options:

 • Globally Recommended Sharing : This feature allows application partners to configure recommended data sharing permissions so districts will share the correct data with your application. Essentially, you are providing your districts with data-sharing best practices when connecting with your application for the first time. 

 •
