 Please visit the District Experience section of this article for details about the different options available for districts when sharing their data with your application. The information in the District Experience section will help you to provide the appropriate recommend sharing permissions. For detailed setup instructions, see our guide to global recommended sharing settings .

 
 
 
• Custom setup wizard : This feature allows you to add a custom message which will display for district admins in Clever who are adding your application for the first time and have configured their data sharing. We recommend using this feature to inform district admins about additional setup steps for successfully launching your application, providing them with other specifics they might need, or just letting them know you'll be in touch! Utilizing this feature to inform district admins will help mitigate potential challenges for districts that are setting up your application in Clever. Once you've saved your updates, you will have the option to preview your changes. 
• Enable safety thresholds : This feature helps to ensure that significant data changes from a district (e.g., updating their sharing permissions for your application) will place the next application sync on an automatic hold before processing the data, giving your team time to review the changes before they update your application. For detailed instructions, see our guide to safety thresholds .
• Request API V3 Migration CSVs : This option will map the legacy "role-based" Clever IDs to the more recent API v3.0 User IDs. When choosing this option, a zip file containing one CSV for every one of your connected districts will be provided for download for one week after being created. In the rare case that a district mapping cannot be completed, there will be an error file created documenting this information.
 
 

 
 District Sharing Experience

 When a district either requests your application or is sent an invite by you, they have the option to create "sharing permissions" to determine what district data they want synced to your application. 

 District admins can configure application sharing permission in any one of the following ways:

 • Share by District : All district data in Clever will be synced to the application, including district admins, schools, teachers, students, staff, sections, and enrollments. 
• Share by Schools : Clever will only share any data associated with the schools the district has selected for your application.
• Share by Rules : Clever will only share data that meets the specific criteria the district sets. This is the most recommended sharing type as it provides districts with the most flexibility in specifically determining what data should be included, or excluded, in the sharing. Read this article to learn more about how to Share by Rules! 
• School Site Sharing : If the district has designated School Tech Leads in Clever, a district admin can provide them with the ability to configure application sharing permissions for their associated schools. More info here! 
• Copy Sharing : Clever will copy the sharing permissions from another application that has already been set up.

title: For Staff: How do I bring Clever to my school?
text:
Before Clever can help students and teachers access learning applications at your school, the Clever account needs to be set up by a district administrator. Clever supports all US and Canadian school districts. Clever is a no-cost, secure integration solution that is available to both public and private institutions. Clever allows international districts to create Clever accounts, but these accounts are self-service . Clever’s services are provided to schools in the European Economic Area (the “EEA”), the United Kingdom (the “UK”), and Switzerland in compliance with the European Union’s (“EU”) General Data Protection Regulation ( “GDPR”) and its UK GDPR counterpart. For more information, please see our Additional Terms for Schools and the European Data Processing Addendum that applies to the EEA, UK, and Switzerland. 

 Please reach out to your IT or Curriculum Director to set up Clever today. He re is a template email to send to your district administrators:

 Hi [ admin name ], My name is [ your name ] and I am a staff member at [ school name ]. Please consider deploying Clever at [ district name ], because [ your reason ]. Clever's single sign-on (SSO) will help students and teachers at my school access online learning apps, without needing to remember a new username or password. Clever is a no-cost, secure integration solution that is used by over 60,000 schools and takes just minutes to roll out. You can sign up our district by visiting Clever.com . If you have any questions, the Clever team will be happy to help. Just email success@clever.com . Thank you for your help, [ your name ] [ your email ]

title: For Staff: How do I log in to Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Directions 3. What to do if you've forgotten your password 

 

 Overview 

 Learn more in this article about how to log in to Clever as a staff (non-teaching) user in your school organization.

 

 
 

 How do I log into Clever as a staff member?

 • Go to your district's Clever Portal login , which should follow the format: https://clever.com/in/<district name> (e.g., clever.com/in/cleverusd ). If you don't know your district's Clever login page, you can go to clever.com/login and search for your school. 
• Log in to Clever with your username and password . Your username and password credentials are set up by your school district. They may be your teacher number, ID, or email address.
 If you are both a teacher and a staff member in Clever, you can access both user accounts using the same set of credentials in most instances. After logging in, you'll be able to toggle to switch between accounts using the Profile button in the top right of your Portal.

 

 An example of the dropdown menu of a user who can switch accounts within their Portal. 
 Please note: If your district uses custom usernames and passwords for single sign-on (SSO) (rather than through a third party like Google), you may have two distinct set of credentials: one for your teacher account and one for your staff account. This means your Username will be different between your two accounts. Please check with your district admin for more information on what your credentials should be for your teacher account and staff account. 

• Once logged in to your Clever Portal, you can access your applications! 
 
 
 What to do if you've forgotten your password

 There are a few different methods districts can choose for how you log into Clever. See below for more information. If you district has set up Log in with Clever If your district has set up custom username and passwords through Clever then you may be able to reset your password. Here's how: 

 • Go to your Clever Portal URL and click Get help signing in! under the password field. 
• On the Login Help page, click Reset your password 
• On the Reset Password page, submit your email address and Clever will send you a link to set a new password. 
 
 If your email is associated with both a teacher and staff account in Clever, the email will contain two links - one for each user type. Select the link for the account you wish to reset! 

 If you district has set up any other login method 

 

 Depending on your school organization's configuration, you may be able to reset your password if your organization uses a third-party identity provider for login. Please see here for more information to determine if you can reset your third-party password on the Clever login page. If you don't see the option to reset your password, please contact your Clever admin or IT team for further assistance. The Clever team doesn't have access to your login information.

title: For App Partners: Troubleshooting district data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Browsing District Data 3. Missing Data 4. Incorrect Data 

 Overview

 If you hear from a school organization that's running into issues with missing or incorrect data in your app, these are the steps to view, troubleshoot, and help schools to resolve issues with their student information system (SIS) data.

 Clever Tip: Enable App Sync Notifications to stay updated on school sync statuses. Utilize our API to verify the last successful SIS upload and proactively manage data updates. Learn more in our dev docs or reach out to our support team to request this feature be turned on for your account. 

 

 

 
 Browsing District Data

 To filter and view specific records that are shared with your app, click the Data Browser in the left-hand sidebar: 

 Select the user or data type that you are searching for, and click Add Filter to narrow down your results. You will only see data that has been shared with your application: 

 Click on each record to view more information and to see the "Detail View": 

 

 Clever Tip: You're able to debug instant login problems using the Detailed View as well by clicking "Log in as <user's name>" at the top! 

 

 
 Missing Data

 You will only see data that has been shared with your application. Districts control the data that is shared with your app by setting up data sharing rules and data access .

 There are two potential reasons that district data is missing from your app dashboard: 

 1. The district has not shared the data with your application. Check the Sharing Rules to make sure that the data-sharing rules are configured correctly. 

 

 2. The district's data has not made it from their SIS into Clever. You can pass along or view these SIS-specific articles for troubleshooting steps based on SIS-type. If the district doesn't see the data in their own data browser, something specific to the SIS sync is likely keeping the data out of Clever: 

 • Aeries 
• Infinite Campus API Sync 
• PowerSchool 
• SchoolInsight Auto Sync 
• Skyward Auto Sync 
• Skyward API Sync 
• SFTP 
 
 

 
 Incorrect Data

 If data is incorrect, Clever might be pulling the field (e.g. Course_name) from the wrong field within the district's SIS. For most SIS Auto Syncs , Clever can customize the fields that we pull data from, or filter out unnecessary data.

 Reach out to Clever support so that we can get that all set up!

title: For Application Partners: Viewing information about a connected district
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing school organization data on your Home page 3. Viewing school organization data on their profile page 

 

 Overview

 This article describes how to find and view key details about school organizations connected to your app via Clever. This includes information such as how to confirm the school organizations connected to your application, their SIS sync status, login methods, and more granular details, such as the users syncing from a school. 

 
 Viewing school organization data on your Home page

 

 

 On your homepage, you’ll see a list of your districts. The tags under each district indicate whether the district is: 

 • No Data Shared - the district has approved your invitation, the launch date is in the future, but the district still needs to setup their sharing permissions. No action is required on the app-side unless you would like to recommend any sharing rules for the district.
• Setup Wizard Complete - the district has completed their setup (i.e. the setup wizard you created and sharing permissions) and the connection is now pending the launch date. During this phase, your team has the opportunity to confirm that you're receiving the data you need ahead of the launch date (i.e. when the icon will appear on user Portals and allow logins). You can also choose to move the launch date up if you're ready to launch earlier.
• Launched - this district is ready to use your app through Clever! 
• Setting Up - during this phase, teachers and students cannot see or access your application. 
• Licenses Exceeded - the district has overshared students or schools with your application and need to adjust their sharing permissions. 
• Requires Review - references the data safety thresholds your team has placed (Clever Dashboard > Settings > District Setup page ). This tag indicates the district's data has changed significantly according to your thresholds. The changes will not sync until a member of your team has had a chance to review and approve them.
• Sync Paused - the school district has paused the data sync with your application. This temporarily disables data updates. 
 
 

 Please note: The licensing feature makes it easier to manage and communicate with districts about licenses, but it does not limit how many students or schools can be shared. 

 

 
 Viewing school organization data on their profile page

 From your home page, if you click on a school organization's name, you'll see a few key details about it. At the top of the page, you’ll find the following information: 

 

 • Sync Status: The icon indicates the status of the district's sync. For details on possible sync statuses, please see: District Sync Statuses . 
• Sharing type: This indicates how a district has scoped their data for your application. Any permissions updates must be made by a district administrator. Possible descriptions include:
 • Sharing no data
• Sharing all data
• Sharing selected schools
• Sharing selected sections
• Sharing data by rules
 

• Shared schools : This is the number of schools shared by the district. Here, you are also able to set the number of licenses that you have allotted to the district. For more information on using the license feature in Clever, see Set Up License Usage Limits . 
• Launch date : This is the date that end-users will have access to your application. Usually, this date is agreed upon by you and the district, but the Launch Date in Clever is set by your team. For more information about managing launch dates, reference Setting and managing launch dates .
 
 Further down on the overview page, you will see the following 3 sections: 

 • District Info 
• LMS Connect 
• Sync Info 
• Instant Login Info 
• Clever SSO Info 
• Data Compatibility 
• Schools 
• Clever Training Usage 
 
 

 

 

 

 District Info: 

 In this section, you can find contact information for the district administrator maintaining the Clever account, in addition to the district’s Clever ID, MDR number, and OAuth token. 

 

 LMS Connect: 

 In this section, you can enable sharing with the district’s LMS. Click Invite to connect and get started. Learn more about LMS Connect here . 

 Sync Info: 

 Here, you can view which SIS and sync type (SFTP, Auto-Sync, etc.) the school organization is using to sync data into Clever, and if the sync from Clever is paused by them. The last successful sync date indicates when the school org last synced fresh data with Clever. If the school org's syncs are configured to occur nightly or hourly, you’ll also find the predicted next sync time. 

 Clever Tip: For the most precise information and data regarding a district’s last sync, please contact our support team to enable App Sync Notifications. Utilize our API to verify the last successful SIS upload and proactively manage data changes. Learn more in our dev docs here. 

 

 Clever SSO info: 

 This section provides information on the district’s Clever Portal URL, as well as their login method into Clever. If the district does not use the Clever Portal, they can use the listed Instant Login Link to embed Clever authentication for your application into a different SSO portal. (Note: the district has access to the Instant Login link for your application in their Clever dashboard, as well.) 

 

 Data Compatibility: 

 Review how well the district’s data matches the required fields you set in your Data Access Settings. This section provides a quick overview of the data. Click Details to see more information about what’s syncing, or click Download CSV to export all of the data in one consolidated file.

 Schools: 

 This section lists all schools associated with the district. The table shows whether each school is shared and how many students, sections, and teachers are shared. You can filter the list by All Schools , Shared , or Not Shared .

 

 Clever Training Usage: 

 This section shows Clever Training usage for the district. You can see whether admins associated with the district have engaged with Clever Academy (Y or N). You can also see whether they are a Clever Champion, which means they’ve completed Clever Admin Certification Levels 1–4.

title: For App Partners: Managing district launch dates
text:
Setting Launch Dates

 The "launch date" is the optional calendar date that your application-district connection goes live. On this date, students and teachers will begin to see and access your application from their district's Clever Portal.

 When districts request your app, they can optionally request a specific launch date. When you invite a district to connect, you have that option as well. If you you leave this blank, the default is 30 days after the district accepts the invitation. 

 

 Managing Launch Dates

 You can manage, set, and reschedule launch dates for districts by navigating to your app dashboard 's District Overview page and searching for the district you need to update. Click on their hyperlinked name to open the district's overview page.

 Then click on the pencil icon to the right of the existing launch date. 

 

 From there you can select an updated date to Re-schedule or choose Launch Now if you want the district to go live immediately:

 

 Prior to the launch date, the connection status will show as “Setting Up.” This means that you are able to see the data that the district has shared with you, but district users cannot yet view or access your application from their Clever Portal. During the “Setting Up” stage, no action is required, but the district admin can use the time to make sure they are sharing the correct data with you and check that they are not hitting any data warnings or errors. You are able to modify a launch date up to the date of the launch. If you want to change a launch date after the original date has passed, you can contact our support team for assistance.

title: Edtech Analytics - Overview and FAQs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Viewing Edtech Analytics data 4. FAQs 

 Getting started with Analytics? Check out our Product Spotlight: Supporting your school and district with Clever Analytics, available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 Edtech Analytics shows you all of your school organization's app usage in one place down to the school and grade level, whether or not they are integrated with Clever. Best of all, it’s free! 

 Through our opt-in-only secure browser extension, you’ll get visibility into how many students and teachers are using apps added by your school organization through Clever, added by teachers through Clever Library , and hundreds of others accessed outside of Clever. Edtech Analytics is currently available for Chromebook and Windows devices managed by your school organization. 

 If the scope of your administrator role is set to specific schools, you’ll only see Edtech Analytics data for your scoped schools. Learn more about custom roles and scoped schools here. 

 

 

 
 Setup

 To enable Edtech Analytics in your school organization, you’ll need to set up the Clever Plus extension. Even if your schools already use the standard Clever browser extension for students and teachers to access SSO ( Saved Passwords) applications, you’ll still need to set up the Clever Plus extension, which will replace the standard Clever extension. Clever Plus supports both SSO (Saved Passwords) applications and Edtech Analytics. You can find a short setup guide in your Clever Dashboard > Analytics > Edtech analytics page which will allow you to download the new extension. 

 The setup guide is only visible to Clever Admins. If you're setting up Edtech Analytics on Chromebooks, we recommend adding your Google Console admin to Clever if they don't already have an account. Learn more about adding additional Clever Admins here . 

 

 
 To learn about setting up EdTech Analytics via the Google Admin console, click here. 
 

 1. In your Google Admin console navigate to Device Management > Chrome > Apps & extensions.

 Chrome > Apps & extensions page" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000cFhB&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl00000M2DVx">

 2. If you have already force-installed the Clever extension, please remove it. You can identify the Clever extension by its ID - nopfnnpnopgmcnkjchnlpomggcdjfepo.

 3. Choose the organizational unit (OU) in which you want to install the Clever Plus extension. We recommend you configure the Clever Plus extension for all OUs.

 4. Add the new extension. First, click the yellow plus sign in the bottom right, then choose Add Chrome app or extension by ID. 

 Extension ID: dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa

 Source: From a custom URL

 URL: https://crx.clever.com/updates 

 

 5. Click Save . After saving, you should automatically be taken to the configuration details for the extension.

 6. On this page, set Policy for extensions then click Save in the top right corner. The Policy for extension will follow the format below. Please be sure to replace DISTRICT ID with your District ID value in your Data Browser .

 { "AnalyticsToken": { "Value": "DISTRICTID" } }

 

 7. Set the app's installation policy to Force Install then click Save. 

 

 8. To verify the install was successful, log in on a targeted device and confirm the following:

 • In Chrome, navigate to chrome://extensions and look for Clever Plus 
• In Chrome, navigate to chrome://policy and ensure the AnalyticsToken is set
 
 
 

 To learn more about how to deploy Edtech Analytics using Microsoft Intune, please see: 

 • For Chrome 
• For Edge 
 
 Edtech Analytics is available for Chrome and Edge browsers. Please note that Edtech Analytics is supported on MacBooks that have the Chrome browser enabled. Edtech Analytics is not supported for mobile devices.

 

 
 Viewing Edtech Analytics data

 Once your usage data is ready, you can access it from your Clever Dashboard > Analytics > Edtech analytics page. 

 If the scope of your administrator role is set to specific schools, you’ll only see Edtech Analytics data for your scoped schools. If you need additional access please reach out to your Clever Admin account owner. 

 Filters: 

 • Admins can filter data by time range, including last school year, this school year, the last 28 days, or the last 7 days. You can also filter by school and grade level. Only schools and grades with usage data will be shown in drop-downs. 
 
 Reported Users 

 Students reported and teachers reported provides insight into the usage from devices where the Clever Plus extension is installed. To be counted, users need to: 

 • Be synced to Clever (learn more here about syncing to Clever) 
• Have an email address (Chromebook) or a username (Windows device) 
• Have an Active Directory or Google session running on their device. The Google or Active Directory session running on a user's device must be for the Google or Active Directory account associated with the user in Clever for Edtech Analytics to track it. For example, if a student is logged into their personal gmail address ( jsmith@gmail.com ), but their email address in Clever is john.smith@exampleschool.com , their edtech usage will not be tracked. 
• Be actively using the application (i.e. spends at least 10 seconds on the app page) 
 
 These numbers are updated daily. 

 You can view reported users by day by hovering over the historical trendline. 

 

 Please note: Edtech Analytics only reports data from devices with the Clever Plus extension enabled and does not include usage on personal mobile devices, including iOS devices. 

 

 Applications 

 • Total apps: This shows the total number of allow listed edtech apps identified by Clever. We’re constantly adding to this list, so you may see the number of apps increase as these are added. Don’t see an app on our list ? Let us know so we can add it. 
• Clever apps: Applications that have been added at the district level.
• Library apps : The t otal number of Clever Library applications added to Clever Portal by your teachers. 
• Other apps: Show the total number of allow listed Edtech applications not added through Clever, or any weblink—regardless of whether it’s accessed through Clever or not. 
 Each section is clickable so you can view only those specific application categories in the full list underneath. 

 
 

 Applications - Usage data 

 • The Students % and Teacher % metrics show the percent of students and teachers using the app, out of the total reported by the Clever Plus extension. 
• Active time per user is the average time of all user sessions during the time range selected on the page (since initial setup, in the last 28 days, in the last 7 days, etc.). A user session occurs when a student or teacher is actively using the app. Active time is when the user is on the application tab in their browser. If they navigate away from the tab, they're not considered active. 
 
 In the example above: 

 • 984 students and 97 teachers are reported which includes users that are: 
 • synced to Clever 
• have the Clever Plus extension installed on Chromebooks and/or Windows devices 
• have been actively using a allow listed Edtech application. 
 

• 4% of the 984 students and 4% of the 97 teachers used Word Munchers for 10 minutes on average during the current school year. 
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Below are some common questions about EdTech Analytics If you have more questions not answered in this article, please feel free to reach out to support! 

 What devices and browsers are supported? 

 • Clever's extension is available for Chrome and Edge browsers. Clever's setup guide provides instructions for Chromebooks and Windows devices using Chrome browsers . Districts using MacBooks can access Edtech Analytics as long as they run Chrome with the extension enabled. While Clever does not currently provide setup guidelines for mobile devices (e.g. iOS, Android, etc.), the extension

title: Participation & Resource Usage Reports
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Participation 3. Resource Usage 4. Exporting Reports 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 With participation reports, Clever provides greater insight into your users' participation and daily activities. To opt in to participation reports, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Analytics > Reports .

 Clever currently requires a two-week setup time to aggregate data and make reports available to districts. Once you have signed up, Clever email you when participation reports are ready for your account.

 Subject: Your participation reports are ready! 

 Once Clever has prepared participation reports for your schools, navigate to Analytics > Reports to view the data. On this page, you can view reports for the current week, Monday-Sunday. To view previous weeks, use the date selector in the top left: 

 At this time, reports are available via manual download or automated SFTP delivery .

 Participation 

 Participation reports provide a daily snapshot that summarizes usage for students, teachers, and staff in your district, including inactive ones.

 Participation reports can be downloaded individually or in bulk. Clicking Download week will download a zip file with the student, teacher, and staff files for each day of the selected week.

 

 The data fields available in the download files are:

 
 Field Name

 
 Description

 
 date Date when user was active in the district timezone 
 sis_id The SIS ID (student_id) associated with the Clever record for the user. If the user's record is no longer active in Clever, the SIS ID field will display as blank. 
 clever_user_id The Clever ID for the user 
 clever_school_id The Clever ID of the school associated with the Clever record for the user. 
 active User was active on Clever on this date . 
 The active use of Clever is currently defined if the user performs any of the following actions:

 • Log into their district Clever account
• Access an application by clicking on the app icon in the Clever Portal
• Access an application through an Instant Login Link 
• Logged in to an application using a Log in with Clever button
• Accessing any link added to the Clever portal by the district, school, or teacher
 
 
 num_logins The number of times the user accessed resources through Clever during the day 
 num_resources_accessed The number of unique resources accessed through Clever during the day 
 school_name The name of the primary school assigned for the user 
 
 

 Resource Usage

 Resource Usage reports provide a daily snapshot of each resource accessed by a user on a given day and are available for active students, teachers, and staff. 

 Resource Usage reports can be downloaded individually or in bulk. Clicking Download week will download a zip file with the student, teacher, and staff files for each day of the selected week.

 

 The data fields available in the download files are:

 
 Field

 
 Description

 
 date Date when user was active in the district timezone 
 sis_id The SIS ID (student_id) associated with the Clever record for the user. If the user's record is no longer active in Clever, the SIS ID field will display as blank. 
 clever_user_id The Clever ID for the user 
 clever_school_id The Clever ID of the school associated with the Clever record for the use 
 resource_type The type of resource accessed by the user. Can be one of: 
 • clever-district-added
• clever-teacher-added
• district-link
• teacher-link
 
 
 resource_name The name of the resource on the portal. Could be the URL in case an unnamed link was added to the Clever portal 
 resource_id The Clever ID of the resource accessed by the user. Useful in case the name of the resource is changed. 
 num_access The number of times the specific resource was accessed through Clever during the day 
 school_name The name of the primary school assigned for the user 
 
 

 Exporting Reports 

 Navigate to Analytics > Analytics settings to enable SFTP exports of reports. Once enabled, Clever will display the information to connect to the SFTP directory for your schools. Clever will automatically upload reports to the SFTP directory at the same time the data is made available in the Clever Dashboard. Daily activity reports are available the next day by 1pm local time. Reports will be organized as follows:

 • Folder: daily-participation
 • File: {yyyy-mm-dd}-daily-participation-students.csv
• File: {yyyy-mm-dd}-daily-participation-teachers.csv
• File: {yyyy-mm-dd}-daily-participation-staff.csv
 

• Folder: resource-usage
 • File: {yyyy-mm-dd}-resource-usage-students.csv
• File: {yyyy-mm-dd}-resource-usage-teachers.csv
• File: {yyyy-mm-dd}-resource-usage-staff.csv
 

 
 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Which roles have access to Participation Reports? 

 • Clever Admins and District Curriculum Leads can enable reports. 
• Clever Admins and District Curriculum Leads can view reports.
 
 How can teachers view student participation data? 

 • Teachers have a version of participation reports called Analytics . You can find information on Analytics here . 
 
 When are Participation Reports updated? 

 • Reports for daily activity are available the next day by 1 pm local time. Example: the participation activity for Tuesday will be available on Wednesday at 1 pm local time. 
 
 Is this the same data that is available to teachers through Analytics? 

 • Yes, Clever generates the exports using the same information visible to teachers in the Portal. This ensures that the teacher and school can access the same information!
 
 Will teachers be able to export the reports in the Portal? 

 • No. Export functionality will only be available to Clever Admins.
 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 
 • Analytics Overview 
• Portal Analytics 
• Edtech Analytics - Overview and FAQs

title: Portal Analytics
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Filters 3. Metrics 

 Overview

 At Clever, we understand how important it is to track application logins and usage for your district users. Using Portal Analytics , district admins have access to graphs and statistics that show how many users are accessing their applications, how often they log in, and which schools or apps are being used the most! This article provides a breakdown of the available data and how it can be filtered. 

 Portal Analytics includes data for all logins powered by Clever, including instant login links . 

 

 

 

 
 Filters

 First, let's take a look at the available filters. You can apply different filters to narrow down the usage data and focus on specific users, applications, and school sites. If the selected filters don't apply to a chart, the chart will temporarily be hidden.

 Time & date range 

 You can filter Portal Analytics by time and date ranges. Click the time range to choose from the las 7 days, 28 days, by school year, or Custom dates, which allows for a custom time range any time in the past year. The Export all data button will download data associated with your selected filters. Choose thestart and end date to select a custom date range! 

 Units 

 Select whether you'd like to view your data as a percentage (e.g., 75% of all students have logged in) or in absolute terms (e.g., 7,500 students have logged in). 

 

 App 

 Select All Apps to view login data for all apps connected to your district's Clever account. To look more closely at a specific app's usage, select the application from the dropdown. When you select a specific app, the 'Usage by App' graph is disabled. 

 Users, Grades, and Schools 

 Here you can select whether you want to see usage data for students, teachers, or both. 

 

 To look at the Portal Analytics for specific grade levels, select the grade(s) from the dropdown. Grades are based on student grades, not the grade level for sections or teachers. 

 • Please note: The teacher filter can't be used with the grade filter. 
 
 

 To focus on a specific site instead of All Schools , you can select the school location from the dropdown. Multiple school sites can be selected from the dropdown as well. 

 • Please note: The Usage by School graph is disabled when you select a single school. 
 
 

 

 
 Metrics

 Now that we know how to use filters to focus on specific areas, let's look at the different measures! The statistics shown are based on the users being shared with the apps . The metrics are not based on the total number of district users in Clever. 

 Usage by scoped users 

 

 Usage by scoped users: This number reflects the number of users shared with the application. 

 Unique Users 

 

 Unique users: This number reflects the unique number of shared users who have logged into the application. 

 Total Logins 

 Total Logins: This number reflects the overall number of user logins.

 

 Daily Unique Users 

 

 Daily unique users: This chart reflects the unique number of users who have logged into an application per day. Hover your mouse over a date to see the number of daily unique users for that day. 

 • 
 Please note: This chart is disabled if you're using the School year to date time frame filter or Custom dates filter. 

 
 

 Logins by app 

 

 Logins by App: This number reflects the number of unique user logins for an individual application. Select the Ascending or Descending option to order apps by the number of unique logins.

 

 Logins by school 

 Logins by school: This number reflects the number of unique user logins from users at each school site. Select the 'Ascending' or 'Descending' option to order schools by the number of unique logins.

 Login Method / Device / Browser 

 

 Login Method : This number reflects the number of unique user logins by login type. Users will be counted twice if they log in using different login methods, but only once if they log in multiple times using the same Identity Provider (IDP).

 Device : This number reflects the number of unique user logins for each device. Users will be counted twice if they are logging into different devices, but only once if they log in multiple times to the same device type.

 Browser : This number reflects the number of unique user logins for each browser. Users will be counted twice if logging in through different browsers, but only once if they log in multiple times through the same browser.

 
 • Please note: These charts are disabled if you're using the Custom dates filter. 
 
 
 Usage by Additional Resources 

 If you've added any links to your district's Clever Portal , you can view the number of clicks each resource has gotten under the "Usage by Additional Resources" section! For instance, let's say you added a link to your Student Information System for teachers. You can see how many times this was clicked during a specific timeframe as set by the filters mentioned above. 

 • Please note: This section is disabled if you're using the Custom dates filter.

title: Analytics: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Portal Analytics 3. Participation Reports 4. Edtech Analytics 

 

 Overview

 Getting started with Clever Analytics? Check out our Product Spotlight: Supporting your school organization with Clever Analytics available in Clever Academy! 

 Clever offers several ways to view edtech usage in your school organization. This article provides a high-level overview of our analytics offerings, including who can access them and where to find more information.

 The Analytics tools outlined below are provided to school organizations at no cost.

 
 

 

 
 Portal Analytics

 

 Using Portal Analytics , your Clever administrative team can access graphs and statistics on application usage in the Clever Portal. Here you can see how many users are accessing their applications, how often they log in, and which schools or apps are used most.

 

 Portal Analytics are available automatically and require no setup. While data can be filtered and viewed in many different ways, Portal Analytics doesn’t provide insights into individual users. If you are interested in viewing data for specific users, please see Participation Reports . 

 For additional information on Portal Analytics, see this article . 

 
 

 
 Clever Admin 

 
 District Curriculum Lead 

 
 District Help Desk 

 
 School Tech Lead 

 
 Staff (Portal Access) 

 
 Teacher 

 
 View For associated schools - - 
 Export Data For associated schools - - 
 
 

 
 Participation Reports

 

 Participation reports allow teachers and your Clever administrative team to view student participation for Clever-integrated applications at the user level. There are two reports provided:

 • Participation reports provide a daily snapshot that summarizes usage for students, teachers, and staff in your school organization, including those who may be inactive. If you're interested in determining if a specific user accessed an application, you can do so with participation reports. 
• Resource usage reports provide a daily snapshot of each resource accessed by a user on a given day and are available for active students, teachers, and staff. 
 
 This data is automatically available to teachers in their Clever Portal on the Analytics page. Participation Reports must be enabled by a member of your administrative team with proper permissions. For additional information on Participation Reports, see this article . 

 
 

 
 Clever Admin 

 
 District Curriculum Lead 

 
 District Help Desk 

 
 School Tech Lead 

 
 Staff (Portal Access) 

 
 Teacher 

 
 Enable reporting for the school organization - - - 
 View School organization reporting must be enabled to view School organization reporting must be enabled to view School organization reporting must be enabled to view - - - 
 Export Data 
 

 School organization reporting must be enabled to export data 

 - - - - - 
 
 

 
 Edtech Analytics

 

 Edtech Analytics shows all of your school organization's app usage in one place, down to the school and grade level, whether or not they're integrated with Clever . 

 Edtech Analytics must be enabled and configured by a member of your admin team. Edtech Analytics is currently available for Chromebooks and Windows devices managed by your school organization and requires installing a secure browser extension.

 

 Once configured, Edtech Analytics will provide visibility into student and teacher usage of apps, whether added through Clever, added by teachers through Clever Library, or accessed outside of Clever. Please note that exporting Edtech Analytics data is not supported at this time.

 For additional information on Edtech Analytics, see this article . 

 
 

 
 Clever Admin 

 
 District Curriculum Lead 

 
 District Help Desk 

 
 School Tech Lead 

 
 Staff (Portal Access) 

 
 Teacher 

 
 Enable - - - 
 View For associated schools - - 
 Export Data - - - - - - 
 
 

 Class Analytics: While teachers can't access the Analytics mentioned above, they have their own version, called Class Analytics. Learn more here ! 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Portal Analytics 
• Edtech Analytics - Overview and FAQs 
• Participation & Resource usage reports

title: For Clever Admins: Clever’s email policy for teachers
text:
Overview

 This article details Clever's policy around teacher email communications. If enabled from the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Communication page, there are three types of emails Clever may send to teachers:

 • Professional development: Emails about training, certification, webinars, tutorials, and Clever Academy , our free online training and certification program for educators.
• News and product updates: Emails that cover new feature announcements, partnerships, new product resources, and Clever seasonal programs just for teachers.
• Service-related: Emails that facilitate the teacher's login and use of Clever according to the features enabled by their district. Examples of these emails include:
 
 • Shared district resources
• Password retrieval
• Changes in terms of service
• Product notifications
 
 Teacher Communications

 Teachers will only receive professional development and news and product update emails from Clever if:

 • Their district has subscribed them to receive emails from the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Communications page.
• They have a teacher account in Clever.
• The teacher has not unsubscribed themselves from receiving Clever emails.
• Their district has committed to using the Clever Portal throughout the school year.
 
 Service-related emails are critical for account functionality; for this reason, they are not subject to the district or teacher's email subscription settings. However, teachers can disable service-related emails by using the unsubscribe link in the email(s) they receive. In addition, teachers will only receive service-related emails from Clever if their user account is synced with Clever.

 Unsubscribe: Every teacher email will contain a clear unsubscribe link providing the ability to opt out of receiving Clever emails.

 Third-parties: Clever promises to never rent, sell or share teacher email addresses with third-party vendors for marketing purposes, per Clever's privacy policy .

 Clever's Teacher Email Policy

 Clever receives frequent requests from teachers and districts for more information about Clever utilization best practices. Our email content helps equip teachers with the knowledge and tools they need to succeed, whether teaching from school or remotely. Our goal is to help teachers save time while managing their digital classrooms and staying connected with students and families or guardians. In addition, we provide them with resources that enable them to address questions and issues quickly and independently. 

 Aside from professional development and news/product updates, Clever also sends service-related emails or account alerts to teachers (e.g., resetting password emails, downloading Clever Badges, etc.). These transactional emails facilitate the teacher's login and use of Clever according to the features contracted and enabled by the district and are often critical to account functionality. 

 We're excited to help teachers get the most out of the Clever Portal for their classroom and continue to save them valuable teaching time!

title: For Clever Admins: Announcements
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Creating announcements 3. Announcement preview 4. Setting a priority notification 

 

 Overview

 District Admins can send announcements to various district users in Clever! This article details the process for creating, previewing and sharing announcements.

 Creating announcements

 To create an announcement for users in your district:

 • Navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Communication page.
• Select 'Create notification', and then complete the form.
 • Dates: When should this announcement appear? 
 • The announcement will be visible in Clever Portal for selected users between 12:00AM on the Start Date and 11:59PM on the End Date (in your district's time zone)
 

• Title & Body: What should this announcement say? 
 • You can create an announcement of up to 250 characters for your users to see.
 

• Visibility: Who should see this announcement? 
 • Students, teachers and/or staff
 

• Links (Optional): Add a link to more information 
 • You can include a hyperlink to direct users to resources or additional information.
 

 

• Once the announcement is created, you will be able to see whether it is 'Live', 'Upcoming' or 'Expired' for the selected users in Clever.
 
 

 

 Please note: Notifications for users in your district can be created by district-level admins and School Tech Leads. For more information on user roles, click here . 

 Announcement preview

 When a selected user logs in to Clever, they will see a bell icon in the top-right corner with the number of unread notifications. They can click on the bell icon to open the announcement.

 

 

 Setting a priority notification

 A priority notification can also be assigned to announcements. Priority notifications will display at the top of the list for selected users. Only one notification can be priority across the entire district. 

 • To set a notification as priority, select the star icon in the 'Actions' column. Priority notifications will also be noted under the 'Status' column. 
• Priority notifications will display in Clever Portal at the top of the notifications dropdown and are denoted with a blue star. 
 
 

 FAQs

 How do School Tech Leads create announcements? 

 • School Tech Leads can create announcements for the schools they are connected to. For instructions on how School Tech Leads can create announcements, visit: For School Tech Leads: Announcements 
 
 Can I create an announcement for select schools? 

 • Announcements can only be filtered by User Type. If you wish to create an announcement for a specific school, we recommend adding a School Tech Lead for the school and posting the announcement from the STL user account. Directions on adding School Tech Leads can be found here .

title: For Clever Admins: Using and recommending Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Recommending the Library to teachers 3. Recommending Library applications to teachers 4. Viewing Library installs 5. Additional Library settings 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 The Clever Library is a safe way for teachers to explore new classroom tools. Instead of finding new applications anywhere on the internet that may have varying data privacy standards, teachers can try new apps in the Clever Library with built-in data-privacy guardrails and admin oversight.

 When a teacher adopts a Library application with SSO, Clever shares only the absolute minimum information: student first name, last initial, grade level, and course enrollment. Clever does not share any sensitive information with Library applications, including student numbers, SIS IDs, emails, state IDs, or demographic information. Furthermore, all Library applications sign a universal data privacy agreement.

 This article will guide you through supporting the use of the Clever Library and making recommendations for teachers. If you need information on setting up Library restrictions, please see this article .

 
 Recommending the Library to teachers

 Clever Library handles rostering automatically and ensures data minimization, making it safer than teachers signing up for apps independently. You can actively recommend the Library as the primary place for teachers to discover tools. Below is a suggested email template you can utilize to share more about the Clever Library with your teachers.

 
 Email template to recommend teachers use the Library for resources 
 
 Subject: The safest way to find new classroom apps 

 Hi Team,

 We know how important it is to find the right digital tools to engage your students. To help you do this safely and save you time on setup, we recommend using the Clever Library to discover new applications.

 Why use the Clever Library? 

 • 
 It's Safer: Apps in the Library sign a universal data privacy agreement and only receive the minimum necessary student data (just first name and last initial).

• 
 It's Faster: You don't need to manually create student accounts. Rostering is handled automatically through Clever.

• 
 It's Approved: Tools in the Library operate within our data privacy guardrails.

 
 You can access the Library directly from your Teacher Portal, and learn more about adding applications in this Clever article .

 

Best, [Admin Name] 
 

 
 Recommending Library applications to teachers

 To recommend resources, go to Clever Dashboard > Applications > Library Controls > Recommended. 

 Recommend a resource by searching for it by name. We also encourage adding notes to your recommendations to highlight to teachers why and for what purpose a resource is recommended. You may also delete a recommendation at any time.

 

 

 Please note: The Clever Library is a place for teachers to find and adopt resources - therefore, the only resources that can be recommended are those that support teacher sign-ups. School or district-adopted resources, such as Dreambox, Lexia, or iReady, aren't available in the Library. 

 

 Resources that are recommended will appear front and center when a teacher visits the Library. 

 

 When a teacher visits a recommended resource, your recommendation and associated notes are featured prominently. 

 

 

 
 Viewing Library Installs

 To view applications that have been installed by teachers, navigate to your Library controls > Applications tab . Here you'll see a column next to each application that shows you how many Teacher Installs the app has. 

 To view installs with greater detail, scroll to the bottom and select Export teacher install data. 

 

 The information provided in the downloadable CSV is described below:

 
 Field

 
 Description

 
 teacher_id The Clever ID of the teacher that installed the resource 
 first_name The first name of the teacher that installed the resource 
 last_name The last name of the teacher that installed the resource 
 email The email address of the teacher that installed the resource 
 school The school of the teacher that installed the resource 
 app_id The Clever ID of the resource installed 
 app_name The name of the installed resource 
 install_date The date the teacher added the resource 
 
 

 
 Additional Library settings

 To ensure administrators always have control over their school organization’s EdTech usage through Clever, there are additional controls admins can utilize to manage the Library. Below is a short list of these additional controls. You can learn more in this article.

 • 
 New application approvals: Configure settings so that any newly added Library application requires your approval before teachers can begin utilizing it.

• 
 New application notifications: Ensure you are informed about new Library applications by setting up email notifications.

• 
 Block a resource: If there is an application your school organization has determined your teachers shouldn’t utilize, you can block it in the Library. You can also bulk block applications.

• 
 Turn off the Clever Library: You can also elect to turn off the Clever Library for your school organization.

 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Can I block the Library or specific applications? 

 • 
 Yes, please see this article for more information .

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Managing Library restrictions and application approvals 
• For Clever Admins: Clever Library privacy

title: Summer School: Application requirements
text:
Every application that your schools will want to use during the summer has different considerations for how they support Summer School. This section breaks down some of the common circumstances and suggests guiding questions you can ask your app partner to make sure your district has all the information you need to offer that app this summer. 

 Please note: If you want to pause syncing your data to a particular application, please see this article: For Clever Admins: Pausing your sync for a specific app. This is a perfect tool for semester rollover and summer school, but shouldn't be confused with an SIS sync pause. 

 

 
 ⚙️ Integration details

 You will want to speak with your application partners about their Clever integrations as part of preparing for summer school. Certain realities of summer school (such as students transferring to a new campus) can provide difficulties for the ways in which applications process your data. Your district will want to reach out to your application partners as soon as possible to plan for any of these considerations. 

 Questions for your app partners: 

 • Does your application accept students enrolled at multiple schools and/or sections? 
• Does your application accept Clever’s extension fields? 
• Does your application recommend specific sharing permissions? 
 
 

 
 ⚠️ Data requirements

 Every application has slightly different data requirements. While some require just first name, last name, and class associations, others may require further data points.

 Make sure to ask your vendors what data is required for summer school and if it is different than during the rest of the year to avoid needing to make last-minute changes to your rosters to meet requirements.

 Questions for your app partners: 

 • What data is required for accounts to be generated?
• What data is recommended to take full advantage of your application features?
 
 

 
 

 📑 Contract

 Check with your procurement department and/or the application “owner” at the district to confirm your contract terms. Some contracts are due for renewal during the summer which might mean a new contract is needed to maintain service, even if your sync is paused. Other contracts might limit the number of users your district has access to and assigning that application over summer school might exceed that limit. Have a conversation within your district to ensure you are ready for these limitations.

 Questions for your app partners: 

 • When does our contract for your service expire? Does it include summer school?
• How many licenses have we purchased for this service? Do we have remaining licenses for summer school?
 
 

 

 📚 Content Mapping

 Certain textbooks require a manual content mapping process after rostering through Clever is complete to ensure the right students get the right books. Reach out to your application partner to understand if and how they map content and get tips from their experts on best practices for the summer.

 Most apps will require a specific class or course codes to help with that content assignment that may need to be passed to your summer school staff for activation. Questions for your app partners: 

 • Do you require content mapping from Clever? 
 
 

 
 

 To continue setting up Clever for summer school, proceed to Step 2: Sync settings

title: Summer School: Final details
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sharing permissions 3. Notify teachers 4. Review your Admin Team and account owner 5. Update tech support contact 6. Review Clever Badges 

 

 Overview 

 This article outlines the final steps to ensure a smooth transition to summer school. We'll walk you through reviewing application sharing permissions, including recommended best practices for sharing summer school data. Additionally, you'll find essential reminders on communicating plans to teachers, updating your tech support contact information, and managing the annual review process for Clever Badges.

 

 
 Sharing permissions 

 Once you finalize your summer school rosters in Clever, ensure that all of your users have access to the correct applications. In your Clever Dashboard , review the sharing rules for each application, taking care to ensure that you are sharing the new sections, students, teachers, and staff with the applications they need. 

 Questions to ask: 

 • Which applications do we need to use over the summer? 
• Who needs to use these apps? 
• How can we easily identify and share summer school users? (Recommended: extension fields ) 
 
 View instructions for adjusting sharing permissions here . Please note, when using a Sharing by Schools rule, s ections with a school association that is different from a teacher's primary school won't be shared. For example, a teacher has sections at two schools: School A and School B. If you set the sharing permissions for the application to share School A, only the teacher's section at School A will be shared. This is important for teachers who will be providing instruction at an alternate school location during summer school. 

 Clever Tip: We recommend using a Share by Rules sharing type for summer school data. 

 

 
 

 Notify teachers 

 We highly recommend notifying teachers of your summer school plan! You can use templates found here . 

 Please note: When you start your new school year in Clever, our system will automatically update any Teacher Pages shared with specific sections to Just me sharing. This excludes Just Me and All sections pages. We recommend you notify your teachers so they can take action. Learn more here . 

 Clever's announcements tool is available to all school organizations and delivers notifications directly to students and teachers in the Clever Portal. To learn more about announcements, please see this article .

 

 
 

 Review your Admin team and account owner

 Changes in personnel and procedures often occur at the end of the school year. For account security, please review the administrators listed on your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page. You'll want to remove any administrators who are no longer working at your school organization. If new administrators are joining your Clever team, have them check out our quick start guides in Clever Academy !

 Additionally, please ensure that your Clever Admin (owner) will continue to work for your school organization. Only the account owner can transfer ownership to another administrator. If your current owner is leaving your school organization, Clever recommends that the owner transfer ownership of the Clever account to another admin before they leave.

 

 
 

 Update tech support contact 

 As we mentioned above, we know that changes in personnel and procedures often occur at the end of the school year. In addition to reviewing your Admin team, please review your tech support contact to ensure that students and teachers can receive help from your team if needed. You can find directions for updating your tech support contact here . 

 
 Review Clever Badges 

 Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature an expiration date. If a printed Badge has an expiration date, it will continue to work past that date. Newly printed Badges will not have an expiration date. 

 Admins should still void and print Badges during your district’s natural transition period, like summer or winter rollover. 

 If your rollover period is in the summer, we recommend voiding and reprinting all Badges during the summer so you can plan to do so annually each summer moving forward.

title: For Clever Admins: Purchasing Clever's paid products
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Purchasing Clever’s paid products 3. Reviewing your paid products 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 The Clever Dashboard is your one-stop shop for educators to purchase Clever's paid products, including Clever IDM, Classroom MFA, and Premium Support, as well as consulting and training services . This article addresses making purchases, locating order information, managing quotes, invoices, and more. If you have additional questions, this guide may help resolve them without needing to reach out. 

 
 Purchasing Clever’s paid products

 To get started with purchasing a Clever product, follow the steps below:

 • 
 Log into your Clever Admin account by heading to schools.clever.com.

• 
 Navigate to the Clever Product you’d like to purchase:

 • 
 Clever IDM: Clever Dashboard > User Management > IDM 

• 
 Classroom MFA: Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Classroom MFA 

• 
 Premium Support: Training & Support > Premium Support

• 
 Consulting: Training & Support > Clever Consulting

• 
 Training: Training & Support > Clever Training

• 
 CI Solutions & Clever Badges: Authentication > Badges > Badges+ 

 

• 
 Review the overview of the product and if it feels like a good fit, click Get started .

• 
 Enter the number of licenses or sessions you wish to purchase, then click Proceed to pricing summary. 

• 
 Select your desired subscription length and dates before reviewing your pricing summary. If all looks good, click Proceed to payment method .

 • 
 Please note that the total number of users may exceed the amount specified in step 1, as each product may have a minimum user requirement for access.

 

• 
 To purchase, select whether you’d like to pay by credit card or by purchase order and enter the required information.

• 
 Next, click Review your order/quote (depending on the payment method selected)

• 
 Lastly, review your order or quote and complete your purchase.

 
 If you currently use a paid product with Clever, you can also renew your subscription via the App Store! For more information on renewals, please see our article Clever products: Purchasing, billing, and renewals .

 
 Reviewing your paid products

 You can also review any current paid product subscriptions in the Clever Dashboard. Navigate to your Billing page , and you’ll see an overview of your subscriptions, in-progress orders, and saved items:

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I send a check to pay for my order? If so, where do I send my check? 

 • 
 Yes, you can send us a check to pay for your order! Please send it to 75 Remittance Drive, Dept. 6598 Chicago, IL 60675.

 
 

 Where do I need to send my PO and/or Tax-Exempt forms? 

 • 
 POs and Tax-Exempt forms are uploaded on the order page which is accessible in the Sidebar when you click Billing .

 
 

 Why do I see more student licenses than I wanted to purchase? 

 • 
 Some of Clever’s paid products have a minimum amount you must spend to use the product. So if your quote is below the minimum, Clever will update the total to meet the minimum for that product. 

 
 

 How do I request a demo? 

 • 
 To request a demo for one of Clever’s products, simply fill out this form and Clever will reach out to get the demo scheduled for you.

 
 

 Who do I contact to manage my subscription? 

 • 
 If you need to manage your subscription and you don’t have a direct contact, please reach out to our Support Team at support.clever.com.

 
 

 How to edit a quote to change the number of licenses? 

 • 
 To update your quote, you will need to start a new order. 

 
 

 How do I switch my quote from one product to another? 

 • 
 If you created a quote for the wrong product, you must start a new order for the correct product.

 
 

 My badges aren’t working with the CI Solutions + Clever Badges integration. Help! 

 • 
 We recommend reaching out to our award-winning support team at support.clever.com.

 
 

 Find more info about Clever’s paid products: Clever IDM Classroom MFA Premium Support Training and consulting CI Solutions + Clever Badges Google for Education Plus + Clever IDM

title: Summer School: Creating data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What is custom data? 2. How does custom data support rostering for summer school? 3. How can I upload custom data? 

 What's custom data? 

 Custom data is a way to create data records within Clever that don't exist in your district’s SIS (Student Information System) or CSV data files. This can be helpful in cases such as creating summer school data, organizing unique groups of students, and adding staff members who are not in the district’s information system. Custom data can be created, edited, and browsed from the Custom Data page at Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data. 

 Please note: Custom data should not duplicate data that is already in Clever that is synced by your SIS. If a user or a section is already in Clever, do not add it to your custom data. 

 

 
 

 How does custom data support rostering for summer school? 

 We know that many districts house summer school data outside of the SISs or CSV data files used during the school year. Custom data enables you to easily upload new summer rosters into Clever without reconfiguring the regular school-year sync. Custom data also allows you to consistently update summer school data while "freezing" previous school-year data in Clever via the sync pause .

 If you plan to add new summer school users and sections to Clever and share them with learning applications after your sync is paused you’ll need to use custom data. Please note, you will only need to upload custom students, teachers, and staff if the users are not already synced to Clever. 

 

 Please note: If you pause your sync for specific app(s) , custom data will not be shared with any apps you pause the sync for. 

 

 
 How can I upload custom data? 

 The easiest way to upload custom data is usually by manually uploading custom data in the Clever Dashboard. 

 Alternatively, all schools can upload data via your SFTP client using the directions below. If your primary sync type is not an SFTP sync type , contact Clever support to get access to your own Clever SFTP Directory before proceeding. 

 • Log in to your SFTP client.
• Enter your Clever SFTP credentials. If you don’t have them handy, you can access them in Clever under Data sources > SIS sync > Settings .
• Upload your custom data. If you’re new to adding custom data, reference the following Help Center articles: 
 • Custom Data: Sections 
• Custom Data: Teachers 
• Custom Data: Staff 
• Custom Data: Students 
 

 
 Before getting started, there are a few important points to keep in mind: 

 • Schools cannot be added via custom data. Please ensure all necessary school sites are syncing from your SIS before any sync pauses. 
• You can bulk upload custom teachers, staff, students, sections, and enrollments via the Clever Dashboard or SFTP. 
• Custom students cannot be added to existing non-custom sections. 
• Co-teachers can be added manually to custom sections. 
• You can bulk upload extension fields . Adding extension fields may help with setting up sharing rules. 
 
 Learn more about how to upload custom data here . 

 The final step of the process is Stage 4: Final details

title: Summer School: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Guiding questions for summer school setup 3. Steps for success 

 

 Overview

 We're here to help ensure that your schools have the best summer with Clever yet. Each of our summer school Help Center articles listed in the steps for success below has guiding questions and resources for you to review to ensure that your rosters and applications are ready for summer school. 

 

 For more robust support, including activities, email templates for teachers, and more, check out Prepping for Summer School in Clever Academy! 

 

 This guide is designed to be useful for all schools regardless of their summer school plans.

 

 
 Guiding questions for summer school setup

 The first step to prepare for summer school is to ask three key questions:

 • Which applications will you be using over the summer?
• Will you need to add new students, teachers, staff, or sections to your Clever sync?
• How does my district generate summer school rosters?
 
 

 
 Steps for success

 Summer school is unique because most districts need to have two sets of rostering data:

 • Rostering data from the previous school year (Spring, second semester, Q4, etc.) is typically paused over the summer to preserve access to learning applications and resources for students, teachers, and staff not enrolled in summer school.
• Rostering data for summer school. To maintain both rosters in Clever, we recommend that districts pause their regular Clever sync and supplement their summer school rosters using custom data.
 
 

 To set up your Clever account for summer, you'll need to review:

 • Step 1: Application requirements 
• Step 2: Sync settings 
• Step 3: Creating data 
• Step 4: Final details 
 
 All steps must be completed in order to ensure student, teacher, and staff access during the summer.

 To get started configuring your Clever account for summer school, please move on to Step 1: Application requirements . 

 

 
 Related articles: 

 • Summer School: Application requirements 
• Summer School: Sync settings 
• Summer School: Creating data 
• Summer School: Final details

title: How do I contact Clever Support?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Hours of operation 3. Holidays 4. How to contact support 5. Ensure you can receive emails from Clever 

 Overview

 At Clever Support, our goal is to provide the information you need as quickly as possible. Our Help Center provides 24/7 access to our extensive knowledge base and learning courses to support you. If you're unable to find the solution to your question or issue, you can click Contact Support to submit a request to our support team. When filling out the support request form, please provide as much detail as possible, including example users, records, error messages, steps to reproduce the problem, etc. This will ensure we route your request to the correct team and specialist to meet your needs.

 

 Hours of Operation

 Monday - Friday, 7am - 8pm ET If you do not receive a response from our team within 1 business day, please check your spam and junk folders. Additionally, ensure your email provider has enabled access for both @ clever.com and @ salesforce.com .

 As Clever team members, we value the opportunity to come together during special events and important meetings. This may mean our response times are a bit slower or that we’re unavailable by phone for a short period. If you need help, reach out at support.clever.com — we’ll be glad to assist you via email. Need quick help? Clever’s Virtual Assistant is available 24/7 with product tips and guidance 

 

 
 Holidays 

 

 
 Holiday

 
 Date

 
 Day of Week

 
 Winter Holidays December 24, 2026 - January 1, 2027 Thursday- Following Wednesday 
 Martin Luther King Jr. Day January 19, 2026 Monday 
 President's Day February 16, 2026 Monday 
 Memorial Day May 25, 2026 Monday 
 Juneteenth June 19, 2026 Friday 
 Independence Day July 4, 2026 Friday 
 Labor Day September 7, 2026 Monday 
 Thanksgiving Day November 26, 2026 Thursday 
 Day after Thanksgiving November 27, 2026 Friday 
 Winter Holidays December 24, 2025 - January 1, 2026 Wednesday - Following Friday 
 
 

 

 
 

 How to contact support 

 

 IMPORTANT Please do not share sensitive data with Clever Support, such as Personally Identifiable Information (PII), including: • Students' names • Any credentials (such as usernames and passwords) • API Tokens for applications When you need to specify a user running into trouble, please instead share their Clever ID, or, if that's unavailable, their Student Information System ID. When in doubt, please contact us first and clarify if it's safe to share data before sending anything.

 Clever Support is most efficient at quickly solving issues submitted via the Support button, which is available at the bottom right of the Clever Help Center. 

 

 ⚠️ If you do not see the button for support, please contact your district IT department. They will need to allow network access to IP addresses associated with Zoom Contact Center . For additional network questions, please contact ipaddresses @ clever.com 

 

 Select your Clever user type. Once selected, select a common question or enter your own in the chat. To get the best results, please try to provide a short description of your question or problem. This helps us deliver the most accurate answer possible. For example, instead of writing " Login", a better entry would be " Students using Google can't log in to Clever" or " I am a teacher that can not access Newsela". 

 

 

 

 Once your question is submitted, you will be presented with directions or knowledge related to the question. Use the scrollbar to the right to scroll through the articles provided. To view an article, select Read more 

 When prompted, if the provided information was helpful, select Yes If the provided information did not answer your question, select No 

 

 Our virtual assistant will attempt twice to assist with your problem or question. If it's unable to help, select how you would like to contact support to submit a request with our support team. Clever Support is open Monday - Friday, 7am - 8pm ET. 

 

 
 Ensure you can receive emails from Clever 

 In order to ensure you can properly receive mail sent from Clever's help desk platform (Salesforce), please check with your IT department to ensure that: 

 • Antivirus system is not blocking Salesforce emails (including how your rules and filters are set up)
• SPF, DKIM, and DMARC are properly configured
• Firewall is not blocking IPs
• Mail is not arriving in the SPAM folder
• Issues with TLS - notes Salesforce currently only supports TLS 1.2 for email encryption
• TLS Mail Delivery - Supported TLS Cipher suites 
 
 Additional information such as IP Addresses and Domains to Allow can be found in this article .

title: For App Partners: Inviting & connecting districts to your app
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Inviting districts • Inviting districts that are already using Clever • Inviting districts that are not on Clever 3. Viewing pending invites 4. Setting up a District Outreach Contact email address 5. Filtering for district statuses on the District Overview page 

 

 Overview

 This article briefly overviews how to invite districts to connect with your application via Clever and how to follow up on these requests. For instructions on how to approving inbound requests from districts, please see our article For App Partners: Manage inbound district requests . 

 

 

 Inviting districts

 You can invite districts individually, or if you want to send bulk invitations (to 50 or more districts), please contact your Application Success Manager. 

 Inviting districts that are already using Clever:

 1. Type in the name of the district that you want to search for. 

 2. Click the district's name or Invite to Connect . 

 

 3. You can see whether the district is already utilizing Clever, their sync status, and how well their data matches up against your application's schema. Learn more about data compatibility here . You can also Invite a district to connect with your app.

 Please note: I nvitations to connect through Clever are intended for districts that have already subscribed to your software services. 

 

 

 

 4. Once you click the Invite , fill out your message and the contact information. You can use the default message or edit it. You can also add a primary contact at the district or school. You can include multiple contacts through the Additional Contact Emails field. 

 

 Please note: The default app launch date is set to 30 days from district acceptance. District Administrators will still be able to edit their sharing rules and modify data before the date, but teachers and students won't see your application until the launch date has passed. You can also set a custom launch date if you wish!

 5. If the district is already on Clever you'll see an option to add a sharing request, requesting specific data to be shared.

 6. Once you send the invite, the email will automatically copy you, the district, and a member of the Clever team.

 7. Repeat! 

 Inviting districts that are not on Clever:

 1. Type in the name of the district you would like to search for. If they are not on Clever, it will be tagged as "Off Clever" when you search for it: 

 

 2. From there, you will need to fill out the following information for this district and specify a curriculum contact that will be notified: 

 

 3. The contact that you specify in this form will receive an email inviting them to connect their district through Clever so that they can connect with your application. It will look something like this: 

 

 4. From this email, the district or school can create a Clever account for themselves. If they create an account, a member of our District Onboarding team will reach out to help them get set up. 

 
 Viewing pending invites

 To view and manage your existing invitations to districts, head to District Overview > Pending Invites . From there you can view and filter results based on the status of the invitation. Invitation statuses are either "pending", "approved", or "rejected". You can even send a one-time email reminder to districts who haven't yet accepted your invite! 

 
 Setting up a District Outreach Contact email address

 You can also set a District Outreach Contact email when initiating the following communications with a district:

 • app invite
• app un-invite
• app invite reminder
• data warning
• district request approval
• district request rejection
• district disconnects
• sync requests
• app-side safety thresholds
 
 This will allow you to send these communications with the name and email of your choice from any user without creating and maintaining a shared email account.

 This contact will also surface to Clever Administrators in their Dashboard if they utilize your application, providing them with a quick reference for who to contact should they need help with the application. When adding the contact, you can check a box to prevent the Outreach Contact from showing to Clever Admins. Application team members with edit permissions can set a District Outreach Contact by:

 • ​​​Navigating to your Clever App Dashboard >Settings > General tab > Profile Settings > District Outreach Contact . 
 
 

 

 • Add the name and email address of your District Outreach Contact. An example would be Onboarding Team and onboarding@app.com 
 
 

 

 • Once added, when inviting districts to connect, you'll see a dropdown where you can set a Sender contact.
