This will be the contact from whom emails to districts, such as app invites or sync requests, will come from. There are two options: the app-wide District Outreach Contact or you as the current user.
 
 

 

 If no outreach contact is set, then you won’t see the dropdown, and you, as the current user, will default as the sender.

 
 Filtering for district statuses on the District Overview page

 You can use the filters on your District Overview page to find which districts have connected with your application, the status of their setup process, their launch date, and if they’ve shared data.

 Here’s a list of filters that can be used:

 • District name
• District status
 • Any
• Setting Up
• Launched 
• Licenses Exceeded
• Licenses Expired
• Setup Wizard Complete
 

• Launch date
 • Any
• Next Week
• Overdue
 

• Shared schools
 • Any
• No Data Shared
• Data Shared
 

• Starred
 • Any
• Starred
 

 
 

 Related articles: 

 • For App Partners: Manage inbound district requests

title: For App Partners: Using the Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. How your product appears in the Clever Library ​ 2. How to customize your listing in the Clever Library ​ 3. How to evaluate the performance of your listing 

 

 Overview

 If you support Sign Up with Clever , you can showcase your product in the Clever Library, a destination for teachers to discover new resources to use in their classroom. 

 How your product appears in the Clever Library

 Teachers looking for products in the Clever Library can search for resources by name or browse by subject and grade range. When browsing resources, teachers are shown your product’s icon, name, and a short tagline. 

 Please note: The Clever Admin for each school organization can turn off the Clever Library and block individual resources that they don’t want teachers to access. Please see this article for more information on district and school controls over Library applications. 

 When they click through to your listing, teachers are additionally shown: 

 • A 50-100 word description of your product 
• The subject, platforms, and grade levels supported by your product 
• Up to 5 screenshots and accompanying descriptions showing key features of your product 
• The number of teachers using your product in their school, and the number of schools using your product in their district 
• Whether your product is a signatory of the Student Data Privacy Pledge or has a review from CommonSenseMedia 
 
 In your Clever Dashboard, you can see the current icon, description, and other information that populates your listing in the Clever Library. 

 How to customize your listing in the Clever Library

 Clever currently reviews all updates to the Clever Library - if you would like to change any information about your listing, please reach out to your Application Partnership Manager or Clever Support with the changes you would like to make. 

 How to evaluate the performance of your listing

 In your Clever Dashboard, you can see the number of visitors to your listing in the Clever Library over the last 30 days, as well as the total signups. If you would like more detailed reports on the activity and demographics of teachers visiting your listing, contact discovery@clever.com .

title: For App Partners: The School Tech Lead user role in Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is a School Tech Lead? 3. School Tech Lead tools and permissions 4. How School Tech Leads interact with your apps 

 

 Overview

 This article will give our application partners an overview of the School Tech Lead role in Clever and how School Tech Leads can interact with applications. 

 
 What is a School Tech Lead?

 A School Tech Lead is a staff user role in Clever with additional permissions. District Admins can assign certain users the School Tech Lead role, which provides them with a set of specific permissions in Clever for their associated school(s). 

 For instructions on how a district admin can assign a staff member to the School Tech Lead Role, please see Clever Roles: School Tech Lead 

 These permissions are designed to empower this user in their role as an edtech coach, instructional technology specialist, digital learning specialist, etc. Users with School Tech Lead permissions are often the individuals at a school who evaluate, roll out, support, and troubleshoot technology. 

 School Tech Leads play an important role in helping teachers and students access your application through Clever. Continue reading to learn more about their permissions and how you can collaborate with them to drive engagement for your application. 

 
 School Tech Lead tools and permissions

 In the past, School Tech Leads only had limited admin permissions . In July 2020, Clever launched a new and improved user experience to empower School Tech Leads. They have the following tools in Clever: 

 Help districts roll out edtech 

 • Personalizing school portals 
• Creating custom sections 
• Monitoring edtech usage 
 
 Help train teachers and students 

 • Become a certified Clever Trainer 
• Access all new Clever Academy training for School Tech Leads 
 
 Quickly resolve issues with new troubleshooting tools 

 • Temporarily access a teacher’s portal 
• Troubleshoot logins to quickly diagnose the root cause of access issues 
 
 Evaluate Clever+ products in the Clever App Store 

 • Find add-on Clever+ products, such as Clever IDM, in the Clever App Store 
 
 
 How School Tech Leads interact with your apps

 School Tech Leads (STLs) play an important role in helping teachers and students access your application through Clever. There are three ways a School Tech Lead might interact with your application: 

 • SSO into your application: If you have shared data with the staff user role for School Tech Leads. 
• Viewing your application in the school’s portal: When customizing the school’s portal, an STL can view any applications the district has shared with that school.

title: For App Partners: District Data Compatibility
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I check a district's data compatibility? 3. How do I set up required and optional fields? 

 Overview 

 Data compatibility allows your application's implementation team to gather insight on a district's data coverage before sending them an application invite. This feature allows you to quickly check which of your application's required fields are currently syncing from the district's student Information system (SIS) to Clever so that your team can ensure a smooth implementation process with less time spent troubleshooting missing data. Please note that, in most cases, additional fields can be added to the SIS sync to Clever. Please reach out to Clever Support for additional information.

 

 
 

 How do I check a district's data compatibility? 

 1. Search for the district at the top search bar and select Invite to connect . 

 

 2. Scroll down to the Data Compatibility section of the district overview page to see which required fields are missing.

 • Please Note: These statistics are based on the district's last successful student information sync (SIS) sync to Clever.
 
 

 3. To drill into specific data collections, click Details for more information including the coverage of specific fields. Selecting the Download CSV option will provide a spreadsheet with all of the district's field coverage statistics 

 

 Details Page: 

 

 
 

 How do I set up required and optional fields? 

 Start with your developer application! By setting and testing changes on your developer application, you can avoid any disruptions for your end-users.

 To begin, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Settings > Application Settings > Data Access tab. Listed here are separate modules for each data type that provide the option to edit both required and optional fields for sensitive and non-sensitive data types ( More information here ).

 Data Access page with highlights around Edit Required and Edit Optional buttons" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl0000003gHF&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EM1P0000036Tlq">

title: For App Partners: Browsing district data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Searching the district's shared data 3. Using the Data Browser to view data shared by a district 4. Differences between v2 & v3 API 5. Downloading data shared by a district 

 Overview

 The Data Browser is a great tool for application partners to view district data. This article will go over how to use the Data Browser and its' features, including viewing data using Clever’s v2 API or v3 API. 

 Please note: This feature is only available for applications with a Secure Sync integration. 

 
 Searching District Data

 From the district's profile in your Clever dashboard, you can use the search bar at the top of the page to find any record that the district is currently sharing with your application: 

 

 
 Using the Data Browser

 Another way to view the district's data in Clever is by clicking the Data Browser in the left-hand sidebar. Here, you can browse the district's data by record type, and use the Add Filter button to narrow down your results. You can also click on each record to view a full list of the fields that the district is sharing for the record. 

 
 Differences between v2 & v3 API

 Clever now offers access to the v3 API as well as v2. These are some updates to note:

 • Version Switcher 
 • The Version Switcher allows apps to toggle between the v2 and v3 Clever APIs, thus better understanding how Clever's API works, which can benefit app developers.
• For applications that have access to the v3 API, they’ll see a label on the top right indicating what version they're on and that they can switch to the other version. 
• Clicking on this button will open a new tab with the different version of the data browser and preserve all other states, such as any current collection being viewed as well as any filters currently applied.
 

• Data updates 
 • While the v3 API contains similar information to v2, the data browser will now display additional record fields when clicking any record in any collection. When viewing v3 data for a user type, the ID field refers to the v3 user ID which is used for identifying users with multiple roles. Any similarly named or ambiguous fields related to v2 data are prefixed with legacy_, such as legacy_id on a teacher record. 
• In the Detail view, you'll see both the ID field for a specific teacher, representing their user Clever ID, their v2 Clever ID, and their sis_id. 
 

 
 If you want to know more about the differences between v2 and v3, please take a look at our API documentation .

 
 Downloading data shared by a district

 From the Data Browser, you can also download the data the district has shared with your application. This feature can come in handy if your team would like to manipulate the district's data, or perform a manual matching of existing accounts for your application. 

 To use this feature, navigate to the record type you'd like to download information for (e.g. schools, students, teachers, sections, etc.), and click the ' Download ' button: 

 

 The export will include all data that the district has shared for the record type you've selected and is also based on API type. For example, if downloading a list of student records in the v3 data browser, they will have both user and legacy IDs, whereas downloading from v2 will only contain relevant v2 API data. Any filters applied to view data in the Data Browser tab will not be reflected in the final data export.

 After clicking the Download button, a warning will appear to notify you that the CSV file being exported may contain PII. Please keep in mind that files containing sensitive student information must be treated carefully.

title: For App Partners: Setting up global recommended sharing for districts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting globally recommended sharing rules for all districts 

 

 Overview

 Globally recommended sharing settings allow you to communicate sharing best practices to districts who are connecting with your app for the first time. Note: You can also recommend sharing settings for specific districts .

 

 
 Setting globally recommended sharing rules for all districts

 To configure globally recommended sharing rules, navigate to Settings > District Setup in the dashboard and select Edit. T his will open a setup guide that will walk you through the following steps: 

 

 1. Choose sharing type 

 You can recommend that districts:

 • Share all records in the district
• Share all records in a specific school 
• Share custom records 
 
 

 

 2. Set custom rules (optional) 

 If you select 'Share custom records' you will be able to configure custom sharing rules for districts. To find more information about the different types of sharing permissions, please see this article . 

 

 3. Preview and save 

 Preview and save the sharing settings you configured in the earlier steps of the guide. 

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • 
 For App Partners: Recommending sharing for specific districts

title: For App Partners: District setup recommendations
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Globally Recommended Sharing 3. Custom Setup wizard 4. Data quality settings 5. Enable safety thresholds 6. Request App Sync Notifications for your account 7. Populate a District Outreach Contact 8. Request API V3 Migration CSVs 

 Overview

 Application partners can provide recommendations for districts that are adding your application(s) to Clever for the first time. To do this, please navigate to your Clever Portal > Settings > District Setup page to configure the requirements and recommended settings for connected districts. The available options include:

 • Globally Recommended Sharing
• Custom Setup wizard
• Enable safety thresholds
• Request API V3 Migration CSVs
 
 
 Globally Recommended Sharing 

 Configure your application's recommended sharing settings to include best practices for sharing district data with your application. For detailed information about sharing permissions, please visit Sharing Permissions: Overview .

 
 Custom Setup wizard

 The custom setup wizard allows you to create suggested steps to help guide districts that recently launched your application in Clever. This is the perfect place to detail recommended setup instructions and settings for district Clever Admins. Examples include:

 • Recommendations for how to share data with your application. Click here for more.
• Instructions on how to resolve data quality errors. Click here for more.
• Additional recommended setup steps. 
 
 

 This feature can also be used to link to external setup instructions located on your district's website, manage district expectations for finalizing their connection with your application, and more! Once your customized setup is configured, it will display to districts on the last step of the setup wizard within their Clever Dashboard. 

 
 Data quality settings

 Data validation is key to ensuring that districts resolve data issues before their official launch date for your application! These settings should accurately reflect your application's data quality requirements. For more information, please visit our guide to verifying district data quality . 

 
 Enable safety thresholds

 Safety thresholds ensure that significant data changes made by your districts in their student information systems (SIS) are placed on an automatic hold before syncing to your application, giving you time to review the changes before they process in your application. For more, please visit our guide to safety thresholds .

 

 
 Request App Sync Notifications for your account

 For the most accurate information and data regarding a district’s last sync, please contact our support team to enable App Sync Notifications. Use our API to confirm the last successful SIS upload from a school organization to:

 • 
 Minimize your nightly syncs to only the school organizations that have had data changes or

• 
 Proactively sync data changes throughout the day to prevent support tickets and minimize friction for school organizations.

 
 Learn more here in our dev docs .

 
 Populate a District Outreach Contact

 If you haven’t already, provide a district outreach contact on your Settings > General page to increase efficiency and minimize customer frustration. This contact will surface to Clever Administrators in their Dashboard if they utilize your application, providing them with a quick reference for who to contact should they need help with your application.

 Clever will also use this contact for school communications, such as app invites, sync requests, etc.

 If you’d only like the contact to appear only on direct communications, and not in the Clever Dashboard for Clever Admins, you can check the box for Don’t show District Outreach Contact as a support option for districts .

 

 
 Request API V3 Migration CSVs

 This tool maps legacy role-based Clever IDs to the new API v3.0 user IDs. A zip file with one CSV for every connected district will be available to download for one week after its creation. In the rare case that a district mapping cannot be completed, Clever will send an error file detailing the issues to you.

 Clever Tip: Please make sure that your application's data quality warnings and errors are current by navigating to the 'Warnings' tab of any district in your Clever Dashboard. Contact your Application Success Manager or Clever Support if updates are needed.

title: For App Partners: Recommending sharing for specific districts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to set recommended sharing for a district 

 Overview

 Smarter data sharing allows you to recommend sharing settings to districts that are connected with your app. You can send updated recommendations even after districts have enabled data sharing with you. If you do not set specific recommended sharing rules for the specific district, the district will receive your global sharing recommendation .

 When you send a sharing recommendation to a district, they can preview the sharing settings and choose to accept or decline the sharing update. If a district accepts your recommendation, the recommended sharing settings will replace any current sharing rules that the district has configured for your application. After accepting, the district can still update its sharing settings at any time.

 This article covers how to send sharing recommendations to a district. To view the district experience, see our district admin guide for sharing recommendations .

 

 How to set recommended sharing for a district

 To request a sharing update from a district, go to the district’s profile page in your dashboard and use the ‘Request sharing’ button in the upper right. Alternatively, you can navigate to the 'Sharing' tab from the district profile and select ‘Edit’. 

 

 This will open a setup guide that will walk you through the following steps to recommend sharing rules for a district: 

 1. Choosing a sharing type: 

 • You can recommend that districts: 
• Share all records in the district 
• Share all records in specific schools 
• Share custom records 
• You can also
• Edit an existing recommendation if you’ve already sent one to the district
• Import the district’s current sharing setting and recommend changes 
 

 

 2. Setting customized recommendations 

 • If you choose to recommend sharing all records in the district, you’ll skip this step. 
• If you select ‘Share all records in specific schools’, you can select which schools should be shared. 
 
 
 • If you select ‘Share custom records’, you can configure custom sharing rules for the district. For an in-depth guide to configuring sharing rules, see this guide . 
 

 3. Preview and save 

 • On this step, preview the sharing settings you configured in the earlier steps of the guide. 
• Add a message for the district that will appear in their dashboard when they review your recommended sharing settings. 
 

 Once you save your sharing recommendation, the district will receive a notification via email as well as within the Clever Dashboard. To view the district experience, you can reference this district-facing article .

title: For App Partners: Sending custom sharing recommendations to districts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is a sharing recommendation? 3. How to recommend custom record sharing 4. Constructing custom record recommendations 5. Tips and best practices 6. Editing or deleting a sharing recommendation 

 

 Overview

 By configuring custom sharing recommendations for districts, applications can suggest, as closely as possible, the correct users and rosters you need to set up your district customers. 

 When choosing to “share custom records”, once the recommendations have been accepted by the district, they will only share the rostering information that meets the specific criteria you've set. Please make sure all the data you need is included in the recommendation! 

 The data shared with the application via sharing rules updates based on the data synced to Clever by the district. 

 What is a sharing recommendation?

 A sharing recommendation is a request for specific data that you can send to a district to ensure only the data you want to receive is being shared. A district administrator can review the recommendation and immediately accept (or decline) the sharing update. 

 For example, a sharing recommendation might include:

 • All the math classes in a particular school 
• The math classes for grades 3-6 in the district, except for in two schools 
• One specific teacher’s 11th-grade homeroom class 
 
 Think of a custom recommendation as a sentence:

 • "Share all sections where <a field in Clever> <is/is not/contains/does not contain> <value>” 
 
 How to recommend custom record sharing

 To request a sharing update for a district, go to the district’s profile page in your dashboard and use the ‘Request sharing’ button in the upper right. Alternatively, you can navigate to the Sharing tab from the district profile and select ‘Edit’. 

 

 Once you select 'Edit' to recommend sharing for a specific district, select 'Share custom records' to configure specific data sharing rules. 

 

 For other sharing types, see our guide to sending sharing recommendations for specific districts . 

 

 Constructing custom record recommendations

 Our interface allows you to create recommendations with the same structure as outlined in the ' What is a sharing recommendation? ' section above (e.g. share all sections where <a field in Clever> <is/is not/contains> <value>). 

 • There are a number of 'Fields' that applications can request to either include or exclude data with the sharing recommendation. Fields include teachers, sections, students, and school admins (staff) 
• We recommend adding non-school constraints first as the larger the district, the longer the school list can be! 
• The modifiers available are: 
 • Is 
• Is not 
• Is one of 
• Is not one of 
• Contains text 
• Does not contain text 
 

 
 

 Tips and best practices

 • You can include and/or exclude subsets of data. Data can be excluded by selecting the 'is not one of' modifier. 
• For values, you can either type in any value you’d like, or search to find and click on the value you want. Some values will not be available in a dropdown as the district has not authorized an application to see them. 
 • Warning: if you are typing an option in a text field, you must click 'Add: [value]’ or else the value will not be saved 
 

• If you’d like to filter down a rule further, you can select the ‘+ADD' option to add new criteria. This will act as an 'and' statement, rather than an 'or' statement. 
 • Example Rule with Filters: Share all sections that are located at Pineapple Elementary School AND their section grade level is 1 AND the section name must contain the text 'homeroom'. 
 

• If you recommend sharing specific students, you will also have the option to select which of these students' subjects or courses you'd like to receive. You can even choose to share no sections at all! If sections are shared, you will also receive the sections' associated teacher(s). 
 
 • Once you have finished creating your first rule, you can click “Add Rule” to add more sharing recommendations. 
 • When you have multiple rules, the two rules don’t interact with each other. This means if you want to build off the existing rule, you'll want to add a filter to the rule itself, rather than create a new rule entirely. 
• If a rule is a sentence, having multiple rules is a paragraph: "Share all sections where grade is one of 6, 7, 8.” Also , "Match all staff where school is Pineapple Elementary.” 
 

• We highly recommend adding a message so that the district administrator can easily understand the request being made 
 
 Once you have finished creating the rules and associated filters, select ‘Next', add a message, and then 'Save' to send the recommendation to the district. You can only have one open sharing recommendation for a specific district at a time. 

 Please note: A district administrator must accept your sharing recommendation in order for their sharing to be updated. They may also choose to decline this recommendation. If a district has accepted a recommendation, they can still edit their sharing settings at any time. 

 

 Editing or deleting a sharing recommendation

 If you’d like to change or remove a sharing recommendation, you can do so by going to the district’s page and going to the Sharing tab. 

 If you have any questions about setting up your sharing recommendations or what you are requesting from connected districts, please reach out to Clever Support !

title: For App Partners: Update marketing collateral to showcase your product in Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How your app is showcased in Clever Library 3. Updating marketing collateral 

 Overview

 Your app's profile in Clever Library is how teachers discover and learn about what your product does. Your profile helps explain to teachers what you do and what makes you unique. You can update your app's profile under the marketing collateral tab in your dashboard.

 How your app is showcased in Clever Library

 When an educator searches for your application in Clever Library, they will be directed to your app's profile page. The following information appears on your app profile:

 • Basic information about your application (Name, tagline, description) 
• Details about your application (categories, grade levels, standards alignment, tags, device and platform compatibility, case studies and links to your website) 
• Images to show what your application looks like (app icon, banner, and screenshots) 
 
 The application profile will also include verified teacher reviews, which includes a 5-star rating scale for how engaging, educational and easy-to-use your application is, as well as comments. Learn more about teacher reviews . 

 To see what your profile page looks like to a user, visit the public-facing Clever Library and search for your application. 

 Updating your marketing collateral

 You can update what appears on your app's profile page by adding content under the marketing collateral tab in your Clever Dashboard. Every Clever Library application partner starts with a basic profile consisting of their application name and icon. Follow these instructions to update your profile with screenshots, description, and other information: 

 • Log in to Clever at https://apps.clever.com/ 
• Navigate to Library > Marketing Collateral in the left-hand navigation menu 
 
 There, you can add the following information: 

 Basic information 

 • App name: The name of your product 
• Tagline: A short description (60 characters max) 
• Long description: A full description of your applications capabilities and features (1000 characters max) 
 
 App details 

 • Subject and categories: Choose from a list of subject or categories your application should be listed under 
• Grade levels: Select all grade levels your product supports 
• Standards Alignment: Link to web pages that outline standards alignments for your application. 
• Tags: Add up to 10 tags to help your application better appear in search ( learn more about tags ) 
• Device and platform compatibility: Select which platforms and operating systems your product supports 
• Pricing: Your application’s pricing model (Freemium, Free Trial, and Free) 
• Research and case studies: Link to web pages that host research, case studies or relevant content. 
• Links to your application’s website, terms of service, and privacy policy 
 
 Artwork 

 • App icon: 2 00px wide by 200px high, PNG, no transparency 
• Feature banner: 400px wide by 240px high, PNG, no transparency 
• Screenshots: Include up to 5 screenshots, along with an associated text description to describe what the screenshot displays. 1200px wide by 800px high, PNG, no transparency. 
 
 For more detail on designing great artwork for your profile, download the artwork spec .

title: Troubleshooting: Enable "Access Portal as" for School Tech Leads
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Enabling "Access Portal as" for STLs 3. Using the "Access Portal as" feature 

 Please note: This tool needs to be enabled by a district admin from the Team > Role Settings page of the dashboard in order for the STLs at your district to start using this feature 

 Overview

 Clever makes it easy for School Tech Leads (STLs) to access the Clever Portal as an individual teacher and see exactly what they see. When troubleshooting, this can help STLs diagnose an issue, retrieve an error code or message, or gain visibility into what might be confusing. This is overall a great way to empower more folks in your district to support teachers with their technical requests. 

 This tool enables STLs to view a portal as a teacher in their scoped school. Unlike District Admins , STLs cannot view a student’s Clever Portal through this tool. 

 This tool needs to be enabled by a District Admin in order for the STLs at your district to start using this feature. 

 Enabling "Access Portal as" for STLs

 As a district admin, you have full control over whether your School Tech Leads (STLs) can use the "Access Portal as" feature for teachers at their associated school sites. This is a setting you can turn on/off by navigating to Team > Role Settings . This setting is turned off by default. 

 If you decide to turn it on, Clever will walk you through a few informational steps prior to switching the tool on for your STLs. 

 

 As a district admin, you'll have the option to send an email to your STLs about their new tool. We recommend communicating this update to them so they have the opportunity to take advantage of this tool while supporting teachers and students. 

 

 Using the "Access Portal as" feature

 After you’ve turned on this tool for the STLs in your district, they will start seeing the button to Access Portal as user on teacher profiles. 

 

 Upon clicking this option, STLs will first need to offer a reason for accessing the Clever Portal as this particular user. Next, the STL will need to confirm that they understand teacher profiles may contain sensitive user information. Only then can they proceed to Begin Portal Session. 

 

 

 Please note: As an additional transparency measure, the teacher whose portal is being accessed, will receive an automatic email from Clever with the message that the STL included

title: Clever and North Carolina third-party data integration
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. North Carolina DPA 3. Third-party collection worksheet 4. Vendor Readiness Assessment Report (VRAR) 5. Third-party assessment 6. Statewide Information Security Manual 7. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 In May 2023, the North Carolina Department of Public Instruction (NC DPI) released new data security standards for any system that receives student information from a statewide system. Since Clever connects to the North Carolina student information system (SIS), we understand that these requirements apply to Clever. This article will provide districts with information and materials about Clever’s compliance with these new requirements. The requirements go into effect January 1, 2024 (originally August 1, 2023), and must be met before any new service agreement or renewal can be signed. Clever’s Secure Sync service does not operate on a renewal schedule, however, we still intend to meet the requirements on or before August 1, 2023, to give school districts assurance of compliance and non-interruption of service. The North Carolina requirements are:

 • Signed Data Confidentiality and Security Agreement for Online Service Providers and Public School Units (Student Data)
• Completed Third-Party Collection Worksheet
• Completed Vendor Readiness Assessment Report (VRAR) self-assessment 
• Third-Party Assessment Report
• Compliance with North Carolina’s Statewide Information Security Manual 
 
 
 North Carolina DPA

 NC DPI has determined that all vendors who connect with North Carolina’s statewide SIS need to sign the Data Confidentiality and Security Agreement for Online Service Providers and Public School Units (Student Data) (“DCSA”) in whole with no modifications. Please follow this DocuSign link to access a pre-signed version of the document. Using the link, you may countersign and download your own copy of the DCSA. If you have trouble with DocuSign, please contact legal@clever.com to find a different process.

 
 Third-party collection worksheet

 Please follow this link to access a completed Third Party Collection Worksheet using the NC DPI’s required template. You can fill out the top portion with your own school district’s information and keep a copy for your records.

 
 Vendor Readiness Assessment Report (VRAR)

 Please follow this link to access Clever’s signed and completed VRAR self-assessment using the NC DPI’s required template.

 
 Third-party assessment report

 The NC DPI requires a third-party conducted security assessment report, such as the Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) authorization, SOC (Security Operations Center) 2 Type 2 audit, ISO 27001 certification, or HITRUST certification, dated within the last 12 months.

 Clever has obtained and intends to maintain a SOC 2 Type 2 Security report on an annual basis. The SOC 2 organization requires that an NDA be entered into before sharing the report with existing or potential customers. If you are a school district that is connected to or connecting to Clever, please click this link to sign an NDA if you would like to review a copy of our report.

 
 Statewide Information Security Manual

 The NC DPI requires alignment with the Statewide Information Security Manual. While Clever complies with a majority of the elements, there are some gaps that we expect to close during the next year during our SOC 2 Type 2 assessment process. In the meantime, if you would like to review Clever’s most recent penetration testing and/or vulnerability scans, please sign an NDA using this DocuSign link and we will provide a copy to the email address of the person signing the document. If you have trouble with DocuSign, please contact legal@clever.com to find a different process.

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Where can I find out more information about the North Carolina requirements? 

 • The North Carolina DPI has created a website to provide schools and vendors information about the new requirements and updates.
 
 What if I have more questions about Clever’s compliance with the North Carolina requirements? 

 • Please contact legal@clever.com if you have any additional questions.
 
 

 Related Resources: 

 • For Families: Clever security policies for student data 
• To learn more about data privacy, you can read Clever's full Privacy Policy 
• To learn more about our security practices, you can read our comprehensive security program

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Passwords - Custom Configurations
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Instructions 3. Available Fields 4. Examples 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 When setting up Clever Passwords as an Identity Provider, you may want a combination of the fields you're syncing to Clever as the username/password format, rather than using a single field. You can create a custom format to combine fields directly in the Clever Passwords configuration modal on the SSO Settings page.

 Please note: This article is for the Clever passwords (classic) interface . If you are using the updated interface please see this article for custom formula guidance. Not sure which interface you're using? Check the screenshots below to determine which matches your experience: Clever Passwords (classic): Clever Passwords (updated): 

 

 
 Instructions

 • Set up Clever Passwords. For more information see Single Sign-On (SSO): Clever Passwords - Log in with Clever .
• Enter any available code from below or select a configuration from the dropdown for username and/or password.
• Choose reset all usernames and passwords now for any user types you've modified, and save.
• Test a login!
 • Look up the login credentials for a user
• Navigate to your district's portal URL 
• Enter the login credentials for the user to confirm the login works.
 

 
 

 
 Available Fields

 • {{.SISID}}
 • The unique ID number shared with Clever from your SIS. Seen as "Student ID", "Staff ID" or "Teacher ID" in Clever.
 

• {{.StaffID}}
 • The unique ID number shared with Clever from your SIS or SFTP upload. Seen as "Staff ID" in Clever.
 

• {{.StudentNumber}}
 • If shared, seen as "Student Number" in Clever.
 

• {{.Name.First}}
• {{.Name.Last}}
• {{.Name.Middle}}
• {{.TeacherNumber}}
 • If shared with Clever, seen as "Teacher Number" in Clever. 
 

• {{formatDate.Dob}}
 • If shared with Clever, the user's date of birth.
• Uses M/D/YYYY format.
 

• {{.Email}}
 • If shared with Clever, the user's email address.
 

• {{.Credentials.DistrictUsername}}
 • If shared with Clever, seen as "District Username" in Clever.
 

• {{.Credentials.DistrictPassword}}
 • If shared with Clever, seen as "District Password" in Clever.
 

• Individual words or email domains
 • For instance, if you want username to include @ cleverusd.k12.edu , you'd add that to the end of any other formulas. You might also add a period/dot in between two formulas (like if you want to add a period between the first and last name formulas for username). 
 

 
 
 Examples

 Username "John.Doe" would be formatted as: {{.Name.First}}.{{.Name.Last}} Username " John.Doe@cleverusd.k12.edu " would be formatted as: {{.Name.First}}.{{.Name.Last}}@ cleverusd.k12.edu 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I concatenate partial fields? (i.e. first two letters of last name) 

 • Currently, formulas can't be concatenated.
 
 Are usernames and passwords case-sensitive? 

 • Yes, all usernames and passwords are case-sensitive!
 
 I used {{formatDate.Dob}}, what format is the DOB in? 

 • This field uses the M/D/YYYY format. For example, January 1st, 2001 would be 1/1/2001 and December 1st, 2001 would be 12/1/2001.

title: Deploy Edtech Analytics for Google Chrome using Microsoft Intune
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Step 1: Silently install the Clever Plus Chrome Extension 3. Step 2: Add verification domain to the Authentication allowlist 4. Step 3: Configure the Extension with your District ID Analytics Token 5. Step 4: Verify 

 

 Overview

 This article will go over the steps on how to deploy Edtech Analytics for Google Chrome using Microsoft Intune. 

 
 Step 1: Silently install the Clever Plus Chrome Extension

 • Sign in to Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) .
• Select Devices in the left-hand navigation pane.
• From the Devices | Overview right-hand pane, select Configuration Profiles .
 
 

 

 • On the top command bar, select Create profile .
• From the drop-down list: 
 • Below Platform, select Windows 10 and later .
• Below Profile Type, select Templates .
• Below Template name, select Administrative Templates and then click the Create button.
 

 
 

 

 • On the Basics tab, enter a descriptive Name, such as [Google Chrome] Clever Plus Extension Install Policy . Select Next . 
• On the Configuration settings tab, search and select Configure the list of force-installed apps.. under Path \Google\Google Chrome\Extensions 
 
 

 • The settings will be shown on the right pane. 
 • Set it to Enabled 
• For the Extension/App IDs use dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa; https://crx.clever.com/updates then click OK and Next to progress.
 

• Set Scope tags if applicable, click Next .
• Set Group Assignments & Exclusions with Filters as applicable, click Next. 
• Review the profile then click Create. 
 
 

 
 Step 2: Add verification domain to the Authentication allowlist

 This next section is very similar to the previous - a new Configuration Profile is needed.

 • Sign in to Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) .
• Select Devices in the left-hand navigation pane.
• From the Devices | Overview right-hand pane, select Configuration Profiles .
• On the top command bar, select Create profile .
• In the drop-down list:
 • below Platform, select Windows 10 and later .
• below Profile Type, select Templates .
• below Template name, select Administrative Templates and then click the Create button.
 

• On the Basics tab, enter a descriptive Name, such as [Google Chrome] Authentication service allowlist for EdtechAnalytics . Select Next .
• On the Configuration settings tab, search and select Authentication server allowlist under Path \Google\Google Chrome\HTTP authentication 
• The settings will be shown on the right pane. 
 • Set it to Enabled 
• For the servers use autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com , login.microsoftonline.com , analytics-domainverify.clever.com then click OK and Next to progress.
 

 
 

 

 
 Step 3: Configure the Extension with your District ID Analytics Token

 The following steps set the AnalyticsToken for the extension, which enables usage data collection.

 • Sign in to Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) .
• Select Devices in the left-hand navigation pane.
• From Devices | Overview , select Scripts and remediations, then the Platform Scripts tab. 
• On the top command bar, select Add , and Windows 10 and Later 
• On the Basics tab, enter a descriptive Name, such as Set_Clever_Chrome_Token . Select Next .
 • Create a powershell script with the .ps1 extension (e.g. Set_Clever_Chrome_Token.ps1), save it with the following command replacing YOURANALYTICSTOKEN then upload to Script location New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\ -Name Google New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google -Name Chrome New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome -Name 3rdparty New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\3rdparty -Name extensions New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\3rdparty\extensions -Name dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\3rdparty\extensions\dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa -Name policy New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\3rdparty\extensions\dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa\policy -Name AnalyticsToken -PropertyType String -Value YOURANALYTICSTOKEN 
• Toggle each of these options to No 
 • Run this script using the logged on credentials No 
• Enforce script signature check No 
• Run script in 64 bit PowerShell Host No 
 

 

 
 

 • Set Group Assignments & Exclusions with Filters as applicable, click Next .
• Review the profile then click Add .
 
 

 
 Step 4: Verify

 To verify the install worked, log in on a targeted device and confirm the following:

 • In Chrome, navigate to chrome://extensions and look for Clever Plus . Click into it to see details.
 
 

 • Check Registry Editor on a target Endpoint, navigate to HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\3rdparty\extensions\dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa\policy\AnalyticsToken, and check that the Analytics Token string value is set accurately.
 
 

 • In Chrome, navigate to chrome://policy and ensure the AnalyticsToken and other policies are appropriately set.
 
 

 
 

 Related articles: 

 • Edtech Analytics - Overview and FAQs

title: Deploy Edtech Analytics for Edge using Microsoft Intune
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Step 1: Silently install the Clever Plus Edge Extension 3. Step 2: Add verification domain to the Authentication allowlist 4. Step 3: Configure the Extension with your District ID Analytics Token 5. Step 4: Verify 

 

 Overview

 This article will go over the steps on how to deploy Edtech Analytics for Edge using Microsoft Intune. 

 
 Step 1: Silently install the Clever Plus Edge Extension

 • Sign in to Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) .
• Select Devices in the left-hand navigation pane.
• From the Devices | Overview right-hand pane, select Configuration Profiles .
 
 

 

 • On the top command bar, select Create profile .
• From the drop-down list: 
 • Below Platform, select Windows 10 and later .
• Below Profile Type, select Templates .
• Below Template name, select Administrative Templates and then click the Create button.
 

 
 

 

 • On the Basics tab, enter a descriptive Name, such as Microsoft Edge Clever Plus Policy . Select Next . 
 
 

 

 • 
 On the Configuration settings tab, search and select Control which extensions are installed silently under Path\ Microsoft Edge\Extensions 

 
 

 • The settings will be shown on the right pane. 
 • Set it to Enabled 
• For the Extension/App IDs use dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa; https://crx.clever.com/updates then click OK and Next to progress
 

 
 

 • Set Scope tags if applicable, click Next .
• Set Group Assignments & Exclusions with Filters as applicable, click Next .
• Review the profile then click Create. 
 
 

 
 Step 2: Add verification domain to the Authentication allowlist

 This next section is very similar to the previous - a new Configuration Profile is needed.

 • Sign in to Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) .
• Select Devices in the left-hand navigation pane.
• From the Devices | Overview right-hand pane, select Configuration Profiles .
• On the top command bar, select Create profile .
• In the drop-down list:
 • below Platform, select Windows 10 and later .
• below Profile Type, select Templates .
• below Template name, select Administrative Templates and then click the Create button.
 

• On the Basics tab, enter a descriptive Name, such as Allowlist of servers Edge can delegate to . Select Next .
• On the Configuration settings tab, search and select Specifies a list of servers that Microsoft Edge.. under Path \ Microsoft Edge\HTTP authentication 
• The settings will be shown on the right pane. 
 • Set it to Enabled 
• For the servers use autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com , login.microsoftonline.com , analytics-domainverify.clever.com then click OK and Next to progress.
 

 
 

 

 
 Step 3: Configure the Extension with your District ID Analytics Token

 The following steps set the AnalyticsToken for the extension, which determines which sessions should be tracked and enables usage data collection. The AnalyticsToken for your district or school's Clever account is the District ID found on your Clever Dashboard > Data browser .

 

 To add your AnalyticsToken:

 • Sign in to Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) .
• Select Devices in the left-hand navigation pane.
• From Devices | Overview , select Scripts and Remediations, then the Platform Scripts Tab. 
• On the top command bar, select Add , and Windows 10 and Later 
• On the Basics tab, enter a descriptive Name, such as Set_Clever_Edge_Token . Select Next .
 • Create a powershell script with the .ps1 extension (e.g. Set_Clever_Edge_Token.ps1), save it with the following command replacing YOURANALYTICSTOKEN, then upload to Script location 
 New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge -Name 3rdparty 

 New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\3rdparty -Name extensions 

 New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\3rdparty\extensions\ -Name dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa 

 New-Item -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\3rdparty\extensions\dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa -Name policy 

 New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\3rdparty\extensions\dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa\policy -Name AnalyticsToken -PropertyType String -Value YOURANALYTICSTOKEN 

 
• Toggle each of these options to No 
 

 
 
 • 
 • Run this script using the logged on credentials No 
• Enforce script signature check No 
• Run script in 64 bit PowerShell Host No 
 

 

 

 • Set Group Assignments & Exclusions with Filters as applicable, click Next .
• Review the profile then click Add .
 
 

 
 Step 4: Verify

 To verify the install worked, log in on a targeted device and confirm the following:

 • ​In Edge, navigate to edge://extensions and look for Clever Plus . Click into it to see details.
 
 

 • Check Registry Editor on a target Endpoint, navigate to HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge\3rdparty\extensions\dikiaagfielfbnbbopidjjagldjopbpa\policy, and check that the Analytics Token string value is set accurately.
 
 

 

 
 

 Related articles: 

 • Edtech Analytics - Overview and FAQs

title: For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Google Workspace SAML Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Troubleshooting 4. Using Classroom MFA Google Workspace SAML for Chromebook Logins 

 Please note: The Google Workspace app requires an MFA subscription. If you're interested in adding MFA to your Clever account, you can learn more here or get started directly at your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Classroom MFA page. 

 

 Overview

 This article is a step-by-step walkthrough for setting up Google SAML with Clever's Classroom MFA. Before reading this setup guide, please make sure your district is set up with Classroom MFA and Google! If your district has not purchased Classroom MFA, you can get started in your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Classroom MFA page , or if you are new to Clever, get started by going to this link: https://www.clever.com/district-signup . For more information on setting up Classroom MFA, please see this article . Classroom MFA protects the Clever Portal, Google Workspace, and Google Chromebooks. With the classroom-friendly MFA methods and the ability to require re-authentication for login attempts outside your designated geographical location or network, you can rest assured that potential bad actors will hit your MFA wall and get turned away.

 

 
 Setup

 • Search for the Google Workspace app and click Request Application . You can set up user sharing now or do so later.
 
 

 • If you’re getting a 404 error, copy and paste directly into the address bar: https://schools.clever.com/applications/add/saml-google-workspace 
• If you already have this app installed and you don’t see the Request application button, go to step 2 below.
 
 

 • Share the Google Workspace app with any Clever users who need access to Google through Clever but don’t use Badges to access a Chromebook. Typically, this includes older students, teachers, and staff users in Clever. Your students who use Badges to access Chromebooks should not be shared with this app. Students who use Badges to access Chromebooks will use the Badge configuration to access their Google accounts through Clever. Learn more here . Please note: Sharing the Google Workspace app with the same users in the Google OUs you will set up in step 9 below is required for those users to successfully access Google from Clever. 

 
• On the Google Workspace app page, click Settings . Scroll down to find the SAML Details section.
 
 

 

 • Go to your organization’s Google Admin SSO page by navigating to Admin console > Menu > Security > Authentication > SSO with third party IdP . Click ADD SAML PROFILE :
 
 

 • 
 Add Clever as an Identity Provider to your new Google SAML Profile by providing SAML information from the Clever dashboard Google Workspace page (left) to the Google Admin New SAML SSO Profile (right):

 
 a. Identity Provider Entity ID > IDP entity ID b. Copy Identity Provider ACS URL , add “ ?specify_auth=sis ” to the end > Sign-in page URL c. Download Verification Certificate > Upload Certificate d. Make sure to add an SSO Profile name e. Click Save 

 

 • Your new Google SSO Profile should now show your own SAML information. 
• In the Clever dashboard, on the Google Workspace page, click the Edit button under the SAML Details section, and provide SAML information from the Google Admin New SAML SSO Profile (right) to the Clever dashboard Google Workspace page (left):
 
 a. Entity ID > Entity ID b. ACS URL > Assertion Consumer Service URL 

 

 

 • If needed, you can change the attribute mappings for any user type necessary. This is usually not required. Click Confirm to finish setup.
 
 

 

 • From your new Google SSO Profile (screen in Step 5 above), click Back to return to your Google Admin SSO page . Next, assign your new SSO Profile to an OU by scrolling down to the Manage SSO profile assignments section. You'll find one of two views:
 
 a. If you’ve never set up an SSO assignment, click on GET STARTED : b. If you already have SSO assignments set up, click on Manage :

 

 

 • Select both the intended device OUs and user OUs for the devices and users you want to target in the left sidebar. Whatever OU you have successfully selected will be shown at the top of the page. Please remember that the users in this OU must also be shared with the Google Workspace application ( step 2 above ) to successfully access Google from Clever.
 
 IMPORTANT Google does not allow Superadmins to SSO via another IdP, so if you apply the Clever SSO profile you just made to a Superadmin at your district, it will not work. 

 

 • Next, click Another SSO profile, and in the dropdown, select the new SSO Profile you created in the above steps.
 
 

 • Make sure to Have Google prompt for their username … selected. Finally, click OVERRIDE to finish setup. That’s it, you’re all set!
 
 

 Please note: Once you have successfully set up the above steps, the user will need to log in with Clever Password to access their Google Workspace. 

 

 

 You can optionally turn on a setting in your SSO profile so that when a user enters their Google username on the Google sign-in page, they’re automatically redirected to Clever with their username already filled in.

 
 • If your Clever Passwords username is set to “email,” we recommend enabling this setting.
• If your Clever Passwords username is set to anything else, you should not enable this setting in the Google Admin Console. For example: If your Clever Passwords username is "email username" (i.e. username before "@" in domain) then this would autofill the wrong username)
 
 

 You can enable this setting by navigating to your SSO Profile > update Autofill email settings under SP details 

 Clever supports the Send email address in the URL as the login_hint parameter option

 

 

 

 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Users are looping between Google sign in and Clever without gaining access 

 • This can sometimes be caused by the individual user setting in the Google Admin Console to force a password reset on the next login. If your users are experiencing this, ensure the the Google Admin Console: Directory > Users > [Individual User] > Security > Require password change is set to “off.” Also be sure that your Google account creation process is not automatically setting this setting to “on” by default. Learn more here .
 
 Google Super Admins can’t use Clever SSO to access Google 

 • This is a Google limitation. Google does not allow SSO access for Super Admin users. Learn more here .
 
 
 Using Classroom MFA Google Workspace SAML for Chromebook logins

 To configure Chromebook logins with Clever credentials and Classroom MFA, you'll need to enable:

 • 
 Clever to access the device camera on the login screen

• 
 Re-prompt for a Badge after the device is reopened after being closed

• 
 Sign the user into Clever automatically when they sign in to the device

 
 • 
 Navigate to Devices > Chrome > Settings > Users & browsers and scroll down to the Security section

• 
 Set Lock Screen settings to: "Do not allow locking screen" 

• 
 Scroll a little bit until you reach the Single sign-on and SAML single sign-on login frequency settings in the Security section.

 • 
 Set Single sign-on to "Enable SAML-based single sign on for Chrome devices". 

• 
 Set SAML single sign-on login frequency to an appropriate duration. We recommend "Every 2 weeks" for the best experience. 

 

• 
 Scroll to the Power and Shutdown section. Set the following Idle settings

 
 • 
 Set Action on lid close to Logout 

• 
 Set Lock screen on sleep to Don't lock screen 

• 
 Set AC idle action to Logout 

• 
 Set Battery idle action to Logout 

 
 

 • 
 Next, scroll down to the Startup section further down on the same page

 • 
 Change the Homepage configuration to Homepage is always the URL set below . Set your Homepage URL to your Clever Portal URL. You can find your Clever Portal URL here . 

 

• 
 Click the Device settings tab at the top of the settings panel and scroll down to Sign-in settings 

• 
 Set Guest Mod

title: Clever + Raptor Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding staff 3. Sharing considerations 4. Contacts 5. Users at multiple school sites 6. Co-teachers 7. Duplicate sections 

 

 Overview

 Founded in 2002, Raptor has partnered with over 52,000 schools globally, including over 5,000 K-12 U.S. school districts, to provide integrated visitor, volunteer, emergency management, safeguarding, and early intervention software and services that cover the full spectrum of school and student safety. By adding Raptor Technologies to your district’s Clever account, you're securely and automatically sending your most up-to-date roster data from your SIS to Raptor. In this article, we’ll go over some best practices to consider when connecting with Raptor through Clever. Since each district’s sync setup is different, please reach out to Clever Support with any questions or concerns. 

 
 Adding staff

 For Raptor Technologies and in general, you will want to have all of your staff in Clever, including non-teaching and non-admin staff. Please see Clever Roles: Staff - How to add to Clever for instructions. We typically recommend the “uploading staff via CSV” method, but please reach out to Clever Support if this does not work for your district’s setup. 

 
 Sharing considerations

 For Raptor Technologies, we recommend sharing all district data with the app. Please see Sharing Permissions: Overview and the Share by District option. One common concern is that districts want to make sure their staff members, especially non-teaching and non-admin staff, don't have access to some/all of the district’s other applications. You can avoid oversharing staff with your other applications by setting your other apps to Share by Rules and making sure only to include the appropriate staff users.

 You'll also want to utilize Share by Rules if you need to share users with multiple roles in Clever (i.e., users with both a teacher and a staff account). At this time, Raptor does not support users with dual roles. Raptor will create duplicate profiles if multiple account types sync to the application. To prevent this, you should determine which account type should be shared with the application and set your sharing rules so that only that account is shared. If there are staff members you only want to share with Raptor, and none of your other applications, we recommend giving them a title such as “Raptor Only” when creating your Staff.csv file. This way, they will be easy to exclude from your other apps’ sharing rules. 

 
 Contacts

 Contacts are important to include in your sync with Raptor, since parents/guardians will need to be notified if there is an emergency. To make sure your student contacts are syncing from your SIS to Clever, please visit your Data browser and check the Contacts tab. Contacts are automatically shared with Raptor. You can make sure they are shared by visiting Raptor’s Settings page within your Clever Dashboard. If you're concerned about oversharing contacts with your other applications, you can go into the settings for your other apps and make sure that contacts are not shared if not appropriate. See For District Admins: Apps - Field Access for how to do this. If you’re unsure about this, please reach out to Clever Support for assistance. 

 
 Users at multiple school sites

 Raptor prefers that all users are associated with one school site only. If possible, you can edit your Staff.csv file to ensure staff are each associated with only one school site.

 If needed, Raptor can support users at multiple schools, with the caveat that staff assigned to multiple buildings will most likely impact the reporting of emergency accountability at schools that they are not at during the time of the emergency. For example, if a staff person is at School B today but School A has an emergency, they will be seen as "missing" during School A’s emergency because they are physically at School B. The same would apply to students assigned to multiple sites. 

 
 Co-teachers

 Raptor doesn't currently support syncing co-teachers/secondary teachers from Clever. The primary teacher will sync to Raptor for each section. 

 
 Duplicate sections

 Raptor doesn't sync duplicate sections. For any two sections that have the same teacher and the same section name, the following is true:

 • If the section name and the student list are identical on both rosters, then all shared students on both rosters do not sync.
• If the section name is the same but the student list is different on the two sections, Raptor will combine them into one larger section.
 
 To avoid this, we recommend making sure that teachers have unique section names for each of their sections. It is not an issue if two different teachers have sections with the same names since Raptor will recognize they're different sections belonging to different teachers.

title: For App Partners: Resetting app dashboard passwords
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Password strength 3. Reset password 4. Update password 5. Frequently asked questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever app team members can easily reset their passwords in case of a lost or forgotten one. This article details the process for resetting an app team member's password. This only applies to app partners who log in with an email address and password. App partners who log in with Sign in with Google or App Dashboard SSO should reset their IDP passwords in their IDP. 

 
 Password strength

 Clever requires a certain level of complexity to ensure a strong password and also does privacy-preserving checks. During account creation, password reset, and login, Clever will determine if a password is unsafe if it's been made public in an unrelated data breach. If an app team member’s password is determined unsafe, we require the user to select a different password. For recommendations on selecting strong passwords, see the FAQ. 

 
 Reset password

 To reset your app team member password for Clever,

 • Visit apps.clever.com and click Forgot your password?
• Enter the email address associated with your admin account, click the Captcha, and click Reset 
• An email will be sent with a link to reset your password. If you don't receive the email, please check your spam filter and make sure no-reply@clever.com is added as a contact to your email account.
 
 Quick link: https://apps.clever.com/reset?captcha=true 

 
 Update password

 If you'd like to update your Clever password, click on your name in the top-right of the app dashboard and click Profile Settings. Enter your existing password and new password and then click Update to update your password. 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 When attempting to log in to Clever, why do I see a note about my password being part of a public data breach? 

 • Clever determines that a password is unsafe if it's been made public in an unrelated data breach. We perform privacy-preserving checks for passwords which have been leaked online. If an app team member password matches any of these, we require the user to reset their password. 
 
 Are there recommendations for choosing a secure password? 

 • Use a password management solution like 1Password to use strong/unique passwords for all logins.
• Combine three or four random words. For example: “student-task-fish-fly”. These combinations are usually easy to remember and very secure. 
 
 

 Related articles: 

 • For App Partners: Securing users 
• For App Partners: How do I enable two-factor authentication? 
• For App Partners: App Dashboard SSO

title: For Clever Admins: SSO access to the Clever Dashboard
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Configuration 3. Logging into the admin Clever Dashboard and Portal 4. SSO and Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 In addition to logging in using your email address and password at schools.clever.com , admin users can also log into the district dashboard and admin Portal using their identity provider (IdP) via single sign-on (SSO). SSO logins use the “Log in with” button from your Clever Portal URL. This article applies to users with the following roles: Clever Admins, District Curriculum Lead, District Help Desk. 

 
 Configuration

 Requirements for admin SSO access:

 • You must have an external IdP, such as Google or Entra ID, configured for SSO into Clever on the Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings tab. You can learn more about configuring SSO here .
 
 

 

 • The email address associated with your admin account as seen on the Profile or Admin Team pages must match the email address in your IdP. See details for your IdP below.
 • Admins must update their own email addresses from the Profile page . You can learn more about how to update your profile settings here .
 

• Your username or login claims rule in your IdP must be your email address.
 
 Google Google will always use an email address as the username. If you have added Google as a login method and your admin user email address is a Google email address, you are configured for Clever Dashboard SSO. Entra ID A claims rule for clever.district_admin.email or clever.any.email should be added in Entra ID as outlined here. The claims rule should indicate which field in Active Directory contains the same email address that matches the email address on the Clever Admin's Profile in Clever. Active Directory A claims rule for clever.district_admin.email should be added in Active Directory as outlined here. The claims rule should indicate which field in Active Directory contains the same email address that matches the email address on the Clever Admin's Profile in Clever. Canvas By default, Canvas will try to match on email. Ensure that your admins have an email address in Canvas that matches the email address on the Clever Admin's Profile in Clever to enable SSO access with Canvas. If these default matches do not work for your district, please let us know in your support request. 

 
 Logging into the admin Clever Dashboard and Portal

 To log in as an admin user using SSO, you will need to navigate to your district's Portal URL. This is the URL that your students, teachers, and staff use to log in, and can be found on your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page. When logging in directly from your Portal URL, you will first access your admin Clever Portal. To navigate to the Clever Dashboard, click Dashboard at the top of your Portal. 

 
 SSO and Multi-factor authentication (MFA)

 By default, admin users still need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA) to access the Clever Portal when using SSO (learn more about MFA for admin users here ). If your district admin team already uses MFA when accessing your identity provider (IdP) and you would like to by-pass admin MFA when accessing Clever, contact our support team and be prepared to provide details about your admin team and IdP MFA. Learn more about managing your Admin Team here . Disabling password login for your admin team As an additional layer of security, Clever can disable password login for your admin team. This means that email address and password logins will no longer work at schools.clever.com and your team must use SSO through your district Portal URL. If you would like to disable password logins for your district, contact our support team. 

 Please note: The Clever Account Owner can always use an email address with a password login and MFA to access the Clever Dashboard. This is to provide a backup in case your IdP configurations change and other admins lose access. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can admins use Clever Passwords to log in to the Clever Dashboard? 

 • No. SSO admin login through the Portal URL must use an external IdP.
 
 Sometimes I log in directly to the Clever Dashboard when using SSO. Why does that happen? 

 • If you start at schools.clever.com and click School Login to navigate to your district Portal URL, you will log into the Clever Dashboard directly instead of the admin Portal.
 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Setting up multi-factor authentication (MFA) for Clever Admin 
• Managing your District Clever Admin Team 
• Single Sign-On (SSO): Setup & Configuration

title: For App Partners: What is a sync conversion?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is a sync or SIS ID conversion? 3. What is the Clever ID vs the SIS ID? 4. How Clever preserves user accounts 5. Application requirements 6. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 This article is to provide our application partners with information on the sync or SIS ID conversion process for districts and schools.

 
 What is a sync or SIS ID conversion?

 A sync conversion is when schools change their way of syncing their data to Clever. This means they have either:

 • Changed their sync type (e.g. going from a manual SFTP sync to a Clever-managed auto sync)
• Changed their Student Information System (SIS) (e.g. going from Infinite Campus to PowerSchool)
 
 An SIS ID conversion is when schools keep the same syncing system but need to change their users’ SIS IDs. Both of these require Clever go through the conversion process to preserve the Clever IDs for users. 

 
 What is the Clever ID vs the SIS ID?

 The primary identifiers for records in Clever are known as SIS IDs. The following fields are SIS IDs: 

 Data type Column Header/Field Name Notes 
 School School_id Typically the school local id. Used in all other files. Corresponds to ’sis id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Student Student_id Unique and constant id for student, may be student number, state id, or other unique identifier, to be used in enrollments.csv upload. Corresponds to ’sis id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Teacher Teacher_id Unique and constant id for teacher, may be teacher number, state id, or other unique identifier, to be used in sections.csv upload. Corresponds to ’sis id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district. 
 Sections Section_id Unique and constant id for section, to be used in enrollments.csv upload. Corresponds to ’sis id’ field in Clever. Must be unique across the district 
 Staff Staff_id An identifier provided by the district when syncing or creating this staff member. Must be unique across the district. 
 Contacts Contact_sis_id The unique identifier for this contact, if one exists 
 
 Clever ID refers to the globally unique identifier that's assigned to a record in Clever. This ID is based on the SIS ID. If the SIS ID changes, the Clever ID will change. If schools change their SIS IDs, this causes the users' accounts to be deleted and will generate new accounts. This can cause the loss of historic data in connected applications as most apps use the Clever ID as the identifier to create accounts.

 

 
 How Clever preserves the user accounts

 When schools reach out about starting either conversion process, we enable protection settings on their account to ensure teachers and students are minimally affected by the changes in data. During this time, all syncing of data between the district and Clever will be paused, however, users will still be able to access Clever and their applications. We can then work with the district to make sure that their data is error-free and use our matchmaker tool to ensure that the new sis_ids are associated with the old sis_ids for each user, thus preserving the Clever IDs for each user. This process can take up to 3 weeks during peak times for various reasons such as troubleshooting sync errors, the need to notify applications for any reason, cleaning up data, etc. The schools’ role in the conversion is to supply accurate, error-free data. Schools should not reuse SIS IDs. If schools do re-use SIS IDs or have duplicate accounts for users, this can add to the sync conversion delay. 

 
 Application requirements

 The best practice for our application partners is to use the Clever ID for account creation. However, if you’re unable to match the Clever ID for account creation (you use another field), we need to know what your requirements are when our districts go through a sync or SIS ID conversion.

 • If you're not using the Clever ID for account creation, what fields are you using to create these accounts? 
• Do you require a crosswalk file (a file that matches up the old data to the updated)? 
• Is there any additional support or information you need during the conversion process? 
 
 Please reach out to Clever Support if you need any of this additional support. If you don’t require a crosswalk file or additional support, there is no need to reach out. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 What fields are required by Clever? 

 • For school data:
 • School_id 
• School_number 
• School_name 
 

• For student data:
 • School_id 
• Student_id 
• First_name 
• Last_name 
 

• For teacher data
 • School_id 
• Teacher_id 
• First_name 
• Last_name 
 

• For section data
 • School_id 
• Section_id 
• Teacher_id (Unique identifier of primary teacher for section, must match a Teacher id found in teachers.csv)
 

• For enrollments data
 • School_id (School id must match a School id provided in schools.csv upload)
• Section_id (Section id must match a Section id provided in sections.csv upload)
• Student_id (Student id must match a Student id provided in students.csv upload)
 

• Contact data is not required but if the district decides to sync this data, we require the following:
 • Contact_relationship (Contact’s relationship to student - e.g. ”Father”, ”Aunt” or ”Neighbor”)
• Contact_type (Type of contact - e.g. ”family”, ”emergency” or ”guardian”. If providing student contacts, then Contact type is required)
• Contact_name 
 

• Staff data is not required but if the district decides to sync this data, we require the following: • Staff_id 
• Staff_email 
• First_name 
• Last_name 
 

 
 If your application requires certain fields that you are not seeing as synced, please reach out to the district to make sure they are syncing that field to Clever.

 Who handles the sync or SIS ID conversion? 

 • Clever completes the conversion process while working with the schools 
 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to Clever Support !

title: Secure Sync: Sync or SIS ID conversions
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What is a sync or SIS ID Conversion and why is this important? 3. Sync Conversion requirements + pre-work 4. The Sync or SIS ID Conversion process 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 This article will explain a Sync or SIS (S tudent Information System) ID conversion is , why it matters, and how your school or district can prepare. 

 Please note: Sync or SIS ID conversions can take 2-3 weeks to complete. 

 

 
 What is a Sync or SIS ID conversion and why is it important?

 You need a Sync Conversion when you change how data syncs to Clever. This includes switching SIS providers or changing sync types, such as moving from a manual SFTP sync to a Clever-managed Auto Sync.

 You need a SIS ID Conversion when you change school_ids, student_ids, teacher_ids, or staff_ids in Clever.

 These conversions help preserve Clever records and historical data in connected applications.

 Most transitions require both a sync conversion and a SIS ID conversion. If your SIS provider and sync type stay the same, you’ll only need a SIS ID conversion. The Clever team will help you determine the best approach. 

 
 Sync Conversion requirements + pre-work

 Before starting a sync or SIS ID conversion, make sure the following requirements are met:

 • 
 Your SIS includes all users who still need access to Clever.

• 
 Your SIS includes rosters for the current calendar year. If rosters aren’t final for a new school year, you can still start as long as at least one active section or enrollment exists.

 
 

 Clever also recommends completing this pre-work to ensure a smooth process:

 • Identify how you will sync data to Clever: Clever provides districts with multiple methods to sync their data. If your district is transitioning to one of the SISs listed here , you may be able to use Auto Sync to send data to Clever. If your SIS isn't listed, you'll use an SFTP sync. Learn more about syncing data via SFTP here . 
• Confirm the primary identifiers for records: Clever recommends keeping student_id and teacher_id the same when transitioning SISs to avoid duplicate or lost records. If IDs must change, Clever can match records to preserve historical data in Clever and connected applications.
• Clear duplicate teacher or student accounts. Duplicates often occur when users have separate SIS records for multiple schools. These duplicates can cause login issues, rostering problems, or data loss. Consolidate records in your SIS before starting the conversion whenever possible.
• Confirm that SIS IDs are not being reused. Clever treats SIS IDs as unique identifiers. Reusing an ID within 300 days of its last sync can cause data issues both within Clever and connected applications. Confirm with your SIS admin that IDs won’t be recycled. If your SIS reuses IDs automatically, assign unique identifiers that haven’t synced with Clever in the past 300 days.
• Populate data in your new SIS. Your new SIS must contain active data before the conversion. Plan to have data ready 3–4 weeks before you want it live in Clever.
• Proactively start notifying SSO, Rostering apps: App coordination often takes the most time. Work with Clever Support to identify impacted SSO and rostering apps. Notify those vendors early so they can prepare for the data change. We also recommend pausing the sync with these specific applications to prevent data changes during the sync conversion process. See our article For Clever Admins: Pause your sync for a specific app for more information on how to pause the sync with an application. 
 
 

 
 The Sync or SIS ID conversion process
