 • Reach out to Clever : Reach out to Clever Support before syncing new or updated data. A specialist will guide you through the process and help protect your data.
• Configure your Auto Sync or SFTP files: You need to set up your new sync connection before migration begins. If you've chosen an Auto Sync, work with our support team or your SIS provider as appropriate to set up the data sync. If you'll be syncing via SFTP, use the directions found here. Please allow up to one additional week to troubleshoot getting your sync or SFTP files successfully configured.
• Migrate data: Once your new data is ready, notify Clever Support. Clever will enable protection settings to ensure your teachers, students, and staff are minimally affected by the changes in data by preventing SIS > Clever syncing during the migration. Existing users will still access Clever and apps, but new users won’t gain access until the conversion finishes. N o new data will sync until the conversion process is complete. 
• Confirm data changes, sync with apps: The final step of the process is to approve the data changes. After migration, Clever will place your sync on hold so you can review changes. No new or custom data will sync during this time. Approve or discard changes from your Sync page as appropriate. You can find more details on Holds here . Once data goes live, allow up to 48 hours for apps to reflect updates. App vendors control how and when they ingest data. If you have any questions about the effect of data changes in an application, please get in touch with the application's support team. 
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Is there an option if I need more guidance and would like additional services from Clever for my sync conversion? 

 • Yes! We offer Sync Conversion Consulting Services for school organizations that want extra guidance and hands-on support. Opting for our paid services can provide additional consultation and support to further streamline the process. If you're interested in exploring this option or have any questions, please let us know!
 
 Can this be done during a sync pause? 

 • Yes, and Clever recommends it. Since no new data or data changes process during a conversion, a planned pause works best.
 
 Why does the sync conversion take up to 2-3 weeks? 

 • Sync conversions involve complex data matching, which carries risk if done incorrectly. Larger or more complex datasets take longer. Many school organizations schedule conversions during winter or summer breaks.
 
 What are common issues that occur during the sync conversion process that I should be aware of? 

 • 
 Incorrect credentials for the new sync

• 
 Duplicate user accounts in the SIS. In Clever, users shouldn’t have multiple accounts if they are at more than one school; Clever associates a user with various schools via sections and enrollments. If a user is duplicated in your SIS to associate them with multiple schools, this can cause issues in the data-matching process in Clever. 

• 
 SIS ID reuse

• 
 Unexpected SIS ID changes 

 
 When should I reach out to start this process? 

 • Contact Clever at least 3 weeks before your desired completion date and only after your SIS data is fully populated. Our support team cannot schedule sync conversions in advance, so please be sure the data in your SIS is ready for upload when you contact our team. If you'd like to schedule a sync conversion in advance, please read about our sync conversion consulting services . 
 
 Can I only change the SIS ID of one user? 

 • Not at this time. Our internal tools only work for large numbers of SIS ID changes (e.g., 100 IDs or more). Anytime you execute record linkage between data sets, the integrity of the data is always at risk. If you need to change student IDs for individual students, the best course of action is to contact your connected applications and let them know so that they can attempt to match up the new student ID and Clever ID to the student's historical data within their application.
 
 What are some Clever-managed sync options districts can switch over to? 

 • This article details the types of Managed Auto Syncs that Clever offers.
 
 Who do I reach out to when doing a sync conversion? 

 • Contact our Support Team. 
 
 Are section IDs preserved during sync conversions? 

 • Our migrating tool is not configure

title: For App Partners: App Dashboard SSO
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Requirements 3. Configuration 4. Multiple Domains 5. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 App Dashboard SSO allows app partners to configure organization level logins using SAML from their existing Identity Provider (IdP). You’ll work with our Partner Engineering team to configure the settings on your IdP to allow for seamless logins using the Log in with SSO button found at apps.clever.com.

 

 
 Requirements

 Team Review 

 • Please review your current app team to ensure that you have accounts for the correct users. To view your team, navigate to the Team section of your Clever dashboard.
• It is required that the email addresses associated with user accounts in Clever match the email addresses associated with accounts in your IdP.
 
 Organization Review 

 • If your team administers multiple apps on Clever or your organization spans multiple email domains, you may need Multiple Domain support. To prepare for App Dashboard SSO across your organization, ensure that the correct users have user accounts created with all of the appropriate applications in Clever using credentials that align with the IdP you intend to use for App Dashboard SSO.
 
 

 
 Configuration

 To get started: If you would like to configure single sign-on for your organization, please contact our team at integrations@clever.com with the following information: Email Domain: 

 • This will be the @ of your email address. ex: At Clever, our email address has a “ clever.com ” domain.
 
 Encrypted Assertion?: 

 • Will the SAML assertions that their IdP sends to Clever be encrypted or unencrypted?
 
 metadata.xml Info: 

 • This can either be provided via an URL (recommended) or a file.
• Your metadata info must include the following information:
 • EntityDescriptor with an Entity ID
• KeyDescriptor tag that includes the X509Certificate
• SingleSignOnService tag with HTTP-Redirect Binding
 

 
 Configuration Steps to take In your IdP: 

 • Add https://clever.com/oauth/saml/metadata.xml as the EntityID
• Add https://clever.com/oauth/saml/assert as the Assertion Consumer Service URL (ACS URL)
• Add a SAML assertion attribute “clever.email” that is mapped to email addresses in your IdP. Please note that the email addresses in your IdP will need to match the email addresses in Clever.
 
 Our integrations team will contact you with next steps to complete the configuration. 

 
 Multiple Domains

 App Dashboard SSO can now support multiple email domains across the same organization. This will allow you to set up App Dashboard SSO across all of your applications. Reach out to integrations@clever.com to explore your options. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Which IdPs can we use as our SSO Provider for App Dashboard SSO? 

 • Any IdP that can set up a SAML connection will work. See your IdP documentation for IdP-specific instructions on configuring the SAML connection.
 
 Will Dashboard logins using email and password continue to work? 

 • Dashboard logins will continue to work through the configuration and transition period and when you are ready to fully transition to App Dashboard SSO, we will disable logins using email and password.
 
 We have a Third-party development team that needs access, but we don’t have them as part of our SSO org. How can they have access? 

 • We believe that our Multiple Domain solution will work for your situation. Reach out to integrations@clever.com to explore your options.
 
 We have team members from two different organizations, both of which have their own SSO solutions. What’s the best solution for us? 

 • We believe that our Multiple Domain solution will work for your situation. Reach out to integrations@clever.com to explore your options.
 
 We don’t have an IdP; what options are available for securing our user logins? 

 • In addition to App Dashboard SSO, Clever offers both Sign in with Google and two-factor authentication. See the following articles for additional information.
 • For App Partners: Securing Users 
• For App Partners: How do I enable two-factor authentication

title: For App Partners: Securing Users
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sign in with Google 3. App Dashboard SSO 4. Two Factor Authentication 5. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 Securing logins with SSO or two-factor authentication (2FA) is a simple, effective way to protect accounts and data. Many enterprise applications make secure logins standard - and now, so is Clever. Explore your options for becoming more secure below. 

 
 Sign in with Google

 Sign in with Google helps you to quickly and easily manage user authentication into the Clever dashboard. Users sign into the Google account associated with their Clever user email address and then simply click the Sign in with Google button found at apps.clever.com To support Sign in with Google, ensure that your Clever user account email addresses match your Google email addresses. You can review your team on the Team page of your Clever Dashboard. 

 
 App Dashboard SSO

 App Dashboard SSO allows app partners to configure organization level logins using SAML from their existing Identity Provider (IDP). You'll work with our Partner Engineering team to configure the settings on your IDP to allow for seamless logins using the Login in with SSO button found at apps.clever.com Learn more about configuration in the For App Partners: App Dashboard SSO article.

 
 Two Factor Authentication

 Two-factor authentication adds another level of security to your account. If you enable two-factor authentication, you'll be asked to enter a verification code in addition to your username and password every time you log in to Clever. This extra verification prevents unauthorized parties from accessing your account, even if your password has been compromised. Learn more about configuration in the For App Partners: How do I enable two-factor authentication? article. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Why is there a focus on improving account security now?

 • Cybercrime is on the rise, and school district technology is a popular target. We want to help increase the security of app partner accounts as soon as possible to ensure all data is protected. 
 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • For App Partners: How do I enable two-factor authentication? 
• For App Partners: App Dashboard SSO

title: Clever IDM: Prerequisites and Planning
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Set up your sync 3. How IDM decides whether to match an account or create a new one 4. Email Notifications 5. Planning steps 6. Additional tools 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM helps districts automate account provisioning and lifecycle management for Microsoft Active Directory services and Google Workspace. This affordable solution can create user accounts and organizational units based on data in Clever. However, before using this service, we recommend making sure your data is in tip-top shape. This article will guide you through a few things to check before starting your IDM journey and some tips to ensure you make the most of it after you complete the setup. As always, if you have any questions, feel free to open a new case in our Help Center . A note on verbiage - Clever IDM may be confusing if you haven't used an identity management system before. Here's some quick lingo: 

 • Destination - Google Workspace, ADFS, or Entra ID, where Clever IDM will create and manage your users. 
• Formula - the part of your Clever IDM configuration that determines how we create email addresses and Organization Units (OUs). This uses "templates", or combinations of data or predetermined values, to specify what Clever puts into your destinations. 
• OU (organizational unit) - the "folder" of your destinations, a group where users are placed when managed by Clever IDM.
 
 

 
 Set up your sync

 You'll want to ensure that your sync from your SIS to Clever is running before adding IDM to the mix. Everything else in Clever depends on Secure Sync to work! You can read about Secure Sync in this Help Center article: What is Secure Sync? A wide variety of sync types are available, each with its own set of quirks and advantages. Check out your specific sync type in the list below, and pay close attention to any field limitations or the timings of automated syncs. 

 • Which sync type should I choose? 
• SFTP 
• Aeries 
• PowerSchool 
• Infinite Campus Auto 
• Infinite Campus API 
• SchoolInsight 
• Skyward API 
• Blackbaud API Sync 
 
 For instance, if your sync type doesn't automatically include student emails, you should review our options for populating that field, such as field mapping. Clever Support is happy to assist you with these mappings. Note that not all sync types support mappings - you'll want to check out your respective sync type above for details.

 Additionally, if you plan to use Clever IDM to manage non-teaching staff in Clever, there are various options for syncing staff users, including CSV files, custom data, or an Active Directory sync. You'll want to ensure that you sync staff users to Clever before setting up Clever ID M . Learn more in our article about adding staff to Clever . 

 Note that Clever IDM can create emails using a template you've set in your Clever Dashboard even if the email field isn’t populated by data from your SIS. If you're syncing data to Clever’s email field, we'll use that instead of your template. In addition to the email address, you'll want to consider getting other data to Clever that you want to sync over including data like student lunch numbers, building IDs, or other information you may need to populate in your destination automatically.

 Additionally, if your sync only runs once daily, you may want to schedule your IDM sync to run after your SIS sync. Our teams can assist with configuring a time for your IDM sync.

 
 How IDM decides whether to match an account or create new one 

 

 IDM matches and creates users using the standard fields: the email from your Student Information System (SIS) for Google, and the district username field from your SIS for AD/Entra.

 • 
 If your SIS syncs the email you want into Clever, you don’t need to change anything. 

• 
 If it doesn’t, IDM will use the template you create in Step 4: Set login credentials to create the user. 

 
 Below is a breakdown of the most common paths that IDM may take depending on whether you’re syncing with Google or AD/Entra ID.

 

 IDM logic for Google 

 • 
 If Clever already has an email from SIS: 

 • 
 IDM looks for a Google account with that email. 

• 
 If it finds one, it links the account. 

• 
 If it doesn’t find one, IDM creates a new Google account with that email. 

 

• 
 If Clever does not have a SIS email: 

 • 
 IDM creates an email using the template you set in Step 4: Set login credentials . 

 

• 
 If the templated email already exists in Google: 

 • 
 If IDM manages that account, it will add a 1 to the username (or use your fallback option configured in Step 4). 

• 
 If IDM does not manage that account, it shows up as a Conflict with Existing User in the Issues report. You can manually match these accounts under the Tasks tab in IDM after IDM syncs. 

 

 
 

 IDM logic for AD or Entra ID 

 • 
 IDM matches accounts based on District Username . If you would prefer for IDM to match by email prefix, please let our team know so that we can update this on our end. 

• 
 If Clever already has a username or email from SIS: 

 • 
 IDM combines that username or email with the domain set in your IDM configuration. 

• 
 If this matches a UPN in AD/Entra, IDM links the account. 

• 
 If it doesn’t match, IDM creates a new account using that format. 

 

• 
 If Clever does not have a username/email in Clever and formula doesn't match, IDM creates using template configured in Step 3: Set login credentials 

 • 
 If the templated formula produces a value, IDM moves forward with creating the UPN with that value. If that UPN configuration is already assigned to another user, this will result in a conflict.

• 
 If the templated formula results in a blank value or there is a missing attribute (username or email in most cases), then IDM assigns '1' to the end of the username by default, or uses the custom fallback configured in Step 3: Set login credentials .

 
 

• 
 If the UPN already exists on another account: 

 • 
 If IDM manages that account, it will add a 1 to the username or use your fallback from Step 4. 

 

 
 If IDM does not manage that account, it shows as a Conflict with Existing User in the Issues report. You can manually match these in the Tasks tab.

 

 You can start setting up Clever IDM free from your Clever Dashboard . For detailed instructions on the setup process, use the following resources: 

 • Clever IDM: Setup for Google 
• Clever IDM: Setup for Active Directory 
• Clever IDM: Entra ID Setup 
• Clever Academy Course: Quick Start - Setting up Clever IDM 
 
 
 
 Email Notifications

 The Clever Admin Owner is sent weekly IDM Sync Report emails that contain key metrics for IDM syncs over the prior week. You can determine the Clever Admin Owner at your district by checking the Role column on the Team Page of your District Dashboard. You can request daily sync report emails by contacting Clever Support. If additional team members need access to these emailed reports, please have the Clever Admin Owner configure a forwarding filter in their email system.

 
 

 Planning steps

 After you've got your IDM sync up and running, plan out your long-term implementation. For example, if you intend to use Clever's Badges into Chromebooks solutions or Badges into Windows solutions, you'll want to ensure you've applied the necessary settings to the OUs or devices where they're required. If you'll be managing your OUs with Clever IDM, think about which groups of students will need access to those solutions, such as K-5. 

 • Single Sign-On (SSO): Chromebooks - Setup & Troubleshooting 
• Clever Badges: How do I enable Badges into Windows devices? 
 
 You'll also want to disable any other IDM or automated software or scripts that may be running - Clever IDM can't override or disable these other tools, and you may see unintended behavior with multiple automations running. Some may work well in tandem, but we'd recommend just using one at a time until you're sure they'll complement each

title: Managing your Clever administrative team
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What are Clever admin users? 3. Do you have the right Clever account owner? 4. Adding and removing users from your admin team 5. Admin users and rostering 6. Do users have the right role? 7. Removing outdated accounts 8. Frequently Asked Questions 

 Overview

 Keeping your admin team users in Clever up to date is an essential part of maintaining account security. There are numerous roles and custom roles that you can assign to your administrative team in Clever to minimize risk and ensure that team members have the minimum access required in Clever. This article will guide you through reviewing and cleaning up your team. To view your Clever team, navigate to the Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team .

 
 What are Clever admin users?

 Your Clever administrative team may include users with one of the following roles: Clever Admin (Owner), Clever Admin, District Curriculum Lead, District Help Desk, or a Custom Role that you've created in the Clever Dashboard. To learn more about the default admin team roles, see this article .

 While School Tech Leads (STLs) appear on your User management > Admin team page they are not admin users. School Tech Leads are staff users with additional permissions in Clever, allowing them to provide support and troubleshoot for your schools. If you need to delete or edit an STL in your account, please edit them via your staff data (custom data or staff.csv). Learn more about School Tech Leads here .

 
 Do you have the right Clever account owner?

 The Clever Admin Owner holds unique privileges and is designated as the primary point of contact for all Clever-related issues. See this article to learn more about Clever Admin Owners. The Clever Admin Owner account must be in the possession of the person at your schools who is empowered to make important decisions about your Clever account. If your account owner leaves your district or schools, you may experience delays when making some requests. To transfer the account owner, see the instructions here .

 
 Adding and removing users from your admin team

 Any users who are assigned the Clever Admin role are also able to add other Clever Admins to Clever by following these steps:

 • 
 Log in to the Clever Dashboard. 

• 
 Select User management > Admin team from the left navigation menu.

• 
 Select Actions in the top right of the page.

• 
 Select Add team member from the options available.

• 
 Add the new Clever Admin's information in the pop-up box and select their role. Remember to select the role that will provide the minimum permissions required in Clever for security.

• 
 Select Add. 

 
 Once a team member has been added to the account, they will receive an email with a link to create an account. If they do not receive this message, please have them check their spam folder for a message from no-reply@clever.com . 

 To remove an administrator from your team, click the trash can icon next to their name.

 If you are utilizing an SFTP or SIS-Managed SFTP sync you can also sync, remove, and update Clever Admins via CSV. Please see here for more information, including steps for removing admins synced via CSV . If you utilize a SIS-Managed sync and want to automate the syncing of your administrators.csv, you’ll need to work with your SIS directly to add this file to your exports.

 

 
 Admin users and rostering

 By default, Clever shares admin users with all rostering applications. Before making any changes to your team, consider whether you are using the default admin rostering in any of your connected applications. If you are, any users added to the team will be shared with these applications, and any users removed will lose access to these applications. If you’re considering removing team members, consider adding them as staff users to Clever and sharing them with the appropriate applications so they maintain application access if needed. Learn about Customizing District Admin field access here . Learn about Staff users here.

 
 Do users have the right role?

 When reviewing your team, you can edit which role your users have. See this article to determine the best default Clever role for a user, or this article to learn more about creating custom roles .

 You can review all roles assigned to a given user on the Admin team page .

 
 Removing outdated accounts

 To keep your Clever account secure, we recommend reviewing your Team page periodically to:

 • Delete any admin users who are no longer with your schools.
• Delete any admin users who have moved to a new position where they no longer need an admin role.
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 What should I do if another admin or I can’t remember our login information? 

 • If an admin can’t sign in, they can follow the steps below to recover their account:
 • On the admin login page, click Forgot your password? 
• On the Recover account page, enter the email address connected to the Clever account. If you’re unsure which email address was used, another admin on your team can check the Team page to find the correct one.
 

 
 Will deleted admins receive an email notifying them that their account has been deleted? 

 • No. However, you may choose to reach out to inform your team about updated accounts. 
 
 I am the Clever account owner and will be leaving my role soon. Do I need to transfer ownership before I leave? 

 • It is best practice for a seamless transition to transfer the Clever account ownership before you leave while you still have access to the Clever account as the account owner.
 
 How do I know which permissions I should assign a role? 

 • Clever recommends assigning the minimum required permissions to user for their role. There is no limit to the number of custom roles in Clever, so you can create as many unique roles as needed to ensure proper permissions are in place for each admin.
 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • Clever Roles: Overview of Roles & Permissions 
• For Clever Admin: Custom admin roles 
• Clever Roles: Clever Admin 
• Clever Roles: Staff - How to add to Clever

title: For Clever Admins: Course Data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How are courses created? 3. Viewing courses in the data browser 4. How is course data used? 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 This article will explain how you can import course data to Clever, how to view it, and how you can use it!

 
 How are courses created?

 Course data is synced to Clever as part of your standard sync, usually through sections data (sections.csv). Course data is not required by Clever, but it may be required by certain applications. The fields associated with courses are: 

 • Course_name
• Course_number
• Course_description
 
 Clever auto-generates a unique Clever ID for each course, based on the course_number and course_name fields. This value is distinct to the course and is different than a section Clever ID. The course Clever ID is used to identify unique courses within your data and is used for content mapping . Since we use the course_number and course_name fields to generate the course ID, any changes to the values in those fields will auto-generate a new course Clever ID. If a Clever ID for a course is regenerated, it will require updating the content mappings for any applications that use the content mapping feature. Unfortunately, Clever is unable to revert to any previous values of this number, since the IDs are auto-generated and old IDs are auto-deprecated when changed, as they behave just like a Clever ID.

 
 Viewing courses in the data browser

 To view the course data that has synced to Clever, you can go to the Data Browser and look at the Courses tab . The Courses tab includes the course number, the course name, the Clever ID of the course, a link to sections related to the course, and when that course was last modified. 

 
 How is course data used?

 Course data can be used to share with applications. When constructing sharing rules, you can Custom Share by Section when Sharing by Rules . You can use this sharing type to filter by section attributes such as subject, school, course, and term. For more information on this, please see Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules . This will allow you to share your data with an application by specific courses. As an example, you can see below there is a section sharing rule that has been set up to share sections with a specific course name. This will share sections that have a course name of Algebra II/Trigonometry. Course data can also be used for content mapping as some applications allow you to map resources to specific courses. For more information on content mapping, please click here: For Clever Admins: Content Mapping . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What if our Student Information System doesn’t sync course data?

 • If you have a Clever-managed auto sync, you can reach out to Clever support to see if your sync can be customized to include course data.
• If your sync type does not allow for the syncing of course data, we can discuss changing your account to a sync type that does allow for this data to be synced. 
 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • For District Admins: Sync Reports 
• Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules

title: For Clever Admins: Term Data
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How are terms created? 3. Viewing term data in the data browser 4. How is term data used? 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 This article will help to explain how you can import term data to Clever, how to view it, and how you can use it! 

 
 How are terms created?

 Term data is synced to Clever from your SIS as part of your section data. Term data is not required by Clever, but it may be required by certain applications. The fields associated with term data are: 

 • Term_name
• Term_start
• Term_end
 
 For most Clever-managed auto syncs, term data is included by default. If your Clever-managed auto sync does not include term data, please contact Clever Support to discuss what options are available for your sync type. Clever auto-generates a unique Clever ID for each term based on term_start, term_end, and term_name. If any of those values change, a new term Clever ID will be generated which could affect term data in some connected applications. 

 
 Viewing term data in the data browser

 To view the terms data that has synced to Clever, you can go to the Clever Dashboard > Data browser > Terms tab. The Terms tab includes the term name, the start and end dates, the Clever ID of the term, when it was created, when it was last modified, and a link to the Sections tab. 

 
 How is term data used?

 Term data can be used to share with applications. When constructing sharing rules, you can Custom Share by Section when Sharing by Rules . You can use this sharing type to filter by section attributes such as subject, school, course, and term. For more information on this, please see Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules . This will allow you to share your data with an application by certain terms. As an example, you can see below there is a sections sharing rule that has been set up to share sections with a specific term. This will share sections that are associated with the Spring 2022 term. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I side-load term data?

 • No, it is not possible to manually upload term data (term_name, term_start, term_end) for sections. For SFTP syncing districts or schools that are unable to export term data in their sections.csv, we recommend manually updating the sections.csv file to include term data and to update/upload their sections.csv on a regular cadence (i.e. every week) 
 
 Since section data is relatively static (student enrollments can change without any section data (name, course, period, etc.), schools will only need to manually update and upload their sections.csv when sections are added or removed from their school's SIS. 

 What if our Student Information System doesn’t sync term data?

 • If you have a Clever-managed auto sync, you can contact Clever support to see if your sync type can be customized to pull term data.
• If your sync type doesn’t allow for the syncing of term data, we can discuss changing you to a sync type that does allow for this data to be synced. 
 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • For District Admins: Sync Reports 
• Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules

title: Skyward: Sync Comparison
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Comparison 

 

 Overview

 Clever offers two syncs for districts who use Skyward for their student information system:

 • Skyward: Auto Sync - Export Method: Skybuild
• Skyward: API Sync (Qmlativ/OneRoster API or LMS API) - Export Method: API
 
 

 
 Comparison

 Both sync methods have pros and cons depending on your district's data needs. This article is here to provide a high-level comparison of the two syncs. If you have additional questions, please contact Clever Support . 

 
 

 
 Skyward Auto Sync

 
 Skyward API Sync

 
 Sync frequency 6 hours 6 hours 
 Synced records - Schools - Students - Teachers - Sections - Enrollments - Co-teachers - Schools - Students - Teachers - Sections - Enrollments - Staff - Co-teachers 
 Can sync IEP_status, FRL_status, ELL_status for students Yes No 
 Syncs contacts Yes Yes - cannot be filtered 
 Default field mappings View here View here 
 Custom field mappings Yes, however, the field must be exportable via Skybuild. Please reach out to Clever support for assistance with mapping. Yes, please contact Clever Support to set up custom field mappings. 
 Pros - Fast and easy set up - Highly customizable - Fast and easy set up - Supports multiple student contacts per student - Can sync staff 
 Cons - Cannot sync staff - The API Sync cannot sync data resources that contain forward slashes /, ampersands & , or hashes # in the ID value. 
 Application considerations: Should consider the auto sync because of field requirements: - NWEA - HMH - CollegeBoard Should consider the API sync because of contact requirements: - Raptor - ParentSquare

title: SFTP Syncs: SFTP Sync FAQ
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Onboarding to Clever and want to learn about uploading CSV files? Check out our Onboarding - Getting your schools’ data into Clever course, available in Clever Academy! 

 

 Overview

 Whether you’re exporting data for an SFTP sync directly from your Student Information System (SIS) or building your own CSV files to sync data to Clever, there are several commonly asked questions our team receives, as shown below:

 General SFTP sync questions

 • What CSV files are required to sync with Clever? 
• How do my data files work together? 
• What if I’m not sure how to export data from my SIS? 
• Is there a way to manually sync data to Clever, or add users one at a time? 
• Can your team review my data files before I upload them? 
• Can I upload the files one at a time so you can make sure I'm on the right track before uploading all of them? 
• How do I know if my data upload went through? 
 
 File requirements and formatting questions

 • Do I need to include a schools.csv file if we’re a single school? 
• Do I need to include a sections.csv file? 
• What's the difference between teacher_id and teacher_number (or school_id and school_number, or student_id and student_number)? 
• How do I know if additional fields are required for a particular application? 
 
 Updating or editing data

 • Can I manually trigger a sync? 
• How do I archive classes in Clever? 
• How do I remove data from Clever? 
 
 Troubleshooting

 • Why didn't my data update after pushing a sync? 
• Why is grade level showing as "Other" in Clever? 
• Do you have content to help me troubleshoot the data errors on my last attempted sync page? 
 
 Please note: This article is specifically about SFTP syncs. If you use a Clever-managed auto-sync, please click here .

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What CSV files are required to sync with Clever? 

 • The Clever SFTP Specifications outline the five required CSV files for a data sync to complete: 
 • schools.csv
• teachers.csv
• students.csv
• sections.csv
• enrollments.csv
 

 
 The staff.csv is an optional additional file for adding non-teaching staff to Clever. Learn more in this article .

 How do my data files work together? 

 • This graphic is helpful to explain how your files work together:
 
 

 Is there a way to manually sync data to Clever or add users one at a time? 

 • Your 5 required CSV files must initially be synced simultaneously. They can be synced to Clever directly from your SIS, via an FTP client, or manually uploaded to your Clever Dashboard via the Web Upload Tool found on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page.
• You can make any updates or changes to users by editing and re-uploading these data files. Please note that the entire file must be uploaded as each file overwrites the previous.
• Clever doesn't recommend adding or managing accounts through the Custom Data tool if you’re managing your own CSV files manually, as this often creates confusion on the primary source of data for your district. Custom data is intended to complement data coming from the primary CSV files, not replace it.
 
 What if I’m not sure how to export data from my SIS? 

 • We recommend reaching out to your SIS team directly or checking for online help center documentation if you have questions about using the export tools in your SIS.
• While our teams are happy to answer any questions you have regarding our Clever SFTP Specifications , file formatting, or the manual data upload process to Clever, we don’t have insight into your individual SIS instance and the export processes.
 
 Can your team review my data files before I upload them? 

 • It is most secure to upload your files to Clever for our team to review. For security reasons, the Clever team does not recommend sending CSV files via email.
• Additionally, our system automatically surfaces errors that our team may not manually detect! We recommend completing all five required CSV files and uploading them to Clever, and then our team is happy to review the files and provide additional insight into any errors that may occur.
 
 Can I upload the files one at a time so you can make sure I'm on the right track before uploading all of them? 

 • For your first data sync, you must upload all five files simultaneously. When troubleshooting, we also recommend uploading all five files to ensure they work properly together during the sync.
• As you add each file to Clever via the upload tab , our system automatically surfaces basic errors.
• Once you’ve uploaded your CSV files to Clever, our team is happy to review the files and provide additional insight into any errors that may occur.
 
 Do I need to include a schools.csv file if we are a single school? 

 • Yes! The schools.csv is required even if you’re a single school. This is how Clever’s sync system will know to associate your teachers and students with the school.
 
 Do I need to include a sections.csv file? 

 • Yes! The sections.csv file is how Clever syncs your classes and assigns students to their class(es).
• Even if you only have one section/class per grade, a sections.csv is required. The section_id can be any unique value that is kept consistent across the sections.csv and enrollments.csv. If you’re making your own files, our team recommends keeping it simple and using single numbers (section_id = 1, section_id = 2 for each section/class).
 
 How do I know if my data upload went through? 

 • A sync will occur any time data is successfully uploaded to Clever.
• Syncs generally take 15-30 minutes to complete. If you’ve uploaded new data to Clever, please give the sync at least 30 minutes to complete.
• Once 30 minutes have passed, please review your Data sources > SIS sync page for the status of your sync. You should also receive an email to the email address in your Clever profile. 
• Click here to learn more about your sync status! 
 
 

 

 
 Why didn't my data update after pushing a sync? 

 • First, make sure the sync actually processed by looking at the sync status at the top of the home page of your Clever Dashboard .
 
 

 • After confirming that the sync went through and that the data did not update by reviewing the SIS sync page , the next step is to check your files. 
• Head to your FTP directory to ensure you don't have duplicate files. Having duplicate files will cause Clever to continue reading the first file that was uploaded while ignoring the newest one. Click here for a tutorial on accessing your remote site via an FTP client, or follow the instructions below:
 
 • Open the FTP client (like Filezilla , which is what this example uses) and enter the directory details found on your Sync > Settings page. Click Quickconnect. 
 
 

 

 • Once connected, on the right, you will see the CSV files in your Clever SFTP directory (Remote site). Check to see if any files are duplicated. In the example below, you can see the teachers.csv file has a duplicate file. 
 
 

 • Right-click the duplicate files and select "delete."
• Re-upload the correct file to replace the duplicates.
• Clever will begin processing the updated file approximately five minutes after it has been uploaded to your FTP box. Please note that it may take 30 minutes for the sync to fully complete
 

 What's the difference between teacher_id and teacher_number (or school_id and school_number, or student_id and student_number)? 

 • All of the user_id fields (school_id, teacher_id, student_id) are required fields for syncing data to Clever as this ID is the basis for the creation of a Clever ID, and the source of truth for which historical data is tied to in Clever. Our Clever SFTP Specifications requires this field be unique to each user in the district.
 
 
 • While school_number is required, any additional user_number fields (teacher_number, student_number) are optional in Clever. 
• Many schools will re-use their chosen user_id for this fie

title: Clever IDM: Formulas
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Building formulas 3. Common operations 4. Supported operations on fields 5. Supported field names 6. Complex modifications 7. Random password custom formulas 7. Using the forEach function 

 

 Overview

 This article will walk you through common operations that you can use to manipulate data in Clever to create formulas for Clever IDM. These allow you to build a format for many different products in Clever - from IDM email addresses to SSO (Saved Passwords) application credentials and many other places in the Clever Dashboard . These formulas work all across Clever - so if you’re setting up Clever IDM for Google, Active Directory, or even Entra ID, you’ll still be able to transform data into the format you need!

 

 
 Building formulas

 Easily tailor formulas with Clever IDM's format builder. This intuitive tool guides you through crafting formats for IDM fields like sub-OUs and login credentials. Choose between the standard block builder for simplicity or the advanced format editor for more customization.

 Please note: The block builder approach for building formats doesn’t currently support extension fields or advanced use of functions. If you need to use your extension fields or write more complex functions that you know Clever supports but don’t see as an option, you’ll need to use the advanced option of entering a format string. See the Complex modifications for assistance with format strings. 

 

 Click use the advanced format editor to access the advanced format string format. 

 

 Clever Tip: Dark purple SIS variables, such as School Name (as shown above), indicate a field that is required in your sync. Light purple SIS variables, such as Graduation Year, indicate that they're an optional variable that is not required in your sync. If you use an optional variable and don't sync data for that field for all users, any user who doesn't have that data populated shows as an error and won't sync successfully. 

 Add an SIS Variable

 This option allows you to select from the available fields that you are syncing to Clever. 

 

 Add Custom Text

 This option allows you to type out custom text that you would like to use in your formula.

 

 Add a Function

 This option allows you to use functions to build a formula. For more context on how to use functions, review Common Operations . 

 

 
 Common operations

 

 Clever Tip: Copy these formats from this article and directly paste them in the IDM setup for your email or password format. Then, click Add format to set the format (see image below). 

 

 

 

 
 Description

 
 Custom Format

 
 First three letters of last name {{substr name.last 0 3}} 
 Last two letters of student graduation year {{substr student.graduation_year -2 2}} 
 First name initial {{substr name.first 0 1}} 
 Last 6 of SIS ID for student {{substr student.sis_id -6 6}} 
 Last 4 of SIS ID for teacher {{substr student.sis_id -4 4}} 
 First word of last name {{textBefore name.last " "}} 
 Replace - with . in last name {{replace name.last "-" "."}} 
 Date from student date of birth {{formatDate student.dob "DD"}} 
 Initials of school name {{initials school_name}} 
 
 

 
 

 Supported operations on fields

 

 Clever Tip: Note that field names that are not available for all users have a prefix with the user type e.g. student.<field name> or teacher.<field name> 

 

 
 Formula

 
 Example

 
 Description

 
 substr {{substr student.graduation_year -2 2}} For graduation_year 2034, result is 34 The “-2 2” means start from 2 characters from the end, and grab the next 2 digits If you want just a portion of a field, but not the whole thing 
 
 subtract

 {{subtract student.graduation_year 4}}

 For graduation_year 2034, result is 2030 
 If you want to subtract from a number.

 For example, if the graduation year provided to Clever IDM is for high school graduation, but you want to adjust it to 8th grade graduation.

 
 
 add

 {{add student.graduation_year 4}}

 For graduation_year 2034, result is 2038. 
 If you want to add to a number.

 For example, if the graduation year provided to Clever IDM is for 8th grade graduation, but you want to adjust it to high school graduation.

 
 
 formatDate {{formatDate student.dob "MMDDYYYY"}}

 

 Note: “MMM” produces Jan/Feb/etc and “MMMM” produces January/February/etc 

 Student Birthday of 21st October, 2008 results in 10212008 Reformat something that’s coming into Clever as a date 
 replace {{replace name.first "-" "."}} For Anne-marrie the formula returns Anne.marrie Replace a character with another 
 textBefore {{textBefore email "@"}} For email maya@district.edu returns maya Return all characters before the one specified. Can be used to get the first word or the username only from the email field. 
 textAfter {{textAfter email “@”}} For email maya@example.edu returns example.edu Return all characters after the one specified. 
 {{textAfterLast}} i.e. {{textAfterLast name.last "-"}} For a user with the last name Doe-Smith-Jenkins, {{textAfterLast name.last "-"}} output would be Jenkins. Will take the text after the last occurrence of a substring. 
 trimLeft {{trimLeft email “C”}} For email CCCmaya@example.edu , returns maya@example.edu Returns all characters after trimming off all occurrences of the specified characters from the front. 
 toUpper {{toUpper name.first}} For Maya, the formula results in MAYA Makes all the characters uppercase. 
 toLower {{toLower name.first}} For Maya, the formula results in maya Makes all the characters lowercase. 
 alphanumeric {{alphanumeric name.first}} For Anne-marrie the formula below results in Annemarrie Returns a field with all non-alphanumeric characters removed. 
 initials {{initials student.school_name}} For school_name Star Falls Elementary, the formula results in SFE Returns the initials of a value. The value can be more than one word, as shown in the example. 
 concat {{concat name.first name.last}} For name John Doe, the formula results in JohnDoe Concatenates two values 
 ignoreIfNull {{ignoreIfNull name.middle}} For the full name “John Jacob Doe”, the formula results in “Jacob”. For the full name “John Doe”, where the user does not have a middle name, the formula results in nothing and will display as blank. If you want to use fields that may not exist for all users in formulas. 
 if {if equals student.sis_id "12345" "yes" "no"}} For the sis_id 12345, the formula results in “yes” For any other sis_id, the formula results in “no” Allows different values to be returned based on the included condition. Follows the form: {{if <condition> <value if condition is true> <value if condition is false>}} 
 equals {{equals student.sis_id “12345”}} For the sis_id 12345, the formula results in “true”. For any other sis_id, the formula results in “false”. Returns “true” when the two values equal each other, and “false” otherwise. 
 not {{not equals student.sis_id “12345”}} For the sis_id 12345, the formula results in “false”. For any other sis_id, the formula results in “true”. Flips true values to false and false values to true. 
 and {{and equals student.sis_id “12345” equals name.first “John”}} For sis_id 12345 and name “John Doe”, the formula results in “true”. Returns true when both the first and second values are true. 
 or {{or equals student.sis_id “12345” equals name.first “John”}} For sis_id 12345 and the name “John Doe”, the formula results in true. Returns true when either the first or second values are true. Also returns true when both values are true. 
 greater {{greater student.graduation_year “2034”}} For graduation_year 2035 and above, this returns true. Otherwise, this returns false. Returns true when the first value is greater than the second value. 
 less {{less student.graduation_year “2034”}} For graduation_year 2033 and below, this returns true. Otherwise, this returns false. Returns true when the first value is less than the second value. 
 
 contains

 {{contains "2024" email

title: Clever + Google Classroom: Reprovision Classes that Teachers Declined Accidentally
text:
How to reprovision any classes that teachers declined by accident

 Be sure to let your teachers know that they need to accept the synced classes in Google Classroom: If a teacher hit the Decline button by accident, a Clever Admin can reprovision those classes in the Clever Dashboard by following these steps: 1. Find the declined section in the Clever Dashboard by using the search bar or the data browser. 2. From the section's profile page in the Clever Dashboard, you'll see the option to reprovision the class: Please note: each accidentally-declined class will need to be reprovisioned following this method. Only declined and archived classes will show the option to Reprovision Section.

title: Clever Portal: Sharing Teacher Pages & resources with other teachers
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Sharing Pages with other teachers 3. Sharing resources with other teachers 4. Accessing pages & resources shared with you 5. How to copy resources from a page shared with you 6. Sharing resources with other teachers 7. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 You can share Teacher Pages and resources with other teachers in your school , and they can share their pages and resources with you as well. Sharing Teacher Pages is perfect for:

 • Teachers covering a class who want to know what resources students have access to
• Sharing content with teachers in your department to spark more collaboration
• Sharing a stellar Teacher Page as an exemplar for other teachers
• A common unit of study that a class team might be doing in tandem (so other teachers don’t need to reinvent the wheel).
 
 If you’re not yet familiar with Teacher Pages, check out this article first! 

 

 
 Sharing Pages with other teachers

 You can increase collaboration with colleagues by sharing your page with other teachers within your school. Click the More page actions > Share Page button at the top of the Teacher Page you are sharing and enter the person you’d like to share the page with. Rest assured, they won’t be able to modify your page! They’ll only be able to see the resources you’ve added to your page and add any desired resources to their own page. 

 

 
 Sharing resources with other teachers

 You can also share a single specific resource, such as an app, PDF, or link. To share a resource, click on the three vertical dots (⋮) on the resource, then click Send . You can send the resource to a specific teacher or your entire grade level. 

 

 
 Accessing pages & resources shared with you

 When a page or resource is shared with you, you’ll see a notification in the bell icon of your Clever Portal. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 You can also access shared pages by going to the Shared with Me Pages tab of your Clever Portal under the Pages drop-down on the left-hand side of your Portal. 

 

 
 How to copy resources from a page shared with you

 See a resource on another teacher’s Page that you like? You can add that resource to your own Teacher Page by clicking on the Add button. If the resource is a Clever Library app that you don’t already have, you’ll be able to provision accounts for your students when adding the app to your Page. Please remember that if a district app on a Teacher Page isn’t shared with you in Clever, you won’t have access to it. For instance, let’s say you’re a science teacher. If an ELA teacher shares their Page, which has a district-added reading program that only reading teachers can view in your school, you’ll see a grayed-out icon and won’t be able to access the app. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Who can I share my Teacher Page or resource with? Currently, you can select to share with any teacher who works at your school site. Your district assigns your school site. If you teach at more than one school, you'll only be able to share at your primary school, which is either the school your district has assigned as your primary school or the school where you teach the most classes/sections. Can I revoke shared access from someone after I have shared my Teacher Page with them? Yes! You can remove view access from others at any time via the sharing settings. Can I dismiss Pages that are shared with me that I am not interested in? Not at this time. Those Pages will only be accessible under the “Shared with Me” tab of your homeroom. Can teachers edit or change my Page while they’re viewing it? No! At this time, teachers can view your Page and add resources from it to their own Page. They won't be able to make any changes to your Page. If I share a Page with another teacher, can their students access my Teacher Page? No, only your students can view your Page(s). When you share with another teacher, only that teacher can view your Page. How can I add a resource from another teacher’s Page to my own? Click the Add button on a resource that you’d like on your own Page. You’ll be able to select the Page and category you’d like to add that resource to! If it is a Clever Library app, Clever will prompt you to provision accounts for your students first. Why can’t I add a grayed-out resource to my Page? Your district may add resources that aren't provisioned for your use. If this is the case, the resource will appear grayed out, and you’ll need to contact your district for permission. How will I know if another teacher has shared a page with me? You will receive a notification on your Homeroom Page and see an icon for their Teacher Page underneath the Shared with Me tab. 

 
 Related Articles: Clever Portal: Teacher Pages

title: For Clever Admins: I'm a new Clever Admin at an existing Clever District
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Recommended actions 3. How to access Clever Support 

 ​

 Overview

 ​

 Welcome, new district admin! If you’ve recently been added to your district’s Clever account as a district administrator, this article is for you.

 ​

 
 Recommended actions

 ​

 Sometimes, we hear about Clever district administrators who are added to Clever but not trained on how to support their district with Clever. We want to ensure you feel ready to support your district with Clever, so we’ve laid out some recommended steps:

 • Watch this overview of Clever video : it’ll give you a quick rundown of what Clever is, and how it works to support your district, from your role as a District Admin to school tech coaches, teachers, and students. 
• Be sure to update your email preferences : We send a variety of emails to support you and your district throughout the year, including important emails about your Clever sync, data pauses, and more. These are disabled by default , so it's vital that you turn them on if you want to be notified via email.
• Work through our Clever Admin checklist : this checklist is perfect for new and returning districts. For some of the steps, like “Adding a team member,” you might not have anyone to add just yet, but being able to check on who is already in your Clever account can help you take stock of how your account is set up.
• Complete Clever Academy for Clever Admins : This comprehensive set of courses will ensure you’re a Clever Champion in no time! You’ll learn how to use Clever to share data, troubleshoot, and more. 
 
 ​

 
 How to access Clever Support

 ​

 Everyone needs a little help from time to time. You can rely on our award-winning support team when you need assistance. To access support, click the chat icon in the bottom right corner of any page on Support.Clever.com . You’ll be asked to select your user type (Clever Admin), then to explain your issue. If we can’t identify a Help Center article that solves your problem, click “No” or “Contact Support.” You’ll be able to submit a ticket to our team, and we’ll get back to you as soon as possible.

 ​

title: Clever + EBSCO Information Services Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. EBSCO Information Services Requirements 3. Adding EBSCO Information Services 4. Additional Customization Options 5. FAQs 6. EBSCO Customer Support 7. Clever Customer Support 

 ​

 Overview​

 Now, it’s easier than ever to enhance student research and classroom instruction with user-friendly research tools from EBSCO, including Explora, EBSCOhost, EBSCO Discovery Service and subject-specific Reference Centers. Clever also integrates with EBSCO eBooks, Flipster digital magazines and NoveList, a database of book recommendations to support readers’ advisory. 

 ​

 
 EBSCO Information Services Requirements

 Setting up Clever to access your EBSCO resources is simple and entirely self-service. First, you will add the EBSCO Information Services app and configure sharing permissions within the Clever dashboard. Then, you will enable schools for Clever access in EBSCO Experience Manager. This step maps schools to EBSCO accounts, which allows EBSCO to direct Clever users to the specific resources for their school. This step is important because every district subscribes to a different set of EBSCO products. Before you begin, it’s important to understand how your EBSCO products are set up on your district and school EBSCO customer accounts. Every school and district has an EBSCO account that their EBSCO resources are associated with. When enabling schools in EBSCO Experience Manager, you will need to know if any schools should be enabled in their own EBSCO account, or if all schools can be enabled in the district account. The easiest way to decide is to find out if any schools in your district have subscribed to EBSCO products just for their school. If they have, you will need to enable those schools in their own EBSCO accounts. This way, users at those schools will have access to their school’s unique content as well as any shared content across the district. If none of your schools have their own subscriptions, you can enable every school in the district’s EBSCO account. For help with this, you can contact EBSCO support , ask your school or district librarians, or reach out to your district’s EBSCO administrator. Your EBSCO administrator has primary responsibility for configuring EBSCO resources for your school or district and may be someone associated with your school library, district office, or consortium office.

 ​

 
 Adding EBSCO Information Services

 ​To add EBSCO Information Services to your Clever Dashboard, please search for it from the Applications > Add Applications page.

 We recommend sharing all records in your district with EBSCO Information Services. Once the sharing permissions have been configured, you will need to enable schools in the Authentication & Access module of EBSCO Experience Manager. For instructions, please visit EBSCO Connect: Setting Up Clever for Your District or School . Completing these steps will allow users to anonymously access EBSCO resources from within the Clever Portal. All users will be directed to the same EBSCO product. To change the product your users are directed to, ask your EBSCO administrator to help you change the top-ranked profile so that it corresponds with that product. To direct your users straight to different EBSCO products, you can create EBSCO links that prompt for Clever authentication and add these to Clever as External Links. For example, using links you can direct primary school students to Explora, and high schoolers to EBSCOhost, and provide a separate link to access Flipster. To learn more, please visit EBSCO Connect: Clever SSO - Additional Customization Options .

 ​

 
 Additional Customization Options

 Once you have enabled schools in EBSCO Experience Manager and confirmed that your integration is working, there’s more that you can do. You can customize the user experience within the Clever portal, and your district can use Clever to authenticate users to EBSCO resources from outside the portal as well. For more information on these options, please see: Clever SSO – Additional Customization Options . 

 

 You can also control whether and when MyEBSCO accounts are automatically created for users. MyEBSCO accounts allow users to persistently save items they find in EBSCO products, download eBooks, and engage in other activities, such as placing a hold on an ILS item in EBSCO Discovery Service. No personally identifiable information is shared for accounts created for students. For more information on the options available, please see How do I configure personalization for Clever? 

 

 
 FAQs

 For questions about your EBSCO account settings and configuration, please review Clever SSO - Frequently Asked Questions for Administrators on EBSCO Connect or reach out to your EBSCO administrator. ​​

 
 EBSCO Customer Support

 Please review:

 • Setting Up Clever for Your District or School 
• Clever SSO - Additional Customization Options 
• How do I configure personalization for Clever? 
• Clever SSO - Frequently Asked Questions for Administrators on EBSCO Connect.
 
 If you still have questions, or if you see an EBSCO-branded error message, please submit a support case. ​ 

 
 Clever Customer Support

 ​For additional help requesting the EBSCO Information Services app in your Clever Dashboard or questions on setting up sharing permissions, you can open a ticket with Clever Support by clicking the messaging icon in the lower right corner of the Clever Help Center. ​

title: Clever and Canadian Educational Institutions
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever’s Compliance with Canadian Privacy Laws 3. Schools are in control of student personal information 4. Clever lets schools maintain strong privacy settings by default 5. Clever does not rent or sell data, permit advertising or profile students 6. Clever does not use biometrics 7. Clever has a comprehensive security program 8. Cross-border transfers of Student Data 9. Privacy impact assessments 10. Clever’s privacy team 

 

 Overview

 Schools in Canada are permitted to use online services, such as Clever. Clever’s services follow privacy principles and data security standards that can help schools comply with regulations applicable to Canadian schools. In order to meet the needs of Canadian schools, Clever has:

 • Updated its Terms of Use 
• Updated its privacy notice for Canada-specific regulatory requirements 
• Created a Canadian Data Processing Addendum 
 
 

 
 Clever’s Compliance with Canadian Privacy Laws 

 Clever adheres to internationally recognized privacy principles. Clever understands that Canadian schools are subject to specific Canadian privacy laws. With Clever’s services, public schools can comply with provincial Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy legislation and private schools can comply with private sector personal information protection laws such as the British Columbia Personal Information Protection Act, the Alberta Personal Information Protection Act and the Quebec Act respecting the protection of personal information in the private sector. Clever is also a proud signatory of the Student Data Privacy Pledge , the first recipient of the IMS TrustEd Apps Seal , and has received CommonSense Privacy Program ’s highest possible rating, Use Responsibly , based on a full privacy evaluation. 

 
 Schools are in control of student personal information

 Clever understands that Student Data is generally considered to be sensitive under Canadian privacy laws and that schools must retain control over that Student Data. With Clever, schools can see and control all data shared with their authorized partners in one Clever dashboard rather than having to access multiple systems. When a school uses Clever, it maintains complete control over what data to share and in what circumstances. Clever will delete Student Data and other school information from Clever’s systems upon request. 

 
 Clever lets schools maintain strong privacy settings by default

 Clever only shares data with applications that have been explicitly authorized by the school. Student Data is private by design. Schools are able to minimize the amount of student information shared with third-party applications. Role-based access allows authorized school organization users to access only the features they need, without being able to modify important settings. And Clever proactively prevents end-user mistakes from leaking unauthorized data by placing automatic security holds on unusual data transfers. 

 
 Clever does not rent or sell data, permit advertising or profile students

 Clever doesn't sell or monetize Student Data. Clever does not display advertising to students through Clever’s services. Clever does not profile students based on their behaviour. Clever doesn't allow behavioral tracking by any third-parties on Clever for the purpose of targeted advertising or to show ads on Clever. 

 
 Clever does not use biometrics 

 Clever doesn't sell or monetize Student Data. Clever doesn't display advertising to students through Clever’s services. Clever doesn't profile students based on their behaviour. Clever doesn't allow behavioral tracking by any third parties on Clever for the purpose of targeted advertising or to show ads on Clever. 

 

 
 Clever has a comprehensive security program

 Clever’s comprehensive security program is designed to secure Student Data from loss, unauthorized access, unauthorized use, unauthorized alternation and unauthorized disclosure. In accordance with Canadian legal obligations, Clever considers the sensitivity of the information in designing its security practices. Clever regularly aligns its security practices with the internationally recognized NIST Cybersecurity Framework. Clever’s Security Overview describes Clever’s approach to security. To dive deeper, please see the Clever Security Practices. As part of Clever’s security program, Clever evaluates its vendors and works with vendors that meet Clever’s standards. 

 

 
 Cross-border transfers of Student Data

 Clever’s robust security practices allow Canadian schools to access and use Clever’s services hosted in the United States. Canadian laws do not prohibit the use of U.S.-based cloud services as long as the student personal information is protected in an equivalent manner as it would be in Canada. In Nova Scotia, schools may also need permission of the head of the school board as defined under the Nova Scotia Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. Clever is also a proud signatory of the Student Data Privacy Pledge , a member of the Student Data Privacy Consortium , the first recipient of the IMS TrustEd Apps Seal , and have received CommonSense Privacy Program ’s highest possible rating, "Use Responsibly," based on a full privacy evaluation. 

 
 Privacy impact assessments

 Clever doesn't have a one-size fits all privacy impact assessment because each school’s use of the Clever platform may vary depending on how the school configures the Clever platform services. However, Clever understands that public bodies in Canada and some private sector organizations are required to conduct privacy impact assessments or PIAs. Clever makes information publicly available on its privacy practices: Working with schools to safeguard data privacy. We are here to help if a school needs to conduct a PIA and Clever’s publicly available information does not provide all of the information the school needs. 

 
 Clever’s privacy team

 Every member of the Clever team is responsible for ensuring that Clever meets its commitments with respect to Student Data. Clever’s privacy team can be contacted at: trust@clever.com . For more information on privacy, see this page: Working with schools to safeguard data privacy .

title: Clever IDM: Group Membership Management
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Prerequisites 3. Configuration 4. Frequently Asked Questions 

 ​

 Overview

 This article describes the group membership management functionality available in Clever IDM. This functionality is only available to Clever IDM subscribers who have configured Clever IDM with Active Directory or Google. Clever IDM can create, update, and archive users in your Active Directory or Google Workspace instances. In addition, Clever IDM can now manage these users’ memberships in groups – including both security and mailing (or distribution) groups. 

 
 Prerequisites

 ​In order to set up group membership management, you will need to start by confirming the groups you want managed are properly configured. All Clever-managed groups must have unique names. 

 Also, if you want to set up a rule to put users into different groups, the relevant group names must follow the same conventions. For example, if you want a rule set up that will add each user to a group for their school, the relevant groups need to be consistently named – e.g. “Toll Gate Elementary - Students”, “Hopewell Valley High School - Students." 

 
 Configuration

 You can configure your group membership in your IDM setup under the Configure Groups step. First, select a user type you would like to manage groups for. You can manage groups for students, teachers, and or staff. Next, select all of the groups you would like Clever IDM to manage. Note: you should only select groups that you want Clever IDM to add users to and remove them from. If a user is in a group but none of Clever IDM’s membership rules indicate they should be in that group, Clever IDM will remove the user from the group. Once you’ve selected all the groups you’d like Clever IDM to manage for your selected user type, you can set up membership rules for those groups. Click Add membership rule and create a rule for your groups. In this example, the rule will place users in groups based on their graduation years. 

 Please note: Navigate to the Clever IDM: Formulas Help Center article t o learn more about common formulas and operations that you can use to manipulate data in Clever to create formulas! 

 You can add as many rules as you would like for each user type. Once you have created rules, you can always go back and edit or delete them. Click Review Summary and run a sync preview to see any changes Clever IDM would make to users’ group memberships.

 

 Active Directory: Nested Groups 

 Active Directory allows for group nesting. This is the process of making one Active Directory group a member of another group. A nested group inherits all the permissions and privileges of each group it is a member of.

 In the example below, the group is SamTest, the sub-group is Groups , and the nested groups are 3-Group 4-Group, PreKindergarten-Group, etc. 

 

 In order for Clever IDM to manage nested groups, special group membership rule formatting is required. For the nested groups to be managed, the whole file path has to be in brackets (see below) otherwise the rule will cause an error.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 ​Can membership rules place users in distribution/email groups and security groups? Yes! Clever IDM does not distinguish between security and distribution/email groups, so will add/remove users from any groups per the district’s membership rules. What happens when a user leaves my district? When a user leaves a district, Clever IDM will archive that user. If group membership management is enabled, Clever IDM will remove the user from all groups, including default AD groups. If the user returns, Clever IDM will unarchive them and place them back in the appropriate groups per the district’s membership rules. Will users’ group membership update automatically? Yes! Whenever Clever IDM syncs, it will re-evaluate all the membership rules for each user type and adjust users’ group memberships accordingly. For example, if a user moves schools and a district’s membership rules are school-based, Clever IDM will remove that user from their old school and add them to the new school group. Will Clever IDM create a new group if one doesn’t exist? No, Clever IDM will never create, edit, or delete existing groups. If Clever IDM evaluates a membership rule and the resulting group doesn’t exist, it will create an issue in the sync report. Once the relevant group is created in AD, the relevant users will be added to it, and the issue will be resolved. Why are some users not being added to groups? Clever IDM won't manage group membership for groups outside of your chosen OU. You may manually add users to groups outside of your OU, and Clever IDM will not remove a user from that group except when the users are archived.

 What will happen if a user has both a teacher and a staff account in Clever? Clever IDM will manage their groups based on the primary role selected during Clever IDM setup.

 Can I review any potential changes before enabling group management? Yes, after you set up your rules, Clever IDM will generate a preview. You'll review any changes to be made, and if satisfied, click Provision !

title: Clever IDM: Multi-Destination Support
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Adding additional destinations 3. Managing multiple active destinations 4. FAQs 

 Overview 

 Clever IDM allows you to manage accounts in on-premise Active Directory, Entra ID, and Google Workspace simultaneously. 

 
 

 Adding additional destinations

 If you haven’t started setting Clever IDM up yet, please visit this article . Once you have your first destination set up, you can add another destination by simply clicking the Add new destination button on your Clever IDM homepage:

 After adding an additional destination, you can start configuring it. For guidance on setting up the new destination, see Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup , Clever IDM: Entra ID Setup , or Clever IDM: Google Setup .

 Of note, each destination will be set up independently, meaning that you can use the same credential and OU conventions or different ones – depending on the setup that works best for you. 

 
 Managing multiple active destinations

 Once Clever IDM is syncing with multiple destinations, you’ll see them each as cards on your Clever IDM homepage. The cards contain summary metrics from your most recent sync. To view more about a destination, click the card to see the relevant tasks, reports, and more. 

 

 
 FAQs

 Can we manage different sets of users for each destination? 

 • Yes. Clever IDM allows you to configure each destination independently, so you can select whichever user types you’d like to be managed for each destination. If you’d like to filter down users by school or grade level for a given destination, reach out to Clever Support and we can help. 
 
 Can we keep the passwords the same across multiple destinations? 

 • Clever IDM will set passwords based on your chosen convention whenever accounts are created. Clever IDM also supports random passwords across multiple destinations. Once one destination is configured, you’ll see the option to use that destination’s password in other destinations.
• School districts using Clever IDM can also enable recovery and reset functionality for any connected destinations. 
• Once accounts are created, passwords can be managed via Clever. See this article on how to do so: Clever Admin: Account recovery and password reset. 
 
 

 Does it cost more to add multiple destinations? 

 • No! Clever IDM charges based on the total number of managed users, regardless of how many destinations we manage. The only case where adding another destination will increase the cost of Clever IDM is if you use the new destination to manage users that aren’t already being managed by the first destination you configured.
 
 Can I add two different destinations of the same type? 

 • We support multiple destinations of the same type, e.g., you can use Clever IDM to sync users to two or more Active Directory, Entra ID, or Google Workspaces servers. If you’re interested in this feature, reach out to implementation@clever.com . 
 
 I use Google Directory sync and Google Password sync to keep my AD and Google in sync. Should I still keep that running if I set up IDM for both? 

 • Clever IDM will manage users and their placement in OUs in Google. If those are the only things you use Google Directory Sync to manage in Google Workspace, running it in tandem with Clever IDM is unnecessary.
• If there are other elements, like Groups or Passwords, that you use Google Directory Sync to keep in sync between AD and Google, we recommend you continue to use GDS alongside Clever IDM. Ensure you only use GDS to sync the data Clever IDM isn’t already managing. 
 
 If you still need support or have additional questions, please reach out to support.clever.com .

title: Sharing Permissions: Guided Sharing
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Guided Sharing • Choose setup method • Choose who needs access • Preview & share user data • Data validation • Finalize setup 

 

 Overview

 As part of the process of adding an application, Clever Admins can configure sharing permissions in order to determine what district/school data should be shared with the app, and which district/school users should have access to the application from their Clever Portal. Clever offers two experiences for sharing data: Guided Sharing and Standard Sharing .

 • Guided Sharing is best for new users or those who don't set up sharing settings often.
• Standard sharing is best for experienced Clever users or those who need to use School Site Sharing .
 
 The choice in experience is custom to each user -- a new admin at your district can choose to use Guided Sharing while an admin with more Clever experience may choose Standard Sharing. Users can choose to switch between these two experiences at any time by selecting Edit Sharing for an application, and selecting Switch Sharing setup view in the top-right. Your choice in sharing experience will be remembered and used anytime you edit sharing permissions for an app. 

 

 

 
 Guided Sharing

 

 Step 1: Choose sharing method 

 You have options for how to share data, however, you can only choose to share based on one of the criteria below.

 • Share specific sections, teachers, students, and/or staff: Clever will only share data that meets the specific criteria you've set. This is the most recommended sharing type as it provides districts with the flexibility in determining specifically what data should be included, or excluded, in the sharing. Read this article to learn more about this sharing option . 
 
 Clever tip: Need help with structuring a rule to Share by specific sections, teachers, students, and/or staff? Check out our tutorial videos for Guided sharing to help create rules to share by share by section , share by teacher, share by student , and share by staff .
