 • Share everyone in specific schools: Clever will only share any data associated with the schools you've selected (e.g. students, teachers, sections, staff). 
 
 

 • Share everyone in the district: All district data in Clever will be synced to the application. This includes Clever admins, schools, teachers, students, staff and sections. 
 
 

 • Use an application’s suggested data sharing template: Some applications have set general recommendations for how to share data. You can view the recommendation and see if it’s a good fit for you.
 
 

 • Use an application’s customized data sharing template for your district: In addition to general recommendations, sometimes an application team member will create a custom recommendation for your district. You can view the recommendation and see if it’s a good fit for you.
 
 

 • Copy another app’s data sharing rules: If you’ve already set up sharing with another application and want to apply the same settings to this application, you can select this option. Read this article to learn more about copying sharing rules !
 
 The data shared with the application will dynamically update based on the sharing permissions configured. As a new record meets the sharing criteria, please allow up to 24 hours for the record to be created in the application. 

 
 Step 2: Choose who needs access 

 In Step 2, you will select which users to share with an application using the selected sharing method. For additional information on using the various sharing methods, please see these articles: 

 • Specific sections, teachers, students, and/or staff 
• Use an application’s customized data sharing template for your district 
• Copy another app's data sharing rules 
 
 During this step, you can configure sharing rules and filters as many times as you’d like. Data will not be changed in your learning applications until you click Save at the end of the Guided Sharing experience. In the next step you’ll be able to preview the data that will be shared.

 

 If you have a lot of rules, use the Jump to end/Jump to start button to scroll to the bottom/top of your rules. 

 

 

 

 Once you’re ready to preview that data that will be shared based on your rules, click Next. 

 
 Step 3: Preview & share user data 

 In Step 3 you can preview the records that will be shared by your data sharing settings. Clever provides both the total number of shared records as well as a table of the individual records that will be shared with the application. After you have reviewed the shared records for accuracy, click Share with application. The data is now successfully shared with the application! 

 
 Step 4: Data validation 

 Many applications set data errors and warnings to flag potential data issues that will affect students and teachers. If any data errors or warnings are triggered, they will display on this page. For more information on data errors and warnings, please see this article . This step may or may not appear depending on the application's settings. When ready to proceed, click Next . 

 
 Step 5: Finalize Setup 

 Some applications will provide additional instructions for finalizing setup for their application. If any final instructions have been provided, they will be surfaced here. If this is your first time setting up sharing for the application you may also see a Launch Date. This is the date when the application will be available to users in the Clever Portal. If you need to adjust this date, please contact the application's support team directly. Once you read the setup information and complete any required steps, click Finish. 

 If you haven’t chosen a category for the application in your Clever Portal (it’s in the More Apps section) you’ll receive a prompt to add the application to a category. Choose a category from the dropdown if you’d like.

title: Clever IDM: Summer Rollover
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Default behavior 3. Pausing Clever IDM syncs 4. Options for syncing during the summer 5. Back to school 6. Preserving access 

 Overview

 Clever IDM is built to work seamlessly with your existing summer rollover practices, from preparing for summer school to getting ready for back to school to preserving access for graduated students. This article is your guide for what to expect and the tools and processes you can use to customize Clever IDM – and Clever, more broadly – to fit your needs. 

 
 Default Behavior

 Clever syncs are set to pause at the end of the school year and unpause just before the back to school season. See this article for more on sync pauses and when default sync pause dates are set for this year. When your sync is paused, data in Clever won’t be automatically updated. By default, Clever IDM’s nightly syncs will continue to run. Since there are no automatic data updates happening in Clever, these syncs usually have no impact. There are two exceptions:

 • Custom data . If your school organization modifies custom data in Clever during your sync pause, Clever IDM will sync newly created or modified custom data users to your identity provider.
• Manual changes in a destination. If someone in your school organization manually updates users in Google or Active Directory, Clever IDM may revert those changes on its next sync – if they conflict with Clever data. For example, if you move a Clever-managed user to a new OU in Google, Clever IDM will move that user back to its original OU during the next sync. This is only true for users that Clever IDM actively manages; users not managed by Clever IDM won't be affected by any changes made in Clever.
 
 

 
 Pausing Clever IDM syncs

 If you'd like Clever IDM’s nightly syncs to pause during this time, please use the “Pause <destination> Syncs” button, found on the IDM dashboard. If you have multiple IDM destinations (e.g., Google and Entra ID, or Entra ID AND Active Directory), you must pause both of them, as pausing only applies to the currently selected destination. You can resume Clever IDM syncs at any time.

 Learn more about pausing IDM syncs here . 

 
 

 Options for syncing during the summer

 Some school organizations may want Clever IDM to automatically create and manage user accounts over the summer. The easiest way to manage this is to use custom data. Our Summer School: Overview article includes a comprehensive guide for how to manage summer school data in Clever. Any users added via custom data will be automatically synced through Clever IDM’s nightly syncs. If custom data isn’t a good option for your school organization you can consider delaying your sync pause so that summer school data is updated in Clever before your pause starts. In some cases, it may make sense not to pause your Clever sync at all. For example, if you’d like to continue syncing staff and teachers to Clever – so they have their accounts created – but don’t want to use custom data. School orgs using an SFTP sync can achieve this by continuing to sync the teachers.csv and staff.csv, but temporarily pausing the syncing of all other files. We recommend discussing this option with the Clever team so you understand the implications. Lastly, as need be, you can manually provision users in Google, Entra ID, or Active Directory while your sync is paused. When your sync resumes, Clever IDM will match the newly created users and begin to manage them.

 

 
 Back to school

 Once your sync is unpaused for the coming school year, new data will begin flowing into Clever, and old data will be removed. Once the data for the upcoming year is successfully synced, Clever IDM will update data in Google, Entra ID, and/or Active Directory accordingly. Here are the most common changes you can expect from Clever IDM:

 • Creating new users who’ve joined your schools for the new school year. This includes the newest class of students, any students who’ve transferred from other school organizations, as well as any teachers or staff who have joined.
• Updating existing users. The most common updates are OU changes. If your school organization's OU structure involves schools or grade levels, you should expect Clever IDM to move students to their new OUs and sub-OUs.
• Archiving removed users. Any users who've been removed from your Clever sync during your pause will be archived by Clever IDM. That includes graduated or promoted students as well as any students, teachers, and staff who have left your schools for other reasons.
 
 If you’re using the graduation year field in Clever IDM and aren’t syncing graduation year values directly to Clever from your SIS, you’ll also see the following prompt in Clever IDM when you unpause: Clever IDM calculates the graduation year based on the student’s synced grade level if you are not already sending graduation year data via your SIS. This prompt ensures that this calculation associates your data with the correct school year. Data synced before the graduation rollover date will be associated with the previous school year for graduation year calculations. Data synced after the graduation rollover date will be associated with the upcoming school year for graduation year calculations. Providing a graduation rollover date enables Clever IDM to automatically update any information based on the graduation year (OUs, groups, etc.) as grade levels change during rollover. If your expected graduation rollover date changes, you can edit the graduation rollover date anytime by navigating to the Select Users step in Clever IDM. Once you confirm that the graduation rollover date is correct, Clever IDM will continue syncing with the correct graduation years. 

 
 Preserving access 

 By default, Clever IDM will archive graduated students as soon as they’re removed from Clever. In most cases, graduated students remain in Clever until a school organization's sync is unpaused. That means, they’ll retain access to Google, Entra ID, and/or Active Directory during that period. Once the sync resumes, these users will then lose access. If you’d like graduated users to retain access beyond your unpause date, you can:

 • Set Clever IDM to not suspend or deactivate users in ignored OU in the OUs setup step (see below), and
• Move those graduated students to an ignored OU. Once in an ignored OUs, these users will retain access until you move them out of an ignored OU or manually deactivate them.
 
 If you’d like graduated users not to retain access beyond your unpause date, you can either remove them from your Clever sync before you pause, or move them to an ignored OU in Google or Active Directory and manually deactivate them there. If you have any questions about summer rollover with Clever IDM that haven’t been answered here, please reach out to Support.Clever.com .

 
 Related articles: Clever IDM: Overview Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup Clever IDM: Purchasing & Billing Clever IDM: Sync Reports Clever IDM: Pausing Syncs

title: For Clever Admins: Field mappings and customizing your data sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing field mappings 3. Editing field mappings 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Sync field mappings refer to the relationship between fields from a student information system (SIS) and fields in Clever. A field mapping is when you specify the field in your SIS that you want to map to a specific data value in Clever. For example, Clever accepts student_email as a data value. Depending on the field (e.g., emailaddress.emailaddress, SchoolEmail, etc.) in which you store emails in your SIS, you may want to map a specific field to the student_email field.

 For SFTP syncs , Clever has an SFTP web upload feature with field mapping! Find out more here . 

 

 
 Viewing field mappings

 To view sync mappings, navigate to Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings . This page shows the relationship between each Clever Field & SIS Field.

 Clever Field ¹ 

 Clever Field refers to the data field in Clever where the SIS data is being stored.

 SIS Field ² 

 SIS Field refers to the name of the field in the SIS where the data is being pulled from. In the below screenshot, we can see that for students, School_id is being synced from HomeSchoolId³. If the SIS Field is followed by (custom mapping) ⁴, it means the mapping was added custom for your account. If the SIS field is blank⁵, Clever is not pulling any information from the SIS for the corresponding Clever Field.

 Actions ⁶ 

 Edit⁷ indicates that Clever Admins may customize which SIS field to sync to the corresponding Clever Field. If the value you are looking for is unavailable from the dropdown, please contact Clever Support with the exact name of the SIS field you want to map, and our team will investigate whether the mapping is supported. If the Action field is blank⁸, the SIS Field either is not customizable or requires a Clever Support team member to customize the field for your account. Contact Clever Support to learn about your available options.

 Record Types ⁹ 

 Use the view record type selectors to view the mappings for each record type. If you require a field mapping for a record type not listed, please contact Clever Support to learn about your options. 

 

 
 Editing field mappings

 Depending on your sync type, you may be able to customize which field in your SIS correlates to which field in Clever through a field mapping. If you utilize an SFTP sync, please see this article for more information on field mapping.

 If you utilize a Clever-managed auto sync, you may be able to complete basic field mappings yourself via your Clever Dashboard, or you can reach out to Clever Support, and our team can assist with more complex field mappings.

 

 For the following auto-syncs, some custom fields can be added and customized via your Clever Dashboard for self-service field mapping:

 • 
 Illuminate Auto Sync

• 
 PowerSchool Plugin Sync 

• 
 SchoolInsight Auto Sync 

• 
 Skyward LMS API 

 
 For the following syncs, custom fields can be added and customized by reaching out to Clever support:

 • 
 Aeries Auto Sync 

• 
 Blackbaud API sync 

• 
 Infinite Campus API sync 

• 
 Skyward Auto Sync 

• 
 Skyward OneRoster (Qmlativ) API 

 
 If you utilize one of the syncs above that supports self-service mappings and want to determine if you can edit a field mapping yourself, navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page, locate the Sync Mappings box, and click on the View field mappings button .

 

 

 

 If you see an Edit button in the actions column, you can customize the field mapping for a specific data type (schools, teachers, students, sections, enrollments).

 Click on Edit to view a dropdown of available SIS Fields to map from.

 

 

 

 If you do not see the SIS field you wish to map from in the dropdown, please contact Clever Support to see if the field is available for mapping by our team.

 If the field is available, please select it and then click Update . This will save the field mapping, and the data will be pulled from the new field the next time your sync runs. You can push a manual sync following these instructions .

 Depending on the percentage of changes, this may cause a sync hold . Please review the hold carefully via your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page . If the changes are intended, you can approve them and the new data will sync to Clever.

 
 

 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Which sync types support self-service mappings? 

 • 
 Illuminate Auto Sync

• 
 PowerSchool Plugin Sync 

• 
 SchoolInsight Auto Sync 

• 
 Skyward LMS API 

 
 What if I do not see an edit button on the Field Mappings page? 

 • 
 You cannot complete a self-service field mapping for this field. Please contact Clever Support for additional information.

 
 What if I do not see the SIS field I want to map from? 

 • 
 Please contact Clever Support for additional information.

 
 What if I accidentally chose the wrong field for my mapping? 

 • 
 If your sync is on hold, discard the hold, then navigate back to your field mappings page and edit the mapping.

• 
 If your sync is not on hold or you already approved the hold, navigate to your field mappings page and edit the mapping. If more than 24 hours have passed since you completed the mapping, you may want to notify users or application partners that there may have been accidental data changes.

title: Clever Professional Development & Training
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Training options 3. Clever Professional Development offerings 

 

 Overview

 The world of digital learning is changing. With 8 in 10 teachers using more technology now than before the pandemic, it’s growing more complex for district IT administrators to manage technology and train teachers. As a partner in helping you simplify digital learning, we’re also here to help you simplify teacher and school tech lead training. We offer incredible free training options as well as customized Professional Development for school districts to ensure that your users are set up for success. 

 
 Training options

 Clever Academy

 Clever Academy is our free online training and certification program for educators. You can access it through your Clever Portal or at clever.academy . Go through all the courses and after successful completion, you’ll become a certified Clever Champion.

 District-led Training

 Lead your own training with help from our free Clever Trainers program .

 Clever Professional Development

 Let Clever take the work off your plate by delivering customized training tailored to your school district’s needs! Clever offers in-depth, 90-minute trainings for teachers and school tech leads. Learn more about the offering and pricing here . 

 
 Clever Professional Development offerings

 Customizable virtual teacher and school tech lead training for districts that desire training beyond Clever Academy and the Clever Trainers program. 

 Trainings are: 

 Created by and for educators: Our trainings are developed by former teachers. Trainings are designed with best practices straight from the field to support every learner's development. Tap the technical experts: Learn from former Clever support specialists that have worked to solve technical issues for thousands of districts, teachers, and tech coaches. Certified professional development: Trainees receive certificate of completion, which your district can count towards professional development time.

 Trainings include:

 • Live, interactive virtual training (90 minutes)
• Customized to meet your district’s needs 
• Live Q&A and Troubleshooting support 
• Follow-up emails with additional tips 
• Access to Clever Resource Hub 
• Certificates of completion 
• Access to online recording (30 days) 
 
 How to learn more about Clever-led Training

 Please use this link to learn how we can set your school district up for success through Clever Professional Development. We can work together to customize your training sessions to best meet your district’s needs!

title: Setting up Single Sign-On (SSO) for HMH
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. HMH SAML Applications 3. Setup Instructions 4. Common Troubleshooting Help 

 

 Please note: We are in the process of updating this article, and some information may be out-of-date. We apologize for any inconvenience and are working to resolve this as quickly as possible. 

 Overview

 Clever has multiple different application types which determine how you should set up your applications. This article describes how to set up various different HMH SAML applications.

 
 HMH SAML Applications

 The following directions are for configuring Single Sign-On (SSO) for these HMH products: 

 • HMH ThinkCentral 
• HMH MyHRW 
• HMH Ed Learning Platform 
• HMH SAML 
• Heinemann Flight SSO 
 
 These are the directions that should be used if your HMH application displays the status: Incomplete SAML Setup 

 

 Please note: These steps will need to be followed for each of your HMH applications. If you're looking to set up rostering for these products, you'll also need to follow the directions here . 

 

 
 Setup

 1. In the Clever Dashboard, navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications . Search for the desired application and select Request application . It is optional to set your sharing at this point.

 

 2. On the application’s page, click Settings . Under SAML Details , copy the Metadata URL. 

 

 3. In a new browser tab, log in to your HMH Admin account. From the left-hand navigation, select Roster. Under Roster information, click on Roster Actions and select Manage SSO Configuration. 

 

 4. Click Add SAML. 

 

 5. Paste the Metadata URL from Step 2 and give the SSO Configuration a name (e.g. “Clever”). Click Add and wait for HMH to complete the setup (~10 seconds).

 

 6. Copy the SSO endpoint for the application you are setting up in Clever.

 
 Clever App

 
 HMH SSO Endpoint

 
 HMH ThinkCentral ThinkCentral (grades PK-8) SSO Endpoint 
 HMH myHRW Holt McDoughal Online (Grades 6-12) SSO Endpoint 
 HMH Ed Learning Platform HMH Ed SSO Endpoint 
 HMH SAM Student Achievement Manager (SAM) 
 Heinemann Flight Flight 
 
 

 

 7. Return to the browser tab containing the Clever Dashboard. On the application’s settings tab, find the section titled SAML Details and select Edit .

 

 8. Paste the SSO endpoint from Step 6 into the textbox titled SSO Endpoint 

 

 

 Please note: For Heinemann Flight, the textbox for the SSO Endpoint is titled "Login URL." For this application, please put the SSO Endpoint from Step 6 in the Login URL box: 

 

 

 9. Return to the HMH Admin dashboard . Copy the HMH Metadata URL and enter it in your SAML app settings. Click Parse Metadata, which will automatically populate the Asset URL and Encryption Certificate, and then Confirm. 

 

 10. Your SAML connection is pre-configured with a set of default attributes that should work for most SSO configurations with HMH. If necessary, you can change the mappings here at any time. Otherwise, click Confirm. 

 

 11. If you have not already, set your sharing permissions and verify the configuration with a test user! If you need to configure any other HMH apps, you can do so by copying the relevant SSO Endpoint from HMH, and re-using the same HMH Metadata URL from the Clever app you just configured.

 

 
 Common Troubleshooting Tips

 Below are some common pitfalls in setting up HMH SAML Connections through Clever. Please use these if you are running into issues:

 • Incorrect SAML configuration 
 • If a user sees a Clever login error screen after launching an HMH SAML application the application is most likely set up with the SAML information for another Clever application (possibly one the school had been connected to previously). The resolution for this issue is to delete the SAML connection in the HMH admin dashboard and create a new one with the metadata link from the application. 
 

 
 • Encryption Certificate Issues 
 • Sometimes, the Encryption Certificate is pasted into the SAML Details with extra spaces. This will lead to an “Unknown error occurred” message for the user. To resolve this issue, check for any spaces in the Encryption Certificate field (a valid certificate will not have any spaces).

title: For Clever Admins: Copying Sharing Rules
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I copy sharing settings from another application? 3. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 By configuring sharing permissions for applications, district admins can determine what data will be shared with each district app, and which district users will be able to access the application from their individual Clever portals. If you’ve already set up sharing for an application and need to use the same sharing settings for another application, you can copy the settings over to simplify your setup process. When you use copy sharing, Clever will take the sharing settings from the source application and apply them to the new application. This is a one-time copy that allows you to then update each application’s sharing settings independently.

 Clever Tip: For an overview of application sharing permissions, please visit: Sharing Permissions: Sharing data with new applications . 

 

 
 How do I copy sharing settings from another application?

 When selecting a sharing type, choose the “Copy Sharing” option. Next, choose which existing application’s sharing settings you’d like to copy. Once you’ve selected a source application, you’ll see a preview of the sharing settings that will be applied. If the settings are correct, click “Apply settings.” By configuring sharing permissions for applications, district admins can determine what data will be shared with each district app, and which district users will be able to access the application from their individual Clever portals. When sharing by rules, Clever will only share data that meets the specific criteria you've set. This is the most recommended sharing type as it provides districts with the flexibility in determining specifically what data should be included, or excluded, in the sharing. Additionally, applications are dynamically updated as new data is synced to Clever from your district's student information system (SIS). The data shared with the application via sharing rules evolves based on the new data in Clever. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 If I copy sharing settings, then change the sharing settings for the source application, will the updated sharing settings be applied to the second application?

 • Since this is a one-time copy, you’d need to update the sharing settings for the second application separately.

title: Clever IDs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Changing Clever IDs 3. Clever ID Retention Policy 

 Clever ID refers to the globally unique identifier that is assigned to a record in Clever. This value is important as it ensures that every student, teacher, section, etc. on Clever has a unique ID across all active schools on Clever. Since it is globally unique, most application partners use the Clever ID as the primary identifier for a record when pulling data from Clever. Clever IDs are an alpha-numeric string that is generated by Clever and can be viewed on the profile page for a record. Clever IDs are directly connected to the following primary ids provided by the district to Clever. For more information on Clever's field specifications, please see this document . 
 
 Record Type

 
 Primary ID Field

 
 School School_id 
 Staff Staff_id 
 Teacher Teacher_id 
 Student Student_id 
 Section Section_id 
 

 

 
 

 Changing Clever IDs

 Changes in Clever IDs are the result of the primary id field for the record being changed. If the primary id field is changed for a record, a new Clever ID will be provisioned. For example: IF Student A is uploaded from the SIS to Clever with Student_id: 1234 . THEN A new student record with Student_id: 1234 / Clever ID: 53446ae050bb279fb03d2a3e is created. IF Student A is updated in the SIS and uploaded from the SIS to Clever with Student_id: 5678 . THEN Student record with Student_id: 1234 / Clever ID: 53446ae050bb279fb03d2a3e is deleted. A new student record with Student_id: 5678 / Clever ID: 54d263946cc968010000007a is created. 

 
 Clever ID Retention Policy

 Clever retains the relationship between a primary id and Clever ID for 300 days. For example, if a student is deleted from Clever and added back within 300 days, the student will be assigned the same Clever ID. If a student is deleted from Clever and added back after 300 days, the student will be assigned a NEW Clever ID. Since applications build off the Clever ID, students that are added back within 300 days will be restored with their historical data (progress, scores, etc.) Students that are added back after 300 days will have a new account provisioned in the application and will have no historical data. If your district has a student population that regularly cycles between enrollment and withdrawal, Clever can extend this 300-day window to one that will preserve Clever IDs for longer. (i.e. 730 days). If interested in extending your Clever ID retention policy, please submit a support request at support.clever.com .

title: Clever + Benchmark Education Company Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following information to help your district get set up with Clever and Benchmark: 1. Overview 2. Benchmark Education requirements 3. Adding Benchmark Education 4. Setting up your Benchmark Education sharing rules 5. Sharing staff 6. Next steps 

 Overview 

 Benchmark’s award-winning print and digital solutions feature content-rich leveled texts in English and Spanish; explicit instruction in close reading and analysis, genre, and cross-text study; and a wide array of differentiated resources that enable educators to target literacy intervention. Since Clever and Benchmark began their partnership in 2013, it’s easier than ever for students and teachers to access their learning resources. 

 Clever Tip: If you’re interested in learning more about Clever, such as using our SSO Portal and connecting other applications through our dashboard, you can learn a lot more here in our Help Center or by visiting Clever Academy. 

 

 IMPORTANT: Please read before proceeding with the setup process. 

 Communication: Setting up a Clever integration requires a conversation with Benchmark Education Company. After requesting Benchmark through Clever, please make sure to email or schedule a call with Benchmark with any questions. Switching rostering methods: If you’re moving to Clever from self-registered accounts, please be ready to work with Benchmark on a user clean-up process. The Benchmark team can work with you to map accounts and avoid losing historical data. Please contact the Benchmark team before making any changes. Data readiness: Your district should be sharing with Benchmark only the subjects and grade levels specified below in the “Benchmark Education requirements - overview” section. Please be sure to only share schools and users with Benchmark subscriptions. 

 Benchmark Education requirements - overview 

 Here are the requirements and recommendations for sharing data with Benchmark. Please note: school site must be shared!

 

 • Subjects:
 • Please only share the relevant sections from any of the following subjects with Benchmark Education:
 • English/Language Arts
• Language
• Homeroom/Advisory
• Social Studies
• Other
 

• Please exclude the following subjects:
 • PE and Health
• Art and Music
• Math
• Science
• Technology and Engineering
 

 

• Grades:
 • Typical grades using Benchmark Education are K-5 but it can be used across preK-12. Please check in with your curriculum purchaser to determine who should have access.
 

• Student data must include the following:
 • Grade level
• Username (highly recommended)
 

• Teachers:
 • Email address (recommended)
 

• Sections:
 • Should have unique names to avoid confusion
 

• Staff/Admins:
 • Unique email address 
• A user cannot be shared as both a teacher and a staff member - one role only can be shared 
 

 
 For an overview of sharing rules, please visit: Sharing Permissions: Share by Rules . If you still need help setting up your sharing rules after reading this article, please contact Clever’s support team by clicking the message icon in the lower right corner of the page. Be sure to include “Benchmark Education Sharing Rule” in the subject line of your email. 

 Adding Benchmark Education

 To add Benchmark Education to your Clever Dashboard, please search for it under Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications . 

 Setting up your Benchmark Education sharing rules

 Using sharing rules, you can customize precisely the data you want shared with Benchmark Education. 

 IMPORTANT: Please remember to ONLY share schools and users with Benchmark subscriptions. 

 

 Example: All elementary ELA/homeroom sections and middle school ELA and Spanish sections.

 English/Language Arts and Language classes (as subscribed) should be shared with Benchmark Education. However, you may need to include other subjects to cover your entire student body. For example, elementary school students may only be enrolled in one section where all instruction occurs, and that section may have the subject listed as “Homeroom/Advisory” or “Other.” 

 How do I know how subjects synced to Clever? Check the “Sections” tab of the Data Browser ( schools.clever.com/browser ). “Subject” is one of the columns listed. 

 Step 1 : If you’ve never set up the sharing rules before, you will be able to customize them from the setup guide (pictured). Alternatively, click Edit sharing from the Benchmark Education overview page to edit rules you’ve previously set up. Step 2 : You will likely need to set up multiple rules. We recommend Share by Section Step 3 : Set up your filters to include only the data you want to share. For example, if your elementary school students are only enrolled in one section where all instruction occurs, and that section has “Homeroom/Advisory” listed as the subject, you can include filters for the school, grades, and subject. Click Done editing once you are finished adding filters.

 Step 4 (optional) : Add additional rules to capture different users. For example, your middle school or junior high students may be enrolled in sections where the subject is listed as “English/Language Arts” and “Language.” You can create another rule to broaden your sharing to include elementary students where the section’s subject equals Homeroom/Advisory OR middle school students where the section’s subject equals English/Language Arts or Language. From here, you can again set up a rule with filters to include the new group of users. After creating each rule, select Done editing to add an additional rule or Save your changes. Step 5 : Once you’ve captured the appropriate users, click Save Sharing Rules . 

 Sharing staff

 If you have non-instructional staff (users listed in the “Staff” tab in your Data Browser ) who also need access to Benchmark Education, they will not be included in a sections sharing rule. You will need to set up an additional rule to capture these users.

 • Click Edit Sharing Rules 
• Click Add Another Rule 
• Click Custom Share Staff 
 
 We recommend taking a look at your list of staff to determine who will need access rather than sharing all staff. Once you have the list of staff members ready, you can share them by an identifier such as email, ID, or title. As always, don’t forget to click Done editing and Save Sharing Rules before leaving the page! 

 Please note: Clever Admins are shared by default with all connected learning applications. Staff, Teachers, and Students must be explicitly shared with each application in order for data to be shared for each account type. 

 

 Next steps

 Data errors or warnings will show up here on your Clever Dashboard. 

 This most likely means that one of the fields required by Benchmark Education is missing (for example, student grade). This will need to be resolved in your SIS data or CSV uploads to Clever. If you need to change your sharing rules in the future, you can always do so by clicking “Sharing by rules” on the My Applications page or “Edit sharing” on the Benchmark Education overview page .

title: Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup • Connect to Active Directory • Select your IDM Management Level • Select Clever users • Data written to Active Directory • Manage group membership • Summary • Preview and provision • Events 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 Clever IDM helps districts automate account provisioning and lifecycle management for Microsoft Active Directory and Google Workspace. This affordable new offering provides school districts with a simple workflow that can be completed in minutes to automate organizational unit (OU) and account management directly with Clever. Clever IDM automatically syncs users from your SIS to Active Directory or Google daily for an entire year. Your Clever IDM dashboard can be found here . A full overview of Clever IDM and the setup process can be viewed below:

 
 

 

 Note: Clever IDM does not yet support home folder provisioning or exchange mailbox provisioning. If you’re interested in this functionality, we’d love to hear from you! Please reach out to Clever Support. 

 
 Setup

 You can work through setting up your IDM configuration at any time; however, you won’t be able to complete the setup to start provisioning accounts without review from an onboarding specialist or our support team. Our team must review your setup before your first provision to protect your destination accounts and prevent issues with SSO for your end users. Contact your onboarding specialist for configuration review. If you're not sure who to contact, reach out to Clever Support .

 Click on the following links to find information about: • Setting IDM login credentials • Organizing OUs • Building sub-OUs with the IDM format builder 

 Connect to Active Directory 

 To provision accounts, Clever needs permission to connect to your Active Directory server. To establish a successful connection with Clever, you will need to provide the following information for your Active Directory server:

 • LDAPS URL (your server’s external IP address or domain name after “ldaps://”)
• SHA-256 fingerprints of SSL certificates
• Base Distinguished Name (Base DN)
• Username and password for a bind account with:
 • Read/write access to users and organizational units
• Replicating directory changes permissions
 

 
 In addition to providing the information above, if you use a firewall please give access to the Clever external IP addresses ( 54.241.134.131, 50.18.217.135, 54.241.154.41 ) on port 636 to ensure a successful connection with your server. Also please ensure your firewall reroutes Clever to a domain controller for your Active Directory with write access . For a detailed, step-by-step guide on connecting and troubleshooting your connection to Active Directory, see Clever IDM: Connecting to Active Directory via LDAPS .

 

 
 

 Select your IDM Management Level 

 

 Next, choose whether you’d like to set up Full Provisioning and Password Management or Password Management Only . Both options support Identity Management in Clever—it just depends on what works best for your district’s timing and goals.

 

 Full Provisioning and Password Management 

 This option automates account security and password management through Clever IDM. Since it requires more setup time, many districts choose this path at the beginning of the school year or during a long break. It’s best for:

 • 
 Automatically provisioning, updating, and archiving user accounts

• 
 Configuring password reset, account recovery, and account claiming settings

• 
 Building and maintaining OUs and groups from your SIS data

 
 

 Password Management Only 

 This lighter setup gives you access to Clever’s secure password management tools for matched users, with the flexibility to configure it at your own pace.

 It includes the same powerful IDM matching and collision detection as the full setup to ensure the right Clever user is matched to the right destination user—but requires less time to implement. It’s best for:

 • 
 Piloting Clever IDM or starting setup mid-year

• 
 Accessing Clever’s password management tools

• 
 Managing matched users between systems

 
 

 
 Select Clever Users 

 Clever IDM can provision accounts for students, teachers, and staff. You may select for Clever to provision accounts for one or more of the following: Students Students are users that are enrolled as students in educational courses or sections. Teachers "Teachers" is the term Clever uses to refer to a district's instructional staff. These are the individuals that are directly managing a classroom and students. Staff "Staff" is the term Clever uses to refer to both non-instructional staff (e.g. secretaries, bus drivers, counselors) and instructional staff that are not directly in charge of a classroom (e.g. school tech leads, instructional coaches, and principals). These users are often a different employment designation than teachers and may even be managed outside the Student Information System (SIS) in a HR Information System (HRIS) 

 Users with multiple user roles If a user is assigned to multiple user roles (e.g. Teacher and Staff) in Clever, they will be provisioned an Active Directory account if either role is selected for Clever IDM provisioning. If the district chooses to provision accounts for both teachers and staff and a user is assigned to both teacher and staff roles in Clever, during Clever IDM setup the district will be able to select the primary role to be used for OU and group structure. Users with existing Active Directory accounts Users with the same email address in Clever and Active Directory will be matched/organized in the OU structure as outlined in step: Organize Active Directory OUs . Clever will not update the UserPrincipalNames or sAMAccountNames for existing Active Directory accounts unless a new SIS username is synced for a user. To ensure accurate matching, please make sure that usernames are synced to Clever from your student information system (SIS) and that the usernames match your UserPrincipalNames in AD. 

 

 Additionally, Clever IDM allows you to customize which subsets of users that you would like to provision. If you would like to filter by user type, grade, school, or extension field please contact Clever Support so that we can make those adjustments for you.

 

 Graduation rollover date If Clever IDM is set to manage your students, you will be prompted during this step to configure a graduation rollover date. 

 

 If you do not sync student graduation year directly from your SIS, Clever IDM will use this rollover date to calculate a graduation year for use in formulas. To ensure that this calculation associates your data with the correct school year, set a graduation rollover date. Clever IDM calculates the graduation year based on the student’s synced grade level. Data synced before the graduation rollover date will be associated with the previous school year for graduation year calculations. Data synced after the graduation rollover date will be associated with the upcoming school year for graduation year calculations. Providing a graduation rollover date enables Clever IDM to automatically update any information based on the graduation year (OUs, groups, etc.) as grade levels change during rollover. If your expected graduation rollover date changes, you can edit the graduation rollover date anytime by navigating to your Select Users step in Clever IDM.

 
 
 Data written to Active Directory 

There are two pathways for Clever to set user data in IDM: creation and user modification. 
 Creation In the case of user creation, the following fields are synced to Active Directory:

 • User first name
• User last name
• User type (student, teacher, staff)
• CommonName
• UserPrincipalName as configured by the district
• sAMAccountName as configured by the district
• Primary mail address as configured by the district
• Organizational unit as configured by the district
• Initial password as configured by the distric

title: Clever IDM: Connecting to Active Directory via LDAPS
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Connection Steps 3. Final Connection Test 4. Common Errors 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 Clever IDM uses LDAPS to establish a connection with your district’s Active Directory instance. LDAPS is different from LDAP and requires different configurations at the firewall and server levels (learn more about the differences here ). This article outlines the steps you can take to set up your LDAPS connection and can help you troubleshoot any issues you might encounter during that process. If you have any questions or feedback, email us at Clever Support. 

 
 Connection Steps

 To provision accounts, Clever needs permission to connect to your Active Directory server. To establish a successful connection with Clever, you will need to provide the following information for your Active Directory server:

 • LDAPS URL 
• SHA-256 fingerprints of AD server’s SSL certificate 
• Username and password for a bind account 
• Base Distinguished Name (Base DN) 
 
 In addition to providing the information above, if you use a firewall please give access to the Clever external IP addresses ( 54.241.134.131, 50.18.217.135, 54.241.154.41 ) on port 636 to ensure a successful connection with your server. Also please ensure your firewall reroutes Clever to a domain controller for your Active Directory with write access . 

 Providing the LDAPS URL 

 An LDAPS URL lets Clever know where to look when trying to connect with your Active Directory server. It is a URL that starts with “ldaps://” and ends with your server’s external IP address or domain name. To provide the LDAPS URL for your server:

 • Locate your server’s external IP address or domain name. We recommend using your IP address. To find your IP address do one of the following:
 • Google “What is my IP address?” -or-
• Go to the “Server Manager” application on your Windows device and navigate to “All Servers”, where you will see the IP addresses listed for all of your servers.
 

• Copy your server’s IP address.
• Paste your server’s IP address into the LDAPS URL input in step 2 of the Connect to Active Directory setup. 
 
 

 • Click “Test connection”
• If the connection to the LDAPS server is successful, click “Next step”. If there is an error connecting to the LDAPS server, please ensure you have: Entered your LDAPS URL following proper formatting (e.g. “ldaps://123.123.123.123” or “ldaps:// district.org ”) Given access through your firewall to Clever’s external IP addresses ( 54.241.134.131, 50.18.217.135, 54.241.154.41 ) over port 636 and try again. Also please ensure your firewall reroutes Clever to a domain controller for your Active Directory with write access .
 
 

 Providing the SHA-256 fingerprints of your server’s SSL certificate Your server’s SSL certificate establishes a secure, encrypted connection between Clever and your Active Directory server, which keeps your data safe. The fingerprint is a hash of your SSL certificate, which safely stores all of the certificate’s data. 

 To provide the SHA-256 fingerprints of your server’s SSL certificate:

 • Click “Add fingerprint” in step 3 of the Connect to Active Directory step. If you already have a fingerprint, you'll need to remove it and add a new one.
• Clever will automatically download the SHA-256 fingerprints of your server’s SSL certificate. Click “Add fingerprint” once your fingerprints have been downloaded.
• You will now see the added fingerprint in the “Certificate fingerprints” table. If you would like to add additional certificate fingerprints, contact Clever Support.
 
 

 

 Providing a bind account username and password with read and write access The bind account username and password are used to request authentication when performing searches on your Active Directory server. Clever needs access to a bind account with read and write permissions to successfully provision accounts. 

 To apply the necessary permissions on your bind account:

 • Go to the “Active Directory Users and Computers” application on your Windows device and select your server’s top level domain.
• Click the “Action” menu then click “Properties”.
• Click the “Security” tab in the Properties window.
• If you don’t see your bind account user listed in the “Group or user names” menu, click “Add…” If you do see your bind account user listed, skip to step 6.
• Type the name of your bind account user and click “OK”. In this case, we have named our bind account “Bind Account”, but yours may be named something else.
• You should now see your bind account selected at the top of the “Group or user names” menu.
• Click “Advanced” to open the advanced security settings window.
• In the “Permission entries” menu, double-click your bind account user.
• In the “Permissions” menu, click the checkbox next to:
 • List contents
• Read all properties
• Write all properties
• Read permissions
• All validated writes
• All extended rights
• Create all child objects
• Delete all child objects
• Delete
 

• Change the Applies to to be This object and all descendant objects. 
 
 NOTE: Once you have applied the necessary permissions to your bind account, please ensure your server configuration allows Clever’s simple bind authentication method. Learn more about allowing simple bind authentication here .

 

 To provide your bind account username and password: 

 • Go to the “Active Directory Users and Computers” application on your Windows device and select your bind account user (this is typically found in the “Users” or “Administrators” OUs).
• Click the “Action” menu and select “Properties”
• Click the “Account” tab
• Write the full expression of the “User logon name” in the “Bind account username” field in step 4 of the Connect to Active Directory step. In this case, the Bind account username is “ bind-account@test-ad.clever-idm.com ”.
• Write your bind account password in the “Bind account password” field. Passwords in Active Directory are stored using encrypted hashes and the method of storing passwords will be specific to your server configuration. If you don’t know your bind account password, please contact your network administrator or read this article to learn more about how passwords are stored in Active Directory.
 
 

 

 Providing the Base Distinguished Name (Base DN) The Base DN is the starting point when performing a search on your Active Directory server. Clever will use the Base DN to navigate your server’s data. 

 To provide the Base DN for your server:

 • Go to the “Active Directory Users and Computers” application on your Windows device and select your server’s top level domain.
• Click the “Action” menu and select “Properties”
• Click the “Attribute Editor” tab
• Scroll down to the “distinguishedName” attribute
• Write the “distinguishedName” value in the “Base DN” field in step 5 of the Connect to Active Directory step. In this case, the Base DN is “DC=test-ad,DC=clever-idm,DC=com”
 
 NOTE : Your Base DN should be the same domain used in your bind account username, but written in Domain Controller, or “DC”, format. In this case, the domain used in the bind account username was “@ test-ad.clever-idm.com ” so the DC format for the Base DN would be “DC=test-ad,DC=clever-idm,DC=com”. 

 

 

 
 Final Connection Test

 Once you have completed all of the connection steps, you will be asked to run a final connection test to ensure everything is working as expected. If the test is successful, you can save your Active Directory connection configuration and proceed to the next step: Select users. If you encounter an error during the final connection test, the error message will explain what has happened and redirect you to the specific part of the connection step that needs attention. 

 
 Common Errors

 Here are three common errors that might come up when Connecting to Active Directory via LDAPS:

 • LDAPS URL failing to connect 
• Bind account authentication was unsuccessful 
• Could not perform a search with the Base DN 
• Connecti

title: Clever + Google Classroom: Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Integration Overview 2. Requirements 3. Teacher experience 4. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 The Google Classroom roster import application allows admins in the U.S. and Canadian school districts with a Google Standard Edition subscription or a Google Workspace for Education Plus subscription for staff and students to automatically create classes, populate, and sync rosters directly to Classroom from their Student Information System (SIS) through Clever Secure Sync. Learn more below about the integration and setup requirements.

 Integration Overview

 The Google Classroom roster import application is available to request in the Clever application gallery ! 

 • The integration is available at no additional cost to U.S. and Canadian school districts with a Google Standard Edition subscription or a Google Workspace for Education Plus subscription for staff and students.
• Once the application is connected, and the scheduled sync start date has arrived, Clever will provision Google Classroom courses based on your district’s SIS + Clever data. 
• Google Classroom course names will match to Clever section names, and rosters will update automatically as students change sections in your SIS + Clever.
 
 The Google Classroom roster import application will: 

 Create Classroom courses and enroll students and teachers based on Clever section data Automatically update rosters based on changes in Clever Allow teachers to manually add and remove students and co-teachers to Clever-created courses Allow teachers to rename Clever-created courses Allow teachers to accept or reject Clever-created courses Add co-teachers to Clever-created courses Allow district admins to customize access dates for teachers and students to ensure students don’t see rosters prematurely

 Allow district admins to bulk archive Google Classroom courses based on the creation date for courses made by Clever.

 The application will NOT: 

 Create, modify, or delete user accounts Modify courses that teachers manually create in Classroom Write Classroom grades back to your district’s SIS Overwrite any changes that teachers make to their courses Sync Guardian info to Classroom or modify/overwrite any Guardian data

 

 If you are already the district administrator for a Clever account, you can request Google Classroom Roster Import from your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications . Districts who do not already use Clever and are interested in Classroom rostering can create a Clever account here and then request the application.

 

 Requirements

 • Districts must have a Google Standard Edition or Google Workspace for Education Plus subscription for staff and students in order to utilize the Google Classroom roster import application via Clever. If your district has questions about upgrading to Education Plus, please contact na-k12-gwfe@google.com . 
 • If you’re interested in finding out about your Google Workspace subscription or for help identifying your Google Workspace Super Admin, please log into your Google admin console .
 

• Your district’s Google Workspace Super Administrator will need to help set up Google Classroom roster import.
 • For more detailed setup info, see the setup guide here . 
 

 
 

 Teacher Experience

 Teachers will have the option to accept Clever-created courses. They can also decline them and continue managing their rosters manually. Courses created outside Clever will not be modified or deleted by the Clever connection. 

 FAQs

 What will the courses be named when they sync to Google Classroom? 

 • Clever-created courses will utilize the section name in Clever as the course name in Google Classroom. You can also customize the course names directly from the Google Classroom roster import settings tab in your Clever Dashboard using fields that already exist in the data you sync to Clever. Teachers can modify this course name if they want, and their changes will not be overwritten by the sync.
 
 Can we manage when the Google Classroom roster import application will start syncing and creating courses in Classroom? 

 • Yes, during the setup, you can configure your first sync date as well as input access dates for teachers and students separately.
 
 What time will data sync when you set access dates? 

 • Clever will sync with Google Classroom roster import at 12:30 AM PST/3:30 AM EST on the access date set in the application’s settings.
 
 How often is the sync from Clever to Google Classroom? 

 • The sync runs on a nightly basis. The sync will also run after each SIS sync and after any changes to sharing permissions.
 
 Can I pause the sync between Clever and Google Classroom? 

 • Yes! If you need to pause the sync between Clever and Google Classroom, head to Clever Dashboard > Google Classroom roster import > Settings > Schedule Google Classroom Roster Import Sync Pause and set the pause dates for teachers and students.
 
 What if some of my district’s teachers want to continue manually managing their Classrooms? 

 • Teachers can decline Clever-created courses and continue manually managing their rosters. Courses created outside Clever will not be modified or deleted by the Clever connection. The Clever team can’t help troubleshoot courses that are manually managed by teachers.
 
 Do co-teachers sync with the courses? 

 • Yes, co-teachers that you have in the Clever data are synced to Google Classroom roster import. Co-teachers won’t be able to see a course until the primary teacher has accepted the course.
 
 Can I use sharing rules to control which schools or sections are rostered in Classroom? 

 • Yes! Clever will provision Classroom courses based on section data that is shared with Google Classroom rostering. You can use sharing rules to prevent certain types of sections from being synced, or to ensure that only the right schools are synced.
 
 What if the student roster of a Google Classroom course does not match the roster of its section in Clever? 

 • Teachers are able to add and remove students manually without Clever's sync overwriting those changes. Google Classroom also has a feature that allows students to unenroll themselves from courses. You can adjust unenrollment settings for Google Classroom so students cannot remove themselves from a class. Learn more here .
 
 Interested in deep-diving into how to best use Clever and Google together? Check out our course, Clever & Google: Secure Connected Classroom Certification available in Clever Academy! 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Clever + Google Classroom: Setup Guide 
• Clever + Google Classroom: Requirements & Troubleshooting 
• For Teachers: Clever + Google Classroom 
• Clever + Google Classroom: Semester Rollover

title: For Clever Admins: Section Names
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Auto-generated section names 2. Custom section names 

 

 Auto-generated section names

 Section names in Clever are auto-generated to ensure unique names, making it easier for teachers and students to recognize and use them. The auto-generated name is: "course_name" - "teacher_last_name" - "period" If course_name isn't provided, the section " name" provided in your SIS data will be used. If neither course name nor section name are provided, Clever will use ”teacher_last_name - period” ⚠️ Clever can't customize the format of auto-generated section names. 

 
 

 Custom section names

 Districts can choose to sync their own section names to Clever. Below are the directions for each sync type. Your sync type can be found on the Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync page. 

 SFTP

 • Confirm the desired section name is populated in your SIS and exportable. The custom section name must be contained in a single field . Other than our default formula, Clever can't combine multiple fields to create a custom section name. 
• Contact your SIS manager and request they update your district's sections.csv file to send the field "name", with the desired data field from your SIS. 
• Contact Clever Support and request we disable auto-generated section names for your district. 
 
 

 SFTP (sis-managed) sync:

 • Confirm the desired section name is populated in your SIS and exportable. The custom section name must be contained in a single field . Other than our default formula, Clever can't combine multiple fields to create a custom section name. 
• Contact the support team for your SIS provider and request they update your district's sections.csv file to send the field "name", with the desired data field from your SIS. 
• Contact Clever Support and request we disable auto-generated section names for your district. 
 
 

 Clever-managed sync:

 • Confirm the desired section name is populated in your SIS and exportable. The custom section name must be contained in a single field . Other than our default formula, Clever can't combine multiple fields to create a custom section name. 
• Contact Clever Support and provide the name of the field as it appears in your SIS. Our team will customize the sync to pull the manual section names.

title: For Clever Admins: Locate your District ID
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Locating your account Clever ID 

 

 Overview

 

 Every district account in Clever has a unique Clever ID. Application partners may ask you for this ID to help their team locate your account in their system or for troubleshooting. Read below for step-by-step guidance on finding your account's Clever ID in your Clever Dashboard. 

 

 
 Locating your account Clever ID

 

 1. Log in to the Clever Dashboard: https://schools.clever.com 2. Click on Data browser on the left hand sidebar. 3. Your District ID is up at the top!

title: SFTP Syncs: Viewing Clever CSV files
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Downloading SIS files from your Clever Dashboard 2. Downloading SIS files using an FTP client 

 

 Overview

 This article is for school organizations that use an SFTP sync or SIS-Managed Auto Sync who want to view the CSV files being exported from their student information system (SIS) to Clever. Your sync type can be found at the top of the Sync page in the Clever Dashboard. There are two ways to view the data:

 • Downloading the files via the Last Attempted Sync page of your Clever Dashboard 
• Using an FTP client (like Filezilla, shown below). 
 
 

 
 Downloading SIS files from your Clever Dashboard

 Head to your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page. On the right side of the table, you'll see a Download SIS File column. Click the download icon for any file(s) you'd like. After clicking the download button, a download link for the file(s) will be emailed to the address connected to your Clever account. Clicking the Sections download icon will email a link for both the sections.csv and enrollments.csv files.

 Please note, for security purposes, you will need to be logged into the Clever Dashboard to use the download link. 

 
 Downloading SIS files using an FTP client

 Open the FTP client (like Filezilla , which is what this example uses) and enter the directory details found on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page. Click Quickconnect. Clever Sync > Settings page Filezilla application 

 

 Once you're connected, you'll see the CSV files in your Clever SFTP directory on the right side (labeled Remote site). On the left side (labeled Local site), select where you'd like to download the files. We recommend your Desktop or Downloads folder for easy access. Once selected, right-click on the files you would like to download and click Download . The files will immediately download to the local site selected. The files are now available for you to review on your computer!

 For a clickable tutorial on accessing your remote site via an FTP Client, click here . 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • SFTP Syncs: How do I format my CSV files? 
• SFTP Syncs: Troubleshooting CSV formatting errors 
• SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever

title: For Clever Admins: Renew with Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Who should answer this question? 3. Why does Clever ask if we're renewing? 4. What does it mean if I say yes to using Clever? 5. What if I only plan to use Clever for some schools in my school organization? 6. What happens when I select applications I want to connect with for the upcoming year? 7. When should I answer? 8. Paid renewals 

 Overview

 For the past few years, we’ve asked schools whether they plan to use Clever Portal with a simple question at the top of their Clever Dashboard. This year, you’ll find the question located in your tasks on the right side of your dashboard!

 

 

 

 

 

 This article describes why we ask this question, who can answer it, and what will happen when you select your answer. 

 
 Who should answer this question?

 Only users assigned to the role “Clever Admin” can answer this question. No other users (curriculum leads, help desk, and School Tech Leads) can answer at this time. Whether you are a Clever Admin or not, if you don’t think you’re the right person to answer this question, please start the conversation with your team and let us know so that we can best support you this year. 

 
 Why does Clever ask if we're renewing?

 Our goal is to better understand how our school organizations plan to use Clever. When we understand who will use it, we can provide: 

 • New teacher training resources
• Support connecting with applications that you want to roster on Clever
 
 

 Please note: You cannot renew your Clever IDM or Classroom MFA subscription via this form. If you would like to renew your subscription, please visit schools.clever.com/orders . 

 

 
 What does it mean if I say yes to using Clever?

 If you say yes to using Clever Portal , it means you plan to use Clever Portal in your school organization for students, teachers, and/or staff to access resources and applications. If you say yes to using Clever for rostering , it means you plan to use Clever to roster applications for students, teachers, and/or staff to access resources and applications.

 

 
 What if I only plan to use Clever for some schools in my school organization?

 Please still answer “Yes”! We want you to have the resources you need to help those schools use Clever. 

 
 What happens when I select applications I want to connect with for the upcoming year?

 When you select applications, we’ll coordinate with those application providers to send your school organization an invitation to connect.

 You can also request an application at any time. If you don’t have a contract with the application yet, contact the application provider directly to get started.

 
 When should I answer?

 The sooner you answer, the better we’ll be able to provide you with training resources in advance of your strategic planning for the upcoming school year.

 

 
 Paid Renewals

 The renewal form on the right side of your dashboard is only intended to help us understand how you plan to use Clever Portal and Clever rostering in the next school year, and it is not a place to renew any paid subscriptions. If your school organization has a Clever IDM and/or Classroom MFA subscription that you would like to extend, please visit: schools.clever.com/orders.

title: For App Partners: Learn about Clever Academy
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever Academy for App Partners 3. How can I view my courses or certifications? 

 

 Overview

 Clever Academy is our free online training and certification program for Clever users. App Partners can access Clever Academy via the App Dashboard ( https://apps.clever.com/ ). Meanwhile, District Administrators and School Tech Leads can access it through their Clever Dashboards and teachers can access it through their Clever Portal. Alternatively, users can go directly to Clever Academy: https://clever.academy 

 By completing courses, you can learn how to get the most out of Clever for you and your districts. In addition to your own course (Clever Academy for App Partners), you also have access to all District Admin, School Tech Lead, and teacher content.

 

 

 
 Learning topics for Application Partners

 Level 1: Intro to Clever & getting started (for Application Partners) 

 In this course you'll learn what Clever is and the basics of how you and school organizations use Clever to simplify setup, maintenance, and troubleshooting.

 Level 2: Clever Dashboard deep dive (for Application Partners) 

 In this course you'll take a deeper dive into your Clever Dashboard learning about more advanced features and how to utilize them effectively to increase efficiency for your teams. 

 

 When you complete these courses, you'll become a Certified Clever Champion. You'll earn a badge and certificate so you can show off your accomplishment!

 For directions and information about the Clever Academy specifically for teachers and School Tech Leads (STLs), see these articles: For Teachers: How can I learn more about Clever? (Clever Academy) For School Tech Leads: Clever Academy - Learn more about Clever! For Clever Admin: Clever Academy Course Catalog - Learn more about Clever! 

 

 
 How can I view my courses or certifications?

 You can see the courses you've registered for or completed at any time by navigating to Clever Academy , clicking person icon in the top right, and selecting My Profile. From here, you'll see a list of all your learning paths and courses. Any course with a certification that you've completed will have a View certificate link next to it. Click the link to view the certificate, and then print it if you'd like! Certificates are valid for two years. If it has been more than two years since you completed a course, you would need to re-take the course to become re-certified.

title: PowerSchool Auto Sync: Student Emails
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. If student emails are stored in a field exportable via DDE 3. If student emails are stored on the Email Configuration page - Troubleshooting email deletions 4. Other options 

 Overview

 For the PowerSchool Auto Sync, student email addresses are not synced by default, but they can be synced using the following methods. However, districts have 3 options to sync student email addresses, depending on where they are housed in PowerSchool. District Admins must reach out to Clever Support and request emails to be synced using one of these options.

 

 Please Note: • This sync type is no longer available for new setups. If new to Clever or updating your configuration to pull data from PowerSchool, please reference our article PowerSchool Plugin Sync. • If your schools use ESchoolPLUS as your SIS, please see our article on SFTP Syncs . This article is intended for districts using PowerSchool as their SIS.

 To review student email data currently in Clever, District Admins can navigate to the Clever Dashboard > Data browser > Students tab and add a filter for students with an email that contains @. 

 

 
 If student emails are stored in a field exportable via DDE

 If your student emails are stored in a field that is exportable by Direct Database Export (DDE), contact Clever Support with the exact field name as it appears in the DDE. Our team can assist with updating your sync to pull student emails from this location. To determine if the field is exportable via DDE, please follow these directions. 

 • In PowerSchool, navigate to System > Data Management > Direct Database Export (DDE) 
• Select Current Table: Students 
• Click Select all records in this table 
• Click Export Records 
• Review the available fields in the dropdown. If you find the field, please provide the exact name of the field (including capitalization and punctuation) to Clever Support. If you cannot locate the field, it is not exportable via DDE.
 
 

 
 If student emails are stored on the Email Configuration page

 If student emails are stored on the Email configuration page in PowerSchool, Clever can pull this data with additional PowerSchool permissions. The Email configuration page looks similar to this:

 In order to sync your student email addresses, we'll need you to enable page level-permissions for Quick Export. 

 1. Navigate to System > Security > Access to Page Permissions (/admin/security/changepageaccesspriv.html). Set Turn modify permissions = On and click Submit. 

 2. Navigate to Quick Exports: System > Page and Data Management > Quick Export (admin/importexport/exportstudents.html). Click Modify access privileges. 

 3. Find the security group assigned to Clever and set Level of Access to Full . Click Submit (The security group should be the group assigned to the PowerSchool user you've shared with Clever).

 4. Navigate to System > Security > Access to Page Permissions (/admin/security/changepageaccesspriv.html). Set Turn modify permissions = Off and click Submit. 

 

 5. To confirm the permissions, log in to PowerSchool using the credentials provided to Clever. Navigate to Quick Exports: System > Page and Data Management > Quick Export (admin/importexport/exportstudents.html). If permissions were correctly applied, the Clever user account should have access to the Submit button. 

 In the Clever Dashboard, navigate to Data sources > SIS sync > Settings and press Sync Now. You can review the results on the Sync page once the sync completes (15-20 minutes).

 

 Troubleshooting email deletions 

 When your district chooses to sync student emails via the Email Configuration page, this is a separate process from the rest of your sync. For some districts, on occasion, our system may fail to successfully sync all student emails, resulting in some student emails being deleted from Clever. If this occurs, try rerunning a sync by navigating to your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Settings page and pressing Sync Now . This should resolve the issue. If your district regularly encounters issues with student email deletions, contact our team and we can discuss applying a safety threshold for the sync. For example, we can reject the email sync if more than 10% of student emails fail to export, preventing large changes to active Clever email data. Please contact our support team if you would like to learn more.

 
 Other options

 If emails are not exportable via DDE or the Email Configuration page, Clever won't be able to pull student emails as part of the auto sync. However, you do have the ability to manually sideload emails. Please see the following Help Center article to upload student emails: How do I upload emails that are not in my SIS? .

title: For App Partners: Extension Fields
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Syncing extension fields to Clever 3. Accessing extension fields 

 

 Overview

 If a district needs to send data fields outside of the standard fields listed in Clever's SFTP Specifications , they can do so by uploading extension fields. District admins can add custom fields for the following data types: • Schools • Sections • Students • Teachers • Staff We do not accept custom fields for enrollments or student contacts.

 

 
 Syncing extension fields to Clever

 A district can upload extension fields in two ways: 1. Manual upload via the Clever Dashboard: Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data page. 2. Automatic upload through the district's current student information system (SIS) sync. Directions for districts that would like to add extension fields can be found here . 

 
 Accessing extension fields

 Extension field data is available for v2.1 and higher. These fields will appear on the associated record as an extension field. Full documentation for supporting extension fields can be found in our Dev Docs . 

 Update your application settings 

 In order to receive extension fields, you'll need to add the fields to the list of fields you will accept in your Data Access settings ( Settings > Data Access ). You'll need to mark the ext field as optional for any record types you'll support. Additionally, a district will need enable access to extension fields from their Clever Dashboard under the Settings > Field Access for your application. Once the district has enabled access, the data will be available to your application in the Clever API. 

 Field Normalization 

 Because fields are created and populated by the district outside of our schema, we cannot normalize the data within the field. Additionally, there are no constraints on what districts can use for field name(s). 

 
 Related Articles: 

 For Clever Admins: Syncing custom (i.e., extension) fields to Clever

title: Managing Rollover with Google Classroom Roster Import
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Plan for rollover: pause and access dates, and course archiving 3. Review sharing rules 4. Let your teachers know what to expect! 5. Monitor the sync history page for errors and successfully-synced data 6. Steps to take as-needed 7. Bulk Archive Tool 

 Overview

 Google Classroom roster import makes setting up courses in Google Classroom easier than ever for school-year rollover! We’ve created this guide to help ensure that you’re set up for a smooth semester or summer term rollover with Google Classroom roster import. If you’re new to using roster import, be sure to accept your invite and initiate your sync first! You can find an overview of the integration here , and our full setup guide here. 

 
 Step 1: Plan for rollover: pause and access dates, and course archiving

 When a district's student information system's (SIS) data rolls over to the new semester or school year, courses are synced automatically with Google Classroom once they are active in Clever and shared with Google Classroom roster import. Once a district approves its SIS sync hold and rolls over to the new semester’s rostering data in Clever, those shared sections will create corresponding courses in Google Classroom! 

 

 Please note: If sections are removed from your SIS and Clever account, it won't result in the corresponding Google Classroom course being removed from Google Classroom. The course will remain active unless one of the following occurs: - A Clever Admin archives the section using the Bulk Archive Tool - The section is archived or deleted by the teacher or a Clever Admin - The section is archived 

 However, districts can delay syncing new courses to Google Classroom by pausing their Google Classroom sync and setting teacher and student access dates. Please note that this pause is separate from any pauses to a district's SIS sync to Clever. To pause your district's Google Classroom sync, please follow these steps:

 • Navigate to the Settings tab of your Google Classroom roster import application.
• Add a start for your pause. Clever will stop syncing new information to Google Classroom when your start date arrives. Pausing will not remove or change any existing data in Google Classroom. To pause the sync immediately, set the start date to today. Please see the default Summer 2026 pause start dates below, based on your school organization’s country:
• 
 • 
 USA: May 29, 2026

• 
 Canada: June 30, 2026

• 
 UK: July 15, 2026

• 
 Australia and Brazil: December 20, 2026

• All other countries follow USA dates
 

• Add access to dates for teachers and students.
 • Teachers will see courses when the teacher access date arrives, allowing them to prepare for the upcoming semester. The teacher will not see students in these courses until the student access date.
• Students will be added to courses on the student access date, allowing them to see the courses teachers have accepted.
• If you would like students to see courses as soon as teachers accept them, you can set the access dates to the same day. 
• The sync will run for each user type at 12:30 AM PST/3:30 AM EST on the corresponding access date
 

• Select the Update option to save the pause start date and access dates.
• By default the pause end dates for teachers and students were set to your SIS sync pause end dates if configured.
 
 Please select the Clear option to unpause the sync, and the Google Classroom roster import will begin syncing again for both teachers and students.
