The Google Classroom roster import sync pause is a great tool to use if your district does not want students to see their courses in Google Classroom before schedules are officially announced. Simply set a teacher access date for the day schedules are updated in Clever and set a student access date for the day your district schedule will be released. Please note that previously created courses that are no longer shared can be archived using the Bulk Archive Tool . 

 Clever Tip: For additional tips on preventing students from viewing their schedules prematurely in Clever, please visit this article detailing Clever's Access Control feature. 

 

 
 Step 2: Review sharing rules 

 Navigate to your district's sharing rules for Google Classroom roster import. Do the sharing rules you have in place now work for the coming semester’s data as well? This is a great time to ensure that your district is sharing all necessary courses with Google Classroom. Need help with sharing rules? Clever Support can help. Many districts prefer to share only the current term's sections to avoid confusion for their teachers. See the term rollover article for more information. 

 
 Step 3: Let your teachers know what to expect! 

 This is one of the most important steps! For a great rollout, make sure your teachers know the following three things:

 • When they should see courses populated in Google Classroom (and what they look like - see below). Google Classroom courses should be synced within about an hour after your teacher access date arrives.
• When students will be added to those courses. If your student access date is after your teacher access date, make sure to let teachers know when students will be added - so they don't add them on their own and are prepared for when they appear! 
• That they need to "accept" courses for them to be editable and visible to students. Courses that aren't accepted will not be visible to anyone except their primary teacher. 
 
 

 
 Step 4: Monitor the sync history page for errors and successfully-synced data 

 Navigate to the Sync History page for Google Classroom roster import to monitor the courses that are being created and updated. Be sure to review any sync error reports, including the reason and resolution for any sync issues. 

 Please note: When sections match, we may or may not have updated the class. It'll also show as an update, but this doesn't mean that anything changed. It's just indicating that it is the new current state of the Google Classroom class. 

 

 
 Steps to take as-needed 

 Reprovision any courses that teachers declined by accident

 Be sure to let your teachers know that they need to accept the synced courses in Google Classroom! If you need to reprovision any courses that teachers declined or archived by accident, Clever Admins can do that from the section's profile page in the Clever Dashboard: An example screenshot of what the course will look like if declined. 

 Customize course names

 If the default naming convention or a previous year’s customized naming convention needs to be changed during rollover, you can utilize the course naming tool from the Google Classroom roster import settings page in your Clever Dashboard. These changes will only be reflected in courses created in future syncs and will not overwrite previously synced courses. If courses are created and already in use, the names will not update dynamically when using the custom course naming feature. 

 Teachers can customize course names

 If a teacher has courses with the same name syncing due to a previous year or term's course having the same name as a new course, the teacher can change one of the course names manually to differentiate them. The sync will not overwrite any changes the teacher makes to the course name.

 Disable student access to Google Classroom in the Google Workspace Admin Console

 If you do not set a pause during a rollover period (e.g., summer, winter) students will be able to see schedules as soon as the teacher accepts their course in Google Classroom. This can cause students to see their schedule before the school is ready. If this occurs, the school can disable the student's access to Google Classroom in the Google Workspace Admin Console to prevent schedules from being revealed to students.

 

 
 Bulk Archive Tool

 The Bulk Archive Tool in Google Workspace offers a streamlined way to manage your courses by allowing you to archive multiple

title: Clever + Google Classroom: Resolving License Errors
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Related Errors 3. Google Workspace Admin Help Resources 

 Overview

 Google Classroom requires all teachers and students to have an active and assigned license before they can be a part of the roster sync. To avoid sync errors, ensure that Edu Plus licenses are assigned to all students and teachers whose sections will be synced with Classroom. To review, assign, or changes licenses, go to Billing > License Settings in the Google Admin Console. 

 

 Related Errors

 If a user doesn’t have an active license, you will see the following error in your sync errors report: Failed Precondition: Joining User Not *Eligible* For Roster Import Download the sync errors log to see which student and teacher accounts are running into the error:

 

 Make sure that a Google Workspace Education Plus or Google Standard Edition license is assigned to the students in teachers with the error in the Sync Error Log file. To review, assign, or changes licenses, go to Billing > License Settings in the Google Admin Console.

 

 Google Workspace Admin Help Resources

 If you are uncertain about how to assign licenses to students and teachers, please review these resources: 

 • How to assign, remove, and reassign licenses 
• Provisioning Google Workspace for Education Paid Licenses 
 
 If you are still having challenges assigning licenses – please contact Google Workspace support: 

 • How to contact Google Workspace support

title: Clever + ARC Setup Guide
text:
1. Overview of applications 2. Setup process 3. Recommended sharing rules 4. Setting up SchoolPace for co-teachers and support staff 5. FAQ 

 

 Overview of applications

 SchoolPace: SchoolPace is ARC’s analytics platform for schools and districts. This web-based platform is accessed by teachers, school administrators, and district administrators. Teachers use the built-in assessment tools to evaluate student reading skills and record observations. Data is aggregated into graphs and charts to help schools and districts monitor progress. 

 myARC: myARC is a web-based platform that allows ARC to organize and distribute curricular content, digital books, videos, and other intellectual property securely with educators, families, and students. 

 ARC Bookshelf: ARC Bookshelf is American Reading Company’s digital book platform, and fully integrated with SchoolPace (for analytics) and myARC (for sharing). Students can read digital books on the web, or on native apps for iOS and Android.

 ARC Adventures: ARC Adventures allows students to practice foundational reading skills in a fun, 3D game world. 

 ARC Reads®- A student portal for the 100 Book Challenge® and ARC Core® classrooms, offers powerful tools to enhance reading growth, practice, and engagement. With the Digital Logbook, students log their reading practice, receive book recommendations, and track their reading habits. The My IRLA tool identifies where a student might be able to handle text independently through phonics and vocabulary questions. 

 Important note: Not all license purchases will include myARC, ARC Bookshelf, ARC Adventures and ARC Reads. 

 

 
 Setup process

 1. Make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup. 2. Submit Implementation Checklist: Once your district’s licenses have been activated, districts and schools will be emailed a link to the “Implementation Checklist”. Districts must complete and submit the “Implementation Checklist” and choose Clever as their rostering method. 

 

 3. SchoolPace Support confirmation email: Once the implementation checklist has been submitted and finalized, SchoolPace Support will email confirming the rostering method and rostering dates, and include information on next steps. 4. Accept Invitations: After submitting the “Implementation Checklist”, the SchoolPace Support team will invite your district or school to Clever. You should see a Clever invite for each application purchased in your email inbox. 

 

 5. Notify SchoolPace Support when Accepting Clever Invitation: Districts and schools should accept the invitation and notify SchoolPace Support of the acceptance. Notifying SchoolPace Support of acceptance helps ensure that syncing with Clever is set up properly. To notify SchoolPace support simply reply to the email sent to you by SchoolPace Support, or email schoolpace@americanreading.com . 6. Set sharing rules: Districts set up sharing rules in Clever. 7. Start syncing: SchoolPace starts syncing with Clever on the “Rostering Start Date” as provided on the implementation checklist. 

 
 Recommended sharing rules

 It’s important to only share the sections that are using SchoolPace in the classroom. The simplest way to do so is to choose “Share by Rules” in Clever. You can create a sharing rule by following these steps: 

 • Access the Sharing Permissions page for SchoolPace. To do this, log in to your Clever account and click the icon for the name of the application on the Home page. From the 'Data Sharing' tab, click Share by Rules
• Select the type of rule you want to create. We recommend choosing “Custom share by section”, as it will allow you to create your own rule that can apply specifically to classes that will be using SchoolPace. 
• Create your rule. You can share classes with certain course names or numbers, grades, subjects or schools. 
 
 We also recommend using this Help Center article when setting up custom sharing rules. If you need any further assistance setting up sharing rules please contact Clever Support. 

 Important Notes:

 • SchoolPace is the main rostering application. You must set up your rules for SchoolPace and copy those rules over to myARC, ARC Bookshelf, and ARC Adventures to enable sign sign-on (SSO), if purchased. If you set up rules for myARC, ARC Bookshelf, or ARC Adventures that are different from the sharing rules for SchoolPace, there can be access errors. 
• Students can only be in one school at a time. If a student is shared in two classrooms, the classrooms must be in the same school. For example, if you share student A to a homeroom section and an ELA section, the sections must be assigned to the same school.
 
 

 
 Setting up SchoolPace for co-teachers and support staff

 Co-teachers: The best way to add co-teachers is to add them as a co-teacher in the Student Information System (SIS) to the section they will be supporting. If you manually add the co-teacher in SchoolPace, they will lose access the next day due to the nightly sync with Clever. SpEd teachers and other support staff: You can manually add users as staff members, through a CSV upload to Clever through your Clever Dashboard , or by creating a custom staff account. When you upload a staff file, they will have admin access. For more information on adding staff users to Clever, check out the following Help Center article: Clever Roles: Staff - How to add to Clever . Once the staff user accounts have been created in Clever, create a sharing rule that will share that staff member with SchoolPace and accompanying apps. 

 
 FAQ

 Why is a student or teacher missing from SchoolPace? 

 • Schoolpace is rostered nightly through Clever. So if a student or teacher was missing from SchoolPace, it may be because they have been removed from the Clever share. 
• If a student is missing from a classroom, check to see if the student is assigned to two classrooms. If a student is assigned to two classrooms, SchoolPace will roster the student in only one of those classrooms. 
• If a teacher doesn’t have access to SchoolPace, check if the section was shared in Clever, and check if there are students assigned to those sections. 
 
 I made updates and it’s reflected in Clever. Why don’t I see those changes in SchoolPace? 

 • Changes from Clever will be reflected in SchoolPace the next day. 
 
 For support, please email SchoolPace@americanreading.com

title: Clever + HMHCore Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. HMH Rostering Service Rostering 2. Setup instructions 3. Common troubleshooting tips 

 ​

 HMH Rostering Service 

 The HMH Rostering Service integration with Clever supports automated rostering only for the following product lines: 

 • HMH ThinkCentral
• HMH MyHRW 
• HMH Ed Learning Platform
 
 Please note: If you wish to set up SSO for these products, you will need to follow the instructions here separately. 

 ​

 
 Setup instructions

 To set up rostering for HMH Rostering Service, you will need to follow one of two sets of steps. We highly recommend following the API setup unless you are already using the SFTP setup:

 • If you've already completed the SFTP setup, you should wait until the end of the school year to convert to the API option . 
 
 API Setup (Recommended)

 • Request the HMH Rostering Service application here. 
• Follow the setup instructions here. 
 
 WARNING When you change rostering methods mid-year, IDs can change, which may result in the loss of data. There are specific and detailed warnings about precautions that must be taken in the setup instructions. 

 

 SFTP Setup

 • Request the HMH Rostering Service application here. 
• ​If you are rostering HMH for the first time or do not wish to retain historical data, you can follow these instructions. 
• If you already roster with HMH and would like to now roster through Clever, you can follow these instructions. 
 
 

 
 Common troubleshooting tips

 ​Below is a common pitfall in setting up HMH Rostering Service setup on the Clever side:

 • Lack of Terms or Courses In order to use this rostering setup for HMH, your district must have populated term data and it must be shared with HMH Rostering Service. If you only wish to share users and not sections (which include terms), the current setup between HMH Rostering Service and Clever will not be usable. If you do not have terms data currently due to SIS or sync limitations, please contact Clever support to discuss potential options.
 
 tags: hmh rostering service

title: Clever + Canva for Education: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Important information 4. FAQs 5. Support contacts 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through how to get set up with Canva for Education in Clever, which is 100% free for districts to use. Before reading this guide, please make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: clever.com/signup or by accepting your invitation from Canva for Education. For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide . 

 
 Setup process 

 IMPORTANT Please do not skip any steps, as doing so might lead to access issues, account duplication, or data loss. If you have any questions during your setup, please contact your Canva representative or the Canva SSO support team . If you haven’t already, your district will need to complete an application form to receive the Canva for Districts product, after which point the Canva team will provision a special version of the product which you can integrate with Clever. Apply here to get started. 

 Step 1: Connect the Canva for Education Application in Clever If your district is already on Clever, you can connect the Canva for Education application in one of two ways: Invitation: If Canva for Education invites you to connect, Accept the invitation in your Clever Dashboard home page and continue with the next setup step. Request: You can also request the Canva for Education app by following the steps below.

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and click Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for Canva for Education.
 • 
 

• You’ll then be brought to a page where you can authorize your Canva request. As Clever SSO and rostering for Canva is provided at no cost to the district, you can mark that it has already been purchased for the district. 
• You’ll be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the Canva icons in their Clever portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day (pending approval from Canva).
• Once you have made your selections, click Request !
• The app status will then appear as Waiting on Approval . Once the Canva team grants you access to the Canva for Education app, the status will change to No data shared and you can continue the set up.
 
 Step 2: Set up the Canva SAML & Rostering Configuration Set up the Canva SAML & Rostering Configuration for Clever using this guide . Please don’t skip any steps, as doing so might lead to access issues, account duplication, or data loss. Come back here once you're successfully connected to the app to set up data sharing, to indicate which school(s) should have access to Canva. Step 3: Recommended Sharing Share data with Canva so they know who should have access. Before sharing:

 • Ensure that students and teachers are in their correct grade levels and that classes are updated for the current school year in your Student Information System (SIS).
 
 Canva recommends that you share all data for your district. The Canva for Education platform is free to you so there are no license concerns with this sharing setting. The simplest way to do so is to select Share All Data in Clever. If you plan to share specific schools, you can find more info about “Share by Rules” here . You can create a sharing rule by going to the Sharing Permissions page for Canva for Education. To do this, log in to your Clever account and click the icon for Canva in the sidebar. From the Data Sharing tab, click Share All Data . 

 
 Important information

 Canva for Education is a SAML application in Clever What does this mean?

 • The application is automatically configured to SSO via SAML with Canva.
• To view SAML Details in your Clever Dashboard, click the Canva for Education app, click on Settings and scroll to SAML Details .
 • 
 

• For more information on how to configure SAML with Canva see the Clever SAML & Rostering Configuration Guide. 
 
 Required Fields Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. Canva requires the following data fields be sent:

 • Student: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, email, grade
• Contact: district, name, sis_id, students, relationship, student_relationships, type
• Teacher: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, email, title
• School Admin: district, name, schools, staff_id, email, roles
• District Admin: district, name.first, name.last, email, title 
 
 
 FAQs

 Who can configure this application in Canva? This feature is available to Canva for Districts. However, only team administrators and owners can enable, configure, and change their team or district’s SSO & Provisioning settings. Why is a student or teacher missing from Canva? Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Canva:

 • In your SIS data
• In your Clever data
• Is shared with Canva in your data sharing permissions
 
 Can multiple teachers/staff be assigned to the same course/section? Canva can support multiple teachers or staff assigned to the same course/section. Are staff supported? Yes, Canva supports staff with Clever. Who should I ask for help with an issue? If it’s related to data within Clever, or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever. If you are receiving an error message within the Canva URL, please contact Canva. 

 
 
 

 Support contacts

 Canva Support Contact: Canva.com/help/get-in-touch/contact-support Contact Canva with issues that relate to:

 • An error message within any Canva URL
 
 Clever Support Contact: Support.clever.com (click the chat icon in the corner of the screen) Contact Clever with issues that relate to:

 • Data within Clever
• Data coming from Clever

title: For Clever Admins: Why Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Modernizing access with single sign-on (SSO) 3. Automating data rostering and management 4. Prioritizing cybersecurity and data privacy 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever serves as the central identity platform for your school organization, connecting all your digital learning tools in one secure location. It simplifies the digital classroom by providing students and teachers with a single point of entry for every application they use.

 You'll find that Clever automates the manual tasks of account creation and data management, allowing your team to focus on critical tasks instead of repetitive processes. By standardizing your technology ecosystem, Clever helps you create a more efficient and secure learning environment for everyone.

 Through Clever’s unique business model , in which application partners pay Clever for secure, automated rostering, we’re able to offer many of Clever’s features at no cost to schools. Application partners agree not to charge schools a premium charge or any additional fee that acts as a premium for use of the application via Clever, in addition to any regular fees they are already charging the schools. 

 Of note, Clever does not sell student data. Student data privacy is a top priority for Clever; we are both FERPA and COPPA compliant and meet additional federal and state requirements.

 We offer the following services at no charge to school organizations:

 • 
 Secure Sync 

• 
 Clever Portal 

• 
 Single-sign on (SSO) 

• 
 Clever Badges 

• 
 Clever Analytics 

• 
 Clever Library 

• Clever Support 
 
 You’ll also be able to contact our support team with any questions or issues. Our team has agents available to assist you Monday - Friday, 7am - 8pm ET.

 Clever IDM and Classroom MFA are additional, optional paid products we offer at the request of school organizations to further secure identity and access and streamline technology in one simple platform. 

 
 Modernizing access with single sign-on (SSO)

 Managing dozens of different usernames and passwords creates a significant barrier to learning and increases the workload for your technical support team. Clever’s Single Sign-On (SSO) removes this hurdle by allowing users to access all their authorized applications with one set of credentials. For younger learners, we offer Clever Badges, which allow students to log in simply by holding a physical Badge up to a device camera. This streamlined access ensures that teachers don’t waste valuable learning time helping students login. If you’re already using SSO through an IdP like Google or Microsoft, or simply don’t want your students and teachers to have to learn another set of credentials, you can use your current IdP with Clever to keep credentials consistent for students. This allows for a seamless transition to Clever for your end users.

 Beyond just logging in, the Clever Portal acts as a personalized digital home base for every user. Admins and teachers can organize resources by grade level, subject, or school, ensuring that students see exactly what they need the moment they sign in. When you provide a consistent, reliable way to access tools, you'll see higher engagement across all your digital resources. You're not just adding a new tool; you're creating a gateway that makes every other piece of software in your school organization more effective.

 
 Automating data management and rostering

 Many school organizations manage multiple syncs or data uploads for the various learning applications they utilize. Managing data per application can lead to data errors and lapses in user access. Clever’s Secure Sync connects directly to your Student Information System (SIS) to automate the entire provisioning process, ensuring you no longer need to manage individual syncs per application. When you update student or staff information in your SIS, Clever automatically reflects those changes across all your connected learning applications. This system ensures that every user has the correct access from their first day in the school organization.

 You'll no longer need to spend hours managing spreadsheets or manually creating accounts for every new software tool. This automation reduces the administrative burden on your IT staff and ensures that student data remains accurate across different platforms. We've built this infrastructure to handle the heavy lifting of data management so your staff can reclaim their time for more impactful work.

 

 
 Prioritizing cybersecurity and data privacy

 Protecting student privacy remains a top priority for every school organization. Clever provides a secure infrastructure that complies with federal regulations like FERPA and COPPA by design. We give you full control over the data you share, letting you decide exactly which pieces of information each application can access. You'll find that our platform offers a level of visibility into data usage that's difficult to achieve when managing applications individually.

 Our platform also includes advanced security features, such as Classroom Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). Classroom MFA was designed to protect all of your end user’s accounts with school-friendly second factors that don’t require a second device, like login pictures, Badges, and PIN codes. By using Clever, you're implementing a robust security layer that safeguards your digital ecosystem against unauthorized access while maintaining ease of use for students and staff.

 We also offer tools to help you audit and manage app security. You can see which applications have access to what data and revoke that access at any time. This transparency helps build trust with parents and stakeholders who worry about student data privacy. We've designed Clever to be the safest way to manage a digital learning environment, so you don't have to choose between convenience and security.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Why should our school organization use Clever instead of managing apps individually? 

 • Clever centralizes your tools to increase adoption and secure your data in one place. You’ll save hundreds of hours by eliminating manual data entry and password resets.
 
 How does Clever protect our student data? 

 • We encrypt your data and strictly follow federal privacy laws like FERPA and COPPA. You maintain full ownership and control over exactly what information you share with each app.
 
 Is it difficult to set up a connection between our SIS and Clever? 

 • We’ve made setup straightforward by offering several easy ways to sync data to Clever. Our team provides clear documentation and support to guide you through every step.
 
 How do we encourage teachers who are resistant to adopting a new platform? 

 • Help teachers understand how Clever works with your current tools to save them time. Share training resources that help them to understand how Clever works and highlight the simplicity of helping students access their learning resources from Badges to backup login codes. You can also book a paid Clever Training with a Clever expert customized to your teachers' needs and knowledge!
 
 What's the cost for a school organization to use the platform? 

 • Most of Clever's products and features, including SSO and Secure Sync, costs nothing for school organizations. Our application partners cover the costs so we can provide these essential services to you for free. Additional features such as Clever IDM and Classroom MFA are offered at a low price to ensure schools have access to secure digital learning.
 
 
 Related articles: 

 • 
 What’s Clever? 

• 
 Setting up Clever: Overview 

• 
 For Clever Admins: What is SSO? 

• 
 For Clever Admins: What’s secure sync?

title: For Clever Admins: Accepting app invites or requesting applications you already roster
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What to expect during the transition 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 This article outlines the process for adding applications to your Clever account that you are currently rostering via other means. It includes information on both accepting an application invite or requesting an application to begin rostering via Clever.

 Please note: You can add applications in Clever at any time of year! 

 

 
 What to expect during the transition

 When accepting an application invite

 If you already have an established relationship with an application, and it's also available on Clever, you may receive an invitation from the app partner to roster via Clever in your dashboard.

 

 You can view and accept the application invite at any time, and it will not impact your current rostering or login setup for the application. The application partner won’t be able to access data until you configure sharing permissions.

 If an application partner has sent you an invite in Clever, they are ready to assist you in transitioning to rostering and SSO via Clever. Each application partner will have their processes for supporting you in the transition.

 We recommend that once you have a timeline for the transition with the application’s team, that you notify your staff, teachers, and students. You may want to communicate any changes to links or login instructions for users.

 When requesting an application

 You can request to add a learning application in Clever at any time of year, and it will not impact your current rostering or login setup with the application until you begin sharing data. Each application partner will have their processes for supporting you in the transition. If you have questions about the transitioning process during the school year, we recommend contacting the application directly.

 Once you have established a timeline for the transition with your application partner, we recommend you notify your staff, teachers, and students. You may want to communicate any changes to links or user login instructions. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 What will happen if I accept an application invite? 

 • 
 No changes will immediately take place. The application’s team will work with you to ensure a smooth transition. They will be able to assist with matching existing accounts and preserving historical data. Once you’re ready to share data with the application, you’ll want to set up your sharing rules. 

 
 What happens when I request an application? 

 • 
 The application will receive a notification that you’d like to start rostering and/or using SSO via Clever, but there won’t be any immediate changes. The application team will need to approve the request and collaborate on your setup. You’ll want to complete your sharing permissions when you’re ready to share data with the application. We recommend that you contact the application directly for more information on your transition plan.

 
 Why did I receive an application invite? 

 • 
 If you already have an established relationship with an app partner, and the app is also available on Clever, you may receive an invitation from the app partner to roster via Clever in your dashboard.

• 
 If you’ve received an invitation to connect from an application, it’s likely because the application has detected active users in your district and believes your district could benefit from using its Clever integration. If you have any questions about the invitation, feel free to respond directly or reach out to the support contact listed for more details.

 
 How do I limit the data I share with each application? 

 • You can customize the data you share with each application via your Sharing Permissions . You can further limit the specific fields shared with an application via Field Access Settings . 
 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Apps - App invites 
• For Clever Admins: Apps - Add/request or remove apps 
• Sharing Permissions: Overview

title: For Application Partners: Clever Complete Overview
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features 3. Benefits 4. Learn more 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Clever Complete is the platform solution purpose-built for edtech scalability—connecting your application to any school through a single, secure integration for rostering, SSO, and LMS connections.

 

 
 Features

 Today, Clever Complete extends Clever’s existing 1EdTech OneRoster® integrations to enable application partners to automate rostering via OneRoster for any school, even if they’re not using Clever.

 In the future, Clever Complete will enable:

 • 
 Access to a single integration point for rostering, SSO, and LMS for any school or district, regardless of whether they use Clever.

• 
 Additional data fields related to demographics and attendance

• 
 Best-in-class data visibility and management

 
 

 
 Benefits

 With a single, powerful integration that consolidates and secures edtech interoperability, edtech applications, and schools benefit from the following:

 • 
 One integration for all schools. Stop maintaining custom builds for every school or system—connect once and support them all.

• 
 Cut integration costs and optimize efficiency. Free up engineering resources by consolidating fragile, one-off integrations—allowing your team to focus on growth and innovation.

• 
 Built-in data privacy and security. Offload compliance and security concerns while ensuring school trust by decreasing attack surface for users to gain unauthorized access.

• 
 Expand faster and reduce time to value—without extra engineering work. Reach new schools quickly while reducing manual processes, custom builds, and technical overhead. 

 
 
 Learn more

 If you're interested in utilizing Clever Complete, you can: 

 • 
 Sign up for a call to learn more.

• 
 Learn more in Clever Academy . 

 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 How does SSO work with Clever Complete? 

 • 
 Clever Complete enables edtech applications to authenticate users through Clever’s secure SSO infrastructure, regardless of whether a school is currently on Clever or not. Schools and applications can continue using their preferred authentication methods while ensuring a seamless login experience for students and educators.

 
 Can apps filter or manage connections when importing data via CSV or OneRoster? 

 • 
 Yes, partners will be able to control which data they import using the existing filters in their Clever dashboards. Additionally, depending on the data source, districts may be able to filter what data is sent to Clever and the application.

 
 How will districts and schools currently using our application be impacted if we begin using Clever Complete? 

 • 
 Districts and schools already on Clever will continue to use Clever as they currently do- there will be no changes. For districts or schools that are not currently using Clever, they may need to configure additional settings to begin the data flow through Clever. Our support teams will assist in this process!

 
 How will Clever Complete impact our district integrations? 

 • 
 Clever Complete acts as a unified integration layer for application partners. Districts, on the other hand, will be able to choose whatever rostering solution they want to use -- whether that be Clever, ClassLink, GG4L, direct SIS syncs, or manual uploads. Clever will be integrated with all of those sources, in doing so, will allow applications to support any of those sources as well. Districts and schools will still need to meet Clever’s field requirements for data ingestion, ensuring consistency and data integrity across all integrations.

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Clever Complete

title: For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA rollout
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Rolling out Classroom MFA for the Clever Portal 3. Google-only: Setting up and rolling out Clever Passwords 4. Google-only: Setting up and rolling out Classroom MFA protection for Google 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 We know that setting up a multi-factor authentication process requires a lot of change management for yourself, staff, teachers, and students! This article summarizes our recommended Classroom MFA rollout process based on what we have found to be successful for other districts setting up Classroom MFA. 

 Since each school’s needs are unique, your rollout plan may vary! This article is an informational guide. As you work with a Sales and Onboarding engineer, they will help you plan for a successful rollout of Classroom MFA.

 If you need guidance on the technical setup steps for Classroom MFA, please see our article For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA setup . 

 

 
 Rolling out Classroom MFA for the Clever Portal

 Starting with a small test group and adding Classroom MFA to larger groups of end users over time often results in the smoothest rollout. While you will be able to develop a custom plan with a Sales and Onboarding Engineer, we recommend the following:

 • 
 Configuring a policy for a test group. This group may include your technology staff members and 1-2 tech-savvy teachers at each school willing to help you in the testing process. Test with this group for approximately a week before moving to the next stage.

• 
 Begin rolling out to wider groups. For example, you may start with one Kindergarten class, then all Kindergarten classes at a particular school, then all Kindergarten classes across the district, etc. This rollout step may take 1-2 weeks.

• 
 Once you feel comfortable with these initial groups, continue your rollout more widely. For example, instead of rolling out to a single classroom at a single school, create a policy to roll out to all second-grade classrooms at a school or all second-grade classrooms across the district. You can pace your rollout at a speed that feels right for your district!

 
 Clever Tip: Using the staged rollout model recommended above typically takes around four weeks for a district to complete Classroom MFA setup and rollout. Please see our article For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA Implementation steps and timeline for a closer look at the overall implementation steps and timeline.

 

 
 Google-only: Setting up and rolling out Clever Passwords

 If you will be using Clever’s Classroom MFA to secure your users’ Google accounts, and your users are currently using Google as a login method to Clever, you will need to familiarize your users with using Clever Passwords as their identity provider (IdP). We recommend the following steps for familiarizing users with the new login process:

 • 
 Add Clever Passwords as a second login option on your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page if you haven’t already. This article will guide you through the setup process. By adding Clever Passwords along with Google, users will gain comfort using Clever Passwords to log in instead of Google.

• 
 Consider turning off Google logins to Clever if all students, teachers, and staff will utilize Clever Passwords. Please discuss this with your onboarding engineer to plan the best option for your school. You'll want to communicate with your students, teachers, and staff any changes to Clever login methods. For example, if your students will move to use Clever Passwords for logins, but your teacher and staff logins will remain the same, you’ll want to share this with teachers (and perhaps students directly) that student logins will be changing.

• 
 If all users will be using Clever Passwords, remove Google as a login option in Clever on your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page.

• 
 Users will begin using Clever Passwords and Classroom MFA to access the Clever Portal.

 
 

 
 Google-only: Setting up and rolling out Classroom MFA protection for Google

 Once your users know the process outlined above for utilizing Clever Passwords to access the Clever Portal, you’ll complete the final steps of configuring Clever as your IdP and setting up Classroom MFA. You’ll follow the recommended steps below:

 • Users who use a Clever Badge to log in to their Chromebook are already set up with Clever as an IdP for Google and will automatically be protected at device sign-in with Classroom MFA. To begin using Classroom MFA with these users, simply add them to an MFA policy in your Clever Dashboard. If you also want Classroom MFA to protect Google logins directly at the Google service for these users, you may need to adjust the SSO profile settings in Google Admin Console > Security > Authentication > SSO with 3rd Party IdP > Manage SSO profile assignments. Select the OU you've assigned to use Clever Badges into Chromebooks. Select Have Google prompt for their username... Then click OVERRIDE . Your onboarding engineer can provide more guidance on this update if needed. You can slowly complete this step by starting with a single class, then moving to a grade level at a single school, then transitioning to an entire grade level across all schools, etc.
• 
 For non-Badge users, you’ll need to configure the Google Workspace SAML Connection. This article, has instructions for completing this setup. You should configure a test group organizational unit (OU) in Google for initial testing and troubleshooting.

• 
 For non-Badge users, once your test group is successfully up and running, begin to roll out to additional OUs. You can do this one grade or one school at a time.

 
 Clever Tip: If you have any students in upper grades (6th grade or higher) using Clever Badges, we recommend talking to your onboarding engineer for test group recommendations.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 How long does setting up and rolling out Classroom MFA typically take? 

 • 
 Most districts complete the Classroom MFA setup and rollout within 4 weeks.

 
 How do I decide whether to set up Classroom MFA for just the Clever Portal or my Google accounts and devices? 

 • 
 We recommend reviewing your district’s cybersecurity insurance. Many companies are requiring districts to secure all accounts with multi-factor authentication.

 
 What resources are available to help me with the Classroom MFA setup for my users? 

 • 
 Check out our Clever Academy course, Clever Classroom MFA Setup and Rollout Quick Start Guide or our Help Center article For Clever Admins: Classroom MFA setup .

 
 How can I monitor Classroom MFA activation? 

 • 
 Under your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Classroom MFA > Users tab you can see each individual’s Activation Status per policy. 

 
 What is the best way to share these new MFA practices with teachers and students? 

 • 
 Clever has a number of resources to help you and your end users adjust to Classroom MFA:

 • 
 For teachers and staff we recommend:

 • 
 For Teachers & Staff: Setting up Classroom MFA 

• 
 For Teachers: Setting up Classroom MFA 

• 
 Our step-by-step interactive setup tutorial found here 

 

• 
 For students we recommend:

 • 
 Teachers can use this slide deck to help students through the Classroom MFA setup

• 
 This Kahoot! quiz to familiarize students with what MFA is

• 
 For Students: Setting up Classroom MFA 

• 
 For Families: What’s Classroom MFA? 

 

• 
 We also have email templates to help you share more:

 • 
 For teachers and staff 

• 
 For students and families 

• For school principals 
 

 

 
 How can teachers and staff get help with Classroom MFA? 

 • 
 As a Clever Admin, you can either reset a user’s MFA method or generate a bypass code to share with them via their Profile Page in Clever.

 
 How can students get help with Classroom MFA? 

 • 
 Depending on the MFA methods configured, teachers and staff may have various tools to help either reset or view student MF

title: For Application Partners: Clever LMS Connect and AnySchool
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Inviting an off-Clever district or school 3. The district/school experience 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever LMS Connect allows you to integrate with district LMSs easily via the Clever API. Through the AnySchool capability in Clever Complete, you can connect with districts and schools that don't use Clever, enabling them and you to benefit from Clever LMS Connect.

 Clever Complete with AnySchool enables any district or school to launch your application directly from their LMS using SSO.. Clever LMS Connect also saves teachers time with gradebook syncs and allows students to jump into learning with seamless assignment syncs. You can learn more about Clever LMS Connect here .

 This article covers the setup of Clever LMS Connect for off-Clever districts and schools.

 

 
 Inviting an off-Clever school or district

 To connect with a district or school that isn’t currently utilizing Clever, you must have a Clever Complete contract, which includes AnySchool connections. If you don’t have a Clever Complete agreement in place and are interested in learning more, please contact our team here .

 If you have an existing Clever Secure Sync or Clever LMS Connect integration with V3.0+ of the API, you can use your existing integrations to utilize all of the Clever Complete features, including AnySchool.

 Clever hosts the setup flow and it’s white-labeled to align with your application’s branding.

 To use AnySchool to invite an off-Clever district or school to utilize Clever LMS Connect, the district or school must first set up rostering via AnySchool. This setup will enable Clever to make the necessary connections between their users and courses in their data and their LMS. If the district or school has not set up AnySchool rostering yet, please refer to our article For App Partners: Rostering schools via the AnySchool district setup flow . Once you’ve worked with the school to complete their rostering setup, you’ll be able to proceed with the Clever LMS Connect setup. To do so, please: 

 • 
 Confirm you have the email address of the administrator at the district or school who will be setting up Clever LMS Connect with your application.

• 
 Navigate to the district/school’s AnySchool account in your Clever account using the Search bar.

• 
 In the LMS Connect Access Link box, select the button to Generate access link. 

• 
 Input the District Email. Clever will use this email address to verify the user during the setup process. Then click Generate link. 

• 
 Copy the generated link and provide it to the admin. The link will expire in 48 hours.

• 
 Once the administrator receives the link, they’ll complete the steps below to set up Clever LMS Connect with your application.

 
 

 
 The district/school experience

 Once you’ve generated the access link and provided it to the school administrator, they’ll be able to begin the setup process on their side. To complete the set up, they’ll:

 • 
 Navigate to the link you’ve provided.

• 
 Review the information for utilizing AnySchool and click the Get started button.

• 
 They’ll receive an additional confirmation email to verify their account. They’ll need to select the Verify account button in this email.

• 
 Once they verify the account, they’ll be logged in to begin their setup. They'll start by selecting their LMS as either Canvas, Schoology, or Google Classroom.

 Clever Tip: Once a district begins their setup, they have 48 hours to complete the process. If they don’t complete their setup in this timeframe, you’ll need to provide a new setup link. 

• 
 They’ll receive a prompt to enter additional information for their LMS, such as their URL and account connection information.

• 
 Once the admin establishes the connection with their LMS, Clever will begin a record-matching process. Recording-matching typically takes 2-3 days.

• 
 While waiting for record matching to complete, the administrator can generate the LTI 1.3 SSO link for your application. This link will enable users to access your application directly from their LMS. Logins won’t work until the record matching process completes.

 
 Once matching is complete and the admin has configured the SSO setup in their LMS, users will be able to begin accessing your application directly from their LMS! 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Why does a district or school need to set up Secure Sync rostering with AnySchool to utilize Clever LMS Connect? 

 • 
 Clever will use the data provided via rostering to connect users and courses for SSO, gradebook sync, and assignment syncs.

 
 Who should we contact if there are issues during the setup process? 

 • 
 For issues during the initial setup and matching process, the Clever support team can assist you.

• 
 Beyond initial setup, depending on the issue, you can always start with the Clever support team. We might be able to help you diagnose the problem and either resolve it or point you in the right direction.

• 
 For questions regarding where to find information within their LMS, the district should reach out directly to their LMS provider.

 
 Will other LMSs be supported? 

 • 
 Google Classroom will be coming soon! 

 
 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For App Partners: Clever Complete 

• For App Partners: Rostering schools via the AnySchool district setup flow 
• Rostering applications with AnySchool

title: For Clever Admin: Google License Manager
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Step 1: Connect your Google account 3. Step 2: Turn off automatic licensing in Google Workspace 4. Step 3: Select Organizational Units (OUs) and license type(s) 5. Sync History 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 For schools using Google products, we offer an optional Google License Manager. Google License manager will manage any paid license access for selected users via your Organizational Units (OUs) in Google, so you don’t have to apply or remove them manually!

 Read on to learn more about the feature and how to use it. This feature is currently being rolled out, so if you may not see the License Manager available yet under Applications in your Clever Dashboard.

 Clever Tip: We recommend utilizing Clever IDM for Google to manage any free Fundamentals, Fundamentals-archive, and Gmail-only licenses. If you're unsure whether your Google licenses are paid or free, we recommend checking Google's documentation on license types here and here . 

 

 
 Step 1: Connect your Google account

 Go to your Clever Dashboard > User management > License manager . To start, you must connect your Google Workspace for Education+ account to Clever. 

 The following permissions are required for access:

 • 
 read and write access to licensing

• 
 read-only access to users

• 
 read-only access to customers

• 
 read-only access to organizational units

• 
 read only access to app orders

 
 
 Step 2: Turn off automatic licensing in Google Workspace

 Go to your Google Workspace admin dashboard > Billing > License settings and ensure "Automatic Licensing" is turned off.

 Next, go to Billing > Subscriptions and select your license subscription. Ensure that you don't have any conflicting "Locally applied settings" for OUs that you want Clever to manage. 

 
 Step 3: Select Organizational Units (OUs) and license type(s)

 Next, click Select OUs . Select the Google OU(s) you want to assign Google licenses to. Keep in mind, the Google License Manager does not handle billing. You will need to continue to manage license subscriptions and ensure you have enough licenses to cover users you apply licenses to via the Google License Manager.

 

 You can apply the following license types:

 • 
 Google for Education Plus

• 
 Google for Education Plus - Legacy

• 
 Google for Education Standard

• 
 Google for Education - Teacher & Learning Upgrade.

 
 Learn more about the different license types available here .

 You can also remove the license for:

 • 
 Users not in the selected OU(s) and

• 
 Users who are suspended

 
 

 Please note: If you’d like to manage licenses for users with free fundamentals licenses, fundamentals-archive, or gmail-only licenses you can use Clever IDM for Google to do so. When setting up your OUs, you’ll be able to set the action for a user removed from Clever: 1. Move to archive OU, suspend, and archive 2. Move to archive OU and suspend 3. Move to archive OU If you don't currently have a Clever IDM subscription please fill out this form to speak with our team and learn more . 

 

 You can add rules for different licenses applied to them by clicking + Add rule .

 

 We recommend using the Preview functionality to see what license changes your Google License Manager will apply when you first configure your settings.

 Once you’ve identified all OU settings and confirmed your Preview, click Apply at the bottom to apply the licenses. Ongoing syncs with few changes will take up to 10 minutes. For syncs with large numbers of changes, the sync could take multiple hours.

 

 

 After you apply the license rules, the license manager will automatically apply these rules to your users nightly based on any users added or removed from the OU(s) you’ve selected.

 You can always return to edit or remove license access by OU as needed!

 
 Sync History

 Click on the Sync History tab to view a table of recent Google License Manager syncs and download a CSV of license updates the Google License Manager applied. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 If I use Clever IDM for Google, will my licenses get applied automatically? 

 • 
 Yes, a successful IDM sync will kick off a license manager sync to apply licenses to new users automatically, and remove licenses for suspended users if you have configured the manager to do so.

 
 I’m seeing error 412 in my Google License Manager sync log what is that? 

 • 
 This means you have gone over your purchased Google licenses. You need to ensure you have enough licenses to cover the OU(s) selected in the Google License manager.

 
 Are users notified of their license access? 

 • 
 No, they are not notified. 

 
 Am I notified once licenses are applied? 

 • 
 You can return to the Google License Manager page to see once the licenses are applied.

title: Skyward API Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up the API sync 3. School Year Rollover 4. Troubleshooting • Sync errors 5. Special Characters 6. Default field mappings 

 Overview 

 ✓ Runs every six hours, by querying the Skyward API ✓ Pulls all students and teachers who have an active school association ✓ Supports co-teachers for sections ✓ Supports multiple student contacts per student; these are pulled from the guardian data in Skyward ✓ Supports custom field mappings (please contact Clever Support to set this up).

 Please note: • T he Skyward API doesn't sync IEP, ELL, FRL, or Home_language fields. • For student contacts we are unable to: - filter contacts - customize contact data

 Clever supports three Skyward API sync options. You can learn more about each one below:

 Skyward API SMS 2.0 

 This sync option is no longer available to new school organizations joining Clever. If you were hoping to use this sync type, we recommend choosing one of the other options or using the SFTP sync instead.

 

 Skyward API LMS 

 This is a custom API sync that allows for more flexibility. It supports:

 • 
 Field mappings

• 
 Value mappings 

• 
 Filtering (for example, excluding certain schools or sections)

• 
 Staff syncing, including non-teaching staff*

• 
 Co-teachers

• 
 Student contacts (with some limitations)

 
 

 Clever can pull student contacts from the guardian endpoint and can sync multiple contacts per student. However, we don’t support custom filtering or field mapping for student contacts. For the default Skyward fields Clever uses, refer to the default field mappings .

 

 Skyward API Qmlativ (OneRoster 1.1) 

 This sync option uses the OneRoster v1.1 standard API format. Because it follows a strict standard:

 • 
 Custom field mappings aren’t supported

• 
 Filtering isn’t supported

 
 The Skyward API Qmlativ sync includes teaching staff only. It doesn’t support syncing non-teaching staff.

 

 Version

 To use the Skyward API sync, your district must be on version 05.16.10.00 or newer (any add-on update to that version is fine). This requirement applies to all Skyward API sync types, including:

 • Skyward API Qmlativ
• Skyward API LMS
• Skyward SMS 2.0 (not available for new school organizations joining Clever)
 
 

 Please note: If you’d like to sync additional data to Clever, you can do so via our automated custom data sync for auto sync districts and schools. Please reach out to Clever Support for access to your Clever SFTP Directory. You'll need to set up any automatic exports to the remote site, as Clever doesn't provide support for automation scripting. 

 

 

 
 Setting up the API sync

 Setting up the API sync depends on how your district is hosted. The instructions below apply to all Skyward API syncs and will guide you through connecting the API to Clever so your data can start syncing.

 1. Obtain API credentials Finding your API credentials will differ depending on how you’re hosted. Please follow the directions below: 

 • Secure Cloud (ISCorp) Hosted Districts : Create an IT Services service call to have ISCorp set up the Read Only API by visiting the Support Center or ISCorp Helpdesk .
• Managed Services / TN State Hosted Districts : Create an IT Services service call to have Skyward set up the Read Only API install by visiting the Support Center .
• Self-Hosted Districts : Use the API Server Launch Kit to set up the Read Only API. Optionally, districts can create an IT Services service call to have Skyward set up the Read Only API as a billable service by visiting the Support Center .
 
 We recommend checking page 19 of Skyward’s official guide on sharing API details .

 The page shows you how to find your district's specific connection link (URL). Once you've got that link, follow the steps below to connect Skyward to Clever and finish your setup.

 

 Please note: When setting up Skyward API, districts must choose OA2 - OAuth 2.0. If you experience sync issues with OAuth 2.0, please reach out to Clever Support for assistance. 

 

 2. Change sync type of Clever account If you already have a Clever account and are switching to the API sync, please contact Clever Support . Our team will need to switch your account sync type before you can move forward. If you signed up for a new account, the Skyward API sync will be the default sync type for your account and no switch needs to be made. You're free to move on to the next step! 

 3. Enter API credentials in Clever Once your account's sync type is the Skyward API sync, please take the credentials for the API and enter them into the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page in your Clever Dashboard: 

 • Web URL: API URL (does not include swagger suffix) 
• Username: Key 
• Password: Secret
 
 4. Sync Push Sync Now on the Sync Settings page to kick off the initial Skyward API sync! 

 Please note: Skyward syncs can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every six hours, but the time of day that the syncs occur is not customizable. Syncs can always be initiated at any time from your Clever Dashboard on the SIS sync > Settings tab > Sync Controls box > Sync Now button. 

 
 School Year Rollover

 Rollover behavior is dependent upon which version of the Skyward API your district is syncing from: LMS API Once you complete the rollover process in your Skyward database, those changes should reflect in the Skyward API for Clever 

 OneRoster API 

 The OneRoster API requires that students be active in order to be synced through the API. Once a school year ends, students are no longer active in the OneRoster API. This causes the synced data to no longer include enrollments or student files. You may see the following error in your Dashboard: "Encountered zero students data". Once students and other record types are marked "Active" in Skyward for the next school year, the sync will resume as expected. Alternately, Clever will pull data 21 days prior to the beginning of the district's next active academic session. For example, if school begins on August 22nd, Clever will pull the upcoming data on August 1st. To preserve access for your users between school years, Clever recommends that you put your sync on pause during the summer to prevent deletions. 

 
 Troubleshooting

 IMPORTANT The Skyward API Qmlativ integration does not support syncing non-teaching staff. The troubleshooting tips below for staff apply only to the Skyward LMS API sync. 

 If an entire record is missing, we recommend checking the following: 

 
 Record Type

 
 Troubleshooting

 
 Students 
 • Student must have an Active SchoolID
• Under Web Access, the login name and password fields need to be populated
• 'Allow student access' must be checked
 
 
 Teachers 
 • Teacher must have an Active SchoolID
• Teacher must have a status of isTeacher = true or isSubstitute = true
• PrimarySchoolID must be assigned
 
 
 Staff 
 • Staff must have an active school association
• Staff must have isTeacher = False
• Title must be blank or contain one of the following key terms: 
 • Superintendent
• Principal
• Instruction
• Curriculum
• Director
• Coordinator
• Technology
• Coach
• Counselor
• Admin
 

 
 
 Sections 
 • Is the Skyward instance on version 05.16.10.00.xx or newer?
 
 
 
 To prevent a record from syncing, ensure it doesn't meet all the criteria listed above for its record type.

 

 If the record (student, teacher, section) is in Clever but data is missing or incorrect for the record (i.e. incorrect email, missing username), navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings page. 

 Here you can view which fields Clever is pulling for each record and field. In the below example: 

 • If a teacher's email address was incorrect, confirm the email is correct in the "StaffMember_Email" field in Skyward 
• If a teacher is missing State_teacher_id, you will see that Clever is not currently pulling this data. Please contact Clever Support to see what opti

title: Bromcom API sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. Customizations 4. School year rollover 5. Default field mappings 

 

 Overview

 ✓ Runs every 4 hours ✓ Syncs staff X Doesn’t sync student contacts 

 
 Setup

 New to Clever? You can sign up here to talk to a specialist.

 The Bromcom sync integration allows Clever to connect to your Management Information System (MIS) and import data into Clever for Badges, rostering, and other services.

 To set up a Bromcom sync connection, please follow these directions:

 • Log in to https://cloudmis.bromcom.com/ and navigate to Config > Setup > Partner Applications: Partner Applications menu in Bromcom" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000XG1B&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl00000bf9A5"> 
• Your onboarding specialist will provide you with the name of the application you. See similar format below: Clever-Trust-Name 
• Once you’ve found the application, click Manage and you’ll see what access permissions Clever is requesting: 
• Click Grant and then Confirm . The application will then have a status of Installed . 
• Once the application has been installed and accepted, our team will complete steps on our backend to complete the sync setup!
 
 
 Customizations

 The Bromcom sync can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every four hours, but the time of day at which the sync occurs isn’t customizable. 

 
 School year rollover

 We automatically pause your data each year to reduce disruption during your busiest time. Pausing the sync will enable you to update data in your MIS without interrupting pupil, teacher, or staff access over the summer. Learn more about sync pauses here .

 Your syncs will be automatically paused in late July and resume in early September unless you manually modify the dates in the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab of your Dashboard.

 When you update your Pause dates, Clever also recommends updating your rollover dates in your sync settings. Learn more about rollover dates here. 

 
 Default field mappings

 Below are the default field mappings for Bromcom syncs managed by Clever. Please note:

 • 
 If a field is blank, the field is either not pulled by default or inaccessible to Clever.

• 
 If you want to change a field mapping, please contact Clever Support to learn the available options. 

• 
 If a field is not coming through with the correct value, Clever Support may need to complete a value mapping. Value mappings are common for grade, FRL_Status, ELL_Status, and IEP_Status.

 
 

 Schools

 
 Clever

 
 BromCom

 
 Notes

 
 School_ID schoolID Passed from the request context. 
 School_Name firstSchool.SchoolName Taken from the first match in school results. 
 School_Number schoolID Currently uses the ID as a placeholder for number. 
 
 

 

 Students

 
 Clever

 
 BromCom

 
 School_ID schoolID 
 Student_ID schoolID + _ + student.StudentID 
 Student_Email studentEmail 
 First_Name student.FirstName 
 Last_Name student.LastName 
 Grade student.YearGroup 
 
 

 

 Teachers

 
 Clever

 
 BromCom

 
 School_ID schoolID 
 Teacher_ID schoolID + _ + staff.StaffID 
 Teacher_Email staff.WorkEmail 
 First_Name staff.FirstName 
 Last_Name staff.LastName 
 
 

 

 Staff

 
 Clever

 
 BromCom

 
 School_ID schoolID 
 Staff_ID schoolID + _ + staffMember.StaffID 
 Staff_Email staffMember.WorkEmail 
 First_Name staffMember.FirstName 
 Last_Name staffMember.LastName 
 
 

 

 Sections

 
 Clever

 
 BromCom

 
 School_ID schoolID 
 Section_ID schoolID + _ + collection.CollectionID 
 Teacher_ID schoolID + _ + execStaffID 
 Name collection.CollectionName 
 Course_Name collection.CollectionName 
 Term_Start collection.StartDate 
 Term_End collection.EndDate 
 
 

 

 Enrollments

 
 Clever

 
 BromCom

 
 School_ID schoolID 
 Section_ID schoolID + _ + enrollment.ClassID 
 Student_ID schoolID + _ + enrollment.StudentID

title: For App Partners: Clever Integrations
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Student information systems (SIS) 3. Learning management systems (LMS) 4. Identity providers (IdPs) 

 

 Overview

 This article provides information on the various integrations available with Clever. Our teams consistently work to add new integrations. If you have questions about available integrations or those that may be in progress, please contact integrations@clever.com . 

 
 Student information systems

 Clever is compatible with all Student Information Systems, as well as any program that allows for the creation and export of CSV files (i.e., Google Sheets, Excel, etc.). Depending on the SIS, we offer the option to utilize a Clever-Managed Auto Sync, an SIS-Managed Auto Sync, or an SFTP Sync.

 For Clever-Managed Auto Syncs, Clever has written an integration that exports data directly from the SIS or its API and securely transfers it to Clever.

 For SIS-managed auto syncs, SIS partners have collaborated with Clever to develop an automated export option from their system that meets Clever’s data specifications.

title: For Clever Admins: Syncing administrators via CSV
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Creating administrators via CSV 3. Removing administrators synced via CSV 4. Viewing and editing team members 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever's SFTP data syncs allow schools to automate the creation, management, and deletion of administrators. This enhancement brings the same automation to administrative users that Clever already provides for staff, teachers, and students, making the process of provisioning administrator accounts more secure.

 Please note: This feature is only available to school organizations utilizing an SFTP sync. If you use a Clever-managed auto sync, you cannot sync administrators via CSV at this time. 

 

 
 Creating administrators via CSV

 Administrators include individuals such as central and school office staff who need to manage applications, identities, and users. 

 When creating the administrators.csv, there are three standard admin roles on Clever that can be included:

 • 
 Clever Administrator

• 
 District Help Desk

• 
 District Curriculum Lead

 
 For an outline of the permissions associated with each role above, please see this Help Center article .

 You can also create custom administrator roles directly in the Clever Dashboard. Learn more about custom roles here . If you intend to include custom roles in your administrators.csv, you must first create the role in the Dashboard.

 All assigned user roles will be displayed on the Admin team page in the Roles column.

 The administrators.csv file provides information about users that you want to have management responsibilities and permissions for your Clever account.

 The table below outlines the required headers for your administrators.csv. Headers are case-sensitive. 

 You can view example CSV files here .

 

 
 Column Header 

 
 Required 

 
 Format 

 
 Notes 

 
 Admin_id Yes String An identifier provided by the district when syncing or creating this administrator 
 Admin_email Yes Email The user's email address 
 First_name Yes String The user's first name 
 Last_name Yes String The user's last name 
 Title Yes String The user's title 
 Role Yes String 
 User’s administrative role in Clever. Acceptable role values are:

 • 
 Clever Administrator, CleverAdministrator, CA

• 
 District Curriculum Lead, DistrictCurriculumLead, DCL

• 
 District Help Desk, DistrictHelpDesk, DHD

 
 Users can also be assigned a custom role. To assign a custom role list the title of the custom role here. The custom role must exist in the Clever Dashboard to be used in the administrators.csv.

 If left blank, or containing any unrecognized value across all administrative roles, the administrator role will not be created.

 
 
 

 

 To assign multiple roles to a single administrator, please include a unique row for each role. You’ll need to use the same Admin_id for each row.

 Once your administrators.csv is ready to upload, please add this file to your remote SFTP site. As a reminder, you can find the credentials for your remote site on your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings p age in the SFTP Settings box. Once you upload the file to the remote site, a data sync will run. The sync may take up to 15 minutes to complete and for the new users to show up in your Clever Dashboard.

 For guidance on uploading files to your remote CSV site, please see the Option 2: FTP Client section of our article SFTP Syncs: Uploading data to Clever .

 

 

 

 Any administrator included in the administrators.csv that has an existing administrator account that was manually added in Clever will have their information updated to match the administrators.csv.

 

 Please note: Once a user is ‘matched,’ their account is fully managed by administrators.csv. Removing a ‘matched’ user from the administrators.csv will delete their administrator account. 

 

 If a user has an administrator account today and isn’t included in the administrators.csv, they’ll retain their existing account and permissions. You can continue to manage those users directly on the Admin team page .

 
 Removing administrators synced via CSV

 To remove administrators included in the administrators.csv, delete their data from the file and reupload it. For admins added manually via the Admin team page , then later added to the administrators.csv, deleting them from the administrators.csv will also delete their account.

 For administrators added manually via the Admin team page , their account can be deleted by clicking the trash icon next to their record on the Team page. 

 
 Viewing and editing team members

 To view any existing team members, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team . On the Admin team page, you’ll see an icon next to each user’s name indicating if a colleague manually added them via the Clever Dashboard or if an admin added their account via CSV. Hovering over the icon will provide more information.

 You can view and edit team members added manually via the Admin team page by clicking the pencil icon.

 To edit team members added via administrators.csv, please make changes within your administrators.csv and reupload to your remote site.

 If you're using an SFTP sync, you can also view manually created team members via your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > Custom data > Admins tab. If you use a Clever-managed auto sync you won't see the admin tab and will need to edit and view admins via the Admin team page . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 How will I know if the sync created an administrator? 

 • 
 You'll see new administrators on the Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page in the District Admins column or on your Clever Dashboard > User management > Admin team page. Since a data sync must complete, it may take up to 15 minutes for users to appear. 

 
 How will administrators be notified that their account was created? 

 • 
 If the user is having an administrative account created for them, they’ll be sent an email automatically. If the user has an existing administrator account, then they won’t be notified.

 
 Is there a limit to the number of roles a single administrator can hold? 

 • 
 No, one user can be assigned every role in your Clever account.

 
 What is the user experience for an administrator who holds multiple administrative roles? 

 • 
 A user who has multiple administrative roles will see every element of Clever assigned to them based on the totality of their permissions.

 
 Can a user be an administrator and a non-administrator role (i.e., Teacher or Staff?) 

 • 
 Yes. A user can hold multiple user types in Clever.

 
 What if I include a user in the admini strators.csv but d on’t see them on the Last attempted sync page or the Team page? 

 • There's been an error. Please check the file in the Download Changes column on your Last attempted sync page to review any formatting errors. You will need to resolve the errors in your administrators.csv and reupload the file to the remote site. If this doesn’t resolve the issue, please reach out to Clever Support, and we’ll help troubleshoot.
 
 What if I accidentally upload a blank or inaccurate administrators.csv? 

 • 
 No problem! Please update and reupload your file. If you need assistance, contact Clever Support, and we’ll help you troubleshoot.

 
 What if I attempt to assign an administrator a role that doesn’t currently exist in my Clever account? 

 • 
 The administrator will not sync to Clever. You must include a role that exists in your Clever account.

 
 I noticed that School Tech Leads are listed on the Team page. Are STLs considered administrators? 

 • 
 While you can see STLs on the Team page, they are managed through the Staff file, not the Administrators file. Please don’t include anyone in the administrators.csv that shouldn’t have an administrative role.

 
 Will existing administrators be deleted through this process? 

 • 
 You can delete existing administrators through this process, but

title: For Clever Admin: Short-term substitute access to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to enable this feature 3. The Clever Admin Experience 4. Teacher Experience 5. Substitute Experience 6. Resources for teachers and substitutes 7. Rollout Processes 8. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Our new Substitute Access to Clever feature makes it easy for teachers to securely provide temporary access to Clever for their substitute. Instead of sharing their personal login credentials, teachers can now generate a Sub Badge that allows short-term substitutes to log in and view the resources and Teacher Page they have provided them access to. This allows teachers to maintain secure credentials, while also giving substitutes the tools they need to manage their online resources effectively for the day.

 Please note: This feature is for short-term subs (up to 14 days). If you need longer coverage, like for parental leave, please see this article on options for longer-term subs . 

 

 
 How to enable this feature

 To enable this feature for your teachers:

 • 
 Log in to your Clever Dashboard. 

• 
 Navigate to Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings .

• 
 Click the Substitute Access tab.

• 
 Toggle the Enable Substitute Access to enabled.

• Choose to enable for all schools in your district, or select the schools where you would like Substitute Access to be available for teachers by clicking the blue Edit button that appears after you've turned the Enable Substitute Access button on. 
• You can create a notification to inform teachers of the substitute access feature.
• You also have the option to customize which applications substitutes will have access to. Use this feature to restrict access to applications with particularly sensitive data like test scores, IEP information etc. Use the block button to block individual applications, or use the checkboxes next to applications and the Block X selected button to block them in bulk. 
 
 Please note: For security purposes, Clever blocks PowerSchool and Google applications by default. These applications can’t be removed from the block list. 

 

 

 
 The Clever Admin Experience

 If a teacher is unexpectedly absent and cannot generate a Substitute Badge, you can impersonate the teacher to create one on their behalf using the Access Portal as feature and following the steps below under the Teacher Experience section. 

 If a Substitute Badge is lost or misused, you can impersonate the teacher to void the Badge at any time by impersonating the teacher using the Access Portal as feature and clicking “Void Badge” in the top right-hand corner of the teacher’s Teacher Page.

 

 Clever Tip: School Tech Leads can also generate and void Substitute Badges using their impersonation tool and following the steps in the Teacher Experience section below. 

 

 
 Teacher Experience

 Teachers can provide access to substitutes so they can access their resources and Teacher Page by following the steps below:

 • 
 Log in to your Clever Portal. 

• 
 Navigate to Pages > Teacher Pages on the left side of your Clever Portal.

• 
 Click on the Teacher Page you want to provide the substitute access to.

• 
 In the top right-hand corner, click Share with substitute. 

 
 

 • This will take you to a preview of what the substitute will see when they log in with their Badge. You'll see in the image below that Google is greyed out. For security purposes, the subset of “restricted impersonation” apps won't be accessible. You'll find more information on which applications have restricted impersonation below .
 
 

 • To generate a Sub Badge that will provide login access for the substitute, click Create Substitute Badge and select the date you want the Badge to expire. The expiration date must be within 14 days.
 
 

 • Once the date is selected, click Create Substitute Badge to confirm the date.
• Now you are ready to click Download Substitute Badge to download the Badge to your computer.
 
 

 
 Substitute Experience
