 Substitutes will scan their Sub Badge to access Clever and will be limited to viewing only the Teacher Page that the teacher provided them access to. From there, they can log in to and use any applications available on that page as the main teacher. For security reasons, certain "restricted impersonation" apps won't be accessible to substitutes, but students will still be able to use these apps from their own Clever Portal. By default, Clever prevents users from accessing the Google suite applications (e.g., Google Sheets, Google Docs, etc.) and PowerSchool. You can add restricted applications on the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Substitute Access tab. 

 

 

 

 

 
 Resources for teachers and substitutes 

 Teachers will see instructions about how to use Substitute Access when they generate a Badge for substitutes. 

 

 

 The substitute Badge includes instructions on how to log in to Clever:

 

 

 Here are additional resources you can share with teachers and substitutes to help them use this new tool: 

 Teachers: 

 • Teacher experience video 
• 
 Clickable walkthrough 

• 
 Handout with instructions 

• 
 For Teachers: Substitute Access to Clever 

 
 Substitutes: 

 • 
 For Substitutes: Substitute Access to Clever 

 
 

 
 Rollout Processes

 Here are a few simple steps to help you introduce the Substitute Access feature. Following these steps will help your team feel supported as they learn about the feature and confident using it on their own.

 • 
 Gain Leadership Support: Work with school administrators to implement the feature and highlight its importance.

• 
 Conduct Training: Offer short hands-on training sessions to show teachers how to create a substitute page and generate a Badge. This gives them time to practice and helps them feel confident using the feature. 

• 
 Standardize the Process: Pick one approach for your district, either have teachers share their existing Teacher Pages with subs, or train teachers to create a dedicated substitute page with a consistent set of resources your district wants all subs to have access to. 

• 
 Involve Key School Staff: Train anyone who works with substitutes, like principals or front office staff, so they can check in substitutes and hand out the Badge easily.

• 
 Expand and Communicate: Partner with your relevant regional offices to include the feature to substitute onboarding and training so that substitutes understand the benefits of using the feature in their role.

 
 Click here to learn how Bend-La Pine Schools implemented substitute access and now uses it to support their substitute teachers.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Can subs use a Badge to access/log into their Chromebook? 

 • 
 Not at this time, no. We recommend providing guest access to a device and then having a sub go to clever.com /badges.

 
 What if a sub’s device does not have a camera? 

 • 
 At this time, Sub Badges are only available as QR codes, so the sub’s device must have some sort of camera to scan the Badge.

 
 How should my teachers provide the Sub Badge to their substitute? 

 • 
 We suggest including it with their sub plans!

 
 Will subs be able to access everything the teacher can in the third party app? 

 • 
 Yes, any applications on the Teacher Page will be accessible to the sub, and the sub will be logged into the application as the main teacher.

 
 Can I block applications that I don’t want substitutes to access? 

 • Yes! By default, Clever blocks sub-access to Google and PowerSchool. You can add additional applications on the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Substitute Access tab . It's not possible to remove Google and PowerSchool from this list.
 
 How long can a teacher give subs access to their Teacher Page? 

 • 
 Teachers can grant subs access for up to 14 days, allowing them to set a custom date within that timeframe.

 
 A teacher is unexpectedly absent. How could I, as an administrator, generate a Sub Badge for them? 

 • 
 Admini

title: For Clever Admin: Instructions to remove Clever+ Extension
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Remove the Clever+ extension from Google Admin console 3. Remove the Clever+ extension from Active Directory Group Policies 

 

 Overview

 If you need to remove the Clever+ Chrome extension because you no longer wish to gather edtech analytics data, please follow the instructions below. 

 Please remember to install the Clever public extension after you’ve removed the Clever+ extension. The public extension helps Clever features work better, including Clever's SSO (Saved Passwords) and teacher login tools (like opening a resource on student devices or logging students out of Clever). 

 There are two ways to remove the extension: from your Google Admin console, or from Active Directory Group Policies (depending on your district's setup). 

 

 
 Remove the Clever+ extension from Google Admin console

 • From your Google Admin console, go to Devices > Chrome > Apps and extensions > Users and browsers. 
• Select the organization unit (OU) that you want to allow or block the extension for.
 • Note: The OU will inherit the settings of the parent OU, but you can override per sub OU.
 

• Select the Clever+ extension.
• In the installation policy column, select Block .
• Click Save. 
 
 

 
 Remove the Clever+ extension from Active Directory Group Policies

 • In the Group Policy Editor, open the Google Chrome template you had created for the Clever+ extension.
 
 

 • Browse to Google > Google Chrome > Extension > Configure Extension installation blocklist .
• In the settings, select Enabled .
• Click Show. 
• Enter the app ID of the Clever+ extension.

title: For Clever Admins: Clever Badges and cybersecurity
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Security review: Threat modeling 3. Behind Badge security: Technical specifications 4. Clever Badges versus standard credentials: How Badges help security in schools 5. Security recommendations for Badges 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQS) 

 

 Overview

 We know that cybersecurity and digital citizenship are leading priorities for schools. At Clever, we want to partner with you in securing all of your users’ digital identities and providing a seamless authentication experience.

 Clever Badges are well known for simplifying student logins, and our teams have taken measures to ensure simple is still secure.

 

 This article outlines the measures we take to build products that are secure by default in our product development process, the safeguards in place for our Clever Badges system, ongoing steps to maintain security across our platform, and recommendations to help you maintain security when implementing Clever Badges with your users.

 As always, we recommend evaluating your current cybersecurity practices, environment, and risk tolerance when making decisions for your school regarding authentication and digital identity management. 

 
 Security review: Threat modeling

 Threat modeling is a structured process for identifying security risks in a system. With threat modeling, a team considers what a product will do, who will use it, and what may go wrong. Clever uses threat modeling when developing our products, specifically keeping in mind that many of our end users are students and teachers.

 For Clever Badges, this means that our teams continue to analyze the various types of Badges, how they’ll be used for logins, and the users utilizing them. For example, a student can use a Clever Badge to authenticate into just the Clever Portal, or an administrator may configure additional settings so that the student can use their Badge to log into a Chromebook and the Clever Portal.

 Alternatively, students or teachers can use Classroom Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) Badges as a second authentication factor to log into Clever or a device. There are a number of ways to utilize Clever Badges, and our team has worked to consider all use cases during threat modeling.

 To add an additional level of security, Clever runs a bug bounty program , and any researchers who report a security vulnerability in Clever Badges will receive a cash payout. We also perform code reviews and audits for all security-sensitive feature changes.

 Check out our Security recommendations section below to learn more about how our team has considered Badges security across various use cases and settings. 

 
 Behind Badge security: Technical specifications

 Clever Badges are QR codes that encode four pieces of data:

 • 
 a unique identifier that maps to the student

• 
 a version number for the badge

• 
 a sequence number that increments each time a new badge is created (login Badges only)

• 
 a token consisting of 12 bytes of entropy

 
 Think of the unique identifier as a ‘username’ and the token as a ‘password.’ Together, they log the user in. The token is the security mechanism that prevents an attacker from guessing the QR code.

 Due to the complexity of the token, forging a Clever Badge is extremely difficult. 12 bytes of entropy means that, on average, you’d need 2^(12*8)/2 attempts to guess the token in a Clever Badge – approximately 40 octillion attempts. Even if someone made one million guesses per second (which we would detect in our logs and actively block) it would take over a quadrillion years to guess. A Clever Badge is approximately as secure as a random 15-character alphanumeric password with symbols.

 A cryptographically secure random number generation algorithm is used to generate the token. The token is stored on our servers as a SHA256 hash, which helps mitigate timing attacks.

 Using client-side Javascript to parse the values from the QR code image from the web camera, data is transferred for logins to the server side over a TLS-encrypted connection, where they are validated. No video data is sent to our servers. 

 On the server, the validation process first validates that the data is well-formatted, looks up the record associated with the student's unique identifier and sequence number, and verifies that the token matches using SHA256. If all elements are confirmed, Clever sets a cookie on the user’s browser, allowing it to use OAuth 2.0 to sign in to other applications.

 

 
 Clever Badges versus standard credentials: How Badges improve security in schools

 PreK-12 schools and districts have a wide range of users with an even wider range of technical competencies. For the youngest learners and those with special needs, physically typing in and remembering complex passwords presents a very real challenge when accessing digital education resources.

 To mitigate these login difficulties, many teachers and administrators resort to solutions like writing usernames and passwords on post-its, creating simple passwords (i.e., 12345), or using the same username and password for an entire class. These solutions create security gaps that range from other students and teachers stealing login credentials to bad actors easily guessing simple passwords and gaining access to a user’s account.

 With a Clever Badge, the primary security gap is if the physical Badge is stolen. For young students, the threat of an insecure username and password is much greater than that of a physical Badge. Simply put, Clever Badges have a ‘physical access’ attack surface that is similar to that of passwords printed and distributed by teachers, but they are more resistant to brute forcing or password guessing. Clever doesn’t offer Badges for teachers because the most secure method for a teacher to log in is a complex username and password; however this same complexity of login is not accessible to a young student.

 Clever requires a PIN code for students in seventh grade and above. Additionally, schools can add a PIN code to their Badge settings for students below seventh grade. 

 
 Security recommendations for Badges

 

 Clever Badges 

 • 
 For security reasons, Clever Badges can only be utilized by students. Teachers should log in with a secure username and password.

• 
 Clever recommends that you void and reprint badges during your annual rollover period, such as the summer or winter. 

• 
 Clever requires students in grades seven and above to use a PIN code with their Badge (with exceptions for students with an IEP or ELL status) to provide an additional layer of security.

• 
 Teachers can also bulk print Clever Badges for their students which helps reduce the steps for Badge distribution. They can void Badges as well. We recommend sharing our article For Teachers: Clever Badges for students with your teachers so they can familiarize themselves with Badge management options to minimize risk if a student’s Badge is lost.

• 
 Just like passwords, Clever Badges are considered sensitive login information. Never share Badges via an insecure medium such as email. Badges should be physically handed to the user.

 
 Classroom MFA Badges 

 Multi-factor authentication includes three categories: something you know, something you have, or something you are. When configuring MFA, each method should come from a different category. For example, if you log into your bank account with a username and password and a fingerprint reader, you are logging in with something you know and something you are.

 • 
 Students shouldn’t use a Clever login Badge and a Classroom MFA Badge as both factors when using multi-factor authentication. This login would combine two items you have, which is not the best practice for MFA.

 • 
 For students who use a Clever login Badge as their primary method for logging in, we recommend picture logins as a second factor.

 

• 
 For security reasons, only student MFA Badges can be bulk printed by Clever

title: Clever Roles: Switching between Clever accounts
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Login process 3. Switching accounts 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Some Clever users have multiple types of accounts to accommodate the various roles and permissions they need within their district or school. Multiple accounts are often required when users work in various positions to support their schools.

 For example, some teachers work as classroom teachers and IT support persons for their school. In this case, these users often have both a teacher and a staff account. They may use their teacher account to manage and interact with their students and the staff account to help manage sharing permissions at their school site.

 Similarly, many administrators choose to add themselves as teachers in Clever to better understand the teacher experience. They’ll use their admin account for any administrative duties (troubleshooting logins, adjusting sharing permissions, adding applications) and use their teacher account to test out Teacher Pages or the Clever Library.

 Clever empowers these users with multiple accounts to easily switch between accounts within their Clever Portal.

 

 
 Login process

 You need to take no special steps when logging in to switch between your Clever accounts! As long as your various accounts have the same email address associated with them (or sis_id if your email address isn’t syncing to Clever), the accounts will automatically be linked within Clever.

 Generally, when you log in on a device for the first time, you’ll automatically log into the account with the lowest level permissions. For example, you'll be logged into your teacher account if you are both a district administrator and a teacher. Each time following this, Clever will remember which account you were logged into last on the device and log you into that account. 

 At times, Clever may be able to identify that you are attempting to log in to a specific account. For example, if you use the district administrator login page, Clever will assume you are trying to log in to your admin account and automatically log into that account. You can still switch accounts once logged in. 

 
 Switching accounts

 While Clever admin and teacher accounts may look very different, it can be trickier to differentiate between a staff and teacher portal! Simply click on your name in the top right corner to determine which account you are currently logged into.

 To switch between accounts, click on which account you’d like to switch to. See this in action in the video below:

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 What if I do not see my other account listed in the drop-down? 

 • 
 Clever wasn’t able to match your accounts based on the data syncing. Please work with a Clever Admin to review the data in Clever and ensure you have the same email address and sis_id on both accounts.

 
 How does Clever know which account to log me into? 

 • 
 By default, the first time you log in on a device, you’ll be logged into the account with the lowest permissions. For example, if you have a staff and teacher account, you’ll be logged into your teacher account. Each time after the first login, as long as you are on the same device, Clever will remember the account in which you ended your last session.

• 
 At times, Clever may be able to identify that you are attempting to log in to a specific account. For example, if you use the district administrator login page, Clever will assume you are trying to log in to your admin account and automatically log into that account. You can still switch accounts once logged in.

 
 How does Clever associate multiple user accounts? 

 • 
 Clever references email address or sis_id to match your accounts.

 
 What if my district or school utilizes Classroom MFA? 

 • 
 Clever will prompt you for your MFA upon login but not when switching accounts. Clever will enforce the highest-ranking MFA policy for a multi-role user. For example, you will receive an admin MFA prompt if you are both a Clever Admin and a teacher.

 
 What if I am a Clever Admin that uses Clever’s MFA when logging in? 

 • 
 Clever will enforce the highest-ranking MFA policy for a multi-role user. If you have Clever’s admin MFA configured, Clever will require your Clever Admin MFA method.

 
 If I disable access control for Clever logins for one of the non-DA roles (staff or teacher), can I still toggle between my role as a Clever Admin and a disabled user type? 

 • Yes, you can toggle between all roles if any of your roles are allowed to log in. For example, if teacher logins are disabled via access control, but you have both a Clever Admin and a teacher role, you'll be able to log in as a Clever Admin, and toggle to your teacher role still.

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Clever passwords - Log in with Clever (updated)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up Clever passwords • To setup Clever passwords for your district • Configuring credentials for students, teachers, and staff • Configuring password reset and account recovery options • Static credentials 3. District details and login hints • Login hints • Customize login options • Tech support contact 4. Resetting passwords • Clever admins resetting individual user passwords • Clever admins resetting all district passwords • Students, teachers, and staff resetting their passwords 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 

 In addition to other Clever-supported Identity Providers (IdPs) like Google and Active Directory , districts and schools can also choose to utilize Clever passwords for Clever single sign-on (SSO). With Clever passwords, administrators determine what login credentials students, teachers, and staff will use to log in to Clever.

 

 When users navigate to your school's Clever login page, they will enter their username and password. In cases where a district or school uses multiple IdPs, users will select 'Log in with Clever' to enter their credentials.

 In June 2024, the Clever team began implementing updates to Clever passwords to improve the district administrator experience and enhance security.

 Please continue to reference this article if your Clever passwords setup on your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page is as pictured below:

 

 If your Clever passwords setup looks like the image below, please reference our Single Sign-on (SSO): Clever passwords - Login with Clever (classic) article: 

 

 

 If you'd like to learn more about the classic and updated Clever passwords interfaces and how they compare, please see our Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) section . To upgrade to the updated interface, please contact Clever Support .

 

 Please note: • Regardless of the version of Clever passwords you’re utilizing, Clever passwords are provisioned once and are not dynamically updated to match newly synced data from your Student Information System (SIS). District Administrators must manage passwords after the first provision. Please see Static Credentials and Resetting Passwords for more on this! • Provisioning Clever passwords takes approximately 15 minutes after they are initially configured or reset by editing. 

 

 
 Setting up Clever passwords

 To setup Clever passwords for your district: 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings 
• Select Add Login Method 
• Select Clever Passwords Authentication 
• Select the user types that will log in to Clever with Clever passwords. 
• Work through steps 2-4 to enter the credentials you'd like for your students, teachers, and/or staff to enter when logging in to Clever. For guidance on choosing usernames and passwords see our sections below on configuring credentials and setting passwords . Click here for help with creating custom usernames/passwords. 
• You will have different setting options for each user type. Look through them and check off the ones you'd like to set for that user type. 
• Once usernames/passwords and settings are configured, you will be able to review them on the last step. 
• Click Preview accounts and Provision these new credentials.
• After completing the initial setup, Clever will automatically create or remove accounts for any users based on the data syncing from your SIS. Users' accounts will be created or deleted shortly after the SIS sync completes with Clever.
 
 
 Configuring credentials for students, teachers, and staff

 There are a variety of usernames and passwords available based on the data that is synced to Clever from your Student Information System (SIS). Clever does recommend using the same provisioning configuration for both teachers and staff so that your multirole users have a consistent experience. To learn more about the multirole experience, see this FAQ .

 Username Options: 

 • student number / teacher number / staff ID
• email address
• SIS ID
• or the district username uploaded in your sync
 • For this option, you'll need to make sure your district has usernames populated in your SIS that are syncing to Clever. If you're syncing data to Clever via SFTP, please see the Clever SFTP Specifications for instructions on adding usernames. If you're using an Auto Sync, please contact Clever support so we can assist with adding this information into the Clever sync. 
 

 
 Password options: 

 • Student number / teacher number / staff ID
• SIS ID
• Birthday in the following formats: MM/DD/YYYY; MMDDYYYY; M/D/YYYY
• Random String (ex: *kJkJ4J5)
• Random Word
• District password uploaded in your sync
 • For this option, you'll need to make sure your district has passwords populated in your SIS that are syncing to Clever. If you're syncing data to Clever via SFTP, please see the Clever SFTP Specifications for instructions on adding usernames. If you're using an Auto Sync, please contact Clever support so we can assist with adding this information into the Clever sync. 
 

• Custom password (these can be an individual word(s), or a custom formula
 • Learn more about creating custom formulas here .
 

• IDM generated password - Districts that use Clever IDM can use the password they use for Google, Entra, or Active Directory for Clever Passwords also. Learn more about Clever IDM .
 
 Password requirements for individual passwords: 

 • Students: 6 character minimum / Teachers or Staff: 8 character minimum
• Not the user’s current username
• Not the user’s current email address
• If the new password has been made public in an unrelated data breach, it will not be allowed
• Not the same as the current password
• Passwords cannot start/end with a space
• Characters cannot repeat more than 3 times
• Passwords cannot contain any part of the user’s name
 
 Password advice: 

 • Combine random words to create a secure but memorable password. For example: “truck-dog-fly”
• Password exports are available for a short, limited amount of time if enabled. Refer to step 6 to learn how to enable this. 
 
 Please note: You can also complement your Clever passwords setup with an SSO Policy that dictates exactly which users can (or can’t) utilize Clever passwords for logins. Learn more here . 

 

 
 Configuring password reset and account recovery options

 Administrators can always reset student or teacher passwords individually or by user type. Please see Resetting Passwords for more on how to reset a user’s password.

 You can also customize password reset settings for staff, teachers, and students. Password reset options can be configured via your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Password Settings tab . In Step 1: Select Identity Providers, you’ll select the IdP for which you want to configure password and account reset settings for. For Clever passwords, select Clever . 

 In Step 2 : Select user groups , you’ll be able to check boxes to determine if users can change their own passwords, as well as delegate if you’d like teachers and staff to be able to reset student passwords. You can also configure settings so that only students in specific grades or schools can reset their passwords.

 

 In the next steps, you’ll configure password requirements and account recovery settings for each selected user group (students, teachers, or staff).

 

 Setting up account recovery methods will allow your users to receive either an SMS or email or answer security questions to regain access to their Clever account if they forget their Clever passwords credentials.

 If your schools use Classroom MFA, and SMS or voice calls are used as MFA methods in one of your district's MFA policies, SMS can’t be selected as a recovery method. If you select SMS or email as an account recovery method, you’ll also have the option to choose a Clever field as the default for account recovery. For example, if you select email as the account recovery opti

title: For Clever Admin: Account claim email notifications for new users
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up notifications 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 In addition to setting up account claim for new users, you can also trigger an automated email. This feature is exclusively available to Clever IDM customers and those on our updated Clever Passwords SSO method .

 This article will guide you through the process of setting up automated emails, so your users are promptly and securely informed about their new accounts. If you’re only looking for account claim (without a notification) see this article . 

 
 Setting up notifications

 • Head to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Account Notifications. 
• Choose a user type to start with.
 
 

 • You have two options:
 • 
 Send an email informing users of their new account, without providing a link to access the account. In this case, your users will need to receive their login credentials in some other way, like directly from their supervisor or teacher, or by using Account Claim .

• 
 Send an email with a custom secure link for users to set up their new account.

 

 
 

 • If you chose to create an account claim link as part of the notification, you’ll need to determine the password format. Skip this step if you chose Account creation email. Users can either set their own password or have one generated for them. 
 • 
 If users set their own passwords, it must meet the complexity requirements your district has set in Password Settings .

• 
 A generated password uses the template you determined in Clever IDM (for Google, Active Directory and/or Entra ID) or Clever Passwords if using the updated version of Clever Passwords .

 

 
 

 • 
 If you chose to create an account claim link, decide how many days before the claim link expires. After expiration, the link will no longer grant access to see or set the user’s password.

• 
 Select the destination(s). A separate email will be sent for each destination you select. If you would like all destinations to have synced passwords, we recommend only sending a notification for the destination your users use to log into Clever, and ensuring that users must reset password on first login. 

 
 

 • Select the data field from your sync that contains the end user’s existing email address. Please confirm that it’s accurate, as this is where email notifications will go for new users. Be sure to select a field containing an email address that your end users already have access to.
 
 

 • Review the email template and add an optional custom message. This is the perfect place to include any additional instructions and recommended next steps for your users.
• Click Enable Account Notifications for [user type] to turn notifications on. Then, click Complete setup .
 
 

 

 Once an account is created, users will receive an email notification (with or without their claim link, depending on how you configured it).

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 

 Can we use account claim and account notifications? 

 • 
 Yes: you might set up notifications for users who have an accurate email on file (like teachers). On the other hand, you could use account claim without notifications for users like new students who might not have a reliable email account on file with the district.

 
 

 How do I ensure that my end users have synchronized passwords across destinations? 

 • 
 Determine the user’s login method to access Clever. For example, do your user’s log into Clever using Google, SAML, or Clever passwords?

• 
 Configure a notification for the destination that users will use to access Clever.

• 
 In Password Settings, ensure that users can reset all destinations that you want in sync.

• 
 In Password Settings, ensure that users will have to reset their password on their first login.

 
 User experience: 

 • 
 Users will receive the notification for the destination they use to log into Clever allowing them to see or set their password for that destination

• 
 Users will log into Clever using the password that they set or saw in step 1

• 
 Users will be prompted to reset their password. When they reset their password in Clever, the password for every enabled destination in Password Settings for that user type will now be synchronized. 

 
 

 Why should I choose a data field that contains verified email addresses? 

 • 
 The recipient of the email will receive notification of the creation of their new accounts and may, if configured to send a link, gain access to that account. Using unverified email addresses may grant access to the incorrect recipient.

 
 

 Why should I choose a data field that contains an email address that users already have access to? 

 • 
 Your end users need to already have access to the email address that you are sending the notification to. For example, if you select a field containing the Gmail address of your users to grant them access to their Google account, they will never be able to log into that Google account to read that Gmail.

 
 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admin: Setting up account claim for your users

title: Troubleshooting: Clever passwords login issues
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Troubleshooting 3. Troubleshooting Checklist 4. Resetting passwords 

 Overview 

 In addition to other Clever-supported Identity Providers (IdPs) like Google and Active Directory , districts can also choose to utilize Clever passwords for Clever single sign-on (SSO). With Clever passwords, district admins determine which login credentials should be used for students, teachers, and staff. These credentials are configured from the Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. When district users navigate to your district's Clever Portal URL , they will enter their username and password. In cases where a district uses multiple IdPs, users will select Log in with Clever to enter their credentials. 

 Occasionally, users will have issues logging in, and troubleshooting is required by a Clever Admin. This article details the recommended steps for troubleshooting and resolving Clever Passwords user login issues. 

 For information about single sign-on (SSO) with Clever passwords, including how to set it up, please read this article . 

 

 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Occasionally errors do occur with Clever passwords logins. Here are the common errors that we identify, and how to resolve them: 

 • Wrong Password • The user input the wrong password associated with their account. Please reset their password and have them attempt to login again.
 

• Username matches to multiple users in Clever • Please check your data in Clever to ensure that each user has a unique username for Clever passwords logins.
 

• The ID used during login doesn't match any user in Clever 
 • There isn't a user with the given username in Clever. Please check your SSO Settings and the user's profile page to confirm their Clever passwords username.
 

 
 

 
 Troubleshooting Checklist 

 In addition to the Troubleshoot login tool mentioned above, you can use these tips if you need additional support:

 • The user must have an active Clever account.
• The fields the district is using for username and password for Clever passwords must be populated for the student, teacher, or staff. Review the current fields being used on the SSO settings page.
• Confirm if the values in the above fields are unique for each user. The username must be unique .
• Try resetting their password using the directions found here .
• If resetting the password does not work, it is possible credentials need to be re-synced. To re-sync credentials, follow the directions found here . 
• If a user is excluded from using Clever passwords via SSO Policy , they'll see a "sso_policy_exclusion error" if they try to log in, which indicates that the user entered the correct Clever password username and password combination to log in, but the user isn't allowed to use Clever passwords to login. This will also show up in login logs, which are explained below. 
 
 

 
 

 Resetting Passwords 

 
 
 It is important to note that a fter the initial setup of Clever passwords for your district, user passwords will not be dynamically updated as new or updated data is synced to Clever from your SIS. 

 • For example, If your district has set up student passwords to be their birthdates, and then you update a student's birthdate in your SIS, their password will not automatically be updated in Clever to reflect the new birthdate. 
 
 To resolve this, it is sometimes necessary to manually update a student or teacher's password in Clever or run a password reset for all students, teachers, or staff. Depending on your current SSO settings for each user type, the password reset option might be available for some users to reset their own passwords or for teachers to reset student passwords. Users who reset their own passwords must have an email address in Clever for the password reset to work. 

 Reset Password (Directions for Teacher/Student) 

 • From the district's login page, select Get help logging in! 
• Select Reset your password and enter your school-provided email address
• An email will be sent with a link to reset your password
 
 Please note: If your district has enabled this feature, teachers can reset student passwords by following the instructions in this article: For Teachers: Troubleshooting student login issues. 

 
 Reset Password (Directions for Clever Admin) 

 To reset individual user passwords: 

Clever Admins can manually reset passwords for individual students, teachers, and staff members.
 • Use the search bar at the top of the Clever Dashboard to search for and view the student or teacher's profile
• In the top right, click Support tools > Set new Clever password 
 
 

 

 • Enter a new password and then confirm that password
• If you wish to require users to change their password upon their next login, check off the box to do so
• Click Save 
 
 To reset all credentials back to the default setting by user type (student, teacher, or staff): 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page. 
• Select the Edit settings option for Clever passwords 
• If you would like to reset all student passwords, check the box Reset all student usernames and passwords now . If you do not want student passwords updated, leave the box unchecked
• Select Next 
• You can also choose to reset teacher and staff credentials in steps 2 and 3 if desired 
• Select Next until you get to the option to Save 
• Please allow up to 15 minutes for Clever to reset all passwords. Once complete, users will be able to log in with their default credentials.

title: For Substitutes: Substitute Access to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How it works 

 

 Overview

 The new Substitute Access feature in Clever allows you to easily access the resources and Teacher Page set up by the teacher for the day. Using a unique Sub Badge provided in the teacher’s sub plans, you’ll be able to log in and view only the specific materials and tools needed to manage the classroom’s online resources. This ensures that you have everything you need to support the students without needing the teacher’s personal login credentials. 

 
 How it works

 On the day you are subbing, follow the steps below to gain access to the teacher’s Teacher Page and resources:

 • 
 Navigate to www.clever.com/badges . 

• 
 Scan your Substitute Badge that the teacher provided to log in.

• That’s it! Now just click on whichever resource you need to access for the class. 
 
 When you click on a resource, if it is an application, you will be logged in as the teacher you are subbing for. Please proceed with caution and make sure to only access the things needed for instruction!

 You may notice that some applications are grey and inaccessible. These are applications that the school’s Clever Admin has chosen not to provide substitute access to for security reasons. Please contact your school's IT team if you need to access one of these applications.

 

 

 

 Please note: Google Suite and PowerSchool applications are always restricted applications for security reasons.

title: For App Partners: Substitute Access to Clever
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. The app experience 

 

 Overview

 Our new Substitute Access to Clever feature makes it easy for teachers to securely provide temporary access to Clever for their substitute. Instead of sharing their personal login credentials, teachers can now generate a Sub Badge that allows substitutes to log in and view the resources and Teacher Page they have provided them access to. This allows teachers to maintain secure credentials while also giving substitutes the tools they need to manage their online resources effectively for the day. 

 
 The app experience

 If a district toggles this feature on, subs will then be able to log in as the teacher in Clever and access their applications. The access given to the substitute will only last for 14 days and will auto-expire, unless the teacher suspends access before the auto-expire date.

 Since this is just providing access to the sub as the main teacher while they are out, the licensing for the district will not be affected by this new feature.

 

 District and school Clever Admins have the option to customize which applications substitute teachers will be able to access. They may block any application if they see substitute access as a security concern. Blocking an application will only block the substitute from impersonating into the application as the teacher. Students and the primary teacher will still have access. If the substitute has access through their own Clever Portal, they will also maintain this access.

title: Single Sign-On (SSO): Clever passwords - Log in with Clever (classic)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up Clever passwords • Configuring credentials for students, teachers, and staff • Configuring password reset options • Static credentials 3. District details and login hints • Login hints • Customize login options • Tech support contact 4. Resetting passwords • Clever admins resetting individual user passwords • Clever admins resetting all district passwords • Students, teachers, and staff resetting their passwords 5. Upgrading your Clever passwords setup 

 

 Overview

 In addition to other Clever-supported Identity Providers (IdPs) like Google and Active Directory , districts can utilize Clever passwords for Clever single sign-on (SSO). With Clever passwords, Administrators determine what login credentials should be used for students, teachers, and staff. 

 When users navigate to your school's Clever login page , they will enter their username and password. In cases where a school uses multiple IdPs, users will select Log in with Clever to enter their credentials. 

 In June 2024, the Clever team began implementing updates to Clever passwords to improve the administrator experience and enhance security.

 Please continue to reference this article if your Clever passwords setup on your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings page is as pictured below:

 

 

 Clever Tip: To learn more about the differences between classic Clever passwords and updated Clever passwords, please see this section. Clever does recommend upgrading to the newer version of Clever passwords. To do so, please contact Clever Support . 

 

 If your Clever passwords setup looks like the image below, please reference our article Single Sign-on (SSO): Clever passwords - Login with Clever (updated). 

 

 

 

 Please note: • Clever passwords are provisioned once and are not dynamically updated to match newly synced data from your Student Information System (SIS). District Administrators must manage passwords after the first-time provision. Please see Static Credentials and Resetting Passwords for more on this! • Provisioning Clever passwords takes approximately 15 minutes after they are initially configured or when reset by editing. 

 

 
 Setting up Clever passwords

 To setup Clever passwords for your district:

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings 
• Select Add Login Method 
• Select Clever Passwords Authentication 
• Follow the prompts to set up Clever passwords for each user type. If you need assistance configuring usernames or passwords please see the sections below.
 
 

 
 Configuring credentials for students, teachers, and staff 

 There are a variety of usernames and passwords available based on the data that is synced to Clever from your Student Information System (SIS).

 Username options: 

 • student number / teacher number / staff ID
• email address
• SIS ID
• or the district username uploaded in your sync 
• For this option, you'll need to make sure your district has usernames populated in your SIS that are syncing to Clever. If you're syncing data to Clever via SFTP, please see the Clever SFTP Specifications for instructions on adding usernames. If you're using an Auto Sync, please contact Clever support so we can assist with adding this information into the Clever sync. 
 
 Password options: 

 • Student number / teacher number / staff ID
• District password uploaded in your sync
• SIS ID
• Birthday in the following formats: MM/DD/YYYY; MMDDYYYY; M/D/YYYY
• Custom password (these can be an individual word(s), or a custom formula
 • Learn more about custom configurations here !
 

 
 Password Requirements for individual passwords: 

 • Students: 6 character minimum / Teachers or Staff: 8 character minimum
• Not the user’s current username
• Not the user’s current email address
• If the new password has been made public in an unrelated data breach, it will not be allowed
• Not the same as the current password
 
 Password Advice: 

 • Combine random words to create a secure but memorable password. For example: “truck-dog-fly”
 
 Please note: You can also complement your Clever passwords setup with an SSO Policy that dictates exactly which users can (or can’t) utilize Clever passwords for logins. Learn more here . 

 

 
 Configuring password reset options

 District Administrators can always reset student or teacher passwords individually or by user type. Please see Resetting Passwords for more on how to reset a user’s password.

 You can also customize password reset settings for staff, teachers, and students. Password reset options can be configured via your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > SSO settings > Password Settings tab .

 You’ll be able to check boxes to determine if users can change their own passwords, as well as delegate if you’d like teachers and staff to be able to reset student passwords. You can also configure settings to specify that only students in certain grades or schools can reset their passwords.

 Please note: Account recovery and password reset options have recently been updated in Clever. If your password reset flows do not match those described below, please reach out to our support team and we’d be happy to help update you to the newest system! 

 

 
 A note about password management with Clever Identity Management (IDM) 
 If you use Clever IDM you’ll also have the option to manage password resets for any destination you provision via Clever IDM such as Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID. For any IdP that you choose to reset passwords for, passwords will synchronize across accounts. This means your users will have the same password for any account managed by Clever.

 For example, if you use Clever IDM to manage Google, and you use both Google and Clever passwords as identity providers for Clever, you’ll be able to configure password reset settings for both Google and Clever passwords. When a student updates their password via Clever, the password will update in Google as well. The passwords will sync across all accounts managed by Clever.

 
 

 
 Static Credentials

 A fter the initial setup of Clever Passwords for your district, user passwords will not be dynamically updated as new or updated data is synced to Clever from your SIS. 

 • For example: If your district has set up student passwords to be their birthdates, and then you update a student's birthdate in your SIS, their password will not automatically be updated in Clever to reflect the new birthdate. 
 
 To resolve this, it is sometimes necessary to manually update a student or teacher's password in Clever, or run a password reset for all students, teachers or staff. Please see Resetting Passwords for more on this! 

 

 
 District Details and Login Hints

 Login Hints 

 As a way to help district users, you can add login hints for Clever passwords that will appear on your district's Clever login page (e.g. Student Password = 10-digit Student Number , Teacher Username = Teacher ID ).

 To add login hints, navigate to the Authentication > SSO settings > Customize tab . In the example below, hints have been provided for the Login with Clever option. 

 Customize Login Options 

 If you have multiple IdPs set up for login methods you can customize your login options further. If Clever passwords in your only SSO method, users will simply see a username and password box when accessing your district portal, and will not be provided with a selection screen to choose their login method. If you have multiple IdPs configured, the Clever login page can be further customized by:

 • Editing selection option text (e.g. the box users click to choose their login method can say Log in with Google or Google Login )
• Editing login method order
 
 

 

 Tech Support Contact 

 We also highly recommend you enter a Tech Support Contact for your district to aid users with difficulties logging in to Clever. This information will be available to users on your district's Clever login pa

title: Connecting your learning applications and LMS with AnySchool
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What's AnySchool? 3. Setting up your AnySchool connection 4. Obtaining LMS credentials to configure your connection 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever AnySchool enables applications to connect with districts or schools that haven't adopted Clever in one centralized place.

 If you currently use Clever, or are interested in signing up , you can easily roster your learning applications, connect with your LMS, and get all of the benefits of Clever! Learn more here. 

 If you do not currently utilize Clever, application partners you work with may use Clever AnySchool to more easily synchronize data from your schools, leveraging your existing systems, such as your student information system (SIS) or another OneRoster API data source. This doesn’t require you to set up a Clever account. Instead, Clever acts as an extension of the learning application with no additional signup or credentials required. Clever is a sub-processor of the learning application and follows the terms of any data processing agreements you have in place with the application. Your student data privacy is a top priority for Clever. Learn more about our practices, FERPA, COPPA, GDPR, and state-level compliance in our Trust Center .

 In this article you’ll learn more about the AnySchool integration and setup process.

 

 
 What's AnySchool?

 If you use one of the data sources listed below, application partners using Clever may invite you to connect directly to their application using the AnySchool connection. As always, your student data privacy is a top priority for Clever. Learn more about our practices, FERPA, COPPA, GDPR, and state-level compliance in our Trust Center .

 If an application requests that you connect via AnySchool, you don’t need to set up a Clever account. Instead, you’ll use Clever to connect your preferred data source (e.g. Infinite Campus) with your application. Once you’ve set up rostering via AnySchool, you can also configure Clever LMS Connect to launch applications directly from your LMS.

 AnySchool supports syncing roster data from the following sources:

 • 
 Infinite Campus

• 
 Skyward

• 
 Blackbaud

• 
 Another OneRoster API connection (please reach out to your application for more details)

• 
 CSV files (users.csv, sections.csv)

 
 

 
 Setting up your AnySchool connection

 If an application partner is interested in syncing your data via AnySchool and setting up Clever LMS Connect, they’ll contact you to confirm that you use one of the data sources listed above and to confirm your email address for setup. 

 Before you can utilize Clever LMS Connect, you must first complete your AnySchool rostering setup. For detailed instructions in this setup process, please see our article Rostering applications with AnySchool . 

 Once you’ve completed your rostering setup, you’ll be able to configure your Clever LMS Connect configuration. To start, the application will confirm the email of the administrator that will be completing your Clever LMS Connect setup. Once confirmed, they’ll send that admin a custom setup link.

 Clever Tip: The link the application partner provides you will expire in 48 hours and is unique to your email address and can’t be shared with other admins. 

 This link can’t be shared with other administrators as it is unique to your email address. To complete the set up:

 • 
 Navigate to the link the application provided.

• 
 Review the information for utilizing AnySchool and click the Get started button.

• 
 You’ll receive an additional confirmation email to verify your account. Click the Verify your email button in this email.

• 
 Once you verify the account, you’ll be logged in to begin your setup. You’ll start by selecting your LMS (Canvas or Schoology). Then click Continue. At this time, it is only possible to connect with one LMS.

• 
 You’ll receive a prompt to enter additional information for your LMS such as a URL and account connection information. Detailed instructions on obtaining this information for each LMS are below .

• 
 Once the connection is established with their LMS, Clever will begin a record matching process. This typically takes 2-3 days. You’ll receive an email once matching is complete.

• 
 While waiting for the matching process to complete, you can generate LTI 1.3 SSO links for your application. These links enable users to access the application directly from your LMS. Logins won’t work until you receive the email that your record matching process is complete.

 
 

 
 Obtaining LMS credentials to configure your connection

 
 Setting up Schoology 
 After choosing Schoology as your LMS:

 • 
 You’ll be prompted to enter your LMS URL or check No Custom URL. 

• 
 You’ll connect to your LMS account by entering your administrator username and password.

• 
 You’ll select Validate Configuration to validate the information you’ve entered.

• 
 After your LMS is successfully connected, Clever will begin account matching to connect accounts. This can take a few days. You’ll be notified via email when it’s complete. 

 
 When using Clever LMS Connect with Schoology, you must utilize LTI SSO for application logins for the best experience. You can begin setting up LTI SSO for your applications while you wait for the matching process to complete. For detailed instructions on setting up LTI SSO for your applications please see here .

 
 

 
 Setting up Canvas 
 After choosing Canvas as your LMS:

 • You'll be prompted to enter your Canvas URL. Be sure to include https:// at the beginning.
• Create your Canvas Developer Keys. Click Open Canvas Developer Keys in the second step to get to the needed screen. You must be a Canvas Super Admin to access the Developer Keys screen. • 
 
 • Click the + Developer Key > + API Key button, then complete the fields as follows: 
• 
 Key Name

 • 
 Edlink

 

• 
 Owner Email

 • 
 accounts@ed.link 

 

• 
 Redirect URIs (One Per Line)

 • 
 https://ed.link/api/authentication/canvas 

• 
 https://ed.link/sso/administrator 

 

• 
 Redirect URI (Legacy):

 • 
 Please leave blank.

 

• 
 Vendor Code (LTI 2):

 • 
 Please leave blank.

 

• 
 Icon URL

 • 
 https://ed.link/icon.png 

 

• 
 Notes

 • 
 You can add any private notes about this integration.

 

• Click Save .
 

• Enable the key in Canvas by clicking the checkmark under the State column on the Developer Keys page.
• Copy/paste the number above Show Key and paste into the Client ID field in Clever. Click Show Key and copy/paste the Key into the Secret Key field in Clever. 
• Sign into Canvas. 
 
 • Once signed in, you’ll see confirmation that your account is connected.
• Scroll down in the window and click Validate Configuration. 
• You’ll now see the validation results and can click Continue Setup. 
• After your LMS is successfully connected, Clever will begin account matching to connect accounts. This can take a few days. You’ll be notified via email when it’s complete. You can send connection requests to your available applications while matching is in progress, so there’s no need to wait!
• On your LMS Connect page, you’ll see that matching is in progress. 
 
 

 Please note: We match IDs between your LMS and Clever. If those IDs don't match up, we'll let you know and help you create and upload a simple file to map the IDs and get everything working smoothly. 

 • When using Clever LMS Connect with Canvas, you must utilize LTI SSO for application logins for the best experience. You can begin setting up LTI SSO for your applications while you wait for the matching process to complete. For detailed instructions on setting up LTI SSO for your applications please see here .
 
 
 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Why is an application partner using AnySchool? 

 • 
 AnySchool simplifies data rostering, SSO, and LMS integrations for learning applications regardless of your tech stack. Clever Complete securely automates rostering, and ens

title: For Clever Admins: Pause your sync for a specific app
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Pausing and unpausing app syncs 3. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 You can pause syncing data to any app using App Sync Pause. This allows currently shared users to maintain stable access during transitions in SIS data (for example, during summer school or annual rollover). Disabling the pause will make the most current data in Clever available to the app.

 When the app sync is paused:

 • All users shared prior to enabling can continue to use the app
• Changes in your Clever data, including custom data, will not be reflected in the app
• Sharing settings can be edited, but the resulting data changes will not take effect until the app sync is unpaused. 
 
 This is not to be confused with an SIS sync pause , which is best for when you want to prevent updated SIS data from flowing into Clever, as explained in the chart below.

 

 
 SIS sync pause

 
 Application sync pause

 
 Pauses the sync from your SIS Pauses the sync from Clever to an application, including any sharing permissions 
 Prevents new data from syncing to Clever Prevents data changes in Clever from syncing to a specific app. However, any data shared before the app pause was implemented can still be synced by the application. As such, the application sync with Clever may still overwrite manual changes made within the application. If you need to fully prevent any syncs between Clever and your application, please reach out to the app team directly. 
 All users shared with apps prior to pause can continue to use those apps All users shared with apps prior to pause can continue to use those apps 
 Any updates in your SIS will not be updated in Clever Changes synced to Clever will not be reflected in the app 
 
 

 
 Pausing and unpausing app syncs

 You can pause your app sync from your Clever Dashboard > Applications > My Applications page by selecting the app you want to pause the sync for and navigating to the Settings tab for that application. 

 To turn on the pause 

 Click Turn on App Sync Pause and confirm that you want to pause the sync.

 

 

 To turn off the pause 

 If your app sync is paused and you want to start syncing again, you can turn on the sync from the same Settings tab.

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Does pausing app sync hide the app or prevent logins? 

 • Pausing the app sync does not hide the app nor prevent logins for those already shared with the app. If you’d like to hide the app or prevent logins, see Clever Portal: Hiding applications and disabling logins .
 
 Can I modify sharing settings while the sync is paused to the app? 

 • You can modify sharing settings, but they won’t go into effect until the pause is turned off. Pausing the sync is helpful if you want to spend some time modifying sharing settings but don’t want to implement them just yet.
 
 How long does it take the sync pause to go into effect or be removed? 

 • It takes ~5 minutes to turn the pause on. When you unpause, we’ll trigger a new Clever sync, which will apply any modified sharing permissions and send any modified data to the app.
 
 What does last sync time on the application overview page indicate? 

 • The last sync time on the application overview page indicates the last time an application queried Clever's API. It does not indicate that a full sync has been completed.
 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 Clever Portal: Hiding applications and disabling logins .

• 
 For Clever Admins: Pausing the sync with your SIS

title: For Clever Admins: Bulk Security Actions
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setting up bulk security actions • Step 1: Select user groups • Step 2: Select security action(s) • Step 3: Configure security action(s) • Step 4: Schedule action(s) • Step 5: Preview and confirm 3. Monitoring activity 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Bulk security actions let you reset user passwords in just a few clicks, so you can respond to a security concern or prepare for a new school year without disrupting learning.

 From your Clever Dashboard, a step-by-step wizard walks you through the entire process: selecting your users, choosing your action, configuring settings, scheduling, and previewing before anything goes live. This article will walk you through each step. 

 
 Setting up bulk security actions

 To get started with taking bulk security actions, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Security > Bulk security actions .

 

 From here, you can click into the wizard that will take you through five steps. Learn more about each step below. Please note that you will need to complete the wizard and click Save in order for the actions to be set

 

 Step 1: Select user groups

 

 Start by selecting the group of users you want to apply actions to. You can select one or more user groups: Teachers, Students, and or Staff. 

 With each user group you have the option to select whether the security action should be taken for:

 • 
 All users: all users who are synced as the user type you have selected will apply to the security action

• 
 Select users by school: you will use the drop down to select schools you would like included or excluded in the prepopulated sharing rule. When complete the sharing rule will read either School equals X or School does not equals X 

• 
 Select specific users: you can add a checkmark to the users you would like to continue with

 
 Once you've made your selections, click Continue to select security action(s) .

 Step 2: Select security action(s)

 

 

 Choose the following action to apply to your selected users: Update passwords 

 Please note: Update passwords is only available for IDM destinations and Clever Passwords. 

 Select how you'd like to handle the password reset:

 • 
 Reset passwords only

 • 
 Sets users' existing passwords to one you specify. Best if you plan to distribute the new passwords directly.

 

• 
 Force a reset on next login only

 • 
 Users are prompted to create their own new password the next time they log in. Best when most users have permission to reset their own passwords.

 

• 
 Reset passwords and force reset on next login

 • 
 Sets an initial password you choose, then prompts users to create their own after first login. Great for back-to-school or first-time setup.

 

 
 Step 3: Configure security action(s)

 

 

 This step is where you fine-tune the details of your selected action(s). What you see here depends on what you chose in step 2.

 If you selected a password reset option, you’ll configure:

 1. Where the force reset happens

 Choose whether users are prompted to reset their password when they log in to Clever, or when they log in to another destination:

 • 
 Force reset on next login to Clever 

 • 
 The reset is triggered when users log in to Clever using their Clever password.

• 
 Note: if you want this to apply regardless of what credentials users use to log in, you’ll need to contact Clever Support to enable that setting.

 

• 
 Force reset on next login to other destination(s)

 • 
 The reset is triggered when users log in to a specific identity provider (IdP), such as Microsoft Active Directory (AD), Microsoft Entra ID, or Google Workspace. If a user logs in with a different IdP (e.g. Google), the reset happens via that IdP.

 

 
 2. Which destination(s)’ passwords to reset

 Select which provider(s) should have their passwords reset: Microsoft Active Directory (AD), Microsoft Entra ID, Google Workspace, or Clever passwords. Only the destinations you select here will be updated. If your school uses multiple providers, make sure to select each one you want included.

 3. What to reset passwords to

 Choose how new passwords are generated:

 • 
 Existing IDM formula

 • 
 Uses the password formula already set up in your identity data management (IDM) system (e.g. Google Workspace). The formula is shown for your reference, for example: {{randomWord}}-{{randomWord}}-{{randomWord}}.

 

• 
 Temporary password formula 

 • 
 Uses a customizable formula based on student or teacher attributes, like last name, Student Information System (SIS) ID, or graduation year. For example: {{name.last}}-{{student.sis_id}}-{{student.graduation_year}}. You can edit the formula for both students and teachers by clicking Edit temporary student formula or Edit temporary teacher formula .

 

 
 

 Once you’ve filled in the required fields, click Next step: Schedule action(s) .

 Step 4: Schedule actions

 

 

 Please note: Scheduled actions can be canceled or edited before they run. Immediate actions are permanent once confirmed. 

 

 Choose when your bulk action(s) should run:

 • 
 Immediately 

 • 
 Actions run as soon as you confirm.

• 
 If an IDM configuration sync is actively running, you can't trigger an immediate bulk action. Clever can't run both at the same time.

 

• 
 On a specific date and time 

 • 
 Schedule it for a future date within the next two weeks. Scheduling overnight or before the school day starts can help minimize disruption. Times are based on your local timezone.

• 
 Clever won't let you schedule a bulk action at a time that overlaps with an existing IDM configuration sync. 

 

 
 Step 5: Preview and confirm

 

 

 Before anything runs, you’ll see a full summary of your configuration. Use this to verify everything looks right:

 • 
 User types and total number of users affected

• 
 Applied filters (e.g. school name)

• 
 Bulk security actions selected

• 
 Password formula and identity provider

• 
 Scheduled time (e.g. Immediately)

 
 You can also browse the full list of affected users (by teacher, staff, or student tab) before confirming.

 

 To confirm, check the acknowledgment box:

 

 Please note: Once executed by clicking Complete bulk actions for X users , bulk security actions cannot be undone. We recommend reviewing the preview step closely before proceeding. 

 

 Once you’ve reviewed and checked the box, click Complete bulk actions to kick things off. 

 
 Monitoring activity

 

 

 To monitor progress, navigate to Clever Dashboard > Security > Bulk security actions > Activity . Here you can:

 • 
 See which admin on your team ran or scheduled security actions

• 
 Review actions that have been completed or are scheduled to run

• 
 Download a CSV of new passwords for distribution

• 
 Cancel or edit actions that are still scheduled 

 
 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Can I undo a bulk security action after it runs?

 • 
 Bulk security actions can't be reversed automatically. If you need to make changes after the fact, you'll need to run a new bulk action. Scheduled bulk security actions can be canceled. 

 
 Why can I only schedule ahead up to 2 weeks?

 • 
 Beyond the two week window, the possibility of a surprise action that will disrupt access for your users is too great. If you would like to schedule out further than two weeks, we recommend a calendar reminder in your calendar of choice reminding you to schedule the action closer to the actual date to ensure that conditions haven’t changed.

title: For Clever Admins: Edtech quality indicators
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. The importance of edtech quality indicators 3. Using the edtech quality indicators in your Clever Dashboard 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is making it easier for districts and schools to create safer, smarter decisions when connecting to edtech and AI tools by surfacing trusted quality indicators in the Clever Dashboard.

 The EdTech Index, powered by ISTE+ASCD’s Learning Technology Directory, gives educators a trusted resource for identifying high-quality edtech solutions. The Five Common Quality Indicators are central to this effort, providing a shared framework for evaluating edtech and AI tools.

 These five indicators help answer essential questions about whether an edtech product is:

 • 
 Safe

• 
 Interoperable

• 
 Evidence-based 

• 
 Inclusive

• 
 Usable

 
 Validated information comes from trusted third-party sources to illuminate key areas to consider when exploring and vetting new tools. These indicators are part of a national initiative to help reduce the burden for school systems to identify quality digital learning tools. 

 
 The importance of the edtech quality indicators in your Clever Dashboard

 Schools today face an overwhelming number of edtech tools and growing pressure to evaluate each one for safety, accessibility, and impact. Yet, many lack the time, resources, or frameworks to do so consistently.

 That pressure is reflected in the numbers: nearly half of administrators report challenges protecting students from online threats (45%) and maintaining student data privacy (45%). Additionally, 61% cite broader cybersecurity concerns as their top identity management challenge, according to Clever’s Cybersecure 2025 Report . By highlighting these indicators, we’re helping schools quickly identify which tools meet national standards for quality and trust.

 Clever includes the indicators in the Clever Dashboard to ease the burden of evaluating applications for Clever admins. We believe that by including these indicators, we can:

 • 
 Help schools better understand their vendors’ certifications and use them to inform how they share data.

• 
 Provide applications a platform to showcase their certifications to districts and schools to encourage use and connections.

• 
 Build trust across the ecosystem for administrators and applications.

 
 

 
 Using the edtech quality indicators

 You can find the Five EdTech Quality Indicators directly in the Clever Dashboard when adding a new application:

 • 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications page.

• 
 Search for the application you’d like to add.

• 
 If available, Clever will display the indicators along with trusted certifications and badges to help inform your decisions.

• 
 Learn more in our press release .

 
 

 An example of the EdTech Quality Index certifications you may see when adding an application. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What if I don’t see the edtech quality indicators for a specific application? 

 • 
 The edtech quality indicators for that application aren’t available via Clever at this time. If you have questions about the application and how they meet the indicators, we recommend reaching out to the app partner directly.

 
 Which third party validations are included as part of the Edtech Index Quality Indicators? 

 • 
 ISTE + ACSD work alongside partners on a collective vision: to provide education leaders and educators with easy access to valuable information, including alignments, badges, certifications about edtech interoperability, privacy, accessibility, usability, and efficacy, to find the right tools for learners. These validations are critical but might be hard to find, so we've provided links to expert organizations to help you and your team dig deeper as you progress along your product decision-making journey. Read the descriptions on the Edtech Index to learn more about the different validation areas included in the Index and why they are critical to helping you determine if a product or tool will meet your needs.

 
 How often are the validations updated? 

 • 
 Clever works with ISTE to update the validations every quarter.

title: For Clever Admins: Google SSO 3rd party confirmation
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Steps to take for SSO with Google 3. Steps to take for Google Classroom roster import and Clever IDM 4. Google documentation 5. Helping teachers and students navigate this change 6. For Teachers & Students 

 

 Overview

 IMPORTANT Confirm your third-party app settings for Clever in the Google Admin Console to prevent issues with teachers and students accessing Clever. If you're a teacher or student receiving the error " Access blocked: Your institution’s admin needs to review ," please contact your school or district's tech support contact for help and provide them with this Help Center article. 

 To protect student data privacy, Google will restrict access to third-party applications (including Clever) for students under the age of 18 unless the admin confirms access in the Google Admin Console. Admins must mark Clever as a trusted app in Google by October 23, 2023, or students, teachers, and administrators may lose access to Clever. Google will automatically designate users to under 18. Please ensure that Clever Admins are designated as over 18. This article provides instructions for updating your settings in Google for Clever apps. More information is available here: Manage access to unconfigured third-party apps for users designated as under 18 . 

 
 
 

 Steps to take for SSO with Google

 To confirm Clever as a trusted third-party configured app:

 • To get started, sign in to the Google Admin console (note: you must be a Google Admin to access this).
• Head to Menu > Security > Access Control and Data > API Controls in the Admin Console. You should see a section titled “App access control”. 
• Click Manage Third-Party App Access to configure third-party apps. You should see a table listing your configured apps. 
• 
 
 If Clever appears in the table, click here for instructions. 
 

 • Hover over Clever in the table and click on the View Details button that appears.
• If the details panel lists Clever as " Trusted" then you are all set.
• If Clever is not listed as "Trusted" , hover over the table row again and click Change access then follow the instructions in the article below under Changing App Access .
 
 
 
 If Clever does not appear in the table, click here for instructions. 
 • Click on Configure new app. 
• Search for Clever or 423491012759. 
• Select the Clever app from the list.
• Follow the instructions in the article below for Changing app access 
 
 

 
 Changing App Access 

 • Select the appropriate scope (all users in the domain or specific organizational units) for app access then click Next . 
• Select “ Trusted ” to allow access. 
• Review your settings. If they look good, click Change Access 
 
 
 Steps to take for Google Classroom roster import and Clever IDM

 Since users don’t log into Google Classroom roster import and Clever IDM, Google Admins must simply ensure the Clever Admin authorizing the application is over 18. Districts that have already authorized these apps before October 23, 2023 won't be affected. View the Google Help Center for additional information: Manage access to unconfigured third-party apps for users designated as under 18 . 

 
 Google documentation

 For the most up to date content, please visit Google’s Help Center and blog:

 • Third-party app access enhancements for Google Workspace for Education 
• Confirm your third-party app settings by October 23, 2023 
• Manage access to unconfigured third-party apps for users designated as under 18 
• Google Workspace for Education Admin Console: How to review existing configured apps 
• Third-party app access enhancements for Google Workspace for Education 
 
 

 

 
 Helping teachers and students navigate this change

 As long as teachers are designated over 18 years of age, they will not be impacted by third-party app access enhancements directly. However, their students may be - so it is important they understand why students may not be able to log into applications, and what to do next.

 We recommend that you proactively reach out to teachers to let them know Google’s updatewhat error message they might see, and who to reach out to with questions. You can use this example email template to reach out to them. Please ensure that you have marked Clever as trusted before you send this email so that teachers and students have a way to access their applications! 

 

 Teachers,

 Google Workspace for Education recently updated their settings to help admins control how third-party apps (like Clever) access their organizations' Google data when users sign-in using their Google Workspace for Education accounts. 

 The IT team has configured the applications that you and your students need to access. However, if your students encounter an error message stating: " Access blocked: Your institution’s admin needs to review ," here’s what you should do:

 • Ask the student to access the application through Clever - we have confirmed Clever as a trusted application.
• If they are still unable to login, ask the student to request access to the application using the link provided on the screen.
• Contact our help desk [link] with the request. Please include the date, application, and reason for your request.
 
 Please do not contact the application directly. They will not be able to help approve requests. 

 We appreciate your patience as we work through outstanding requests. We appreciate your partnership in keeping student data safe and private!

 Thank you,

 -[Your name]

 

 

 
 For Teachers and Students

 If your student encounters the error Access blocked: Your institution’s admin needs to review Clever , please contact your district's tech support contact listed on your Clever login page . 

 

 You can use the following language: My student(s) are having issues logging into Clever due to Google's 3rd party confirmation. Can you please assist with confirming this resource has been approved in Google as outlined here: https://support.clever.com/hc/s/articles/000001588 

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • Single Sign-On (SSO): Log in with Google

title: Customer Interaction Policy for Support
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Our Commitments to You 3. Customer Interaction Standards 4. Violation of Interaction Standards 5. Tips for the Best Customer Experience 6. Accessibility Accommodations 7. Closing Remarks 

 

 Overview

 In our efforts to build stronger relationships with our districts, schools, and App Partners, we want to establish clear, consistent, and high-quality customer interaction standards that enhance the customer experience, improve satisfaction, and align with our company’s values and service expectations.

 This article will define best practices for customer interactions across various channels, including phone and email engagements. Additionally, it covers tone, responses, issue resolution, escalation procedures, and overall customer engagement strategies. 

 
 Our commitments to you

 • 
 Professionalism : Our team will treat all customers with courtesy, respect, and professionalism at all times.

• 
 Responsiveness : We aim to get back to you with accurate and timely answers as quickly as we can.

• 
 Empathy : We understand that issues can be frustrating, and we’re here to help resolve them in a compassionate manner. 

 
 
 Customer interaction standards

 • 
 Respectful Language : Our support team is here to help, and we ask that all conversations remain kind and respectful. We understand that issues can be frustrating, and we can best assist you when interactions remain positive and professional.
