To ensure a safe and supportive environment for everyone, we won’t be able to continue conversations that include threats, intimidation, or discriminatory language. In such cases, we may need to end the interaction and escalate the issue to management to handle the situation further.

• 
 Working Together : We’re here to help and want to make things right for you! If you have any concerns or aren’t satisfied with a solution, we encourage you to share your feedback with us in a constructive and respectful way. We’ll do our best to find a resolution that works for you.

• 
 Call Procedures Cooperation : If a support agent indicates they need to end the call to conduct additional investigation before following up, please respect their need to do so. Prolonging a call unnecessarily can hinder our ability to assist you and other customers effectively. If needed, the support agent will end the call.

 • 
 Phone Escalation Pathway: We don’t have a phone escalation pathway. Our support agents will be your primary point of contact for all support cases. If management or engineering involvement is required, arrangements must be made in advance. If you have a complaint about your interaction, you can submit a complaint in writing via the blue support button in the bottom right of the Help Center by following the prompts. Please input the subject as “Customer Interaction Complaint - [Case Number].” Please include a detailed explanation of the incident and the reason for your complaint. A senior Clever Support Manager who wasn’t involved in the original interaction will review your message and provide a written response to you within 15 business days of receipt.

• 
 Call Transfers: If you call in for assistance with an existing case, we’ll connect you with the assigned agent if available, as they are most familiar with the issue and the troubleshooting steps that have already been taken.

 

• 
 Scheduling a Call : If you wish to discuss your existing case via phone, it’s recommended to schedule a time with your agent by replying via email on the existing case to ensure availability. Attempting to reach out via the general support line doesn’t guarantee direct contact with your assigned support agent, as they may be assisting other districts or schools.

• 
 Confidentiality & Data Security : We ask that customers respect the need for privacy and security during interactions. Please ensure that any personal information shared with support agents is relevant to the issue and is shared in a secure manner. The below table provides confirmation on Clever’s views on personal identifiable information (PII).

 
 
 Can share 

 
 Avoid sharing 

 
 Audience 

 
 Email Correspondence: User’s Clever ID (which can be found in the District Dashboard ). Phone Correspondence: User’s Clever ID or other identifiers like name or email can be shared. Email Correspondence: 
 • 
 Full names

• 
 Local or State IDs

• 
 Username

• 
 Email Address

• 
 Preferred names

 
 All Clever roles + user types ( Clever admin roles, non-instructional staff, teachers, and students ) 
 
 

 

 
 Violation of interaction standards

 While we’re dedicated to resolving issues and providing support, repeated or severe violations of these standards may result in additional action from our Support Mangers. We'll assess the level of violation, and follow-up may include the steps indicated below:

 • 
 Level 1 Violation : A Clever Support Manager may reach out to discuss the incident with the individual in violation.

• 
 Level 2 Violation : A Clever Support Manager will reach out to discuss the behavior with the individual in violation, including District/Clever admin. We’ll also aim to discuss and solidify a better method for working together.

• 
 Level 3 Violation : The situation will be escalated both internally and at the district-level or school-level. All stakeholders will meet to discuss how we should move forward with servicing the district or school.

 
 If you believe that a violation has been incorrectly assessed, you can initiate an appeal to ensure fair treatment for all customers. We value your partnership and want to ensure that all concerns are addressed appropriately. 

 Appeals must: 

 • 
 Be initiated within 10 business days of being notified of a violation. 

• 
 Be submitted in writing via the Clever Support button in the bottom right of the Clever Help Center. 

• 
 Have the subject line [Violation Appeal - Case Number]. 

• 
 Include a detailed explanation of why you believe the assessment was incorrect. 

 
 A different Clever Support Manager who wasn’t involved in the original violation assessment will review your appeal and provide a written decision to you within 15 business days of receipt. If additional time is needed due to complexity, we’ll notify you of the extended timeline. The decision made through this appeals process will be considered final. However, we remain committed to working with you to ensure positive interactions moving forward.

 
 Tips for the best customer experience

 • 
 Openness to Resolution Options : We’re always striving to provide the best possible solutions for your needs. While we’ll do everything we can to assist you, there may be times when our current product isn’t able to support a specific request. If this happens, we’ll be transparent with you, log it as feedback and offer alternative suggestions or workarounds when possible.

• 
 Details Matter : To help resolve issues efficiently, we encourage customers to provide as much detail and context as possible when submitting a request via email or on the call with a support agent. The more specific you can be about the issue, the quicker and more efficient our agents can assist you in resolving the issue 

 
 Examples of what to include are as follows: 

 • 
 Account Details

• 
 Clever IDs for example users who are impacted

• 
 Error Messages

• 
 Screenshots to assist the agent in diagnosing the problem

 
 
 Accessibility accommodations

 At Clever, we’re committed to ensuring all customers can effectively communicate with our support team, regardless of disability status. We strive to provide accessibility to our support services in compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and related accessibility laws. If you’d like to request an accommodation, please contact us via the support button at the bottom right of the Help Center. Please make the subject Accommodation Request - Case Number and/or mention your accommodation nee

title: Classe365 Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup 3. API overview 4. Important endpoints 

 

 Overview

 Classe365 is a unified Student Information System (SIS), Learning Management System (LMS), and Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform designed for educational institutions of all sizes. It streamlines student management processes such as admissions, enrollment, attendance, and grading. The LMS enables course creation, assignment management, and assessments, while the CRM helps institutions engage with students, parents, and staff.

 

 Extra things to note about the SIS functionality: 

 ✓ Uploads all academic sessions, sections, and courses into Clever. ✓ Supports custom student and teacher attributes. ✓ Syncs staff.

 X Doesn't have an automatic sync. The API needs to be triggered manually from “App Settings > Clever > Upload Files to Clever”.

 

 
 Setup

 Copy your SFTP credentials in Clever 

 • Log in to the Clever Dashboard .
• Navigate to the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab page.
• Copy your SFTP Username and Password
 
 

 

 Setup in Classe365 

 • Log in to Classe365 .
• Navigate to Accounts > Org Settings > Apps Settings > Clever .
 
 

 • Paste the copied SFTP username and STFP Password from Clever to Classe365's App Setting and Save the configuration. 
• After saving the configurations, open the Clever App settings again. Click Upload Files in Clever .
 
 

 • Now, the system will push the information securely to Clever and show a success message after sending the information.
 
 For more information, visit the Classe365 Clever Integration Guide. 

 
 API overview

 Classe365 offers a user-friendly API designed to simplify integration for developers. Built on REST architecture, the API provides consistent, resource-oriented URLs and clear HTTP response codes to assist with troubleshooting. It supports standard HTTP features, including authentication and verbs, and allows for secure cross-origin resource sharing. For security reasons, developers should avoid exposing secret API keys in public-facing client-side code. All API responses, including errors, are returned in JSON format.

 

 Classe365 uses Basic HTTP authentication. To authenticate, users must provide a base64-encoded string in the format <subdomain>:<api_key> .

 

 For more details, visit the Classe365 API Authentication Guide .

 

 
 Important endpoints

 • Academics API : Insert/Update and Retrieve Academic Data 
• Academic Sessions API : Retrieve Academic Session Details 
• Teachers API : Add/Update and Retrieve Teacher Details 
• Admin Staff API : Add/Update and Retrieve Admin Staff Details 
• Student Data API : Retrieve Student Data 
• Enrollment API : Enroll Students in Academic Sessions, Classes, and Sections 
• Elective Subject API : Manage Elective Subject Enrollment

title: Clever IDM: FAQs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) • Provisioning • Updating account information • Deprovisioning • Issues & troubleshooting 

 

 Overview

 This article is for IDM customers who have an active subscription. You’ve completed the setup process, and now you’re wondering how to ensure everything is running smoothly and IDM is doing its intended job in the background with every sync. This article provides an overview of commonly asked questions by schools whose IDM sync is running.

 By default, IDM syncs occur once per day, in the evening hours. At that time, Clever will compare the data in Clever to the accounts in Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID and create, update, or archive users appropriately.

 Contact Clever Support if you’d like to customize your district’s IDM sync time or frequency. 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Provisioning

 How do I make sure IDM creates an account? 

 • Users added to Clever will have accounts created by IDM in Google, Active Directory, and/or Entra ID. Clever IDM will provision if a user has an active record that exists in your SIS sync and the user is syncing to Clever.
• To troubleshoot specific missing users, please first check your Clever sync report and the specific requirements of your Student Information System (SIS) and sync type for any missing records. If the user is syncing to Clever and is still missing from Clever IDM, please contact Clever Support for additional assistance.
 
 What will happen during summer when we pause our Clever sync? 

 • Clever SIS syncs typically pause at the end of the school year and unpause just before the Back to School season. When your sync is paused, data in Clever won’t be automatically modified or changed.
• By default, Clever IDM’s nightly syncs will continue to run. Since no automatic data updates are happening in Clever, most of the time Clever IDM syncs won’t have any impact. 
• We recommend using Custom data to add additional users you need to sync with IDM during your summer pause. See this article for more on IDM and Summer Pauses. 
 
 After a new user is created, how is the information synced/written back to our SIS? 

 • Clever IDM provides automated syncing only in one direction (from Clever to your destination). The Clever IDM page allows you to download a CSV file of usernames via the Clever IDM page to upload to your SIS.
• Clever also provides SFTP access to user emails managed by Clever IDM. You can access the SFTP files by enabling Participation Reports and accessing the associated SFTP server as described in this article .
• The CSV files in the SFTP server contain mappings of user emails to their Clever ID, SIS ID, and User Type. Schools can use these files to personally build automation for writeback into their SIS on their end. Clever doesn’t support direct SIS write back automation outside of this documentation . 
 
 Where can I learn more about account recovery and password management for my users? 

 • Please see our article Clever IDM: Account recovery & password resets. 
 
 

 Updating account information

 What will IDM automatically update on an account? How does that happen? 

 • IDM will automatically update user information when it changes in the SIS for fields like role, name, grade, graduation year, and location. 
• Updates occur when a user's first name, last name, user type, or organizational unit (OU) changes. When updating a user, the following fields are synced:
 • 
 User’s first name (if changed)

• 
 User’s last name (if changed)

• 
 Organizational unit as configured by the district (if changed)

• 
 Suspension status

 • 
 Suspended if a user exists in the Google Directory under a managed OU but not in the most recent Clever data set.

• 
 Unsuspended if a user was previously suspended (i.e., not in the Clever data set) but has been restored in the Clever data set within 300 days.

 

 

• Note: If you are currently syncing an email field via your SIS, Clever IDM will not automatically update the destination’s email/username based on the formula you have set. You’ll need to update the email/username in your SIS, or contact support for further assistance.
 
 Can I review the changes Clever IDM is making to our users? 

 • Yes! After your initial Clever IDM sync, you can continue to track changes to your users via the User management > Events tab . On this tab, you can select a date range for which you’d like to review Clever IDM creates, updates, archives, and matched users. You can also apply filters to examine changes by destination (if you have multiple configured), event type (create, update, etc.), or specific users by name or SIS ID.
• Once you find the event for the user and destination you’d like to review, click the dropdown arrow to examine more details.
 
 

 Deprovisioning

 How do I ensure IDM deprovisions an account? 

 • Clever IDM will deprovision accounts when you remove the user from the Clever data. If you want to keep students until a specific date, configure your Clever sync to include those students through that date.
• Clever IDM doesn’t consider graduation date or any other field into consideration for deprovisioning, only whether the student has an active record that exists in the SIS sync.
• Users deleted from Clever will have their accounts moved to the Archive OU in Google/Active Directory. In Entra ID, the account will be disabled. Users moved to the Archive OU are suspended if you turn on the "suspend on archive” setting. 
• When users leave your district or school, Clever IDM will place them in the Archive OU during the next sync. If the user returns to Clever within one year of departure, their Clever account will be restored, and their Google account will be moved from the Archive OU to the appropriate active OU based on your Parent/Sub-OU settings. If a user returns after one year, Clever will generate a new Clever and Google account.
 
 How will users be deprovisioned during summer when we pause our Clever sync? 

 • Clever syncs typically pause at the end of the school year and unpause just before the Back to School season. When your sync is paused, data in Clever won’t be automatically modified or changed.
• By default, Clever IDM’s nightly syncs will continue to run. Since no SIS data updates are happening in Clever, generally the IDM syncs won’t have any impact.
• If you want to archive users during this period, we recommend doing so directly in your destination (Google, Active Directory, or Entra).
 
 

 Issues & troubleshooting

 Where do I go to see IDM’s activity? 

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > User management > IDM tab . At the bottom of this page, you will see Notifications and Issues flagged for your attention as Tasks. You can also see your Sync History and export your data here.
• Clever administrators also have the option to subscribe to Clever IDM email notifications from the email tab of the profile page . Subscription options include weekly reports, daily reports, and issue alerts. Clever Admin owners will be subscribed to weekly reports and issue alerts by default.
 
 How do I solve an issue I see in my IDM report? 

 • Some issues will be solvable within your Clever Dashboard. Other issues may require Clever to resolve the issue. Please contact Clever Support if you need assistance.
 
 What if I want to update a setting in my IDM configuration? 

 • You can update settings anytime by navigating to your Clever Dashboard > User management > IDM tab and then clicking the Edit destination provisioning button.
• Clever IDM will generate a preview. Review any changes made, and if you are satisfied click Provision .
 
 Where can I find additional content on Clever IDM? 

 • We have many articles on all of Clever IDM's features! Check out the resources below:
 • Clever IDM: Formulas 
• Clever IDM: Sync reports & issue troubleshooting 
• Clever IDM: Preview sync visualizer & events 
• Clever IDM: Account r

title: Clever + Checkology: Setup guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process 3. Required fields 4. Support contacts 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through how to get set up with Checkology in Clever. 

 Before reading this guide, please ensure you and your schools are set up with Clever! If you haven't set up Clever yet, you can get started by clicking here or by accepting your invitation from Checkology. Please see this guide for more information on setting up Clever for the first time. 

 

 
 Setup process

 If you're already on Clever, you can connect Checkology by accepting your invite, or following these instructions: 

 • 
 • Log in to your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation. 
• Search for Checkology. 
• Clever will bring you to a page where you can authorize your Checkology request. On this page, you can mark that it has already been purchased since Clever SSO and rostering for Checkology are provided at no cost to your schools. 
• You can also request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the Checkology icon in their Clever Portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day. 
• Once you have made your selections, click Request ! A request will be sent to the Checkology team for approval. 
 

 
 Once you have shared data with Checkology, the Checkology team will set up the initial sync and will reach out to you to discuss communicating with teachers.

 Recommended Sharing 

 Before sharing: 

 • Make sure to match product applications with purchased license counts. 
• Ensure that students and teachers are in their correct grade levels and that you've updated classes for the current school year in your Student Information System (SIS). 
 
 

 Checkology recommends that you “Share by Rules” in Clever. You can find more info about Share by Rules here . Create a sharing rule by following these steps: 

 • To access the Sharing Permissions page for Checkology, log in to your Clever account, navigate to My Applications , and click the icon for Checkology. From the Data Sharing tab, click Share by Rules. 
• Configure your sharing rules by limiting sharing to only the schools and students that should be synced and by sharing only sections with the appropriate subject. 
 
 

 
 Required fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever Dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. Checkology requires the following data fields:

 • 
 Student : district, name, school, schools, sis_id, student_number

• 
 Teacher : district, name, school, schools, sis_id, email, title

• 
 School Admin : district, name, schools, staff_id, email, roles, title

• 
 District admin : district, name.first, name.last, email, title 

 
 A few things to keep in mind for Checkology products:

 • It is a web-based application
• Videos are on three different platforms: Wistia, Vimeo, and YouTube (default).
 
 
 Support contacts

 Checkology 

 Contact info: nlp.product@newslit.org 

 Contact Checkology with problems that relate to any issues using Checkology or issues while in the Checkology URL.

 

 Clever 

 Support.clever.com (click the chat icon in the corner of the screen)

 Contact Clever with issues that relate to:

 • 
 Syncing iep_status and other required fields to Clever

• 
 Customizing field access

 
 

 
 FAQs

 Why am I getting a “data warning” or a “data error”? 

 • 
 A data error occurs when the record is incompatible with Checkology’s integration with Clever, and the user or section won't appear in the application. Data errors must be resolved for users or sections to be successfully processed by the application. Reach out to Clever Support if you have questions about how to resolve your data error.

• 
 A data warning occurs when the user or section has a compatibility issue with the application that may result in a degraded app experience. We recommend addressing these issues or contacting the application directly to determine the impact of a particular data warning.

 
 Why is a student or teacher missing from Checkology? 

 • Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements before reaching out to Checkology:
 • 
 In your SIS data

• 
 In your Clever data

• 
 Is shared with Checkology in your data sharing permissions

 

 
 Can multiple teachers/staff be assigned to the same course/section? 

 • Checkology can support multiple teachers or staff assigned to the same course/section as co-teachers.
 
 Are staff supported? 

 • Yes, Checkology supports staff with Clever. 
 
 Who should I ask for help with an issue? 

 • If it’s related to data within Clever, or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever.
• If you're receiving an error message within the Checkology URL, please contact Checkology.

title: Clever IDM: Setting login credentials
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Syncing usernames 3. Creating user email formats 4. Collisions and fallback format 5. Passwords • Random passwords option • Multiple destinations • Distribution • Emails and passwords using custom formulas 6. Mapping additional fields 

 

 Overview

 Setting up your login credentials for Clever IDM is part of the setup process. You can find the general setup for each destination type here:

 • Clever IDM: Google Setup 
• Clever IDM: Active Directory Setup 
• Clever IDM: Entra ID Setup 
 
 Login credential setup for Clever IDM is similar for each destination: Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID (formerly Azure).

 Clever IDM provisions login credentials based on the format you specify, and you can customize these formats per user role (e.g., students can have a different format than teachers).

 

 Please note: Some examples cover all three destinations, and portions of those examples may not apply to all (e.g., sAMAccountName would apply to Active Directory but not Entra ID and Google). 

 

 
 Syncing usernames

 Clever IDM syncs usernames based on your SIS data and the destination: Google, Active Directory, and Entra ID, and can also update usernames in the destinations if desired. A basic understanding of what username fields sync for each destination is beneficial, especially if you need to troubleshoot.

 Google :

 • Clever IDM identifies and matches existing Google accounts automatically.
• We match accounts on the SIS Email field.
• Users with the same email address in Clever and Google will be matched and updated.
 • Ensure existing Google email addresses are updated in your SIS so Clever can match them.
 

• To view guidelines for username formatting, please view this Google Support article .
 
 Active Directory :

 • Clever IDM matches your existing AD users based on their district username from the District_Username field in Clever.
 • Ensure district usernames are updated in your SIS so Clever can match them.
 

• Please contact Clever support if your UserPrincipalName convention does not match your sAMAccountName convention.
• Once you have selected a username format for a particular user role, Clever will use it to generate the sAMAccountName for that user role.
• If unable to match on district username, Clever IDM will attempt to match using your login credentials.
 
 Entra ID :

 • Clever IDM identifies and matches existing Microsoft Entra ID accounts automatically based on users’ District_Username field in Clever.
• By default, Clever IDM will use the username synced from the SIS for UserPrincipalName and the email synced from the SIS for email. If the SIS data is blank, Clever IDM will use the fallback format you specify.
• If unable to match on district username, Clever IDM will attempt to match using your login credentials.
 
 If you need to change the default usernames to match on, we’re happy to help! Please reach out to Support.Clever.com . 

 Active Directory and Entra ID :

 • IDM can update userPrincipalName (primary username) in Active Directory and Entra ID.
• Users whose username in Clever matches an existing UPN in Active Directory or Entra ID are matched to that account.
• Once matched, Clever will update the UserPrincipalNames and sAMAccountNames for existing Active Directory accounts on subsequent syncs if a new username is synced from the SIS. 
 
 Please contact Clever support at Support.Clever.com to turn UPN update behavior on or off.

 

 
 Creating user email formats

 Please note: Navigate to the Clever IDM: Formulas article to learn more about common formulas and operations that you can use to manipulate data in Clever to create formulas. 

 

 To create an email format for users, combine a username and a domain. Clever IDM will only use this format if the user's email address isn't already populated in Clever.

 Clever IDM provides options in the format builder for email and password formats that utilize existing fields imported from your SIS, or custom text. School districts can also use custom formulas to learn about supported field names and operations.

 

 

 

 

 Active Directory :

 • Clever IDM writes values to the Mail field but cannot currently provision mailboxes in the Exchange Server.
 
 

 
 Collisions and fallback format

 In the event of a collision, when two or more users have the same sAMAccountName, UserPrincipalName, or email field, Clever IDM will, by default, append a number to the original format until we find a unique value.

 For example: 

 • johndoe@clever.com 
• johndoe1@clever.com 
• johndoe2@clever.com 
 
 If desired, you can create your own custom fallback formats for use in the event of a collision by selecting Add fallback format . 

 Please note: Fallback formats will only work if IDM is generating your email addresses. 

 

 

 

 Here, you can create a custom fallback format by starting with a premade email format, building an email format using blocks, or entering a format string. Please note that the last option is advanced and will require you to type a string that fits the format you would like to see applied. To learn more about your options with building a format, review our Clever IDM: Formulas article .

 

 

 

 Once you add the fallback format, you can change the order in which Clever IDM will attempt to use them. When there’s a collision, Clever IDM will go through each fallback format in order until there isn’t a collision. If there’s a collision on every fallback format, Clever IDM will append a number to the original format until we find a unique value.

 

 Active Directory :

 • Clever IDM does not support fallback formats for the CommonName field. In the case of a collision, CommonName will have a number appended and incremented until a unique value is found. For example:
 • John Doe
• John Doe 1
• John Doe 2
 

 
 

 
 Passwords

 The password format needs to meet domain-level restrictions. Clever IDM requires passwords to have a minimum of 8 characters.

 Google :

 • There are a few specific restrictions for this destination, outlined here .
 
 Teachers and staff are required to change their passwords upon first login. As such, the format selected in Clever IDM will only be utilized for the teacher or staff member's first login. To learn more about password formats available, please review our Clever IDM: Formulas article .

 

 Random passwords option Clever IDM offers a random password format as part of your Clever IDM password credentials, as well as ways to export these generated passwords out of Clever IDM. Whenever possible, we recommend using random passwords to reduce the risk of compromised accounts. 

 To configure random passwords for users on account creation, go to the Set login credentials step of the Provisioning Setup:

 • Click Edit your format 
• Click Add a Function 
• Select one of the random password options from the drop-down.
• Check off the Allow password export . 
 
 

 

 When Allow password export is checked, this also enables the IDM-generated student passwords to be viewed by teachers in their Portal. This will help teachers to assist with student logins.

 You can manipulate these random features using an IDM Custom Formula found here .

 Multiple Destinations Clever IDM also supports using the same password across multiple destinations as long as the district has added a check next to Allow password export . Once you have one destination configured, you’ll see the option to use that destination’s password in other destinations. 

 Please note: For security purposes, passwords will only be available for ten days after they are created . If AD is configured to use passwords from Google, an AD account must be created within ten days after the same account is created in Google. Otherwise, Clever IDM will throw an issue for the affected account. 

 

 

 This image shows the ability to use the password format from Active Directory, but you can use it from Google or Entr

title: For Clever Admins: Clever + Kahoot! EDU Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Initial Setup Process 3. What to expect if your district is new to Kahoot! 4. What to expect if your district is connected to the legacy Kahoot! App 5. What to expect if your district has teachers using the Library app 6. Frequently Asked Questions 6. Support Contacts 

 

 Overview

 This guide will walk you through the steps needed to successfully set up Kahoot! EDU for your district via Clever. Follow the instructions below to ensure a smooth integration and setup process. 

 
 Initial Setup Process

 1. Kahoot Invitation 

 When working with your Kahoot representative, you’ll be asked to confirm your Clever District ID for them to locate your record in Clever. Then, you can expect to receive an email notification when the Kahoot team has sent you an invitation to connect via Clever. 

 You can use the link on the email or log into your Clever Dashboard to accept the invitation. Make sure to take note of any sharing recommendations your representative may have included as part of the invite.

 

 2. Setting Up 

 After accepting the invitation, you can begin setting up your sharing permissions. When you accept the invitation, please share only schools that will be utilizing Kahoot! EDU. You can find more information about setting up sharing permissions here .

 For your staff to be able to use open-ended and word cloud questions in Kahoot! EDU, you’d need to agree to our terms and conditions and student privacy policy. Click here to view these documents and accept these policies before your launch date. 

 Over the course of the next 24 hours, the Kahoot team will automatically begin setting up your organization in Kahoot.

 

 3. Launch Date 

 On the launch date, the Kahoot! EDU application will appear in your teachers’ Clever Portals based on the sharing permissions you set.

 

 Please note: If the organization has not been set up via the Kahoot sync, users will receive a “this user does not exist” message until the sync has run. 

 

 

 
 What to expect if your district is new to Kahoot!

 The Kahoot! EDU app will appear in your teachers’ Clever Portals on the launch date, according to the sharing permissions you set.

 Teachers will be able to SSO into both a Kahoot! EDU workspace and a Kahoot teacher/private workspace. 

 
 What to expect if your district is connected to the legacy Kahoot! app

 The Kahoot! EDU app will still appear in your teachers’ Clever Portals on the launch date, according to the sharing permissions you set.

 Teachers will be able to SSO into both a Kahoot! EDU workspace and a Kahoot teacher/private workspace.

 After the initial SSO into the Kahoot! EDU app, teachers will also be able to access the EDU workspace via the legacy app.

 

 Tip: For simplicity, we advise you to uninstall the legacy app once the Kahoot! EDU application is up and running to avoid confusion. The Kahoot! EDU application will give you access to the most up-to-date features. 

 

 
 What to expect if your district has teachers using the Library app

 The Kahoot! EDU app will still appear in your teachers’ Clever Portals on the launch date, according to the sharing permissions you set. 

 Teachers will be able to SSO into both a Kahoot! EDU workspace and a Kahoot teacher/private workspace.

 After the initial SSO into the Kahoot! EDU app, teachers will also be able to access the EDU workspace via the Library app.

 

 Tip: We advise teachers uninstall the Library app once the Kahoot! EDU application is up and running to avoid confusion. The Kahoot! EDU application will give you access to the most up-to-date features. 

 

 For further assistance, please contact Clever Support or refer to the Clever Help Center.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Will existing teacher accounts be preserved? 

 • 
 Yes, existing accounts can be preserved if the email associated with their account matches their email in Clever. If a teacher had been using a personal email address or an email that does not appear in Clever, they should update their email address to match what appears in Clever to ensure their account is matched. 

• 
 If the emails do not match, the existing accounts will still be preserved but the teacher may have a duplicate account created for them.

 
 What is the best way to direct students to Kahoot? 

 • Create an external link to share with students directing them to https://kahoot.it/ . You can find instructions on how to create an external link here .
 
 How do I know if I have the legacy app installed? 

 • You can identify if you have the legacy app installed by checking the app icon, which appears as a purple ‘K.’ If you see this icon, it indicates the legacy version is still installed.
 
 

 
 Support Contacts

 Kahoot 

 Support Contact: CleverSSO@kahoot.com 

 Contact Kahoot! with issues that relate to:

 • 
 An error message within any Kahoot URL

 
 

 Clever 

 Support Contact: support.clever.com (click the chat icon in the corner of the screen)

 Contact Clever with issues that relate to:

 • 
 Data within Clever

• 
 Data coming from Clever

 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 Sharing Permissions: Overview 

• 
 For Clever Admins: Apps - App invites 

• 
 For Clever Admins: Apps - Set launch date

title: For Clever Admins: District Data Privacy Agreements (DPAs)
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever’s Terms of Use 3. Clever Pre-signed DPA 4. Student Data Privacy Consortium 5. Using your District’s Template 6. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 This Help Center article outlines best practices and processes for managing Data Privacy Agreements (DPAs), also known as Data Sharing Agreements, between U.S. school districts and Clever. DPAs specify how Clever handles, stores, and protects student information for our school district partners. These agreements help ensure Clever upholds the privacy and confidentiality of student data and may be required by state student data privacy laws. As a "data processor," Clever processes student data solely for educational purposes as directed by the school district.

 Depending on your district’s needs, there are several options for managing DPAs with Clever:

 • Use Clever’s online Terms of Service and Privacy Policy , which include key provisions commonly found in a DPA.
• Sign Clever’s pre-signed DPA.
• Join Exhibit E through the Student Data Privacy Consortium (SDPC).
• Submit your district’s DPA template or form for Clever’s review.
 
 Read on for more details about each option and how to proceed.

 

 Note for Application Partners: This article does not apply to Application Partners. Clever does not sign DPAs with App Partners because apps do not share student data with Clever. Instead, Clever acts solely as a data processor for school districts, which retain ownership and control of student data. For App Partners, the most important DPA is the one established directly with the school districts you serve. 

 

 
 Clever Terms of Use

 During the initial onboarding process, districts typically sign up for Clever through an automated flow. As part of this process, districts agree to Clever’s Privacy Policy , Terms of Service , and Additional Terms of Use for Schools . These terms are designed to be fair, transparent, and compliant with federal and state student data protection laws, ensuring that districts maintain control over their data.

 To provide clarity and legal protection, our terms outline the parameters of specific clauses related to student data privacy and security. We believe that these terms, by themselves, may meet many DPA requirements so please feel free to review and confirm.

 You can review the documents using the links below:

 • Terms of Service 
• Additional Terms of Use for Schools 
• Privacy Policy 
 
 

 
 Clever Pre-signed DPA

 Clever has created a DPA template modeled on the national agreements from the Student Data Privacy Consortium (SDPC), providing robust protections for districts.

 If your district would like to use Clever’s pre-signed DPA, you have two options:

 • Complete the agreement using this DocuSign Powerform .
• Download and sign the Clever DPA , then send a copy to legal@clever.com .
 
 

 
 Student Data Privacy Consortium

 The Student Data Privacy Consortium arose to streamline the process of contracting between districts and edtech vendors. The consortium has created a SDPC Resource Registry that uses a template National Data Privacy Agreement ( NDPA ) for districts and applications to use. The NDPA includes an Exhibit E “General Offer of Privacy Terms” for other districts in the state to sign onto that same DPA. 

 To check if your state has a signed NDPA that your district can join, follow these steps:

 Steps to Search the SDPC Database

 • Visit the SDPC Resource Registry .
• On the homepage, locate the State Alliances section and click it.
• Select your state from the list, then click Visit Alliance .
 • (The state’s name will appear on the left side of the page.)
 

• In the middle of the page, find the Search the Database box and click Start Exploring .
• Under Search by Vendor Member's Resources , use the dropdown menu to search for “Clever.”
• Select "Clever" (not Clever Badges) from the results and click Search .
 
 This will display contracts Clever has signed with districts in your state. Most agreements are linked directly, allowing you to download them. If your state has a signed NDPA, you can simply download the agreement and sign onto Exhibit E .

 

 
 District’s DPA Template

 What if your District has their own template they would like to use?

 Please contact legal@clever.com with the following information:

 • 
 Name of your School District

• 
 State

• 
 Attach any documents that you would like reviewed.

 
 

 Please note: This option will take longer as we will have to review, likely redline, and negotiate the agreement. 

 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 • Where can I find out more information about the State NDPA requirements and the State NDPA template? 
 • Student Data Privacy Consortium 
• NDPA 
 

 
 • Where can I find more information about Clever Security? 
 • Clever’s Security page . 
 

 
 • What if I have a general legal question? 
 • Please reach out to legal@clever.com 
 

 
 

 
 Related Resources: 

 • Trust Center 
• Privacy Policy 
• Terms of Service 
• Additional Terms of Use for Schools 
• Third Party Subprocessors page 
• Data Elements Table

title: For App Partners: Rostering schools via the AnySchool district setup flow
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Inviting an off-Clever district or school 3. The district/school experience • Set up SSO 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever Complete is the platform solution purpose-built for edtech scalability—connecting your application to any school through a single, secure integration for rostering, SSO, and LMS connections. Through the AnySchool capability within Clever Complete, you can connect with school organizations that don't use Clever. 

 This article provides information on using the AnySchool district/school set up flow to invite districts and schools without a Clever account to connect to your application. This setup flow is hosted by Clever and white-labeled to align with your application’s branding. 

 AnySchool supports syncing roster data from the following sources for schools that don't directly utilize Clever:

 • 
 Infinite Campus (API v1.2)

• 
 Skyward

• 
 Blackbaud

• 
 Additional OneRoster API connections (please reach out to your success manager for more details)

• 
 CSV upload (users.csv, sections.csv)

 
 If you have a district or school using one of these systems, invite them to connect for rostering via AnySchool in your Clever Dashboard.

 

 
 Inviting an off-Clever district or school

 To connect with a district or school that isn’t currently utilizing Clever, you must have AnySchool connections included in your Clever contract. If you don’t have an AnySchool agreement in place and are interested in learning more, please contact our team here .

 If you have an existing Clever Secure Sync or Clever LMS Connect integration, you can use your existing integrations to utilize all of the Clever Complete features, including AnySchool.

 For schools or districts connected via AnySchool, you’ll have access to relevant data management features like the Overview tab, Filters, Warnings, Analytics, etc.

 

 A screenshot of the Overview tab for an example AnySchool district. 

 To use AnySchool to invite an off-Clever district or school to connect please:

 • 
 Confirm they utilize one of the following for storing data:

 • 
 Infinite Campus (API v1.2) 

• 
 Skyward 

• 
 Blackbaud

• 
 Another OneRoster API connection (please reach out to your success manager for more details)

• 
 Or they can upload two CSV files, a users.csv and a sections.csv

 

• 
 Confirm you have the email address of the administrator at the district or school who will be setting up rostering with your application.

• 
 Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Home > Invite Districts tab.

• 
 Select the button to Connect to an AnySchool district.

• 
 Input the district or school name and click Create new district. 

• 
 Select Send email invite , then enter the District Email, Email subject, and Email body. The district will use this email address to verify the user during setup. You can customize the subject and message so the school understands that this email will guide them through setup. When you're ready, click Send email . 

• When the administrator receives the link, they’ll follow the steps below to set up their sync with your application. If they click the link but don’t finish setup within 48 hours, they’ll need to request a new link. 
 
 Once the administrator completes the setup process on their side, our system will automatically run a sync. When the sync completes, you’ll be able to review the data in your Clever Dashboard. Clever will continue to run syncs on a nightly basis. If you’d like to customize your sync time, please reach out to Clever Support.

 

 
 The district/school experience

 Once you’ve generated the access link and provided it to the school administrator, they’ll be able to begin the setup process on their side. To complete the setup, they’ll:

 • 
 Navigate to the link within the automated email.

• 
 Review the setup information and click the Get started button.

• 
 They’ll be logged in to begin their setup. They’ll start by selecting their data source: Infinite Campus, Skyward, Blackbaud, another OneRoster API source, or CSV upload. Once they select a data source, they’ll click Continue. 

 Please note: Once a district begins setup, they have 48 hours to complete the process. If they don’t complete their setup in this timeframe, you’ll need to provide a new setup link. 

 
• 
 They’ll receive a prompt to enter additional information for their data source.

 • 
 If they’re utilizing Infinite Campus, Skyward, or Blackbaud, they’ll enter the credentials for connecting to their data source’s API. This includes Base URL, Client ID, and Client Secret. Clever will provide instructions for generating and locating this information in their SIS, however, if the admin needs assistance, we suggest they reach out to their data source’s support team directly (i.e., if using Infinite Campus to connect, they should reach out to the Infinite Campus support team for assistance locating the Base URL, Client ID, and Client Secret).

• 
 If they’re utilizing another OneRoster API connection, they’ll follow instructions for locating credentials for this data source. Once they complete the setup in their data source system, they'll confirm any necessary details in the AnySchool flow

• 
 If they’re uploading a users.csv and sections.csv, they’ll be able to download file templates and must prepare their CSV files. When the files are ready, the admin will upload them via the provided link. If the link expires before the admin has completed the upload, you may need to provide a new link. You can do this by repeating the steps outlined in the Inviting an off-Clever district or school section.

 

• 
 Once the data source credentials are entered, or they've uploaded the CSV files, they'll click Continue. 

 
 Once the school administrator clicks continue, their data will begin syncing to the application. Administrators using CSV files will receive a sync report email with any errors or issues that may prevent data from syncing. They’ll want to resolve these errors in the files and reupload them to their provided AnySchool link.

 You can view two examples of setup below:

 

 

 

 Set up SSO

 School organizations can set up Single Sign-On (SSO) using a supported OneRoster API data source. Many schools use providers such as ClassLink for this setup process.

 To complete setup, the school organization will need to locate and exchange metadata URLs between systems. Schools should reference their SSO provider’s help center or support team for provider-specific instructions. Depending on the school’s familiarity with SSO setup, you may need to provide additional guidance during this process.

 Schools can also use Instant Login Links (ILLs). Instant Login Links allow schools to place direct login links within their SSO portal, website, or Learning Management System (LMS). When users select an Instant Login Link, they are automatically signed into the application through SSO.

 Review the example below to understand the setup experience schools may follow when configuring SSO with ClassLink.

 

 
 Classlink 
 • 
 Copy the Clever metadata URL. 

• 
 Review this article to learn more about how to configure Clever’s metadata URL if needed.

• 
 Log in to ClassLink LaunchPad and paste the Clever metadata URL.

• 
 Copy the ClassLink metadata URL.

• 
 Return to the Set Up SSO screen and paste the ClassLink metadata URL.

• 
 (Optional) Copy the Instant Login Link to add it to the school’s SSO portal, website, or LMS.

• 
 Click Continue to finish setup.

 Once a school organization completes their setup, the app will see the SSO method updated to SAML on the AnySchool district overview page. 

 
 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What if a district or school I’m working with is concerned about data privacy and Clever? 

 • You can share that student data privacy is a top priority for Clever, and Clever never sells student data. 77% of schools in the United States trust Clever to a

title: Clever Portal: Ohio Department of Education - 988 hotline
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How to add the application 3. Setting up sharing permissions 4. Additional information 5. FAQs 

 

 Overview

 In April 2025, the Ohio Revised Code (RC) 3313.474 went into effect, requiring schools to make information about the telephone number for the national suicide and crisis lifeline available to students. Specifically, electronic portals administered by the district or schools and accessed by students in grades 9-12 must include the phone number for the national suicide and crisis lifeline, "988."

 To support this requirement, Clever offers a way to add direct access to the national suicide and crisis lifeline website in students’ Clever Portals . Clever consulted with the Ohio State Department of Education and Workforce on creating this application to support Local Education Agencies (LEAs) as they work to meet the requirements of the law. 

 

 
 How to add the application

 Administrators can add this application which links to the Ohio suicide lifeline 988 website this link to the Ohio suicide lifeline 988 using the same steps to add any other application in Clever:

 1. Log into your Clever Administrator account

 2. Navigate to the Add Applications tab.

 3. Search for the Ohio suicide lifeline support, or paste this link directly into your browser.

 

 
 Setting up sharing permissions

 Click Add Application to begin the sharing permissions process. Sharing permissions will determine which students/schools will have access to this application. Ohio law requires this information to be shared at a minimum with students in grades 9-12.

 Please note: While this appears like any other application in Clever, no data will be shared in anyway . Adding this “application” simply sets up a link to the suicide hotline that a student can access at any point. 

 

 To configure sharing permissions for the application:

 • 
 If you aren’t already at the sharing permissions page once adding the application, navigate to your Applications > My Applications page .

• 
 Click on the application and then select Begin setup .

• 
 Select I’d like to customize my sharing , then select the Click here to customize your sharing button. 

• 
 Select Custom Share by Section .

• 
 Configure the rule so that Section grade [equals any of] 9, 10, 11, 12. 

• 
 Select Done editing and then Save Sharing Rules. For any student or teacher associated with a section in grades 9-12, the application will now appear in their Clever Portal. 

 
 
 Click here for a brief tutorial video on setting up Sharing Permissions for the application. 
 

 

 

 
 
 Additional information

 Clever partnered with the Ohio State Department of Education on the creation of this application to ensure that the information and assets included support districts and schools in meeting the requirements of the law. However, please note that Clever cannot confirm compliance with the state law on your behalf .

 
 FAQs

 If we don’t want to add another application, is there another way to meet this requirement? 

 • 
 If you don’t wish to add another application, you have a few other options:

 • 
 You can send a Portal Announcement (under the SSO & Portal > Communications tab) to students including information about how to access the suicide and crisis hotline. Learn more here . 

• 
 You can add a “custom link” to your District’s Clever Portal, titled however you like and linking to a URL of your choice. Learn more here . 

• 
 You can add a category to specific schools’ Portal (likely upper schools), which would be done via a School Tech Lead who has permissions to edit the Portal for their specific school(s).

 

 
 Will any data be shared with Ohio or the “application”? 

 • 
 No, there will be no data shared. The “application” is only structured this way to help you as the administrator scope who it will appear to via Clever.

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • 
 Sharing Permissions: Overview 

• 
 For Clever Admins: Customizing Clever Portal 

• 
 For Clever Admins: Announcements

title: NWEA MAP Growth to HMH Rostering Service Migration
text:
This article contains the following: 1. August 2025 updates about the NWEA to HMH Migration • FAQ regarding the pause 2. Overview 3. Clever migration wizard - districts with both NWEA and HMH today 4. Clever migration wizard - districts with only NWEA today 5. Districts with HMH only 6. Next steps 

 

 August 2025 updates about the NWEA to HMH migration

 Some districts that migrated to the new HMH Rostering Service may have experienced challenges rostering NWEA MAP Growth on Clever.

 • 
 For districts who used the Clever migration wizard and can successfully roster: You’re all set! Please roster for NWEA and HMH solutions using the new HMH Rostering Service core app.

• For all other districts : Clever is working with HMH to make the legacy NWEA MAP Growth app available again in your Clever Dashboard to meet your immediate rostering needs. Once the NWEA Map Growth app is live, you can use it to roster NWEA MAP Growth as you have in the past.
 
 FAQ regarding the pause 

 We already started the migration. What happens now? 

 • 
 HMH has paused the migration, so even if you have made changes in Clever, no further action is needed at this time. Once you see the NWEA MAP Growth app restored in your Clever Dashboard, you can continue using your current setup for MAP Growth, and data will flow as it did before. When HMH is ready to resume the migration, they’ll provide updated guidance and timelines.

 
 When will I see changes made in my Clever Dashboard? 

 • The HMH team is working through this. If you have questions on the status of your district or school, please contact HMH support: https://support.hmhco.com/s/contactsupport 
 
 Will this impact MAP Growth access for our teachers? 

 • No, it should not. Since HMH has paused the migration, you’ll continue to access MAP Growth using your existing setup. Nothing changes about how your data is shared at this time. Please contact HMH support if you have questions or concerns: https://support.hmhco.com/s/contactsupport 
 
 Will Clever still support the migration when HMH resumes it? 

 • Yes. Clever remains committed to supporting the migration when HMH is ready. We’ve developed tools like the migration wizard to make the process easier for districts, and we'll continue to collaborate closely with HMH to ensure clear, timely communication.
 
 I can’t find the migration wizard in Clever anymore—where did it go? 

 • Clever removed the migration wizard on July 24 at HMH’s request. Since the migration is paused, the wizard is no longer needed. We’ll bring it back or provide updated tools if the migration resumes in the future.
 
 Who should I contact with additional questions? 

 • 
 If you have any questions, please reach out to the NWEA and HMH teams:

 • 
 NWEA support: https://connection.nwea.org/s/support?language=en_US 

• 
 HMH support: https://support.hmhco.com/s/contactsupport 

 

 
 
 Overview

 Please note: As of July 24th, HMH is pausing the NWEA MAP Growth migration to the HMH Rostering Service. Districts are no longer required to complete the migration for the 2025 Back to School season. For more information, please click here or see this section in our article . 

 HMH is introducing a new rostering application called HMH Rostering Service! On July 15, 2025, the NWEA MAP Growth application and the HMHCore application will be merged into HMH Rostering Service. 

 Who’s impacted?

 • 
 Districts with HMH and NWEA 

• 
 Districts with NWEA only 

• 
 Districts with HMH only (no Clever action needed)

 
 You can start this upgrade the week of June 23rd or whenever you plan to begin rostering for the new school year. All districts with NWEA MAP Growth on Clever will see a migration wizard in your Clever Dashboard starting the week of June 23rd to automatically migrate your data from NWEA MAP Growth to HMH Rostering Service. Please see clever.com/clever-hmh for more information. 

 
 
 Please note: Be advised that if you have an NWEA MAP growth testing event between now and July 15th we recommend that you hold off on this migration until July 15th or until your summer testing events are over. If you proceed to migrate while you have summer testing events before the 15 th you may lose track of some students, necessitating a call and some work with the HMH technical support team to restore those students. If you are through all of your testing windows for the year, please click continue to migrate your app to the latest version in preparation for the coming school year. 

 

 

 
 Clever migration wizard - districts with both NWEA and HMH today

 Beginning the week of June 23rd, 2025, all districts with NWEA MAP Growth on Clever will see the migration wizard pictured below in your Clever Dashboard. The purpose of the migration wizard is to automatically move your data and field access shared with NWEA MAP Growth over to HMH Rostering Service (which is the new name of the HMHCore app).

 First, you will see the HMH Rostering Service migration as a task on your Home page . Click Review migration to proceed.

 

 

 

 Within the wizard, you'll review your sharing rules and field access. The sharing you have currently set for NWEA MAP Growth will transfer to HMH Rostering Service. For districts that already have both HMH and NWEA, the sharing rules will include the sum of all data shared with both apps. If you are using MAP Growth for summer testing, please hold off on completing the migration wizard until your testing is over or July 15th. 

 Please note, HMH already has this data since HMH acquired NWEA; this is just a streamlined way of rostering with no change to who is receiving the data.

 The wizard will also migrate the field access previously set for NWEA MAP Growth to HMH Rostering Service. This transfer will ensure that the fields needed for MAP testing are still being rostered. We recommend Always share all fields requested by HMH Rostering Service .

 

 

 

 If you’d prefer to use the Customize fields shared with HMH Rostering Service option, you'll be able to review the special and custom fields you’ve previously shared with NWEA MAP Growth and individually move them over to share with HMH Rostering Service as shown below.

 

 

 

 Click Confirm migration to finish your migration from NWEA MAP Growth to HMH Rostering Service.

 

 

 You should see this message, and click Done to dismiss it.

 

 
 Clever migration wizard - districts with only NWEA today

 Beginning the week of June 23, 2025, all districts with NWEA MAP Growth on Clever will see the migration wizard pictured below in your Clever Dashboard. The purpose of the migration wizard is to automatically move your data and field access shared with NWEA MAP Growth over to HMH Rostering Service (which is the new name of the HMHCore app).

 First, you will see the HMH Rostering Service migration as a task on your Home page . Click Review migration to proceed.

 

 

 Within the wizard, you'll review your sharing rules and field access. The sharing that you have currently set for NWEA MAP Growth will be transferred to HMH Rostering Service. For districts with NWEA only, the same data shared with NWEA will migrate to HMH Rostering Service. If you are using MAP Growth for summer testing, please hold off on completing the migration wizard until your testing is over or July 15th. 

 Please note, HMH already has this data since HMH acquired NWEA; this is just a streamlined way of rostering with no change to who is receiving the data. The wizard will also migrate the field access previously set for NWEA MAP Growth to HMH Rostering Service. This will ensure that the fields needed for MAP testing are still being rostered. We recommend Always share all fields required by HMH Rostering Service .

 

 If you’d prefer to use the Customize fields shared with HMH Rostering Service option, you'll be able to review the special and custom fields you’ve previously shared with NWEA MAP Growth and individually move them over to share with HMH Ros

title: Clever + MAP Accelerator: Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup process • Recommended Sharing • Setting up your MAP Accelerator sharing rules - Scenario 1 • Setting up your MAP Accelerator sharing rules - Scenario 2 • Sharing staff 3. Required Fields 4. Support Contacts 

 

 Overview

 This guide walks through how to get set up with MAP Accelerator in Clever. Before reading this guide, please make sure your district is set up with Clever! If your district has not been set up with Clever, you can get started by going to this link: https://clever.com/signup . For more information on setting up Clever for the first time, please see this guide . A personalized learning tool for grades 3-8, MAP Accelerator reduces the manual work of differentiating math instruction by automatically integrating class rosters, MAP® Growth™ scores, and Common Core-aligned Khan Academy content to generate learning pathways—complete with lessons, instructional videos, and practice problems. 

 
 Setup process

 IMPORTANT Before MAP Accelerator will be approved for setup, your district must be using Clever for auto-rostering with the NWEA MAP Growth application. 

 If your district is already on Clever, you can connect the MAP Accelerator application by following these instructions:

 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications .
• Search for MAP Accelerator .
• You'll then be brought to a page where you can authorize your MAP Accelerator request. 
• You'll be asked if you'd like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can see the MAP Accelerator icons in their Clever Portal. Product access may or may not be available on this day and is determined by the application.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request. 
 
 

 
 Recommended Sharing 

 MAP Accelerator recommends that you S hare by Rules in Clever . Create a sharing rule by following these steps:

 • Subjects:
 • Only the following subjects can be shared with MAP Accelerator: Math, Other, Homeroom/Advisory, and Interventions/Online Learning
 

• Grades:
 • Section grade should be 3-8
• 2nd and 9th grade sections can be rostered, but are not recommended and will produce a warning.
 

• Student data must include the following:
 • Unique email address
• Date of birth
• Grade level
• Students can only be assigned to one class/teacher within Accelerator. If they are enrolled in multiple math sections, only one of those sections can be shared.
 

• Teachers:
 • Unique email address
 

• Sections:
 • Should have unique names to avoid confusion
 

• Staff/Admins:
 • Unique email address
• Only users from the Staff tab can be shared, not District Admins from the Team page.
 

 
 

 
 Setting up your MAP Accelerator sharing rules - Scenario 1 

 Scenario 1: All math classes have the subject listed as math in Clever.

 Clever Tip: How do I know how subjects synced to Clever? Check the Sections tab of the Data Browser . Subject is one of the columns listed. 

 Step 1 : From the MAP Accelerator overview page , click Begin setup . Please note, if you have previously set up sharing rules, you will be able to click Edit sharing . Alternatively, you can set up the sharing rules by clicking Share data now from the Applications > My Applications page. Step 2 : You can select MAP Accelerator’s recommended sharing rules: The sharing rule above will share grade 3-8 math sections. 

 
 Setting up your MAP Accelerator sharing rules - Scenario 2 

 Scenario 2 : Not all sections where math instruction occurs have the subject listed as math in Clever. For example, elementary school students may only be enrolled in one section where all instruction occurs, and that section may have the subject listed as homeroom/advisory or other . Step 1 : If you’ve never set up the sharing rules before, you will be able to customize them from the setup guide (pictured). Alternatively, click Edit sharing from the MAP Accelerator overview page to edit rules you’ve previously set up. Step 2 : You will likely need to set up multiple rules. We recommend Custom share by section because section rules include students and teachers associated with the shared sections. Step 3 : Set up your filters to include only the data you want to share. For example, if your elementary school students are only enrolled in one section where all instruction occurs, and that section has homeroom/advisory listed as the subject, you can include filters for the school, grades, and subject. Click Done editing once you are finished adding filters. Step 4 : Add additional rules to capture different users. For example, your middle school or junior high students may be enrolled in sections where the subject is listed as math . You can create another rule to broaden your sharing to include elementary students where the section’s subject equals homeroom/advisory OR middle school students where the section’s subject equals math. From here, you can again set up a rule with filters to include the new group of users. Step 5: Once you’ve captured the appropriate users, click Save Sharing Rules . 

 
 Sharing staff 

 If you have non-instructional staff (users listed in the Staff tab in your Data browser ) who also need access to MAP Accelerator, they will not be included in a section's sharing rule. You will need to set up an additional rule to capture these users. Click Edit Sharing Rules and Add Another Rule to find the Custom share staff option: We recommend taking a look at your list of staff to determine who will need access rather than sharing all staff. Once you have the list of staff members ready, you can share them by using an identifying factor such as email, ID, or title. As always, don’t forget to click Done editing and Save Sharing Rules before leaving the page!

 
 Required Fields

 Check the Data Quality tab in your Clever dashboard to see whether you’re meeting the required data fields. MAP Accelerator requires the following data fields be sent: Student: name, school, district, sis_id, schools, dob, email, grade Teacher: district, name, school, schools, sis_id, email School Admin: district, schools, name, staff_id, roles, email, title District Admin: district, name.first, name.last, email, title 

 
 Support Contacts

 MAP Accelerator techsupport@nwea.org Clever Support.clever.com (click Contact Us ) Contact Clever with issues that relate to: Setting up sharing rules Syncing required fields to Clever

title: For Clever Admin: Access logs
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Features of access logs 3. How to use Access logs with login error messages from end users 4. Frequently asked questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Access logs give you a holistic snapshot of any attempts to access Clever. Use Access logs to spot any suspicious activity early, to keep your schools’ users and data safe. You can also use Access logs to troubleshoot users who are having trouble accessing Clever.

 

 

 

 For example, you might notice that a user’s login keeps getting stuck on an MFA timeout, meaning the user has entered their password correctly, but that they are not completing their MFA security method. That could mean that their password has been compromised, and that they need a reset. 

 This article covers each feature of access logs and how you might use them.

 

 
 Features of access logs

 Filter functionality 

 You can quickly filter access logs by date, IP Address, status, user, and user type. For instance, if you believe a certain user’s account has been compromised, you can drill down to that user to see their access history. In addition, you can look via reference ID (such as an error code a user has shared with you).

 

 User access activity 

 All users (students, teachers, short-term substitute teachers , staff, and Clever Admin) access activity shows up as a summary: 

 

 

 Individual access log summary 

 If you click the down arrow, you can see useful information, including:

 • the authentication method (e.g. Substitute Badge, MFA, etc.)
• 
 the final user action

• 
 their user ID

• 
 email address

• 
 error code(s) if relevant

 
 

 

 Access logs will also include details on Clever Admin and School Tech Lead (STL) impersonations. The authentication method listed will indicate impersonation, and the log summary will contain details such as the impersonation’s start and end time, the user account impersonated into, device details, etc.

 

 An example of an impersonation event in Access logs 

 

 Emailing CSV exports 

 Exports are available only as downloads and will be emailed to the admin who exports them from the Clever Dashboard. You can also email a CSV export by day, which includes all access log data organized by column. If you want to send these exports to a security management solution, you’ll need to send them manually.

 

 

 To pull an export:

 • Click Export CSV 
• Select your dates. You can export data for up to seven days at a time, up to 60 days in the past. If you need more than 7 days of data, you can export the data for multiple 7-day timeframes.
• Click Export CSV: 
 
 

 

 CSV exports include: 

 • 
 Timestamp

 • 
 When the event took place

 

• 
 Authentication_context_id

 • 
 This ID relates to a single user’s single login attempt. For instance, if a user logged in with a primary method, requested an MFA code, had an MFA timeout, and then successfully entered an MFA prompt, these would all be under the same Authentication_context_id.

 

• 
 User_id

 • 
 The user’s Clever ID

 

• 
 User_type

 • 
 Teacher, student, staff, or Clever Admin

 

• 
 Client_ip

 • 
 The user’s IP address

 

• 
 Auth_method

 • 
 Their primary authentication method, such as SIS, Google, or Clever Passwords.

 

• 
 Second_factor

 • 
 Their MFA method (such as Mobile app MFA, SMS code, etc.)

 

• 
 Event

 • 
 Part of the login process, such as:

 • 
 Login_success

 • 
 Part of the login process was successful (such as a first method, second method, or backup login code). 

 

• 
 Login_failure

 • 
 Part of the login process was unsuccessful.

 

• 
 MFA_prompted

 • 
 The user was prompted to enter an MFA prompt

 

 

 

• 
 Error

 • 
 There was an issue with the login process, such as a duplicate ID, no match to a user in your schools, etc.

 

 
 

 
 How to use Access logs with login error messages from end users

 When students or teachers can’t log in to Clever, they’ll receive an error message that provides information about the error, a copyable Reference ID, possible troubleshooting steps, and instructions to contact their school’s IT support.

 

 While the login error may suggest steps towards resolution, if you need additional information, please navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Access logs and input the Reference ID for more details.

 If you’re still unsure how to resolve the issue, please contact Clever Support and provide the user’s Clever ID and the Reference ID so our team can help troubleshoot further. 

 

 
 Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

 

 • 
 What’s the length of logging shown in Clever? 

 • 
 Five days.

 

 
 

 • 
 What’s the length of logging shown from the CSV download? 

 • 
 You can download up to seven days of data from the past 60 days. 

 

 
 • 
 What does it mean if an authentication method is “Impersonation?” 

 • 
 This means that a Clever Admin impersonated a teacher or student account, or if this setting is enabled for your school, an STL impersonated into a teacher account. Click the Access log entry for more details on the impersonation.

 

 
 

 • 
 What fields can you search via? 

 • 
 As of now, you can only search via reference ID.

 

 
 

 • 
 What errors might we see in a log? 

 • 
 Wrong email, wrong pw, incorrect 2nd factor

• 
 Pending MFA: users have 10 minutes to complete their MFA challenge, if nothing happens, Clever will fail the login. 

• 
 Timeout error: Meaning it took longer than 15 seconds for Clever to download your data.

 

 
 

 • 
 What are the differences between our classic login logs versus new access logs? 

 • 
 Length of logs shown:

 • 
 2,000 logs or 14 days in login logs

• 
 5 days for access logs with no limit on # of logs

 

• 
 Download limits:

 • 
 5k logs for login logs

• 
 14 days with access logs (you can also request a longer history by contacting Clever Support).

 

• 
 Access logs also include:

 • 
 Combined logs into one view when there are multiple access touch points, so it’s easier to troubleshoot.

• 
 More detail around methods of authentication, such as MFA, Substitute Badge access, etc.

• 
 Better error messaging with more detail on access errors.

title: Clever Portal: Hiding applications and disabling logins
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Hiding apps vs disabling logins 3. Hiding SSO or SSO (Saved Password) applications 4. How Clever Admins and School Tech Leads hide resources in the Clever Portal 5. How do hidden applications appear in Clever Portal 

 Overview

 This article is an overview of the various methods for preventing logins and hiding applications in Clever. A district may wish to hide applications in a Clever Portal for many reasons, from scheduling an application launch to hiding resources during testing periods. Clever's visibility settings allow districts to manage application access with ease. 

 
 Hiding apps vs disabling logins

 There are several options for Clever Admins and teachers to hide or limit access to applications through Clever. 

 If you want to:

 • Allow logins to Clever and the application, but hide the application in the Portal, you'll want to use settings for Hiding in Portal. 
• Allow logins to Clever, but disable logins to the application and hide it in the Portal, use App Visibility .
• Prevent logins to Clever and to applications via Clever using Access Control. 
 
 To better understand this, we have created a table that provides insight into these options.

 Please note that n one of the features listed in the table below will block direct access to apps rostered through Clever (e.g. users can still log into the app at the app's website outside of Clever if the application supports this). 

 

 
 Feature

 
 Access Control

 

 
 App Visibility Settings

 
 Hiding in Portal

 
 Teacher Page Category Hiding

 
 
 About the feature 

 
 Clever Admins can temporarily block Clever Portal access so that users can’t access Clever and their applications through Clever. 

 
 Clever Admins can hide specific applications in Clever and prevent users from using Log in with Clever (LIWC) buttons. The app will show up as disabled on Teacher Pages. 

 
 Clever Admins and School Tech Leads (if enabled) can hide specific apps and links on the District Page, but applications that are on Teacher Pages are still visible and can be used. 

 
 Teachers can enable this to hide categories and resources within the category on a Teacher Page. This doesn’t affect visibility outside of the specific category on that Teacher Page. 

 
 
 Who can configure 

 
 Clever Admins 

 
 Clever Admins 

 
 Clever Admins/School Tech Leads (STLs) 

 
 Teachers

 
 
 Staff/Teacher/Student Portal Experience 

 
 Specified user types can't log into Clever or use Login with Clever (LIWC) buttons 

 
 Specified user types can't log into Specific App or use LIWC buttons to access Specific App 

 
 Specified user types can't see the app in the Portal resource list or District Page (but can still see, use, and add on Teacher Pages) 

 
 Students will not be able to see the hidden category on a Teacher Page 

 
 
 Is the app visible in the Clever Portal? 

 
 No - there is no access to the Portal 

 
 No - the app isn't visible in Portal and shows up as disabled in Teacher Pages 

 
 Not in the resources list or District Page but is visible on Teacher Pages and the Clever Admin Portal 

 
 This feature doesn't affect visibility outside of the specific category on that Teacher Page 

 
 
 Does it block all Clever-Powered SSO (LIWC)? 

 
 Yes 

 
 Yes 

 
 No 

 
 No 

 
 
 Staff/Teacher/Student SSO Experience 

 
 See error above if users try to log into Clever or use the LIWC button 

 
 See error above if users try to log in using the LIWC button 

 
 Not visible in resource list or district page - LIWC works to log in 

 
 Category and category's resources not visible for students 

 
 
 Can it be set for specific dates? 

 
 Yes 

 
 Yes 

 
 Yes 

 
 No 

 
 
 Which users can this be applied to? 

 
 Staff, Teachers, Students - set by user type 

 
 Staff, Teachers, Students - set by user type 

 
 Staff, Teachers, Students - set by user type 

 
 Students 

 
 
 Does it hide the app everywhere? 

 
 Yes - no access to the Portal 

 
 Yes 

 
 No - hides the Portal link - apps on Teacher Pages will still be visible 

 
 No 

 
 
 Does it work for links? 

 
 Yes - no access to the Portal 

 
 No - only apps 

 
 Yes - can be used for apps or links 

 
 Yes - it hides all resources in that category while on that specific Teacher Page 

 
 
 Help Center Articles 

 
 Temporarily disable Clever logins using Access Control 

 
 Clever Portal: Hiding Applications Clever Portal: Disable logins to applications 

 
 Clever Portal: Hiding Applications 

 
 Clever Portal: Teacher Pages 

 
 
 Recommended Uses 

 
 Block user access during pre-school-year rollover to prevent early visibility of schedules/assignments 

 
 Block Clever-powered access to apps 

 
 • For links 
• Usable by STLs 
• When you want teachers to be able to add apps to Teacher Pages but don't want an app visible on general Portal 
 
 
 For teachers to hide resources they aren't currently using on their Teacher Pages 

 
 
 

 Hiding SSO or SSO (Saved Passwords) applications

 Step 1: Log in to the Clever Dashboard and navigate to Clever Dashboard > Applications > My applications and click the app you'd like to hide. This will disable the application for the selected users. Step 2: Go to the Settings tab and click Edit in the Application’s visibility box.

 

 Step 3: Select the user type you wish to hide the application from. Step 5 outlines hiding from multiple user types.

 

 Step 4: Select the Start and End Dates for hiding the application. If you aren’t sure when you’d like to unhide the application, you can leave that field blank to keep the application hidden until you set a date or unhide it.

 

 Step 5: To hide the application from an additional user type, just click Hide and set the dates for that user type. 

 

 

 How do I change when an application will be disabled?
