 You can return to the application visibility options to edit Start and End Dates at any time. You can also immediately unhide an application by clicking Visible next to the relevant user type.

 

 
 How Clever Admins and School Tech Leads hide resources in the Clever Portal

 Please note: Applications must be put into a category before they can be hidden. For more detailed instructions please read: How do I manage the Clever Portal? 

 

 Clever Admins, and if enabled, School Tech Leads, can hide applications directly via Portal settings. Hiding an application via Portal settings will hide the application in the District Resources section of the Portal, but if the teacher has favorited the app or added it to a Teacher Page, it won’t be hidden. Similarly, if a student or staff member has favorited the app, it won’t be hidden.

 When hiding an application in the Portal, you’ll also have the option to set different hidden dates per user type. For example, you can hide an application from teachers from June 20th to August 1st, but hide it from students June 20th to August 15th. This can be helpful when rolling out a new application.

 To hide an application:

 • Navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page. 
• 
 Locate the application you wish to hide and click the three vertical dots.

• 
 Select Hide/show in Portal. 

• 
 Select Hide temporarily .

• 
 Select all users (student, teachers, staff) you want to hide the application from for the same timeframe. If you want to hide the application for a separate user type for a different timeframe, you can use the Add another user type button after you configure the timeframe for your first user type.

• 
 Fill in the start and end dates for hiding.

• 
 Click Save. 

• 
 Be sure to select Publish changes in the top right corner of the Organize district portal page to apply the visibility settings.

 
 How do hidden applications appear in Clever Portal?

 When hiding is enabled, the application icon will display on the Clever Dashboard > Portal > Organize district portal page was grayed out to indicate it's hidden from us

title: For Families: What is Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. What is Clever? 2. How can my child log in to Clever? 3. What should I do if I have a problem with Clever or a learning program? 4. Is Clever safe for my child to use? 

 What is Clever?

 Clever is an easy way for your child to log in and learn with all of the online programs they use at school. With their own personal portals, students can learn with resources selected by their teacher and district. 

 Schools use Clever so students can have all of their digital resources in one place. Clever also eliminates the need to remember multiple usernames and passwords. 

 

 
 How can my child log in to Clever?

 Your student can visit clever.com/login to find their school and log in to Clever with their school-provided username and password. If your child has a Clever Badge, they can use their Badge to sign in at clever.com/badges . 

 Your school district may create a parent/guardian account that you can use to help your child log in. Learn more about parent/guardian accounts. 

 
 

 

 
 What should I do if I have a problem with Clever or a learning program?

 If your child is unable to log in to Clever or if you encounter a problem with one of the programs on Clever, please contact your school. Your school will be able to help your child access their account, and can help troubleshoot any issues you may have run into. 

 Similarly, if you'd like more information on the applications and tools your child has access to, or believe they should have access to additional learning materials, please contact your school directly. The Clever team can't assist with student access to learning materials as your school manages this.

 

 
 Is Clever safe for my child to use?

 Clever has worked with school districts and learning applications to create a safe, secure, private, experience for every student. Clever is set up and managed by schools - so every resource your child sees on Clever has been selected by their school. The data in Clever is only used to support your child’s education - it is never sold or used for advertising. 

 Detailed information about Clever’s privacy, security and compliance policies and practices can be found in the Trust Center .

title: For Clever Admins: iOS devices & the Clever app
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I add the Clever iOS app? 3. Expected Login Behavior 4. What happens when a user selects a learning application? 5. Best practices for using multiple apps in one session 6. Best practices for logging out of Clever 7. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Overview

 Students can log in to Clever on iPads using the Clever Single sign-on (SSO) application. The application allows students to access the Clever Portal and their Teacher Pages directly in the application, rather than launching Clever in a web browser. This article outlines the process of adding the app and its functionality for students.

 

 Please note: The Clever iOS application doesn't support teacher, staff, or administrator logins. These users should access Clever via a web browser on their iOS device. 

 

 
 How do I add the Clever iOS app?

 The Clever application is available for free via the Apple App Store . You can add the application to your devices manually, or use the Apple School Manager (ASM) and your MDM solution to find the Clever application and install it wirelessly to your iOS devices. Please see more information below on updating versions.

 The Clever iOS app requires iOS 16.6 or later and is compatible with iPhones and iPads.

 If you are using older devices that don’t support iOS 16.6+, your users can still access Clever via a web browser on their iOS device by opening the browser and navigating to your school organization’s Portal URL (found on your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings page ). From the browser, students can continue to log in to their Portal with their Badge or login credentials.

 

 
 Expected Login Behavior

 Students logging in with Badges 

 • 
 Please be sure your devices have the Clever iOS app from the Apple App Store.

• 
 Users will click on the Clever app to open it.

• 
 The first screen students will see is the Clever Badge camera.

• 
 Students have the ability to toggle between the front-facing and back-facing cameras.

• 
 Students will need to hold their badge up to the camera on the mobile device.

• 
 If the student is using a valid badge, they will see a green checkmark with sparkles signifying a successful login!

 • 
 If the student sees a badging error message, they may need to reach out to their teacher or a Clever Admin for assistance. 

 

 
 • 
 The student will then be logged into the Clever application and their Clever Portal. They can access all features of their Clever Portal, including their Teacher Pages, directly in the Clever app.

• 
 Please see our section below on what happens when a user selects a learning application to learn more about the user experience beyond logging in.

 
 You can preview the student experience in our videos below:

 

 

 An example of what the login could look like for students using a Badge and Classroom MFA pictures.

 

 

 
 

 Logging in with credentials 

 • 
 Please be sure your devices have the Clever iOS app from the Apple App Store .

• 
 When opening the Clever app, users will see the Badge Camera.

• 
 They’ll select the “Password” tab at the bottom of the screen.

• 
 The user will locate their school from the Clever login page.

• 
 They’ll then select the method they want to use to login (ex: Login with Google or Login with Clever).

• 
 The user will type their credentials and then be logged into the Clever application and their Clever Portal. They can access all features of their Clever Portal, including their Teacher Pages, directly in the Clever app.

• 
 Please see our section below on what happens when a user selects a learning application to learn more about the user experience beyond logging in.

 
 

 

 Please note: The Clever app will remember the user's school organization after their first login on the device, so this user won't need to go through the process of selecting their school again if the Safari browser and/or Clever app were never fully closed, and device history has not been deleted. 

 

 
 What happens when a user selects a learning application?

 After users sign into the Clever app and they select an application, they'll be automatically authenticated into that learning application either in your device’s default web browser or the app’s standalone iOS application.

 The login functionality is determined by application partners on a per-app basis.

 

 
 Click here for a list of apps that currently redirect to their standalone iOS app

 Alongside

 Please note: Some applications don't provide mobile support, which means that you may not be able to access them via your phone or tablet. If you're unsure, please reach out to the application to confirm. 

 

 
 Best practices for using multiple apps in one session

 We aim to connect students, teachers, and staff to as many of their resources as possible. After a user is logged in to Clever: 

 • 
 They can click on one of their connected applications.

• 
 This will open the application or external link either in the device’s default web browser or the iOS application.

• 
 If a user wants to go back to their Clever Portal, they should go back to the Clever application. From here they can launch an additional application, or access the same app again.

 
 
 

 
 Best practices for logging out of Clever

 Whether using a shared device or not, the best practice is to always log out of your school organization's Identity Provider (IDP) in addition to Clever when you are finished. To do this, follow these steps: 

 Step 1: Log out of the Clever application.

 • From the Clever Portal, click on your name in the top right corner, and select Log out.
 
 Step 2: Log out of any learning applications and your school organization's Identity Provider (IdP) on your web browser.

 • For example: Google, Active Directory, LDAP, Canvas, Clever Passwords
 
 

 For information regarding best practices on shared devices, please see this Help Center article: For Families: Logging your students out of Clever on a shared device. 

 

 If you have any additional questions, please reach out to Clever support ! We are happy to help! 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Will the Clever application log students out after any period of time? 

 • Clev

title: Troubleshooting Claims Rules
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Common Issues 3. Missing Claims Rules 

 

 Overview

 Claims rules define which attributes are sent to Clever from the identity provider and which fields Clever should use to perform the match and log the user into their Clever account. If you’re using any of the following login methods with Clever, learning how to troubleshoot claims rules related login issues can come in handy: 

 • Active Directory 
• Lenovo Stoneware / webNetwork 
• LDAPS 
• Office 365 / Entra ID 
 
 
 Common Issues

 For solving individual login issues, the Access Logs are your best friend! If you see a login failure that says Unable to match SAML data with a user in Clever , click on View details next to the failed login record, and you can see what’s failing to match: 

 

 The Login Failure Details in your Clever Dashboard > Authentication > Access logs will show you what we tried to match. Please check there first and then look up the corresponding information in your student information system (SIS) or Clever Data Browser to see if you can spot any inconsistencies that would cause the login failure.

 Alternatively, consider adjusting the claims rules to match users on a different field in Clever. You can find commonly used Clever fields here: Claims Rules. 

 In the example below, the user is failing to log in because the username and email returned to Clever from the identity provider don't match any student username (Claim #1) or any email (Claim #2) currently in Clever. 

 In essence, a user (let's call her "Susan") was successfully able to prove who she was to the district's identity provider (IdP), but when the IdP spoke to Clever and said, "Hey, can you log Susan in for me?", Clever responded, "Sorry, we don't have a Susan here!" This is often caused by a user trying to log in that hasn't been synced to Clever. Other issues that can cause a login issue: 

 • The field we match on, say username or email address, is missing or incorrect in the SIS upload for the user in question.
• There are duplicate or multiple users in the Clever account that we're able to match the login to (e.g., two users have the same email, which is also their username).
 
 

 
 Missing Claims Rules

 Another common issue is that claims rules aren’t being configured to match all user types who may be trying to sign into Clever using your identity provider. If your district uses separate claims rules for each user type and notices logins failing for one particular user type, your claims rules may need to be adjusted. Most commonly, districts will need to add additional claims rules in their identity provider for staff users. The following fields can be used to build your staff rules:

 • clever. staff . email 
• clever. staff . staff_id 
• clever. staff . credentials.district_username 
 
 We recommend matching across the same field for multiple user types by using any in place of the user type:

 • clever. any . email 
• clever. any . sis_id 
• clever. any . credentials.district_username

title: For School Tech Leads: Account recovery and password reset for Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Clever passwords
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Set up Account Recovery 3. Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page 4. Resetting your password in Clever Portal 5. Resetting a student or teacher's password 

 

 Overview 

 If your school district has enabled Active Directory (AD), Entra ID, Google, or Clever passwords as a login method for Clever, you may be able to reset your passwords from Clever. Please note that this setup will only be available to you if your district-level admin has enabled this. In your Clever Portal, if you click on your name and do not see the Account recovery or Set new password in your Clever Portal, you must contact your district to reset your Google, Active Directory, Entra ID (i.e., third-party), or Clever password. 

 

 

 If you do see the option for password reset, you can reset your Google, Active Directory, Entra ID, and Clever passwords from within the Clever Portal. To ensure you can reset your password outside of the Clever Portal, you will need to set up an account recovery method.

 
 

 Set Up Account recovery

 You will need to set up account recovery to have the ability to reset your password for a third-party when you are not signed in to your Clever account. To configure account recovery, from your Portal click your name, Account recovery. 

 

 Once clicked, a modal will pop up where you can select your preferred account recovery method. Depending on what your school district has enabled, you may have the option to enter an email address, a phone number, or answer security questions when you need to reset your password. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 It’s also possible that your school organization may have provided a recovery method for you using information in their records. For example, if your schools collect personal email addresses, they may set up your account recovery email as your personal email address. If you’d like to update the information your school organization provides, you can do so using the recovery method setup described above. 

 

 
 Recovering your account and resetting your password on the Clever login page 

 To reset Clever and third-party passwords 

 • 
 From the Clever login screen, click on get help logging in. 

• 
 Choose Reset your account passwords. 

• Input your school district email address (not to be confused with a recovery email you have set up). 
• You’ll be prompted to confirm your identity using a method you set up in your Portal settings. Select how you’ll confirm your identity. 
• You can now set up your new password for both your Clever and third-party accounts by inputting a new password. Your school may have password requirements, which can be viewed by clicking on See requirements . Once completed, you can use your new password for your third-party account(s). 
 
 

 To reset only your Clever password 

 • Navigate to your district's login page and select Get help logging in. 
• Click Reset only your Clever password. 
• Please enter your school email address. If you are unsure of the email address to utilize, please contact your district support contact. Clever Support cannot provide this information to you.\ 
• After you enter your email, Clever will send you a verification code through the method selected when setting up account recovery. Once you enter the verification code, you can set a new password: 
 
 The password must match the requirements set by your district and Clever. Here are some of the requirements from Clever:

 • Passwords cannot start/end with a space
• Characters cannot repeat more than 3 times
• Passwords cannot contain any part of the user’s name
 
 

 Once you've reset your password, you will be prompted to log in to the Clever Portal using your new password.

 

 
 

 Resetting your password in the Clever Portal

 To reset your password while in the Clever Portal:

 • Click on your name then Set new password . 
• Input your new password, confirm the new password, and click Set new password to save. 
 
 The password must match the requirements set by your district (example: minimum of 10 characters, including 1 number).

 
 Resetting a student or teacher's password (for school admin)

 If you're a Clever School Tech Lead trying to reset a student or teacher's password, please check out this article !

 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • 
 For Clever Admins: Account recovery and password reset 

• 
 For Students: Troubleshooting - Logging in to Clever 

• 
 For Students: Password recovery and resets 

• 
 For Teachers: How to reset passwords for yourself and students

title: For Clever Admins: Teacher Pages
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What resources can teachers add to a Teacher Page? 3. Disabling Teacher Pages during school breaks 4. Limiting Teacher Page customization 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Teachers can create personalized Teacher Pages to organize school applications, Clever Library applications, and their own links and PDFs that they want their students to access. While Teacher Pages provide teachers with a customized space to create a digital classroom environment, we recognize that, as the Clever Administrator, you may also need controls over Teacher Page settings.

 As the Clever Administrator, you have additional controls to manage what resources your teachers can share via Teacher Pages and when students can access their Teacher Pages. Learn more below! 

 
 What resources can teachers add to a Teacher Page?

 Teachers may add the following resources to their Teacher Page:

 • 
 District-wide applications you have added as the Clever admin

• 
 External links (URLs) to online websites and tools (e.g., Schooltube, Dictionary.com , a local library website, etc.)

• 
 External links to homework or assignments in Google Drive, Office Online, or Google Classroom

• 
 PDFs for students, like vocab lists, homework assignments, permission slips, etc.

• 
 Clever Library applications (if you have the library enabled ) 

 
 
 Disabling student access to Teacher Pages during school breaks

 During school breaks, most schools are working to organize students, teachers, and schedules. While schedules are in progress, users' classes may change, and it’s important to maintain the privacy of schedules until the school is ready to share them widely with students and teachers.

 To prevent accidental exposure of student schedules via Teacher Pages during school breaks, Clever administrators can turn off Teacher Pages for up to 90 days. When Teacher Pages are turned off, teachers can still access and edit their pages, but students won’t be able to see the pages. You can customize the date range during which you’d like to turn off pages. Your teachers will see a warning that their Teacher Page has been turned off until that date. Students will see a note in their Portal that Teacher Pages will be right back.

 To turn off Teacher Pages, navigate to your Clever Dashboard > Portal > Portal settings > Teacher Page Settings tab . Use the toggle to turn off Teacher Pages, then set your start and end dates within a 90-day range:

 

 

 

 Your teachers will see the following banner letting them know that Teacher Pages are disabled:

 

 

 

 Your students will see the following message in their Clever Portal:

 

 

 An example of a student’s Portal with the notification that Teacher Pages will be right back. 

 
 Limiting Teacher Page customization

 As the Clever Administrator, you have the best understanding of cybersecurity and school guidelines on content teachers can use with their students. If you’re concerned that teachers may add unapproved tools and websites to their Teacher Page, you can turn off the ability for teachers to add new links and PDFs to their Teacher Page.

 

 

 Please note: Enabling this setting will hide any resources previously added by teachers on their Teacher Page. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Can I permanently disable Teacher Pages? 

 • 
 Teacher Pages can be disabled up to 90 days.

 
 Can I block Teachers from adding library applications? 

 • 
 Yes! Learn how to recommend and block library applications here. 

 
 Will teachers and students be notified if I disable Teacher Pages? 

 • 
 Yes, both teachers and students will see a note in their Clever Portal that Teacher Pages have been temporarily disabled.

 
 Will teachers be notified if I turn off the ability to add links and PDFs? 

 • 
 Yes, teachers will receive a Portal announcement letting them know that links and PDFs have been turned off. Additionally, if they try to add a new link or PDF, they’ll see more information letting them know the feature has been disabled.

 
 
 Related articles: 

 • Clever Portal: Teacher Pages

title: For Teachers: Clever Badges for students
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. How do I print Badges for a class? 3. How do I print a Badge for a student? 4. How do I void a Badge for a student? 5. Logging in with a Clever Badge 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Badges allow students to hold up a physical badge to their device's webcam to get logged in, instead of typing in usernames and passwords. Badges are perfect for students who have trouble typing or remembering complex passwords - especially younger learners. Teachers get more time to teach, and students get more time to learn!

 Badges are tied to the student. As such, changes in school, teacher, or section will not affect the Badge.

 If the administrators on your school organization's Clever account have enabled Badges there are several ways you can manage Clever Badges as a teacher. You can print, regenerate, and void Badges for individual students, print Badges for whole classes, and even void Badges for all students en masse at the end of a school year.

 

 

 Please note: If you don't see the option to download student Badges, from your Student Roster tab, this means that Badges are not enabled for your school or grade level. Please reach out to your school organization's tech support for more information. 

 

 Pro Tip: Security Badges provide direct access to a student's Clever account and should be protected just like a password. Anyone who has access to a Badge can sign in as the student and access their accounts in both Clever and connected applications. Please protect the security of your students and do not share images of the Badge publicly on social media. 

 

 
 How do I print Badges for a class?

 • Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu 
 Student Roster of the teacher Clever Portal" src="/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0Pl000000cGrl&feoid=00N1P00000A2RcS&refid=0EMPl000003y2Jl"> 

• Select the desired Class. 
• Click Download class badges . This will download all Badges for eligible students in your class.
 
 The Badges PDF format matches the Avery label template for 2” square Avery labels . 

 Please note: Newly generated and printed Badges will no longer feature an expiration date. Existing printed Badges will continue to work past the expiration date. Admins are expected to void and print Badges annually during transition periods, like summer, to keep them secure ahead of the first day of school. 

 

 
 How do I print a Badge for a student?

 Classes 

 • Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu.
• Select the student's class.
• Select the student from your roster and click Download Badge. 
• The Badge will download to your computer, which you can print and give to the student.
 
 

 
 How do I void a Badge for a student? 

 Classes 

 • Navigate to the Student Roster tab under My Students in the left-hand menu 
• Select the student's class
• Select the student from your roster and click Void Badge. 
• The Badge will be voided. You can then click Download Badge to download a brand new Badge for that student.
 
 

 To help your student log in with a backup code , please visit: For Teachers: Troubleshooting - Student logins 

 ​​

 
 Logging in with a Clever Badge

 • Students navigate to www.clever.com/badges . If prompted, click Allow to provide Clever access to the device camera.
• Hold the Badge up to the camera. Once the Badge is scanned, a green checkmark will appear.
• Students will then be redirected to their Clever Portal.
 
 Please note: Clever Badges are designed only to be used at clever.com/badges . Scanning the Badge with a QR code reader won't log you in to Clever. 

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Do all students have Badges? 

 • Not all students have access to Badges. Your school organization's administrators can choose which users have access to Badges. If your student doesn't have access to a Clever Badge and will need one, please reach out to your school organization's tech support contact .
 
 Can teachers and staff login using Clever Badges? 

 • No. Clever Badges are only available for students as a primary login method. Teachers can use a Clever Badge as a second factor if your schools use Clever’s Classroom Multi-factor authentication (MFA).
 
 What do I do if I see in my student has a Badge that expires soon? 

 • 
 Existing printed Badges will continue to work past the expiration date. You can reach out to an admin to learn more about when you should expect Badges to be annually voided and reprinted.

title: For Clever Admins: Field mappings and customizing your data sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Viewing field mappings 3. Editing field mappings 4. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 

 

 Overview

 Sync field mappings refer to the relationship between fields from a student information system (SIS) and fields in Clever. A field mapping is when you specify the field in your SIS that you want to map to a specific data value in Clever. For example, Clever accepts student_email as a data value. Depending on the field (e.g., emailaddress.emailaddress, SchoolEmail, etc.) in which you store emails in your SIS, you may want to map a specific field to the student_email field.

 For SFTP syncs , Clever has an SFTP web upload feature with field mapping! Find out more here . 

 

 
 Viewing field mappings

 To view sync mappings, navigate to Data sources > SIS sync > Settings > Sync Mappings box > View field mappings . This page shows the relationship between each Clever Field & SIS Field.

 Clever Field ¹ 

 Clever Field refers to the data field in Clever where the SIS data is being stored.

 SIS Field ² 

 SIS Field refers to the name of the field in the SIS where the data is being pulled from. In the below screenshot, we can see that for students, School_id is being synced from HomeSchoolId³. If the SIS Field is followed by (custom mapping) ⁴, it means the mapping was added custom for your account. If the SIS field is blank⁵, Clever is not pulling any information from the SIS for the corresponding Clever Field.

 Actions ⁶ 

 Edit⁷ indicates that Clever Admins may customize which SIS field to sync to the corresponding Clever Field. If the value you are looking for is unavailable from the dropdown, please contact Clever Support with the exact name of the SIS field you want to map, and our team will investigate whether the mapping is supported. If the Action field is blank⁸, the SIS Field either is not customizable or requires a Clever Support team member to customize the field for your account. Contact Clever Support to learn about your available options.

 Record Types ⁹ 

 Use the view record type selectors to view the mappings for each record type. If you require a field mapping for a record type not listed, please contact Clever Support to learn about your options. 

 

 
 Editing field mappings

 Depending on your sync type, you may be able to customize which field in your SIS correlates to which field in Clever through a field mapping. If you utilize an SFTP sync, please see this article for more information on field mapping.

 If you utilize a Clever-managed auto sync, you may be able to complete basic field mappings yourself via your Clever Dashboard, or you can reach out to Clever Support, and our team can assist with more complex field mappings.

 

 For the following auto-syncs, some custom fields can be added and customized via your Clever Dashboard for self-service field mapping:

 • 
 Illuminate Auto Sync

• 
 PowerSchool Plugin Sync 

• 
 SchoolInsight Auto Sync 

• 
 Skyward LMS API 

 
 For the following syncs, custom fields can be added and customized by reaching out to Clever support:

 • 
 Aeries Auto Sync 

• 
 Blackbaud API sync 

• 
 Infinite Campus API sync 

• 
 Skyward Auto Sync 

• 
 Skyward OneRoster (Qmlativ) API 

 
 If you utilize one of the syncs above that supports self-service mappings and want to determine if you can edit a field mapping yourself, navigate to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page, locate the Sync Mappings box, and click on the View field mappings button .

 

 

 

 If you see an Edit button in the actions column, you can customize the field mapping for a specific data type (schools, teachers, students, sections, enrollments).

 Click on Edit to view a dropdown of available SIS Fields to map from.

 

 

 

 If you do not see the SIS field you wish to map from in the dropdown, please contact Clever Support to see if the field is available for mapping by our team.

 If the field is available, please select it and then click Update . This will save the field mapping, and the data will be pulled from the new field the next time your sync runs. You can push a manual sync following these instructions .

 Depending on the percentage of changes, this may cause a sync hold . Please review the hold carefully via your Clever Dashboard > SIS sync > Last Attempted Sync page . If the changes are intended, you can approve them and the new data will sync to Clever.

 
 

 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 Which sync types support self-service mappings? 

 • 
 Illuminate Auto Sync

• 
 PowerSchool Plugin Sync 

• 
 SchoolInsight Auto Sync 

• 
 Skyward LMS API 

 
 What if I do not see an edit button on the Field Mappings page? 

 • 
 You cannot complete a self-service field mapping for this field. Please contact Clever Support for additional information.

 
 What if I do not see the SIS field I want to map from? 

 • 
 Please contact Clever Support for additional information.

 
 What if I accidentally chose the wrong field for my mapping? 

 • 
 If your sync is on hold, discard the hold, then navigate back to your field mappings page and edit the mapping.

• 
 If your sync is not on hold or you already approved the hold, navigate to your field mappings page and edit the mapping. If more than 24 hours have passed since you completed the mapping, you may want to notify users or application partners that there may have been accidental data changes.

title: Infinite Campus API Sync
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview ​ 2. Requirement: Portal Accounts 3. Setup 4. Troubleshooting ​ 5. School Year/Term Rollover 6. Default Field Mappings 

 Overview

 This article provides information on the setup, maintenance, and troubleshooting of the Infinite Campus API sync. 

 Please note: In July 2025, Infinite Campus recently updated portions of its URL format. If you are encountering a sync error, please start by confirming the URL on your sync settings page matches the Base URL in your Infinite Campus instance. For more information, see below. 

 If you utilize or plan to utilize this sync, please note:

 • Your system version
 • Clever pulls data from Infinite Campus via the OneRoster Provisioning API, which is free for districts. In order to sync your data through the OneRoster API you'll need to make sure your Infinite Campus is on version 1733 or higher. • Districts using OneRoster provisioning prior to Campus 1733 are free to continue using it • District who were not using OneRoster provisioning prior to Campus 1733 may contact Campus Support to have the functionality enabled 
 

• Which version of OneRoster you plan to utilize. 1edtech (formerly IMS Global) has released the final version of the OneRoster v1.2 format. See below for information on how to set up your connection with OneRoster 1.2 or how to upgrade your existing connection. 
 
 The Infinite Campus API Sync:

 • Runs every six hours, by querying the OneRoster API
• Syncs all students who are designated as "active today" (activeToday = 1)
• Syncs all active staff members (employment.active = 1)
• Supports co-teachers for sections (sectionInfo_teacherPersonID, sectionInfo_teacher2PersonID, etc)
• Students and teachers need Infinite Campus Portal accounts in order to sync to Clever (see below)
• Support custom field mappings (please contact Clever Support to set this up)
• The available fields can be found here 
 
 Please Note: Infinite Campus OneRoster API sync cannot sync ell_status, frl_status, and iep_status. They can be uploaded via Custom data. Visit this article for instructions . SFTP upload of custom data is available to all schools. Please reach out to Clever Support for assistance with gaining access to your own Clever SFTP Directory. You'll need to set up any automatic exports to the remote site, as Clever doesn't provide support for automation scripting. 

 

 
 Requirement: User (Portal) Accounts

 In order for a person to be accessible in the Infinite Campus OneRoster API (and thus synced to Clever) the person needs a User (Portal) account in Infinite Campus.

 For documentation on creating user accounts, please review this documentation . To create user accounts in bulk, please review this documentation .

 To verify if a person has a user account, search for the user in Infinite Campus. If the person has a username, they have a user account. In the example below, "Amelkin, Federique" has a user account but "Freda, Irene" does not. As a result, "Freda, Irene" will not be accessible to Clever via the Infinite Campus OneRoster API. To add a user account in Infinite Campus, click Add User. 

 

 
 Setup

 1. Obtain API credentials 

 In order to configure the API sync you'll first generate API credentials in Infinite Campus: 

 • Navigate to Instruction > Settings > Digital Learning Application Configuration (or System Administration > Learning Interoperability in the Classic Navigation)
• Click Add Application , scroll to the bottom, and click Connect to Other Applications 
• Enter Clever as the application name 
• Click Generate New OneRoster Connection 
• For OneRoster version, select your OneRoster version > Generate 
• Share ClientID, Client Secret, Base URL and Token URL 
 
 

 Please Note: If you already have connected Infinite Campus to Clever and would like to move from OAuth 1.0 OneRoster v1.2 with OAuth 2.0, please go to your Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page and click the Upgrade button. Then, start at step 4 in the directions above. After moving to OneRoster 1.2 with OAuth 2.0, we recommend deleting or disabling your old connection that used OAuth 1.0. 

 IMPORTANT Regarding Staff: Before upgrading, be sure to check that any non-instructional staff you wish to include are associated with one of the following roles in Infinite Campus: counselor , districtadministrator , principal , siteadministrator , systemadministrator , or proctor . 

 

 

 

 2. Change sync type of Clever account 

 If you already have a Clever account and are switching to the API sync, please contact Clever Support . In your message, please specify which version of OneRoster (v1.1 or v1.2) you plan to use. Our team will need to switch your account sync type before you can move forward. If you are just getting started with Clever, the IC API sync will be the default when you choose your sync type for your account. You're free to move on to the next step!

 3. Enter API credentials in Clever 

 Please note: As of July 2025, Infinite Campus has recently updated portions of its URL format for the API sync. In order to continue to successfully sync to Clever using the Infinite Campus API sync, you may need to ensure your BaseURL in Infinite Campus matches the URL on your sync settings page. Please confirm the <portaldomain> and <appName> portions of the URL match in Infinite Campus and on your sync settings page . You can see examples of the URL format under Base URL below. 

 Once your account's sync type is the IC API sync, please take the credentials for the API and enter them into the Sync Settings page in your Clever Dashboard:

 • Base URL : Enter the base URL copied from Infinite Campus 
 • For OneRoster v1.2, the URL should look like this: /campus/api/oneroster/v1p2/ /ims/oneroster">https://<portaldomain>/campus/api/oneroster/v1p2/<appName>/ims/oneroster 
• For your OneRoster v1.1, the URL should look like this: /api/oneroster/v1p1/ /ims/oneroster/v1p1">https://<portaldomain>/api/oneroster/v1p1/<appName>/ims/oneroster/v1p1 
 

• Client ID: Enter the Client ID copied from Infinite Campus
• Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret copied from Infinite Campus
• Confirm Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret copied from Infinite Campus 
 
 4. Sync 

 Once you input and save your sync credential, the sync will automatically begin running and complete within approximately 30 minutes. You can also click the Sync Now on the Sync Settings page to start a sync at any time. 

 Please note: Infinite Campus syncs can be customized to sync nightly, instead of every six hours, but the time of day that the syncs occur is not customizable. Syncs can always be initiated at any time from your Clever Dashboard on the Data sources > SIS sync > Settings tab > Sync Controls box > Sync Now button. 

 

 
 Troubleshooting

 Common sync errors and troubleshooting steps 

 Below are some sync errors you may see in your Clever Dashboard. We recommend the following steps to resolve:

 
 Error

 
 Context and resolution

 
 There are currently no active academic sessions in your Infinite Campus instance. Your sync will resume once your academic sessions become active again. 
 Your current school year has ended, but the new school year has not yet begun.

 Clever begins pulling enrollments 21 days before the enrollments become active. If the start of school is more than 21 days away but you'd like to see their new school year enrollments syncing into Clever, please reach out to Clever Support and let us know the first day of school, as well as when you'd like your enrollments to begin syncing.

 
 Invalid Key/Secret specified. Please check SIS credentials on settings page. The credentials you have provided Clever on your Clever Dashboard > Data sources > SIS sync > Settings page don't match the credentials in Infinite Campus. Please follow these setup instructions and ensure the URL, client ID, and client secret match in Infinite Campus and Clever. 
 InfiniteCampus API is not currently

title: For Clever Admins: Using and recommending Clever Library
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Recommending the Library to teachers 3. Recommending Library applications to teachers 4. Viewing Library installs 5. Additional Library settings 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 The Clever Library is a safe way for teachers to explore new classroom tools. Instead of finding new applications anywhere on the internet that may have varying data privacy standards, teachers can try new apps in the Clever Library with built-in data-privacy guardrails and admin oversight.

 When a teacher adopts a Library application with SSO, Clever shares only the absolute minimum information: student first name, last initial, grade level, and course enrollment. Clever does not share any sensitive information with Library applications, including student numbers, SIS IDs, emails, state IDs, or demographic information. Furthermore, all Library applications sign a universal data privacy agreement.

 This article will guide you through supporting the use of the Clever Library and making recommendations for teachers. If you need information on setting up Library restrictions, please see this article .

 
 Recommending the Library to teachers

 Clever Library handles rostering automatically and ensures data minimization, making it safer than teachers signing up for apps independently. You can actively recommend the Library as the primary place for teachers to discover tools. Below is a suggested email template you can utilize to share more about the Clever Library with your teachers.

 
 Email template to recommend teachers use the Library for resources 
 
 Subject: The safest way to find new classroom apps 

 Hi Team,

 We know how important it is to find the right digital tools to engage your students. To help you do this safely and save you time on setup, we recommend using the Clever Library to discover new applications.

 Why use the Clever Library? 

 • 
 It's Safer: Apps in the Library sign a universal data privacy agreement and only receive the minimum necessary student data (just first name and last initial).

• 
 It's Faster: You don't need to manually create student accounts. Rostering is handled automatically through Clever.

• 
 It's Approved: Tools in the Library operate within our data privacy guardrails.

 
 You can access the Library directly from your Teacher Portal, and learn more about adding applications in this Clever article .

 

Best, [Admin Name] 
 

 
 Recommending Library applications to teachers

 To recommend resources, go to Clever Dashboard > Applications > Library Controls > Recommended. 

 Recommend a resource by searching for it by name. We also encourage adding notes to your recommendations to highlight to teachers why and for what purpose a resource is recommended. You may also delete a recommendation at any time.

 

 

 Please note: The Clever Library is a place for teachers to find and adopt resources - therefore, the only resources that can be recommended are those that support teacher sign-ups. School or district-adopted resources, such as Dreambox, Lexia, or iReady, aren't available in the Library. 

 

 Resources that are recommended will appear front and center when a teacher visits the Library. 

 

 When a teacher visits a recommended resource, your recommendation and associated notes are featured prominently. 

 

 

 
 Viewing Library Installs

 To view applications that have been installed by teachers, navigate to your Library controls > Applications tab . Here you'll see a column next to each application that shows you how many Teacher Installs the app has. 

 To view installs with greater detail, scroll to the bottom and select Export teacher install data. 

 

 The information provided in the downloadable CSV is described below:

 
 Field

 
 Description

 
 teacher_id The Clever ID of the teacher that installed the resource 
 first_name The first name of the teacher that installed the resource 
 last_name The last name of the teacher that installed the resource 
 email The email address of the teacher that installed the resource 
 school The school of the teacher that installed the resource 
 app_id The Clever ID of the resource installed 
 app_name The name of the installed resource 
 install_date The date the teacher added the resource 
 
 

 
 Additional Library settings

 To ensure administrators always have control over their school organization’s EdTech usage through Clever, there are additional controls admins can utilize to manage the Library. Below is a short list of these additional controls. You can learn more in this article.

 • 
 New application approvals: Configure settings so that any newly added Library application requires your approval before teachers can begin utilizing it.

• 
 New application notifications: Ensure you are informed about new Library applications by setting up email notifications.

• 
 Block a resource: If there is an application your school organization has determined your teachers shouldn’t utilize, you can block it in the Library. You can also bulk block applications.

• 
 Turn off the Clever Library: You can also elect to turn off the Clever Library for your school organization.

 
 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 Can I block the Library or specific applications? 

 • 
 Yes, please see this article for more information .

 
 

 
 Related articles: 

 • For Clever Admins: Managing Library restrictions and application approvals 
• For Clever Admins: Clever Library privacy

title: Achieve3000 and Actively Learn to McGraw Hill Migration
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Key steps: Districts already connected to McGraw Hill app on Clever 3. Key steps: Districts not yet connected to McGraw Hill app on Clever 4. Data requirements 5. Getting support 

 

 Overview

 Access for Achieve3000 and Actively Learn is transitioning to the McGraw Platform beginning with the 2025-26 school year.

 Who’s impacted? All districts that are currently connected to the Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn apps. 

 The McGraw Hill integration through Clever can do the following:

 • 
 Provide Single Sign-On (SSO) access to McGraw Hill content and classes.

• 
 Create and update class rosters on the McGraw Hill platform.

• 
 Assign McGraw Hill content to the appropriate users.

 
 We will be sharing a webinar recording that walks you through the following steps on our joint resource hub, clever.com/mcgraw-hill .

 Please note: If anyone at your district or school is using Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn for summer school, please hold off on making the changes detailed below until summer school is over. 

 

 
 Key steps: Districts already connected to McGraw Hill app on Clever

 • 
 Please review and update any data sharing rules set in the Clever Dashboard to include Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn roster data in the McGraw Hill application. You can click into your apps from your Clever Dashboard to review the data currently shared with Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn, then edit the sharing for McGraw Hill to include this data.

• 
 Assign McGraw Hill online content to the relevant courses/classes via McGraw Hill’s web-based tool: Access Manager. The McGraw Hill team can help with any questions about Access Manager.

• 
 Once the transition is completed, please confirm with the McGraw Hill team and then disconnect the Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn apps.

 
 

 
 Key steps: Districts not yet connected to McGraw Hill app on Clever

 • 
 Please request the McGraw Hill app on Clever by going to your Clever Dashboard > Applications > Add Applications and searching for McGraw Hill, then requesting the app.

• 
 Set your data sharing rules for McGraw Hill to reflect what is currently shared with Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn. Please see this article for more guidance on setting data sharing in Clever.

• 
 After the McGraw Hill team has received and reviewed your data, they’ll guide you through the remaining steps to complete your McGraw Hill integration through Clever, including guidance on when to disconnect Achieve3000 and/or Actively Learn.

 
 

 
 Data requirements

 The following fields must be successfully synced to Clever in order for your data to successfully sync with McGraw Hill:

 • 
 Term names, start dates, and end dates

• 
 Course names 

• 
 Teacher, student, and staff usernames

• 
 Teacher email addresses 

• 
 Student email (recommended but not required)

 
 Please note that user IDs (sis_ids) must be globally unique across your district. In other words, a teacher and a student cannot have the same sis_id in Clever.

 You can check if your data meets the requirements by navigating to the McGraw Hill > Data Quality tab in your Clever Dashboard. 

 For more information about the McGraw Hill Clever integration, please see this article . 

 
 Getting support

 Please stay tuned for our webinar recording detailed this migration process, which will be posted to clever.com/mcgraw-hill .

 Clever Support: 

 • 
 For help troubleshooting rosters, SIS Clever syncs, login settings, or data sharing rules, check out our Help Center where you can open a ticket with Clever Support using the blue chat box in the bottom right corner.

 
 McGraw Hill Digital Technical Support: 

 • For help with McGraw Hill platforms, please contact integrationsupport@mheducation.com .

title: For App Partners: What's Clever?
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Clever Complete 3. Clever's products for application partners 4. Clever's products for schools 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever is the global identity platform for education that works with any school, unifying rostering, single sign-on (SSO), and Learning Management System (LMS) connections. By automating data transfers directly from school organizations, Clever ensures that your application always has up-to-date roster information. Using Clever means that you’ll have fewer custom integrations, faster onboarding, and more time spent building great learning experiences.

 Clever also supports Single Sign-On (SSO) for students and teachers through the Clever Portal, making it easier and more secure for users to access your application with just one login.

 Best of all, Clever is free for school organizations in the U.S. and Canada, so there’s no cost barrier for them to connect with your app. You can learn more about Clever’s partner pricing here .

 Clever is built to be the most secure and reliable way for schools to connect with edtech applications—streamlining implementation and improving the user experience for everyone involved. 

 
 Clever Complete

 Clever Complete is the strategic solution purpose-built for edtech scalability. It connects your application to any school through a single, secure integration for rostering, SSO, and LMS connections, whether they use Clever or not.

 The Secure Sync rostering integration includes additional data fields, including demographics and attendance.

 

 Clever Complete provides universal connectivity to any school. For school organizations not currently on the Clever platform, this is enabled via the AnySchool capability, which is included in your Clever Complete agreement. 

 
 Clever's products for application partners

 Clever products for application partners include:

 • 
 Secure Sync rostering: Real-time, standardized rostering

• 
 Clever LMS Connect: LMS integrations with Schoology, Google Classroom, and Canvas that simplify single sign-on and save teachers time with gradebook syncs.

• 
 Clever SSO: Single sign-on into your application for all users

• 
 Clever Library: An integration for partners with free and freemium versions of their application that helps you promote and track teacher-driven adoption of your education products.

 
 Depending on the Clever integration you choose, you’ll also gain access to additional features via your Clever Dashboard that help with school data management, such as:

 • 
 Safety thresholds, which help prevent unintended data changes

• 
 Filters to block schools from sending unnecessary rostering data

• 
 Extended data fields such as demographics and attendance

• 
 Tools for troubleshooting

• 
 And more!

 
 Learn more about Clever’s product offerings here , or schedule a call with our team to learn more . 

 
 Clever's products for schools

 In addition to simplifying rostering and SSO for schools, Clever provides several other products and features that help simplify and secure edtech usage in schools, such as Analytics, Clever Identity Management (IDM), and Classroom MFA. Learn more about the school experience with Clever here . 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 What is the cost of Clever? 

 • 
 You can find general pricing information here , or set up a call with our team t o learn more and receive a quote .

 
 Where can I learn more about the integration process? 

 • 
 Consult our developer documentation here , or schedule a call with our team here .

 
 What if I already use Clever, but I’m interested in changing our integration? 

 Please reach out to your Application Success manager, or contact our support team for further assistance .

title: For Clever Admins: Downloading & deleting announcement and messaging history
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Downloading the announcement and messaging history for a student or teacher 3. Viewing the announcement and messaging transcript 4. Deleting transcripts 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview 

 Clever admins can download the announcement and messaging history for an individual student or teacher from that user’s Profile page in the Clever Dashboard!

 Clever Messaging was discontinued in January 2026 to allow the Clever teams to focus on features that enhance our identity platform offerings, including recent free tools like Substitute Access , role-based access control , and access logs . 

 Clever messaging previously enabled teachers and students to engage in bidirectional messaging via their Clever Portal.

 Announcements allow teachers to send unidirectional announcements to an entire class or all students assigned to them. Announcements are still available.

 You can still access transcripts for both messaging and announcements via a user’s Profile page. Clever maintains message history based on your settings here .

 

 
 

 Downloading the announcement and messaging history for a student or teacher

 1. Navigate to the student or teacher’s profile page in Clever by searching for their name, SIS ID, or Clever ID using the search tool in the upper right-hand corner of your Clever Dashboard. 2. From the student or teacher’s profile page, select Download message history . You should see a notification that an email with the transcript will be sent shortly. 3. You’ll receive an email with a link to the transcript. Please note that you’ll need to be logged in to your Clever account to open the link. The transcript will then open as a CSV file. 

 
 Viewing the announcement and messaging transcript

 The transcript will include every message or announcement the user has ever sent, including any that were deleted by the user. The transcript won’t include any messages or announcements that were deleted as part of the summer rollover process if you've opted into this (see below) . The transcript file will be organized into four fields:

 
 Field

 
 Description

 
 Date_created The timestamp of the message in UTC-0 
 From The Clever ID of the user that sent the message 
 To The Clever ID of the user that received the message. For an announcement this field will be blank. 
 Body The message sent from the sender to the recipient 
 
 

 

 
 Deleting messaging and announcement transcripts

 Clever has retained all messaging and announcement transcripts since the messaging product was launched in August 2020. Districts can opt in to have messages and announcements deleted by navigating to this page . If the toggle on the messaging settings page is turned on, the previous school year's messages and announcements will be deleted starting after August 1 of the new school year. This toggle will gray out after August 1 until the last year's messages and announcements are deleted.

 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

 Will I see any messages or announcements that a user has received in the transcript, or will it only list the messages the user has sent to other users? 

 • 
 Yes! The transcript will contain the messages the user has received, as well as the messages they sent before messaging was deprecated in January 2026, depending on your messaging history settings. Announcements beyond January 2026 will be included.

 
 Can I download the Messaging and announcement history for multiple users at one time? 

 • 
 No. Message and announcement history can only be downloaded on a per-user basis. 

 
 Can I delete a user’s messages or announcements from the Clever Dashboard? 

 • 
 No. This feature allows Clever Admins to view a user’s Messaging history, but not to delete messages. If you need to delete a Messaging thread for a user, please contact Clever Support with the Clever IDs of the sender and recipient, and the date of the thread that needs to be deleted, and we will be happy to assist!

title: Converting to HMHCore API Integration
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Warning 3. How do I know which connection I'm using? 4. Steps in Clever 5. Steps in HMH 6. Frequently Asked Questions 

 

 Overview

 In Summer 2022, HMH enabled the ability for districts to roster through Clever. At the time, districts who set up rostering were able to export data from Clever to HMH in One Roster format. Now, HMH is making use of Clever’s API to roster. We recommend all new districts use the API Connection to reduce points of failure. This article will guide those districts who wish to convert from the One Roster integration to the API integration for easier support and reliability. 

 
 Warning

 When you change rostering methods mid-year, IDs can change, which may result in the loss of data. It is suggested to make changes such as this one during the summer period. There are specific and detailed warnings about precautions that must be taken in the setup instructions. 

 
 How do I know which connection I'm using?

 In your Clever admin dashboard, confirm if you are set up with HMHCore. If you are, check under Settings > SFTP credentials. If this data is blank, your district is either already set up on the API or has not yet set up the HMHCore integration . If this information is complete, you are most likely making use of Clever’s OneRoster export to HMH. 

 
 Steps in Clever

 In order to switch to the API, you will first need to stop sending rostering data to HMH through Clever’s One Roster Connection by following these steps:

 • Navigate to HMHCore in your Clever Dashboard 
• Click on Settings 
• Scroll down to the bottom and disable your One Roster Exports 
 
 
 Steps in HMH

 If you have followed the steps above, you can start at Step 3 in the onboarding steps here: Rostering HMH by Clever API connection 

 
 Frequently Asked Questions

 Can I make this change mid school year?

 • As there is the possibility that IDs and data can change in this conversion, it is recommended to take place during a off-school period between academic years
 
 

 
 Related Articles: 

 • HMHCore Setup 
• HMH SAML SSO

title: Clever + easyCBM Setup Guide
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. Setup Process 3. Recommended Sharing Rules 4. FAQs 

 Overview

 The district version of easyCBM with Riverside Insights has partnered with Clever to make rostering easier. This guide contains important information about the setup process. 

 IMPORTANT: Please read before proceeding with the setup process

 Communication: Setting up a Clever integration requires a conversation with Riverside Insights Data Integration Team. Switching from self-rostering: To move to Clever, Riverside Insights and easyCBM need to work with districts to ensure teachers or school administrators who need ‘building’ or ‘district’ level access in easyCBM are be marked ‘staff’ in Clever and have ‘building’ access in Clever to the buildings shared with easyCBM. In the case of teachers who need easyCBM district-level access, a second ‘staff’ user with the same email address as the teacher user can be created in Clever, and they will match up and work as one user with both teacher and district-level (or building level) access. Further, teachers will be paired with students in easyCBM who are in common sections in Clever. When data is shared, easyCBM will review to make sure the Student ID matches the student’s sis_id in Clever. If it is not matching:

 • easyCBM requests district to send us a file with old/new mappings for Student ID matching the Clever sis_id field. CSV file with oldid,newid.
• District sends CSV file and halts further imports from manual or automated processes of CSV uploads themselves.
• EasyCBM migrates Student ID to new (matching) numbers using data sent and verifies students match up with Clever. This will re-match your historic data with your shared Clever data to retain historical data.
 

 
 Setup Process

 • Make sure your district is set up with Clever. If your district does not yet have a Clever account, you can get started by navigating to https://clever.com/signup https://clever.com/signup . 
• If your district is already on Clever, you can add easyCBM by following these steps:
 • Log into your Clever Dashboard and select Applications > Add Applications from the left navigation.
• Search for easyCBM
• When you request a connection with the application, you will also be asked if you would like to request a custom Launch Date. The Launch Date is the first day users can access the application through Clever.
• Once you have made your selections, click Request.
 
 
• These requests will create a notice to easyCBM, who will schedule an implementation call with you. The timing of these requests will be based on current volume and having a conversation with easyCBM to develop your plan.
• If you are already discussing integration with easyCBM, you may receive an invitation to connect directly in your Clever Dashboard. If so, please check your Clever Dashboard to accept your app invite . 
• After a meeting and once the sync occurs, easyCBM recommends logging in a few users to confirm that they can sign in.
 

 
 Recommended Sharing Rules

 For more information on sharing data with applications, please see: How do I set my sharing permissions for applications? 

EasyCBM recommends sharing only the grades, classes, and users at the school sites that will be using the program. This can include grades K-8 and specific subjects like reading or math. You can find more information about setting up sharing rules in this guide . easyCBM supports co-teachers, aids, or interventionists who may not be currently in Clever to use easyCBM. These staff will need to be set up in Clever by a system administrator and cannot be set up by individual teachers. In Clever, data quality issues will be represented as “data warnings” or “data errors”. For more information on resolving data quality issues, please review this article . 

 

 
 FAQs

 Why is a student or teacher missing from easyCBM? Please check to see if this user meets the following requirements:

 • Student or teacher is present in your SIS data
• Student or teacher is present in our Clever data and shared with easyCBM
 

 If the user is missing from your Clever data, but is in your SIS, please contact Clever Support . Who should I ask for help? If the issue is related to data within Clever or data coming from Clever, please contact Clever Support. If the issue is related to functionality within easyCBM, please contact CleverSupport@service.riversideinsights.com or Riverside Insights Tech Support, techsupport@service.riversideinsights.com .

title: Rostering applications with AnySchool
text:
This article contains the following: 1. Overview 2. What’s AnySchool? 3. Setting up your AnySchool connection 4. Obtaining SIS or other OneRoster API configuration instructions 5. Set up SSO 6. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 

 

 Overview

 Clever AnySchool enables applications to connect with districts or schools that haven't adopted Clever in one centralized place.

 If you currently use Clever, or are interested in signing up , you can easily roster your learning applications with Clever’s rostering services. Learn more here. 

 If you do not currently utilize Clever, application partners you work with may utilize Clever AnySchool to more easily synchronize data from your schools using your existing systems like your student information system (SIS) or another OneRoster API data source. This doesn’t require you to set up a Clever account. Instead, Clever acts as an extension of the learning application with no additional signup or credentials required. Clever is a sub-processor of the learning application and follows the terms of any data processing agreements you have in place with the application. Your student data privacy is a top priority for Clever. Learn more about our practices, FERPA, COPPA, GDPR, and state-level compliance in our Trust Center .

 In this article you’ll learn more about the AnySchool integration and setup process. 

 
 What’s AnySchool?

 If you use one of the data sources listed below, application partners using Clever may invite you to connect directly to their application using AnySchool, a capability within Clever Complete. As always, your student data privacy is a top priority for Clever. Learn more about our practices, FERPA, COPPA, GDPR, and state-level compliance in our Trust Center .

 If an application requests that you connect via AnySchool, you don’t need to set up a Clever account. Instead, you’ll use Clever to connect your preferred data source (e.g. Infinite Campus) with your application.

 AnySchool supports syncing roster data from the following sources:

 • 
 Infinite Campus (API v1.2)

• 
 Skyward

• 
 Blackbaud

• 
 Another OneRoster API connection (please reach out to your application for more details)

• 
 CSV upload (users.csv, sections.csv)

 
 
 Setting up your AnySchool connection

 If an application partner is interested in syncing your data via AnySchool, they’ll contact you to confirm that you use one of the data sources listed above and to confirm your email address for setup. Once confirmed, they’ll send you a custom setup link.

 Please note: Once you click the link to begin setup, you will have 48 hours to complete the process. If you don’t complete your setup within this timeframe, please contact the application partner to request a new setup link. 

 

 This link can’t be shared with other administrators as it is unique to your email address. To complete the set up:

 • 
 Navigate to the link the application provided.

• 
 Review the information for utilizing AnySchool and click the Get started button.

• 
 You’ll start by selecting your data source as either Infinite Campus, Skyward, Blackbaud, another OneRoster API data source, or CSV upload. Once you select a data source, click Continue. 

• 
 You’ll receive a prompt to enter additional information for your data source.

 • 
 If you’re utilizing Infinite Campus (API v1.2), Skyward, or Blackbaud, you’ll enter the credentials for connecting to your data source’s API. This includes Base URL, Client ID, and Client Secret. You can follow the instructions linked in the setup to obtain this information from your student information system (SIS), or find instructions below . If you need assistance locating this information, please contact your SIS team directly (i.e., if using Infinite Campus, reach out to the Infinite Campus support team for assistance locating the Base URL, Client ID, and Client Secret in their system).

• 
 If you’re utilizing another OneRoster API data source, follow the instructions and enter the credentials for your data source. If you have any questions, please reach out to the application you are connecting with.

• 
 If you’re utilizing CSV files, you’ll be provided with file templates for the users.csv and sections.csv. You’ll complete these files and then upload them to sync your data. Remember, your access link will last 48 hours. If you need a new link, please contact your application partner to provide it.

 

• 
 Once you’ve entered your SIS credentials, or you’ve completed the setup in your OneRoster API data source, or uploaded your CSV files, you’ll click Continue. 

• 
 A sync will automatically begin running with the application and they’ll provide next steps on the setup to you! If you upload CSV files, you’ll receive a sync report via email to warn of any data quality issues that may need to be resolved in your files.

 
 

 
 Obtaining SIS or other OneRoster API configuration instructions

 
 Infinite Campus Instructions 
 Please note, you must be using Infinite Campus API v1.2 for this setup. To generate or locate your Infinite Campus base URL, Client ID, and Client Secret:

 • 
 Navigate to Instruction > Settings > Digital Learning Application Configuration (or System Administration > Learning Interoperability in the Classic Navigation)

• 
 Click Add Application , scroll to the bottom, and click Connect to Other Applications 

• 
 Enter Clever as the application name 

• Click Generate New OneRoster Connection 
• For OneRoster version, select OneRoster 1.2 > Generate 
• Share ClientID, Client Secret, Base URL and Token URL 
 
 
 

 
 Skyward Instructions 
 Finding your API credentials for Skyward will differ depending on how you’re hosted. Please follow the instructions below:

 • 
 Secure Cloud (ISCorp) Hosted Districts : Create an IT Services service call to have ISCorp set up the Read Only API by visiting the Support Center or ISCorp Helpdesk. 

• 
 Managed Services / TN State Hosted Districts : Create an IT Services service call to have Skyward set up the Read Only API install by visiting the Support Center. 

• 
 Self-Hosted Districts : Use the API Server Launch Kit to set up the Read Only API. Optionally, districts can create an IT Services service call to have Skyward set up the Read Only API as a billable service by visiting the Support Center. 

 
 When setting up Skyward API, you must choose OA2 - OAuth 2.0. 

 
 

 
 Blackbaud Instructions 
 To generate credentials, follow these directions . Please note that your Web Access URL will look like this: https://{ schoolDomain}. myschoolapp.com/ims/oneroster/v1p1/ 

 We recommend the following settings:

 Provider 

 • 
 Name: Clever AnySchool

• 
 Provider: Standard

• 
 Select Use Legacy OAuth 1.0 authentication 

 
 Rostering Options 

 • 
 School Years: Current year

• 
 Multi-term classes: One record per term

• 
 Offering Types

 • 
 Academic Groups: Required

• 
 Activity Groups: Optional

• 
 Advisory Groups: Optional

 

• 
 Courses: Return all

• 
 Parents: Include or exclude all parents

 
 Gradebook Options No options need to be selected.

 

 Integration Mappings 

 The following fields require the district to set Integration Mappings in Blackbaud in order for the data to pass via Clever AnySchool: • Gender • Ethnicity For more information on Integration mappings, click here .

 
 

 
 Other OneRoster API Instructions 
 To set up a connection with another OneRoster API, please utilize your data source’s help center for setting up the API, or reach out to your application partner to see if they can provide additional setup steps.
